Distinct from `landlord_lien_prohibition` (statutory prohibition on landlord lien over tenant personalty), `tenant_abandonment` (procedures for treating possessions abandoned after tenant departure), and `abandoned_property_handling` (general personal-property abandonment) вҖ” this module addresses the SPECIFIC VEHICLE-REMOVAL pathway under state vehicle/transportation codes.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Signage | Notice/timing | Penalty for non-compliance |
|--------|-----------|---------|----------------|------------------------------|
| **California** | Cal. Veh. Code В§ 22658(a)/(l)(1) | 17" Г— 22" with 1" lettering at all entrances; phone numbers of local enforcement + towing companies under written agreement | 96 hours since parking-violation notice (for UnauthorizedParking reason) | **DOUBLE** storage/towing charges liability under В§ 22658(l)(1) |
| **Texas** | Tex. Occ. Code В§В§ 2308.252, 2308.253, 2308.255 | per В§ 2308.252 | 10-day advance written notice for apartment-complex tow based on missing registration/license plate (В§ 2308.253); written verification to towing company (В§ 2308.255) | per statute (towing company violation) |
| **Florida** | Fla. Stat. В§ 715.07(2)(a)(3)/(a)(4)/(c) + В§ 715.07(4) | required UNLESS single-family residence (В§ 715.07(2)(c) personal-notice exception) | 30-minute police/sheriff notification (В§ 715.07(2)(a)(4)); stop-mid-tow + half-fee redemption (В§ 715.07(4)) | per statute |
| **Default** | common-law trespass to chattel + state-specific vehicle code | required | reasonable advance notice | conversion claim exposure |

**California's В§ 22658(l)(1) DOUBLE-CHARGES LIABILITY is the heaviest landlord-side penalty across the four regimes.** When a CA landlord fails signage requirements OR tows under В§ 22658 before 96 hours have elapsed since the parking-violation notice, the landlord is liable for DOUBLE the storage or towing charges. Pinned by `ca_missing_signage_double_liability_engages`, `ca_signage_compliant_but_under_96_hours_violation_and_double_liability`, and `double_liability_only_engages_in_california_invariant` (3-regime sweep confirms TX/FL/Default do NOT engage double liability).

**Florida's storage-distance radius depends on county population.** В§ 715.07(2)(a)(3) requires the storage location to be within 10 miles of the point of removal in counties of 500,000+ population, and within 15 miles in counties under 500,000. Storage further than the radius is a statutory violation. Pinned by `fl_compliant_signage_storage_within_10_miles_500k_county_lawful` (5 mi in 500K+ county compliant), `fl_storage_exceeds_10_mile_radius_500k_county_violation` (11 mi in 500K+ county violates), `fl_storage_within_15_mile_radius_under_500k_county_lawful` (13 mi in under-500K county compliant), `fl_storage_exceeds_15_mile_radius_under_500k_county_violation` (16 mi in under-500K county violates).

**Florida's 30-minute police-notification deadline is a hard boundary.** В§ 715.07(2)(a)(4) requires the towing company to notify municipal police or sheriff WITHIN 30 MINUTES after completion of the tow вҖ” at minute 30 compliant, at minute 31 violation. Pinned by `fl_law_enforcement_notification_within_30_minutes_lawful` and `fl_law_enforcement_notification_at_31_minutes_violation`.

**Florida's В§ 715.07(2)(c) single-family residence exception** permits personal notice in lieu of posted signage. A single-family landlord may lawfully tow without entrance signage if personal notice is given to the vehicle owner. Pinned by `fl_single_family_residence_personal_notice_exception_signage_not_required` (no signage + single-family = compliant) vs `fl_no_signage_no_single_family_violation` (no signage + multifamily = violation).

**Texas's В§ 2308.253 apartment-complex carve-out for registration/plate-only tows.** A vehicle parked at an apartment complex may NOT be towed merely for lacking current registration or license plate UNLESS 10 days of advance written notice was given to the last known registered owner. Pinned by `tx_apartment_registration_tow_requires_10_day_notice_violation_when_absent` (no notice вҶ’ violation) and `tx_apartment_registration_tow_lawful_with_10_day_notice` (10-day notice вҶ’ lawful).

**California's 96-hour rule applies ONLY to UnauthorizedParking tows.** Other tow reasons (AbandonedVehicle, BlockingAccess) do not require the 96-hour wait. Pinned by `ca_abandoned_vehicle_no_96_hour_requirement_with_signage_lawful` and `ca_blocking_access_no_96_hour_requirement_with_signage_lawful`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/vehicle-towing-from-rental-property`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CA compliant signage + 96 hours lawful + zero double liability**; **CA missing signage triggers double liability** (В§ 22658(l)(1)); **CA signage compliant but under 96 hours triggers double liability**; **CA abandoned vehicle bypasses 96-hour rule with signage** (96-hour rule applies only to UnauthorizedParking); **CA blocking access bypasses 96-hour rule with signage**; **TX compliant signage and verification lawful**; **TX missing signage violation** (В§ 2308.252); **TX missing written verification violation** (В§ 2308.255); **TX apartment registration tow requires 10-day notice violation when absent** (В§ 2308.253); **TX apartment registration tow lawful with 10-day notice**; **FL compliant signage + storage within 10 miles in 500K+ county lawful**; **FL storage exceeds 10-mile radius 500K+ county violation** (11 mi); **FL storage within 15-mile radius under-500K county lawful** (13 mi); **FL storage exceeds 15-mile radius under-500K county violation** (16 mi); **FL law-enforcement notification within 30 minutes lawful**; **FL law-enforcement notification at 31 minutes violation**; **FL half-fee redemption not offered violation** (В§ 715.07(4)); **FL single-family residence personal-notice exception signage not required**; **FL no signage no single-family violation**; **default compliant signage lawful**; **default missing signage violation with conversion warning** (common-law trespass-to-chattel); **citation CA pins В§ 22658(a) + (l)(1)**; **citation TX pins all three sections** (В§В§ 2308.252, 2308.253, 2308.255); **citation FL pins В§ 715.07 subsections** ((2)(a)(3) + (a)(4) + (c) + В§ 715.07(4)); **double liability only engages in California invariant** (3-regime sweep TX/FL/Default never engage double); **CA 10-mile FL storage irrelevant invariant** (FL fields ignored under CA regime).

`traderview-expense::water_heater_earthquake_strap` is the **California Health & Safety Code В§ 19211 residential water-heater earthquake strap / brace / anchor compliance module** вҖ” when must a residential water heater be braced, anchored, or strapped to resist falling or horizontal displacement due to earthquake motion? Trader-landlord critical for CA rental owners: В§ 19211 violation categorically renders the building a NUISANCE under В§ 19211(c) AND breaches the implied warranty of habitability. Insurance carriers may deny earthquake / fire / flood claims tied to non-compliant water heaters. Distinct from siblings `meth_contamination_disclosure` (substance hazard), `mold_disclosure` (substance hazard), `fire_sprinkler_disclosure` (fire-suppression system), and `detector_requirements` (smoke + CO detectors).

**Two regimes** вҖ” California strict В§ 19211 vs Default no-statute:

| Regime | Authority | Scope | Standard | Nuisance trigger | Seller cert |
|--------|-----------|-------|----------|------------------|-------------|
| California | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 19210-19217 (Article 8) | вүӨ 120-gallon residential | California Plumbing Code Title 24 Part 5 | Yes (В§ 19211(c)) | Yes (В§ 19211(d), written on sale) |
| Default | International Plumbing Code (where adopted) + common-law premises liability | N/A | Local adoption | No statutory nuisance | No statutory cert |

**В§ 19211(a) braced/anchored/strapped requirement** вҖ” applies to all NEW + REPLACEMENT + EXISTING residential water heaters; minimum standard = California Plumbing Code (Title 24, Part 5) or city/county modifications. Pinned by `ca_50_gallon_strapped_compliant`, `ca_50_gallon_unstrapped_violates_engages_nuisance`, and `ca_uniquely_engages_strapping_requirement_invariant` (CA engages; Default does not).

**В§ 19211(b) 120-gallon capacity scope** вҖ” "water heater" means standard water heater with capacity вүӨ 120 gallons for which a pre-engineered strapping kit is readily available. Boundary tests pin 120 gallons вҶ’ in scope; 121 gallons вҶ’ out of scope. Pinned by `ca_120_gallon_boundary_inside_scope` (exactly 120), `ca_121_gallon_outside_scope` (over 120), `ca_119_gallon_within_scope_boundary` (under 120), `ca_zero_gallon_within_scope_boundary`, `ca_oversize_water_heater_no_violations` (200 gallons вҶ’ no strapping requirement), `capacity_boundary_truth_table_california` (8-cell sweep: 0/40/75/119/120 = in scope; 121/200/500 = out of scope), and `ca_note_pins_120_gallon_scope_definition`.

**В§ 19211(c) nuisance + habitability liability** вҖ” building or dwelling unit in violation is deemed a NUISANCE, breaching the implied warranty of habitability. Only engaged when within scope AND unstrapped (out-of-scope oversize water heaters do NOT trigger nuisance). Pinned by `ca_50_gallon_unstrapped_violates_engages_nuisance`, `ca_nuisance_only_engaged_when_within_scope_and_unstrapped` (200-gallon unstrapped вҶ’ no nuisance), `ca_uniquely_engages_nuisance_invariant` (CA only), and `ca_note_pins_nuisance_and_habitability`.

**В§ 19211(d) seller written certification gate** вҖ” seller of real property containing a water heater must certify В§ 19211 compliance IN WRITING to prospective purchaser at transfer of title. Applies only on property sale (not during ongoing tenancy). Pinned by `ca_property_sale_no_cert_violates`, `ca_property_sale_with_cert_compliant`, `ca_tenancy_no_cert_obligation`, `ca_tenancy_strapping_satisfied_no_cert_required`, and `ca_note_pins_seller_certification_obligation`.

**Two-violation stack on full failure** вҖ” unstrapped + missing seller certification on sale = exactly 2 violations. Pinned by `ca_both_violations_stack`.

**Default IPC + common-law fallback** вҖ” other seismic states (WA + OR + AK + HI) may have analogous building-code provisions adopted via the International Plumbing Code but lack CA's habitability-nuisance + civil-liability framework. Pinned by `default_no_strap_no_violation`, `default_no_seller_cert_obligation`, `default_citation_pins_ipc_and_premises`, and `default_note_pins_seismic_state_caveat` (WA + OR + AK + HI).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/water-heater-earthquake-strap`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **CA 50-gallon strapped compliant**; **CA 50-gallon unstrapped violates engages nuisance**; **CA 120-gallon boundary inside scope**; **CA 121-gallon outside scope**; **CA property sale no cert violates** (В§ 19211(d)); **CA property sale with cert compliant**; **CA tenancy no cert obligation**; **CA both violations stack** (2 simultaneous); **CA oversize water heater no violations** (200 gallons); **CA citation pins Article 8**; **CA note pins subsections** ((a)/(b)/(c)/(d)); **CA note pins 120-gallon scope definition**; **CA note pins nuisance and habitability**; **CA note pins seller certification obligation**; **default no strap no violation**; **default no seller cert obligation**; **default citation pins IPC and premises**; **default note pins seismic state caveat** (WA + OR + AK + HI); **two regimes routed correctly**; **CA uniquely engages strapping requirement invariant**; **CA uniquely engages nuisance invariant**; **capacity boundary truth table California** (8-cell sweep); **CA zero-gallon within scope boundary**; **CA 119-gallon within scope boundary**; **CA compliant with no property sale no cert compliant**; **CA tenancy strapping satisfied no cert required**; **CA unstrapped outside scope no violation**; **CA nuisance only engaged when within scope and unstrapped**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_lien_prohibition` is the **state landlord-lien-on-tenant-property compliance check** вҖ” addresses the landlord's right to assert a possessory lien on tenant's personal property DURING tenancy as security for unpaid rent. Distinct from `abandoned_property_handling` (disposition of belongings LEFT BEHIND after vacating) and `lockout_penalties` (unlawful self-help lockouts).

**Six regimes diverge along five axes**:

| Regime | Authority | Statutory lien | Court order required | Livelihood carve-out | Conspicuous bold required |
|--------|-----------|----------------|----------------------|------------------------|----------------------------|
| `Texas` | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 54.041(a) + В§ 54.043 | **YES** | No (statutory lien is automatic) | No | **YES** for contractual lien |
| `California` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1861(a) + В§В§ 1861.5вҖ“1861.27 | No | **YES** | **YES** вҖ” livelihood + household necessary items | No |
| `NewYork` | NY General Obligations Law | No | **YES** | No | No |
| `Massachusetts` | UCC Article 9 (voluntary only) | No | **YES** | No | No |
| `Illinois` | 735 ILCS 5/9 et seq. | No | **YES** (judgment required first) | No | No |
| `Default` | Common law | No | **YES** | No | No |

**Texas is the strongest pro-landlord regime in this matrix.** В§ 54.041(a) creates a STATUTORY landlord lien that attaches automatically to nonexempt property in the residence for unpaid rent вҖ” no court order needed. В§ 54.043 imposes a formatting requirement on the contractual lien: must be underlined or in conspicuous bold print in the lease. Pinned by `texas_statutory_lien_exists`, `texas_contractual_lien_without_bold_print_unenforceable`, `texas_contractual_lien_in_conspicuous_bold_enforceable`, `texas_self_help_permitted_without_court_order`, and the 6-regime invariants `only_texas_has_statutory_lien_invariant`, `only_texas_does_not_require_court_order_invariant`, `only_texas_requires_conspicuous_bold_for_contractual_lien_invariant`.

**California В§ 1861(a) imposes the dual gate: court order AND livelihood carve-out.** Even with a court order, the lien may NOT be enforced against property necessary to the tenant's livelihood or any necessary household items. The carve-out is a non-waivable statutory protection. Pinned by `california_court_order_required`, `california_self_help_without_court_order_violation`, `california_with_court_order_no_self_help_violation`, `california_livelihood_property_carve_out_violation`, `california_livelihood_carve_out_even_with_court_order_violation`, and the 6-regime invariant `only_california_has_livelihood_carve_out_invariant`.

**Five non-Texas regimes uniformly require a court order before the landlord may take possession of tenant property.** California, New York, Massachusetts, Illinois, and Default all bar self-help possession without judicial process. Pinned by `self_help_without_court_order_violates_5_regimes_invariant` (5 regimes confirmed violate on self-help without order; Texas confirmed does NOT).

**Illinois additionally requires a JUDGMENT for unpaid rent before lien enforcement.** Unpaid rent alone does not automatically create a lien вҖ” the landlord must file a lawsuit, obtain judgment, and proceed via judgment-lien process. Pinned by `illinois_self_help_without_judgment_violation` and `illinois_self_help_with_judgment_no_violation_specific_to_il`.

**Massachusetts permits only voluntary UCC Article 9 liens for ordinary residential tenancies.** No statutory landlord's lien exists; storage liens are available to warehouse operators only.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-lien-prohibition`. Twenty-three tests pin: **TX statutory lien exists + contractual lien without bold print unenforceable + with bold print enforceable + self-help permitted without court order**; **CA court order required + self-help without court order violation + with court order no violation + livelihood property carve-out violation + livelihood carve-out even with court order**; **NY no statutory lien + self-help violation**; **MA no statutory lien (UCC Article 9 only)**; **IL judgment required first + self-help without judgment violation + with judgment compliant**; **Default court order required**; **only-Texas-has-statutory-lien 6-regime invariant**; **only-California-has-livelihood-carve-out 6-regime invariant**; **only-Texas-requires-conspicuous-bold 6-regime invariant**; **only-Texas-does-not-require-court-order 6-regime invariant**; **self-help without court order violates 5 regimes invariant** (TX excluded); **citation pins authority per regime**; **sibling-module note across all 6 regimes** (UX-text regression for abandoned_property_handling + DURING tenancy scope).

`traderview-expense::landlord_master_key_retention` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord master key retention and access disclosure framework module** вҖ” when may a landlord retain master/duplicate keys to tenant-occupied units, what disclosure obligations attach, and what restrictions govern landlord access? Distinct from sibling `entry_notice` (general 24-hour pre-entry notice), `landlord_emergency_entry_notice` (post-entry notice after emergency), `landlord_mid_tenancy_rekeying` (mid-tenancy rekey requests), `lock_change_between_tenancies` (between-tenancy locks), `dv_survivor_lock_change` (DV-survivor locks), and `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (biometric locks). Trader-landlord critical because traders operating multi-unit rental portfolios routinely maintain master-key systems for emergency response + repair access + pest control; mismanagement exposes landlord to statutory civil penalties for unauthorized entry, tort claims for invasion of privacy and trespass, tenant constructive eviction, emotional distress damages (iter 453), and criminal trespass liability.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Key Statutes | Penalty for Unauthorized Entry |
|--------------|--------------|--------------------------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954 (24-hour notice + normal business hours) | В§ 1954(c) **CIVIL PENALTY up to $2,000 per incident** |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.156 (7-day rekey + landlord master-key expense) | В§ 92.156 statutory remedies + В§ 92.157 tenant request rekey |
| **New York** | NY Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 51 + В§ 53; NYC HMC В§ 27-2008; NY RPL В§ 235-d; NYC Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 | **$1,000-$10,000 civil penalty per unauthorized entry incident** |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B + В§ 15F; 105 CMR 410.480; Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973) | **TRIPLE DAMAGES** + attorney fees + injunctive relief |

**Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954 permitted entry categories (six)**:
1. **В§ 1954(a)(1)** вҖ” EMERGENCY (fire, gas leak, burst water pipe; no notice required)
2. **В§ 1954(a)(2)** вҖ” necessary or agreed-upon repairs/decorations/alterations/improvements (24-hour notice)
3. **В§ 1954(a)(3)** вҖ” show unit to prospective tenants/purchasers/mortgagees/lenders/appraisers/contractors (24-hour notice; oral notice permitted for sale within 120 days of listing)
4. **В§ 1954(a)(4)** вҖ” court order
5. **В§ 1954(a)(5)** вҖ” tenant has abandoned or surrendered premises
6. **В§ 1954(a)(6)** вҖ” install/repair/test/maintain water-conserving plumbing fixtures (40-hour notice)

**Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954 notice requirements (5 elements)**: (1) written notice (oral permitted only for sale within 120 days of listing); (2) 24 hours presumptively reasonable; (3) date + approximate time + PURPOSE of entry; (4) entry only during NORMAL BUSINESS HOURS (typically 8 AM - 6 PM Monday-Friday); (5) В§ 1954(c) civil penalty up to $2,000 per unauthorized-purpose incident.

**Texas В§ 92.156 rekey requirements (4 elements)**:
1. В§ 92.156(a) вҖ” security device must be REKEYED by landlord at landlord's expense not later than **7th day after tenant turnover date**
2. В§ 92.156(b) вҖ” expense of rekeying for use or change of landlord's master key **MUST be paid by landlord**
3. В§ 92.156(c) вҖ” landlord cannot require tenant to pay for landlord-master-key change
4. В§ 92.157 вҖ” tenant request for rekey at tenant expense permitted; landlord must perform within 7 days of tenant request

**NYC Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 51 + В§ 53 key management (4 elements)**:
1. В§ 51 вҖ” owner shall furnish front door key + apartment door key + mailbox key to tenant at occupancy
2. В§ 53 вҖ” owner-installed locks must permit tenant lock change at tenant expense subject to providing copy to landlord
3. NYC HMC В§ 27-2008 вҖ” locks must conform to minimum standards
4. NYC Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 вҖ” $1,000-$10,000 civil penalty per unauthorized entry incident

**Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B + В§ 15F (3 elements)**: (1) В§ 15B landlord must provide keys at lease start; cannot retain keys after tenant vacates; (2) 105 CMR 410.480 minimum lock standards; (3) **В§ 15F UNAUTHORIZED ENTRY exposes landlord to TRIPLE DAMAGES** + attorney fees + injunctive relief; combined with c. 186 В§ 14 quiet enjoyment breach.

**Master-key system best-practice elements (6)**: (1) WRITTEN LEASE DISCLOSURE that landlord retains master/duplicate keys; (2) LOG of every key issuance to maintenance staff + contractors + vendors; (3) В§ 1954/В§ 235-d/В§ 15B advance notice for every non-emergency entry; (4) RECEIVE RECEIPTS / log entries with date + time + purpose + person entering; (5) CONTROLLED ACCESS protocols (master copy in locked landlord safe; key cards electronically logged); (6) PROHIBITION on landlord personal use of master keys for non-business purposes.

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) CA trader enters tenant unit with master key without 24-hour notice for routine inspection вҖ” В§ 1954 violation plus civil penalty up to $2,000 per incident plus invasion of privacy claim plus IIED under iter 453; (2) TX trader fails to rekey unit within 7 days of new tenancy вҖ” В§ 92.156 violation plus actual damages plus one month's rent plus $500 civil penalty plus attorney's fees; (3) NY trader enters rent-stabilized unit repeatedly without notice claiming non-emergency вҖ” NY RPL В§ 235-d landlord harassment plus NYC Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 $1,000-$10,000 per violation plus constructive eviction; (4) MA trader enters with master key during tenant vacation without notice вҖ” c. 186 В§ 15F UNAUTHORIZED ENTRY TRIPLE DAMAGES plus attorney fees plus injunctive relief plus quiet enjoyment breach; (5) trader best-practice mitigation: written lease disclosure + key-issuance log + В§ 1954 notice + locked safe + per-contractor receipt + electronic log.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-master-key-retention`. Thirty-one tests pin: **CA compliant repair authorized**; **CA emergency no notice required**; **CA repair no 24-hour notice unauthorized**; **CA repair outside business hours violation**; **CA routine inspection without notice civil penalty** ($2,000); **CA show unit with notice authorized**; **CA water conservation with notice authorized**; **TX rekey within 7 days paid by landlord compliant**; **TX no rekey within 7 days violation**; **TX tenant paid master key violation** (В§ 92.156(b) + В§ 92.157); **NY unauthorized entry civil penalty per violation** ($1,000-$10,000); **MA unauthorized entry triple damages engaged** (В§ 15F + В§ 14); **MA authorized entry no triple damages**; **best-practice score maxed** (5/5); **no lease disclosure best-practice gap**; **no key log best-practice gap**; **court order entry authorized**; **tenant abandonment entry authorized**; **entry purpose truth table eight cells**; **MA uniquely triple damages invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954 + В§ 1954(a)(1)-(6) + В§ 1954(c) + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.156 + В§ 92.156(a) + В§ 92.156(b) + В§ 92.157 + NY Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 51 + В§ 53 + NYC HMC В§ 27-2008 + NY RPL В§ 235-b + В§ 235-d + NYC Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 + Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B + В§ 15F + 105 CMR 410.480 + Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway + HSTPA 2019); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins CA six entry categories**; **note pins CA notice requirements five elements**; **note pins TX В§ 92.156 four elements**; **note pins NYC MDL В§ 51 + В§ 53 four elements**; **note pins MA В§ 15B + В§ 15F three elements**; **note pins master-key best-practice six elements**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::abandoned_property_handling` is the **state abandoned-tenant-personal-property landlord compliance check** вҖ” addresses WHAT the landlord must do with BELONGINGS the tenant left behind after vacating. Distinct from `tenant_abandonment` (which addresses when the tenant has vacated and the landlord may declare the unit abandoned); this module handles the BELONGINGS leftover. Three states ship detailed statutory procedures with materially different timeline + value thresholds + sale duties.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Min waiting period | Value threshold | Required delivery | Disposition |
|--------|---------|---------------------|-------------------|---------------------|--------------|
| `California` | Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1980-1991 | **15 days** personal delivery / **18 days** first-class mail | **$700** | Personal OR first-class mail | Under $700 keep/dispose; $700+ MUST public auction (proceeds to county treasury) |
| `Texas` | Tex. Prop. Code В§В§ 54.044 + 92.0081 | **30 days** before sale | $0 (no value gate) | **BOTH** first-class AND certified mail return-receipt | Public auction permitted |
| `Washington` | RCW 59.18.310 | **45 days** if value вүҘ $250 OR includes keepsakes; **7 days** if value < $250 + no keepsakes | **$250** | Written notice | Keep/dispose under $250 (no keepsakes); auction at or above $250 |
| `Default` | вҖ” | 30 days (common-law proxy) | $0 | Reasonable | Keep/dispose |

**Three regression-critical distinguishers**:
- **CA's $700 auction threshold** is unique вҖ” TX has none; WA uses $250.
- **TX's dual-method delivery** (BOTH first-class AND certified return-receipt) is unique вҖ” CA accepts either; WA accepts written notice generally.
- **WA's keepsake carve-out** (personal papers + family pictures + keepsakes always require 45-day rule regardless of value) is unique вҖ” CA and TX have no such category.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/abandoned-property-handling`. Twenty-five tests pin: **CA personal-delivery 15 days under $700 вҶ’ keep/dispose** + В§ 1988 citation; **CA first-class-mail 18 days at/above $700 вҶ’ public auction** (regression вҖ” must auction over threshold); **CA at $700 boundary must auction** (вүҘ strict); **CA at $699.99 under threshold keep/dispose**; **CA 14 days personal-delivery insufficient**; **CA 17 days first-class insufficient (18 required)** + note regression; **CA no notice blocks** + В§ 1983 citation; **TX 30-day dual-method satisfied** + В§ 92.0081 citation; **TX only first-class insufficient delivery** + "CERTIFIED" citation regression (regression-critical вҖ” dual-method required); **TX only certified insufficient delivery**; **TX 29 days insufficient**; **WA 45-day above $250 threshold** + auction route; **WA 7-day under $250 no keepsakes** + keep/dispose route; **WA under-$250-with-keepsakes-requires-45-days** (regression вҖ” keepsake carve-out forces 45-day rule); **WA at $250 boundary uses 45-day** (вүҘ strict); **WA above $250 with keepsakes 45-day**; **WA no notice blocks**; **default 30 days compliant**; **default no notice blocks**; **state routing CA/TX/WA/Default**; **case-insensitive routing**; **only-CA-has-$700-auction-threshold** (single-test 3-regime invariant); **only-WA-has-keepsake-carve-out** (single-test 2-regime invariant вҖ” same low-value-with-keepsakes input only triggers WA 45-day requirement); **citations pin authorities**; **CA personal-delivery 15 < first-class-mail 18** boundary (same property + different delivery methods yield different clocks).

`traderview-expense::adverse_action_notice` is the **tenant adverse action notice compliance table (federal FCRA + state add-ons)** вҖ” when a landlord denies an applicant, requires a higher deposit, requires a cosigner, or otherwise takes unfavorable action based on a consumer report (credit, criminal, eviction history), **FCRA В§ 615 (15 U.S.C. В§ 1681m)** requires a written notice with specific federally-mandated elements. Three states (CA, WA, NY) layer additional state-specific content requirements on top. Two regimes:

| Regime                       | States                  | Source                                                                |
|------------------------------|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **StateAddsRequirements**    | CA, WA, NY              | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1785.20.5 + В§ 1786 ICRA (specific reason + but-for + 12-point font); Wash. RCW 59.18.257 + RCW 19.182.110 (specific reason + but-for analysis); N.Y. GBL В§ 380-b (written notice with stated reasons + tenant screening report copy delivery) |
| **FederalFcraOnly**          | 47 other states + DC    | FCRA В§ 615 (15 U.S.C. В§ 1681m) floor only; no state-added content requirements ([FTC Using Consumer Reports guide](https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/using-consumer-reports-what-landlords-need-know)) |

**FCRA В§ 615 required elements** (federal floor вҖ” all five must be present in the written notice):

1. Statement that adverse action was taken
2. **Name, address, and telephone number** of the consumer reporting agency (CRA) that supplied the report
3. Statement that the **CRA did NOT make the adverse decision** and cannot explain why
4. Notice of applicant's right to obtain a **FREE copy of the report within 60 days** from the CRA
5. Notice of applicant's right to **dispute inaccurate information** with the CRA

**What constitutes "adverse action"** triggering the notice вҖ” the model includes 5 explicit AdverseActionType variants: `DenialOfApplication`, `HigherDeposit`, `HigherRent`, `CosignerRequired`, `ConditionalShorterTerm`, plus `None`. The notice obligation triggers only when (a) the type is not None AND (b) `consumer_report_contributed_to_decision: true`. Adverse action taken without consumer report contribution (e.g., insufficient income on application alone) does NOT trigger FCRA В§ 615 ([CFPB on tenant screening report denials](https://www.consumerfinance.gov/ask-cfpb/what-should-i-do-if-my-rental-application-is-denied-because-of-a-tenant-screening-report-en-2105/)).

**California's three additional requirements** are the strictest in the country: (1) the notice must specify the SPECIFIC reason for the adverse action (not just "based on consumer report"); (2) but-for reason analysis required; (3) credit check disclosure must be in 12-point font separately. Pinned by `only_ca_has_formatting_requirements` invariant.

**Washington requires specific reason + but-for analysis** but NOT formatting standards вҖ” distinguished from California in `wa_missing_formatting_does_not_violate`.

**New York requires specific reason ONLY** вҖ” does not impose but-for analysis or formatting requirements вҖ” pinned by `ny_specific_reason_required_but_for_not` (which separately verifies that missing but-for in NY still complies but missing specific-reason violates).

**Module invariants** (2 separately pinned):
- StateAddsRequirements regime contains only CA + WA + NY (`state_adds_regime_only_3_states`).
- Only CA has formatting standards (`only_ca_has_formatting_requirements`).

**No fixed timing pin**: FCRA В§ 615 specifies only "reasonable time" вҖ” module does NOT invent a specific day count. Industry best practice is 5 business days from decision.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/adverse-action-check`. Twenty-one tests pin: **TX FCRA-only minimum satisfies** (no state additions); TX missing CRA contact violates; TX missing 60-day-free-copy right violates; **CA FCRA-only minimum violates state additions** (3 state-specific elements missing flagged separately); CA fully compliant passes; **CA HigherDeposit type triggers notice requirement**; **WA missing but-for violates**; **WA missing formatting does NOT violate** (not a WA requirement вҖ” distinguishes from CA); **NY specific reason required but but-for not** (missing but-for complies in NY; missing specific-reason violates); **None adverse action type no notice required**; **adverse action without consumer report contribution no FCRA trigger**; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **StateAddsRequirements regime only 3 states** invariant; **only CA has formatting requirements** invariant; unknown state falls back to FCRA-only; lowercase normalizes; **CosignerRequired triggers notice**; HigherRent triggers notice; note compliant includes regime label; note violation lists missing elements.

`traderview-expense::adverse_possession_claim` is the **squatter / adverse-possession statutory limitations check** вҖ” when may a third party acquire title to landlord property by continuous unauthorized occupation? Critical for absentee landlords (snowbirds, trader-investors with multiple rentals, out-of-state owners) where property may be occupied without notice for extended periods. Distinct from `landlord_possession_delivery` (delivery of possession at tenancy start), `tenant_abandonment` (declaring tenant possessions abandoned), and `foreclosure_tenant_rights` (post-foreclosure PTFA tenant occupation) вҖ” this module addresses the title-acquisition pathway under state statutes of limitations.

**Five regimes**:

| Regime | Period | Statute | Tax payment | Special requirement |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|----------------------|
| **California** | 5 years | Cal. Civ. Proc. Code В§ 325 | required; must be in assessed year per Gilardi v. Hallam (1981) | five common-law elements |
| **Texas** | 3 / 5 / 10 / 25 years | Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code В§В§ 16.024вҖ“16.028 | required only for 3-year + 5-year paths | tiered by color of title / cultivation / recorded deed / disability |
| **Florida** | 7 years | Fla. Stat. В§В§ 95.16 (with color), 95.18 (without color) | required throughout for both paths | В§ 95.18 requires 1-year tax cure + 30-day appraiser return (strictest in US) |
| **New York** | 10 years | N.Y. RPAPL В§В§ 501(3), 511, 521 + CPLR В§ 212(a) | not required | post-2008 claim-of-right reasonable-basis under В§ 501(3) вҖ” bad-faith possessor barred (Walling v. Prysbylo overruled) |
| **Default** | 20 years | common-law (state-specific 15-30 years) | varies | five common-law elements |

**California's 5-year period is the SHORTEST adverse-possession statute in the US.** Pinned by `shortest_required_period_california_5_years_invariant` (4-regime sweep confirms CA 5 < FL 7 < NY 10 < TX 10-year cultivation path). California's tax-payment requirement is the strictest enforcement mechanism вҖ” under Gilardi v. Hallam, the claimant must pay taxes in the year they are ASSESSED (not back taxes). Pinned by `ca_5_years_with_taxes_and_five_elements_claim_satisfied`, `ca_5_years_without_taxes_fails`, `ca_notes_gilardi_assessed_year_rule`.

**Texas's four-tier Ch. 16 framework is the most complex.** В§ 16.024 (3-year color of title) вҖ” shortest absolute period in TX, but requires an instrument purporting to convey valid title. В§ 16.025 (5-year recorded deed + cultivation/use + taxes). В§ 16.026 (10-year peaceable + cultivation; NO tax payment, NO color of title required вҖ” the bare possession path). В§ 16.027 (25-year regardless of disability) and В§ 16.028 (25-year void recorded deed). Pinned by `tx_3_year_color_of_title_satisfied`, `tx_5_year_recorded_deed_requires_tax_payment`, `tx_10_year_cultivation_no_taxes_required`, `tx_25_year_satisfied_regardless_of_disability`, `tx_three_year_is_shortest_subsection_invariant`.

**Florida В§ 95.18 is the strictest US adverse-possession regime.** Without color of title, the claimant must (a) pay all outstanding taxes and matured special improvement liens WITHIN 1 YEAR of entering possession (cure-window deadline), (b) file a return with the property appraiser by proper legal description within 30 days of paying taxes (appraiser-return requirement), and (c) continue paying taxes for all remaining years. Failure to pay taxes defeats the claim entirely. Pinned by `fl_without_color_full_compliance_satisfied`, `fl_without_color_missing_appraiser_return_fails`, `fl_without_color_missing_one_year_tax_cure_fails`, `fl_with_color_no_appraiser_or_one_year_cure_needed`, `fl_no_tax_payment_fails_in_both_paths`.

**New York's 2008 amendment (effective July 8, 2008) overruled Walling v. Prysbylo.** Under RPAPL В§ 501(3), claim of right now requires "a reasonable basis for the belief that the property belongs to the adverse possessor." Bad-faith possessors who KNOW they do not own the property can no longer acquire title. This applies to both В§ 511 (under written instrument) and В§ 521 (not under written instrument). Pinned by `ny_10_years_with_claim_of_right_satisfied`, `ny_without_reasonable_basis_fails`, `ny_notes_walling_overruled`.

**Five common-law elements** (actual + open and notorious + hostile + exclusive + continuous) are universally required regardless of regime. Pinned by `five_elements_missing_universal_violation` (CA path with element gap fails despite tax compliance + 5-year period).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/adverse-possession-claim`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **CA 5 years with taxes and five elements claim satisfied**; **CA 5 years without taxes fails**; **CA 4 years with taxes fails period**; **CA notes Gilardi v. Hallam assessed-year rule**; **TX 3-year color of title satisfied**; **TX 5-year recorded deed requires tax payment**; **TX 5-year recorded deed with tax payment satisfied**; **TX 10-year cultivation no taxes required** (bare possession path); **TX 25-year satisfied regardless of disability**; **TX no path specified violation** (Ch. 16 subsection required); **FL color of title 7 years with taxes satisfied** (В§ 95.16); **FL without color full compliance satisfied** (В§ 95.18 7-year + appraiser return + 1-year tax cure); **FL without color missing appraiser return fails**; **FL without color missing 1-year tax cure fails**; **FL with color no appraiser or 1-year-cure needed** (В§ 95.16 cuts the burdens); **FL no tax payment fails in both paths**; **NY 10 years with claim of right satisfied**; **NY without reasonable basis fails** (В§ 501(3) post-2008); **NY notes Walling overruled by July 8 2008 amendment**; **five elements missing universal violation** (all regimes); **default regime returns 20-year period**; **default regime 19 years fails period**; **citation CA pins В§ 325 + Gilardi 1981**; **citation TX pins all five subsections** (В§В§ 16.024, 16.025, 16.026, 16.027, 16.028); **citation FL pins both sections** (В§В§ 95.16, 95.18); **citation NY pins В§ 501(3) + CPLR В§ 212(a) + Walling overruled by July 8 2008**; **shortest required period California 5 years invariant** (4-regime sweep: CA 5 < FL 7 < NY 10, CA 5 < TX 10 cultivation); **TX 3-year is shortest subsection invariant** (3 < 5).

`traderview-expense::tenant_topa` is the **state Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act (TOPA) / right of first refusal on landlord sale compliance table** вҖ” directly affects every landlord-trader's ability to sell a rental property. In jurisdictions with TOPA, the seller must give the tenant first opportunity to match a third-party offer, and the tenant has a statutory window to exercise the right. Recent legislative wave (2020-2024). Four regimes:

| Regime                          | States | Source                                                                |
|---------------------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **AllSalesGeneralTopa**         | DC     | D.C. Code В§ 42-3404.02 et seq. (TOPA, 1980); В§ 42-3404.08 right of first refusal вҖ” ALL residential sales, ANY unit count, 15-day exercise window; foreclosure/tax/bankruptcy EXEMPT from "sale" definition ([D.C. Council Code В§ 42-3404.02](https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/42-3404.02), [DC OTA TOPA page](https://ota.dc.gov/page/tenant-opportunity-purchase-act-topa)) |
| **NarrowResidentialTopa**       | MD     | Md. HB 693 of 2024 вҖ” Renter's Rights and Stabilization Act; 3-or-fewer dwelling units ([Ballard Spahr вҖ” DC/MD Multifamily Update 2024](https://www.ballardspahr.com/insights/alerts-and-articles/2024/05/the-latest-rent-control-news-new-md-state-housing-laws-eviction-challenges)) |
| **ForeclosureOnlyPriority**     | CA     | Cal. SB 1079 of 2020 (eff. 2021-01-01 through 2026-01-01); 1-4 unit SFR; post-foreclosure priority for eligible tenant buyers, prospective owner-occupants, community nonprofits вҖ” 15-day same-bid window + 45-day exceeding-bid window ([Cal. Mortgage Assn. on SB 1079](https://www.californiamortgageassociation.org/news-articles/article/senate-bill-1079-californias-new-foreclosure-and-post-foreclosure-process/)) |
| **NoStateTopa**                 | 47 other states + DC subtracted (already counted) | No statewide TOPA; local ordinances may apply (Boston / Somerville pending; MA H.1260 / S.786 statewide opt-in pending; MN Twin Cities considering) |

**DC TOPA вҖ” the prototype**: D.C. Code В§ 42-3404.02 (1980, oldest in country) is uniquely aggressive вҖ” applies to ALL residential rental sales regardless of unit count or building type. Under В§ 42-3404.08, the tenant or tenant organization has 15 days after receiving a valid third-party sales contract to exercise the right of first refusal at the same price and terms. The В§ 42-3404.02 definition of "sell" / "sale" explicitly EXEMPTS foreclosure sale, tax sale, and bankruptcy sale вҖ” so a landlord facing financial distress can still complete a forced sale without triggering TOPA. Pinned by `dc_voluntary_sale_creates_tenant_rofr` + `dc_large_apartment_complex_still_covered` (200-unit building covered) + 3 exemption tests + 15-day boundary tests (day 15 within / day 16 expired) + tenant-exercised stops further exercisability.

**Maryland HB 693 of 2024**: 3-or-fewer unit residential properties only. Concept-similar to DC TOPA (often called "Maryland TOPA") but far narrower scope вҖ” excludes apartment buildings, focuses on single-family-through-triplex stock. Pinned by `md_3_unit_property_covered` (boundary), `md_4_unit_property_not_covered` (boundary), `md_single_family_covered`.

**California SB 1079 вҖ” distinct framework, NOT true TOPA**: applies ONLY to FORECLOSURE sales of 1-4 unit single-family residences. Tenants, prospective owner-occupants, and community nonprofits can submit a bid matching the highest bidder's price within **15 days** after the initial trustee sale, or a bid EXCEEDING the highest bidder's price within **45 days**. SB 1079 sunsets January 1, 2026 unless extended. Pinned by `ca_voluntary_sale_not_covered` (key regression target вҖ” CA does NOT have voluntary-sale TOPA) + `ca_foreclosure_1_unit_covered` + `ca_foreclosure_4_unit_covered` + `ca_foreclosure_5_unit_not_covered` (boundary).

**Module invariants** (3 separately pinned):
- DC only on AllSalesGeneralTopa regime (`only_dc_uses_all_sales_general_topa`)
- MD only on NarrowResidentialTopa regime (`only_md_uses_narrow_residential_topa`)
- CA only on ForeclosureOnlyPriority regime (`only_ca_uses_foreclosure_only_priority`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/topa-check`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **DC voluntary sale creates ROFR** + 200-unit building still covered; **DC foreclosure exempt** + tax sale exempt + bankruptcy sale exempt (3 sale-type exemptions individually); **DC 15-day window** day-15 within / day-16 expired; tenant-already-exercised no longer exercisable; **MD 3-unit covered / 4-unit not covered** (boundary); MD single-family covered; **CA foreclosure 4-unit covered / 5-unit not covered / 1-unit covered**; **CA voluntary sale NOT covered** (key regression вҖ” CA is foreclosure-only); CA tax sale not covered; **NoStateTopa 7-state sweep** (TX/FL/NY/WA/MA/OR/GA never create ROFR); MA citation mentions H.1260 pending; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 regime-uniqueness invariants** (DC / MD / CA); unknown state falls back to NoStateTopa; lowercase normalizes; **DC note describes AllSalesGeneralTopa path** with foreclosure-exempt callout; MD note describes вүӨ3 unit cap; CA note describes 15-day + 45-day priority window; expired window described in DC note ("EXPIRED").

`traderview-expense::lease_auto_renewal` is the **state lease automatic renewal / evergreen-clause disclosure compliance table** вҖ” when a residential lease contains an auto-renewal clause that requires either party to give specific notice to prevent renewal, **four states statutorily require the LANDLORD to send a written reminder notice before the tenant's non-renewal-notice deadline**. Without that reminder, the auto-renewal clause is **UNENFORCEABLE** вҖ” the lease simply expires at the end of the original term. Three regimes:

| Regime                                          | States      | Source                                                                |
|-------------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **PreNonrenewalNotificationFifteenToThirtyDays** | FL, WI, NY  | Fla. Stat. В§ 83.575 (15-30 days + fees/penalties listing required); Wis. Stat. В§ 704.15 (15-30 days); N.Y. GBL В§ 5-905 (15-30 days, must "call attention" to the clause) |
| **PreCancellationDeadlineThirtyToSixtyDays**     | IL          | 815 ILCS 601/10 Automatic Contract Renewal Act вҖ” 30-60 days for 12-month+ contracts with renewal > 1 month; clause must be "clear and conspicuous" in the original contract |
| **NoStateDisclosureRequirement**                | 46 other states + DC | Auto-renewal clauses generally enforceable as written; common-law unconscionability may apply in extreme cases |

**Florida's unique fees-listing requirement** is the strictest content rule: in addition to the 15-30 day window, the reminder notice MUST list "all fees, penalties, and other charges applicable to the tenant" upon renewal under Fla. Stat. В§ 83.575. Missing the fees enumeration alone voids the auto-renewal regardless of timing compliance. Pinned by `only_fl_requires_fees_listing` invariant + `fl_notice_missing_fees_listing_violates` + `wi_does_not_require_fees_listing` (regression target вҖ” distinguishes WI from FL on same window).

**Illinois's clear-and-conspicuous clause requirement** is the unique 2-prong test: the original auto-renewal clause itself must be drafted in clear-and-conspicuous form (boldface, separately initialed, etc.) AND the 30-60 day reminder must be sent. Both required. Pinned by `only_il_requires_clear_conspicuous_clause` invariant + `il_clause_not_clear_and_conspicuous_violates`.

**Boundary tests pinned for all 4 regulated states**:
- FL day-14 outside / day-15 boundary / day-30 boundary / day-31 outside
- WI 15-30 day window similar
- NY 15-30 day window similar  
- IL day-29 outside / day-30 boundary / day-60 boundary / day-61 outside

**Module invariants** (4 separately pinned):
- FL + WI + NY only on PreNonrenewalNotificationFifteenToThirtyDays (`fl_wi_ny_share_15_30_day_regime`)
- IL unique on PreCancellationDeadlineThirtyToSixtyDays (`il_unique_30_60_day_regime`)
- FL only with fees-listing requirement (`only_fl_requires_fees_listing`)
- IL only with clear-and-conspicuous clause requirement (`only_il_requires_clear_conspicuous_clause`)

Sources: [Fla. Stat. В§ 83.575 (FindLaw)](https://codes.findlaw.com/fl/title-vi-civil-practice-and-procedure/fl-st-sect-83-575/), [Wis. Stat. В§ 704.15 / Tenant Resource Center](https://www.tenantresourcecenter.org/notice_when_ending_a_tenancy), [815 ILCS 601/10 (FindLaw)](https://codes.findlaw.com/il/chapter-815-business-transactions/il-st-sect-815-601-10/), [N.Y. GBL В§ 5-903 (newyork.public.law)](https://newyork.public.law/laws/n.y._general_obligations_law_section_5-903).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/auto-renewal-check`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **FL compliant with 20-day notice + fees listed**; **FL 14-day before-window violates / 15-day exact boundary complies / 30-day exact boundary complies / 31-day after-window violates** (4 boundary pins); FL missing fees listing violates (regression against WI); **FL no notice at all unenforceable** with UNENFORCEABLE note; **WI compliant + WI does NOT require fees listing** (regression target); **NY 15-30 day window complies / NY outside-window violates**; **IL 30-60 day window with 45-day complies / 29-day below minimum violates / 30-day exact / 60-day exact / 61-day above max violates** (4 boundary pins); **IL clause not clear-and-conspicuous violates** (state-specific); **NoState 6-state sweep** (TX/CA/MA/OR/DC/OH always enforceable); **no auto-renewal clause вҶ’ no disclosure required**; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **4 invariants** (FL+WI+NY share regime / IL unique regime / FL unique fees-listing / IL unique clear-conspicuous); unknown state falls back; lowercase normalizes; FL violation note lists fees issue; enforceable note describes 15-30 day window satisfaction.

`traderview-expense::lease_translation` is the **state mandatory lease translation requirement compliance table** вҖ” when a landlord-trader leases to a non-English-speaking tenant, California's Civ. Code В§ 1632 is uniquely strict in the country and grants the tenant a CONTRACT RESCISSION RIGHT if the translation rule is violated. Three regimes:

| Regime                                          | States | Source                                                                |
|-------------------------------------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **MandatoryTranslationFiveLanguages**           | CA     | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1632 вҖ” residential lease > 1 month negotiated PRIMARILY in **Spanish, Chinese, Tagalog, Vietnamese, or Korean** requires complete accurate translation BEFORE execution; failure = tenant right to rescind contract + restitution ([Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1632 (LegInfo)](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&sectionNum=1632.), [Bornstein Law вҖ” CA Translation Act](https://bornstein.law/california-translation-act-for-landlords/)) |
| **EnglishRequiredTranslationsNotBinding**       | FL     | Florida вҖ” English required for legal contracts; translations are courtesy copies but not binding (Fla. Stat. В§ 636.015 narrow exception for prepaid health / discount plan orgs only вҖ” NOT residential leases) ([Maspons вҖ” English Language Requirement in FL](https://maspons.com/the-english-language-requirement-in-florida-for-legal-documents/)) |
| **NoStateTranslationRequirement**               | 48 other states + DC | No state-level private-landlord translation mandate; federal FHA limited English proficiency (LEP) guidance applies to FEDERALLY-FUNDED housing only |

**California's exact 5-language list** is pinned as the load-bearing distinguishing feature:

1. Spanish
2. Chinese
3. Tagalog
4. Vietnamese
5. Korean

These are the only 5 languages covered by В§ 1632. Russian, Arabic, Hindi, Japanese, Punjabi, etc. are NOT covered. Pinned by `ca_covered_languages_exactly_5` invariant + `ca_other_foreign_language_no_requirement` (regression target) + individual sweep for each of the 5 covered languages.

**Three statutory scope tests under CA В§ 1632** (all must be present for the requirement to apply):

1. Lease must be **RESIDENTIAL** (not commercial)
2. Lease duration must be **MORE THAN 1 month** (excludes day-rentals, weekly rentals)
3. Negotiation must have been PRIMARILY in one of the 5 covered languages (English-only negotiations don't trigger)

Each scope test independently exits via `not required`. Pinned by `ca_non_residential_lease_outside_scope`, `ca_short_term_lease_one_month_outside_scope` + `ca_two_month_lease_within_scope`, and `ca_english_lease_no_translation_required`.

**Two CA failure modes** both trigger the tenant rescission right:

- Landlord provided NO translation at all (`ca_spanish_no_translation_provided_rescission_right`)
- Landlord provided translation AFTER lease execution (`ca_translation_after_execution_violates`) вҖ” В§ 1632 specifically requires translation BEFORE execution so consumer can review terms in their primary language

**Module invariants** (2 separately pinned):
- CA only on MandatoryTranslationFiveLanguages regime (`ca_unique_mandatory_translation_regime`)
- FL only on EnglishRequiredTranslationsNotBinding regime (`fl_unique_english_required_regime`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-translation-check`. Twenty-four tests pin: **CA Spanish lease with translation complies**; **all 5 covered languages individually required** (Chinese, Tagalog, Vietnamese, Korean sweep); **CA English lease no translation required**; **CA other-foreign-language not covered** (regression вҖ” only 5 listed languages covered); **CA no-translation-provided triggers rescission right** with TENANT MAY RESCIND note; **CA translation-after-execution violates** (before-execution requirement); **CA short-term 1-month lease outside scope** + 2-month within scope (boundary); **CA non-residential lease outside scope**; **FL no translation mandate** + "translations are courtesy copies but not binding" note; **NoStateTranslationRequirement 7-state sweep** (TX/NY/IL/WA/MA/OR/DC always compliant); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **2 regime-uniqueness invariants** (CA / FL); unknown state falls back; lowercase normalizes; **CA covered languages exactly 5** invariant; CA violation note describes RESCIND; CA compliant note describes В§ 1632 SATISFIED.

`traderview-expense::rent_receipts` is the **state rent receipt requirement compliance table** вҖ” universal for any landlord-trader collecting rent in cash. New York post-HSTPA-2019 requires receipts for EVERY payment regardless of method; four states (CA, MD, NJ, IL) mandate receipts for cash payments only; Washington requires receipts upon tenant request; most states have no statewide mandate. Failure to provide a required receipt can preclude the landlord from later denying receipt of the payment in an eviction action вҖ” a costly evidentiary handicap. Four regimes:

| Regime                                       | States      | Source                                                                |
|----------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **MandatoryReceiptEveryPayment**             | NY          | N.Y. RPL В§ 235-e (HSTPA of 2019) вҖ” written receipt for EVERY rent payment regardless of method; receipt must state date, amount, period, apartment, signature/title |
| **MandatoryReceiptCashPaymentsOnly**         | CA, MD, NJ, IL | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1499; Md. Real Prop. В§ 8-208; N.J. Truth-in-Renting Act; IL Chicago RLTO + 765 ILCS 745/8 Mobile Home Park Act вҖ” receipts only for cash payments; check/electronic/money-order payments self-document |
| **ReceiptUponTenantRequest**                 | WA          | Wash. RCW 59.18.063 вҖ” written receipt required ONLY upon tenant request, for any payment method ([WA RCW 59.18.063 official text](https://app.leg.wa.gov/rcw/default.aspx?cite=59.18.063)) |
| **NoStateReceiptRequirement**                | 45 other states + DC | Common-law and local ordinances may apply; includes MA whose ch. 186 В§ 15B(2)(c) receipts cover only security deposit + last month rent (tracked elsewhere), NOT monthly rent |

**NY post-HSTPA вҖ” strictest framework**: under RPL В§ 235-e the landlord MUST provide a written receipt for EVERY rent payment, regardless of whether the tenant paid in cash, check, electronic transfer, or money order. The receipt must state date, amount, period covered, apartment number, and signature plus title of the person receiving payment. Pinned by all 4 payment-method paths (cash / check / electronic / money order) вҖ” all triggering the receipt requirement in NY ([Rentlane вҖ” Rent Receipt Requirements by State](https://getrentlane.com/blog/rent-receipt-template-requirements.html)).

**4 cash-only states share the practical anti-fraud rationale**: cash is untraceable, so the receipt is the tenant's only proof of payment. Check, electronic transfer, and money-order payments generate their own paper trail (bank statement, transaction confirmation, money-order stub), so the landlord-receipt mandate doesn't extend to those methods. Pinned by `ca_check_payment_no_receipt_required` + `ca_electronic_payment_no_receipt_required` + `ca_money_order_no_receipt_required` вҖ” the 3 non-cash methods all comply without receipt under cash-only regime.

**WA on-request regime is uniquely tenant-driven**: the landlord has no automatic duty until the tenant SPECIFICALLY requests a receipt. Pinned by `wa_no_request_no_receipt_required` (compliant by default) + `wa_with_request_requires_receipt` (request triggers duty) + `wa_request_check_payment_also_requires_receipt` (WA covers any method when requested, unlike cash-only states).

**MA carveout pinned explicitly**: Massachusetts has receipt rules under Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 186 В§ 15B(2)(c) but they apply ONLY to security deposit and last month's rent transactions, NOT to monthly rent payments. Module classifies MA as `NoStateReceiptRequirement` with a citation that explains the deposit-only carveout. Pinned by `ma_monthly_rent_no_state_requirement_for_receipt` + `ma_citation_explains_deposit_carveout` ([Mass.gov AG's Guide to Landlord/Tenant Rights](https://www.mass.gov/doc/attorney-generals-guide-to-landlordtenant-rights/download)).

**Module invariants** (3 separately pinned):
- NY only on MandatoryReceiptEveryPayment regime (`only_ny_uses_every_payment_regime`)
- CA + MD + NJ + IL only (exactly 4 states) on MandatoryReceiptCashPaymentsOnly regime (`cash_only_regime_states_4`)
- WA only on ReceiptUponTenantRequest regime (`only_wa_uses_on_request_regime`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-receipt-check`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **NY all 4 payment methods require receipt** (cash + check + electronic + money order); NY with receipt complies; **CA cash payment requires receipt** + check / electronic / money order NO receipt required (3 non-cash paths); MD + NJ + IL cash payments require receipt (3-state sweep); **WA no-request no-receipt-required** (compliant by default) + with-request requires receipt + request-check-payment also requires receipt (any method); **MA monthly rent no state requirement** with deposit-carveout citation; **NoStateReceiptRequirement 6-state sweep** (TX/FL/OR/OH/GA/VA compliant by default); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 regime-uniqueness invariants** (NY only / CA+MD+NJ+IL count exactly 4 / WA only); unknown state falls back; lowercase normalizes; NY violation note mentions RPL В§ 235-e + VIOLATION; CA check-compliant note mentions paper trail; CA cash violation mentions landlord exposure for failure-of-receipt defense in eviction.

`traderview-expense::rent_stabilized_mci_iai_passthrough` is the **NY rent-stabilized Major Capital Improvement (MCI) + Individual Apartment Improvement (IAI) rent passthrough compliance module** вҖ” when may a trader-landlord owning a rent-stabilized NYC building pass through capital-improvement costs to tenants via rent increase? Trader-landlord critical for any rent-stabilized NYC building owner doing building-wide capital work (MCI) or unit-level upgrades (IAI). Improperly calculated MCI/IAI passthrough triggers DHCR audit + tenant complaint + retroactive refund order. Distinct from siblings `rent_control` (general rent control), `rent_control_lease_disclosure` (lease-side disclosure), `landlord_annual_rent_statement`, and `rent_increase_notice_period`.

**Three improvement types**:

| Type | Source | Cost cap | Building amortization | Increase formula |
|------|--------|----------|-----------------------|-------------------|
| MCI | 9 NYCRR В§ 2202.4(d) + HSTPA 2019 | No cost cap | 12 yrs (вүӨ 35 units) / 12.5 yrs (> 35 units) | Subject to **2% annual cap on collectibility** |
| IAI standard | 9 NYCRR В§ 2202.4 + NY Budget eff. Oct 17, 2024 | **$30,000** over 15 years (up from $15K HSTPA 2019) | None | **1/168** (вүӨ 35 units) / **1/180** (> 35 units); now **PERMANENT** |
| IAI special tier | 9 NYCRR В§ 2202.4 + NY Budget 2024 | **$50,000** over 15 years | None | **1/144** (вүӨ 35 units) / **1/156** (> 35 units) |

**MCI (Major Capital Improvement)** вҖ” building-wide capital improvements (boiler + roof + facade + plumbing risers). NY HSTPA 2019 capped MCI rent increases at 2% maximum collectibility per year (down from earlier higher caps). Requires DHCR application + tenant notification + approval before collection. Pinned by `mci_approved_within_2_percent_cap_lawful`, `mci_over_2_percent_cap_capped`, `mci_no_dhcr_approval_violates`, `mci_amortization_35_units_or_less_12_years`, `mci_amortization_over_35_units_12_5_years`, `mci_citation_pins_authorities` (В§ 2202.4(d) + HSTPA 2019 + Pub. L. 2019 ch. 36 + DHCR Fact Sheet #24), `mci_note_pins_2_percent_cap_and_12_year_amortization`, and `mci_uniquely_engages_2_percent_cap_invariant` (only MCI engages 2% cap; IAI does not).

**IAI standard tier (effective October 17, 2024)** вҖ” NY Budget Bill 2024-2025 raised cap from HSTPA 2019's $15,000 to $30,000 over 15 years and made rent increases PERMANENT (previously temporary). Rent increase formula: 1/168 of cost for buildings вүӨ 35 units; 1/180 for buildings > 35 units. Pinned by `iai_standard_within_cap_lawful`, `iai_standard_at_30k_boundary_lawful` (exactly $30K compliant), `iai_standard_over_30k_cap_violates` ($30K + 1Вў violates), `iai_standard_35_units_or_less_uses_168_divisor`, `iai_standard_over_35_units_uses_180_divisor`, `iai_standard_citation_pins_october_17_2024`, and `iai_standard_note_pins_30000_cap_and_permanent`.

**IAI special tier вҖ” $50,000 cap for unique apartment situations** вҖ” applies to units continuously occupied вүҘ 25 years OR registered vacant in 2022 + 2023 + 2024. Rent increase formula: 1/144 of cost (вүӨ 35 units); 1/156 (> 35 units). Pinned by `iai_special_within_cap_lawful`, `iai_special_at_50k_boundary_lawful` (exactly $50K compliant), `iai_special_over_50k_cap_violates`, `iai_special_35_units_or_less_uses_144_divisor`, `iai_special_over_35_units_uses_156_divisor`, `iai_special_citation_pins_special_tier`, `iai_special_note_pins_25_year_or_vacant_2022_2024`, and `iai_special_uniquely_uses_50k_cap_invariant` ($40K cost OK at special tier but violates at standard tier).

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative cost / rent clamped via `.max(0)`; saturating multiplication for 2% cap calculation prevents overflow even at i64::MAX. Pinned by `defensive_negative_cost_clamped`, `defensive_negative_rent_clamped`, and `defensive_overflow_saturating`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-stabilized-mci-iai-passthrough`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **MCI approved within 2% cap lawful**; **MCI over 2% cap capped**; **MCI no DHCR approval violates**; **MCI amortization 35 units or less 12 years**; **MCI amortization over 35 units 12.5 years**; **MCI citation pins authorities** (В§ 2202.4(d) + HSTPA 2019 + Pub. L. 2019 ch. 36 + DHCR Fact Sheet #24); **MCI note pins 2% cap and 12-year amortization**; **IAI standard within cap lawful**; **IAI standard at $30K boundary lawful**; **IAI standard over $30K cap violates**; **IAI standard 35 units or less uses 168 divisor**; **IAI standard over 35 units uses 180 divisor**; **IAI standard citation pins October 17, 2024**; **IAI standard note pins $30,000 cap and PERMANENT**; **IAI special within cap lawful**; **IAI special at $50K boundary lawful**; **IAI special over $50K cap violates**; **IAI special 35 units or less uses 144 divisor**; **IAI special over 35 units uses 156 divisor**; **IAI special citation pins special tier**; **IAI special note pins 25-year or vacant 2022-2024**; **improvement type truth table caps**; **IAI special uniquely uses $50K cap invariant** (special tier permits $40K; standard tier does not); **MCI uniquely engages 2% cap invariant**; **three improvement types routed correctly**; **defensive negative cost clamped**; **defensive negative rent clamped**; **defensive overflow saturating** (i64::MAX).

`traderview-expense::repair_and_deduct` is the **state tenant repair-and-deduct / rent escrow / habitability withholding compliance table** вҖ” universal for any landlord-trader; approximately 35 states + DC recognize some form of tenant remedy when the landlord fails the implied warranty of habitability. Mechanics vary sharply from self-help repair-and-deduct (capped 1-4 months rent) to court-ordered escrow to common-law Marini doctrine. Four regimes:

| Regime                                       | States | Source                                                                |
|----------------------------------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **SelfHelpRepairAndDeduct**                  | 48 states (default) | Cap varies: CA 1 month + max 2/yr (Civ. Code В§ 1942); TX greater of $500 or 1 month (Prop. Code В§ 92.0561); MA up to **4 months** rent вҖ” highest in country (G.L. c. 111 В§ 127L + c. 239 В§ 8A); WA 1 month + 30-day notice (RCW 59.18.100); IL greater of $500 or 1 month (765 ILCS 705 + Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-110) |
| **CourtOrderedRentEscrowOnly**               | MD     | Md. Real Prop. В§ 8-211 вҖ” NO self-help; tenant must petition District Court for rent escrow; court orders escrow ONLY on "substantial-and-serious-threat" finding ([Md. People's Law Library вҖ” Rent Escrow](https://www.peoples-law.org/rent-escrow-when-landlord-fails-make-repairs)) |
| **CommonLawMariniDoctrine**                  | NJ     | Marini v. Ireland, 56 N.J. 130 (1970) вҖ” common-law habitability defense; withheld rent MUST be deposited with court at eviction trial or defense is WAIVED ([Centanni Law on Marini doctrine](https://centannilaw.com/landlord-tenant-eviction-defense/)) |
| **NoticeThenWithholdOrTerminate**            | FL     | Fla. Stat. В§ 83.56(1) вҖ” 7-day written notice of noncompliance required; tenant may then withhold rent OR terminate; no formal repair-and-deduct |

**Massachusetts's 4-month cap is uniquely high** вҖ” the only state in the country where a tenant can spend up to 4 months' worth of rent on repairs and deduct it from rent (Mass. G.L. c. 111 В§ 127L + c. 239 В§ 8A). Most other repair-and-deduct states cap at 1 month. Pinned by `ma_uniquely_has_4_month_cap` invariant (and verified no other state has > 1 month) + `ma_within_4_months_cap_compliant` ($8k under $8k cap at $2k rent) + `ma_exceeds_4_months_cap_violates`.

**Texas / Illinois "greater of $500 or 1 month rent"** вҖ” protects tenants with low rent who need to make repairs that exceed 1 month's rent but stay below $500. Module computes `effective_repair_cap_dollars = max(cap_months Г— monthly_rent, dollar_floor)`. Pinned by `tx_low_rent_uses_500_dollar_floor` ($300 rent вҶ’ $500 effective cap) + `tx_high_rent_uses_1_month_cap` ($2k rent вҶ’ $2k cap) + `tx_low_rent_exceeds_500_floor_violates` ($501 over $500 floor).

**California's "max 2 per 12 months" anti-abuse rule** вҖ” prevents tenants from using repair-and-deduct as a continuous rent reduction strategy. Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942(c). Pinned by `ca_first_use_within_year_ok` (1 prior use OK) + `ca_third_use_in_12_months_violates` (2 prior вҶ’ 3rd is over limit).

**Maryland's no-self-help framework**: a Maryland tenant CANNOT spend their own money on repairs and deduct from rent вҖ” the only remedy is petitioning the District Court for rent escrow under Real Prop. В§ 8-211. The court orders rent into escrow only when the habitability violation constitutes a "substantial and serious threat" to life, health, or safety. Pinned by `md_no_court_order_no_remedy` (without escrow order = no remedy) + `md_court_order_plus_substantial_threat_remedy_available` + `md_court_order_without_substantial_threat_no_remedy` (both required).

**New Jersey's Marini doctrine deposit-at-trial requirement**: in NJ, a tenant withholding rent under Marini v. Ireland MUST deposit the FULL amount of withheld rent with the court on the day of the eviction trial. Failure to deposit WAIVES the habitability defense entirely. The landlord gets a judgment for full back rent even if the apartment was uninhabitable. Pinned by `nj_no_deposit_at_trial_defense_waived` + `nj_deposit_made_defense_preserved`.

**Florida's notice-or-nothing framework**: under Fla. Stat. В§ 83.56(1) the tenant has only two options after giving 7-day written notice of the landlord's noncompliance: WITHHOLD rent OR TERMINATE the rental agreement. Florida does not recognize repair-and-deduct as a separate remedy. Pinned by `fl_7_day_notice_complies` + `fl_6_day_notice_insufficient`.

**Module invariants** (4 separately pinned):
- MD only on CourtOrderedRentEscrowOnly (`only_md_uses_court_ordered_escrow_only`)
- NJ only on CommonLawMariniDoctrine (`only_nj_uses_marini_doctrine`)
- FL only on NoticeThenWithholdOrTerminate (`only_fl_uses_notice_then_withhold_or_terminate`)
- MA uniquely has 4-month cap (`ma_uniquely_has_4_month_cap`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/repair-deduct-check`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **CA within 1-month cap complies / exceeds violates / third use in 12 months violates / first use OK**; **TX $500 floor at low rent / $1 month cap at high rent / $501 over floor violates**; **MA 4-month cap within ($7,500 < $8k) / exceeds ($8.5k violates)**; **WA 30-day notice required / 30-day exact complies / 29-day insufficient**; **MD no court order no remedy + court order + substantial threat both required**; **NJ no deposit defense WAIVED + deposit made preserves**; **FL 7-day notice complies / 6-day insufficient**; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **4 regime-uniqueness invariants** (MD / NJ / FL / MA 4-month cap); unknown state falls back to default 1-month cap; lowercase normalizes; CA exceed-cap note describes EXCEEDS CAP; NJ no-deposit note describes defense WAIVED.

`traderview-expense::cosigner_rules` is the **state cosigner / lease guarantor enforcement rules compliance table** вҖ” practical for landlord-trader operations using cosigners or guarantors on residential leases. Module answers two key questions: (1) is the cosigner liable for RENEWALS / EXTENSIONS, or only the original term? (2) does the landlord owe statutory notice to the cosigner BEFORE pursuing collection action or adverse-credit reporting? Two regimes:

| Regime                                       | States              | Source                                                                |
|----------------------------------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **IllinoisStatutoryNoticeRequired**          | IL                  | 815 ILCS 505/2S вҖ” first-class-mail notice required to cosigner BEFORE adverse-credit reporting or collection; 15-day cure period; **$250 statutory damages + attorney's fees** on violation |
| **CommonLawSuretyRules**                     | 49 other states + DC | Continuing-vs-specific-term guaranty doctrine governs renewal liability (CA Civ. Code В§ 2787-2856 surety provisions; NY common law); no state-mandated pre-collection notice |

**Illinois's unique 815 ILCS 505/2S pre-collection notice** is the strictest landlord-side requirement in the country. Before reporting adverse information to a consumer reporting agency, providing information to a collection agency, or taking ANY collection action against a cosigner, the landlord MUST notify the cosigner by FIRST CLASS MAIL that: (a) the primary obligor has become delinquent or defaulted; (b) the cosigner is responsible for payment; AND (c) the cosigner has 15 days from the date the notice was sent to pay or make arrangements. Failure to comply makes the violation an "unlawful practice" + creates landlord exposure for up to $250 statutory damages + reasonable attorney's fees ([815 ILCS 505/2S statutory text](https://ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/fulltext.asp?DocName=081505050K2S)). Pinned by `il_collection_without_notice_violates` + `il_collection_with_notice_15_days_compliant` + `il_collection_14_days_before_notice_violates_window` (boundary) + statutory-damages + attorney-fees + 15-day-window all individually pinned as invariants.

**Common-law renewal liability** вҖ” the load-bearing distinction is between two guaranty types under CommonLawSuretyRules:

| Guaranty type            | Renewal coverage                                          |
|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| **ContinuingGuaranty**   | Covers original term + ALL renewals automatically         |
| **SpecificTermGuaranty** | Covers original term only; cosigner must RE-SIGN for each renewal |

Pinned by `continuing_guaranty_covers_renewal_automatically` (CA), `specific_term_guaranty_without_resign_no_renewal_liability` (cosigner walks at renewal), `specific_term_guaranty_with_resign_covers_renewal` (re-signing extends liability), `no_renewal_cosigner_liable_only_for_original_term`.

**State citations differentiated**: CA citation explicitly references Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2787-2856 (suretyship provisions); NY citation references common-law suretyship; all other common-law states share a generic citation. Pinned by `ca_citation_mentions_civ_code` + `ny_common_law_path`.

**Module invariants** (2 separately pinned):
- IL only on IllinoisStatutoryNoticeRequired regime (`only_il_uses_statutory_notice_regime`)
- IL only with statutory damages > 0 (`only_il_has_statutory_damages`)
- IL 15-day window pinned (`il_15_day_window_pinned`)
- IL $250 statutory damages pinned (`il_statutory_damages_250_dollars_pinned`)
- IL attorney fees recoverable (`il_attorney_fees_recoverable`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/cosigner-check`. Twenty-three tests pin: **continuing guaranty covers renewal automatically** (CA); **specific-term guaranty without re-sign no renewal liability**; specific-term guaranty with re-sign covers renewal; no-renewal cosigner liable only for original term; **IL collection without notice violates** with $250 damages; **IL collection with 15-day notice compliant**; IL 14-day before notice violates (boundary); IL no-collection no-notice-required (notice not triggered when no collection action); **IL violation note describes $250 damages exposure**; CA collection without notice no violation (no statutory requirement); NY common-law path + citation; CA citation mentions Civ. Code В§ 2787-2856; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **2 regime-uniqueness invariants** (IL only on IllinoisStatutoryNoticeRequired + IL only with statutory damages); IL 15-day window + $250 damages + attorney fees individually pinned; unknown state falls back to common law; lowercase normalizes; continuing guaranty note describes automatic coverage; no-renewal note describes original term only.

`traderview-expense::mobile_home_park` is the **state mobile home park / manufactured housing community compliance table** вҖ” distinct trader-investor asset class with state-specific MHP statutes that displace generic landlord-tenant law. Four states have enacted comprehensive MHP-specific frameworks; the rest fall under standard residential landlord-tenant acts. Three regimes:

| Regime                                       | States | Source                                                                |
|----------------------------------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **JustCauseWithRentCap**                     | CA, OR | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 798 Mobilehome Residency Law (90-day rent notice + just-cause + local rent control under В§ 798.17); Or. ORS Ch. 90 + SB 608 of 2019 (90-day notice + 7% + CPI capped at 10% statewide rent cap + just-cause after first year) |
| **NoticeAndJustCauseNoCap**                  | FL, WA | Fla. Stat. Ch. 723 Mobile Home Act (applies to parks of **10+ lots**; 90-day notice + just-cause + homeowner meeting right); Wash. RCW 59.20 Manufactured/Mobile Home Landlord-Tenant Act + RCW 59.20.370 (eff. 2025-05-07 rent increase caps in covered scenarios + penalties) |
| **GenericLandlordTenantLaw**                 | 47 other states + DC | No MHP-specific statute; standard residential landlord-tenant act applies (typically 30-day notice + contract-governed termination) |

**California Civil Code В§ 798 Mobilehome Residency Law** is the foundational pro-resident framework. Homeowners who own their mobile home and rent the lot CANNOT be evicted because their lease expired вҖ” only for nonpayment of rent or violation of park rules. Park owners must provide a 12-month or month-to-month renewal agreement on request. 90-day written notice required for rent increases. Local rent control ordinances under В§ 798.17 apply except for long-term leases that elect out ([CA MRL HTML version](https://mhphoa.com/mrl/html)).

**Oregon SB 608 of 2019** is uniquely strict on rent: caps annual rent increases at **7% + CPI** with an absolute maximum of **10%** in any 12-month period. 90-day notice required. Just-cause eviction after first year of occupancy. Module pins the 10% absolute cap regardless of CPI volatility ([ORS В§ 90.323](https://oregon.public.law/statutes/ors_90.323)).

**Florida Ch. 723 Mobile Home Act** has a critical scope limit: applies ONLY to parks of **10 or more lots offered for rent or lease**. Small mom-and-pop parks of 9 lots or fewer fall back to generic landlord-tenant law. 90-day notice required for rent increases + homeowner meeting right. Pinned by `fl_small_park_under_10_lots_not_subject` + `fl_park_10_lots_exact_boundary_subject` ([Fla. Stat. В§ 723 official text](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2023/Chapter723/All)).

**Washington RCW 59.20 with 2025 amendments**: the May 7, 2025 amendments added RCW 59.20.370 introducing rent increase caps in certain covered scenarios plus penalties for violating landlords. Just-cause eviction required ([WA RCW 59.20 official text](https://app.leg.wa.gov/rcw/default.aspx?cite=59.20)).

**Module invariants** (4 separately pinned):
- CA + OR only on JustCauseWithRentCap regime (`just_cause_with_rent_cap_only_ca_and_or`)
- FL + WA only on NoticeAndJustCauseNoCap regime (`notice_and_just_cause_only_fl_and_wa`)
- OR only with statewide rent cap of 1000bp/10% (`only_or_has_statewide_rent_cap`)
- FL only with 10-lot threshold (`only_fl_has_10_lot_threshold`)

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/mobile-home-park-check`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CA 90-day notice complies / 89-day insufficient**; **CA landlord termination requires just cause** + just-cause termination complies; **OR within 10% cap complies / exact 10% boundary complies / above 10% violates**; **FL small park under 10 lots NOT subject** (boundary test) + 10-lot exact boundary subject + 50-lot park subject; **FL no statewide rent cap** (50% increase passes); **WA 90-day notice required** + just-cause termination required; **TX generic law applies вҖ” no MHP statute**; NY generic law no just-cause required; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **4 regime-uniqueness invariants** (CA+OR / FL+WA / OR rent cap / FL 10-lot threshold); unknown state falls back to generic; lowercase normalizes; **CA note describes JustCauseWithRentCap**; FL note describes "no statewide rent cap"; FL small-park note mentions lot threshold.
