`traderview-expense::tenant_rent_judgment_wage_garnishment` is the **post-judgment wage garnishment for tenant rent debt** module вҖ” when a trader-landlord obtains a money judgment for unpaid rent + damages after eviction, can it enforce the judgment by garnishing the former tenant's wages at the employer level? Distinct from `damage_deduction_itemization` (security deposit deductions at move-out), `rent_credit_reporting` (positive rent reporting to consumer reporting agencies), and `prevailing_party_attorney_fees` (lease attorney-fee shifting) вҖ” this module addresses ONLY the post-money-judgment wage garnishment enforcement pathway.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | States | Authority | Civil-debt outcome |
|--------|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| **Fully Prohibited** | TX, NC, PA, SC | Tex. Const. art. XVI В§ 28; N.C. Const. art. X В§ 1 + N.C. Gen. Stat. В§ 1-362; 42 Pa. Cons. Stat. В§ 8127; S.C. Code В§ 37-5-104 | **ABSOLUTELY BARRED** at employer level |
| **State More Protective** | CA, MA, VA, others | Cal. Code Civ. Proc. В§ 706.050 (50Г— state min); Mass. G.L. c. 246 В§ 28 (50Г— state min); Va. Code В§ 34-29 (75% / 40Г— federal min) | lesser of federal CCPA + state-specific exemption |
| **Federal Floor** | most other states | 15 U.S.C. В§ 1673(a)(1) | lesser of 25% disposable OR excess over 30Г— federal minimum |

**Four states absolutely prohibit civil-debt wage garnishment at the employer level.** Texas, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and South Carolina bar private creditors (including landlords) from garnishing wages on civil judgments regardless of judgment size. Trader-landlords in these states must pivot to bank-account levy (the prohibition operates at the employer, not the bank) or other enforcement tools. Pinned by `texas_civil_debt_garnishment_prohibited`, `fully_prohibited_note_lists_all_four_states`, `fully_prohibited_notes_bank_account_carveout`, `fully_prohibited_uniquely_zero_garnishment_for_civil_debt_invariant` (3-regime sweep confirms only FullyProhibited yields zero), and `fully_prohibited_disposable_earnings_irrelevant_to_outcome` (4-cell income sweep all return zero).

**Federal CCPA В§ 1673(a)(1) вҖ” lesser of 25% disposable or excess over 30Г— federal minimum.** At the current $7.25/hour federal minimum wage, the 30Г— threshold is $217.50/week. A tenant earning exactly $217.50/week or less is fully exempt from garnishment. A high earner is capped at 25% of weekly disposable. Pinned by `federal_floor_high_earner_25_percent_governs`, `federal_floor_at_30x_threshold_zero_garnishment` (exactly at $217.50), `federal_floor_below_30x_zero_garnishment`, and `federal_floor_low_earner_excess_governs` (small excess governs because it's smaller than 25%).

**State-more-protective exemptions stack with federal CCPA.** Caller supplies the state-specific minimum wage and exemption multiplier (e.g., CA 50Г— $16/hour = $800 weekly exemption). The garnishment maximum is the MINIMUM of (a) federal CCPA limit and (b) state exemption excess. Pinned by `state_more_protective_california_50x_state_min_governs`, `state_more_protective_high_state_min_blocks_garnishment`, `massachusetts_50x_state_min_at_15_dollar_state_wage_750_dollar_exemption`, and `high_earner_state_more_protective_capped_at_25_percent_under_federal_floor`.

**Three carve-outs bypass civil-debt prohibitions.** Child support / alimony (В§ 1673(a)(2) 50%/55%/60%/65% tiers), federal/state tax debt, and federally backed student loans (15% Department of Education administrative ceiling) all remain enforceable in the four prohibited states. Pinned by `child_support_bypasses_civil_debt_prohibitions`, `tax_debt_bypasses_civil_debt_prohibitions`, `student_loan_bypasses_civil_debt_prohibitions`, and `only_civil_debt_blocked_in_fully_prohibited_invariant` (3-cell carve-out sweep confirms all non-civil judgment types remain garnishable).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-rent-judgment-wage-garnishment`. Twenty-three tests pin: **Texas civil-debt garnishment prohibited**; **fully prohibited note lists all four states** (TX, NC, PA, SC); **fully prohibited notes bank-account carveout** (employer-level prohibition only); **federal floor high earner 25% governs** ($1K weekly вҶ’ ~$250 garnishment); **federal floor at 30Г— threshold zero garnishment** ($217.50 boundary); **federal floor below 30Г— zero garnishment**; **federal floor low earner excess governs**; **state more protective California 50Г— state min governs**; **state more protective high state min blocks garnishment** ($600 weekly + $16 state min Г— 50 = $800 exempt); **child support bypasses civil-debt prohibitions** (В§ 1673(a)(2) tier); **tax debt bypasses civil-debt prohibitions**; **student loan bypasses civil-debt prohibitions** (15% DoE administrative); **citation pins federal statute + four-state authorities**; **federal floor note describes lesser-of calculation**; **state more protective note describes state multiplier**; **only civil debt blocked in fully-prohibited invariant** (3-cell carve-out sweep); **federal minimum 725 cents 30Г— threshold pinned**; **fully prohibited uniquely zero garnishment for civil debt invariant** (3-regime sweep); **high earner state more protective capped at 25% under federal floor**; **Massachusetts 50Г— state min at $15 state wage $750 exemption**; **note for state more protective includes min formula**; **empty disposable earnings zero garnishment all regimes** (defensive); **fully prohibited disposable earnings irrelevant to outcome** (4-cell income sweep).

`traderview-expense::tenant_rent_receipt_requirement` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant rent receipt requirement framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because (1) cash rent receipts are mandatory in many states regardless of tenant request; (2) receipt-issuance failures create per-violation civil exposure plus an evidentiary presumption against the landlord in rent-payment disputes; (3) record retention obligations (3-year NY, similar elsewhere) extend long beyond tenancy termination; (4) modern payment methods (Zelle, Venmo, ACH) require careful receipt practices to satisfy state mandates. Companion to `landlord_annual_rent_statement`, `tenant_late_fee_cap`, `tenant_positive_rent_reporting`, `rental_junk_fee_transparency`.

**Five-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Trigger | Receipt Required |
|--------------|--------|---------|------------------|
| **New York** | N.Y. Real Prop. Law В§ 235-e | Cash / money order / cashier's check / non-personal-check OR personal-check + written request | Yes; immediate in-person OR 15 days non-in-person + 3-year retention for cash |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1499 | Tenant request | Yes; signed + dated at TIME OF PAYMENT |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. G.L. c. 186 В§ 15B | LAST MONTH'S RENT at commencement of tenancy | LIMITED mandate; regular monthly rent NOT required |
| **Washington** | Wash. Rev. Code В§ 59.18.063 | Cash payment OR non-cash payment + tenant request | Yes |
| **Default** | Common-law + local ordinances (Chicago RLTO, San Francisco) | None statewide | Payment-of-rent dispute defense |

Pinned by `ny_cash_in_person_with_receipt_compliant`, `ca_request_triggers_obligation`, `ma_last_month_rent_receipt_required`, `wa_cash_mandatory_obligation`, `default_jurisdiction_no_obligation`, `jurisdiction_truth_table_five_cells`, `ny_uniquely_engages_3_year_retention_invariant`.

**NY В§ 235-e six required content elements**:

| # | Element |
|---|---------|
| 1 | PAYMENT DATE |
| 2 | AMOUNT received |
| 3 | PERIOD for which rent was paid |
| 4 | APARTMENT NUMBER |
| 5 | SIGNATURE of person receiving payment |
| 6 | TITLE of person receiving payment |

Pinned by `ny_missing_content_violation`.

**NY В§ 235-e timing вҖ” immediate vs 15-day split**:

| Payment Mode | Required Timing |
|--------------|------------------|
| In-person cash or money order | **IMMEDIATE** at time of payment |
| Non-in-person (mail, ACH, electronic) | Within **15 DAYS** |

Pinned by `ny_cash_in_person_no_receipt_violation`, `ny_15_day_boundary_non_in_person_compliant` (day 15 = compliant), `ny_16_day_non_in_person_violation` (day 16 = violation).

**NY В§ 235-e personal-check special rule** вҖ” tenant may REQUEST in writing a rent receipt for personal-check payment; after first request, landlord must provide receipt EVERY MONTH thereafter. Pinned by `ny_personal_check_no_request_no_obligation`, `ny_personal_check_with_request_obligation_triggered`.

**NY В§ 235-e 3-year record retention** вҖ” landlord must keep proof of CASH rent receipts for **3 YEARS**. Pinned by `ny_no_3_year_retention_violation`, `ny_uniquely_engages_3_year_retention_invariant` (only NY mandates retention).

**CA В§ 1499 time-of-payment requirement** вҖ” receipt must be provided AT TIME OF PAYMENT (not year-end / not in lump sum at tax time); applies to ALL payment methods (cash, check, money order, electronic). Pinned by `ca_year_end_lump_sum_violation`.

**MA В§ 15B limited last-month-only mandate** вҖ” regular monthly rent does NOT require receipt (even cash); last month's rent at commencement of tenancy requires signed receipt with amount + date + description + landlord name + tenant name + recipient name. Pinned by `ma_regular_cash_no_obligation`, `ma_last_month_no_receipt_violation`.

**WA В§ 59.18.063 cash + request dual mandate** вҖ” cash payments MANDATORY receipt; non-cash payments require receipt on tenant request. Pinned by `wa_cash_no_receipt_violation`, `wa_non_cash_request_triggers_obligation`, `wa_non_cash_no_request_no_obligation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-rent-receipt-requirement`. Thirty-six tests pin: **NY cash in person with receipt compliant**; **NY cash in person no receipt violation**; **NY money order in person no receipt violation**; **NY cashier's check no receipt violation** (15-day non-in-person); **NY personal check no request no obligation**; **NY personal check with request obligation triggered**; **NY 15-day boundary non-in-person compliant**; **NY 16-day non-in-person violation**; **NY missing content violation** (6 elements); **NY no 3-year retention violation**; **CA request triggers obligation**; **CA no request no obligation**; **CA request no receipt violation**; **CA year-end lump sum violation**; **MA last month rent receipt required**; **MA regular cash no obligation**; **MA last month no receipt violation**; **WA cash mandatory obligation**; **WA cash no receipt violation**; **WA non-cash request triggers obligation**; **WA non-cash no request no obligation**; **default jurisdiction no obligation**; **jurisdiction truth table five cells**; **NY uniquely engages 3-year retention invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 235-e + В§ 1499 + c. 186 В§ 15B + В§ 59.18.063 + Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-080(g)); **note pins NY immediate vs 15-day split**; **note pins NY six content elements**; **note pins NY personal check recurring**; **note pins NY 3-year retention**; **note pins CA request signed dated time of payment**; **note pins MA limited last month only**; **note pins WA cash mandatory + request non-cash**; **note pins default local ordinances**; **note pins trader-landlord modern payment methods**; **note pins cross-jurisdictional architecture** (METHOD-TRIGGERED + REQUEST-TRIGGERED + LIMITED LAST-MONTH-ONLY + CASH-TRIGGERED); **multiple NY failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::condominium_conversion_protection` is the **state landlord condominium-conversion tenant protection compliance check** вҖ” operational concern for any landlord planning to convert a rental building to condominium / cooperative ownership. Companion to `tenant_relocation_assistance` (general no-fault displacement) and `foreclosure_tenant_rights` (owner default), focused specifically on the OWNER-INITIATED conversion path. Five states + DC have layered protections; other jurisdictions rely on lease enforcement plus common law.

**Six regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Tenant majority vote | Senior/disabled bar | Notice (standard) | First refusal | Relocation | Unique feature |
|--------|-----------|----------------------|---------------------|--------------------|---------------|-----------|-----------------|
| `DistrictOfColumbia` | DC Code В§ 42-3401.01 et seq. (Rental Housing Conversion and Sale Act) + В§ 42-1904.08 | **YES** вҖ” majority vote in certified election + Mayor certification | вҖ” | 120d | 60d | вҖ” | Tenant **majority-vote prerequisite** to conversion |
| `Massachusetts` | G.L. c. 527 of Acts of 1983 | вҖ” | вҖ” | 365d (1 yr) | **90d** | **$750/$1,000** | Applies only to **4+ unit buildings**; elderly/disabled get 4-year notice + $1,000 relocation |
| `NewJersey` | N.J.S.A. 2A:18-61.22 (Senior Citizens and Disabled Protected Tenancy Act) | вҖ” | **YES** вҖ” up to 40-year protected tenancy | 365d | 60d | вҖ” | Senior + disabled tenants effectively **BARRED** from conversion eviction |
| `NewYork` | N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law В§ 352-e + В§ 352-eee + В§ 352-eeee | вҖ” | Senior/disabled get **99-year non-eviction tenure** | 365d / 1095d | 90d | вҖ” | **EVICTION PLAN 51%** vs **NON-EVICTION PLAN 15%** purchase commitment thresholds |
| `MarylandMontgomery` | Mont. County Code Ch. 11A + Md. Code Real Property В§ 11-102.1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | 180d | 60d | вҖ” | 180-day state + county pre-recording notice |
| `Default` | None | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | No statewide protection; lease + common law control |

**DC majority-vote prerequisite is the unique state expansion.** DC Code В§ 42-3401.01 et seq. requires the owner to obtain (a) Mayor certification of compliance AND (b) a MAJORITY of tenants voting for conversion in a certified election. Without the majority vote, conversion is BARRED at the threshold вҖ” no notice or first-refusal procedure cures the gap. Pinned by `dc_majority_vote_obtained_compliant`, `dc_no_majority_vote_conversion_barred`, and the 6-regime invariant `only_dc_requires_tenant_majority_vote_invariant` (5 other regimes confirmed do NOT bar conversion on absence of tenant majority vote).

**Massachusetts is the only state with a building-size threshold.** G.L. c. 527 of the Acts of 1983 reaches only buildings of 4 or more rental units. A 3-unit building is outside the statute entirely and the landlord has no statutory notice / first-refusal / relocation obligation. Pinned by `massachusetts_3_units_below_threshold_no_statute`, `massachusetts_4_units_at_threshold_statute_applies`, and the 6-regime invariant `only_massachusetts_has_size_threshold_invariant`.

**Massachusetts tenant-class tiering** ties notice AND relocation to tenant status:
- Standard tenant: 365-day notice + $750 relocation
- Low/moderate income: 730-day notice + $1,000 relocation
- Elderly OR disabled: 1,460-day (4-year) notice + $1,000 relocation

Pinned by `massachusetts_senior_4_year_notice_required`, `massachusetts_disabled_4_year_notice_and_1000_relocation`, `massachusetts_low_moderate_income_2_year_notice_and_1000_relocation`, and the regression tests `massachusetts_under_notice_violation` and `massachusetts_relocation_below_threshold_violation`.

**New Jersey's senior/disabled protected tenancy BARS conversion eviction.** N.J.S.A. 2A:18-61.22 establishes up to a 40-year protected tenancy period for senior (62+) and disabled tenants вҖ” effectively prohibiting the landlord from bringing an action for possession during that window. Standard tenants are not barred. Pinned by `new_jersey_senior_protected_tenancy_bars_conversion`, `new_jersey_disabled_protected_tenancy_bars_conversion`, `new_jersey_standard_tenant_no_bar`, and the 6-regime invariant `only_nj_bars_conversion_for_protected_senior_disabled_invariant` (5 other regimes confirmed do NOT statutorily bar conversion for senior tenants).

**New York's plan-type purchase-commitment thresholds.** В§ 352-eeee requires either (a) EVICTION PLAN вҖ” at least 51% of occupied apartments purchased by tenants in occupancy or eligible non-purchaser purchasers (allows eviction of non-purchasing tenants after a transition period); or (b) NON-EVICTION PLAN вҖ” at least 15% of all units purchased by tenants OR by non-tenant purchasers committed to non-eviction (non-purchasing tenants remain as rent-regulated). Pinned by `new_york_eviction_plan_at_51_percent_compliant`, `new_york_eviction_plan_below_51_percent_violation`, `new_york_non_eviction_plan_at_15_percent_compliant`, `new_york_non_eviction_plan_below_15_percent_violation`, and the invariant `only_ny_uses_plan_type_threshold_branching_invariant` (5 other regimes ignore plan type entirely).

**NY senior + disabled 99-year non-eviction tenure** is a unique senior/disabled protection layered ON TOP of either plan type. Pinned by `new_york_senior_99_year_non_eviction_note` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/condominium-conversion-protection`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **DC majority vote compliant + missing majority vote BARRED**; **MA 4+ units standard tenant compliant + 3 units below threshold no statute + 4 units at threshold**; **MA senior 4-year notice + disabled 4-year notice + $1000 relocation + low-moderate 2-year + $1000**; **MA under notice violation + relocation below threshold violation**; **NJ senior protected tenancy BARS conversion + disabled BARS + standard no bar**; **NY eviction plan at 51% + below 51% violation + non-eviction at 15% + below 15% violation + senior 99-year non-eviction note**; **MD-Montgomery 180-day notice compliant + under 180 violation**; **Default no statutory protection**; **missing first-refusal offer violation + first-refusal acceptance window too short violation**; **only-DC-requires-tenant-majority-vote 6-regime invariant**; **only-NJ-bars-conversion-for-protected-senior-disabled 6-regime invariant**; **only-MA-has-size-threshold 6-regime invariant**; **only-NY-uses-plan-type-threshold-branching 6-regime invariant**; **citation pins authority per regime** (В§ 42-3401.01 for DC + c. 527 for MA + 2A:18-61.22 for NJ + В§ 352-e for NY + Mont. County Code Ch. 11A for MD + "No statewide" for Default); **sibling-module note across all 6 regimes** (UX-text regression for tenant_relocation_assistance + foreclosure_tenant_rights + OWNER-INITIATED scope).

`traderview-expense::tenant_relocation_assistance` is the **landlord-paid tenant relocation assistance compute module** вҖ” three U.S. jurisdictions ship concrete dollar formulas for relocation assistance owed when a no-fault eviction or qualifying displacement event occurs. Most other states leave the question to local ordinance or the lease itself. Modeling the three that have legislated amounts gives the calculator real cash-flow numbers while keeping the default branch honest (no obligation).

**Five regimes** with distinct trigger sets and dollar formulas:

| Regime | Trigger set | Amount | Notes |
|--------|-------------|--------|-------|
| `CaliforniaAb1482` (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2(d)(3)) | owner move-in / withdrawal / demo / gov order | ONE MONTH rent | Paid within 15 days of notice OR waived from last month; strict-compliance voids non-compliant notice; post-2020-07-01 leases require written tenant agreement OR lease clause for owner move-in |
| `PortlandOr` (PCC 30.01.085) | no-cause / qualifying landlord-cause / rent вүҘ10% / owner move-in / demo | $2,900 studio В· $3,300 1BR В· $4,200 2BR В· $4,500 3BR+ | 90-day notice required; non-compliance up to 3Г— rent + actual damages + attorney fees |
| `SeattleTrao` (SMC Ch. 22.210) | demo / substantial-rehab / change-of-use | $2,776 landlord + $2,776 City = $5,552 total | Only households вүӨ50% Seattle AMI qualify |
| `SeattleEdra` (SMC Ch. 22.212, eff. 2022-07-01) | rent increase вүҘ10% in rolling 12-month window | 3Г— monthly housing cost | Households вүӨ80% AMI only; City advances payment; landlord reimburses |
| `Default` | вҖ” | $0 | No statewide statute identified; local ordinance or lease may apply |

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-relocation-assistance`. `traderview-expense::tenant_rights_statement_disclosure` is the **Tenant Rights Statement disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a residential landlord distribute the official state-prepared statement of tenants' rights and responsibilities? Distinct from siblings `lease_disclosures` (mandated lease content), `plain_language_lease` (lease readability), and `landlord_identification_disclosure` (party-identification). New Jersey Truth in Renting Act is uniquely aggressive вҖ” annual distribution + new-tenant delivery + prominent posting + no tenant waiver permitted + Consumer Fraud Act treble damages exposure.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Annual distribution | Posting requirement | Treble damages |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|------------------|
| New Jersey В§ 46:8-43 to -50 | YES (30 days post-DCA publication) | YES (prominent location) | YES (CFA В§ 56:8-1) |
| Maryland В§ 8-208 | Limited (county supplements) | Limited | Common-law / county |
| New York HSTPA 2019 | NO statewide mandate | Voluntary | Per HSTPA discrete-disclosure framework |
| Default | NO statewide mandate | Voluntary | Municipal ordinance dependent |

**New Jersey вҖ” Truth in Renting Act, N.J.S.A. 46:8-43 to -50**. Most aggressive framework in the country. **В§ 46:8-44** вҖ” NJ Department of Community Affairs (DCA) publishes Statement of Legal Rights and Responsibilities of Tenants and Landlords in BOTH English and Spanish, posted on DCA website, UPDATED ANNUALLY. **В§ 46:8-45** вҖ” landlord MUST distribute one copy to each tenant within **30 DAYS** after DCA publication AND provide copy to each new tenant at or prior to occupancy. **В§ 46:8-46** вҖ” landlord MUST post current Statement in one or more prominent accessible locations. **В§ 46:8-47** вҖ” applies to buildings with > 2 units (or non-owner-occupied > 3 units). **В§ 46:8-48** вҖ” no tenant waiver alters landlord obligations. **В§ 46:8-49** вҖ” penalties + private right of action via N.J.S.A. 56:8-1 Consumer Fraud Act **treble damages**.

**Scoping rule**: NJ Truth in Renting Act applies to (a) any building with > 2 units OR (b) non-owner-occupied buildings with > 3 units. Owner-occupied 2-3 unit buildings are OUT of scope. Pinned by `scoping_truth_table_owner_occupied_vs_unit_count` (6-cell truth table across unit_count Г— owner_occupied).

**Maryland вҖ” Md. Code, Real Property В§ 8-208**. Limited tenant-rights notice; landlord must provide certain notices but not the comprehensive Statement-of-Rights document NJ requires. County / municipal supplements (Montgomery County, Prince George's County) extend coverage.

**New York вҖ” HSTPA of 2019**. Imposes discrete-disclosure mandates (rent stabilization status, security deposit handling) but NO statutory annual-distribution mandate equivalent to NJ. NY DHCR Residential Tenants' Rights Guide + NY AG Residential Tenants' Rights Guide вҖ” voluntary distribution recommended but not statutorily mandated.

**Default**. Most states have NO statewide Tenant-Rights-Statement distribution mandate. Municipal ordinances may impose distribution requirements (Chicago Tenants in Foreclosure Notification Ordinance, San Francisco Rent Ordinance В§ 37).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-rights-statement-disclosure`. Twenty-six tests pin: **NJ full compliance passes**; **NJ missing 30-day distribution violates** (В§ 46:8-45); **NJ missing new-tenant delivery violates**; **NJ missing posting violates** (В§ 46:8-46); **NJ relying on tenant waiver violates** (В§ 46:8-48); **NJ 2-unit non-owner-occupied out of scope** (В§ 46:8-47); **NJ 3-unit non-owner-occupied in scope**; **NJ 3-unit owner-occupied out of scope**; **NJ 4-unit owner-occupied in scope**; **NJ DCA annual publication note** (В§ 46:8-44 ANNUALLY); **NJ bilingual English + Spanish note**; **NJ penalties note with Consumer Fraud Act** (В§ 56:8-1 treble damages); **NJ all four violations simultaneous**; **NJ citation pins 46:8-43 to 46:8-50**; **MD no violations even when no distribution**; **MD county / municipal supplement note** (Montgomery County + Prince George's County); **MD citation pins В§ 8-208**; **NY no violations + no mandate note** (HSTPA NO statutory annual-distribution mandate); **NY DHCR + AG guide note**; **default no obligation compliant**; **default municipal examples note** (Chicago + San Francisco В§ 37); **NJ unique distribution mandate invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **four regimes routed correctly**; **NJ 2-unit owner-occupied out of scope**; **NJ out-of-scope compliant even with no distribution**; **scoping truth table owner-occupied vs unit count** (6-cell sweep across 1/2/3/4 units Г— owner-occupied / non-owner-occupied).

`traderview-expense::tenant_window_air_conditioner_install_right` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant window air conditioner installation right and landlord cooling provision compliance framework module** вҖ” when may a tenant install a window AC unit, what bracket safety + falling-AC liability rules apply, when must landlord provide cooling under emerging state and municipal heat-mandate laws, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a heat-stress fatality or falling-AC injury? Distinct from siblings `cooling_requirements` (general cooling habitability), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification), `tenant_solar_installation`, `tenant_ev_charging_installation_right`, `rental_window_guard_installation` (fall protection from window), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (iter 485). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **NYC Cool Homes Act (Int 0994 of 2024, 2026 effective)** is the leading state-level AC mandate вҖ” non-compliance creates Housing Maintenance Code violation + per-month rent reduction; (2) falling-AC injuries from upper-floor windows are among the highest-stakes NYC premises-liability claims вҖ” settlements routinely exceed $1M; (3) approximately **500 NYC heat-related deaths annually** drove legislative momentum; (4) landlord absolute prohibition of window AC in non-NYC jurisdictions is generally enforceable BUT cannot be unreasonable under habitability doctrine; (5) reasonable accommodation requests under Fair Housing Act for disabled tenants must be granted absent undue hardship.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **New York City (MOST RECENT + MOST PRESCRIPTIVE)** | NYC Int 0994 of 2024 ("Cool Homes for All Act"); NYC Admin. Code В§ 27-2029; NYC Window AC Bracket requirements | Landlord must provide AC + maintain bedroom temp **вүӨ78В°F from June 15 to September 15** when outdoor temp exceeds 82В°F; market-rate AND rent-stabilized apply (public housing excluded); buildings **taller than 6 stories** require AC brackets |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 implied warranty; Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17920.3 substandard conditions | Heating universally required; cooling not statutorily required at state level but Palm Springs + Coachella Valley municipal codes require landlord cooling |
| **Arizona** | Phoenix City Code В§ 39-16; Pima County Code 8.20; ARS В§ 33-1324(C) | Landlord-provided cooling required in summer months (Phoenix 78В°F max June-September) |
| **Default** | Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974) | Common-law habitability + tort negligence + premises liability for heat-stress events |

**Window AC bracket safety requirements** (NYC + best practice nationally):
1. **UNITS IN BUILDINGS TALLER THAN 6 STORIES require bracket**
2. **Installation must prevent tilt, slip, or fall** вҖ” units cannot project beyond approved tolerance
3. **Window frame load capacity**: if window cannot support unit weight, bracket required regardless of building height
4. **Bracket must be installed per manufacturer specifications** вҖ” fasteners into structural framing (not just window sash)
5. **Annual inspection + re-tightening** recommended pre-cooling season

**Three install paths**:
1. **TENANT-INSTALLED WITH LEASE PROVISION** вҖ” most common; lease must not unreasonably prohibit safety-compliant installation
2. **LANDLORD-PROVIDED PRE-INSTALLED** вҖ” NYC Cool Homes Act applies; landlord owns + maintains
3. **REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION REQUEST** вҖ” disability/health-condition basis under **Fair Housing Act 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)** + state equivalents requires landlord to permit install (and sometimes pay)

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Landlord prohibits window AC** despite no legitimate safety basis вҶ’ habitability dispute + tenant rescission claim
2. **Tenant installs AC without bracket on upper floor** вҶ’ falling-AC injury liability + landlord secondary common-law negligence
3. **NYC Cool Homes Act violation post-2026** вҶ’ Housing Maintenance Code violation + ECB civil penalty + tenant rent reduction + HPD complaint
4. **Heat-stress event during heat wave** вҶ’ tort negligence + wrongful death + IIED (`tenant_emotional_distress_damages` iter 453) with $1M+ settlements routine
5. **AC bracket not maintained** вҶ’ premises liability + falling-AC injury + insurance denial

**NYC heat-related fatality data**: approximately 500 NYC heat-related deaths annually drove the Cool Homes for All Act legislation. Falling-AC injuries from upper-floor windows are among the highest-stakes NYC premises-liability claims with settlements routinely exceeding $1M.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-window-air-conditioner-install-right`. Thirty-three tests pin: **NYC compliant baseline**; **falling AC injury top severity** ($1M+ settlement); **heat stress event severity** (500 NYC deaths annually); **not applicable install path**; **NYC Cool Homes Act violation** (78В°F + 82В°F + June 15-Sept 15); **NYC Cool Homes compliant with AC**; **CA no Cool Homes Act no violation on temp**; **bracket required tall building not installed**; **bracket required weak window frame not installed**; **bracket installed tall building compliant**; **bracket maintenance deficient**; **unreasonable prohibition**; **landlord provided prohibition no violation**; **disability accommodation denied** (42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)); **disability accommodation granted compliant**; **NYC jurisdiction cites Int 0994** (Cool Homes + В§ 27-2029 + 6-story brackets); **CA jurisdiction cites В§ 1941.1 + В§ 17920.3** (Palm Springs); **AZ jurisdiction cites Phoenix Code + ARS** (В§ 33-1324(C)); **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + 478 A.2d 202); **severity priority falling > heat > accommodation > Cool Homes > prohibition > bracket > maintenance**; **citation pins all authorities** (NYC Int 0994 of 2024 + Cool Homes for All Act + 78В°F + 82В°F + В§ 1941.1 + В§ 17920.3 + Phoenix City Code В§ 39-16 + ARS В§ 33-1324(C) + 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f) + Hilder + 478 A.2d 202); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins five bracket safety requirements**; **note pins three install paths** (FHA В§ 3604(f)); **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins 500 heat deaths annually**; **note pins companion modules**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **NYC uniquely Cool Homes Act invariant**; **multiple violations stack in actions**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_utility_account_designation` is the **tenant right to designate utility service account in tenant's own name module** вҖ” when may the residential tenant become the direct customer of record with the utility company (vs being forced onto the landlord's master account)? Distinct from siblings `submetering_rules` (sub-metering setup framework), `utility_shutoff` (shutoff procedural protections), and `non_refundable_cleaning_fees` (move-out fees). Trader-landlord operational concern when landlord defaults on utility bills вҖ” CA В§ 777 lets tenant assume the account WITHOUT paying landlord's arrears.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Scoping | Right engaged when | Arrears protection |
|--------|---------|---------------------|----------------------|
| California Cal. Pub. Util. Code В§ 777 | Individually metered only (MASTER CARVED OUT) | Landlord delinquent + tenant requests + verification | YES (tenant not liable for landlord arrears) |
| New York N.Y. Pub. Serv. Law В§В§ 32, 33, 33-a (HEFPA) | Individually OR sub-metered (master carved out) | Tenant requests + landlord is customer | Via PSC tariff |
| Default | Individually OR sub-metered | No statewide right | None |

**California вҖ” Cal. Pub. Util. Code В§ 777**. Most explicit tenant-protection framework. Applies to landlord-tenant arrangements where landlord is customer of record for INDIVIDUALLY METERED residential service (electric, gas, heat, water). Tenant has right to become customer WITHOUT being required to pay any delinquent amount due on landlord's account. Utility corporation MUST inform occupants of right. Verification methods: lease, rent receipts, or government document confirming tenancy. **Master-metered apartment buildings are CARVED OUT** вҖ” RUBS / master-meter pass-through scenarios fall outside В§ 777 protection. В§ 777.1 supplements with related utility-corporation disclosure obligations.

**Three conjunctive elements for CA right** вҖ” landlord is customer of record AND tenant requests to become customer AND tenant can verify landlord's status. All three required. Pinned by `ca_three_conjunctive_elements_required_for_right` (8-cell truth table sweep).

**New York вҖ” N.Y. Pub. Serv. Law В§В§ 32, 33, 33-a (Home Energy Fair Practices Act / HEFPA)**. Residential utility customer protections under PSC tariff framework. Tenant in shared-meter arrangement may petition NY PSC for separate account designation. Sub-metered units within scope (unlike CA В§ 777 which only covers individually metered).

**Default вҖ” lease + utility tariff**. No statewide tenant utility-designation right. Rules set by state-PUC-regulated utility tariff and lease provisions. Master-metered buildings typically lack tenant-side designation right.

**CA master-meter carve-out is the most distinctive feature**. Across CA, NY, and Default, master-metered buildings (RUBS pass-through scenarios) all fall outside protection вҖ” but CA's В§ 777 has the strongest individually-metered protection of any state. Pinned by `ca_master_meter_carveout_unique_invariant` (3-regime sweep all reject master-metered).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-utility-account-designation`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA individually metered full request right engaged**; **CA master metered not applies no right** (В§ 777 master-meter carve-out); **CA landlord not customer of record no right**; **CA tenant cannot verify landlord status no right**; **CA tenant does not request no right**; **CA utility failed to notify violation** (В§ 777 utility MUST inform); **CA no delinquency no violation for missing notice**; **CA right engaged note describes delinquent-amount protection** (tenant NOT required to pay landlord arrears); **CA verification methods note present** (lease + rent receipts + government document); **CA citation pins В§В§ 777 + 777.1**; **CA sub-metered does not apply**; **NY HEFPA individually metered right engaged**; **NY master metered does not engage right**; **NY PSC petition note for shared meter**; **NY citation pins HEFPA В§В§ 32 + 33 + 33-a**; **default no statewide right no engagement**; **default individually metered within scope note** (statute-set tariff + lease); **default master metered outside scope**; **default citation references PUC tariff and lease**; **CA unique explicit В§ 777 protection invariant** (default lacks protection); **three regimes routed correctly**; **metering type truth table California** (3-type sweep: IndividuallyMetered / MasterMetered / SubMetered); **NY sub-metered outside master meter engages right** (NY broader than CA on sub-metering); **CA master meter carveout unique invariant** (3-regime sweep all reject master); **CA three conjunctive elements required for right** (8-cell truth table); **CA В§ 777.1 disclosure note present**; **CA master meter no violation even when landlord delinquent** (carve-out absolute).

`traderview-expense::tenant_kitchen_appliance_replacement` is the **tenant kitchen appliance replacement and California AB 628 mandate framework module** (iter 507) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord provide and maintain refrigerator + stove under California AB 628 (effective January 1, 2026), what CPSC recall response window applies, when can tenant opt out, and what habitability remedies attach to a failed replacement? Distinct from siblings `tenant_in_unit_appliance_repair_responsibility` (broader landlord-provided appliance maintenance), `rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision` (iter 501 W/D framework), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification mandate context вҖ” converting gas stove to induction range qualifies for IRA HEEHRA rebate), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (gas stove leak protocol), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when tenant must vacate during major appliance replacement). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **California AB 628 effective January 1, 2026** amends Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 to make refrigerator + stove NEW MANDATORY habitability provisions вҖ” first-of-its-kind state-mandated appliance provision; (2) CPSC recall response window is 30 days under AB 628 вҖ” failure to repair/replace recalled appliance within 30 days breaches habitability; (3) refrigerator opt-out is permitted ONLY by voluntary written tenant election at lease signing, NOT mid-tenancy; (4) stove opt-out is NOT permitted regardless of tenant preference; (5) exemptions: permanent supportive housing, single-room occupancy (SRO), residential hotels, communal-kitchen assisted living facilities; (6) energy-efficiency replacement triggers state rebates (CalEnergy + Inflation Reduction Act В§ 50121 HOMES rebate + В§ 50122 HEEHRA rebate for ENERGY STAR + induction range upgrades).

**Two appliance types**: Refrigerator (opt-out eligible by voluntary written election at lease signing), Stove (no opt-out regardless of tenant preference).

**Four appliance conditions**: WorkingProperly, FailingPastNoticeWindow, CpscRecalled (30-day cure window), NotProvided.

**Four housing scopes**: StandardResidentialRental (AB 628 + state habitability apply), PermanentSupportiveHousingExempt (AB 628 exempt), SroSharedKitchenExempt (AB 628 exempt), ResidentialHotelCommunalKitchenExempt (AB 628 exempt).

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 amended by AB 628 effective January 1, 2026 + В§ 1942 self-help repair after 30-day notice + Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17920.3 substandard housing classification + В§ 1946.7 lease termination right + Inflation Reduction Act В§ 50121 HOMES rebate + В§ 50122 HEEHRA rebate for ENERGY STAR / induction range upgrades) vs Massachusetts (105 CMR 410.100(E) State Sanitary Code requires kitchen sink + range cookstove вҖ” stove provision REQUIRED since 1976 + M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 quiet enjoyment + M.G.L. ch. 111 В§ 127A enforcement) vs New York (NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2017.2 cooking-gas + electrical service for kitchen + MDL В§ 76 plumbing fixtures + NY RPL В§ 235-b implied warranty extends to provided appliances + HPD inspection cycle) vs Illinois (Chicago Municipal Code В§ 5-12-110 RLTO habitability вҖ” kitchen with sink + stove + heat + cold water вҖ” outside Chicago, Illinois Mobile Home Landlord and Tenant Rights Act and common-law habitability) vs Default (common-law implied warranty of habitability + federal CPSC recall framework 15 U.S.C. В§ 2064 substantial product hazard).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantWorkingProperly, ExemptHousingType, RefrigeratorOptOutVoluntaryDocumented (opt-out properly executed at lease signing вҖ” opt-out does NOT carry forward to subsequent tenants), StoveOptOutAttemptedProhibited (50% rent at risk вҖ” void against public policy), AppliancePastDueRepairOrReplacementHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk), CpscRecalledNotReplacedWithin30DaysHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk), NoApplianceProvidedAb628Violation (100% rent at risk).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-kitchen-appliance-replacement`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **standard rental working refrigerator compliant**; **permanent supportive housing exempt**; **SRO shared kitchen exempt**; **residential hotel communal kitchen exempt**; **CA no stove post-2026 AB 628 violation** (cannot opt out language); **CA no refrigerator no opt-out violation**; **CA no refrigerator with voluntary opt-out documented** (lease addendum + does not carry forward); **CA stove opt-out attempted prohibited** (50% rent at risk + void against public policy); **CPSC recalled under 30 days compliant path**; **CPSC recalled over 30 days habitability breach** (100% rent at risk); **CPSC recalled at exactly 30 days compliant** (boundary); **failing appliance past notice habitability breach** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941/В§ 1942 + M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 + RPL В§ 235-b + Chicago В§ 5-12-110); **pre-2026 CA lease no AB 628 mandate** (boundary cliff at January 1, 2026); **AB 628 effective year pins 2026**; **AB 628 CPSC recall response days pins 30**; **MA stove provision since year pins 1976**; **MA jurisdiction pins 105 CMR 410.100(E) + stove since 1976 + M.G.L. ch. 111 В§ 127A**; **NY jurisdiction pins NYC Admin В§ 27-2017.2 + MDL В§ 76 + RPL В§ 235-b**; **IL jurisdiction pins Chicago Municipal Code В§ 5-12-110 RLTO**; **CA jurisdiction pins AB 628 + HEEHRA + HOMES + В§ 50121 + В§ 50122**; **default jurisdiction pins CPSC 15 U.S.C. В§ 2064**; **coordination note references tenant_in_unit_appliance_repair_responsibility + rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision + rental_gas_appliance_ban + rental_natural_gas_leak_response**; **coordination note pinned in all 5 jurisdictions**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (5-cell sweep); **zero rent zero risk does not panic**; **severity priority exempt overrides failing appliance**; **severity priority CPSC overrides not-provided**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_voting_address_protection` is the **tenant voting address protection and Address Confidentiality Program (ACP) framework module** (iter 505) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord refuse to disclose a tenant's actual residential address (substituting an ACP-issued PO box or substitute address) under state ACP statutes protecting survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, and human trafficking? Distinct from siblings `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (biometric privacy at unit access), `security_camera_disclosure` (landlord-installed surveillance), `tenant_data_privacy` (general tenant data handling), `landlord_tenant_recording_consent` (audio recording consent), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim parallel framework), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (emergency relocation if confidentiality breached). Trader-landlord critical because (1) ACP enrollment provides a state-issued substitute address that legally REPLACES the tenant's residential address on public records (voter registration, driver's license, school enrollment, court records); (2) unauthorized disclosure of an ACP participant's actual address by a landlord is a CRIMINAL OFFENSE in all five jurisdictions covered вҖ” misdemeanor under state ACP statute regardless of resulting harm, FELONY under MA M.G.L. ch. 9A В§ 8 + NY Executive Law В§ 108(5) + WA RCW 40.24.090 when disclosure causes actual harm to participant; (3) civil damages plus attorney's fees available; (4) parallel non-ACP confidentiality duty attaches when tenant discloses victim status without enrolling (common-law privacy torts: intrusion upon seclusion + public disclosure of private facts); (5) voter-registration challenger and opposition-research firm requests for tenant addresses must be DENIED when tenant has invoked ACP or victim status.

**Three ACP statuses**: EnrolledWithSubstituteAddress (full state ACP statutory protection), VictimStatusDisclosedNotEnrolled (common-law + state-statutory confidentiality duty), NoAcpAndNoVictimDisclosure.

**Eight disclosure-request types**: VoterRegistrationChallenger, LawEnforcementWithWarrantOrSubpoena (compliant вҖ” warrant exception), LawEnforcementWithoutWarrant (DENY), ProcessServerCivilUnrelated (substitute address only), DebtCollectorOrSkipTracer (FDCPA 15 U.S.C. В§ 1692c + ACP substitute), FamilyMemberOrEstrangedSpouse (DENY вҖ” most common perpetrator pattern), RoutineLandlordBusiness, NoRequest.

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Gov. Code В§ 6206-6210 Safe at Home Program administered by Secretary of State + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.7 lease termination right + В§ 1161.3 unlawful detainer protection + Penal В§ 273.6 protective order violation вҖ” proof of abuse required) vs Massachusetts (M.G.L. ch. 9A ACP administered by Secretary of the Commonwealth + M.G.L. ch. 209A Abuse Prevention + M.G.L. ch. 186 В§В§ 24-29 lease termination + M.G.L. ch. 151B В§ 4 fair housing вҖ” intentional breach causing harm is FELONY under M.G.L. ch. 9A В§ 8) vs New York (N.Y. Executive Law В§ 108 ACP administered by Department of State + RPL В§ 227-c lease termination + Social Services Law В§ 459-a вҖ” expanded post-Dobbs to reproductive health care services providers, employees, volunteers, patients, immediate family) vs Washington (RCW 40.24 вҖ” FIRST state to enact ACP in 1991 + RCW 59.18.575 lease termination + RCW 59.18.585 adverse action prohibition вҖ” expanded to Criminal Justice Affiliates + Election Officials + Protected Health Care Workers) vs Default (federal VAWA 42 U.S.C. В§ 14043e protections for federally-assisted housing + 24 C.F.R. В§ 5.2003 reasonable accommodation + common-law privacy torts).

**Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantConfidentialityMaintained, DisclosureToVoterChallengerProhibited (50% rent at risk), DisclosureToFamilyOrEstrangedSpouseProhibited (50% rent at risk), DisclosureToDebtCollectorRequiresAcpSubstitute, DisclosureToProcessServerRequiresAcpSubstitute, DisclosureToLawEnforcementWithoutWarrantProhibited (50% rent at risk), LawEnforcementWithValidWarrantCompliant, ConfidentialityBreachAcpParticipantCriminalLiability (100% rent at risk + misdemeanor universal + felony when harm), ConfidentialityBreachNonAcpVictimTortLiability (100% rent at risk + intrusion upon seclusion + public disclosure of private facts).

`traderview-expense::tenant_smart_thermostat_install_right` is the **tenant smart-thermostat installation right and landlord-permission framework module** (iter 497) вҖ” parallel to siblings `tenant_solar_installation` (right-to-install rooftop solar), `tenant_ev_charging_installation_right` (right-to-charge EV), `tenant_clothesline_drying_right` (utility-conservation right), `tenant_window_air_conditioner_install_right` (HVAC alteration analog), `rental_solar_panel_disclosure` (iter 495 energy-infrastructure disclosure), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification mandate context). Trader-landlord critical because Nest / Ecobee / Honeywell smart thermostats reduce HVAC runtime by 12% per EPA-validated study but require wiring change (24V C-wire control circuit) and landlord permission under standard alteration clauses.

**Four HVAC wiring categories**: 24V low-voltage with C-wire (compatible вҖ” Nest 4th-gen / Ecobee SmartThermostat Premium), 24V without C-wire (needs C-wire adapter or power-stealing model), 120V baseboard heat (incompatible with standard smart thermostats вҖ” requires line-voltage Mysa / SinopГ© Technologies / Stelpro Maestro), steam or hot-water radiator (no electronic thermostat compatibility вҖ” smart-TRV alternative such as Honeywell Home HR92).

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.6 energy-efficiency improvement allowance + В§ 1942.1 self-help repair right after 30-day notice + Title 24 Building Energy Efficiency Standards eff. January 1, 2023 + SB 1136 [introduced February 14, 2024 вҖ” energy-efficiency rental disclosure expansion] PENDING) vs New York (NYC Local Law 97 Climate Mobilization Act signed April 18, 2019 + eff. 2024 carbon caps for properties > 25,000 sq ft + NYC Admin Code В§ 28-320 + Real Property Law В§ 235-b + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2029 68В°F day / 62В°F night) vs Massachusetts (DOER Mass Save $100 instant rebate per smart thermostat per residence per program year via Eversource / National Grid / Unitil + 520 CMR 13.00 Stretch Code adopted by 299 municipalities as of June 2025 + M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 quiet enjoyment) vs Federal Fair Housing Act / ADA (42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + 24 C.F.R. В§ 100.203 reasonable-modification requirement for disability accommodation) vs Default (common-law habitability + lease alteration clause).

**Four installation-request categories**: TenantPaysInstall, LandlordInstallsCostAmortized, AdaFhaReasonableAccommodation (disability), UtilityRebateProgramUpgrade (Mass Save / PG&E SmartAC / Eversource).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, InstallationPermittedRoutine, WiringIncompatibilityRefusalPermitted (steam-radiator or 120V baseboard), HvacWarrantyVoidRefusalPermitted (OEM thermostat warranty conditioning), HistoricDistrictRestrictionApplies (NYC LPC permit per NYC Admin Code В§ 25-303), AdaFhaReasonableAccommodationRequired (100% rent at risk вҖ” disability accommodation duty), EnergyEfficiencyRebateEligibleApprovalLikely (Mass Save pre-approval pathway), LandlordRefusalUnreasonableHabitabilityBreach (50% rent at risk вҖ” RPL В§ 235-b NY / M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 MA breach).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-smart-thermostat-install-right`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **standard request with consent and restoration routine**; **ADA FHA reasonable accommodation required with disability** (100% rent at risk + 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + 24 C.F.R. В§ 100.203); **ADA request without certification falls to default path**; **steam radiator incompatibility refusal permitted** (smart-TRV alternative noted); **baseboard 120V incompatibility refusal permitted** (Mysa noted); **historic district restriction applies** (NYC Admin Code В§ 25-303); **HVAC warranty void refusal permitted** (indemnification analog); **MA utility rebate program default to Mass Save $100**; **CA utility rebate with explicit amount uses input value**; **lease prohibits no consent unreasonable breach** (50% rent at risk); **CA jurisdiction pins В§ 1947.6 + В§ 1942.1 + Title 24 + SB 1136**; **NY jurisdiction pins Local Law 97 + Real Property Law В§ 235-b + April 18, 2019 + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2029**; **MA jurisdiction pins Mass Save + 520 CMR 13.00 + 299 municipalities + M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14**; **default jurisdiction pins 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + 24 C.F.R. В§ 100.203 FHA**; **coordination note references solar + EV + clothesline + window AC + solar disclosure**; **coordination note pinned in all 4 jurisdictions**; **Nest/Ecobee savings pct constant pins 12%**; **MA Mass Save rebate pins $100**; **NYC Local Law 97 effective year pins 2024**; **CA Title 24 effective year pins 2023**; **estimated savings passed through**; **full annual rent at risk for ADA violation**; **half annual rent at risk for unreasonable refusal**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic** (boundary); **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (4-cell sweep); **refusal grounds priority wiring overrides warranty**; **refusal grounds priority historic overrides warranty**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` is the **tenant right to refuse landlord-installed BIOMETRIC smart lock module** вҖ” biometric privacy framework when landlord installs fingerprint / facial recognition / retinal scanner smart locks. Distinct from siblings `security_camera_disclosure` (landlord-installed surveillance cameras), `lock_change_between_tenancies` (between-tenancy lock changes), `landlord_tenant_recording_consent` (audio recording consent), and `tenant_data_privacy` (general tenant data handling). Emerging trader-landlord privacy issue in IL, WA, TX (biometric privacy states) + CA/NY frameworks.

**Five regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Statutory damages | Private right of action |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|---------------------------|
| Illinois 740 ILCS 14 BIPA | В§ 15(b) consent + В§ 15(c) no-profit + В§ 20 private action | $1,000 negligent / $5,000 reckless per violation | YES вҖ” В§ 20 |
| Washington RCW 19.375 | Biometric Identifiers Act | AG-only enforcement | NO |
| Texas Bus. & Comm. В§ 503.001 | Capture or Use of Biometric Identifier | $25,000 per violation AG civil penalty | NO |
| California CCPA/CPRA + Civ. Code В§ 1940.2 | Sensitive personal information classification | Data-breach private action only | Limited |
| Default | FTC Act В§ 5 + state UDAP + common-law privacy | Varies by state | Varies |

**Illinois вҖ” 740 ILCS 14 BIPA (most aggressive framework in the country)**. В§ 15(b) requires private entity to (1) inform individual in writing biometric information is being collected, (2) identify in writing specific PURPOSE and LENGTH OF TERM for collection/storage/use, (3) receive WRITTEN RELEASE from individual. В§ 15(c) prohibits sale, lease, trade, or other profit from biometric information. В§ 20 PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION with $1,000 negligent / $5,000 reckless statutory damages (or actual damages, whichever greater) + reasonable attorney fees + costs + injunctive relief. **Cothron v. White Castle System, Inc., 2023 IL 128004** вҖ” each biometric scan is a SEPARATE BIPA violation (per-scan accrual). Pinned by `il_cothron_per_scan_stacking` (50 scans Г— $1,000 = $50,000 exposure), `il_reckless_and_50_scans_per_scan_5000` (50 Г— $5,000 = $250,000), `il_negligent_vs_reckless_damages_5x_ratio`.

**Washington вҖ” RCW 19.375 Biometric Identifiers Act**. Enrollment of biometric identifier in database for COMMERCIAL PURPOSE requires NOTICE and CONSENT. Enforcement limited to Washington Attorney General вҖ” **NO statutory private right of action** (unlike IL BIPA). Pinned by `wa_no_private_right_of_action`.

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Bus. & Comm. Code В§ 503.001 Capture or Use of Biometric Identifier**. Capture of biometric identifier for COMMERCIAL PURPOSE requires NOTICE and CONSENT. Civil penalty **$25,000 PER VIOLATION** enforced by Texas Attorney General. NO statutory private right of action. Pinned by `tx_civil_penalty_25000_per_violation`.

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1798.80 + 1798.100 et seq. (CCPA/CPRA)**. Biometric identifiers classified as SENSITIVE PERSONAL INFORMATION subject to disclosure + deletion rights. No California-specific biometric statute. Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.2 prohibited harassment includes coercive conduct (relevant when landlord conditions access on biometric enrollment).

**Default вҖ” no biometric statute**. Federal FTC В§ 5 (15 U.S.C. В§ 45) UDAP doctrine applies. State UDAP statutes (47 states + DC) and common-law invasion-of-privacy torts available.

**Anti-tying principle across ALL regimes** вҖ” even where landlord may install a biometric smart lock with proper consent, tenant retains right to TRADITIONAL KEY ACCESS as alternative when tenant refuses biometric enrollment. Landlord conditioning entry solely on biometric enrollment violates anti-tying + quiet enjoyment. Pinned by `anti_tying_violation_across_all_regimes_when_no_key_alternative` (5-regime sweep).

**Illinois unique private right of action invariant** вҖ” only IL provides per-violation statutory damages with private right of action. WA, TX use AG-only enforcement. Pinned by `il_unique_private_right_of_action_invariant` (4-regime sweep).

**Illinois unique per-scan stacking invariant** вҖ” only IL stacks damages per scan under Cothron. Pinned by `il_unique_per_scan_stacking_invariant` (IL 100 scans Г— $1,000 = $100,000 vs TX fixed $25,000).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-smart-lock-biometric-consent`. Thirty-six tests pin: **IL full compliance no violation**; **IL missing consent triggers violation** (14/15(b)(3) WRITTEN RELEASE); **IL missing purpose disclosure triggers violation** (14/15(b)(2) PURPOSE and LENGTH OF TERM); **IL profit from biometric triggers В§ 15(c) violation**; **IL negligent damages $1,000 per violation**; **IL reckless damages $5,000 per violation**; **IL Cothron per-scan stacking** (50 Г— $1,000); **IL reckless and 50 scans per-scan $5,000** (50 Г— $5,000); **IL no violations no exposure**; **IL private right of action always available**; **IL attorney fees recoverable when violation**; **IL anti-tying violation when no key alternative**; **IL citation pins BIPA subsections and Cothron**; **WA missing consent violation** (RCW 19.375); **WA no private right of action**; **WA no statutory damages per violation**; **TX missing consent violation** (В§ 503.001 $25,000 per violation); **TX civil penalty $25,000 per violation**; **TX no private right of action**; **CA CCPA classifies biometric as sensitive PI**; **CA missing consent violation** (В§ 1798.100 CCPA/CPRA); **CA no state-specific biometric statute note**; **default no state-specific statute note** (FTC Act В§ 5); **default anti-tying violation when no key alternative**; **traditional key access required when biometric lock installed** (5-regime sweep); **no lock installed no key access required**; **five regimes routed correctly**; **IL unique private right of action invariant** (4-regime sweep); **IL unique per-scan stacking invariant** (vs TX fixed); **IL negligent vs reckless damages 5x ratio**; **IL saturating mul no overflow on extreme scans** (u32::MAX); **IL zero scans floors at one scan**; **anti-tying violation across all regimes when no key alternative** (5-regime sweep); **full IL compliance no violations**; **tenant refusal engaged when refused**; **tenant refusal not engaged when consented**.

`traderview-expense::source_of_income_discrimination` is the **source-of-income (SOI) discrimination ban module** вҖ” when may a landlord lawfully refuse to accept Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8), HUD-VASH, SSI/SSDI, child support/alimony, TANF, or other lawful non-wage income as the basis for rejecting an applicant? Trader-landlord critical: refusing a Section 8 voucher in a SOI-protected jurisdiction triggers five-figure penalties + actual damages + attorney fees + punitive damages; in non-protected jurisdictions the federal Fair Housing Act does NOT make voucher refusal a per se violation. Distinct from `fair_chance_housing` (criminal background screening) and `tenant_in_foreclosure_protection` (post-foreclosure protections).

**Four regimes** вҖ” SOI-protected (CA + NJ + NY) versus federal FHA baseline:

| Regime | Authority | Max civil penalty | First-offense penalty | Subsequent offense |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|-----------------------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Gov. Code В§В§ 12955 + 12987 + 12989.2 + SB 329 (eff. 2020) | $50,000 | Same | Same |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 10:5-12.5 (NJ LAD) + DCR | **Tiered**: $10K first / $25K subsequent | $10,000 | $25,000 |
| New York | N.Y. Exec. Law В§ 296(5)(a)(1) (eff. April 2019) + NYC Admin. Code В§ 8-107(5)(a)(5) (eff. 2008) | $250,000 | Same | Same |
| Default | 42 USC В§ 3604 (federal FHA baseline) | $0 вҖ” no per se SOI protection | N/A | N/A |

**California SB 329 (Housing Opportunities Act of 2019)** вҖ” Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers explicitly added to FEHA source-of-income definition under Cal. Gov. Code В§ 12955. Remedies under В§ 12987 + В§ 12989.2: actual damages + emotional distress + civil penalty + attorney fees + injunctive relief. Narrow owner-occupied single-family-dwelling carve-out (вүӨ4 units) available but rarely satisfied for investor traders. Pinned by `ca_voucher_refusal_violates`, `ca_disparate_income_multiple_violates` (disparate income-multiple application), `ca_note_pins_sb329_eff_2020`, `ca_max_penalty_50k_cents`, and `ca_small_landlord_carveout_pins_warning_note`.

**New Jersey LAD tiered penalty structure** вҖ” uniquely tiered among the three protected regimes: DCR administrative $1,000-$5,000 initial enforcement; LAD up to $10,000 first offense / $25,000 subsequent offense. Penalty escalation triggered by `prior_findings_count > 0`. Pinned by `nj_first_offense_penalty_10k`, `nj_subsequent_offense_penalty_25k`, `nj_uniquely_tiered_penalty_invariant` (tiered escalation vs CA + NY fixed-cap), and `nj_note_pins_dcr_penalty_range`.

**New York dual-layer state + NYC enforcement** вҖ” N.Y. Exec. Law В§ 296(5)(a)(1) state-level SOI ban (eff. April 2019) layered on top of NYC Admin. Code В§ 8-107(5)(a)(5) city-level ban (eff. 2008). NYC Commission on Human Rights has obtained over $780K in damages + penalties since 2014 enforcement. Pinned by `ny_voucher_refusal_violates`, `ny_citation_pins_state_and_nyc`, `ny_nyc_enforcement_note_pinned` ($780K + 2014), and `ny_note_pins_eff_april_2019`.

**Default federal FHA baseline (42 USC В§ 3604) вҖ” NO per se SOI protection**. Section 8 voucher refusal is NOT a federal FHA violation absent disparate-treatment or disparate-impact showing. Local protections may apply (Cook County IL, Seattle WA, DC, etc.) but not universal. Pinned by `default_no_protection_no_violation`, `default_uniquely_no_protection_invariant` (3-regime sweep: CA + NJ + NY engage, Default does NOT), and `default_note_pins_local_protections_warning` (Cook County + Seattle hints).

**Protected source truth table** вҖ” Section 8 + HUD-VASH + SSI/SSDI + child support/alimony + TANF all engage SOI protection; employment wages do NOT (income-multiple screening lawful). Pinned by `protected_source_truth_table` (6-source sweep), `ssi_ssdi_protected_in_ca`, `child_support_protected_in_ny`, `tanf_protected_in_nj`, `hud_vash_protected_in_ca`, and `ca_employment_wages_no_protection`.

**Three remedies engage together in protected regimes** вҖ” actual damages + attorney fees + punitive damages are cumulative when a SOI violation is established. Pinned by `three_remedies_engage_together_in_protected_regimes` (3-regime sweep) and `protected_regimes_all_punish_voucher_refusal`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/source-of-income-discrimination`. Thirty-two tests pin: **CA voucher refusal violates** (FEHA В§ 12955); **CA employment wages no protection**; **CA disparate income multiple violates** (income-multiple applied to voucher portion); **CA unrelated legitimate reason no violation**; **CA citation pins SB 329 and subsections**; **CA max penalty 50k cents**; **NJ voucher refusal violates**; **NJ first offense penalty 10k**; **NJ subsequent offense penalty 25k**; **NJ citation pins LAD**; **NY voucher refusal violates**; **NY citation pins state and NYC**; **NY NYC enforcement note pinned** ($780K + 2014); **default no protection no violation**; **default citation pins FHA baseline**; **protected source truth table** (6-source sweep); **four regimes routed correctly**; **protected regimes all punish voucher refusal** (3-regime sweep); **default uniquely no protection invariant** (Default vs CA + NJ + NY); **refusal reason truth table California** (4-cell); **CA small landlord carveout pins warning note** (вүӨ4 units); **NJ uniquely tiered penalty invariant** (NJ tiered vs CA + NY fixed); **defensive prior findings overflow clamped** (u32::MAX вҶ’ tier 2); **SSI SSDI protected in CA**; **child support protected in NY**; **TANF protected in NJ**; **HUD VASH protected in CA**; **CA note pins SB 329 eff 2020**; **NJ note pins DCR penalty range** ($1K-$5K + $25K); **NY note pins eff April 2019**; **default note pins local protections warning** (Cook County + Seattle); **three remedies engage together in protected regimes** (3-regime sweep).

`traderview-expense::fair_chance_housing` is the **landlord criminal-background-check restriction compliance check** вҖ” three U.S. jurisdictions have enacted statutes layering on top of the federal FCRA (15 U.S.C. В§ 1681c) to restrict how residential landlords may inquire about and consider applicants' criminal history. Distinct from `tenant_data_privacy` (data-handling) and `application_fees` (cost of the background check itself); this module models the substantive limits on what convictions a landlord may consider and when.

**Four regimes** with distinct pre-/post-conditional-offer rules, barred categories, and lookback windows:

| Regime | Statute | Pre-offer inquiry | Lookback window | Barred categories | Other reqs |
|--------|---------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|
| `NewJerseyFcha` | N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq. (S250 / A1919, eff. 2022-01-01) | Prohibited | FCHA formula (e.g. 1st-deg indictable 6yr; 4th-deg 1yr) | arrests, pending cases, sealed/expunged, juvenile adj. | Individualized assessment + 30-day appeal window |
| `NewYorkCityFchha` | NYC Admin Code В§ 8-107.1 (Local Law 24 of 2024, eff. 2025-01-01) | Prohibited | Felony 5yr / Misdemeanor 3yr (from release or sentencing) | arrests, pending cases, ACDs, juvenile adj., sealed/expunged, non-criminal-violation convictions | Sex-offender-registry convictions always considerable |
| `CaliforniaFeha` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1786.18 + 2 Cal. Code Regs. В§ 12266 | Allowed | None statutory; CRA reporting limit applies | arrests, sealed/expunged | No blanket bans; individualized assessment (nature/severity + time + rehabilitation) |
| `Default` | 15 U.S.C. В§ 1681c (FCRA) | Allowed | 7-year ceiling on CRA arrest reports; conviction reports have no federal time limit | arrests > 7 years old (FCRA cap) | None state-specific |

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/fair-chance-housing`. Twenty-five tests pin: **NJ pre-offer inquiry violation** (inquiry_before_offer=true + conditional_offer_made=false); **NJ post-offer inquiry OK** with individualized assessment; **NJ arrest barred category**; **NJ juvenile-adjudication barred** (regression вҖ” NJ FCHA bars 4 categories); **NJ no-individualized-assessment violation** + "30-day appeal" citation regression; **NYC pre-offer inquiry violation** + "Local Law 24" citation; **NYC felony at 5-year boundary OK** (regression вҖ” вүӨ 5 strict); **NYC felony 6 years outside lookback** (regression вҖ” > 5 strict); **NYC misdemeanor at 3-year boundary OK**; **NYC misdemeanor 4 years outside lookback** (regression вҖ” > 3 strict); **NYC sex-offender-registry always considerable** regardless of age (30 years old still OK); **NYC non-criminal-violation barred** + "non-criminal" citation regression (NYC-only barred category not in NJ); **NYC ACD-disposition barred** + "ACDs" citation; **CA arrest barred**; **CA no-individualized-assessment violation** + "rehabilitation" note regression + "12266" citation; **CA with individualized assessment OK**; **default FCRA 7-year arrest window expired** + "1681c" + "FCRA" citation; **default no-violation recent conviction**; **jurisdiction routing** (NJ вҶ’ NJ FCHA, NY/New York + NY/NYC вҶ’ NYC FCHHA, NY/Buffalo вҶ’ Default, CA вҶ’ CA FEHA, TX вҶ’ Default); **case-insensitive jurisdiction lookup**; **NJ-only single-state-uniqueness invariant**; **NYC lookback off-by-one boundary exhaustive** (felony 5 OK / 6 expired + misdemeanor 3 OK / 4 expired in single test); **citations pin authorities** (46:8-52 + FCHA + 8-107.1 + Local Law 24 + 1786.18 + 12266 + FCRA + 1681c).

`traderview-expense::family_childcare_home_right` is the **tenant family child-care home (FCCH) operation right module** вҖ” when may a landlord lawfully prohibit a tenant from operating a licensed family day care home for children in a residential rental unit? Trader-landlord critical: in California, a lease provision prohibiting family child care home operation is VOID, and a landlord cannot reject a tenant for intending to operate one. Distinct from siblings `tenant_organizing` (tenant-association-formation right), `tenant_data_privacy` (data handling), and `fair_chance_housing` (criminal background screening).

**Three regimes** вҖ” California's "void as a matter of law" framework vs New York's reasonableness review vs Default federal FHA familial status only:

| Regime | Authority | Lease prohibition | Notice required | Deposit cap | License authority |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| California | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 1597.40-1597.46 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 | **VOID** under В§ 1597.40(a) | **30 days advance written** | **1 month rent** (AB 12) | Title 22 CDSS |
| New York | N.Y. Social Services Law В§ 390 + N.Y. RPL В§ 235-b | Subject to reasonableness review | 0 days statutory | None statutory | OCFS |
| Default | 42 USC В§ 3604 (FHA familial status) | Allowed (covers families with children but NOT childcare-business) | 0 days | None | N/A federal |

**California В§ 1597.40(a) void-as-a-matter-of-law lease prohibition** вҖ” every restriction or prohibition on use of property for a family day care home for children is VOID, whether by covenant, condition upon use or occupancy, or upon transfer of title. State preemption under В§ 1597.40(e) occupies the field to exclusion of municipal zoning + building + fire codes. Pinned by `ca_lease_prohibition_void`, `ca_uniquely_voids_lease_prohibition_invariant` (3-regime sweep: only CA voids prohibition), `ca_note_pins_state_preemption`, and `ca_citation_pins_six_health_safety_sections` (В§В§ 1597.40 + 1597.41 + 1597.42 + 1597.43 + 1597.44 + 1597.46 + Civ В§ 1950.5).

**California В§ 1597.40(c) 30-day advance written notice** вҖ” prospective FCCH provider tenant must provide 30 days' written notice to landlord prior to commencement of operation. Pinned by `ca_30_day_notice_boundary_compliant` (boundary), `ca_29_day_notice_violates`, `ca_zero_days_notice_violates`, `ca_60_day_notice_compliant`, `ca_uniquely_requires_30_day_notice_invariant` (CA = 30 days; NY + Default = 0 days), and `ca_note_pins_30_day_notice`.

**California В§ 1597.40(d) increased deposit subject to AB 12 cap** вҖ” landlord may require increased security deposit specifically for FCCH operation (notwithstanding lower amount required of non-FCCH tenants), but TOTAL security deposit may NOT exceed Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 ceiling (AB 12 = 1 month's rent for most landlords post-July 2024). Pinned by `ca_deposit_at_1_month_boundary_compliant`, `ca_deposit_over_1_month_violates`, `ca_max_total_deposit_equals_one_month_rent`, `ca_increased_deposit_within_cap_does_not_violate`, `ca_deposit_cap_uniquely_engaged_invariant` (CA engages cap; NY + Default do not), and `ca_note_pins_ab_12_deposit_cap`.

**California В§ 1597.42 Title 22 licensing required** вҖ” FCCH operation requires license from California Department of Social Services Community Care Licensing under Title 22 California Code of Regulations. Pinned by `ca_unlicensed_fcch_violates`.

**California 4-violation stack on full failure** вҖ” lease prohibition + < 30-day notice + over-AB 12 deposit + unlicensed = 4 simultaneous violations. Pinned by `ca_stacks_4_violations_when_all_4_failures`.

**New York reasonableness-review framework** вҖ” N.Y. Social Services Law В§ 390 protects licensed family day care and group family day care home operation; landlord may not unreasonably withhold consent. Lacks CA's explicit void-as-matter-of-law provision. OCFS licensing required. Pinned by `ny_compliant_passes`, `ny_lease_prohibition_violates_reasonableness`, `ny_unlicensed_fcch_violates` (OCFS), and `ny_citation_pins_state_law`.

**Default federal FHA familial-status baseline** вҖ” 42 USC В§ 3604 covers families with children from refusal to rent, but does NOT extend to childcare-business operation. Lease prohibition is enforceable at federal default level. Local protections may apply (Cook County IL, Seattle WA, Minneapolis MN). Pinned by `default_no_prohibition_permitted`, `default_lease_prohibition_blocks_operation`, `default_citation_pins_fha_baseline`, and `default_note_pins_local_protections` (Cook County + Seattle).

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative rent / negative deposits clamped to 0 via `.max(0)` and `.saturating_add()`. Pinned by `defensive_negative_rent_clamped` and `defensive_negative_deposit_clamped`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/family-childcare-home-right`. Thirty-two tests pin: **CA compliant passes**; **CA lease prohibition void** (В§ 1597.40(a)); **CA 29-day notice violates**; **CA 30-day notice boundary compliant**; **CA deposit at 1-month boundary compliant**; **CA deposit over 1-month violates** (AB 12); **CA unlicensed FCCH violates** (В§ 1597.42 Title 22); **CA citation pins six Health & Safety sections**; **CA note pins state preemption**; **CA note pins 30-day notice**; **CA note pins AB 12 deposit cap**; **NY compliant passes**; **NY lease prohibition violates reasonableness**; **NY unlicensed FCCH violates** (OCFS); **NY citation pins state law**; **default no prohibition permitted**; **default lease prohibition blocks operation**; **default citation pins FHA baseline**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **CA uniquely voids lease prohibition invariant**; **CA uniquely requires 30-day notice invariant**; **CA stacks 4 violations when all 4 failures**; **defensive negative rent clamped**; **defensive negative deposit clamped**; **CA 60-day notice compliant**; **CA zero-day notice violates**; **CA increased deposit within cap does not violate**; **NY no deposit tracking**; **default no deposit tracking**; **CA deposit cap uniquely engaged invariant**; **default note pins local protections**; **CA max total deposit equals one month rent**.

`traderview-expense::fha_design_construction` is the **Fair Housing Act design and construction requirements for covered multifamily dwellings** module вҖ” 24 CFR В§ 100.205. Federal accessibility floor for new-construction multifamily buildings first occupied AFTER March 13, 1991. Trader-landlord operational concern when acquiring, developing, or substantially renovating a covered multifamily dwelling. Distinct from `reasonable_accommodation_modification` (individual tenant accommodation requests under FHA В§ 3604(f)(3)), `service_animal` (ADA/FHA service-animal framework), and `emotional_support_animal_documentation` (ESA reliability).

**Seven В§ 100.205(c) requirements** all must be satisfied:

| # | Requirement | Subsection |
|---|-------------|------------|
| 1 | Accessible building entrance on accessible route (terrain-impracticality defense available, burden on builder) | В§ 100.205(c)(1) |
| 2 | Accessible and usable public/common-use areas (lobbies, mailboxes, parking, recreation) | В§ 100.205(c)(2) |
| 3 | Usable doors (32" clear opening for wheelchair passage) | В§ 100.205(c)(3) |
| 4 | Accessible route INTO AND THROUGH covered dwelling unit | В§ 100.205(c)(4) |
| 5 | Light switches, outlets, thermostats, environmental controls in accessible locations | В§ 100.205(c)(5) |
| 6 | Reinforced walls in bathrooms for later grab-bar installation | В§ 100.205(c)(6) |
| 7 | Usable kitchens and bathrooms (wheelchair maneuverability) | В§ 100.205(c)(7) |

**Two applicability gates** kill the rule at the door:

| Gate | Effect |
|------|--------|
| First occupancy on or before March 13, 1991 | Grandfathered вҖ” rule does NOT apply |
| Not a covered multifamily dwelling (< 4 units / not elevator-served / not ground-floor) | Rule does NOT apply |

**Federal enforcement carries dual private + administrative paths.** 42 U.S.C. В§ 3613(c) private suit вҖ” actual damages + punitive + attorney fees. 42 U.S.C. В§ 3612 HUD administrative civil penalty вҖ” $25,597 first violation / $63,991 prior violation within 5 years (2025 inflation-adjusted). Pinned by `first_violation_penalty_25597`, `prior_violation_within_5_years_penalty_63991`, `private_suit_remedies_in_violation_note`, `no_civil_penalty_when_compliant`.

**Terrain-impracticality defense is В§ 100.205(a)-specific to Requirement 1.** Burden is on designer/builder to prove that terrain or unusual site characteristics make the accessible building entrance impractical. The defense extends ONLY to Requirement 1 вҖ” it does NOT excuse failures on Requirements 2-7. Pinned by `missing_requirement_1_with_terrain_impracticality_no_violation`, `impracticality_defense_does_not_extend_to_other_requirements`, `impracticality_carveout_only_applies_to_requirement_1`.

**All seven requirements must be satisfied вҖ” single gap defeats compliance.** Pinned by `all_seven_requirements_must_be_satisfied_invariant` (7-iteration sweep flipping each requirement to false confirms each individually breaks compliance), `all_seven_violations_accumulate_when_nothing_satisfied` (all 7 violations stack), `multiple_violations_accumulate` (3 simultaneous failures = 3 violations listed).

**Federal floor вҖ” state codes may add but cannot reduce.** CA Title 24, MA Architectural Access Board (AAB), and NY Human Rights Law all add stricter accessibility requirements; the FHA design and construction baseline cannot be reduced by state law. Pinned by `federal_floor_note_always_present`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/fha-design-construction`. Twenty-six tests pin: **full compliance passes**; **pre-March 13, 1991 grandfathered** (rule does NOT apply); **non-covered multifamily dwelling rule does not apply**; **missing Requirement 1 without impracticality violation**; **missing Requirement 1 with terrain impracticality no violation** (defense documented); **missing Requirement 2 public/common areas violation**; **missing Requirement 3 usable doors violation** (wheelchair passage); **missing Requirement 4 accessible route violation**; **missing Requirement 5 environmental controls violation** (thermostats); **missing Requirement 6 reinforced walls violation** (grab-bar); **missing Requirement 7 kitchens/bathrooms violation** (wheelchair maneuverability); **first violation penalty $25,597**; **prior violation within 5 years penalty $63,991**; **private suit remedies in violation note** (В§ 3613(c) actual + punitive + attorney fees); **all seven requirements must be satisfied invariant** (7-iteration sweep); **multiple violations accumulate**; **federal floor note always present**; **citation pins subsections and authorities** (В§ 3604(f)(3)(C) + В§ 100.205(a)/(c)(1)-(7) + Subpart D + В§ 3613(c) + В§ 3612 + HUD Design Manual); **rule applies only when post-March 13 1991 AND covered**; **note describes civil penalty for first violation** ($25,597); **note describes civil penalty for prior violation** ($63,991); **no civil penalty when compliant**; **impracticality defense does not extend to other requirements** (Req 3 still violates with Req 1 defense); **all seven violations accumulate when nothing satisfied**; **impracticality carveout only applies to Requirement 1**; **pre-March 13, 1991 with full violations remains compliant** (grandfathered building immune).

`traderview-expense::demolition_tenant_notice` is the **state demolition-tenant-notice landlord compliance check** вҖ” addresses the NOTICE PERIOD a landlord must provide BEFORE a tenant must vacate for demolition. Distinct from `owner_move_in_eviction` (landlord MOVES INTO the unit) and `tenant_relocation_assistance` (DOLLAR amount owed); this module models the notice-time floor.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Standard notice | Senior/disabled extension | First-year prohibition |
|--------|---------|------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|
| `CaliforniaEllisAct` | Cal. Govt Code В§ 7060.4(a)/(b) | **120 days** | **365 days** for tenants вүҘ 62 OR disabled AND вүҘ 1 year residency | No |
| `Oregon` | ORS 90.427 + ORS 90.323(3) | **90 days** | None | **Yes** вҖ” no termination in first 12 months |
| `Washington` | RCW 59.18.650 | **120 days** | None | No |
| `Default` | вҖ” | 0 | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**California Ellis Act В§ 7060.4(b) 365-day extension stacked prongs**: tenant must be (вүҘ 62 OR disabled) AND have вүҘ 1 year residency вҖ” both prongs required. Under-62 + non-disabled OR < 1 year residency falls back to the 120-day default.
