Twenty-one tests pin: **Florida pre-amendment owner no consent grandfather engaged**; **Florida pre-amendment owner consented no grandfather**; **Florida post-amendment owner no grandfather**; **Florida heir acquires grandfather preserved**; **Florida transfer to unrelated party erodes grandfather**; **Florida short-term rental restriction binds all owners**; **Florida вүӨ3 rentals restriction binds all owners**; **Florida pre-July-2021 amendment not subject to grandfather**; **Arizona declaration permits no enforceable restriction**; **Arizona declaration restricts enforceable**; **Arizona agent designation validly invoked**; **Arizona no agent designation no engagement**; **Default declaration permits no enforceable restriction**; **Default declaration restricts enforceable** (no grandfather in Default); **only Florida offers grandfather protection invariant** (3-regime sweep); **only Arizona has statutory agent designation invariant** (3-regime sweep); **Florida grandfather truth table** (4-cell pre/post Г— consent sweep); **Florida exception overrides grandfather invariant** (both exceptions bind regardless of grandfather-favoring facts); **citation pins all regime authorities** (В§ 720.306(1)(h) + В§ 720.306(1)(h)1 + В§ 720.306(1)(h)2 + В§ 720.306(1)(h)3 + A.R.S. В§ 33-1806.01 + В§ 33-1806.01(A) + В§ 33-1806.01(B)); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for HOA private covenants vs. municipal STR public regulation); **Florida grandfather eroded by transfer truth table** (4-cell heir Г— transferred sweep вҖ” transfer to unrelated party always erodes grandfather under В§ 720.306(1)(h)3).

`traderview-expense::hoa_fee_tenant_enforcement` is the **HOA fee enforcement against tenant in single-family / townhome / condominium rentals** module вҖ” when can a homeowners association enforce delinquent dues DIRECTLY against the tenant rather than only the owner-landlord? Critical trader-landlord operational risk: trader-landlord defaults on HOA dues вҶ’ HOA may seize rent directly from tenant and evict tenant for nonpayment in Florida. Sibling to `hoa_rental_restriction` (HOA's restrictions ON renting), not duplicative.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Direct tenant enforcement | Tenant immunity | Tenant rent credit | HOA eviction authority |
|--------|---------------------------|------------------|---------------------|--------------------------|
| Florida В§ 720.3085(8) + В§ 718.116(11) | YES (after demand) | YES | YES | YES |
| Texas В§ 209.0064 | NO (owner only) | N/A | N/A | NO |
| California Davis-Stirling В§В§ 5650, 5710, 5715 | NO (foreclose on owner) | N/A | N/A | NO |
| Default | NO | N/A | N/A | NO |

**Florida вҖ” Fla. Stat. В§ 720.3085(8)(a)-(d) (single-family HOA) + В§ 718.116(11) (condominium)**. Most aggressive direct-tenant enforcement framework in the country. When parcel owner is delinquent on ANY monetary obligation to the association, **В§ 720.3085(8)(a)** authorizes the HOA to DEMAND that the tenant pay subsequent rental payments directly to the association. Demand MUST be by hand delivery OR United States mail (verbal demand insufficient). **В§ 720.3085(8)(b)** grants tenant IMMUNITY from any landlord claim for rent timely paid to the association after written demand. **В§ 720.3085(8)(c)** grants tenant a RENT CREDIT against landlord-owed rent equal to amounts paid to the association вҖ” tenant liability capped at amount due to landlord. **В§ 720.3085(8)(d)** authorizes the HOA to sue the tenant directly for eviction if tenant fails to pay after written demand.

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§ 209.0064 (Texas Residential Property Owners Protection Act)**. HOA enforces ONLY against OWNER (landlord), not tenant. If lease passes through HOA dues to tenant, landlord may evict for nonpayment per lease terms вҖ” but HOA itself has no direct authority over tenant. В§ 209.0064 third-party collection requires (i) written notice by certified mail to OWNER listing delinquent amounts + payment plans, (ii) 45-day cure period.

**California вҖ” Davis-Stirling Common Interest Development Act (Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 5650, 5710, 5715)**. HOA enforces only against OWNER. В§ 5710 authorizes HOA to foreclose on owner's interest in unit, NOT tenant. No statutory direct-tenant collection mechanism.

**Default вҖ” HOA enforces only against owner**. Most states follow CA / TX pattern. Federal **FDCPA (15 U.S.C. В§ 1692)** applies if HOA hires third-party collector.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/hoa-fee-tenant-enforcement`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **FL full demand with notice engages enforcement** (all 4 flags green); **FL demand without written notice voids enforcement** (В§ 720.3085(8) hand-delivery / USPS requirement); **FL no landlord delinquency no enforcement**; **FL no demand no enforcement**; **FL demand with notice but tenant not paid eviction engaged**; **FL condominium parallel rule note present** (В§ 718.116(11)); **FL citation pins subsections (a)-(d) and condo section**; **FL tenant credit note describes liability cap**; **TX owner-only enforcement invariant**; **TX delinquent without 45-day notice violation** (В§ 209.0064); **TX lease passthrough note describes landlord eviction**; **TX citation pins В§ 209.0064**; **CA owner-only enforcement invariant**; **CA Davis-Stirling foreclose owner not tenant note** (В§ 5710); **CA citation pins В§В§ 5650, 5710, 5715 + Davis-Stirling Act**; **default no direct tenant authority invariant**; **default FDCPA note for third-party collector** (15 U.S.C. В§ 1692); **Florida unique direct tenant enforcement invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **Florida unique tenant immunity invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **Florida unique rent credit invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **Florida unique HOA eviction authority invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **four regimes routed correctly** (FL + TX + CA + Default sweep); **lease passthrough note in three non-Florida regimes**; **FL delinquency without demand no enforcement**; **FL demand without delinquency no enforcement**; **FL four subsection notes all present** ((a) + (b) + (c) + (d)); **TX clean compliance no violations**; **FL clean compliance no violations**.

`traderview-expense::rent_abatement_construction_nuisance` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant rent abatement during landlord construction nuisance framework module** вҖ” when is tenant entitled to rent abatement for landlord-caused construction noise, dust, vibration, debris, blocked egress, or loss of building-wide services? Distinct from sibling `landlord_water_heat_emergency_response` (iter 451 emergency response timelines), `habitability_remedies` (general repair-and-deduct framework), `landlord_harassment` (anti-harassment civil penalties), `tenant_noise_nuisance_enforcement` (tenant-on-tenant nuisance). Trader-landlord critical because traders who own multi-unit rental buildings routinely undertake major capital improvements (MCI), individual apartment improvements (IAI), facade restoration, roof replacement, elevator modernization, plumbing risers вҖ” each potentially triggering tenant rent abatement claims for reduced services and breach of warranty of habitability and covenant of quiet enjoyment.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Key Source | Remedies |
|--------------|-----------|----------|
| **NYC rent-stabilized** | 9 NYCRR В§ 2523.4 + DHCR Form RA-84; NYC HMC В§ 27-2005.1 + В§ 27-2005(d); HSTPA 2019; Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 | DHCR rent reduction to LEVEL IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING DECREASE; 22-40% industry-standard abatement; civil penalty $1,000-$10,000 per construction harassment violation |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1927 + В§ 1941.1 + В§ 1942 + В§ 1942.4; Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974) | Repair-and-deduct up to one month's rent; В§ 1942.4 prohibits rent collection during uninhabitability; SF Rent Ordinance В§ 37.10B local harassment ordinance |
| **Default** | Park West Mgmt. Corp. v. Mitchell, 47 N.Y.2d 316 (1979); Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973); Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 5.4 + В§ 6.1; URLTA В§ 2.104 + В§ 4.103 + В§ 4.107 | Warranty of habitability rent abatement; covenant of quiet enjoyment claim; constructive eviction with 100% rent abatement for period vacated |

**Five categories of construction nuisance**:
1. **NOISE** вҖ” jackhammering, drilling exceeding NYC Noise Code В§ 24-218 dB limits (daytime max 80 dB at 7 AM - 6 PM weekdays)
2. **DUST** вҖ” uncontrolled construction dust requiring frequent unit cleaning + air-quality impact
3. **VIBRATION** вҖ” heavy machinery causing wall cracks, damage to tenant property, structural concerns
4. **DEBRIS** вҖ” falling materials in common areas or unit egress paths
5. **SERVICE INTERRUPTION** вҖ” temporary loss of elevator, hot water, heat, gas, electricity, laundry, lobby access

**Rent abatement amount calculation framework**:
- NYC DHCR rent reduction (rent-stabilized): sets rent to LEVEL IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING DECREASE per В§ 2523.4; reductions stack across multiple decreased services
- Industry-standard negotiated abatement: **22-40%** of monthly rent for entire construction period; 50-80% for severe service interruption
- Repair-and-deduct (CA В§ 1942): up to ONE MONTH'S RENT; max twice in any 12-month period
- Constructive eviction: **100% rent abatement** for period tenant vacates due to landlord-created uninhabitable conditions

**Construction harassment / bad-faith elements (7 categories)**: (1) construction timed to coincide with tenant protected activities; (2) work outside permitted hours violating noise ordinances; (3) construction without required municipal permits; (4) tenant access blocked beyond reasonable necessity; (5) vibrations/dust beyond industry-standard mitigation; (6) failure to provide NYC HMC В§ 27-2005.1 24-72 hour tenant notification; (7) construction designed to force tenant out of rent-controlled unit.

**NYC HMC В§ 27-2005.1 tenant notification requirements**: (1) 24-72 hour written notice before work commencing in or near unit; (2) notice must identify date/time, duration, nature of work, contractor identity, building permit number; (3) NYC Department of Buildings construction permit posting required at job site; (4) failure violates NYC HMC В§ 27-2005(d) + Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 вҖ” **$1,000-$10,000 civil penalty per violation**.

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) NYC 50-unit rent-stabilized facade restoration 8 months; tenants file DHCR Form RA-84; 35% rent reduction; (2) CA multifamily 4-month elevator modernization; В§ 1942 repair-and-deduct plus 25% negotiated abatement; (3) default-jurisdiction roof replacement with debris and vibration; tenants withhold rent under warranty of habitability; (4) NYC retaliatory jackhammering 6 AM Saturday after rent-overcharge complaint вҖ” В§ 27-2005.1 notice violation plus В§ 27-2005(d) civil penalty $1,000-$10,000 plus Tenant Anti-Harassment Act plus IIED; (5) MCI rent increase capped at 2%/year under DHCR Operational Bulletin 2024-1; cannot evict during MCI; failure to provide В§ 27-2005.1 notice voids permit plus triple damages under HSTPA.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-abatement-construction-nuisance`. Thirty-four tests pin: **NYC DHCR RA-84 rent reduction available**; **NYC DHCR 35% default calculation**; **service interruption habitability breach** (Park West v. Mitchell); **noise within permitted hours quiet enjoyment only**; **noise outside permitted hours habitability breach**; **vibration quiet enjoyment breach**; **dust habitability breach**; **debris habitability breach**; **no notice construction harassment engaged**; **outside permitted hours construction harassment engaged** (Noise Code В§ 24-218); **no permits construction harassment engaged**; **bad-faith construction harassment engaged**; **CA repair-and-deduct one-month rent** (В§ 1942 + В§ 1942.4); **CA quiet enjoyment citation** (В§ 1927); **constructive eviction full abatement** (8 months Г— rent); **negotiated abatement overrides default**; **default jurisdiction 30% abatement warranty breach**; **DHCR RA-84 not filed no DHCR rent reduction**; **non-NYC no DHCR rent reduction**; **non-NYC no construction harassment penalty**; **category truth table five cells**; **NYC uniquely engages DHCR invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (9 NYCRR В§ 2523.4 + DHCR Form RA-84 + NYC HMC В§ 27-2005(d) + В§ 27-2005.1 + NYC Noise Code В§ 24-218 + NYC Tenant Anti-Harassment Act 2018 + NY RPL В§ 235-d + HSTPA 2019 + DHCR Operational Bulletin 2024-1 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1927 + В§ 1941.1 + В§ 1942 + В§ 1942.4 + Green v. Superior Court + Park West v. Mitchell + Boston Housing v. Hemingway + SF Rent Ordinance В§ 37.10B + URLTA В§ 2.104 + В§ 4.103 + В§ 4.107 + Restatement (Second) Property В§ 5.4 + В§ 6.1); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins five nuisance categories**; **note pins abatement calculation framework**; **note pins construction harassment seven elements**; **note pins NYC HMC В§ 27-2005.1 notice**; **note pins NYC DHCR Form RA-84**; **note pins California framework**; **note pins default common law three states**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::rent_acceleration_enforceability` is the **rent acceleration clause enforceability module** вҖ” when may a landlord demand the full unpaid rent balance for the remainder of the lease term as a lump sum upon tenant default? States diverge sharply on (a) whether such a clause is enforceable as liquidated damages, (b) whether the landlord must mitigate or apply a present-value discount, or (c) whether the clause is void entirely as an unenforceable penalty. Trader-critical for landlord-tenant disputes on multi-year commercial leases or residential leases drafted by landlord counsel. Distinct from siblings `duty_to_mitigate_damages` (general mitigation rules), `late_fee_caps` (per-month late charges), `lease_cure_period` (non-rent breach cure), and `holdover_tenant_damages` (post-lease-term recovery).

**Three regimes** with sharply different outcomes:

| Regime | Authority | Lease type | Outcome |
|--------|-----------|------------|---------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1671(d) | Residential | **PRESUMED INVALID** вҖ” landlord must rebut |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1671(b) + В§ 1951.2 | Commercial | Reasonableness test + mitigation duty |
| New York | Holy Properties Ltd., L.P. v. Kenneth Cole, 87 N.Y.2d 130 (1995) | Commercial | **Enforceable WITHOUT mitigation duty**; PV discount required |
| New York | Common law (residential) | Residential | Penalty doctrine + mitigation duty |
| Default | Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 356 + Property В§ 12.1 | Either | Penalty doctrine + mitigation + PV discount |

**California В§ 1671(d) вҖ” strongest residential tenant protection.** Liquidated damages provisions in residential leases are PRESUMED INVALID until the landlord affirmatively proves the amount was a reasonable estimate of damages at contract formation. Expert testimony often required to rebut. В§ 1671(b) governs commercial reasonableness test. В§ 1951.2 imposes mitigation duty on commercial landlords. Pinned by `california_residential_presumed_invalid_no_rebuttal` (no rebuttal вҶ’ enforceable amount = 0), `california_residential_rebutted_capped_at_actual_damages` (rebutted вҶ’ capped at actual-damages estimate), `california_commercial_reasonable_enforceable_with_mitigation` (mitigation offset applied), and `california_commercial_unreasonable_void_as_penalty` ($120K acceleration vs. $30K damages > 2Г— threshold вҶ’ penalty void).

**New York Holy Properties commercial rule вҖ” most landlord-favorable in the U.S.** The 1995 Court of Appeals decision holds that a commercial landlord NEED NOT mitigate damages after a tenant's material breach. Acceleration clauses enforceable absent fraud, exploitative overreaching, or unconscionable conduct. BUT вҖ” Court of Appeals also requires that accelerated rent be DISCOUNTED TO PRESENT VALUE when the landlord has possession, so that damages are not disproportionate to actual loss. Residential treatment less settled; falls back to penalty doctrine + mitigation duty. Pinned by `new_york_commercial_enforceable_with_pv_discount`, `new_york_commercial_no_pv_discount_violation` (PV discount required), `new_york_commercial_unconscionable_void` (fraud/overreaching/unconscionability voids), `new_york_residential_falls_back_to_penalty_doctrine`, and `new_york_residential_unreasonable_void`.

**Default common-law penalty doctrine + mitigation duty + PV discount.** Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 356: liquidated damages enforceable only if (a) reasonable forecast of actual damages AND (b) actual damages difficult to estimate at contract formation. Modern majority adopts Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 12.1 mitigation duty. Default applies BOTH mitigation reduction AND present-value discount. Pinned by `default_reasonable_with_pv_enforceable`, `default_unreasonable_void_as_penalty`, `default_no_pv_discount_violation_even_when_reasonable`, and `default_mitigation_not_attempted_violation`.

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_california_residential_has_presumption_invariant` (3-regime sweep вҖ” only CA residential triggers PresumedInvalidResidential); `only_new_york_commercial_skips_mitigation_invariant` (Holy Properties unique among the 3 regimes); `pv_discount_required_outside_california_invariant` (CA does not require PV explicitly вҖ” addresses via mitigation; NY commercial + Default both require PV); `unconscionable_conduct_voids_all_regimes_for_commercial` (NY-specific exception).

**Mitigation offset math** вҖ” `mitigation_offset_reduces_recovery` ($60K - $40K = $20K); `mitigation_offset_exceeds_acceleration_zero_recovery` (clamped at 0; never negative). Standard i64 arithmetic with `.max(0)` clamp to handle the case where mitigation income exceeds the demand.

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references siblings `duty_to_mitigate_damages`, `late_fee_caps`, `lease_cure_period`, `holdover_tenant_damages`, and pins the structural divergence: California most protective for RESIDENTIAL (В§ 1671(d) presumption); New York most landlord-favorable for COMMERCIAL (Holy Properties no-mitigation); Default sits between with full penalty doctrine + mitigation + PV discount. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-acceleration-enforceability`. Twenty-three tests pin: **California residential presumed invalid no rebuttal** ($0 enforceable); **California residential rebutted capped at actual damages**; **California commercial reasonable enforceable with mitigation** ($6M - $1M offset = $5M); **California commercial unreasonable void as penalty** (acceleration > 2Г— actual damages); **New York commercial enforceable with PV discount**; **New York commercial no PV discount violation**; **New York commercial unconscionable conduct void**; **New York residential falls back to penalty doctrine**; **New York residential unreasonable void** ($120K vs $10K вҶ’ void); **default reasonable with PV enforceable**; **default unreasonable void as penalty**; **default no PV discount violation even when reasonable**; **default mitigation not attempted violation**; **only California residential has presumption invariant** (3-regime sweep); **only New York commercial skips mitigation invariant** (Holy Properties); **PV discount required outside California invariant**; **unconscionable conduct voids all regimes for commercial**; **mitigation offset reduces recovery** ($60K - $40K = $20K); **mitigation offset exceeds acceleration zero recovery** (saturating at $0); **citation pins all regime authorities** (В§ 1671 + В§ 1671(b) + В§ 1671(d) + В§ 1951.2 + Holy Properties 87 N.Y.2d 130 (1995) + Restatement Contracts В§ 356 + Property В§ 12.1); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `duty_to_mitigate_damages` + `late_fee_caps` + `lease_cure_period` + `holdover_tenant_damages` + structural CA/NY/Default divergence); **defensive negative acceleration clamped**; **defensive negative mitigation offset clamped**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_in_foreclosure_protection` is the **tenant-in-foreclosure protections module** вҖ” what notice and timing must a successor in interest provide to existing tenants when a residential property is foreclosed? Trader-critical for landlord-investors purchasing foreclosed properties вҖ” buying property with existing bona fide tenants triggers a federal floor plus state-specific overlays. Three regimes built on the federal PTFA baseline, with California adding pre-sale posting requirements and New York extending protections to non-named and rent-regulated tenants.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Distinctive feature |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| Federal PTFA (Default) | Pub. L. 111-22 В§ 702 (restored permanently by Pub. L. 115-174 В§ 304, eff. June 23, 2018) | National floor вҖ” greater of 90 days OR lease remainder |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2924.8 + В§ 1161b | Pre-sale notice posting + first-class mailing; В§ 2924.8(d) **$100 infraction** for tearing down notice within 72 hours |
| New York | N.Y. RPAPL В§ 1305 | В§ 1305(4) preserves additional rights for rent-control/stabilization/subsidy tenants AND tenants not named in foreclosure action |

**PTFA bona fide tenant вҖ” three-prong test** (BOTH first conditions + the conjunctive rent test):

| Prong | Rule |
|-------|------|
| (1) | Tenant is NOT the mortgagor or mortgagor's spouse, parent, or child |
| (2) | Lease is an arm's-length transaction |
| (3) | Rent is NOT substantially less than fair market rent (UNLESS reduced/subsidized by federal, state, or local program) |

All three required. Pinned by `bona_fide_baseline_all_prongs_pass`, `mortgagor_family_disqualifies`, `non_arms_length_disqualifies`, `rent_substantially_below_market_disqualifies`, `subsidy_rescues_below_market_rent` (subsidy rescues the third prong even when rent is substantially below market), and `bona_fide_prongs_truth_table` (8-cell sweep вҖ” only the all-three-true cell engages bona fide status).

**PTFA notice timing вҖ” greater of 90 days or lease remainder**:

| Scenario | Notice period |
|----------|---------------|
| Bona fide + written lease + lease remaining вүҘ 90 days | Lease remainder |
| Bona fide + written lease + lease remaining < 90 days | 90 days minimum |
| Bona fide + no lease (month-to-month) | 90 days minimum |
| Non-bona-fide tenant | No PTFA notice protection (state law still applies) |
| Owner-occupant buyer primary residence | 90 days minimum (lease early-terminated) |

Pinned by `bona_fide_with_lease_gets_lease_end` (180 days remaining вҶ’ 180 days), `bona_fide_lease_below_90_days_gets_90`, `bona_fide_no_lease_gets_90_days_only`, `non_bona_fide_no_notice_period`, `owner_occupant_exception_engages`, `early_eviction_violation_before_90_day_window` (eviction within 90-day window flagged as PTFA violation), `day_90_passed_no_ptfa_window_violation`, and `ptfa_90_day_floor_all_regimes_invariant` (3-regime sweep confirms 90-day minimum is universal floor).

**California В§ 2924.8** layers pre-sale notice posting + first-class mailing addressed to "Resident of property subject to foreclosure sale" on top of PTFA. Failure to post + mail violates procedural prerequisites. В§ 2924.8(d) makes it an INFRACTION to tear down the posted notice within 72 hours of posting вҖ” $100 fine (applies to person who tore it down, NOT to landlord). В§ 1161b parallels PTFA for unlawful detainer post-foreclosure proceedings. Pinned by `california_pre_sale_notice_posted_compliant`, `california_pre_sale_notice_missing_violation`, `california_tear_down_infraction_engages`, `california_tear_down_only_applies_in_california`, and `only_california_has_tear_down_infraction_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

**New York RPAPL В§ 1305** mirrors PTFA's greater-of-90-or-lease-end rule. В§ 1305(4) preserves additional rights for (a) federally subsidized tenants, (b) rent-control/rent-stabilization tenants, and (c) tenants NOT NAMED in the foreclosure action. These tenants retain ALL pre-foreclosure protections layered ON TOP of В§ 1305 + PTFA floor. Pinned by `new_york_rent_control_preserves_additional_rights`, `new_york_not_named_in_foreclosure_preserves_rights`, `new_york_preservation_only_in_ny` (CA + Default ignore NY-specific fields), `new_york_no_preservation_when_neither_condition_met`, and `only_new_york_preserves_additional_rights_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references siblings `foreclosure_tenant_rights`, `mid_tenancy_ownership_change`, and `eviction_notices`. Federal PTFA creates a national FLOOR (90-day notice + lease-end honoring for bona fide tenants); state law adds CEILING when more protective. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-in-foreclosure-protection`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **bona fide baseline all prongs pass**; **mortgagor family disqualifies**; **non-arms-length disqualifies**; **rent substantially below market disqualifies**; **subsidy rescues below-market rent**; **bona fide with lease gets lease end** (180 days); **bona fide lease below 90 days gets 90**; **bona fide no lease gets 90 days only**; **non bona fide no notice period**; **owner occupant exception engages**; **early eviction violation before 90-day window**; **day 90 passed no PTFA window violation**; **California pre-sale notice posted compliant**; **California pre-sale notice missing violation**; **California tear-down infraction engages**; **California tear-down only applies in California**; **New York rent control preserves additional rights**; **New York not named in foreclosure preserves rights**; **New York preservation only in NY**; **New York no preservation when neither condition met**; **PTFA 90-day floor all regimes invariant** (3-regime sweep); **bona fide prongs truth table** (8-cell sweep вҖ” only all-three-true cell engages); **only California has tear-down infraction invariant** (3-regime sweep); **only New York preserves additional rights invariant** (3-regime sweep); **citation pins all regime authorities** (Pub. L. 111-22 В§ 702 + Pub. L. 115-174 В§ 304 + В§ 2924.8 + В§ 2924.8(a)/(d) + В§ 1161b + RPAPL В§ 1305 + В§ 1305(2)/(4) + June 23, 2018 effective date); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `foreclosure_tenant_rights` + `mid_tenancy_ownership_change` + `eviction_notices` + national-FLOOR concept); **defensive negative lease remaining clamped** (negative вҶ’ 0 вҶ’ 90-day fallback); **defensive negative days since notice no violation** (notice not yet served); **CA tear-down fine constant** ($100 = 10,000 cents; 72-hour window; PTFA 90 days).

`traderview-expense::tenant_in_unit_appliance_repair_responsibility` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant in-unit appliance repair and replacement responsibility compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord provides appliances (refrigerator, stove/range, dishwasher, washer/dryer, microwave, HVAC) as part of a rental tenancy, what repair and replacement obligations attach under the implied warranty of habitability and state-specific mandatory-provision statutes, what tenant remedies apply (rent withholding + repair-and-deduct + lease termination), and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord when an appliance fails? Distinct from siblings `rental_hot_water_temperature` (water heater specifically), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification policy), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (iter 471), `rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement` (iter 473), `rental_hardwired_smoke_alarm_responsibility` (iter 481), `rental_garage_door_safety_compliance` (iter 483), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (iter 485), `landlord_repair_response_timeframe`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **New York (MOST PRESCRIPTIVE for refrigerator)** | NYC Admin. Code В§ 27-2014 + В§ 27-2008; N.Y. Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 78; N.Y. Real Property Law В§ 235-b; 28 RCNY В§ 32-04 | NYC multi-unit dwellings must provide **refrigerator and stove**; HPD enforcement + ECB violation issuance |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.052 + В§ 92.053 + В§ 92.056 | Landlord duty to repair conditions materially affecting health/safety; tenant remedies: **rent withholding + repair-and-deduct** up to one month's rent + lease termination + judicial order |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941 + В§ 1941.1; В§ 1941.1(a)(8); В§ 1942(a) + В§ 1942.1; Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); Hinson v. Delis, 26 Cal. App. 3d 62 (1972) | **В§ 1941.1(a)(8) mandates landlord stove/range provision**; В§ 1942(a) repair-and-deduct (max twice in 12-month period); В§ 1942.1 voids any waiver |
| **Default** | Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Lemle v. Breeden, 51 Haw. 426 (1969) | Common-law implied warranty of habitability + tort negligence + premises liability |

**Six categories of in-unit appliances**:
1. **STOVE/RANGE** вҖ” California В§ 1941.1(a)(8) and NYC require; most states require if sole cooking/heating source
2. **REFRIGERATOR** вҖ” NYC requires; most states optional but warranty if landlord-provided
3. **DISHWASHER** вҖ” generally not statutorily required; warranty if provided
4. **WASHER/DRYER** вҖ” generally not required; emerging "in-unit laundry" trend
5. **MICROWAVE** вҖ” not required; warranty if provided
6. **HVAC** (heat + cooling) вҖ” heating universally required; cooling required in NYC Local Law 18 (post-2022) and select CA cities

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Refusal to repair landlord-provided appliance** after timely tenant notice вҶ’ habitability breach + rent withholding (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.056) + repair-and-deduct
2. **Constructive eviction** from extended non-repair (Hilder v. St. Peter) вҶ’ tenant lease termination + relocation cost
3. **Replacement with materially lower quality** than original вҶ’ habitability dispute + small claims
4. **Tenant-caused damage** allocation dispute вҶ’ governed by lease terms; landlord cannot waive statutory minimum habitability under В§ 1942.1
5. **Used appliance failure** due to end-of-useful-life вҶ’ IRS В§ 168 5-7 year useful life; common-law tort if injury (fridge fire, gas explosion)

**Reasonable repair window** typically 7 days post-notice for non-emergency appliance; 24-72 hours for major appliance affecting habitability (refrigerator with spoilage risk + stove in winter + HVAC in extreme weather).

**Tenant remedies for landlord refusal-to-repair**:
- **California Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942(a)** REPAIR-AND-DEDUCT вҖ” tenant may make repair + deduct cost (up to one month's rent, max twice in 12-month period)
- **Texas Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.056** вҖ” rent withholding + repair-and-deduct + judicial order + lease termination
- **New York** rent-impairment-of-services + HPD complaint + ECB violation issuance + rent reduction in housing court

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) NYC refrigerator requirement is among the most surprising compliance traps for trader-landlords entering Manhattan/Brooklyn markets вҖ” vacant units must include working refrigerator before showing; (2) California В§ 1941.1(a)(8) stove/range requirement is mandatory regardless of lease terms вҖ” В§ 1942.1 voids any waiver; (3) tenant repair-and-deduct under Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942(a) caps at one month's rent per repair + max twice in 12-month period; (4) appliance failure causing fire/flood/CO event escalates to wrongful death exposure; (5) when in-unit washer/dryer is provided as amenity but breaks for extended period, tenant rent abatement claims are common; (6) 14-day non-repair after timely notice typically establishes habitability breach.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-in-unit-appliance-repair-responsibility`. Prop. `traderview-expense::tenant_late_fee_cap` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential tenant late fee cap framework module** вҖ” five-jurisdiction comparison reveals dramatically different rule architectures: NY hard cap, CA reasonableness/liquidated-damages test, TX percentage safe-harbor with treble damages, FL manufactured-home reasonableness regime, Default common-law liquidated damages. Trader-landlord critical because late-fee over-collection is one of the most common landlord mistakes вҖ” each over-cap charge can trigger statutory damages plus attorney fees. Distinct from siblings `rental_security_deposit_interest`, `landlord_self_help_eviction_prohibition`, `landlord_retaliation_damages`, `rental_junk_fee_transparency`.

**Five-jurisdiction statutory framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Late Fee Cap | Grace Period | Remedy |
|--------------|--------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1671(d) + Orozco v. Casimiro (2004) | LIQUIDATED-DAMAGES REASONABLENESS test (5-6% threshold) | None statutory | Fee VOID in toto; tenant recovers charged amount |
| **New York** | N.Y. Real Prop. Law В§ 238-a (HSTPA of 2019) | **LESSER of $50 OR 5% of monthly rent** | **5 days (mandatory)** | Refund of excess + no eviction solely for late fees |
| **Florida (manufactured home)** | Fla. Stat. В§ 83.808 | Reasonable: common $20 OR 20% of monthly rent (whichever GREATER) | None statutory | Court reasonableness |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.019 (revised 2019) | **12% safe harbor for вүӨ 4 units / 10% for > 4 units** | **2 days (mandatory)** | **$100 + TREBLE late fee collected + attorney fees** |
| **Default** | Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 356 | Reasonable relationship to actual/anticipated damages; NOT a penalty | None statutory | Common-law damages |

Pinned by `ca_5_percent_late_fee_with_both_prongs_compliant`, `ny_50_dollar_or_5_percent_lesser_cap` ($200K monthly rent вҶ’ $50 cap), `ny_low_rent_5_percent_lower_than_50_dollars` ($50K rent вҶ’ $25 cap from 5%), `fl_manufactured_home_park_20_percent_or_20_dollar_floor`, `fl_low_rent_20_dollar_floor_overrides_20_percent`, `tx_4_or_fewer_units_12_percent_safe_harbor`, `tx_5_or_more_units_10_percent_safe_harbor`, `default_jurisdiction_5_percent_cap`.

**California Orozco v. Casimiro two-prong validity test вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1671(d)**:

| Prong | Test | Source |
|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | Impracticable OR extremely difficult to fix actual damage at lease execution | В§ 1671(d)(1) |
| 2 | Amount is REASONABLE ENDEAVOR to estimate FAIR AVERAGE COMPENSATION for loss sustained | В§ 1671(d)(2) |

BOTH prongs required. Late fee struck down is VOID IN TOTO вҖ” landlord recovers $0; tenant recovers charged amount as damages. Pinned by `ca_missing_impracticable_prong_voids_fee`, `ca_missing_reasonable_endeavor_prong_voids_fee`, `ca_void_fee_recovers_charged_amount_as_damages` ($50K charged вҶ’ $50K tenant damages).

**New York mandatory 5-day grace + no-eviction rule вҖ” N.Y. Real Prop. Law В§ 238-a**: no late fee unless rent unpaid at least 5 days past due date; landlord CANNOT EVICT solely for unpaid late fees (warrant of eviction tracks rent + permissible fees only). Pinned by `ny_4_day_grace_period_violation` (day 4 = violation), `ny_5_day_grace_period_boundary_compliant` (day 5 = compliant), `ny_eviction_restriction_note_present`.

**Texas TREBLE damage uniquely engages вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.019(d)**: $100 PLUS TREBLE the late fee collected in violation PLUS reasonable attorney fees. Pinned by `tx_late_fee_above_cap_triggers_treble_plus_100` ($30K late fee Г— 3 + $100 = $90,100 = 90,100 + 10,000 cents). Pinned by `tx_uniquely_engages_treble_damages_invariant` (TX only вҖ” CA + NY + FL + Default do not).

**NY uniquely hard-caps at $50 dollars** вҖ” pinned by `ny_uniquely_hard_caps_at_50_dollars_invariant`: at $100K monthly rent, NY caps at $50 ($5,000 cents); CA + TX caps scale with rent and are higher.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-late-fee-cap`. Thirty-six tests pin: **CA 5% late fee with both prongs compliant**; **CA missing impracticable prong voids fee**; **CA missing reasonable endeavor prong voids fee**; **CA void fee recovers charged amount as damages**; **NY $50 or 5% lesser cap** ($200K rent = $50 cap); **NY low rent 5% lower than $50** ($50K rent = $25 cap); **NY late fee at $50 cap exactly compliant**; **NY late fee one cent above cap violation**; **NY 4-day grace period violation**; **NY 5-day grace period boundary compliant**; **NY eviction restriction note present**; **FL manufactured home park 20% or $20 floor**; **FL low rent $20 floor overrides 20%**; **TX 4-or-fewer units 12% safe harbor**; **TX 5-or-more units 10% safe harbor**; **TX late fee above cap triggers treble + $100**; **TX 1-day grace period violation**; **TX 2-day grace period boundary compliant**; **default jurisdiction 5% cap**; **default above cap violation cites Restatement**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1671(d) + Orozco + В§ 238-a HSTPA 2019 + В§ 83.808 + В§ 92.019 + Restatement 356); **note pins California two-prong test**; **note pins California 5-6% threshold + void in toto**; **note pins New York $50 or 5% hard cap**; **note pins New York 5-day grace + no eviction**; **note pins Florida manufactured home park $20 or 20%**; **note pins Florida general no statutory cap**; **note pins Texas 12/10 safe harbor**; **note pins Texas 2-day grace**; **note pins Texas treble + $100 remedy**; **note pins default Restatement 356**; **note pins cross-jurisdictional invariant**; **NY uniquely hard-caps at $50 invariant**; **TX uniquely engages treble damages invariant**; **jurisdiction truth table five cells**; **defensive overflow clamped with saturating mul**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_lease_guarantor_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant lease guarantor disclosure and restriction framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because (1) lease guarantors are widely used by landlords to secure rent payments from high-risk tenants, but state law increasingly restricts both the amount a guarantor can be obligated to pay AND the procedural protections required when soliciting guarantors; (2) NY HSTPA 2019 + DHCR guidance limits ALL deposits and guarantor security to ONE MONTH'S RENT for rent-stabilized apartments; (3) Federal FCRA requirements apply when landlord pulls guarantor's consumer credit report; (4) common-law suretyship principles (Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty) STRICTLY CONSTRUE guarantor liability + EXTINGUISH guaranty on material modifications absent consent. Companion to `tenant_data_privacy`, `rental_application_denial_disclosure`, `tenant_late_fee_cap`, `tenant_rent_receipt_requirement`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Key Restriction |
|--------------|--------|-----------------|
| **New York** | HSTPA 2019 + DHCR Operational Bulletin 2020-1 + GOL В§ 5-701(a)(1) + GOL В§ 7-108 | **ONE MONTH AGGREGATE** security + guaranty cap for rent-stabilized; NO retroactive guarantor; tenant blacklist prohibition $500-$1,000 penalty; > 12-month guaranty must be in writing |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2787-2856 + В§ 2819 + В§ 1670.5 + В§ 2799 | Material modification rule (В§ 2819 discharges guarantor); unconscionability (В§ 1670.5); continuing guaranty revocation (В§ 2799) |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.S.A. 46:8-26 + NJ Consumer Fraud Act | Landlord must provide guarantor copy of lease + EXACT MONETARY LIMIT |
| **Default** | Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty (1996) | В§ 41 material modification extinguishes; В§ 39 novation extinguishes; strict construction |

Pinned by `ny_one_month_aggregate_compliant`, `ny_aggregate_exceeds_one_month_violation`, `ny_non_rent_stabilized_no_aggregate_cap`, `ny_retroactive_guarantor_prohibited`, `ny_blacklist_violation_500_to_1000_penalty`, `ca_material_modification_extinguishes_2819`, `default_material_modification_restatement_41`, `nj_no_copy_of_lease_violation`, `nj_no_monetary_limit_violation`, `jurisdiction_truth_table_four_cells`, `ny_uniquely_engages_blacklist_prohibition_invariant`.

**Statute of Frauds вҖ” NY GOL В§ 5-701(a)(1)** вҖ” lease guaranty for term LONGER THAN 12 MONTHS must be IN WRITING signed by guarantor or authorized agent. Pinned by `statute_of_frauds_24_month_oral_unenforceable`, `statute_of_frauds_12_month_oral_enforceable`, `statute_of_frauds_12_month_boundary` (12 = OK orally), `statute_of_frauds_13_month_boundary_requires_writing` (13 = writing required).

**Federal FCRA framework (15 USC В§ 1681 et seq.)** when landlord pulls guarantor's credit report:

| Section | Rule |
|---------|------|
| В§ 1681b | **Permissible purpose** required |
| В§ 1681e | Accuracy requirements for user |
| В§ 1681m | **Adverse action notice** required (CRA identity + 60-day free copy + dispute right) |
| В§ 1681n | **Willful violation** = actual + PUNITIVE damages + attorney fees |
| В§ 1681o | Negligent violation = actual damages + attorney fees |

Pinned by `fcra_adverse_action_notice_required_engages`, `fcra_no_adverse_action_no_notice_required`, `fcra_no_credit_report_no_notice_required`, `fcra_adverse_action_with_notice_no_violation`.

**Common-law suretyship principles (Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty (1996))**:

| Section | Rule |
|---------|------|
| В§ 41 | MATERIAL MODIFICATION of underlying obligation without guarantor's consent EXTINGUISHES guaranty |
| В§ 39 | NOVATION of underlying obligation EXTINGUISHES guaranty |
| General | Guaranty STRICTLY CONSTRUED against the creditor (landlord); limited-amount guaranties enforced strictly within monetary cap |

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-lease-guarantor-disclosure`. Thirty-two tests pin: **NY one-month aggregate compliant**; **NY aggregate exceeds one-month violation**; **NY non-rent-stabilized no aggregate cap**; **NY retroactive guarantor prohibited**; **NY blacklist $500-$1,000 penalty**; **Statute of Frauds 24-month oral unenforceable**; **Statute of Frauds 12-month oral enforceable**; **Statute of Frauds 12-month boundary**; **Statute of Frauds 13-month boundary requires writing**; **CA material modification extinguishes В§ 2819**; **default material modification Restatement В§ 41**; **NJ no copy of lease violation**; **NJ no monetary limit violation**; **FCRA adverse action notice required engages**; **FCRA no adverse action no notice required**; **FCRA no credit report no notice required**; **FCRA adverse action with notice no violation**; **jurisdiction truth table four cells**; **NY uniquely engages blacklist prohibition invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (HSTPA 2019 + DHCR + В§ 5-701(a)(1) + В§ 7-108 + В§ 2787-2856 + В§ 2819 + В§ 1670.5 + В§ 2799 + N.J.S.A. 46:8-26 + Consumer Fraud Act + В§ 1681 + Restatement); **note pins NY HSTPA one-month aggregate**; **note pins NY blacklist prohibition**; **note pins NY GOL В§ 5-701 Statute of Frauds**; **note pins CA В§ 2819 material modification**; **note pins CA В§ 1670.5 unconscionability**; **note pins CA В§ 2799 continuing guaranty revocation**; **note pins NJ В§ 46:8-26 lease copy + monetary limit**; **note pins FCRA adverse action notice**; **note pins FCRA willful punitive damages**; **note pins Restatement (Third) Suretyship 1996**; **note pins trader-landlord critical summary**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_estoppel_certificate` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant estoppel certificate requirements and protections module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because traders who own rental property routinely need signed estoppel certificates from tenants when REFINANCING the property OR SELLING it to a buyer. The estoppel certifies (1) actual lease terms (rent, security deposit, term, modifications), (2) absence of landlord defaults, and (3) lease in full force and effect. A buyer or lender RELIES on the estoppel; tenant is BOUND BY ITS CONTENTS under promissory estoppel. Companion to `lease_disclosures`, `lease_copy_delivery`, `tenant_lease_guarantor_disclosure`, `tenant_rights_statement_disclosure`, `lease_waiver_enforceability`, `landlord_identification_disclosure`.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Key Rules |
|--------------|-----------|
| **New York** | Commercial enforceable with EXPRESS WRITTEN PROVISION; residential deemed-admission and attorney-in-fact clauses VOID AS UNCONSCIONABLE under NY RPL В§ 235-c + NY GOL В§ 5-321; NY GOL В§ 5-703 Statute of Frauds for leases > 1 year |
| **California** | Commercial enforceable with express provision; Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1962 landlord identification disclosure; Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1668 NO EXCULPATION FOR FRAUD limits scope of deemed-admission clauses |
| **Default / Restatement** | Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 90 promissory estoppel: clear and definite promise/statement + foreseeable reliance + actual reliance + injustice avoidable only by enforcement |

**Required estoppel contents** (industry standard plus ALTA/ACSM survey practice plus Fannie Mae Multifamily Guide Form 6402): (1) lease commencement plus expiration dates; (2) current monthly rent plus paid-through date; (3) security deposit amount plus form (cash / letter of credit); (4) all modifications and amendments listed by date; (5) no landlord default / known breach; (6) no prepaid rent beyond current month; (7) no assignments or sublets without consent; (8) lease unmodified and in full force and effect; (9) tenant's current address for notices.

**Standard COMMERCIAL response window: 10-15 business days**. Failure to return triggers DEEMED-ADMISSION (contents deemed true), ATTORNEY-IN-FACT (landlord may sign on tenant's behalf), MONETARY PENALTY ($50-$500/day in some leases), or EVENT OF DEFAULT (material breach + eviction trigger).

**Tenant protections against false estoppels**:
- Cannot bind tenant to FACTS NOT KNOWN to tenant (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1668; NY common law)
- Cannot waive STATUTORY rights (e.g., NY rent-stabilization succession rights; CA security deposit limits)
- Cannot serve as PRE-DISPUTE WAIVER of yet-to-accrue claims
- Deemed-admission clauses VOID in NY RESIDENTIAL leases as unconscionable under RPL В§ 235-c

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) trader refinancing $5M apartment building вҖ” lender requires estoppel from every tenant; tenant refusal delays closing plus triggers rate-lock expiry damages; (2) trader selling rental вҖ” buyer's lender requires estoppels; failure to obtain triggers contract contingency; (3) tenant signs estoppel reciting $1,200 rent when actual rent $1,000 вҖ” tenant BOUND by representation; (4) NY rent-stabilized tenant signs estoppel waiving succession rights вҖ” VOID under DHCR rules; (5) residential tenant signs estoppel with landlord-induced misrep on no-defaults вҖ” tenant retains breach claim.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-estoppel-certificate`. Thirty-six tests pin: **commercial with express provision enforceable**; **lease without express provision not enforceable**; **NY residential deemed-admission void**; **NY residential attorney-in-fact void**; **NY commercial deemed-admission enforceable**; **CA commercial deemed-admission enforceable**; **statutory waiver attempt void**; **pre-dispute waiver attempt void**; **tenant bound by signed estoppel promissory estoppel**; **landlord-induced misrep voids binding effect**; **contents outside tenant knowledge voids binding**; **failure to respond within window triggers deemed admission**; **NY residential no response no deemed admission trigger**; **no third-party reliance no binding effect**; **15-day window response failure**; **jurisdiction truth table six cells**; **NY residential uniquely voids deemed admission invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 90 + NY GOL В§ 5-703 + NY GOL В§ 5-321 + NY RPL В§ 235-c + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1962 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1668 + ALTA/ACSM + Fannie Mae Multifamily Guide Form 6402); **note pins estoppel function**; **note pins Restatement В§ 90 promissory estoppel**; **note pins response window consequences**; **note pins NY GOL В§ 5-321**; **note pins NY RPL В§ 235-c**; **note pins NY GOL В§ 5-703 Statute of Frauds**; **note pins Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1962**; **note pins Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1668 no fraud exculpation**; **note pins required contents nine items**; **note pins tenant protections four**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**; **tenant retains rights pre-dispute waiver invariant**; **tenant retains rights statutory waiver invariant**; **deemed admission requires express provision invariant**; **NY residential attorney-in-fact uniquely void invariant**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_holdover_security_deposit_setoff` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant holdover security deposit setoff limits framework module** вҖ” when tenant holds over past lease expiration, may landlord apply security deposit to (1) holdover rent; (2) double-rent damages under FL В§ 83.58 + similar statutes; (3) physical damage beyond normal wear and tear; (4) eviction costs and attorney fees; and what itemization / return timing rules apply? Distinct from sibling `holdover_tenant_damages` (general holdover-damages framework), `damage_deduction_itemization` (itemization of damage deductions), `deposit_return_windows` (general refund timelines), `security_deposit_bank_disclosure` (where deposit is held), `landlord_property_sale_notice` (iter 437 sale-transfer security deposit disclosure). Trader-landlord critical because holdover tenants routinely trigger collateral disputes over security deposit application; mishandling exposes landlord to penalty multipliers (CA up to 2x, NY up to 2x punitive, TX 3x + $100 + attorney fees, MA 3x triple damages).

**Four-jurisdiction framework with itemization windows + penalty structures**:

| Jurisdiction | Itemization Window | Bad-Faith / Willful Penalty |
|--------------|--------------------|------------------------------|
| **California** | **21 days** from move-out (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(g)(1)) | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(l) вҖ” actual damages + UP TO 2Г— DEPOSIT PUNITIVE DAMAGES |
| **New York** | **14 days** from vacate date (NY GOL В§ 7-108(1-a)(e)) | NY GOL В§ 7-108(1-a)(g) вҖ” actual damages + UP TO 2Г— DEPOSIT PUNITIVE; FORFEITURE of retention right if 14-day window missed |
| **Texas** | **30 days** from surrender (Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.103 + В§ 92.104) | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.109(a) вҖ” BAD-FAITH: $100 + **3Г— WRONGFULLY WITHHELD** + attorney fees + court costs |
| **Massachusetts** | **30 days** from termination (Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(4); with interest credited annually) | Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(7) вҖ” **TRIPLE DAMAGES** + interest + attorney fees + court costs |

**Seven permitted deduction categories**:
1. **UNPAID HOLDOVER RENT** universally permitted (CA В§ 1950.5(e)(1) + NY В§ 7-108(1-a)(g)(i) + TX В§ 92.104(a) + MA В§ 15B(4)(i)) provided lease provides
2. **DOUBLE RENT DAMAGES** under FL В§ 83.58 + similar вҖ” NOT directly deductible from security deposit unless lease specifically authorizes; typically requires separate court judgment
3. **PHYSICAL DAMAGE BEYOND NORMAL WEAR AND TEAR** universally permitted with itemization
4. **EVICTION ATTORNEY FEES AND COURT COSTS** permitted ONLY if (1) lease provides; (2) statute permits; (3) landlord prevailed in eviction
5. **CLEANING TO RETURN PREMISES TO BASELINE** permitted (CA + MA explicit; TX implicit)
6. **NON-PAYMENT OF UTILITIES PAYABLE TO LANDLORD** permitted under NY GOL В§ 7-108(1-a)(g)(iii)
7. **MOVING AND STORAGE OF TENANT'S BELONGINGS** permitted under NY GOL В§ 7-108(1-a)(g)(iv)

**Three universally prohibited deductions**:
1. **NORMAL WEAR AND TEAR** universally excluded
2. **PRE-EXISTING CONDITIONS** excluded under Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(e) "less wear and tear" language
3. **LIQUIDATED-DAMAGES PROVISIONS** that exceed actual damages and are unconscionable under Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1670.5 + NY GOL В§ 5-321 + Mass. unconscionability doctrine

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) CA trader applies entire $2,500 deposit to $3,000 unpaid holdover rent; mails 21-day itemization showing deduction; sustains defense under В§ 1950.5(e)(1) PROVIDED lease authorized; (2) NY trader exhausts deposit + sues for $1,500 excess for floor damage; must file В§ 7-108(1-a)(g) itemization within 14 days or FORFEIT entire retention right; (3) TX trader fails to itemize within 30 days вҖ” В§ 92.109(a) bad-faith $100 + 3Г— wrongfully withheld + attorney fees + court costs; (4) MA trader retains $2,400 without itemizing вҖ” В§ 15B(7) TRIPLE DAMAGES $7,200 + interest + fees; (5) trader uses deposit for eviction attorney fees $5,000 вҖ” permitted only if lease + statute + landlord prevailed.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-holdover-security-deposit-setoff`. Thirty-seven tests pin: **CA holdover rent within 21 days permitted**; **CA past 21 days violation**; **NY 14-day window**; **NY past 14 days forfeiture**; **TX 30-day window**; **MA 30-day window**; **physical damage beyond wear universally permitted**; **cleaning to baseline permitted**; **normal wear and tear prohibited**; **pre-existing conditions prohibited**; **unconscionable liquidated damages prohibited** (В§ 1670.5 + В§ 5-321); **eviction attorney fees with lease and prevailed permitted**; **eviction attorney fees without prevailing prohibited**; **double rent damages without lease authorization prohibited**; **NY unpaid utilities permitted**; **CA unpaid utilities not permitted outside NY**; **NY moving and storage permitted**; **CA bad-faith 2Г— punitive** ($500K); **NY willful 2Г— punitive** ($500K with forfeiture); **TX bad-faith $100 + 3Г— + fees** ($760K); **MA triple damages engaged** ($750K); **deduction category truth table ten cells**; **jurisdiction window truth table four cells**; **NY uniquely 14-day window invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 + В§ 1950.5(e) + В§ 1950.5(g)(1) + В§ 1950.5(l) + В§ 1670.5 + NY GOL В§ 7-108 + В§ 7-108(1-a)(e) + В§ 7-108(1-a)(g) + В§ 5-321 + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.103 + В§ 92.104 + В§ 92.104(c) + В§ 92.109(a) + Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(4) + В§ 15B(7) + HSTPA 2019 + CA AB 2801); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins seven permitted categories**; **note pins prohibited deductions**; **note pins itemization window comparison**; **note pins penalty multiplier comparison**; **note pins CA В§ 1950.5(e) four categories**; **note pins NY В§ 7-108(1-a)(g) four categories with forfeiture**; **note pins TX В§ 92.104 framework**; **note pins MA В§ 15B four categories with triple damages**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::security_deposit_bank_disclosure` is the **security deposit bank-location disclosure module** вҖ” when must a landlord disclose to the tenant WHERE the security deposit is held? Distinct from sibling modules `security_deposit_caps` (statutory caps on amount), `deposit_interest` (interest-payment obligation on the deposit), `deposit_return_windows` (refund timelines), and `damage_deduction_itemization` (deductions from the deposit). Four regimes ranging from NY's trust-fund + 6+ unit interest-bearing rule to Massachusetts' harsh immediate-return remedy.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Window | Required disclosures | Distinctive feature |
|--------|-----------|--------|----------------------|---------------------|
| New York | N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 7-103 | n/a (immediate) | Bank name + address + amount | **6+ unit interest-bearing requirement**; 1% admin retention |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 46:8-19 | 30 days from receipt | Bank name + address + account type + interest rate + amount | Re-notification on bank/landlord change + annual interest payment |
| Massachusetts | Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(3)(a) | 30 days from receipt | Bank name + location + amount + account number | **В§ 15B(6) IMMEDIATE RETURN remedy** вҖ” harshest in U.S. |
| Default | None (CA/TX/FL/etc.) | n/a | None required | Lease provision controls if any |

**New York В§ 7-103 вҖ” trust-fund + 6+ unit interest-bearing.** Bank name + address + amount disclosure required. Trust-fund principle: deposit may NOT be commingled with landlord's personal funds. В§ 7-103(2) imposes INTEREST-BEARING ACCOUNT requirement at prevailing rate for buildings with SIX OR MORE family dwelling units; landlord may retain 1% per annum as administration expense; remaining interest belongs to tenant. В§ 7-108 added 2019 HSTPA disclosure for 6+ unit buildings. Pinned by `ny_baseline_compliant`, `ny_missing_bank_name_violation`, `ny_5_units_no_interest_bearing_requirement`, `ny_6_units_interest_bearing_required`, `ny_6_units_no_interest_bearing_account_violation`, `ny_100_units_interest_bearing_required`, and `ny_6_unit_threshold_boundary_truth_table` (4-cell sweep at units 5/6/7/100).

**New Jersey В§ 46:8-19 вҖ” 30-day five-component disclosure + re-notification.** Five required components within 30 days of receipt: (1) bank name; (2) bank address; (3) account type; (4) current interest rate; (5) amount. Re-notification within 30 days of (a) transfer of deposit to new landlord OR (b) move to different account/bank. Annual interest payable to tenant in cash, credited to rent on lease anniversary, OR on January 31 if landlord has provided written election notice. Pinned by `nj_baseline_compliant`, `nj_missing_account_type_violation`, `nj_missing_interest_rate_violation`, `nj_30_day_window_expired_violation`, `nj_transfer_event_30_day_expired_violation`, and `nj_annual_interest_not_paid_violation`.

**Massachusetts В§ 15B(3)(a) + В§ 15B(6) вҖ” harshest non-compliance remedy in the U.S.** 30-day receipt window. Receipt must include bank name + location + amount + account number. Annual statement required at end of each tenancy year. В§ 15B(6) IMMEDIATE-RETURN REMEDY: failure to comply with ANY В§ 15B(3) requirement entitles tenant to immediate return of the security deposit вҖ” Massachusetts imposes the strictest compliance standard in U.S. landlord-tenant law; minor procedural defects trigger full forfeiture. **Default вҖ” no statutory disclosure requirement.** Most states (California Civ. Code В§ 1950.5, Texas Prop. Code В§ 92.103 + В§ 92.108, Florida Stat. В§ 83.49) regulate deposit AMOUNT + RETURN procedures but impose no LOCATION-DISCLOSURE obligation. Lease provision may still require disclosure as matter of contract. Pinned by `default_no_disclosure_required` and `default_no_violation_even_with_all_disclosure_missing` (all 6 disclosure fields off вҶ’ still compliant).
