`traderview-expense::landlord_foreclosure_status_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord foreclosure status disclosure to tenant framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because rental property held with mortgage financing routinely cycles through default events; landlord must time disclosures to (1) preserve enforceability of new leases against the lender's successor-in-interest; (2) avoid lease-void elections; (3) prevent statutory damages and treble-damage claims; and (4) align with Federal PTFA 90-day successor-notice obligations. Distinct from sibling `foreclosure_tenant_rights` (post-foreclosure tenant occupation rights under PTFA). Companion to `landlord_property_sale_notice`, `security_deposit_bank_disclosure`, `landlord_identification_disclosure`, `tenant_estoppel_certificate`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Trigger | Window | Penalty |
|--------------|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2924.8 | Notice of trustee's sale | **5 business days** | В§ 2924.8(d) punitive damages + injunction + attorney's fees for KNOWING OR INTENTIONAL violation |
| **New York** | NY RPAPL В§ 1305 + В§ 1306 | Judgment of foreclosure | **10 business days** | Tenant retains 90-day-plus-lease remainder rights against successor |
| **Federal** | 12 USC В§ 5220 (PTFA, made permanent by Pub. L. 115-174 В§ 304 June 23, 2018) | Foreclosure sale completed | **90 days** before termination | Bona fide tenant entitled to remainder of lease |
| **Default** | Restatement (Second) of Torts В§ 551 | New lease during default period | Disclosure at lease execution | Fraudulent misrepresentation claim |

**CA Civ. Code В§ 2924.8 notice content (four elements)**: (1) sale date/time; (2) foreclosure status; (3) Federal PTFA 90-day successor-tenant rights; (4) tenant's right to remain for remainder of lease or 90 days (whichever longer). Notice must be POSTED on property AND MAILED to known tenants.

**NY RPAPL В§ 1305 successor notice content (four elements)**: (1) successor identity; (2) tenant's right to remain for remainder of lease or 90 days (whichever LONGER); (3) successor contact for rent; (4) notice that lease may be terminated for cause. NY RPAPL В§ 1306 requires lender to file foreclosure action information with NY DFS within 3 business days of service.

**Federal PTFA В§ 5220(b) bona fide tenant three-element test**:
1. NOT mortgagor or child/spouse/parent of mortgagor
2. Lease was ARM'S-LENGTH transaction
3. Lease requires rent NOT SUBSTANTIALLY LESS than fair market rent

**Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2924.85 вҖ” REPEALED January 1, 2018** вҖ” historically required landlord with single-family or 1-4 unit multifamily dwelling who received notice of default to disclose to prospective tenants before lease execution; violation voided lease at tenant's election; entitled tenant to recovery of one month's rent or twice actual damages (whichever greater) plus all prepaid rent. Cite for legacy leases predating January 1, 2018.

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) CA trader fails 5-business-day В§ 2924.8 post-and-mail вҖ” punitive damages under В§ 2924.8(d); (2) NY trader-owned property at judgment of foreclosure вҖ” successor's failure to send RPAPL В§ 1305 within 10 business days preserves tenant's 90-day-plus-lease rights; (3) federal PTFA 90-day successor notice trumps 30-day state termination; (4) trader signs new lease 3 months after default notice without disclosure вҖ” Restatement В§ 551 fraudulent-misrepresentation claim; (5) landlord's son at $500 rent vs $2,000 FMV вҖ” fails 12 USC В§ 5220(b) bona fide test.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-foreclosure-status-disclosure`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **CA within 5 business days compliant**; **CA past 5 business days violation**; **CA 5-day boundary compliant**; **CA no posting violation**; **CA knowing violation punitive damages**; **CA non-knowing no punitive**; **NY within 10 business days compliant**; **NY past 10 business days violation**; **NY no notice violation**; **Federal PTFA 90-day notice compliant**; **Federal PTFA failure to give 90-day notice**; **Federal PTFA non-bona-fide no protection**; **PTFA bona fide tenant protections apply note**; **prospective tenant disclosure compliant**; **prospective tenant undisclosed fraudulent misrep**; **no new lease no disclosure obligation**; **jurisdiction truth table three cells CA 5-day**; **CA uniquely engages punitive damages invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2924.8 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2924.85 REPEALED + NY RPAPL В§ 1305 + NY RPAPL В§ 1306 + 12 USC В§ 5220 + Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009 + Pub. L. 115-174 В§ 304 + 12 USC В§ 5220(b) + Restatement (Second) of Torts В§ 551); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins CA В§ 2924.8 four elements**; **note pins NY RPAPL В§ 1305 four elements**; **note pins Federal PTFA**; **note pins bona fide tenant three elements**; **note pins Restatement (Second) Torts В§ 551**; **note pins CA В§ 2924.85 repeal historical**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_harassment` is the **landlord harassment liability module** вҖ” civil-penalty exposure for landlord conduct intended to cause tenant to vacate. Distinct from sibling modules `lockout_penalties` (specific lockout statute), `quiet_enjoyment` (general common-law duty + constructive eviction), and `retaliation_windows` (timing-based retaliation presumptions). Trader-critical for landlord-investors operating in California or NYC where the affirmative-harassment statutes create direct civil-penalty exposure on top of common-law claims.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Penalty | Distinctive feature |
|--------|-----------|---------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.2 | Up to **$2,000 per violation** | 5 enumerated prohibited acts; good-faith warning exception under В§ 1940.2(c) |
| NYC | NYC Admin. Code В§ 27-2004(a)(48) + В§ 27-2005(d) + В§ 27-2115(m) | **$1,000-$10,000 per dwelling unit per violation** | Requires both (i) prohibited act AND (ii) intent or causation to vacate; **per-unit multiplier** |
| Default | Common law | Compensatory damages only | IIED, conversion, quiet enjoyment breach, constructive eviction |

**California В§ 1940.2 вҖ” five prohibited acts** (all requiring purpose to influence tenant to vacate):

| Subsection | Conduct |
|------------|---------|
| В§ 1940.2(a)(1) | Penal Code В§ 484 (theft) violations |
| В§ 1940.2(a)(2) | Penal Code В§ 518 (extortion) violations |
| В§ 1940.2(a)(3) | Force, willful threats, or menacing conduct interfering with quiet enjoyment |
| В§ 1940.2(a)(4) | Significant and intentional Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954 entry-rules violation |
| В§ 1940.2(a)(5) | Threat to disclose tenant's immigration or citizenship status |

В§ 1940.2(b) civil penalty: up to $2,000 per violation, recoverable in small claims or civil court by tenant who prevails. В§ 1940.2(c) good-faith warning exception вҖ” oral/written warning about lease violations given in good faith and in the normal course of business is NOT harassment. Pinned by `ca_force_or_threats_engages`, `ca_immigration_threat_engages`, `ca_section_1954_entry_violation_engages`, `ca_theft_or_extortion_engages`, `ca_multiple_violations_multiply_penalty` (5 Г— $2K = $10K), `ca_good_faith_warning_exception`, `ca_good_faith_warning_does_not_excuse_prohibited_act`, and `ca_engagement_does_not_count_unrelated_nyc_acts` (NYC-specific act categories don't engage В§ 1940.2).

**NYC Admin. Code В§ 27-2004(a)(48) + В§ 27-2005(d) вҖ” Tenant Harassment Prevention Act (TYPHA, 2008, amended 2017)**. "Harassment" defined as act or omission by or on behalf of owner that (i) causes or is intended to cause tenant to vacate or waive occupancy rights, AND (ii) includes one or more enumerated acts (force/threats, service interruptions, repeated buyout offers, baseless court proceedings, false statements, etc.). В§ 27-2115(m) civil penalty: $1,000-$10,000 per dwelling unit per violation. **Per-unit multiplier** means landlords harassing across an entire building face geometric penalty growth вҖ” 10 units Г— 3 violations Г— $10K max = $300K. Pinned by `nyc_force_with_intent_engages`, `nyc_force_without_intent_no_engagement` (intent element required), `nyc_service_interruption_with_intent_engages`, `nyc_baseless_court_proceedings_engages`, `nyc_per_unit_per_violation_multiplier` (10 units Г— 3 Г— $10K = $300K), and `nyc_requires_intent_element_truth_table` (4-cell truth table вҖ” only act+intent engages).

**Default вҖ” common-law claims only**. No statutory civil penalty. Tenant remedies are case-law-driven (intentional infliction of emotional distress, conversion, breach of implied covenant of quiet enjoyment, constructive eviction). Compensatory damages limited to actual harm; punitive damages require extreme conduct. Pinned by `default_force_or_threats_common_law_claim` ($50K actual damages вҶ’ $50K compensatory; no civil penalty) and `default_no_act_no_claim`.

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_ca_uses_2000_per_violation_invariant` (3-regime sweep); `nyc_dwelling_unit_multiplier_only_in_nyc_invariant` (CA + Default don't scale with units); `only_ca_has_good_faith_warning_exception_invariant` (3-regime sweep); `nyc_requires_intent_element_truth_table` (4-cell sweep вҖ” act Г— intent); `penalty_constants_invariant` (CA $2K; NYC $1K-$10K; NYC max = 5Г— CA penalty).

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references `lockout_penalties`, `quiet_enjoyment`, and `retaliation_windows` and clarifies that this module covers AFFIRMATIVE HARASSMENT statutes (not lockout, not general quiet enjoyment, not timing-based retaliation). Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-harassment`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CA no prohibited act no engagement**; **CA force or threats engages**; **CA immigration threat engages**; **CA В§ 1954 entry violation engages**; **CA theft or extortion engages**; **CA multiple violations multiply penalty** (5 Г— $2K = $10K); **CA good-faith warning exception**; **CA good-faith warning does not excuse prohibited act**; **CA engagement does not count unrelated NYC acts**; **NYC no act no engagement**; **NYC force with intent engages**; **NYC force without intent no engagement** (intent element required); **NYC service interruption with intent engages**; **NYC baseless court proceedings engages**; **NYC per-unit per-violation multiplier** (10 Г— 3 Г— $10K = $300K); **default force or threats common-law claim** ($50K actual damages); **default no act no claim**; **only CA uses $2,000 per violation invariant** (3-regime sweep); **NYC dwelling-unit multiplier only in NYC invariant**; **only CA has good-faith warning exception invariant**; **NYC requires intent element truth table** (4-cell act Г— intent sweep); **penalty constants invariant** (CA $2K; NYC $1K-$10K; 5Г— ratio); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1940.2 + (a)(1)-(5) + (b) + (c) + Penal Code В§ 484 + В§ 518 + В§ 1954 + NYC В§ 27-2004(a)(48) + В§ 27-2005(d) + В§ 27-2115(m) + 2008 + 2017); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `lockout_penalties` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `retaliation_windows` + AFFIRMATIVE HARASSMENT + multiplier-effect references); **defensive negative violations clamped**; **defensive zero dwelling units floors at one for NYC**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_annual_rent_statement` is the **mandatory landlord-provided annual rent statement to tenant for tax-credit purposes module** вҖ” when must a residential landlord issue an annual rent-paid statement to enable the tenant to claim a state-level renter's tax credit or property tax refund? Distinct from siblings `rent_receipts` (per-payment receipts), `security_deposit_interest_statement` (annual security-deposit interest statement), and `lease_disclosures` (mandated lease content). Trader-landlord operational concern in MN, VT, ME, and other renter-tax-credit states.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Form | Deadline | Affidavit fallback |
|--------|------|----------|---------------------|
| Minnesota Minn. Stat. В§ 290A.19 | Certificate of Rent Paid (CRP) | January 31 each year | Rent Paid Affidavit via MN DOR |
| Vermont Vt. Stat. tit. 32 В§ 6066 | Form LRC-147 | Annual | вҖ” |
| Default | No mandate | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**Minnesota вҖ” Minn. Stat. В§ 290A.19 (Certificate of Rent Paid)**. Most explicit framework. Landlord MUST issue Certificate of Rent Paid (CRP) form to each tenant by **JANUARY 31** each year. CRP details total rent paid by tenant over previous calendar year and supports tenant's claim for **Property Tax Refund (Renter's Credit)** under В§ 290A. CRP may be provided as ELECTRONIC OR HARD COPY. **Rent Paid Affidavit fallback** вҖ” when landlord fails to provide CRP by January 31, tenant may request a "Rent Paid Affidavit" from the Minnesota Department of Revenue as alternative. Noncompliance triggers state-law penalties. Pinned by `mn_missing_january_31_deadline_violates`, `mn_affidavit_alternative_engaged_when_nonissuance`, `mn_affidavit_invocation_note_describes_audit`.

**Vermont вҖ” Vt. Stat. tit. 32 В§ 6066 (Renter Rebate Program)**. Landlord must provide annual statement on **Form LRC-147** to tenant for renter rebate claim. Form must include landlord name + property address + total rent paid in calendar year. No affidavit fallback (unlike MN).

**Default вҖ” no statutory obligation**. Most states have no statewide landlord annual-rent-statement mandate. Renter-tax-credit states (MA Renter Deduction, IN Renter Deduction, MI Homestead Property Tax Credit, WI School Property Tax Credit, IA Property Tax Credit, ME Renters Rebate Program, MD Homeowner's Property Tax Credit) typically have tenants claim based on their own records; landlord must produce records on tenant request but is not required to proactively issue a statement.

**MN affidavit alternative is uniquely MN's** вҖ” neither VT nor Default offer this consumer-side recourse when landlord fails. Pinned by `mn_affidavit_alternative_unique_to_mn_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-annual-rent-statement`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **MN compliant clean path**; **MN missing January 31 deadline violates** (В§ 290A.19); **MN affidavit alternative engaged when nonissuance** (Rent Paid Affidavit via Minnesota Department of Revenue); **MN affidavit invocation note describes audit** (state-law penalties on landlord); **MN missing required information violates** (landlord name + property address + total rent); **MN electronic or hard copy note present**; **MN Property Tax Refund + Renter's Credit note present**; **MN citation pins В§В§ 290A.19 + 290A**; **VT compliant clean path**; **VT missing deadline violates** (Form LRC-147); **VT missing required information violates**; **VT Renter Rebate note present**; **VT citation pins tit. 32 В§ 6066**; **VT no affidavit alternative unlike MN**; **default no obligation compliant even without statement**; **default renter-tax-credit cross-references note** (MA Renter Deduction + IN Renter Deduction + ME Renters Rebate + WI School Property Tax Credit + MI Homestead Property Tax Credit + IA Property Tax Credit + MD Homeowner's Property Tax Credit); **default records-on-request obligation note**; **default citation references no statewide mandate**; **MN unique January 31 deadline invariant**; **VT unique Form LRC-147 invariant**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **MN affidavit alternative unique to MN invariant** (3-regime sweep); **MN full compliance no affidavit alternative**; **MN both violations simultaneous when only deadline missed** (single violation due to early-return on first failure); **state penalty exposure engaged with any violation**; **state penalty exposure not engaged when compliant**; **default no state penalty exposure ever**; **VT no affidavit pathway ever**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_emergency_entry_notice` is the **mandatory landlord-paid post-entry notice after emergency entry module** вҖ” when landlord enters tenant's unit without prior notice due to true emergency (fire + water leak + gas leak), what post-entry written notice obligation attaches? Distinct from siblings `entry_notice` (general 24-hour pre-entry notice), `pesticide_application_notice` (treatment-specific), and `landlord_harassment` (unauthorized entry as harassment).

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Specific post-entry obligation |
|--------|-----------|-----------------------------------|
| California Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1954(e) + 1940.2 | $2,000/violation for pretext | Written notice on premises + reasonable time |
| Texas Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0081 | Civil penalty $1,000 + 1 month rent | No specific written-notice obligation |
| New York N.Y. Mult. Dwell. Law В§ 78 + common law | Trespass + conversion | Post-entry notice + PREMISES SECURED |
| Default | Trespass + breach of quiet enjoyment | Reasonable post-entry notice |

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1954(e) + 1940.2**. Landlord may enter without prior notice "in case of emergency." Post-entry, the landlord MUST (1) leave WRITTEN NOTICE describing the date + time + purpose of entry on the premises AND (2) provide such notice within a REASONABLE TIME. Pretextual emergency entries actionable under В§ 1940.2 prohibited harassing acts with **$2,000 per-violation** civil penalty. Pinned by `ca_pretext_engages_harassment`, `ca_missing_written_notice_violates`, `ca_missing_reasonable_time_notice_violates`.

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0081**. Emergency entry permitted. Texas does NOT impose specific post-entry written-notice obligation but landlord remains liable for unauthorized entry: **actual damages + one month's rent + $1,000 + reasonable attorney fees**. Pinned by `tx_pretext_engages_civil_penalty`, `tx_no_specific_post_entry_notice_obligation_note`.

**New York вҖ” N.Y. Mult. Dwell. Law В§ 78 + common-law quiet enjoyment**. Emergency entry permitted but landlord must (1) provide post-entry notification to tenant AND (2) leave premises **SECURED**. Failure to secure exposes landlord to trespass + conversion claims. Pinned by `ny_missing_secured_premises_violates`, `ny_missing_post_entry_notice_violates`, `ny_pretext_engages_breach_of_quiet_enjoyment`, `ny_unique_premises_secured_requirement_invariant`.

**Default вҖ” common-law necessity doctrine + quiet enjoyment**. Most states permit emergency entry under common-law necessity doctrine. Most impose reasonable post-entry notice obligation under quiet enjoyment covenant. Failure constitutes actionable trespass + breach of quiet enjoyment + common-law harassment.

**CA uniquely requires specific written-notice content** (date + time + purpose). Pinned by `ca_uniquely_requires_specific_written_notice_invariant` (2-regime sweep).

**NY uniquely requires premises secured after entry**. Pinned by `ny_unique_premises_secured_requirement_invariant` (2-regime sweep).

**Pretext engages across all regimes** вҖ” actionable as harassment / trespass / breach of quiet enjoyment regardless of regime. Pinned by `pretext_engages_across_all_regimes` (4-regime sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-emergency-entry-notice`. Twenty-five tests pin: **CA compliant passes**; **CA pretext engages harassment** ($2,000 per-violation); **CA missing written notice violates** (В§ 1954(e) date + time + purpose); **CA missing reasonable time notice violates**; **CA no emergency entry no violation**; **CA citation pins В§В§ 1954(e) + 1940.2**; **TX compliant passes**; **TX pretext engages civil penalty** ($1,000 + month rent + attorney fees); **TX no specific post-entry notice obligation note**; **TX citation pins В§ 92.0081**; **NY compliant passes**; **NY missing secured premises violates** (В§ 78); **NY missing post-entry notice violates**; **NY pretext engages breach of quiet enjoyment**; **NY citation pins Mult. Dwell. Law В§ 78**; **default compliant when actual emergency with notice**; **default pretext violates common law**; **default missing post-entry notice violates** (common-law quiet enjoyment); **NY unique premises secured requirement invariant** (2-regime sweep); **CA uniquely requires specific written notice invariant**; **pretext engages across all regimes** (4-regime sweep); **emergency exception engaged when actual emergency**; **four regimes routed correctly**; **CA clean no violations**; **NY multiple violations simultaneous** (3 violations).

`traderview-expense::landlord_mid_tenancy_rekeying` is the **mandatory landlord-paid mid-tenancy rekeying obligations module** вҖ” when tenant requests landlord to rekey locks during ongoing tenancy (lost keys, roommate departed, burglary, security upgrade) what notice, timing, and cost-allocation requirements attach? Trader-landlord operational concern: failure to comply with statutory rekeying timeframe exposes landlord to actual damages + punitive + civil penalty + one month's rent + court costs + attorney fees (TX framework). Distinct from siblings `lock_change_between_tenancies` (between-tenancy locks), `dv_survivor_lock_change` (DV-survivor locks), and `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (biometric locks).

**Three regimes** вҖ” TX maximal statutory framework versus CA limited common-law versus default reasonable-time:

| Regime | Authority | Required window | Master-key change | Interior doors | Remedies |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|----------------|----------|
| Texas | Tex. Prop. Code В§В§ 92.156-92.158 + 92.164 + 92.165 | **7 days = 168 hours** after tenant request | **Landlord pays** | **EXCLUDED** | Actual + punitive + $500 + one month's rent + court costs + attorney fees |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1954 + 1941.3 | Common-law reasonable time (~10 days = 240 hours) | Landlord pays | Not statutorily excluded | Common-law damages + В§ 1941.3(b) maintenance obligation |
| Default | Common-law quiet enjoyment + lease | Reasonable time | Landlord pays | Not specifically addressed | Common-law damages |

**TX uniquely explicit 7-day window** вҖ” boundary tests pin: 168 hours = compliant; 169 hours + not completed = violation. Pinned by `tx_at_168_boundary_compliant`, `tx_169_hours_violates`, and `tx_uniquely_explicit_7_day_window_invariant` (3-regime sweep: TX = 168, CA = 240, Default = 0).

**TX В§ 92.156 interior-door exclusion** вҖ” closet doors and other interior doors fall outside the statutory framework entirely; landlord owes no statutory rekeying duty regardless of tenant request or timing. Pinned by `tx_interior_door_excluded`.

**Cost allocation truth table** вҖ” landlord pays for master-key changes and security upgrades; tenant pays for everything else (lost keys, roommate departures, tenant-driven security concerns, burglary under TX/Default). CA additionally shifts burglary-driven rekeying to landlord under В§ 1941.3(b). Pinned by `rekey_reason_cost_allocation_truth_table` (6-reason sweep), `tx_landlord_pays_for_master_key_change`, `tx_landlord_pays_for_security_upgrade`, `tx_tenant_pays_for_key_lost`, `ca_burglary_landlord_pays`, `default_tenant_pays_for_key_lost`, and `default_landlord_pays_for_security_upgrade`.

**TX unique remedies stack** вҖ” actual damages + punitive damages + $500 civil penalty + ONE MONTH's rent + court costs + attorney fees is uniquely cumulative; CA + Default rely on common-law damages without statutory multipliers. Pinned by `tx_remedies_note_describes_one_month_rent_and_500` and `tx_unique_remedies_invariant` (TX engages full remedies stack; CA does NOT).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-mid-tenancy-rekeying`. Twenty-six tests pin: **TX compliant passes**; **TX tenant pays for key lost**; **TX landlord pays for master key change**; **TX landlord pays for security upgrade**; **TX past 7-day window violates**; **TX at 168 boundary compliant**; **TX 169 hours violates**; **TX no new keys violates**; **TX interior door excluded**; **TX remedies note describes one month rent and $500**; **TX citation pins Subchapter D sections**; **CA compliant within reasonable time**; **CA burglary landlord pays**; **CA past reasonable time violates**; **CA citation pins subsections**; **default compliant always**; **default tenant pays for key lost**; **default landlord pays for security upgrade**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **TX unique remedies invariant**; **rekey reason cost allocation truth table** (6-reason sweep); **no tenant request no engagement**; **TX clean no violations**; **CA clean no violations**; **default clean no violations**; **TX uniquely explicit 7-day window invariant** (3-regime sweep).

`traderview-expense::landlord_negative_credit_reporting` is the **landlord negative credit reporting framework module** вҖ” when may a landlord report unpaid rent, collection accounts, or eviction history to consumer reporting agencies (Equifax/Experian/TransUnion + tenant-screening CRAs)? Distinct from sibling `rent_credit_reporting` (POSITIVE rent reporting under Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.06 / AB 2747) which addresses the landlord's OFFER to report positive payment history. This module addresses NEGATIVE reporting of delinquent rent / collection accounts. Trader-landlord critical because furnishing inaccurate or unverifiable negative information to a CRA exposes landlord to STATUTORY DAMAGES of $100-$1,000 per violation plus actual and attorney's fees plus PUNITIVE damages for willful violations.

**Federal FCRA framework**:

| Provision | Rule |
|-----------|------|
| **В§ 1681s-2(a)** | Accuracy and integrity of information; cannot report known-inaccurate info; must promptly correct and notify all CRAs |
| **В§ 1681s-2(b)** | **MANDATORY AND NON-DISCRETIONARY** investigation duty upon CRA dispute notice (5-element: investigate + review + report + cross-CRA correction + delete unverifiable) |
| **В§ 1681c(a)(4)** | **7-YEAR limitation** on negative information from DATE OF DELINQUENCY (not collection placement or judgment) |
| **В§ 1681c(a)(1)** | Chapter 7 bankruptcies вҖ” 10 years |
| **В§ 1681n** | **WILLFUL** noncompliance вҖ” $100-$1,000 statutory damages per violation OR actual damages whichever greater + PUNITIVE + attorney's fees (Safeco Ins. Co. v. Burr, 551 U.S. 47 (2007)) |
| **В§ 1681o** | NEGLIGENT noncompliance вҖ” actual damages + attorney's fees only |
| **В§ 1681e(b)** | CRA accuracy |
| **В§ 1681i** | CRA investigation of disputes within 30 days (45 days if consumer provides additional information) |
| **В§ 1681m** | Adverse action notice when landlord denies/raises rent based on CRA report |

**FDCPA В§ 1692 interaction** when landlord places debt with third-party collector:
- **В§ 1692e** вҖ” prohibits false or misleading representations including disputed debt not flagged as disputed
- **В§ 1692f** вҖ” prohibits unfair or unconscionable collection means
- **В§ 1692g(a)** вҖ” 5-day VALIDATION NOTICE with debt amount + original creditor + dispute rights
- **В§ 1692g(b)** вҖ” tenant disputes in writing within 30 days, collector must VERIFY before continuing
- **В§ 1692k** вҖ” civil liability statutory damages up to $1,000 + actual + fees
- **CFPB Regulation F** (12 CFR В§ 1006) requires itemization date

**Tenant-screening CRA subset** (CoreLogic SafeRent + TransUnion SmartMove + RentBureau by Experian + LexisNexis) subject to all FCRA В§ 1681 requirements; eviction records controversial вҖ” many jurisdictions seal or expunge under Just Cause laws making CRA reporting impermissible.

**State law overlay**:
- **NEW YORK** вҖ” NY GBL В§ 380 NY Fair Credit Reporting Act mirrors FCRA; В§ 380-d investigation requirement; HSTPA 2019 + NY RPL В§ 227-f **TENANT BLACKLIST PROHIBITION** with $500-$1,000 civil penalty per violation
- **CALIFORNIA** вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1785 CA Consumer Credit Reporting Agencies Act + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1786 Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act covering tenant-screening CRAs
- **OREGON / WASHINGTON** вҖ” Oregon SB 970 + Washington RCW 59.18.367 eviction record sealing prohibits CRA reporting of sealed evictions
- **CONNECTICUT** вҖ” Conn. Gen. Stat. В§ 47a-71 requires **30-DAY PRE-REPORTING NOTICE** before landlord may report to CRA

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) trader reports $5K unpaid rent + tenant disputes within 30 days + trader fails to investigate within 30 days вҖ” В§ 1681s-2(b) MANDATORY investigation violated + $100-$1,000 statutory damages + actual + fees; (2) trader reports 8 years after original delinquency вҖ” В§ 1681c(a)(4) 7-year limit exceeded + willful В§ 1681n punitive; (3) NY trader reports to tenant blacklist database вҖ” NY RPL В§ 227-f $500-$1,000 civil penalty; (4) collection agency without В§ 1692g(a) 5-day validation notice вҖ” FDCPA В§ 1692k $1K statutory + actual + fees; (5) CA trader engages CRA that reports SEALED EVICTION вҖ” В§ 1786 + В§ 1681e(b) accuracy violation against CRA AND furnishing landlord.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-negative-credit-reporting`. Thirty-two tests pin: **compliant baseline no violations**; **inaccurate information violation** (В§ 1681s-2(a)); **investigation failure violation** (В§ 1681s-2(b) MANDATORY); **7-year aging compliant at 7 years boundary**; **7-year aging violation at 8 years**; **FDCPA no validation notice violation** (В§ 1692g(a) + Regulation F); **FDCPA dispute without verification violation** (В§ 1692g(b)); **NY tenant blacklist violation** (HSTPA + В§ 227-f); **NY tenant blacklist not engaged outside NY**; **CT 30-day notice required in CT** (В§ 47a-71); **CT 30-day notice with notice compliant**; **CT 30-day notice not required outside CT**; **sealed eviction record violation** (OR SB 970 + WA RCW 59.18.367); **willful violation punitive damages available** (Safeco standard); **willful without underlying violation no punitive**; **jurisdiction truth table four cells**; **NY uniquely engages tenant blacklist invariant**; **CT uniquely requires 30-day notice invariant**; **statutory damages range pinned** ($100-$1,000 cents); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1681s-2(a) + В§ 1681s-2(b) + В§ 1681c + В§ 1681c(a)(4) + В§ 1681n + В§ 1681o + В§ 1681e(b) + В§ 1681i + В§ 1681m + В§ 1692e + В§ 1692f + В§ 1692g + В§ 1692k + 12 CFR В§ 1006 + NY GBL В§ 380 + NY GBL В§ 380-d + NY RPL В§ 227-f + Cal. `traderview-expense::landlord_pest_extermination_timeline` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord pest extermination response timeline framework module** вҖ” general pest infestation response (rodents, cockroaches, ants, termites, beyond bed bugs). Distinct from sibling `bedbug_extermination_cost` (bed-bug specific cost allocation), `bedbug_disclosure` (bed-bug prior-occurrence disclosure within 1-3 years), `rental_pesticide_application_notification` (24-72 hour pre-application notice), `pesticide_application_notice` (general pesticide framework). Trader-landlord critical because pest infestations routinely give rise to tenant rent-withholding claims, NYC HPD violations + $250-$1,000 daily fines, civil penalties under state habitability warranties, and tort liability for disease transmission.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **New York City** | NYC HMC В§ 27-2018 + NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 (IPM) | Continuous eradication; 3+ unit buildings require annual inspection + least-toxic methods + 72-hour pesticide notification + 3-year recordkeeping |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1(a)(8) + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 | Rodent/vermin-free standard; tenant В§ 1942 repair-and-deduct up to $1,000 OR 1 month rent |
| **Massachusetts** | 105 CMR 410.550 + Mass. Gen. Laws c. 111 В§ 127A | Owner extermination duty; Board of Health enforcement; criminal contempt for non-compliance |
| **Default** | URLTA В§ 2.104(a)(2) + Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 5.1 | Implied warranty of habitability |

**NYC HMC В§ 27-2018 pest definitions (broad coverage)**:
1. Class Insecta: houseflies, lice, bees, cockroaches, moths, silverfish, beetles, bedbugs, ants, termites, hornets, mosquitoes, wasps
2. Phylum Arthropoda: spiders, mites, ticks, centipedes, wood lice
3. Order Rodentia: mice, Norway rats, other unwanted rodents
4. Any other unwanted plant, animal or fungal life that is destructive, annoying or a nuisance

**NYC HPD violation classes**:
- **CLASS A** (non-hazardous) вҖ” 90-day correction; no daily civil penalty
- **CLASS B** (hazardous) вҖ” 30-day correction; **$25-$250 daily civil penalty**
- **CLASS C** (immediately hazardous) вҖ” 24-hour correction; **$250-$1,000 daily civil penalty**

Pest infestations involving rodents in multi-family buildings typically Class B or Class C.

**NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 (Integrated Pest Management) вҖ” 5 requirements for 3+ unit buildings**:
1. ANNUAL inspection for pest infestations and conducive conditions
2. CONTINUOUS eradication measures upon discovery
3. LEAST-TOXIC pest control methods first
4. Tenant 72-hour pre-pesticide notification under NYC HMC В§ 27-2017.3
5. 3-year recordkeeping of all pest control activities

**Severity-graded response timeline (industry-standard interpretation)**:
- **EMERGENCY** (rodent infestation with health hazard, severe occupied-unit cockroach) вҖ” **24 HOURS** to commence
- **STANDARD** (visible cockroach activity, mouse sightings) вҖ” **14 DAYS** NY/MA + **30 DAYS** CA/Default
- **PREVENTIVE** (conducive conditions only, no active pests) вҖ” **30 DAYS** to remediate

**Tenant remedies under habitability warranty**:
1. **RENT WITHHOLDING** вҖ” Park West Mgmt. Corp. v. Mitchell, 47 N.Y.2d 316 (1979) in NY; Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974) in CA; Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973) in MA
2. **REPAIR AND DEDUCT** вҖ” CA Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 up to $1,000 OR 1 month rent; URLTA В§ 4.103 up to $300 per repair / $600 per year
3. **RENT ABATEMENT** вҖ” 50-100% of rent during habitability breach period
4. **CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION** вҖ” vacate with full rent relief
5. **PUBLIC ENFORCEMENT** вҖ” NYC HPD complaint with 7-10 day inspection / CA local building inspector or environmental health / MA Board of Health under Mass. Gen. Laws c. 111 В§ 127A with criminal contempt

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) NYC 8-unit building rodent infestation 30-day delay вҖ” HPD CLASS C violation $1,000/day plus tenant rent withholding; (2) CA SFR cockroach infestation 60-day delay вҖ” В§ 1942 repair-and-deduct up to $1,000 OR 1 month rent; (3) MA triple-decker mouse infestation вҖ” 105 CMR 410.550 Board of Health enforcement with criminal contempt; (4) NYC LL 55 missed annual inspection вҖ” Class B violation $25-$250 daily; (5) URLTA-jurisdiction failure вҖ” В§ 2.104(a)(2) breach with В§ 4.105 termination remedy.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-pest-extermination-timeline`. Thirty tests pin: **NYC standard 14-day timely**; **NYC standard past 14 days breach**; **emergency 24-hour window**; **CA standard 30-day window**; **MA standard 14-day window**; **default URLTA 30-day window**; **preventive 30-day window**; **no eradication commenced breach**; **NYC LL 55 missing annual inspection**; **NYC LL 55 missing least-toxic**; **NYC LL 55 missing 72-hour notification**; **small building no LL 55 obligation**; **NYC Class C max civil penalty** ($1,000 daily); **NYC Class B max civil penalty** ($250 daily); **NYC Class A no civil penalty**; **habitability breach unlocks tenant remedies**; **NYC unlocks HPD complaint remedy**; **CA unlocks local code enforcement remedy**; **MA unlocks Board of Health remedy** (В§ 127A + criminal contempt); **jurisdiction-severity truth table** (4 Г— 3 = 12 cells); **emergency uniquely 24-hour invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (NYC HMC В§ 27-2018 + NYC HMC В§ 27-2017.1 + NYC HMC В§ 27-2017.3 + NYC LL 55 2018 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1(a)(8) + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 + 105 CMR 410.550 + Mass. Gen. Laws c. 111 В§ 127A + URLTA В§ 2.104(a)(2) + URLTA В§ 4.105 + Restatement (Second) Property + Park West v. Mitchell + Green v. Superior Court + Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins NYC В§ 27-2018 pest definitions**; **note pins NYC HPD violation classes**; **note pins Local Law 55 five requirements**; **note pins response timeline**; **note pins habitability remedies with case law**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_property_sale_notice` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord property sale notice and security deposit transfer disclosure module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because traders who exit rental property via sale must comply with state-by-state security deposit transfer plus new owner notice requirements within tight statutory windows. Failure triggers **JOINT AND SEVERAL LIABILITY** of seller and buyer for the deposit, exposes seller to **TREBLE DAMAGES** in Massachusetts, and stacks Federal PTFA 90-day notice obligations during foreclosure sales. Companion to `security_deposit_bank_disclosure`, `deposit_interest`, `deposit_return_windows`, `foreclosure_tenant_rights`, `landlord_identification_disclosure`, `tenant_estoppel_certificate`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Window | Required Notice Method |
|--------------|--------|--------|------------------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(h) | Reasonable time (30-day benchmark) | PERSONAL DELIVERY or FIRST-CLASS MAIL plus deposit amount plus claims plus successor name/address/phone |
| **New York** | NY GOL В§ 7-105 plus NY GOL В§ 7-103(2) | **5 DAYS** | REGISTERED OR CERTIFIED MAIL; accrued interest transfer required for 6+ family dwelling buildings |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(7) | **45 DAYS** | Written notice from NEW OWNER; willful violation = TREBLE DAMAGES plus attorney's fees |
| **Default** | Common law plus Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 12.1 | Reasonable time (60-day benchmark) | Written notice; common-law successor liability |

**California В§ 1950.5(h) five-element notice requirements**: (1) transfer deposit (less lawful deductions) to successor in interest; (2) notify tenant by PERSONAL DELIVERY or FIRST-CLASS MAIL; (3) AMOUNT of deposit transferred; (4) any CLAIMS made against the security; (5) successor's name, address, and telephone number. Failure triggers joint and several liability of seller and buyer.

**New York В§ 7-105 requirements**: within 5 DAYS of conveyance, (1) deliver deposit to grantee/assignee AND (2) notify tenant by REGISTERED OR CERTIFIED MAIL of new owner's name and address. Failure causes grantee jointly and severally liable with seller for repayment PLUS accrued interest. NY GOL В§ 7-103(2) requires 6+ family dwelling units in interest-bearing account; 1% per annum administration retained by landlord; balance belongs to tenant and must transfer to successor.

**Massachusetts В§ 15B(7) requirements**: within 45 DAYS of sale, NEW OWNER must provide tenant written notice that (1) new owner has received deposit and (2) where it is being held. Successor responsible for return REGARDLESS of receipt from prior owner. Willful violation = TREBLE DAMAGES plus attorney's fees.

**12 USC В§ 5220 Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009** вҖ” sale during foreclosure STACKS 90-day notice obligation on top of state-law deposit transfer/notice requirements; failure to comply with EITHER triggers separate liability.

Trader-landlord fact patterns: (1) trader sells 12-unit NYC building $5M, transfers $36K deposits but fails 5-day certified-mail notice вҖ” joint and several liability; (2) CA seller mails notice by regular mail вҖ” INVALID under В§ 1950.5(h); (3) MA new owner fails 45-day notice вҖ” TREBLE DAMAGES; (4) sale during foreclosure stacks PTFA 90-day on top; (5) CA pending deduction claim вҖ” В§ 1950.5(h) requires NET transfer plus disclosure of claims.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-property-sale-notice`. Thirty-one tests pin: **CA compliant no liability**; **CA personal delivery valid**; **CA regular mail invalid**; **CA missing deposit amount invalid**; **CA missing deduction claims invalid**; **NY 5-day window**; **NY window exceeded**; **NY first-class mail invalid requires certified**; **NY 6+ unit accrued interest required**; **MA 45-day window**; **MA window exceeded**; **MA willful violation treble damages**; **MA non-willful no treble**; **no notice invalid all jurisdictions** (4-jurisdiction sweep); **deposit not transferred violation**; **PTFA stacks on state obligations**; **jurisdiction window truth table four cells**; **NY uniquely 5-day invariant**; **MA uniquely treble damages invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(h) + NY GOL В§ 7-103(2) + NY GOL В§ 7-105 + Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 В§ 15B(7) + 12 USC В§ 5220 + Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009 + Restatement (Second) of Property: Landlord and Tenant В§ 12.1); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins CA В§ 1950.5(h) five elements**; **note pins NY GOL В§ 7-105**; **note pins NY GOL В§ 7-103(2) 6+ unit**; **note pins MA В§ 15B(7)**; **note pins PTFA federal**; **note pins Restatement (Second) Property В§ 12.1**; **note pins joint and several liability rule**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_post_eviction_tenant_property_storage_disposal` is the **multi-jurisdictional LANDLORD POST-EVICTION TENANT PROPERTY storage and disposal compliance framework module** вҖ” when a tenant vacates (voluntarily, by eviction, or by abandonment) and leaves personal property in or around the rental unit, what notice procedures, storage durations, valuation thresholds, and disposal pathways apply, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord to conversion + statutory damages? Distinct from siblings `tenant_abandonment` (premises abandonment without eviction), `landlord_self_help_eviction_prohibition` (illegal lockout/property hold), `squatter_unauthorized_occupant_removal` (squatter scenario), `abandoned_property_handling` (general framework), `landlord_retaliation_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) conversion liability under California Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1989 is **strict-liability** вҖ” even good-faith disposal without proper notice triggers $250 minimum + actual damages + attorney fees; (2) California's **$700 threshold** determines whether public sale required вҖ” must value property in good faith before disposal; (3) Texas В§ 92.0081 requires notice but allows discard if no response; (4) New York requires **sheriff-supervised removal** under RPAPL В§ 749(3) вҖ” landlord cannot unilaterally remove; (5) bailee liability for property held in storage means landlord owes ordinary-care duty during storage period; (6) cumulative storage costs over months for valuable items can exceed property value, motivating tenant abandonment of redemption.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **California (MOST PROCEDURALLY DETAILED)** | Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1980-1991 ("Disposition of Personal Property Remaining on Premises at Termination of Tenancy") | В§ 1983 written notice with **15-day personal / 18-day mail** response window; В§ 1984 notice content (description + location + deadline + reasonable storage cost); В§ 1985 **$700 valuation threshold** determining public-sale requirement; В§ 1988 storage cost reimbursement; В§ 1989 tenant redemption right + **$250 minimum statutory damages** + actual damages + attorney fees |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0081 (Removal of Property and Exclusion of Residential Tenant); В§ 92.014 | Written notice required + reasonable storage period + post-storage sale or disposal procedure |
| **New York** | N.Y. RPAPL В§ 749(3) (post-eviction property retention); N.Y. RPL В§ 235-b; NYC Admin. Code В§ 26-521 | **Sheriff/marshal supervised removal REQUIRED** вҖ” landlord cannot unilaterally remove; warrant of eviction authorizes physical removal to designated storage |
| **Default** | Common-law conversion + bailee liability; Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 | Conversion liability + bailee ordinary-care duty + tort negligence + implied warranty of habitability |

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Disposal without notice** вҶ’ common-law conversion + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1989 minimum $250 statutory damages + actual damages + attorney fees
2. **Notice deficient** (missing description / location / deadline) вҶ’ notice ineffective + same conversion liability as no-notice
3. **Premature disposal** (before statutory storage window expires) вҶ’ conversion + tort negligence + statutory damages
4. **Sale below FMV** without public-sale procedure on $700+ property вҶ’ conversion + breach of duty to obtain reasonable value
5. **Loss or damage during storage** вҶ’ common-law bailee liability (ordinary care duty) + insurance dispute

**California В§ 1984 notice content requirements** (four):
1. Description of property sufficient for owner identification
2. Location where property may be claimed
3. Reasonable storage cost
4. Date by which tenant must respond

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) CA tenant evicted, leaves $400 of household goods вҶ’ personal-delivery notice + wait 15 days + dispose under В§ 1985 ($400 < $700 threshold); (2) CA tenant evicted, leaves $2,000 piano вҶ’ notice + wait 18 days mailing + public sale with newspaper notice (В§ 1985); (3) CA disposes after only 5 days from personal delivery вҶ’ premature disposal + conversion + $250+ statutory damages; (4) CA fails to send notice at all вҶ’ conversion + $250+ + actual damages + attorney fees; (5) NY landlord changes locks unilaterally and tosses property вҶ’ violates RPAPL В§ 749(3) + NYC В§ 26-521 unlawful eviction; (6) TX landlord stores property in own garage, water-damages tenant's electronics вҶ’ bailee liability for ordinary care breach; (7) tenant pays storage costs and demands return mid-window вҶ’ В§ 1989 redemption right requires immediate return.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-post-eviction-tenant-property-storage-disposal`. Thirty-five tests pin: **property returned compliant**; **CA under $700 compliant after 15 days personal delivery**; **CA personal delivery window active under 15 days**; **CA mail window active under 18 days**; **CA mail window exactly 18 days compliant**; **no notice served disposal taken conversion liability** (В§ 1989 + $250); **notice missing description conversion when disposed**; **notice missing location conversion when disposed**; **notice missing deadline conversion when disposed**; **notice required when no disposal taken yet**; **premature disposal 5 days personal delivery** (15-day window violation); **CA over $700 no public sale violation** (В§ 1985); **CA over $700 with public sale compliant**; **CA exactly $700 triggers public sale**; **CA under $700 no public sale required**; **property damaged during storage bailee liability**; **NY no sheriff-supervised removal unilateral conversion** (В§ 749(3)); **NY with sheriff-supervised removal compliant**; **TX compliant baseline** (В§ 92.0081); **Default jurisdiction common-law conversion** (Hilder + В§ 1941.1); **tenant demanded return redemption right** (В§ 1989); **severity priority bailee > conversion > premature > public sale**; **severity conversion > premature > public sale**; **severity premature > public sale**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§В§ 1980-1991 + В§ 1983/В§ 1984/В§ 1985/В§ 1988/В§ 1989 + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0081/В§ 92.014 + N.Y. RPAPL В§ 749(3) + N.Y. RPL В§ 235-b + NYC Admin. Code В§ 26-521 + Hilder + 478 A.2d 202 + В§ 1941.1); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins CA 15/18-day response window**; **note pins $700 valuation threshold**; **note pins В§ 1984 notice content** (description + location + storage cost + date); **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins NY sheriff-supervised removal**; **note pins companion modules**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **CA uniquely $700 public sale invariant**; **multiple violations stack in actions** (В§ 1989 + redemption).

`traderview-expense::landlord_possession_delivery` is the **landlord-possession-delivery module** вҖ” when must a landlord deliver ACTUAL possession (not just legal right) of the leased premises to the tenant at lease commencement, and what are the tenant's remedies if a prior tenant is holding over or squatters remain? Trader-critical for landlord-investors whose new acquisitions come with unanticipated occupants. Distinct from sibling modules `holdover_tenant_damages` (prior tenant's liability for remaining past lease end), `quiet_enjoyment` (post-delivery covenant against landlord interference), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery uninhabitable-condition remedies), and `eviction_notices` (procedures landlord must follow to evict).

**Three regimes** вҖ” the structural English-vs-American divergence with URLTA codification:

| Regime | Authority | Duty | Tenant remedies | Lease cancellation |
|--------|-----------|------|-----------------|---------------------|
| URLTA states | URLTA В§ 2.103 + В§ 4.102 | **Yes вҖ” actual possession** | **Greater of 3 months' rent OR 3Г— actual damages** + attorney's fees + injunctive relief | Yes |
| English Rule | Restatement (2d) of Property В§ 6.2 + modern majority | **Yes вҖ” actual possession** | Actual damages + rent abatement for delay period (common law) | Yes |
| American Rule | Hannan v. Dusch, 153 S.E. 824 (Va. 1930) | **No вҖ” only legal right** | Tenant must evict holdover party + recover from THAT party | No (lease remains in force) |

**URLTA В§ 4.102 statutory remedies вҖ” greater of 3 months' rent OR threefold actual damages**. The "whichever is greater" clause means the 3-months floor protects tenants with low actual out-of-pocket damages; the triple-actual multiplier scales penalty with actual harm when damages are large. Pinned by `urlta_3_months_rent_greater_than_triple_damages` ($1K actual Г— 3 = $3K vs 3 mo Г— $2K = $6K вҶ’ $6K wins), `urlta_triple_damages_greater_than_3_months` ($5K Г— 3 = $15K vs $6K вҶ’ $15K wins), `urlta_no_actual_damages_3_months_floor` ($0 actual вҶ’ $6K floor), `urlta_no_breach_no_damages`, and `urlta_dollar_for_dollar_test_with_3_months_rent_versus_actual_damages_truth_table` (5-cell sweep).

**English Rule actual damages + rent abatement**. Common-law rule provides actual damages with rent abatement for the delay period. Module uses `monthly_rent Г— days / 30` (multiply-before-divide) to preserve precision. 30 days delay at $2K/month = $2,000 exact abatement. Pinned by `english_rule_failure_actual_damages_plus_abatement` ($5K actual + $2K abatement = $7K), `english_rule_no_actual_damages_abatement_only` ($2K abatement only), `english_rule_no_breach_no_damages`, and `english_rule_long_delay_large_abatement` (365 days Г— $2K / 30 = $2,433,333 abatement).

**American Rule shifts burden to tenant**. Landlord delivers only LEGAL possession. New tenant has standing to evict holdover prior tenant or squatter directly and recover damages from that party вҖ” NOT the landlord. Lease remains in force; rent continues to accrue. Tenant cannot cancel. Pinned by `american_rule_no_landlord_duty`, `american_rule_no_statutory_remedies`, and `american_rule_holdover_party_remedy_note` (note must reference burden-shifting to tenant for eviction proceeding).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_urlta_has_statutory_remedies_invariant` (3-regime sweep вҖ” only URLTA states get В§ 4.102 statutory remedies + attorney-fee shifting); `only_american_rule_shifts_burden_to_tenant_invariant` (only American rule sets `tenant_must_evict_prior_tenant_party = true`); `american_rule_only_regime_without_duty_invariant` (URLTA + English impose duty; American does not); `urlta_constants_invariant` (3-multiplier, 3-months floor).

**Damages calculation method enum** вҖ” `DamagesCalculationMethod::UrltaTripleActualOrThreeMonths` for URLTA; `EnglishRuleActualWithAbatement` for English rule; `NoLandlordDamages` for American rule. Pinned by 3 dedicated enum-coverage tests.

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references related modules `holdover_tenant_damages`, `quiet_enjoyment`, `habitability_remedies`, and `eviction_notices`, and clarifies that this module covers LEASE-COMMENCEMENT DELIVERY DUTY specifically. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-possession-delivery`.
