Dusch); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `holdover_tenant_damages` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `habitability_remedies` + `eviction_notices` + LEASE-COMMENCEMENT DELIVERY DUTY); **defensive negative rent clamped**; **defensive negative damages clamped**; **defensive negative days no abatement**.

`traderview-expense::lease_waiver_enforceability` is the **lease waiver clause enforceability module** вҖ” when may a residential or commercial lease validly waive tenant rights or landlord liability via contract language? Distinct from sibling modules `landlord_harassment` (affirmative-harassment penalties), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery habitability obligations), `quiet_enjoyment` (general post-delivery covenant), and `plain_language_lease` (statutory clarity requirement). This module focuses on the LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISION itself вҖ” when the contract clause attempting to extinguish a right is void as a matter of statute or public policy.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Scope | Lease type |
|--------|-----------|-------|------------|
| New York | N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 5-321 | **Narrow** вҖ” only landlord-negligence exculpatory clauses void | Both residential AND commercial |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1953 | **Broad** вҖ” voids 6 categories of tenant rights waivers | Residential only |
| Default | Common law + Restatement (Second) of Contracts В§ 178 | Knowing + voluntary + public policy test | Both |

**New York В§ 5-321 вҖ” narrow scope, broad application.** Voids any clause "exempting the lessor from liability for damages for injuries to person or property caused by or resulting from the negligence of the lessor." Applies to BOTH residential and commercial leases. INDEMNIFICATION clauses remain enforceable (Great N. Ins. Co. v. Interior Constr. Corp., 7 N.Y.3d 412 (2006)) вҖ” landlord may still seek indemnity from tenant for liability paid to injured third parties; only the clause exempting LANDLORD from liability to tenant for landlord's own negligence is void. Pinned by `ny_landlord_negligence_exculpatory_void`, `ny_negligence_exculpatory_applies_to_commercial`, `ny_jury_trial_waiver_outside_5_321_scope` (jury waiver enforceable in NY with knowing consent), `ny_jury_trial_waiver_without_consent_unenforceable`, and `ny_indemnity_distinction_note_when_5_321_engages` (UX-text regression for indemnity-vs-exculpation distinction).

**California В§ 1953 вҖ” broadest residential protection in the U.S.** Six categories of waivers automatically void in residential leases:

| Subsection | Voided waiver category |
|------------|-------------------------|
| В§ 1953(a)(1) | В§ 1950.5 (security deposit) or В§ 1954 (landlord entry) rights |
| В§ 1953(a)(2) | Right to assert cause of action against lessor |
| В§ 1953(a)(3) | Right to notice or hearing required by law |
| В§ 1953(a)(4) | Procedural rights in litigation (including jury trial waiver) |
| В§ 1953(a)(5) | Right to have lessor exercise duty of care to prevent personal injury or property damage |
| В§ 1953(a)(6) | Cumulative remedies under the lease |

В§ 1953 applies ONLY to residential leases. Commercial waivers in California fall under common-law analysis. Pinned by `ca_residential_jury_trial_waiver_void`, `ca_residential_deposit_rights_waiver_void`, `ca_residential_future_cause_of_action_void`, `ca_residential_notice_hearing_void`, `ca_residential_habitability_void`, `ca_residential_cumulative_remedies_void`, `ca_commercial_lease_outside_1953_scope` (commercial waiver enforceable with consent), `ca_1953_applies_only_to_residential_invariant` (residential vs commercial head-to-head), and `ca_residential_all_seven_waiver_types_void` (7-prong sweep вҖ” all enum types void in CA residential).

**Default common-law analysis вҖ” knowing/voluntary + public policy**. Waiver enforceable if BOTH (a) knowing and voluntary consent (no adhesion-contract procedural unconscionability) AND (b) waiver does not violate public policy. Public-policy-protected categories (habitability, duty of care, landlord negligence exculpation) are void REGARDLESS of consent. Pinned by `default_knowing_voluntary_enforceable`, `default_not_knowing_voluntary_unenforceable`, `default_habitability_waiver_void_regardless_of_consent`, `default_negligence_exculpatory_void_regardless_of_consent`, and `default_public_policy_voids_regardless_of_consent_truth_table` (4-cell sweep вҖ” public-policy-protected category voids regardless of consent; non-protected category requires knowing consent).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_california_1953_engages_residential_only_protection` (3-regime sweep); `ny_5_321_applies_to_both_residential_and_commercial_invariant` (both residential and commercial coverage); `ca_1953_applies_only_to_residential_invariant` (residential vs commercial divergence). `ca_residential_all_seven_waiver_types_void` confirms all enum waiver types are void in CA residential.

**No-waiver-clause short circuit** вҖ” when `waiver_in_lease_agreement` is false, no analysis is performed; result returned immediately as compliant with explanatory note. Pinned by `no_waiver_clause_no_analysis`.

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references `landlord_harassment` (affirmative-harassment penalties), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery habitability obligations), `quiet_enjoyment` (general post-delivery covenant), and `plain_language_lease` (statutory clarity requirement), and clarifies that this module covers LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISIONS specifically. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-waiver-enforceability`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no waiver clause no analysis**; **NY landlord-negligence exculpatory void**; **NY negligence exculpatory applies to commercial**; **NY jury trial waiver outside В§ 5-321 scope**; **NY jury trial waiver without consent unenforceable**; **CA residential jury trial waiver void** (В§ 1953(a)(4)); **CA residential deposit rights waiver void** (В§ 1953(a)(1)); **CA residential future cause of action void** (В§ 1953(a)(2)); **CA residential notice/hearing void** (В§ 1953(a)(3)); **CA residential habitability void** (В§ 1953(a)(5)); **CA residential cumulative remedies void** (В§ 1953(a)(6)); **CA commercial lease outside В§ 1953 scope** (residential-only protection); **default knowing voluntary enforceable**; **default not knowing voluntary unenforceable**; **default habitability waiver void regardless of consent**; **default negligence exculpatory void regardless of consent**; **only California В§ 1953 engages residential-only protection** (3-regime sweep); **NY В§ 5-321 applies to both residential and commercial invariant**; **CA В§ 1953 applies only to residential invariant**; **CA residential all seven waiver types void**; **default public policy voids regardless of consent truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 5-321 + В§ 1953 + (a)(1)-(6) + В§ 1950.5 + В§ 1954 + Restatement В§ 178 + Great N. Ins. Co. 7 N.Y.3d 412); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `landlord_harassment` + `habitability_remedies` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `plain_language_lease` + LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISIONS); **NY indemnity distinction note when В§ 5-321 engages** (UX-text regression вҖ” indemnification clauses remain enforceable per Great N. Ins. Co.).

`traderview-expense::residential_lease_arbitration_clause` is the **pre-dispute arbitration clause enforceability in residential leases** module вҖ” when can a landlord include a binding arbitration provision in a residential lease that requires the tenant to arbitrate disputes (habitability, eviction, deposit, fee challenges) rather than file court action? Distinct from `lease_waiver_enforceability` (general lease-drafted waiver framework) and `prevailing_party_attorney_fees` (fee-shifting). This module focuses ONLY on the ARBITRATION / CLASS-ACTION WAIVER question.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Default rule |
|--------|-----------|--------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1953(a)(4); Brooks v. Greystar Real Estate Partners (S.D. Cal. 2024) | Traditionally voids procedural-rights waivers; **eroded by Brooks** under FAA preemption; CONTESTED status for habitability / unlawful detainer / deposit disputes |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.S.A. 2A:23B-1 + Atalese v. U.S. Legal Services Group (220 N.J. 220, 2014) | Requires EXPLICIT judicial-forum-waiver language in clear, unambiguous terms; boilerplate clauses fail Atalese |
| **Default** | 9 U.S.C. В§В§ 1/2/4 Federal Arbitration Act + AT&T Mobility v. Concepcion (563 U.S. 333, 2011) + Epic Systems v. Lewis (584 U.S. 497, 2018) | FAA enforces; В§ 2 savings clause permits state defenses applied neutrally; class-action waivers enforceable under Epic Systems |

**Universal Speak Out Act of 2022 (117 Stat. 2192) carve-out for sexual harassment / assault.** Pre-dispute arbitration clauses are UNENFORCEABLE for sexual harassment and sexual assault claims regardless of state regime. Pinned by `sexual_harassment_pre_dispute_arbitration_unenforceable_universal` (3-regime sweep confirms all three regimes bar arbitration), `sexual_harassment_disables_class_action_waiver_too`, `note_speak_out_act_references_117_stat_2192`.

**В§ 2 FAA savings clause permits unconscionability + duress defenses.** State-law contract defenses (unconscionability, duress, misrepresentation, fraud) apply through FAA В§ 2 if applied NEUTRALLY (not specifically against arbitration agreements) per AT&T Mobility v. Concepcion (563 U.S. 333, 2011). Pinned by `unconscionability_voids_arbitration_universal`, `duress_or_misrepresentation_voids_arbitration`, `unconscionability_disables_class_action_waiver`, `note_unconscionability_references_concepcion`.

**California В§ 1953(a)(4) status is CONTESTED post-Brooks v. Greystar (S.D. Cal. 2024).** The federal court ruled that FAA preemption analysis applies to residential lease arbitration clauses, eroding the traditional В§ 1953(a)(4) void rule. Module marks all CA habitability / unlawful detainer / deposit disputes as "CONTESTED status вҖ” FAA savings clause analysis required" rather than absolute void. Pinned by `ca_habitability_now_contested_under_brooks`, `ca_traditional_void_pathway_includes_brooks_caveat`, `ca_traditional_void_categories_three_specific_disputes_only` (3-dispute sweep confirms only habitability/UD/deposit fall in traditional void category вҖ” Other disputes arbitrable).

**NJ Atalese v. U.S. Legal Services Group (220 N.J. 220, 2014) requires EXPLICIT judicial-forum-waiver language.** Boilerplate arbitration clauses fail because they lack clear, unambiguous waiver of the right to a judicial forum. Pinned by `nj_explicit_judicial_waiver_satisfies_atalese`, `nj_missing_explicit_waiver_violates_atalese`, `nj_pre_atalese_boilerplate_clause_unenforceable`, `nj_unique_atalese_requirement_invariant` (CA/Default do not require Atalese explicit waiver).

**Class-action waivers enforceable under Epic Systems v. Lewis (584 U.S. 497, 2018) when underlying arbitration is enforceable.** When the underlying arbitration clause fails (no clause, sexual harassment exception, unconscionability, duress, Atalese failure), the class-action waiver also fails. Pinned by `class_action_waiver_enforceable_under_epic_systems`, `class_action_waiver_voids_when_underlying_arbitration_voided`, `no_class_action_waiver_no_enforcement_note`, `no_clause_in_lease_disables_class_action_waiver`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/residential-lease-arbitration-clause`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **no arbitration clause no enforcement**; **sexual harassment pre-dispute arbitration unenforceable universal** (3-regime Speak Out Act sweep); **unconscionability voids arbitration universal** (Concepcion savings clause); **duress or misrepresentation voids arbitration**; **CA habitability now contested under Brooks** (S.D. Cal. 2024); **CA unlawful detainer traditional В§ 1953 void**; **CA security deposit traditional В§ 1953 void**; **CA other dispute arbitrable under FAA**; **NJ explicit judicial waiver satisfies Atalese**; **NJ missing explicit waiver violates Atalese** (220 N.J. 220, 2014); **default FAA general enforceability**; **class-action waiver enforceable under Epic Systems** (584 U.S. 497, 2018); **no class-action waiver no enforcement note**; **citation CA pins В§ 1953(a)(4) + Brooks + Concepcion + Epic + Speak Out Act**; **citation NJ pins Atalese + N.J.S.A. 2A:23B-1 + 220 N.J. 220 (2014)**; **citation Default pins FAA 9 U.S.C. В§В§ 1/2/4 + Concepcion + Epic + Speak Out Act**; **sexual harassment disables class-action waiver too**; **unconscionability disables class-action waiver**; **no clause in lease disables class-action waiver**; **CA habitability arbitrable post-Brooks but marked contested**; **NJ unique Atalese requirement invariant** (CA/Default do not require explicit waiver); **5 dispute types routed correctly** (Habitability/UnlawfulDetainer/SecurityDeposit/SexualHarassment/Other); **CA traditional void categories 3 specific disputes only**; **note Speak Out Act references 117 Stat. 2192**; **note unconscionability references Concepcion**; **CA traditional void pathway includes Brooks caveat**; **NJ pre-Atalese boilerplate clause unenforceable**; **class-action waiver voids when underlying arbitration voided** (cascade rule).

`traderview-expense::rental_bedroom_egress_window` is the **rental property bedroom egress window requirement compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord ensure every bedroom in a residential rental unit has an emergency escape and rescue opening (EERO) meeting International Residential Code (IRC) В§ R310 minimum standards? Trader-landlord critical for any rental owner: failure to provide compliant egress window breaches implied warranty of habitability + creates fire-safety liability exposure + violates state building code adoption (CA + NY + 49 other state IRC adoptions). Distinct from siblings `detector_requirements` (smoke + CO detector hardware), `fire_sprinkler_disclosure` (suppression system), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (HPD signage), and `window_guard_requirements` (NYC HMC В§ 27-2046.1 child-safety).

**Two regimes** вҖ” IRC R310 (49 state-adopting jurisdictions) vs Default:

| Regime | Authority | Net opening | Height | Width | Sill height |
|--------|-----------|-------------|--------|-------|--------------|
| IRC R310 | IRC В§ R310.2.1 + В§ R310.2.2 + Cal. Residential Code В§ R310 + 2020 NY State Residential Code В§ R310 | **вүҘ 5.7 sq ft** (5.0 sq ft grade exception) | **вүҘ 24 in** | **вүҘ 20 in** | **вүӨ 44 in** above finished floor |
| Default | Local building code + common-law habitability | N/A statewide | N/A | N/A | N/A |

**IRC В§ R310.2.1 four-element simultaneous requirement** вҖ” every sleeping room must have at least one EERO meeting ALL FOUR dimensions simultaneously. Net clear opening of 5.7 sq ft (821 sq in) standard; 5.0 sq ft (720 sq in) grade-floor exception when sill вүӨ 44 inches above or below adjacent ground. Pinned by `ircr310_compliant_passes`, `ircr310_opening_at_5_7_sqft_boundary_compliant` (exactly 821 sq in = compliant), `ircr310_opening_below_5_7_sqft_violates` (820 fails), `ircr310_grade_floor_5_0_sqft_compliant` (720 = compliant at grade), `ircr310_grade_floor_below_5_0_sqft_violates`, `ircr310_height_at_24_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_height_below_24_in_violates`, `ircr310_width_at_20_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_width_below_20_in_violates`, `ircr310_sill_at_44_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_sill_above_44_in_violates`, `ircr310_all_four_violations_stack` (4 violations), `ircr310_grade_floor_uniquely_lower_threshold_invariant` (5.0 grade vs 5.7 standard), and `ircr310_note_pins_four_dimensions`.

**IRC В§ R310.2.3 window well requirements** (if EERO below adjacent ground level):
- Minimum horizontal area = **9 sq ft** (1,296 sq in)
- Minimum horizontal projection = **36 inches** from exterior face of wall
- Permanently affixed ladder/steps required if well depth **> 44 inches**

Pinned by `ircr310_below_grade_well_required`, `ircr310_below_grade_well_below_9_sqft_violates` (1,200 sq in violates 1,296 minimum), `ircr310_well_projection_below_36_in_violates` (35 in violates), `ircr310_well_depth_over_44_in_requires_ladder` (48 in depth without ladder violates), `ircr310_well_depth_over_44_in_with_ladder_compliant`, `ircr310_well_depth_at_44_in_boundary_no_ladder_required` (exactly 44 in = no ladder), `ircr310_well_required_only_below_grade_invariant`, `ircr310_ladder_required_only_when_well_depth_over_44_invariant` (44 in = no ladder; 45 in = ladder required), and `ircr310_note_pins_window_well_requirements`.

**Default fallback** вҖ” no statewide IRC adoption; local building code or common-law warranty of habitability governs. Most U.S. jurisdictions adopt IRC; some legacy areas use older codes. Pinned by `default_no_violations`, `default_citation_pins_local_code`, and `two_regimes_routed_correctly`.

**Habitability breach engages with any violation** вҖ” pinned by `ircr310_habitability_breach_engages_with_any_violation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-bedroom-egress-window`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **IRC R310 compliant passes**; **IRC R310 opening at 5.7 sq ft boundary compliant** (821 sq in); **IRC R310 opening below 5.7 sq ft violates** (820 fails); **IRC R310 grade floor 5.0 sq ft compliant** (720 sq in); **IRC R310 grade floor below 5.0 sq ft violates**; **IRC R310 height at 24 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 height below 24 in violates**; **IRC R310 width at 20 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 width below 20 in violates**; **IRC R310 sill at 44 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 sill above 44 in violates** (В§ R310.2.2); **IRC R310 below grade well required**; **IRC R310 below grade well below 9 sq ft violates** (В§ R310.2.3); **IRC R310 well projection below 36 in violates**; **IRC R310 well depth over 44 in requires ladder**; **IRC R310 well depth over 44 in with ladder compliant**; **IRC R310 well depth at 44 in boundary no ladder required**; **IRC R310 all four violations stack**; **IRC R310 citation pins authorities** (В§ R310 + В§ R310.2.1 + В§ R310.2.2 + В§ R310.2.3 + Cal. Residential Code + 2020 NY State Residential Code); **IRC R310 note pins four dimensions**; **IRC R310 note pins window well requirements**; **IRC R310 note pins state adoption** (ALL FOUR); **default no violations**; **default citation pins local code**; **two regimes routed correctly**; **IRC R310 grade floor uniquely lower threshold invariant** (5.0 grade vs 5.7 standard); **IRC R310 well required only below grade invariant**; **IRC R310 ladder required only when well depth over 44 invariant**; **IRC R310 habitability breach engages with any violation**.

`traderview-expense::rental_attached_garage_carbon_monoxide_disclosure` is the **rental attached-garage carbon monoxide (CO) detector disclosure framework module** (iter 523) вҖ” covers landlord obligations under state CO detector laws when rental unit shares wall + floor + or ceiling with attached garage (vehicle-exhaust CO migration pathway) regardless of presence of gas appliances within the unit. Distinct from siblings `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (gas-line leak protocol вҖ” distinct from CO detection), `rental_pellet_stove_disclosure` (iter 499 вҖ” solid-fuel appliance with separate CO concern), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (chimney-vented appliance CO), `rental_oil_tank_replacement_disclosure` (iter 493 вҖ” oil-heat fuel-storage framework), `rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision` (iter 501 вҖ” gas dryer CO concern subset), `rental_radiator_steam_heat_safety` (iter 517 вҖ” distinct steam-heat framework), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when unit unsafe pending detector installation or remediation), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for CO-poisoning psychological injury). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CDC reports approximately 450 US deaths annually from non-fire carbon monoxide poisoning plus 20,000 nonfatal injuries** вҖ” CO is the LEADING cause of poison-related death in the US; (2) **California SB 183 (Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Prevention Act of 2010)** mandates CO alarms in all rental units with gas appliances + fossil-fuel-burning appliances + fireplaces + OR ATTACHED GARAGES вҖ” attached garage triggers mandate regardless of in-unit gas appliance presence; (3) **UL 2034 Standard for Single and Multiple Station Carbon Monoxide Alarms** required listing for state-compliant CO detector; alarm must report before CO reaches levels causing loss of ability to react; (4) **Washington RCW 19.27.530** required CO alarms in all newly constructed residential buildings as of January 1, 2011 + all other residential occupancies by January 1, 2013; (5) **NY Amanda's Law (NY Public Health Law В§ 1399-bbb-1)** named after Amanda Hansen who died age 16 from CO poisoning at sleepover вҖ” requires UL 2034-listed CO detector adjacent to bedroom in all residential structures with attached garage or fossil-fuel-burning appliance, effective February 22, 2010; (6) **MA Nicole's Law (M.G.L. ch. 148 В§ 26F)** named after Nicole Garofalo who died age 7 from CO poisoning, effective March 31, 2006; (7) attached-garage CO migration to unit can reach lethal 400 ppm within 5 minutes of vehicle idling per CDC field studies; (8) HPD penalties + tenant-injury litigation routinely exceeds $1M-$5M for permanent neurological injury or wrongful death.

**Four CO exposure risks**: AttachedGarageSharedBoundary (vehicle-exhaust CO migration pathway), DetachedGarageWithSharedHvacReturn (breezeway + shared HVAC return path), InUnitFossilFuelAppliance (gas/oil/wood appliance), MinimalNoFossilFuelOrGarage (no exposure pathway).

**Four detector statuses**: UL2034InstalledAndCurrent, InstalledButPastEndOfLifeWindow (7-year UL 2034 manufacturer-listed end-of-life), InstalledButNotUL2034Listed, NotInstalledViolation.

**Six-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. SB 183 Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Prevention Act of 2010 + Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17926-17926.2 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.13) vs New York (NY Public Health Law В§ 1399-bbb-1 Amanda's Law + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2046.2 + HPD Local Law 7 of 2004 + 1 RCNY В§ 12-12 + $25 per detector per day HPD penalty) vs Washington (RCW 19.27.530 effective January 1, 2011 new construction / January 1, 2013 all residential + WA DOH) vs Connecticut (C.G.S. В§ 29-292(b) + В§ 47a-7 + CT State Fire Marshal Office) vs Massachusetts (M.G.L. ch. 148 В§ 26F Nicole's Law effective March 31, 2006 + 527 CMR 1.00 Comprehensive Fire Safety Code) vs Default (CDC public-health surveillance + UL 2034 federal standard + 30+ state mandates).

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantUL2034InstalledAndDisclosed, DetectorNotInstalledStatutoryViolation (100% rent at risk), DetectorPastEndOfLifeReplacementRequired (50% rent at risk), DetectorNotUL2034ListedNonCompliant (50% rent at risk), DisclosureRequiredAtLeaseSigning (50% rent at risk), CoExposureInjuryHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk + $1M-$5M settlement exposure + 400 ppm lethal within 5 minutes of vehicle idling per CDC).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-attached-garage-carbon-monoxide-disclosure`. Twenty-five tests pin: **no exposure risk not applicable**; **compliant UL 2034 with disclosure** (CDC public-health surveillance cross-reference); **detector not installed statutory violation full rent at risk** (CA SB 183 + Amanda's Law); **detector past end-of-life replacement required** (10-year sealed-battery recommendation); **years since manufacture over 7 triggers replacement** (boundary); **detector not UL 2034 listed non-compliant**; **missing disclosure at lease signing** (Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17926.1); **CO injury habitability breach full rent at risk** (400 ppm + $1M-$5M + carboxyhemoglobin); **CA jurisdiction pins Senate Bill 183 + В§ 17926 + January 1, 2011**; **NY jurisdiction pins Amanda's Law + Amanda Hansen + В§ 1399-bbb-1 + 2010-02-22**; **WA jurisdiction pins RCW 19.27.530 + 2013-01-01**; **CT jurisdiction pins C.G.S. В§ 29-292(b) + В§ 47a-7**; **MA jurisdiction pins Nicole's Law + M.G.L. ch. 148 В§ 26F + Nicole Garofalo + March 31, 2006**; **default jurisdiction pins CDC + UL 2034 + (30+) state mandates**; **coordination note references rental_natural_gas_leak_response + rental_oil_tank_replacement_disclosure + rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision + rental_radiator_steam_heat_safety siblings**; **CDC annual CO deaths pins 450**; **CDC annual CO injuries pins 20,000**; **UL 2034 end-of-life pins 7 years**; **CA SB 183 effective year pins 2011**; **NY Amanda's Law effective pins 2010-02-22**; **WA RCW residential deadline pins 2013-01-01**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (6-cell sweep); **severity priority injury overrides compliant status**; **detached garage with shared HVAC triggers disclosure**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic**.

`traderview-expense::rental_application_denial_disclosure` is the **rental application denial reason written disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when a trader-landlord rejects a tenant applicant, what statutory written-disclosure obligations attach? Trader-landlord operational concern: incomplete or untimely denial-reason disclosure exposes landlord to statutory damages + applicant claim for discriminatory denial + reputational risk. Distinct from siblings `adverse_action_notice` (FCRA-only consumer-report denial notice), `fair_chance_housing` (criminal-background screening framework), `application_fees` (caps on application fee itself), and `credit_check_authorization` (pre-screening consent).

**Four regimes** вҖ” three jurisdictions with state-statutory protection vs federal FCRA baseline:

| Regime | Authority | Pre-fee disclosure | Individualized assessment | Federal FCRA only |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|----------------------------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1950.6 + 1786.40 (ICRAA) | No | No | Statutory protection engaged |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq. (NJ Fair Chance in Housing Act) | **YES** (criminal history) | **YES** | Statutory protection engaged |
| New York City | NYC Local Law 24 of 2023 (Fair Chance for Housing Law eff. Jan 1, 2025) + NYC FARE Act (eff. June 11, 2025) | No | **YES** | Statutory protection engaged |
| Default | 15 USC В§ 1681m (FCRA В§ 615(a)) | No | No | Federal baseline only |

**California В§ 1950.6 + В§ 1786.40 ICRAA** вҖ” written notice required when credit score or history was reason for denial; specific written reason for denial required. Pinned by `ca_credit_denial_with_notice_compliant`, `ca_credit_denial_without_written_notice_violates`, `ca_no_specific_reason_violates`, `ca_citation_pins_authorities` (В§В§ 1950.6 + 1786.40 ICRAA), and `ca_note_pins_icraa` (Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act).

**New Jersey Fair Chance in Housing Act (N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq.)** вҖ” uniquely requires PRE-FEE written disclosure of criminal-history consideration before accepting application fee + written statement allowing applicants to provide rehabilitation evidence + individualized assessment for criminal-background-based denials. Pinned by `nj_criminal_denial_compliant`, `nj_no_pre_fee_disclosure_violates`, `nj_no_rehabilitation_right_disclosed_violates`, `nj_no_individualized_assessment_violates`, `nj_non_criminal_basis_no_pre_fee_required` (non-criminal denials don't trigger pre-fee requirement), `nj_citation_pins_fcha`, `nj_note_pins_before_accepting_fee`, `nj_uniquely_requires_pre_fee_disclosure_invariant` (4-regime sweep: only NJ requires pre-fee), and `denial_basis_truth_table_nj` (5-cell sweep: only CriminalBackground triggers pre-fee).

**NYC Local Law 24 of 2023 + FARE Act** вҖ” conditional offer + lookback + individualized assessment framework for criminal-history denials; written notice with specific reason and appeal rights required (effective January 1, 2025). NYC FARE Act prohibits broker fees from being charged to tenants (effective June 11, 2025); landlords/agents must disclose other fees in listings and rental agreements. Pinned by `nyc_criminal_denial_compliant`, `nyc_no_individualized_assessment_violates`, `nyc_no_rehabilitation_evidence_violates`, `nyc_no_written_notice_violates`, `nyc_citation_pins_local_law_24_and_fare_act`, and `nyc_note_pins_january_1_2025_and_june_11_2025`.

**Default federal FCRA baseline (15 USC В§ 1681m)** вҖ” adverse action notice required when consumer report was basis for denial; must include CRA contact info + dispute right + free-copy right + statement that landlord made decision. Less stringent than CA / NJ / NYC. Pinned by `default_credit_denial_with_cra_notice_compliant`, `default_no_cra_contact_violates_fcra`, `default_citation_pins_fcra_baseline`, `default_note_pins_fcra_less_stringent`, and `statutory_protection_engaged_for_ca_nj_nyc_only_invariant` (CA + NJ + NYC engage statutory protection; Default does not).

**NJ + NYC uniquely require individualized assessment invariant** вҖ” both NJ FCHA and NYC FCHA require individualized assessment for criminal-history-based denials (blanket criminal-record bar unlawful in both). CA + Default do NOT impose this requirement. Pinned by `nj_and_nyc_uniquely_require_individualized_assessment_invariant`.

`traderview-expense::rental_bed_bug_disclosure` is the **rental property bed bug infestation pre-lease disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord disclose bed bug infestation history to prospective tenants before lease signing? Trader-landlord operational concern: missed bed bug disclosure exposes landlord to statutory damages + applicant constructive eviction claim + reputational risk in competitive multifamily markets. Distinct from siblings `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA-style adverse action), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (fire safety), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural).

**Four regimes** вҖ” California AB 551 vs NYC Multiple Dwelling Law vs Arizona tenant-request vs Maine strictest single-unit rule:

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.603 (AB 551 of 2015, effective January 1, 2017 new tenants / July 1, 2017 existing tenants)** вҖ” written notice to prospective tenants BEFORE initiating new tenancy in at least 10-point type describing bed bug appearance, behavior, lifecycle, infestation indicators; landlord may NOT show, rent, or lease vacant unit known to have current infestation (В§ 1954.603(b)); 2-business-day post-inspection notification to tenant (В§ 1954.603(c)).

**New York City вҖ” NYC Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 27-2018.1** вҖ” annual building-wide bed bug report (NYC Form RA-89) filed with HPD between December 1 and December 31 each year; prior year's filing provided to every new tenant before lease signing; $250 civil penalty for failure to file annual report; tenant remedies include rent abatement and constructive eviction.

**Arizona вҖ” A.R.S. В§ 33-1319** вҖ” tenant-request-only ADHS educational materials disclosure; no proactive pre-lease disclosure required; landlord may not place tenant in unit known to be currently infested; least stringent regime among comparators.

**Maine вҖ” 14 M.R.S. В§ 6021-A (strictest single-unit rule)** вҖ” pre-rental written disclosure of any bed bug infestation in unit OR adjacent unit within prior 12 months; landlord must inspect within 5 days of tenant written notice of suspected infestation; 24-hour written disclosure of inspection results; treatment at landlord's expense unless tenant caused infestation; $250 to $1,500 per-violation civil penalty.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-bed-bug-disclosure`. Thirty-five tests pin: **CA clean disclosure compliant**; **CA no pre-lease disclosure violation** (В§ 1954.603(a)); **CA missing educational content violation** (10-point type); **CA renting known infested unit violation** (В§ 1954.603(b)); **CA known infested not rented no violation**; **NYC clean disclosure compliant**; **NYC missing annual HPD filing violation** (В§ 27-2018.1 Form RA-89); **NYC missing prior year disclosure violation**; **AZ no tenant request no disclosure required**; **AZ tenant requested disclosure required** (ADHS materials); **AZ tenant requested disclosure provided compliant**; **AZ renting known infested violation**; **ME clean disclosure compliant**; **ME prior 12-month infestation without disclosure violation** (В§ 6021-A(2)); **ME inspection within 5 days compliant** (boundary); **ME inspection at 6 days violation**; **ME 24-hour disclosure compliant** (boundary); **ME 25-hour disclosure violation**; **ME penalty at minimum in range** ($250); **ME penalty at maximum in range** ($1,500); **ME penalty below minimum out of range violation**; **ME penalty above maximum out of range violation**; **citation pins CA / NYC / AZ / ME authorities** (all four); **NYC strictest annual report invariant** (only NYC requires annual HPD filing); **AZ least stringent no pre-lease default** (only AZ skips); **ME strictest inspection and disclosure dual deadlines invariant** (5-day + 24-hour stack); **known infestation truth table all regimes** (4-regime sweep вҖ” all four prohibit renting known infested); **note pins CA AB 551 effective dates** (January 1, 2017); **note pins NYC December filing window**; **note pins AZ tenant-request-only model**; **note pins ME civil penalty range**; **defensive Maine penalty zero treated as compliant**.

`traderview-expense::rental_short_term_subletting_airbnb_restriction` is the **rental short-term subletting (Airbnb / VRBO / Booking.com) restriction framework module** (iter 513) вҖ” covers tenant subletting on STR platforms WITHOUT landlord consent (breach of lease assignment clause), tenant operation of STR violating municipal STR registration, primary-residence host requirement (NYC LL 18 + LA + Boston), annual night caps (LA 120 / SF non-hosted 90), and Room Occupancy Excise Tax collection. Distinct from siblings `rental_lease_guarantor_disclosure` (lease cosigner separate framework), `tenant_voting_address_protection` (iter 505 ACP confidentiality during STR enforcement), `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (STR-installed smart locks separate framework), `rental_storage_unit_lease_disclosure` (iter 509 storage lease pattern), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim parallel framework), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when primary-resident host displaces self during STR rental window). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **NYC Local Law 18 of 2021** (effective September 5, 2023) requires Mayor's Office of Special Enforcement (OSE) registration plus $145 non-refundable registration fee plus PRIMARY-RESIDENCE host present plus < 30 days rental вҖ” Class A multifamily entire-unit STR effectively banned; STR listings in NYC plunged > 90% post-effective date per Brick Underground tracking; (2) **Los Angeles Home-Sharing Ordinance** (LAMC В§ 12.22 A.32) requires primary-residence host + 120-night annual cap + landlord written approval for renters; (3) **San Francisco Office of Short-Term Rentals** (SF Admin Code Ch. 41A) requires $925 (2025) registration fee + dual registration + 90-night non-hosted annual cap; (4) **Boston Short-Term Rental Ordinance** (Boston Ord. В§ 9-14 + Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 64G) three categories all requiring permanent residency + 5.7% state Room Occupancy Excise + 6% Boston locally-set additional = 11.7% effective rate + Cape Cod and Islands additional 2.75% Wastewater + 3% Community Impact.

**Three lease term types**: ExplicitNoSublettingAirbnbClause, SublettingRequiresLandlordConsent, LeaseSilentDefaultStateRule.

**Four STR statuses**: OperatingWithoutLandlordConsent (breach risk), OperatingWithWrittenLandlordConsent, NotOperatingStr, PrimaryResidenceHostPresentLimitedShare (NYC LL 18 compliant).

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” NYC (LL 18 of 2021 + NY RPL В§ 226-b + 6 RCNY В§ 1-04 + OSE + $145 registration + primary residence + < 30 days + Class A vs B classification per NYC Admin Code В§ 27-265) vs LA (LAMC В§ 12.22 A.32 Home-Sharing Ordinance + 120-night annual cap + LA County Code В§ 4.72 TOT + Extended Home-Sharing permit for compliant hosts) vs SF (SF Admin Code Ch. 41A + Office of Short-Term Rentals + $925 (2025) registration + 90-night non-hosted cap + Treasurer-Tax Collector dual registration + SF Rent Ordinance В§ 37 + Costa-Hawkins Rental Housing Act) vs Boston (Boston Ord. В§ 9-14 + Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 64G Room Occupancy Excise 5.7% state + Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 64G В§ 3A Boston 6% additional + Cape Cod Water Protection Fund 2.75% + Community Impact 3% + three categories limited share / home share / owner-adjacent) vs Default (common-law lease assignment + state-specific TOT framework).

`traderview-expense::rental_balcony_inspection_seismic_safety` is the **rental balcony plus Exterior Elevated Element (EEE) inspection compliance framework module** (iter 511) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating a 3+ unit multifamily building with weather-exposed wood-framed balconies, decks, walkways, stairs, or landings obtain mandatory periodic inspection under California SB 721 (rental) plus SB 326 (HOA / condominium)? Distinct from siblings `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (basement water вҖ” water-damage cause of wood EEE deterioration cross-reference), `rental_window_guard_installation` (parallel fall-protection framework), `tenant_window_air_conditioner_install_right` (window-frame safety analog), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when tenant must vacate during major EEE repair), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for post-collapse psychological injury), `rental_storage_unit_lease_disclosure` (iter 509 storage area may include exterior wood-framed deck subject to inspection). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **SB 721 first-cycle inspection deadline January 1, 2026** (post-AB 2579 one-year extension from original January 1, 2025 deadline per Pub. L. 202 of 2024) вҖ” any covered building that has NOT obtained inspection by deadline faces local-code-enforcement civil penalties of $100-$500 per day until cured plus civil liability exposure on EEE-failure injury claims; (2) inspector must be qualified per Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17973(d): licensed architect, licensed civil/structural engineer, building contractor with A/B/C-5 license + 5 years experience, or certified building inspector; (3) minimum 15% of each EEE type must be inspected per В§ 17973(c) (40 balconies вҶ’ minimum 6); (4) post-2026 SB 721 subsequent cycle every 6 years, SB 326 cycle every 9 years; (5) SB 326 EEEs must be inspected by licensed structural engineer SPECIFICALLY per Cal. Civ. Code В§ 5551 вҖ” architect plus contractor inspections not sufficient; (6) "immediate threat" identification triggers 120-day repair window plus permitting; (7) Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942.4 tenantability claim attaches if EEE is unsafe and tenant is exposed.

**Legislative context**: Berkeley balcony collapse June 16, 2015 killed six students at Library Gardens apartments; legislative response in SB 721 (signed September 17, 2018, effective January 1, 2019) plus SB 326 (signed August 30, 2019). AB 2579 (signed 2024) extended the SB 721 first-cycle deadline from January 1, 2025 to January 1, 2026.

**Five building types**: Multifamily3PlusRentalSb721 (SB 721 applies), HoaCondominiumSb326 (SB 326 applies вҖ” structural engineer required), SingleFamilyOrDuplex (outside SB 721/326 scope вҖ” common-law habitability), NoWoodFramedEees (concrete/steel construction вҖ” out of scope), NycSixPlusStoryFisp (NYC Local Law 11 faГ§ade regime instead).

**Five inspector qualifications**: LicensedArchitect (SB 721 qualified only), LicensedCivilOrStructuralEngineer (SB 721 + SB 326 qualified вҖ” SB 326 REQUIRES structural engineer), ContractorWithABorC5LicenseAndFiveYears (SB 721 qualified only вҖ” A, B, or C-5 license plus minimum 5 years experience), CertifiedBuildingInspector (SB 721 qualified only), NotQualified.

**Three-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17973 SB 721 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 5551 SB 326 + AB 2579 + В§ 1942.4 tenantability) vs New York City FISP (NYC Local Law 11 of 1998 + NYC Administrative Code В§ 28-302.1 + RCNY 1 В§ 103-04 вҖ” 5-year faГ§ade inspection cycle for 6+ story buildings) vs Default (common-law habitability + premises-liability tort + Florida and Hawaii pending legislation post-2021 Champlain Towers South Surfside collapse killing 98).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantWithFirstCycleInspection, FirstCycleInspectionPastDeadlineViolation (100% rent at risk + $100-$500/day penalty), InspectorNotQualified (50% rent at risk), InspectionSampleBelow15Pct (50% rent at risk), ImmediateThreatRepairOverdue120Day (100% rent at risk), HoaSb326UsedNonStructuralEngineerInvalid (100% rent at risk вҖ” В§ 5551 violation), TenantOccupiedUnsafeEeeHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk вҖ” В§ 1942.4).

`traderview-expense::rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` is the **rental property basement water intrusion / mold disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord disclose water intrusion, flood history, visible mold, and remediation history to prospective and existing tenants? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed water intrusion creates breach of warranty of habitability + constructive eviction + per-tenant statutory damages + multi-tenant class action exposure when mold-related health claims arise. Distinct from siblings `mold_disclosure` (general mold framework), `flood_disclosure` (flood zone), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums).

**Four regimes** вҖ” Maryland (Tenant Mold Protection Act) vs Virginia (move-in inspection) vs New York (PCDS + NYC LL55) vs Default:

**Maryland вҖ” Maryland Tenant Mold Protection Act of 2024 (eff. July 1, 2025) + MD Real Property В§ 8-211 + В§ 8-211.1** вҖ” landlord must disclose **visible mold + water intrusion + documented remediation history in writing BEFORE move-in**; **mold information pamphlet** required at lease signing; **15-day mold assessment deadline** from receipt of tenant written notice; **45-day remediation completion deadline** from assessment; breach of warranty of habitability + tenant rescission right + statutory damages for violations.

**Virginia вҖ” Va. Code В§ 55.1-1220 + В§ 8.01-226.12 + В§ 55.1-1216** вҖ” landlord must provide **itemized move-in inspection report within 5 days** of move-in disclosing **visible mold** in readily accessible interior areas; В§ 55.1-1220 requires landlord to take steps to **prevent moisture accumulation and mold growth**; if report discloses mold, tenant may terminate OR stay вҖ” if stay, landlord must **remediate within 5 days** of tenant decision.

**New York вҖ” Property Condition Disclosure Act + NY GOL В§ 5-905 + NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2017** вҖ” Property Condition Disclosure Act requires past/present mold problem disclosure on PCDS; **natural flood event history** required under NY GOL В§ 5-905 (plumbing/mechanical floods NOT voluntarily required); NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 вҖ” buildings with **more than one unit** must keep dwellings free of allergens including mold; **annual inspection required**; indoor allergen hazard reduction protocols under В§ 27-2017.

**Default вҖ” common-law implied warranty of habitability + EPA Mold Remediation Guidance + CDC Stachybotrys + Fair Housing Act + ADA** вҖ” no specific statewide water intrusion / mold disclosure mandate; general implied warranty of habitability covers uninhabitable conditions; federal Fair Housing Act + ADA may apply for tenants with mold-induced respiratory conditions; EPA 402-K-01-001 mold remediation best practices.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-basement-water-intrusion-disclosure`. Thirty-four tests pin: **MD clean compliant**; **MD no pre-move-in disclosure violation** (Mold Protection Act); **MD no mold pamphlet violation**; **MD assessment at 15-day boundary compliant**; **MD assessment at 16 days without completion violation**; **MD remediation at 45-day boundary compliant**; **MD remediation at 46 days without completion violation**; **VA clean compliant**; **VA no move-in report violation** (В§ 55.1-1216 5-day); **VA mold present but not disclosed violation** (В§ 8.01-226.12); **VA mold present disclosed compliant**; **VA tenant stay election 5-day remediation compliant**; **VA tenant stay election 6-day remediation violation**; **NY clean compliant**; **NY no PCDS mold disclosure violation** (Property Condition Disclosure Act); **NY no flood history disclosure violation** (В§ 5-905); **NYC LL55 multi-unit no annual inspection violation**; **NYC LL55 single-unit no annual inspection no violation** (out of scope); **default no violations**; **citation pins MD / VA / NY / default authorities** (all four); **note pins MD July 2025 effective date**; **note pins MD 15-day + 45-day deadlines**; **note pins VA В§ 55.1-1216 5-day move-in report**; **note pins VA 5-day remediation after tenant stay**; **note pins NY natural-vs-mechanical flood distinction**; **note pins NYC LL55 multi-unit annual inspection**; **note pins default EPA Mold Remediation Guidance** (EPA 402-K-01-001); **MD uniquely requires mold pamphlet invariant**; **VA 5-day remediation uniquely shorter than MD 45 invariant**; **NYC LL55 uniquely requires annual inspection invariant**; **multiple MD violations stack** (3+ violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` is the **rental property carbon monoxide (CO) detector compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord install, maintain, and certify CO alarms in dwelling units with fossil-fuel-burning appliances or attached garages? Trader-landlord operational concern: missing or non-functional CO alarms create criminal-misdemeanor exposure (IL Class B), civil penalties (CA $100/violation), and wrongful-death liability when CO poisoning occurs. Distinct from siblings `rental_bed_bug_disclosure` (lease disclosure), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (fire safety), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification).

**Four regimes** вҖ” California SB 183 vs New York Amanda's Law vs Illinois CO Alarm Detector Act vs Massachusetts Nicole's Law (strictest):

**California вҖ” SB 183 of 2010 (Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 13260-13263, Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Prevention Act)** вҖ” CO alarm required in all dwelling units intended for human occupancy with fossil-fuel-burning appliance OR attached garage; **State Fire Marshal-certified device required**; installed outside each sleeping area AND on every level including basements; compliance deadlines вҖ” single-family fossil-fuel/garage by July 1, 2011; multifamily by January 1, 2013; $100/violation statutory damages under В§ 13263.

**New York вҖ” Amanda's Law (NY Exec. Law В§ 378(5-a), effective February 22, 2010)** вҖ” named for Amanda Hansen who died from CO poisoning at a 2009 sleepover; CO alarm required in every one-and-two-family dwelling and multiple dwelling where carbon-monoxide source is present; **alarm within 15 feet of each sleeping area entrance**; UL 2034 listed.

**Illinois вҖ” Carbon Monoxide Alarm Detector Act (430 ILCS 135/1 et seq., effective January 1, 2007)** вҖ” CO detector within 15 feet of every sleeping room in dwelling unit with fossil-fuel-burning appliance OR attached garage; **failure to install is a Class B misdemeanor** (430 ILCS 135/15); UL 2034 listed.

**Massachusetts вҖ” Nicole's Law (M.G.L. c. 148 В§ 26FВҪ, effective March 31, 2006)** вҖ” strictest among comparators; named for Nicole Garofalo who died of CO poisoning in 2005; **interconnected (hardwired or wireless) CO alarms required on every level**; within 10 feet of each bedroom door (stricter than NY/IL 15-foot rule); **certificate of compliance from local fire department REQUIRED BEFORE selling or renting** the dwelling; UL 2034 listed.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-carbon-monoxide-detector`. Thirty-five tests pin: **CA clean compliant**; **CA no alarm with CO source violation** (SB 183); **CA no CO source no alarm compliant**; **CA missing State Fire Marshal certification violation** (В§ 13261); **CA not on every level violation** (В§ 13262); **NY clean compliant**; **NY no alarm with CO source violation** (Amanda's Law В§ 378(5-a)); **NY not UL 2034 listed violation**; **NY 15-foot boundary compliant**; **NY 16-foot distance violation**; **IL clean compliant**; **IL no alarm Class B misdemeanor violation** (430 ILCS 135/15); **IL 15-foot boundary compliant**; **IL 16-foot distance violation**; **MA clean compliant**; **MA battery-only not interconnected violation**; **MA hardwired-not-interconnected violation**; **MA interconnected wireless compliant**; **MA 10-foot boundary compliant**; **MA 11-foot violation**; **MA missing fire department certificate violation**; **MA strictest 10-foot distance invariant** (11 ft NY ok, 11 ft MA fails); **MA uniquely requires interconnection invariant** (CA battery ok, MA battery fails); **MA uniquely requires fire department certificate invariant**; **citation pins CA / NY / IL / MA authorities** (all four); **note pins CA July 2011 + January 2013 deadlines**; **note pins NY Amanda Hansen origin**; **note pins IL Class B misdemeanor**; **note pins MA Nicole Garofalo origin**; **no CO source no violations all regimes** (4-regime sweep); **power source truth table for MA** (4-cell вҖ” only interconnected variants pass); **multiple MA violations stack** (4+ violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_pellet_stove_disclosure` is the **rental pellet-stove EPA NSPS Step-2 certification + disclosure compliance framework module** (iter 499) вҖ” when a trader-landlord rents a property with a pellet-fuel residential heating appliance, what federal EPA NSPS certification tier applies, what state non-attainment-area restrictions apply, what CO detector + chimney-inspection + owner's-manual + auger-jam-risk disclosure is required at lease signing? Distinct from sibling `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (broader chimney + fireplace + solid-fuel framework вҖ” coordination cross-reference), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (CO detector framework cross-reference), `rental_oil_tank_replacement_disclosure` (legacy heating-appliance disclosure pattern), `rental_propane_tank_lease_disclosure` (alternative heating fuel). Trader-landlord operational concern: pellet stoves under EPA NSPS 40 C.F.R. Part 60 Subpart AAA (wood stoves) plus Subpart QQQQ (hydronic heaters + forced-air furnaces) must meet Step-2 emissions limit of 2.0 g/hr PM effective May 15, 2020 per EPA Method 28 crib-wood protocol; auger jam is a frequent failure mode in residential pellet appliances causing incomplete combustion + CO release into living space.

**Five EPA certification tiers**: NoPelletStove, Pre1988NonCertified, Phase2_1988To2015Certified, Step1_2015To2020Certified, Step2_2020OrLaterCertified.

**Six-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” Vermont (10 V.S.A. В§ 583 + Vermont Air Pollution Control Regulations В§ 5-204.4 + 9 V.S.A. В§ 2882 CO detector law) vs Maine (38 M.R.S. В§ 581 + Maine Solid Fuel-Burning Appliance Regulations + 25 M.R.S. В§ 2468-A CO + NFPA 211) vs New Hampshire (RSA 153:4-a Solid Fuel-Burning Appliance Installation + Saf-C 6000 fire chief permit + RSA 153:10-a CO + NFPA 211) vs Washington (WAC 173-433 Solid Fuel Burning Device Standards вҖ” strictest in US prohibiting sale of non-certified stoves in PM2.5 NAAQS non-attainment counties [Pierce, Spokane, Yakima, Klickitat] + WAC 173-433-130 landlord disclosure + RCW 70A.15) vs Colorado (5 CCR 1001-10 Regulation No. 4 Statewide Wood Stove Program + designated-area burn ban Aspen / Telluride / Vail / Crested Butte / Steamboat Springs + APCD Reg No. 4) vs Default (federal 40 C.F.R. Part 60 Subpart AAA + Subpart QQQQ + NFPA 211 + state common-law habitability).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (no stove), CompliantStep2WithFullDisclosure, DisclosureRequiredAtLeaseSigning (50% rent at risk), CoDetectorMissingViolation (100% rent at risk вҖ” universal state-fire-marshal violation), ChimneyAnnualInspectionOverdueViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” NFPA 211 12-month interval + fire-insurance coverage void), NonCertifiedInNonAttainmentAreaProhibited (100% rent at risk вҖ” WAC 173-433-130 / 5 CCR 1001-10 prohibition), OwnersManualNotProvidedViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” 40 C.F.R. Appendix I to Part 60 retention requirement), AugerJamCoReleaseExposureWithoutDisclosure (50% rent at risk вҖ” symptom-recognition + evacuation protocol).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-pellet-stove-disclosure`. Twenty-six tests pin: **no pellet stove not applicable**; **compliant Step-2 full disclosure with PFI Standards Program note**; **non-certified in non-attainment prohibited** (full rent at risk + WAC 173-433-130); **non-certified outside non-attainment falls through compliant**; **CO detector missing full rent at risk** (9 V.S.A. В§ 2882 + UL 2034-listed citation); **chimney inspection overdue half rent at risk** (NFPA 211 + CSIA + fire-insurance void); **owner's manual not provided half rent at risk** (40 C.F.R. Appendix I to Part 60 + PFI Standards Program); **auger jam CO risk undisclosed half rent at risk**; **disclosure missing at lease signing half rent at risk**; **disclosure missing operation responsibility half rent at risk**; **VT jurisdiction pins В§ 2882 CO detector + В§ 583 + В§ 5-204.4**; **ME jurisdiction pins 38 M.R.S. В§ 581 + 25 M.R.S. В§ 2468-A + NFPA 211**; **NH jurisdiction pins RSA 153:4-a + Saf-C 6000 + RSA 153:10-a**; **WA jurisdiction pins WAC 173-433 + PM2.5 NAAQS + Pierce + Spokane non-attainment counties**; **CO jurisdiction pins 5 CCR 1001-10 + Aspen + APCD**; **default jurisdiction pins federal NSPS Subparts AAA + QQQQ + May 15, 2020 + 2.0 g/hr Step-2 limit**; **coordination note references chimney inspection + gas leak + oil tank + propane tank siblings**; **coordination note pinned in all 6 jurisdictions**; **EPA Step-2 PM limit constant pins 2 g/hr**; **EPA Step-2 effective date constant pins 2020-05-15**; **NFPA 211 inspection interval pins 12 months**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (6-cell sweep); **severity priority CO detector overrides chimney**; **severity priority non-attainment overrides CO detector**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic** (boundary); **Phase-2 1988-to-2015 certified compliant with disclosure**.

`traderview-expense::rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property CHIMNEY + FIREPLACE + SOLID-FUEL-BURNING APPLIANCE inspection and disclosure compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property with a wood-burning fireplace, pellet stove, wood stove, vented gas fireplace, or chimney-vented oil furnace, what inspection schedule applies, what disclosure must be given to tenant, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a chimney fire or carbon monoxide event? Distinct from siblings `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO alarm), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water damage from chimney crown failure), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification policy), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (different appliance), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED parallel), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) chimney fires cause approximately 22,000 residential structure fires annually in the US per NFPA, resulting in $150M+ in property damage; (2) carbon monoxide events linked to fireplace/chimney malfunction kill 50-150 Americans annually with thousands of non-fatal poisonings; (3) inherited rural property with neglected chimney is among the highest-stakes habitability risks in rental portfolios; (4) Maine landlord faces statutory disclosure obligation at lease execution; (5) chimney inspection costs $150-$500 per Level II inspection but mitigates wrongful-death litigation exposure that can exceed $5M.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **Maine (MOST STRINGENT for rental disclosure)** | State of Maine Professional Financial Regulation Form 2079 chimney/fireplace construction disclosure; 14 M.R.S. В§ 6021 implied warranty of habitation | Form 2079 disclosure required at sale/rental + **Level II NFPA 211 inspection at title transfer** per state fire code adopting NFPA 211 by reference |
| **Connecticut** | Conn. Gen. Stat. В§ 29-292 (State Fire Safety Code adopting NFPA 211 by reference); Conn. Public Health Code В§ 19-13-B105(o) combustion-air/venting; Conn. Gen. Stat. В§ 47a-7 landlord duties | Annual NFPA 211 inspection recommended; combustion-air + venting compliance |
| **Default** | NFPA 211 (Standard for Chimneys, Fireplaces, Vents, and Solid Fuel-Burning Appliances); CSIA Chimney Safety Institute of America; Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 | NFPA 211 VOLUNTARY national standard recommending annual inspection enforceable as law only when adopted by local code + CSIA certification + common-law habitability |

**NFPA 211 three-level inspection framework**:
1. **Level I** вҖ” basic visual inspection when no changes to chimney system, fuel type, or appliance; appropriate for routine annual maintenance check
2. **Level II** вҖ” comprehensive accessible inspection including all accessible portions of chimney interior and exterior; required on **OCCUPANCY CHANGE** (rental transition + sale), fuel change, damage event, system modification, or weather event (storm, earthquake, lightning); **Maine mandates at title transfer**
3. **Level III** вҖ” invasive inspection when hazards suggest concealed problem; removal of components permitted; only when Level I or Level II findings indicate concealed defect

**Six appliance types modeled**: `None`, `WoodBurningFireplace`, `PelletStove`, `WoodStove`, `GasFireplaceVented`, `OilFurnaceChimneyVented`.

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Creosote buildup beyond Stage 3** (glazed creosote) вҶ’ Class A chimney fire risk + structural damage + tenant emergency-relocation duty
2. **Cracked flue liner** вҶ’ carbon monoxide release into living space (cross-reference `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector`) + IIED + wrongful death exposure
3. **Damaged crown / brick deterioration / spalling** вҶ’ water intrusion into walls and roof (cross-reference `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure`)
4. **Animal nesting in flue** (birds, raccoons, squirrels) вҶ’ CO + fire risk; landlord ordinary maintenance duty
5. **Improper combustion** (negative-pressure backdraft, blocked vent) вҶ’ CO poisoning + IIED parallel to `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` iter 453

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) Maine trader-landlord rents 1890 farmhouse with brick chimney + wood stove вҖ” Form 2079 disclosure required at lease execution + Level II NFPA 211 inspection at title transfer + 14 M.R.S. В§ 6021 implied warranty exposure; (2) Connecticut trader-landlord with rental cottage and unused fireplace вҖ” annual NFPA 211 inspection recommended even on dormant chimneys (animal nesting + spalling); (3) Vermont trader-landlord ignored cracked flue liner for two seasons вҖ” tenant CO event during winter, wrongful death + IIED + criminal negligence; (4) tenant requests chimney sweep before winter heating season вҖ” CSIA-certified sweep costs $150-$500, well below liability exposure; (5) chimney fire during tenancy вҖ” Level III NFPA 211 invasive inspection required before reuse + structural engineer evaluation; (6) inherited rural property with creosote Stage 3 glazed buildup вҖ” do NOT operate appliance until professional removal.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-chimney-fireplace-inspection-disclosure`. Thirty-three tests pin: **no appliance not applicable**; **Maine compliant baseline**; **inspection overdue over 12 months** (CSIA-certified + 12-month NFPA 211); **inspection exactly 12 months compliant**; **occupancy change requires Level II**; **occupancy change with Level II compliant**; **damage event requires Level II**; **creosote Stage 3 defect-observed severity** (Class A chimney fire + CSIA-certified sweep); **cracked flue cross-references CO module** (rental_carbon_monoxide_detector + reline before reuse); **damaged crown cross-references water intrusion** (rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure + tuckpointing); **chimney fire event top severity** (Level III invasive); **CO event top severity** (wrongful death + tenant_emotional_distress_damages); **Maine Form 2079 missing disclosure severity** (Maine statutory disclosure); **Maine Form 2079 missing in CT not required**; **Maine Form 2079 missing in Default not required**; **CT cites В§ 29-292 + В§ 19-13-B105(o) + В§ 47a-7**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + В§ 1941.1 + CSIA); **severity priority event > defect > inspection > disclosure**; **severity defect > inspection > disclosure**; **severity inspection > disclosure when no defect**; **pellet stove treated as appliance**; **wood stove treated as appliance**; **vented gas fireplace treated as appliance**; **chimney-vented oil furnace treated as appliance**; **citation pins all authorities** (NFPA 211 + Form 2079 + 14 M.R.S. В§ 6021 + В§ 29-292 + В§ 19-13-B105(o) + В§ 47a-7 + В§ 1941.1 + Hilder + 478 A.2d 202 + CSIA); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins NFPA three-level framework** (Level I/II/III + OCCUPANCY CHANGE); **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins NFPA 22K fire data + $150M**; **note pins companion modules**; **Maine uniquely requires Form 2079 invariant**; **3-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **multiple defects stack in actions** (Class A + reline + tuckpointing).

`traderview-expense::rental_emergency_action_plan_high_rise` is the **rental high-rise Emergency Action Plan (EAP) framework module** (iter 527) вҖ” covers NFPA 1 Fire Code + International Fire Code (IFC) В§ 404 + NYC FDNY 3 RCNY В§ 404-01 emergency planning and preparedness requirements for high-rise residential buildings. Distinct from siblings `rental_elevator_safety_inspection` (elevator inspection framework вҖ” coordination with emergency-recall and Phase II firefighter operation), `rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement` (NFPA 10 fire extinguisher framework), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (chimney inspection вҖ” distinct), `rental_grill_propane_bbq_restriction` (iter 515 NFPA 1 В§ 10.10.5 balcony grilling framework), `rental_balcony_inspection_seismic_safety` (iter 511 EEE structural inspection вҖ” balcony as evacuation refuge), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when EAP-noncompliant building requires tenant temporary relocation), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for fire-related psychological injury post-Grenfell + post-9/11 trauma precedents). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **NYC Local Law 26 of 2004** post-9/11 expanded EAP requirements to all high-rise (вүҘ 75 feet OR 7+ stories) office AND residential buildings; (2) NYC FDNY 3 RCNY В§ 404-01 + Administrative Code В§ 404.2 + Fire Code Title 29 require Fire Safety Director (FSD) + Fire Safety Plan (FSP) + Emergency Action Plan (EAP) + annual tenant emergency-instructions distribution; (3) **NFPA 1 В§ 10.8 + В§ 11.10 high-rise framework + IFC В§ 404** require comprehensive EAP including tenant evacuation procedures + FSD on-site for fires plus non-fire emergencies (medical + active shooter + hazardous-material release) + annual fire drill + post-emergency reentry protocol + accessible-evacuation provisions for mobility-impaired tenants; (4) FDNY civil penalties **$1,000-$25,000 per violation per day** under NYC Admin Code В§ 28-202.1; (5) **wrongful-death litigation post-high-rise fire routinely exceeds $5M-$50M per fatality**; (6) NYC FDNY **F-32 certification** required for fire safety director; FSD on-site required 24/7 in many occupancy classes.

**High-rise threshold**: вүҘ 75 feet OR 7+ stories.

**Three building classifications**: HighRiseResidential75FtOr7Stories (full EAP required), MidRiseResidential4To6Stories (limited EAP), LowRiseResidential1To3Stories (EAP framework inapplicable).

**Seven compliance statuses**: AllRequirementsCurrent, FireSafetyDirectorNotPresent, FspEapNotFiledWithAhj, AnnualFireDrillMissed, TenantInstructionsNotDistributed, AccessibleEvacuationMissing (FHA 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + ADA Title II/III + NFPA 101 В§ 7.2.12 Areas of Refuge + IFC В§ 1009 accessible means of egress), EmergencyIncidentDuringNonCompliance.

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” NYC (Local Law 26 of 2004 + FDNY 3 RCNY В§ 404-01 + NYC Admin Code В§ 28-202.1 civil penalty + F-32 FSD certification + 24/7 on-site requirement) vs Chicago (Municipal Code В§ 13-160-070 + Chicago Fire Department + post-2003 Cook County Administration Building fire enhanced requirements) vs Los Angeles (Cal. Fire Code + LAMC В§ 57.4901 + LA County Code Title 32 + LAFD certification) vs IFC Adopted (В§ 404.2.1 Comprehensive fire safety/emergency action plan Level 1 + В§ 404.3 implementation + В§ 404.4 staff training) vs Default (NFPA 1 В§ 10.8 high-rise framework + В§ 11.10 EAP + NFPA 101 Life Safety Code В§ 4.7 + OSHA 29 C.F.R. В§ 1910.38).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantAllRequirements, FireSafetyDirectorPresenceViolation (50% rent at risk + $1,000-$25,000/day FDNY penalty), FspEapFilingDelinquentViolation (50% rent at risk), AnnualDrillMissedViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” NFPA 101 В§ 4.7), TenantInstructionsNotDistributedViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” NFPA 1 В§ 11.10), AccessibleEvacuationFhaAdaViolation (100% rent at risk вҖ” Areas of Refuge + evacuation chairs + tenant disability registry + 2-way communication), PostIncidentNonComplianceFatalityRisk (100% rent at risk + $5M-$50M wrongful-death litigation post-high-rise fire).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-emergency-action-plan-high-rise`. Twenty-four tests pin: **low-rise not applicable**; **compliant all requirements** (Local Law 26 cross-reference); **FSD not present violation half rent at risk** (F-32 + FDNY 3 RCNY В§ 404-01); **NYC FSD without F-32 certification violation**; **FSP/EAP filing delinquent violation** (IFC В§ 404.2.1); **annual fire drill missed violation** (NFPA 101 В§ 4.7); **tenant instructions not distributed violation** (NFPA 1 В§ 11.10); **accessible evacuation missing violation full rent at risk** (NFPA 101 В§ 7.2.12 + В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + Areas of Refuge); **post-incident non-compliance fatality risk full rent at risk** ($5M-$50M wrongful-death); **NYC jurisdiction pins Local Law 26 + F-32 certification + В§ 28-202.1**; **Chicago jurisdiction pins В§ 13-160-070 + Cook County Administration Building fire**; **LA jurisdiction pins LAMC В§ 57.4901 + LAFD**; **IFC jurisdiction pins В§ 404.2.1 + Level 1**; **default jurisdiction pins NFPA 1 В§ 10.8 + NFPA 101 + 29 C.F.R. В§ 1910.38**; **coordination note references rental_elevator_safety_inspection + rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement + rental_balcony_inspection_seismic_safety siblings**; **high-rise threshold pins 75 feet**; **high-rise threshold pins 7 stories**; **FDNY civil penalty pins $1,000-$25,000**; **NYC Local Law 26 year pins 2004**; **FDNY FSD certification pins F-32**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (5-cell sweep); **severity priority incident overrides other violations**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic**; **building below 75 feet below 7 stories not applicable** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` is the **illegal-lockout / self-help-eviction landlord exposure module** (iter 529) вҖ” covers landlord civil + criminal exposure for changing locks without court order, shutting off utilities, removing doors or windows, dumping tenant belongings, or installing bootlocks to force tenant exit. Self-help eviction is prohibited in every US jurisdiction with a residential tenancy code; landlord must use judicial process (writ of possession executed by sheriff or marshal). Distinct from siblings `rental_emergency_action_plan_high_rise` (fire-safety EAP вҖ” different harm vector), `rental_property_registration` (state-law landlord registration вҖ” operational precondition), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (LL 152 вҖ” utility-shutoff prohibition during emergency overlaps but distinct authority), `vehicle_towing_from_rental_property` (parking enforcement, not tenancy exclusion). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA Civ. Code В§ 789.3** assesses $100/day continued penalty with $250 statutory minimum floor + actual damages + attorney fees + potential punitive damages and **CA AG Bulletin 2022-DLE-05** directs law enforcement to treat lockout as criminal trespass / unlawful-detainer interference; (2) **NY RPAPL В§ 853 + RPL В§ 768** triggers treble damages on property lost + cost of alternative accommodation + value of permanently lost tenancy (rent-stabilized / rent-controlled tenancy values often six figures), and **RPL В§ 768 makes unlawful eviction a Class A misdemeanor** with criminal exposure on top of civil; (3) **TX Prop. Code В§ 92.0081** assesses $1,000 statutory penalty + one month rent + actual damages + court costs + attorney fees, with three safe-harbor branches (bona-fide repairs/construction/emergency, abandoned-contents removal, rent-delinquent door lock change with strict procedural compliance per В§ 92.0081(c)-(f): 24/7 key availability + no replacement-key fee + written notice with statutory disclosures); partial procedural compliance is treated as full violation; (4) **WA RCW 59.18.290** assesses greater of actual damages or 3Г— monthly rent + court costs + attorney fees, and tenant may terminate the rental agreement; (5) **FL Stat. В§ 83.67** assesses greater of actual+consequential damages or 3 months' rent + costs + attorney fees and expressly prohibits bootlocks; (6) **IL 735 ILCS 5/9-101 et seq.** Forcible Entry and Detainer Act requires court process with common-law wrongful-eviction baseline, and **Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-160** adds two months' rent or twice damages (greater) + attorney fees for Chicago-located rentals.

**Seven lockout methods**: ChangedLocksWithoutCourtOrder, ShutOffUtilitiesWaterGasElectric, RemovedDoorsOrWindows, DumpedTenantBelongings, InstalledBootlockOrSimilarDevice, BlockedAccessByOtherMeans, NoLockoutCourtProcessUsed.

**Five Texas safe-harbor branches**: NotInvoked, BonaFideRepairsOrEmergency, AbandonedContentsRemoval, RentDelinquentLockChangeStrictProceduralCompliance, RentDelinquentLockChangeProceduralComplianceFailed.

**Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoLockoutLawfulCourtProcess, TexasSafeHarborSatisfiedNoViolation, TexasRentDelinquentLockoutProceduralFailureViolation, CaliforniaCivCode789_3PerDayPenaltyViolation, NewYorkRpapl853TrebleDamagesViolation, TexasPropCode92_0081MinThousandPlusMonthRentViolation, WashingtonRcw59_18_290TripleMonthlyRentViolation, FloridaStat83_67ThreeMonthsRentViolation, IllinoisForcibleEntryDetainerCommonLawWrongfulEvictionViolation, DefaultJurisdictionStateLawSelfHelpEvictionViolation.

`traderview-expense::rental_retaliation_prohibition` is the **landlord retaliation prohibition module** (iter 531) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for evicting, increasing rent, decreasing services, refusing to renew, or harassing tenant within statutory presumption window after tenant exercised a protected right (habitability complaint to landlord or government agency, joining tenants' association, exercising repair-and-deduct or rent-withhold, filing fair-housing complaint, commencing legal proceeding). Direct sibling to `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (iter 529 вҖ” self-help-eviction parallel-claim exposure), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (pre-tenancy fair-housing screening protections), `rental_property_registration` (state-law landlord operational precondition). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA Civ. Code В§ 1942.5** creates a 180-day rebuttable presumption + $100-$2,000 per retaliatory act punitive damages on showing fraud/oppression/malice + actual damages + attorney fees, with Ellis Act withdrawal from rental market one of few documented legitimate-business-reason rebuttals; (2) **NY RPL В§ 223-b** creates 1-year presumption for unregulated units and EXPANDED 3-year (1,095-day) presumption for rent-stabilized / rent-controlled units, with permanently lost stabilized tenancy values often six figures; (3) **WA RCW 59.18.240 + В§ 59.18.250** creates the shortest 90-day presumption window of any surveyed state but pairs it with RCW 59.18.060 remedies (greater of actual damages or 3Г— monthly rent) + attorney fees; (4) **TX Prop. Code В§В§ 92.331-92.335** creates 6-month (180-day) presumption + $500 statutory civil penalty + one month's rent + actual damages + court costs + attorney fees; (5) **FL Stat. В§ 83.64** prohibits retaliatory conduct without codifying a presumption window вҖ” common-law reasonable-temporal-proximity analysis applies, with Florida courts applying 90-180-day heuristic; (6) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-150** creates 1-year presumption + GREATER OF two months' rent or twice damages + attorney fees + tenant may recover possession or terminate the rental agreement; Illinois statewide has NO codified anti-retaliation statute, and common-law retaliatory-eviction doctrine via **Clore v. Fredman, 59 Ill. 2d 20 (1974)** operates as defense to forcible-entry-and-detainer.

**Six protected tenant acts**: ComplaintToLandlordAboutHabitability, ComplaintToGovernmentAgencyHousingCodeOrHealth, JoinedTenantsAssociationOrOrganization, ExercisedRepairAndDeductOrRentWithholdRight, FiledFairHousingDiscriminationComplaint, LegalProceedingAgainstLandlord (plus NoProtectedActExercised sentinel).

**Five landlord adverse actions**: EvictionOrTerminationServed, RentIncreaseImposed, ServiceDecreaseOrMaintenanceWithheld, RefusalToRenewLease, HarassmentOrThreatsToTenant (plus NoAdverseAction sentinel).

**Three landlord rebuttal-evidence branches**: None, DocumentedLegitimateBusinessReason, ActionPredatesTenantProtectedAct (temporal-sequence rebuttal).

**Fourteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoProtectedActNoPresumptionTriggered, NoAdverseActionNoRetaliationClaim, LandlordRebuttalProbablySuccessful, OutsidePresumptionWindowBurdenOnTenant, CaliforniaCiv1942_5PresumptionRetaliationPunitiveDamages, NewYorkRpl223BOneYearPresumption, NewYorkRpl223BThreeYearPresumptionRentStabilized, WashingtonRcw59_18_240NinetyDayPresumption, TexasPropCode92_331SixMonthPresumptionFiveHundredPenalty, FloridaStat83_64CommonLawTemporalProximityPresumption, IllinoisChicagoRlto5_12_150OneYearPresumptionTwoMonthRent, IllinoisStatewideCommonLawCloreVFredmanDefense, DefaultJurisdictionStateLawRetaliationPresumption.

`traderview-expense::rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` is the **landlord notice-to-enter requirement module** (iter 533) вҖ” covers landlord compliance with statutory advance-notice rules before entering an occupied rental unit for non-emergency purposes. Direct sibling to `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” adverse-action timing-window parallel), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (iter 529 вҖ” entry-without-court-order parallel), `rental_video_surveillance_retention` (in-unit surveillance is per-se unpermitted purpose), `rental_property_registration` (operational precondition). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA Civ. Code В§ 1954** requires 24 hours WRITTEN notice with three permissible purposes only (necessary repairs, showing to prospective buyers/tenants/appraisers, court-ordered) and **48 hours WRITTEN notice** for initial moveout inspection under В§ 1950.5(f); (2) **WA RCW 59.18.150** requires 48 hours notice generally with reduced 24-hour standard for showing to prospective buyers/tenants/appraisers; (3) **FL Stat. В§ 83.53** requires "reasonable notice" with 12 hours recognized as reasonable for tenant-requested repairs, and **oral notice is permitted**; (4) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-050** requires 2 days (48 hours) WRITTEN notice for Chicago-located rentals while Illinois statewide has NO codified statute (common-law reasonable-notice standard, 24-hour default); (5) **CO Rev. Stat. В§ 38-12-510** (HB 23-1095, effective Aug 7 2023) requires 48 hours WRITTEN notice; (6) **TX Prop. Code** has NO statewide notice statute and lease terms control, though many local ordinances impose 24-hour requirements; (7) **NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2008 + DHCR rent-stab regs** require reasonable advance notice under common-law standard. Emergency exceptions (fire, gas leak, water leak causing damage, suspected deceased tenant, immediate health/safety threat) authorize entry without notice in every jurisdiction. Unpermitted or pretextual purpose (harassment, surveillance, retaliation, lifestyle monitoring) BREACHES the implied covenant of quiet enjoyment regardless of notice compliance вҖ” common-law cause of action everywhere.

**Eight entry purposes**: NecessaryRepairsOrMaintenance, InspectionRoutineOrAnnual, ShowingToProspectiveBuyersTenantsOrAppraisers, InitialMoveOutInspectionCaSection1950_5F, EmergencyImmediateThreat, TenantAbandonedUnit, CourtOrderedEntry, UnpermittedOrPretextual.

**Four notice methods**: WrittenAdvanceNotice, OralAdvanceNotice, NoNoticeGiven, NoticeReceivedAfterEntry.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, EmergencyEntryNoNoticeRequired, TenantAbandonedUnitNoNoticeRequired, CourtOrderedEntryAuthorized, CompliantWrittenNoticeWithinWindow, CompliantOralNoticeWithinWindow, UnpermittedPurposeQuietEnjoymentBreach, InsufficientNoticeTimeStatutoryViolation, NoNoticeGivenStatutoryViolationPlusQuietEnjoymentBreach.

`traderview-expense::rental_nyc_scrie_drie_rent_freeze` is the **NYC SCRIE / DRIE Rent Freeze Program compliance module** (iter 629) вҖ” direct NYC-portfolio trader-landlord companion to `rental_nyc_loft_law_article_7c` (iter 597 вҖ” Loft Law statutory tenant), `rental_nyc_childhood_lead_poisoning_prevention_act` (iter 599), `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (companion regulated-tenancy regime), `rental_rent_increase_notice_requirement` (iter 619 вҖ” RGB increase notice cross-reference), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573), `rental_ny_rpl_235f_roommate_law` (iter 605 NY RPL В§ 235-f), `rental_ny_rent_receipt_late_notice_requirements` (iter 603). Trader-landlord critical because SCRIE / DRIE-frozen units create a **NYC-only property-tax-abatement revenue stream** that must be properly applied and reported; failure to honor the freeze creates statutory violations + **clawback exposure** for landlord-charged post-freeze rent increases. Both programs **PERMANENTLY FREEZE tenant rent** at the prior level; every future Rent Guidelines Board (RGB) increase is credited back to the landlord as a property tax abatement on **DOF Form RP-467-C** rather than charged to the tenant. **NYC Admin Code В§ 26-509** (**Local Law 6 of 1986**) вҖ” **SCRIE**: tenant aged **62 or older**;
