legal tenant of record in a rent-regulated apartment (rent-stabilized, rent-controlled, or rent-regulated Mitchell-Lama / HDFC co-op); household income at or below **$50,000 per year** for 2025; paying **more than one-third (33.33 %)** of monthly income in rent ([NYC Department of Finance Rent Freeze Qualifications](https://www.nyc.gov/site/rentfreeze/qualifications/qualifications.page), [HelpNewYork вҖ” NYC Rent Freeze Program 2026](https://helpnewyork.com/nyc-rent-freeze-program-scrie-drie-2026/)). **NYC Admin Code В§ 26-509.1** (**Local Law 76 of 2005**) вҖ” **DRIE**: tenant aged **18 or older** currently receiving a qualifying federal disability benefit (SSI / SSDI / VA disability compensation OR currently receiving Medicaid based on disability determination); same **$50,000 income cap** and **> 1/3 rent burden** as SCRIE ([ACCESS NYC вҖ” DRIE program](https://access.nyc.gov/programs/disability-rent-increase-exemption-drie/), [LegalClarity вҖ” What Is DRIE NYC Disability Rent Freeze](https://legalclarity.org/what-is-drie-nyc-disability-rent-freeze-explained/)). **Renewal**: required every **24 months**; NYC Department of Finance mails renewal notice approximately **60 days** before benefit expires. **Proposed 2026 income limit increase**: Governor Hochul's 2026 State of the State proposal would raise SCRIE / DRIE income eligibility limit from **$50,000 to $75,000** for NYC (bill tracking under [NY Senate S01457 (2025-2026 session)](https://www.nysenate.gov/legislation/bills/2019/S4281)). **Fourteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNotInNyc, NotApplicableNotInRentRegulatedUnit, CompliantScrieFrozenRentAndPropertyTaxAbatementApplied, CompliantDrieFrozenRentAndPropertyTaxAbatementApplied, CompliantRenewalSubmittedWithin2YearWindow, ViolationLandlordChargedTenantPostFreezeIncreases, ViolationLandlordFailedToReflectPropertyTaxAbatement, ViolationTenantNotTenantOfRecord, ViolationIncomeLimitExceeded50000, ViolationRentBurdenBelowOneThirdThreshold, ViolationRenewalNotSubmittedExpirationLapsed, ViolationTenantUnderAge62ForScrie, ViolationTenantUnderAge18ForDrie, ViolationTenantNotReceivingQualifyingFederalDisabilityForDrie. **Two programs**: ScrieSeniorCitizen, DrieDisability. **Five regulated unit types**: RentStabilized, RentControlled, MitchellLamaRentRegulated, HdfcCoopRentRegulated, MarketRateUnregulated. **Five federal disability benefits**: Ssi, Ssdi, VaDisabilityCompensation, MedicaidBasedOnDisabilityDetermination, None. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-nyc-scrie-drie-rent-freeze`. **Twenty-four tests pin**: **property not in NYC not applicable**, **unregulated unit not applicable**, **SCRIE compliant baseline**, **DRIE compliant with SSDI**, **tenant under 62 SCRIE violation** (boundary -1), **tenant at exactly 62 compliant** (boundary), **DRIE no qualifying disability violation**, **DRIE under 18 violation**, **not tenant of record violation**, **income over $50,000 violation**, **income at exactly $50,000 compliant** (boundary), **rent burden below 1/3 violation**, **rent burden at exactly 1/3 violation** (statutory > reading), **rent burden above 1/3 compliant**, **renewal lapsed violation** (30 months without renewal), **renewal within window compliant** (23 months + submitted), **landlord charged post-freeze increases violation** (statutory clawback), **landlord failed to apply abatement violation** (Form RP-467-C not filed), **DRIE with VA disability compensation compliant**, **DRIE with Medicaid disability compliant** (4th qualifying benefit type), **Mitchell-Lama compliant** (alternate regulated unit), **HDFC co-op compliant**, **citations pin local laws and Admin Code** (NYC Admin Code В§ 26-509 + Local Law 6 of 1986 + В§ 26-509.1 + Local Law 76 of 2005 + $50,000 + $75,000 + S01457 + 60 days + Form RP-467-C + ACCESS NYC), **constant pin program thresholds** (62 + 18 + $50,000 + $75,000 + 3333 basis points + 24 months + 60 days + 1986 + 6 + 2005 + 76 + 1457 bill). Sibling cluster: `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (companion regulated tenancy), `rental_rent_increase_notice_requirement` (RGB increase cross-reference), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (overlay tenant protections), `rental_nyc_loft_law_article_7c` (statutory tenant cross-reference), `rental_property_tax_pass_through_disclosure` (property tax interaction), `rental_ny_rpl_235f_roommate_law` (NY RPL tenancy cross-reference).

`traderview-expense::rental_pre_foreclosure_tenant_notification` is the **multi-state pre-foreclosure tenant notification compliance module** (iter 627) вҖ” state-law overlay on the federal Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act (PTFA). Direct trader-leveraged-portfolio companion to `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (federal PTFA 90-day base), `rental_vehicle_towing_notice_sign_requirements` (iter 625), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” DV is a categorical bar to no-cause eviction in many jurisdictions), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607), `rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` (iter 617), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter`. Trader-landlord critical because **cross-state leveraged portfolios face multi-state foreclosure-tenant-notification cascade with non-uniform notice windows and content**; defective notices invalidate foreclosure sales + delay eviction + expose lenders / new owners to statutory damages. **California Civ. Code В§ 2923.5 / В§ 2924.85** (Homeowner Bill of Rights; AB 278 of 2012): servicer must contact borrower at least **30 days** before commencing foreclosure to discuss financial situation and explore options to avoid foreclosure ([California AG вҖ” Homeowner Bill of Rights](https://oag.ca.gov/hbor), [FindLaw CA Civ Code В§ 2924](https://codes.findlaw.com/ca/civil-code/civ-sect-2924/)). **California Civ. Code В§ 2924.8**: trustee must post tenant notice within **5 business days** of recording the Notice of Trustee's Sale (NOTS). **New York RPAPL В§ 1303**: tenant notice in a foreclosure action must be delivered within **10 days** of service of the summons and complaint on the mortgagor ([NY State Senate / FindLaw RPAPL В§ 1303](https://codes.findlaw.com/ny/real-property-actions-and-proceedings-law/rpa-sect-1303.html)). **New York RPAPL В§ 1304**: lender must send pre-foreclosure notice to home-loan borrower at least **90 days** before commencing legal action; notice must inform of avoidance options and nonprofit housing counselor referral ([NYS DFS Pre-Foreclosure Notice Filing Guide](https://www.dfs.ny.gov/apps_and_licensing/mortgage_companies/pre-foreclosure_filing_guide_faqs)). **Illinois 735 ILCS 5/15-1701** (Foreclosure Fairness Act): possessory order requirements upon confirmation of sale; **bona fide lease protection** for tenants вҖ” lease is bona fide if mortgagor or mortgagor's child/spouse/parent is not the tenant AND lease was arm's-length transaction ([Illinois General Assembly 735 ILCS 5/15-1701](https://ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/fulltext.asp?DocName=073500050K15-1701)). **Washington RCW 61.24.143**: trustee must mail notice to residents stating foreclosure has begun, the property may be sold **90 or more days** after the notice date, and the renter may receive either a new rental agreement or a 60-day notice to vacate from the new owner ([RCW 61.24.143 official](https://app.leg.wa.gov/RCW/default.aspx?cite=61.24.143)). **Washington RCW 61.24.146**: tenant or subtenant in possession at time of foreclosure sale must receive **60 days' written notice to vacate** before removal. **Massachusetts G.L. c. 244 В§ 35C** (Massachusetts Tenants in Foreclosed Buildings law): foreclosing owner must post notice of new owner in prominent location; cannot evict bona fide tenant for rent nonpayment until **30 days after posting** required notices; **90-day notice to quit** otherwise ([Mass.gov вҖ” Information for tenants in foreclosed buildings](https://www.mass.gov/info-details/information-for-tenants-in-foreclosed-buildings)). **Federal PTFA** (P.L. 111-22 of 2009; made permanent by Dodd-Frank 2018): 90-day federal floor notice to bona fide tenants. **Seventeen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoForeclosurePending, NotApplicableJurisdictionPtfaOnly, CompliantCa292485PreForeclosureContact30DaysBefore, CompliantNyRpapl1303TenantNoticeWithin10Days, CompliantNyRpapl130490DayPreForeclosureNoticeToBorrower, CompliantWaRcw6124143NoticeOfTrusteeSaleToResidents, CompliantWaRcw612414660DayNoticeToVacate, CompliantMa244_35cBonaFideTenantProtectionWithPosting, CompliantIl735ILCS5_15_1701BonaFideLeaseProtected, ViolationCaServicerContact30DayWindowMissed, ViolationCa2924_8TrusteePostingMissedAfterNots, ViolationNy1303_10DayTenantNoticeMissed, ViolationNy1304_90DayPreForeclosureNoticeMissed, ViolationWaTrusteeSale90DayNoticeMissed, ViolationWa60DayWrittenNoticeToVacateMissed, ViolationMaNewOwnerNoticeNotPostedRequiredForRentNonpayment, ViolationIl1701NonBonaFideLeaseDisplacedWithoutPossessoryOrder. **Six jurisdictions**: California292485, NewYorkRpapl1303_1304, Illinois735Ilcs5_15_1701, WashingtonRcw6124, Massachusetts244_35c, OtherStateUnderPtfaOnly. **Six foreclosure stages**: PreNoticeOfDefault, PostNodPreFiling, LegalActionFiled, ForeclosureSaleScheduled, ForeclosureSaleCompleted, PostSaleEviction. **Three lease statuses**: BonaFideArmsLengthLease, NonBonaFideLeaseMortgagorOrFamily, NoLeaseTenantAtWill. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-pre-foreclosure-tenant-notification`. **Twenty tests pin**: **pre-NOD not applicable**, **other state PTFA only not applicable**, **California 30-day contact compliant**, **California 29-day contact violation**, **California В§ 2924.8 trustee posting violation**, **NY RPAPL В§ 1304 90-day pre-foreclosure compliant**, **NY RPAPL В§ 1304 89-day violation**, **NY RPAPL В§ 1303 tenant notice within 10 days compliant** (boundary), **NY RPAPL В§ 1303 tenant notice at 11 days violation**, **Washington 61.24.143 90-day compliant**, **Washington 89-day trustee sale violation**, **Washington 61.24.146 60-day compliant**, **Washington 59-day vacate violation**, **Massachusetts 244 В§ 35C compliant**, **Massachusetts 29 days since notice violation** (boundary -1), **Massachusetts no posting violation**, **Illinois bona fide lease protected compliant**, **Illinois non-bona-fide no possessory order violation**, **citations pin jurisdictional statutes** (В§ 2923.5 + В§ 2924.85 + В§ 2924.8 + RPAPL В§ 1303 + В§ 1304 + 735 ILCS 5/15-1701 + Foreclosure Fairness Act + RCW 61.24.143 + 61.24.146 + Mass. В§ 35C + PTFA + P.L. 111-22 + Dodd-Frank 2018), **constant pin notice days** (CA 30 + 5 business + NY 10 + 90 + WA 60 + 90 + MA 30 + 90 + PTFA 90). Sibling cluster: `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (federal PTFA 90-day base), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (post-foreclosure just-cause overlay), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (lease provision enforceability), `rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` (transactional scenario overlap), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (post-foreclosure access).

`traderview-expense::rental_vehicle_towing_notice_sign_requirements` is the **multi-state residential property vehicle towing notice + sign requirements compliance module** (iter 625) вҖ” direct portfolio-operator companion to `rental_vacant_property_registration` (iter 621), `rental_tenant_bill_of_rights_handout` (iter 623), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (cross-reference for tenant property access), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (analogous wrongful-removal exposure), `rental_application_denial_disclosure`. Trader-landlord critical because non-compliant towing exposes landlord to **double towing + storage charges** under CA Veh. Code В§ 22658(e), tenant damages claims, and state-AG enforcement actions. **California Vehicle Code В§ 22658**: signs must be in plain view at ALL entrances, at least **17 inches by 22 inches** in size, with lettering **at least 1 inch high**, prohibit public parking, indicate that vehicles will be removed at owner's expense, and contain the telephone number of the local traffic law enforcement agency PLUS the name and telephone number of each towing company with a **written general towing authorization agreement** ([California Legislative Information CVC В§ 22658](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=VEH&sectionNum=22658), [FindLaw California Vehicle Code В§ 22658](https://codes.findlaw.com/ca/vehicle-code/veh-sect-22658/), [SignifyLA вҖ” What Is California Vehicle Code 22658](https://signifyla.com/cvc-22658-explained/)). **В§ 22658(e)**: if landlord tows without a compliant sign, landlord may be liable for **DOUBLE the towing + storage charges**. **Tenant vehicle**: landlord must provide **written notice** before towing tenant vehicle except when (a) the vehicle is parked in someone else's assigned space and reported by that tenant OR (b) the vehicle is blocking a fire lane or emergency holding. **Los Angeles Municipal Code В§ 80.71.4**: minimum sign **24 inches by 24 inches** (larger than CA state minimum) + must include **LAPD telephone**. **Texas Occ. Code В§ 2308** (Towing, Booting, and Storage Facility Act): signage at private property + **24-hour storage facility access** + statutory damages. **Florida Stat. В§ 715.07** (Towing of vehicles from private property): signage requirements + **30-day vehicle storage** before disposal. **New Jersey Predatory Towing Prevention Act** (P.L. 2007, c.193; N.J.S.A. 56:13-7 et seq.): sign + maximum charge regulation + 24-hour storage facility access. **Illinois Commercial Safety Towing Law** (625 ILCS 5/18a): ICC-regulated rates + sign + storage standards. **Eighteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoTowingPerformed, NotApplicableFireLaneOrEmergencyTow, CompliantCa22658FullSignageAndAuthorization, CompliantLa80714LargerSignWithLapdPhone, CompliantTx2308SignAndStorageAccess, CompliantFl71507SignAnd30DayStorage, CompliantTenantVehicleWrittenNoticeProvided, CompliantNjPredatoryTowingMaxChargesObserved, CompliantIlVehicleCode18aIccRegulatedRates, ViolationCaliforniaSignSizeBelowMinimum, ViolationCaliforniaLetteringBelow1Inch, ViolationLosAngelesSignBelow24x24, ViolationTenantVehicleNoWrittenNoticeProvided, ViolationDoubleChargesOwedDueToCa22658eNoncompliance, ViolationMissingTowingCompanyAuthorizationContactInfo, ViolationMissingLawEnforcementPhoneOnSign, ViolationFlorida30DayStorageNotObserved, ViolationTexas24HourStorageFacilityAccessNotProvided. **Seven jurisdictions**: California22658, LosAngeles80714Municipal, Texas2308, Florida71507, NewJerseyPredatoryTowingAct, IllinoisVehicleCode18a, OtherStateWithoutCodifiedTowingRequirements. **Five tow scenarios**: NonTenantTrespassingVehicle, TenantVehicleInOwnAssignedSpaceViolating, TenantVehicleInOthersAssignedSpaceReported, TenantVehicleBlockingFireLaneOrEmergencyHolding, AbandonedVehicleNoActivityCharge. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-vehicle-towing-notice-sign-requirements`. **Twenty-four tests pin**: **no tow performed not applicable**, **fire lane tow not applicable** (statutory exception), **California В§ 22658 minimum signage compliant** (17Г—22 boundary), **California sign below 17 inches height violation** (boundary -1), **California sign below 22 inches width violation**, **California sign not at all entrances violation**, **California lettering below 1 inch violation**, **missing law enforcement phone violation**, **missing towing company contact violation**, **missing written general authorization violation**, **missing owner-expense language double-charges violation** (В§ 22658(e) DOUBLE), **Los Angeles 24Г—24 compliant**, **Los Angeles below 24Г—24 violation**, **Los Angeles missing LAPD phone violation**, **tenant vehicle in own space no notice violation**, **tenant vehicle in others' space reported compliant** (statutory carve-out), **Texas 24-hour storage compliant**, **Texas no 24-hour storage violation**, **Florida 30-day storage compliant**, **Florida 30-day storage not observed violation**, **New Jersey Predatory Towing Act compliant**, **Illinois 18a compliant**, **citations pin state statutes** (Cal. Veh. Code В§ 22658 + 17Г—22 + В§ 22658(e) + DOUBLE + Los Angeles Mun. Code В§ 80.71.4 + 24Г—24 + Tex. Occ. Code В§ 2308 + Fla. Stat. В§ 715.07 + N.J. Predatory Towing + P.L. 2007 + 625 ILCS 5/18a), **constant pin sign dimensions and dates** (17 + 22 + 1 + 2Г— multiplier + 24Г—24 + 30-day FL + 24-hour TX + 2007 NJ). Sibling cluster: `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (tenant property access analogue), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (wrongful removal exposure), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (notice regime analog), `rental_vacant_property_registration` (parking-area registration overlap), `rental_video_surveillance_retention` (parking area surveillance).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_bill_of_rights_handout` is the **multi-state tenant disclosure / handout compliance module** (iter 623) вҖ” direct cross-state portfolio trader-landlord companion to `rental_vacant_property_registration` (iter 621), `rental_rent_increase_notice_requirement` (iter 619), `rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` (iter 617), `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (iter 611), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607), `rental_lead_paint_disclosure`, `rental_application_denial_disclosure`. Trader-landlord critical because cross-state portfolios must apply at least four different handout regimes; defective handouts can void monetary lease provisions and trigger statutory penalties. **Michigan Truth in Renting Act** (Public Act 454 of 1978; MCL В§ 554.631 et seq.): all rental agreements must prominently state the statutory notice **"NOTICE: Michigan law establishes rights and obligations for parties to rental agreements. This agreement is required to comply with the Truth in Renting Act..."** in **at least 12-point type OR legible print with letters not smaller than 1/8 inch**. Required disclosures: lead-based paint (Title X 1992), security deposit location, environmental hazards, owner/manager identity, utility billing arrangements, **domestic abuse protection notice** ([iPropertyManagement Michigan Landlord Tenant Laws 2026](https://ipropertymanagement.com/laws/michigan-landlord-tenant-rights), [LeaseRunner Michigan Truth in Renting Act](https://www.leaserunner.com/laws/truth-in-renting-act-michigan)). **New Jersey Truth-in-Renting Act** (N.J. Stat. В§ 46:8-43 to В§ 46:8-50): every landlord must distribute one copy of the DCA-published Truth in Renting booklet to each tenant at lease signing AND within **30 days** for existing tenants; **$100 penalty per non-distribution** ([NJ DCA Truth in Renting booklet](https://www.nj.gov/dca/codes/publications/pdf_lti/t_i_r.pdf), [tenant-rights.com NJ Truth-in-Renting Act](https://tenant-rights.com/new-jersey/new-jersey-tenant-rights-truth-in-renting-act)). **District of Columbia Tenant Bill of Rights** (D.C. Code В§ 42-3502.22b): landlord must provide tenant with copy of lease + addendums + copies of certain D.C. housing regulations + **DC Tenant Bill of Rights document** (published by D.C. Office of the Tenant Advocate) at lease signing ([DC OTA Tenant Bill of Rights publication](https://ota.dc.gov/publication/tenant-bill-rights)). **California Civ. Code В§ 1962**: owner identity + address + authorized property manager identity must be disclosed in writing within **15 days** of tenancy or by lease attachment. **California Civ. Code В§ 1962.5 / В§ 1962.7**: where security deposit is held (institution name, address, account type). **Florida Stat. В§ 83.49**: deposit location + landlord/manager identity disclosed within **30 days** of receipt. **Federal Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act (Title X of 1992)** applies cross-state to all pre-1978 housing. **Nineteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoLeaseExecuted, NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksMandate, CompliantAllRequiredDisclosuresIncluded, CompliantMichiganStatutoryNoticeAndDisclosuresIncluded, CompliantNewJerseyTruthInRentingDistributed, CompliantDcTenantBillOfRightsProvided, CompliantCaliforniaOwnerIdentityDisclosed, CompliantFloridaDepositLocationDisclosed, ViolationMichiganStatutoryNoticeMissing, ViolationMichiganStatutoryNoticeFontTooSmall, ViolationMichiganDomesticAbuseNoticeMissing, ViolationNewJerseyTruthInRentingNotDistributed, ViolationDcTenantBillOfRightsNotProvided, ViolationCaliforniaOwnerIdentityNotDisclosedWithin15Days, ViolationFloridaDepositLocationNotDisclosedWithin30Days, ViolationLeadBasedPaintTitleXFederalDisclosureMissing, ViolationSecurityDepositLocationOrOwnerManagerIdentityMissing, ViolationUtilityBillingArrangementsNotDisclosed, ViolationEnvironmentalHazardKnownButNotDisclosed. **Six jurisdictions**: MichiganTruthInRenting, NewJerseyTruthInRenting, DistrictOfColumbiaTenantBillOfRights, CaliforniaSection1962, FloridaStatute83_49, OtherStateWithoutHandoutMandate. **Three Michigan font states**: NoticeAtLeast12PointTypeOrLegiblePrintWithLettersAt1_8Inch, NoticeTooSmallBelow12Point, NoticeMissingEntirely. **Three NJ distribution states**: DistributedAtLeaseSigning, DistributedToExistingTenantWithin30Days, NotDistributed. **Two DC handout states**: DcTenantBillOfRightsProvidedWithLease, DcTenantBillOfRightsNotProvided. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-bill-of-rights-handout`. **Twenty tests pin**: **no lease not applicable**, **other jurisdiction not applicable**, **Michigan compliant**, **Michigan notice missing violation**, **Michigan notice font too small violation** (below 12-point), **Michigan domestic abuse notice missing violation**, **Michigan security deposit or owner missing violation**, **Michigan utility billing not disclosed violation**, **lead paint disclosure missing violation** (Title X federal), **environmental hazard known but not disclosed violation**, **New Jersey distributed compliant**, **New Jersey not distributed violation** ($100 penalty), **DC Tenant Bill of Rights provided compliant**, **DC Tenant Bill of Rights not provided violation**, **California owner identity within 15 days compliant** (boundary), **California owner identity 16 days violation**, **Florida deposit location within 30 days compliant** (boundary), **Florida deposit location 31 days violation**, **citations pin jurisdictional statutes** (Michigan Truth in Renting Act + Public Act 454 of 1978 + MCL В§ 554.631 + N.J. Stat. В§ 46:8-43 + В§ 46:8-50 + D.C. Code В§ 42-3502.22b + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1962 + В§ 1962.5 + В§ 1962.7 + Fla. Stat. В§ 83.49 + Title X of 1992), **constant pin jurisdictional thresholds** (Michigan 1978 + Public Act 454 + 12-point + 1/8 inch + NJ 30-day + $100 + California 15-day + Florida 30-day). Sibling cluster: `rental_lead_paint_disclosure` (Title X federal cross-reference), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (lease-signing disclosure regime), `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (cross-state lease provision enforceability), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (cross-state lease provision matrix), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (deposit-related disclosure).

`traderview-expense::rental_vacant_property_registration` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential vacant property registration compliance module** (iter 621) вҖ” direct portfolio-operator companion to `rental_property_registration` (general rental registration), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (mortgagee-following-foreclosure overlap), `rental_unpermitted_unit_disclosure`, `rental_facade_inspection_fisp_local_law_11` (city building code overlap), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607 вҖ” Chicago В§ 13-12-125 attorney-fee lien). Trader-landlord critical because **vacant-property registration failures trigger doubled fees, code-enforcement liens, daily penalties, and reputational exposure** for portfolio operators with even one underperforming asset. **Chicago Municipal Code В§ 13-12-125 (owner)**: owner must register vacant building within **30 days** after the building becomes vacant OR within 30 days after assuming ownership of the building. Registration / renewal fee = **$300 per registered building**; renewal required every **6 months**. Fee is **DOUBLED** if registration is the result of City identification of violation rather than voluntary timely compliance. Inspector + attorney's fees recoverable as a **LIEN on the property** ([Chicago Code Library В§ 13-12-125](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/chicago/c7209359-81de-4059-a679-f6a211f04dea/chicagobuilding_il/0-0-0-326268), [City of Chicago Department of Buildings vacant property guidance](https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/bldgs/provdrs/inspect/svcs/check_status_of_vacantproperty.html), [DAWGS Inc. Chicago Vacant Property Ordinances guide](https://www.dawgsinc.com/chicagos-vacant-property-ordinances-a-quick-guide-to-compliance-and-community-safety/)). **Chicago Municipal Code В§ 13-12-126 (mortgagee)**: mortgagee of vacant unregistered building must file within the **later of** (a) 30 days after the building becomes vacant and unregistered OR (b) 10 days after default. Initial fee = **$700**; renewal every 6 months at **$300** ([Chicago Code Library В§ 13-12-126](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/chicago/c7209359-81de-4059-a679-f6a211f04dea/chicagobuilding_il/0-0-0-326284)). **Detroit Vacant Property Registration Ordinance**: owners must register vacant properties with **BSEED** (Buildings, Safety Engineering and Environmental Department) within **30 days** of vacancy; exterior maintenance required to prevent nuisance/hazard ([Detroit MI Vacant Property Registration Ordinance Fact Sheet](https://detroitmi.gov/document/vacant-property-registration-ordinance-fact-sheet)). **Cleveland OMC В§ 367.131**: Cleveland Department of Building and Housing vacant property registration. **Baltimore Building Code В§ 102.5**: vacant house notice + registration. **Philadelphia Vacant Property Strategy**: Department of Licenses and Inspections (L&I) vacant property compliance. **Common municipal requirements**: 30-day threshold; annual or semi-annual fee; local agent designation for service of process; maintenance plan; access for inspection; lien rights for code-enforcement costs; daily penalties for non-registration accumulating until cure. **Twelve-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableUnitNotVacant, NotApplicableLessThan30DaysVacant, NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksMandate, CompliantOwnerRegisteredWithin30DaysAndFeePaid, CompliantMortgageeRegisteredAfterForeclosure, CompliantRenewalFilingTimely, ViolationOwnerFailedToRegisterWithin30Days, ViolationDoubleFeeAppliedForCityIdentifiedNonCompliance, ViolationMortgageeFailedToRegisterAfterForeclosure, ViolationRenewalNotFiledEverySixMonths, ViolationLocalAgentNotDesignated, ViolationCodeEnforcementLienAccruedFromNonRegistration. **Six jurisdictions**: ChicagoMunicipalCode, DetroitBSEED, ClevelandOMC, BaltimoreBuildingCode, PhiladelphiaVacantPropertyStrategy, OtherJurisdictionWithoutVacantRegistration. **Three actor roles**: OwnerOfRecord, MortgageeAfterForeclosureOrDefault, PropertyManagerOnOwnerBehalf. **Two discovery modes**: VoluntaryTimelyCompliance, CityIdentifiedViolation. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-vacant-property-registration`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **property not vacant not applicable**, **less than 30 days vacant not applicable**, **at exactly 30 days vacant registration triggered** (boundary), **other jurisdiction not applicable**, **Chicago owner 30-day compliant** ($300 fee), **Chicago owner filed at 31 days violation**, **Chicago city-identified double fee violation** ($300 Г— 2 = $600), **Chicago mortgagee 30-day compliant** ($700 initial), **Chicago mortgagee 31 days violation**, **Chicago fee shortfall violation** ($200 paid on $300 required), **no local agent designated violation**, **renewal not filed after 6 months violation**, **renewal filed at exactly 6 months compliant** (boundary), **code enforcement lien accrued violation** ($5,000 lien), **Detroit BSEED 30-day compliant**, **Detroit 31 days violation**, **Cleveland/Baltimore/Philadelphia 30-day compliant** (multi-jurisdiction loop), **citations pin Chicago + Detroit + Cleveland + Baltimore + Philadelphia** (В§ 13-12-125 + В§ 13-12-126 + BSEED + OMC + Building Code + L&I + $300 + $700 + DOUBLED), **constant pin Chicago fees and intervals** (30-day owner registration + $300 owner fee + 6-month renewal + 2Г— multiplier + 30-day mortgagee + $700 initial + $300 renewal + 10-day post-default + Detroit 30-day + default 30-day). Sibling cluster: `rental_property_registration` (general rental property registration regimes), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (foreclosure scenario triggering mortgagee registration obligation), `rental_facade_inspection_fisp_local_law_11` (city-code compliance overlap), `rental_unpermitted_unit_disclosure`, `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (Chicago В§ 13-12-125 attorney-fee lien).

`traderview-expense::rental_rent_increase_notice_requirement` is the **multi-state residential rent-increase notice timing compliance module** (iter 619) вҖ” direct cross-state trader-landlord companion to `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (rent cap regimes), `rental_late_fee_cap` (lease fee timing), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607), `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (iter 611), `rental_positive_rent_payment_credit_reporting` (iter 613), `rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` (iter 617). Trader-landlord critical because most cross-state operations must apply at least 4 different rules; defective notice voids the increase and continues tenancy at the prior rate until proper notice expires. **California Civ. Code В§ 827** (amended by **AB 1110** (2019), effective **January 1, 2020**): for residential periodic tenancies, landlord must serve **30 days** written notice for rent increases of **10 % or less**, and **90 days** written notice for increases **greater than 10 %** ([CA Legislative Information AB 1110 Bill Text](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=201920200AB1110), [Wallace, Richardson, Sontag & Le LLP вҖ” 90 Days' Notice Required to Increase Rent by More Than 10 %](https://www.wallacelaw.com/blog/90-days-notice-required-to-increase-rent-by-more-than-10-for-a-residential-dwelling-with-a-month-to-month-tenancy/)). **Washington RCW 59.18.140** (amended effective **May 7, 2025**): landlord must provide written notice a minimum of **60 days** before the effective date of an increase in rent for residential periodic tenancies ([RCW 59.18.140 official](https://app.leg.wa.gov/rcw/default.aspx?cite=59.18.140)). **Oregon ORS 90.323 / 90.600** (SB 608 of 2019): landlord must provide **90 days** written notice for any rent increase. **New York Real Property Law В§ 226-c** (added by HSTPA of 2019): if landlord intends to raise rent by **5 % or more** OR intends not to renew the lease, advance written notice required keyed to tenancy length вҖ” **30 days for tenancy < 1 year (or lease term < 1 year)**; **60 days for tenancy 1-2 years (or lease term 1-2 years)**; **90 days for tenancy вүҘ 2 years (or lease term вүҘ 2 years)**. Failure consequence: tenant's lawful tenancy continues under existing terms until notice period has expired ([NY State Senate В§ 226-c](https://www.nysenate.gov/legislation/laws/RPP/226-C), [tenant-rights.com NY 30/60/90-day guide](https://tenant-rights.com/new-york/30-60-90day-notice-of-rent-increase-or-nonrenewal-rpl-226c)). **Thirteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoIncrease, NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksStatutoryMandate, NotApplicableNyIncreaseBelow5PctThreshold, CompliantNoticeServedWithinStatutoryWindow, ViolationNoNoticeServed, ViolationCaliforniaUnder30DaysShortIncrease, ViolationCaliforniaUnder90DaysLargeIncrease, ViolationWashingtonUnder60Days, ViolationOregonUnder90Days, ViolationNyUnder30DayShortOccupancy, ViolationNyUnder60DayMediumOccupancy, ViolationNyUnder90DayLongOccupancy, ViolationOccupancyMisclassifiedToReduceNoticeWindow. **Five jurisdictions**: California, Washington, Oregon, NewYork, OtherStateWithoutStatutoryMandate. **Three occupancy/lease length tiers**: LessThan1Year, Between1And2Years, TwoYearsOrMore. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-rent-increase-notice-requirement`. **Twenty-three tests pin**: **no increase not applicable**, **other jurisdiction not applicable**, **California small increase 30 days compliant**, **California at exactly 30 days compliant** (boundary), **California 29 days short-increase violation**, **California at exactly 10 % small increase 30-day window** (statutory вүӨ boundary), **California 10.01 % large increase 90-day required**, **California 15 % increase 90-day compliant**, **Washington 60 days compliant**, **Washington 59 days violation**, **Oregon 90 days compliant**, **Oregon 89 days violation**, **NY under 5 % not applicable** (В§ 226-c trigger threshold), **NY at exactly 5 % triggers В§ 226-c** (boundary 500 basis points), **NY short occupancy 29 days violation** (< 1 year tier), **NY medium occupancy 60 days compliant** (1-2 years tier), **NY medium occupancy 59 days violation**, **NY long occupancy 90 days compliant** (вүҘ 2 years tier), **NY long occupancy 89 days violation**, **no notice served violation**, **landlord misclassified occupancy violation NY**, **citations pin statutes across jurisdictions** (Cal В§ 827 + AB 1110 + Wash RCW 59.18.140 + Or ORS 90.323 + SB 608 + NY RPL В§ 226-c + HSTPA), **constant pin notice days + thresholds** (2020 + 30 + 90 + 1000 basis points + 60 + 2025 + 90 + 2019 + 500 + 30/60/90 + 2019 HSTPA). Sibling cluster: `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (CPI-based rent cap regimes вҖ” companion limits), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (CA AB 1482 + OR SB 608 вҖ” same statutes impose just-cause + notice requirements), `rental_late_fee_cap` (lease monetary cap enforceability), `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (lease provision enforceability across same states), `rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` (trader-transactional cross-state lease provision).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_estoppel_certificate` is the **residential tenant estoppel certificate enforceability + binding-statement compliance module** (iter 617) вҖ” direct trader-landlord transactional companion to `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607 вҖ” lease provision enforceability), `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (iter 611 вҖ” lease provision enforceability), `rental_positive_rent_payment_credit_reporting` (iter 613), `rental_dc_topa_tenant_opportunity_purchase` (transactional disclosure), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (mortgage default scenario). Trader-landlord critical because **estoppel certificates are demanded during refinancing and sale transactions**; defective demands and false statements create transaction-failure and litigation exposure under California Evidence Code В§ 622 binding-statement rule. **General rule**: a tenant is required to sign an estoppel certificate ONLY when the written lease contains a provision requiring the tenant to do so; absent a lease provision, a tenant is NOT required to complete and sign an estoppel certificate ([Tobener Ravenscroft LLP Estoppel Certificates](https://www.tobenerlaw.com/estoppel-certificate/), [California Lawyers Association Tenant Estoppel Certificates](https://calawyers.org/real-property-law/tenant-estoppel-certificates-a-peek-behind-the-curtain/), [Tenant Law Group SF](https://tenantlawgroupsf.com/blog/2023/05/what-is-a-tenant-estoppel-certificate-and-should-i-fill-it-out/)). **Refusal to sign when lease requires** is a breach of lease ([Wolford Wayne LLP Oakland Tenant Attorneys](https://wolford-wayne.com/what-is-a-tenant-estoppel-certificate/)). **Typical response timeframe**: 10 days from landlord request per standard lease clause: "Tenant agrees, from time to time, within 10 days after request of Landlord, to execute and deliver to Landlord ... any estoppel certificate requested by Landlord." **California Evidence Code В§ 622** вҖ” the facts recited in a written instrument are conclusively presumed to be true as between the parties thereto, or their successors in interest; statements made in an estoppel certificate are BINDING and tenants cannot later contradict them. **Required content**: rent amount + lease terms + protected tenancy status (rent stabilization / rent control / good cause) + oral agreements with landlord + lease amendments + landlord promises + utility payment arrangements. **Common transactional triggers**: property sale, refinancing, line-of-credit application, mortgage loan modification. **Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoTransactionPending, CompliantTenantSignedEstoppelWithinTimeframe, CompliantTenantSignedWithCorrectionsNoted, CompliantTenantRefusedAbsentLeaseClause, CompliantTenantNotYetWithinResponseWindow, ViolationTenantRefusedToSignDespiteLeaseClause, ViolationLandlordDemandedEstoppelWithoutLeaseAuthority, ViolationEstoppelTimeframeShorterThanLeaseProvision, ViolationLandlordIncludedFalseStatementsInEstoppelDraft, ViolationLandlordWaivedTenantProtectedTenancyStatus, ViolationTenantMissedResponseDeadline. **Five transactional triggers**: PropertySale, Refinancing, LineOfCreditApplication, MortgageLoanModification, NoTransactionPending. **Four lease clause types**: NoEstoppelClauseInLease, TenDayEstoppelClause, SevenDayEstoppelClause, StatutoryReasonableTimeNoExplicitDays. **Four tenant responses**: TenantSignedAsDrafted, TenantSignedWithCorrectionsNoted, TenantRefusedToSign, TenantNotYetResponded. **Seven content defect categories**: NoDefects, OmittedProtectedTenancyStatus, UnderstatedOralAgreements, OverstatedRentAmount, OmittedLandlordPromises, OmittedUtilityPaymentArrangements, WaivedTenantRentStabilizationStatus. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-estoppel-certificate`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **no transaction pending not applicable**, **no lease clause tenant refused compliant**, **no lease clause landlord demand violation**, **10-day clause signed compliant**, **signed with corrections noted compliant** (curative approach), **tenant refused despite lease clause violation** (breach of lease), **landlord shortened timeframe violation** (3 days vs. 10-day lease provision), **waived rent stabilization status violation** (non-waivable protected tenancy), **overstated rent amount violation**, **omitted oral agreements violation**, **omitted landlord promises violation**, **tenant not yet responded within window compliant**, **tenant at exactly 10 days still within window** (statutory вүӨ boundary), **tenant missed response deadline at 11 days violation**, **7-day clause compliant** (alternate lease window), **statutory reasonable time defaults to 10 days**, **refinancing trigger treated same as sale**, **citations pin Evidence Code В§ 622 + typical clause** (binding statement + "within 10 days after request" + "absent lease provision" + "breach of lease"), **constant pin response window + Evidence Code** (10 + 7 + 622). Sibling cluster: `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (lease provision enforceability), `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (lease provision enforceability), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (mortgage default scenario triggers estoppel demand), `rental_dc_topa_tenant_opportunity_purchase` (sale-trigger transactional disclosure), `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (protected tenancy status pinning).

`traderview-expense::rental_domestic_violence_lock_change_lease_termination` is the **multi-jurisdictional domestic violence tenant lock-change + early lease termination compliance module** (iter 615) вҖ” cross-jurisdictional companion to `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (general lockout law), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” DV is a categorical bar to no-cause eviction in many jurisdictions), `rental_tenant_right_to_counsel_eviction` (iter 609), `rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` (DV intersects with disability discrimination claims). Trader-landlord critical because **DV-related lock-change failures expose landlords to statutory damages, treble damages (Arizona), and tenant safety claims**; В§ 1941.6 violation can trigger punitive damages in California. **California Civ. Code В§ 1941.5** вҖ” when the restrained person is NOT a tenant, landlord shall change locks of protected tenant's dwelling unit upon written request **within 24 hours** after the protected tenant gives a copy of a court order or police report; tenant bears actual cost. **California Civ. Code В§ 1941.6** вҖ” when the restrained person IS a tenant, landlord shall, at the **landlord's own expense**, change locks **within 24 hours** after the protected tenant gives a copy of a court order excluding the restrained person from the dwelling unit. If landlord fails within 24 hours, protected tenant may change locks without landlord's permission notwithstanding any lease provision; tenant must change in **workmanlike manner with locks of similar or better quality** ([WomensLaw.org В§ 1941.6](https://www.womenslaw.org/laws/ca/statutes/19416-tenant-protected-restraining-order-against-another-tenant-change-locks)). **California Civ. Code В§ 1946.7** вҖ” early lease termination right for victims of DV, sexual assault, stalking, human trafficking, elder abuse, dependent adult abuse; tenant may move out at any time but must continue paying rent for **14 days** after written notice given. **Texas Property Code В§ 92.016** вҖ” family violence victim may break residential lease without penalty after giving landlord **30 days** written notice. If lease lacks the required statutory rights language ("Tenants may have special statutory rights to terminate the lease early in certain situations involving family violence or a military deployment or transfer"), tenant is **NOT liable for unpaid rent** at termination. If abuser is co-tenant or occupant, tenant does NOT need to give 30 days' advance notice. Landlords must change locks within **3 business days** of tenant's request + documentation; landlord may charge actual cost ([Texas Property Code В§ 92.016 FindLaw](https://codes.findlaw.com/tx/property-code/prop-sect-92-016/)). **New York Real Property Law В§ 227-c** вҖ” tenant or household member who is DV victim and reasonably fears remaining in rental may terminate lease by written notice with termination date at least **30 days** after delivery; within **25 days** of notice, tenant must provide proof. Tenant remains liable for 30-day notice period rent ([NY RPL В§ 227-c Justia](https://law.justia.com/codes/new-york/2014/rpp/article-7/227-c/)). **Arizona Rev. Stat. В§ 33-1318** вҖ” early termination + lock replacement + access refusal protections + **treble damages** + landlord immunity for compliant lock change ([AZ Legislature В§ 33-1318](https://www.azleg.gov/ars/33/01318.htm)). **VAWA federally subsidized housing** (Violence Against Women Act of **1994**; **2013 Reauthorization**; **2022 Reauthorization Act**): federally subsidized housing (Section 8, public housing, LIHTC, HOME) bars DV-victim eviction for DV-related incidents; permits lease bifurcation to remove abuser tenant; requires emergency transfer plans. **Sixteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoDvDocumentationProvided, NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksDvMandate, CompliantCa1941_5LandlordChangedLocksWithin24HoursTenantPaid, CompliantCa1941_6LandlordChangedLocksWithin24HoursLandlordExpense, CompliantTexasLockChangeWithin3BusinessDaysActualCostCharged, CompliantCa1946_7EarlyTerminationWith14DayPostNoticeRent, CompliantNyRpl227cTerminationWith30DayNoticeAnd25DayProof, CompliantTexas92_016EarlyTerminationWith30DayNotice, CompliantTexas92_016ZeroNoticeAbuserIsCoTenant, CompliantVawaSection8FederalProtectionApplied, CompliantTenantSelfChangedLocksAfterLandlord24HourFailure, ViolationLandlordFailedToChangeLocksWithinStatutoryWindow, ViolationLandlordChargedTenantForCa1941_6LockChange, ViolationLandlordRefusedEarlyTerminationDespiteCompliantNotice, ViolationTexasLeaseLackedRequiredStatutoryRightsLanguage, ViolationCa1946_7ImproperNoticeOrTerminationDate, ViolationNyRpl227cTerminationDateLessThan30DaysFromNotice. **Six jurisdictions**: California, Texas, NewYork, Arizona, VawaFederallySubsidizedHousing, OtherStateWithoutDvLockChangeMandate. **Three scenarios**: LockChangeRequestRestrainedPersonNotTenant, LockChangeRequestRestrainedPersonIsTenant, EarlyLeaseTerminationRequest. **Four documentation types**: CourtOrderOrPoliceReport, CourtOrderExcludingRestrainedPersonFromUnit, DvAffidavitOrCertification, NoDocumentationProvided. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-domestic-violence-lock-change-lease-termination`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **no documentation not applicable** (prerequisite), **CA В§ 1941.5 compliant within 24 hours**, **CA В§ 1941.5 at exactly 24 hours compliant** (boundary), **CA В§ 1941.5 25 hours violation**, **CA В§ 1941.5 tenant self-change after landlord failure compliant** (curative right), **CA В§ 1941.6 compliant landlord expense**, **CA В§ 1941.6 landlord charged tenant violation** (must be landlord's own expense), **Texas lock change 3 business days compliant**, **Texas lock change 4 business days violation**, **CA В§ 1946.7 14-day compliant**, **CA В§ 1946.7 13-day violation**, **Texas В§ 92.016 30-day compliant**, **Texas В§ 92.016 zero notice abuser co-tenant compliant** (statutory waiver), **Texas lease lacks statutory rights language violation**, **NY RPL В§ 227-c 30-day notice + 25-day proof compliant**, **NY RPL В§ 227-c termination 29 days violation** (boundary), **NY RPL В§ 227-c proof at 26 days violation** (boundary), **VAWA federally subsidized housing compliant**, **other jurisdiction lock change not applicable**, **citations pin jurisdictional statutes** (В§ 1941.5 + В§ 1941.6 + В§ 1946.7 + В§ 92.016 + RPL В§ 227-c + В§ 33-1318 + VAWA 1994 + 2013 Reauthorization + 2022 Reauthorization Act), **constant pin statutory windows** (24 hours + 14 days + 3 business days + 30 days + 25 days + 1994/2013/2022 VAWA years). Sibling cluster: `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (general lockout вҖ” DV is a permitted lockout/lock-change), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (DV is categorical bar to no-cause eviction in many jurisdictions), `rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` (DV intersects with disability discrimination claims), `rental_tenant_right_to_counsel_eviction` (RTC frequently invoked in DV-related housing court matters), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (retaliating against DV-victim tenant prohibited statewide).

`traderview-expense::rental_positive_rent_payment_credit_reporting` is the **California AB 2747 (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.06) positive rent payment credit reporting offer compliance module** (iter 613) вҖ” direct CA-specific companion to `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (iter 611 multi-state lease provision enforceability), `rental_late_fee_cap`, `rental_security_deposit_return_notice`, `rental_just_cause_eviction`. **California AB 2747** (2023-2024 session) added **Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.06** with **effective date January 1, 2025** and **key offer requirements beginning April 1, 2025** ([California Legislative Information AB 2747 Bill Text](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202320240AB2747), [HBR Rentals AB 2747](https://www.hbrrentals.com/landlord-law-hub/ab-2747-new-california-law-mandates-credit-reporting-for-rental-applicants), [FrontLobby Guide](https://frontlobby.com/en/2025/03/understanding-californias-new-rent-reporting-law-ab-2747/)). Requires covered landlords to **OFFER tenants the option** of having positive rental payment information reported to **at least one nationwide consumer reporting agency** (Equifax / Experian / TransUnion). **Small-landlord exemption**: building with **15 or fewer dwelling units** is exempt UNLESS the landlord (a) **owns more than one** residential rental building AND (b) is a **REIT, corporation, or LLC with at least one corporate member**. Both conditions must be met for the exemption to disappear. **Effective-date matrix**: leases entered on/after April 1, 2025 вҖ” offer at lease inception + annually thereafter; leases outstanding as of January 1, 2025 (pre-existing) вҖ” offer no later than April 1, 2025 + annually thereafter. **Fee cap**: landlord may charge electing tenant the **LESSER of $10 per month OR actual landlord cost**; if landlord incurs no actual cost (free reporting service), no fee may be charged. **Thirteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableExemptSmallLandlord, NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksMandate, CompliantOfferMadeAtLeaseInceptionAndAnnual, CompliantTenantDeclined, CompliantTenantElectedAndReportingActive, ViolationOfferNotMadeAtLeaseInception, ViolationAnnualOfferNotProvided, ViolationOfferToPreExistingTenantsMissedApril1_2025, ViolationFeeExceedsLesserOf10DollarsOrActualCost, ViolationFeeChargedWhenLandlordIncursNoActualCost, ViolationNoQualifyingNationwideConsumerReportingAgency, ViolationSmallLandlordExemptionInvalidlyClaimed, ViolationTenantElectedButReportingNotActivated. **Two jurisdictions**: California, OtherStateWithoutPositiveRentReportingMandate. **Four ownership structures**: IndividualOrLlcWithoutCorporateMember, Reit, Corporation, LlcWithCorporateMember. **Two lease timings**: NewLeaseOnOrAfterApril1_2025, PreExistingLeaseOutstandingJanuary1_2025. **Four tenant election states**: TenantNotYetOffered, TenantDeclinedOffer, TenantElectedAndReportingActive, TenantElectedButReportingNotActivated. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-positive-rent-payment-credit-reporting`. **Twenty tests pin**: **other jurisdiction not applicable**, **small landlord individual exempt** (10 units, individual owner), **small landlord at exactly 15 units exempt** (statutory вүӨ 15 boundary), **small landlord at 16 units not exempt**, **small landlord corporate multi-building loses exemption**, **small landlord REIT multi-building loses exemption**, **small landlord LLC-with-corporate-member multi-building loses exemption**, **small landlord individual multi-building STILL exempt** (no corporate disqualifier), **new lease offer not made at inception violation**, **pre-existing lease April 1, 2025 deadline missed violation**, **annual re-offer not provided violation**, **fee charged when zero actual cost violation**, **fee exceeds $10 cap violation** (Excess of $5 from $15 charge - $10 cap), **fee exceeds actual cost when below $10 cap violation** ($7 charged on $3 actual cost), **fee at exactly $10 cap compliant when actual cost higher** ($10 charged, $12 actual cost), **tenant declined compliant**, **tenant elected but reporting not activated violation**, **tenant elected no nationwide CRA violation**, **citations pin AB 2747 + Civ Code В§ 1954.06** (Jan 1, 2025 + Apr 1, 2025 + 15 units + $10/month + Equifax/Experian/TransUnion), **constant pin AB 2747 dates and thresholds** (2025-01-01 effective + 2025-04-01 offer deadline + $10/month fee cap + 15-unit threshold). Trader-landlord critical: California's largest residential markets (San Francisco / Los Angeles / San Diego) are dominated by вүҘ 16-unit buildings OR REIT/corporate-LLC multi-building owners; most California trader-landlords are covered by AB 2747. Sibling cluster: `rental_renters_insurance_requirement` (multi-state lease provision enforceability), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (tenant credit-reporting screening adverse-action analog), `rental_tenant_criminal_background_screening`, `rental_tenant_data_privacy_compliance`, `rental_source_of_income_discrimination`.

`traderview-expense::rental_renters_insurance_requirement` is the **multi-state lease-imposed renter's insurance requirement enforceability compliance module** (iter 611) вҖ” cross-jurisdictional companion to `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607 вҖ” lease provision enforceability across states), `rental_late_fee_cap` (lease monetary cap enforceability), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (lease deposit-handling enforceability), `rental_pet_deposit_separate_security` (additional charge enforceability), `rental_junk_fee_transparency`, `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure`. **All 50 U.S. states permit landlords to require renter's insurance contractually**; renter's insurance is NOT legally required by any state ([Progressive Renters Insurance Requirements by State](https://www.progressive.com/answers/state-renters-insurance-requirements/), [Travelers Insurance Can a Landlord Require](https://www.travelers.com/resources/home/landlords/can-a-landlord-require-renters-insurance), [Allstate Is Renters Insurance Required](https://www.allstate.com/resources/renters-insurance/is-renters-insurance-required)). **California**: landlord-imposed requirement permitted only when **clearly stated in writing before the lease is signed or renewed** ([Cost U Less Direct CA Landlords Renters Insurance Requirements](https://www.costulessdirect.com/blog/california-landlords-renters-insurance-requirements/)). **Texas / Florida / Ohio**: landlords routinely embed the requirement in lease; courts uphold unambiguous lease language ([MoneyGeek Can a Landlord Require Renters Insurance](https://www.moneygeek.com/insurance/renters/can-a-landlord-require-renters-insurance/)). **Industry-standard coverage upheld as reasonable**: **$100,000 liability** + **$10,000-$30,000 personal property** ([Steadily Can a Landlord Require Renters Insurance](https://www.steadily.com/blog/can-a-landlord-require-renters-insurance)). **Excessive amounts** (вүҘ $1,000,000 liability) **effectively prohibitive of affordable policies** and unenforceable per case law. **Typical lease clause**: "Tenant must maintain renters insurance with minimum $10,000 personal-property coverage and $100,000 liability coverage, naming landlord as additional insured." **Verbal agreements are NOT sufficient** for enforcement ([The Credit People Landlord Mandate Renters Insurance](https://www.thecreditpeople.com/evictions/landlord-mandate-renters-insurance)); written lease provision required. **Mid-lease imposition requires written amendment signed by tenant**; unilateral imposition unenforceable. **HUD does NOT require renter's insurance for Section 8 voucher tenants by federal regulation**. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoLeaseRequirement, CompliantWrittenRequirementWithStandardCoverageAndProof, CompliantWrittenRequirementWithAdditionalInsuredNamingAndProof, CompliantMidLeaseAmendmentSignedByTenant, ViolationVerbalRequirementOnlyNotInLease, ViolationAmbiguousLeaseClauseNotEnforceable, ViolationMidLeaseImpositionWithoutAmendment, ViolationExcessiveLiabilityRequirementProhibitive, ViolationTenantFailedToMaintainPolicy, ViolationLandlordRequiredProofNotProvidedByTenant. **Six jurisdictions**: California, Texas, Florida, NewYork, Ohio, OtherStatePermittingContractualRequirement. **Six lease-requirement formality states**: NoRequirementInLease, WrittenLeaseClauseClearAndUnambiguous, WrittenLeaseClauseAmbiguous, VerbalRequirementOnly, AttemptedMidLeaseImpositionWithoutAmendment, AttemptedMidLeaseImpositionWithSignedAmendment. **Four additional-insured statuses**: LandlordNamedAdditionalInsuredProofProvided, LandlordNamedButProofNotProvided, LandlordNotNamedButPolicyInForce, NoPolicyInForce. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-renters-insurance-requirement`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **California standard written requirement compliant**, **additional-insured naming with proof compliant**, **no requirement in lease not applicable**, **verbal requirement only violation**, **ambiguous lease clause violation** (contra proferentem against drafting landlord), **mid-lease imposition without amendment violation**, **mid-lease imposition WITH signed amendment compliant** (curative), **excessive $1M liability requirement violation** (boundary at exclusive $1M threshold), **excessive $2M liability requirement violation**, **standard $100K liability compliant**, **at $999,999 below threshold still compliant**, **tenant no policy violation**, **tenant policy but landlord not named when required violation**, **tenant policy but no proof provided violation**, **Texas/Florida/Ohio uphold unambiguous clause compliant** (multi-jurisdiction loop), **NY/other state compliant with standard coverage** (multi-jurisdiction loop), **citations pin industry and jurisdictional sources** (50 states + California + Texas/Florida/Ohio + $100K standard + verbal not enforceable + mid-lease amendment + HUD non-requirement), **constant pin industry-standard thresholds** (100000/10000/30000/1000000). Trader-landlord critical: most form leases include renter's insurance requirement but many are unenforceable due to formality defects (verbal-only, ambiguous, mid-lease imposition without amendment, excessive coverage demands).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_right_to_counsel_eviction` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant Right to Counsel (RTC) in eviction proceedings compliance module** (iter 609) вҖ” cross-jurisdictional companion to `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607 multi-state attorney-fee mutualization), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” RTC frequently invoked in just-cause eviction defense), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (RTC overlay in lockout damage actions), `rental_retaliation_prohibition`, `rental_tenant_data_privacy_compliance`. Trader-landlord critical because **RTC programs dramatically increase tenant defense rates and case duration** (NYC RTC reduced eviction judgments by ~50 % per CSS NY first-year report); failure to honor RTC procedure (notice + appointment + continuance for attorney) can **invalidate eviction judgments**, requiring relitigation. **NYC (FIRST U.S. jurisdiction August 2017)**: Intro 214-b enacted as **Local Law 136 of 2017**, codified at **NYC Admin Code В§ 26-1301** et seq. Tenants with household income вүӨ **200 % of federal poverty level** facing eviction in housing court receive right to court-appointed counsel via DSS-OCJ contracts with legal services providers ([NYC Bar Report on Statewide RTC](https://www.nycbar.org/reports/report-in-support-of-creating-a-statewide-right-to-counsel-in-eviction-proceedings/), [CSS NY First Year Results](https://www.cssny.org/news/entry/nyc-right-to-counsel)). **Newark 2018**: Newark Right to Counsel Ordinance; 200 % FPL threshold; covers appeals. **San Francisco 2018 вҖ” Proposition F**: full **UNIVERSAL right to counsel with NO income test** ([Civilrighttocounsel.org Tenant RTC organizing](https://civilrighttocounsel.org/resources/organizing_around_right_to_counsel/)). **Cleveland 2019**: eligibility вүӨ 100 % FPL with minor child / elderly / disabled. **Philadelphia 2019**: Bill 190386; phased rollout. **Boulder 2020**: Ordinance 8377; 200 % FPL. **Kansas City MO 2022**: Ordinance 220448; 200 % FPL. **Washington statewide 2021 вҖ” RCW 59.18.640** (added by **Senate Bill 5160**, Apr 22 2021) вҖ” **FIRST U.S. STATEWIDE RTC**: "Superior courts MUST appoint counsel for an indigent tenant in an unlawful detainer proceeding." Indigent defined as anyone receiving certain types of public assistance OR annual post-tax income at or below **200 percent of federal poverty level** ($25,760 for individual at enactment). Washington State Office of Civil Legal Aid (OCLA) administers the program ([Civilrighttocounsel.org Major Developments вҖ” Washington groundbreaking statewide RTC](https://civilrighttocounsel.org/major_developments/all-about-washington-states-groundbreaking-eviction-right-to-counsel/), [RCW 59.18.640 official](https://app.leg.wa.gov/RCW/default.aspx?cite=59.18.640)). **Maryland statewide 2021**: Access to Counsel in Evictions Act (HB 18 / SB 154); 50 % AMI threshold. **Connecticut statewide 2021**: Public Act 21-34; 80 % AMI threshold; phased rollout. **Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableJurisdictionLacksRtc, CompliantAttorneyAppointedUnderRtc, CompliantSanFranciscoUniversalRtcNoIncomeTest, CompliantTenantAboveThresholdSelfRepresentedOrPrivateCounsel, CompliantTenantWaivedRepresentationAfterAdvisement, ViolationCourtFailedToAppointEligibleTenant, ViolationLandlordObstructedTenantAccessToCounsel, ViolationCourtDeniedContinuanceForAttorneyAppointment, ViolationCourtFailedToNotifyTenantOfRtcRight. **Eleven jurisdictions**: NewYorkCity, Newark, SanFranciscoPropF, Cleveland, Philadelphia, Boulder, KansasCityMo, WashingtonStatewide, MarylandStatewide, ConnecticutStatewide, OtherJurisdictionWithoutRtc. **Four tenant income bands**: AtOrBelowJurisdictionalThreshold, BetweenThresholdAndModerateMeansCeiling, AboveAllRtcThresholds, ReceivingPublicAssistanceCategoricallyIndigent. **Six representation statuses**: NoAttorneyTenantSelfRepresented, CourtAppointedRtcAttorneyEnrolledLegalServicesProvider, PrivateAttorneyRetained, TenantRequestedAttorneyButCourtFailedToAppoint, LandlordObstructedTenantAccessToCounsel, CourtDeniedContinuanceForAttorneyAppointment. **Five proceeding types**: Nonpayment, HoldoverNoCause, HoldoverForCause, AdministrativeAgencyProceeding, AppellateProceeding. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-right-to-counsel-eviction`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **NYC eligible tenant with appointed counsel compliant**, **NYC eligible tenant self-represented court failed violation**, **NYC eligible tenant waived after advisement compliant**, **NYC above threshold not entitled**, **SF Prop F no-income-test universal compliant** (above-threshold tenant still entitled), **SF high-income self-represented not advised violation** (notice still required even without income test), **Washington statewide indigent appointed compliant** (RCW 59.18.640), **Washington tenant requested court failed violation**, **Cleveland lower 100 % FPL threshold** (10000 basis points = 100 %), **Maryland 50 % AMI threshold** (5000 basis points), **Connecticut 80 % AMI threshold** (8000 basis points), **other jurisdiction without RTC not applicable**, **landlord obstructed tenant access violation**, **court denied continuance violation**, **NYC court failed to notify eligible tenant violation**, **citations pin jurisdictional statutes** (NYC Local Law 136 + SF Prop F + RCW 59.18.640 + SB 5160 + MD Access to Counsel + CT PA 21-34), **constant pin jurisdictional years + thresholds** (NYC 2017 + Newark 2018 + SF Prop F 2018 + Cleveland 2019 + Philadelphia 2019 + Boulder 2020 + KC 2022 + Washington 2021 + SB 5160 + Maryland 2021 + Connecticut 2021 + default 20000 bp + Cleveland 10000 + Maryland 5000 + Connecticut 8000), **private attorney retained compliant even when income-eligible**. Sibling cluster: `rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` (iter 607 вҖ” RTC cases frequently involve fee-shifting against landlord), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (RTC dramatically affects just-cause eviction defense success rates), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (RTC overlay in lockout damage actions), `rental_eviction_record_sealing_screening` (iter 579 вҖ” RTC reduces eviction-record creation rate), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (PTFA tenant defenses frequently raised via RTC counsel).

`traderview-expense::rental_attorney_fee_clause_reciprocity` is the **multi-state residential lease attorney-fee clause reciprocity / mutualization compliance module** (iter 607) вҖ” cross-jurisdictional companion to `rental_ny_rpl_235f_roommate_law` (iter 605 NY RPL В§ 235-f), `rental_ny_rent_receipt_late_notice_requirements` (iter 603 NY RPL В§ 235-e), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 CA AB 1482 + OR SB 608 + NY Good Cause), `rental_late_fee_cap` (cross-state late-fee limits), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (cross-state deposit return). Trader-landlord critical because **attorney fees often dwarf the underlying claim** in residential disputes, and form leases overwhelmingly contain unilateral landlord-favor fee clauses that are voidable or auto-mutualized in tenant-protective jurisdictions. **California Civil Code В§ 1717**: in any action on a contract where the contract specifically provides that attorney's fees and costs incurred to enforce the contract shall be awarded to one of the parties or to the prevailing party, the party determined to be the prevailing party SHALL be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees. В§ 1717's stated purpose is to protect the weaker contracting party by making contractual fee clauses bilateral; "any provision in a contract which provides for a waiver of attorney's fees is void" ([California Legislative Information В§ 1717](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?sectionNum=1717.&lawCode=CIV), [FindLaw Civ Code В§ 1717](https://codes.findlaw.com/ca/civil-code/civ-sect-1717/)). **Florida Statute В§ 83.48 (Residential Landlord and Tenant Act)**: "in a civil action brought to enforce the provisions of the rental agreement or this part, the party in whose favor a judgment or decree has been rendered may recover reasonable attorney fees and court costs from the nonprevailing party. The right to attorney fees in this section may not be waived in a lease agreement. Attorney fees may not be awarded under this section in a claim for personal injury damages based on a breach of duty under s. 83.51." ([Florida Senate В§ 83.48](https://www.flsenate.gov/laws/statutes/2021/83.48)). **Florida Statute В§ 57.105(7)**: general contract one-way вҶ’ bilateral statute (seven states including FL, OR, WA, MT, OK, NC, HI recognize bilateral reading regardless of contract type). **New York Real Property Law В§ 234**: residential lease landlord-favor attorney-fee clause is automatically read to include a covenant by the landlord to pay the tenant's reasonable attorney fees and expenses incurred as a result of tenant's successful defense of any action; non-waivable. **Washington RCW 4.84.330**: bilateral reading of unilateral contractual attorney-fee provisions; prevailing-party rule. **Oregon ORS 20.096**: reciprocal attorney-fee provisions automatically mutualized. **Texas Property Code В§ 92.0563(c)**: tenant may recover reasonable attorney fees against landlord for violations; landlord-favor clauses enforceable as written without statutory mutualization for landlord side. **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantBilateralClauseOriginalDrafting, CompliantUnilateralClauseStatutoryMutualization, CompliantFloridaPrevailingPartyByOperationOfStatuteNoClause, CompliantPersonalInjuryClaimExcludedPerFloridaStatute, ViolationUnilateralClauseInJurisdictionWithoutMutualizationStatute, ViolationLeaseAttemptedToWaiveStatutoryReciprocity, ViolationLeaseFeesCapBelowReasonablenessStandard, ViolationPersonalInjuryClaimAttemptedToInvokeFeeShifting. **Seven jurisdictions**: California, Florida, NewYork, Washington, Oregon, Texas, OtherStateWithoutMutualizationStatute. **Six clause types**: NoClause, UnilateralLandlordFavor, UnilateralTenantFavor, Bilateral, BilateralWithCap, LandlordFavorWithExplicitNonWaiverOverride. **Three prevailing-party states**: Landlord, Tenant, NoJudgmentYet. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-attorney-fee-clause-reciprocity`. **Seventeen tests pin**: **California mutualizes unilateral landlord-favor clause for tenant** (В§ 1717 baseline), **Florida prevailing-party no clause landlord recovers** (В§ 83.48 operation by statute), **Florida personal injury no clause excluded** (В§ 83.51 carve-out), **Florida personal injury with clause violation**, **Florida lease attempt to waive reciprocity violation** (waiver void), **California bilateral with cap below reasonable violation** (В§ 1717 reasonable-fees standard), **Texas unilateral landlord-favor enforced as written** (no mutualization statute), **Texas unilateral landlord-favor tenant prevails no recovery** (asymmetric outcome), **New York mutualizes unilateral landlord-favor under RPL В§ 234**, **Washington mutualizes under RCW 4.84.330**, **Oregon mutualizes under ORS 20.096**, **bilateral clause as drafted compliant**, **no judgment yet zero recovery** (PrevailingParty::NoJudgmentYet sentinel), **American Rule other state no clause** (default fall-through), **citations pin jurisdictional statutes** (В§ 1717 + В§ 83.48 + В§ 57.105 + RPL В§ 234 + RCW 4.84.330 + ORS 20.096 + В§ 92.0563), **constant pin California Civ Code 1717**, **Florida landlord-favor-with-non-waiver-override no special treatment** (still mutualized). Sibling cluster: `rental_late_fee_cap` (cross-state monetary limit on lease provisions), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (cross-state lease provision constraints), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (overlay tenant protections that frequently produce attorney-fee disputes), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (statutory tenant damages frequently include attorney fees), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (statutory tenant attorney-fee recovery in retaliation actions).

`traderview-expense::rental_ny_rpl_235f_roommate_law` is the **NY Real Property Law В§ 235-f Roommate Law (Unlawful Restrictions on Occupancy) compliance module** (iter 605) вҖ” NY State-wide tenant-protection companion to `rental_ny_rent_receipt_late_notice_requirements` (iter 603 NY RPL В§ 235-e), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” NY Good Cause Eviction overlay), `rental_nyc_loft_law_article_7c` (iter 597 NYC Loft Law вҖ” statutory tenant cross-reference), `rental_eviction_record_sealing_screening` (iter 579 NY portion). Enacted **1983** as a tenant-protection statute prohibiting landlord restrictions on tenant rights to share residential premises with immediate family, additional occupants, and dependent children. Codified at **NY RPL В§ 235-f**. **В§ 235-f(1) definitions**: **Tenant** вҖ” person occupying or entitled to occupy residential rental premises who is either party to the lease or rental agreement OR a statutory tenant under the emergency housing rent control law, NYC rent and rehabilitation law, or Article 7-C of the Multiple Dwelling Law (Loft Law); **Occupant** вҖ” a person, other than a tenant or a member of a tenant's immediate family, occupying a premises with the consent of the tenant or tenants. **В§ 235-f(2) single-tenant lease occupancy permitted**: any lease entered into by ONE tenant shall be construed to permit occupancy by (1) the tenant; (2) immediate family of the tenant; (3) **ONE additional occupant**; (4) dependent children of the occupant; PROVIDED that the tenant or the tenant's spouse occupies the premises as their primary residence. **В§ 235-f(3) multi-tenant lease occupancy permitted**: any lease entered into by TWO OR MORE tenants shall be construed to permit occupancy by tenants, immediate family of tenants, occupants, and dependent children of occupants, PROVIDED that the total number of tenants and occupants (EXCLUDING occupants' dependent children) does not exceed the number of tenants specified in the current lease or rental agreement, AND at least one tenant or tenant's spouse occupies as primary residence. **В§ 235-f(4) tenant notice requirement**: tenant shall inform landlord of the name of any occupant within **30 days** following the commencement of occupancy OR within 30 days following a request by the landlord. **В§ 235-f(5) limited occupant rights**: no occupant nor occupant's dependent child shall, without express written permission of the landlord, acquire any right to continued occupancy in the event that the tenant vacates the premises, NOR acquire any other rights of tenancy. **В§ 235-f(6) unlawful lease restrictions**: it is unlawful for a landlord to restrict occupancy of residential premises, by express lease terms or otherwise, to a tenant or tenants or to such tenants and immediate family. Any such restriction in a lease or rental agreement is UNENFORCEABLE as against public policy. **В§ 235-f(7) waiver void**: any provision of a lease purporting to waive a provision of В§ 235-f shall be NULL AND VOID. **В§ 235-f(8) Multiple Dwelling Law and rent stabilization interaction**: nothing in В§ 235-f shall be construed to limit the right of the landlord to enforce occupancy standards contained in MDL В§ 4(7) (overcrowding) or rent stabilization laws regarding succession rights of family members. **Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantSingleTenantWith1OccupantAndDependents, CompliantMultiTenantWithinNumericCap, CompliantOccupantNoticeProvidedWithin30Days, ViolationLeaseClauseRestrictingOccupancyUnenforceable, ViolationLandlordSoughtEvictionForUnlistedOccupant, ViolationSingleTenantExceedsOneAdditionalOccupant, ViolationMultiTenantLeaseExceedsNumericCap, ViolationNoTenantPrimaryResidence, ViolationOccupantNoticeNotProvidedWithin30Days, ViolationWaiverOfRpl235fNullAndVoid. **Two lease types**: SingleTenant, MultiTenant. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-ny-rpl-235f-roommate-law`. **Seventeen tests pin**: **single tenant with 1 occupant compliant** (baseline: 1 tenant + 1 occupant + 2 dependent children OK), **single tenant with 2 occupants violation** (exceeds 1-occupant max), **multi-tenant within numeric cap compliant** (3 tenants in lease + 0 additional occupants), **multi-tenant exceeds numeric cap violation** (2 tenants + 2 occupants = 4 > 2 statutory cap), **lease clause restricting occupancy unenforceable** (per se В§ 235-f(6) violation), **waiver of В§ 235-f null and void**, **no tenant primary residence violation**, **occupant notice within 30 days compliant**, **occupant notice at exactly 30 days compliant** (statutory вүӨ reading), **occupant notice 31 days violation**, **landlord seeking eviction for excess occupant violation**, **dependent children not counted against cap** (5 dependent children OK in multi-tenant scenario), **citations pin RPL В§ 235-f subsections** ((1) + (2) + (3) + (4) + (5) + (6) + (7) + (8)), **citations pin MDL В§ 4(7) overcrowding + Loft Law Article 7-C**, **constant pin 30-day occupant notice**, **constant pin 1-additional-occupant single tenant max**, **constant pin 1983 original enactment year**. Trader-landlord critical because В§ 235-f is the foundational tenant-protection statute for shared-occupancy arrangements in NY and is the basis for the vast majority of "illegal sublet" eviction defense actions вҖ” tenants facing eviction for unauthorized roommates routinely raise В§ 235-f as a complete defense. The 30-day occupant notice requirement is the most-overlooked tenant obligation вҖ” many tenants never inform landlord of occupant additions, which can be used by landlord to challenge occupant status if eviction proceeding commenced. The void-waiver provision under В§ 235-f(7) is particularly powerful вҖ” boilerplate lease clauses purporting to restrict occupancy automatically fail without further analysis. The numeric cap in multi-tenant leases creates counterintuitive results: a 2-tenant lease cannot accommodate more than 2 total people (excluding dependent children), preventing 2 tenants from each bringing a roommate. Sibling cluster: `rental_ny_rent_receipt_late_notice_requirements` (iter 603 вҖ” adjacent NY tenant-protection statute), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” NY Good Cause Eviction overlays В§ 235-f protections), `rental_nyc_loft_law_article_7c` (iter 597 вҖ” В§ 235-f(1) explicitly cross-references Article 7-C statutory tenants), `rental_eviction_record_sealing_screening` (iter 579 вҖ” NY RPAPL В§ 745(2)(c)(iv) вҖ” В§ 235-f-based eviction proceedings produce records subject to sealing).

`traderview-expense::rental_ny_rent_receipt_late_notice_requirements` is the **NY Real Property Law В§ 235-e Duty to Provide Written Receipt + Five-Day Late Rent Notice compliance module** (iter 603) вҖ” NY State-wide landlord obligation companion to `rental_late_fee_cap` (cap on late fees), `rental_just_cause_eviction` (iter 573 вҖ” NY Good Cause Eviction portion), `rental_security_deposit_interest` (NY 1.5% interest requirement), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter`, `rental_eviction_record_sealing_screening` (iter 579 вҖ” NY portion under RPAPL В§ 745(2)(c)(iv)). NY RPL В§ 235-e establishes three distinct landlord obligations: **В§ 235-e(a) written receipt requirement** вҖ” upon receipt of rent payment in cash OR any instrument other than the personal check of the lessee, lessor (or agent) must provide tenant with a written receipt containing FIVE required elements (date + amount + premises + period + signature/title); **В§ 235-e(b) recordkeeping** вҖ” lessor must maintain records of cash payments for at least 3 years; **В§ 235-e(d) five-day late rent notice** (HSTPA 2019 amendment, Laws of 2019, c. 36) вҖ” if landlord fails to receive rent payment within 5 days of the date specified in the lease agreement, landlord must send tenant a written notice by **CERTIFIED MAIL** stating the failure; email and text messages are NOT sufficient even if acknowledged. **Receipt timing**: personally-transmitted payment вҶ’ IMMEDIATELY; indirectly-transmitted payment вҶ’ within **15 DAYS** of lessor's or agent's receipt. **Affirmative defense**: failure of lessor or agent to send the five-day certified-mail late notice may be used by the tenant as an AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE in any eviction proceeding based on non-payment of rent вҖ” tenant raising this defense need not prove actual prejudice; the failure itself is the defense. **Coverage**: В§ 235-e(d) applies to BOTH residential and commercial tenancies (one of the very few NY tenant-protection provisions that covers commercial leases). **HSTPA 2019** (Housing Stability and Tenant Protection Act of 2019, Laws of 2019, c. 36) overhauled NY landlord-tenant law and added the В§ 235-e(d) certified-mail late-notice requirement along with numerous other tenant protections. **Twelve-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantCashReceiptProvidedImmediately, CompliantIndirectPaymentReceiptWithin15Days, CompliantFiveDayLateNoticeSentByCertifiedMail, CompliantAllReceiptAndNoticeObligationsMet, ViolationCashReceiptNotProvidedImmediately, ViolationIndirectPaymentReceiptNotProvidedWithin15Days, ViolationReceiptMissingRequiredElements, ViolationFiveDayLateNoticeNotSentByCertifiedMail, ViolationFiveDayLateNoticeSentByEmailOrTextOnlyNotSufficient, ViolationRecordsNotMaintained3Years, AggravatedViolationAffirmativeDefenseAvailableToTenantInNonpaymentProceeding. **Four payment methods**: Cash, NonPersonalCheckInstrument, PersonalCheck, ElectronicTransfer. **Two tenancy types**: Residential, Commercial. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-ny-rent-receipt-late-notice-requirements`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **cash personal immediate receipt compliant** (baseline), **cash personal receipt not immediate violation**, **indirect payment receipt within 15 days compliant** (10 days OK), **indirect payment receipt at exactly 15 days compliant** (statutory вүӨ reading), **indirect payment receipt 16 days violation** (boundary), **receipt missing required elements violation**, **cash records not 3 years violation** (2 years), **five-day late notice sent by certified mail compliant**, **five-day late notice not sent violation**, **five-day late notice email only not sufficient violation** (HSTPA-specific вҖ” email/text NOT sufficient even if acknowledged), **aggravated violation when nonpayment proceeding commenced** (tenant affirmative defense available), **personal check no receipt required** (statutory carve-out), **rent within 5 days no late notice required** (statutory вүӨ 5 day reading), **commercial tenancy 5-day notice also required** (unusual commercial-side coverage), **citations pin RPL В§ 235-e subsections** ((a) + (b) + (d)), **citations pin HSTPA 2019 + RPAPL В§ 711**, **constant pins 5-day late notice**, **constant pins 15-day indirect payment receipt**, **constant pins 3-year records retention**, **constant pins 5 required receipt fields**, **constant pins HSTPA 2019 year**.
