Month-to-month and shorter tenancies are excluded. **Commercial coverage added by SB 1103 (Caballero, 2024)**, effective January 1, 2025, codified at В§ 1632(b)(1)(C): nonresidential-zoned commercial leases with a "qualified commercial tenant" вҖ” a microenterprise, a restaurant with FEWER THAN 10 EMPLOYEES, or a nonprofit organization with FEWER THAN 20 EMPLOYEES, who has provided the landlord with written notice of qualified status and a self-attestation regarding employee count. **Own-interpreter exemption** under В§ 1632(h) вҖ” three conjunctive prongs: (1) interpreter is a person, NOT A MINOR (18+ years); (2) able to speak fluently and read with full understanding BOTH English AND the negotiated language; (3) NOT employed by or made available through the landlord/business. Failing any prong defeats the exemption and the translation is required. **Non-compliance remedy** under В§ 1632(k): the aggrieved party may **RESCIND the contract**. Statutory rescission is the central enforcement mechanism вҖ” no separate damages provision in В§ 1632 itself. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableEnglishNegotiation, NotApplicableOtherLanguageNotCovered, NotApplicableResidentialMonthToMonthShortTenancy, NotApplicableCommercialNonQualifiedTenant, CompliantTranslationProvidedBeforeExecution, ExemptTenantUsedOwnInterpreter, ViolationInterpreterDoesNotMeetStatutoryDefinition (rescindable), ViolationTranslationNotProvidedBeforeExecutionRescindable, ViolationTranslationInWrongLanguageRescindable, ViolationPartialTranslationMissingTermsRescindable. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-multilingual-lease-translation`. **Twenty-five tests pin**: **Spanish residential full translation compliant** (baseline), **English negotiation not applicable** (no translation required), **Chinese negotiation covered same as Spanish**, **Tagalog negotiation covered**, **Vietnamese negotiation covered**, **Korean negotiation covered** (each of the five covered languages individually verified), **other uncovered language not applicable** (e.g., French, Russian, Arabic NOT covered by statute), **residential month-to-month not applicable** (statutory minimum > 1 month), **commercial non-qualified tenant not applicable**, **commercial qualified tenant SB 1103 covered**, **translation not provided before execution rescindable** (per se violation вҖ” timing is everything), **translation in wrong language rescindable** (right language is the negotiated one, not English), **partial translation missing terms rescindable** (must cover every term and condition), **tenant's own qualifying interpreter exempt** (all three prongs met), **interpreter under 18 disqualifies exemption** (minor cannot serve as own interpreter), **interpreter provided by landlord disqualifies exemption** (independence requirement), **interpreter not fluent disqualifies exemption**, **citations pin В§ 1632(b) + (h) + (k) subsections**, **citations pin SB 1103 + Reyes v. Superior Court case law** (Reyes v. Superior Court, 118 Cal. App. 3d 159 (1981) вҖ” original Spanish-language translation case that established the policy framework underlying В§ 1632), **constant pins 5 covered languages**, **constant pins interpreter age 18**, **constant pins SB 1103 effective 2025**, **constant pins SB 1103 restaurant 10 employees**, **constant pins SB 1103 nonprofit 20 employees**, **interpreter exactly 18 qualifies** (statutory boundary). Trader-landlord critical because California's residential trader-landlord inventory is concentrated in markets with the highest non-English-speaking household densities in the country (LA, San Diego, San Francisco/Bay Area, Sacramento, Fresno) вҖ” a trader-landlord acquiring multifamily property in California who uses a Spanish-speaking property manager or Spanish-language marketing has triggered В§ 1632(b) on every subsequent residential lease executed in Spanish, and the rescission remedy is exercisable AT ANY TIME during the lease term, meaning a single missed translation can void rent collections and trigger refund of advance payments years after lease commencement. Sibling cluster: `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (CA Civ. Code В§ 1950.6 + В§ 1786.40 ICRAA tenant-screening denial regime), `adverse_action_notice` (FCRA В§ 615 federal floor), `rental_source_of_income_discrimination` (FEHA Cal. Gov. Code В§ 12955 source-of-income protection), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (parallel California-specific application-stage regime), `rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` (FHA + state-FEHA accommodation regime).

`traderview-expense::rental_heat_minimum_temperature_season` is the **multi-jurisdiction heat-season minimum-temperature compliance module** (iter 575) вҖ” daily-operational landlord compliance regime that pins the most-cited housing-code violation type in NYC and similar cold-climate jurisdictions. Direct companion to `rental_radiator_steam_heat_safety` (NYC LL 79/2018 + Ben Z's Law radiator burn prevention вҖ” distinct downstream safety obligation that attaches to the same steam-radiator infrastructure governed by the heat-minimum regime), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (CA 120В°F hot water minimum at faucet вҖ” parallel temperature-floor obligation), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (winter heat-season CO risk peaks). **Four major jurisdictions pinned**: (1) **NYC** вҖ” NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2029 (Article 8 of Chapter 2 of Title 27, Heat and Hot Water): heat season **October 1 - May 31**; daytime (6 AM - 10 PM) 68В°F when outdoor < 55В°F; nighttime (10 PM - 6 AM) 62В°F regardless of outdoor вҖ” NYC HPD heat violations are the #1 cited housing-code violation. (2) **Chicago** вҖ” Chicago Municipal Code В§ 13-196-410 + В§ 5-12-110: heat season **September 15 - June 1**; daytime (8:30 AM - 10:30 PM) 68В°F; nighttime (10:30 PM - 8:30 AM) **66В°F** вҖ” among the highest nighttime minimums in the country. (3) **Boston / Massachusetts** вҖ” 105 CMR 410.201 (MA State Sanitary Code Chapter II Article II) + Boston Housing Code: heat season **September 15 - June 15** (longest heat season in the country at nine months); daytime (7:00 AM - 11:00 PM) 68В°F; nighttime (11:01 PM - 6:59 AM) 64В°F. (4) **Philadelphia** вҖ” Section PM-602 of the Philadelphia Property Maintenance Code: heat season **October 1 - April 30**; **all hours 68В°F** (simplest regime вҖ” no day/night split); 68В°F also required in May and September when outdoor < 40В°F (shoulder-season conditional trigger). **All four jurisdictions prohibit portable space heaters / ovens / cooking appliances as primary heat source** вҖ” landlords cannot discharge their heat obligation by giving tenants a space heater (one of the most common audit-discovered landlord shortcuts). **Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableOutsideHeatSeason, TemperatureCompliantMeetsLocalMinimum, TemperatureViolationBelowDaytimeMinimum, TemperatureViolationBelowNighttimeMinimum, TemperatureViolationDuringHeatSeasonNoHeatProvided, ViolationProhibitedSpaceHeaterPrimarySource, DefaultJurisdictionNoStatutoryHeatRegime. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-heat-minimum-temperature-season`. **Twenty-six tests pin**: **NYC day 70В°F indoor meets 68 minimum**, **NYC day 65В°F indoor below 68 violation**, **NYC day outdoor above 55 no trigger** (conditional outdoor temperature), **NYC night 60В°F indoor below 62 violation**, **NYC night outdoor irrelevant for 62 minimum** (nighttime is unconditional), **NYC June outside heat season**, **Chicago day 68 minimum at 8:30 AM window starts** (9 AM tests window inclusion), **Chicago night 66 minimum higher than NYC 62**, **Boston day 68 minimum at 8 AM**, **Boston night 64 minimum**, **Philadelphia all hours 68 no day/night split**, **Philadelphia shoulder September outdoor below 40 triggers**, **Philadelphia shoulder September outdoor above 40 no trigger**, **space heater primary source violation any jurisdiction**, **default jurisdiction no statutory regime**, **citations pin all four jurisdictions** (В§ 27-2029 + В§ 13-196-410 + 105 CMR 410.201 + PM-602), **constant pins NYC 68 day minimum**, **constant pins NYC 62 night minimum**, **constant pins NYC 55 outdoor trigger**, **constant pins Chicago 66 night minimum**, **constant pins Boston 64 night minimum**, **constant pins Philadelphia 68 all hours**, **constant pins Philadelphia 40 shoulder trigger**, **NYC October 1 heat season starts** (month boundary), **NYC May 31 heat season still active** (month boundary), **very cold indoor below zero still violation** (negative-temperature math defense). Trader-landlord critical because heat violations carry significant compounding exposure: HPD daily-accruing penalties (up to $1,000 per day per violation in NYC), 7-day repair window before complaint escalation, $25K+ administrative-tribunal civil penalties on repeat violations, plus tenant rent-escrow rights under habitability warranty + criminal mischief charges in extreme cases. The HOUR-OF-DAY enforcement means a single missed boiler-cycling event at 2 AM produces a documented violation, and HPD enforcement officers conduct night-shift inspections specifically to catch nighttime heat-floor breaches. Sibling cluster: `rental_radiator_steam_heat_safety` (steam-system child-burn prevention вҖ” paired with heat-delivery infrastructure), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (CA 120В°F minimum вҖ” parallel temperature-floor regime), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (winter heat-season gas-system risk), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (combustion heat-source CO risk), `rental_tenant_rent_escrow_habitability_dispute` (rent escrow remedy when heat is denied), `rental_boiler_inspection_compliance` (boiler-system inspection upstream of heat delivery).

`traderview-expense::rental_just_cause_eviction` is the **multi-state just-cause / good-cause eviction compliance module** (iter 573) вҖ” covers the five major state regimes that override the common-law lease-termination default by requiring a landlord to assert a statutorily-enumerated good cause before non-renewing a lease or evicting a tenant, and that cap annual rent increases at a CPI-indexed local rate. Direct companion to `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (iter 571) as a state-level layer stacked atop the federal PTFA floor. **Five state regimes pinned**: (1) **NJ Anti-Eviction Act of 1974** (N.J.S.A. 2A:18-61.1) вҖ” first state in the country to enact just-cause eviction; 18 enumerated grounds; broadest applicability (no rent-increase cap, but most stringent eviction-grounds test); (2) **CA Tenant Protection Act of 2019 / AB 1482** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2 + В§ 1947.12) вҖ” just-cause for evictions + annual rent cap of 5% plus regional CPI with hard ceiling at 10%; (3) **OR SB 608 (2019)** (ORS 90.323 + В§ 90.427) вҖ” first statewide just-cause + rent-cap regime; 7% plus annual CPI; exemption for properties built within last 15 years (the only major regime that uses property AGE rather than absolute year cutoff); (4) **NY Good Cause Eviction Law of 2024** (Part HH of L. 2024, c. 56 + RPL В§ 226-c) вҖ” effective April 20, 2024; "local rent standard" = CPI + 5% with hard ceiling at 10%; exempts small landlords (вүӨ 10 units statewide), post-2009 construction (the bright-line year cutoff approach), owner-occupied buildings with < 11 units, rent-stabilized housing, public housing, and condo/co-op rentals; (5) **WA HB 2114 (2024)** (RCW 59.18.650) вҖ” just-cause + 7% plus annual CPI rent cap. **Three action types**: NotApplicable, NonRenewalOrEviction, RentIncrease. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, GoodCauseLandlordHasValidReasonEvictionAllowed (statutorily-enumerated cause asserted), GoodCauseLandlordHasNoValidReasonEvictionBarred (no enumerated cause = eviction barred), RentIncreaseWithinLocalCapAllowed, RentIncreaseExceedsLocalCapPresumedUnreasonable (rebuttable presumption in court), ExemptSmallLandlordUnder10Units (NY), ExemptNewConstructionPostThresholdYear (NY post-2009 OR OR < 15 years), ExemptOwnerOccupiedUnderUnitThreshold (NY < 11 units), ExemptSubsidizedOrRentStabilizedHousing (NY separate regulatory regime), DefaultJurisdictionNoJustCauseRegime (common-law fallthrough). Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-just-cause-eviction`. **Twenty-five tests pin**: **NY rent increase within 8.82% cap allowed** (3.82% CPI + 5% base = 8.82% cap, 7.5% proposed вҶ’ within), **NY rent increase exceeds cap presumed unreasonable** (10% proposed vs 8.82% cap), **NY rent increase hard ceiling 10% with high CPI** (CPI 8% + 5% = 13% would apply but capped at 10%), **NY small landlord exempt under 10 units**, **NY small landlord boundary exactly 10 units exempt** (вүӨ 10 threshold), **NY small landlord boundary 11 units NOT exempt**, **NY new construction post-2009 exempt** (2015 build), **NY 2009 construction boundary NOT exempt** (year > 2009 is exempt; 2009 itself stays in), **NY owner-occupied under 11 units exempt**, **NY subsidized rent-stabilized exempt**, **CA AB 1482 rent cap with CPI** (3.5% CPI + 5% base = 8.5%), **OR SB 608 new construction under 15 years exempt** (2015 build вҶ’ age 11 in 2026 вҶ’ exempt), **OR SB 608 old property caps at 7% + CPI** (1990 build, 3% CPI вҶ’ 10% cap), **NJ Anti-Eviction Act good cause enumerated allowed**, **NJ Anti-Eviction Act no good cause eviction barred**, **default jurisdiction no regime falls through**, **citations pin all five state regimes** (N.J.S.A. 2A:18-61.1 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2 + ORS 90.323 + NY April 20 2024 + RCW 59.18.650), **constant pins NY 5% base Г— 100** (500), **constant pins NY 10% max cap Г— 100** (1,000), **constant pins NY small landlord 10 units**, **constant pins NY 2009 construction cutoff**, **constant pins OR 15-year new-construction threshold**, **constant pins NJ 18 enumerated good cause grounds**, **zero current rent returns 0% change** (division-by-zero defense), **proposed rent below current no increase** (no negative percentage). Trader-landlord critical because just-cause regimes are spreading rapidly вҖ” NY 2024, WA 2024, plus active legislative consideration in MA, MD, CO, RI, and CT as of 2026 вҖ” and the operational impact is total: a trader-landlord acquiring property in a just-cause jurisdiction can no longer rely on lease-end non-renewal to remove a tenant, must track local CPI to set rent increases, must navigate property-class exemption tests, and faces statutory damages plus tenant attorneys' fees on rent-cap violations. Sibling cluster: `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (rent-stabilization adjacent but distinct вҖ” rent stabilization applies to specific covered units, just-cause applies to ALL non-exempt units), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (eviction-pretext defense), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (eviction-procedure adjunct), `rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` (federal 90-day floor stacks under state just-cause for foreclosure successors), `rental_late_fee_cap`, `rental_security_deposit_return_notice`.

`traderview-expense::rental_foreclosure_tenant_protection_ptfa` is the **federal Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act (PTFA) compliance module** (iter 571) вҖ” direct federal companion to `rental_lead_paint_disclosure` (iter 569 В§ 4852d) as a universal-applicability landlord-side federal obligation triggered the moment a trader-landlord becomes the "immediate successor in interest" at a foreclosure sale (typically auction purchase or REO purchase from the foreclosing bank). **Statutory chain**: originally enacted as **Pub. L. 111-22 Title VII В§В§ 701-704** (Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009, May 20, 2009) with a December 31, 2014 sunset; briefly expired January 1, 2015 through June 22, 2018; **reinstated PERMANENTLY by Pub. L. 115-174 В§ 304** (Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief, and Consumer Protection Act of 2018, effective June 23, 2018) вҖ” no further sunset. **Three core obligations** on the immediate successor in interest: (1) **bona fide WRITTEN LEASE**: successor takes title SUBJECT TO the tenant's right to occupy through the remaining lease term; (2) **bona fide MONTH-TO-MONTH or non-written tenancy**: must serve minimum **90 days** written vacate notice before requiring tenant to vacate per В§ 702(a)(2)(A); (3) **owner-occupy exception** (the ONLY carveout that can terminate a bona fide lease early): if the successor sells to a purchaser who will occupy as PRIMARY RESIDENCE, the lease terminates but the tenant STILL receives 90 days written notice. **Bona fide tenant three-prong definition** under В§ 702(b) вҖ” all three must be satisfied: (1) tenant is NOT the mortgagor or spouse/parent/child of the mortgagor; (2) lease was the result of an ARM'S-LENGTH transaction; (3) rent is NOT substantially less than fair market rent (FMR), OR rent is reduced/subsidized due to federal/state/local subsidy (Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher, project-based Section 8, HOME, public housing). **"Substantially less than FMR" test** вҖ” no statutory bright line; HUD guidance and most circuit-court interpretations treat rent below 80% of FMR as substantially less, so the module implements the 80%-of-FMR threshold with explicit constant pinning. **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, BonaFideLeaseAllowedFullTermRemaining, BonaFideMonthToMonthMustGive90DayVacateNotice, BonaFideLeaseEarlyTermByOwnerOccupyExceptionStill90DayNotice (lease cut short, 90-day notice still required), NonBonaFideTenantNoProtectionMortgagorOrImmediateFamily, NonBonaFideTenantNoProtectionNonArmsLengthTransaction, NonBonaFideTenantNoProtectionBelowMarketRentNotSubsidized, ViolationLessThan90DayNoticeServed (notice is void). Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-foreclosure-tenant-protection-ptfa`. **Twenty-two tests pin**: **bona fide lease protected through remaining term** (6 months вҶ’ 180 days), **bona fide month-to-month gets 90-day notice**, **owner-occupy exception terminates lease but still 90-day notice**, **mortgagor or family not protected** (prong 1 fails), **non-arm's-length lease not protected** (prong 2 fails), **below-80%-FMR not subsidized not protected** (prong 3 fails), **below-80%-FMR Section 8 subsidized STILL PROTECTED** (subsidy carveout), **FMR threshold boundary 80% is protected**, **FMR threshold boundary 79% not protected** (one percentage point below 80% loses protection), **not acquired at foreclosure PTFA not applicable**, **less than 90-day notice is violation** (30 days served вҶ’ void, tenant still entitled to full 90 days), **exactly 90-day notice satisfies minimum**, **citations pin Pub. L. 111-22 + Pub. L. 115-174 + June 23, 2018**, **citations pin В§ 702(a) + В§ 702(b) subsections**, **citations pin Bank of Am., N.A. v. Owens case law**, **constant pins 90-day vacate minimum**, **constant pins 80% FMR threshold**, **constant pins 2018 permanent reinstatement year**, **long-lease remaining term capped at remaining days** (36 months вҶ’ 1,080 days), **very-short-lease remaining falls to 90-day floor**, **zero FMR assumed at market protected** (division-by-zero defense), **very large rent saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense). Trader-landlord critical because PTFA exposure attaches at the AUCTION/REO PURCHASE moment, not at downstream eviction вҖ” a trader-landlord who bids successfully at a foreclosure auction without first identifying bona fide tenants and computing the 90-day vacate horizon takes title to property that they CANNOT clear of tenants for at least 90 days (or up to multiple years for written-lease bona fide tenants), and any premature eviction action is voidable with tenant standing to sue for wrongful eviction. Sibling cluster: `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (different notice-period regime for non-foreclosure context), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (general anti-retaliation), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (self-help eviction is per se illegal regardless of PTFA posture), `rental_tenant_relocation_assistance` (some states require additional relocation payments stacked on PTFA notice), `rental_property_registration` (most jurisdictions require new owner to register within 30 days of acquisition вҖ” a parallel obligation triggered by the same closing event).

`traderview-expense::rental_lead_paint_disclosure` is the **federal Title X В§ 1018 pre-lease lead-based paint disclosure compliance module** (iter 569) вҖ” the foundational UNIVERSAL federal landlord obligation for any pre-1978 target housing. Codified at **42 U.S.C. В§ 4852d** (Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992, Pub. L. 102-550 Title X В§ 1018) + implementing regulations at **24 C.F.R. Part 35 Subpart A** (HUD Lead Disclosure Rule) + **40 C.F.R. Part 745 Subpart F** (EPA Lead Disclosure Rule). Direct companion to `rental_lead_pipe_disclosure` (lead service line water вҖ” Safe Drinking Water Act not paint), `rental_post_construction_lead_dust_clearance` (post-renovation EPA RRP dust-wipe clearance вҖ” distinct downstream obligation triggered by renovation work, not pre-lease disclosure). **Five mandatory disclosure elements** every pre-1978 lease must satisfy before becoming binding: (1) the federal Lead Warning Statement inserted verbatim into the lease; (2) disclosure of any known lead-based paint and/or lead-based paint hazards in the unit OR an affirmative statement of no knowledge; (3) all available LBP records and reports in the landlord's possession provided to tenant; (4) the EPA **"Protect Your Family From Lead in Your Home"** pamphlet (publication EPA-747-K-12-001) delivered; (5) signed tenant acknowledgment of receipt. **Four narrow exemptions per 24 C.F.R. В§ 35.82(b)**: (a) zero-bedroom dwellings (efficiencies, lofts, dormitories) UNLESS a child under 6 is expected to reside; (b) short-term leases of **вүӨ 100 days** where no renewal or extension is possible; (c) housing specifically designated for the elderly or persons with disabilities UNLESS a child under 6 is expected to reside; (d) post-1977 housing (built on or after 1978-01-01, so not "target housing" by statutory definition). **Civil penalty up to $22,263 per violation** under 42 U.S.C. В§ 4852d(b)(5) (2025 inflation-adjusted maximum) AND **joint-and-several liability for treble actual damages** under 42 U.S.C. В§ 4852d(b)(3) вҖ” the treble-damages exposure is what makes this disclosure rule the most-litigated federal landlord obligation. **Twelve-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, DisclosureCompleteAllFiveElementsLandlordCompliant, DisclosureIncompleteMissingEpaPamphletViolation, DisclosureIncompleteMissingKnownLbpDisclosureViolation, DisclosureIncompleteMissingLeadWarningStatementInLeaseViolation, DisclosureIncompleteMissingSignedAcknowledgmentViolation, DisclosureIncompleteMissingAvailableReportsViolation, DisclosureIncompleteMissingMultipleElementsAggravatedViolation (вүҘ2 elements missing), ExemptZeroBedroomNoChildExpected, ExemptShortTermLeaseUnder100Days, ExemptElderlyOrDisabledHousingNoChildExpected, ExemptPost1977Housing. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-lead-paint-disclosure`. **Twenty-six tests pin**: all-five-elements-satisfied compliant outcome, **post-1977 housing exempt** with year-built note, **1978 unit itself exempt threshold boundary**, **1977 unit target housing disclosure required**, short-term lease вүӨ100 days no renewal exempt, short-term lease with renewal possible NOT exempt, zero-bedroom no child expected exempt, zero-bedroom with child expected disclosure required, elderly-only no child expected exempt, elderly-only with child expected disclosure required, missing-Lead-Warning-Statement-only per se violation, missing-known-LBP-only per se violation, missing-EPA-pamphlet-only per se violation, missing-signed-acknowledgment-only per se violation, missing-two-elements aggravated violation, **treble damages compute on actual damages** ($10K damages вҶ’ $30K treble + $22,263 civil = $52,263 total exposure), citations pin 42 U.S.C. В§ 4852d + Title X В§ 1018 + 24 C.F.R. Part 35 + 40 C.F.R. Part 745, citations pin В§ 4852d(b)(3) treble + В§ 4852d(b)(5) civil-penalty subsections, **constant pins $22,263 civil penalty max (2,226,300 cents)**, **constant pins 100-day short-term lease threshold**, **constant pins child-under-6 trigger**, **constant pins 1978 pre-1978 target housing threshold**, **constant pins 3Г— treble damages multiplier**, **very large damages saturating does not overflow** (u64::MAX defense), **100-day lease no-renewal exempt boundary**, **101-day lease no-renewal disclosure required boundary**. Trader-landlord critical because pre-1978 housing stock is concentrated in the urban-core multifamily inventory most active trader-landlords acquire вҖ” and the treble-damages exposure attaches even when the actual lead-exposure injury is small, making В§ 4852d the highest-leverage compliance failure in the federal landlord-liability map.

`traderview-expense::rental_dog_bite_liability` is the **landlord dog-bite liability compliance module** (iter 567) вҖ” covers landlord negligence-based exposure when a tenant's dog bites a third party (visitor, guest, neighbor, delivery person). Direct sibling to `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` (pet-policy parallel), `rental_pet_deposit_separate_security` (pet-financial parallel), `rental_emergency_action_plan_high_rise` (premises-safety parallel), `rental_video_surveillance_retention` (premises-liability evidentiary parallel). Trader-landlord critical because dog-bite liability for LANDLORDS turns on a NEGLIGENCE standard (knowledge + ability to abate + failure to act) вҖ” strict-liability statutes apply to the DOG OWNER (tenant), not the landlord. (1) **CA Cal. Civ. Code В§ 3342** imposes strict liability on dog owners; landlord exposure follows **Uccello v. Laudenslayer (1975) 44 Cal. App. 3d 504** knowledge-plus-ability-to-act test + **Code Civ. Proc. В§ 335.1** 2-year statute of limitations; (2) **NY Agriculture & Markets Law В§ 121 + В§ 123** establishes dangerous-dog statute + **Bard v. Jahnke (2006) 6 N.Y.3d 592** vicious-propensities standard; (3) **IL 510 ILCS 5/16 (Animal Control Act)** dog-owner strict liability for unprovoked injuries to lawful-presence victims + landlord premises-liability framework; (4) **WA RCW 16.08.040** is the STRICTEST in the country вҖ” dog-owner strict liability for bites to lawful-presence victims REGARDLESS of prior viciousness or owner knowledge; landlord on common-law negligence + premises liability; (5) **TX one-bite rule** + **Marshall v. Ranne (1974) 511 S.W.2d 255** vicious-propensity test; (6) **FL Fla. Stat. В§ 767.04** dog-owner strict liability with comparative-negligence reduction + **В§ 767.13(2)** dangerous-dog classification procedure; (7) **OH ORC В§ 955.28** dog-owner strict liability for property/personal injury subject to statutory exceptions.

**Three landlord-knowledge statuses**: ActualKnowledgeOfDangerousPropensity, ConstructiveKnowledgePresumed, NoKnowledgeReasonablyAttributable.

**Three landlord-control-over-premises types**: BiteInCommonAreaControlledByLandlord, BiteInTenantsLeasedPremises, BiteOffPremisesDuringWalk.

**Three landlord-action statuses**: TookReasonableAbatementSteps, FailedToAbateDespiteKnowledge, NoAbatementRequiredAbsentKnowledgeOrControl.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoLandlordLiabilityNoKnowledgeOrNoControl, LandlordLiableKnowledgeFailedToAbateCommonArea, LandlordLiableKnowledgeFailedToAbateTenantPremises, LandlordTookReasonableAbatementNoLiability, DogOwnerStrictLiabilityNoLandlordExposure.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-dog-bite-liability`. Eighteen tests pin: **no knowledge no landlord liability** (negligence elements not met); **reasonable abatement no liability** (notice + lease termination + common-area restriction); **CA knowledge failed to abate common area liability** ($50K + $15K + $50K = $115K exposure + Uccello + В§ 3342); **CA knowledge failed to abate tenant premises liability**; **off-premises walk without knowledge dog owner only**; **NY negligence standard pins Bard v. Jahnke** (В§В§ 121 + 123); **IL 510 ILCS 5/16 Animal Control Act**; **WA RCW 16.08.040 strictest in country**; **TX one-bite rule Marshall v. Ranne**; **FL В§ 767.04 comparative-negligence reduction**; **OH ORC В§ 955.28 strict liability**; **default jurisdiction one-bite rule common law**; **typical dog-bite tort baseline constant pins $50,000**; **typical emotional distress constant pins $15,000**; **very large damages no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating); **zero damages uses baseline emotional plus tort** ($65K floor); **constructive knowledge with common area failure liability**; **note pins Uccello v. Laudenslayer California**.

`traderview-expense::rental_waste_recycling_collection_mandate` is the **waste collection + recycling + organic composting compliance module** (iter 565 вҖ” milestone) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure under state and municipal waste-diversion mandates requiring separate streams for trash, recycling, and organic/compostable waste. Direct sibling to `rental_energy_benchmarking` (environmental-compliance parallel), `rental_pesticide_application_notification` (chemical-disclosure parallel), `rental_ev_charging_accommodation` (iter 563 вҖ” climate-policy parallel), `rental_organic_waste_collection_disclosure` (pre-existing organic-waste sibling). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA SB 1383 (Short-Lived Climate Pollutants Reduction Strategy; Cal. Pub. Res. Code В§В§ 42652-42653)** collection effective Jan 1, 2022, penalties effective Jan 1, 2024 вҖ” ALL multifamily properties (3+ units; 5+ in some cities) must provide organics cart for tenant food waste + landscaping debris + tenant education at move-in + 2-weeks-pre-move-out + annually; **CA AB 939** (Integrated Waste Management Act of 1989) foundational 50%-diversion mandate; **75% diversion goal** by Jan 1, 2025; (2) **NYC LL 87/2009 + NYC RCNY В§ 16 + Mandatory Curbside Composting Law** (2024-2025 staged borough rollout) requires residential building organic-waste separation; **NYC LL 142/2013** textile recycling; **NYC LL 199/2017** commercial waste zones; (3) **VT Universal Recycling Act 2012 (Act 148)** bans recyclables + leaf and yard debris + food scraps from landfill; multifamily food scrap diversion required since Jul 1, 2020; (4) **MA Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 21A + 310 CMR 16.00** commercial organic waste ban effective Oct 1, 2014; threshold lowered Nov 1, 2022 to 0.5 ton/week; (5) **WA HB 1799 (2023)** organic-waste-diversion expansion; (6) **OR HB 2065** organic-waste diversion; (7) **CO HB 22-1355** Producer Responsibility Program for Statewide Recycling effective 2024+; (8) **NJ A4416 Food Waste Recycling Act + N.J.A.C. 7:26-2A.13** 1 ton/week commercial generator threshold. SB 1383 daily penalty escalation: $50/day first-tier 30-day window then $100/day repeat tier.

**Nine jurisdictions**: CaliforniaSb1383, NewYorkCity, Vermont, Massachusetts, Washington, Oregon, Colorado, NewJersey, Default.

**Five compliance statuses**: AllStreamsProvidedWithSignageAndEducation, OrganicWasteBinNotProvidedViolation, RecyclingBinNotProvidedViolation, TenantEducationProtocolNotFollowed, BinsProvidedButSignageOrMonitoringMissing.

**Two building-size-coverage statuses**: CoveredByMandate, BelowJurisdictionalThreshold.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, BelowJurisdictionalThresholdNoMandate, CompliantAllStreamsAndEducationProvided, OrganicWasteBinNotProvidedSb1383Violation, RecyclingBinNotProvidedViolation, TenantEducationProtocolNotFollowed, SignageOrContaminationMonitoringMissing, DefaultJurisdictionStandardMunicipalCollectionOnly.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-waste-recycling-collection-mandate`. Twenty-one tests pin: **default jurisdiction no mandate** (Seattle/Boston/Cambridge/Portland local-ordinance overlay note); **below threshold no mandate** (1-2 unit + 5+ unit thresholds); **CA compliant all streams with signage and education** (В§В§ 42652-42653); **CA organic waste bin not provided violation** (10 days Г— $50 = $500); **CA organic violation repeat tier after 30 days** (30 Г— $50 + 30 Г— $100 = $4,500); **CA recycling bin not provided violation**; **CA tenant education protocol not followed** (move-in + 2-weeks-pre-move-out + annual); **CA bins provided but signage or monitoring missing** (multilingual signage); **NYC Local Law 87 compliance**; **Vermont Act 148 compliance** (Jul 1 2020 effective); **Massachusetts 310 CMR 16 compliance** (0.5 ton/week threshold); **Washington HB 1799 compliance**; **Oregon HB 2065 compliance**; **Colorado HB 22-1355 compliance**; **New Jersey Food Waste Act compliance** (N.J.A.C. 7:26-2A.13); **SB 1383 daily penalty first tier constant pins $50**; **SB 1383 daily penalty repeat tier constant pins $100**; **NYC LL 97 first offense constant pins $100**; **VT Act 148 base penalty constant pins $500**; **very large days no overflow in CA penalty calc** (u32::MAX saturating); **note pins 75% diversion goal 2025**.

`traderview-expense::rental_ev_charging_accommodation` is the **EV charging accommodation compliance module** (iter 563) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure under state right-to-charge statutes for refusing or unreasonably restricting tenant installation of EV charging stations. Direct sibling to `rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` (iter 547 вҖ” accommodation parallel), `rental_solar_panel_disclosure` (renewable energy parallel), `rental_satellite_dish_installation_right` (tenant installation parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.6 (AB 2565 + AB 2863)** requires landlord approval of tenant EV charging request at tenant cost; applies to leases executed, renewed, or extended after July 1, 2015; tenant must agree to comply with installation/use/maintenance/removal requirements + UL listing + licensed-electrician installation + liability insurance certificate + pay all costs upfront; (2) **IL Electric Vehicle Charging Act (765 ILCS 1085)** effective Jan 1, 2024 grants tenant right to install + mandates new-construction EV-capable parking spaces; (3) **CO C.R.S. В§ 38-12-601** authorizes tenant Level 1 or Level 2 EV charging installation at tenant expense with landlord prohibited from charging fee for placement/use except actual electricity cost or reasonable access fee; safety + 30-day registration + reasonable aesthetic conditions permitted; (4) **HI Haw. Rev. Stat. В§ 196-7.5** voids and renders unenforceable lease/instrument provisions prohibiting EV charging at multi-family residential dwelling or townhouse parking stalls; (5) **NY RPL В§ 234 + state energy law amendments** prohibit HOA / condo association restrictions on EV charging in assigned parking space; (6) **FL Fla. Stat. В§ 718.113(8)** applies to CONDOMINIUM OWNERS only вҖ” Florida tenants of rentals are NOT protected by statewide right-to-charge; (7) federal **В§ 30C alternative fuel vehicle refueling property credit** provides up to 30% credit (max **$1,000 residential / $30,000 commercial**) for EV charging infrastructure; **IRA 2022 (Pub. L. 117-169)** extended through 2032.

**Seven jurisdictions**: California, Illinois, Colorado, Florida, Hawaii, NewYork, Default.

**Four landlord response types**: GrantedApprovalWithReasonableConditions, OutrightDeniedWithoutBasis, UnreasonableConditionsImposed, NoTimelyResponseGiven.

**Two tenant-compliance statuses**: AgreedToAllStatutoryConditions, RefusedBonaFideSafetyOrCostConditions.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantLandlordApprovalWithReasonableConditions, OutrightDenialViolatesRightToChargeStatute, UnreasonableConditionsViolation, NoTimelyResponseViolatesStatutoryWindow, TenantRefusedBonaFideSafetyOrCostConditionsDeniedReasonably, FloridaCondoOnlyRentalNotProtected, DefaultJurisdictionNoRightToChargeStatute.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-ev-charging-accommodation`. Eighteen tests pin: **California compliant approval with reasonable conditions** (В§ 1947.6 + В§ 30C federal credit); **California outright denial violation** ($5K + $10K + $15K = $30K exposure); **California unreasonable conditions violation** ($5K + $10K = $15K); **California no timely response violation** (deemed-approval framework); **tenant refused safety conditions denial reasonable** (no liability for reasonable denial); **Illinois 765 ILCS 1085 effective January 1 2024**; **Colorado В§ 38-12-601 Level 1 or 2 at tenant expense** (no landlord fee except electricity + 30-day registration); **Hawaii В§ 196-7.5 void and unenforceable**; **New York RPL В§ 234 HOA / condo restrictions prohibited**; **Florida condo only rental not protected** (В§ 718.113(8) condominium-owner scope); **default jurisdiction no right-to-charge statute** (15+ state list); **typical EV charging tort baseline constant pins $15,000**; **typical right-to-charge civil penalty constant pins $10,000**; **very large damages no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero damages uses baseline penalty plus tort** ($25K floor); **note pins В§ 30C alternative fuel credit** ($1,000 / $30,000 + IRA 2022); **note pins IRS 2022 extended through 2032**; **CA lease July 1 2015 threshold pinned**.

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_data_privacy_compliance` is the **tenant data privacy compliance module** (iter 561) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure under federal FCRA + state and local consumer-privacy regimes for collection, use, storage, and deletion of tenant personal data. Direct sibling to `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA adverse-action parallel), `rental_tenant_criminal_background_screening` (iter 539 вҖ” HUD disparate-impact parallel), `rental_video_surveillance_retention` (surveillance-data parallel), `rental_tenant_relocation_assistance` (iter 559 вҖ” tenant-protection parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), 15 U.S.C. В§В§ 1681-1681x** requires written consent before pulling consumer report, adverse-action notice if denied, and В§ 1681n willful violation civil penalty $100-$1,000 per violation + actual damages + В§ 1681o negligent actual damages + attorney fees; (2) **CA CCPA + CPRA (Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1798.100-1798.199.100)** requires notice at collection + right to know/access/delete/correct + opt-out of data sales + limit-use of sensitive personal information; CPPA enforces; В§ 1798.150 private right of action for security breaches; $2,500 non-intentional / $7,500 intentional per violation; (3) **IL BIPA (Biometric Information Privacy Act, 740 ILCS 14/15)** requires written consent + written retention/destruction policy BEFORE biometric collection (fingerprint, face, iris, voiceprint); **$1,000 per negligent / $5,000 per intentional violation per record** with class-action magnification; **Rosenbach v. Six Flags 432 Ill. Dec. 654 (Ill. 2019)** established no actual injury required for statutory standing; (4) **NYC Tenant Data Privacy Act (NYC Admin. Code В§В§ 26-3001 to 26-3007)** requires smart-access buildings to provide privacy notice + obtain consent + limit data use + implement security + delete data after tenancy; (5) **NY SHIELD Act (Gen. Bus. Law В§ 899-aa + В§ 899-bb)** statewide reasonable-data-security + breach-notification overlay; (6) **VA CDPA (Va. Code В§В§ 59.1-575 to 59.1-585)** treats biometric data as sensitive requiring opt-in + data-protection assessment; (7) **TX CUBI (Tex. Bus. & Com. Code В§В§ 521.052 + 503.001)** biometric identifier consent + reasonable security + retention limits; (8) **CO CPA (C.R.S. В§В§ 6-1-1301 to 6-1-1313)** opt-in for sensitive data + universal opt-out signal.

**Eight jurisdictions**: FederalFcraOnly, California, Illinois, NewYork, Virginia, Texas, Colorado, Default.

**Six data categories**: FcraConsumerReport, BiometricIdentifier, SmartAccessOrIotUsageData, SurveillanceCameraFootage, StandardRentalApplicationPii, RentReportingToCreditBureaus.

**Five consent-and-notice statuses**: WrittenConsentAndNoticeProvided, ConsentObtainedButNoticeInsufficient, NoConsentObtained, FcraAdverseActionNoticeProvided, FcraAdverseActionNoticeNotProvided.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantConsentAndNoticeFramework, FcraConsumerReportConsentObtainedAdverseActionDuty, FcraAdverseActionNoticeMissingViolation, BipaBiometricCollectionWithoutWrittenConsentViolation, CcpaCpraConsumerRightsNotProvidedViolation, NycTenantDataPrivacyActViolation, NySchieldActDataSecurityNonCompliance, DefaultJurisdictionFcraAndCommonLawOnly.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-data-privacy-compliance`. Twenty tests pin: **California compliant with consent and notice** (CCPA + CPRA); **California no consent CCPA violation** (В§В§ 1798.100 + CPPA + В§ 1798.150 private right of action); **Illinois BIPA biometric without consent violation** (740 ILCS 14/15 + Rosenbach v. Six Flags); **Illinois BIPA biometric 100 records $5,000 intentional** ($500K class-action exposure); **FCRA adverse-action notice missing violation** (15 U.S.C. В§ 1681m(a) + В§ 1681n + В§ 1681o); **FCRA consumer report consent obtained compliant** (В§ 1681b(b)(2) permissible-purpose); **NYC smart access without consent Tenant Data Privacy violation** (В§В§ 26-3001-3007 + SHIELD Act); **default jurisdiction FCRA and common-law only** (CDPA + CUBI + CPA + 2022-2026 state-law sweep); **Virginia compliant framework** (CDPA В§В§ 59.1-575); **Texas compliant framework** (CUBI В§В§ 521.052); **Colorado compliant framework** (CPA В§В§ 6-1-1301); **BIPA negligent per record constant pins $1,000**; **BIPA intentional per record constant pins $5,000**; **CCPA civil penalty constant pins $2,500**; **CCPA intentional constant pins $7,500**; **FCRA willful per violation constant pins $1,000**; **very large records no overflow in BIPA calc** (u32::MAX records + saturating_mul defense); **zero records zero BIPA exposure aside from damages** (boundary); **note pins Rosenbach v. Six Flags no actual injury required**; **NY IoT data without consent Tenant Data Privacy Act violation**.

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_relocation_assistance` is the **tenant relocation assistance compliance module** (iter 559) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for failure to pay statutory relocation assistance during no-fault eviction + condo conversion + demolition + substantial-rehabilitation displacement. Direct sibling to `rental_rent_control_stabilization` (iter 557 вҖ” just-cause eviction parallel), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” landlord-adverse-action parallel), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (iter 529 вҖ” wrongful-dispossession parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA AB 1482 (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2 + В§ 1947.12) + CA SB 567 (effective April 1, 2024)** require **ONE MONTH'S RENT** as relocation assistance for any no-fault eviction; SB 567 owner-move-in eviction requires (1) owner/relative move in within 90 days, (2) 1-year primary-residence requirement, (3) eviction notice disclose name and relationship, (4) no other vacant similar unit on property; (2) **NYC RENT-STABILIZED DEMOLITION** under **NYC RSL В§ 26-511(c)(9) + 9 NYCRR В§ 2524.5** requires NY State DHCR approval + relocation to comparable housing at same/lower regulated rent + reasonable moving expenses + **$5,000 stipend**; condo conversion under non-eviction plan preserves rent-stabilized tenancy; (3) **WA RCW 59.18.440 + RCW 59.18.450** authorize local-option relocation assistance for **low-income tenants** (вүӨ 50% area median income) upon demolition / substantial rehabilitation / change of use / removal of use restrictions; (4) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-130 + В§ 5-14-050** condo conversion requires **GREATER OF $1,500 OR one month's rent capped at $2,500**; **Keep Chicago Renting Ordinance (KCRO)** requires **$10,600** post-foreclosure relocation if no lease-renewal offer; (5) **DC TOPA (D.C. Code В§ 42-3404.01 et seq.)** gives tenant right of first refusal + relocation assistance capped at **lesser of one year's rent or $12,000** (annually adjusted); building-affordability + organizing expenses + attorney fees + improvements also negotiable.

**Six jurisdictions**: CaliforniaAb1482Sb567, NewYorkCityRentStabilized, Washington, IllinoisChicagoRlto, DcTopa, Default.

**Seven displacement-basis types**: OwnerMoveInEviction, WithdrawalFromRentalMarketEllis, CondoOrCoOpConversion, Demolition, SubstantialRehabilitation, ChangeOfUseNonResidential, AtFaultEvictionNoRelocationDuty.

**Two tenant-income statuses**: LowIncomeBelowFiftyPctAmi, AboveFiftyPctAmi.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, AtFaultEvictionNoRelocationDuty, CompliantRelocationAssistancePaid, InsufficientRelocationAssistanceViolation, SbFiveSixSevenOwnerMoveInNonCompliance, NycDhcrApprovalRequiredDemolitionViolation, WashingtonNonLowIncomeNotCovered, DcTopaRightOfFirstRefusalIncludingRelocation.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-relocation-assistance`. Twenty-two tests pin: **at-fault eviction no relocation duty**; **California compliant one month rent** (В§ 1946.2 + В§ 1947.12); **California insufficient relocation violation** ($1K paid vs $3K required вҶ’ $2K shortfall); **California SB 567 owner move-in non-compliance** (90-day + 1-year requirements + April 1 2024 effective date); **California SB 567 compliant owner move-in**; **NYC DHCR demolition approval required violation** (DHCR + В§ 26-511(c)(9) + $5,000 stipend); **NYC compliant demolition with DHCR approval**; **Washington non-low-income not covered** (RCW 59.18.440 + 50% AMI threshold); **Washington low-income 3 months rent required**; **Chicago RLTO condo conversion greater of $1,500 or one month compliant** (max + min cap); **Chicago RLTO low rent uses $1,500 floor** (boundary); **DC TOPA compliant one year rent under cap** (12 Г— $800 = $9,600); **DC TOPA high rent caps at $12,000** ($60K вҶ’ cap $12K); **DC TOPA insufficient relocation violation**; **Chicago RLTO flat constant pins $1,500**; **Chicago RLTO rent cap constant pins $2,500**; **Chicago KCRO post-foreclosure constant pins $10,600**; **NYC demolition stipend constant pins $5,000**; **DC TOPA cap constant pins $12,000**; **very large rent no overflow in DC one-year calc** (saturating_mul defense); **zero rent no panic in California** (boundary); **default jurisdiction no statutory duty** (common-law constructive-eviction fallback).

`traderview-expense::rental_rent_control_stabilization` is the **rent control + rent stabilization compliance module** (iter 557) вҖ” covers landlord rent-increase-cap compliance under statewide and municipal rent control/stabilization frameworks. Direct sibling to `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” landlord-adverse-action protection parallel), `rental_late_fee_cap` (iter 537 вҖ” rent-related state-by-state cap parallel), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (iter 535 вҖ” rent-related deadline parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA AB 1482 (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.12 + В§ 1946.2) Tenant Protection Act of 2019** (effective Jan 1, 2020 - Jan 1, 2030) caps annual rent increases at the **LESSER OF 5% + local CPI OR 10%** for units 15+ years old (rolling exception), with В§ 1946.2 just-cause eviction protection after 12+ months of tenancy and single-family-home exemption requiring notice under В§ 1947.12(d)(5); (2) **OR SB 608** (ORS 90.323 + ORS 90.427) was the **first statewide US rent cap** at **7% + CPI** capped at **10%** under SB 611 (effective Jul 6, 2023); just-cause eviction after first year; new construction exempt for 15 years; (3) **NY State HSTPA 2019 + NYC RSL В§ 26-501 et seq.** applies to NYC pre-1974 buildings of 6+ units; Rent Guidelines Board sets annual % cap each June; vacancy decontrol eliminated; preferential-rent / IAI / MCI caps tightened; (4) **NJ Anti-Eviction Act N.J.S.A. 2A:18-61.1** + municipal rent-control ordinances in 100+ NJ municipalities (Newark, Jersey City, Hoboken, etc., typically 4-6% cap); (5) **DC Rental Housing Act of 1985** (D.C. Code В§ 42-3501 et seq.) caps rent-controlled buildings (pre-1976 + 5+ units) at **CPI + 2%** per year (10% maximum; 5% for elderly/disabled tenants); (6) **MN St. Paul Charter Amendment (Nov 2021)** imposes **3% annual rent cap** on all residential rentals with eligibility-based exemptions; (7) most states (TX, FL, TN, AZ, MI, IL outside Chicago) PREEMPT local rent control via state preemption statutes вҖ” only common-law unconscionability + state UDAP fraud-against-tenant frameworks restrict rent levels.

**Eight jurisdictions**: CaliforniaAb1482Statewide, OregonSb608Statewide, NewYorkRentStabilizedNyc, NewJerseyAntiEvictionAct, DcRentalHousingAct, MinnesotaStPaulCharter, StatePreemptedNoRentControl, Default.

**Five property-exemption statuses**: NotExemptSubjectToRentCap, CaSingleFamilyNonCorporateExemptWithNotice, NewConstructionWithinExemptionWindow, BuildingTooSmallForCoverage, OwnerOccupiedSmallProperty.
