`traderview-expense::rental_smoke_free_cannabis_restriction` is the **smoke-free housing + cannabis-use restriction compliance module** (iter 555 вҖ” milestone) вҖ” covers landlord enforcement and disclosure obligations under HUD smoke-free public housing rule + state-level smoke-free housing statutes + cannabis-prohibition framework. Direct sibling to `rental_smoke_free_housing_disclosure` (pre-existing disclosure parallel), `rental_marijuana_cultivation_restriction` (cultivation parallel), `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` (lease-restriction parallel), `rental_pesticide_application_notification` (indoor-environmental-policy parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **HUD 24 C.F.R. Parts 200 + 982 + 5 final rule (effective February 3, 2017)** MANDATES smoke-free policy in HUD-subsidized public housing вҖ” bans lit tobacco smoking in apartments, common areas, administrative offices, and grounds within 25 feet; Public Housing Agencies must implement and enforce; Section 8 voucher properties NOT subject to federal mandate (private landlord discretion); (2) **CA SB 332 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.5 (effective January 1, 2012)** authorizes private landlords to prohibit smoking with lease provision required, pre-existing-tenant grandfathering preserved, and В§ 1947.5(b) designated-smoking-areas disclosure mandatory; (3) **NYC Local Law 147 of 2017 + NYC Admin. Code В§ 17-505 et seq.** require smoking-policy disclosure for NYC multi-unit residential buildings; (4) **WA Clean Indoor Air Act RCW 70.160** bans smoking in workplaces + public accommodations + within 25 feet of entrances; (5) **IL Smoke Free Illinois Act 410 ILCS 82** bans smoking in public-accommodation indoor areas + within 15 feet of entrances; (6) **OR Smoke-Free Workplace Law ORS 433.835-990**; (7) **CANNABIS LANDLORD CONTROL**: **CA Health & Safety Code В§ 11362.45(h)** + **NY Cannabis Law В§ 222 + Penal Law В§ 222.05** + **CO Amendment 64 + В§ 12-43.4 + HB 1287** + **WA RCW 69.50.4014** all preserve landlord authority to prohibit cannabis use; federal Controlled Substances Act Schedule I classification independently supports prohibition; FHA does NOT generally require accommodation of cannabis use per **Forest City Residential Mgmt. v. Beasley, 71 F. Supp. 3d 715 (E.D. Mich. 2014)** and **James v. City of Costa Mesa, 700 F.3d 394 (9th Cir. 2012)**.

**Eight jurisdictions**: HudSubsidizedPublicHousing, California, NewYorkCity, Washington, Illinois, Oregon, Colorado, Default.

**Five substance categories**: LitTobaccoCigarettesOrCigarOrPipe, ElectronicCigaretteVaping, CannabisCombustion, CannabisVaporization, CannabisEdiblesOrNonSmoked.

**Three lease-disclosure statuses**: DisclosedInLeaseWithDesignatedAreas, DisclosedButDesignatedAreasMissing, NoDisclosureProvided.

**Two tenancy statuses**: PreExistingTenantGrandfathered, NewTenancyPostPolicyImplementation.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantSmokeFreePolicyEnforceable, HudFederalMandatePreemptsLocalLawSubsidizedHousing, PreExistingTenantGrandfatheredPolicyUnenforceable, LeaseDisclosureDefectivePolicyUnenforceable, NoDisclosureSmokeFreePolicyUnenforceable, CannabisEdiblesNotSubjectToSmokeFreePolicy, LandlordMayProhibitCannabisOnPremises.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-smoke-free-cannabis-restriction`. Twenty tests pin: **California compliant smoke-free policy enforceable** (SB 332 + В§ 1947.5); **California pre-existing tenant grandfathered unenforceable** (lease-amendment-consent requirement); **California designated areas missing defective** (В§ 1947.5(b)); **California no disclosure unenforceable** (В§ 1947.5(a)); **HUD public housing federal mandate** (24 C.F.R. Parts 200 + 982 + 5 + 25-foot perimeter + Controlled Substances Act); **cannabis edibles not subject to smoke-free policy**; **cannabis combustion prohibitable by landlord** (В§ 11362.45(h) + Forest City + James v. City of Costa Mesa); **cannabis vaporization prohibitable by landlord**; **NY cannabis prohibition pins MRTA** (В§ 222.05); **CO cannabis prohibition pins Amendment 64**; **WA cannabis prohibition pins RCW 69.50.4014**; **NYC disclosure pins Local Law 147 of 2017** (В§ 17-505); **WA disclosure pins Clean Indoor Air Act** (RCW 70.160); **IL disclosure pins Smoke Free Illinois Act** (410 ILCS 82); **OR disclosure pins ORS 433.835-990**; **electronic cigarette vaping treated as smoke-free subject**; **HUD smoke-free rule effective year constant pins 2017**; **CA SB 332 effective year constant pins 2012**; **HUD takes priority over all other branches** (severity ladder ordering); **cannabis edibles overrides all other branches** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::rental_ada_accessible_parking_compliance` is the **ADA + FHA accessible-parking compliance module** (iter 553) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure under ADA Title III public-accommodation parking standards + FHA reasonable-modification + reasonable-accommodation duties. Direct sibling to `rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` (iter 547 вҖ” FHA accommodation parallel), `rental_elevator_safety_inspection` (mobility-access parallel), `rental_emergency_action_plan_high_rise` (iter 527 вҖ” accessible-evacuation parallel). Trader-landlord critical because multifamily properties face dual ADA + FHA exposure: ADA Title III for public-accommodation areas + FHA for tenant accommodation requests. (1) **2010 ADA Standards В§ 502 + В§ 208.2 minimum-table** requires 1 accessible space per 25 (first 100); 2 per 50; 3 per 75; 4 per 100; 5 per 150; 6 per 200; 7 per 300; 8 per 400; 9 per 500; sliding scale to 1 per 50 then 1 per 100 above 1,000; (2) **В§ 208.2.4 van-accessible ratio** requires at least 1 of every 6 accessible spaces (rounded up) to be van-accessible; (3) **В§ 502.3 access-aisle width** 60-inch minimum for car spaces / 96-inch for van-accessible (or 60-inch aisle + 132-inch space); (4) **В§ 502.7 vertical clearance** 98 inches for van-accessible; (5) **В§ 502.6 signage** with International Symbol of Accessibility; (6) **FHA В§ 3604(f)(3)(C) covered-multifamily design+construction** (post-March-13-1991 first occupancy; 4+ units with elevator OR 4+ units without with ground-floor coverage) requires 2% of parking spaces accessible (minimum 1); (7) **FHA В§ 3604(f)(3)(A) + (B) reasonable-modification + reasonable-accommodation duty** requires individualized tenant accommodation requests at landlord expense unless undue burden per **HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Modifications (March 5, 2008)**; (8) **42 U.S.C. В§ 3612(g)(3)** $19,787 inflation-adjusted civil penalty; (9) **28 C.F.R. В§ 36.501 + В§ 36.502** private action + administrative referral; (10) state codes (e.g., **CA Health & Safety Code В§ 19955 + В§ 19956**, **IL 410 ILCS 25/3 Environmental Barriers Act**, **NJ Barrier Free Subcode N.J.A.C. 5:23-7**) layer additional state-law requirements.

**Four regime categories**: AdaTitleIiiPublicAccommodation, FhaCoveredMultifamily, FhaReasonableAccommodationRequest, NotCoveredByAdaOrFha.

**Eight compliance statuses**: AccessibleSpaceCountMeetsRequirement, AccessibleSpaceCountBelowRequirement, VanAccessibleRatioMet, VanAccessibleRatioNotMet, AccessAisleWidthNonCompliant, SignageMissingOrIncorrect, FhaTenantAccommodationGranted, FhaTenantAccommodationDenied.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotCoveredByAdaOrFhaNoComplianceRequirement, Compliant2010AdaStandardsSection502, InsufficientAccessibleSpaceCountAdaViolation, VanAccessibleRatioViolationOnePerSixRule, AccessAisleWidthOrSignageViolation, FhaCoveredMultifamilyTwoPercentRequirementViolation, FhaReasonableAccommodationGranted, FhaReasonableAccommodationDeniedSection3604F3aViolation.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-ada-accessible-parking-compliance`. Twenty-one tests pin: **not covered no compliance required** (CA В§ 19955 + IL Environmental Barriers fallback); **ADA 100-space lot 4 accessible compliant** (В§ 502 + 4 / 6 ceiling = 1 van); **ADA 25-space lot 1 accessible required**; **ADA 50-space lot 2 accessible required**; **ADA 200-space lot 6 accessible required**; **ADA 500-space lot 9 accessible required**; **ADA insufficient accessible space count violation** (28 C.F.R. В§ 36.501); **ADA van-accessible ratio violation** (В§ 208.2.4 + В§ 502.7); **ADA aisle width violation signage violation** (В§ 502.3 + В§ 502.6); **FHA covered multifamily 100 spaces requires 2 accessible** (2% Г— 100 = 2); **FHA covered multifamily below 2% violation**; **FHA covered multifamily minimum 1 accessible floor** (10 spaces Г— 2% = 0 вҶ’ floor 1); **FHA reasonable accommodation granted compliant** (В§ 3604(f)(3)(A) + (B)); **FHA reasonable accommodation denied violation** ($15K + $19,787 = $34,787 exposure); **van-accessible ratio constant pins 1-in-6**; **FHA covered multifamily percent constant pins 2%**; **FHA covered multifamily design date constant pins 1991**; **ADA civil penalty constant pins $19,787**; **ADA emotional distress constant pins $15,000**; **very large lot size no overflow** (10,000 spaces вҶ’ 110 required via sliding scale); **zero spaces zero required** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_rent_escrow_habitability_dispute` is the **tenant rent escrow + repair-and-deduct compliance module** (iter 551) вҖ” covers landlord nonpayment-of-rent eviction exposure analysis when tenant withholds rent or executes repair-and-deduct during habitability dispute. Direct sibling to `rental_mold_disclosure_remediation` (iter 545 вҖ” habitability-triggering-condition parallel), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (iter 533 вҖ” landlord-side procedural parallel), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (iter 535 вҖ” end-of-tenancy escrow parallel), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” landlord-adverse-action parallel). Trader-landlord critical because tenant rent withholding executed compliantly defeats nonpayment eviction; non-compliant withholding (no notice, premature action, no escrow, exceeded cap) preserves the eviction. (1) **CA Civ. Code В§ 1942 + В§ 1942.4** authorizes repair-and-deduct capped at one month's rent twice per 12-month period, with В§ 1942.4 prohibiting rent collection if landlord knew of substandard conditions 60+ days; (2) **NY RPL В§ 235-b** (Warranty of Habitability) + RPL В§ 235-a + HP proceedings under **RPAPL В§ 110** provide statutory + court-supervised escrow paths; (3) **WA RCW 59.18.115** requires rent withholding ONLY through the statutory escrow process (written notice + statutory cure window + local-authority certification + deposit in approved escrow account); RCW 59.18.100 repair-and-deduct cap at 2 months' rent per repair, 2 months' rent per year; (4) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-110** requires 14-day written notice with withholding cap at GREATER of $500 or 50% of monthly rent; (5) **TX Prop. Code В§ 92.0561** repair-and-deduct cap at GREATER of one month's rent or $500 in narrow circumstances (sewage, flood, material defect); (6) **MA Gen. L. ch. 239 В§ 8A** permits IMMEDIATE withholding upon notice (no cure period required), raised as defense or counterclaim in summary process; (7) default jurisdictions apply common-law implied warranty of habitability + constructive-eviction doctrine (**Marini v. Ireland 265 A.2d 526 (NJ 1970)** + **Pugh v. Holmes 384 A.2d 1234 (PA 1979)**).

**Four tenant remedy actions**: RentWithholdingWithoutRepair, RentWithholdingDepositedToEscrowAccount, RepairAndDeductFromRent, LeaseTerminationConstructiveEviction.

**Four notice-and-cure statuses**: WrittenNoticeAndCureWindowElapsed, WrittenNoticeWithinCureWindowPrematureAction, NoWrittenNoticeToLandlord, NoCureRequiredImmediateAction (MA-specific).

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantStatutoryRentEscrowOrRepairDeduct, TenantRemedyCapExceededLandlordEvictionExposure, NoNoticeOrPrematureActionTenantEvictionRisk, EscrowAccountNotEstablishedTenantWaivesDefense, WashingtonRcw59_18_115EscrowProcessNotFollowed, ChicagoRltoWithholdingCapExceededExposure, MassachusettsImmediateWithholdingNoCureRequired, CommonLawWarrantyImpliedDefense.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-rent-escrow-habitability-dispute`. Twenty-two tests pin: **California repair-and-deduct within one month rent compliant** (В§ 1942 + В§ 1942.4); **California repair-and-deduct exceeds one month rent cap violation** ($4K > $3K cap вҶ’ $1K excess); **California no notice no self-help eviction risk**; **California premature action within cure window eviction risk**; **Washington pure withholding without escrow violation** (RCW 59.18.115 + local-authority certification); **Chicago RLTO within cap compliant** ($1500 = 50% Г— $3K cap); **Chicago RLTO exceeds 50% rent cap violation** ($2000 > $1500 вҶ’ $500 excess); **Chicago RLTO low rent uses $500 dollar floor**; **Texas repair-and-deduct within cap compliant** (В§ 92.0561); **Texas repair-and-deduct exceeds one month rent violation**; **Massachusetts immediate withholding no cure required** (В§ 8A); **pure withholding without escrow waives defense**; **default jurisdiction common-law warranty** (Marini v. Ireland + Pugh v. Holmes); **New York compliant rent withholding with escrow** (В§ 235-b + RPAPL В§ 110); **CA repair-and-deduct periods per year constant pins 2**; **WA repair-and-deduct cap per repair constant pins 2 months**; **WA repair-and-deduct cap per year constant pins 2 months**; **Chicago RLTO flat floor constant pins $500**; **Chicago RLTO percent constant pins 50%**; **TX repair-and-deduct flat floor constant pins $500**; **very large rent no overflow in Chicago 50% calc** (saturating_mul defense); **zero rent falls back to Chicago floor** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_boiler_inspection_compliance` is the **boiler and gas-piping inspection compliance module** (iter 549) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for missed boiler and gas-piping periodic inspections under ASME BPVC Section IV + Section VI engineering standard plus municipal/state code overlays. Direct sibling to `rental_elevator_safety_inspection` (ASME A17.1 periodic-inspection parallel), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (gas-emergency parallel), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (combustion-safety parallel), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (combustion-system parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **NYC Local Law 152 of 2016 + NYC Admin. Code В§ 28-318** require gas-piping systems in all buildings except one- and two-family R-3 occupancies to be inspected by a **Licensed Master Plumber (LMP)** every **4 years** on a community-district-based schedule; LMP delivers inspection report within 30 days; **GPS1 Certification** must be filed with DOB within 60 days; **Cycle 2 enforcement is significantly stricter than Cycle 1**, with stop-use orders and FDNY emergency gas shutoff authority for hazardous conditions; (2) **NY State Industrial Code Rule 4** requires annual high-pressure boiler inspection and biennial low-pressure boiler inspection; (3) **CA Cal/OSHA Title 8 Subchapter 1 В§В§ 750-784** Pressure Vessel Unit jurisdiction requires annual inspection for boilers above 15 psi steam or 160 psi water; (4) **IL 430 ILCS 75 Boiler and Pressure Vessel Safety Act** requires annual external + 6-year internal inspection; (5) **MA Gen. L. ch. 146 В§ 46** requires annual external + internal inspection by Department of Public Safety, Office of Public Safety and Inspections; (6) **TX Health & Safety Code В§ 755** boiler inspection program by Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR); (7) default jurisdictions adopt **ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section IV** engineering standard with annual external + periodic internal inspection.

**Three building types**: MultiFamilyMoreThanTwoUnits, OneOrTwoFamilyHomeR3OccupancyExempt, CommercialOrMixedUse.

**Five inspection statuses**: InspectionPerformedAndReportFiledTimely, InspectionPerformedReportNotFiledWithinSixtyDays, InspectionPerformedByUnqualifiedIndividualNotLicensedMasterPlumber, InspectionNotPerformedByCycleDeadline, CorrectionsFromPriorInspectionNotAddressed.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, OneOrTwoFamilyExemptNoInspectionRequired, CompliantInspectionAndReportFiled, LateFilingWithinThirtyDayCureWindow, NycLocalLaw152UnqualifiedInspectorViolation, InspectionMissedDeadlineDobCivilPenaltyExposure, UnaddressedCorrectionsClassCViolationEscalatedEnforcement.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-boiler-inspection-compliance`. Twenty-four tests pin: **NYC one or two-family exempt** (R-3 + В§ 28-318); **NYC compliant inspection and filing** (Local Law 152 + 4-year cycle + 60-day filing window); **NYC unqualified inspector violation** ($5,000 LMP-required); **NYC missed cycle deadline civil penalty** ($10K base + $250/day Class-C escalation + 30 days вҶ’ $17,500); **NYC unaddressed corrections escalated enforcement** (Class C + FDNY emergency shutoff); **NYC late filing within cure window** (75 days < 90-day cure вҶ’ $1,000 penalty); **NYC late filing outside cure window** (95 days > 90 вҶ’ no-inspection penalty); **NY State ICR4 annual cycle** (Industrial Code Rule 4); **California Cal/OSHA annual cycle** (В§В§ 750-784); **Illinois 430 ILCS 75 annual external**; **Massachusetts ch. 146 В§ 46**; **Texas Health & Safety В§ 755 TDLR**; **default jurisdiction ASME BPVC Section IV**; **NYC LL 152 cycle constant pins 4 years**; **NYC LL 152 filing window constant pins 60 days**; **NYC LL 152 report delivery constant pins 30 days**; **NY State high pressure cycle constant pins 1 year**; **NY State low pressure cycle constant pins 2 years**; **IL external cycle constant pins 1 year**; **IL internal cycle constant pins 6 years**; **NYC LL 152 no inspection penalty constant pins $10,000**; **NYC Class C daily penalty constant pins $250**; **very large days past deadline no overflow** (saturating_mul + .min() defense); **zero days late no panic** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_fair_housing_reasonable_accommodation` is the **Fair Housing Act reasonable-accommodation + reasonable-modification compliance module** (iter 547) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for failure to grant reasonable accommodations under 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) and reasonable modifications under В§ 3604(f)(3)(A). Direct sibling to `rental_source_of_income_discrimination` (iter 541 вҖ” fair-housing protected-class parallel), `rental_tenant_criminal_background_screening` (iter 539 вҖ” HUD disparate-impact parallel), `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` (FHA assistance-animal exception parallel), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA adverse-action parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) reasonable accommodation** requires housing providers to make reasonable accommodations in rules, policies, practices, or services when necessary for disabled tenants' equal opportunity to use and enjoy the dwelling вҖ” provider expense unless undue financial / administrative burden; (2) **42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(A) reasonable modification** allows tenant-expense physical changes with no restoration requirement for normal wear; (3) **HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Accommodations (May 17, 2004)** and **HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Modifications (March 5, 2008)** establish the federal framework requiring cooperative interactive dialogue; (4) **HUD's May 22, 2026 internal memorandum** permanently cancelled prior HUD guidance and instructed agency staff to stop pursuing complaints involving ESAs that have not been individually trained for disability-related work or tasks вҖ” state laws continue to apply at higher floor; (5) **CA FEHA Cal. Gov. Code В§В§ 12927(c)(1) + 12955** provides state-law floor that SURVIVES the 2026 federal reversal, and **AB-468 (effective Jan 1, 2022)** requires 30-day LMHP relationship before ESA letter; (6) **NY State HRL Exec. Law В§ 296(5)(c)** + **NYC HRL NYC Admin. Code В§ 8-107(5)** codify interactive-dialogue duty explicitly; (7) **NJ LAD N.J.S.A. 10:5-12.4** and **MA Gen. L. ch. 151B В§ 4(7A)** provide parallel state protection; (8) **PER SE violations**: imposing pet fee or deposit on assistance animal; requiring restoration of normal-wear modification; outright denial without interactive dialogue. **42 U.S.C. В§ 3612(g)(3)** civil penalty (inflation-adjusted, $19,787 for first violation) under FHA enforcement.

**Seven accommodation request types**: ServiceAnimalAdaStandard, EmotionalSupportAnimal, LiveInAideCaregiverSupport, AccessibleParkingSpaceAssignment, LeaseTermsModification, PhysicalModificationToDwelling, PolicyException.

**Four disability-nexus statuses**: DisabilityAndNexusDocumented, DisabilityObviousOrKnown, DisabilityClaimedNotDocumented, NoClaimOfDisability.

**Five landlord-response statuses**: GrantedAccommodationOrModification, EngagedInInteractiveDialogueProposedAlternative, OutrightDeniedNoInteractiveDialogue, ImposedPetFeeOrDepositOnAssistanceAnimal, RequiredRestorationOfNormalWearModification.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoClaimOfDisabilityNoAccommodationDuty, CompliantAccommodationGranted, CompliantInteractiveDialogueAlternativeOffered, EsaPost2026FederalReversalStateLawFloorMayStillApply, PetFeeOnAssistanceAnimalPerSeFhaViolation, RestorationDemandedForNormalWearModificationViolation, OutrightDenialFailureToEngageInteractiveDialogue.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-fair-housing-reasonable-accommodation`. Nineteen tests pin: **no claim of disability no accommodation duty** (В§ 3604(f)(3)(B)); **compliant accommodation granted** (CA FEHA + AB-468); **pet fee on assistance animal per se violation** (HUD/DOJ Joint Statement + $19,787 civil penalty); **pet fee on service animal per se violation**; **restoration demanded normal-wear modification violation** (В§ 3604(f)(3)(A) + March 5 2008 Joint Statement); **outright denial no interactive dialogue violation** (NYC В§ 8-107(5)); **engaged in interactive dialogue alternative compliant**; **federal-only ESA post-2026 reversal state floor note** (May 22 2026 + higher state floor); **California state floor survives federal reversal**; **New York interactive dialogue codified** (В§ 296(5)(c) + В§ 8-107(5)); **New Jersey LAD citation** (N.J.S.A. 10:5-12.4); **Massachusetts ch. 151B В§ 4(7A) citation**; **service animal request separately protected**; **live-in aide request compliant when granted**; **accessible parking compliant when granted**; **FHA civil penalty constant pins $19,787**; **typical emotional distress constant pins $15,000**; **very large damages no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero damages uses baseline emotional distress plus penalty** ($15K + $19,787 = $34,787).

`traderview-expense::rental_mold_disclosure_remediation` is the **mold disclosure + remediation compliance module** (iter 545) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for failure to disclose known visible mold or promptly remediate indoor mold contamination. Direct sibling to `rental_radon_mitigation_disclosure` (radon parallel environmental disclosure), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water-damage precursor parallel), `rental_lead_pipe_disclosure` (toxic-contamination parallel), `rental_pesticide_application_notification` (environmental-health parallel), `rental_tenant_abandoned_personal_property` (iter 543 вҖ” strict-liability conversion parallel). Trader-landlord critical because indoor mold poses serious health hazards (asthma triggers, allergic reactions, respiratory infection) with toxic-mold tort settlements ranging $50K-$5M+ depending on health impact and proof of causation. (1) **CA Health & Safety Code В§В§ 26100-26156 (Toxic Mold Protection Act of 2001) + CA Civ. Code В§ 1102.17 + В§ 1941.7** require written disclosure if landlord knows or has reasonable cause to believe mold present; effective Jan 1, 2022, landlords must provide the CDPH mold booklet to all prospective tenants before lease signing; (2) **NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 (Asthma-Free Housing Act)** codified at NYC HMC В§ 27-2017.1 requires multi-dwelling property owners to investigate AND remove indoor mold with **licensed-professional remediation required for mold > 10 square feet**, annual inspections, tenant informational materials, and HPD class-C immediately-hazardous violation framework; (3) **WA RCW 59.18.060(13)** requires landlord to notify tenants of health hazards associated with indoor mold + provide WA Department of Health mold-prevention information at lease execution; (4) **TX Prop. Code В§ 92.052** diligent-effort-to-repair duty + **Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act (DTPA)** requires disclosure of known mold infestation; (5) **VA Code В§ 55.1-1215** (lease-execution disclosure) + **В§ 55.1-1226** (itemized inspection report within 5 days of move-in) + **В§ 8.01-226.12** (visible-mold duty) + **В§ 55.1-1220(A)(5)** (moisture-prevention duty); (6) **FL Stat. В§ 83.51** landlord duty to maintain вҖ” no explicit mold statute; (7) **IL** has no statewide mold statute вҖ” common-law implied warranty of habitability + Jack Spring v. Little doctrine controls.

**Three mold-knowledge statuses**: LandlordActualKnowledgeOfVisibleMold, LandlordConstructiveKnowledgeReasonableCause, NoKnowledgeOrMoldNotPresent.

**Four disclosure statuses**: WrittenDisclosureProvidedAtLeaseExecution, CaCdphBookletProvidedSection1941_7, VaItemizedInspectionReportProvidedWithinFiveDays, NoDisclosureProvidedDespiteKnowledge.

**Four remediation statuses**: RemediatedByLicensedProfessional, SmallAreaRemediatedByTenantOrLandlordUnderTenSqFt, RemediationNotCompletedMoldOngoing, RemediationAttemptedButFailed.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoKnowledgeOfMoldNoDisclosureObligation, CompliantWrittenDisclosureAndRemediation, DisclosureProvidedRemediationOngoing, NoDisclosureViolatesStateMoldStatute, NycLocalLaw55LicensedProfessionalRequiredViolation, UnremediatedMoldImpliedWarrantyHabitabilityBreach, PersonalInjuryToxicTortExposureRisk.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-mold-disclosure-remediation`. Twenty-two tests pin: **no knowledge of mold no disclosure obligation**; **California compliant written disclosure and remediation** (Toxic Mold Protection Act + В§ 1941.7); **California no disclosure violates statute**; **NYC Local Law 55 under 10 sq ft no licensed required** (Asthma-Free Housing Act); **NYC Local Law 55 over 10 sq ft licensed required**; **NYC unlicensed remediation over 10 sq ft violation** (В§ 27-2017.1 + HPD class-C); **Washington compliant disclosure** (RCW 59.18.060(13)); **Texas no disclosure DTPA violation** (В§ 92.052 + DTPA); **Virginia no disclosure violation pins В§ 55.1-1215** + В§ 55.1-1226; **unremediated mold implied warranty breach** (constructive-eviction + repair-and-deduct); **failed remediation implied warranty breach**; **constructive knowledge triggers disclosure obligation**; **Illinois default common-law warranty** (Jack Spring v. Little); **Florida default В§ 83.51 landlord duty**; **default jurisdiction common-law warranty**; **NYC licensed threshold constant pins 10 sq ft**; **typical toxic-mold tort average constant pins $50,000**; **typical civil penalty constant pins $5,000**; **very large damages no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero damages uses baseline civil penalty**; **NYC boundary exactly 10 sq ft no licensed required** (boundary); **NYC boundary 11 sq ft licensed required** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_abandoned_personal_property` is the **tenant-abandoned personal property handling compliance module** (iter 543) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for premature disposal, conversion, or non-compliant handling of personal property left behind by vacating tenants. Direct sibling to `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (iter 533 вҖ” post-tenancy access pattern), `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (iter 535 вҖ” move-out timing parallel), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (iter 529 вҖ” pre-vacancy parallel). Trader-landlord critical because conversion is a **strict-liability tort independent of any breach-of-contract claim** with punitive damages available for willful or bad-faith disposal. (1) **CA Civ. Code В§В§ 1983-1991** require written notice + minimum 15-day claim window (personal delivery) or 18-day (mailed) + $700 sale-vs-disposal threshold under В§ 1988 (auction sale required if value > $700, landlord discretion if вүӨ $700); (2) **WA RCW 59.18.310** requires 45-day storage window from notice (longest of surveyed states) with deceased-tenant exception; (3) **TX Prop. Code В§ 54.045 + В§ 92.014** landlord-lien procedure requires 30-day notice before sale by BOTH first-class AND certified mail return receipt requested вҖ” single-channel delivery is a per se statutory violation under В§ 54.046 with attorney fees + court costs exposure; (4) **FL Stat. ch. 715 (В§В§ 715.10-715.111)** is OPTIONAL procedure with minimum 10-day claim window (personal delivery) or 15-day (mailed) and $500 sale-vs-disposal threshold per В§ 715.109 + В§ 715.107; (5) **IL** has NO landlord-tenant-specific abandoned-property statute statewide вҖ” common-law reasonable-time standard plus 765 ILCS 1026 Revised Uniform Unclaimed Property Act applies to bona-fide unclaimed property after multi-year escheat period; (6) **MA Gen. L. ch. 239 В§ 4 + ch. 105A** require court-supervised storage procedure; (7) **CO Rev. Stat. В§ 38-20-116** requires 30-day notice before sale.

**Five delivery methods**: PersonalDelivery, FirstClassMail, CertifiedMail, BothFirstClassAndCertifiedRequiredTexas, NoNoticeGiven.

**Five disposal actions**: LandlordRetainsForPersonalUseUnderValueThreshold, PublicAuctionSaleWithReasonableNotice, DisposedAsTrashAfterStorageWindow, PrematureDisposalBeforeNoticeWindow, StoredPendingTenantClaim.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantStoredPendingTenantClaim, CompliantBelowSaleValueThresholdLandlordRetention, CompliantPublicAuctionSaleAfterNoticeWindow, PrematureDisposalConversionTortLiability, NoticeNotGivenStrictLiabilityConversion, NoticeDeliveryMethodNonCompliantPerJurisdiction, TexasCertifiedAndFirstClassMailDualRequirementViolated, CommonLawReasonableTimeUnverified.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-abandoned-personal-property`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **stored pending claim compliant**; **no notice given strict-liability conversion** ($1K property + $500 damages = $1.5K exposure); **California mailed notice 18-day compliant**; **California personal delivery 15-day compliant**; **California premature disposal before 18 days conversion** (boundary); **California high value landlord retention not permitted** ($1.5K > $700 threshold + В§ 1988); **California low value landlord retention permitted** ($500 вүӨ $700); **Washington 45-day storage window compliant**; **Washington 44 days premature conversion** (boundary); **Texas dual mail requirement satisfied** (first-class + certified); **Texas only certified mail violates dual requirement** (В§ 54.045 + В§ 54.046); **Florida personal delivery 10-day compliant**; **Florida mailed 15-day compliant**; **Florida high value landlord retention not permitted** ($800 > $500); **Florida under $500 landlord retention permitted** (boundary); **Illinois common-law only returns unverified** (765 ILCS 1026 escheat fallback); **explicit premature disposal flag triggers conversion**; **CA personal delivery notice constant pins 15 days**; **CA mailed notice constant pins 18 days**; **CA sale value threshold constant pins $700**; **WA storage window constant pins 45 days**; **TX pre-sale notice constant pins 30 days**; **FL personal delivery notice constant pins 10 days**; **FL mailed notice constant pins 15 days**; **FL sale value threshold constant pins $500**; **CO pre-sale notice constant pins 30 days**; **default notice days constant pins 30 days**; **very large property value no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero value no panic** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_source_of_income_discrimination` is the **source-of-income (SOI) housing discrimination compliance module** (iter 541) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for refusing Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers and other lawful income sources. Direct sibling to `rental_tenant_criminal_background_screening` (iter 539 вҖ” HUD/FCHA disparate-impact parallel), `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` (FHA accommodation parallel), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” state-by-state protected-class parallel), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA adverse-action parallel). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA SB 329** (Cal. Gov. Code В§В§ 12921 + 12955, effective Jan 1, 2020) makes SOI an explicit protected class including federal/state/local public assistance and housing subsidies (Section 8, VASH, SSI, TANF); CA Gov. Code В§ 12987 authorizes up to $25,000 punitive damages; (2) **NY State Human Rights Law** (NY Exec. Law В§ 296(2-a), effective Apr 12, 2019) + NYC HRL (NYC Admin. Code В§ 8-107(5)) prohibit SOI discrimination statewide with unlimited mental-anguish damages under Exec. Law В§ 297(9); (3) **NJ LAD** (N.J.S.A. 10:5-12.5, amended 2026) prohibits application of minimum-income requirements not based exclusively on tenant's portion of rent; NJ DCR has brought multiple enforcement actions; (4) **WA RCW 59.18.255** (HB 2578, effective Sept 30, 2018) protects voucher-program participation as protected class with the SPECIFIC requirement that landlords must DEDUCT the voucher subsidy from monthly rent before applying rent-to-income screening ratios вҖ” applying minimum-income test to FULL rent instead of tenant portion is a per se statutory violation under В§ 59.18.085; (5) **MA Gen. L. ch. 151B В§ 4(10) + 4(11)** protects Section 8 voucher holders and public-assistance recipients; (6) **Chicago Fair Housing Ordinance + Cook County Human Rights Ordinance** prohibit SOI discrimination for properties within those municipal boundaries; Illinois statewide has NO SOI antidiscrimination statute; (7) **Federal Fair Housing Act floor** under 42 U.S.C. В§ 3601 et seq. does NOT explicitly include SOI as a protected class вҖ” HUD Section 8 program under 24 C.F.R. Part 982 is voluntary for landlords absent state/local SOI law, but racially-disparate application of voucher refusal still creates 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604 disparate-impact liability per **Texas Dept. of Housing v. Inclusive Communities Project, 576 U.S. 519 (2015)**.

**Seven income source types**: Section8HousingChoiceVoucher, VashVeteransAffairsSupportiveHousingVoucher, SsiSupplementalSecurityIncome, TanfTemporaryAssistanceNeedyFamilies, SocialSecurityRetirementOrDisability, StateOrLocalRentalAssistanceProgram, EmploymentOnlyIncomeNoSoiTrigger.

**Five landlord actions**: OutrightRefusalToRentDueToVoucher, StatedNoSection8PolicyOnListing, MinimumIncomeTestAppliedToFullRentNotTenantPortion, DiscriminatoryHigherDepositDemand, CompliantAcceptedVoucherTenantPortionScreening.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, EmploymentOnlyIncomeNoSoiAnalysisTriggered, CompliantSoiAcceptanceWithTenantPortionScreening, IllinoisStatewideNoStateCoverageNoSoiClaim, FederalFhaDisparateImpactAvailable, StateSoiStatuteViolationActualAndPunitiveDamages, NjLadMinimumIncomeRuleViolationTenantPortionOnly, WashingtonRcw59_18_255VoucherDeductionFailureViolation.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-source-of-income-discrimination`. Twenty-two tests pin: **employment only income no SOI analysis triggered**; **compliant voucher acceptance with tenant-portion screening** ($3K rent - $2K voucher = $1K tenant portion); **California outright refusal state violation** (SB 329 + В§В§ 12921 + 12955 + $25,000 punitive ceiling); **New York outright refusal state violation** (NY Exec. Law В§ 296(2-a) + NYC Admin. Code В§ 8-107(5) + April 12, 2019 effective date); **New Jersey minimum income rule violation** (N.J.S.A. 10:5-12.5 + NJ DCR enforcement); **Washington voucher deduction failure violation** (RCW 59.18.255 + В§ 59.18.085); **Massachusetts state violation** (ch. 151B В§ 4(10)); **Illinois statewide no statute no claim** (Inclusive Communities 576 U.S. 519 disparate-impact fallback); **Illinois Chicago ordinance state violation** (Chicago Fair Housing Ordinance); **federal FHA floor only disparate impact available** (42 U.S.C. В§ 3601 + Inclusive Communities 2015 + $19,787 civil penalty); **VASH voucher triggers SOI analysis**; **SSI triggers SOI analysis**; **TANF triggers SOI analysis**; **Social Security triggers SOI analysis**; **state or local rental assistance triggers SOI analysis**; **stated no Section 8 policy triggers violation**; **discriminatory higher deposit triggers violation**; **FHA civil penalty constant pins $19,787**; **NJ typical civil penalty constant pins $10,000**; **NYS HRL typical damages constant pins $5,000**; **very large damages no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero damages uses baseline typical damages** ($5,000 default).

`traderview-expense::rental_tenant_criminal_background_screening` is the **tenant criminal-background-screening compliance module** (iter 539) вҖ” covers landlord compliance with HUD 2016 disparate-impact guidance and state and local Fair Chance Housing laws on tenant criminal-background screening. Direct sibling to `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA + adverse-action notice parallel screening regime), `rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` (FHA accommodation parallel), `rental_late_fee_cap` (iter 537 вҖ” state-by-state cap parallel grid), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (iter 533 вҖ” tenant-protection state-by-state grid). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **HUD 2016 GUIDANCE** (April 4, 2016 Helen Kanovsky General Counsel memorandum) establishes that blanket criminal-history bans create a DISPARATE IMPACT on protected classes (race, color, national origin) under the Fair Housing Act (42 U.S.C. В§ 3601 et seq.) absent a substantial, legitimate, nondiscriminatory interest backed by INDIVIDUALIZED ASSESSMENT вҖ” arrest records cannot serve as basis for adverse action (PER SE discriminatory because arrest is not proof of criminal conduct); conviction-record bans subject to three-factor analysis (nature/severity, time elapsed, relevance to tenancy); (2) **NYC LOCAL LAW 24 of 2024** (Fair Chance for Housing Act, effective Jan 1, 2025) prohibits consideration of criminal history until other qualifications determined with **3-year lookback for misdemeanors** + **5-year lookback for felonies**; sex crimes only convictions categorically considerable; (3) **CALIFORNIA AB 2052 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1786.21** imposes sequential-screening regime вҖ” criminal background check only after applicant meets other qualifications; **7-year lookback** for conviction-only consideration; (4) **NEW JERSEY FAIR CHANCE IN HOUSING ACT** (P.L. 2021, c. 197, codified at N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq.) bars housing providers from asking about criminal history before extending conditional offer; **1-year lookback for misdemeanors** + **4-year lookback for indictable felonies**; individualized assessment required post-conditional-offer; (5) **ILLINOIS HB 4366 (2024)** Fair Housing Act amendment restricting criminal-history screening for federally-assisted housing; (6) **42 U.S.C. В§ 3612(g)(3)** civil penalty (inflation-adjusted, currently $19,787 for first violation, $49,468 for second within 5 years, $98,935 for third within 7 years) under FHA enforcement; (7) **42 U.S.C. В§ 3613** private right of action with actual + emotional-distress + punitive damages + attorney fees.

**Three screening stages**: PreApplicationOrInitialApplication, AfterOtherQualificationsConfirmedConditionalStage, PostTenancyAdverseAction.

**Seven record types considered**: ArrestRecordWithoutConviction, MisdemeanorConvictionWithinLookbackWindow, MisdemeanorConvictionOutsideLookbackWindow, FelonyConvictionWithinLookbackWindow, FelonyConvictionOutsideLookbackWindow, SexOffenseConvictionCategoricallyConsiderable, NoCriminalHistoryIdentified.

**Two individualized-assessment statuses**: AssessmentPerformedAndDocumented, BlanketPolicyWithoutIndividualizedAssessment.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoCriminalHistoryNoSection804OrFchaViolation, CompliantSequentialScreeningAndIndividualizedAssessment, SexOffenseExceptionCategoricallyPermissiblePerJurisdiction, ArrestRecordReliancePerSeDiscriminatoryHud2016, PreApplicationInquiryViolatesFairChanceLaw, BlanketBanWithoutIndividualizedAssessmentDisparateImpact, LookbackWindowExceededFairChanceViolation.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-tenant-criminal-background-screening`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **no criminal history no violation** (В§ 804 + HUD 2016); **arrest record reliance per se discriminatory** (HUD 2016 + 42 U.S.C. В§ 3613 + В§ 3612(g)(3) + $19,787 civil penalty); **sex offense categorically considerable** (Adam Walsh Pub. L. 109-248); **NYC pre-application inquiry violates Local Law 24**; **California pre-application inquiry violates AB 2052** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1786.21); **New Jersey pre-application inquiry violates FCHA** (N.J.S.A. 46:8-52); **NYC misdemeanor 3-year lookback window**; **NYC felony 5-year lookback window**; **California 7-year lookback both types**; **NJ misdemeanor 1-year lookback**; **NJ felony 4-year lookback**; **NYC misdemeanor outside lookback window violation** (4 years > 3-year cap); **NYC misdemeanor at 3-year boundary compliant** (boundary); **blanket ban without individualized assessment violation** (HUD 2016 three-factor analysis + NATURE/TIME/RELEVANCE); **compliant sequential screening and individualized assessment**; **felony within lookback window compliant**; **NYC felony at 5-year boundary compliant** (boundary); **NYC felony outside 5-year window violation**; **NYC misdemeanor lookback constant pins 3 years**; **NYC felony lookback constant pins 5 years**; **CA lookback constant pins 7 years**; **NJ misdemeanor lookback constant pins 1 year**; **NJ felony lookback constant pins 4 years**; **HUD default lookback constant pins 7 years**; **arrest record overrides compliant individualized assessment** (severity ladder ordering); **sex offense overrides lookback window analysis** (30-year-old conviction still considerable); **no history overrides all other branches** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::rental_late_fee_cap` is the **late-fee cap + grace-period compliance module** (iter 537) вҖ” covers landlord compliance with state-by-state statutory caps on residential late fees and minimum grace periods before any fee may attach. Direct sibling to `rental_security_deposit_return_notice` (iter 535 вҖ” return-deadline parallel state grid), `rental_junk_fee_transparency` (transparency-disclosure parallel), `rental_security_deposit_interest` (interest-bearing parallel). Trader-landlord critical because excessive late fees are unenforceable under common-law liquidated-damages doctrine (must be reasonable estimate of actual damages, not penalty) and create statutory exposure under consumer-protection statutes. (1) **NY RPL В§ 238-a (HSTPA 2019)** caps late fees at the LESSER of $50 OR 5% of monthly rent + 5-day grace period; (2) **WA RCW 59.18.170** has NO statewide dollar cap but prohibits any late fee until rent is more than 5 days late; (3) **CO Rev. Stat. В§ 38-12-105 (HB 23-1099)** caps late fees at the GREATER of $50 OR 5% of monthly rent + 7-day grace period; (4) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-140(h)** caps late fees at $10 + 5% per month for rent over $500; (5) **TX Prop. Code В§ 92.019** statutory safe-harbor cap of 10% for properties with 4 or fewer units and 12% for properties with 5 or more units + 2-day grace period; (6) **CA Civ. Code В§ 1671** requires fee be reasonable estimate of damages with no statutory dollar cap (industry standard 5% or $50); (7) **MA Gen. L. ch. 186 В§ 15B(1)(c)** prohibits ANY late fee for the first 30 days after rent due (longest grace period of surveyed states); (8) **FL** has NO statewide cap or grace period for residential tenancies (Fla. Stat. В§ 83.808 covers only mobile-home tenancies).

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, LateFeeWithinCapAndGraceCompliant, LateFeeChargedBeforeGracePeriodExpired, LateFeeExceedsStatutoryCap, NoStatutoryCapCommonLawReasonablenessTest, NoLateFeeChargedCompliant, LongestGracePeriodMassachusettsThirtyDays.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-late-fee-cap`. Thirty-five tests pin: **no late fee charged compliant**; **New York $50 late fee within cap compliant** (RPL В§ 238-a + 5-day grace); **New York high rent 5% cap caps at $50** ($10K rent вҶ’ 5% = $500 > $50 flat вҶ’ cap $50); **New York low rent 5% cap caps below $50** ($500 rent вҶ’ 5% = $25 вҶ’ cap $25); **New York $75 late fee exceeds cap** (excess $25); **New York late fee before grace period unenforceable**; **New York at 5-day boundary compliant** (boundary); **Colorado greater of $50 or 5% cap** (HB 23-1099); **Colorado 7-day grace period violated at 6 days**; **Illinois Chicago RLTO $10 + 5% formula** ($2K rent вҶ’ $10 + 5% Г— $1.5K = $85); **Illinois Chicago low rent under $500 only flat $10**; **Texas 4 or fewer units 10% cap** (В§ 92.019 + $2K rent вҶ’ $200); **Texas 5 or more units 12% cap** ($2K rent вҶ’ $240); **Texas 2-day grace period violated at 1 day**; **Washington no statutory cap uses common-law test**; **Washington grace period 5 days violated at 4 days**; **California no cap common-law reasonableness** (В§ 1671); **Florida no statewide cap or grace period** (В§ 83.808 mobile-home only); **Massachusetts 30-day grace period longest of surveyed states** (В§ 15B(1)(c)); **Massachusetts within 30-day grace period unenforceable**; **default jurisdiction no cap common-law**; **NY flat cap constant pins $50**; **NY percent cap constant pins 5%**; **NY grace period constant pins 5 days**; **WA grace period constant pins 5 days**; **CO flat floor constant pins $50**; **CO grace period constant pins 7 days**; **IL Chicago flat cap constant pins $10**; **IL Chicago rent threshold constant pins $500**; **TX 4 or fewer units percent constant pins 10%**; **TX 5 or more units percent constant pins 12%**; **TX grace period constant pins 2 days**; **MA grace period constant pins 30 days**; **very large rent no overflow in percent calc** (saturating_mul defense u64::MAX / 10); **zero rent no panic** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_security_deposit_return_notice` is the **security-deposit return notice + itemized-deduction compliance module** (iter 535) вҖ” covers landlord civil exposure for failing to return tenant security deposits within statutory deadlines and provide itemized deductions. Direct sibling to `rental_security_deposit_interest` (state-by-state interest-bearing requirements), `rental_landlord_notice_to_enter` (iter 533 вҖ” pre-move-out access pattern), `rental_retaliation_prohibition` (iter 531 вҖ” adverse-action timing windows), `rental_illegal_lockout_self_help_eviction` (iter 529 вҖ” extra-judicial dispossession). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **CA Civ. Code В§ 1950.5(g)** requires 21 calendar days from move-out + itemized written statement + receipts for deductions вүҘ $125, with В§ 1950.5(l) bad-faith retention triggering up to 2Г— deposit + actual damages + attorney fees; (2) **NY Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 7-108(1-a)(e)** imposes the shortest 14-day deadline, with failure forfeiting retention right + 2Г— damages; (3) **WA RCW 59.18.280** requires 30 days (extended from 21 days) + full itemized statement + documentation + actual damages + court costs + attorney fees on default; (4) **TX Prop. Code В§ 92.103** requires 30 days + itemized written description, with **В§ 92.109** bad-faith retention triggering $100 statutory penalty + 3Г— the wrongfully withheld amount + attorney fees; (5) **FL Stat. В§ 83.49(3)(a)** uses 15 days if no claim asserted vs 30 days if claim asserted, with strict CERTIFIED-MAIL delivery requirement вҖ” first-class mail of deduction claim fails Florida's specific method requirement; (6) **IL Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-080** requires 45 days + 2Г— deposit + attorney fees while **765 ILCS 710/1** applies 30 days for buildings with 5+ units statewide; (7) **MA Gen. L. ch. 186 В§ 15B(4)(iii)** requires 30 days + sworn statement of damages, with **В§ 15B(7)** bad-faith retention triggering 3Г— deposit + interest + attorney fees вҖ” the most aggressive bad-faith multiplier of any surveyed state.

**Three deductions-claimed branches**: NoDeductionsClaimedFullReturnDue, DeductionsClaimedItemizedStatementProvided, DeductionsClaimedNoItemizedStatementProvided.

**Four delivery methods**: PersonalDeliveryOrFirstClassMail, CertifiedMailRequiredFlorida, EmailWithoutTenantConsent, NoDelivery.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantWithinStatutoryWindow, CompliantNoDeductionsFullReturn, LateButReturnedRiskOfForfeitureOnly, DeductionsWithoutItemizedStatementForfeitsRetentionRight, BadFaithRetentionDoubleOrTripleDamages, NoDeliveryFullForfeitureAndStatutoryDamages.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-security-deposit-return-notice`. Thirty-three tests pin: **California compliant within 21-day window** (boundary); **California 22 days late triggers bad-faith 2Г— damages** (В§ 1950.5(l) + 2 Г— $3K = $6K); **California at 21-day boundary compliant** (boundary); **California no deductions full return compliant**; **deductions without itemized statement forfeits retention** (В§ 1950.5(g)(2) + $125 receipt threshold); **New York 14-day deadline**; **New York 15 days triggers bad-faith 2Г—** (NY GOL В§ 7-108 + 2 Г— $3K = $6K); **Washington 30-day deadline extended from 21**; **Texas 30-day deadline with bad-faith triple plus $100** (В§ 92.109 + $100 + 3 Г— $500 = $1,600); **Florida no deductions 15-day deadline**; **Florida with deductions 30-day deadline certified mail required** (В§ 83.49(3)(a) + CERTIFIED MAIL); **Florida certified mail with deductions compliant**; **Illinois Chicago RLTO 45-day deadline**; **Illinois Chicago bad-faith 2Г— damages**; **Illinois statewide 30-day deadline**; **Massachusetts bad-faith 3Г— damages** (В§ 15B(7) + 3 Г— $3K = $9K); **no delivery full forfeiture** (CA 2 Г— $3K = $6K); **bad-faith flag triggers multiplied damages within window**; **CA deadline days constant pins 21**; **NY deadline days constant pins 14**; **WA deadline days constant pins 30**; **TX deadline days constant pins 30**; **FL no claim deadline constant pins 15**; **FL claim deadline constant pins 30**; **IL Chicago deadline constant pins 45**; **IL statewide deadline constant pins 30**; **MA deadline constant pins 30**; **CA receipt threshold constant pins $125**; **TX bad-faith base penalty constant pins $100**; **TX bad-faith multiplier pins 3**; **MA bad-faith multiplier pins 3**; **very large deposit no overflow** (saturating_mul defense u64::MAX / 2); **zero deposit no panic** (boundary).

Fredman defense** (59 Ill. 2d 20 (1974)); **default jurisdiction returns state-law presumption note**; **habitability complaint to landlord triggers protection**; **joined tenants' association triggers protection**; **rent increase qualifies as adverse action**; **service decrease qualifies as adverse action**; **CA presumption window constant pins 180 days**; **CA punitive damage min constant pins $100**; **CA punitive damage max constant pins $2,000**; **NY presumption window constant pins 365 days**; **NY rent-stabilized presumption window constant pins 1,095 days**; **WA presumption window constant pins 90 days**; **TX presumption window constant pins 180 days**; **TX statutory civil penalty constant pins $500**; **IL Chicago RLTO presumption window constant pins 365 days**; **very large monthly rent no overflow in Washington triple calc** (saturating_mul defense); **zero monthly rent no panic in Chicago two-month calc** (boundary).
