Thirty tests pin: **no lockout court process used returns not applicable**; **California 30-day lockout calculates $3,000 penalty plus damages**; **California 1-day lockout applies $250 statutory minimum floor** (boundary); **New York lockout triggers treble damages plus alternative accommodation** ($2K Г— 3 + $5K alt = $11K); **Texas not invoked safe harbor triggers full statutory penalty** ($1K + month rent + property = $6K); **Texas bona-fide repairs safe harbor satisfied no violation**; **Texas rent delinquent procedural compliance satisfied no violation**; **Texas rent delinquent procedural failure triggers full penalty**; **Washington lockout triple monthly rent or actual damages greater** (3 Г— $3K = $9K > $2K actual вҶ’ $9K); **Washington high actual damages exceed triple rent use actual** ($20K > $9K вҶ’ $20K); **Florida lockout three months rent default** ($9K > $2K actual вҶ’ $9K); **Illinois lockout common-law wrongful eviction baseline**; **default jurisdiction returns state law violation note**; **utility shutoff method triggers violation same as lock change**; **dumped belongings method triggers violation**; **bootlock method explicitly prohibited in Florida** (В§ 83.67 verbatim); **CA per day penalty constant pins $100**; **CA statutory minimum floor constant pins $250**; **TX statutory penalty constant pins $1,000**; **NY treble multiplier pins 3**; **WA treble rent multiplier pins 3**; **FL three months rent multiplier pins 3**; **very large monthly rent no overflow in Washington triple** (saturating_mul defense); **extreme days count no overflow in California per day** (u32::MAX defense); **CA note pins AG Bulletin 2022-DLE-05**; **NY note pins Class A misdemeanor criminal exposure**; **NY note describes rent-stabilized tenancy value**; **Texas note describes 24/7 key availability requirement**; **Illinois note pins Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-160**; **zero monthly rent no panic in Florida three months calc** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::rental_elevator_safety_inspection` is the **rental property elevator safety inspection compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating a multifamily building with elevators comply with state-specific periodic inspection, testing, and certification requirements grounded in ASME A17.1 Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators? Trader-landlord operational concern: expired elevator certification creates strict-liability exposure for elevator injury claims + per-day civil penalty + emergency department shutdown authority + tort liability multiplier. Distinct from siblings `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` (VGB Act), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector`, `rental_bedroom_egress_window`, `soft_story_seismic_retrofit`.

**Three regimes** вҖ” California (Cal/OSHA + Form 80) vs NYC (DOB Category 1/3/5 + PVT-A) vs Default (ASME A17.1-2025):

**California вҖ” Cal. Labor Code В§В§ 7300-7324.2 + Title 8 Subchapter 6 Elevator Safety Orders** вҖ” Cal/OSHA Elevator Unit at California DIR issues elevator permits. **Annual inspection required**; permit valid 1 year. В§ 7317 вҖ” inspectors must have at least **4 years mechanical/electrical experience** including 1 year elevator-specific. В§ 7300.1 adopts ASME A17.1 by reference. В§ 7320 вҖ” **Form 80 permit-to-operate** must be posted in elevator; **$200/day civil penalty** for operation without valid permit.

**New York City вҖ” NYC Admin Code В§ 28-304 + NYC Building Code Chapter 30 + Table N1 of ASME A17.1 as modified by Chapter K1 of Appendix K** вҖ” two annual inspections required: **Category 1 test (PCT)** by approved elevator agency + Department of Buildings (DOB) periodic inspection. **Category 5 test** required every **5 years**; **Category 3 hydraulic test** every **3 years**. В§ 28-304.6.1 вҖ” inspections must be performed by **DOB-approved elevator agency**. **PVT-A Form** (Periodic Test and Inspection Affirmation) must be filed with DOB within **60 days** of inspection. В§ 28-304.6.5 вҖ” civil penalty **$3,000-$10,000 per violation** + DOB stop-use order authority.

**Default вҖ” ASME A17.1-2025 (Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators) + ANSI/ASME A17.1/CSA B44-2025** вҖ” ASME A17.1-2025 published; periodic inspection per Table N1; state adoption timelines vary. **QEI (Qualified Elevator Inspector)** certification by ASME or state authority. **Category 1 test (annual)** + Category 3 (3-year hydraulic) + Category 5 (5-year full). Building owner responsible for engaging certified inspector + maintaining inspection records on premises.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-elevator-safety-inspection`. Thirty-eight tests pin: **CA clean compliant**; **CA annual 366 days violation** (В§ 7320); **CA uncertified inspector violation** (В§ 7317 4 years); **CA Form 80 not posted violation**; **CA $200/day penalty exact compliant**; **CA wrong penalty amount violation**; **NYC clean compliant**; **NYC annual Category 1 366 days violation** (В§ 28-304 PCT); **NYC hydraulic Category 3 3-year boundary compliant**; **NYC hydraulic Category 3 over 3-year violation**; **NYC non-hydraulic no Category 3 check**; **NYC Category 5 5-year boundary compliant**; **NYC Category 5 over 5-year violation**; **NYC unapproved agency violation** (В§ 28-304.6.1); **NYC PVT-A not filed violation** (60 days); **NYC penalty at $3,000 minimum in range**; **NYC penalty at $10,000 maximum in range**; **NYC penalty below minimum violation**; **NYC penalty above maximum violation**; **default clean compliant**; **default QEI certified inspector accepted**; **default no certification violation**; **citation pins CA / NYC / default authorities** (all three); **note pins CA $200/day penalty**; **note pins CA 4 years inspector experience**; **note pins NYC three test categories** (Category 1 PCT + Category 3 hydraulic + Category 5 full); **note pins NYC PVT-A 60-day filing**; **note pins NYC $3,000-$10,000 penalty range**; **note pins default ASME A17.1-2025**; **note pins default three category schedule**; **Category 3 only engages for hydraulic invariant**; **CA uniquely requires Form 80 invariant**; **NYC uniquely requires PVT-A 60-day filing invariant**; **NYC Category 5 5-year invariant**; **inspector certification truth table for default** (4-cell sweep вҖ” 3 certified types + None); **multiple NYC violations stack** (3 violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property FIRE EXTINGUISHER installation, inspection, and maintenance compliance framework module** вҖ” when must a landlord provide portable fire extinguishers to tenants, what travel-distance and visibility standards apply, what inspection cycle is required, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a fire event? Distinct from siblings `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (iter 471), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector`, `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure`, `rental_bedroom_egress_window`, `rental_window_blind_cord_safety` (iter 469), `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety`, `landlord_retaliation_damages`, `tenant_emotional_distress_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) Texas trader-landlord has explicit statutory duty under В§ 92.252 + В§ 92.255 to inspect at occupancy and respond to tenant written request, with no statewide installation mandate but strict inspection-after-provision duty; (2) Massachusetts trader operating multifamily in Boston/Worcester must comply with NFPA 10 in common areas per 527 CMR 1.00 + local ordinance; (3) New Jersey trader-landlord avoiding contractual extinguisher commitment limits statutory exposure but must still maintain any installed unit per NFPA 10; (4) annual inspection cost $5-$25 per extinguisher vs. tort liability exposure exceeding $1M per fire injury; (5) 6-year internal examination + 12-year hydrostatic test are key date-tracking compliance obligations easily missed in portfolio management.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **Texas (MOST SPECIFIC landlord duty)** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.252 + В§ 92.255 | Landlord must inspect any installed **1A10BC residential extinguisher** at **beginning of tenant's occupancy** and within **reasonable time after tenant's written request**; repair/replace duty for non-functional + incorrect pressure + tenant-used units; no statewide installation mandate |
| **Massachusetts** | 527 C.M.R. 1.00 (Comprehensive Fire Safety Code adopting NFPA 10); M.G.L. c. 148 В§ 26G + В§ 28A | Multifamily 3+ unit common-area extinguishers per local fire-marshal authority |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.A.C. 5:70-3 (NJ Uniform Fire Code adopting NFPA 10); N.J.S.A. 46:8-39 to -50 | No in-unit statutory mandate absent lease contract or local ordinance; installed units must be maintained per NFPA 10; hotel/multiple-dwelling registration triggers fire-safety inspections |
| **Default** | NFPA 10 (current edition); Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 | NFPA 10 voluntary national standard adopted by reference in most state fire codes + common-law habitability + state implied warranty |

**NFPA 10 five-cycle inspection framework**:
1. **Monthly visual inspection** вҖ” gauge in green, tamper seal intact, label legible, weight check
2. **Annual maintenance** вҖ” certified technician inspection including hose/nozzle/discharge mechanism + recharge if needed
3. **Six-year internal examination** вҖ” required for dry-chemical units; complete disassembly + recharge
4. **Twelve-year hydrostatic test** вҖ” pressure-vessel integrity test
5. **Recharge after every use** вҖ” including partial discharge; Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.255 explicit landlord duty

**NFPA 10 placement requirements**:
- **Class A** (ordinary combustibles): maximum **75-ft travel distance**
- **Class B** (flammable liquids): 30-ft or 50-ft based on hazard
- Mounting height: max **5 ft** (handle from floor) for extinguishers up to 40 lbs; max **3 ft 6 in** for over 40 lbs
- Visibility: must be conspicuously located + clearly marked; signage required if not visible

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Provided extinguisher non-functional during fire** вҶ’ tort negligence + premises liability + implied-warranty breach
2. **No extinguisher provided in multifamily building** where required by local code вҶ’ fire code violation + civil penalties + tenant rescission
3. **Extinguisher used by tenant + landlord refuses recharge** вҶ’ Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.255 explicit violation + constructive eviction
4. **Tenant complaint regarding gauge/pressure/expiration ignored** вҶ’ habitability breach + retaliation (`landlord_retaliation_damages`)
5. **Fire injury during tenancy with non-compliant extinguisher** вҶ’ tort negligence + wrongful death + IIED (`tenant_emotional_distress_damages` iter 453)

**Four building types modeled**: `SingleFamily`, `DuplexTwoUnit`, `MultifamilyThreeToFive`, `MultifamilyOverFive`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-fire-extinguisher-requirement`. Thirty-two tests pin: **Texas compliant baseline**; **no extinguisher single family compliant**; **no extinguisher MA multifamily breach** (527 C.M.R. 1.00 + В§ 26G); **no extinguisher NJ multifamily no breach** (no statutory mandate); **annual inspection overdue over 12 months** (12-month + certified technician); **annual inspection exactly 12 months compliant**; **6-year internal exam overdue** (dry-chemical); **12-year hydrostatic overdue**; **gauge not green defect observed**; **travel distance over 75 ft placement violation** (Class A 75-ft cap); **travel distance exactly 75 ft compliant**; **used by tenant not recharged statutory duty breach Texas** (В§ 92.255); **used by tenant recharged compliant**; **tenant written request unaddressed breach Texas** (В§ 92.255 reasonable-time); **tenant written request addressed compliant**; **fire injury event top severity** (tenant_emotional_distress_damages); **MA jurisdiction cites 527 C.M.R. 1.00 + В§ 26G + В§ 28A**; **NJ jurisdiction cites N.J.A.C. 5:70-3 + N.J.S.A. 46:8-39**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + В§ 1941.1); **severity priority injury > statutory > placement > defect > inspection**; **severity statutory > placement > defect > inspection**; **severity placement > defect > inspection**; **severity defect > inspection**; **citation pins all authorities** (NFPA 10 + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.252/В§ 92.255 + 527 C.M.R. 1.00 + В§ 26G + В§ 28A + N.J.A.C. 5:70-3 + N.J.S.A. 46:8-39 + Hilder + В§ 1941.1); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins five-cycle inspection framework** (monthly/annual/6-year/12-year/recharge); **note pins NFPA 10 placement** (75-ft + Class A/B + mounting height); **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins companion modules**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **Texas uniquely strictest inspection duty invariant**; **multiple problems stack in actions**.

`traderview-expense::rental_flood_hazard_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential rental flood hazard disclosure framework module** вҖ” climate-era statutory disclosure regime added across multiple states between 2018 and 2024. Trader-landlord critical because waterfront / coastal / floodplain-zone investment properties (common in trader real-estate portfolios) trigger mandatory written pre-lease disclosure under state law, and failure routes directly to tenant LEASE TERMINATION right plus statutory damages exposure. Distinct from siblings `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (subsurface water), `rental_sinkhole_disclosure` (FL specific), `rental_property_registration` (registration regimes), `tenant_in_foreclosure_protection`.

**Four-jurisdiction statutory framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Effective Date | Trigger | Tenant Remedy |
|--------------|--------|----------------|---------|---------------|
| **California** | Cal. Gov. Code В§ 8589.45 (AB 646 of 2017) | **July 1, 2018** | ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE + FEMA Special Flood Hazard Area OR Cal OES area of potential flooding | Civil penalties + lease damages |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0135 (HB 531 of 2021) | **January 1, 2022** | 100-year floodplain (unless elevation above per federal regulations) OR 5-year prior flooding damage | **TERMINATE LEASE within 30 days of SUBSTANTIAL LOSS** (50%+ of personal property value) |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.S.A. 46:8-50 et seq. (Flood Risk Notification Law 2024) | **March 20, 2024** | Commercial OR 3+ residential units (4+ if owner-occupied) + FEMA 100-year OR 500-year floodplain | **TERMINATE LEASE + statutory damages + attorney fees** |
| **Default** | Common-law fraudulent concealment + implied warranty of habitability | N/A | Actual/constructive knowledge + concealment | Common-law damages |

Pinned by `ca_special_flood_hazard_compliant`, `ca_no_landlord_knowledge_no_obligation`, `ca_pre_july_2018_no_obligation` (boundary precision вҖ” June 2018 = no obligation, July 2018 = obligation), `texas_floodplain_100_year_triggers_obligation`, `texas_elevation_above_floodplain_exception_lifts_obligation`, `texas_pre_2022_no_obligation` (boundary), `nj_3_unit_floodplain_triggers_obligation`, `nj_2_unit_no_obligation`, `nj_pre_march_2024_no_obligation` (boundary вҖ” Feb 2024 = no, March 2024 = obligation).

**California content requirements вҖ” Cal. Gov. Code В§ 8589.45**:

| Content element | Source |
|------------------|--------|
| Special Flood Hazard Area / area of potential flooding statement | В§ 8589.45(a)(1) |
| Cal OES MyHazards website URL | В§ 8589.45(a)(2) |
| Renter's insurance + flood insurance recommendation | В§ 8589.45(a)(3) |
| Owner not required to provide additional info statement | В§ 8589.45(a)(4) |
| 8-point minimum type size | В§ 8589.45(c) |

Pinned by `ca_missing_myhazards_url_violation`, `ca_missing_insurance_recommendation_violation`, `ca_under_8_point_type_violation`, `ca_missing_disclosure_violation`.

**Texas two-prong trigger вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0135**:

| Prong | Source | Trigger | Exception |
|-------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 1 (geographic) | В§ 92.0135(a)(1) | Dwelling in 100-year floodplain | Elevation above per federal regulations |
| 2 (historical) | В§ 92.0135(a)(2) | Landlord knowledge of flooding damage within preceding 5 years | None |

Pinned by `texas_5_year_prior_flooding_triggers_obligation`. The TX 30-day tenant termination right requires: (1) failed disclosure; (2) tenant suffered SUBSTANTIAL LOSS (50%+ of personal property repair/replacement value); (3) termination filed within 30 days of loss. Pinned by `texas_substantial_loss_within_30_days_engages_termination_right` (day 15 = engaged; day 31 = no longer engaged).

**New Jersey unit-threshold matrix вҖ” N.J.S.A. 46:8-50**:

| Owner-occupied? | Unit count | Disclosure obligation |
|------------------|------------|------------------------|
| No (investor-owned) | 1-2 | NOT required |
| No (investor-owned) | 3+ | REQUIRED |
| Yes | 1-3 | NOT required |
| Yes | 4+ | REQUIRED |
| Any | Commercial | REQUIRED regardless of unit count |

Pinned by `nj_3_unit_floodplain_triggers_obligation`, `nj_2_unit_no_obligation`, `nj_owner_occupied_4_unit_triggers_obligation`, `nj_owner_occupied_3_unit_no_obligation`, `nj_commercial_property_triggers_regardless_of_units`. NJ uniquely engages the **500-year Moderate Risk Flood Hazard Area** trigger вҖ” pinned by `nj_uniquely_engages_500_year_moderate_risk_invariant` (CA + TX only engage 100-year).

**New Jersey format requirements вҖ” N.J.S.A. 46:8-50(c)**: separate rider + 12-point minimum typeface + signed/acknowledged by tenant. Pinned by `nj_missing_rider_with_signature_violation`.

**Lease termination right uniquely engages in TX (substantial-loss-conditioned) and NJ (categorical)** вҖ” pinned by `nj_failure_to_disclose_engages_lease_termination_right`. CA does not provide statutory lease termination right (general civil + lease damages instead).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-flood-hazard-disclosure`. Thirty-five tests pin: **CA special flood hazard compliant**; **CA no landlord knowledge no obligation**; **CA pre-July 2018 no obligation** (boundary precision); **CA missing disclosure violation**; **CA missing MyHazards URL violation**; **CA missing insurance recommendation violation**; **CA under-8-point type violation**; **Texas floodplain 100-year triggers obligation**; **Texas elevation above floodplain exception lifts obligation**; **Texas 5-year prior flooding triggers obligation**; **Texas substantial loss within 30 days engages termination right** (day 15 engaged; day 31 no longer engaged); **Texas pre-2022 no obligation**; **NJ 3-unit floodplain triggers obligation**; **NJ 2-unit no obligation**; **NJ owner-occupied 4-unit triggers obligation**; **NJ owner-occupied 3-unit no obligation**; **NJ commercial property triggers regardless of units**; **NJ pre-March 2024 no obligation** (boundary); **NJ moderate risk 500-year floodplain triggers obligation**; **NJ missing rider with signature violation**; **NJ failure to disclose engages lease termination right**; **default no obligation**; **citation pins all four jurisdictions**; **note pins CA July 2018 effective date**; **note pins CA MyHazards website**; **note pins CA renter's flood insurance recommendation**; **note pins CA 8-point type requirement**; **note pins Texas January 2022 two-prong framework**; **note pins Texas 30-day substantial loss remedy**; **note pins NJ March 2024 effective date unit threshold**; **note pins NJ 12-point rider signature requirement**; **note pins NJ lease termination remedy**; **note pins FEMA NFIP separate from state disclosure**; **jurisdiction truth table four cells**; **NJ uniquely engages 500-year moderate risk invariant**.

`traderview-expense::rental_energy_benchmarking` is the **rental property energy benchmarking + GHG emissions disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord owning a large multifamily building annually report energy/water consumption AND comply with carbon emissions caps? Trader-landlord critical for any large multifamily owner in NYC + Boston + other jurisdictions with building-emissions ordinances: failure triggers ongoing per-ton penalties ($268/ton NYC LL97; $300/ton Boston BERDO 2.0) plus annual benchmarking penalties ($2,000/year max NYC LL84). Distinct from siblings `rental_property_registration` (general landlord registration), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (new construction electrification mandate), and `landlord_annual_rent_statement` (rent disclosure).

**Three regimes** вҖ” NYC most aggressive vs Boston BERDO 2.0 vs Default:

| Regime | Benchmarking threshold | Emissions threshold | Per-ton penalty |
|--------|------------------------|---------------------|------------------|
| NYC | LL84: >25,000 sq ft OR groups >100,000 sq ft aggregate | LL97 covered buildings | **$268/ton CO2e** |
| Boston BERDO | вүҘ35,000 sq ft | Net-zero by 2050 | **$300/ton CO2e** |
| Default | Verify local (CA AB 802 = 50,000 sq ft + Seattle BAEDO + DC GBES + Chicago) | Less common | N/A statewide |

**NYC Local Law 84 of 2009 + Local Law 97 of 2019 (Climate Mobilization Act)** вҖ” LL84 requires annual energy + water benchmarking via ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager by May 1st; LL84 penalties $500 missed deadline + $500/quarter additional + $2,000/year max; LL97 requires annual GHG emissions reporting to NYC DOB with $268/metric ton CO2e exceedance penalty. Pinned by `nyc_compliant_passes`, `nyc_under_25000_sqft_not_in_scope` (exactly 25,000 = not in scope), `nyc_at_25001_sqft_in_scope`, `nyc_group_over_100000_sqft_in_scope` (group threshold), `nyc_ll84_missed_deadline_500_dollar_penalty` (50,000 cents = $500), `nyc_ll84_one_quarter_past_1000_dollar_penalty` ($1,000), `nyc_ll84_three_quarters_past_2000_dollar_penalty` ($2,000 cap), `nyc_ll84_capped_at_2000_dollar_annual_max` (100 quarters вҶ’ still capped at $2,000), `nyc_ll97_emissions_under_cap_no_penalty`, `nyc_ll97_emissions_at_cap_boundary_no_penalty`, `nyc_ll97_emissions_1_ton_over_268_dollar_penalty` (1 ton Г— $268 = 26,800 cents), `nyc_ll97_emissions_100_tons_over_26800_dollar_penalty` (100 tons Г— $268 = $26,800), `nyc_combined_ll84_and_ll97_penalties_stack`, `nyc_citation_pins_ll84_ll97`, `nyc_note_pins_268_per_ton_penalty`, and `nyc_note_pins_may_1st_deadline`.

**Boston BERDO 2.0 (Building Emissions Reduction and Disclosure Ordinance, eff. 2021)** вҖ” annual energy + water consumption reporting + GHG emissions standards with declining limits through net-zero by 2050; penalties $300/metric ton CO2e above limits (uniquely higher per-ton penalty than NYC). Pinned by `boston_compliant_passes`, `boston_under_35000_sqft_not_in_scope` (34,999 fails threshold), `boston_at_35000_sqft_in_scope` (exactly 35,000 = in scope), `boston_emissions_over_cap_300_dollar_per_ton` (100 tons Г— $300 = $30,000), `boston_missed_deadline_violates`, `boston_citation_pins_berdo`, `boston_note_pins_300_per_ton_penalty`, and `boston_uniquely_higher_per_ton_penalty_invariant` ($300 Boston > $268 NYC).

**Default вҖ” locality-controlled fallback** вҖ” no federal benchmarking mandate; most major U.S. cities adopt similar programs with size thresholds 25,000-50,000 sq ft: CA AB 802 (50,000 sq ft statewide), Seattle BAEDO, DC GBES, Chicago Energy Benchmarking, Minneapolis. Emissions caps less common than NYC + Boston. Pinned by `default_no_violations_regardless` (out of scope regardless of inputs), `default_citation_pins_ca_ab_802`, and `three_regimes_routed_correctly`.

**NYC 25,000 sq ft threshold uniquely lower than Boston 35,000 invariant** вҖ” 30,000 sq ft building IS in scope NYC; NOT in scope Boston. Pinned by `nyc_unique_25000_sqft_threshold_vs_boston_35000_invariant`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” emissions exceedance via saturating subtraction prevents underflow; emissions cap = 0 + emissions = u32::MAX produces large penalty without overflow. Pinned by `defensive_emissions_under_cap_zero_penalty` and `defensive_emissions_overflow_saturating`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-energy-benchmarking`. Thirty tests pin: **NYC compliant passes**; **NYC under 25,000 sq ft not in scope**; **NYC at 25,001 sq ft in scope**; **NYC group over 100,000 sq ft in scope**; **NYC LL84 missed deadline $500 penalty**; **NYC LL84 one quarter past $1,000 penalty**; **NYC LL84 three quarters past $2,000 penalty**; **NYC LL84 capped at $2,000 annual max**; **NYC LL97 emissions under cap no penalty**; **NYC LL97 emissions at cap boundary no penalty**; **NYC LL97 emissions 1 ton over $268 penalty**; **NYC LL97 emissions 100 tons over $26,800 penalty**; **NYC combined LL84 + LL97 penalties stack**; **Boston compliant passes**; **Boston under 35,000 sq ft not in scope**; **Boston at 35,000 sq ft in scope**; **Boston emissions over cap $300 per ton**; **Boston missed deadline violates**; **default no violations regardless**; **NYC citation pins LL84 + LL97**; **Boston citation pins BERDO**; **default citation pins CA AB 802 + Seattle BAEDO**; **NYC note pins $268 per ton penalty**; **NYC note pins May 1st deadline**; **Boston note pins $300 per ton penalty**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **NYC unique 25,000 sq ft threshold vs Boston 35,000 invariant**; **Boston uniquely higher per-ton penalty invariant** ($300 > $268); **defensive emissions under cap zero penalty**; **defensive emissions overflow saturating** (u32::MAX).

`traderview-expense::rental_broadband_mte_rules` is the **FCC Multiple Tenant Environment (MTE) broadband-access rules module** вҖ” when may a trader-landlord lawfully enter into a contract with an internet service provider granting exclusive access + revenue sharing + bulk billing arrangements for residential or commercial multifamily buildings? Trader-landlord critical for any multifamily property considering ISP partnership: an exclusive contract OR an exclusive revenue-sharing agreement OR a graduated revenue-sharing agreement for broadband-only is categorically PROHIBITED under 47 CFR В§ 64.2500 et seq. and the FCC's 2024 Open Internet Order. Distinct from siblings `tenant_data_privacy`, `tenant_organizing`, `landlord_identification_disclosure`, and `lease_disclosures`.

**Two regimes** вҖ” Federal FCC vs Default state law:

| Regime | Authority | Scope |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| Federal FCC | 47 CFR В§ 64.2500-64.2503 + FCC Open Internet Order FCC 24-52 (July 2024) + FCC 22-12 + FCC 10-49 | Telecom + cable + broadband providers in MTEs |
| Default | Common-law lease analysis + state-specific broadband disclosure (CA SB 1130 + NY HSTPA 2019) | When FCC rules silent |

**Federal FCC arrangement-type truth table** вҖ” six contract arrangements, three prohibited for broadband-only:

| Arrangement | Telecom | Cable | Broadband-only |
|-------------|---------|-------|----------------|
| Exclusive access | **PROHIBITED** | **PROHIBITED** | **PROHIBITED** |
| Exclusive revenue-sharing | **PROHIBITED** | **PROHIBITED** | **PROHIBITED** |
| Graduated revenue-sharing | Permitted | Permitted | **PROHIBITED** (Open Internet Order) |
| Flat licensing fee | Permitted | Permitted | Permitted |
| Bulk billing | Permitted | Permitted | Permitted (Carr Jan 2025 withdrew ban) |
| Non-exclusive marketing | Permitted | Permitted | Permitted |

Pinned by `arrangement_truth_table_fcc_mte_broadband` (6-arrangement sweep for broadband-only), `provider_type_distinguishes_graduated_revenue_sharing` (3-provider sweep: telecom + cable permitted; broadband-only prohibited), `fcc_categorical_prohibition_invariant_for_exclusive_access_and_exclusive_revenue` (2-arrangement Г— 3-provider sweep = all 6 prohibited), `fcc_flat_licensing_fee_lawful_for_all_provider_types` (3-provider sweep), `fcc_bulk_billing_lawful_for_all_provider_types` (3-provider sweep).

**Exclusive access categorical prohibition** вҖ” 47 CFR В§ 64.2500-64.2503 prohibits exclusive access contracts between landlords and providers for telecom + cable + broadband in MTEs. Pinned by `fcc_exclusive_access_prohibited` and `fcc_exclusive_revenue_sharing_prohibited`.

**FCC Open Internet Order FCC 24-52 (July 2024) broadband-only graduated revenue-sharing ban** вҖ” graduated (revenue-tied) sharing agreements between landlord and broadband-only ISP are prohibited; flat licensing fees not tied to revenue remain permitted. Pinned by `fcc_broadband_only_graduated_revenue_sharing_prohibited`, `fcc_telecom_graduated_revenue_sharing_lawful` (telecom NOT subject to 2024 ban), `fcc_cable_graduated_revenue_sharing_lawful`, and `fcc_note_pins_open_internet_order_broadband_only`.

**Bulk billing arrangements REMAIN PERMITTED** вҖ” FCC 10-49 (2010) confirmed bulk billing is permitted; Chairman Carr withdrew the 2024 proposed bulk-billing ban in January 2025. Bulk billing arrangements where a provider serves every tenant and tenants are billed a prorated share remain lawful. Pinned by `fcc_bulk_billing_lawful` and `fcc_note_pins_bulk_billing_carr_withdrawal` (FCC 10-49 + Carr + January 2025 + REMAIN PERMITTED).

**MTE engagement gate** вҖ” FCC rules apply only when the property is a multiple tenant environment (residential or commercial multifamily). Single-tenant properties are not subject to MTE rules. Pinned by `fcc_single_tenant_not_subject_to_mte_rules`, `fcc_mte_required_for_prohibition_invariant` (exclusive access permitted on single-tenant property; prohibited on MTE), and `fcc_uniquely_engages_mte_rules_invariant`.

**Default state law fallback** вҖ” common-law lease analysis governs when FCC rules silent; California SB 1130 imposes broadband disclosure obligations on landlords; New York HSTPA 2019 contains broadband access provisions. Pinned by `default_no_fcc_rules_apply` (exclusive access not prohibited at default level since FCC engagement not triggered), and `default_citation_pins_state_disclosure` (CA SB 1130 + NY HSTPA 2019).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-broadband-mte-rules`. Twenty-four tests pin: **FCC flat licensing fee lawful**; **FCC bulk billing lawful**; **FCC non-exclusive marketing lawful**; **FCC exclusive access prohibited** (В§ 64.2500); **FCC exclusive revenue sharing prohibited**; **FCC broadband-only graduated revenue sharing prohibited** (FCC 24-52); **FCC telecom graduated revenue sharing lawful**; **FCC cable graduated revenue sharing lawful**; **FCC single tenant not subject to MTE rules**; **FCC citation pins authorities** (В§ 64.2500-64.2503 + FCC 24-52 + July 2024 + FCC 22-12 + FCC 10-49); **FCC note pins exclusive access prohibition**; **FCC note pins exclusive revenue sharing prohibition**; **FCC note pins Open Internet Order broadband-only**; **FCC note pins bulk billing Carr withdrawal** (FCC 10-49 + Carr + January 2025 + REMAIN PERMITTED); **default no FCC rules apply**; **default citation pins state disclosure** (CA SB 1130 + NY HSTPA 2019); **two regimes routed correctly**; **arrangement truth table FCC MTE broadband** (6-arrangement sweep); **provider type distinguishes graduated revenue sharing** (3-provider sweep); **FCC uniquely engages MTE rules invariant**; **FCC MTE required for prohibition invariant**; **FCC categorical prohibition invariant for exclusive access and exclusive revenue** (2-arrangement Г— 3-provider sweep); **FCC flat licensing fee lawful for all provider types**; **FCC bulk billing lawful for all provider types**.

`traderview-expense::rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision` is the **rental in-unit laundry appliance provision plus dryer-vent fire-safety and mold-liability framework module** (iter 501) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord provide washer/dryer, what is the continuing maintenance duty once provided, what dryer-vent code requirements apply (IRC Section M1502 termination + UL 2158A transition duct + NFPA 211 annual lint cleaning), and what mold-liability exposure attaches to improper venting into attic/crawlspace? Trader-landlord critical because (1) once landlord PROVIDES W/D, continuing-duty maintenance attaches under common-law habitability вҖ” repair-overdue triggers tenant rent withholding + repair-and-deduct + constructive eviction; (2) approximately 2,900 US residential dryer fires occur annually per US Fire Administration causing $35M property damage вҖ” lint-buildup uncleaned beyond 12-month NFPA 211 interval is the most common cause; (3) most common safety code violation per InterNACHI surveys is dryer venting into attic/crawlspace вҖ” IRC M1502.2 PROHIBITS, and moisture deposition causes mold growth + ceiling damage + structural rot; (4) non-UL 2158A or longer-than-8-foot flex transition duct is fire hazard; (5) gas dryer without adequate IRC G2407.5 combustion-air ventilation causes CO accumulation. Distinct from siblings `tenant_in_unit_appliance_repair_responsibility` (broader landlord-appliance maintenance framework), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (NFPA 211 chimney analog), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (gas leak / CO cross-reference), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification mandate context), `rental_pellet_stove_disclosure` (iter 499 solid-fuel appliance), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (laundry-room water-leak source).

**Four appliance-provision statuses**: NoLaundryAppliances, TenantOwnedTenantMaintained (no landlord duty вҖ” appliance + landlord retains utility connection duty only), LandlordProvidedWithContinuingDuty (full maintenance duty), SharedCoinOpLaundryRoom.

**Four dryer-vent termination types**: ExteriorWallWithBackdraftDamper (code-compliant), AtticTerminationProhibited (IRC M1502.2 violation + mold), CrawlspaceTerminationProhibited (same), GarageOrUnconditionedSpace (fire + CO risk).

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 implied warranty includes mold per Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17920.3 substandard housing classification + В§ 1942 self-help repair after 30-day notice + AB 628 effective January 1, 2026 [refrigerator + stove only вҖ” no statewide W/D mandate]) vs Massachusetts (M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 quiet enjoyment + 105 CMR 410 State Sanitary Code Chapter II + 527 CMR 50.00 fire prevention + Mass.gov DFS Dryer Fire Safety guidance) vs New York (Real Property Law В§ 235-b + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2017.3 mold remediation under NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 + MDL В§ 78 maintenance of dwelling) vs Washington (RCW 59.18.060 + RCW 59.18.060(11) Disclosure of Mold and Indoor Air Quality at Tenant Move-In Act + DOH 'Got Mold?' statutorily-required attachment) vs Default (IRC Section M1502 + NFPA 211 + IFC 504 + UL 2158A Clothes Dryer Transition Duct + common-law habitability).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantWithMaintenanceCadence, LandlordProvidedRepairOverdueHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk), ImproperVentingAtticOrCrawlspaceMoldRisk (100% rent at risk), NonCompliantTransitionDuctFireRisk (100% rent at risk вҖ” non-metal plastic/foil OR >8 feet OR not UL 2158A-certified), LintBuildupAnnualMaintenanceOverdueFireRisk (50% rent at risk вҖ” 2,900 US dryer fires/year per USFA), GasDryerCoExposureWithoutAdequateVentilation (100% rent at risk вҖ” IRC G2407.5), DisclosureRequiredAtLeaseSigning (50% rent at risk).
