Thirty tests pin: **no laundry appliances not applicable** (AB 628 cross-ref); **tenant-owned no landlord duty** (utility-connection-only duty retained); **compliant with maintenance cadence**; **attic termination mold risk full rent at risk** (IRC Section M1502.2 violation); **crawlspace termination mold risk full rent at risk**; **plastic/foil non-metal duct fire risk** (430В°F + UL 2158A); **transition duct over 8 feet fire risk**; **transition duct at exactly 8 feet compliant** (boundary); **not UL 2158A-certified fire risk**; **gas dryer without ventilation CO risk** (IFC 504 + IRC G2407.5); **gas dryer with compliant ventilation no CO severity**; **landlord-provided overdue repair habitability breach** (100% rent at risk); **lint cleaning overdue half rent at risk** (2,900 dryer fires constant + IAQA Standard 3000); **disclosure missing at lease signing half rent at risk**; **CA jurisdiction pins В§ 1941.1 + AB 628 + В§ 17920.3**; **MA jurisdiction pins M.G.L. ch. 186 В§ 14 + 105 CMR 410 + 527 CMR 50.00**; **NY jurisdiction pins Real Property Law В§ 235-b + Local Law 55 of 2018 + MDL В§ 78**; **WA jurisdiction pins RCW 59.18.060 + 'Got Mold?' DOH publication**; **default jurisdiction pins IRC Section M1502 + NFPA 211 + IFC 504**; **coordination note references tenant_in_unit_appliance_repair_responsibility + rental_natural_gas_leak_response + rental_gas_appliance_ban + rental_pellet_stove_disclosure siblings**; **coordination note pinned in all 5 jurisdictions**; **UL 2158A max length constant pins 8 feet**; **NFPA 211 lint cleaning interval pins 12 months**; **US dryer fires constant pins 2,900 annually**; **severity priority attic overrides lint**; **severity priority metal check overrides length**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic** (boundary); **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (5-cell sweep); **shared coin-op treated as landlord-provided**.

`traderview-expense::rental_hot_water_temperature` is the **rental property minimum hot water temperature compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord supply hot water at a statutorily-specified minimum temperature? Trader-landlord critical for any rental owner: failure to supply hot water at required minimum temperature breaches implied warranty of habitability + triggers tenant remedies (rent withholding + repair-and-deduct + state-specific penalties + HPD/health-department complaints). Distinct from siblings `heat_requirements` (space heat temperature standards), `cooling_requirements`, `lead_in_drinking_water_disclosure`, and `water_heater_earthquake_strap` (CA В§ 19211 seismic anchoring).

**Three regimes** вҖ” California vs NYC vs Default:

| Regime | Authority | Minimum temp | Year-round required |
|--------|-----------|--------------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 114192, 17920.3 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 + 22 CCR В§ 81088 | **120В°F** at faucet | No statutory year-round mandate |
| NYC | NYC HMC В§ 27-2031 (Article 8 Heat and Hot Water) + HPD В§ 27-2115 | **120В°F** | **YES вҖ” 365 days per year** |
| Default | International Plumbing Code В§ 607.1.1 + state habitability | **110В°F** at outlet | No federal mandate |

**California 120В°F at faucet** вҖ” Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 114192 requires minimum 120В°F at faucet; Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 implied warranty of habitability covers hot water supply; 22 CCR В§ 81088 establishes 105-120В°F range for care facilities. Pinned by `ca_125f_compliant` (125В°F = compliant), `ca_at_120f_boundary_compliant` (exactly 120В°F compliant), `ca_119f_violates` (1В°F below threshold violates), `ca_no_hot_water_violates`, `ca_citation_pins_authorities` (В§В§ 114192, 17920.3 + В§ 1941.1 + 22 CCR В§ 81088), `ca_note_pins_120_at_faucet`, `ca_note_pins_warranty_of_habitability`, and `ca_note_pins_22_ccr_81088`.

**NYC 120В°F constant 365 days/year** вҖ” NYC HMC В§ 27-2031 (Article 8 Heat and Hot Water) requires constant minimum 120В°F supply year-round; failure triggers HPD violation + tenant remedies + civil penalties + В§ 27-2115 private right of action. Pinned by `nyc_125f_year_round_compliant`, `nyc_at_120f_boundary_compliant` (exactly 120В°F compliant), `nyc_119f_violates`, `nyc_seasonal_supply_violates` (year-round requirement), `nyc_no_hot_water_violates`, `nyc_3_violations_stack_at_seasonal_below_no_supply` (2 violations: no supply + not year-round), `nyc_citation_pins_authorities`, `nyc_note_pins_365_days`, `nyc_note_pins_hpd_enforcement`, and `nyc_uniquely_requires_year_round_invariant` (only NYC requires 365-day supply).

**Default IPC В§ 607.1.1 110В°F threshold** вҖ” International Plumbing Code В§ 607.1.1 requires minimum 110В°F at outlet; many states default to 120В°F via plumbing code adoption; state-specific habitability standards may impose higher minimums. Pinned by `default_110f_compliant`, `default_at_110f_boundary_compliant`, `default_109f_violates` (1В°F below IPC threshold violates), `default_119f_below_ca_nyc_threshold_but_compliant` (119В°F below CA/NYC 120В°F but above IPC 110В°F), `default_citation_pins_ipc` (IPC В§ 607.1.1 + state habitability), `ca_and_nyc_120f_default_110f_threshold_invariant` (CA 120В°F + NYC 120В°F + Default 110В°F).

**Habitability breach engages across all regimes when below minimum or no hot water** вҖ” pinned by `habitability_breach_engages_when_below_minimum` (3-regime sweep at 50В°F) and `no_hot_water_engages_habitability_breach_all_regimes`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-hot-water-temperature`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA 125В°F compliant**; **CA at 120В°F boundary compliant**; **CA 119В°F violates** (В§ 114192); **CA no hot water violates** (В§ 1941.1 warranty of habitability); **CA citation pins authorities**; **CA note pins 120В°F at faucet**; **CA note pins warranty of habitability**; **CA note pins 22 CCR В§ 81088** (105-120В°F range); **NYC 125В°F year-round compliant**; **NYC at 120В°F boundary compliant**; **NYC 119В°F violates** (В§ 27-2031); **NYC seasonal supply violates** (365-day requirement); **NYC no hot water violates** (Article 8); **NYC citation pins authorities** (В§ 27-2031 + Article 8 + HPD В§ 27-2115); **NYC note pins 365 days**; **NYC note pins HPD enforcement** (В§ 27-2115); **NYC 2 violations stack at seasonal + no supply**; **default 110В°F compliant**; **default at 110В°F boundary compliant**; **default 109В°F violates** (IPC В§ 607.1.1); **default 119В°F below CA/NYC threshold but compliant** (above IPC 110В°F); **default citation pins IPC**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **NYC uniquely requires year-round invariant**; **CA + NYC 120В°F + Default 110В°F threshold invariant**; **habitability breach engages when below minimum** (3-regime sweep); **no hot water engages habitability breach all regimes** (3-regime invariant).

`traderview-expense::rental_garage_door_safety_compliance` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property AUTOMATIC GARAGE DOOR OPENER safety compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property with an automatic garage door opener, what entrapment-protection standards apply (UL 325 photoelectric eye + auto-reverse + sensing edge), what pre-1993 legacy opener replacement obligations attach, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a child-entrapment injury or fatality? Distinct from siblings `rental_window_blind_cord_safety` (iter 469 вҖ” child strangulation product safety), `rental_window_guard_installation` (fall protection), `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` (VGB Act), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (iter 471), `rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement` (iter 473), `rental_hardwired_smoke_alarm_responsibility` (iter 481), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) pre-1993 garage door openers are among the highest-stakes legacy safety-equipment compliance issues вҖ” **child-entrapment fatality settlements routinely exceed $5M**; (2) UL 325 photoelectric eye disable/obstruction is leading cause of post-1993 entrapment events; (3) annual 2x4 test at start of tenancy is single most important verification task; (4) California SB 969 battery-backup mandate (effective July 1, 2019) for new installations creates retrofit obligation; (5) CPSC recall enforcement under 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 applies if landlord continues to lease unit with recalled opener.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **Federal/CPSC (universal floor)** | Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 1990, Pub. L. 101-608 (15 U.S.C. В§ 2056); 16 C.F.R. Part 1211; ANSI/UL 325 | Mandatory standard since **JANUARY 1, 1993** вҖ” external entrapment protection via photoelectric eyes or sensing edge + internal motor monitoring + auto-reverse |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 implied warranty per Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 7026 (C-61/D-28 contractor licensing); Cal. SB 969 (2018, effective July 1, 2019) | Battery-backup requirement for new installations + licensed installer + implied warranty |
| **Default** | Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068; 16 C.F.R. В§ 1115 | Common-law habitability + tort negligence + premises liability + CPSC recall enforcement |

**UL 325 (post-1993) five safety requirements**:
1. **EXTERNAL ENTRAPMENT PROTECTION** via PHOTOELECTRIC INFRARED SENSORS (mounted 4-6 inches above floor) OR SENSING-EDGE contact strips
2. **INTERNAL ENTRAPMENT MONITORING** via motor strain detection вҖ” door reverses if obstruction detected during travel
3. **AUTO-REVERSE** on contact with ground obstruction within 2 seconds + 3-second timeout
4. **5.5-SECOND CLOSE TIME** enforced вҖ” minimum door-motion time before close completes
5. **ANNUAL 2x4 LUMBER TEST**: place 2x4 piece of lumber flat on ground in door path; door should contact + auto-reverse without crushing

**Three opener generations**:
1. **PRE-1993** вҖ” NOT equipped with external entrapment protection; CPSC + manufacturers strongly recommend **REPLACEMENT** (not repair); insurance carriers commonly **DENY COVERAGE** for pre-1993 opener incidents
2. **1993-2010** вҖ” first-generation UL 325 compliant; photoelectric eye + auto-reverse; aging components may have intermittent failures
3. **POST-2010** вҖ” current UL 325 edition with enhanced safety features including motor cutoff after excessive cycles + battery backup (**CA SB 969 effective July 1, 2019** requires battery backup for new installations in California for emergency egress)

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Pre-1993 opener still in service** вҶ’ child-entrapment liability + insurance denial
2. **Photoelectric eye obstructed or disabled** вҶ’ entrapment protection failure
3. **Auto-reverse failure on 2x4 test** вҶ’ physical impact liability + product defect
4. **Pinch-point unprotected** on spring-loaded sections вҶ’ finger amputation liability (heightened with young-children tenants)
5. **CA SB 969 battery backup missing** on post-July-1-2019 installation in California вҶ’ fire-safety code violation + emergency-egress habitability claim

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) Inherited rural property with 1985-installed garage door opener вҖ” pre-1993 legacy + replacement required ($300-$800 cost vs $5M+ child-entrapment exposure); (2) tenant complained photoelectric eye disabled at start of tenancy вҶ’ landlord verify operability via 2x4 test annually; (3) 2x4 test failure during routine maintenance вҶ’ engage licensed C-61/D-28 California garage door installer; (4) California 2020-built rental without battery backup вҶ’ SB 969 violation + emergency-egress habitability claim; (5) CPSC recall outstanding for installed opener model вҶ’ 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 prohibited acts; (6) child entrapment event reported вҶ’ $5M+ wrongful death + IIED exposure + tort negligence + premises liability.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-garage-door-safety-compliance`. Thirty-two tests pin: **no opener not applicable**; **CA compliant baseline**; **child entrapment event top severity** ($5M+ + tenant_emotional_distress_damages); **CPSC recall outstanding enforcement** (15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 + 16 C.F.R. В§ 1115); **pre-1993 opener legacy severity** (REPLACEMENT not repair + insurance denial); **photoelectric and sensing edge both failed** (UL 325 external entrapment failure); **photoelectric failed sensing edge operable compliant** (one device sufficient); **auto-reverse 2x4 test failure** (C-61/D-28 licensed installer); **CA SB 969 battery backup missing violation** (July 1, 2019 + emergency egress); **CA SB 969 battery backup present compliant**; **CA SB 969 pre-July-2019 no battery backup required**; **annual test overdue 12 months**; **annual test exactly 12 months compliant**; **first gen 1993-2010 compliant**; **CA jurisdiction cites В§ 1941.1 + В§ 7026 + C-61/D-28 + SB 969**; **federal jurisdiction cites Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act 1990 + 16 C.F.R. Part 1211 + ANSI/UL 325 + January 1, 1993**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + 478 A.2d 202); **severity priority entrapment > recall > pre-1993**; **severity recall > pre-1993 > entrapment failure**; **severity pre-1993 > entrapment failure > auto-reverse**; **severity auto-reverse > battery backup**; **citation pins all authorities** (Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act 1990 + 16 C.F.R. Part 1211 + ANSI/UL 325 + В§ 1941.1 + В§ 7026 + SB 969 + July 1, 2019 + Hilder + Green v. Superior Court + 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins UL 325 five safety requirements**; **note pins three opener generations**; **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins $5M settlement data + $300-$800 replacement cost**; **note pins companion modules**; **3-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **CA uniquely SB 969 battery backup invariant**; **pre-1993 with no external protection legacy wins** (severity hierarchy); **multiple failures stack in actions**.

`traderview-expense::rental_grill_propane_bbq_restriction` is the **rental balcony grill plus propane / LP-gas BBQ restriction framework module** (iter 515) вҖ” covers NFPA 1 Fire Code В§ 10.10.5 (no open-flame grills on combustible multifamily balconies within 10 feet of structure) plus В§ 6.18 LP-gas cylinder storage restrictions (no propane above first floor or inside residential units) plus state-specific fire code amendments plus tenant lease enforcement plus insurance coverage exclusions. Distinct from siblings `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (indoor solid-fuel framework), `rental_natural_gas_leak_response` (gas line leak protocol вҖ” distinct from LP-gas cylinder), `rental_propane_tank_lease_disclosure` (large stationary propane tank disclosure framework), `rental_pellet_stove_disclosure` (iter 499 wood-pellet appliance), `rental_balcony_inspection_seismic_safety` (iter 511 EEE structural вҖ” balcony BBQ damage may affect structural integrity), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for neighbor exposure to grill smoke or fire risk). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **NFPA 1 В§ 10.10.5** prohibits hibachi, gas-fired grill, charcoal grill, or other open-flame cooking device from being used on any multifamily balcony or overhanging portion or within 10 feet of any structure; (2) **NFPA 1 В§ 6.18** prohibits LP-gas cylinders > 1-pound disposable size (2.5-pound water capacity max) from being stored above first floor or inside residential units вҖ” 20-pound BBQ tanks categorically prohibited in multifamily balcony storage due to explosion risk in elevated/enclosed areas; (3) **carve-out**: listed equipment permanently installed per its listing (gas grill hardwired into building gas supply by licensed plumber per IFGC В§ 401) IS permitted; (4) approximately **17 US BBQ fire deaths plus 8,800 injuries annually** per US Fire Administration data; (5) standard ISO HO-3 dwelling policy fire-cause exclusions broadly exclude balcony-grill fire losses; (6) FDNY + LA Fire Department + Boston Fire Department per-violation civil penalties $250-$1,000 plus lease eviction grounds.

**Three building types**: Multifamily3PlusUnits (NFPA 1 В§ 10.10.5 applies), SingleFamilyOrDuplex (NFPA 1 inapplicable вҖ” HOA + lease govern), DetachedCottage.

**Seven grill types**: PropaneTwentyPoundCylinder (standard BBQ tank), PropaneOnePoundDisposable (camping size вҖ” permitted), Charcoal, Hibachi, Electric (no open flame вҖ” permitted), NaturalGasHardwired (NFPA 1 В§ 10.10.5 carve-out вҖ” permitted), NoGrill.

**Six grill locations**: BalconyAboveFirstFloor (prohibited), GroundFloorPatioWithin10Feet (prohibited for multifamily), GroundFloorPatioAtLeast10Feet (compliant), DesignatedCommonAreaGrillStation (compliant вҖ” landlord-maintained), Indoor (categorically prohibited вҖ” CO death risk), NotApplicable.

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Fire Code В§ 308.1.4 mirrors NFPA 1 В§ 10.10.5 + CFC В§ 308.1.6.2 LP-gas BBQ multifamily balcony + Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 18900 + CAL FIRE advisories during red flag warnings) vs New York City (FDNY 3 RCNY 102-01 + NYC Admin Code Title 29 + NYC FC 307.5 LP-gas > 1-pound cylinders prohibited on multifamily balconies + FDNY Bureau of Fire Investigation summons + $250-$1,000 per violation) vs Massachusetts (527 CMR 1.00 Comprehensive Fire Safety Code adopted IFC + NFPA 1 + M.G.L. ch. 148 В§ 26G LP-gas storage + State Fire Marshal Office) vs Texas (Texas Local Gov't Code Ch. 235 municipal fire code authority + TX State Fire Marshal Office + Houston/Dallas/Austin/SA adopt IFC + NFPA 1) vs Default (NFPA 1 2018 + 2021 editions standalone В§ 10.10.5 + В§ 6.18).

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantElectricOrPermanentInstall, CompliantBeyond10FeetGroundFloor, CompliantDesignatedCommonAreaStation, NfpaOpenFlameOnBalconyViolation (100% rent at risk вҖ” adds NFPA 1 В§ 6.18 secondary violation when 20-pound LP-gas cylinder also present), NfpaPropaneAbove1PoundStoredAboveFirstFloorViolation (100% rent at risk), NfpaWithin10FeetOfStructureViolation (50% rent at risk), IndoorGrillCategoricallyProhibited (100% rent at risk вҖ” CO death risk + В§ 10.10.4 + IFC В§ 308.1), LeaseEnforcementRequiredTenantViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” 3-day Notice to Cure).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-grill-propane-bbq-restriction`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **no grill not applicable**; **indoor grill categorically prohibited** (В§ 10.10.4 + CO death risk); **electric grill compliant** (no open flame); **natural gas hardwired compliant** (IFGC В§ 401 carve-out); **designated common area station compliant** (Class K + NFPA 10); **balcony open flame violation full rent at risk** (В§ 10.10.5 + 17 US BBQ fire deaths); **balcony open flame with propane double violation** (В§ 6.18 + 20-pound BBQ tank); **charcoal balcony violation no propane secondary** (single violation only when no LP-gas); **hibachi balcony violation**; **within 10 feet ground floor violation half rent at risk**; **beyond 10 feet ground floor compliant** (boundary); **1-pound disposable propane balcony compliant** (NFPA 1 В§ 6.18 disposable carve-out); **lease prohibition tenant violation half rent at risk** (3-day Notice to Cure); **CA jurisdiction pins Cal. Fire Code В§ 308.1.4 + CAL FIRE**; **NYC jurisdiction pins FDNY 3 RCNY 102-01 + NYC FC 307.5 + $250-$1,000 penalty range**; **MA jurisdiction pins 527 CMR 1.00 + M.G.L. ch. 148 В§ 26G**; **TX jurisdiction pins Texas Local Gov't Code Ch. 235 + State Fire Marshal Office**; **default jurisdiction pins NFPA 1 (2018 + 2021 editions) + В§ 10.10.5 + В§ 6.18**; **coordination note references rental_propane_tank_lease_disclosure + rental_natural_gas_leak_response + rental_pellet_stove_disclosure + rental_balcony_inspection_seismic_safety siblings**; **coordination note pinned in all 5 jurisdictions**; **NFPA 1 distance pins 10 feet**; **NFPA 1 LP-gas max disposable pins 2-pounds water capacity**; **FDNY NYC penalty pins $250 to $1,000**; **US BBQ fire deaths pins 17 per year**; **US BBQ fire injuries pins 8,800 per year**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (5-cell sweep); **severity priority indoor overrides grill type** (indoor electric still prohibited per CO risk); **zero rent zero risk does not panic**; **single-family within 10 feet no NFPA violation** (multifamily-only application).

`traderview-expense::rental_gas_appliance_ban` is the **rental property gas appliance ban / electrification mandate compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord building NEW CONSTRUCTION OR substantially renovating an existing rental property comply with statutory bans on natural gas / propane hookups and fossil-fuel-burning appliances? Trader-landlord critical for any new-construction or substantial-renovation project in NY (post-January 1, 2026 pending Second Circuit ruling) and CA (2025 Energy Code effective January 1, 2026). DOES NOT require existing landlords to replace existing gas appliances. Distinct from siblings `cooling_requirements` (cooling habitability standards), `heat_requirements` (heat habitability standards), `detector_requirements` (CO + smoke detector requirements), and `landlord_repair_response_timeframe` (repair obligations).

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | New construction trigger | Enforcement status |
|--------|-----------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| New York | All-Electric Buildings Act, S4006C / A3006C Part RR (2023) | Bans fossil-fuel hookups in MOST new homes | **STAYED pending 2nd Cir. ruling** (fall 2026 expected) |
| California | Title 24 Part 6 (2025 Energy Code) | Heat pumps required for most space + water heating | Active (no stay) |
| Default | Locality-controlled | None statewide; many locality variations | EPCA preemption limits direct bans |

**New York All-Electric Buildings Act (effective January 1, 2026)** вҖ” bans fossil-fuel hookups in MOST new homes. Covers natural gas mains + propane tanks + boilers + furnaces + tank/tankless water heaters + gas ranges + gas dryers + gas fireplaces + supply/distribution/delivery piping. State agreed to NOT enforce zero-emissions standard until Second Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals resolves challenge (expected fall 2026). Does NOT apply to existing buildings or existing appliances. Pinned by `ny_new_construction_gas_appliance_violates`, `ny_new_construction_all_electric_compliant`, `ny_existing_building_appliance_replacement_compliant`, `ny_substantial_renovation_does_not_engage_ban` (only NEW CONSTRUCTION engages, not renovation), `ny_maintenance_compliant`, `ny_gas_appliance_violation_message`, `ny_fossil_fuel_piping_violation_message`, `ny_enforcement_stayed_flag`, `ny_citation_pins_act` (S4006C + A3006C + Part RR), `ny_note_pins_january_1_2026`, `ny_note_pins_appliance_list` (boilers + furnaces + water heaters + ranges + dryers + fireplaces), `ny_note_pins_second_circuit_stay`, `ny_new_construction_2_violations_stack` (gas appliance + fossil fuel piping = 2 simultaneous violations), `ny_uniquely_engages_ban_invariant` (only NY engages ban), and `ny_uniquely_enforcement_stayed_invariant`.

**California 2025 Energy Code (effective January 1, 2026; Title 24, Part 6)** вҖ” new construction permits require heat pumps for most space and water heating. Does NOT require existing landlords to replace existing gas appliances. San Francisco considering ordinance for major-renovation electrification by 2027 (not yet enacted statewide). Pinned by `ca_new_construction_heat_pump_compliant`, `ca_new_construction_gas_without_heat_pump_violates`, `ca_existing_landlord_no_retroactive_replacement` (replacement on existing building does not engage heat-pump requirement), `ca_citation_pins_title_24`, `ca_uniquely_requires_heat_pump_invariant`, and `ca_note_pins_2027_sf_ordinance`.

**Cal. Restaurant Ass'n v. City of Berkeley, 89 F.4th 1094 (9th Cir. 2023)** вҖ” Berkeley's 2019 gas-hookup ban was ENJOINED as federally preempted by Energy Policy and Conservation Act (42 USC В§ 6297). CA cities post-Berkeley pursue indirect bans via building/health code. Pinned by `ca_note_pins_berkeley_injunction` and `default_note_pins_epca_preemption`.

**Default fallback** вҖ” federal law silent on gas appliance bans; locality-controlled with substantial status variation; EPCA В§ 6297 limits direct-ban approach. Pinned by `default_no_ban_compliant`, `default_citation_pins_epca_preemption`, and `default_note_pins_epca_preemption`.

**4-cell project-type truth tables** вҖ” only NewConstruction triggers ban/heat-pump engagement in NY/CA; SubstantialRenovation + AppliancePeplacement + Maintenance do not. Pinned by `project_type_truth_table_ny` and `project_type_truth_table_ca_heat_pump_requirement`. Existing appliance replacement is compliant across ALL regimes. Pinned by `existing_appliance_replacement_compliant_in_all_regimes`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-gas-appliance-ban`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **NY new construction gas appliance violates** (All-Electric Buildings Act); **NY new construction all-electric compliant**; **NY existing building appliance replacement compliant**; **NY substantial renovation does not engage ban**; **NY maintenance compliant**; **NY gas appliance violation message**; **NY fossil fuel piping violation message**; **NY enforcement stayed flag**; **NY citation pins Act** (S4006C + A3006C + Part RR); **NY note pins January 1 2026**; **NY note pins appliance list**; **NY note pins Second Circuit stay**; **CA new construction heat pump compliant**; **CA new construction gas without heat pump violates** (Title 24 Part 6); **CA existing landlord no retroactive replacement**; **CA citation pins Title 24** (2025 Energy Code + 89 F.4th 1094 + В§ 6297); **CA note pins Berkeley injunction** (EPCA preemption); **CA note pins 2027 SF ordinance**; **default no ban compliant**; **default citation pins EPCA preemption**; **default note pins EPCA preemption**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **NY uniquely engages ban invariant**; **CA uniquely requires heat pump invariant**; **NY uniquely enforcement stayed invariant**; **project type truth table NY** (4-cell sweep); **project type truth table CA heat pump requirement** (4-cell sweep); **NY new construction 2 violations stack**; **existing appliance replacement compliant in all regimes** (3-regime invariant).

`traderview-expense::rental_junk_fee_transparency` is the **rental junk fee + non-rent fee transparency compliance module** вҖ” when a residential landlord advertises, leases, or renews a rental, what statutory disclosure obligations attach to fees, charges, or other amounts beyond base rent? Distinct from siblings `application_fees` (application-stage caps), `late_fee_caps` (post-default late charges), `pet_fees` (pet deposits), and `broker_fee_allocation` (broker fee party-allocation). Trader-landlord operational concern in an emerging transparency-regulation wave: MA 940 CMR 38.00 (eff. 2025-09-02), CO HB25-1090 (eff. 2026-01-01), pending federal FTC 16 CFR Part 464.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Total-price-single-number | Utility markup ban | CAM ban | Markup fee cap | Treble damages |
|--------|----------------------------|---------------------|----------|----------------|------------------|
| Massachusetts 940 CMR 38.00 | YES + clear/conspicuous | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | YES (M.G.L. c. 93A) |
| Colorado В§ 38-12-1101 (HB25-1090) | YES (single number) | YES | YES | 2% / $10/mo | вҖ” |
| California В§ 1950.5 (AB 12) | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” (security cap 1 mo rent) | вҖ” |
| Default 16 CFR Part 464 + FTC Act В§ 5 | (short-term rentals + hotels primarily) | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**Massachusetts вҖ” 940 CMR 38.00 (effective September 2, 2025)**. Issued March 3, 2025 under M.G.L. c. 93A Consumer Protection Act. Landlords must CLEARLY AND CONSPICUOUSLY disclose the TOTAL PRICE inclusive of ALL fees, charges, or other expenses when the rental is initially advertised. Applies broadly to advertising, leasing, AND renewals. **TREBLE damages + attorney's fees + private right of action** under Chapter 93A. Pinned by `ma_missing_total_price_violates`, `ma_buried_disclosure_violates`, `ma_chapter_93a_treble_damages_note_present`, `ma_effective_date_2025_09_02_note_present`, `ma_advertising_leasing_renewals_scope_note`.

**Colorado вҖ” Colo. Rev. Stat. В§ 38-12-1101 et seq. (HB25-1090, effective January 1, 2026)**. Honest Pricing / Junk Fee Law. Six prohibitions: (1) total price must be SINGLE NUMBER without separation into individual fees; (2) **utility markup ABOVE provider's actual charges prohibited**; (3) **Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges prohibited** for residential rentals; (4) markup fees may NOT exceed **2% OR $10 per month**, whichever is greater; (5) charges for services NOT actually provided prohibited; (6) costs associated with landlord's responsibilities (property tax, maintenance, insurance) may NOT be passed through as separate tenant fees. Pinned by `co_utility_markup_violates`, `co_cam_charge_violates`, `co_markup_above_2_percent_or_10_violates`, `co_undelivered_service_charge_violates`, `co_landlord_responsibility_passthrough_violates`, `co_separating_fees_in_advertisement_violates`, `co_all_six_violations_simultaneous`.

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 (AB 12, effective July 1, 2024)**. Non-rent security capped at ONE month's rent in TPA-covered units. Pending broader junk fee transparency legislation as of 2026-06.

**Default вҖ” 16 CFR Part 464 + 15 U.S.C. В§ 45 (FTC Act В§ 5)**. Federal FTC Unfair or Deceptive Fees Rule (finalized 2024) primarily covers short-term rentals + hotels; residential long-term rental coverage incomplete. State UDAP statutes may apply for materially misleading advertising.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-junk-fee-transparency`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **MA clean disclosure compliant**; **MA missing total price violates** (940 CMR 38.00); **MA buried disclosure violates** (CLEARLY AND CONSPICUOUSLY); **MA Chapter 93A treble damages note**; **MA effective date 2025-09-02 note**; **MA advertising/leasing/renewals scope note**; **MA citation pins 940 CMR 38.00 + Chapter 93A**; **CO clean disclosure compliant**; **CO utility markup violates**; **CO CAM charge violates**; **CO markup above 2% / $10 violates**; **CO undelivered service charge violates**; **CO landlord responsibility passthrough violates**; **CO separating fees in advertisement violates**; **CO effective date 2026-01-01 note**; **CO all six violations simultaneous**; **CO citation pins В§ 38-12-1101 + HB25-1090**; **CA AB 12 security deposit cap note** (ONE month's rent in TPA-covered units); **CA no statewide mandate note when missing disclosure**; **CA compliant even without full disclosure**; **default FTC Part 464 note** (short-term rentals); **default FTC Act В§ 5 note** (15 U.S.C. В§ 45 UDAP); **default UDAP note when no disclosure**; **default no violations even with aggressive fees**; **CO unique CAM prohibition invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **CO unique utility markup prohibition invariant** (3-other-regime sweep); **four regimes routed correctly** (MA + CO + CA + Default sweep); **MA compliant clean path no violations**; **CO compliant clean path no violations**.

`traderview-expense::rental_post_construction_lead_dust_clearance` is the **rental post-construction lead-dust clearance testing framework module** (iter 503) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord conduct dust-wipe clearance testing under EPA RRP rule 40 C.F.R. Part 745 Subpart E, HUD Lead Safe Housing Rule 24 C.F.R. Part 35, or state-level mandates before tenant re-occupancy after renovation work on pre-1978 target housing? Distinct from sibling `rental_lead_pipe_disclosure` (lead-in-drinking-water вҖ” different exposure pathway under EPA Lead and Copper Rule), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water/mold), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when tenant must vacate during clearance work). Trader-landlord critical because (1) approximately **24 million pre-1978 US homes** contain lead-based paint hazards per CDC; (2) EPA civil penalty schedule under 40 C.F.R. В§ 19.4 imposes **$51,796 per violation per day** for RRP non-compliance (2024 inflation-adjusted rate); (3) MA 105 CMR 460 private right of action allows tenant or parent to recover **triple damages plus attorney's fees** for CLPPP violations; (4) blood lead level greater than 3.5 Ојg/dL is CDC reference value triggering case management вҖ” lead poisoning litigation routinely exceeds $1M for permanent neurodevelopmental injury; (5) **effective January 12, 2026 EPA final rule** (October 24, 2024) ANY reportable level of lead in dust constitutes a dust-lead hazard (zero-tolerance regime).

**Three housing scopes**: Pre1978TargetHousing (RRP + HUD LSHR applies), Pre1960Housing (NYC Local Law 1 of 2004 specific trigger), Post1978NotApplicable (federal residential lead paint ban effective January 1, 1978 per 16 C.F.R. В§ 1303).

**Two federal-assistance statuses**: HudAssistedLeadSafeHousingRuleApplies (24 C.F.R. Part 35 mandatory dust-wipe clearance per В§ 35.1340), PrivateMarketRrpOnly (RRP cleaning verification mandatory but dust-wipe clearance only by state law or contract).

**TSCA В§ 402(c) dust-lead clearance levels (post-Federal-Register June 21, 2019 final rule effective January 6, 2020)**:
- Floor: 10 Ојg/ftВІ (down from pre-2020 40 Ојg/ftВІ)
- Window sill: 100 Ојg/ftВІ (down from pre-2020 250 Ојg/ftВІ)
- Window trough: 400 Ојg/ftВІ (unchanged)
- **Effective January 12, 2026**: ANY reportable lead level = dust-lead hazard (zero-tolerance under October 24, 2024 EPA final rule).

**Four-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” Massachusetts (105 CMR 460 Lead Poisoning Prevention and Control вҖ” MOST STRINGENT in US, applies to all rental property regardless of federal RRP scope + CLPPP Letter of Interim Control + Letter of Compliance + M.G.L. ch. 111 В§В§ 190-199A + triple-damages private right of action) vs California (Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17920.10 + Cal. Code Regs. Title 17 Div. 1 Ch. 8 CLPPB) vs New York (NYC Local Law 1 of 2004 + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2056 + DOH Lead Regulation В§ 11-101 for pre-1960 housing with children under 6 + Local Law 31 of 2020 requires XRF testing of all rental units within 5 years + NY PHL В§ 1373) vs Default (federal RRP rule 40 C.F.R. Part 745 Subpart E + HUD LSHR 24 C.F.R. Part 35 + TSCA В§В§ 402+403 + January 12, 2026 zero-tolerance shift).

**Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantClearancePassedAllLocations, UncertifiedFirmRrpViolation (100% rent at risk вҖ” $51,796/day EPA penalty), NoCleaningVerificationViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” 40 C.F.R. В§ 745.85(a)(2)(v)), NoTenantNotificationPreWorkViolation (50% rent at risk вҖ” В§ 745.84(a)(1) Renovate Right pamphlet missing), DustWipeClearanceRequiredButNotPerformed (50% rent at risk вҖ” HUD/MA/NY mandatory), FloorDustExceedsClearanceStandard (100% rent at risk вҖ” В§ 745.227(e)(8)(viii)), WindowSillDustExceedsClearanceStandard (100% rent at risk), WindowTroughDustExceedsClearanceStandard (100% rent at risk), TenantReoccupancyBeforeClearancePass (100% rent at risk вҖ” 24 C.F.R. В§ 35.1340 violation), ChildPregnancyExposureMaximumLiability (100% rent at risk вҖ” CDC 3.5 Ојg/dL blood lead reference value).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-post-construction-lead-dust-clearance`. Thirty-five tests pin: **post-1978 housing not applicable** (16 C.F.R. В§ 1303 lead paint ban); **below RRP threshold not applicable** (40 C.F.R. В§ 745.82(a)(2) minor repair exemption); **RRP trigger pins 6 interior sq ft**; **RRP trigger pins 20 exterior sq ft**; **post-2020 floor clearance pins 10 Ојg**; **post-2020 sill clearance pins 100 Ојg**; **pre-2020 floor clearance pins 40 Ојg** (boundary); **pre-2020 sill clearance pins 250 Ојg**; **trough clearance pins 400 Ојg**; **EPA civil penalty pins $51,796 per day**; **compliant clearance passed all locations** (3-year recordkeeping per В§ 745.86(a)); **uncertified firm RRP violation full rent at risk** (В§ 745.81(a)); **uncertified renovator on-site violation** (В§ 745.85(a)(1)); **no Renovate Right pamphlet violation half rent at risk** (EPA-740-K-10-001); **no cleaning verification violation half rent at risk** (В§ 745.85(a)(2)(v)); **HUD-assisted requires clearance violation half rent at risk**; **floor dust exceeds post-2020 10 Ојg violation**; **floor dust at 10 Ојg compliant** (boundary); **floor dust pre-2020 40 Ојg compliant** (boundary); **floor dust pre-2020 at 41 Ојg violation**; **window sill exceeds post-2020 100 Ојg violation**; **window trough exceeds 400 Ојg violation**; **tenant reoccupied before clearance full rent at risk** (24 C.F.R. В§ 35.1340); **child pregnancy exposure maximum liability full rent at risk** (CDC 3.5 Ојg/dL + mid-tenancy temporary relocation cross-ref); **MA jurisdiction pins 105 CMR 460 + M.G.L. ch. 111 В§В§ 190-199A + CLPPP + triple damages**; **CA jurisdiction pins В§ 17920.10 + Title 17 Div. 1 Ch. 8 CLPPB**; **NY jurisdiction pins NYC Local Law 1 of 2004 + LL 31 of 2020 + XRF testing**; **default jurisdiction pins January 12, 2026 + zero-tolerance + October 24, 2024**; **coordination note references rental_lead_pipe_disclosure + mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation + tenant_emotional_distress_damages**; **coordination note pinned in all 4 jurisdictions**; **severity priority child pregnancy overrides clearance fail**; **severity priority uncertified firm overrides pamphlet**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (4-cell sweep); **zero rent zero risk does not panic**; **EPA civil penalty pinned in action**.

`traderview-expense::rental_marijuana_cultivation_restriction` is the **rental marijuana / cannabis cultivation restriction framework module** (iter 521) вҖ” covers tenant home-grow rights under state recreational and medical cannabis laws, landlord prohibition rights via lease, federal Controlled Substances Act preemption considerations, Fair Housing Act medical-cannabis reasonable-accommodation doctrine, and mold + moisture damage liability from indoor cultivation operations. Distinct from siblings `tenant_cannabis_use_protection` (smoking and consumption protection framework вҖ” distinct from cultivation), `rental_smoke_free_housing_disclosure` (smoke-free designation framework вҖ” may incorporate cannabis prohibition), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (mold-infiltration framework вҖ” coordinates with grow-room 60-80% humidity), `rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision` (iter 501 high-humidity-appliance ventilation analog), `rental_short_term_subletting_airbnb_restriction` (iter 513 вҖ” NYC LL18 disqualifies cultivation premises from STR), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when tenant must vacate during mold remediation), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for wrongful eviction in legal-cultivation state). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **as of 2025, at least 16 states + DC allow adult home cultivation for recreational use** (CA + CO + MA + NY + MI + ME + VT + DC + AZ + MT + NV + OR + RI + VA + NM + MO + MN); state plant counts range from 2 (MD) to 12 (MI); (2) **federal Controlled Substances Act 21 U.S.C. В§ 812 classifies marijuana as Schedule I**; DEA scheduling proposal to move marijuana to Schedule III pending as of June 2025; CSA conflict creates limited federal preemption argument for landlords; (3) **HUD Notice PIH 2011-25 prohibits federally-assisted tenants from any cannabis use** including state-legal medical (potential 24 C.F.R. В§ 5.852 lease termination); (4) **FHA 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) DOES NOT require landlord accommodation** of state-legal medical cannabis per HUD General Counsel memorandum вҖ” federal CSA Schedule I preempts state medical-cannabis-as-disability framing; (5) cultivation damages include mold from 60-80% humidity grow rooms + electrical-fire risk from high-wattage grow lights + property odor migration + insurance policy exclusion for cannabis-related losses.

**Eight-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 11362.1(a)(3) + В§ 11362.45(h) Prop 64 вҖ” 6 plants per residence + landlord lease prohibition expressly permitted) vs Colorado (Colo. Const. Art. XVIII В§ 16(3)(b)(I)-(II) вҖ” 6 plants total / 3 mature + tenant cultivation permitted unless lease prohibits in writing per В§ 16(3)(b)(II)) vs New York (NY Cannabis Law В§ 222 MRTA вҖ” 3 mature + 3 immature post-18-month delay + NY Office of Cannabis Management OCM + NYC LL18 disqualifies cultivation from STR) vs Massachusetts (M.G.L. ch. 94G В§ 7 вҖ” 6 plants per person + 12 per residence + MA Cannabis Control Commission) vs Illinois (410 ILCS 705 recreational possession but PROHIBITS home cultivation + 410 ILCS 130 medical 5 plants) vs NoHomeCultivationState (WA recreational + NJ вҖ” cultivation illegal regardless of lease) vs CannabisIllegalState (full state-statute-violation termination ground) vs Default (21 U.S.C. В§ 812 CSA Schedule I + HUD Notice PIH 2011-25 + 16 states permitting home grow as of 2025 + DEA Schedule III rescheduling pending June 2025).

**Two housing programs**: HudAssistedFederalCsaEnforcement (HUD PIH 2011-25 + 24 C.F.R. В§ 5.852 termination + HUD General Counsel FHA non-accommodation), PrivateMarketStateLawPrimary.

**Five cultivation statuses**: NoCultivation, RecreationalWithinStateLimit, ExceedingStatePlantCountLimit, MedicalCannabisQualifyingPatient, BlackMarketCommercialCultivation.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantStateLegalCultivationWithLeaseConsent, LandlordProhibitionEnforceable (50% rent at risk), HudFederalCsaTerminationGround (100% rent at risk + HUD Notice PIH 2011-25 + 24 C.F.R. В§ 5.852), FhaMedicalCannabisAccommodationDenied (50% rent at risk вҖ” HUD General Counsel preemption), ExceedsStatePlantCountViolation (50% rent at risk + 3-day Notice to Cure), BlackMarketCommercialEvictionGround (100% rent at risk + federal RICO civil-forfeiture exposure), MoldOrElectricalFireDamageHabitabilityBreach (100% rent at risk + 60-80% humidity grow rooms + insurance exclusion).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-marijuana-cultivation-restriction`. Twenty-six tests pin: **no cultivation not applicable**; **compliant state-legal with lease consent** (4 plants within 6-plant CA limit); **lease prohibition enforceable** (3-day Notice to Cure); **HUD-assisted federal CSA termination** (HUD Notice PIH 2011-25 + 24 C.F.R. В§ 5.852); **exceeds state plant count violation** (12 vs 6); **plant count actual over limit triggers violation**; **black market commercial eviction ground** (federal RICO); **mold or fire damage habitability breach** (60-80% relative humidity); **FHA medical cannabis accommodation denied in HUD**; **CA jurisdiction pins Prop 64 + В§ 11362.1(a)(3) + В§ 11362.45(h)**; **CO jurisdiction pins Constitutional Art. XVIII В§ 16 + Marijuana Enforcement Division**; **NY jurisdiction pins MRTA + Cannabis Law В§ 222 + OCM + LL18**; **MA jurisdiction pins ch. 94G В§ 7 + Cannabis Control Commission**; **IL jurisdiction pins 410 ILCS 705 + 410 ILCS 130 + PROHIBITS home cultivation**; **default jurisdiction pins CSA Schedule I + HUD PIH 2011-25 + 16 states home grow**; **coordination note references tenant_cannabis_use_protection + rental_smoke_free_housing_disclosure + rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure + rental_short_term_subletting_airbnb_restriction siblings**; **CA Prop 64 plant limit pins 6**; **CO plant limit pins 6 total + 3 mature**; **NY MRTA total limit pins 6**; **MA plant limit per residence pins 12**; **CSA schedule pins 1**; **states permitting home grow 2025 pins 16**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (6-cell sweep + state-no-cultivation + cannabis-illegal); **severity priority mold overrides lease prohibition**; **severity priority HUD overrides lease silence**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic**.

`traderview-expense::rental_lead_pipe_disclosure` is the **rental property lead service line / lead pipe disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating a property served by a water service line containing lead notify tenants of lead service line presence and replacement plans under EPA Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR, eff. October 16, 2024) and Lead and Copper Rule Improvements (LCRI, eff. November 1, 2027)? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed lead service line creates breach of warranty of habitability + per-tenant statutory damages + tort liability for lead poisoning + post-Flint heightened regulatory scrutiny. Distinct from siblings `rental_underground_storage_tank_disclosure` (UST), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water/mold), `rental_sinkhole_disclosure`, federal В§ 4852d lead-based paint disclosure (paint, not pipes).

**Three regimes** вҖ” Federal LCRR/LCRI vs Illinois LSLRNA vs New Jersey Lead Service Line Replacement Act:

**Federal вҖ” 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart I (EPA Lead and Copper Rule Revisions + Lead and Copper Rule Improvements) + Safe Drinking Water Act 42 USC В§ 300f et seq.** вҖ” **LCRR compliance date October 16, 2024**: public water systems must complete initial **service line inventory** + notification to persons served of known or potential lead service lines + **Tier 1 public notification within 24 hours** after lead action level exceedance. **LCRI compliance date November 1, 2027**: reduces lead action level to **10 ppb** (from prior 15 ppb); mandates community water systems replace **ALL lead service lines by November 1, 2037** (10-year mandate); information must be accessible to consumers INCLUDING renters and limited English proficiency.

**Illinois вҖ” 415 ILCS 5/17.12 (Illinois Lead Service Line Replacement and Notification Act, eff. January 1, 2022)** вҖ” community water supplies must maintain lead service line inventory and submit to IL EPA by April 15, 2023; all lead service lines must be replaced on defined population-based schedule (50-year statewide goal); **landlords must provide written notice to tenants of known or potential lead service line within 30 DAYS of receiving water supply notification**; IL EPA Act В§ 42 civil penalty up to **$50,000 per violation + $10,000 per day continuing**.

**New Jersey вҖ” N.J.S.A. 58:12A-40 et seq. (NJ Lead Service Line Replacement Act, P.L. 2021, c.183) + N.J.S.A. 58:10A-10** вҖ” all lead service lines must be replaced **within 10 years (by July 2031)**; property owners must disclose lead service line presence to tenants **BEFORE LEASE SIGNING**; NJDEP administers replacement program with cost-sharing between water utility and property owner; civil penalty under В§ 58:10A-10 up to **$50,000 per violation PER DAY** for failure to replace.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-lead-pipe-disclosure`. `traderview-expense::rental_hardwired_smoke_alarm_responsibility` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property HARDWIRED SMOKE ALARM installation, maintenance, and operability compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property, what smoke alarm technology (hardwired with battery backup vs 10-year sealed battery), installation locations (every bedroom + hallway outside sleeping areas + each floor including basement), and post-tenancy verification duties apply, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a fire injury or fatality? Distinct from siblings `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO alarm), `rental_chimney_fireplace_inspection_disclosure` (iter 471), `rental_fire_extinguisher_requirement` (iter 473), `rental_window_blind_cord_safety` (iter 469), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_smoke_free_housing_disclosure` (smoking policy NOT alarms), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure`, `landlord_retaliation_damages`, `tenant_emotional_distress_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) fire-injury settlement values with non-functioning smoke alarm routinely exceed $1M-$5M; (2) California's 2014 sealed-battery mandate + 2016 hardwired mandate create rolling-replacement obligation as legacy alarms fail; (3) operability verification at new tenancy is the single most important compliance task; (4) NFPA 72 interconnected alarms are significantly more protective than standalone units and increasingly mandatory; (5) photoelectric vs ionization debate matters вҖ” photoelectric detects smoldering fires faster, ionization detects flaming fires faster, dual-sensor is best practice; (6) MA bedroom photoelectric mandate is sensor-type-specific; CA does not mandate specific sensor type.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **California (MOST PRESCRIPTIVE)** | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 13113.7 + В§ 13113.8; Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1(a)(7); State Fire Marshal 2014 regulations | **2014 10-YEAR SEALED-BATTERY mandate** for all battery-operated alarms; **JANUARY 1, 2016 HARDWIRED-WITH-BATTERY-BACKUP mandate** for newly installed alarms in new construction or substantial renovation; landlord operability duty at new tenancy + correction duty on tenant report |
| **Massachusetts** | 527 C.M.R. 1.00 Comprehensive Fire Safety Code; M.G.L. c. 148 В§ 26F | **PHOTOELECTRIC** technology mandate in bedrooms and adjacent sleeping areas; 10-year sealed-battery for new installations; hardwired-with-backup in new construction |
| **Default** | NFPA 72 (National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code); Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 | NFPA 72 placement standards referenced by state/local fire codes + common-law implied warranty of habitability |

**NFPA 72 placement requirements** (five):
1. **INSIDE EVERY BEDROOM** and sleeping area
2. **OUTSIDE EACH SEPARATE SLEEPING AREA** in immediate vicinity of bedrooms (hallway)
3. **ON EACH ADDITIONAL STORY** of the dwelling including basements (not crawl spaces or unfinished attics)
4. **INTERCONNECTED** so all alarms activate when any one detects smoke (typical hardwired installation)
5. **AT LEAST 4 INCHES** from wall when ceiling-mounted; AT LEAST 4 INCHES from ceiling when wall-mounted; **NOT WITHIN 36 INCHES** of HVAC supply registers

**Six smoke alarm types modeled**: `HardwiredWithBatteryBackup` (required in new construction and substantial renovation), `TenYearSealedBattery` (CA mandate since 2014 вҖ” eliminates battery-removal vandalism), `ReplaceableBattery` (pre-2014 CA legacy; rolling replacement at end of life), `Photoelectric` (MA bedroom mandate; better for slow smoldering fires), `Ionization` (better for flaming fires; high cooking false-alarm rate), `DualSensor` (combines photoelectric + ionization; best detection across fire types).

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Alarm inoperable at start of tenancy** вҶ’ Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 13113.7 violation + habitability breach (Hilder v. St. Peter)
2. **Tenant reported inoperable alarm not corrected** вҶ’ constructive eviction + retaliation (`landlord_retaliation_damages`)
3. **No alarm in bedroom or hallway** вҶ’ NFPA 72 placement violation + fire-injury negligence per se
4. **Fire injury or fatality during tenancy** вҶ’ tort negligence + wrongful death + IIED (`tenant_emotional_distress_damages` iter 453); $1M-$5M settlement routine with non-functioning alarm
5. **Battery-removal failure** (replaceable-battery alarms where tenant removed battery) вҶ’ weaker landlord defense than 10-year sealed-battery or hardwired-with-backup

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) CA landlord rents 2018-renovated unit with 10-year sealed-battery alarms вҖ” violates January 1, 2016 hardwired mandate; (2) CA landlord rents pre-2016 property with replaceable-battery alarm вҖ” current technology downgrade risk, consider upgrade at next end-of-life; (3) MA landlord rents unit with ionization-only bedroom alarms вҖ” violates M.G.L. c. 148 В§ 26F photoelectric mandate; (4) tenant reports inoperable alarm Monday, landlord ignores until fire-injury event Friday вҶ’ wrongful death + IIED + $5M+ settlement; (5) operable HardwiredWithBatteryBackup + DualSensor + every-bedroom + every-floor + interconnected = full compliance + best-practice life-safety; (6) battery-removed by tenant on 10-year sealed-battery alarm вҖ” physically impossible, providing strong landlord defense; (7) battery-removed on replaceable-battery вҖ” landlord defense weaker because design enables tenant misuse.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-hardwired-smoke-alarm-responsibility`. Thirty-three tests pin: **CA compliant baseline**; **no alarm installed top violation** (per-se NoAlarmInstalled severity); **fire injury event top severity** ($1M-$5M settlement); **inoperable at start of tenancy operability violation** (В§ 13113.7 + В§ 1941.1(a)(7)); **tenant reported inoperable not corrected violation** (retaliation exposure); **tenant reported inoperable corrected compliant**; **missing bedroom alarm placement violation**; **missing hallway alarm placement violation**; **missing floor alarm placement violation**; **CA replaceable-battery legacy downgrade risk** (pre-2016 property); **CA post-2016 property must be hardwired** (В§ 13113.7 + January 1, 2016 mandate); **CA pre-2016 property sealed-battery OK**; **CA 10-year sealed-battery no downgrade risk**; **MA bedroom photoelectric mandate violation** (M.G.L. c. 148 В§ 26F + Ionization-only insufficient); **MA bedroom dual-sensor no photoelectric warning**; **non-interconnected alarms warning**; **CA jurisdiction cites В§ 13113.7 + В§ 13113.8 + В§ 1941.1(a)(7) + 2014 State Fire Marshal + January 1, 2016**; **MA jurisdiction cites 527 C.M.R. 1.00 + В§ 26F**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (NFPA 72 + Hilder + В§ 1941.1); **severity priority fire injury > no alarm > operability**; **severity no alarm > operability > placement**; **severity operability > placement**; **severity placement > downgrade risk**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 13113.7 + В§ 13113.8 + В§ 1941.1(a)(7) + 527 C.M.R. 1.00 + В§ 26F + NFPA 72 + Hilder + 2014 + JANUARY 1, 2016); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins NFPA 72 five placement requirements**; **note pins six alarm types**; **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins companion modules**; **3-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **MA uniquely photoelectric invariant**; **CA uniquely 2016 hardwired invariant**; **multiple violations stack in actions**.

`traderview-expense::rental_hoa_disclosure_at_lease` is the **rental property HOA / Condominium Association disclosure at lease signing compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord renting a unit governed by a Homeowners Association (HOA) or Condominium Owners Association (COA) disclose association rules, CC&Rs, fee structure, rental restrictions, and tenant-information sharing to prospective tenants at lease execution and to the association before/after lease execution? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed HOA rules create breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment + tenant rescission claim + per-incident assessment exposure when HOA fines landlord for tenant rule violation. Distinct from siblings `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (screening), `tenant_data_privacy` (general data handling), `rental_property_registration` (state/municipal registration), `short_term_rental_conversion` (STR restrictions).

**Three regimes** вҖ” California (Davis-Stirling) vs Florida (В§ 720.401 + В§ 718.503) vs Nevada (NRS В§ 116.335):

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 4740 + В§ 4525 + В§ 1102 et seq. (Davis-Stirling Common Interest Development Act)** вҖ” landlord must provide HOA with **tenant's name and contact information BEFORE lease execution** (В§ 4740); landlord may **REDACT** personal and financial information from signed lease before sending to HOA (financial privacy protection); В§ 4525 вҖ” HOA must furnish governing documents to prospective tenant on request; pre-acquisition rental prohibitions enforceable; post-acquisition prohibitions grandfathered for existing owners; В§ 1102 TDS required at sale.

**Florida вҖ” FL Statute В§ 720.401 + В§ 718.503 (Condominium Act)** вҖ” **HOA Disclosure Summary** required before contract execution; notifies tenant/buyer of (1) HOA membership subject + (2) potential assessments + (3) failure to pay results in lien on property; В§ 718.503 вҖ” condominium association may make **written demand to TENANT** to submit rental payments to association until delinquent balance paid (rent diversion remedy); HOA documents (CC&Rs + rules + fee structure) furnished on request.

**Nevada вҖ” Nev. Rev. Stat. В§ 116 + В§ 116.335 (Uniform Common-Interest Ownership Act) + В§ 118A Landlord and Tenant** вҖ” association may NOT prohibit unit owner from renting or leasing UNLESS declaration prohibited at **TIME OF PURCHASE**; pre-acquisition restrictions grandfathered; post-acquisition prohibition unenforceable against existing owner; В§ 118A general landlord-tenant disclosure framework applies in addition to Chapter 116 HOA framework.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-hoa-disclosure-at-lease`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **CA clean compliant**; **CA no tenant info to HOA violation** (В§ 4740); **CA sensitive info shared without redaction violation** (financial privacy); **CA pre-acquisition rental prohibition enforceable**; **CA post-acquisition rental prohibition not enforceable**; **CA owner leased despite pre-acquisition prohibition violation** (Davis-Stirling); **FL clean compliant**; **FL no Disclosure Summary violation** (В§ 720.401); **FL no CC&Rs/rules furnished violation**; **NV clean compliant**; **NV post-acquisition prohibition unenforceable violation** (В§ 116.335); **NV owner leased despite pre-acquisition prohibition violation**; **NV pre-acquisition only enforceable**; **citation pins CA / FL / NV authorities** (all three); **note pins CA tenant info requirement** (BEFORE lease execution); **note pins CA redaction right**; **note pins FL Disclosure Summary three elements** (HOA membership + assessments + lien); **note pins FL Condominium rent diversion** (В§ 718.503); **note pins NV В§ 116.335 pre-acquisition grandfathering** (TIME OF PURCHASE); **note pins NV В§ 118A landlord-tenant framework**; **CA uniquely requires tenant info to HOA invariant**; **FL uniquely requires Disclosure Summary invariant**; **NV uniquely prohibits post-acquisition restriction invariant**; **pre-acquisition rental prohibition invariant across CA + NV**; **CA redaction right uniquely available invariant**; **multiple CA violations stack** (3 violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_pesticide_application_notification` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential rental pesticide application notification framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because pesticide application is routine maintenance (ant colonies, cockroach extermination, termite treatment, mosquito spraying, rodent bait) but failure to satisfy state pre-application notice requirements creates per-violation civil exposure PLUS tenant common-law tort claims for chemical exposure injuries (especially when a tenant has chemical sensitivity, asthma, or pregnancy that the landlord knew or should have known about). Distinct from siblings `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor framework), `rental_organic_waste_collection_disclosure` (organic waste diversion), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water damage), `landlord_emergency_entry_notice` (entry without notice).

**Four-jurisdiction statutory framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Required Notice |
|--------------|--------|------------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.8.5 (SB 328 of 2015, effective January 1, 2016) | **24-HOUR** pre-application notice with 5 delivery methods |
| **New York** | NY ECL В§ 33-1004 + В§ 33-1005 (Pesticide Neighbor Notification Law + 2020 amendment) | **48-HOUR** notice + visual markers + English/Spanish multilingual requirement |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. G.L. c. 132B В§ 6F + В§ 6I (Children and Families Protection Act of 2000) | **48-HOUR** notice + visual markers + IPM plans |
| **Default** | 40 CFR Part 170 (federal Worker Protection Standard) + EPA FIFRA 7 USC В§ 136 | No statewide pre-application notice; common-law negligence claims preserved |

Pinned by `ca_24_hour_advance_notice_compliant`, `ny_48_hour_advance_notice_compliant`, `ma_48_hour_advance_notice_compliant`, `default_no_advance_notice_compliant_but_warning_engaged`, `jurisdiction_truth_table_four_cells`, `ca_uniquely_provides_24_hour_standard_invariant` (CA 24 < NY 48 = MA 48), `ny_ma_share_48_hour_standard_invariant`.

**California Civ. Code В§ 1940.8.5 four-prong notice requirement**:

| Subsection | Element |
|-------------|---------|
| В§ 1940.8.5(a) | Definitions: pesticide + active ingredient + dwelling unit + authorized agent |
| В§ 1940.8.5(b) | Required notice content: brand name + active ingredient + scheduled date/time + reasonable application statement + label warnings |
| В§ 1940.8.5(c) | **24-HOUR** pre-application notice via 5 delivery methods (first-class mail + personal delivery + under-door + electronic + posted) |
| В§ 1940.8.5(d) | Emergency exception: immediate health/safety threat вҶ’ post-notice within **1 HOUR after application** |
| В§ 1940.8.5(e) | Tenant-requested oral agreement exception: must include **PESTICIDE PRODUCT BRAND NAME** |

Pinned by `ca_23_hour_advance_notice_violation` (23 hours = violation), `ca_missing_content_violation`, `ca_emergency_within_one_hour_post_notice_compliant`, `ca_emergency_post_notice_2_hours_violation` (2 hours = violation), `ca_tenant_oral_agreement_with_brand_name_compliant`, `ca_tenant_oral_agreement_without_brand_name_violation`.

**New York ECL 33-1004 + 33-1005 dual-format requirements**:

| Element | Source | Rule |
|---------|--------|------|
| Pre-application notice | NY ECL 33-1004 | **48-HOUR** for commercial lawn applications + residential one-or-two-family dwelling pesticide label |
| Visual notification markers | NY ECL 33-1004 | Signs/flags posted at residential lawn application sites |
| Multilingual notice (2020 amendment) | NY ECL 33-1004 | **English AND Spanish** for commercial lawn applications (plus other language Commissioner deems necessary) |
| Schools | NY ECL | 48-hour notice to registered staff/parents |
| Daycare facilities | NY ECL | 48-hour pre-application notice posted in common area visible to those dropping off/picking up children |

Pinned by `ny_47_hour_advance_notice_violation`, `ny_no_multilingual_notice_violation`, `ny_no_visual_markers_violation`.

**Massachusetts Mass. G.L. c. 132B В§ 6F + В§ 6I вҖ” Children and Families Protection Act of 2000**: 48-HOUR written notice to occupants of indoor pesticide application + visual notification markers + posted in common area + mandatory IPM (Integrated Pest Management) plans for schools and daycare facilities. Pinned by `ma_under_48_hours_violation`.

**California Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 8538 parallel structural pest control operator framework** вҖ” licensed companies have parallel pre-application notice obligations under SPCB regulations; В§ 1940.8.5 closes the gap for LANDLORD-applied pesticides.

**EPA FIFRA 7 USC В§ 136 federal preemption carveout**: federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act preempts state pesticide labeling but PRESERVES state notice and disclosure requirements for landlord-tenant relationships.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-pesticide-application-notification`. Thirty-three tests pin: **CA 24-hour advance notice compliant**; **CA 23-hour advance notice violation** (1-hour boundary precision); **CA missing content violation**; **CA emergency within 1-hour post-notice compliant**; **CA emergency post-notice 2 hours violation**; **CA tenant oral agreement with brand name compliant**; **CA tenant oral agreement without brand name violation**; **NY 48-hour advance notice compliant**; **NY 47-hour advance notice violation**; **NY no multilingual notice violation** (2020 amendment); **NY no visual markers violation**; **MA 48-hour advance notice compliant**; **MA under 48 hours violation**; **default no advance notice compliant but warning engaged**; **jurisdiction truth table four cells**; **NY/MA share 48-hour standard invariant**; **CA uniquely provides 24-hour standard invariant**; **CA emergency only engages with immediate threat**; **CA oral agreement only engages with tenant request**; **citation pins all four jurisdictions** (В§ 1940.8.5 + SB 328 + В§ 8538 + NY ECL В§ 33-1004 + В§ 33-1005 + В§ 33-0903 + В§ 33-0905 + Mass. c. 132B В§ 6F + В§ 6I + 40 CFR Part 170 + EPA FIFRA 7 USC В§ 136); **note pins CA 24-hour five delivery methods** (first-class mail + personal + under-door + electronic + posted); **note pins CA required content** (brand + active ingredient + warnings); **note pins CA emergency exception 1-hour**; **note pins CA tenant oral agreement brand name**; **note pins NY ECL 48-hour visual markers**; **note pins NY 2020 amendment English+Spanish**; **note pins NY school daycare 48-hour registry**; **note pins MA IPM Children and Families Protection Act**; **note pins default federal floor** (40 CFR Part 170 agricultural + common-law negligence); **note pins CA SPCB В§ 8538 parallel**; **note pins FIFRA federal preemption carveout**; **defensive zero hours routine non-compliant**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::rental_pet_breed_restriction_disclosure` is the **rental pet breed restriction disclosure plus FHA reasonable-accommodation framework module** (iter 525) вҖ” covers landlord enforcement of pet-breed restrictions in residential leases, FHA reasonable-accommodation exception for assistance animals (service + emotional support), state preemption of breed-specific landlord restrictions, insurance breed-ban interaction, and disclosure requirements. Distinct from siblings `rental_pet_deposit_separate_security` (pet deposit framework вҖ” distinct from breed restriction), `fair_housing_reasonable_modification` (broader FHA modification framework), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for wrongful denial of assistance animal), `rental_attached_garage_carbon_monoxide_disclosure` (iter 523 parallel landlord-disclosure framework), `rental_storage_unit_lease_disclosure` (iter 509 separate disclosure framework analog). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **Fair Housing Act 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B)** + 24 C.F.R. В§ 100.204 require landlord reasonable accommodation of assistance animals (service animals + emotional support animals) and PREEMPT lease pet-breed restrictions; **HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01 (January 28, 2020)** provides assistance-animal assessment framework; (2) HUD interpretation: "housing providers may not limit the breed or size of a dog used as a service animal or support animal just because of the size or breed" вҖ” must look at actual animal behavior not breed stereotypes; (3) **insurance carrier breed bans** (Liberty Mutual + State Farm + Allstate + Farmers + Nationwide exclude pit bulls + Rottweilers + Akitas + Dobermans + Chow Chows + German Shepherds + Wolf hybrids) CANNOT justify denying assistance animal per FHA вҖ” federal disability accommodation preempts insurance coverage exclusion per multiple federal court decisions; (4) **Nevada SB 245 (effective 2025)**: prohibits landlord liability insurance breed discrimination вҖ” Nevada Division of Insurance enforces; (5) **Maryland Pet Policy Transparency Act of 2025**: requires landlords to post pet policies on rental websites and rental applications including number + weight limits + restricted breeds + vaccination requirements + pet deposits + pet rent; (6) state preemption status varies вҖ” 29 states preempt local breed-specific legislation; 21 states permit local BSL (MD + MI + OH + KS); (7) CDC reports approximately 4.5M dog bites annually in US with about 800K requiring medical care; landlord premises liability for tenant or guest bite injuries routinely $50K-$500K settlement exposure.

**HUD civil penalties**: $25,645 first offense + $128,225 subsequent offense per 24 C.F.R. В§ 180.671 + 28 C.F.R. В§ 85.5 (2024 inflation-adjusted). HUD complaint may be filed within 1 year per 42 U.S.C. В§ 3610. Compensatory damages + statutory damages + attorney's fees plus injunctive relief available.

**Three animal types**: AdaServiceAnimal (Title III task-trained for disability вҖ” landlord may ask only two questions per ADA Title III: "Is the dog required because of a disability?" + "What work or task has the dog been trained to perform?"), FhaEmotionalSupportAnimal (alleviates disability symptoms вҖ” landlord may request written letter from licensed mental-health provider establishing tenant disability and animal-disability nexus per HUD guidance), RegularPetNoFhaProtection.

**Four breed restriction types**: NoBreedRestriction, SpecificBreedsProhibited, WeightOrSizeRestriction, TotalPetProhibition.

**Six-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” Maryland (Pet Policy Transparency Act of 2025 + no state preemption of BSL + 21 states permit local BSL) vs Michigan (no state preemption + local BSL permitted + Michigan Civil Rights Commission) vs Nevada (SB 245 effective 2025 prohibits insurance breed discrimination + Nevada Division of Insurance) vs California (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942.7 + В§ 54.2 service-animal access + DFEH + AB 468 ESA 30-day client/provider relationship) vs New York (NY Civ. Rights Law В§ 47-a + NY RPL В§ 235-b + NYC Admin В§ 8-107(15) + NYS Human Rights Law В§ 296(2-a) + NYC HPD + NY Division of Human Rights) vs Default (federal FHA 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + 24 C.F.R. В§ 100.204 + HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01 + 29 states preempt local BSL + CDC 4.5M annual US dog bites).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, BreedRestrictionEnforceableNonAssistance (regular pet вҖ” CDC dog-bite + $50K-$500K premises-liability exposure), FhaPreemptsBreedRestrictionAssistanceAnimal (50% rent at risk), InsuranceBreedBanIneffectiveVsFha (50% rent at risk), MdPetPolicyTransparencyActDisclosureRequired (50% rent at risk), NvSb245InsuranceBreedDiscriminationProhibited (50% rent at risk), DocumentationRequestedFromTenantPermissible (ADA Title III two-question protocol), DiscriminatoryDenialFhaViolationFineExposure (100% rent at risk + $25,645 first-offense + $128,225 subsequent-offense HUD civil penalty).

В§ 180.671 + В§ 3610); **documentation requested permissible without qualifying docs** (ADA Title III two questions); **insurance breed ban ineffective vs FHA** (Liberty Mutual + State Farm); **Maryland Pet Policy Transparency Act disclosure required** (vaccination requirements); **Nevada SB 245 insurance breed discrimination prohibited** (Nevada Division of Insurance); **MD jurisdiction pins Pet Policy Transparency Act + 21 states permit local BSL**; **MI jurisdiction pins no state preemption + Michigan Civil Rights Commission**; **NV jurisdiction pins SB 245 + Division of Insurance**; **CA jurisdiction pins В§ 1942.7 + DFEH + AB 468**; **NY jurisdiction pins В§ 47-a + В§ 8-107(15) + В§ 296(2-a)**; **default jurisdiction pins FHA В§ 3604(f)(3)(B) + HUD FHEO-2020-01 + CDC 4.5M dog bites**; **coordination note references rental_pet_deposit_separate_security + rental_attached_garage_carbon_monoxide_disclosure siblings**; **HUD first-offense penalty pins $25,645**; **HUD subsequent-offense penalty pins $128,225**; **states with BSL preemption pins 29**; **CDC annual dog bites pins 4.5M**; **MD Pet Policy Transparency Act year pins 2025**; **NV SB 245 effective year pins 2025**; **HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01 date pins 2020-01-28**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (6-cell sweep); **severity priority discriminatory denial overrides FHA preemption**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic**.

`traderview-expense::rental_pet_deposit_separate_security` is the **rental property pet deposit and separate security charge compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord fold pet deposits into the general security deposit cap vs. when may they collect separate pet deposits, pet fees, and pet rent simultaneously? Trader-landlord operational concern: post-2024 California framework folds all pet deposits into a single security deposit cap; misclassifying a "pet deposit" as separate from the security deposit creates per-violation statutory damages and refund liability. Distinct from siblings `pet_fees` (general pet fee framework), `security_deposit_caps` (general security deposit caps), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (screening).

**Four regimes** вҖ” California (single-cap fold-in) vs Washington ($150 pet damage cap + separate non-refundable fee) vs New York (no separate pet category) vs Texas (no cap):

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 + AB 12 of 2023 (eff. July 1, 2024) + SB 611 of 2023 (eff. July 1, 2025)** вҖ” pet deposits **fold into single security deposit cap**; standard cap **1 month's rent** (furnished or unfurnished, unified post-AB 12); **small-landlord exception 2 months** for natural persons owning вүӨ 2 residential rental properties with collectively вүӨ 4 units; monthly pet rent permitted as recurring fee IF disclosed upfront in lease; SB 611 prohibits military security deposit surcharge and requires itemized fee disclosure; В§ 1950.5(g) requires refund within 21 days with itemized statement.

**Washington вҖ” RCW 59.18.260 + RCW 59.18.285** вҖ” **refundable pet damage deposit capped at $150**; non-refundable pet fee permitted if **CLEARLY LABELED non-refundable AND separate from security deposit**; distinction matters: pet damage deposit (refundable) capped at $150; pet non-refundable fee uncapped but must be labeled non-refundable; monthly pet rent permitted as recurring charge.

**New York вҖ” NY GOL В§ 7-103 (HSTPA of 2019) + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2009.1 (NYC Pet Law)** вҖ” **ONE security deposit maximum: one month's rent; FULLY REFUNDABLE**; **NO separate pet deposit permitted** (distinct from CA single-cap framework вҖ” NY explicitly PROHIBITS pet-labeled deposit category); monthly pet rent permitted only if clearly in lease; **PROHIBITED in rent-stabilized apartments** under NY rent regulation (hidden-rent doctrine); HSTPA 14-day return deadline + itemized statement.

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§В§ 92.101-92.110** вҖ” **no statutory cap** on pet deposit or pet fee; pet deposit + pet fee + pet rent may be charged simultaneously; refundable unless lease specifies non-refundable; В§ 92.103 вҖ” 30-day return after surrender + forwarding address; most permissive regime among comparators.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-pet-deposit-separate-security`.
