Penal Code В§ 632 + 720 ILCS 5/14-2 + MD Cts. & Jud. Proc. В§ 10-402); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins Illinois BIPA eight elements**; **note pins Texas CUBI**; **note pins California CCPA + CPRA**; **note pins Restatement В§ 652B intrusion**; **note pins retention best-practice six elements**; **note pins sensitive areas six categories**; **note pins trader fact patterns six**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::rental_water_submetering_disclosure` is the **rental property water submetering / RUBS disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord register with the state PUC, disclose the submetering arrangement at lease signing, and follow billing-transparency rules before billing tenants separately from rent for water/wastewater service via submeters or RUBS (ratio utility billing system)? Trader-landlord operational concern: failure to register and disclose creates PUC enforcement action (Texas), tenant refund claims, and statutory damages. Distinct from siblings `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability), `rental_bed_bug_disclosure` (lease disclosure), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification), `rental_property_registration` (general landlord registration).

**Four regimes** вҖ” California SB 7 mandatory submetering vs Texas PUCT registration + 30% admin fee cap vs Florida voluntary vs Default RUBS:

**California вҖ” SB 7 of 2016 (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.201 et seq.) + Cal. Public Utilities Code В§ 739.5** вҖ” **mandatory** submetering for newly constructed multiunit and mixed-use structures submitting application for new water connection after **January 1, 2018**. Pre-lease written disclosure required before execution of rental agreement if landlord intends to charge for water separately from rent. В§ 1954.203 disclosure must describe billing method (volumetric vs allocated) + billing frequency + dispute resolution process + service charges + administrative fees. Cal. Public Utilities Code В§ 739.5 governs master-meter customers serving submetered tenants вҖ” tariff discount passed through.

**Texas вҖ” Texas Water Code В§ 13.503 + 16 TAC В§ 24.275 et seq. (PUCT submetering rules)** вҖ” property owner using submetered or allocated billing **must register with PUCT** (Public Utility Commission of Texas) BEFORE billing tenants. Tenant guide to submetered service required (must include billing method + past usage + dispute rights). **30 percent maximum administrative fee cap** for water/wastewater services. Quarterly past-usage disclosure required.

**Florida вҖ” Florida PSC voluntary framework** вҖ” submetering encouraged for water conservation but NOT mandated statewide. FL PSC guidelines provide best practices. No statewide PUC registration required (distinct from Texas). Pre-lease disclosure recommended by FL PSC but not statutorily required.

**Default вҖ” RUBS (Ratio Utility Billing System) general framework** вҖ” 38+ states permit some form of submetering or RUBS. Pre-lease disclosure of method + billing frequency + dispute process recommended. Generally no maximum administrative fee cap (unlike Texas 30% cap). Habitability + just-and-reasonable charges remain implied warranties.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-water-submetering-disclosure`. Thirty-one tests pin: **CA clean compliant**; **CA new construction post-2018 must have submeters** (RUBS violates В§ 1954.201); **CA old construction RUBS no mandatory violation**; **CA no pre-lease disclosure violation** (В§ 1954.202); **CA missing billing method disclosure violation** (В§ 1954.203); **CA missing billing frequency disclosure violation**; **CA missing dispute process disclosure violation**; **CA master-metered no billing no disclosure required**; **TX clean compliant**; **TX no PUC registration violation** (В§ 13.503); **TX no tenant guide violation** (В§ 24.275); **TX 30 percent admin fee boundary compliant** (boundary); **TX 31 percent admin fee violation** (В§ 24.275(b)); **TX no quarterly past-usage violation**; **TX master-metered no billing no PUC registration required**; **FL voluntary framework no violation**; **default no pre-lease disclosure violation**; **default clean compliant**; **citation pins CA / TX / FL / default authorities** (all four); **note pins CA January 2018 effective date**; **note pins TX 30 percent cap**; **note pins FL voluntary framework**; **note pins default RUBS 38+ states framework**; **CA uniquely requires mandatory submetering invariant** (only CA mandatory); **TX uniquely requires PUC registration invariant** (only TX); **FL least stringent no pre-lease default invariant**; **billing method truth table for CA** (3-cell вҖ” Submetering OK, RUBS fails, MasterMetered OK); **multiple TX violations stack** (4 violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_well_water_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property PRIVATE WELL WATER testing + disclosure compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property served by a private water well rather than a municipal/community water supply, what testing schedule applies, what contaminants must be tested for, what disclosure must be given to tenant, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord? Distinct from siblings `rental_septic_system_disclosure` (iter 465 вҖ” OSTDS), `rental_lead_pipe_disclosure` (lead service lines), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure`, `rental_water_submetering_disclosure` (water billing), `rent_abatement_construction_nuisance`, `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) New Jersey PWTA lessor obligation is STRICT вҖ” failure to test costs $5,000 statutory penalty per N.J.S.A. 58:12A-31 plus tenant rescission; (2) inherited rural property in NJ must complete PWTA test BEFORE rental; (3) PFAS exceedance from agricultural / military / firefighting-foam source can render property unrentable until point-of-entry treatment installed ($3K-$8K capex); (4) Connecticut + Massachusetts properties have growing private-well disclosure expectations driven by arsenic + radon + PFAS public-health litigation; (5) Pennsylvania trader-landlords face NO state statutory regime but inherit common-law habitability risk on top of newly-released EPA PFAS National Primary Drinking Water Regulation (April 10, 2024).

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Lessor Obligation / Penalty |
|--------------|--------|------------------------------|
| **New Jersey (MOST STRINGENT вҖ” first-in-nation)** | Private Well Testing Act N.J.S.A. 58:12A-26 et seq. (signed March 2001, effective September 2002); N.J.S.A. 58:12A-32; N.J.A.C. 7:9E | Lessor must obtain + pay for **full PWTA test every 60 months (5 years)** AND provide written results to each rental unit **within 30 days** of receipt AND to new lessee **at lease execution**; **$5,000 statutory penalty per N.J.S.A. 58:12A-31** |
| **Connecticut** | Conn. Gen. Stat. В§ 19a-37 + Public Act 16-66 (2016) + Public Health Code В§ 19-13-B51d + Conn. Gen. Stat. В§ 47a-7 | Testing at well construction + title transfer; common-law habitability for rental |
| **Pennsylvania** | 25 Pa. Code Ch. 109 + Pa. Const. art. I В§ 27 | **NO state-level rental disclosure statute**; PA DEP voluntary recommendations; common-law habitability |
| **Default** | Safe Drinking Water Act 42 U.S.C. В§ 300f (private well **exemption** < 25 individuals / 15 connections); Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 | Federal SDWA exempts; common-law habitability + state implied warranty |

**Nine-contaminant universal baseline panel** (PWTA-style):
1. **Total coliform bacteria** (pathogen contamination indicator)
2. **Nitrate/nitrite** вҖ” 10 mg/L MCL (methemoglobinemia / blue-baby syndrome risk; agricultural runoff)
3. **Lead (Pb)** вҖ” 15 ppb EPA action level (plumbing corrosion)
4. **Arsenic (As)** вҖ” 10 Вөg/L MCL (Group 1 IARC carcinogen; NJ statewide since 2021)
5. **VOCs** вҖ” TCE, PCE, benzene, MTBE (industrial / UST contamination)
6. **pH and corrosivity** (drives lead leaching)
7. **Iron and manganese** (aesthetic / secondary MCLs)
8. **Gross alpha radioactivity** вҖ” 15 pCi/L MCL (radium / uranium)
9. **PFAS** вҖ” EPA April 10, 2024 National Primary Drinking Water Regulation MCLs (PFOA 4 ppt + PFOS 4 ppt + GenX/HFPO-DA 10 ppt + PFNA 10 ppt + PFHxS 10 ppt) вҖ” recently added in NJ + several states

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Failure to test or disclose** вҶ’ habitability breach + constructive eviction (Hilder v. St. Peter) + tenant rescission right in NJ
2. **Contaminant exceedance of MCL** вҶ’ habitability breach + landlord duty to remediate via point-of-entry treatment, well deepening, or municipal connection
3. **Lead exceedance + child residents** вҶ’ lead poisoning class-of-claims (parallel to `rental_lead_pipe_disclosure`)
4. **PFAS exceedance** вҶ’ emerging litigation + **CERCLA 42 U.S.C. В§ 9607(a) owner/operator strict liability** after EPA April 2024 PFOA/PFOS hazardous-substance designation
5. **Well casing or pressure-tank failure during tenancy** вҶ’ loss-of-water habitability breach + temporary relocation duty

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-well-water-disclosure`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **property on municipal water not applicable**; **NJ compliant baseline**; **NJ PWTA test over 60 months overdue** ($5K + N.J.S.A. 58:12A-31); **NJ PWTA test exactly 60 months compliant**; **NJ PWTA missing 30-day disclosure**; **NJ PWTA missing lease-execution disclosure**; **coliform exceedance contaminant severity** (chlorination + UV); **nitrate exceedance methemoglobinemia + blue-baby warning**; **lead exceedance 15 ppb action level + child-resident cross-reference**; **arsenic exceedance IARC Group 1 + NJ statewide since 2021**; **VOC exceedance cross-references UST module** (TCE/PCE/benzene/MTBE + GAC); **PFAS exceedance with 2024 EPA rule + CERCLA liability** (PFOA 4 ppt + PFOS 4 ppt + GenX 10 ppt); **gross alpha exceedance radium/uranium**; **loss-of-water habitability-failure severity** (well casing + relocation); **remediation installed suppresses contaminant severity**; **Connecticut jurisdiction cites В§ 19a-37 + PA 16-66 + В§ 19-13-B51d + В§ 47a-7**; **Pennsylvania no state statute** (В§ 109 + Pa. Const. art. I В§ 27); **Default jurisdiction SDWA private-well exemption** (25 individuals / 15 connections + В§ 1941.1); **severity priority failure > contaminant > testing > disclosure**; **severity contaminant > testing when no failure**; **severity testing > disclosure**; **citation pins all authorities** (N.J.S.A. 58:12A-26/-31/-32 + N.J.A.C. 7:9E + В§ 19a-37 + PA 16-66 + 25 Pa. Code Ch. 109 + 42 U.S.C. В§ 300f + В§ 1941.1 + Hilder v. St. Peter + EPA April 10, 2024 + PFOA/PFOS + В§ 9607(a)); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins nine-contaminant baseline**; **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins companion modules**; **NJ uniquely strictest invariant**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **multiple contaminants stack**.

`traderview-expense::rental_window_blind_cord_safety` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property WINDOW BLIND CORD STRANGULATION SAFETY compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a unit, what window-covering cord-safety standards apply, what retrofit obligations attach when child residents under age 8 occupy the unit, what product-recall enforcement risks expose landlord, and what failure-mode liabilities apply after a strangulation incident? Distinct from siblings `rental_window_guard_installation` (fall protection вҖ” child age 10 or younger), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` (VGB Act), `landlord_security_device_obligations` (locks), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED), `landlord_retaliation_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) the 2022 CPSC mandatory standard 16 C.F.R. Part 1260 (effective May 30, 2023) does NOT retroactively require landlords to replace existing corded blinds, BUT (2) implied warranty of habitability cases routinely treat corded blinds in child-occupied units as actionable defects, (3) settlement values in child strangulation cases routinely exceed $1M with wrongful death awards routinely exceeding $5M, (4) CPSC recall enforcement under 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 can apply if landlord continues to lease units with recalled corded blinds, (5) the most efficient mitigation is proactive retrofit to cordless or inaccessible-cord designs at tenant turnover.

**Three-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **Federal / CPSC** (universal floor) | 16 C.F.R. Part 1260; 87 Fed. Reg. 73118 (November 28, 2022); Consumer Product Safety Act 15 U.S.C. В§ 2056; ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2018; ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2022 | Mandatory standard for CUSTOM window coverings manufactured after **May 30, 2023** вҖ” eliminates free-hanging operating cords + free-hanging tilt cords + multiple cords into cord connectors; ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2018 cordless or inaccessible-cord on STOCK products since May 2022; ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2022 extends to custom products effective June 2024 |
| **California / progressive states** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1 + В§ 1942.4 + В§ 1942(a) + Title 24 | Implied warranty of sanitary facilities + untenantable-conditions framework + tenant **repair-and-deduct** remedy |
| **Default** | Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); Consumer Product Safety Act 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068; 16 C.F.R. В§ 1115 | Common-law implied warranty of habitability + tort negligence for child-resident properties + CPSC recall enforcement |

**CPSC strangulation fatality data**: ~120 child fatalities from window-covering cord strangulation 2002-2017 to children age 8 or younger.

**Six covering types modeled**: `Cordless` (compliant), `InaccessibleCordOnlyWand` (compliant), `AccessibleCordedStock`, `AccessibleCordedCustom`, `PreEffectiveDateExisting` (pre-May-30-2023), `None`.

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Corded blinds + child resident age 8 or younger** вҶ’ habitability breach + negligence + premises liability
2. **Tenant retrofit request ignored** вҶ’ constructive eviction + retaliation (parallel to `landlord_retaliation_damages`)
3. **CPSC-recalled product in tenant unit** вҶ’ 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 prohibited-acts enforcement + state consumer-protection liability
4. **Custom window covering installed after May 30, 2023** that violates 16 C.F.R. Part 1260 вҶ’ federal enforcement + state AG action
5. **Strangulation injury during tenancy** вҶ’ tort negligence + wrongful death + IIED (parallel to `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` iter 453)

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) Landlord installs custom blinds with accessible cords on June 1, 2023 вҖ” VIOLATES 16 C.F.R. Part 1260, federal CPSC enforcement + state AG action; (2) landlord retains pre-May-2023 corded blinds in unit rented to family with toddler вҖ” no per-se federal violation but implied warranty of habitability + premises liability exposure if injury occurs; (3) CPSC recall issued for specific brand/model вҖ” 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 prohibits continued leasing with recalled product + 16 C.F.R. В§ 1115 substantial-product-hazard reporting; (4) tenant requests retrofit to cordless blinds, landlord ignores вҖ” constructive eviction + retaliation exposure; (5) child strangulation injury occurs вҖ” tort negligence + wrongful death + IIED with $1M-$5M+ exposure; (6) California landlord вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942(a) repair-and-deduct allows tenant self-help retrofit charged against rent.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-window-blind-cord-safety`. Thirty-two tests pin: **no window covering not applicable**; **cordless compliant baseline**; **inaccessible cord wand compliant**; **pre-effective-date existing no child no violation**; **pre-effective-date with child habitability risk**; **custom corded installed after May 30, 2023 federal violation** (16 C.F.R. Part 1260 + 87 Fed. Reg. 73118); **custom corded before May 30, 2023 no federal violation**; **stock corded after May 2022 federal violation** (ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2018); **CPSC recall product recall-enforcement severity** (15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 + 16 C.F.R. В§ 1115); **strangulation incident top severity**; **tenant retrofit request unaddressed constructive-eviction note**; **tenant retrofit addressed no warning**; **California cites В§ 1941.1 + В§ 1942.4 + В§ 1942(a)**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + Green); **severity priority incident > recall > federal > habitability**; **severity recall > federal > habitability**; **severity federal > habitability**; **child age 8 with accessible corded stock habitability risk**; **no child pre-effective-date compliant**; **cordless with child compliant**; **inaccessible cord wand with child compliant**; **citation pins all authorities** (16 C.F.R. Part 1260 + May 30, 2023 + 87 Fed. Reg. 73118 + ANSI/WCMA A100.1-2018/2022 + June 2024 + 15 U.S.C. В§ 2068 + В§ 1941.1 + В§ 1942.4 + В§ 1942(a) + Hilder + 478 A.2d 202); **note pins three-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins no retroactive replacement requirement**; **note pins CPSC 120-fatality data**; **note pins companion modules**; **covering type truth table** (4 cells); **CA uniquely cites repair-and-deduct invariant**; **custom corded after May 30, 2023 pinpoints Part 1260**; **recall enforcement pins В§ 2068 + В§ 1115**; **strangulation incident notes CPSC В§ 1115 reporting**.

`traderview-expense::rental_window_guard_installation` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential rental window guard installation requirement framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because child window-fall injuries are among the highest-stakes premises liability claims вҖ” wrongful death awards routinely exceed $5M, and many jurisdictions impose STRICT LIABILITY when window guards are required by statute but absent. NYC alone mandates window guards in every apartment of a building with 3+ units where a child age 10 or younger resides; failure exposes landlord to NYC Department of Health civil penalties + tort liability + tenant private rights of action. Distinct from siblings `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor), `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` (VGB Act), `landlord_security_device_obligations`.

**Five-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Trigger | Penalty |
|--------------|--------|---------|---------|
| **NYC** | NYC Health Code Article 131 В§ 131.15 + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2043.1 | **3+ APARTMENTS + child age 10 or younger resides** | $1,000/day per violation under NYC Health Code В§ 3.11 |
| **Chicago** | Chicago Building Code В§ 13-196-550 | Every dwelling unit | Operable window guards limiting opening to 4 inches or less |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. G.L. + 105 CMR 410 (State Sanitary Code) | **Tenant request + child under age 10** + applicable-window 3-prong test | Habitability violation + tenant private right of action |
| **Montgomery County MD** | DHCA Code В§ 29-23 | Tenant request + child under age 6 | Habitability violation |
| **Default** | Common-law negligence + ASTM F2090-23 voluntary | None statutory | Negligence-based |

Pinned by `nyc_3_plus_units_child_under_11_obligation_engaged`, `nyc_2_unit_building_no_obligation`, `nyc_3_unit_building_boundary_engages`, `nyc_no_child_no_obligation`, `nyc_no_window_guards_violation`, `nyc_daily_penalty_accrues_at_1000_per_day` (30 days Г— $1,000 = $30,000), `jurisdiction_truth_table_five_cells`, `nyc_uniquely_engages_daily_penalty_invariant`.

**NYC Health Code В§ 131.15 four-prong**:

| # | Element |
|---|---------|
| 1 | Multiple dwelling with 3+ apartments |
| 2 | Child age 10 or younger resides in apartment |
| 3 | Install window guards on EVERY window (carveout: fire escape windows) |
| 4 | Notice: 30-day lease notice + annual notice January 1вҖ“16 |

Pinned by `nyc_fire_escape_window_carveout`, `nyc_no_30_day_notice_violation`, `nyc_no_annual_notice_violation`.

**Massachusetts 105 CMR 410 applicable-window three-prong test**:

| Prong | Requirement |
|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Window is **GREATER THAN 6 FEET ABOVE GRADE** |
| 2 | Window capable of opening sufficiently for **5-INCH DIAMETER BALL** to pass through |
| 3 | Window is **NOT connected to fire escape** |

All three required for "applicable window" classification + tenant-request-trigger. Pinned by `massachusetts_three_prong_applicable_window_with_child_request`, `massachusetts_no_tenant_request_no_obligation`, `massachusetts_window_under_6_feet_not_applicable`, `massachusetts_window_blocks_5_inch_ball_not_applicable`, `massachusetts_fire_escape_window_not_applicable`. MA annual notice requirement pinned by `massachusetts_no_annual_notice_violation` (with statutory quote: "Parents with children under the age of 10 have the right, at no additional charge, to have window guards installed within the rented apartment and the common areas of the building").

**Chicago Building Code В§ 13-196-550** вҖ” operable window guards limiting window opening to **4 INCHES OR LESS** in dwelling units; screens required April 15 through November 15 each year. Pinned by `chicago_4_inch_opening_compliant`, `chicago_above_4_inch_opening_violation`.

**Montgomery County MD DHCA Code В§ 29-23** вҖ” window guard lease addendum + tenant-request trigger when child under age 6 resides. Pinned by `montgomery_md_child_under_6_with_request`, `montgomery_md_no_child_under_6_no_obligation`, `montgomery_md_installation_failure`.

**Default вҖ” common-law negligence + ASTM F2090-23 voluntary standard** вҖ” no statewide window guard mandate; general premises liability applies; ASTM F2090-23 voluntary standard for window fall prevention devices including emergency escape (egress) release mechanisms may inform negligence analysis but does NOT satisfy strict-liability statutes.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-window-guard-installation`. Thirty-six tests pin: **NYC 3+ units child under 11 obligation engaged**; **NYC 2-unit building no obligation**; **NYC 3-unit building boundary engages**; **NYC no child no obligation**; **NYC no window guards violation**; **NYC daily penalty accrues at $1,000/day** (30 days = $30K); **NYC no 30-day notice violation**; **NYC no annual notice violation**; **NYC fire escape window carveout** (compliant despite no guards); **Chicago 4-inch opening compliant**; **Chicago above-4-inch opening violation**; **Massachusetts three-prong applicable window with child request**; **MA no tenant request no obligation**; **MA window under 6 feet not applicable**; **MA window blocks 5-inch ball not applicable**; **MA fire escape window not applicable**; **MA no annual notice violation**; **Montgomery MD child under 6 with request**; **Montgomery MD no child under 6 no obligation**; **Montgomery MD installation failure**; **default no obligation**; **jurisdiction truth table five cells**; **NYC uniquely engages daily penalty invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (NYC Article 131 В§ 131.15 + В§ 27-2043.1 + В§ 3.11 + В§ 27-2115 + Chicago В§ 13-196-550 + Mass. 105 CMR 410 + Montgomery MD В§ 29-23 + ASTM F2090-23); **note pins NYC 3+ units threshold**; **note pins NYC dual notice requirements**; **note pins NYC daily penalty DHMH**; **note pins Chicago 4-inch opening + seasonal screens**; **note pins MA three-prong test**; **note pins MA annual notice quote**; **note pins Montgomery MD DHCA В§ 29-23**; **note pins default ASTM F2090-23**; **note pins trader-landlord $5M award + strict liability**; **note pins ASTM voluntary standard**; **note pins cross-jurisdictional architecture**; **multiple NYC failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::rental_unpermitted_unit_disclosure` is the **rental property unpermitted / illegal dwelling unit disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when a trader-landlord rents a unit lacking a Certificate of Occupancy (in-law apartment, garage conversion, basement apartment, attic unit, ADU without permits), what statutory disclosure and rent-collection rules apply? Trader-landlord operational concern: an unpermitted-unit lease creates **asymmetric enforceability** вҖ” tenant may enforce lease and recover damages, but landlord may NOT collect rent or enforce eviction without first legalizing the unit. Distinct from siblings `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (safety), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability), `rental_property_registration` (general registration).

**Four regimes** вҖ” California (asymmetric enforceability doctrine) vs Oakland (strictest relocation overlay) vs New York City (MDL В§ 325 3+ unit threshold) vs Default:

**California вҖ” common law + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942.4 + В§ 1102 et seq. + Espinoza v. Calva, 169 Cal.App.4th 1393 (Cal. Ct. App. 2008)** вҖ” landlord **CANNOT legally collect rent** for unit lacking Certificate of Occupancy; tenant **CAN enforce lease and sue for damages** despite illegality (asymmetric enforceability doctrine); tenant may recover past rent + pursue relocation under local ordinances; on property sale, **Transfer Disclosure Statement (TDS)** required under В§ 1102 et seq. disclosing unpermitted unit as material defect.

**Oakland вҖ” Oakland Municipal Code В§ 8.22 (Tenant Protection Ordinance + Just Cause for Eviction Ordinance + В§ 8.22.450 relocation)** вҖ” strictest among California comparators. Treats unpermitted-unit eviction as **no just cause**; eviction prohibited except substantial-rehab carve-out. **Relocation payments REQUIRED** when displacing tenant вҖ” base **$7,931 + $5,287 per senior/disabled/minor** household member (2024 amounts). Rent-control overlay prohibits rent increase above annual CPI even in unpermitted units.

**New York City вҖ” NYC Multiple Dwelling Law В§ 325 + NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2107 + В§ 27-2115 + NYC Building Code В§ 28-118** вҖ” buildings of **3+ units** MUST have Certificate of Occupancy; unauthorized cellar/basement apartments illegal under MDL. Landlord **CANNOT collect rent** for illegal unit until legalized (DOB approval). **$1,000 + $50 per day** civil penalty for continuing violation under В§ 27-2115. **NYC Loft Law (MDL Article 7-C)** provides legalization pathway for converted loft tenants with rent-stabilization protections post-legalization.

**Default вҖ” common-law warranty of habitability + state building code** вҖ” no specific statewide unpermitted-unit disclosure mandate. General implied warranty of habitability + state landlord-tenant statutes provide constructive eviction + damages remedies. HUD Section 8 housing quality standards apply for federally-subsidized units.

`traderview-expense::rental_security_deposit_interest` is the **multi-jurisdictional security deposit interest framework module** вҖ” Chicago RLTO + Massachusetts G.L. c. 186 В§ 15B + Connecticut Gen. Stat. В§ 47a-21 + N.J.S.A. 46:8-19 + N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 7-103. Trader-landlord critical because the **STRICT-LIABILITY DOUBLE AND TREBLE DAMAGES** penalties for non-compliance are among the steepest tenant remedies in residential landlord-tenant law вҖ” Massachusetts and Chicago award tenants 3x or 2x the deposit (or interest) even WITHOUT proof of bad faith, actual loss, or fault. Distinct from siblings `landlord_annual_rent_statement`, `tenant_rent_judgment_wage_garnishment`, `rental_property_registration`, `landlord_identification_disclosure`.

**Five-jurisdiction statutory rate comparison**:

| Jurisdiction | Rate | Source | Strict-Liability Penalty |

| **Chicago** | **0.01%** (2026) | RLTO В§ 5-12-080(c) + В§ 5-12-081 (set annually by City Comptroller, Chase Bank avg) | **2x deposit + attorney fees** (В§ 5-12-080(f) вҖ” no fault required) |
| **Massachusetts** | **5% OR actual lesser** | G.L. c. 186 В§ 15B(3)(b) | **TREBLE damages (3x interest) + costs + attorney fees** (В§ 15B(6)(e) + В§ 15B(7) вҖ” MA SJC strict liability) |
| **Connecticut** | Avg savings deposit rate set quarterly | В§ 47a-21(i) + В§ 47a-21(j) (Banking Commissioner) | **DOUBLE damages + $100 + costs + attorney fees** (В§ 47a-21(d)(2) вҖ” beyond 30 days after demand) |
| **New Jersey** | Bank rate (annual) | N.J.S.A. 46:8-19 (1% landlord admin fee) | Tenant self-help offset (В§ 46:8-19(c)) |
| **New York** | Bank rate (6+ unit buildings) | Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 7-103(2) (1% landlord admin fee in lieu of all custodial expenses) | Trust + commingling violation (В§ 7-103(1)) |

`traderview-expense::rental_septic_system_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property SEPTIC SYSTEM disclosure + inspection compliance framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property served by a private on-site sewage treatment and disposal system (OSTDS or "septic system") rather than connection to municipal sewer, what disclosure rules apply, what inspection certifications are required, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord? Distinct from siblings `rental_underground_storage_tank_disclosure` (UST + LUST), `rental_water_submetering_disclosure` (water billing), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water intrusion), `rental_sinkhole_disclosure` (sinkhole), `rental_flood_hazard_disclosure` (flood plain), `rent_abatement_construction_nuisance` (rent abatement). Trader-landlord critical because (1) Massachusetts Title 5 inspection failure at title transfer can require $30,000+ in system replacement before closing; (2) Florida OSTDS in BMAP basin triggers 5-year inspection cycle cost $400-$800 per inspection; (3) sewage backup incidents are leading source of habitability litigation in rural rental properties; (4) inherited septic systems in distressed-property purchases frequently discovered non-permitted ("wildcat") at the time of resale.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Trigger / Penalty |
|--------------|--------|--------------------|
| **Massachusetts (MOST STRINGENT)** | 310 C.M.R. 15.000 ("Title 5") + 310 C.M.R. 15.301; July 7, 2023 amendments | Pass-or-fail inspection at title transfer or within **24 months** prior (**36 months** if pumped annually); nitrogen-sensitive watershed (Cape Cod + Nantucket + Buzzards Bay) require I/A nitrogen-reducing technology |
| **Florida** | Fla. Stat. В§ 381.0065 + В§ 381.00655 + Fla. Admin. Code Ch. 64E-6 + 2020 Clean Waterways Act SB 712 (June 30, 2020) | **5-year inspection cycle** for OSTDS in Basin Management Action Plan (BMAP) basins; В§ 381.0065(2)(a) installation permit + В§ 381.0065(4) operating permit + В§ 381.0065(4)(g) voluntary inspection notification duty |
| **Texas** | Tex. Health & Safety Code В§ 366.011 et seq. + В§ 366.051 + В§ 366.071 + 30 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 285 | OSSF registration with **TCEQ Authorized Agent** (typically county); installation permit + authorization to operate for performance-based; TCEQ Form 20021 notification |
| **Default** | California Water Boards OWTS Policy (June 19, 2012) + Cal. Water Code В§ 13290 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1; Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); CERCLA 42 U.S.C. В§ 9607(a) | No statutory rental disclosure; common-law habitability + implied warranty of sanitary facilities + CERCLA owner/operator strict liability for groundwater contamination |

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Sewage backup into unit** вҶ’ habitability breach + constructive eviction (Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984)) + tenant remedies escrow / rent abatement
2. **Groundwater contamination** of private well (if dual well + septic) вҶ’ CERCLA 42 U.S.C. В§ 9607(a)(1) owner/operator strict liability + state cleanup liability
3. **Pump-out frequency neglect** вҶ’ Title 5 violation in MA; В§ 381.0065(4) operating permit violation in FL
4. **Drainfield failure** on hill / slope вҶ’ soil percolation re-engineering cost $15K-$45K typical
5. **Tenant misuse** (flushing wipes, grease, harsh chemicals) вҶ’ tenant liability under lease; landlord cannot disclaim system condition entirely

**Six system types modeled**: ConventionalTank, AerobicTreatmentUnit, InnovativeAlternativeNitrogenReducing (Cape Cod / Nantucket / Buzzards Bay required), PerformanceBased (FL В§ 381.0065(4) + TX В§ 366.071 operating permit required), Cesspool (illegal in most jurisdictions), Wildcat (non-permitted), None.

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) Massachusetts trader buys distressed rural property in Cape Cod вҖ” Title 5 inspection at title transfer reveals failing leach field + non-I/A nitrogen-reducing system, requires $35K I/A upgrade BEFORE closing; (2) Florida trader buys lake-adjacent property in BMAP basin (e.g., Indian River Lagoon, Lake Apopka, Silver Springs) вҖ” 5-year inspection cycle missed вҶ’ SB 712 enforcement + corrective action plan; (3) Texas trader-landlord renting OSSF property never registered with county Authorized Agent вҖ” В§ 366.051 backflow + retroactive permit fees + criminal misdemeanor exposure; (4) Default jurisdiction trader landlord вҖ” sewage backup into tenant unit triggers Hilder constructive eviction + rent abatement (rent_abatement_construction_nuisance) + temporary relocation (mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation); (5) inherited cesspool (illegal in MA since 1995) discovered at resale вҖ” full septic system replacement required, $20K-$40K cost; (6) wildcat (non-permitted) system discovered during sale вҖ” retroactive permitting + design review + soil testing.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-septic-system-disclosure`. Thirty-six tests pin: **property on municipal sewer not applicable**; **Massachusetts compliant baseline**; **MA Title 5 inspection overdue default 24 months**; **MA Title 5 pumped annually 36-month cap applies**; **MA Title 5 pumped annually over 36 months overdue**; **MA nitrogen-sensitive requires I/A technology**; **MA nitrogen-sensitive with I/A no warning**; **Florida BMAP basin inspection overdue 60 months**; **Florida non-BMAP no 5-year cycle compliant**; **Florida performance-based requires operating permit**; **Texas performance-based requires authorization to operate** (В§ 366.071); **Texas pump-out over 60 months warning**; **Default jurisdiction uses common-law habitability** (Green v. Superior Court); **wildcat system permit-missing severity**; **wildcat Texas cites TCEQ Authorized Agent**; **wildcat Florida cites 64E-6**; **wildcat Default CERCLA strict liability warning**; **sewage backup event system-failure severity** (Hilder v. St. Peter); **missing lease disclosure severity**; **severity priority failure > permit > inspection > disclosure**; **severity permit > inspection when no backup**; **severity inspection > disclosure when permitted**; **citation pins all authorities** (310 C.M.R. 15.000 + В§ 381.0065 series + В§ 366.011/В§ 366.051 + В§ 1941.1 + Green v. Superior Court 10 Cal. 3d 616 + Hilder v. St. Peter 478 A.2d 202 + В§ 9607(a)); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins five failure-mode liabilities**; **note pins MA Title 5 inspection caps** (24 / 36); **note pins FL BMAP 60-month cycle**; **note pins companion modules**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **MA uniquely strictest inspection invariant**; **FL uniquely BMAP triggers 60-month cycle**; **cesspool via wildcat path when no permit**; **aerobic treatment unit no permit violation**; **Texas jurisdiction with FL BMAP flag ignored**; **MA pumped annually boundary 12 months exactly**; **MA pumped 13 months back to 24-month cap**.

`traderview-expense::rental_sewer_lateral_responsibility` is the **multi-jurisdictional rental property PRIVATE SEWER LATERAL (PSL) inspection, compliance, and landlord-responsibility framework module** вҖ” when a landlord rents a property connected to municipal sewer service, what ownership/maintenance/inspection obligations apply to the private sewer lateral (the pipe connecting the building to the public sewer main), what point-of-sale compliance certificate requirements apply, and what failure-mode liabilities expose landlord after a sewage backup into the dwelling unit? Distinct from siblings `rental_septic_system_disclosure` (iter 465 вҖ” OSTDS for non-municipal sewer), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure`, `rental_water_submetering_disclosure`, `rent_abatement_construction_nuisance`, `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation`, `tenant_emotional_distress_damages`. Trader-landlord critical because (1) property owners in EBMUD region face MANDATORY point-of-sale compliance certificate at $4,500 deposit risk; (2) Bay Area homes built pre-1965 routinely have failing clay or Orangeburg laterals requiring $15K-$40K replacement at sale; (3) sewage backup into tenant unit is among the most actionable habitability claims because raw sewage exposure to children + pregnant tenants triggers heightened tort + IIED liability; (4) tree-root intrusion from city-owned street trees can shift municipal liability but landlord remains primary defendant for tenant claims; (5) inherited PSL deferred maintenance is leading hidden cost in trader-landlord acquisition due diligence.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Standard |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| **EBMUD Regional PSL Program** (MOST PRESCRIPTIVE) | EBMUD Regional Private Sewer Lateral Program Ordinance | Covers Alameda + Albany + Emeryville + Oakland + Piedmont + El Cerrito + Kensington + Richmond Annex; THREE point-of-sale triggers: (a) property SALE; (b) BUILDING/REMODELING > $100K; (c) WATER METER SIZE CHANGE. Compliance Certificate: **20-year** validity for complete replacement / **7-year** for repair. **$4,500 Time Extension Certificate deposit** for 6-month grace period |
| **Berkeley** | Berkeley Municipal Code Chapter 17.16 (effective November 3, 2014) | Separate program from EBMUD with distinct compliance requirements |
| **Massachusetts** | M.G.L. c. 83 В§ 7 (owner-maintenance duty); M.G.L. c. 21 В§ 26-53 (Clean Waters Act); 314 C.M.R. 12.00 (sewer use regulations) | Owner-maintenance duty for entire lateral from premises to public sewer line |
| **Default** | Hilder v. St. Peter, 478 A.2d 202 (Vt. 1984); Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1; Clean Water Act 33 U.S.C. В§ 1342 NPDES | Common-law habitability + tort negligence + premises liability |

**Lateral material aging risk profile**:

| Material | Era | Risk |
|----------|-----|------|
| **Vitrified Clay** | 1900-1965 | Brittle, root-prone вҖ” recommends inspection |
| **Orangeburg** (tar-paper composite) | 1945-1972 | Collapse-prone вҖ” recommends inspection |
| **Cast Iron** | 1900-1980 | Corrosion-prone вҖ” recommends inspection |
| **PVC/ABS (modern)** | 1980+ | Robust 50+ year lifespan |

**Five universal failure-mode liabilities**:
1. **Tree-root intrusion** (leading cause) вҶ’ sewage backup + habitability breach (Hilder v. St. Peter) + emergency relocation duty
2. **Aged clay or Orangeburg pipe collapse** вҶ’ emergency $5K-$15K trenchless CIPP lining or $15K-$40K open-cut
3. **Stormwater inflow and infiltration (I&I)** вҶ’ municipal violation + consent-decree enforcement under Clean Water Act 33 U.S.C. В§ 1342 NPDES
4. **Cross-connection with storm drain** вҶ’ illicit discharge violation + municipal cleanup cost-recovery
5. **Failure to obtain POS Compliance Certificate** (EBMUD/Berkeley regions) вҶ’ escrow delay + $4,500 EBMUD deposit + close-of-escrow inability

**Inspection methodology**: video camera scope from cleanout + hydro-jet cleaning + air-pressure test + dye/smoke test for cross-connection detection ($400-$1,000 per inspection); 5-year general best-practice interval; sewage backup IIED exposure routinely exceeds $100K-$1M per tenant claim.

**EBMUD ownership boundary rule**: Property owner responsible for ENTIRE lateral from home to public sewer main, **EXCEPT** Alameda and Albany where responsibility ends at property line or curbside cleanout.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-sewer-lateral-responsibility`. В§ 1342 NPDES); **cross-connection storm drain defect observed** (illicit discharge + dye-test); **EBMUD POS sale no certificate POS required** ($4,500 deposit + Time Extension); **EBMUD POS remodel over $100K POS required**; **EBMUD POS water meter change POS required**; **EBMUD POS with valid certificate compliant**; **EBMUD POS with time-extension deposit compliant**; **Berkeley no EBMUD POS certificate required** (separate program вҖ” Chapter 17.16); **vitrified clay recommends inspection**; **Orangeburg recommends inspection**; **cast iron recommends inspection**; **last inspection over 60 months inspection recommended**; **modern PVC within 60 months compliant**; **EBMUD jurisdiction cites Alameda + Albany exception**; **Berkeley jurisdiction cites Chapter 17.16 + November 3, 2014**; **MA jurisdiction cites c. 83 В§ 7 + Clean Waters Act + 314 C.M.R. 12.00**; **Default jurisdiction common-law habitability** (Hilder + В§ 1941.1 + 33 U.S.C. В§ 1342 + Hilder + 478 A.2d 202 + В§ 1941.1); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins EBMUD three POS triggers** ($100K + water meter); **note pins five failure modes**; **note pins inspection methodology** (video + hydro-jet + dye/smoke + $400-$1,000); **note pins aged lateral materials** (clay 1900-1965 + Orangeburg 1945-1972 + cast iron); **note pins companion modules**; **EBMUD uniquely strictest POS certificate invariant**; **4-cell jurisdiction truth table**; **multiple defects stack in actions**.

`traderview-expense::rental_sex_offender_registry_notice` is the **rental property sex offender registry notice disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord include a statutory Megan's Law notice in residential rental agreements, and what restrictions apply to the landlord's use of registry information? Trader-landlord operational concern: failure to include statutory Megan's Law disclosure in lease creates per-violation penalty exposure + tenant rescission claim; landlord USE of registry information to deny tenancy or evict triggers Fair Housing Act discrimination liability. Distinct from siblings `fair_chance_housing` (criminal-background screening), `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (denial reason), `tenant_data_privacy` (general data handling).

**Three regimes** вҖ” California (mandatory lease disclosure) vs New Jersey (tier-based use restrictions) vs Default (federal SORNA + HUD disparate-impact):

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 2079.10a + Cal. Pen. Code В§ 290.46 + Cal. Gov. Code В§ 12955** вҖ” every residential rental agreement must include **exact statutory Megan's Law notice** directing tenant to www.meganslaw.ca.gov; disclosure required **BEFORE tenant signs lease** in at least **10-point type**; landlord **CANNOT use registry information to deny tenancy or evict** a registered sex offender (Fair Housing Act В§ 3604 + Cal. Gov. Code В§ 12955 violation); per-violation statutory damages + tenant rescission right available when disclosure omitted.

**New Jersey вҖ” N.J.S.A. 2C:7-21 + N.J.S.A. 2C:7-2 (NJ Megan's Law) + NJ Attorney General Guidelines + NJ Law Against Discrimination** вҖ” NJ does **NOT require landlord disclosure in lease**; registry publicly accessible via NJ State Police Sex Offender Internet Registry. Community notification three-tier framework вҖ” **Tier 1** (low risk, law enforcement only) + **Tier 2** (moderate risk, schools and community organizations) + **Tier 3** (high risk, broad public notification). Landlords prohibited from using Tier 1 / Tier 2 registry information to deny tenancy under NJ LAD; **Tier 3 broad public notice permitted** under N.J.S.A. 2C:7-8.

**Default вҖ” federal Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 (SORNA, 42 USC В§ 16901 et seq.) + Fair Housing Act (42 USC В§ 3604) + HUD 2016 Guidance on Use of Criminal Records by Providers of Housing** вҖ” no federal mandate requiring landlord lease disclosure; state registries publicly accessible via state law enforcement. HUD 2016 disparate-impact analysis applies to blanket registry-based denials (individualized assessment required). Many states (TX, FL, IL, NY) have no specific landlord disclosure mandate.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-sex-offender-registry-notice`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA compliant passes**; **CA missing notice violation** (В§ 2079.10a); **CA disclosure after signing violation** (BEFORE tenant signs); **CA below 10-point type violation**; **CA missing website direction violation** (meganslaw.ca.gov); **CA using registry to deny Fair Housing violation** (В§ 12955 + В§ 3604); **CA tenant rescission right engaged when notice omitted**; **CA tenant rescission not engaged when notice provided**; **NJ no disclosure required compliant**; **NJ Tier 1 use to deny violation** (NJ LAD); **NJ Tier 2 use to deny violation**; **NJ Tier 3 use to deny no violation** (broad public notice permitted); **default no disclosure required compliant**; **default blanket use disparate-impact violation** (HUD 2016 Guidance); **citation pins CA / NJ / default authorities** (all three); **note pins CA exact statutory notice requirement** (meganslaw.ca.gov); **note pins CA anti-discrimination protection** (В§ 12955 + CANNOT use registry); **note pins NJ three-tier framework** (Tier 1 + Tier 2 + Tier 3); **note pins default SORNA 42 USC В§ 16901**; **note pins default HUD 2016 Guidance** (disparate-impact); **CA uniquely requires lease disclosure invariant** (NJ + default do not); **NJ tier truth table** (3-cell sweep вҖ” Tier 1 + Tier 2 violate, Tier 3 OK); **multiple CA violations stack** (2 violations); **Fair Housing violation invariant across regimes** (CA + default both engage); **rescission only engages when notice omitted AND tenant invokes invariant** (4-cell sweep).

`traderview-expense::rental_sinkhole_disclosure` is the **rental property sinkhole disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating Florida (or other karst-prone) rental property disclose past sinkhole claims, paid insurance proceeds, and known sinkhole conditions to prospective tenants and buyers? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed sinkhole history creates breach of duty under FL Statute В§ 627.7073 + Johnson v. Davis common-law material-fact disclosure doctrine + buyer rescission claim + multi-tenant constructive eviction exposure. Distinct from siblings `rental_underground_storage_tank_disclosure` (UST), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water/mold), `flood_disclosure`, `radon_disclosure`.

**Two regimes** вҖ” Florida (statutory + Johnson v. Davis) vs Default (common-law material fact + PA Seller Disclosure Law):

**Florida вҖ” FL Statute В§ 627.7073(1)(c) + В§ 627.707 + В§ 627.706 + В§ 689.25 + В§ 689.261 + Johnson v. Davis, 480 So. 2d 625 (Fla. 1985)** вҖ” В§ 627.7073(1)(c) seller of property upon which sinkhole claim has been made by seller and **PAID by insurer** must disclose to buyer **BEFORE CLOSING** (1) claim has been paid AND (2) whether or not full amount of proceeds were used to repair damage. В§ 627.707 sinkhole loss claim investigation framework вҖ” professional engineer or geologist report and certification required. В§ 627.706 sinkhole loss definition (structural damage consistent with sinkhole activity). В§ 689.25 narrow disclosure exemptions (homicide, suicide, deaths, HIV/AIDS NOT material). В§ 689.261 Florida Property Tax Disclosure Summary required at or before contract execution. **Johnson v. Davis common-law duty** to disclose all material facts not readily observable affecting property value INCLUDING sinkhole conditions REGARDLESS of whether insurance claim made.

**Default вҖ” common-law material fact doctrine + Pennsylvania Real Estate Seller Disclosure Law (68 Pa.C.S. В§ 7301 et seq.) + state-specific karst geology disclosure requirements** вҖ” no federal sinkhole disclosure mandate; other karst-prone states (TX hill country, PA limestone regions, MO, KY, TN) rely on common-law material fact doctrine. Pennsylvania Seller Disclosure Statement requires geological conditions including sinkholes. Most states do NOT impose specific landlord disclosure mandate for sinkholes (rental leases governed by common-law warranty of habitability + fraud doctrine).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-sinkhole-disclosure`. Twenty-five tests pin: **FL no claim no sale compliant**; **FL paid claim sale without disclosure violation** (В§ 627.7073(1)(c) BEFORE closing); **FL paid claim sale with disclosure compliant**; **FL known sinkhole concealment violation** (Johnson v. `traderview-expense::rental_smoke_free_housing_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional residential rental smoke-free housing disclosure framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because modern shift toward smoke-free properties for insurance discounts + tenant health protection has made smoke-free policies a routine landlord choice; failure to disclose smoking policy in lease can expose landlord to constructive-eviction claims from secondhand-smoke-affected tenants; misclassifying tobacco-product coverage creates lease enforcement disputes; HUD public housing smoke-free rule applies to all federally-funded units including LIHTC trader-landlord investments. Distinct from siblings `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO sensor framework), `rental_pesticide_application_notification` (pesticide notice), `tenant_data_privacy`, `landlord_emergency_entry_notice`.

**Five-jurisdiction statutory framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Trigger | Required Disclosure |
|--------------|--------|---------|---------------------|
| **California (private market)** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.5 (SB 332 of 2011, effective January 1, 2012) | Landlord adopts smoke-free policy | Post-2012 leases: in-lease provision specifying prohibited areas; pre-2012 leases: В§ 827 change-of-terms notice |
| **HUD Public Housing** | HUD 24 CFR В§ 965.653 + Part 965 Subpart G + Part 966 Subpart G (final rule November 30, 2016 at 81 Fed. Reg. 87430; mandatory deadline July 31, 2018) | All federally-funded public housing | Living units + interior areas + **25-FOOT outdoor perimeter** |
| **New York** | NY MDL В§ 17 + В§ 17-101 + В§ 17-179 + NY Public Health Law В§ 1399-n | Buildings with 3+ dwelling units | Written smoking policy disclosed in lease |
| **Massachusetts** | Mass. G.L. c. 270 В§ 22 + В§ 22A (Smoke-Free Workplace Law) | Multifamily buildings (2+ units) with common-area workplaces | Common-area smoking prohibition + posted signs |
| **Default** | Common-law nuisance + breach of warranty of habitability | None (statutory) | Common-law tenant remedies preserved |

Pinned by `ca_post_2012_smoke_free_with_lease_provision_compliant`, `hud_public_housing_all_three_prongs_compliant`, `ny_3_unit_building_triggers_obligation`, `ny_2_unit_building_no_obligation`, `ma_2_unit_building_engages_workplace_law`, `default_jurisdiction_no_obligation`, `jurisdiction_truth_table_five_cells`.

**California В§ 1947.5 three-prong framework**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 1947.5(a) | Landlord MAY prohibit smoking on property OR in building/portion (units, balconies, common areas) |
| В§ 1947.5(b) | Post-January 1, 2012 leases: lease MUST include provision specifying prohibited areas; carveout if lessee previously occupied |
| В§ 1947.5(c) + В§ 827 | Pre-2012 leases: prohibition is CHANGE OF TERMS OF TENANCY requiring adequate written notice |

Pinned by `ca_post_2012_smoke_free_no_lease_provision_violation`, `ca_lessee_previously_occupied_no_obligation` (carveout), `ca_pre_2012_lease_requires_change_of_terms_notice`, `ca_pre_2012_lease_with_change_of_terms_notice_compliant`, `ca_uniquely_engages_change_of_terms_procedure_invariant`. Covers cigarettes + cigars + pipes + tobacco products per Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 22950.5; e-cigarettes added effective 2017 per SB 5.

**HUD 24 CFR В§ 965.653 three-prong mandatory framework** (effective July 31, 2018):

| # | Element |
|---|---------|
| 1 | All public housing **living units** |
| 2 | All **interior areas** (hallways, rental/admin offices, community centers, daycare, laundry) |
| 3 | **Outdoor areas within 25 FEET** of public housing and administrative office buildings |

Pinned by `hud_missing_25_foot_perimeter_violation`, `hud_missing_interior_prohibition_violation`, `hud_uniquely_engages_25_foot_perimeter_invariant` (only HUD jurisdiction sets `hud_twenty_five_foot_perimeter_engaged: true`). В§ 965.653(b) PHA flexibility: designated smoking areas outside 25-foot zone OR additional smoke-free areas OR entire grounds smoke-free. В§ 965.651 "prohibited tobacco products" = cigarettes + cigars + pipes (e-cigarettes NOT federally mandated but PHAs may add). Part 966 Subpart G вҖ” single incident NOT grounds for eviction (compassionate enforcement).

**HUD perimeter rationale** вҖ” 25-foot outdoor perimeter prevents secondhand smoke from entering open windows in lower-level units and exposing tenants on lower-floor balconies/porches.

**New York MDL В§ 17 вҖ” 3+ dwelling unit threshold** вҖ” buildings with 3+ units must disclose written smoking policy specifying (1) whether smoking is permitted/prohibited/restricted; (2) common-area prohibitions per NY Public Health Law В§ 1399-n; (3) outdoor smoking-prohibition areas. Pinned by `ny_no_written_policy_violation`.

**Massachusetts G.L. c. 270 В§ 22 + В§ 22A Smoke-Free Workplace Law** вҖ” common areas of multifamily buildings constituting workplaces (lobbies, hallways, maintenance areas) are smoke-free; landlord must POST SIGNS in common areas. Boston Public Health Commission Tobacco Control Regulations add municipal overlays. Pinned by `ma_no_common_area_signs_violation`, `ma_single_family_no_obligation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-smoke-free-housing-disclosure`. Thirty-four tests pin: **CA post-2012 smoke-free with lease provision compliant**; **CA post-2012 smoke-free no lease provision violation** (В§ 1947.5(b)); **CA lessee previously occupied no obligation** (carveout); **CA pre-2012 lease requires change of terms notice** (В§ 827 + В§ 1947.5(c)); **CA pre-2012 lease with change of terms notice compliant**; **CA no policy no obligation**; **HUD public housing all three prongs compliant**; **HUD missing 25-foot perimeter violation**; **HUD missing interior prohibition violation**; **HUD non-HUD unit no obligation**; **NY 3-unit building triggers obligation**; **NY 2-unit building no obligation**; **NY no written policy violation**; **MA 2-unit building engages workplace law**; **MA single-family no obligation**; **MA no common-area signs violation**; **default jurisdiction no obligation**; **jurisdiction truth table five cells**; **HUD uniquely engages 25-foot perimeter invariant**; **CA uniquely engages change-of-terms procedure invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1947.5 + SB 332 + В§ 22950.5 + В§ 827 + 24 CFR В§ 965.651-655 + Part 965/966 Subpart G + 81 Fed. Reg. 87430 + NY MDL В§ 17/17-101/17-179 + В§ 1399-n + Mass. c. 270 В§ 22/22A); **note pins CA landlord authority**; **note pins CA lease disclosure**; **note pins CA change of terms**; **note pins CA e-cigarette 2017 SB 5**; **note pins HUD three-prong framework**; **note pins HUD 25-foot perimeter rationale**; **note pins HUD PHA flexibility**; **note pins HUD prohibited tobacco definition**; **note pins HUD compassionate enforcement**; **note pins NY MDL 3-unit threshold**; **note pins MA Smoke-Free Workplace Law**; **note pins default nuisance/habitability**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` is the **rental property swimming pool drain safety compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating a multifamily building with pool or spa comply with federal Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGB Act) drain cover + anti-entrapment requirements? Trader-landlord operational concern: multifamily rental pools are treated as "public pools" under VGB Act; non-compliant drain covers create per-incident strict liability + CPSC civil penalty exposure + wrongful death tort liability. Distinct from siblings `swimming_pool_safety` (general pool fencing/barrier framework), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` (CO), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural).

**Three regimes** вҖ” Federal VGB Act + California (VGB + 5-foot fence) + Florida (VGB + DOH recertification):

**Federal вҖ” Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act of 2007 (Pub. L. 110-140 EISA Title 14, eff. December 19, 2008; 15 USC В§В§ 8001-8008)** вҖ” applies to ALL **public pools and spas** including **apartment complexes**, hotels, schools, community centers (treats multifamily rentals as public). All pools/spas must have **ASME/ANSI A112.19.8-2007 drain covers** (now **ANSI/APSP/ICC-16** successor standard per 2019 CPSC rule). Single-drain pools/spas (other than unblockable drains) must employ at least ONE additional anti-entrapment safeguard: (1) separated drain systems; (2) safety vacuum release systems (SVRS); (3) suction-limiting vent systems; (4) gravity drainage systems; (5) automatic pump shutoff; or (6) equivalent system approved by CPSC. CPSC civil penalties up to **$120,000 per violation** per 15 USC В§ 2069. Private residential pools NOT covered by federal VGB Act mandate.

**California вҖ” Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 116064.1 + В§ 115922 + В§ 116064.4 (Pool Safety Act + 2008 SB 442 + Cal. SB 442 of 2017)** вҖ” adopts VGB Act drain cover + anti-entrapment standards for public pools. Adds **5-foot pool fence + self-latching gate** requirement under В§ 115922 (CA-unique structural requirement). California Building Code Ch. 31B aquatic facility chapter incorporates ANSI/APSP/ICC-16 by reference. В§ 116064.4 CDPH (state health department) enforcement authority.

**Florida вҖ” Florida Building Code В§ 454.2.17 + FL Statute В§ 514.0315 (Florida Public Swimming Pool and Bathing Place Safety Act)** вҖ” adopts VGB Act federal standards for public pools. Florida-specific drain cover inspection and **recertification cycle** (typically every 5 years). FL Department of Health enforcement under FL Statute В§ 514.0315. FL Pool Safety Act adds residential pool fencing requirements (separate from VGB-covered).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-swimming-pool-drain-safety`. Thirty-one tests pin: **federal clean compliant**; **federal private residential not covered** (VGB Act exclusion); **federal non-compliant drain cover violation** (ASME/ANSI A112.19.8-2007); **federal single drain with secondary compliant**; **federal single drain without secondary violation** (В§ 8003(c)(1)(A) + SVRS); **federal separated drain systems adequate**; **federal gravity drainage system adequate**; **federal automatic pump shutoff adequate**; **federal CPSC approved equivalent adequate**; **federal CPSC penalty at $120K cap compliant**; **federal CPSC penalty above $120K cap violation** (В§ 2069); **CA clean compliant**; **CA no pool fence violation** (В§ 115922 + 5-foot); **CA non-compliant drain cover violation** (В§ 116064.1 + ANSI/APSP/ICC-16); **FL clean compliant**; **FL no recertification violation** (В§ 514.0315); **FL non-compliant drain cover violation** (В§ 454.2.17); **citation pins federal / CA / FL authorities** (all three); **note pins VGB December 19, 2008 effective date**; **note pins apartment complexes in scope**; **note pins six secondary protection options**; **note pins $120K CPSC penalty cap**; **note pins CA 5-foot fence**; **note pins FL recertification cycle**; **CA uniquely requires 5-foot pool fence invariant**; **FL uniquely requires DOH recertification invariant**; **secondary protection truth table 7 cells** (6 protect, 1 None fails); **private residential pool not covered invariant across regimes** (3-regime sweep); **multiple federal violations stack** (3 violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_underground_storage_tank_disclosure` is the **rental property underground storage tank (UST) disclosure compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating a property with an active, inactive, or abandoned-in-place underground storage tank (home heating oil tank, industrial chemical tank, abandoned gas station tank) disclose UST presence to prospective and existing tenants and prospective buyers? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed UST contamination creates joint-and-several CERCLA Superfund liability + state environmental cleanup orders + multi-million-dollar remediation cost exposure + buyer rescission claim. UST contamination is one of the largest single landlord-exposure categories. Distinct from siblings `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (water/mold), `radon_disclosure`, `asbestos_disclosure`, `mold_disclosure`.

**Four regimes** вҖ” Federal RCRA Subtitle I vs California Chapter 6.7 + В§ 25288 vs Florida Johnson v. Davis vs New Jersey (strictest):

**Federal вҖ” RCRA Subtitle I (42 USC В§ 6991 et seq.) + 40 CFR Part 280** вҖ” federal regulatory program for petroleum and CERCLA hazardous substance USTs containing **more than 10% below ground surface**; **heating oil USTs serving on-premises residential** buildings EXEMPT from federal regulation but subject to state regulation; 2015 EPA final rule expanded UST integrity testing + secondary containment requirements; enforced through delegated state UST programs; federal CERCLA joint-and-several liability for contamination remains regardless of UST regulatory exemption.

**California вҖ” Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 25280-25299.8 + В§ 25288 + Chapter 6.7** вҖ” California State Water Resources Control Board administers UST program through Certified Unified Program Agencies (CUPAs); **annual inspection required** of all USTs including abandoned USTs (В§ 25288); Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1102 et seq. Transfer Disclosure Statement (TDS) must disclose UST on property sale; California residential leases do NOT require specific UST disclosure (no statutory affirmative duty) but common-law fraud claim available if landlord conceals known UST contamination.

**Florida вҖ” FL Statute В§В§ 376.30-376.317 + В§ 689.25 + Johnson v. Davis, 480 So. 2d 625 (Fla. 1985)** вҖ” FL Storage Tank System Rules administered by FL Department of Environmental Protection (FDEP); В§ 376.317 petroleum UST regulation supersedes conflicting state or local laws; FL В§ 689.25 + **Johnson v. Davis common-law duty** вҖ” home sellers must disclose all material facts not readily observable affecting property value, INCLUDING UST presence and contamination; FL Petroleum Liability Insurance and Restoration Program вҖ” state-funded cleanup for eligible USTs.

**New Jersey вҖ” N.J.A.C. 7:14B + NJ Seller's Property Condition Disclosure Statement** вҖ” **STRICTEST among comparators**. NJ Seller's Property Condition Disclosure Statement **explicitly requires disclosure of UST presence** вҖ” whether **active, inactive, or abandoned in place** вҖ” including tanks landlord knows about but hasn't addressed; buyer/tenant may request documentation (photos + soil analyses + NJDEP contractor certifications) to verify proper removal or abandonment; NJ Spill Compensation and Control Act (N.J.S.A. 58:10-23.11) вҖ” strict joint-and-several liability for petroleum contamination; NJEDA Petroleum Underground Storage Tank Program (PUSTP) provides up to **$250,000 grant** for residential heating oil tank removal/cleanup.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-underground-storage-tank-disclosure`. Thirty-one tests pin: **federal clean compliant**; **federal no inspection violation** (40 CFR Part 280); **federal heating oil residential exempt**; **federal removed with documentation no inspection required**; **CA clean compliant**; **CA no annual inspection violation** (В§ 25288); **CA property sold without TDS violation** (В§ 1102); **CA property sold with TDS compliant**; **CA known concealment fraud violation**; **FL clean compliant**; **FL property sold without Johnson v. Davis violation** (В§ 689.25); **FL known concealment violation** (Johnson v. Davis common-law); **NJ clean compliant**; **NJ no property disclosure violation** (Seller's Property Condition Disclosure Statement); **NJ abandoned in place must be disclosed**; **NJ known concealment strict liability** (Spill Compensation and Control Act 58:10-23.11); **citation pins federal / CA / FL / NJ authorities** (all four); **note pins federal heating oil residential exemption**; **note pins CA В§ 25288 annual inspection**; **note pins FL Johnson v. Davis doctrine**; **note pins NJ strictest three tank statuses** (active + inactive + abandoned in place); **note pins NJ unregulated heating oil $250K grant** (NJEDA PUSTP); **no tank no violations regardless of inputs**; **NJ uniquely requires property disclosure regardless of sale invariant** (only NJ requires lease-stage disclosure); **CA uniquely requires annual inspection invariant** (federal applies only to non-residential heating oil; FL does not specifically require annual); **known concealment invariant across regimes** (CA + FL + NJ all engage fraud); **tank status truth table 5 cells** (Active + Inactive + AbandonedInPlace + RemovedWithDocumentation + NoTank); **multiple NJ violations stack** (2 violations).

`traderview-expense::landlord_retaliation_damages` is the **landlord retaliation damages math module** вҖ” how much may a tenant recover when the landlord has retaliated against the tenant's exercise of statutory rights or good-faith complaint? Distinct from sibling module `retaliation_windows` (timing-based rebuttable-presumption windows); this module focuses on the DAMAGES RECOVERY math after retaliation has been established. Trader-critical for landlord-investors operating across multiple jurisdictions вҖ” state divergence ranges from common-law actual damages to multi-month rent statutory minimums plus punitive damages.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Damages formula | Attorney fees | Presumption window | Rebuttal standard |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|---------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942.5(h) | Actual + **$100-$2,000 punitive per act** (malice req'd) | Statutory | 180 days | Preponderance |
| Massachusetts | G.L. c. 186 В§ 18 | **max(1 month rent floor, actual)**; ceiling = max(3 mo, actual) | Statutory | 6 months | **Clear and convincing** |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 2A:42-10.10 | Actual damages + injunctive/equitable | Statutory | ~90 days | Preponderance (case-by-case) |
| Default | Common law + Restatement (2d) of Property В§ 14.8 | Actual damages only | Only if lease permits | n/a | n/a |

**California В§ 1942.5(h) вҖ” punitive damages $100-$2,000 per retaliatory act**. When tenant shows fraud, oppression, or malice, court may award punitive damages clamped between $100 floor and $2,000 ceiling per retaliatory act, multiplied by the count of separate retaliatory acts. Without malice showing, only actual damages recoverable. Statutory attorney's fees + costs available. 180-day presumption window under В§ 1942.5(a). Pinned by `ca_actual_only_when_no_malice`, `ca_actual_plus_punitive_when_malice_shown` ($5K actual + 1 Г— $2K = $7K), `ca_multiple_retaliatory_acts_multiply_punitive` (5 acts Г— $2K = $10K), `ca_punitive_per_act_clamped_to_max` (over $2K вҶ’ $2K), `ca_punitive_per_act_clamped_to_min` (under $100 вҶ’ $100), `ca_180_day_presumption_window`, and `ca_punitive_clamped_truth_table` (5-cell sweep).

**Massachusetts В§ 18 вҖ” strongest rebuttal standard in the U.S.** Statutory damages = MAX(1 month's rent floor, actual damages); effective ceiling = MAX(3 months' rent, actual). 6-month presumption window. Rebuttal requires CLEAR AND CONVINCING evidence (highest civil standard; vs. preponderance in CA/NJ). Waiver of В§ 18 in any lease void and unenforceable. Pinned by `ma_actual_below_one_month_floored_at_one_month` (1-month floor wins on low actual), `ma_actual_above_three_months_uses_actual` (10K actual wins over 3mo cap), `ma_actual_between_floor_and_three_months_uses_actual`, `ma_clear_and_convincing_rebuttal_flagged`, `ma_6_month_presumption_window`, and `only_ma_requires_clear_and_convincing_invariant` (4-regime sweep).

**New Jersey В§ 2A:42-10.10 вҖ” Reprisal Law**. Actual damages + injunctive/equitable relief. Statutory presumption of retaliation when reprisal follows protected tenant activity; landlord may rebut by showing commencement INDEPENDENT of protected activity. Applies to all rental dwellings EXCEPT owner-occupied premises with вүӨ 2 rental units. Pinned by `nj_actual_damages_only_plus_attorney_fees` and `nj_90_day_presumption_window`.

**Default common-law analysis**. Actual damages only. Attorney's fees recoverable only if lease expressly permits. Most states without explicit retaliation statutes follow this default. Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 14.8 governs jurisdiction-specific common-law retaliation analysis. Pinned by `default_actual_only_no_attorney_fees` and `default_no_presumption_window`.

**Rebuttal short-circuit** вҖ” when landlord successfully rebuts the presumption (regime-specific standard), no damages recoverable under this module's regime-specific math; common-law fallback claims (constructive eviction, IIED) remain available. Pinned by `rebutted_presumption_no_damages_all_regimes` (4-regime sweep).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_ca_engages_punitive_damages_invariant` (4-regime sweep вҖ” only CA generates punitive damages); `only_ma_floors_at_one_month_rent_invariant` (only MA has a statutory rent-based floor on damages); `only_ma_requires_clear_and_convincing_invariant` (only MA imposes the highest civil rebuttal standard); `statutory_attorney_fees_three_regimes_invariant` (CA + MA + NJ all provide statutory attorney fees; Default does not); `penalty_constants_invariant` (CA $100-$2,000 / 180 day; MA 1-3 months / 6 month; NJ 90 day).

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references `retaliation_windows` (timing-based presumption windows), `landlord_harassment` (affirmative-harassment civil penalty statutes), `lockout_penalties` (lockout-specific damages), `landlord_possession_delivery` (lease-commencement delivery duty + URLTA damages), and clarifies that this module covers DAMAGES RECOVERY math after retaliation finding. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-retaliation-damages`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA actual only when no malice**; **CA actual plus punitive when malice shown** ($7K total); **CA multiple retaliatory acts multiply punitive** (5 Г— $2K = $10K); **CA punitive per act clamped to max** ($2K ceiling); **CA punitive per act clamped to min** ($100 floor); **CA 180-day presumption window**; **MA actual below one month floored at one month**; **MA actual above three months uses actual**; **MA actual between floor and three months uses actual**; **MA clear and convincing rebuttal flagged**; **MA 6-month presumption window**; **NJ actual damages only plus attorney fees**; **NJ 90-day presumption window**; **default actual only no attorney fees**; **default no presumption window**; **rebutted presumption no damages all regimes** (4-regime sweep); **only CA engages punitive damages invariant** (4-regime sweep); **only MA floors at one month rent invariant**; **only MA requires clear and convincing invariant**; **statutory attorney fees three regimes invariant** (CA + MA + NJ vs Default); **CA punitive clamped truth table** (5-cell sweep over input vs output); **penalty constants invariant** (CA $100-$2K / 180 day; MA 1-3 mo / 6 mo; NJ 90 day); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1942.5(h) + В§ 1942.5(a) + c. 186 В§ 18 + В§ 2A:42-10.10 + В§ 2A:42-10.11 + Restatement В§ 14.8 + clear-and-convincing); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `retaliation_windows` + `landlord_harassment` + `lockout_penalties` + `landlord_possession_delivery` + DAMAGES RECOVERY focus); **defensive negative rent clamped**; **defensive negative actual damages clamped**; **defensive negative retaliation acts clamped**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_self_help_eviction_prohibition` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord self-help eviction prohibition framework module** вҖ” among the steepest direct-exposure statutes in residential landlord-tenant law, because every state criminalizes or imposes per-day liquidated-damage liability for utility shutoff, lock changes, or property removal performed WITHOUT a court order and marshal's writ of possession. Trader-landlord critical because the trader-landlord pattern (out-of-state owner + non-paying tenant + lost rental income + emotional decision to "just turn off the water") matches precisely the fact pattern these statutes were designed to deter. Distinct from siblings `landlord_retaliation_damages` (retaliation against tenant for exercising rights), `landlord_emergency_entry_notice` (entry without notice), `landlord_lien_prohibition` (landlord lien on tenant property), `tenant_relocation_assistance` (court-ordered relocation).

**Five-jurisdiction statutory damage comparison**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Damages Formula | Unique Features |
|--------------|--------|-----------------|------------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 789.3 | **$100/day + $250 minimum + actual + attorney fees** | INTENT TO TERMINATE OCCUPANCY element required |
| **New York** | RPL В§ 235 + RPAPL В§ 853 + RPAPL В§ 768 | **TREBLE damages + attorney fees** | CLASS A MISDEMEANOR criminal exposure (max 1 year imprisonment) |
| **Florida** | Fla. Stat. В§ 83.67 | **Greater of actual/consequential OR 3 MONTHS' RENT + attorney fees + costs** | IRREPARABLE HARM for injunctive relief; SUBSEQUENT non-contemporaneous violations SEPARATELY recoverable |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.0081(h) + В§ 92.008 | **Actual + $1,000 + 1 MONTH'S RENT (less actual) + attorney fees** | Allows tenant abandonment exception + lease bona-fide-repairs clause exception |
| **Default** | Common law wrongful eviction tort | **Actual damages + PUNITIVE in some states** | Breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment + tortious interference |

Pinned by `california_utility_shutoff_with_intent_violation_engaged`, `california_10_day_violation_damages_equal_1000_dollars` ($100/day Г— 10 days = $1,000 = 100,000 cents), `california_2_day_violation_floor_locks_at_250_minimum` ($250 minimum overrides $200 day-damages floor), `new_york_treble_damages_engaged_class_a_misdemeanor` ($500,000 actual Г— 3 = $15,000), `florida_3_month_rent_floor_overrides_lower_actual_damages` ($2K rent Г— 3 = $6K floor > $1K actual), `florida_higher_actual_damages_overrides_3_month_rent_floor` ($10K actual > $6K floor), `texas_damages_formula_actual_plus_1000_plus_net_one_month` ($500 + $1,000 + $1,500 = $3,000), `default_jurisdiction_actual_damages_only`.

**California intent element вҖ” Civ. Code В§ 789.3** вҖ” "with intent to terminate occupancy" is a required element; mere utility lapse from non-payment of bill by landlord is insufficient if not intended to force tenant out. Pinned by `california_no_intent_no_violation`.

**New York criminal exposure вҖ” only NY among the framework** вҖ” pinned by `criminal_exposure_only_in_ny_invariant` (CA + FL + TX + Default all lack criminal exposure).

**New York treble uniquely highest multiplier** вҖ” pinned by `ny_treble_uniquely_highest_multiplier_invariant`: only NY engages `treble_damages_engaged: true`.

**Florida irreparable harm engages injunctive relief** вҖ” pinned by `florida_irreparable_harm_engages_injunctive_relief` (only FL among the framework engages `injunctive_relief_available: true`).

**Court eviction judgment + marshal's writ of possession is the ONLY lawful pathway** across all five jurisdictions вҖ” pinned by `note_pins_court_order_only_lawful_pathway_invariant`. Pinned by `california_court_order_obtained_no_violation`, `ca_court_judgment_no_violation_even_with_intent`.

**Action prohibitions across all five jurisdictions**:
1. Utility shutoff (water, heat, light, electricity, gas, telephone, elevator, refrigeration)
2. Lockout / access denial (lock change, bootlock, door/window removal)
3. Personal property removal from dwelling
4. Structural removal (doors, locks, roof, walls, windows)

Pinned by `jurisdiction_action_truth_table` (5 jurisdictions Г— 4 action types = 20-cell sweep вҖ” every cell engages violation).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-self-help-eviction-prohibition`. `traderview-expense::landlord_security_device_obligations` is the **mandatory landlord-provided security devices obligations module** вҖ” when does a residential landlord have an affirmative statutory duty to install and maintain locks, deadbolts, door viewers, and sliding-door security devices? Distinct from siblings `lock_change_between_tenancies` (between-tenancy lock changes), `dv_survivor_lock_change` (DV-survivor lock changes), and `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (biometric smart lock refusal right). Trader-landlord operational concern in TX, CA, and other states with explicit security-device statutes.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Statutory damages |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| Texas Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.151 et seq. (Subchapter D) | Most detailed framework | One month's rent + $500 + actual damages + attorney fees + court costs (В§В§ 92.164, 92.165) |
| California Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1941.1, 1941.3 | Required security devices + implied warranty | Rent withholding + repair-and-deduct + lease termination |
| Default | Hilder + Javins common-law implied warranty | Negligence-per-se for break-in / assault claims |

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.151 et seq. (Subchapter D Security Devices)**. Most detailed statutory framework in the country. **В§ 92.153(a)** вҖ” landlord MUST install on each exterior door: (1) **keyless bolting device** (operable only from inside) AND (2) **door viewer**. **В§ 92.153(b)** вҖ” keyed deadbolts and doorknob locks NOT required on all exterior doors as long as **ONE DOOR has both keyed and keyless deadbolts** (other doors require only keyless deadbolts). **В§ 92.153(c)** вҖ” for dwellings whose construction was completed on or after **September 1, 1993**, landlord MUST install on each exterior sliding glass door: **sliding door pin lock OR sliding door handle latch OR sliding door security bar**. **В§ 92.153(d)** вҖ” security devices MUST be installed at **LANDLORD'S EXPENSE**. **В§ 92.153(e)** вҖ” security devices MUST BE **OPERABLE** throughout the time a tenant is in possession. **В§В§ 92.164, 92.165** вҖ” tenant remedies: **one month's rent + $500 + actual damages + attorney fees + court costs**.

**Pre-1993 construction sliding-door carve-out**. Dwellings whose construction was completed before September 1, 1993 are NOT subject to the sliding-door security-device requirement (effective date for new construction trigger). Pinned by `tx_pre_1993_construction_no_sliding_door_requirement`.

**California вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§В§ 1941.1, 1941.3 (Required Security Devices)**. Landlord MUST install and maintain (a) operable **deadbolt** locking system on main swinging entry doors, (b) operable **window security or locking devices** on accessible windows, (c) operable **locking mechanisms on swinging garage doors** (if applicable). В§ 1941.3(b) вҖ” devices must be installed and maintained in **WORKING ORDER** throughout tenancy at **LANDLORD'S EXPENSE**. Failure constitutes breach of implied warranty of habitability under В§ 1941.1 with attendant tenant remedies (rent withholding, repair-and-deduct, lease termination).

**Default вҖ” common-law implied warranty of habitability**. Most states apply Hilder v. St. Peter, 144 Vt. 150 (1984) + Javins v. First National Realty Corp., 428 F.2d 1071 (D.C. Cir. 1970) framework requiring landlord to maintain unit in reasonably safe and habitable condition INCLUDING functional locks on doors.
