No statutory enumeration of specific security devices required. Common-law negligence + premises liability framework reaches landlord failure to provide reasonably secure entry.

**TX unique keyless bolting requirement invariant** вҖ” only TX specifically requires keyless bolting + door viewer on EACH exterior door. CA + Default impose only the broader deadbolt + working-order requirement. Pinned by `tx_unique_keyless_bolting_requirement_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-security-device-obligations`. Thirty-one tests pin: **TX full compliance passes**; **TX missing keyless bolting violates** (В§ 92.153(a)); **TX missing door viewer violates** (В§ 92.153(a)); **TX missing keyed deadbolt pair violates** (В§ 92.153(b) ONE door); **TX missing sliding door device violates after 1993** (В§ 92.153(c)); **TX pre-1993 construction no sliding door requirement** (effective date carve-out); **TX no sliding door no requirement**; **TX tenant expense violates** (В§ 92.153(d) LANDLORD'S EXPENSE); **TX non-operable violates** (В§ 92.153(e) OPERABLE throughout tenancy); **TX statutory damages note present** (В§В§ 92.164, 92.165 one month rent + $500 + actual + attorney fees + court costs); **TX definitions note describes door viewer** (В§ 92.151); **TX citation pins Subchapter D sections**; **CA full compliance passes**; **CA missing deadbolt violates** (В§ 1941.3(a)(1)); **CA missing window security violates** (В§ 1941.3(a)(2)); **CA missing garage lock violates when garage** (В§ 1941.3(a)(3)); **CA no garage no garage lock required**; **CA non-operable violates** (В§ 1941.3(b) WORKING ORDER + LANDLORD'S EXPENSE); **CA implied warranty note present** (В§ 1941.1); **CA citation pins В§В§ 1941.1 + 1941.3**; **default compliant when operable**; **default non-operable violates common-law** (negligence-per-se); **default Hilder + Javins note present**; **default citation references common-law cases**; **TX unique keyless bolting requirement invariant** (3-regime sweep); **three regimes routed correctly**; **TX all violations simultaneous** (6 violations); **habitability breach engages with any violation**; **habitability breach not engaged when compliant**; **TX clean compliance no violations**; **CA clean compliance no violations**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_repair_response_timeframe` is the **mandatory landlord-paid written-response timeframe for tenant repair requests module** вҖ” when tenant submits a written repair request, how many days does the landlord have to respond and act? Distinct from siblings `repair_and_deduct` (tenant self-help when landlord doesn't act), `habitability_remedies` (broader warranty framework), and `landlord_security_device_obligations` (specific security-device duties). Failure to respond within statutory timeframe exposes landlord to tenant rent abatement, repair-and-deduct, and lease termination.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Emergency | Urgent (appliance) | Ordinary |
|--------|-----------|----------------------|----------|
| Texas Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.052(d) | 24h | 168h | 168h (7 days) |
| Illinois Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-110(d) | 72h | 336h | 336h (14 days) |
| Washington RCW 59.18.070 | 24h | 72h | 240h (10 days) |
| Default common-law | Reasonable | Reasonable | Reasonable |

**Texas вҖ” Tex. Prop. Code В§В§ 92.052(d) + 92.056 + 92.0563**. Landlord must make diligent effort to repair within REASONABLE TIME presumed at **7 days** (168 hours) for normal conditions or as soon as practicable for emergency conditions affecting health/safety. **В§ 92.0563 repair-and-deduct cap** greater of one month's rent or $500 + actual damages + attorney fees + court costs.

**Illinois вҖ” Chicago RLTO В§ 5-12-110(d)** (Chicago Residential Landlord and Tenant Ordinance). Landlord must respond to tenant written repair request within **14 days** (336 hours) for ordinary repairs or **72 hours** for emergency conditions affecting health/safety. Tenant may withhold rent pro-rata or terminate lease for noncompliance.

**Washington вҖ” RCW 59.18.070**. Tiered response framework based on severity: **24 hours** for no heat/no hot water/no electricity/no water/imminent threat; **72 hours** for refrigerator/range/major appliances/plumbing fixtures; **10 days (240 hours)** for all other defective conditions. Most tiered of the four regimes. Pinned by `wa_uniquely_tiered_three_levels_invariant`.

**Default вҖ” common-law implied warranty of habitability**. Hilder v. St. Peter, 144 Vt. 150 (1984) + Javins v. First National Realty Corp., 428 F.2d 1071 (D.C. Cir. 1970) reasonable-time-to-cure framework. No specific statutory hour/day limits.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-repair-response-timeframe`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **TX ordinary required hours 168**; **TX emergency required hours 24**; **TX ordinary 169 hours no repair violates** (В§ 92.052(d) + three remedies engaged); **TX emergency 25 hours no repair violates**; **TX boundary 168 hours compliant** (вүӨ boundary); **TX В§ 92.0563 repair-and-deduct cap note present** (one month's rent OR $500); **TX citation pins В§В§ 92.052(d) + 92.056 + 92.0563**; **IL ordinary required hours 336**; **IL emergency required hours 72**; **IL ordinary 337 hours no repair violates** (В§ 5-12-110(d)); **IL citation pins Chicago RLTO**; **WA emergency required hours 24**; **WA urgent required hours 72**; **WA ordinary required hours 240**; **WA emergency 25 hours no repair violates** (RCW 59.18.070); **WA citation pins RCW 59.18.070**; **default no specific timeframe compliant always**; **default Hilder + Javins note present**; **four regimes routed correctly**; **WA uniquely tiered three levels invariant** (24/72/240); **no written request no violation**; **repair completed within timeframe no violation**; **severity tier truth table per regime** (3-tier sweep TX + WA); **timeframe exceeded engages all three remedies** (repair-and-deduct + rent abatement + lease termination); **timeframe within no remedies**; **TX clean no violations**; **IL clean no violations**; **WA clean no violations**; **default clean no violations**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_tenant_recording_consent` is the **audio recording consent for landlord-tenant interactions** module вҖ” federal Wiretap Act (18 U.S.C. В§ 2511) floor overlaid with state-specific all-party consent regimes. Trader-landlord concern when recording meetings with tenants (eviction discussions, repair complaints, lease-renewal negotiations); tenant concern when recording landlord interactions for evidentiary use. Distinct from `tenant_data_privacy` (broader data-handling framework), `landlord_harassment` (post-event remedies), and `security_camera_disclosure` (video surveillance).

**Two-regime framework**:

| Regime | States | Consent requirement |
|--------|--------|----------------------|
| **All-Party Consent** | CA, DE, FL, IL, MD, MA, MT, NV, NH, PA, WA (11) | Both/all parties to private conversation must consent |
| **One-Party Consent** | Federal 18 U.S.C. В§ 2511(2)(d) + 39 states + DC | At least one party consents (recorder usually qualifies) |

**Two universal carve-outs** override the regime analysis:

| Carve-out | Rule | Authority |
|-----------|------|-----------|
| **Third-Party Device** | Landlord installs device to record conversations to which landlord is NOT a party вҶ’ violates 18 U.S.C. В§ 2511 regardless of state regime | One-party consent protects a party, not a stranger |
| **In-Unit Recording** | Audio inside rental unit without all-party consent вҶ’ violates federal + state law due to tenant's reasonable expectation of privacy | Universal |

**Federal penalties** (18 U.S.C. В§ 2511(4) + В§ 2520(c)): up to 5 years imprisonment + $250,000 individual fine; civil damages actual OR $10,000 statutory minimum, whichever greater.

**Federal-state split is the load-bearing distinction.** A landlord in NY (one-party state) recording her own meeting with a tenant is lawful вҖ” but the same landlord in CA (all-party state) must obtain tenant consent. The federal floor is satisfied by one-party consent, but state law adds the all-party requirement in 11 states. Pinned by `one_party_consent_with_at_least_one_consent_lawful`, `all_party_consent_with_only_one_party_unlawful_state` (federal OK but state violated вҖ” federal_criminal_exposure remains false, but state_lawful = false), `fed_one_party_satisfied_state_all_party_violated_split`, `all_party_consent_with_all_parties_lawful` (both federal + state satisfied).

**Third-party device rule is FEDERAL вҖ” applies in all states regardless of state consent regime.** Pinned by `third_party_device_violates_federal_regardless_of_state` (2-regime sweep confirms both AllParty and OneParty regimes yield unlawful when recording party is not a party вҖ” even with all-parties-consented and one-party-consented set to true), `third_party_device_carveout_persists_with_all_parties_consenting`, `third_party_device_dominates_public_space_check` (public-space carve-out runs first, salvaging this scenario).

**In-unit recording requires all-party consent universally.** Pinned by `in_unit_recording_without_all_party_consent_violates_both` (one-party consent in a one-party state still violates), `in_unit_recording_with_all_party_consent_lawful`, `in_unit_recording_with_one_party_consent_violates_in_one_party_state` (regression вҖ” in-unit overrides regime).

**Public space carve-out is the strongest defense.** Recording in a public space with no reasonable expectation of privacy is lawful regardless of regime вҖ” no federal Wiretap Act applicability + no state law violation. Pinned by `public_space_no_privacy_expectation_always_lawful` (2-regime sweep confirms both regimes permit recording).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-tenant-recording-consent`. Twenty-four tests pin: **one-party consent with at least one consent lawful**; **one-party consent with no consent unlawful** (criminal + $10K civil); **all-party consent with only one party unlawful state**; **all-party consent with all parties lawful**; **third-party device violates federal regardless of state** (2-regime sweep); **in-unit recording without all-party consent violates both**; **in-unit recording with all-party consent lawful**; **public space no privacy expectation always lawful** (2-regime sweep); **federal criminal exposure only when recording unlawful federally**; **federal criminal penalty note includes 5 years + $250K**; **federal civil damages note includes $10K minimum**; **all-party state note lists 11 states** (CA / DE / FL / IL / MD / MA / MT / NV / NH / PA / WA); **one-party state note describes federal floor** (39 states + DC); **citation pins federal В§ 2511(2)(d)/(4) + В§ 2520(c) + all 11 state statutes**; **third-party device dominates public space check** (public-space-no-privacy carve-out runs first); **federal one-party satisfied + state all-party violated split**; **federal one-party unsatisfied no civil damages when lawful**; **all-party consent no parties consented unlawful both sides**; **third-party device carveout persists with all parties consenting** (one-party consent does not protect a stranger to the conversation); **note describes civil damages only when federal violation**; **in-unit recording with one-party consent violates in one-party state** (in-unit overrides regime); **one-party consent state does not engage all-party note**; **Nevada inclusion in state list confirms 11-state count**; **third-party device carries both criminal and civil exposure**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_water_heat_emergency_response` is the **multi-jurisdictional landlord water/heat emergency response timeline framework module** вҖ” distinct from sibling `habitability_remedies` (general repair-and-deduct), `landlord_repair_response_timeframe` (general written-response framework), `landlord_pest_extermination_timeline` (iter 449 pest emergencies), `heat_requirements` (winter heat compliance standards), `cooling_requirements` (summer maximum temperature), `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (disclosure not repair), `detector_requirements` (smoke/CO emergencies). Trader-landlord critical because true habitability emergencies trigger SHORT statutory response windows (24-72 hours), high daily civil penalties ($250-$500/day per apartment in NYC), and tenant constructive-eviction or rent-abatement remedies.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Key Standard |
|--------------|--------|--------------|
| **New York City** | NYC HMC В§ 27-2028 to В§ 27-2033 (Article 8); В§ 27-2115 (civil penalties) | Heat 68В°F day / 62В°F night October 1 - May 31; hot water 120В°F year-round; 24/7 emergency contact for 2+ unit buildings; $250-$500/day per apartment Class C |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1(a)(1)-(2) + В§ 1942 | Waterproofing + hot/cold water + repair-and-deduct up to one month rent (max twice in 12 months) |
| **Texas** | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.052 + В§ 92.056 | Landlord duty to repair + tenant remedies (termination + actual damages + one month rent + $500 civil penalty + attorney's fees) |
| **Default** | URLTA В§ 2.104 + В§ 4.103 + Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 5.4 | Emergency repair-and-deduct + implied warranty of habitability + constructive eviction |

**Three-tier severity framework**:

**TIER 1 IMMEDIATE EMERGENCY (24-hour response)**:
1. NO HEAT during heat season (NYC October 1 - May 31)
2. NO HOT WATER (year-round)
3. SEWAGE BACKUP into dwelling unit
4. ACTIVE FLOODING (burst pipe, foundation failure)
5. GAS LEAK
6. SMOKE / CARBON MONOXIDE DETECTOR FAILURE
7. UNSECURED EXTERIOR DOOR (post-burglary)
8. ELECTRICAL HAZARD risking fire
9. STRUCTURAL COLLAPSE risk
10. > 95В°F unit temperature with elderly/infant/medical-condition tenants

**TIER 2 URGENT (72-hour response)**: reduced hot water flow/inconsistent temperature; partial heat loss (one room); slow water leak with minor damage; refrigerator/stove failure; non-functioning toilet (with backup available); window unable to close securely.

**TIER 3 STANDARD (7-14 day response)**: cosmetic water staining; working but slow plumbing; HVAC inefficiency without complete failure.

**NYC HMC В§ 27-2029 heat season requirements**:
- October 1 - May 31
- 68В°F minimum DAYTIME (6 AM - 10 PM) when outside < 55В°F
- 62В°F minimum NIGHTTIME (10 PM - 6 AM)
- Hot water 120В°F minimum 365 days per year (NYC HMC В§ 27-2030)

**NYC HPD violation classes & civil penalties** (NYC HMC В§ 27-2115):
- **CLASS A** non-hazardous вҖ” 90-day correction; no daily penalty
- **CLASS B** hazardous вҖ” 30-day correction; $25-$250 daily per apartment
- **CLASS C** immediately hazardous вҖ” 24-hour correction; **$250-$500 daily per apartment**

**Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 repair-and-deduct (5 elements)**:
1. Tenant gives reasonable notice to landlord
2. Landlord fails to make repair within reasonable time (typically 30 days but 24-72 hours for true emergency)
3. Tenant may repair and deduct from rent up to ONE MONTH'S RENT
4. Limit twice in any 12-month period
5. Tenant may NOT use repair-and-deduct if tenant caused condition

**Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.056 tenant remedies (5 elements)**:
1. Written notice of need to repair specifying condition
2. 7-day waiting period (less in true emergency under В§ 92.056(d))
3. Tenant may TERMINATE LEASE
4. OR obtain judicial order for repair
5. OR sue for actual damages + ONE MONTH'S RENT + $500 CIVIL PENALTY + attorney's fees + court costs

**Tenant remedies under habitability warranty**:
1. **RENT WITHHOLDING** вҖ” Park West Mgmt. Corp. v. Mitchell, 47 N.Y.2d 316 (1979) in NY; Green v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. 3d 616 (1974) in CA; Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973) in MA
2. **REPAIR AND DEDUCT** вҖ” CA Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1942 up to one month rent; URLTA В§ 4.103 up to greater of $300 or 1/2 month rent
3. **RENT ABATEMENT** вҖ” 50-100% during habitability breach period
4. **CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION** вҖ” vacate with full rent relief under Restatement (Second) of Property В§ 5.4
5. **TX В§ 92.056 statutory remedies** вҖ” termination + damages + one month rent + $500 + fees

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) NYC 12-unit building loses heat November 15 (heat season); landlord 48-hour delay = HMC В§ 27-2029 Class C violation $250-$500/day Г— 12 apartments Г— 2 days = up to $12,000 maximum daily penalty; (2) CA SFR burst pipe вҖ” landlord 5-day delay вҶ’ В§ 1942 repair-and-deduct hires emergency plumber up to one month rent; (3) TX duplex sewage backup вҖ” В§ 92.056 written notice + 14-day delay вҶ’ tenant termination + damages + one month rent + $500 civil penalty + fees; (4) NYC missing 24/7 emergency contact вҖ” Class B violation $25-$250 daily + constructive eviction risk; (5) URLTA jurisdiction В§ 4.103 emergency repair-and-deduct up to greater of $300 or 1/2 month rent.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-water-heat-emergency-response`. Thirty tests pin: **NYC Tier 1 within 24 hours timely**; **NYC Tier 1 past 24 hours breach**; **Tier 2 72-hour window**; **Tier 3 7-day window**; **no repair dispatched breach**; **NYC multi-unit 24/7 contact required**; **NYC single-unit no 24/7 requirement**; **NYC missing 24/7 contact violation**; **NYC Class C civil penalty $500 per apartment** ($50,000 cents Г— 12 = $600K cents daily); **NYC Class B civil penalty $250 per apartment**; **NYC Class A no civil penalty**; **NYC heat season disclosure**; **CA repair-and-deduct breach invokes** ($1942 + one month rent + twice/12); **TX lease termination breach invokes** (В§ 92.056 + $500 + fees); **constructive eviction invokes with case law** (Park West + Green + Hemingway); **habitability breach unlocks remedies**; **NYC unlocks HPD hotline remedy**; **jurisdiction-tier truth table** (4 Г— 3 = 12 cells); **Tier 1 uniquely 24-hour invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (NYC HMC В§ 27-2028 / В§ 27-2029 / В§ 27-2030 / В§ 27-2115 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1941.1(a)(1)-(2) / В§ 1942 + Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.052 / В§ 92.056 + URLTA В§ 2.104 / В§ 4.103 + Restatement (Second) Property В§ 5.4 + Park West + Green + Hemingway); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins three emergency tiers**; **note pins NYC heat season temperatures**; **note pins NYC HPD violation classes**; **note pins NYC 24/7 emergency contact**; **note pins CA В§ 1942 5 elements**; **note pins TX В§ 92.056 5 elements**; **note pins habitability remedies with case law**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**.

`traderview-expense::last_month_rent_offset` is the **tenant security deposit-as-last-month's-rent offset right module** вҖ” when may a tenant unilaterally withhold last month's rent payment on grounds that the security deposit serves as security for unpaid rent? Distinct from sibling modules `security_deposit_caps` (statutory caps on deposit amount), `deposit_return_windows` (refund timelines), `damage_deduction_itemization` (deductions from deposit), and `security_deposit_bank_disclosure` (bank location disclosure including NY 6+ unit interest-bearing requirement). This module focuses on the TENANT-SIDE OFFSET RIGHT.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Tenant offset right | Penalty |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|---------|
| Texas | Tex. Prop. Code В§ 92.108 | **PROHIBITED** (with В§ 92.056 health/safety exception) | **TREBLE damages + attorney fees** (strongest in U.S.) |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 | **LABEL-DEPENDENT** вҖ” yes if lease labels payment as "last month's rent"; no if labeled "security" | n/a (label controls) |
| New York | N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law В§ 7-103 | **PROHIBITED** (landlord may apply at end; tenant may not unilaterally) | Common-law remedies |
| Default | Common-law separation | **PROHIBITED** absent express lease provision | Common-law remedies |

**Texas В§ 92.108 вҖ” strongest tenant-side penalty in the U.S.** Strict prohibition on tenant withholding last month's rent against security deposit. В§ 92.108(b) bad-faith violation triggers **TREBLE DAMAGES** (3Г— the rent wrongfully withheld) **plus reasonable attorney's fees** in landlord's suit to recover the rent. Single statutory exception under В§ 92.056: if tenant has properly terminated lease for landlord's failure to repair a health/safety condition, tenant may deduct deposit from rent owed. Pinned by `texas_attempted_offset_violation`, `texas_bad_faith_treble_damages_engages` (3 Г— $2K = $6K), `texas_no_bad_faith_no_treble`, `texas_92_056_repair_exception_permits_offset`, and `texas_treble_math_invariant` (4-cell sweep over rent levels with 3Г— ratio confirmation).

**California В§ 1950.5 вҖ” label-dependent treatment.** Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.5 treats advance payments as security UNLESS the lease expressly labels them as "last month's rent." If lease labels payment as "last month's rent," tenant is RELIEVED of paying that month (effectively pre-paid). If labeled as "security" or "security for last month's rent," tenant must pay last month's rent separately. AB 12 (effective July 1, 2024) capped total security deposit at 1 month's rent for most residential rentals вҖ” eliminated the traditional "first + last + security" combo for landlords. Pinned by `ca_lease_labels_as_last_month_permits_relief`, `ca_lease_labels_as_security_no_relief`, and `ca_ab12_note_present` (UX-text regression for AB 12 + July 1, 2024 effective date).

**New York GOL В§ 7-103 вҖ” trust-fund principle.** Deposit must be held in trust; not commingled with landlord's funds. Landlord may apply deposit to last month's rent at end of lease, but tenant may NOT unilaterally apply. Companion to В§ 7-103(2) 6+ unit interest-bearing requirement covered in `security_deposit_bank_disclosure` module. Pinned by `ny_attempted_offset_violation` and `ny_no_offset_no_violation`.

**Default common-law separation rule.** Security deposit and rent are separate obligations; tenant cannot unilaterally offset absent express lease provision authorizing it. Most states follow this default. Pinned by `default_attempted_offset_violation` and `default_no_offset_no_violation`.

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_texas_engages_treble_damages_invariant` (4-regime sweep вҖ” only TX has statutory treble); `only_ca_label_dependent_invariant` (4-regime sweep вҖ” only CA grants offset relief based on lease label); `texas_treble_math_invariant` (4-cell sweep at $1K/$2K/$5K/$10K monthly rent confirms 3Г— multiplier); `texas_treble_multiplier_invariant` (constant = 3).

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references the four deposit-side sibling modules and clarifies that this module covers TENANT-SIDE OFFSET RIGHT specifically (not the deposit cap, refund timeline, deduction itemization, or bank disclosure). Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/last-month-rent-offset`. Twenty tests pin: **Texas attempted offset violation**; **Texas bad-faith treble damages engages** ($6K on $2K rent); **Texas no bad faith no treble**; **Texas В§ 92.056 repair exception permits offset**; **Texas no attempted offset no violation**; **CA lease labels as last month rent permits relief**; **CA lease labels as security no relief**; **CA AB 12 note present** (effective July 1, 2024); **NY attempted offset violation** (trust-fund principle); **NY no offset no violation**; **default attempted offset violation** (common-law separation); **default no offset no violation**; **only Texas engages treble damages invariant** (4-regime sweep); **only CA label-dependent invariant** (4-regime sweep); **Texas treble math invariant** (4-cell rent-level sweep with 3Г— ratio); **Texas treble multiplier invariant** (= 3); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 92.108 + В§ 92.108(b) + В§ 92.056 + В§ 1950.5 + В§ 1950.5(b) + В§ 1950.5(c) + В§ 7-103 + В§ 7-103(1)/(2) + AB 12 + July 1, 2024); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `security_deposit_caps` + `deposit_return_windows` + `damage_deduction_itemization` + `security_deposit_bank_disclosure` + TENANT-SIDE OFFSET RIGHT); **defensive negative rent clamped**; **Texas extreme rent no overflow** ($1B Г— 3 = $3B via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::emotional_support_animal_documentation` is the **Emotional Support Animal (ESA) documentation requirements module** вҖ” when may a landlord rely on tenant-provided ESA documentation to grant a reasonable-accommodation request, and when may documentation be rejected as unreliable? Distinct from sibling module `service_animal` (ADA-covered trained service animals receive broader protection including ADA Title II/III public accommodation rights). ESAs receive housing-only protection under FHA reasonable accommodation. Both California and Florida enacted documentation reliability statutes in response to widespread fraudulent ESA-letter mills in 2020-2022.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Key requirement | Penalty for fraud |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|
| California | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 122318 (AB 468, eff. Jan 1, 2022) | 5-element practitioner test | Misdemeanor + practitioner discipline |
| Florida | Fla. Stat. В§ 760.27 (eff. July 1, 2020) | Personal knowledge + telehealth FL-licensed or out-of-state in-person visit | 2nd-degree misdemeanor + practitioner discipline |
| Default | Federal FHA + HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01 | Reasonable accommodation; reliable documentation if not readily apparent | None (state-only misdemeanor) |

**California В§ 122318 вҖ” strictest 5-element practitioner test**. Healthcare practitioners may NOT provide ESA documentation unless ALL FIVE elements are satisfied:

| Element | Requirement |
|---------|-------------|
| (1) | Valid, active license + writes all information in document |
| (2) | Licensed in the jurisdiction |
| (3) | **вүҘ 30-day** prior therapeutic relationship |
| (4) | Clinical evaluation completed |
| (5) | Misdemeanor-misrepresentation warning provided (written or verbal) |

Pinned by `ca_all_5_elements_satisfied_reliable`, `ca_missing_license_unreliable`, `ca_missing_jurisdiction_unreliable`, `ca_29_day_relationship_unreliable`, `ca_exactly_30_day_relationship_reliable` (boundary at exact 30 days), `ca_missing_evaluation_unreliable`, `ca_missing_warning_unreliable`, `ca_practitioner_discipline_on_violation`, `ca_5_element_truth_table` (5-prong sweep breaking each element one at a time), and `boundary_29_30_31_day_truth_table` (3-cell precision sweep at 29/30/31 days).

**Florida В§ 760.27 вҖ” personal knowledge + practitioner-jurisdiction test**. Practitioner / telehealth provider must (a) have PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE of the person's disability, (b) act within scope of practice, AND (c) satisfy jurisdiction requirements: telehealth providers must be Florida-licensed; out-of-state practitioners may issue documentation ONLY if they have seen the person IN PERSON at least once. Internet-only registrations, ESA registration cards, vest patches, and similar products are EXPLICITLY UNRELIABLE under В§ 760.27. Knowingly fraudulent documentation = SECOND-DEGREE MISDEMEANOR under В§ 760.27(3). Pinned by `fl_all_elements_satisfied_reliable`, `fl_missing_personal_knowledge_unreliable`, `fl_missing_telehealth_compliance_unreliable`, and `fl_internet_only_explicitly_unreliable`.

**Default вҖ” Federal FHA + HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01**. Landlord may request reliable documentation if disability or disability-related need is NOT readily apparent. HUD guidance generally disfavors internet-only therapeutic relationships but stops short of categorical rejection. Pinned by `default_licensed_practitioner_non_internet_reliable`, `default_internet_only_unreliable`, `default_unlicensed_unreliable`, and `default_fraudulent_documentation_no_state_misdemeanor` (federal regime has no state misdemeanor).

**Readily-apparent disability short-circuit вҖ” applies to all regimes**. Federal FHA + HUD Notice provide that landlord may NOT request documentation when the disability or disability-related need is READILY APPARENT. In this case, no regime's documentation requirements apply; accommodation must be granted absent other lawful basis for denial. Pinned by `readily_apparent_disability_bypasses_documentation` (CA regime, all 5 elements missing but bypassed) and `readily_apparent_applies_to_all_regimes` (3-regime sweep).

**Misdemeanor exposure only in CA and FL** вҖ” federal Default regime has no state misdemeanor for fraudulent documentation. Pinned by `ca_fraudulent_documentation_misdemeanor`, `fl_fraudulent_documentation_misdemeanor`, and `misdemeanor_exposure_only_in_ca_and_fl_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `only_ca_uses_5_element_test_invariant` (3-regime sweep вҖ” only CA tests the 5-element practitioner standard); `only_fl_uses_personal_knowledge_test_invariant` (3-regime sweep вҖ” only FL tests personal-knowledge); `misdemeanor_exposure_only_in_ca_and_fl_invariant`; `ca_30_day_threshold_constant_invariant` (= 30 days).

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result clarifies ESAs are DISTINCT from ADA service animals (covered in `service_animal` module). Service animals are trained for specific disability-related tasks and receive broader protection (ADA Title II/III + FHA + Air Carrier Access Act). ESAs receive housing-only protection. Other related modules: `reasonable_accommodation_modification` (broader FHA framework), `fair_chance_housing` (criminal-background fair housing), `pet_fees` (ESAs are EXEMPT from pet fees under FHA). Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/emotional-support-animal-documentation`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **CA all 5 elements satisfied reliable**; **CA missing license unreliable**; **CA missing jurisdiction unreliable**; **CA 29-day relationship unreliable**; **CA exactly 30-day relationship reliable** (boundary); **CA missing evaluation unreliable**; **CA missing warning unreliable**; **CA practitioner discipline on violation**; **FL all elements satisfied reliable**; **FL missing personal knowledge unreliable**; **FL missing telehealth compliance unreliable**; **FL internet-only explicitly unreliable** (В§ 760.27 categorical rejection); **default licensed practitioner non-internet reliable**; **default internet-only unreliable**; **default unlicensed unreliable**; **readily-apparent disability bypasses documentation** (short-circuit in CA regime with all 5 elements failed); **readily-apparent applies to all regimes** (3-regime sweep); **CA fraudulent documentation misdemeanor**; **FL fraudulent documentation misdemeanor**; **default fraudulent documentation no state misdemeanor**; **only CA uses 5-element test invariant** (3-regime sweep); **only FL uses personal-knowledge test invariant** (3-regime sweep); **misdemeanor exposure only in CA and FL invariant** (3-regime sweep); **CA 5-element truth table** (5-prong sweep breaking each element); **CA 30-day threshold constant invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 122318 + AB 468 + Jan 1, 2022 + В§ 122318(b)/(c) + В§ 760.27 + В§ 760.27(2)(b)/(3) + July 1, 2020 + 42 U.S.C. В§ 3604(f) + HUD Notice FHEO-2020-01 + January 28, 2020 + 24 CFR В§ 100.204 + ADA В§ 12102); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `service_animal` + `reasonable_accommodation_modification` + `fair_chance_housing` + `pet_fees` + ESA-vs-service-animal + pet-fee exemption); **defensive negative relationship days clamped**; **boundary 29/30/31-day truth table** (3-cell precision sweep).

`traderview-expense::lease_nondisparagement_prohibition` is the **federal Consumer Review Fairness Act (CRFA) lease non-disparagement clause prohibition module** вҖ” 15 U.S.C. В§ 45b (Pub. L. 114-258, December 14, 2016). Distinct from sibling `lease_waiver_enforceability` (general lease waivers under N.Y. GOL В§ 5-321 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1953 + common law) and `landlord_retaliation_damages` (retaliation against tenant for exercising rights). This module focuses on the NARROW PROHIBITION вҖ” landlord-drafted clauses in form (adhesion) lease contracts that gag the tenant from posting honest reviews of landlord/property on Yelp, Google, Apartments.com, Reddit, etc. CRFA creates a federal floor; state attorneys general have concurrent enforcement.

**В§ 45b(b) three void-clause categories**:

| Subsection | Provision type | Effect |
|------------|----------------|--------|
| В§ 45b(b)(1) | Prohibits or restricts review of landlord/property | **Void from inception** |
| В§ 45b(b)(2) | Imposes penalty or fee for engaging in review | **Void from inception** |
| В§ 45b(b)(3) | Transfers (or requires transfer of) IP rights in review/feedback content | **Void from inception** |

**В§ 45b(a) form-contract gating** вҖ” CRFA applies ONLY to form contracts (adhesion contracts with standardized terms imposed without meaningful opportunity to negotiate). Genuinely negotiated leases fall outside CRFA scope; non-disparagement clauses remain subject to common-law contract enforcement under sibling `lease_waiver_enforceability` analysis. Pinned by `non_form_contract_crfa_does_not_apply` (3-clause sweep where CRFA inapplicable even with all 3 voiding clauses present).

**В§ 45b(c) four exceptions** (any single exception preserves the challenged clause):

| Subsection | Carve-out |
|------------|-----------|
| В§ 45b(c)(1) | Legally actionable content (defamation, harassment, knowingly false statements) |
| В§ 45b(c)(2) | Trade secret or personal information protected under existing law |
| В§ 45b(c)(3) | Medical-record content (HIPAA) |
| В§ 45b(c)(4) | Otherwise unlawful content (child pornography, true threats) |

Pinned by individual exception tests for all 4 carve-outs, `exception_blocks_all_three_void_categories` (single exception protects all 3 clause types), and `exception_truth_table_for_4_carve_outs` (**16-cell sweep** вҖ” any single exception true triggers engagement; only all-false leaves clause void).

**В§ 45b(d) offering prohibition вҖ” independent violation**. Offering a form contract containing a void provision is ITSELF unlawful, separate from any attempt at enforcement. Landlord exposed to FTC enforcement under В§ 5 of FTC Act (15 U.S.C. В§ 45, unfair or deceptive acts) and state attorney general civil action. Pinned by `offering_violation_independent_of_enforcement_attempt`.

**В§ 45b(e) concurrent enforcement** вҖ” FTC enforces under В§ 5 of FTC Act (unfair or deceptive act). State attorneys general have CONCURRENT enforcement authority; may bring federal-district-court civil action for injunctive relief, damages, restitution, civil penalties, or other equitable relief. FTC Bulletin 2022-05 (March 2022) provides interpretive guidance.

**Three-clause truth table** вҖ” `three_clause_truth_table` (**8-cell sweep** over non-disparagement Г— penalty Г— IP-transfer; void count matches sum of present-clauses-without-exception). Pinned by `all_three_clauses_void_simultaneously`.

**Sibling distinction note** вҖ” every result references `lease_waiver_enforceability` (general lease waivers), `landlord_retaliation_damages` (retaliation for tenant review may be parallel cause of action), and `landlord_harassment`, clarifying CRFA is a federal FLOOR вҖ” state-law remedies remain available and may provide additional damages. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present`.

**Enforcement note** вҖ” every result references FTC + State attorneys general concurrent enforcement + FTC Bulletin 2022-05. Pinned by `ftc_state_ag_enforcement_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-nondisparagement-prohibition`. Twenty-two tests pin: **non-form contract CRFA does not apply** (3-clause sweep inapplicable); **form contract no voiding clauses compliant** (baseline); **non-disparagement clause void no exception** (В§ 45b(b)(1)); **penalty clause void no exception** (В§ 45b(b)(2)); **IP transfer clause void no exception** (В§ 45b(b)(3)); **legally actionable content exception preserves clause** (В§ 45b(c)(1)); **trade secret exception preserves clause** (В§ 45b(c)(2)); **medical record exception preserves clause** (В§ 45b(c)(3)); **unlawful content exception preserves clause** (В§ 45b(c)(4)); **exception blocks all three void categories** (single exception covers); **all three clauses void simultaneously**; **offering violation independent of enforcement attempt** (В§ 45b(d)); **no void clause no offering violation**; **FTC and state AG enforcement available on voiding**; **no enforcement available on compliant lease**; **three-clause truth table** (8-cell sweep over non-disparagement Г— penalty Г— IP-transfer); **exception truth table for 4 carve-outs** (16-cell exhaustive sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (15 U.S.C. В§ 45b + Pub. L. 114-258 + Dec 14, 2016 + В§ 45b(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(b)(3)/(c)/(d)/(e) + В§ 45 FTC Act + FTC Bulletin 2022-05); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `lease_waiver_enforceability` + `landlord_retaliation_damages` + `landlord_harassment` + federal-floor concept); **FTC + state AG enforcement note present** (UX-text regression for concurrent enforcement + Bulletin 2022-05); **lease with no offending provisions compliant**; **lease with exception only carve-out compliant**.

`traderview-expense::plain_language_lease` is the **state plain-language lease / consumer-contract requirement compliance table** вҖ” only 4 states have enacted statutes specifically requiring residential leases to be written in clear, readable language understandable to the average tenant. The 1970s consumer-protection movement produced the original wave: CT (1979, U.S. pioneer), NY (1978-effective), NJ (1981), PA (1993). The 46 other states + DC rely on common-law unconscionability + contract-of-adhesion doctrines.

**Five regimes** with sharply different remedies:

| Regime | States | Standard | Statutory penalty | Defenses |
|--------|--------|----------|--------------------|----------|
| `NewYorkClearCoherent50DollarPenalty` | NY | "Clear and coherent" + "common and everyday meanings" + captioned sections | **$50** + actual damages | Good-faith compliance attempt; **barred after both parties fully performed** |
| `NewJersey100DollarMinimumPlusAttorneyFees` | NJ | "Simple, clear, understandable and easily readable" | Greater of **$100 minimum** OR actual damages + **reasonable attorney fees** | (none) |
| `PennsylvaniaPlainLanguageNineTests` | PA | Substantial compliance with **9 objective tests** (short sentences + everyday words + personal pronouns + simple/active verbs + readable type + ink contrast + boldface captions + layout/spacing + clear/coherent organization) | Private remedy + attorney fees | AG preapproval procedure |
| `ConnecticutDescriptiveReadability` | CT | Hybrid: descriptive standard + objective readability score (Flesch Reading Ease) | Private remedy + attorney fees | (none) |
| `NoStatewidePlainLanguageRequirement` | 46 other states + DC | (none) | (none) | Common-law unconscionability only |

**NY uniquely combines two defenses**: GOL В§ 5-702 gives the landlord both a good-faith-compliance defense AND a hard bar on tenant actions after both parties have fully performed. NJ has neither.

**PA's 9-test substantial-compliance regime is the most objective**: 8-of-9 tests passed still fails. The statute requires "substantial" compliance with the full list.

**NY effective date gate**: only leases executed on or after 1978-11-01 are covered. Pre-1978 lease forms still in use are grandfathered out.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/plain-language-lease-check`. Thirty-three tests pin: **5 regime classifications** (NY / NJ / PA / CT + default AL/CA/FL/TX/WA/MA/DC/WY); **NY compliant lease no penalty**; **NY non-compliant lease вҶ’ actual $500 + $50 penalty = $550**; **NY good-faith defense applies** (zeros penalty); **NY full-performance barred** (zeros penalty); **NY pre-1978 lease statute does not apply**; **NJ actual $50 below $100 вҶ’ $100 minimum applies**; **NJ actual $5,000 вҶ’ uses actual**; **NJ attorney fees recoverable on violation**; NJ no attorney fees when compliant; **NJ has NO good-faith defense path** (regression вҖ” only NY does); **PA all 9 tests passed + lease complies вҶ’ compliant**; **PA 8-of-9 tests satisfied вҶ’ non-compliant** (regression target вҖ” substantial compliance requires all 9); PA attorney fees recoverable; default state no statute applies; **4 citation regression targets** (NY В§ 5-702 + "$50 statutory penalty" + "good-faith"; NJ 56:12-1 + "$100 statutory minimum" + "attorney fees"; PA 73 P.S. В§ 2201 + "9 objective tests"; CT В§ 42-152 + "oldest"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **5 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (NY-only 50-dollar / NJ-only 100-minimum / PA-only 9-tests / CT-only descriptive-readability / **NY-only good-faith defense**); NY good-faith note mentions defense; NY full-performance note mentions "statutorily barred"; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::roommate_authorization` is the **state tenant roommate / additional-occupant authorization compliance table** вҖ” distinct from `sublet_consent` (which is assignment / re-leasing of the entire unit) and from `occupancy_standards` (building-code limits). This module captures the tenant's STATUTORY right to bring in an unrelated adult occupant despite contrary lease terms. Only 2 states create such a right.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | States | Mechanism | Lease restrictions |
|--------|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| `NewYorkStatutoryRoommateRight` | NY | RPL В§ 235-f Roommate Law вҖ” 1 additional adult occupant per tenant + immediate family + occupant's dependent children + tenant's primary residence + 30-day landlord notification | **VOID and unenforceable as against public policy** under В§ 235-f(7) |
| `CaliforniaTwoPlusOneFormula` | CA | State-law occupancy formula: 2 per bedroom + 1 additional | Enforceable above the statutory floor |
| `DefaultLeaseGoverns` | 48 other states + DC | Lease terms control | Enforceable |

**NY RPL В§ 235-f is uniquely powerful**: lease prohibitions on unrelated roommates are statutorily VOID. The tenant's right does NOT depend on landlord consent. Multi-tenant lease cap: total tenants + occupants вүӨ number of tenants on the lease (so a 2-tenant lease permits 2 additional adult occupants total вҖ” one per tenant).

**CA "2 plus 1" arithmetic**:
- 1-bedroom unit вҶ’ max 3 occupants
- 2-bedroom unit вҶ’ max 5 occupants
- 3-bedroom unit вҶ’ max 7 occupants
- N-bedroom unit вҶ’ max 2N + 1 occupants

**NY 30-day notification window**: tenant must inform the landlord of the new occupant's name within 30 days of occupancy commencement OR within 30 days of the landlord's request, whichever is later. Failure to notify does not void the right but creates a separate lease-compliance issue.

**NJ de-facto co-tenancy doctrine** (default regime): tenants in default-regime NJ can argue de-facto co-tenancy when an unauthorized roommate has shown continuous residence + substantial financial contribution + landlord acknowledgment. Not modeled here as a regime since it's a judicial doctrine, not a statutory right.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/roommate-authorization-check`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **3 regime classifications** (NY / CA + default AL/FL/TX/WA/DC/WY/MA/NJ/IL); **NY single-tenant 1 additional adult permitted**; **NY 2 additional adults exceeds per-tenant cap of 1** (regression вҖ” RPL В§ 235-f permits only 1 per tenant); **NY not-primary-residence вҶ’ no statutory right**; **NY lease restriction VOID when statute permits** (regression target вҖ” only NY does this); **NY 30-day notification window** (day 30 within; day 31 without notification non-compliant); **NY multi-tenant 2-on-lease + 2 additional = OK** (cap 2 tenants Г— 1 additional each = 2); **NY multi-tenant 2-on-lease + 3 additional exceeds cap**; **CA 1-bedroom max 3 occupants** ($N \times 2 + 1 = 3$); **CA 1-bedroom 4 occupants exceeds max**; **CA 2-bedroom max 5**; **CA 3-bedroom max 7**; **CA lease remains enforceable above floor** (regression вҖ” CA does NOT void lease restrictions); default-state no-lease-restriction permitted; default-state lease-restriction blocks roommate; **2 citation regression targets** (NY В§ 235-f + "VOID" + "30 days"; CA "2 plus 1" + "2 per bedroom"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (NY-only / CA-only / **NY-only lease-restrictions-void**); NY note describes per-tenant cap arithmetic; CA note describes bedroom formula; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::otard_antenna_installation` is the **federal FCC Over-the-Air Reception Devices (OTARD) rule compliance check for tenant antenna / satellite-dish / fixed-wireless-receiver installation** вҖ” sibling to `ev_charger_installation` (tenant right-to-install electric vehicle charging). 47 CFR В§ 1.4000 **PREEMPTS** state, local, HOA, and lease restrictions that impair a tenant's ability to install, maintain, or use a covered antenna in an area within the tenant's exclusive use or control. Trader-relevant for traders who depend on satellite-TV market data feeds, broadcast-TV business news (CNBC / Bloomberg over-the-air), or fixed-wireless broadband for low-latency price feeds.

**Five protected antenna types under В§ 1.4000(a)(1)**:

| Type | Size limit | Notes |
|------|------------|-------|
| `DBS` | вүӨ 1 meter diameter | Direct broadcast satellite (DirecTV, Dish Network); no size limit in Alaska |
| `MMDS` | вүӨ 1 meter diameter or diagonal | Multipoint distribution service / wireless cable |
| `BroadcastTV` | **Any size** | Over-the-air TV receive antenna |
| Supporting mast | (none stated) | Mast supporting any of the above |
| `FixedWirelessHubRelay2021` | (2021 expansion) | FCC 21-10 R&O eff. 2021-03-29 вҖ” requires on-premises customer AND broadband-only |

**Three permissible restriction categories under В§ 1.4000(b)**:

| Category | Statute | Subject to no-impairment test |
|----------|---------|--------------------------------|
| `Safety` | В§ 1.4000(b)(1) | YES вҖ” must not unreasonably delay/cost/signal |
| `HistoricPreservation` | В§ 1.4000(b)(2) | YES вҖ” National Register properties only |
| `Aesthetic` | NEVER permissible | n/a |
| `BlanketProhibition` | NEVER permissible | n/a вҖ” "no antennas of any kind" clauses are void |
| `PreApprovalDelay` | NEVER permissible | n/a вҖ” В§ 1.4000(a)(3)(i) |

**В§ 1.4000(a)(3) no-impairment standard** has three independent failure modes вҖ” any one defeats an otherwise-permissible safety or historic-preservation restriction:
- Unreasonably **delays** or prevents installation
- Unreasonably **increases the cost** of installation, maintenance, or use
- **Precludes** reception or transmission of an acceptable-quality signal

**The 2021 OTARD expansion (FCC 21-10) is the trader-critical addition.** Fixed-wireless hub/relay antennas вҖ” the kind that brings high-speed broadband to apartment buildings via roof-mounted distribution antennas вҖ” are protected ONLY IF (a) the antenna serves a customer on whose premises it is located, AND (b) the service provided is broadband-only. Both requirements must be satisfied; failure of either drops the antenna out of OTARD scope. Pinned by `fixed_wireless_2021_with_both_requirements_protected`, `fixed_wireless_2021_without_on_premises_customer_not_protected`, `fixed_wireless_2021_without_broadband_only_not_protected`, and the invariant `fixed_wireless_2021_expansion_thresholds_required_invariant` (4-cell truth table: only (true, true) protects).

**Tenant-exclusive-use scope is the location-side gate.** Patios, balconies, single-tenant rooftops, owned condo unit interior facing exterior all qualify. Common areas (shared rooftops, exterior walls of apartment buildings, hallways, shared yards) are OUTSIDE the rule's scope вҖ” landlord retains restriction authority on those. Pinned by `common_area_installation_not_protected` and `common_area_with_protected_antenna_type_still_unprotected` (confirms location overrides antenna-type protection across all 4 covered types).

**В§ 1.4000(c) burden of proof.** When OTARD applies, the burden of demonstrating that a particular restriction complies with the rule rests on the party seeking to impose or maintain the restriction (the landlord / HOA). Enforcement of conflicting restrictions is stayed pending FCC or court review. Pinned by `burden_on_restricting_party_when_otard_protected` and `no_burden_when_otard_not_protected`.

**Aesthetic and blanket-prohibition restrictions are NEVER permissible regardless of antenna type.** В§ 1.4000 does not list aesthetic concerns as a permissible restriction category. Lease clauses that say "no antennas of any kind" or "satellite dishes prohibited" are void as preempted. Pinned by `aesthetic_restriction_never_permissible`, `blanket_prohibition_never_permissible`, `pre_approval_delay_not_permissible`, and the invariant `aesthetic_blanket_pre_approval_invalidate_regardless_of_antenna_type` (4 antenna types Г— 3 restriction types = 12 confirmed-invalid cells).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/otard-antenna-installation`. Twenty-six tests pin: **DBS on patio protected**; **MMDS on balcony protected**; **broadcast TV any size protected**; **antenna outside OTARD scope not protected**; **fixed-wireless 2021 with both requirements protected**; **without on-premises customer not protected**; **without broadband-only not protected**; **common area installation not protected**; **common area with protected antenna type still unprotected** (location overrides type); **safety restriction without impairment permissible**; **safety with unreasonable delay not permissible**; **safety with cost increase not permissible**; **safety with signal preclusion not permissible**; **historic preservation without impairment permissible**; **historic with impairment not permissible**; **aesthetic never permissible**; **blanket prohibition never permissible**; **pre-approval delay not permissible**; **burden on restricting party when OTARD protected**; **no burden when not protected**; **aesthetic + blanket + pre-approval invalidate regardless of antenna type 12-cell invariant**; **safety + historic subject to no-impairment standard 6-cell invariant** (2 restriction types Г— 3 impairment flags); **citation pins 2021 expansion for fixed wireless**; **preemption note across all 10 paths** (5 antenna Г— 2 location); **fixed-wireless 2021 expansion thresholds required 4-cell invariant**; **no restriction path compliant when protected**.

`traderview-expense::rental_storage_unit_lease_disclosure` is the **rental storage unit lease disclosure plus self-service storage facility lien framework module** (iter 509) вҖ” when must a trader-landlord operating commercial self-storage OR rental storage attached to apartments comply with state Self-Service Storage Facility Acts plus newly-enacted California SB 709 (effective January 1, 2026) first-page disclosure requirements? Distinct from siblings `rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` (basement-storage water disclosure cross-reference), `rental_propane_tank_lease_disclosure` (lease-pass-through analog), `rental_in_unit_laundry_appliance_provision` (iter 501 in-unit appliance overlap), `tenant_voting_address_protection` (iter 505 ACP participant address confidentiality during lien-sale newspaper publication вҖ” substitute address required), `mid_tenancy_temporary_relocation` (when apartment-tenant must relocate stored property during construction), `tenant_emotional_distress_damages` (IIED claim for wrongful sale of sentimental items). Trader-landlord critical because (1) **California SB 709 effective January 1, 2026** amends Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21712 to require six prominent first-page disclosures in commercial self-storage rental agreements (length + renewal terms, promotional fee status, promotional duration, fee-change + 12-month max, termination steps, owner's contact info); (2) Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21702(c) EXPLICITLY EXCLUDES "garages and other storage areas in private residences" from SSF Act coverage вҖ” meaning rental storage attached to apartment is governed by landlord-tenant law plus UCC Article 7 warehouse-keeper lien, NOT the SSF Act lien-and-sale procedures; misuse of SSF lien procedures against residential tenant stored property exposes landlord to conversion liability plus statutory damages; (3) CA lien sequence: 14-day default cure window + 60-day sale notice + 60-day identity document return post-sale + 90-day excess proceeds return; (4) New York Senate Bill S3690 (2025 session, pending) would expand pre-lien-enforcement notice requirements.

**Four storage types**: CommercialSelfServiceStorageFacility (SSF Act + SB 709 apply), ResidentialStorageIncludedWithApartmentLease (SSF Act EXCLUDED per Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21702(c) вҖ” landlord-tenant law applies), SeparateResidentialStorageRental (also SSF Act excluded), NoStorageUnit.

**Four lien actions**: NoLienAction, DefaultWithinCureWindow (14-day CA + 7-day FL), LienSaleNoticeIssued (60-day CA), LienSaleCompleted.

**Five-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21700-21716 Self-Service Storage Facility Act + SB 709 amends В§ 21712 effective January 1, 2026 + AB 1916 (2024) and AB 1108 (2018) prior amendments + В§ 21702(c) residential garage exclusion + В§ 21703.1 alternative electronic-delivery rule effective 2018) vs New York (N.Y. General Business Law В§ 182 SSF Act + Lien Law В§ 184 warehouse-keeper lien + Senate Bill S3690 2025 session pending pre-lien notice expansion) vs Florida (Fla. Stat. В§ 83.801-83.809 Self-Service Storage Space Act + 7-day default trigger + newspaper publication twice in successive weeks at least 7 days apart per В§ 83.806) vs Texas (Tex. Prop. Code В§ 59.001-59.046 Self-Service Storage Liens + В§ 59.044 verified mail notice + В§ 59.041(c) identity document return) vs Default (Uniform Self-Service Storage Facility Act adopted by majority of US states + UCC В§ 7-209 warehouse lien for charges + UCC В§ 7-210 enforcement procedure).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantFullDisclosure, SbSix709FirstPageDisclosureMissing (50% rent at risk вҖ” CA leases on/after January 1, 2026), LienDefaultCureWindowNotMet (50% rent at risk вҖ” Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21705 14-day minimum), LienSaleNoticeRequirementsNotMet (100% rent at risk вҖ” Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21707 60-day notice + sale VOID if premature), IdentityDocumentReturnRequirementBreached (100% rent at risk вҖ” Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21709 + Cal. Penal Code В§ 530.5 identity theft + 18 U.S.C. В§ 1028 federal identification document fraud criminal exposure), ExcessProceedsReturnFailureToTenant (100% rent at risk вҖ” Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code В§ 21710 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1500 escheat), SsfActInapplicabilityResidentialStorage (50% rent at risk вҖ” residential garage explicitly excluded per В§ 21702(c); use landlord-tenant law + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1980-1991 abandoned personal property procedure instead).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-storage-unit-lease-disclosure`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **no storage unit not applicable**; **residential storage excluded from SSF Act** (В§ 21702(c) + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1980-1991); **separate residential storage also excluded**; **compliant full disclosure**; **CA SB 709 post-2026 no disclosures violation** (В§ 21712 amendment); **CA pre-2026 no SB 709 disclosure compliant** (boundary cliff at January 1, 2026); **lien default within cure window violation** (В§ 21705); **lien default at 14 days compliant path** (boundary); **lien sale notice too short violation** (В§ 21707); **lien sale notice at 60 days compliant** (boundary); **identity document not returned violation** (В§ 21709 + Cal. Penal Code В§ 530.5 + 18 U.S.C. В§ 1028); **excess proceeds not returned violation** (В§ 21710 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1500 escheat); **CA jurisdiction pins SB 709 + В§ 21700-21716 + В§ 21702(c) + January 1, 2026**; **NY jurisdiction pins N.Y. General Business Law В§ 182 + S3690 + N.Y. Lien Law В§ 184**; **FL jurisdiction pins Fla. Stat. В§ 83.801-83.809 + 7 days**; **TX jurisdiction pins Tex. Prop. Code В§ 59.001-59.046 + В§ 59.044**; **default jurisdiction pins UCC В§ 7-209 + В§ 7-210 + Uniform Self-Service Storage Facility Act**; **coordination note references basement water + voting address ACP + laundry appliance + emotional distress siblings**; **coordination note pinned in all 5 jurisdictions**; **CA SB 709 effective year pins 2026**; **CA lien default cure pins 14 days**; **CA lien sale notice pins 60 days**; **identity doc return window pins 60 days**; **excess proceeds return window pins 90 days**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (5-cell sweep); **severity priority residential overrides SB 709**; **zero rent zero risk does not panic**.

`traderview-expense::rental_solar_panel_disclosure` is the **rooftop solar PV disclosure and pass-through compliance framework module** (iter 495) вҖ” when a trader-landlord owns or hosts a rooftop solar PV system on a rental property, how must the existence, ownership, financing structure (owned / financed-loan / lease / PPA / community subscription), net-metering credit beneficiary, monthly pass-through charge, and panel-damage Universal Waste protocol be disclosed at lease signing? Distinct from sibling `tenant_solar_installation` (tenant right-to-install on portable / plug-in systems). Trader-landlord operational concern: solar PPA monthly payments passed through to tenant without explicit lease clause violate Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947 / В§ 1947.13 (rent is full agreed amount вҖ” additional charges require lease authorization); net-metering credit captured by landlord while tenant pays utility bill constitutes undisclosed economic offset; cracked CdTe (cadmium-telluride) thin-film panels trigger 40 C.F.R. Part 273 Universal Waste rule RCRA-compliant disposal at landlord cost.

**Five financing structures**: outright-owned by landlord, financed-loan owned by landlord, solar lease (fixed monthly), solar PPA (per-kWh), community solar subscription.

**Seven-jurisdiction framework** вҖ” California (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1102.6c + В§ 1947.13 + Pub. Util. Code В§ 2827 + NEM 3.0 / NBT eff. April 15, 2023 + SB 1340) vs Massachusetts (220 CMR 18.00 + SMART eff. November 26, 2018 + M.G.L. ch. 164 В§ 138-140) vs New Jersey (N.J.S.A. 48:3-87.13 + SuSI eff. August 28, 2021 + N.J.A.C. 14:4-9.1) vs Arizona (A.A.C. R14-2-1801 + A.R.S. В§ 33-1310 + HB 2675 [2021]) vs Hawaii (H.R.S. В§ 269-101.5 + Customer Self-Supply + Customer Grid-Supply Plus tariffs since October 21, 2015 closing NEM + Smart Export March 25, 2020) vs New York (16 NYCRR Part 96 + VDER Value Stack eff. March 9, 2017 + Real Property Law В§ 235-b) vs Default (federal 26 U.S.C. В§ 48 ITC + Notice 2018-59 + 40 C.F.R. Part 273 Universal Waste).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (no solar), Compliant, DisclosureRequiredAtLeaseSigning (50% rent at risk), NetMeteringCreditMisallocation (100% rent at risk вҖ” landlord captures credit while tenant pays utility), PpaPassThroughUnauthorized (100% rent at risk вҖ” Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947 violation), RoofWarrantyVoidRisk (50% rent at risk вҖ” installer-endorsement missing), PpaAccelerationTenantExposure (50% rent at risk вҖ” 240-month default acceleration), UniversalWasteDisposalRiskOnPanelDamage (50% rent at risk вҖ” 40 C.F.R. Part 273).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-solar-panel-disclosure`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **no solar not applicable**; **outright owned with tenant credit compliant**; **net-metering credit landlord undisclosed triggers misallocation** (estimated_monthly_generation_kwh Г— 25Вў/kWh Г— 12 months); **net-metering landlord with disclosure compliant**; **PPA no disclosure at lease signing triggers required status** (Cal SB 1340); **PPA pass-through without lease authorization violation** (Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947); **solar lease with pass-through authorized + roof warranty triggers PPA acceleration exposure** (240-month); **PPA universal waste unaware triggers disposal risk** (40 C.F.R. Part 273); **third-party financed without roof-warranty endorsement triggers void risk** (12-year remaining); **community solar subscription compliant if disclosed**; **CA jurisdiction pins NEM 3.0 NBT effective April 15, 2023 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1102.6c**; **CA jurisdiction pins В§ 1947.13**; **MA jurisdiction pins SMART program + 220 CMR 18.00 + November 26, 2018 effective**; **NJ jurisdiction pins SuSI replacement for SREC + N.J.S.A. 48:3-87.13 + August 28, 2021**; **AZ jurisdiction pins HB 2675 disclosure rule + A.R.S. В§ 33-1310**; **HI jurisdiction pins CSS + CGS+ closure of NEM + October 21, 2015 + Smart Export**; **NY jurisdiction pins VDER Value Stack + 16 NYCRR Part 96 + March 9, 2017**; **default jurisdiction pins federal 26 U.S.C. В§ 48 ITC + Notice 2018-59 + 40 C.F.R. Part 273**; **coordination note references tenant_solar_installation + rental_oil_tank_replacement_disclosure + rental_propane_tank_lease_disclosure**; **coordination note pinned in all 7 jurisdictions**; **full rent at risk for credit misallocation**; **full rent at risk for unauthorized pass-through**; **half rent at risk for disclosure required**; **saturating mul does not panic on extreme generation kWh** (u32::MAX defense); **zero rent zero risk does not panic** (boundary); **rate constant pins 25 cents per kWh**; **PPA default remaining term pins 240 months**; **financed-loan owned treated as owned compliant**; **citation branch for each jurisdiction** (7-cell sweep вҖ” CA/MA/NJ/AZ/HI/NY/Default).

`traderview-expense::tenant_solar_installation` is the **state tenant right-to-install solar-energy-system compliance check** вҖ” emerging area parallel to `ev_charger_installation` (tenant right-to-charge EV). Most state solar-rights laws cover homeowners + HOAs + condos but not rental tenants directly; the push to extend protection to renters is recent (2024-2026), with portable / plug-in solar systems leading the way because they require no permanent installation.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Statutory tenant protection |
|--------|-----------|------------------------------|
| `California` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 714 (Solar Rights Act) + В§ 714.1 + В§ 4600 + В§ 4746 | Plug-in portable + roof-mounted (with exclusive-use) |
| `Colorado` | Colorado HB22-1020 + 2026 plug-in solar legalization bill | Plug-in portable only |
| `NewJersey` | N.J.S.A. 45:22A-48.2 (Planned Real Estate Development Full Disclosure Act) | None for rental tenants (HOA-focused) |
| `Default` | Various state solar-rights laws | None for rental tenants |

**Three installation types**:

| Type | Permissibility | Common protection |
|------|----------------|---------------------|
| `PlugInPortable` | Most permissive (balcony / window mount) | CA + CO |
| `RoofMounted` | Typically requires consent | CA only (with exclusive use) |
| `GroundMounted` | Always requires consent | None |

**California В§ 714 Solar Rights Act renders restrictions on solar energy systems "void and unenforceable."** В§ 714 applies most directly to plug-in portable installations. Roof-mounted installations require the tenant to have exclusive use or control of the installation area (similar to OTARD's tenant-exclusive-use scope). Pinned by `california_plug_in_portable_statutorily_protected`, `california_roof_mounted_with_exclusive_use_protected`, `california_roof_mounted_without_exclusive_use_not_protected`, and the 4-regime invariant `only_california_protects_roof_mounted_with_exclusive_use_invariant`.

**Colorado 2026 plug-in solar legalization** establishes regulatory framework for portable arrays without landlord prohibition. Builds on HB22-1020 (Customer Right To Use Energy 2022). Pinned by `colorado_plug_in_portable_protected_for_renters`, `colorado_roof_mounted_not_statutorily_protected_for_renters`, `colorado_ground_mounted_not_protected`.

**Universal safety thresholds apply across all regimes**:
- Installation must meet electrical / safety code
- Installation must not damage landlord property (or restoration agreement in place)

Failure of either threshold prevents installation regardless of regime or installation type. Pinned by `unsafe_installation_never_permitted_invariant` (12-cell: 4 regimes Г— 3 installation types) and `property_damage_never_permitted_invariant` (4 regimes).

**Ground-mounted yard installation is never statutorily protected.** Across all four regimes, ground-mounted requires landlord consent. Pinned by `ground_mounted_never_statutorily_protected_invariant`.

**Installation-permitted truth table** = `safety_ok AND (statutory_protection OR landlord_consent)`. 8-cell truth table pinned by `installation_permitted_iff_safety_and_either_protected_or_consented_invariant`.

**Plug-in portable protection is unique to CA + CO.** Of the four regimes, only California (via В§ 714 Solar Rights Act) and Colorado (via HB22-1020 + 2026 bill) statutorily protect renter installation of portable solar. NJ + Default require landlord consent. Pinned by `only_california_and_colorado_protect_plug_in_portable_invariant` (4-regime invariant).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-solar-installation`. Nineteen tests pin: **CA plug-in portable statutorily protected + roof-mounted with exclusive use protected + roof-mounted without exclusive use not protected + ground-mounted requires consent**; **CO plug-in portable protected for renters + roof-mounted not protected + ground-mounted not protected**; **NJ HOA focus no tenant rental protection**; **Default no tenant rental protection**; **unsafe installation never permitted 12-cell invariant** (4 regimes Г— 3 installation types); **property damage never permitted 4-regime invariant**; **landlord consent permits otherwise unprotected installation**; **missing consent violation when not statutorily protected**; **only-California-and-Colorado-protect-plug-in-portable 4-regime invariant**; **only-California-protects-roof-mounted-with-exclusive-use 4-regime invariant**; **ground-mounted never statutorily protected 4-regime invariant**; **installation permitted iff safety AND (statutory or consent) 8-cell truth-table invariant**; **citation pins authority per regime** (В§ 714 for CA; HB22-1020 for CO; В§ 45:22A-48.2 for NJ; "Most states" for Default); **sibling-module note across all 12 combos** (UX-text regression for ev_charger_installation pairing).

`traderview-expense::ev_charger_installation` is the **state tenant right-to-install electric-vehicle charging station compliance table** вҖ” modern "right-to-charge" laws addressing demand for at-home EV charging in multi-unit rental housing. Only 4 states grant tenant-specific protections; MD / VA / CO / FL right-to-charge laws cover HOAs and condos but do not extend to standalone rental tenants.

**Five regimes** with sharply different gates:

| Regime | States | Insurance | Building gate | Lease restrictions |
|--------|--------|-----------|----------------|--------------------|
| `CaliforniaInsuranceRequired` | CA | **$1,000,000 general liability** | None | Enforceable above 4-prong requirements |
| `HawaiiLeaseProvisionVoid` | HI | None | Multi-family residential or townhouse | **VOID and unenforceable** under HRS В§ 196-7.5 |
| `IllinoisNewBuildingsOnly` | IL | None | **NEW construction only** + multi-unit | Reasonable-terms review |
| `NewJerseyMultiUnitRight` | NJ | None | Multi-unit residential | Reasonable-terms review |
| `DefaultLeaseGoverns` | 46 other states + DC | None | None | Enforceable |

**CA 4-prong test** вҖ” ALL required for tenant eligibility:
1. Lease signed / renewed / extended on/after 2015-07-01
2. Tenant willing to pay charging-station + installation + utility costs
3. Tenant signs written agreement covering installation, use, maintenance, removal
4. Tenant maintains **$1,000,000 general-liability insurance** policy

**HI is the strongest tenant-protective regime**: any lease provision purporting to restrict EV charger installation is VOID and unenforceable. Protection extends beyond dedicated parking stalls to common-element parking (first-come, first-served).

**IL applies only to new construction**: the Electric Vehicle Charging Act mandates 100% of parking spaces at new multi-unit dwellings be EV-ready (conduit + reserved capacity). Tenants in older buildings remain lease-governed even in IL.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/ev-charger-installation-check`. Thirty-one tests pin: **5 regime classifications** (CA / HI / IL / NJ + default AL/FL/TX/WA/DC/WY/NY/MA); **CA all 4 prongs met вҶ’ eligible** (lease date + pay-costs + written request + agreement + $1M insurance); **CA insurance below $1M not eligible** ($500k вҶ’ fails); **CA insurance exactly $1M eligible**; CA no-insurance-carried not eligible; CA tenant-unwilling-to-pay not eligible; CA no-written-request not eligible; CA no-written-agreement not eligible; **CA lease pre-effective-date statute inapplicable** (regression вҖ” only post-2015-07-01 leases covered); **HI baseline eligible with NO insurance required** (regression вҖ” HI does NOT require $1M); **HI lease restriction VOID when statute permits** (lease unenforceable); **IL new construction eligible**; **IL older construction statute inapplicable** (regression вҖ” IL only covers NEW buildings); IL not-multi-unit statute inapplicable; **NJ multi-unit eligible**; NJ single-family inapplicable; default state no-lease-restriction eligible; default state lease-restriction blocks install; **3 citation regression targets** (CA В§ 1947.6 + "$1,000,000"; HI В§ 196-7.5 + "VOID"; IL 765 ILCS + "NEW"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (CA-only insurance-required / HI-only lease-void / IL-only new-construction); lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::advance_rent_limit` is the **state tenant advance-rent prepayment limit compliance table** вҖ” distinct from `security_deposit_caps` (which caps refundable deposits). Trader-tenants relocating to expensive markets (NYC, SF, Boston) routinely face landlord demands for 6-12 months prepaid rent; only 4 states cap or prohibit these.

**Five regimes** with sharply different remedies:

| Regime | States | Maximum months | Interest required | 6-month gate |
|--------|--------|----------------|--------------------|---------------|
| `NewYorkOneMonthFirstOnly` | NY | **1 month** (first only) | None | None |
| `MassachusettsFirstLastSecurityLock` | MA | 2 months (first + last) | **5% annual on last-month** | None |
| `CaliforniaSixMonthLeaseOnly` | CA | 1 month default; **6+ months only when lease вүҘ 6 months covering вүҘ 6 months** | None | **Yes** |
| `NewJerseyAdvanceRentUnlimited` | NJ | (unlimited вҖ” not subject to 1.5-month security deposit cap) | None | None |
| `NoStateAdvanceRentLimit` | 46 other states + DC | (unlimited) | None | None |

**NY HSTPA 2019 is the strictest regime**: RPL В§ 238-a prohibits demanding last month's rent or multi-month prepayments as a condition of move-in. Combined with the 1-month security deposit cap, NYC landlords are statutorily limited to **first month + 1 month security = 2 months total** at signing. This applies ONLY to unregulated tenancies вҖ” rent-stabilized units retain their pre-HSTPA practices.

**MA's unique 5% interest requirement**: G.L. c. 186 В§ 15B requires landlords who collect advance last-month rent to pay 5% annual interest (or the lower bank-deposit rate). No other state in this table requires interest on advance rent.

**CA's 6-month gate** (Civ. Code В§ 1950.5): tenants may prepay multiple months ONLY when (i) the lease term is at least 6 months AND (ii) the prepaid amount covers at least 6 months. This bars landlords from pressuring month-to-month tenants into multi-month prepayments вҖ” a common Bay Area practice before 1950.5 amendments.

**NJ's interpretive clarification** (N.J.S.A. 46:8-21.2): advance rent is statutorily NOT a security deposit and therefore NOT subject to the 1.5-month security-deposit cap under В§ 46:8-19. NJ is effectively a no-cap state on advance rent specifically.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/advance-rent-limit-check`. Thirty-one tests pin: **5 regime classifications** (NY / MA / CA / NJ + default AL/FL/TX/WA/DC/WY/IL); **NY one-month demand compliant**; **NY first + last violates cap** (regression вҖ” HSTPA prohibits last-month demand); NY 3 months violates cap; **NY regulated tenancy does NOT apply HSTPA cap** (regression вҖ” only unregulated covered); **MA first only compliant**; **MA first + last + 5% interest compliant**; **MA first + last WITHOUT 5% interest non-compliant** (regression вҖ” distinguishing MA from NJ); MA 3 months violates cap; **CA 1-month demand any-lease-term compliant**; **CA 6-month advance with 6-month lease compliant**; **CA 6-month advance with 5-month lease non-compliant** (regression вҖ” strict вүҘ 6 lease months); **CA 3-month advance even with long lease non-compliant** (regression вҖ” must be вүҘ 6 months to use the multi-month path); **NJ unlimited 12-month demand compliant** (no cap); default state any amount compliant; **4 citation regression targets** (NY В§ 238-a + HSTPA + "PROHIBITED"; MA c. 186 В§ 15B + "5% annual interest"; CA В§ 1950.5 + "вүҘ 6 months"; NJ 46:8-21.2); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (NY-only 1-month / MA-only 5%-interest / CA-only 6-month-gate); NY violation note mentions "advance-rent cap violated"; MA interest-violation note mentions "5% interest"; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::fire_sprinkler_disclosure` is the **state fire-sprinkler disclosure requirement compliance table** вҖ” distinct from `detector_requirements` (smoke/CO installation) and `heat_requirements` (heating adequacy). Captures the statutory requirement that residential leases contain a conspicuous notice about the presence/absence of a fire-sprinkler system in the leased premises. Only New York has a specific statutory disclosure rule; other states rely on building/fire codes that do NOT extend to lease-text disclosure.

**Two regimes**:

| Regime | States | Statutory mandate | Bold-face required | Maintenance date required |
|--------|--------|--------------------|---------------------|----------------------------|
| `NewYorkSprinklerSystemNotice` | NY | Yes (RPL В§ 231-a eff. 2014-12-03) | **Yes** (bold face type) | **Yes** when system present (last maintenance and inspection date) |
| `NoStateFireSprinklerDisclosureLaw` | 49 other states + DC | No statewide rule | (n/a) | (n/a) |

**NY В§ 231-a has NO statutory penalty provision** вҖ” the effect of noncompliance is uncertain until tested in litigation. In practice, courts may construe the lease against the landlord (contra proferentem) and could find the lease unenforceable as to fire-related issues. The lease-rider remedy (rather than fixed-dollar penalty) is what distinguishes NY В§ 231-a from other tenant-protection statutes like `plain_language_lease` (NY В§ 5-702's $50 fixed penalty) and `lease_copy_delivery` (MA c. 186 В§ 15D's $300 fixed fine).

**Conditional disclosure structure**: the maintenance-date requirement attaches ONLY when the sprinkler system actually exists. A unit with NO sprinkler system needs only the "no sprinkler system" disclosure вҖ” no maintenance-date prong applies.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/fire-sprinkler-disclosure-check`. Eighteen tests pin: **2 regime classifications** (NY + default AL/CA/FL/TX/WA/DC/WY/MA/NJ/IL); **NY all 4 compliance facets met** (notice in lease + bold face + maintenance date + system present); **NY no notice non-compliant** with diagnostic; **NY notice NOT bold face non-compliant** (regression target вҖ” bold-face prong independently checked); **NY no maintenance date non-compliant when system present**; **NY no sprinkler system вҶ’ no maintenance-date requirement** (regression вҖ” conditional structure); default state no-disclosure-required; default state no-notice still compliant (no obligation); **2 citation regression targets** (NY В§ 231-a + "BOLD FACE TYPE" + 2014-12-03; "NO statutory penalty" pin); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **2 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (NY-only sprinkler-notice / NY-only bold-face-required); NY compliant note mentions regime; default state note says "no statutory disclosure obligation"; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::carpet_replacement_useful_life` is the **multi-state carpet-replacement useful-life landlord security-deposit deduction compliance check** вҖ” addresses the most heavily litigated security-deposit dispute: landlord charges tenant for carpet replacement at move-out; tenant argues the carpet was already old and the charge exceeds the landlord's actual loss. The doctrine has crystallized into per-state "useful life" schedules вҖ” landlord may deduct only the **remaining-life fraction** of replacement cost, never the full amount.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Useful life | Special damage gate | Cost-recovery formula |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|------------------------|
| `ColoradoHb251249` | Colo. Rev. Stat. В§ 38-12-104 (HB 25-1249 eff. 2026-01-01) | **10 years** | **Substantial + irreparable damage AND replaced new within 10 years** | Minimum amount necessary, prorated |
| `California` | Common law вҖ” Killough v. McManus 19 Cal. App. 3d 1141 (1971) + progeny | **8 years** | None (normal wear-and-tear analysis only) | Prorated remaining-life fraction |
| `HudSection8` | HUD Handbook 4350.3 chap. 6 | **7 years** | None (HUD schedule) | Prorated |
| `Default` | Common law actual-damages-net-of-depreciation | 7 years (proxy) | None | Prorated |

**Colorado's 10-year useful life is the longest** (HB 25-1249 extended from 7 years to 10 effective 2026-01-01). Colorado also adds two unique gate requirements: (a) substantial + irreparable damage exceeding normal wear-and-tear AND (b) carpet replaced new within the 10-year window preceding lease termination. Failing EITHER gate вҶ’ $0 deduction allowed. Other states use straight remaining-life proration without these gates.

**Section 8 override**: HUD Section 8 voucher units use the federal HUD schedule (7-year) regardless of state вҖ” Section 8 status overrides state routing.

**Past useful life = $0 deduction across all regimes**: when carpet age вүҘ useful life, the carpet is fully depreciated and landlord cannot charge for replacement. The 10-year threshold for CO + 8-year for CA + 7-year for HUD/Default are the per-state expiration points.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/carpet-replacement-useful-life`.
