Twenty-two tests pin: **CO past 10 years zero deduction** + 10-year useful life; **CO at 10-year boundary past**; **CO 9 years with damage 1 year remaining proration** ($2000 Г— 1/10 = $200); **CO no-substantial-damage no deduction** (regression-critical Colorado-only gate); **CO not-replaced-new-within-10-years no deduction** (regression-critical second gate); **CA past 8 years zero deduction**; **CA at 8-year boundary past**; **CA 4-year proration half** ($2000 Г— 4/8 = $1000); **CA no-damage-gate proration still applies** (regression вҖ” CA does NOT have CO's substantial+irreparable gate); **HUD В§ 8 carpet at 7 years past** + 7-year useful life; **HUD В§ 8 3-year proration** ($700 Г— 4/7 = $400); **default 7-year useful life**; **default past 7 years zero**; **negative cost clamped**; **state routing CO/CA/Default + Section 8 override** (regression вҖ” Section 8 voucher overrides state routing regardless of CO/CA); **CO useful life LONGEST at 10 years** (CO 10 > CA 8 > HUD 7 = Default 7 ordering invariant); **only-CO-has-substantial-damage-gate** 4-regime invariant (regression-critical вҖ” same no-damage scenario yields zero for CO + prorated for others); **only-CO-requires-replaced-new-within-useful-life** 2-regime invariant; **citations pin authorities** (HB 25-1249 + 2026-01-01 + Killough + HUD Handbook 4350.3 + common-law); **CA brand-new carpet full replacement cost minus proration** ($2000 fully recoverable at 0 years age); **CO with damage and recent replacement proration applies** (gates open вҶ’ standard proration kicks in: $2000 Г— 5/10 = $1000).

`traderview-expense::pesticide_application_notice` is the **state landlord pesticide-application notice compliance check** вҖ” operational concern for any landlord using a pest-control company or self-applying pesticides. Sibling to `bedbug_extermination_cost` (treatment-responsibility) and `bedbug_disclosure` (initial-tenancy disclosure). Federal **FIFRA** (7 U.S.C. В§ 136 et seq.) imposes labeling + applicator-licensing requirements but does NOT require advance notice to occupants вҖ” that is purely state law, and the states diverge sharply.

**Six regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Tenant advance notice | Adjacent unit notice | Building posting | Unique content requirement |
|--------|-----------|------------------------|------------------------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| `California` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.8.5 + 14 CCR В§ 6740 | **24 hours** | **Yes** for broadcast / fogger / aerosol | No | Pesticide product + manufacturer + EPA registration + applicator name |
| `NewJersey` | N.J.A.C. В§ 7:30-9.12 | At-application only | No | No | Applicator contact + **National Pesticide Information Center phone** + NJDEP Pesticide Control Program phone |
| `NewYork` | N.Y. ECL В§ 33-1004 | None (on request only for multi-dwelling) | No | No | Label info via applicator; multi-dwelling owner relays on request |
| `Massachusetts` | Mass. G.L. c. 132B В§ 9 + 333 CMR 13.04 | **48 hours** (strictest in matrix) | No | **Yes** at building entrances | Integrated pest management framework |
| `Oregon` | ORS В§ 634.740 | None to tenant | No | **24 hours** "Warning: pesticide-treated area" sign + 72-hour minimum retention | Date/time + contact phone |
| `Default` | FIFRA 7 U.S.C. В§ 136 et seq. | None statewide | No | No | Federal labeling floor only |

**California's adjacent-unit notice requirement is the unique state expansion.** Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.8.5 extends notice to adjacent dwelling units that could "reasonably be impacted" вҖ” but ONLY for broadcast applications, total release foggers, and aerosol sprays. Targeted/spot applications get notice only to the treated unit. Pinned by `california_broadcast_requires_adjacent_unit_notice`, `california_fogger_requires_adjacent_unit_notice`, `california_aerosol_requires_adjacent_unit_notice`, `california_broadcast_missing_adjacent_notice_violation`, and the 6-regime invariant `only_california_requires_adjacent_unit_notice_invariant` (confirmed 5 other regimes do NOT extend to adjacent units even for broadcast applications).

**Massachusetts has the strictest advance-notice window вҖ” 48 hours.** Twice California's 24-hour mandate; double the strictest other regime. Pinned by `massachusetts_under_48h_violation`, `massachusetts_at_48h_boundary_compliant`, `massachusetts_missing_building_posting_violation`, and the comparative invariant `massachusetts_has_strictest_advance_notice_window` (MA > CA, MA > OR, MA > NJ, MA > NY).

**New Jersey's National Pesticide Information Center phone is the unique state-specific notice content.** N.J.A.C. В§ 7:30-9.12 requires the NPIC phone number be included in the at-application label-information delivery. No other regime in this matrix mandates it. Pinned by `new_jersey_missing_npic_phone_violation` and the invariant `only_nj_requires_npic_phone_invariant` (5 other regimes do NOT cite NPIC phone absence as a violation).

**Massachusetts and Oregon are the only building-posting jurisdictions.** MA requires 48-hour posting at building entrances alongside the 48-hour tenant notice; OR requires 24-hour sign posting around the application area but no direct tenant-notice mandate. Pinned by `only_ma_and_or_require_building_posting_invariant` (CA + NJ + NY + Default confirmed not to require posting).

**New York has NO statewide advance-notice mandate for residential rentals.** N.Y. ECL В§ 33-1004 (Pesticide Reporting Law / Neighbor Notification Law) requires the applicator to provide label info AT TIME OF APPLICATION for one- or two-family dwellings. For multi-dwelling buildings, applicator provides info to owner; owner distributes to occupants ON REQUEST. Tenants may negotiate stricter pre-notice as a lease term but cannot rely on statute. Pinned by `new_york_no_statutory_advance_notice_compliant`, `new_york_multi_dwelling_note_present`, and `new_york_single_family_no_multi_dwelling_note`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/pesticide-application-notice`. Thirty tests pin: **CA targeted 24h compliant**; **CA broadcast / fogger / aerosol require adjacent-unit notice**; **CA under 24h violation**; **CA at 24h boundary compliant**; **CA broadcast missing adjacent notice violation**; **CA missing product info violation** (14 CCR В§ 6740 content); **CA missing applicator info violation**; **NJ no advance notice compliant** (at-application only); **NJ missing NPIC phone violation**; **NJ missing applicator info violation**; **NY no statutory advance notice compliant**; **NY multi-dwelling note present**; **NY single-family no multi-dwelling note**; **MA 48h compliant**; **MA under 48h violation**; **MA at 48h boundary compliant**; **MA missing building posting violation**; **OR 24h posting compliant**; **OR under 24h posting violation**; **OR no advance notice to tenant required** (regression вҖ” only sign posting); **Default federal FIFRA only**; **only-California-requires-adjacent-unit-notice 6-regime invariant**; **only-MA-and-OR-require-building-posting 6-regime invariant**; **only-CA-and-MA-have-advance-notice-to-tenant 6-regime invariant**; **only-NJ-requires-NPIC-phone 6-regime invariant**; **MA-has-strictest-advance-notice-window comparative invariant** (MA > CA, OR, NJ, NY); **citation pins authority per regime** (В§ 1940.8.5 for CA + В§ 7:30-9.12 for NJ + В§ 33-1004 for NY + c. 132B В§ 9 for MA + В§ 634.740 for OR + FIFRA for Default); **FIFRA federal-floor note across all 6 regimes** (UX-text regression for federal-floor + state-overlay relationship).

`traderview-expense::bedbug_extermination_cost` is the **state bedbug extermination cost / treatment-responsibility compliance table** вҖ” distinct from existing `bedbug_disclosure` (which covers initial disclosure of past infestation at lease signing). This module addresses who pays when an infestation is discovered MID-TENANCY, what the tenant must do to cooperate, and whether retaliation protection kicks in when the tenant reports.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | States | Investigation window | Follow-up required | Retaliation window | Licensed pest control |
|--------|--------|-----------------------|---------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| `CaliforniaAB551Comprehensive` | CA | None | **Yes** (В§ 1954.604) | **180 days** (В§ 1942.5) | No |
| `MaineLandlordEradicationStatutory` | ME | **5 days** (В§ 6021-A) | No | None | **Yes** |
| `DefaultImpliedWarrantyOfHabitability` | 48 other states + DC | None | No | None | No |

**CA AB 551 (2017) is the most detailed regime**: Civ. Code В§В§ 1954.600-1954.605 plus В§ 1942.5 create three interlocking protections вҖ”
- В§ 1954.602: landlord may NOT show / rent / lease a vacant unit known to have a current bed bug infestation.
- В§ 1954.604: landlord must schedule follow-up and maintenance treatments until eradicated.
- В§ 1942.5: 180-day retaliation protection from tenant's good-faith bedbug report (no eviction / rent raise / service reduction).

**ME В§ 6021-A imposes the only sharp investigation window** (5 days post-tenant report) and the only licensed-pest-control requirement. Maine also creates a tenant re-treatment-cost liability when the tenant recklessly or intentionally introduced bedbugs вҖ” the only state in this table with that allocation rule.

**Default-regime states** (NY, FL, TX, MA, NJ, etc.) apply the common-law implied warranty of habitability (Restatement 2d Property В§ 5.5) вҖ” landlord generally pays unless tenant introduction is proven; tenant has a common-law duty to cooperate with treatment. No statutory windows or fixed protections.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/bedbug-extermination-cost-check`. Thirty tests pin: **3 regime classifications** (CA / ME + default AL/NY/TX/FL/MA/WA/DC/WY); **CA full compliance baseline**; **CA no follow-up treatments non-compliant** (regression вҖ” В§ 1954.604 required); **CA В§ 1954.602 vacant-unit prohibition violation**; **CA 100-day retaliation window active**; **CA day-181 retaliation window expired** (regression вҖ” strict вүӨ 180); **CA retaliation window requires good-faith report** (regression вҖ” not just timing); **ME 5-day window satisfied when engaged within window**; **ME day-6 no engagement window NOT met**; **ME unlicensed pest control non-compliant** (regression вҖ” only ME has this prong); **ME tenant reckless introduction creates re-treatment liability**; **ME no follow-up treatments still compliant** (regression вҖ” distinguishes ME from CA); default state no statutory windows; **default state vacant-unit prohibition not statutory** (regression вҖ” only CA has В§ 1954.602); **3 citation regression targets** (CA AB 551 2017 + В§ 1942.5 + В§ 1954.602 + "180-day"; ME В§ 6021-A + "5 days" + "licensed pest control"; default "Restatement 2d Property В§ 5.5"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **5 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (CA-only AB 551 / ME-only eradication-statutory / CA-only vacant-unit-prohibition / CA-only retaliation-window / ME-only licensed-pest-control-required); CA non-compliant note mentions В§ 1954.602; ME non-compliant note mentions investigation window; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::crime_victim_termination` is the **state crime-victim lease termination compliance table вҖ” broader than the DV-only subset already covered by `dv_termination`**. Goes beyond domestic violence to cover sexual assault, stalking, human trafficking, elder/dependent-adult abuse, and other enumerated violent crimes. Four states have specific statutes expanding the lease-termination right beyond pure DV; the rest cover only DV (handled in the separate `dv_termination` module).

**Five regimes**:

| Regime | States | Scope | Notice | Window | Rent obligation |
|--------|--------|-------|--------|--------|-----------------|
| `CaliforniaBroadestVictimCoverage` | CA | DV + sex assault + stalking + trafficking + elder/dependent + bodily injury / force/threat | **14 days** | None | Tenant liable through notice period |
| `TexasThirtyDayNotice` | TX | Family violence + sex assault + child sex abuse + stalking | **30 days** | None | Tenant liable through 30-day notice |
| `WashingtonNinetyDayWindow` | WA | DV + sex assault + unlawful harassment + stalking | (none) | **90 days from incident** | **Rent terminates at notice** + security deposit return as-if-full-term |
| `IllinoisSafeHomesActDualPath` | IL | DV + sex assault + credible imminent threat | **3 days** (imminent) | **60 days** (past sex violence) | Tenant liable through notice |
| `NoStatewideBroadCrimeVictimTermination` | 46 other states + DC | DV-only (see `dv_termination`) | (n/a) | (n/a) | Full early-termination penalty for non-DV |

**CA has the BROADEST U.S. scope**: В§ 1946.7 (eff. 2011, expanded by AB 1493 in 2022) covers 5 enumerated victim categories PLUS any crime that caused bodily injury or included force/threat. Companion В§ 1161.3 (Civ. Proc. Code) prohibits eviction based on victim status.

**WA's unique rent-obligation termination**: RCW 59.18.575 is the only regime in this table where the tenant pays rent only for the termination month and gets the security deposit back as if the lease ran full term. CA / TX / IL all keep the tenant liable through the notice period.

**IL Safe Homes Act dual-path**: 765 ILCS 750 has TWO separate timing rules depending on whether the threat is credible-imminent (3-day notice) or past-sexual-violence (60-day window from incident).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/crime-victim-termination-check`. Thirty-six tests pin: **5 regime classifications** (CA / TX / WA / IL + default AL/FL/NY/MA/NJ/DC/WY); **CA human trafficking covered + elder abuse covered + force/threat path independent of category**; **CA 14-day notice satisfied vs 13-day not satisfied** (boundary); **TX 30-day notice satisfied + 29-day not satisfied** (boundary); **TX human trafficking NOT covered** (regression вҖ” TX narrower than CA); **TX rent obligation continues during notice period**; **WA 89-day within window + day-90 boundary satisfied + day-91 outside window** (regression target); **WA unlawful harassment covered** (unique to WA); **WA rent obligation terminates at notice** (regression target вҖ” only WA does this); **IL credible imminent threat 3-day path**; **IL past sexual violence within 60-day window + day-61 outside window** (boundary); **IL stalking NOT covered** (regression вҖ” IL Safe Homes Act narrower); default state no broad termination right; CA no documentation fails; **4 citation regression targets** (CA В§ 1946.7 + AB 1493 2022 + "broadest U.S. scope"; TX В§В§ 92.0161 + 30-day; WA RCW 59.18.575 + 90 days; IL 765 ILCS 750 + dual-path + 3 days + 60 days); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **2 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (CA-only broadest / WA-only rent-terminates-at-notice); CA compliant note describes regime; TX human-trafficking note says not covered; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::lease_renewal_offer_timing` is the **multi-jurisdictional lease renewal offer timing and disclosure framework module** вҖ” trader-landlord critical because failure to provide timely renewal offer (or non-renewal notice) can EXTEND tenancy as month-to-month, INVALIDATE rent increase, or FORFEIT landlord's right to non-renew. Distinct from sibling `rent_increase_notice_period` (periodic-tenancy rent increase notices). Companion to `lease_auto_renewal`, `lease_succession`, `lease_assignment_consent`, `lease_copy_delivery`, `rent_increase_notice_period`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Window | Form / Method |
|--------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **NY Rent-Stabilized** | 9 NYCRR В§ 2523.5 plus DHCR Form RTP-8 | **90-150 DAYS** before expiry | Mail OR personal delivery; current RTP-8 form; 1-year AND 2-year option |
| **NY Non-Stabilized** | NY RPL В§ 226-c (HSTPA 2019) | 30/60/90 day tiers | Written notice when increase вүҘ 5% or non-renewal |
| **California TPA** | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2 (AB 1482) | Written notice | Just-cause required; В§ 1946.2(d) ONE MONTH'S RENT relocation for no-fault |
| **D.C.** | D.C. Code В§ 42-3505.54 + В§ 42-3505.01 | 30 days | 12-MONTH RENEWAL mandatory except just-cause grounds |

**NY В§ 226-c tier structure (HSTPA 2019)**:
- Tenancy < 1 year вҶ’ **30 days** advance notice
- Tenancy 1-2 years вҶ’ **60 days** advance notice
- Tenancy 2+ years вҶ’ **90 days** advance notice
- Trigger: rent increase вүҘ 5% OR non-renewal

**NY rent-stabilized RTP-8 content requirements (six elements)**:
1. Offer of 1-year OR 2-year renewal term at tenant's option
2. Current legal regulated rent
3. Proposed new rent based on Rent Guidelines Board (RGB) increase percentages
4. Tenant's right to renew without vacancy or longevity bonus (HSTPA repealed)
5. Notice of MCI or IAI surcharge if any
6. Tenant's right to file challenge with DHCR

**California TPA В§ 1946.2 just-cause categories**:
- AT-FAULT just cause вҖ” nonpayment + breach + nuisance + criminal activity + assignment without consent
- NO-FAULT just cause вҖ” withdrawal from rental market + owner move-in + rehabilitation + government order
- В§ 1946.2(d) вҖ” no-fault grounds require ONE MONTH'S RENT relocation assistance (or rent waiver for final month)

**D.C. RHA В§ 42-3505.01 enumerated just-cause grounds**: nonpayment, violation, landlord personal use, demolition, substantial rehabilitation, sale to qualifying purchaser. Statutory presumption FAVOR OF RENEWAL.

**Trader-landlord critical fact patterns**: (1) NYC trader sends rent-stabilized renewal 60 days before expiry вҖ” UNTIMELY under В§ 2523.5; rent increase INVALIDATED; tenant continues at current rent; (2) NY non-stabilized trader sends 2-year tenant 45-day non-renewal вҖ” UNTIMELY under RPL В§ 226-c (requires 90 days); (3) CA trader seeks TPA non-renewal without just cause вҖ” В§ 1946.2(d) ONE MONTH RELOCATION required for no-fault; (4) DC trader seeks non-renewal without just cause вҖ” presumption FAVOR OF RENEWAL; (5) NYC trader uses outdated RTP-8 form вҖ” RENEWAL INVALID.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-renewal-offer-timing`. Thirty tests pin: **NY stabilized 120-day compliant**; **NY stabilized 60-day untimely**; **NY stabilized 160-day too early**; **NY stabilized 90-day boundary compliant**; **NY stabilized 150-day boundary compliant**; **NY stabilized no RTP-8 form invalid**; **NY stabilized no both-terms invalid**; **NY stabilized email delivery invalid**; **NY non-stabilized under 1-year 30-day tier**; **NY non-stabilized 1-2 year 60-day tier**; **NY non-stabilized 2+ year 90-day tier**; **NY non-stabilized under 5% no invalidation**; **NY non-stabilized 5%+ invalidated**; **CA TPA no just-cause invalidates**; **CA TPA no relocation assistance invalidates**; **DC no just-cause renewal required**; **no renewal offer given at all**; **jurisdiction window truth table five cells**; **NY stabilized uniquely requires RTP-8 form invariant**; **CA uniquely requires relocation assistance invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (DHCR Form RTP-8 + 9 NYCRR В§ 2523.5 + NY RPL В§ 226-c + HSTPA 2019 + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1946.2 + AB 1482 + D.C. Code В§ 42-3505.01 + D.C. Code В§ 42-3505.54 + Rental Housing Act of 1985); **note pins four-jurisdiction framework**; **note pins NY В§ 2523.5**; **note pins NY RPL В§ 226-c tiers**; **note pins RTP-8 content six elements**; **note pins CA TPA just-cause grounds**; **note pins DC RHA just-cause**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::lease_succession` is the **state succession rights вҖ” surviving family member lease-assumption compliance table** вҖ” distinct from `tenant_death_termination` (which covers the estate's right to TERMINATE the lease) by addressing the COMPETING right of a family member living with the deceased tenant to ASSUME or RENEW the lease in their own name. Only NY has a comprehensive succession framework for rent-regulated units; NJ provides limited anti-eviction protections; the rest leave succession to the lease, which extinguishes on the named tenant's death.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | States | Adult residency | Senior/disabled residency | Non-traditional family | Rent-regulated only |
|--------|--------|-----------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| `NewYorkRentRegulatedSuccession` | NY | **2 years** | **1 year** | **Yes** | **Yes** (rent-stabilized or rent-controlled) |
| `NewJerseyAntiEvictionImmediateFamily` | NJ | None | None | No (traditional family only) | No |
| `DefaultLeaseGovernsNoSuccession` | 48 other states + DC | (n/a) | (n/a) | (n/a) | (n/a) |

**NY RSC В§ 2523.5(b)(1)** (DHCR, 1987) is the most expansive U.S. succession framework. The family-member definition reaches BEYOND traditional blood / marriage relationships to include non-traditional family members (aunts, uncles, nieces, nephews, cousins, unmarried couples, LGBQT couples) demonstrating an "emotionally and financially committed and interdependent relationship." This non-traditional-family inclusion is unique to NY among the modeled regimes.

**NY 2-year-vs-1-year residency**: the standard adult successor must show 2 years of primary co-residency before the named tenant's death/permanent vacancy. Senior citizens (age 62+) and disabled persons get a reduced 1-year threshold to recognize their potentially shorter ability to plan and maintain housing stability.

**NY rent-regulated-only scope**: NY succession applies ONLY to rent-stabilized or rent-controlled units. Market-rate NY tenants have no statutory succession right вҖ” they fall through to the lease (extinguishes on death) like the 48 default-regime states.

**NJ Anti-Eviction Act protection** is narrower: В§ 2A:18-61.1 bars no-fault eviction of immediate family members residing with the deceased tenant, but does NOT create an affirmative right to renew the lease in the successor's name. Non-traditional family members get NO protection in NJ.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-succession-check`. Twenty-six tests pin: **3 regime classifications** (NY / NJ + default AL/CA/FL/TX/WA/DC/WY/MA); **NY 2-year residency traditional family eligible**; **NY 1-year residency traditional family NOT eligible** (regression вҖ” strict вүҘ 2 years adult); **NY senior 1-year residency eligible**; **NY non-traditional family eligible** (regression target вҖ” unique to NY); **NY not-family-member not eligible**; **NY non-rent-regulated unit not eligible** (regression вҖ” RSC В§ 2523.5 scope limit); **NY not-primary-resident not eligible**; **NY disabled 1-year residency eligible** (reduced threshold); **NJ traditional family protected**; **NJ non-traditional family NOT protected** (regression вҖ” distinguishes NJ from NY); **NJ no residency requirement** (different mechanism than NY); default state no succession right; default state traditional family still no right; **2 citation regression targets** (NY RSC В§ 2523.5(b)(1) + 2 years + 1 year + non-traditional family; NJ 2A:18-61.1 + Anti-Eviction Act); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **3 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (NY-only rent-regulated-succession / NJ-only anti-eviction / NY-only non-traditional-family-eligible); NY eligible note describes regime; NJ non-traditional note explains "not within statutory family definition"; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::rent_credit_reporting` is the **state tenant rent-payment-to-credit-bureau reporting compliance table** вҖ” California is currently the only state with a mandatory landlord-offer requirement. Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1954.06 (added by AB 2747, eff. 2025-04-01) requires landlords with 15+ residential units to offer tenants positive rent reporting to credit bureaus, with written notice at lease signing AND annually, and a fee cap = lesser of $10/month or actual cost.

**Two regimes**:

| Regime | States | Effective | Unit threshold | Fee cap | Annual notice |
|--------|--------|-----------|----------------|---------|----------------|
| `CaliforniaAB2747RentReporting` | CA | **2025-04-01** | **15 units** | lesser of $10/month or actual cost | **Yes** (lease + annual) |
| `NoStateRentReportingRequirement` | 49 other states + DC | (none) | (n/a) | (n/a) | (n/a) |

**CA 2-prong fee cap**: the maximum permissible monthly fee is the LESSER of $10/month OR the landlord's actual cost. If the landlord incurs zero cost (e.g., uses a free third-party service), the maximum fee permitted is **$0** вҖ” landlords cannot profit from the program. Charging $5/month when actual cost is $3/month is non-compliant.

**CA dual-notice requirement**: AB 2747 requires both initial notice at lease signing AND annual renewal notice. Missing either prong creates non-compliance.

**Effective-date gating**: any lease signed before 2025-04-01 is not subject to the requirement; CA landlords with leases in effect before that date must begin offering the option at the next lease anniversary or annual notice cycle.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-credit-reporting-check`. Twenty-two tests pin: **CA AB 2747 regime classification + default for 49 other states**; **CA pre-effective-date 2025-03-31 statute not in effect**; **CA on-effective-date 2025-04-01 statute in effect**; **CA 14 units below threshold no obligation**; **CA 15 units at threshold obligation applies** (boundary regression target); **CA all-notice-provided compliant**; **CA no-initial-notice non-compliant**; **CA no-annual-notice non-compliant** (regression вҖ” both prongs required); **CA fee at or below actual cost compliant**; **CA fee capped at $10 when actual cost higher** ($15 cost вҶ’ max $10); **CA fee above $10 cap non-compliant**; **CA fee above actual cost non-compliant even when below $10** (regression вҖ” actual cost is the binding cap when lower than $10); **CA zero actual cost zero permitted fee** (regression target вҖ” cannot profit); default state TX no obligation; **citation mentions В§ 1954.06 + AB 2747 + 2025-04-01 + 15+ units + $10/month**; **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **CA-only single-state-uniqueness invariant**; **CA pre-effective note describes "not yet in effect"**; **CA below-threshold note explains "threshold is 15 units"**; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::winter_eviction_protections` is the **state/municipal winter and weather-based eviction protection compliance check** вҖ” addresses statutory restrictions on landlord ability to evict during hazardous weather conditions or specified winter periods. Distinct from `snow_removal_responsibility` (landlord duty to clear snow/ice from premises) and from `heat_requirements` (habitability heat minimums).

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Cold threshold | Heat threshold | Precipitation | Holiday moratorium |
|--------|-----------|----------------|----------------|----------------|---------------------|
| `DistrictOfColumbia` | DC Code В§ 42-3505.01(k) | NWS predicts **< 32В°F** at 8 AM at Reagan National | NWS predicts **> 95В°F** at 8 AM | **Yes** вҖ” actively falling at unit | No |
| `CookCountyIllinois` | Cook County Sheriff Order | **вүӨ 15В°F** at 8 AM | No | No | **Dec 19 вҖ“ Jan 5** annual |
| `Default` | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**DC's three-prong weather rule** is the strongest US statutory weather-based eviction restriction. В§ 42-3505.01(k)(1) (sub-freezing), (k)(2) (precipitation), (k)(3) (extreme heat) operate independently вҖ” any one triggers blocks eviction that day.

**Cook County uses sheriff-execution discretion** rather than statutory restriction вҖ” the sheriff's published policy controls when eviction orders will not be executed. The 15В°F cold cutoff is BELOW DC's 32В°F sub-freezing rule (Cook County is more permissive); the holiday moratorium (Dec 19 - Jan 5) has no DC counterpart.

**Boundary semantics**: DC sub-freezing is STRICT BELOW 32В°F (at 32В°F eviction permitted); DC extreme heat is STRICT ABOVE 95В°F (at 95В°F eviction permitted); Cook County cold is INCLUSIVE (вүӨ 15В°F blocks; 16В°F permits).

**Restriction priority**: in DC, sub-freezing fires before precipitation if both apply; in Cook County, holiday moratorium fires before temperature check.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/winter-eviction-protections`. Twenty-three tests pin: **DC sub-freezing 31В°F blocks** + В§ 42-3505.01(k)(1) citation; **DC at 32В°F boundary eviction permitted** (strict-below required); **DC extreme heat 96В°F blocks** + В§ 42-3505.01(k)(3); **DC at 95В°F boundary permitted**; **DC precipitation blocks** + В§ 42-3505.01(k)(2); **DC mild-weather-no-precipitation permitted**; **Cook County holiday moratorium blocks** + "December 19 through January 5" citation; **Cook County 15В°F blocks**; **Cook County 14В°F blocks**; **Cook County 16В°F permitted** (boundary regression); **Cook County extreme weather safety threat blocks**; **Cook County normal conditions permitted**; **default no obligation even with extreme inputs**; **DC negative-temperature blocks**; **DC temperature-priority-over-precipitation** (sub-freezing fires first when both apply); **Cook County holiday-priority-over-temperature**; **jurisdiction routing DC + IL/Cook + IL/DuPageвҶ’Default + case-insensitive**; **only-DC-has-precipitation-restriction** 3-regime invariant; **only-DC-has-extreme-heat-restriction** 2-regime invariant; **only-Cook-County-has-holiday-moratorium** 3-regime invariant; **DC threshold chronology 31/32/95/96** (single-test 4-temperature regression вҖ” 31 blocks + 32 permits + 95 permits + 96 blocks); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 42-3505.01(k)(1)/(k)(2)/(k)(3) + Cook County Sheriff Order).

`traderview-expense::window_guard_requirements` is the **state window-guard landlord compliance check** вҖ” child-safety regulation for multi-family housing. Two jurisdictions have substantive statutory requirements with sharply different trigger models: NYC uses a **proactive/mandatory** model (install automatically when a qualifying child is present), while NJ uses a **reactive/on-request** model (install only after written tenant request). Cost allocation also differs: NYC landlord bears 100%; NJ allows up to $20 per guard pass-through to the requesting tenant.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Trigger | Lease notice | Annual notice | Cost cap | Maintenance |
|--------|---------|---------|--------------|----------------|----------|-------------|
| `NewYorkCity` | NYC Admin Code В§ 27-2043.1 + NYC Health Code В§ 131.15 (Local Law 31 of 1976) | **PROACTIVE** вҖ” child вүӨ10 resides | Not required | **Required Jan 1вҖ“15** to all tenants of every multiple dwelling | $0 (landlord bears cost) | Tenant-return-or-inspect by Mar 1 |
| `NewJersey` | N.J.S.A. 55:13A-7.13 + 7.14 | **REACTIVE** вҖ” written tenant request + child вүӨ10 | **Required** (conspicuous bold-face per В§ 7.14) | Not required | **$20/guard** pass-through to requesting tenant | **Biannual** maintenance inspection |
| `Default` | вҖ” | None | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**NYC specs** (Health Code В§ 131.15): window guards вүҘ 15 inches tall with horizontal bar spacing вүӨ 4.5 inches; required on every window except one ground-floor emergency-exit window per unit; public-hallway windows must have guards if any unit has a qualifying child.

**The proactive-vs-reactive distinction is the regression-critical pin**: in the same fact pattern (child вүӨ10 resides; no tenant request; annual notice provided), NYC triggers an installation obligation automatically while NJ does not.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/window-guard-requirements`. Twenty-one tests pin: **NYC not-multiple-dwelling no obligation** (< 3 units citation regression); **NYC missing annual notice violation** + В§ 131.15 + "Jan 1-15" citation regression (notice required regardless of child presence); **NYC child present no install violation** + В§ 27-2043.1 citation + "15-inch" + "4.5-inch" specs in note; **NYC child present + installed + notice вҶ’ compliant**; **NYC no child + notice вҶ’ compliant**; **NYC landlord bears cost** (max_cost_passthrough = 0); **NJ missing lease notice violation** + 55:13A-7.14 + "bold-face" citation regression; **NJ excessive cost pass-through violation** ($25 > $20 cap) + "$25 per window guard exceeds the $20" note; **NJ cost at $20 boundary compliant** (вүӨ strict); **NJ request but no install violation** + "written tenant request" citation regression; **NJ no request вҶ’ no install required + no violation**; **NJ installed but no biannual inspection violation** + "twice annually" citation; **NJ installed + inspected + cost cap вҶ’ compliant**; **NJ passes cost through to tenant** (max_cost_passthrough = 20); **default no obligation**; **NYC PROACTIVE vs NJ REACTIVE model invariant** (regression вҖ” same child-present scenario WITHOUT written tenant request triggers NYC violation but no NJ obligation); **jurisdiction routing** (NY/New York + NY/NYC вҶ’ NewYorkCity, NY/Buffalo вҶ’ Default, NJ вҶ’ NewJersey, CA вҶ’ Default); **case-insensitive lookup**; **citations pin authorities**; **NYC annual notice required + NJ not** (regression вҖ” NYC-only annual-notice requirement); **NJ biannual inspection only when installed** (regression вҖ” inspection obligation conditional on prior installation).

`traderview-expense::right_to_counsel_eviction` is the **state/municipal right-to-counsel eviction landlord notice compliance check** вҖ” addresses the affirmative statutory right of low-income tenants to court-appointed counsel during eviction proceedings, with concrete landlord-facing notice obligations. Distinct from `eviction_diversion_program` (pre-filing mediation duty) вҖ” this module addresses RTC DURING the eviction proceeding itself.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Income threshold | Alt eligibility | Notice req | Form language req | Brief services for all |
|--------|---------|--------------------|-------------------|-------------|----------------------|------------------------|
| `NewYorkCity` | NYC Admin Code В§ 26-1301 (Local Law 136 of 2017) | **200% FPL** | None | Yes вҖ” in eviction petition | No | **Yes** (NYC-only вҖ” every tenant regardless of income) |
| `Washington` | RCW 59.18.640 + RCW 59.18.057 (SB 5160 eff. 2021) | **200% FPL** | **Public assistance receipt** (TANF/SNAP/SSI) | Yes вҖ” in 14-day notice | **Yes** вҖ” specific statutory form language about legal aid + dispute resolution centers + appointed counsel | No |
| `Default` | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | None | вҖ” | вҖ” |

**Three regression-critical distinguishers**:
- **NYC-only brief-services-for-all**: NYC Local Law 136 provides brief legal services to EVERY tenant facing eviction in Housing Court, regardless of income. WA + Default tie eligibility strictly to income.
- **WA-only public-assistance alternative eligibility**: receipt of TANF/SNAP/SSI alone qualifies tenant for RTC even if income exceeds 200% FPL. NYC tests only income.
- **WA-only statutory-form-language requirement**: even when notice is provided, missing the specific RCW 59.18.057 form language (legal aid + DRC + appointed counsel) is its own violation. NYC has no form-language prong.

**NYC scope limitation**: В§ 26-1301 applies only to Housing Court + NYCHA administrative proceedings. General civil court is NOT covered.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/right-to-counsel-eviction`. Twenty tests pin: **NYC income under 200% FPL eligible + compliant** + brief-services-for-all; **NYC income above 200% FPL not full-rep but still brief services**; **NYC at 200% FPL boundary eligible** (вүӨ strict); **NYC missing notice violation** + В§ 26-1301 citation; **NYC NYCHA administrative covered**; **NYC general civil NOT covered** (regression вҖ” scope limited to Housing Court + NYCHA); **WA income under threshold eligible + compliant**; **WA public-assistance alternative eligibility** (regression вҖ” high-income + SNAP eligible); **WA high-income no public assistance not eligible**; **WA missing notice** + RCW 59.18.057 + "legal aid" citation; **WA missing statutory form language violation** (regression вҖ” separate prong from missing notice); **WA no brief services for non-eligible** (regression вҖ” only NYC has brief-services-for-all); **default no obligation**; **jurisdiction routing NY/New York+NYC + WA + Default** (NY/Buffalo вҶ’ Default); **case-insensitive routing**; **only-NYC-has-brief-services-for-all** 3-regime invariant; **only-WA-has-public-assistance-alternative** 3-regime invariant; **only-WA-has-statutory-form-language-requirement** 3-regime invariant; **income threshold 200% FPL invariant across NYC + WA**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 26-1301 + Local Law 136 + RCW 59.18.640 + SB 5160).

`traderview-expense::rent_payment_method` is the **state rent-payment-method landlord compliance check** вҖ” addresses HOW the rent may be paid (distinct from `advance_rent_limit` which caps the AMOUNT collectable in advance). Two states have substantive statutory restrictions on what payment methods a landlord can require or refuse; the rest defer to lease terms.

**Three regimes** with distinct floors:

| Regime | Statute | Cash-only | Electronic-only | Fee on non-electronic |
|--------|---------|-----------|------------------|------------------------|
| `California1947_3` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1947.3 | **Prohibited** absent В§ 1947.3(c) bounced-check 3-month carve-out + written notice | **Prohibited** under В§ 1947.3(a) | (Not a В§ 1947.3 modeled prong here) |
| `NewYork235G` | NY RPP В§ 235-g | Allowed (NY's restriction is on electronic-only only) | **Prohibited** under В§ 235-g(1) | **Prohibited** under В§ 235-g(2) |
| `Default` | вҖ” | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |

**California В§ 1947.3(c) bounced-check carve-out** вҖ” landlord may demand CASH ONLY for up to **3 months** after a tenant's dishonored check or stop-payment, BUT only after providing the required written notice that the prior payment was dishonored. Without the written notice, the carve-out is unavailable. Past the 3-month cap, cash-only reverts to a violation.

**Both statutes have anti-waiver provisions** вҖ” В§ 1947.3(d) and В§ 235-g(4) make any lease-clause attempt to waive these protections void as contrary to public policy.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-payment-method`. Twenty tests pin: **CA multiple-methods compliant**; **CA electronic-only violation** + В§ 1947.3(a) citation; **CA cash-only without bounce вҶ’ violation** (no carve-out); **CA cash-only with bounce but no notice вҶ’ violation** + В§ 1947.3(c) citation; **CA cash-only within carve-out (month 2 + notice) compliant**; **CA cash-only at 3-month boundary compliant** (вүӨ strict); **CA cash-only at 4-months beyond carve-out violation** + "4 months after bounced check" note; **CA cash-or-check compliant**; **NY multiple-methods + no-fee compliant**; **NY electronic-only violation** + В§ 235-g(1) citation; **NY fee on non-electronic violation** + В§ 235-g(2) citation; **NY electronic-only takes precedence over fee check** (when both would trigger); **NY cash-only is NOT a NY violation** (regression вҖ” NY's restriction is electronic-only only; cash-only allowed); **CA cash-only IS a CA violation** (regression вҖ” CA bars BOTH cash-only and electronic-only); **default no-violation under any input**; **state routing CA/NY/Default**; **case-insensitive lookup**; **CA fee-field irrelevant to CA path** (regression вҖ” fee field is NY-specific); **CA 3-month boundary carve-out invariants** (months 0-3 compliant; months 4-10 violation in single multi-month test); **citations pin authorities**.

`traderview-expense::rent_increase_notice_period` is the **state rent-increase notice-period landlord compliance check** вҖ” addresses the NOTICE PERIOD a landlord must provide BEFORE the new rent takes effect. Distinct from `late_payment_grace_period` (tenant late payment grace window) and `advance_rent_limit` (amount cap on advance rent collection). Three states have substantive statutory notice-period requirements with sharply different structures.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Notice period | Special rules |
|--------|---------|----------------|----------------|
| `CaliforniaAb1482` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 827(b)(1)/(b)(3) | **Two-tier**: 30 days for вүӨ10% increase; **90 days for >10%** (strict greater-than) | AB 1482 rent cap (5% + CPI or 10% whichever lower) makes the 90-day tier uncommon |
| `Washington` | RCW 59.18.140 (as amended May 2025) | **Uniform 90 days** (was 60 days pre-amendment) | Subsidized tenancy carve-out: **30 days**; RCW 59.18.720 caps increase at 7% + CPI or 10% (out of scope of notice module) |
| `Oregon` | ORS 90.323 | **Uniform 90 days** | **No increase in first 12 months** of non-week-to-week tenancy; **only one increase per 12-month period** |
| `Default` | вҖ” | 0 (lease controls) | None |

**Oregon's two unique extra-prong rules** are the most regression-critical: (1) ORS 90.323(3) bars ANY rent increase during the first 12 months of a non-week-to-week tenancy, regardless of notice; (2) ORS 90.323(2)(b) limits to ONE increase per rolling 12-month window. Neither California nor Washington has these.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-increase-notice-period`. Twenty-three tests pin: **CA under 10% + 30-day notice вҶ’ compliant** + В§ 827(b)(1) citation regression; **CA at exact 10% boundary still 30-day tier** (regression вҖ” > strict required for 90-day); **CA one basis point above 10% jumps to 90-day tier** (off-by-one) + В§ 827(b)(3) citation; **CA over 10% + 90-day notice вҶ’ compliant**; **CA under 10% + 29-day notice insufficient**; **WA uniform 90-day notice** + "amended May 2025" citation; **WA subsidized carve-out 30 days** + "subsidized" citation; **WA subsidized 29 days insufficient**; **WA non-subsidized 60 days insufficient under amended law** (regression вҖ” pre-amendment 60-day no longer suffices); **OR first-year prohibition violation** (11 months in вҶ’ no increase permitted regardless of notice) + ORS 90.323(3) citation; **OR at 12-month boundary no longer first year**; **OR second increase in 12 months violation** + ORS 90.323(2)(b) citation; **OR 90-day notice compliant**; **OR 89-day notice insufficient**; **OR first-year takes precedence over notice check** (regression вҖ” first-year prohibition fires first); **OR multiple-increase check after first year**; **default no violation any inputs**; **state routing CA/WA/OR/Default**; **case-insensitive routing**; **only OR has first-year prohibition** (single-test 3-regime invariant); **only OR has multiple-increase rule** (single-test 3-regime invariant); **only CA has two-tier notice** (single-test 3-regime invariant вҖ” WA needs 90-day for any increase including 5%); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 827(b)(1) + В§ 827(b)(3) + RCW 59.18.140 + ORS 90.323).

`traderview-expense::rent_escrow` is the **state tenant rent escrow / withholding-into-court compliance table** вҖ” distinct from `repair_and_deduct` (which lets tenant deduct repair cost FROM rent). Rent escrow lets the tenant PAY RENT INTO COURT while landlord remedies habitability defects вҖ” the landlord doesn't receive the rent until repairs are made. Four states have detailed statutory rent-escrow regimes; the rest rely on common-law warranty of habitability.

**Five regimes** with distinct procedural mechanisms:

| Regime | States | Mechanism | Trigger | Key feature |
|--------|--------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `MarylandRentEscrowAct` | MD | Standalone rent-escrow action | Health/safety defect + landlord refusal | **Rebuttable 30-day reasonableness presumption** |
| `MassachusettsCounterclaimDefense` | MA | Counterclaim in eviction action | Warranty-of-habitability breach | Court orders **fair use/occupation value** into court |
| `NewJerseyMariniHearingAdministrator` | NJ | Marini hearing (separate court motion) | Substandard dwelling | **Must deposit ALL unpaid rent** + court-appointed administrator |
| `ColoradoLimitedWithholding` | CO | Repair-and-deduct OR court order | Habitability defect | **Entire-rent withholding PROHIBITED** without court relief |
| `NoStatutoryRentEscrowFramework` | 46 other states + DC | Common-law only | (n/a) | Constructive eviction / abatement at trial |

**MD 30-day reasonableness presumption**: Real Property В§ 8-211 creates a rebuttable presumption that any period > 30 days from notice is unreasonable for the landlord to repair. Strict greater (31 days triggers, 30 days doesn't). Tenant may bring the action immediately after this period.

**NJ deposit-all-unpaid-rent gatekeeper**: the Marini-hearing mechanism (Marini v. Ireland, 56 N.J. 130 (1970)) requires the tenant to deposit ALL accrued unpaid rent with the court at the hearing вҖ” failure means the tenant cannot invoke the escrow remedy. Statutorily codified in N.J.S.A. 2A:42-85 et seq. with a court-appointed administrator who holds the rent + remedies defects.

**CO entire-rent withholding bar**: unlike MD/MA/NJ which allow rent into court/escrow as the remedy, CO В§ 38-12-507 forbids the tenant from withholding the entire rent without court order. The tenant must continue paying or pursue repair-and-deduct or formal court relief. The narrowest of the 4 statutory regimes.

**MA counterclaim mechanism**: c. 239 В§ 8A is the only regime in this table that arises through an eviction defense rather than a tenant-initiated action. The tenant raises the warranty-of-habitability counterclaim; the court may order the tenant to deposit "fair value of use and occupation" вҖ” which may be ZERO if the defects are severe enough.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rent-escrow-check`. Thirty-one tests pin: **5 regime classifications** (MD / MA / NJ / CO + default AL/CA/FL/NY/TX/WA/DC/WY); **MD baseline tenant may pursue**; **MD 30-day presumption not triggered at exactly 30 days** (regression вҖ” strict > 30); **MD 31-day presumption triggered**; MD no notice / non-material defect cannot pursue; **MA counterclaim asserted may pursue**; **MA no counterclaim cannot pursue** (regression вҖ” distinguishes MA mechanism); **NJ all unpaid rent deposited may pursue**; **NJ no deposit cannot pursue** (regression target вҖ” strict ALL-unpaid-rent gate); **CO not-withholding-entire-rent may pursue**; **CO withholding entire rent blocked** (regression вҖ” only CO has this bar); default state no statutory remedy; **4 citation regression targets** (MD В§ 8-211 + 30 days + "Rent Escrow Act"; MA c. 239 В§ 8A + "rent withholding statute"; NJ 2A:42-85 + Marini v. Ireland + "court-appointed administrator" + "ALL unpaid rent"; CO 38-12-507 + "ENTIRE rent is NOT permitted"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **5 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (MD-only 30-day-presumption / MA-only counterclaim-mechanism / NJ-only court-appointed-administrator / NJ-only all-unpaid-rent-deposit / CO-only entire-rent-withholding-prohibited); MD compliant note describes regime; MA non-compliant note mentions counterclaim; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::right_to_dry` is the **state tenant "right to dry" clothesline / drying-rack compliance table** вҖ” the broader "right to dry" movement has produced statutes in 19 states voiding HOA / condo clothesline bans, but most do NOT extend to landlord-tenant rentals. California is the only state that has explicitly extended the right to tenants via Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1940.20 (added by AB 1448, eff. 2015-09-08). Trader-tenants in HCOL markets get real savings on dryer energy costs.

**Two regimes**:

| Regime | States | Extends to tenants? | Key conditions |
|--------|--------|----------------------|-----------------|
| `CaliforniaTenantClotheslineRight` | CA | **Yes** (В§ 1940.20 / AB 1448 2015) | Private leased area only + no maintenance interference + no health/safety hazard + building-code/HOA compliance |
| `NoStatewideTenantClotheslineRight` | 49 other states + DC | **No** (HOA/condo statutes don't extend) | (n/a вҖ” lease governs) |

**CA 4-prong compliance test** (all required for installation to be permitted):
1. Installation in the tenant's **private leased area** (balcony, patio, dedicated yard) вҖ” common areas are excluded.
2. Does NOT interfere with landlord's maintenance of the premises.
3. Does NOT create a health or safety hazard.
4. Complies with applicable building codes and any homeowner association rules.

**The 19-state HOA "right to dry" list does NOT extend to tenants**: FL В§ 163.04, CO В§ 38-30-168, HI В§ 196-7.6, ME tit. 33 В§ 1521, MD Real Prop. В§ 2-119, VT 27 V.S.A. В§ 544, AZ В§ 33-1816, OR ORS 105.480, NM В§ 47-16-3.1, NV NRS 111.241, NC В§ 47C-2-119, and similar вҖ” these apply to deed restrictions and HOA / condo covenants only. A FL tenant cannot use these statutes against a landlord's lease prohibition.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/right-to-dry-check`. Eighteen tests pin: **CA tenant clothesline right regime + default for 49 other states**; **CA all 4 prongs met installation permitted**; **CA not private leased area not permitted** + diagnostic; **CA interferes with maintenance not permitted**; **CA health/safety hazard not permitted**; **CA non-compliant building code/HOA not permitted**; **default state FL no tenant statutory right** (regression target вҖ” В§ 163.04 doesn't extend to rentals); default state note mentions HOA-only; **2 citation regression targets** (CA В§ 1940.20 + AB 1448 + 2015-09-08 + "PRIVATE leased area" + "common areas EXCLUDED"; default mentions FL В§ 163.04 + "HOA / condo covenants only" + "do NOT extend to landlord-tenant rentals"); **51-state coverage**; non-empty citations; **2 single-state-uniqueness invariants** (CA-only extends-to-tenants / CA-only clothesline-regime); CA permitted note describes regime; CA multiple prong failures listed; lowercase state code normalizes.

`traderview-expense::sublet_consent` is the **state lease assignment + subletting consent rules table** вҖ” sibling to `mold_disclosure`, `bedbug_disclosure`, `heat_requirements`, `foreclosure_tenant_rights`, `lead_disclosure`, `detector_requirements`, `soi_protection`, `just_cause_eviction`, `dv_termination`, `lockout_penalties`, `application_fees`, `entry_notice`, `retaliation_windows`, `eviction_notices`, `late_fee_caps`, `deposit_interest`, `deposit_return_windows`, `lease_disclosures`, `habitability_remedies`, `rent_control`, `military_termination`, `security_deposit_caps`, and `contractor_1099`. Highly relevant to trader-tenants relocating for work, summer abroad, roommate additions in NYC/SF.

**Two state-law regimes** override the default contract-governs baseline:

| Regime                                | States    | Citation                                         |
|---------------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------|
| **StatuteReasonableStandard** (sublet) | NY / DC / VA / WA | NY RPL В§ 226-b / DC В§ 42-3505.55 / Va. Code В§ 55.1-1224 / RCW В§ 59.18.230 |
| **CaseLawReasonableStandard** (sublet) | CA        | Kendall v. Ernest Pestana, Inc. (1985) + Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1995.260 |
| **ContractGoverns** (sublet, default)  | 45 other states | Lease provisions enforced; landlord may withhold for any stated reason |

**Three assignment-consent regimes:**

| Regime                                              | States                     | Behavior                                              |
|-----------------------------------------------------|----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| **UnconditionalDiscretionButTerminationRight**      | NY (RPL В§ 226-b assignment branch) | Landlord may unconditionally withhold BUT unreasonable withholding gives tenant 30-day lease-termination right |
| **ReasonableStandard**                              | CA / DC / VA / WA          | Same reasonable-standard test as sublet               |
| **ContractGoverns**                                 | 46 other states            | Lease governs                                         |

**NY В§ 226-b sublet vs assignment asymmetry is load-bearing.** Sublet uses the reasonable-standard test (tenant may proceed if landlord refuses unreasonably). Assignment uses a different rule: landlord may UNCONDITIONALLY withhold consent for assignment, but if the withholding is unreasonable, the tenant doesn't get to assign вҖ” instead the tenant gets a 30-day lease-termination right. The compute returns `tenant_termination_right_available: true` for this path, distinct from `tenant_may_proceed`. Pinned by `ny_assignment_unreasonable_refusal_gives_termination_right` + `ny_assignment_reasonable_refusal_no_termination_right` + the `ny_only_state_with_assignment_unconditional_plus_termination` sweep verifying no other state has this regime.

**NY 4-unit building threshold for В§ 226-b sublet** is load-bearing. 4-unit buildings and larger get the statutory reasonable-standard protection; smaller buildings fall back to contract-governs. Pinned by `ny_sublet_reasonable_standard_applies_at_4_units` + `ny_sublet_does_not_apply_below_4_units` (3 units вҶ’ no statute coverage) + `ny_only_state_with_unit_threshold_for_sublet` (sweep verifying every other state has `building_unit_minimum = None`).

**NY 30-day deemed-consent window** is bright-line. Tenant requests consent вҶ’ landlord has 30 days to respond вҶ’ at day 31 without response, consent is DEEMED granted. Pinned by `ny_deemed_consent_at_day_31_after_request` (fires) + `ny_deemed_consent_not_at_day_30` (still within window).

**NY В§ 235-f roommate law is uniquely statewide.** Tenant may add an adult occupant (spouse, family, roommate) regardless of any lease provision restricting occupancy, so long as occupancy doesn't exceed legal limit. NY is the ONLY state with this specific statute. Pinned by `ny_roommate_addition_protected_by_section_235f` + the `ny_only_state_with_roommate_statute` sweep.

**CA case-law standard has NO building-size threshold.** Kendall v. Pestana applies to every residential lease regardless of building size. Even a single-family rental gets the reasonable-standard test. Pinned by `ca_no_building_size_threshold` (1-unit rental qualifies).

**CA assignment uses the same reasonable standard as sublet** вҖ” Kendall doesn't distinguish. Distinct from NY's asymmetric regimes. Pinned by `ca_assignment_uses_reasonable_standard_not_unconditional` (CA tenant may PROCEED with assignment on unreasonable refusal; NY tenant may only TERMINATE).

**Reasonable refusal blocks tenant.** Landlord with objectively reasonable basis (financial unreliability of subletee, criminal history, lease-breach risk) can refuse even in reasonable-standard states вҖ” refusal is not unreasonable. Pinned by `ny_reasonable_refusal_tenant_blocked`.

**DC 30-day window mirrors NY.** В§ 42-3505.55 has the same deemed-consent mechanism. Pinned by `dc_30_day_window_deemed_consent_at_31_days`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/sublet-consent-check`. Twenty-six tests pin: 51-row coverage; NY sublet reasonable-standard at 4-units AND below 4-units (boundary); **NY 30-day deemed-consent window** (fires at day 31, not at day 30); NY unreasonable refusal tenant proceeds; NY reasonable refusal tenant blocked; **NY assignment unreasonable refusal gives lease-termination right** (not assignment-proceed right вҖ” load-bearing asymmetry); NY reasonable refusal no termination right; **NY В§ 235-f roommate-addition statutory protection**; CA case-law reasonable standard applies; CA no building threshold; **CA assignment uses reasonable not unconditional** (regression target distinguishing from NY); CA roommate no statute; TX contract governs; DC 30-day window mirror; VA reasonable standard no unit threshold; WA reasonable standard; unknown state; case-insensitive lookup; sorted all_states; non-empty citations; **5 reasonable-standard states pinned** (NY/DC/VA/WA + CA case-law); **NY-only unit threshold sweep** across 50 other states; **NY-only unconditional-with-termination assignment sweep**; **NY-only roommate-statute sweep**.

`traderview-expense::swimming_pool_safety` is the **California Swimming Pool Safety Act compliance module** вҖ” when a building permit is issued for the construction of a new swimming pool or spa OR the remodeling of an existing pool or spa at a private single-family home, the pool or spa SHALL be equipped with AT LEAST TWO of seven enumerated drowning prevention safety features under Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 115922(a). Trader-landlord critical for CA single-family rental properties with pools: non-compliance breaches the implied warranty of habitability AND exposes landlord to drowning-incident premises liability. Distinct from siblings `detector_requirements` (smoke + CO), `fire_sprinkler_disclosure` (fire suppression), `water_heater_earthquake_strap` (В§ 19211 seismic), and `lead_in_drinking_water_disclosure`.

**Two regimes** вҖ” California strict seven-feature menu vs Default no-statute:

| Regime | Authority | Permit trigger | Property scope | Minimum features |
|--------|-----------|----------------|----------------|------------------|
| California | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 115920-115929 + SB 442 Stats. 2017 ch. 670 | New construction OR remodel | **Private single-family home** | **2 of 7** |
| Default | Common-law premises liability + IPC В§ 305 + local pool ordinances | None statutory | N/A | N/A statutory |

**В§ 115922 seven drowning prevention features menu** вҖ” landlord must implement at least TWO:
1. В§ 115923 enclosure isolating pool from home
2. Removable mesh fencing per ASTM F2286 + self-closing/self-latching gate with key lock
3. Safety pool cover per ASTM F1346-23
4. Exit alarms on doors and windows providing direct pool access
5. Self-closing/self-latching device on doors providing direct pool access; release mechanism вүҘ 54 inches above floor
6. Pool alarm per ASTM F2208 (surface motion + pressure + sonar + laser + infrared)
7. Other equivalent means approved by local building official

Pinned by `ca_two_features_compliant` (2-feature boundary satisfied), `ca_one_feature_violates` (below minimum), `ca_zero_features_violates`, `ca_all_seven_features_compliant`, `ca_three_features_compliant`, `ca_two_minimum_boundary_satisfied`, `ca_features_2_through_7_count_correctly`, `ca_exit_alarms_self_closing_pair_compliant` (features 4+5), `ca_pool_alarm_other_approved_pair_compliant` (features 6+7), and `ca_seven_feature_count_max_at_seven`.

**SB 442 Stats. 2017 ch. 670 amendments (effective January 1, 2018)** вҖ” increased minimum from one to two features and added ASTM-based standards (F2286, F1346-23, F2208). Pinned by `ca_note_pins_sb_442_eff_2018` and `ca_citation_pins_authorities` (В§В§ 115920-115929 + SB 442 + Stats. 2017 ch. 670 + ASTM F2286 + F1346-23 + F2208).

**В§ 115922(a) statute-engagement gate** вҖ” applies only when building permit issued for new construction OR remodel AND property is private single-family home. No permit OR multifamily property вҶ’ no engagement (multifamily pools governed by California Code of Regulations Title 22 + Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 116025 et seq. CalCode framework). Pinned by `ca_no_permit_no_statute_engagement`, `ca_multifamily_no_statute_engagement`, `ca_remodel_triggers_statute`, `ca_remodel_with_one_feature_violates` (remodel + 1 feature still violates), `permit_trigger_truth_table` (3-cell sweep), `ca_minimum_features_required_only_when_statute_engages`, `ca_note_pins_multifamily_carve_out`, and `ca_uniquely_engages_statute_invariant`.

**Default fallback** вҖ” no statutory pool-safety feature requirement at permit issuance; common-law premises liability + IPC В§ 305 (where adopted) + local pool ordinances may apply. Most states require some form of pool barrier (typically 48-inch fencing) but lack CA's seven-feature menu + two-minimum framework. Pinned by `default_compliant_no_statute`, `default_zero_features_still_compliant`, `default_citation_pins_premises_liability`, and `default_note_pins_48_inch_fencing_baseline`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/swimming-pool-safety`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA two features compliant**; **CA one feature violates** (В§ 115922(a)); **CA zero features violates**; **CA all seven features compliant**; **CA no permit no statute engagement**; **CA multifamily no statute engagement** (CalCode framework); **CA remodel triggers statute**; **CA remodel with one feature violates**; **CA citation pins authorities**; **CA note pins two minimum threshold**; **CA note pins seven features enumeration** (ASTM F2286 + F1346-23 + F2208 + 54 inches); **CA note pins SB 442 eff 2018**; **CA note pins multifamily carve-out** (CalCode + Title 22); **CA two minimum boundary satisfied**; **CA three features compliant**; **CA features 2 through 7 count correctly**; **CA exit alarms + self-closing pair compliant** (features 4+5); **CA pool alarm + other approved pair compliant** (features 6+7); **default compliant no statute**; **default zero features still compliant**; **default citation pins premises liability**; **default note pins 48-inch fencing baseline**; **two regimes routed correctly**; **CA uniquely engages statute invariant**; **permit trigger truth table** (3-cell sweep: NewConstruction/Remodel/None); **CA minimum features required only when statute engages**; **CA seven feature count max at seven**.

`traderview-expense::radon_disclosure` is the **state radon disclosure + testing compliance table** вҖ” sibling to `mold_disclosure`, `bedbug_disclosure`, and `lead_disclosure`. EPA-recommended action level is **4.0 pCi/L** (picocuries per liter); levels at/above this are recommended for mitigation though EPA cannot mandate landlord action.

**Five state regimes** across 51 jurisdictions:

| Regime                                            | States                              |
|---------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| **Comprehensive landlord disclosure**             | IL (420 ILCS 46/ Radon Awareness Act eff. 2024) вҖ” uniquely on this regime |
| **Mandatory testing + termination right**         | ME (14 M.R.S. В§ 6030-D) вҖ” strongest in country |
| **Lease-level warning**                           | FL (Fla. Stat. В§ 404.056)           |
| **Real estate transfer disclosure**               | CT / IA / MN / NJ / OR              |
| **No statewide statute**                          | 44 other states                     |

**IL Radon Awareness Act (420 ILCS 46/, eff. Jan 1, 2024) is the most comprehensive landlord-specific radon law in the country.** Applies to residential units on the 2nd floor or lower. Landlord must provide at application (before lease signing) or on tenant request:

1. Radon Guide for Tenants pamphlet
2. Disclosure of Information on Radon Hazards form
3. Copies of any radon test records from past 2 years

Tenant has 90 days from lease start to conduct own test, with 10-day window to share results with landlord.

**IL 2nd-floor coverage threshold is load-bearing.** 3rd floor and above are exempt. 2nd floor exact qualifies (boundary inclusive). Basement (floor 0) is below 2nd floor вҶ’ covered. Pinned by `il_floor_2_at_coverage_boundary` (2nd floor covered) + `il_floor_3_above_coverage_threshold` (3rd floor exempt) + `il_floor_0_basement_covered` (basement = floor 0 covered).

**ME 14 M.R.S. В§ 6030-D mandatory testing + termination right is the strongest regime in the country.** Landlord must conduct radon testing in each building, provide written notice with results, and notify tenants of right to test. If radon вүҘ 4.0 pCi/L is NOT mitigated, **either party may end the lease with 30 days' notice.** Pinned by `me_action_level_unmitigated_triggers_termination_right` ($4.5 pCi/L unmitigated вҶ’ termination right available) + `me_action_level_with_mitigation_no_termination_right` (mitigation cures) + `me_below_action_level_no_termination_right` (3.9 below threshold) + `me_exact_action_level_4_0_triggers` (boundary inclusive).

**EPA 4.0 pCi/L action level universal** across all rows. Pinned by `epa_action_level_universal_4_pcil` (sweep verifying every row has the same threshold).

**IL is uniquely on ComprehensiveLandlordDisclosure regime.** No other state matches. Pinned by `il_only_state_with_comprehensive_disclosure_regime` (sweep across 50 other states).

**ME is uniquely on MandatoryTestingAndTerminationRight regime.** Combination of mandatory testing + lease-termination right is found nowhere else. Pinned by `me_only_state_with_mandatory_testing_and_termination_right` (sweep).

**Three disclosure violations independently pinned for IL**: missing pamphlet, missing disclosure form, missing prior test records. Each produces a distinct violation message.

**Real estate transfer states (CT/IA/MN/NJ/OR)** require radon disclosure in sale-focused real estate transactions; often extended by lease practice to rental but not strictly required for tenancy. The module flags `disclosure_required: true` for these states.

**FL lease-level warning** is universal (every lease contains the statutory warning language) but the module treats default-flag inputs as compliant (the warning is presumed in standard FL lease forms).

**No-statute states (44 jurisdictions)** report `no_statute_in_state: true` and complies even without any radon documentation вҖ” EPA pamphlet practice is common but not legally required.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/radon-disclosure-check`. Twenty-three tests pin: 51-row coverage; **IL floor 1 full disclosure compliant**; IL missing pamphlet / missing disclosure form / missing prior test records each independently violates; **IL 2nd-floor coverage boundary** (floor 2 covered, floor 3 exempt, floor 0 covered); ME mandatory testing required; **ME action level 4.5 unmitigated вҶ’ termination right** + mitigated cures + below-action-level (3.9) no termination + exact 4.0 triggers; FL lease-level warning regime default-compliant; NJ pamphlet required; no-statute states always comply; unknown state handled; case-insensitive; sorted all_states; non-empty citations; **IL-only ComprehensiveLandlordDisclosure sweep** across 50 other states; **ME-only MandatoryTestingAndTerminationRight sweep**; **EPA 4.0 pCi/L universal** sweep across all 51 rows.

`traderview-expense::asbestos_disclosure` is the **state landlord asbestos disclosure compliance check** for buildings containing or presumed to contain asbestos-containing material (ACM) or presumed asbestos-containing material (PACM). Rounds out the hazard-disclosure cluster alongside `lead_disclosure`, `mold_disclosure`, `radon_disclosure`, `flood_disclosure`, `meth_contamination_disclosure`, `fire_sprinkler_disclosure`, `bedbug_disclosure`, and `death_in_unit_disclosure`. Federal floor under 29 CFR В§ 1926.1101(k)(2) (OSHA construction standard) requires building owners to notify tenants of PACM/ACM presence WHEN CONSTRUCTION WORK IS PLANNED in occupied areas вҖ” but the federal standard does NOT impose a lease-signing tenant disclosure mandate.

**Six regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Lease-signing mandate | Notice deadline | Annual re-notification | OSHA construction notice |
|--------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|--------------------------|----------------------------|
| `California` | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§В§ 25915вҖ“25919.7 (Connelly-Areias-Chacon Act, 1989) | **YES** for pre-1979 buildings | **15 days** from knowledge | **YES** annual | If construction planned |
| `NewJersey` | N.J.A.C. 5:23-8 | No | None | No | If construction planned |
| `NewYork` | NY MDL habitability + Industrial Code Rule 56 | No | None | No | If construction planned |
| `FederalOSHA` | 29 CFR В§ 1926.1101(k)(2) | No | None | No | **YES** for multi-tenant/commercial pre-1981 with construction |
| `FederalAHERA` | 40 CFR Part 763 + 15 U.S.C. В§В§ 2641вҖ“2671 | No (schools only) | n/a | n/a | n/a |
| `Default` | Federal OSHA construction floor only | No | None | No | If construction planned |

**California is the only state with a lease-signing asbestos disclosure mandate.** Health & Safety Code В§ 25915 (the Connelly-Areias-Chacon Asbestos Notification Act of 1989) requires building owners of buildings constructed BEFORE 1979 with knowledge of asbestos-containing materials to notify current tenants + employees + contractors + prospective owners + lenders within 15 days of obtaining knowledge. В§ 25916(b) further requires ANNUAL re-notification to current tenants. Penalty $500 per day per В§ 25917. Pinned by `california_pre_1979_with_knowledge_compliant_when_notice_provided`, `california_pre_1979_no_initial_notice_past_15_days_violation`, `california_missing_annual_renotification_violation`, and the 6-regime invariants `only_california_imposes_lease_signing_disclosure_invariant` and `only_california_requires_annual_renotification_invariant`.

**CA В§ 25915 building-year threshold is 1979.** Buildings constructed in 1979 or later are outside the disclosure mandate вҖ” the Connelly-Areias-Chacon Act covers pre-1979 buildings specifically. Pinned by `california_1980_building_outside_pre_1979_threshold` (statute does NOT engage; treated as compliant).

**CA В§ 25916(a) 15-day deadline is inclusive at the boundary.** Day 14 of grace period вҶ’ compliant. Day 15 (at boundary) вҶ’ still compliant per "within 15 days" reading. Day 16 вҶ’ violation. Pinned by `california_at_15_day_boundary_still_compliant`, `california_pre_1979_no_initial_notice_within_15_days_still_compliant`, and the boundary-truth-table invariant `ca_15_day_boundary_strict_in_violation_check`.

**OSHA 1926.1101(k)(2) construction-phase notification has three engagement gates**:
- Building type must be MULTI-TENANT residential OR commercial (single-family residences are OUTSIDE OSHA scope)
- Construction work must be planned in OCCUPIED areas
- Building must be constructed BEFORE 1981 (PACM presumption threshold)

All three must be satisfied; failure of any drops the building out of the OSHA notification mandate. Pinned by `federal_osha_construction_phase_notice_required_for_multi_tenant_pre_1981`, `federal_osha_single_family_outside_scope`, `federal_osha_post_1981_building_not_pacm_presumed`, `federal_osha_no_construction_no_notice_required`, and the 4-cell invariant `osha_construction_notice_only_for_multi_tenant_or_commercial_pre_1981_invariant` (2 covered building types vs 2 uncovered building types).

**Federal AHERA covers schools only, not landlord-tenant rentals.** The Asbestos Hazard Emergency Response Act (40 CFR Part 763) imposes triennial inspection + management-plan requirements on public + private nonprofit schools. Tenants in non-school buildings must look to OSHA + state habitability protections. Pinned by `federal_ahera_school_in_scope` and `federal_ahera_non_school_outside_scope_note` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/asbestos-disclosure`. Twenty-three tests pin: **CA pre-1979 with knowledge compliant when notice provided + 1980 building outside threshold + no initial notice past 15 days violation + within 15 days still compliant + at 15-day boundary still compliant + missing annual re-notification violation + no knowledge no violation**; **NJ no lease-signing disclosure mandate**; **NY no lease-signing disclosure mandate**; **federal OSHA construction-phase notice required for multi-tenant pre-1981**; **federal OSHA compliant when notice provided**; **federal OSHA single-family outside scope**; **federal OSHA post-1981 building not PACM presumed**; **federal OSHA no construction no notice required**; **federal AHERA school in scope**; **federal AHERA non-school outside scope note**; **Default no state specific mandate**; **only-California-imposes-lease-signing-disclosure 6-regime invariant**; **only-California-requires-annual-re-notification 6-regime invariant**; **OSHA construction notice only for multi-tenant or commercial pre-1981 4-cell invariant**; **citation pins authority per regime** (В§ 25915 for CA, N.J.A.C. for NJ, MDL for NY, В§ 1926.1101(k)(2) for OSHA, AHERA for federal-AHERA, Federal OSHA for Default); **sibling-module note across all 6 regimes** (lead_disclosure + mold_disclosure + radon_disclosure pairing UX-text regression); **CA 15-day boundary strict in violation check** (day 15 = compliant, day 16 = violation).

`traderview-expense::mold_disclosure` is the **state mold disclosure + remediation compliance table** вҖ” recent regulatory wave (2001-2025) starting with California's Toxic Mold Protection Act of 2001 (Civ. Code В§ 1941.7). Sibling to `bedbug_disclosure`, `heat_requirements`, `foreclosure_tenant_rights`, `lead_disclosure`, `detector_requirements`, `soi_protection`, `just_cause_eviction`, `dv_termination`, `lockout_penalties`, `application_fees`, `entry_notice`, `retaliation_windows`, `eviction_notices`, `late_fee_caps`, `deposit_interest`, `deposit_return_windows`, `lease_disclosures`, `habitability_remedies`, `rent_control`, `military_termination`, `security_deposit_caps`, and `contractor_1099`.

**Far fewer states have comprehensive mold statutes than bedbug or lead.** Most rely on the implied warranty of habitability. The verified-specific regimes are:

| Regime                                    | State(s)                                                                  |
|-------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Pre-lease known-mold disclosure**       | CA Civ. Code В§ 1941.7 + Toxic Mold Protection Act (Health & Safety Code В§ 26100) |
| **Move-in inspection report mold notation** | VA Code В§ 55.1-1215 (within 5 days of occupancy)                         |
| **Comprehensive annual inspection + remediation** | NY NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 (Asthma-Free Housing Act) вҖ” NYC only       |
| **Remediation standards only**            | MD В§ 8-208.2 (Healthy Homes Act 2008, expanded 2025) вҖ” 30-day window     |
| **Habitability covenant only**            | 32 states вҖ” implied warranty applies, no specific mold statute            |
| **No statewide statute**                  | 9 states (AK / AR / GA / ID / LA / MO / MS / SD / WY)                    |

**CA is uniquely on the pre-lease known-mold disclosure regime.** No other state in the table requires pre-lease disclosure of known mold conditions. Pinned by `ca_only_pre_lease_state_pinned` (sweep verifying no other state has this regime).

**VA is uniquely on the move-in report mold notation regime.** В§ 55.1-1215 requires the move-in report (delivered within 5 days of occupancy) to include disclosure of any visible mold in readily accessible areas. Pinned by `va_only_move_in_report_state_pinned`.

**NY (NYC only) on Comprehensive Annual Inspection.** NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 (Asthma-Free Housing Act) requires annual mold inspections in buildings with 3+ apartments, licensed remediation over 10 sq ft, and informational materials. The state itself has no mold statute вҖ” NYC LL55 is municipal. Pinned by `ny_only_comprehensive_annual_state_pinned` + `nyc_ll55_citation_correct` (citation pin for Asthma-Free Housing Act).

**MD is uniquely on Remediation Standards Only.** The Healthy Homes Act 2008 (expanded by 2025 regulations) imposes a 30-day remediation timeline after tenant report. No pre-lease disclosure required. Pinned by `md_only_remediation_standards_state_pinned` + `md_remediation_30_day_window_violation_at_31_days` + `md_30_day_window_complies_at_30_days` (boundary) + `md_complies_when_remediation_done_past_deadline` (60d post-report but remediated = complies) + `md_no_violation_without_tenant_report` (clock doesn't start without tenant report).

**NJ classification matters: HabitabilityCovenantOnly despite repeated bills.** The NJ Mold Safe Housing Act has been introduced in the legislature multiple times since 2013 (most recently in the 2026-2027 session) but has NOT been enacted as of the current cutoff. NJ landlords still owe habitability remediation but no specific mold-disclosure regime. Pinned by `nj_classified_habitability_only_pending_mold_safe_act` (deliberate classification, not a coverage gap).

**Habitability-only vs no-statute distinction matters at the UI layer.** Habitability-only states still owe habitability remediation under common law / state code; no-statute states have neither. The notes spell out the distinction. Pinned by `note_for_habitability_only_states_distinguishes_from_no_statute` (AL note contains "habitability covenant"; WY note contains "no statewide").

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/mold-disclosure-check`. Twenty-seven tests pin: 51-row coverage; CA pre-lease disclosure violation; VA move-in report notation violation; NY (NYC LL55) annual inspection violation; **MD 30-day remediation boundary** (30d complies, 31d violates, 60d-but-remediated complies, no-report-no-clock); **CA only on pre-lease regime** (sweep all 50 other states have different regimes); **VA only on move-in report regime**; NY only Comprehensive; MD only Remediation Standards with 30-day window; CA full compliance passes; habitability-only 10-state sweep (always comply); no-statute 9-state sweep (always comply); NJ deliberately classified habitability-only (regression target against future Mold Safe Act enactment); unknown state errors; case-insensitive; sorted all_states; non-empty citations; **NYC LL55 + Asthma-Free Housing Act in citation** (UX-text regression target); CA citation includes Toxic Mold Protection Act; note distinguishes habitability-only from no-statute; CA multiple-violation count stays at 1 (only one prong per regime).

`traderview-expense::balcony_inspection` is the **California SB 721 + SB 326 Exterior Elevated Elements (EEE) inspection compliance module** вҖ” when must a trader-landlord commission a licensed-inspector visual inspection of balconies + decks + exterior stairways + landings + similar wood-framed elements supported by wood framing attached to a multifamily building and elevated more than 6 feet above ground? Trader-landlord critical for multi-unit owners: missed first-inspection deadline of January 1, 2026 (per AB 2579's extension from January 1, 2025) exposes building to safety enforcement plus premises-liability exposure on any subsequent structural failure. Distinct from siblings `heat_requirements`, `detector_requirements`, and `cooling_requirements` (habitability-system standards rather than structural-inspection mandates).

**Three regimes** вҖ” SB 721 multifamily rental vs SB 326 condos vs Default:

| Regime | Authority | Scope | First inspection | Recurring cycle | Qualified inspectors |
|--------|-----------|-------|------------------|-----------------|----------------------|
| SB 721 | Cal. Health & Safety Code В§ 17973 + AB 2579 | Multifamily rental 3+ units | **January 1, 2026** | **6 years** | Architect / civil-or-structural engineer / **A/B/C-5 contractor + 5 yrs exp** / **certified building inspector** |
| SB 326 | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 5551 (Davis-Stirling) | Davis-Stirling Act condos | January 1, 2025 | **9 years** | Architect / civil-or-structural engineer **ONLY** |
| Default | Common-law premises-liability + local | Common-law duty | No statutory deadline | None | N/A |

**SB 721 4-element compliance test** вҖ” (1) inspection completed by January 1, 2026 deadline; (2) qualified inspector; (3) minimum 15% of each EEE type by direct visual inspection plus exploratory openings; (4) 120-day repair deadline after identified finding. Pinned by `sb721_compliant_passes`, `sb721_missed_deadline_violates`, `sb721_unqualified_inspector_violates`, `sb721_missing_15_percent_sampling_violates`, `sb721_121_day_repair_window_violation`, `sb721_120_day_repair_window_compliant` (boundary), `sb721_stacks_4_violations_when_all_4_failures` (all 4 elements failing simultaneously), and `sb721_repairs_completed_short_circuits_120_day_violation`.
