Pinned by `holder_exercised_no_realized_event` and `writer_assigned_is_basis_adjustment_event_no_gain_loss`.

**В§1256 override fires before everything else.** A В§1256 contract (broad-based index option, futures option, foreign currency option) bypasses В§1234 entirely. The compute returns `TaxCharacter::Section1256` so the caller routes to `section_1256.rs` for 60/40 MTM. Pinned twice вҖ” once against the dealer-in-options carve-out, once against the holder-exercise basis-adjustment path вҖ” to catch any future branch-ordering regression that would let В§1234 swallow В§1256 cases.

**Sign conventions in the input:** `premium` is always positive (the absolute dollars; sign is implicit from role вҖ” Writer received it, Holder paid it). `close_proceeds_or_cost` is the close-side dollars (sale proceeds for Holder/Sold; buyback cost for Writer/BoughtBack). Lapsed is treated as 0 close-side. This keeps the validation logic simple in the route layer.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1234`. Twenty-one tests pin: writer lapsed вҶ’ ST gain = premium; writer buyback below premium вҶ’ ST gain; writer buyback above premium вҶ’ ST loss; **writer held > 1 year still ST**; writer assigned вҶ’ basis-adjustment event with zero gain/loss; writer dealer-in-options вҶ’ ordinary character; holder sold short-term вҶ’ ST capital; holder sold long-term LEAP вҶ’ LT capital; holder lapsed вҶ’ capital loss of premium; holder LEAP lapsed вҶ’ LT capital loss (the patient-LEAP-holder trap); holder exercised вҶ’ basis-adjustment event; **365-day boundary holder ST**; **366-day boundary holder LT**; holder ordinary underlying вҶ’ ordinary regardless of holding period (В§1234(a)(3) carve-out); В§1256 underlying bypasses В§1234; writer sold to another writer вҶ’ still ST under В§1234(b); zero premium writer lapsed вҶ’ zero gain; **В§1256 override priority over dealer flag** (branch-ordering regression target); **В§1256 override priority over holder exercise** (similar); note describes actual holding-period days for ST/LT-boundary UX; writer note explicitly states "regardless of option holding period" (UX-text regression target).

`traderview-expense::section_1234b` is the **IRC В§1234B securities-futures-contract character module** вҖ” the redirect target from `section_1234a` (which explicitly excludes SFCs) and the holding-period sibling of `section_1256` (which absorbs only DEALER SFCs). Governs the character of gain or loss on the sale, exchange, or termination of any securities futures contract (single-stock future or narrow-based security index future per Securities Exchange Act В§ 3(a)(55)(A) вҖ” В§ 1234B(c) definition) held by a non-dealer.

**Three statutory subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§1234B(a) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1234B(a) | Character **mirrors** the underlying property in the taxpayer's hands. Capital underlying вҶ’ capital; ordinary underlying вҶ’ ordinary. |
| В§1234B(b) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1234B(b) | SFC **to SELL** property (short position) with capital underlying вҶ’ **SHORT-TERM CAPITAL** regardless of holding period. Parallels В§ 1233 short-sale rule. |
| В§1234B(d) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1234B(d) | SFC is **NOT** a commodity futures contract. Prevents accidental fall-in to commodity-futures branches of В§ 1256. |

**Routing precedence** (the load-bearing fact-pattern question this module answers):

| Order | Condition | Outcome |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | Dealer SFC? (В§ 1256(b)(1)(E)) | **В§ 1256 60/40 split** (В§ 1256(a)(3)) вҖ” fires before В§ 1234B |
| 2 | Underlying ordinary? | **В§ 1234B(a) ordinary character** |
| 3 | Short position with capital underlying? | **В§ 1234B(b) SHORT-TERM capital regardless of holding period** |
| 4 | Long position with capital underlying? | **В§ 1234B(a) holding-period split** (В§ 1222(3) > 1 year = LT; else В§ 1222(1) ST) |

**The В§ 1234B(b) short-position-always-short-term rule is the load-bearing trader trap.** A retail trader who holds a SHORT single-stock-future position for 10 years still produces SHORT-TERM capital gain or loss on close вҖ” defeating every long-term-rate planning strategy. The corresponding LONG position uses normal holding-period math. Pinned by `short_sfc_capital_underlying_held_400d_still_short_term`, `short_sfc_held_3650_days_still_short_term_regression_critical` (10-year hold), and the holding-period-invariance test `short_sfc_holding_period_invariance` (5 days = 5000 days produce identical output).

**The В§ 1256(b)(1)(E) dealer override fires before В§ 1234B.** Dealer SFCs are В§ 1256 contracts and get the 60/40 split вҖ” even when the dealer-trader holds a SHORT position that would otherwise route through В§ 1234B(b) to pure short-term. Pinned by `dealer_sfc_short_position_still_60_40_not_short_term`, `dealer_override_fires_before_short_position_short_term_rule`, and `dealer_override_fires_before_ordinary_underlying_rule`.

**Character buckets are mutually exclusive.** Each result populates exactly one of `long_term_cents`, `short_term_cents`, `ordinary_cents` вҖ” or, for the dealer-SFC path, splits LT + ST per the 60/40 rule with ordinary at zero. Pinned by `exactly_one_of_long_short_ordinary_60_40_holds_per_result` and `dealer_60_40_parts_sum_to_gain_property` (property test across `[-1_000_000, -1, 0, 1, 7, 100, 999, 1_000_000]`).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1234b`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **long SFC + capital underlying + 400d вҶ’ LT**; **long SFC + capital underlying + 100d вҶ’ ST**; **long SFC boundary 366d вҶ’ LT**; **long SFC boundary 365d вҶ’ ST** (exactly 1 year not long-term per В§ 1222(3)); **long SFC loss вҶ’ LT**; **short SFC + capital underlying + 5d вҶ’ ST**; **short SFC + capital underlying + 400d вҶ’ still ST**; **short SFC + 3650d вҶ’ still ST (10-year regression critical)**; **short SFC loss вҶ’ ST**; **short SFC holding-period invariance** (5d = 5000d); **SFC + ordinary underlying + long position вҶ’ ordinary**; **SFC + ordinary underlying + short position вҶ’ still ordinary** (regression вҖ” В§ 1234B(b) only fires for capital character); **SFC + ordinary underlying + holding period irrelevant**; **dealer SFC routes to В§ 1256 60/40**; **dealer SFC short position still 60/40 not ST** (regression вҖ” В§ 1256 override beats В§ 1234B(b)); **dealer SFC loss 60/40 split**; **dealer SFC holding period ignored invariant** (5d = 2000d); **dealer SFC 60/40 rounding 1001 вҶ’ 600/401**; **dealer override fires before short-position ST rule**; **dealer override fires before ordinary-underlying rule**; **only-short-position-capital-underlying triggers В§ 1234B(b)** 4-combo invariant; **citation pins subsection per path** (В§ 1234B(a) + В§ 1222(3) for long-cap-LT; В§ 1234B(a) + В§ 1222(1) for long-cap-ST; В§ 1234B(b) + В§ 1234B(a) for short-cap; В§ 1234B(a) for ordinary; В§ 1256(b)(1)(E) + В§ 1256(a)(3) for dealer); **dealer 60/40 parts always sum to gain property** test over 8 amounts; **exactly-one bucket holds gain invariant**; **short-position note documents В§ 1233 parallel** (UX-text regression); **dealer note documents В§ 1256 routing** (UX-text regression); **ordinary-underlying note describes character mirror** (UX-text regression).

`traderview-expense::section_1234a` is the **IRC В§1234A right/obligation-termination character module** вҖ” companion to `section_1234` (option lapse/sale by holder/writer) and `section_1256` (mark-to-market on regulated contracts). Governs the character of gain or loss when a right or obligation with respect to property is **cancelled, lapsed, expired, or otherwise terminated** without becoming an exercise/sale. Covers cash-settled options, cancelled forwards, terminated swaps, abandoned real-property purchase options, and terminated В§1256 contracts.

**Three statutory paths**:

| Path | Statute | Applies when | Character |
|------|---------|--------------|-----------|
| В§1234A(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1234A(1) | Right/obligation on **capital-asset** property; **NOT** a В§1256 contract; **NOT** a securities futures contract | Capital вҖ” long-term if hold > 365 days (В§1222(3)); else short-term (В§1222(1)). Holding period of the **right** governs, not underlying. |
| В§1234A(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1234A(2) + В§1256(a)(3) | Terminated **В§1256 contract** (regulated futures, foreign currency contract, non-equity option, dealer equity option, dealer securities futures contract) | **60/40 split** вҖ” 60% long-term, 40% short-term **regardless of holding period**. |
| Out of scope | В§1234A(1) exclusion + В§1234B | **Securities futures contract** (single-stock or narrow-based index future) OR underlying property is ordinary (В§1221(a)(1) inventory, В§1221(a)(2) В§1231 property) | В§1234B governs SFC; В§165/В§1231 governs ordinary underlying. В§1234A does NOT apply. |

**The real-property-option-trap is the load-bearing trader-and-investor warning.** A taxpayer who pays $50,000 for an option to acquire raw land or a vacation home and then abandons the option does NOT get an ordinary В§ 165 abandonment loss вҖ” В§ 1234A(1) recharacterizes the abandonment as a sale of a capital asset, producing a CAPITAL loss (subject to the $3,000 capital-loss cap under В§ 1211(b) absent offsetting capital gains). Pinned by `real_property_option_abandoned_capital_loss_not_ordinary` with explicit "real-property-option-trap" UX-text regression target.

**В§1234A(2) overrides holding period for В§1256 contracts.** A trader who terminates a regulated futures contract 5 days into the position gets the SAME 60/40 split as a position held 5 years. Pinned by `section_1256_contract_short_hold_5_days_still_60_40` and the holding-period-invariance test `section_1256_holding_period_ignored_invariant` (5 days = 2000 days identical output).

**Securities futures contracts route to В§1234B, not В§1234A.** В§ 1234A(1) explicitly excludes "securities futures contracts" вҖ” a single-stock-future is governed by В§ 1234B (which treats sale/exchange/termination as capital, with > 1-year long-term split by holding period). Pinned twice: `securities_futures_contract_excluded_from_section_1234a` (short hold) and `securities_futures_with_long_hold_still_routes_to_1234b` (proves long hold still does not pull the SFC back into В§ 1234A).

**60/40 split rounding preserves the sum exactly.** Long-term part = `g Г— 60 Г· 100` (integer truncation toward zero); short-term = `g вҲ’ long_term`. For 1001 cents вҶ’ 600 LT + 401 ST. Property-tested across `[-1_000_000, -1, 0, 1, 7, 100, 999, 1_000_000]` by `section_1256_parts_always_sum_to_gain_property` so no rounding drift can ever produce a cent gap.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1234a`. Twenty-five tests pin: **В§1234A(1) capital-asset right held 400d long-term**; **boundary 366d long-term** (В§ 1222(3) requires > 1 year); **held 100d short-term**; **boundary 365d short-term** (exactly 1 year is NOT long-term); **0d short-term** (same-day cancellation); **loss short-term**; **loss long-term**; **real-property-option-trap regression** (UX-text); **В§1256 60/40 basic 1000 вҶ’ 600/400**; **В§1256 short-hold 5d still 60/40**; **В§1256 loss -1000 вҶ’ -600/-400**; **В§1256 zero gain вҶ’ 0/0**; **В§1256 rounding 1001 вҶ’ 600/401**; **В§1256 holding-period-ignored invariant** (5d = 2000d); **В§1256 note text mentions 60/40 + holding period ignored**; **SFC excluded short-hold**; **SFC excluded long-hold** (regression вҖ” long hold does NOT pull into В§ 1234A); **ordinary underlying not in scope** (any hold); **ordinary regardless-of-hold invariant** (10d = 2000d both produce OrdinaryNotInScope); **only-В§1256-routes-to-60/40 3-regime invariant**; **only-SFC-excluded-by-В§1234B 3-regime invariant**; **only-ordinary-skips-В§1234A 2-regime invariant**; **only-non-В§1256-uses-holding-period 2-regime invariant**; **citation pins subsection per path** (В§ 1234A(1) + В§ 1222(3) for LT, В§ 1234A(1) + В§ 1222(1) for ST, В§ 1234A(2) + В§ 1256(a)(3) for В§1256, В§ 1234B for SFC, В§ 1221 for ordinary); **В§1256 parts always sum to gain property test** over 8 amounts.

`traderview-expense::section_1233` is the **IRC В§1233 short-sale character + holding-period rules module** вҖ” paired with `section_1091` (wash sale) and `section_1259` (constructive sale) to close the trader anti-abuse trio for short positions. В§1259 came in 1997 to shut down "short against the box" deferral; В§1233 still applies wherever В§1259's 30-day post-short window doesn't trigger.

Three pieces:

- **В§1233(b)(1) вҖ” gain вҶ’ short-term.** If on the date of the short open the taxpayer holds substantially identical property for вүӨ 1 year, OR acquires substantially identical property between short open and short close, then any **gain** on closing the short is short-term вҖ” regardless of how long the property used to close the short was held. Prevents shorting against a short-term long position to lock in price while preserving capital-gain-rate optionality.
- **В§1233(b)(2) вҖ” holding-period reset.** When В§1233(b)(1) applies, the substantially identical property's holding period is **reset to begin on the date the short is closed**. FIFO across substantially identical lots (oldest acquisition first), capped at `short_shares` per Treas. Reg. В§1.1233-1(c)(3). Only the short-held lots reset вҖ” long-held lots are unaffected (the rule penalizes new positions, not LTCG-qualified existing ones).
- **В§1233(d) вҖ” loss вҶ’ long-term.** If on the date of the short open the taxpayer holds substantially identical property for > 1 year, any **loss** on closing the short is long-term вҖ” regardless of how long the close property was held. Prevents using a short sale to convert a long-term loss into the short-term-loss bucket. STCL is cash-flow-preferable because В§1212(b)(2) absorbs STCL first against the $3,000 ordinary-income offset; this rule forces the long-term character to stick.

**Both rules can trigger simultaneously** when the taxpayer holds BOTH short-held AND long-held substantially identical property at the time of the short. В§1233(d) governs losses; В§1233(b) governs gains; the sign of the close determines which rule fires. Pinned by `both_short_and_long_held_with_loss_subsection_d_wins` and `both_short_and_long_held_with_gain_subsection_b_wins`.

**Holding-period boundary is the В§1222 standard**: вүӨ 365 days = short-term (LTCG requires > 365). Day 365 exact triggers В§1233(b); day 366 does not. Pinned by `one_year_boundary_held_exactly_365_days_is_short_term` + `one_year_boundary_held_366_days_is_long_term`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1233`. Twenty-one tests pin: no substantially identical вҶ’ default ST; short-held at open + gain вҶ’ В§1233(b) + reset; long-held at open + loss вҶ’ В§1233(d); long-held at open + gain вҶ’ no rule (default ST); short-held at open + loss вҶ’ no rule (default ST); **acquired-during-short triggers В§1233(b)(1)(B)**; both short and long held with loss вҶ’ В§1233(d) wins; both short and long held with gain вҶ’ В§1233(b) wins (only short-held lots reset); FIFO resets capped at short_shares (150 candidates вҶ’ first 100 reset); 365-day boundary short-held вҶ’ В§1233(b) triggers; 366-day boundary long-held вҶ’ В§1233(d) on loss path only; zero gain/loss вҶ’ no rule; **reset date equals short-close date not short-open date** (regression target); during-short acquisition resets to close date; long-held lots never appear in reset list; combined (A)+(B) buckets in FIFO order by acquisition date; acquisition on short_sale_date is held_at_open not during_short (boundary classification); only-(A) bucket works; only-(B) bucket works; notes mention short close date when resets emit; notes mention loss amount when В§1233(d) fires.

`traderview-expense::section_1235` is the **IRC В§1235 sale or exchange of patents module** вҖ” provides AUTOMATIC long-term capital gain treatment to a qualifying "holder" who transfers ALL SUBSTANTIAL RIGHTS in a patent **regardless of holding period**. Even royalty-style periodic payments contingent on use, productivity, or disposition qualify вҖ” converting what would otherwise be ordinary royalty income into LTCG ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 1235](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1235), [Cornell LII 26 CFR В§ 1.1235-2](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.1235-2)).

**POST-TCJA importance**: TCJA (P.L. 115-97 В§ 13314) amended **В§1221(a)(3)** to add "patent, invention, model, design, secret formula, or process held by the taxpayer who created the property" to the list of property EXCLUDED from capital-asset treatment. An inventor selling OUTSIDE В§1235 now gets ORDINARY income on the gain. **В§1235 became MORE important after TCJA** вҖ” it is now the primary (often only) path for an inventor or pre-reduction-to-practice financial backer to obtain LTCG ([McDonald Hopkins вҖ” Tax reform's impact on patents](https://mcdonaldhopkins.com/insights/news/Tax-reforms-impact-on-patents), [Sunstein LLP вҖ” New Tax Bill and Inventors](https://www.sunsteinlaw.com/publications/the-new-tax-bill-and-its-effect-on-inventors-a-capital-result)).

**В§1235(b) "Holder" definition** вҖ” exactly two paths qualify:

| Path                                            | Requirements                                                                |
|-------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Inventor**                                    | Any individual whose efforts created the property                           |
| **Financial backer (pre-reduction-to-practice)** | Acquired interest from inventor BEFORE actual reduction to practice; paid consideration in money or money's worth; NOT employer of inventor; NOT related party under В§ 267(b) modified |

**В§1235(d) related-party modification** вҖ” В§1235(a) does NOT apply to transfers between persons in any В§ 267(b) or В§ 707(b) relationship, with **25% substituted for 50%** in the ownership threshold. Critically, brothers and sisters are **EXCLUDED from related-party status** under the В§1235 modification (unlike the normal В§ 267(b) which treats siblings as related). The module surfaces `related_party_under_267b_modified` so callers apply the modified test before calling.

**"All substantial rights"** test (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1235-2(b)) вҖ” transfer must NOT be limited in ANY of these three ways:

1. Limited geographically within the country of issuance
2. Limited in duration to less than the remaining patent life
3. Limited in fields of use to less than all the rights covered by the patent

Each limitation independently disqualifies. Pinned by `geographic_limitation_disqualifies` + `duration_limitation_disqualifies` + `field_of_use_limitation_disqualifies` + `multiple_rights_limitations_all_listed`.

**Three payment types all qualify** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1235-1(b)):

- Lump sum
- Periodic fixed payments
- **Payments contingent on productivity, use, or disposition** (royalties) вҖ” THIS is the load-bearing benefit: royalties would otherwise be ordinary income, but В§1235 transforms them into LTCG. Pinned by `contingent_payment_qualifies_load_bearing`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1235`. Twenty-three tests pin: **inventor baseline qualifies for LTCG**; **inventor with contingent royalty payments still LTCG** (load-bearing В§1235 benefit); **financial backer pre-reduction acquired qualifies**; backer-acquired-after-reduction disqualified; backer-who-is-employer disqualified; backer-without-consideration disqualified; **3 all-substantial-rights limitations each individually disqualify** (geographic / duration / field-of-use); multiple-rights-limitations all listed in note; **В§1235(d) related-party disqualifies** + unrelated party qualifies; **3 payment types all qualify** (lump-sum / periodic-fixed / contingent-load-bearing); not-holder + no-substantial-rights + related-party combined failure scenario all 3 listed in note; **disqualified note mentions TCJA В§ 13314 + В§ 1221(a)(3)** (post-TCJA importance); citation mentions all 5 relevant authorities (В§1235(a) / В§1235(b) / В§1235(d) / В§1.1235-2 / В§1221(a)(3)); $100B precision case still LTCG; zero gain qualifies; qualified note describes holder path "Inventor" + "LTCG regardless of holding period"; qualified backer note describes FinancialBackerBeforeReductionToPractice path; disqualified note says В§1235 DOES NOT APPLY + ORDINARY income.

`traderview-expense::section_41` is the **IRC В§41 Credit for Increasing Research Activities (R&D credit) module** вҖ” practical for algorithmic traders building custom trading systems, data pipelines, ML models, and software that qualifies as research under В§ 41(d). Two computation methods plus the В§ 280C(c) deduction-reduction interaction.

**Two computation methods**:

| Method | Formula | Startup variant |
|--------|---------|------------------|
| `Regular` (В§ 41(a)(1)) | **20%** Г— (QRE вҲ’ base amount); base = max(fixed-base-% Г— 4-yr avg gross receipts, 50% Г— current QRE) | **3%** fixed-base-% (В§ 41(c)(3)(B)) when no QRE in 3 prior years |
| `AlternativeSimplified` (В§ 41(c)(4)) | **14%** Г— (QRE вҲ’ 50% Г— prior 3-yr avg QRE) | **6%** Г— current QRE (В§ 41(c)(4)(B)) when no QRE in 3 prior years |

**Fixed-base-% cap** under В§ 41(c)(3): max 16%. The Regular method's base amount has both a floor (50% Г— current QRE) and a fixed-base-% computation; the floor binds when fixed-base-% Г— gross receipts is small.

**В§ 280C(c) interaction** вҖ” by DEFAULT, В§ 280C(c)(1) requires taxpayer to REDUCE the В§ 174 deduction by the amount of the В§ 41 credit (anti-double-dip). В§ 280C(c)(2) lets the taxpayer ELECT a reduced credit = full credit Г— **(1 вҲ’ 21%)** = 79% of full credit and keep the full В§ 174 deduction in exchange. В§ 280C(c)(3) requires the election be made on the ORIGINAL return вҖ” invalid if made on an amended return.

**В§ 174 capitalization** (post-TCJA 2022, now permanent via OBBBA): R&D expenses MUST be capitalized + amortized (5 yr domestic / 15 yr foreign), but the В§ 41 credit is computed on the FULL QRE despite the capitalization. The credit is independent of the deduction timing.

**Algorithmic trader worked example** (sibling to `section_174`): trader builds custom trading engine + ML model with $500K QRE; prior 3-year average QRE $200K. ASC base = $100K. Excess = $400K. Credit = 14% Г— $400K = **$56,000** gross credit. With В§ 280C(c)(2) election: $56K Г— 79% = **$44,240** net credit + full $500K В§ 174 deduction preserved.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-41`. Twenty-one tests pin: **ASC standard 14% Г— ($200K QRE вҲ’ 50% Г— $100K prior avg) = $21K**; **ASC startup 6% Г— current QRE when no prior 3-year QRE** + В§ 41(c)(4)(B) citation; **ASC QRE below 50% prior avg вҶ’ no credit**; **Regular 20% Г— excess over fixed-base** worked example ($200K QRE + 5% FBP + $1M gross receipts вҶ’ 50% Г— QRE floor binds вҶ’ $20K credit); **Regular fixed-base % capped at 16%** (regression вҖ” В§ 41(c)(3) cap binds even if FBP input is 20%); **Regular startup uses 3% FBP** (regression вҖ” startup overrides input FBP); **Regular 50% QRE floor applies when higher than FBP base**; **В§ 280C(c)(2) reduced-credit reduces by 21%** (79% net retention); **without В§ 280C election deduction-reduction applies**; **ASC vs Regular method selection paths**; **algo trader worked example $56K ASC credit**; **algo trader with В§ 280C election $44,240 net**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 41(a)(1) + В§ 41(c)(4)(A) + В§ 41(c)(4)(B) + В§ 280C(c)(2) + 21%); **negative inputs clamped**; **ASC startup + В§ 280C combination**; **Regular startup with В§ 280C election**; **ASC zero QRE вҶ’ zero credit**; **deduction-reduction flag inverse of В§ 280C election** (regression вҖ” election waives reduction); **Regular at 16% FBP cap boundary**; **ASC 50% base calculation correctness** ($50K prior avg вҶ’ $25K base вҶ’ $75K excess вҶ’ $10,500 credit).

`traderview-expense::section_408m` is the **IRC В§408(m) collectibles in IRA module** вҖ” the gold/silver/crypto IRA trap. Critical for any trader running a self-directed IRA. Companion to `section_408_d3` (IRA 60-day rollover) and `section_408A_d3` (Roth conversion 5-year aging).

**В§408(m)(1) general rule**: acquisition of a "collectible" by an IRA is treated as a **deemed distribution** of the purchase price вҖ” taxable income to the beneficiary plus a 10% additional tax under В§72(t) if under 59ВҪ. The IRA itself isn't disqualified; just the offending acquisition is recharacterized.

**В§408(m)(2) definition of "collectible"** = artwork, antiques, gems, metals, stamps, coins, alcoholic beverages, rugs, and "any other tangible personal property" specified by IRS regulations. Cryptocurrency is treated as a collectible per IRS guidance.

**Two narrow exceptions under В§408(m)(3):**

**(A) Statutory coin exception** вҖ” American Gold / Silver / Platinum / Palladium Eagles AND state-issued coins are permitted regardless of fineness. **The American Gold Eagle is the canonical edge case**: it's only 22-karat (.9167 fineness, BELOW the .995 bullion standard) but explicitly authorized by 31 U.S.C. В§ 5112 so the purity check is waived. Pinned by `american_gold_eagle_exempt_regardless_of_purity` and `eagle_coin_does_not_require_trustee_to_be_exempt` (Eagles don't even need trustee custody).

**(B) Bullion exception** вҖ” physical bullion meets the exception ONLY if BOTH (i) the fineness meets the metal-specific threshold AND (ii) it is in the physical possession of a qualified trustee (a bank or IRS-approved non-bank trustee):

| Metal     | Threshold | Bright-line (per 10,000) |
|-----------|-----------|--------------------------|
| Gold      | .995      | 9950                     |
| Silver    | .999      | 9990                     |
| Platinum  | .9995     | 9995                     |
| Palladium | .9995     | 9995                     |

**The "home storage gold IRA" trap is load-bearing.** Personal possession (gold in a home safe) fails the trustee-custody prong even with .9999 purity вҶ’ deemed distribution fires. Pinned by `gold_bullion_995_without_trustee_home_storage_trap` ($100k purchase price becomes $100k taxable distribution, plus 10% В§72(t) penalty if under 59ВҪ). The note explicitly mentions "home-storage trap" to surface this to the UI.

**Diagnostic flags** distinguish single-prong vs both-prong bullion failures so the caller can suggest the right fix:

| Path                          | `purity_meets_threshold` | `custody_qualifies` | Note phrasing                |
|-------------------------------|--------------------------|---------------------|------------------------------|
| Purity too low                | Some(false)              | true                | "purity below threshold"     |
| Custody fails (home storage)  | Some(true)               | false               | "home-storage trap"          |
| Both prongs fail              | Some(false)              | false               | "BOTH purity ... AND not ..." |
| Eagle/state coin              | None                     | (whatever)          | "statutory coin exception"   |

**Bright-line purity thresholds** are integer math (parts per ten thousand) вҖ” no Decimal precision drift. .995 = 9950 exactly; .9995 = 9995 exactly. Pinned by `gold_bullion_995_with_trustee_passes` + `gold_bullion_994_purity_fails` (9950 passes, 9949 fails). Distinct silver (.999 = 9990) vs gold (.995 = 9950) thresholds pinned by `silver_bullion_995_fails_too_low` вҖ” using gold's threshold for silver mis-classifies.

**Cryptocurrency in IRA вҶ’ prohibited.** IRS has signaled in several rulings that crypto held in an IRA is a collectible. Compute returns deemed distribution = full purchase price. Pinned by `cryptocurrency_in_ira_is_prohibited_collectible`. Self-directed IRA promoters mis-sell crypto IRAs constantly; this is the load-bearing trader-facing answer.

**The non-Eagle non-state foreign coin trap** вҖ” South African Krugerrand and other foreign gold coins aren't on the Eagle whitelist and aren't state-issued вҶ’ prohibited despite being mostly-pure gold. Compute calls this out by name in the note. Pinned by `other_coin_krugerrand_prohibited`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-408m`. Twenty-six tests pin: all four Eagle types exempt; state coin exempt; **Gold Eagle exempt regardless of .9167 purity** (load-bearing statutory exception); gold bullion .995/.9994 boundary; **home storage trap** ($100k вҶ’ deemed distribution with diagnostic flags); both-prongs-fail with combined note; silver .999 boundary distinct from gold; platinum .9995 vs .9994 boundary; palladium same threshold as platinum; crypto / artwork / antique / gem / rug / stamp / alcoholic beverage all prohibited; Krugerrand foreign-coin trap; bullion missing purity treated as failing; diagnostic flag combinations; **Eagle doesn't require trustee** (no В§3(A) custody prong, regression target); В§72(t) penalty mentioned in every prohibited note (UX-text regression target); deemed distribution zero for all exempt paths; very large purchase ($100M) no precision loss; citation correct per path (В§408(m)(3)(A) for Eagles, (3)(B) for bullion, (1) for prohibited).

`traderview-expense::section_83b` is the **IRC В§83(b) restricted-stock election module** вҖ” every founder and early employee receiving restricted stock or restricted stock units needs to decide within 30 calendar days of grant whether to file this election. One of the most consequential tax-position choices in the code.

**Decision economics:**

- **В§83(a) default**: ordinary income at VESTING = `FMV_vesting - amount_paid`. LTCG holding period begins at vesting.
- **В§83(b) election**: ordinary income at GRANT = `FMV_grant - amount_paid`. LTCG holding period begins at grant.

When stock appreciates significantly between grant and vesting (the canonical founder case вҖ” grant FMV вүҲ $0.001/share, vesting FMV $10+/share), the election converts what would be ordinary income at vesting into long-term capital gain at sale. For a founder with $10M of post-vesting appreciation, that's the gap between ~37% federal ordinary + state + FICA-Medicare and 20% federal LTCG + 3.8% NIIT + state вҖ” easily 20%+ savings on the appreciation.

**The 30-day deadline is bright-line and unforgivable.** No extension, no equitable exception, no judicial relief. Carta, Cooley, and the IRS agree: file by day 30 or the option is gone. Day 30 exact = valid; day 31 = invalid. Pinned by `election_filed_day_30_exact_boundary_is_valid` + `election_filed_day_31_invalid_per_bright_line` + `election_filed_day_zero_is_valid` (the three boundary cases).

**В§83(b)(2) forfeiture trap** is load-bearing. If the property is later forfeited (employee leaves before vesting), the В§83(b) election cannot be undone. Taxpayer paid ordinary income tax at grant on property never received. NO refund, NO deduction for the previously included amount. The only loss recognized is the **amount paid for the property** (usually zero for pure stock grants). Compute returns:

| Path                      | Forfeiture outcome                                                  |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Valid election + forfeit  | Grant-day ordinary recognized; NO refund; loss = amount paid only   |
| No election + forfeit     | Clean: В§83(a) never triggered; no ordinary income; loss = amount paid |
| Late election + forfeit   | Same as no-election forfeit вҖ” late election was invalid anyway      |

Pinned by `forfeiture_with_valid_election_no_refund_per_83b2`, `forfeiture_without_election_clean_no_income`, and `forfeiture_with_late_election_clean_no_income`.

**Capital gain character driven by holding-period start.**

| Election path  | Holding start | Sale вүӨ 365d from start вҶ’ STCG | Sale > 365d вҶ’ LTCG |
|----------------|---------------|-------------------------------|--------------------|
| Valid election | Grant date    | `election_sale_within_one_year_of_grant_is_stcg` | `election_sale_at_366_days_is_long_term` |
| No election    | Vesting date  | `no_election_sale_within_one_year_of_vesting_is_stcg` | `no_election_sale_one_year_after_vesting_is_ltcg` |

**Election savings field** compares ordinary income with election vs В§83(a) baseline. Negative = good (election reduced ordinary income); positive = bad (election was wrong choice because FMV dropped between grant and vesting). Pinned by `founder_grant_election_saves_ordinary_income` (вҲ’$9.99 negative = good) and `election_wrong_when_fmv_drops_after_grant` (+$2 positive = bad).

**Ordinary income clamps at zero** when amount paid вүҘ FMV at grant (i.e., underwater grant). Loss is recognized at sale, not as negative ordinary income. Pinned by `negative_grant_minus_paid_clamps_to_zero_ordinary`.

**Pathological inputs are bounded.** Election filed BEFORE grant date returns `days_grant_to_election = -1` and `election_timely = false`. Pinned by `election_before_grant_date_invalid`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-83b`. Twenty-four tests pin: timely election within 30 days valid; **day 0 / day 30 / day 31 boundaries** (the three bright-line cases); no election falls back to В§83(a); founder grant election savings (вҲ’$9.99); election with full $50 appreciation вҶ’ full LTCG; no-election same appreciation вҶ’ ordinary $9.99 + LTCG $40 split; election sale within 1 year STCG; **365 vs 366-day boundary**; no-election sale at 17 months from vesting вҶ’ LTCG; no-election STCG path; FMV grant above paid creates ordinary at grant; **election-was-wrong** (positive savings number); negative ordinary clamps to zero; not-yet-sold returns no gain; election before grant invalid (pathological pin); **very large grant precision** ($0.0001 вҶ’ $5000); day-31 late election uses vesting basis (not grant); note describes 30-day deadline explicitly ("day 14/30"); forfeiture with valid election + В§83(b)(2) call-out in note (UX-text regression target); forfeiture without election clean.

`traderview-expense::section_83c` is the **IRC В§83(c) substantial risk of forfeiture timing-rules module** вҖ” completes the В§83 stock-compensation triplet alongside existing `section_83b` (election to recognize at grant) and `section_83i` (qualified equity grants 5-year deferral). В§83(c) defines WHEN property is "substantially vested" вҖ” the point at which В§83(a) requires income recognition. The two prongs that delay vesting are (1) substantial risk of forfeiture under В§83(c)(1) and (2) non-transferability under В§83(c)(2). В§83(c)(3) carves out a special rule for В§16(b) Securities Exchange Act insiders.

**В§83(a) timing rule**: property transferred to a service provider is taxable on the **earlier** of (a) when transferable OR (b) when not subject to substantial risk of forfeiture.

**В§83(c)(1) substantial-risk-of-forfeiture conditions** вҖ” must be ONE of:
- **Future-performance-of-substantial-services condition** вҖ” full-time job duties qualify; ONE HOUR PER WEEK is NOT substantial.
- **Transfer-purpose condition** вҖ” performance metric, change-of-control, or hurdle event related to a purpose of the transfer.

Plus all three of: substantial possibility of forfeiture + service condition is substantial / purpose condition is likely to occur + forfeiture condition is likely to be enforced.

**В§83(c)(2) transferability**: property is "transferable" only when the transferee is NOT subject to a substantial risk of forfeiture. Restricted stock the holder cannot sell except to other restricted holders is NOT transferable.

**В§83(c)(3) В§ 16(b) insider restriction** вҖ” property held by a В§16(b) "insider" (officer, director, 10%+ shareholder of a В§12 SEA-registered company) is treated as subject to SRF AND non-transferable until earlier of:
- The 6-month В§16(b) short-swing-profit period expires
- The first day sale at a profit would no longer trigger В§16(b) suit

**Other insider-trading restrictions (Rule 10b-5, lock-up agreements) do NOT trigger В§83(c)(3)** вҖ” Congress chose В§16(b) as the ONLY securities-law restriction that delays В§83 recognition.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.83-3(c)** clarifies: SRF can ONLY be established through a service condition or a transfer-purpose condition. Likelihood of both occurrence AND enforcement matters.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-83c`. Fifteen tests pin: **substantial future service unvested** (3-prong gate met вҶ’ SRF exists); **1-hour-per-week NOT substantial вҶ’ vested** (regression target вҖ” Reg. В§ 1.83-3(c) hour-a-week not substantial); **non-substantial forfeiture possibility вҶ’ vested**; **employer unlikely to enforce вҶ’ vested** (regression вҖ” likelihood-of-enforcement prong); **transfer-purpose-condition path** (likely-to-occur вҶ’ unvested; unlikely вҶ’ vested); **В§83(a) earlier-of rule** (transferable property vested even with SRF вҖ” disjunctive trigger); **В§83(c)(3) 30-day-remaining 16(b) period вҶ’ unvested even with transferable=true** (regression target вҖ” 16(b) overrides В§83(c)(2)); **В§16(b) period expired (0 days remaining) вҶ’ vested**; **Rule 10b-5 NOT В§83(c)(3)** (regression вҖ” only В§16(b) delays В§83 recognition); no-condition path вҶ’ immediately vested + В§83(a) recognition required; **citation mentions all 6 relevant authorities** (В§83(a) + В§83(c)(1) + В§83(c)(2) + В§83(c)(3) + В§ 16(b) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.83-3(c) + "EARLIER" + "6-month"); note describes service-condition path; note describes В§16(b) path "30 days remaining"; note describes purpose-condition path.

`traderview-expense::section_83i` is the **IRC В§83(i) qualified equity grant 5-year income-tax deferral module** вҖ” TCJA addition (P.L. 115-97) and the natural companion to В§83(b). Where В§83(b) ACCELERATES income to grant date to lock in low FMV, В§83(i) DEFERS income away from the vesting/exercise date вҖ” up to 5 years вҖ” so the employee can wait out the privateвҶ’public transition before owing federal income tax on illiquid stock. Directly relevant to any pre-IPO startup employee receiving NQSOs or RSUs.

**В§83(i)(2)(C) eligible corporation** test (both required):

1. No stock of the corporation (or any predecessor) was readily tradable on an established securities market in any preceding year.
2. The corporation has a written equity incentive plan under which вүҘ **80% of all US employees** are granted stock options or RSUs with the SAME RIGHTS AND PRIVILEGES to receive qualified stock.

**В§83(i)(3)(B) excluded employees** (any of these вҶ’ election unavailable; family attribution applies):

| Exclusion                          | Look-back window                          |
|------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| 1% owner                           | Current or any of **10 preceding** calendar years |
| CEO or CFO                         | Current or any of **10 preceding** taxable years |
| One of 4 highest compensated officers | Current or any of **10 preceding** taxable years |

**В§83(i)(1)(B) deferral end triggers** (earliest of these ends deferral):

- 5 years from option exercise / RSU settlement (statutory maximum)
- Stock becomes readily tradable on an established securities market (IPO trigger)
- Stock is transferred to the employer (buyback / cash-out)
- Employee revokes the В§83(i) election
- Employee becomes an excluded employee (e.g., promoted to CFO mid-deferral)

**FICA NEVER deferred** вҖ” В§83(i) only defers federal income tax. Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) employment taxes per В§3121 are owed at the normal В§83(a) vesting/exercise date and the employer must withhold them then. This is the single most easily missed feature of В§83(i); the module surfaces FICA owed via `fica_due_at_vesting` regardless of eligibility outcome ([IRS Notice 2018-97](https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-drop/n-18-97.pdf), [Trucker Huss 2018 analysis](https://www.truckerhuss.com/2018/01/new-section-83i-of-the-internal-revenue-code-qualified-equity-grant-programs-permit-employees-to-elect-to-defer-income-taxes-on-stock-options-or-rsus/)).

**В§83(i)(4)(A) 30-day election window** вҖ” strict bright-line: election must be filed within 30 days after the substantial vesting / exercise date. Day 30 complies; day 31 voids the election. Pinned by `election_window_day_30_exactly_complies` + `election_window_day_31_violates`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-83i`. Twenty-one tests pin: **eligible baseline вҶ’ 5-year deferral active** (vest 2025-01-15 вҶ’ end 2030-01-15); **FICA $76,500 due immediately** ($500k Г— 15.3%) despite income-tax deferral; FICA at employee-only 7.65% rate path ($500k Г— 7.65% = $38,250); **all 3 В§83(i)(3)(B) exclusions individually disqualify** (1% owner / CEO-CFO / top-4 paid); corp not private disqualifies; **plan failing 80% broad-based disqualifies**; stock already tradable disqualifies; **30-day window: day 30 exact complies / day 31 voids**; election before vesting void; **IPO trigger ends deferral early** + revocation overrides 5y + employer buyback ends + employee-promoted-to-CFO mid-deferral ends; **earliest-of-multiple-triggers wins** (5y vs IPO 2026 vs revocation 2027 вҶ’ IPO); **FICA still due even when employee excluded** (В§3121 independent of В§83(i)); recognition year set only when eligible; **5-year endpoint uses calendar months not days** (leap-year boundary 2024-02-29 + 60 months = 2029-02-28 via `Months::new(60)`); note describes eligible path including FICA total.

`traderview-expense::section_172` is the **IRC В§172 Net Operating Loss deduction module** вҖ” foundational rule for every business taxpayer including sole-proprietor traders, S-corp shareholders, partnership flow-through partners, and corporations. When deductions exceed gross income, В§172 lets the excess offset taxable income in other years.

**Three statutory regimes classified by NOL year:**

| Regime                | NOL years      | Carryback | Carryforward | 80% limit | Citation                  |
|-----------------------|----------------|-----------|--------------|-----------|---------------------------|
| **Pre-2018 legacy**   | 2017 and prior | 2 years   | 20 years     | None      | Pre-TCJA В§ 172            |
| **CARES Act**         | 2018-2020 only | 5 years   | Indefinite   | None (100% offset) | CARES Act В§ 2303          |
| **Permanent TCJA**    | 2021+          | None      | Indefinite   | **80% of taxable income** | TCJA + post-2020 sunset |

Pinned by `pre_2018_legacy_classified` + `cares_act_2018_2020_classified` + `permanent_tcja_post_2020_classified` + the three boundary tests (`tcja_2017_boundary_last_pre_tcja_year` + `tcja_2018_boundary_first_cares_year` + `tcja_2021_boundary_first_post_cares_year`) вҖ” all six classifications individually pinned.

**80% limit math** under permanent TCJA: NOL deduction allowed = `min(available_NOL, 80% Г— taxable_income_before_NOL)`. If NOL > 80% limit, the limit BINDS and excess carries forward indefinitely. Pinned by `tcja_80_percent_limit_does_not_bind_when_nol_small` ($50k NOL вүӨ $80k limit вҶ’ full deduction) + `tcja_80_percent_limit_binds_when_nol_large` ($200k NOL > $80k limit вҶ’ only $80k deducted, $120k carries) + **boundary tests** (`eighty_percent_limit_boundary_exact_no_binding` at $80k exact and `eighty_percent_limit_boundary_one_over_binds` at $80,001).

**CARES Act 100% offset suspends the 80% limit** for 2018-2020 NOLs. $200k NOL with $100k taxable income absorbs the full $100k, no 80% cap. Pinned by `cares_act_100_percent_offset_no_limit_binding`.

**Pre-2018 legacy has no 80% limit** either. Same full-absorption behavior as CARES. Pinned by `pre_2018_no_80_percent_limit_full_absorption`.

**В§172(b)(1)(B) farming + insurance carve-out**: 2-year carryback preserved even under permanent TCJA. Flag set on the result when `farming_or_insurance_loss` input is true. Pinned by `farming_loss_flag_surfaces_2_year_carryback` + `non_farming_loss_no_carryback_flag_post_2020` (default no carryback).

**Current-year NOL flows to carryforward, NOT absorption.** Loss year has no current-year taxable income to absorb against вҖ” the current loss enters the carryforward pool to absorb against next year's TI. Only PRIOR carryforward absorbs against current TI. Pinned by `current_year_nol_flows_to_carryforward_not_absorption` (current $50k + TI $0 вҶ’ $0 deducted, $50k carries) + `current_year_loss_combined_with_prior_carryforward` (prior $50k absorbs against TI $100k, current $30k loss adds to carryforward вҶ’ $30k carries).

**Boundary regime boundary years individually pinned**: 2017 вҶ’ Pre2018Legacy; 2018 вҶ’ CaresAct; 2020 вҶ’ CaresAct; 2021 вҶ’ PermanentTcja. The CARES Act sunset between 2020 and 2021 is a hard cut.

**Taxable income after NOL never goes negative** under any pathological input combination. Defensive clamping pinned by `taxable_income_after_nol_never_negative` ($10M NOL with $100k TI вҶ’ TI clamped at $0).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-172`. Twenty-four tests pin: all three regimes + boundary year classification; **80% limit boundary $80k exact does not bind, $80,001 binds** (regression target); 80% limit binds with large NOL ($200k вҶ’ $80k + $120k); CARES 100% offset (no 80% limit); pre-2018 100% offset; current-year NOL flows to carryforward not absorption (load-bearing); current loss + prior combined carryforward math; farming flag surfaces 2-year carryback; non-farming default no carryback flag; CARES year flags 5-year carryback regardless of farming; zero TI no absorption; zero NOL no-op; **TI after NOL never negative** (defensive clamping); **$1B / $5B precision** ($800M limit, $4.2B carryforward); note describes regime + 80%-binding flag + 5-year-carryback for CARES paths.

`traderview-expense::section_195` is the **IRC В§ 195 start-up expenditures election** вҖ” the ONLY federal vehicle to deduct pre-operational expenses that would otherwise be capitalized under В§ 263 or non-deductible as "not yet engaged in a В§ 162 trade or business." Trader-relevant for any taxpayer organizing a trading entity (TTS-elected LLC, prop-trading firm, family office, S-corp trading vehicle) and incurring pre-launch costs before the active conduct of trade or business begins.

**Three structural rules**:

| В§ 195 mechanic | Statutory value | Trader application |
|----------------|------------------|----------------------|
| First-year immediate deduction cap | $5,000 (= 500,000 cents) | Initial expense write-off in year active business begins |
| Phase-out floor | $50,000 (= 5,000,000 cents) startup expenditures | Dollar-for-dollar reduction of $5K cap above this threshold |
| Phase-out ceiling | $55,000 (= 5,500,000 cents) startup expenditures | First-year deduction FULLY phased out вҖ” 100% flows to amortization stream |
| Amortization period | 180 months (15 years) starting month active business begins | Remainder amortized ratably over 15 years |

**В§ 195(d)(1) is automatic since T.D. 9542 (September 8, 2011).** The Treasury regulation made the В§ 195(b) election the DEFAULT вҖ” taxpayers no longer need to file an affirmative election statement to claim the deduction. To CAPITALIZE instead (rare, generally tax-inefficient), the taxpayer must affirmatively elect capitalization. Pinned by `affirmative_capitalization_election_zeros_all_deductions` (election zeros all deductions) and `election_applies_default_path_true` (default automatic election applies).

**The phase-out is exactly $1-for-$1 dollar-for-dollar against the $5,000 cap.** At $50,000 startup expenditures the cap is $5,000 (no reduction); at $51,000 the cap drops to $4,000; at $55,000 the cap reaches zero and ALL startup costs amortize over 180 months. Pinned by `fifty_k_startup_at_phase_out_floor_5k_immediate`, `fifty_one_k_startup_1k_phase_out_reduces_immediate_to_4k`, `fifty_three_k_startup_3k_phase_out_reduces_immediate_to_2k`, `fifty_five_k_startup_full_phase_out_no_immediate_deduction`, `phase_out_completes_exactly_at_55k`.

**В§ 195(c)(1) excludes amounts deductible under В§В§ 163(a), 164, or 174.** Interest (В§ 163(a)), state and local taxes (В§ 164), and research and experimental expenditures (В§ 174) follow their own deduction regimes and CANNOT be packed into В§ 195. Callers must EXCLUDE these from `total_startup_expenditures_cents` вҖ” only В§ 162-deductible-if-active items qualify per В§ 195(c)(1)(B).

**Trader-relevant pre-launch costs that DO qualify**: market data subscriptions (Bloomberg, Refinitiv, IBKR data feeds), brokerage account setup fees, trading software licenses (TWS Pro, NinjaTrader, MultiCharts), legal opinion on В§ 475(f) TTS eligibility, accountant fees for entity structure analysis, registered agent fees for trading entity, business plan / pitch deck preparation if seeking outside capital. These costs incurred BEFORE the active trading business begins are В§ 195 startup expenditures.

**В§ 248 / В§ 709 INTERACTION.** Corporate organizational expenditures (incorporation costs, charter legal fees) are covered by В§ 248 with parallel $5K / $50K / 180-month mechanics. Partnership organizational expenditures are covered by В§ 709 with parallel mechanics. В§ 195 covers PRE-OPERATIONAL OPERATING costs separate from entity formation costs вҖ” a trader forming an LLC has BOTH В§ 195 (operating costs) and В§ 709 (LLC formation costs) parallel streams.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-195`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **small startup under $5K full first year no amortization**; **$5K startup full first year no amortization**; **$10K startup $5K immediate plus 180-month amortization** ($5K pool вҶ’ $27.77/mo Г— 12 = $333.24 first-year amortization); **$50K startup at phase-out floor $5K immediate** (zero phase-out reduction); **$51K startup $1K phase-out reduces immediate to $4K**; **$53K startup $3K phase-out reduces immediate to $2K**; **$55K startup full phase-out no immediate deduction** (ceiling boundary); **$60K startup full phase-out**; **$100K startup full phase-out large amortization pool**; **months active in first year caps at 12** (no over-allocation); **months active six proportional amortization** (50% pool / 12 mo); **months active one proportional amortization**; **months active zero no amortization** (active business begins last day of taxable year); **affirmative capitalization election zeros all deductions**; **phase-out threshold boundary $50K вҶ’ $51K** (zero reduction at floor vs $1K at +$1K); **phase-out completes exactly at $55K** (last cent valid at $54,999, zero at $55,000); **first-year total = immediate + amortization invariant**; **citation pins subsections + Treasury Regs + T.D. 9542 Sept. 8, 2011 + Rev. Rul. 99-23**; **zero startup zero deduction no panic**; **negative startup clamped to zero** (defensive); **monthly amortization integer division truncates** ($500,000 / 180 = $2,777 cents = $27.77/mo); **$1M startup full phase-out amortizes over 15 years**; **all four constants pin** (PHASE_OUT_FLOOR=$50K, FIRST_YEAR_CAP=$5K, PHASE_OUT_CEILING=$55K, AMORTIZATION_MONTHS=180); **election applies default path true**; **phase-out engagement note appears when above $50K below $55K**.

`traderview-expense::section_248` is the **IRC В§ 248 corporate organizational expenditures** module вҖ” parallel to `section_195` (operating startup expenditures, built iter 270) with corporation-specific terminology. Trader-relevant when forming a C-corporation for trading operations (incidental to incorporation: drafting charter, bylaws, minutes; legal/accounting services for organization; expenses of organizational meetings; fees paid to state of incorporation). Distinct from В§ 195 (pre-launch operating costs) and В§ 709 (parallel partnership organizational expenditures).

**Same $5K / $50K / $55K / 180-month framework as В§ 195** (post-AJCA 2004 В§ 902 harmonization):

| Mechanic | Statutory value |
|----------|------------------|
| First-year deduction cap | $5,000 |
| Phase-out floor | $50,000 |
| Phase-out ceiling | $55,000 |
| Amortization period | 180 months |

**В§ 248(b) excludes** (per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.248-1(b)): expenses for issuing or selling shares of stock (capitalized to stock basis), В§ 351 transfer-of-assets expenses, В§ 368 reorganization expenses. These are NOT В§ 248 organizational expenditures and follow their own framework.

**В§ 248(c) automatic election deemed via T.D. 9542 (Sept. 8, 2011).** Corporation is deemed to have elected В§ 248(a) deduction unless it affirmatively elects to capitalize. Same automatic-election mechanic as В§ 195(d) and В§ 709(b). Pinned by `affirmative_capitalization_election_zeros_all_deductions`, `automatic_election_note_present`, `election_applies_default_path_true`.

**В§ 248 phase-out matches В§ 195 invariant.** At $53K organizational expenditures with 12 months active: first-year deduction = $5K - $3K phase-out reduction = $2K. Identical to В§ 195 at same input level. Pinned by `section_248_phaseout_matches_section_195_invariant`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-248`. Thirty tests pin: **small organizational under $5K full first year no amortization**; **$5K organizational full first year no amortization**; **$10K organizational $5K immediate plus 180-month amortization**; **$50K organizational at phase-out floor $5K immediate**; **$51K organizational $1K phase-out reduces immediate to $4K**; **$53K organizational $3K phase-out reduces immediate to $2K**; **$55K organizational full phase-out no immediate deduction**; **$60K organizational full phase-out**; **$100K organizational full phase-out**; **months active in first year caps at 12**; **months active six proportional amortization**; **months active zero no amortization**; **affirmative capitalization election zeros all deductions**; **phase-out threshold boundary $50K вҶ’ $51K**; **phase-out completes exactly at $55K**; **first-year total = immediate + amortization invariant**; **citation pins subsections + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.248-1(b)/(c) + T.D. 9542 Sept. 8, 2011 + AJCA 2004 В§ 902 + cross-reference В§ 195 and В§ 709**; **zero organizational zero deduction no panic**; **negative organizational clamped to zero**; **monthly amortization integer division truncates** ($500,000 / 180 = 2,777 cents); **$1M organizational full phase-out amortizes over 15 years**; **all four constants pin** (PHASE_OUT_FLOOR=$50K, FIRST_YEAR_CAP=$5K, PHASE_OUT_CEILING=$55K, AMORTIZATION_MONTHS=180); **election applies default path true**; **phase-out engagement note appears when above $50K below $55K**; **exclusions note always present** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.248-1(b) shares + В§ 351 + В§ 368); **automatic election note present** (T.D. 9542); **В§ 248 phase-out matches В§ 195 invariant** (parallel framework verification).

`traderview-expense::section_709` is the **IRC В§ 709 partnership organizational expenditures + syndication fees** module вҖ” completes the trio with `section_195` (operating startup, built iter 270) and `section_248` (corporate organizational, built iter 296). Trader-relevant when forming a partnership for trading operations (LLP, LP, LLC taxed as partnership). Same $5K / $50K / $55K / 180-month framework as В§ 195 / В§ 248, PLUS the distinctive В§ 709(b)(3) syndication expense carve-out вҖ” capital-raising costs are PERMANENTLY CAPITALIZED to partner basis with NO amortization deduction available.

**Same В§ 195 / В§ 248 framework PLUS В§ 709(b)(3) syndication carve-out**:

| Mechanic | В§ 709 value |
|----------|--------------|
| First-year deduction cap | $5,000 |
| Phase-out floor | $50,000 |
| Phase-out ceiling | $55,000 |
| Organizational amortization period | 180 months |
| **В§ 709(b)(3) syndication expenses** | **PERMANENTLY CAPITALIZED вҖ” no amortization** |

**В§ 709(b)(2) organizational expenses** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.709-2(a)): incident to creation of partnership + chargeable to capital account + amortizable-character. Examples: legal fees for partnership agreement drafting, accounting fees for organizational services.

**В§ 709(b)(3) syndication expenses** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.709-2(b)): brokerage fees + registration fees + legal fees of the issuer (NOT partnership formation) + accounting fees of the issuer + printing costs of the prospectus. These are CAPITAL-RAISING costs distinct from formation costs. PERMANENTLY capitalized to partner basis вҖ” no deduction or amortization ever.

**В§ 709(b)(3) syndication distinct from В§ 248 share-issuance exclusion.** В§ 248 (corporate) excludes share-issuance expenses without separate "syndication" terminology. В§ 709 (partnership) uses the specific syndication framework with Treas. Reg. В§ 1.709-2(b) definition. Pinned by `syndication_distinct_from_section_248_share_issuance_invariant`, `syndication_expenses_permanently_capitalized`, `syndication_expenses_treasury_reg_definition_note`, `syndication_expenses_separately_capitalized_from_organizational_amortization` ($10K organizational + $100K syndication вҶ’ $500K amortization pool + $10M permanently capitalized syndication).

**Syndication treatment INDEPENDENT of organizational phase-out math.** В§ 709(b)(3) capitalization runs separately from В§ 709(b)(1) organizational election. Pinned by `syndication_amount_independent_of_phase_out_calculation` ($53K organizational + $1K vs $100K syndication yields same organizational phase-out reduction = $3K).

**Affirmative capitalization election still capitalizes syndication.** Even when the partnership opts OUT of the В§ 709(b) organizational election, syndication expenses remain permanently capitalized under В§ 709(b)(3). Pinned by `affirmative_capitalization_election_still_capitalizes_syndication`.

**Phase-out matches В§ 195 / В§ 248 trio invariant.** $53K organizational вҶ’ $5K - $3K phase-out = $2K first-year deduction (identical to В§ 195/В§ 248 at same input). Pinned by `section_709_matches_section_195_section_248_phaseout_invariant`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-709`. Thirty-one tests pin: **small organizational under $5K full first year**; **$10K organizational $5K immediate plus 180-month amortization**; **$50K organizational at phase-out floor $5K immediate**; **$53K organizational $3K phase-out reduces immediate to $2K**; **$55K organizational full phase-out**; **syndication expenses permanently capitalized** (В§ 709(b)(3)); **syndication expenses Treasury Reg definition note** (В§ 1.709-2(b) brokerage + registration + legal + accounting + prospectus); **syndication expenses separately capitalized from organizational amortization**; **no syndication no syndication note**; **affirmative capitalization election zeros organizational deductions**; **affirmative capitalization election still capitalizes syndication** (independent path); **months active caps at 12**; **months active zero no amortization**; **phase-out boundary $50K вҶ’ $51K**; **phase-out completes exactly at $55K**; **citation pins all subsections + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.709-1/В§ 1.709-2(a)/(b) + T.D. 9542 Sept. 8, 2011 + AJCA 2004 В§ 902 + cross-reference В§ 195 + В§ 248**; **zero organizational zero deduction**; **negative organizational clamped to zero** (defensive); **negative syndication clamped to zero** (defensive); **$1M organizational full phase-out amortizes over 15 years**; **constants pin $50K/$5K/$55K/180**; **В§ 709 matches В§ 195 / В§ 248 phase-out invariant** (parallel framework verification at $53K input); **syndication distinct from В§ 248 share issuance invariant** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.709-2(b) brokerage definition); **election applies default path true**; **automatic election note present** (T.D. 9542); **organizational expense definition note present** (В§ 1.709-2(a) incident to creation); **syndication capitalized amount returned in result field**; **first-year total = immediate + amortization invariant**; **syndication amount independent of phase-out calculation** ($1K vs $100K syndication yields identical organizational phase-out); **phase-out engagement note appears above $50K below $55K**; **cross-reference to В§ 195 and В§ 248 in citation**.

`traderview-expense::section_197` is the **IRC В§ 197 amortization of goodwill and certain other intangibles** вҖ” 15-year (180-month) straight-line amortization beginning month acquired for any "amortizable section 197 intangible." Trader-relevant when a trading entity acquires another trading business (asset purchase including customer list, workforce in place, goodwill, non-compete with seller). В§ 197 is the EXCLUSIVE deduction pathway вҖ” В§ 197(b) bars В§ 167 depreciation on covered intangibles. Companion to `section_195` (startup expenditures), `section_174` (R&E), and `section_263a` (UNICAP) in the acquisition-cost capitalization landscape.

**Nine amortizable В§ 197(d) categories**:

| Category | Citation | Examples |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Goodwill | В§ 197(d)(1)(A) | premium over identifiable asset FMV |
| Going concern value | В§ 197(d)(1)(B) | continuity premium |
| Workforce in place | В§ 197(d)(1)(C) | composition + terms and conditions of employment |
| Books and records / operating systems | В§ 197(d)(1)(D) | trading desk SOPs, OMS configurations |
| Patent / copyright / process | В§ 197(d)(1)(E) | proprietary algorithms |
| Customer-based or supplier-based intangible | В§ 197(d)(1)(F) | brokerage client lists |
| Government license / permit | В§ 197(d)(1)(G) | SEC / FINRA registrations |
| Covenant not to compete | В§ 197(d)(1)(H) | non-compete with selling principal |
| Franchise / trademark / trade name | В§ 197(d)(1)(I) | brand name |

**Three В§ 197(e) excluded categories** (no В§ 197 amortization):

| Exclusion | Citation | Treatment |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| Land | В§ 197(e)(2) | non-amortizable; held at basis |
| Financial interest | В§ 197(e)(1) | В§ 1234 / capital asset rules |
| Lease of tangible property | В§ 197(e)(4) | В§ 168 separate treatment |

**Three gatekeepers** kill amortization at the door regardless of category:

| Gatekeeper | Authority | Effect |
|------------|-----------|--------|
| Not held in trade or business | В§ 197(c)(2) | amortization unavailable |
| Acquired ON OR BEFORE August 10, 1993 | В§ 197(c)(1) | pre-TRA-93 acquisition; amortization unavailable |
| В§ 197(f)(9) anti-churning engaged | В§ 197(f)(9) | held during 7/25/1991-8/10/1993 transition period by taxpayer or related (> 20%) party, OR acquired from related party with continued use |

**В§ 197(f)(9) anti-churning rules** prevent taxpayers from converting pre-1993 non-amortizable intangibles into post-1993 В§ 197-amortizable intangibles via related-party transfers. "Related person" includes a corporation and an individual who owns, directly or indirectly, more than 20% of the corporation's outstanding stock. Pinned by `anti_churning_transition_period_blocks_amortization` and `anti_churning_related_party_continued_use_blocks_amortization`.

**В§ 197(a) straight-line math: basis Г· 180 months.** $180,000 goodwill вҶ’ $1,000/month вҶ’ $12,000/year вҶ’ fully amortized at 180 months. Pinned by `goodwill_180000_basis_1000_monthly_amortization` (basis $180K вҶ’ monthly $1K вҶ’ annual $12K вҶ’ cumulative at 12 months $12K вҶ’ remaining $168K), `customer_list_15_years_fully_amortized` (180 months вҶ’ cumulative $180K, remaining $0), `cumulative_at_60_months_one_third_of_basis` (cumulative = monthly Г— 60).

**Sports franchises were added to В§ 197 by AJCA 2004 В§ 886.** Previously a В§ 197(e)(7) exception; the 2004 amendment made sports franchise acquisitions В§ 197-amortizable.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-197`. Twenty-five tests pin: **goodwill $180K basis $1K monthly amortization**; **customer list 15 years fully amortized** (180 months); **workforce in place first-month amortization**; **covenant not to compete categorized as В§ 197 intangible**; **franchise/trademark/trade name amortizable**; **government license amortizable**; **going concern value amortizable**; **business books and records amortizable**; **patent or copyright amortizable**; **land excluded no amortization** (В§ 197(e)(2)); **financial interest excluded no amortization** (В§ 197(e)(1)); **lease of tangible property excluded** (В§ 197(e)(4)); **not held in trade or business no amortization** (В§ 197(c)(2)); **pre-August 10 1993 acquisition no amortization** (В§ 197(c)(1) TRA-93 effective date); **anti-churning transition period blocks amortization** (В§ 197(f)(9)); **anti-churning related party continued use blocks amortization**; **negative basis clamped to zero** (defensive); **months exceed 180 caps cumulative at basis**; **citation pins subsections + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.197-2 + Rev. Rul. 2004-49 + AJCA 2004 В§ 886**; **AMORTIZATION_MONTHS constant pins 180**; **annual amortization equals monthly Г— 12 invariant**; **note describes 15-year (180-month) straight-line**; **nine intangible categories В§ 197(d)(1)(A)-(I) all amortizable invariant**; **three exception categories В§ 197(e) all non-amortizable invariant**; **cumulative at 60 months one-third of basis**.

`traderview-expense::section_199a` is the **IRC В§ 199A Qualified Business Income (QBI) deduction** module вҖ” made PERMANENT by One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 (Pub. L. 119-21). 20% deduction of QBI for owners of pass-through entities (sole proprietorships + S-corps + partnerships + LLCs taxed as such). Trader-critical for traders with Section 475(f) MTM election + trader-landlords with rental real estate qualifying as trade or business under Rev. Proc. 2019-38 safe harbor. Distinct from siblings `section_469` (passive activity rules), `section_1411` (NIIT investment income tax), and `section_475c2` (В§ 475(f)(1) MTM trader election).

**В§ 199A(a) basic deduction** вҖ” LESSER of (1) 20% Г— QBI (combined QBI amount) or (2) 20% Г— (Taxable Income вҲ’ Net Capital Gain). Pinned by `single_below_threshold_full_20_percent`, `overall_20_percent_cap_engaged_when_qbi_exceeds_ti`, `net_capital_gain_reduces_overall_cap`, and `notes_pin_section_199a_a_lesser_of_formula`.

**В§ 199A(b)(2) W-2 wage / UBIA phase-in limitation** вҖ” applies when TI exceeds threshold. Limits 20% Г— QBI to GREATER of (1) 50% Г— W-2 wages, OR (2) 25% Г— W-2 wages + 2.5% Г— Unadjusted Basis Immediately after Acquisition (UBIA) of qualified property. Pinned by `single_just_over_phase_in_threshold_engages_w2_limit`, `non_sstb_above_phase_out_w2_limit_applies` (50% Г— $10M W-2 = $5M limit binds), `non_sstb_w2_plus_ubia_alternative` (25% W-2 + 2.5% UBIA = $2.75M when no W-2 + $110M UBIA), and `notes_pin_w2_ubia_limit_section_199a_b_2`.

**В§ 199A(e)(2) 2026 income thresholds**:

| Filing status | Phase-in begin | Phase-out complete | Phase-in window |
|---------------|----------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| Single / Head of Household | **$201,750** | **$276,750** | **$75,000** |
| MFJ / QSS | **$403,500** | **$553,500** | **$150,000** |
| MFS | $201,750 | $276,750 | $75,000 |

Pinned by `single_at_201750_phase_in_threshold_boundary`, `single_just_over_phase_in_threshold_engages_w2_limit` ($201,750 + 1Вў engages limit), `single_at_276750_phase_out_complete_boundary`, `mfj_at_403500_phase_in_threshold_boundary`, `mfj_at_553500_phase_out_complete_boundary`, `filing_status_threshold_truth_table` (5-cell sweep), `mfj_uniquely_double_phase_in_window_invariant` (MFJ exactly 2Г— single), and `mfj_uniquely_double_phase_in_threshold_invariant`.

**В§ 199A(d)(2) SSTB (Specified Service Trade or Business) phase-out** вҖ” health, law, accounting, actuarial science, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage services, investing/investment management/trading. SSTB deduction phases out COMPLETELY above upper threshold. Pinned by `sstb_above_phase_out_zero_deduction`, `sstb_below_threshold_no_phaseout`, `sstb_uniquely_phases_out_invariant` (SSTB above phase-out вҶ’ 0 deduction; non-SSTB вҶ’ W-2 limit applies), and `notes_pin_sstb_categories_section_199a_d_2`.

**OBBBA 2025 changes (Pub. L. 119-21)**:
- Made В§ 199A **PERMANENT** (expiration date removed from В§ 199A(i))
- Expanded phase-in window from $50K вҶ’ **$75K** single; $100K вҶ’ **$150K** joint
- Added В§ 199A(b) NEW **$400 minimum deduction** when QBI вүҘ $1,000 AND material participation

Pinned by `obbba_400_minimum_applies_with_material_participation_and_qbi_over_1000`, `obbba_400_minimum_not_applied_without_material_participation`, `obbba_400_minimum_not_applied_below_1000_qbi`, `obbba_phase_in_window_uniquely_expanded` ($75K single + $150K joint), and `obbba_2025_permanence_in_notes`.

**Rev. Proc. 2019-38 rental real estate safe harbor** вҖ” rental real estate enterprise treated as trade or business for В§ 199A purposes if criteria met (250+ hours/year of rental services + separate books/records + contemporaneous records). Pinned by `notes_pin_rental_real_estate_safe_harbor`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative QBI / taxable income / W-2 wages clamped via `.max(0)`; saturating multiplication for 20% computation prevents overflow. OBBBA minimum gated on positive taxable income. Pinned by `defensive_negative_qbi_clamped`, `defensive_negative_taxable_income_clamped`, and `defensive_negative_w2_wages_clamped`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-199a`. Thirty tests pin: **single below threshold full 20%**; **single at $201,750 phase-in threshold boundary**; **single just over phase-in threshold engages W-2 limit**; **single at $276,750 phase-out complete boundary**; **MFJ at $403,500 phase-in threshold boundary**; **MFJ at $553,500 phase-out complete boundary**; **filing status threshold truth table** (5-cell sweep); **OBBBA $400 minimum applies with material participation and QBI > $1,000**; **OBBBA $400 minimum not applied without material participation**; **OBBBA $400 minimum not applied below $1,000 QBI**; **overall 20% cap engaged when QBI exceeds TI**; **net capital gain reduces overall cap**; **SSTB above phase-out zero deduction**; **SSTB below threshold no phaseout**; **non-SSTB above phase-out W-2 limit applies**; **non-SSTB W-2 + UBIA alternative**; **OBBBA 2025 permanence in notes**; **notes pin В§ 199A(a) lesser-of formula**; **notes pin W-2 / UBIA limit В§ 199A(b)(2)**; **notes pin SSTB categories В§ 199A(d)(2)**; **notes pin rental real estate safe harbor** (Rev. Proc. 2019-38 + 250 hours); **citation pins authorities** (В§ 199A(a)-(i) + Pub. L. 119-21 + Rev. Proc. 2019-38 + Form 8995 + В§ 475(f)); **defensive negative QBI clamped**; **defensive negative taxable income clamped**; **defensive negative W-2 wages clamped**; **MFJ uniquely double phase-in window invariant** ($150K = 2Г— $75K); **MFJ uniquely double phase-in threshold invariant** ($403,500 = 2Г— $201,750); **SSTB uniquely phases out invariant** (SSTB вҶ’ 0 above phase-out; non-SSTB вҶ’ W-2 limit); **OBBBA phase-in window uniquely expanded** ($75K + $150K); **3.8% test does not match В§ 1411** (distinguishing В§ 199A 20% from В§ 1411 3.8% NIIT surtax).

`traderview-expense::section_213` is the **IRC В§213 medical, dental, etc. expenses deduction module** вҖ” universal for any itemizing trader. Allows deduction of qualified medical expenses to the extent they EXCEED **7.5% of Adjusted Gross Income**. The 7.5% floor was made **PERMANENT** by Section 103 of the Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Tax Relief Act of 2019, enacted as part of the Further Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2020 (P.L. 116-94), amending В§ 213(f) вҖ” previously scheduled to revert to 10% ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 213](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/213), [IRS Publication 502 (2025)](https://www.irs.gov/publications/p502)).

**В§213(d) qualified medical care** includes: doctor visits, dental, vision, mental health; prescription drugs and insulin; hospital and clinic charges; long-term-care services (chronic illness, ADL impairment); transportation to medical care; lodging up to $50/night while away from home for medical care; health insurance premiums (subject to В§162(l) limits for self-employed traders).

**В§213(d)(10) eligible long-term-care premium age-tiered caps** indexed annually by IRS Rev. Proc.:

| Insured's attained age at year end | 2025 cap (Rev. Proc. 2024-40) | 2026 cap (Rev. Proc. 2025-32) |
|------------------------------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| вүӨ 40                               | $480                          | $500                          |
| 41-50                              | $900                          | $930                          |
| 51-60                              | $1,800                        | $1,860                        |
| 61-70                              | $4,810                        | $4,810 (2025 fallback вҖ” 2026 not confirmed) |
| 71+                                | $6,020                        | $6,200                        |

Age-tier boundaries pin "вүӨ40 = first tier" and "41 = second tier". Module looks up tier by `(tax_year, age)` tuple with 2025 caps as fallback for any year not explicitly modeled (forward-compatible until updated). ([AALTCI 2026 Tax Deduction Limits](https://www.aaltci.org/news/long-term-care-insurance-association-news/2026-tax-deductible-limits-for-long-term-care-insurance-increase-3-percent), [IRS LTC Premium Limits VITA](https://apps.irs.gov/app/vita/content/00/00_25_005.jsp))

**HSA / FSA / HRA double-deduction prevention**: any amount paid or reimbursed under an HSA, FSA, Archer MSA, or HRA cannot also be deducted under В§213. The module subtracts `hsa_fsa_hra_reimbursements` from qualified expenses BEFORE applying the 7.5% AGI floor, clamping at zero. Pinned by `hsa_reimbursements_reduce_deduction` + `reimbursements_exceed_expenses_clamp_to_zero`.

**Schedule A itemization required** вҖ” the standard deduction takes precedence if larger. Module always returns `requires_itemization: true` so callers know to compare to standard deduction before claiming.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-213`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **7.5% AGI floor baseline** (AGI $100k Г— 7.5% = $7,500 floor; $15k expenses вҶ’ $7,500 deductible); **expenses below floor no deduction**; **expenses exactly at floor no deduction**; **$1 above floor deductible** (boundary); **HSA reimbursements reduce deduction** ($5k reimbursement вҶ’ $10k qualified вҶ’ $2.5k deductible); **reimbursements exceed expenses clamp to zero**; **all 5 LTC 2025 age tiers** (вүӨ40 $480, 50 $900, 60 $1,800, 70 $4,810, 71+ $6,020) with excess-premium pinning; **age boundaries 40 first-tier / 41 second-tier**; **4 LTC 2026 tiers** (вүӨ40 $500, 50 $930, 60 $1,860, 71+ $6,200); LTC within cap full allowed (no excess); **LTC premium added to total qualified** ($15k + $900 = $15.9k вҶ’ $8.4k deductible); **high-AGI high-floor no deduction** ($1M AGI вҶ’ $75k floor); high-AGI with high expenses full floor subtraction ($200k expenses вҲ’ $75k = $125k); **zero AGI zero floor full expenses deductible**; zero expenses zero deduction; **unknown year (2030) falls back to 2025 caps**; note describes 7.5% floor + Schedule A; **note describes LTC excess when capped** ("excess premium NOT deductible"); requires_itemization always true; citation mentions CAA 2020 and Rev. Proc.

`traderview-expense::section_219` is the **IRC В§219 Individual Retirement Account (IRA) contribution module** вҖ” sibling to `section_223` (HSAs). Universal for traders and landlords saving for retirement. В§ 219(a) provides an above-the-line deduction for Traditional IRA contributions; Roth contributions are NEVER deductible but the contribution itself phases out by MAGI. Distinguishes the **Traditional-vs-Roth phaseout treatment**: Traditional phaseout reduces DEDUCTION only (non-deductible contributions still permitted вҶ’ backdoor Roth conversion path); Roth phaseout reduces CONTRIBUTION itself (above the high end вҶ’ $0).

**2026 limits** (per IRS Notice 2025-XX):

| Component | Amount | Citation |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Base IRA contribution | **$7,500** (was $7,000 for 2024/2025) | В§ 219(b)(5)(A) |
| Age 50+ catch-up | **$1,100** (SECURE 2.0 indexed; pre-2024 was $1,000) | В§ 219(b)(5)(B) |
| Combined cap (age 50+) | **$8,600** | В§ 219(b)(5) |
| Earned income cap | min(earned, statutory) | В§ 219(b)(1) |

**2026 Traditional IRA deduction phaseout** (В§ 219(g) вҖ” applies only when taxpayer or spouse covered by workplace plan):

| Filing status | Phaseout range |
|---------------|----------------|
| Single / HoH (covered) | $81,000-$91,000 |
| MFJ (taxpayer covered) | $129,000-$149,000 |
| MFJ (spouse only covered) | $242,000-$252,000 (В§ 219(g)(7) widened range) |
| MFS (covered) | $0-$10,000 |

**2026 Roth IRA contribution phaseout** (В§ 408A(c)(3)):

| Filing status | Phaseout range |
|---------------|----------------|
| Single / HoH | $153,000-$168,000 |
| MFJ | $242,000-$252,000 |
| MFS | $0-$10,000 |

**Backdoor Roth conversion**: even when MAGI exceeds the Roth phaseout high end and direct Roth contribution = $0, the taxpayer may still make a non-deductible Traditional IRA contribution and convert to Roth (subject to pro-rata rule on aggregate IRA basis).

**Excess contribution** triggers В§ 4973 6% excise tax each year the excess remains in the account.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-219`. Twenty-six tests pin: **Traditional 2026 under 50 no workplace plan full deduction**; **Traditional 2026 age 50 catch-up adds $1,100 вҶ’ $8,600**; **Traditional 2024 age 50 catch-up was $1,000** (year-aware regression вҖ” pre-SECURE-2.0 statutory $1,000); **Traditional Single covered above $91K вҶ’ no deduction but still $7,500 contribution permitted** (backdoor Roth setup); **Traditional Single covered at $81K boundary full deduction**; **Traditional Single covered at $86K midpoint partial phaseout**; **Traditional MFJ covered above $149K вҶ’ no deduction**; **Traditional MFJ spouse-only-covered widened phaseout** (В§ 219(g)(7) $242K-$252K regression); **Traditional MFJ neither covered вҶ’ no phaseout at $500K income**; **Roth Single 2026 below $153K full contribution**; **Roth Single 2026 above $168K NO contribution** + 6% excise on full $7,500 = $450 excise; **Roth MFJ 2026 above $252K no contribution**; **Roth MFJ 2026 $247K midpoint partial**; **Roth MFS 2026 $0-$10K phaseout**; **Roth MFS 2026 above $10K no contribution**; **earned income caps contribution** ($3K earned вҶ’ $3K limit even with $7,500 statutory cap; $4,500 excess); **excess contribution triggers 6% excise**; **age 49 no catch-up**; **age 50 boundary catch-up applies** (вүҘ strict); **citations pin В§ 219(b)(5) + В§ 219(g) + В§ 408A(c)(3) + В§ 4973**; **negative inputs clamped**; **Roth at $153K low-end boundary full contribution**; **Roth at $168K high-end fully phased out**; **Traditional above phaseout backdoor-Roth-eligible** (regression вҖ” deduction zero but contribution allowed); **Roth-vs-Traditional distinct phaseout behavior** (regression-critical вҖ” same $200K MAGI Single + covered: Traditional allows $7,500 nondeductible contribution; Roth allows $0); **year-aware 2025 $7,000 base limit**.

`traderview-expense::section_221` is the **IRC В§221 student loan interest deduction module** вҖ” universal for traders and landlords still paying off education debt. В§ 221(a) provides an **above-the-line deduction** of up to **$2,500** for interest paid on qualified education loans вҖ” available even without itemizing. The $2,500 cap is **statutory** (В§ 221(b)(1)) and does NOT inflation-adjust.

**2026 MAGI phaseout** (В§ 221(b)(2) per Rev. Proc. 2025-XX):

| Filing status | Phaseout range |
|---------------|----------------|
| Single / HoH / QW | **$85,000-$100,000** ($15K window) |
| Married Filing Jointly / QW | **$175,000-$205,000** ($30K window) |
| **Married Filing Separately** | **EXCLUDED** under В§ 221(e)(2) вҖ” cannot claim regardless of MAGI |

**2025 MAGI phaseout**: Single $80K-$95K; MFJ $165K-$195K.
