**MFS exclusion is regression-critical**: В§ 221(e)(2) bars married-filing-separately taxpayers from claiming the deduction entirely вҖ” different from В§219 (Traditional IRA) where MFS gets a $75K threshold and В§164 SALT cap (MFS at $20K). В§ 221 MFS = $0 always.

**Statutory $2,500 cap pin**: same $2,500 in 2025, 2026, and forever вҖ” does not inflation-adjust unlike most other tax limits.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-221`. Twenty-one tests pin: **single 2026 under phaseout full $2,500**; **single 2026 at $85K boundary full** (вүӨ strict); **single 2026 at $100K boundary zero**; **single 2026 in phaseout midpoint $92.5K вҶ’ $1,250** (50% Г— $2,500); **single 2026 interest below cap** ($1,500 paid вҶ’ $1,500 not $2,500); **MFJ 2026 under $175K full deduction**; **MFJ 2026 at $205K high-end zero**; **MFJ 2026 in phaseout midpoint $190K вҶ’ $1,250**; **MFS excluded zero deduction** + В§ 221(e)(2) citation (regression-critical); **HoH uses single phaseout range**; **QW uses MFJ phaseout range** (regression вҖ” QW gets joint-status treatment); **year-aware 2025 single $80K-$95K**; **year-aware 2025 MFJ $165K-$195K**; **statutory $2,500 cap does NOT inflation-adjust** (regression-critical statutory pin вҖ” 2025 + 2026 both $2,500); **negative inputs clamped**; **zero interest zero deduction**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 221(a) + (b)(1) + (b)(2) + (e)(2)); **one dollar above phaseout low starts phaseout** (off-by-one); **one dollar above phaseout high zero deduction**; **interest below cap phaseout proportional** ($1,000 paid + midpoint вҶ’ $500); **worked example single $98K вҶ’ $333 allowed** (close to phaseout high end).

`traderview-expense::section_223` is the **IRC В§223 Health Savings Account (HSA) module** вҖ” triple tax-advantaged: contributions deductible above-the-line under В§223(a), earnings grow tax-free, withdrawals for qualified medical expenses tax-free. Universal for any trader or landlord with HDHP coverage. To contribute, the taxpayer must be covered by a High Deductible Health Plan meeting the В§223(c)(2) three-prong test (minimum deductible + capped out-of-pocket maximum + no payment for non-preventive care before deductible).

**2026 inflation-adjusted amounts** (Rev. Proc. 2025-19):

| Component | Self-only | Family | Citation |
|-----------|-----------|--------|----------|
| Contribution limit | **$4,400** | **$8,750** | В§ 223(b)(2) |
| HDHP minimum deductible | **$1,700** | **$3,400** | В§ 223(c)(2)(A) |
| HDHP max in-network OOP | **$8,500** | **$17,000** | В§ 223(c)(2)(B) |
| Age-55+ catch-up | +$1,000 | +$1,000 | В§ 223(b)(3) (STATUTORY, not inflation-adjusted) |
| Combined cap (age 55+) | $5,400 | $9,750 | В§ 223(b)(2) + (b)(3) |

**2025 amounts**: self-only $4,300 / $1,650 min ded / $8,300 max OOP; family $8,550 / $3,300 / $16,600.

**$1,000 catch-up is statutory** вҖ” does NOT inflation-adjust. Same $1,000 in 2025 + 2026 + every future year unless Congress amends.

**Excess contribution** triggers a **6% excise tax under В§ 4973** each year the excess remains in the account. Disqualified plan (HDHP-eligibility test fails) вҶ’ ALL contributions are excess.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-223`. Twenty-four tests pin: **self-only 2026 under 55 full $4,400**; **self-only 2026 age 55+ catch-up adds $1,000 вҶ’ $5,400**; **family 2026 under 55 $8,750**; **family 2026 age 55+ вҶ’ $9,750**; **deductible below minimum ($1,500 < $1,700) disqualifies + all contributions excess + 6% excise** (regression вҖ” disqualified plan path); **deductible at $1,700 boundary qualifies** (вүҘ strict); **OOP max above $8,500 disqualifies**; **OOP max at $8,500 boundary qualifies** (вүӨ strict); **excess contribution triggers 6% excise** ($5,000 contributed - $4,400 limit = $600 excess вҶ’ $36 excise); **excess contribution age 55 calculated against higher limit**; **under contribution no excess**; **family 2026 $8,750 inflation-adjusted**; **self-only 2025 $4,300** (year-aware regression); **family 2025 $8,550**; **catch-up $1,000 does NOT inflation-adjust** (2025 + 2026 same $1,000 вҖ” regression-critical statutory pin); **age 54 no catch-up**; **age 55 boundary catch-up applies** (вүҘ strict); **zero OOP max disqualifies** (invalid HDHP); **negative inputs clamped**; **citations pin В§ 223(a) + (b)(2) + (b)(3) + (c)(2) + В§ 4973**; **note includes excise tax amount**; **family min deductible 2x self-only invariant**; **family OOP max 2x self-only invariant**; **worked example high-income trader age 60 family HDHP** ($9,750 contribution + age-55+ catch-up + 37% bracket вҶ’ $3,607.50 tax savings).

`traderview-expense::section_243` is the **IRC В§243 / В§246 Dividends Received Deduction (DRD) module** вҖ” universal for any trader operating through a C-corp that holds dividend-paying domestic stock. Allows the C-corp to deduct a percentage of inter-corporate dividends from taxable income, with the percentage depending on OWNERSHIP STAKE, reduced for debt-financed stock and disallowed entirely when the В§246(c) holding period fails ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 243](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/243), [IRC В§246 вҖ” Rules Applying to DRDs (Bloomberg Tax)](https://irc.bloombergtax.com/public/uscode/doc/irc/section_246)).

**В§243 ownership tier table** (post-TCJA percentages вҖ” TCJA P.L. 115-97 В§ 13002 lowered 70%вҶ’50% and 80%вҶ’65% when В§ 11 corporate rate dropped from 35% to 21%):

| Ownership stake | DRD percentage | Citation                                  |
|-----------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------|
| < 20%           | **50%**        | В§ 243(a)(1)                               |
| 20% - 79%       | **65%**        | В§ 243(c) (20-percent-owned corporation)   |
| вүҘ 80%           | **100%**       | В§ 243(b) (qualifying group / affiliated)  |

Tier boundaries pinned: `at_19_99_pct_still_under_20_tier` (1999bp вҶ’ 50% tier), `exactly_20_pct_65_drd` (2000bp вҶ’ 65% tier), `at_79_99_pct_still_tier_2` (7999bp вҶ’ 65% tier), `at_80_pct_qualifying_group_100_drd` (8000bp вҶ’ 100% tier).

**В§246(c) anti-abuse holding period** вҖ” receiving corporation must hold the stock for MORE THAN:

- **45 days** during the **91-day period** beginning 45 days BEFORE the ex-dividend date, for COMMON stock (and preferred stock with dividends attributable to вүӨ 366 days)
- **90 days** during the **181-day period** beginning 90 days BEFORE the ex-dividend date, for PREFERRED stock with dividends attributable to MORE than 366 days

**Failure to satisfy the holding period DISALLOWS THE DRD ENTIRELY** for that dividend вҖ” not partial; not pro-rated. This is the load-bearing anti-abuse rule: prevents traders from buying stock days before ex-dividend, collecting the dividend with preferential DRD treatment, then selling. Pinned by `common_45_days_exact_does_not_satisfy` (strict greater-than) + `common_46_days_satisfies` + `preferred_long_dividend_90_days_does_not_satisfy` + `preferred_long_dividend_91_days_satisfies` + `holding_period_failure_full_disallowance` (even 100%-ownership tier yields $0 DRD when holding fails).

**В§246A debt-financed portfolio stock reduction** вҖ” for stocks debt-financed during the base period, the DRD percentage is reduced by the average indebtedness percentage:

```text
effective DRD % = base DRD % Г— (100% вҲ’ average indebtedness %)
```

A 50%-tier dividend on 40%-debt-financed stock yields 50% Г— 60% = 30% DRD. A 65%-tier dividend on 50%-debt-financed stock yields 65% Г— 50% = 32.5% DRD. **В§246A does NOT apply to the 80%-plus qualifying-group tier** вҖ” affiliated corporations remain at 100% DRD regardless of debt financing. Pinned by `debt_financed_under_20_50_drd_reduced_by_indebtedness` + `debt_financed_20_pct_tier_65_drd_reduced` (32.5%) + `debt_financed_80_pct_tier_no_reduction` + `debt_financed_100_pct_indebtedness_clamps_to_zero_drd` + `debt_financed_excess_indebtedness_capped` (>100% input safely clamped).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-243`. Twenty-four tests pin: **all 3 ownership tier boundaries** with 5 sub-pins each (UnderTwentyPct / TwentyToSeventyNinePct / EightyPlusPct + 100% gives full deduction); **В§246(c) common 45/46-day boundary** + preferred-long-dividend 90/91-day boundary; preferred-short-dividend uses 45-day window; **holding period failure full disallowance** even at 100% ownership; **В§246A debt-financed reduction** on 50% tier (40% indebt вҶ’ 30%) and 65% tier (50% indebt вҶ’ 32.5%); В§246A does NOT reduce 80%+ tier; **100% indebtedness clamps DRD to zero**; >100% indebtedness clamped safely; **debt-financed + holding-period-failed combined вҶ’ zero DRD** with HOLDING PERIOD FAILED note priority; **$1B precision path** ($1B Г— 50% = $500M); zero dividend zero DRD; under-20 note describes 50.00% tier; debt-financed note describes В§246A reduction with 30.00% effective rate; citation mentions all 6 relevant authorities (В§243(a)(1) / В§243(b) / В§243(c) / В§246(c) / В§246A / TCJA В§ 13002).

`traderview-expense::section_250` is the **IRC В§250 GILTI / FDII deduction module (renamed NCTI / FDDEI post-OBBBA 2025)** вҖ” relevant for any US C corporation with foreign-sourced income. TCJA В§ 14202 (P.L. 115-97) added В§250 effective 2018; the **One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025** (OBBBA) substantially modified it for tax years beginning after 2025-12-31. Module returns year-aware deductions and effective rates ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 250](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/250), [EY вҖ” OBBBA International Tax Reforms](https://www.ey.com/en_gl/technical/tax-alerts/united-states-changes-to-gilti-fdii-and-beat-among-others-included-in-final-reconciliation-legislation-but-not-previously-proposed-remedy-for-unfair-foreign-taxes), [Steptoe вҖ” International Tax Changes in OBBBA](https://www.steptoe.com/en/news-publications/international-tax-changes-in-the-one-big-beautiful-bill-act.html)).

**Deduction percentages and effective rates** (assumes В§ 11 21% corporate rate):

| Period          | Income type | Deduction % | Effective rate |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------|----------------|
| 2018-2025       | GILTI       | **50%**     | **10.5%**      |
| 2018-2025       | FDII        | **37.5%**   | **13.125%**    |
| 2026+ (OBBBA)   | NCTI        | **40%**     | **12.6%**      |
| 2026+ (OBBBA)   | FDDEI       | **33.34%**  | **14.0%**      |

Each rate boundary pinned: `pre_obbba_gilti_50_pct_deduction` + `pre_obbba_fdii_37_5_pct_deduction` + `pre_obbba_effective_gilti_rate_10_5_pct` (1050bp) + `pre_obbba_effective_fdii_rate_13_125_pct` (1312bp). Post-OBBBA: `post_obbba_ncti_40_pct_deduction` + `post_obbba_fddei_33_34_pct_deduction` + `post_obbba_effective_ncti_rate_12_6_pct` (1260bp) + `post_obbba_effective_fddei_rate_14_pct` (1399bp).

**OBBBA structural changes** (eff. 2026-01-01):

- **Renaming**: GILTI вҶ’ NCTI (Net CFC Tested Income); FDII вҶ’ FDDEI (Foreign-Derived Deduction Eligible Income)
- **DTIR / NDTIR eliminated**: the 10% return on Qualified Business Asset Investment (QBAI) that reduced pre-OBBBA GILTI inclusion is gone. Module returns `dtir_deduction_dollars: 0` post-OBBBA regardless of QBAI input. Pinned by `pre_obbba_dtir_10_pct_of_qbai` ($2M QBAI вҶ’ $200k DTIR вҶ’ $800k net GILTI) vs `post_obbba_dtir_eliminated` ($2M QBAI ignored, full $1M NCTI inclusion).
- **FTC for deemed-paid taxes raised from 80% to 90%**. Pinned by `pre_obbba_ftc_80_pct` ($100k taxes вҶ’ $80k credit) + `post_obbba_ftc_90_pct` ($100k вҶ’ $90k credit).
- Interest expense and R&E expenditures EXCLUDED from allocable expenses in computing Deduction Eligible Income (DEI) вҖ” caller-side computation.

**Year boundary**: tax years 2018-2025 use pre-OBBBA rates; 2026+ uses post-OBBBA. Pinned by `year_2025_pre_obbba` + `year_2026_post_obbba`.

**Note label changes track regime**: pre-OBBBA note uses "GILTI/FDII" labels; post-OBBBA uses "NCTI/FDDEI" labels + explicitly mentions DTIR elimination. Pinned by `pre_obbba_note_uses_gilti_fdii_labels` + `post_obbba_note_uses_ncti_fddei_labels` + `post_obbba_note_mentions_dtir_elimination`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-250`. Twenty tests pin: **pre-OBBBA GILTI 50% deduction** ($1M Г— 50% = $500k); **pre-OBBBA FDII 37.5% deduction** ($500k Г— 37.5% = $187,500); **pre-OBBBA effective GILTI rate 10.5%** (1050bp); pre-OBBBA effective FDII rate 13.125% (1312bp); **pre-OBBBA DTIR 10% of QBAI** ($2M QBAI вҶ’ $200k DTIR + reduced GILTI to $800k); **pre-OBBBA FTC 80%** ($100k taxes вҶ’ $80k FTC); **post-OBBBA NCTI 40% deduction**; **post-OBBBA FDDEI 33.34% deduction**; **post-OBBBA effective NCTI rate 12.6%**; **post-OBBBA effective FDDEI rate 14%**; **post-OBBBA DTIR eliminated** (regression); **post-OBBBA FTC 90%**; year 2025 pre-OBBBA + 2026 post-OBBBA boundary; **pre-OBBBA note uses GILTI/FDII labels**; **post-OBBBA note uses NCTI/FDDEI labels + DTIR elimination call-out**; citation mentions TCJA В§ 14202 + One Big Beautiful Bill Act + NCTI + FDDEI; $1B precision; zero income zero deduction.

`traderview-expense::section_530` is the **IRC В§530 Coverdell Education Savings Account (ESA) module** вҖ” tax-favored education savings vehicle for trader/landlord parents and grandparents. Contributions are NOT deductible (unlike В§ 219 IRA + В§ 223 HSA siblings) but earnings grow tax-free and qualified withdrawals for K-12 and post-secondary education expenses are tax-free. Distinct from В§ 529 plans which are administered separately.

**В§ 530(b)(1)(A)(ii) statutory cap = $2,000/year per beneficiary** вҖ” UNCHANGED SINCE 2002. Does NOT inflation-adjust. The $2,000 cap is AGGREGATE across all contributors (parent + grandparent + others combined). Sibling to `section_219` IRA $1,000 catch-up + `section_221` student loan interest $2,500 cap вҖ” all three are statutory amounts that don't inflation-adjust unlike most other tax limits.

**Beneficiary age limits**:

| Requirement | Limit | Special-needs waiver |
|-------------|-------|----------------------|
| Contribution eligibility (В§ 530(b)(1)(A)(i)) | Under age 18 | Yes (В§ 530(d)(7)) |
| Distribution deadline (В§ 530(d)(8)) | Age 30 | Yes (В§ 530(d)(7)) |

**MAGI phaseout** (В§ 530(c)) over $15K window (unmarried) / $30K (joint):

| Filing status | Phaseout range |
|---------------|----------------|
| Single / HoH / QW | $95,000-$110,000 |
| Married Filing Jointly / QW | $190,000-$220,000 |
| MFS | Uses single-status range |

**Statutory cap pinning**: $2,000 stays $2,000 in 2025 + 2026 + 2030 + every future year unless Congress amends. (Note: В§ 529 plans got a separate K-12 limit increase from $10K to $20K for 2026 under OBBBA; Coverdell ESAs did NOT receive any parallel increase.)

**В§ 4973 6% excise tax** on excess contributions imposed on the BENEFICIARY (not the contributor) annually each year the excess remains in the account. Distinguishes Coverdell from IRA/HSA where the contributor bears the excise.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-530`. Twenty-two tests pin: **single under $95K full $2,000 contribution**; **single at $95K boundary full** (вүӨ strict); **single at $110K boundary zero**; **single midpoint $102.5K half = $1,000**; **MFJ under $190K full $2,000** + phaseout range invariant; **MFJ at $220K zero**; **beneficiary age 18 not eligible without special needs** (regression вҖ” strict under-18 unless waived); **beneficiary age 17 eligible**; **beneficiary age 30 with special needs still eligible** (В§ 530(d)(7) waiver); **excess contribution 6% excise** ($3,000 contributed вҶ’ $1,000 excess вҶ’ $60 excise); **no excess at $2,000 cap boundary** (вүӨ strict); **one cent above cap triggers excise** (off-by-one); **statutory $2,000 cap does NOT inflation-adjust** (regression-critical 2025/2026/2030 same-cap invariant); **MFJ phaseout midpoint $205K = $1,000** (50% Г— $2K); **HoH uses single phaseout range**; **QW uses MFJ phaseout range**; **MFS uses single range** (regression вҖ” same as section_221 distinguishing pattern); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 530(b)(1)(A)(ii) + $2,000 + unchanged since 2002 + В§ 530(c) + В§ 530(d)(7) + В§ 4973); **negative inputs clamped**; **beneficiary 18 with special needs eligible**; **high excess high excise** ($10K вҶ’ $8K excess вҶ’ $480 excise); **worked example grandparent at $105K MAGI** ($666.67 reduced contribution; $2K Г— 5/15 truncated).

`traderview-expense::section_59a` is the **IRC В§59A Base Erosion and Anti-Abuse Tax (BEAT) module** вҖ” the TCJA anti-base-erosion minimum tax targeting large US multinationals that deduct payments to foreign related parties. Added by TCJA P.L. 115-97 В§ 14401 effective 2018; substantially modified by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) of 2025 effective for tax years beginning after 2025-12-31.

**Applicability gates** вҖ” three gates must all be met (categorically excluded entities short-circuit before gates):

| Gate | Standard C corp | Bank / registered securities dealer |
|------|-----------------|--------------------------------------|
| Gross receipts (3-yr avg) | вүҘ $500M | вүҘ $500M |
| Base erosion percentage | вүҘ 3% | вүҘ 2% |
| Entity excluded | S corp / REIT / RIC | (same) |

**BEAT rate by tax year** вҖ” OBBBA changed the post-2025 rate from TCJA's scheduled 12.5% (13.5% banks) to a permanent 10.5% (11.5% banks):

| Tax year | Standard rate | Bank surcharge |
|----------|---------------|----------------|
| 2018 | 5% | 6% |
| 2019-2025 | 10% | 11% |
| **2026+ (post-OBBBA)** | **10.5%** | **11.5%** |
| 2026+ (TCJA-scheduled, repealed) | 12.5% | 13.5% |

**Computation**: `BEAT = max(0, BEAT_rate Г— MTI вҲ’ Regular_Tax_Liability)` where `MTI = Taxable_Income + Base_Erosion_Payments + NOL Г— BEP`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-59a`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **2018 phase-in 5% standard + 6% banks**; 2019/2025 standard 10%; **2025 banks 11%**; **2026 post-OBBBA permanent 10.5%** (NOT TCJA's scheduled 12.5%); **2026 banks 11.5%**; year boundary 2025 pre-OBBBA / 2026 post-OBBBA; **gross receipts below $500M fails gate**; 3-yr average computed correctly across uneven years (1B + 500M + 300M = 600M); **gross receipts exactly $500M meets test** (boundary); **BEP exactly 3% meets standard threshold** ($30M / $1B); **BEP 2% fails standard** but **meets bank threshold**; S corp / REIT / RIC each categorically excluded under В§59A(e)(2); **basic 2025 computation** ($100M TI + $50M base erosion = $150M MTI Г— 10% = $15M tentative вүӨ $21M regular вҶ’ BEAT = 0); **positive BEAT when tentative exceeds regular** ($15M вҲ’ $10M = $5M); **post-OBBBA 10.5% case** ($150M Г— 10.5% вҲ’ $10M = $5.75M); **BEAT zero when not applicable regardless of arithmetic** (BEP-fail short-circuits); **NOL addback proportional to BEP** ($10M Г— 5% = $500k MTI bump); citation mentions TCJA + В§14401 + OBBBA + 10.5%; note for 2025 says "pre-OBBBA TCJA regime"; note for 2026 says "post-OBBBA permanent regime"; **zero deductions yields zero BEP** (divide-by-zero defensive); **negative taxable income floors tentative at zero** (MTI.max(0) regression target).

`traderview-expense::section_67g` is the **IRC В§ 67(g) TCJA misc itemized deduction suspension module** вҖ” single most important reason traders ELECT trader status under В§ 475(f). Added by Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 В§ 11045 (Pub. L. 115-97, December 22, 2017); originally scheduled to sunset after December 31, 2025; **One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 (H.R. 1, signed July 4, 2025) made В§ 67(g) PERMANENT** вҖ” no further sunset. Companion to `section_475` (trader mark-to-market election), `section_162` (trade-or-business deductions), `section_212` (production-of-income expenses вҖ” now suspended), `section_67e` (estates/trusts carveout), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% вҖ” trader business income exempt).

Trader-critical fact patterns:

- **Investor WITHOUT В§ 475(f) trader status** вҖ” ALL investment-related expenses NON-DEDUCTIBLE under В§ 67(g) (investment advisory fees, custody fees, subscription fees, financial publications, travel to investment seminars, home office, trading platform fees).
- **Trader WITH В§ 475(f) trader status** вҖ” SAME expenses fully deductible as В§ 162 trade-or-business on Schedule C; ESCAPES В§ 67(g) suspension; ALSO exempt from В§ 1411 NIIT.
- **Hedge fund LP investor (non-trader fund)** вҖ” K-1 В§ 212 expenses passed through as misc itemized вҶ’ NON-DEDUCTIBLE.
- **Hedge fund LP investor (trader-status fund)** вҖ” K-1 В§ 162 expenses passed through as Schedule C ordinary deductions вҶ’ fully deductible.
- **Estates and trusts** вҖ” В§ 67(e) carveout preserves administration expense deductibility per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.67-4 + IRS Notice 2018-61.

**В§ 67(g) operative provision** вҖ” notwithstanding subsection (a), NO MISCELLANEOUS ITEMIZED DEDUCTION shall be allowed for any taxable year beginning AFTER DECEMBER 31, 2017. **PERMANENT after OBBBA 2025**.

**В§ 67(b) twelve exempt categories** (NOT subject to В§ 67(g) suspension):

| # | Source | Item |
|---|--------|------|
| 1 | В§ 67(b)(1) | В§ 163 interest |
| 2 | В§ 67(b)(2) | В§ 164 taxes |
| 3 | В§ 67(b)(3) | В§ 165(a) casualty losses |
| 4 | В§ 67(b)(4) | В§ 170 charitable contributions |
| 5 | В§ 67(b)(5) | В§ 213 medical expenses |
| 6 | В§ 67(b)(6) | В§ 691(c) IRD deductions |
| 7 | В§ 67(b)(7) | В§ 215 alimony (pre-2019 divorces) |
| 8 | В§ 67(b)(8) | В§ 217 moving (armed forces post-TCJA) |
| 9 | В§ 67(b)(9) | В§ 1341 claim of right |
| 10 | В§ 67(b)(10) | Gambling losses |
| 11 | В§ 67(b)(11) | В§ 642(c) trust/estate charity |
| 12 | В§ 67(b)(12) | В§ 7702B(a)(2) qualified long-term care insurance |

**В§ 67(e) Estates and trusts carveout** вҖ” preserves deductibility of (1) costs incurred in connection with administration of estate/trust which would not have been incurred but for trust/estate; (2) distributions to beneficiaries; (3) distributable net income computations. Treas. Reg. В§ 1.67-4 + IRS Notice 2018-61 govern.

**В§ 475(f) trader election вҖ” single most important escape valve** from В§ 67(g) suspension: converts В§ 212 investment expenses into В§ 162 trade-or-business deductions on Schedule C; ALSO converts trading gain/loss to ordinary mark-to-market; ALSO exempts trader business income from В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8% surtax.

**Items SUSPENDED by В§ 67(g)** вҖ” unreimbursed employee business expenses (Form 2106), investment advisory fees, investment custody/safekeeping fees, financial publication subscriptions, investment-related professional fees (CPA, attorney), home office (employee or investor), trading platform subscriptions (non-trader status), travel to investment seminars, tax-return preparation fees (individual), hobby losses, safe deposit box fees, В§ 212(1) production-of-income, В§ 212(2) management/conservation/maintenance, В§ 212(3) tax determination/collection.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-67g`. Thirty-six tests pin: **investor В§ 212 expense suspended zero deduction**; **trader В§ 212 expense escapes via В§ 475(f)**; **В§ 162 business expense not suspended**; **В§ 67(b) exempt item not suspended**; **estate admin expense preserved by В§ 67(e) carveout**; **estate В§ 212 expense still suspended**; **pre-TCJA 2017 no suspension**; **TCJA 2018 boundary engages suspension**; **OBBBA 2026 permanent engages**; **OBBBA 2025 not yet permanent**; **unreimbursed employee expense suspended**; **tax prep fee suspended**; **hobby loss suspended**; **hedge fund LP investor fund В§ 212 suspended**; **hedge fund LP trader fund В§ 212 escapes**; **investor В§ 212 post-TCJA emits В§ 475(f) escape valve note**; **taxpayer status truth table five cells**; **expense category truth table seven cells**; **trader status uniquely escapes В§ 212 suspension invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 67(a)-(g) + TCJA 2017 В§ 11045 + Pub. L. 115-97 + OBBBA 2025 + H.R. 1 + July 4, 2025 + В§ 162 + В§ 212 + В§ 67(b)(1)-(12) + В§ 67(e) + В§ 475(f) + В§ 1411 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.67-4 + IRS Notice 2018-61); **note pins subsection (g) no misc itemized**; **note pins TCJA 2017 origin**; **note pins OBBBA 2025 permanent**; **note pins subsection (b) twelve exempt categories**; **note pins subsection (e) estate/trust carveout**; **note pins В§ 475(f) trader election escape valve**; **note pins suspended items comprehensive list**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins PFIC interaction** (В§ 1295 QEF + В§ 1296 mark-to-market); **note pins В§ 1411 NIIT interaction** (3.8% surtax compound); **deduction pathway text** for investor В§ 212 suspension / trader В§ 475(f) / В§ 67(b) exempt / В§ 67(e) estate; **defensive zero expense no failure reason**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::section_6045a` is the **IRC В§6045A broker-to-broker transfer statement module** вҖ” trader-critical for anyone changing brokers (transferring stock, options, debt, or digital-asset positions). В§ 6045A requires the TRANSFERRING broker (or other "applicable person") to furnish a written information statement to the RECEIVING broker within 15 days of the transfer. The receiving broker uses this statement to populate Form 1099-B basis reporting under В§ 6045 on the eventual sale. Direct sibling to `section_6045` (downstream Form 1099-B) and `section_6045b` (upstream issuer Form 8937).

**Four operative subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 6045A(a) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045A(a) | **General rule** вҖ” applicable person transferring custody of covered security to broker must furnish written information statement |
| В§ 6045A(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045A(b)(1) | **Applicable person** вҖ” any broker as defined in В§ 6045(c)(1) |
| В§ 6045A(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045A(b)(2) | **Applicable person** вҖ” other persons per Secretary's regulations |
| В§ 6045A(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045A(c) | **15-day deadline** from date of transfer |
| В§ 6045A(d) | Pub. L. 117-58 В§ 80603 (effective post-2025-12-31) | **Digital-asset transfer return** вҖ” broker transferring digital asset to non-broker account must make return showing transfer information |

**Statement content (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045A-1)** for covered securities includes:
- Transferring + receiving broker name/address/TIN
- Customer name/address/TIN
- Security CUSIP / identifier
- Quantity / share count
- **Adjusted basis**
- **Original acquisition date**
- Type of security
- **Wash-sale-related flag** (per В§ 1091)

**В§ 6045A(d) digital-asset transfer return regime is post-2025-12-31 effective.** Added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act of 2021 В§ 80603. Trigger: (1) security is a digital asset, (2) direction is broker-to-non-broker account, AND (3) receiving party is NOT known by the broker to also be a broker. All three must be satisfied. Pinned by `digital_asset_broker_to_non_broker_return_required`, `digital_asset_broker_to_known_broker_no_d_return`, `digital_asset_broker_to_broker_no_d_return`, and the 4-cell invariant `digital_asset_return_only_triggered_by_specific_combo_invariant`.

**Non-covered securities are entirely outside В§ 6045A scope.** Acquired before 2011 for equity, before 2012 for mutual funds, before 2014 for debt and options вҖ” no basis reporting, no transfer statement required. Pinned by `non_covered_security_outside_scope_compliant` and `non_covered_security_late_furnish_still_compliant` (even 100-day delay without statement is compliant for non-covered).

**В§ 6045A(c) 15-day deadline is INCLUSIVE at the boundary.** Day 14 = compliant; day 15 (at boundary) = compliant; day 16 = violation. Pinned by `covered_security_at_15_day_boundary_compliant`, `covered_security_past_15_day_deadline_violation`, and the boundary invariant `day_14_compliant_day_16_violation_boundary` (5 cells: 0/14/15/16/30 days).

**Three statement-content violations are pinned independently**:
- `missing_basis_violation` вҖ” Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045A-1 adjusted basis missing
- `missing_acquisition_date_violation` вҖ” В§ 1.6045A-1 acquisition date missing
- `missing_wash_sale_flag_violation` вҖ” В§ 1.6045A-1 wash-sale flag missing (cites В§ 1091)

Each is a standalone violation; statement can fail multiple content gates simultaneously.

**Content requirements only apply to covered traditional securities.** Digital-asset transfers don't trigger the same В§ 1.6045A-1 content-check violations because the digital-asset regulation regime is structurally different. Pinned by `content_requirements_only_apply_to_covered_securities_invariant`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6045a`. Twenty tests pin: **covered security broker-to-broker within 15 days compliant**; **at 15-day boundary compliant**; **past 15-day deadline violation** (day 16); **statement not furnished violation**; **missing basis violation** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045A-1); **missing acquisition date violation**; **missing wash-sale flag violation** (cites В§ 1091); **non-covered security outside scope compliant**; **non-covered late furnish still compliant**; **digital asset broker-to-non-broker return required** (В§ 6045A(d)); **digital asset broker-to-known-broker no (d) return**; **digital asset broker-to-broker no (d) return**; **zero days since transfer compliant**; **day 14/15/16/30 boundary truth table 5-cell invariant**; **only-covered-or-digital-asset-requires-statement 3-type invariant**; **digital asset return only triggered by specific combo 4-condition invariant**; **deadline constant 15 days invariant**; **citation pins subsections per security type** (В§ 6045A(a)/(b)(1)/(c) + В§ 1.6045A-1 for covered; В§ 6045A(d) + Pub. L. 117-58 for digital; "non-covered securities" for non-covered); **content requirements only apply to covered securities invariant**; **sibling-module note across all security types** (UX-text regression for В§ 6045 + В§ 6045B pairing).

`traderview-expense::section_6045b` is the **IRC В§6045B issuer reporting of organizational actions affecting basis (Form 8937) module** вҖ” trader-critical for any holder of stock that undergoes a corporate action (stock split, spin-off, merger, return-of-capital distribution). В§ 6045B requires the ISSUER of the specified security to report the organizational action AND the quantitative effect on basis to the IRS via Form 8937. Trader uses the issuer's Form 8937 to adjust basis for the post-action holding. Direct upstream feeder to `section_6045` (broker Form 1099-B reporting that consumes issuer's basis adjustments).

**Five operative subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 6045B(a) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045B(a) | **Issuer return** вҖ” describe organizational action AND quantitative effect on basis |
| В§ 6045B(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045B(b)(1) | **45-day deadline** from date of organizational action |
| В§ 6045B(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045B(b)(2) | **Alternative deadline** вҖ” January 15 of year following calendar year of action |
| В§ 6045B(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045B(c) | **Holder statement** вҖ” furnish written statement to nominees and holders by January 15 of following year |
| В§ 6045B(d) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 6045B(d) | **"Specified security"** defined by В§ 6045(g)(3) cross-reference |
| В§ 6045B(e) | Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045B-1(a)(3) | **Public website waiver** вҖ” posting Form 8937 on public website for **вүҘ 10 years** waives IRS filing duty |

**В§ 6045B(b) deadline = EARLIER of (1) 45 days OR (2) January 15 of following year.** Either trigger requires filing by that earlier date; the 45-day prong drives the immediate compliance, while the Jan-15 prong catches actions taken late in the calendar year. Pinned by `past_45_day_deadline_violation` (day 46), `january_15_passed_triggers_deadline_violation`, and the boundary invariant `deadline_45_days_strict_boundary_truth_table_invariant` (5 cells: 0/44/45/46/100 days).

**Public-website waiver under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045B-1(a)(3) requires вүҘ 10 years of posting.** Issuer that posts a completed, signed Form 8937 in readily accessible format on a public website for at least 10 years is DEEMED to satisfy the IRS filing duty. Posting < 10 years does not qualify. Pinned by `public_website_10_year_posting_waives_irs_filing`, `public_website_15_year_posting_satisfies_waiver`, `public_website_9_year_posting_fails_waiver`, and the 6-cell invariant `website_exception_requires_min_10_years_invariant` (0/5/9/10/15/20 years).

**Website waiver overrides filing deadline violation.** Even where the 45-day deadline and the Jan-15 alternative have both passed, a properly satisfied website waiver excuses the IRS filing failure. Pinned by `public_website_waiver_overrides_deadline_violation`.

**В§ 6045B(c) holder statement is separately required regardless of filing method.** Whether the issuer files Form 8937 with the IRS or posts on a public website, the issuer must furnish the written statement to nominees and security holders by January 15 of the year following the action. Pinned by `missing_holder_statement_violation`.

**Seven action-type notes** cover the most common organizational actions affecting basis:
- Stock split (per-share basis ratio adjustment)
- Stock dividend (В§ 305(a) non-recognition)
- Spin-off / split-off (В§ 358 basis allocation)
- Merger / acquisition (В§ 368 reorganization or В§ 1012 cost basis)
- Recapitalization (В§ 368(a)(1)(E) E-reorganization)
- Return of capital (basis reduction; excess capital gain)
- Other (catch-all)

Pinned by `stock_split_action_note_present`, `stock_dividend_action_note_cites_section_305`, `spin_off_action_note_cites_section_358`, `merger_action_note_cites_section_368`, `return_of_capital_action_note_explains_basis_reduction`, and `all_seven_action_types_produce_distinct_notes_invariant`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6045b`. Twenty-four tests pin: **within 45 days IRS Form 8937 compliant**; **at 45-day boundary compliant**; **past 45-day deadline violation** (day 46); **January 15 passed triggers deadline violation**; **missing basis adjustment description violation** (В§ 6045B(a)(1)); **missing quantitative effect violation** (В§ 6045B(a)(2)); **missing holder statement violation** (В§ 6045B(c)); **public website 10-year posting waives IRS filing**; **public website 15-year posting satisfies waiver**; **public website 9-year posting fails waiver**; **public website waiver overrides deadline violation**; **stock split action note present**; **stock dividend cites В§ 305(a)**; **spin-off cites В§ 358**; **merger cites В§ 368**; **return of capital explains basis reduction**; **deadline 45 days strict boundary truth table 5-cell invariant** (0/44/45/46/100 days); **website exception only satisfied for public website method invariant**; **website exception requires min 10 years 6-cell invariant** (0/5/9/10/15/20 years); **all 7 action types produce distinct notes invariant**; **citation pins subsections per path** (В§ 6045B(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)/(d) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045B-1(a)(3)); **primary deadline constant 45 days invariant**; **website min 10 years constant invariant**; **sibling-module note across all 7 action types** (UX-text regression for В§ 6045 + Form 1099-B downstream).

`traderview-expense::section_6045` is the **IRC В§6045 broker information reporting module (Form 1099-B + Form 1099-DA)** вҖ” affects every trader and crypto investor. В§6045(a) requires brokers вҖ” defined as anyone in the ordinary course of business standing ready to effect sales for others вҖ” to file information returns for customer sales/exchanges. В§6045(g) bifurcates securities into COVERED (broker required to report adjusted BASIS) vs NON-COVERED (gross proceeds only). Sibling to `section_6050w` (Form 1099-K payment-settlement-entity threshold).

**Acquisition-date cutoffs** for covered-security status (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)):

| Instrument | Cutoff | Citation |
|------------|--------|----------|
| Stock | **2011-01-01** | В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(A) |
| Mutual fund / DRIP | **2012-01-01** | В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(B) |
| Less-complex debt | **2014-01-01** | В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(C) |
| More-complex debt | **2016-01-01** | В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(D) |
| **Digital asset** | **2026-01-01** + continuous broker-account holding | В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(F) (NEW per IIJA В§ 80603) |

**Form selection**: digital assets вҶ’ **Form 1099-DA** (new, eff. 2025-01-01); securities вҶ’ **Form 1099-B**. For 2025 transactions: digital asset brokers file 1099-DA but report **gross proceeds only** because no digital assets meet the 2026 covered-status cutoff yet. Beginning 2026, basis reporting phases in for digital assets acquired and held continuously in a broker's account after the cutoff.

**No de minimis** вҖ” unlike most 1099 forms, В§ 6045 has NO dollar threshold for covered securities. Every sale reported regardless of size.

**Digital asset continuous-broker-account requirement** is the regression-critical pin: even a 2026-acquired digital asset is NON-COVERED if it was moved between brokers or held off-platform at any point. The "continuous broker-account holding" requirement under В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(F) bars wallet-to-broker transfers from qualifying.

**IRS Notice 2025-33** extended digital-asset broker transitional relief through 2027 (not modeled in detail here вҖ” caller's responsibility).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6045`. Twenty-three tests pin: **stock acquired 2015 covered** + Form 1099-B; **stock acquired 2010 non-covered**; **stock at 2011-01-01 boundary covered** (вүҘ strict); **stock at 2010-12-31 boundary non-covered** (regression вҖ” one day matters); **mutual fund 2013 covered + 2011 non-covered**; **less-complex debt 2015 covered + 2013 non-covered**; **more-complex debt 2017 covered + 2015 non-covered**; **digital asset 2025 Form 1099-DA but non-covered** (regression вҖ” 2025 transitional period: form filed, no basis); **digital asset 2026 acquired with continuous broker вҶ’ covered** + В§ 1.6045-1(a)(15)(i)(F); **digital asset 2026 WITHOUT continuous broker non-covered** (regression-critical continuous-holding requirement); **pre-2025 digital asset no form required** (IIJA implementation began 2025); **Other instrument outside scope**; **no de minimis вҖ” one cent covered** + still reported (regression вҖ” В§ 6045 has no minimum); **negative proceeds clamped**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 6045(g) + (a)(15) regulatory cross-references for each instrument); **cutoff chronology invariant** (all 4 securities-cutoff boundaries tested in multi-instrument single-test invariant вҖ” at-boundary covered + one-day-before not covered); **digital asset 2026 boundary + one-day-before** (regression); **digital asset вҶ’ 1099-DA vs stock вҶ’ 1099-B form selection**; **pre-2025 digital asset no form required**; **2025 digital asset no basis reporting**.

`traderview-expense::section_6042` is the **IRC В§ 6042 Returns Regarding Payments of Dividends and Corporate Earnings and Profits (1099-DIV framework)** module вҖ” trader-relevant information-return module because traders receive dividend distributions from common stock, preferred stock, ETFs, mutual funds (RIC), REITs, BDCs, and pass-through MLPs. В§ 6042 governs the 1099-DIV reporting framework. Companion to `section_6041` (general info returns), `section_6045` (broker reporting), `section_6049` (interest 1099-INT/OID), `section_6050W` (1099-K payment card), В§ 3406 (backup withholding), В§ 1(h)(11) (qualified dividends), В§ 199A (REIT/PTP), В§ 1411 (NIIT), В§ 1202 (QSBS), В§ 988 (foreign currency), В§ 901 (foreign tax credit).

**В§ 6042(a)(1) Requirement of reporting** вҖ” every person who (1) makes dividend payments aggregating **$10 or more** during calendar year OR (2) receives dividends as nominee aggregating $10+ and pays to another, SHALL make a return setting forth aggregate amount + name and address of recipient.

**В§ 6042(b) Dividend defined** вҖ” (1) corporate distribution per В§ 316 (out of current or accumulated E&P); (2) stockbroker substitute dividend payment on short sales; (3) capital gain distribution by RIC В§ 852 / REIT В§ 857; **EXCLUDES** exempt-interest dividends from RIC В§ 852(b)(5) and В§ 3406 backup-withheld amounts.

**В§ 6042(c) Statements to recipient** вҖ” written statement showing payor name + address + aggregate dividend amount must be furnished by **January 31** of year following payment.

**В§ 6042(d)(1) Substitute dividend payments** вҖ” payments by stockbroker for dividends on short sales treated as dividends for В§ 6042 purposes.

**В§ 6042(d)(2) Uncertain payments rule** вҖ” if payor unable to determine portion of payment that is dividend, must treat **ENTIRE AMOUNT** as dividend for В§ 6042(a) reporting.

**Form 1099-DIV box breakdown** вҖ” Box 1a total ordinary dividends; Box 1b qualified dividends (В§ 1(h)(11) LTCG rates if 60-day holding period satisfied; 120 days for preferred stock with >366-day dividend); Box 2a total capital gain distributions; Box 2b unrecaptured В§ 1250 gain (25%); Box 2c В§ 1202 QSBS gain (50/75/100% exclusion); Box 2d collectibles 28%; Box 3 return of capital (reduces basis); Box 4 federal tax withheld (В§ 3406); Box 5 В§ 199A REIT/PTP dividends (20% deduction); Box 6+7 В§ 897 ordinary/capital gain (FIRPTA); Box 8+9 foreign tax + country (В§ 901 FTC); Box 10+11 liquidation distributions; Box 12 exempt-interest dividends (В§ 852(b)(5) federal tax-exempt); Box 13 specified private activity bond interest (AMT preference).

**Trader-critical** вҖ” В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8% applies to dividend income over MAGI thresholds ($200K single/$250K MFJ); В§ 199A 20% deduction for REIT dividends (no W-2 wage limit for REIT/PTP); В§ 1202 QSBS gain pass-through via RIC; В§ 988 foreign currency gain/loss on ADR conversions reported separately; В§ 901 foreign tax credit on Box 8.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6042`. Thirty-three tests pin: **$50 ordinary dividend filed compliant**; **$10 boundary compliant**; **$9 below threshold no reporting**; **backup withholding overrides threshold**; **exempt-interest dividend no reporting** (Box 12 В§ 852(b)(5)); **not filed violation** (В§ 6042(a)(1) 1099-DIV); **no statement to recipient violation** (В§ 6042(c) January 31); **uncertain payments rule engaged**; **qualified dividend with 60-day holding LTCG eligible** (В§ 1(h)(11)); **qualified dividend without 60-day holding not LTCG**; **capital gain distribution in scope**; **В§ 1202 QSBS gain in scope**; **nondividend distribution in scope**; **В§ 199A REIT dividend in scope**; **substitute dividend broker in scope**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6042(a)-(d) + 26 CFR В§ 1.6042-2 + Form 1099-DIV + В§ 316 + В§ 852 + В§ 857 + В§ 1(h)(11) + В§ 199A + В§ 1411 + В§ 1202 + В§ 988 + В§ 901); **note pins В§ 6042(a) $10 minimum**; **note pins В§ 6042(b) dividend definition** (В§ 316 + RIC + REIT + EXCLUDES exempt-interest); **note pins В§ 6042(c) January 31 statement**; **note pins В§ 6042(d)(1) substitute dividend on short sales**; **note pins В§ 6042(d)(2) uncertain payments ENTIRE AMOUNT**; **note pins 1099-DIV first box set** (Box 1a + 1b + В§ 1(h)(11) + 60-day holding period + Box 2a + 2c + В§ 1202 QSBS); **note pins 1099-DIV second box set** (Box 5 + В§ 199A + В§ 897 + В§ 901 + Box 12 + В§ 852(b)(5) + Box 13 + AMT); **note pins trader-critical qualified dividend 60-day** (В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8% + В§ 199A); **note pins trader-critical QSBS currency FTC** (В§ 1202 + В§ 988 + В§ 901 + AMT); **note pins cross-references** (В§ 6041 + В§ 6045 + В§ 6049 + В§ 6050W + В§ 3406); **dividend type truth table 8 cells**; **$10 threshold boundary invariant**; **backup withholding overrides threshold invariant**; **exempt-interest dividend uniquely excluded invariant**; **qualified dividend LTCG requires 60-day holding invariant**; **multiple failures stack** (2 violations); **defensive zero dividend no reporting**.

`traderview-expense::section_6049` is the **IRC В§ 6049 Returns Regarding Payments of Interest (1099-INT + 1099-OID framework)** module вҖ” trader-relevant information-return module because traders receive interest income from money market funds, T-bills and Treasury securities (federal interest), municipal bonds (tax-exempt for federal but state-reportable), corporate bonds (taxable), original issue discount (OID) on zero-coupon securities, bank deposit interest, brokerage cash-balance interest, and В§ 988 foreign currency contracts. В§ 6049 governs the 1099-INT + 1099-OID reporting framework. Companion to `section_6041` (general info returns), `section_6045` (broker reporting), `section_6042` (dividends), `section_6050W` (1099-K payment card), В§ 3406 (backup withholding), В§ 6038D (foreign asset reporting).

**В§ 6049(a) Requirement of reporting** вҖ” every person who (1) makes payments of interest aggregating **$10 or more** during calendar year OR (2) receives interest as nominee aggregating $10+ and pays to another, SHALL make a return setting forth aggregate amount + name and address of recipient.

**В§ 6049(b) Interest defined** вҖ” seven categories: (1) registered-form obligations; (2) bank deposit interest; (3) mutual savings + cooperative + building/loan + homestead association; (4) insurance company-held interest; (5) **original issue discount per В§ 1272**; (6) broker-dealer custodial interest; (7) Treasury obligations (federal tax but state-exempt).

**В§ 6049(c) Statements to recipient** вҖ” written statement showing name + address of payor + aggregate interest amount must be furnished to recipient by **January 31** of year following payment.

**В§ 6049(d) Nominee/middleman pass-through** вҖ” broker (В§ 6045(c)) or nominee receiving interest for another is "middleman" subject to В§ 6049; OID holder reporting when OID aggregates $10+.

**В§ 6049(e) Backup withholding** вҖ” В§ 3406 backup withholding triggers reporting **IRRESPECTIVE of $10 threshold** (e.g., when payee failed to furnish W-9 with correct TIN).

**Form 1099-INT** вҖ” Box 1 interest income; Box 2 early withdrawal penalty; Box 3 US savings bond/Treasury obligation interest (federal tax + state-exempt); Box 4 federal tax withheld (В§ 3406 backup); Box 8 tax-exempt interest (municipal bonds В§ 103(a)); Box 9 specified private activity bond interest. **Form 1099-OID** вҖ” Box 1 OID current year; Box 2 other periodic interest; Box 5 market discount; Box 6 acquisition premium per В§ 1272.

**Trader-relevant 1099-INT/OID sources** вҖ” Treasury securities (T-bills + TIPS + Series I bonds вҖ” federal tax/state-exempt); municipal bonds (federal tax-exempt В§ 103(a)); corporate bonds (fully taxable); zero-coupon securities (OID); money market funds; bank deposit; brokerage cash-balance; В§ 988 foreign currency.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6049`. `traderview-expense::section_6050i` is the **IRC В§6050I cash-transaction Form 8300 reporting module** вҖ” affects every trader and landlord operating a trade or business who accepts cash. В§ 6050I(a) requires any person engaged in a trade or business who receives more than **$10,000 in cash** in one transaction (or related transactions within 24 hours) to report to **IRS AND FinCEN** within **15 days** on Form 8300. Penalty for willful failure includes both civil (В§ 6721 вҖ” uncapped at intentional disregard) and criminal (В§ 7203) exposure.

**Cash definition** (В§ 6050I(d)):

| Instrument | Qualifies as cash? |
|------------|---------------------|
| Currency | **Yes** |
| Cashier's check, money order, bank draft | **Yes** if face amount вүӨ $10,000 |
| Personal check | No |
| Wire transfer | No |
| **Digital asset** | **EXCLUDED** (see below) |

**Digital asset transitional exclusion** вҖ” IIJA В§ 80603 (P.L. 117-58) added "digital assets" to В§ 6050I(d) effective 2024-01-01. **IRS Announcement 2024-04** issued transitional guidance stating digital assets **should NOT be included** in the $10,000 threshold until the IRS publishes final regulations and updates Form 8300. As of late 2025, this remains in effect вҖ” digital assets are **currently excluded** from В§ 6050I cash for threshold testing. This module reflects the effective regime (excluded) until IRS turns on the digital-asset portion.

**Strict-greater-than $10,000** вҖ” В§ 6050I requires aggregate strictly EXCEEDING $10,000 (вүӨ $10,000 not reportable). Multiple cash transactions from the same payer within 24 hours aggregate; $6,000 + $6,000 within 24 hours = $12,000 reportable.

**Filing deadline** (В§ 6050I(b)): **15 days** from the transaction date.

**Penalties**: В§ 6721(a) standard tier ~$310/violation (2026 inflation-adjusted); **В§ 6721(e) intentional disregard = GREATER OF $250,000 OR the aggregate amount required to be reported** (effectively uncapped); В§ 7203 willful-failure criminal exposure (felony for В§6050I willful failure under В§ 6050I(g)(2)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6050i`. Twenty-three tests pin: **currency $15K in business filed compliant**; **not-in-business no obligation** + В§ 6050I(a) "trade or business" citation; **currency at $10K boundary NOT reportable** (strict greater-than required); **one cent above $10K reportable** (off-by-one); **cashier's check under $10K face qualifies as cash**; **cashier's check above $10K face NOT cash** (face-limit regression); **personal check NOT cash**; **wire transfer NOT cash**; **digital asset currently EXCLUDED per Announcement 2024-04** + IIJA В§ 80603 + "digital assets currently EXCLUDED" citation (regression-critical IRS transitional exclusion); **related 24-hour aggregation triggers** ($6K + $6K вҶ’ $12K вҶ’ reportable); **form-not-filed violation** + В§ 6721 + В§ 7203 citation; **intentional disregard UNCAPPED penalty** ($500K aggregate вҶ’ $500K penalty + criminal exposure); **intentional disregard $250K floor** (smaller aggregate triggers floor); **late filing violation at 16 days**; **at 15-day boundary compliant** (вүӨ strict); **at 16-day boundary late**; **money order face limit applies** (вүӨ $10K cash + > $10K not cash); **bank draft face limit applies**; **standard 15-day deadline**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 6050I + В§ 6721 + В§ 7203 + IIJA В§ 80603 + Announcement 2024-04); **no-business digital asset still excluded** (regression вҖ” digital asset excluded regardless of T/B); **negative inputs handled**; **currency aggregate-exceeds-threshold flag**.

`traderview-expense::section_6050w` is the **IRC В§6050W payment-settlement-entity Form 1099-K reporting threshold module** вҖ” hits every trader, side-business operator, and landlord who accepts payments through Venmo, PayPal, Cash App, Zelle, eBay, Etsy, Airbnb, Stripe, or Square. The threshold has been a bouncing ball: ARPA 2021 dropped it from $20K/200 to $600 with no transaction floor, IRS delayed enforcement three times, and **OBBBA В§ 70432 (eff. 2025-01-01) fully REVERSED the ARPA reduction retroactively** вҖ” restoring the original $20,000 AND 200 transactions strict-greater-than threshold for 2025 and every later year. The transitional $5K (2024) and $2,500 (2025) from IRS Notice 2024-85 were SUPERSEDED вҖ” the 2025 transitional never bound.

**Two PSE categories with very different thresholds**:

| Entity type | Examples | Threshold | Citation |
|-------------|----------|-----------|----------|
| `MerchantAcquiringEntity` | Stripe, Square, Adyen, Worldpay (payment-card processors) | **NO de minimis** вҖ” every dollar reportable | В§ 6050W(d)(1) |
| `ThirdPartySettlementOrganization` (TPSO) | PayPal, Venmo, Cash App, Zelle, eBay, Etsy, StubHub, Airbnb | Year-dependent (see below) | В§ 6050W(d)(3) + В§ 6050W(e) |

**TPSO threshold by year**:

| Year | Threshold | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Pre-2022 | $20,000 AND 200 transactions | Original В§ 6050W(e) |
| 2022 | $600 nominal (ARPA reduction; IRS Notice 2022-1 delayed enforcement) | ARPA 2021 |
| 2023 | $20,000 AND 200 transactions (IRS Notice 2023-74 delayed reduction) | Notice 2023-74 |
| 2024 | $5,000 transitional (no transaction minimum) | IRS Notice 2024-85 |
| **2025+** | **$20,000 AND 200 transactions** (RETROACTIVE restoration; transitional $2,500 superseded) | **OBBBA В§ 70432** |

**Both prongs must be STRICTLY exceeded** for the $20K/200 regime вҖ” exactly $20,000 AND exactly 200 transactions does NOT trigger 1099-K filing. Only $20,000.01 AND 201 (or more) transactions triggers reporting. This strict-greater-than-on-both is the regression-critical boundary.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6050w`. Nineteen tests pin: **merchant acquiring no-minimum** вҖ” one dollar one transaction triggers reporting on Stripe/Square; **merchant zero not reportable**; **TPSO 2026 OBBBA threshold $25K + 300 tx reportable** + "OBBBA В§ 70432" citation; **TPSO 2026 only dollar threshold met NOT reportable** (regression вҖ” BOTH prongs required); **TPSO 2026 only transaction threshold met NOT reportable**; **TPSO 2026 exact $20K AND exact 200 NOT reportable** (strict-greater-than-on-both boundary regression); **TPSO 2026 $20K + 1 cent AND 201 tx reportable** (off-by-one); **TPSO 2025 OBBBA retroactive supersedes $2,500 transitional** (regression вҖ” 2025 transitional from Notice 2024-85 never binds); **TPSO 2024 transitional $5K no transaction minimum** + Notice 2024-85 citation; **TPSO 2024 under $5K not reportable**; **TPSO 2023 IRS Notice 2023-74 delayed to $20K/200** ($5K + 201 tx in 2023 NOT reportable); **TPSO 2022 ARPA $600 threshold pinned** + ARPA citation (historical accuracy); **TPSO 2021 pre-ARPA original threshold**; **TPSO pre-ARPA one-prong-only NOT reportable** (regression вҖ” pre-ARPA also requires BOTH prongs); **merchant ignores year entirely** (no de minimis regardless); **OBBBA retroactive for 2025/2026/2027/2030** (4-year invariant in single test); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 6050W(d)(1) + В§ 6050W(e) + OBBBA В§ 70432 + Notice 2024-85 + ARPA); **note reports both prongs with MET/NOT-MET when applicable**; **boundary invariant 6-case truth table for 2026** (both-at-threshold=false; dollar-at-count-over=false; dollar-over-count-at=false; both-over=true; dollar-under=false; count-under=false).

`traderview-expense::section_6213` is the **IRC В§ 6213 Tax Court petition 90-day rule** вҖ” after IRS issues a В§ 6212 statutory notice of deficiency ("90-day letter"), the taxpayer has EXACTLY 90 days (150 days if addressed to a person outside the United States) from the mailing date to file a petition with the Tax Court for redetermination. Missing this deadline is catastrophic вҖ” the deficiency becomes assessable under В§ 6213(c), the Tax Court loses jurisdiction, and the only remaining pathway is to pay the deficiency in full and sue for refund in district court or Court of Federal Claims.

**Three input dimensions** drive the effective deadline:

| Field | Statutory effect |
|-------|-------------------|
| `addressed_outside_us` | Extends standard 90-day period to 150 days per В§ 6213(a) |
| `last_day_falls_on_weekend_or_dc_holiday` | В§ 6213(a) extends deadline to next business day |
| `secretary_specified_extended_period_days` | В§ 6213(a) last sentence вҖ” petition timely if filed on or before Secretary-specified date in notice, even if later than 90/150 days |

The effective deadline is the MAXIMUM of (statutory period) + (weekend extension if any) and (statutory period + Secretary-specified extension). Pinned by `outside_us_and_secretary_extended_takes_max` (outside-US 150 + Secretary 10 = 160 days effective).

**В§ 6213(a) deadline is JURISDICTIONAL вҖ” no equitable tolling.** The Tax Court held in Hallmark Research Collective, 159 T.C. No. 6 (2022) that the 90-day deadline is jurisdictional and not subject to equitable tolling. The Third Circuit reached the OPPOSITE conclusion in Culp v. Commissioner, 75 F.4th 196 (3d Cir. 2023), finding the deadline non-jurisdictional. The Supreme Court declined to resolve the split in Boechler, P.C. v. Commissioner, 596 U.S. 199 (2022) (which addressed В§ 6330(d)(1) CDP-levy deadline, NOT В§ 6213). The notes always surface the circuit split. Pinned by `notes_always_include_third_circuit_split` and `citation_pins_subsections_and_cases`.

**В§ 6213(a) restriction on assessment vs В§ 6213(c) failure-to-file consequence.** When a petition is TIMELY filed, В§ 6213(a) bars the IRS from assessing the deficiency until the Tax Court decision becomes final. When the petition is NOT filed within the statutory period, В§ 6213(c) lifts the restriction вҖ” the deficiency may be assessed on notice and demand. Pinned by `assessment_restricted_when_petition_timely`, `assessment_unrestricted_when_petition_missed_and_irs_assessed`, `assessment_restricted_even_when_petition_missed_if_irs_holds_off`, and the 4-case truth-table `assessment_unrestricted_only_when_both_late_and_irs_assessed`.

**The outside-US extension is exactly 60 days.** 150 - 90 = 60-day delta. Pinned by `outside_us_50_day_difference_from_default_invariant` (actually 60 вҖ” test name is a typo but the assertion checks correct 60-day delta).

**The weekend/DC-holiday extension is exactly 1 day.** В§ 6213(a) excludes Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday in the District of Columbia at the LAST DAY only. Pinned by `weekend_extension_one_day` (91 days timely when last day extended) and `weekend_extension_does_not_save_92_day_petition` (92 days still late even with 1-day extension).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6213`. Twenty-six tests pin: **90-day period default**; **exactly 90 days timely**; **91 days untimely**; **outside-US extends to 150 days**; **outside-US at 150 days timely**; **outside-US 151 days untimely**; **weekend extension one day**; **weekend extension does not save 92-day petition**; **Secretary specified date extends deadline** (120 days timely with 35-day extension вҶ’ 125-day effective); **Secretary specified date earlier does not shorten**; **assessment restricted when petition timely**; **assessment unrestricted when petition missed and IRS assessed** (В§ 6213(c) notice-and-demand path); **citation pins subsections + Hallmark + Culp + Boechler cases**; **notes always include Third Circuit split**; **weekend and outside-US combine** (151 days timely with both extensions); **outside-US and Secretary extended takes MAX** (160 days effective); **zero days obviously timely**; **one year late far outside period**; **Secretary specified zero days no change**; **assessment restricted even when petition missed if IRS holds off** (В§ 6213(a) restriction preserved by IRS inaction); **boundary exactly 90 days no weekend**; **boundary 91 days no weekend untimely**; **outside-US 60-day difference invariant** (150 - 90 = 60); **assessment unrestricted only when both late and IRS assessed** (4-case truth table); **citation includes 2022 jurisdictional case year + 159 T.C. No. 6**; **citation includes Third Circuit 2023 + 75 F.4th 196**.

`traderview-expense::section_6201` is the **IRC В§ 6201 Assessment Authority** module вҖ” foundational grant of IRS power to determine and assess tax liability. Procedural predicate for `section_6203` (method of assessment), `section_6303` (notice and demand вҖ” already shipped), `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6331` (levy authority). Trader-critical because В§ 6201(d) shifts burden of proof to Secretary in deficiency proceedings where taxpayer disputes a third-party information return (1099-B broker reporting, 1099-K payment-card processor reporting, K-1 partnership reporting) AND has fully cooperated with IRS.

**В§ 6201(a) General rule** вҖ” Secretary is authorized and required to make inquiries, determinations, and assessments of **all taxes** (including interest, additional amounts, additions to the tax, and assessable penalties) imposed by Title 26. Three subparagraphs: (1) **taxes shown on return** вҖ” Secretary shall assess all taxes determined by taxpayer or Secretary as to which returns or lists are made; (2) **unpaid taxes payable by stamp** вҖ” Secretary shall establish by regulations mode and time for collection; (3) **erroneous income tax prepayment credits** вҖ” overstated income-tax-withheld-at-source credit or estimated-tax credit may be assessed by Secretary **in the same manner as a mathematical or clerical error**, EXCEPT that В§ 6213(b)(2) abatement-of-math-error procedures **DO NOT apply** to В§ 6201(a)(3) assessments (one-way ratchet against the taxpayer).

**В§ 6201(b) Amount not to be assessed** вҖ” assessment of deficiency restricted by В§ 6213 (taxpayer entitled to deficiency-procedure protections including В§ 6212 SNOD + 90-day Tax Court petition window (150-day window for taxpayers outside US) before assessment may be made).

**В§ 6201(c) Compensation of child** вҖ” any income tax under subtitle A attributable to compensation for personal services rendered by a child may be assessed against the child OR the parent who must include the income.

**В§ 6201(d) Required reasonable verification of information returns** вҖ” trader-critical burden-shifting rule added by IRS Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998 (RRA 98 В§ 3201). In any **court proceeding**, IF a taxpayer (a) **asserts a reasonable dispute** with respect to any item of income reported on a third-party information return filed under chapter 61 subpart B or C AND (b) **has fully cooperated** with the Secretary (including providing access to and inspection of all witnesses, information, and documents within the control of the taxpayer as reasonably requested), THEN the Secretary shall **have the burden of producing reasonable and probative information concerning such deficiency in addition to such information return**. This is the statutory mechanism by which an active trader can challenge an inflated 1099-B cost basis discrepancy or erroneous K-1 allocation without bearing the burden alone.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6201`. Thirty-one tests pin: **taxes shown on return assessment proceeds**; **no statutory authority fails**; **unpaid stamp taxes authority engaged**; **erroneous prepayment credits authority engaged** (with В§ 6213(b)(2) abatement unavailable); **child personal services income authority engaged**; **substitute for return authority engaged**; **deficiency restriction without SNOD blocks assessment** (В§ 6201(b) + В§ 6213(a)); **deficiency restriction with SNOD and expired window satisfied**; **deficiency restriction with SNOD but window still open blocks**; **deficiency restriction inapplicable proceeds**; **burden shift requires all three conditions** (dispute + reasonable + cooperated); **burden shift fails without dispute assertion**; **burden shift fails without reasonable dispute**; **burden shift fails without full cooperation**; **burden shifted secretary failed to produce evidence assessment fails** (В§ 6201(d)); **burden shifted secretary produced evidence assessment proceeds**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6201(a)-(d) + 26 CFR В§ 301.6201-1 + В§ 6203 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6020(b) + RRA 98 В§ 3201); **note pins general assessment authority**; **note pins three subsections (a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(3)**; **note pins (a)(3) math error abatement unavailable**; **note pins (b) deficiency restriction with 90/150-day windows**; **note pins (c) child compensation**; **note pins (d) burden shift RRA 98 + 1099-B + 1099-K + K-1**; **note pins predicate for collection constellation** (В§ 6203 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331); **assessment basis truth table** (6-cell sweep); **deficiency restriction truth table** (6-cell sweep); **burden shift truth table 8 cells** (only all-three-true triggers shift); **(a)(3) uniquely pins math error abatement unavailability invariant** (4 other bases retain В§ 6213(b)(2) availability); **multiple failures stack** (3 violations); **invariant burden shift requires court proceeding context**; **invariant (a)(3) overstated prepayment no abatement pathway**.

`traderview-expense::section_6203` is the **IRC В§ 6203 Method of Assessment** module вҖ” the mechanical procedure by which an IRS assessment under В§ 6201 becomes effective. Trader-procedural-critical because **no lawful В§ 6321 lien attachment OR В§ 6331 levy authority engages without a valid В§ 6203 record of assessment**. Companion to `section_6201` (assessment authority вҖ” already shipped), `section_6303` (notice and demand вҖ” 60 days after В§ 6203 recording), `section_6321` (lien arising at moment of assessment + notice and demand), `section_6331` (levy authority 10 days after notice and demand), `section_6502` (CSED вҖ” 10 years from В§ 6203 recording).

**В§ 6203 General rule** вҖ” the assessment shall be made by **recording the liability of the taxpayer in the office of the Secretary** in accordance with rules or regulations prescribed by the Secretary. **Upon request of the taxpayer, the Secretary SHALL furnish the taxpayer a copy of the record of assessment.**

**26 CFR В§ 301.6203-1 Method of assessment** вҖ” the assessment shall be made by an **assessment officer signing the summary record of assessment**. The summary record, through supporting records, must provide all four mandatory elements: (1) **identification of the taxpayer**; (2) **character of the liability assessed** (income tax, employment tax, penalty); (3) **taxable period, if applicable**; AND (4) **amount of the assessment**. The date of the assessment is the date the summary record is signed by the assessment officer вҖ” this date triggers В§ 6303(a) 60-day notice and demand window and В§ 6502 10-year collection statute.

**Form 23-C вҖ” Assessment Certificate вҖ” Summary Record of Assessments** вҖ” internal IRS document used to formalize a taxpayer's debt. The creation and signing of this certificate establishes the tax liability in the government's records. **Form 23-C is NOT released to taxpayers** вҖ” IRS treats it as an internal summary document that does not identify individual taxpayers.

**Form 4340 вҖ” Certificate of Assessments, Payments, and Other Specified Matters** вҖ” the document IRS provides when a taxpayer requests a copy of the record of assessment under В§ 6203. Rev. Rul. 2007-21 confirms IRS may choose among documents containing the required regulatory elements; courts have held Form 4340 is **presumptive evidence of valid assessment** (March v. IRS, 335 F.3d 1186 (10th Cir. 2003)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6203`. Thirty tests pin: **fully compliant assessment valid**; **Form 23-C signed compliant**; **no record assessment invalid**; **no record lien and levy predicate fails**; **unsigned record assessment invalid** (В§ 301.6203-1 SIGNING requirement); **missing taxpayer identification invalid** (regulatory element #1); **missing character of liability invalid** (regulatory element #2); **missing taxable period when applicable invalid** (regulatory element #3 conditional); **missing taxable period when NOT applicable valid**; **missing amount invalid** (regulatory element #4); **no taxpayer copy request no obligation**; **taxpayer request Form 4340 satisfies**; **taxpayer request Master File Transcript satisfies**; **taxpayer request no document violation** (В§ 6203 SHALL furnish); **taxpayer request Form 23-C copy invalid response** (NOT released per IRS policy + Rev. Rul. 2007-21); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6203 + 26 CFR В§ 301.6203-1 + Rev. Rul. 2007-21 + Form 23-C + Form 4340 + В§ 6201 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6502 + March v. IRS 335 F.3d 1186); **note pins general rule**; **note pins four regulatory elements** (all four mandatory); **note pins date of assessment triggers collection constellation** (В§ 6303(a) 60-day + В§ 6502 10-year); **note pins Form 23-C NOT released**; **note pins Form 4340 presumptive evidence + March v. IRS**; **note pins Rev. Rul. 2007-21**; **note pins lien and levy predicate chain** (В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6502); **multiple element failures stack** (3 violations); **record format truth table** (3-cell sweep вҖ” Form 23-C/computer-generated valid, NoRecord invalid); **taxpayer copy document truth table** (4-cell sweep вҖ” Form 4340/MFT satisfy, Form 23-C/None violate); **four regulatory elements truth table all combinations**; **unsigned record blocks lien and levy predicate invariant**; **Form 23-C release request uniquely triggers IRS policy failure invariant**; **signed record with all elements satisfies lien predicate invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6212` is the **IRC В§ 6212 Statutory Notice of Deficiency (SNOD вҖ” the "90-day letter" / "ticket to Tax Court")** module вҖ” critical procedural gate before assessment can occur. The IRS must generally issue В§ 6212 SNOD before assessing additional tax, and that SNOD opens the taxpayer's В§ 6213(a) Tax Court petition window. Natural sibling to `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline + restrictions on assessment), `section_6501` (ASED), and `section_6502` (CSED).

**Five subsections govern SNOD validity + procedure**:

| Subsection | Rule | Consequence |
|------------|------|--------------|
| В§ 6212(a) | Secretary authority + certified or registered mail | Other delivery methods invalid |
| В§ 6212(b) | Last known address (Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6212-2) | Wrong address renders SNOD INVALID + assessment invalid |
| В§ 6212(c) | One SNOD per taxable year (exceptions: fraud, omission, В§ 6861 jeopardy, bankruptcy) | Subsequent SNOD barred absent exception |
| В§ 6212(d) | Rescission with taxpayer's WRITTEN consent | SNOD treated as if never issued; В§ 6212(c) does not bar re-issued SNOD |
| В§ 6213(a) | 90-day petition window (150 days if outside US) | Restraint on assessment during window and while petition pending |

**В§ 6212(a) вҖ” Secretary authority**. SNOD must be sent by CERTIFIED or REGISTERED mail. Other delivery methods do not satisfy statute. Pinned by `unauthorized_delivery_method_invalidates_snod` and `registered_mail_satisfies_delivery_requirement`.

**В§ 6212(b) вҖ” Last known address rule (LOAD-BEARING)**. Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6212-2 defines "last known address" as taxpayer's most recent address on IRS records. If IRS uses a stale or wrong address, the SNOD is INVALID вҖ” assessment based on an invalid SNOD is itself invalid. Most common SNOD invalidation defense. Pinned by `wrong_address_invalidates_snod`, `last_known_address_note_when_satisfied`, `invalid_snod_blocks_petition_window`, `snod_invalid_blocks_assessment_bar`.

**В§ 6212(c) вҖ” One SNOD per year**. Generally only one SNOD per taxable year; once issued, additional SNODs barred. Four exceptions: (1) fraud, (2) substantial omission of items required to be shown, (3) jeopardy assessment under В§ 6861, (4) bankruptcy. Pinned by `multiple_snods_without_exception_violates_subsection_c` and `multiple_snods_with_exception_compliant`.

**В§ 6212(d) вҖ” Rescission**. Secretary may rescind SNOD WITH WRITTEN CONSENT of taxpayer. Once rescinded, SNOD treated as if never issued; В§ 6212(c) one-per-year limit does not bar subsequent re-issued SNOD. Pinned by `rescission_with_written_consent_authorized`, `rescission_without_consent_not_authorized`, `rescission_does_not_invalidate_snod_facts`.

**В§ 6213(a) вҖ” Petition deadline + restraint on assessment**. Within 90 days (150 days if taxpayer's address outside US) of SNOD mailing, taxpayer may file Tax Court petition for redetermination of deficiency. During petition window AND while petition pending, IRS is BARRED from assessment. Pinned by `default_petition_deadline_is_90_days`, `outside_us_petition_deadline_is_150_days`, `petition_window_open_within_default_90`, `petition_window_closed_after_90_default`, `petition_window_boundary_day_89_open`, `petition_window_boundary_day_90_closed`, `petition_window_outside_us_boundary_149_open`, `petition_window_outside_us_boundary_150_closed`, `restraint_on_assessment_note_present_when_window_open`.

**Hopkins v. Commissioner (T.C. 2024)** вҖ” taxpayer may equitably rely on "last day to file petition" date stated in SNOD even when that stated date is incorrect (e.g., more than 90 days after actual mailing). Equitable estoppel against IRS where taxpayer relied to detriment. Pinned by `hopkins_reliance_note_when_last_day_statement_present` and `no_hopkins_note_when_last_day_statement_absent`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6212`. Thirty tests pin: **clean SNOD is valid**; **default petition deadline is 90 days**; **outside-US petition deadline is 150 days**; **no SNOD mailed invalid with pre-SNOD note**; **wrong address invalidates SNOD** (В§ 6212(b) load-bearing); **unauthorized delivery method invalidates SNOD** (В§ 6212(a)); **registered mail satisfies delivery requirement**; **multiple SNODs without exception violates В§ 6212(c)**; **multiple SNODs with exception compliant** (fraud / omission / В§ 6861 jeopardy / bankruptcy); **petition window open within default 90**; **petition window closed after 90 default**; **petition window boundary day 89 open**; **petition window boundary day 90 closed**; **petition window outside-US boundary 149 open**; **petition window outside-US boundary 150 closed**; **invalid SNOD blocks petition window**; **rescission with written consent authorized** (В§ 6212(d)); **rescission without consent not authorized**; **Hopkins reliance note when last day statement present** (T.C. 2024); **no Hopkins note when last day statement absent**; **citation pins all subsections** (В§В§ 6212(a), 6212(b), 6212(c), 6212(d), 6213(a)); **citation pins Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6212-2 + IRM 4.8.9 + IRM 8.2.2 + Hopkins**; **restraint on assessment note present when window open** (В§ 6213(a)); **petition deadline note describes days**; **multiple violations all surface** (3-violation simultaneity); **outside-US unique 150-day invariant** (90 vs 150 sweep); **SNOD invalid blocks assessment bar**; **last known address note when satisfied**; **IRM 8.2.2 note always present when SNOD mailed**; **rescission does not invalidate SNOD facts**.

`traderview-expense::section_6330` is the **IRC В§ 6330 Collection Due Process (CDP) levy** module вҖ” companion to В§ 6213 (Tax Court petition from notice of deficiency) and В§ 6320 (CDP for federal tax liens). When the IRS sends a Final Notice of Intent to Levy (Letter 1058 / LT-11), В§ 6330 gives the taxpayer 30 days to request a CDP hearing before IRS Appeals during which collection is SUSPENDED вҖ” the only pre-payment opportunity to raise collection alternatives (В§ 6159 installment agreement, В§ 7122 offer in compromise, currently not collectible), challenge underlying liability (if no prior opportunity), or assert spousal defenses. After IRS Appeals issues a Notice of Determination, the taxpayer has another 30 days to petition the Tax Court.

**Six statutory subsections compose the CDP framework**:

| Subsection | Mechanic |
|------------|----------|
| В§ 6330(a) | 30-day pre-levy written notice required |
| В§ 6330(b) | Right to fair CDP hearing before IRS Appeals |
| В§ 6330(c)(2)(A) | Collection alternatives + spousal defenses |
| В§ 6330(c)(2)(B) | Underlying-liability challenge IF no prior opportunity |
| В§ 6330(d)(1) | 30-day Tax Court petition window |
| В§ 6330(e) | Collection suspension during pending review + В§ 6502/В§ 6531/В§ 6532 limitations periods suspended |
| В§ 6330(f) | Jeopardy / state refund / Federal contractor / disqualified employment tax exceptions |

**Boechler, P.C. v. Commissioner, 596 U.S. 199 (2022) UNANIMOUSLY held the В§ 6330(d)(1) 30-day deadline is NOT JURISDICTIONAL and IS SUBJECT TO equitable tolling.** Sharp contrast to В§ 6213(a) Tax Court petition deadline from a notice of deficiency вҖ” which Hallmark Research Collective (159 T.C. No. 6, 2022) held IS jurisdictional. Boechler missed the deadline by ONE DAY; the Court reversed the Eighth Circuit's dismissal, permitting equitable tolling arguments to proceed. The notes always surface the В§ 6213(a) contrast. Pinned by `tax_court_petition_day_31_without_tolling_facts_untimely`, `tax_court_petition_day_31_with_tolling_facts_timely_under_boechler`, `boechler_one_day_late_with_tolling_facts_timely` (Boechler's actual fact pattern), `section_6213_contrast_note_always_present`, and `citation_pins_subsections_treas_reg_and_cases`.

**Underlying liability challenge gating under В§ 6330(c)(2)(B).** The taxpayer may challenge the EXISTENCE OR AMOUNT of the underlying tax liability at the CDP hearing ONLY IF the taxpayer did not receive a notice of deficiency under В§ 6212 and did not otherwise have an opportunity to dispute. When the taxpayer received a prior notice of deficiency, the CDP hearing is limited to procedural matters and collection alternatives. Pinned by `underlying_liability_challenge_available_when_no_prior_opportunity` and `underlying_liability_challenge_barred_when_prior_opportunity_existed`.

**В§ 6330(f) jeopardy / state-refund / Federal-contractor / disqualified-employment-tax exceptions bypass the entire CDP framework.** Subsections (a), (b), (c), and (e) do not apply вҖ” no 30-day notice, no CDP hearing, no Tax Court review, no collection suspension. Pinned by `jeopardy_exception_bypasses_entire_cdp_framework` and `jeopardy_exception_invariant_no_cdp_or_tax_court_or_suspension` (4-cell truth table sweep confirming jeopardy cancels CDP regardless of timing).

**В§ 6330(e) collection suspension reaches limitations periods too.** When a timely CDP request is filed, the levy actions AND the running of В§ 6502 (collection statute), В§ 6531 (criminal limitations), and В§ 6532 (suit-for-refund limitations) are SUSPENDED for the duration of the pending hearing and appeals. Pinned by `collection_suspension_engages_with_timely_cdp_request` and `collection_suspension_does_not_engage_with_late_cdp_request`.

**Late CDP request (day 31+) loses judicial review path.** The taxpayer may still request an "equivalent hearing" under Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6330-1(i) but has no right to Tax Court review. Pinned by `day_31_cdp_request_loses_judicial_review` and `liability_challenge_unavailable_when_cdp_request_late`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6330`. Twenty-five tests pin: **timely CDP request grants hearing and suspends collection**; **exactly 30 days CDP request entitled to hearing**; **day 31 CDP request loses judicial review** (equivalent hearing only); **Tax Court petition within 30 days timely**; **Tax Court petition at 30 days boundary timely**; **Tax Court petition day 31 without tolling facts untimely**; **Tax Court petition day 31 with tolling facts timely under Boechler**; **Tax Court petition at day one late with tolling вҖ” classic Boechler facts**; **no Notice of Determination no petition pathway**; **jeopardy exception bypasses entire CDP framework** (В§ 6330(f)); **underlying liability challenge available when no prior opportunity** (В§ 6330(c)(2)(B)); **underlying liability challenge barred when prior opportunity existed**; **collection alternative proposal surfaces in notes** (В§ 6330(c)(2)(A)(iii)); **no collection alternative no alternative note**; **В§ 6213 contrast note always present** (Hallmark jurisdictional contrast); **citation pins subsections + Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6330-1 + Boechler + Hallmark**; **collection suspension engages with timely CDP request** (В§ 6502/В§ 6531/В§ 6532); **collection suspension does not engage with late CDP request**; **Tax Court petition unavailable when CDP hearing lost**; **day 30 at boundary CDP request timely**; **day 30 at boundary Tax Court petition timely**; **jeopardy exception invariant no CDP or Tax Court or suspension** (4-cell truth table); **Boechler one-day-late with tolling facts timely**; **Boechler one-year-late with tolling facts still timely under module logic** (caller responsibility to assess merit); **liability challenge unavailable when CDP request late**.

`traderview-expense::section_6331` is the **IRC В§ 6331 levy and distraint authority** module вҖ” foundational IRS levy statute defining when IRS may levy upon taxpayer property. Trader-relevant for any taxpayer facing IRS levy threat: trader-traders with unpaid tax can lose brokerage accounts; trader-landlords with unpaid tax can lose rental income via continuous levy.
