Pairs with `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6323` (lien priority), `section_6325` (release/discharge), `section_6334` (exempt property), and `section_6330` (CDP for levies).

**В§ 6331(a) levy authority three-element test**:

1. В§ 6303 notice and demand for tax issued
2. **10 days** elapsed after notice and demand
3. Taxpayer has neglected or refused to pay

Pinned by `fully_compliant_general_property_levy_authorized`, `no_notice_and_demand_violates`, `within_10_day_window_violates` (9-day fails), `at_10_day_boundary_authorized` (exactly 10 = authorized), `no_neglect_to_pay_violates`, `ten_day_uniquely_satisfied_only_with_neglect_invariant`, and `nine_day_violates_ten_day_compliant_precision`.

**В§ 6331(d) 30-day pre-levy notice** вҖ” Secretary must provide taxpayer 30-day notice of intent to levy before general property levy. Notice may be (a) given in person, (b) left at dwelling or usual place of business, or (c) sent by certified or registered mail to last known address. Paired with В§ 6330 CDP framework. Pinned by `no_30_day_notice_violates`.

**В§ 6331(e) continuous wage levy** вҖ” levy on salary or wages has continuous effect from time of levy until release. Attaches to (1) wages earned but not yet paid; (2) advances subsequent to levy; (3) wages becoming payable subsequent to levy. Pinned by `continuous_wage_levy_engages_continuous_effect` and `note_pins_continuous_wage_levy`.

**В§ 6331(h) continuous federal payment levy** вҖ” up to 15% of specified federal payments (Social Security + federal employee retirement + etc.) subject to continuous levy. Pinned by `continuous_federal_payment_engages_continuous_effect` and `note_pins_15_percent_federal_payment_cap`.

**В§ 6331(j) jeopardy levy exception** вҖ” 30-day pre-levy notice does NOT apply if Secretary finds collection is in jeopardy. Paired with В§ 6861/В§ 6862 jeopardy assessment + В§ 7429 judicial review. Pinned by `jeopardy_levy_bypasses_30_day_notice`, `jeopardy_finding_bypasses_30_day_notice` (Boolean-flag also engages bypass), `jeopardy_bypass_truth_table` (6-cell sweep: levy type Г— jeopardy-finding flag), and `note_pins_jeopardy_levy_exception`.

**В§ 6331(k) levy blockers**:
- **В§ 6331(k)(1)** вҖ” no levy while innocent spouse relief request under В§ 6015 pending
- **В§ 6331(k)(2)** вҖ” no levy while CDP hearing under В§ 6330 pending

Pinned by `innocent_spouse_pending_blocks_levy` (В§ 6331(k)(1) + В§ 6015), `cdp_pending_blocks_levy` (В§ 6331(k)(2) + В§ 6330), `cdp_uniquely_pre_emption_invariant`, `k_subsection_blockers_either_blocks_invariant` (either alone blocks; both together = 2 violations), and `note_pins_k_subsection_blockers`.

**Levy type truth table** вҖ” 4-cell sweep: GeneralProperty (no continuous), ContinuousWageLevy (continuous), ContinuousFederalPayment (continuous), JeopardyLevy (no continuous + bypasses 30-day notice). Pinned by `levy_type_truth_table` and `general_property_no_continuous_effect`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6331`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **fully compliant general property levy authorized**; **no notice and demand violates** (В§ 6303); **within 10-day window violates** (9 days fails); **at 10-day boundary authorized** (exactly 10); **no neglect to pay violates**; **no 30-day notice violates** (В§ 6331(d)); **jeopardy levy bypasses 30-day notice** (В§ 6331(j)); **jeopardy finding bypasses 30-day notice** (Boolean flag); **innocent spouse pending blocks levy** (В§ 6331(k)(1) + В§ 6015); **CDP pending blocks levy** (В§ 6331(k)(2) + В§ 6330); **continuous wage levy engages continuous effect** (В§ 6331(e)); **continuous federal payment engages continuous effect** (В§ 6331(h)); **general property no continuous effect**; **levy type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6331(a)-(k) + В§ 301.6331-1 + IRM 5.17.3 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6311 + В§ 6330 + В§ 6015 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6862 + В§ 7429); **note pins 10-day demand**; **note pins 30-day pre-levy notice**; **note pins continuous wage levy**; **note pins 15% federal payment cap**; **note pins jeopardy levy exception**; **note pins (k) subsection blockers**; **jeopardy bypass truth table** (6-cell sweep); **(k) subsection blockers either blocks invariant** (both = 2 failures); **multiple failures stack for bare levy**; **10-day uniquely satisfied only with neglect invariant**; **9-day violates 10-day compliant precision**; **CDP uniquely pre-emption invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6332` is the **IRC В§ 6332 surrender of property subject to levy** module вҖ” third-party (bank + employer + broker + financial institution) holding taxpayer property who receives IRS levy notice must surrender or face personal liability + 50% penalty. Trader-relevant on BOTH sides: (a) trader-traders facing IRS levy on brokerage accounts вҖ” broker as third party must surrender; (b) trader-landlords as third-party levy recipients (employers, vendors) face В§ 6332 surrender obligations. Pairs with `section_6331` (levy authority), `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6303` (notice and demand), `section_6334` (exempt property), and `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy вҖ” INVERSE pathway when third party believes levy is wrongful).

**В§ 6332(a) general surrender obligation** вҖ” any person in possession of (or obligated with respect to) property/rights to property subject to levy must surrender to Secretary upon demand. Pinned by `general_property_surrendered_compliant`, `general_property_not_surrendered_triggers_personal_liability`, and `discharge_only_when_surrendered_invariant`.

**В§ 6332(c) 21-day bank hold** вҖ” banks surrender deposits (including interest) ONLY AFTER 21 days after service of levy. Allows error-correction window for taxpayer to challenge before bank actually releases funds. Pinned by `bank_deposit_within_21_day_hold_not_required_to_surrender`, `bank_deposit_at_21_day_boundary_required_to_surrender` (exactly 21 days = surrender required), `bank_deposit_at_20_days_within_hold`, `bank_deposit_after_21_day_hold_and_not_surrendered_triggers_liability`, `bank_uniquely_engages_21_day_hold_invariant` (only BankDeposit triggers hold), `twenty_one_day_boundary_precision_invariant`, `within_21_day_hold_no_failure_reasons_for_bank`, and `note_pins_21_day_hold`.

**В§ 6332(b) wage/salary continuous levy** вҖ” cross-references В§ 6331(e) continuous wage levy; no 21-day hold for wages. Pinned by `wage_salary_no_21_day_hold` and `life_insurance_no_21_day_hold` (only banks get 21-day hold).

**В§ 6332(d)(1) personal liability for failure to surrender** вҖ” third party liable in own person and estate to United States equal to value of property NOT surrendered, CAPPED at the LESSER of (1) property value and (2) tax liability + costs + В§ 6621 underpayment interest from date of levy. Pinned by `personal_liability_capped_at_tax_liability` (high property + low tax вҶ’ liability = tax), `personal_liability_capped_at_property_value` (low property + high tax вҶ’ liability = property), `defensive_negative_property_value_clamped`, `defensive_negative_tax_liability_clamped`, and `defensive_overflow_50_percent_saturating`.

**В§ 6332(d)(2) 50% additional penalty for failure WITHOUT REASONABLE CAUSE** вҖ” 50% of В§ 6332(d)(1) recoverable amount; NO credit against underlying tax. Pinned by `failure_without_reasonable_cause_triggers_50_percent_penalty` (5 billion personal liability + 2.5 billion penalty = 7.5 billion total exposure), `fifty_percent_penalty_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: only NotSurrendered + NoReasonableCause triggers penalty), `total_exposure_combines_liability_and_penalty`, and `note_pins_50_percent_penalty`.

**В§ 6332(e) discharge safe harbor** вҖ” compliant surrender DISCHARGES third party from any obligation or liability to delinquent taxpayer arising from surrender or payment. Pinned by `discharge_only_when_surrendered_invariant`, `discharge_engages_only_for_general_compliance_invariant`, and `note_pins_discharge_safe_harbor`.

**Property type 21-day hold truth table** вҖ” 4-cell sweep: only BankDeposit engages 21-day hold; WageOrSalary + GeneralProperty + LifeInsurance do NOT. Pinned by `property_type_truth_table_21_day_hold`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6332`. Twenty-six tests pin: **general property surrendered compliant**; **general property not surrendered triggers personal liability**; **failure without reasonable cause triggers 50% penalty**; **personal liability capped at tax liability**; **personal liability capped at property value**; **bank deposit within 21-day hold not required to surrender**; **bank deposit at 21-day boundary required to surrender**; **bank deposit at 20 days within hold**; **bank deposit after 21-day hold and not surrendered triggers liability**; **wage salary no 21-day hold**; **life insurance no 21-day hold**; **discharge only when surrendered invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6332(a)-(e) + В§ 301.6332-1 + IRM 5.17.3 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6621 + В§ 7426); **note pins 21-day hold**; **note pins 50% penalty**; **note pins discharge safe harbor**; **property type truth table 21-day hold** (4-cell sweep); **fifty percent penalty truth table** (4-cell sweep); **defensive negative property value clamped**; **defensive negative tax liability clamped**; **defensive overflow 50% saturating** (i64::MAX); **bank uniquely engages 21-day hold invariant**; **21-day boundary precision invariant** (20 within / 21 required); **total exposure combines liability and penalty**; **discharge engages only for general compliance invariant**; **within 21-day hold no failure reasons for bank**.

`traderview-expense::section_6334` is the **IRC В§ 6334 property exempt from levy** module вҖ” defines the 13 enumerated categories of property exempt from IRS administrative levy plus the judicial-approval gate for principal residence (В§ 6334(e)(1)) and the administrative-approval gate for self-employed assets + non-rental residence (В§ 6334(e)(2)). Trader-relevant for trader-landlords needing to identify which assets the IRS can and cannot reach when collection actions begin: tools-of-trade (trading rigs / monitors / books) + fuel + household provisions + wages + principal residence + retirement income + workers' comp + Job Training assistance. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + В§ 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS), `section_7811` (TAOs), and `section_6330` (CDP).

**Thirteen В§ 6334(a) exemption categories** вҖ” two with dollar caps, eleven without:

| Category | Section | 2026 cap |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Wearing apparel + school books | В§ 6334(a)(1) | No cap |
| Fuel + provisions + furniture + household + arms + livestock + poultry | В§ 6334(a)(2) | **$11,980** |
| Books + tools of trade / business / profession | В§ 6334(a)(3) | **$5,990** |
| Unemployment benefits | В§ 6334(a)(4) | No cap |
| Undelivered mail | В§ 6334(a)(5) | No cap |
| Certain annuity + pension payments (Railroad / Civil Service) | В§ 6334(a)(6) | No cap |
| Workmen's compensation | В§ 6334(a)(7) | No cap |
| Judgments for support of minor children | В§ 6334(a)(8) | No cap |
| Minimum wage/salary exemption | В§ 6334(a)(9) | В§ 6334(d) computation; 2026 В§ 6334(d)(4)(B) parameter = **$5,300** |
| Service-connected military disability | В§ 6334(a)(10) | No cap |
| Public assistance (TANF + state welfare) | В§ 6334(a)(11) | No cap |
| Job Training Partnership Act assistance | В§ 6334(a)(12) | No cap |
| Residence in small-deficiency cases (unpaid tax вүӨ $5,000) | В§ 6334(a)(13) | Unpaid tax cap |

Pinned by `property_category_truth_table_capped_vs_uncapped` (16-category sweep: 2 capped, 12 uncapped, 2 approval-gated), `fuel_provisions_within_cap_full_exemption`, `fuel_provisions_at_cap_boundary` ($11,980 exact), `fuel_provisions_over_cap_engaged`, `tools_of_trade_within_cap`, `tools_of_trade_at_cap_boundary` ($5,990 exact), `tools_of_trade_over_cap_engaged`, `fuel_at_1_cent_under_cap_compliant`, `fuel_at_1_cent_over_cap_engaged`, and `fuel_provisions_uniquely_higher_cap_than_tools_invariant` ($11,980 > $5,990).

**В§ 6334(e)(1) principal residence judicial-approval gate** вҖ” principal residence within meaning of В§ 121 is exempt from levy except where district court judge or magistrate approves the levy **IN WRITING**. District courts have **EXCLUSIVE jurisdiction**. Pinned by `principal_residence_no_judicial_approval_exempt`, `principal_residence_with_judicial_approval_levied`, `principal_residence_uniquely_requires_district_court_invariant`, and `note_pins_principal_residence_judicial_gate`.

**В§ 6334(e)(2) self-employed assets + non-rental residential real property administrative-approval gate** вҖ” IRS area director approval required before levy. Pinned by `self_employed_no_area_director_approval_exempt`, `self_employed_with_area_director_approval_levied`, `note_pins_self_employed_area_director_gate`, and `approval_satisfied_truth_table_for_residence_gates` (4-cell sweep: 2 categories Г— approved/not-approved).

**В§ 6334(a)(13) small-deficiency residence exemption** вҖ” applies only when unpaid tax вүӨ $5,000 (500,000 cents). $5,000 exactly = exempt; $5,000.01 = levied. Pinned by `small_deficiency_residence_at_5000_boundary_exempt`, `small_deficiency_residence_over_5000_levied`, and `small_deficiency_5000_uniquely_caps_unpaid_tax_invariant`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative property value + negative unpaid tax clamped to 0 via `.max(0)`. Pinned by `defensive_negative_property_value_clamped` and `defensive_negative_unpaid_tax_clamped`.

**Rev. Proc. 2025-32 + Pub. L. 119-21 (OBBBA) вҖ” 2026 inflation-adjusted amounts** verified via WebSearch: В§ 6334(a)(2) = $11,980; В§ 6334(a)(3) = $5,990; В§ 6334(d)(4)(B) = $5,300. Pinned by `note_pins_2026_inflation_amounts` ($11,980 + $5,990 + 2026) and `note_pins_2026_wage_5300_parameter` ($5,300 + В§ 6334(d)(4)(B)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6334`. Thirty-seven tests pin: **wearing apparel exempt no cap**; **fuel provisions within cap full exemption**; **fuel provisions at cap boundary** ($11,980); **fuel provisions over cap engaged**; **tools of trade within cap**; **tools of trade at cap boundary** ($5,990); **tools of trade over cap engaged**; **principal residence no judicial approval exempt**; **principal residence with judicial approval levied** (В§ 6334(e)(1)); **self-employed no area director approval exempt**; **self-employed with area director approval levied** (В§ 6334(e)(2)); **small deficiency residence at $5,000 boundary exempt**; **small deficiency residence over $5,000 levied**; **unemployment benefits exempt**; **workmen's compensation exempt**; **child support exempt**; **wage minimum exemption engaged**; **military disability exempt**; **public assistance exempt**; **Job Training Act exempt**; **annuity or pension exempt**; **undelivered mail exempt**; **not exempt property levied**; **citation pins subsections and Rev. Proc.**; **note pins 2026 inflation amounts**; **note pins principal residence judicial gate**; **note pins self-employed area director gate**; **note pins 2026 wage $5,300 parameter**; **defensive negative property value clamped**; **defensive negative unpaid tax clamped**; **fuel provisions uniquely higher cap than tools invariant** ($11,980 > $5,990); **principal residence uniquely requires district court invariant**; **small deficiency $5,000 uniquely caps unpaid tax invariant**; **property category truth table capped vs uncapped** (16-category sweep); **approval satisfied truth table for residence gates** (4-cell); **fuel at 1Вў under cap compliant**; **fuel at 1Вў over cap engaged**.

`traderview-expense::section_6402` is the **IRC В§ 6402 refund offsets / Treasury Offset Program (TOP)** module вҖ” trader-relevant when a refund is anticipated but multiple federal/state agencies claim offset rights against the overpayment. The statutory hierarchy controls which debt gets paid first. Distinct from `section_6611` (interest on overpayments) and `section_6511` (refund claim limitations) which precede the offset analysis.

**Seven-priority offset hierarchy**:

| Priority | Subsection | Debt category | Administered by |
|----------|------------|----------------|-------------------|
| 1 | В§ 6402(a) | Outstanding IRS internal revenue tax liability | IRS direct (not routed through TOP) |
| 2 | В§ 6402(c)(1) | Past-due support ASSIGNED to a State (Soc. Sec. Act В§В§ 402(a)(26), 471(a)(17)) | BFS / TOP |
| 3 | В§ 6402(d) | Past-due, legally enforceable federal agency non-tax debt (student loans, agency overpayments) | BFS / TOP |
| 4 | В§ 6402(c)(2) | Past-due support NOT assigned to a State | BFS / TOP |
| 5 | В§ 6402(e) | Past-due, legally enforceable state income tax | BFS / TOP |
| 6 | В§ 6402(f) | State unemployment compensation debt | BFS / TOP |
| 7 | В§ 6402(g) | State TANF / past assistance | BFS / TOP |

**В§ 6402(n) injured spouse rule (Form 8379)** protects the non-debtor spouse's share of a joint refund from offset for the other spouse's past-due debt. The non-debtor's share is supplied as basis points (5000 = 50%) and applied as a top-line carve-out BEFORE any offset hierarchy runs. Pinned by `injured_spouse_form_8379_protects_share_from_offset` (50% protection on $10K refund + $8K IRS debt вҶ’ $5K offset + $5K protected), `injured_spouse_share_100_bps_protects_all` (100% share protects entire refund), `injured_spouse_share_capped_at_10000_bps` (defensive cap at 100%), `no_injured_spouse_filing_no_protection`, `injured_spouse_protection_persists_against_all_offset_categories` (5-category sweep confirms protection survives).

**Strict priority order вҖ” debts consumed in В§ 6402 statutory sequence.** When the refund is exhausted before all debts are paid, offsets consume in priority order and lower-priority debts go unsatisfied. Pinned by `full_hierarchy_seven_levels_priority_order_preserved` (7-debt sweep with full statute mapping), `offsets_consume_in_strict_priority_order_when_refund_partially_exhausted` ($3K refund + $2K IRS + $2K child support вҶ’ $2K В§ 6402(a) + $1K В§ 6402(c)(1); federal agency debt unsatisfied), and individual priority tests for each of the 7 levels.

**Treasury Offset Program centralized administration since 1999.** Bureau of the Fiscal Service (BFS, formerly Financial Management Service / FMS) handles all В§ 6402(c)/(d)/(e)/(f)/(g) offsets. IRS retains direct handling of В§ 6402(a) tax-liability offsets. Pinned by `note_describes_top_administration`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6402`. Twenty-six tests pin: **no debts full refund to taxpayer**; **IRS tax liability priority 1** (В§ 6402(a)); **child support state assigned priority 2** (В§ 6402(c)(1)); **federal agency debt priority 3** (В§ 6402(d)); **state income tax priority 5 after federal debts**; **full hierarchy seven levels priority order preserved**; **offset exhausts refund partial satisfaction** ($1K refund + $5K IRS debt вҶ’ $1K offset); **debts exceeding refund only consume available amount**; **injured spouse Form 8379 protects share from offset**; **injured spouse share 100 bps protects all**; **injured spouse share capped at 10000 bps** (defensive); **no injured spouse filing no protection**; **zero overpayment no offsets applied**; **child support non-assigned priority 4 after federal agency**; **state unemployment priority 6 after state income tax**; **state TANF priority 7 lowest**; **citation pins all subsections + 26 CFR В§ 301.6402-6 + 31 CFR В§ 285.8 + Soc. Sec. Act В§В§ 402(a)(26)/471(a)(17) + BFS TOP**; **negative debts clamped to zero** (defensive); **note describes TOP administration** (BFS); **note describes offset count**; **injured spouse protection persists against all offset categories** (5-category sweep); **injured spouse protected amount added to remaining refund**; **offsets consume in strict priority order when refund partially exhausted**; **negative overpayment clamped to zero** (defensive); **all seven categories skip when empty**; **child support non-assigned falls between federal agency and state tax** (priority 4 between 3 and 5).

`traderview-expense::section_6404` is the **IRC В§ 6404 abatement of interest, tax, additions to tax, and penalties** module вҖ” trader-tax cleanup procedure after audit cycle or IRS administrative error. Distinct from siblings `section_6664` (reasonable cause defense to penalties), `section_6601` (interest accrual on underpayments), and `section_6611` (interest on overpayments).

**Six abatement pathways**:

| Pathway | В§ Citation | Test | Limit |
|---------|------------|------|--------|
| General abatement | В§ 6404(a) | Excessive / post-SOL / erroneous assessment | Discretionary; no taxpayer right |
| Small tax balance | В§ 6404(c) | Balance вүӨ $5 | $5 cap |
| Unreasonable error/delay | В§ 6404(e)(1) | Ministerial OR managerial act + post-written-contact + no taxpayer contribution | Interest only |
| Erroneous refund check | В§ 6404(e)(2) | Taxpayer did not cause | $50,000 cap |
| Erroneous written advice | В§ 6404(f) | 3-element test (written request + accurate facts + reasonable reliance) | Any penalty |
| 36-month interest suspension | В§ 6404(g) | Timely return + IRS notification > 36 months | Individuals only |

**В§ 6404(b) вҖ” No statutory right for interest / additions to tax / additional amounts / assessable penalties**. Taxpayer must rely on discretionary IRS authority OR specific В§ 6404(e)/(f)/(g) pathways. В§ 6404(h) вҖ” Tax Court may review IRS refusal to abate interest under В§ 6404(e) for abuse of discretion within 180 days of final determination.

**В§ 6404(e)(1) вҖ” Unreasonable error/delay (most important pathway)**. IRS may abate interest on underpayment attributable to MINISTERIAL or MANAGERIAL act of IRS employee. Three requirements:

1. Error/delay occurred AFTER IRS contacted taxpayer in writing about examination, underpayment, or payment
2. Taxpayer (or representative) did NOT significantly contribute to error or delay
3. Act was MINISTERIAL (procedural/mechanical, no judgment) OR MANAGERIAL (administrative, loss of records or personnel discretion) вҖ” **decisions concerning proper application of federal tax law are NEITHER ministerial nor managerial** and NOT abatable

Pinned by `e_1_clean_path_authorized`, `e_1_no_written_irs_contact_bars`, `e_1_taxpayer_contribution_bars`, `e_1_legal_judgment_act_bars`, `e_1_authorized_note_describes_ministerial_managerial`.

**В§ 6404(f) вҖ” Erroneous written advice three-element test**. ALL THREE elements required (1) advice requested in writing, (2) accurate + adequate facts provided by taxpayer, (3) reasonable reliance. Missing any element defeats abatement. Pinned by `f_three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep across the three elements).

**В§ 6404(g) вҖ” 36-month interest suspension**. For timely-filed individual returns, interest SUSPENDS when IRS fails to notify taxpayer of additional liability within 36 months (1,096 days) of filing. Suspension runs from 36-month mark to 21 days before IRS notification. Pinned by `g_boundary_1095_days_no_suspension` and `g_boundary_1096_days_engages_suspension`.

**Claim mechanics**: File Form 843 within В§ 6511 lookback window (3 years from original return filing OR 2 years from payment of tax, whichever is later). Outside lookback bars abatement regardless of substantive merit. Pinned by `outside_lookback_window_bars_all_pathways` (6-pathway sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6404`. Thirty-three tests pin: **В§ 6404(e)(1) clean path authorized**; **В§ 6404(e)(1) no written IRS contact bars**; **В§ 6404(e)(1) taxpayer contribution bars**; **В§ 6404(e)(1) legal-judgment act bars** (Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6404-2 NEITHER ministerial NOR managerial); **В§ 6404(e)(1) authorized note describes ministerial + managerial**; **outside lookback window bars all pathways** (6-pathway В§ 6511 sweep); **В§ 6404(e)(2) clean refund authorized**; **В§ 6404(e)(2) taxpayer caused refund bars**; **В§ 6404(e)(2) refund above $50K cap note**; **В§ 6404(f) three-element test satisfied authorized**; **В§ 6404(f) missing written request bars**; **В§ 6404(f) missing accurate facts bars**; **В§ 6404(f) missing reasonable reliance bars**; **В§ 6404(f) three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep w Г— f Г— r); **В§ 6404(g) 36-month suspension engages above window**; **В§ 6404(g) 36-month suspension not engaged within window**; **В§ 6404(g) not timely filed bars suspension**; **В§ 6404(g) boundary 1095 days no suspension**; **В§ 6404(g) boundary 1096 days engages suspension**; **В§ 6404(c) $5 balance authorized**; **В§ 6404(c) above $5 balance not authorized**; **В§ 6404(c) zero balance not authorized**; **В§ 6404(a) general abatement authority authorized** (DISCRETIONARY); **Form 843 lookback note always present** (В§ 6511); **В§ 6404(b) no claim right note present**; **В§ 6404(h) Tax Court review note** (180 days for abuse of discretion); **citation В§ 6404(e)(1) pins subsections and Treas. Reg.** (Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6404-2 + IRM 20.2.7); **citation В§ 6404(e)(2) pins subsection (e)(2)**; **citation В§ 6404(f) pins subsection (f)**; **citation В§ 6404(g) pins subsection (g)**; **citation В§ 6404(c) pins subsection (c)**; **citation В§ 6404(a) pins subsection (a)**; **six pathways routed correctly** (UnreasonableErrorOrDelay / ErroneousRefundCheck / ErroneousWrittenAdvice / ThirtySixMonthInterestSuspension / SmallTaxBalance / GeneralAbatement sweep).

`traderview-expense::section_7502` is the **IRC В§ 7502 timely mailing treated as timely filing and paying ("mailbox rule")** module вҖ” critical procedural rule for ALL tax filings (returns, claims for refund, Tax Court petitions, deficiency responses). The date a filing is MAILED (not received) can make the difference between timely and barred. Paired with В§ 6213(a) Hallmark Research Collective jurisdictional deadline and В§ 6330(d)(1) Boechler equitable tolling, В§ 7502 is often the difference between Tax Court jurisdiction and complete loss.

**Eight delivery methods вҶ’ three evidentiary statuses**:

| Method | В§ 7502 protection | Evidentiary status |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| USPS Registered Mail with proof | YES (В§ 7502(c)(1)) | PrimaFacieTimely |
| USPS Certified Mail with receipt | YES (В§ 7502(c)(2)) | PrimaFacieTimely |
| USPS Regular first-class | YES (В§ 7502(a)) | OrdinaryTimely (Anderson v. United States caveat) |
| FedEx designated (First/Priority/Standard Overnight, 2 Day, Intl Priority/First/Economy) | YES (В§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| UPS designated (Next Day Air variants, 2nd Day Air variants, Worldwide Express) | YES (В§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| DHL Express designated (Express 9:00/10:30/12:00, Worldwide, Envelope, Import Express) | YES (В§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| Non-designated PDS (FedEx Ground, UPS Ground, FedEx Home Delivery) | NO | NotProtected |
| Electronic / e-file | governed by 26 CFR В§ 301.7502-1(d) acknowledgment timestamp | OrdinaryTimely |

**Two В§ 7502(a)(2) gatekeepers** must be satisfied before any method protection applies:

| Gatekeeper | Effect |
|------------|--------|
| Postmark within prescribed period | Required for any В§ 7502 protection |
| Properly addressed with sufficient postage | Required for any В§ 7502 protection |

**Certified and registered mail UNIQUELY get prima facie status.** В§ 7502(c) prima facie evidence shifts the burden of proof вҖ” the IRS must rebut the registered/certified mail proof, not the taxpayer. Other methods leave the burden on the taxpayer to prove postmark date. Pinned by `certified_and_registered_uniquely_get_prima_facie_status_invariant` (2-vs-4 method sweep), `usps_certified_with_receipt_prima_facie`, `usps_registered_with_proof_prima_facie`, `usps_certified_without_receipt_falls_back_to_ordinary` (proof retention is the load-bearing fact), `registered_mail_without_proof_falls_back_to_ordinary`.

**Anderson v. United States, 966 F.2d 487 (9th Cir. 1992) displaces common-law mailbox rule** in most circuits. Ordinary first-class USPS mail (no certified/registered tracking) provides no presumption of timely filing вҖ” the taxpayer must prove postmark date. The Anderson note surfaces ONLY for `UspsRegular`. Pinned by `usps_regular_first_class_ordinary_timely_with_anderson_caveat`, `anderson_caveat_appears_only_for_usps_regular` (3-method sweep confirms Certified/Registered/FedEx do NOT trigger the caveat).

**Notice 2016-30 enumerates designated PDSs.** Only specifically enumerated services qualify under В§ 7502(f). FedEx Ground, UPS Ground, FedEx Home Delivery, and similar non-listed services DO NOT qualify regardless of how reliable the delivery. Pinned by `non_designated_pds_does_not_qualify`, `non_designated_pds_uniquely_loses_protection_invariant` (7-method sweep: 6 methods protect, NonDesignated does not), `three_pds_invariant_all_qualify_when_postmark_within_period` (all 3 designated PDS providers qualify).

**Postmark-outside-period and improper-addressing gates dominate method protection.** Even the best delivery method (Registered Mail with proof) cannot save a filing that was mailed AFTER the deadline. Pinned by `postmark_outside_period_overrides_method_protection` (2-method sweep), `improperly_addressed_overrides_method_protection`.

**Electronic filings governed separately.** 26 CFR В§ 301.7502-1(d) вҖ” e-filed returns deemed filed on the date of electronic acknowledgment; В§ 7502 paper-mailing framework does not apply. Pinned by `electronic_filing_acknowledgment_within_period_timely`, `electronic_filing_acknowledgment_outside_period_untimely`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7502`. Twenty-five tests pin: **USPS Certified with receipt prima facie** (В§ 7502(c)(2)); **USPS Certified without receipt falls back to ordinary**; **USPS Registered with proof prima facie** (В§ 7502(c)(1)); **USPS Regular first-class ordinary timely with Anderson caveat** (9th Cir. 1992); **FedEx designated qualifies** (Notice 2016-30); **UPS designated qualifies**; **DHL Express designated qualifies**; **non-designated PDS does not qualify** (FedEx Ground / UPS Ground / FedEx Home Delivery); **postmark outside period not protected**; **improperly addressed envelope not protected**; **filing arrived before deadline no В§ 7502 needed**; **electronic filing acknowledgment within period timely**; **electronic filing acknowledgment outside period untimely**; **citation pins all subsections + 26 CFR В§ 301.7502-1 + Notice 2016-30 + Anderson v. United States**; **registered mail without proof falls back to ordinary**; **FedEx designated does not get prima facie status**; **UPS designated does not get prima facie status**; **three PDS invariant all qualify when postmark within period**; **non-designated PDS uniquely loses protection invariant** (7-method sweep: 6 protect, 1 not); **certified and registered uniquely get prima facie status invariant** (2-vs-4 method sweep); **postmark outside period overrides method protection** (2-method sweep); **improperly addressed overrides method protection**; **Anderson caveat appears only for USPS Regular** (3-method sweep); **before deadline path dominates method check** (2-method sweep); **FedEx note lists designated services** (Priority Overnight + International Priority/First/Economy).

`traderview-expense::section_7503` is the **IRC В§ 7503 Time for performance of acts where last day falls on Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday** module вҖ” the weekend/holiday extension rule that stacks with `section_7502` timely-mailing rule + `section_6213(a)` Tax Court petition window + `section_6511` refund claim limitations. Trader-broadly-applicable timing rule because every tax deadline (return filing, payment, SNOD petition, refund claim, election statement) is subject to В§ 7503's automatic next-business-day extension when the deadline falls on a non-business day. Companion to `section_7502` (timely-mailing rule), `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline), `section_6511` (refund limitations), `section_6072` (return due dates), В§ 6151 (payment due dates).

**В§ 7503 General rule** вҖ” when the last day prescribed under authority of the internal revenue laws for performing any act falls on **Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday**, the performance of such act shall be considered timely if it is performed on the **next succeeding day which is not a Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday**.

**Authorized extensions included** вҖ” any authorized extension of time shall be included in determining the last day for performance of any act.

**Legal holiday defined** вҖ” two clauses: (1) **legal holiday in the District of Columbia** (5 USC В§ 6103 federal holidays: New Year's Day + MLK Day + Washington's Birthday + Memorial Day + **Juneteenth (since 2021)** + Independence Day + Labor Day + Columbus Day + Veterans Day + Thanksgiving + Christmas Day); AND (2) for acts at offices outside DC but within an internal revenue district, the term also means a **statewide legal holiday in the State where such office is located**.

**DC-only holidays** вҖ” **Emancipation Day (April 16)** + Inauguration Day (January 20 every 4 years). DC Emancipation Day (Rev. Rul. 2015-13) regularly extends federal tax filing deadline by 1 business day when April 15 falls on weekend or April 16 itself on weekend вҖ” this is why federal income tax filing deadline is often April 17 or 18.

**Scope of application** вҖ” В§ 7503 applies to acts by **TAXPAYER** (return filing + payment + В§ 6213(a) Tax Court petition + В§ 6511 refund claim + elections) AND acts by **COMMISSIONER** (В§ 6212 SNOD issuance + В§ 6303 notice and demand + В§ 6851 termination assessment notice).

**В§ 7503 stacks with В§ 7502** вҖ” postmark on or before extended deadline counts as timely filing per В§ 7502(a) + В§ 7503 extension.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7503`. Twenty-three tests pin: **business day deadline no extension**; **Saturday deadline engages extension**; **Sunday deadline engages extension**; **federal legal holiday engages extension**; **DC-only holiday engages extension**; **state holiday at outside DC office engages extension**; **state holiday at DC office does NOT engage extension** (В§ 7503 state-holiday clause limited to non-DC offices); **extension engaged but not performed on next business day fails** (В§ 7503 only saves performance on the next non-weekend/non-holiday day); **authorized extension recognized**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 7503 + 26 CFR В§ 301.7503-1 + Rev. Rul. 2015-13 + 5 USC В§ 6103 + DC Code В§ 28-2701 + В§ 7502 + В§ 6213(a) + В§ 6511 + В§ 6072); **note pins general rule weekend/holiday extension** (next succeeding day); **note pins authorized extension inclusion**; **note pins legal holiday definition two clauses** (DC + statewide); **note pins scope taxpayer and Commissioner acts** (В§ 6213 + В§ 6511 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6851); **note pins Rev. Rul. 2015-13 Emancipation Day** (April 16 + April 15); **note pins 5 USC В§ 6103 federal holiday list** (Juneteenth 2021 + Thanksgiving + MLK Day); **note pins DC-only holidays** (Emancipation Day + Inauguration Day); **note pins В§ 7502 + В§ 7503 stacking** (timely-mailing rule interaction); **note pins cross-references collection constellation** (В§ 6213(a) + В§ 6511 + В§ 6072 + В§ 6151 + В§ 6212); **day type truth table DC office** (6-cell sweep вҖ” business day + Sat + Sun + federal holiday + DC-only holiday + state holiday); **day type truth table outside DC office** (6-cell sweep вҖ” state holiday now engages); **state holiday only engages outside DC invariant**; **federal holiday engages regardless of office location invariant** (2-scope sweep).

`traderview-expense::section_7508` is the **IRC В§ 7508 time for performing certain acts postponed by reason of service in combat zone or contingency operation** module вҖ” trader-relevant for active-duty military traders and military spouses serving in designated combat zones (Executive Order designated), Secretary of Defense designated contingency operations outside the United States, or qualified hazardous duty areas (e.g., Sinai Peninsula). Procedural-companion to `section_7508a` (presidentially-declared disaster postponement вҖ” DIFFERENT qualifying event), `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act), `section_7422` (refund suit), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6212` / `section_6213` (deficiency procedures), and `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations).

**В§ 7508(a) postponement formula**:

Total postponement = days of qualifying service + days of qualifying hospitalization + **180 days** bonus.

IRS DISREGARDS time during:
1. В§ 7508(a)(1)(A) вҖ” combat zone service (Executive Order designated)
2. В§ 7508(a)(1)(B) вҖ” Secretary of Defense designated contingency operation outside US
3. В§ 7508(c) вҖ” qualified hazardous duty area (Sinai Peninsula + other SoD designated)

Pinned by `combat_zone_365_days_plus_180` (365 + 180 = 545 days), `contingency_operation_postponement`, `qualified_hazardous_duty_area_postponement`, `not_applicable_no_postponement` (no qualifying service вҶ’ no postponement), `zero_service_days_only_180_day_bonus` (no service days вҶ’ just 180-day bonus), `bonus_180_days_always_180`, `service_type_truth_table` (4-cell sweep), and `note_pins_three_qualifying_categories`.

**В§ 7508(a) hospitalization 5-year cap (1825 days)** вҖ” hospitalization INSIDE the United States is treated as qualified hospitalization only to the extent the period does not exceed 5 years. Hospitalization OUTSIDE the United States is NOT capped. Pinned by `hospitalization_outside_us_not_capped` (3000 days outside вҶ’ not capped), `hospitalization_inside_us_capped_at_5_years` (3000 days inside вҶ’ capped at 1825), `hospitalization_inside_us_at_1825_day_boundary_not_capped` (exactly 1825 = compliant), `hospitalization_inside_us_at_1826_day_capped` (1825 + 1 = capped), `hospitalization_zero_no_capping`, `small_hospitalization_inside_us_not_capped`, `hospitalization_cap_only_inside_us_invariant`, and `note_pins_hospitalization_5_year_cap`.

**В§ 7508(b) military spouse extension** вҖ” same 180-day benefit applies to spouse of qualifying service member. Pinned by `military_spouse_eligible`, `military_spouse_truth_table` (2-cell sweep: ServiceMember + MilitarySpouse both qualify), and `note_pins_military_spouse_extension`.

**В§ 7508(c) qualified hazardous duty area** вҖ” includes Sinai Peninsula of Egypt (if member entitled to special pay) plus other Secretary-of-Defense designated areas. Pinned by `note_pins_sinai_peninsula_qhda`.

**Postponed acts (per 26 CFR В§ 301.7508-1)** вҖ” filing returns + paying tax + filing amended returns + Tax Court petitions + В§ 6511 refund claims + В§ 6212 SNOD responses + В§ 6213 deficiency challenges. Pinned by `note_pins_postponed_acts`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7508`. Twenty-three tests pin: **combat zone 365 days plus 180** (545 day total); **contingency operation postponement**; **qualified hazardous duty area postponement**; **not applicable no postponement** (no qualifying service вҶ’ no postponement); **military spouse eligible**; **hospitalization outside US not capped** (3000 days OK); **hospitalization inside US capped at 5 years** (3000 вҶ’ 1825); **hospitalization inside US at 1825-day boundary not capped**; **hospitalization inside US at 1826-day capped**; **zero service days only 180-day bonus**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(3) + (b)(1)-(2) + (c)(1)-(2) + В§ 301.7508-1 + Notice 2003-21 + Form 15109 + Pub. 3; **note pins three qualifying categories**; **note pins hospitalization 5-year cap** (1825 days); **note pins military spouse extension**; **note pins Sinai Peninsula QHDA**; **note pins postponed acts** (В§ 6511 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213 + Form 15109); **service type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **hospitalization cap only inside US invariant**; **bonus 180 days always 180**; **defensive overflow saturating**; **military spouse truth table** (2-cell); **hospitalization zero no capping**; **small hospitalization inside US not capped**.

`traderview-expense::section_7508a` is the **IRC В§ 7508A presidentially-declared disaster + significant fire + terroristic or military action deadline postponement** module вҖ” trader-relevant for any trader located in a federally-declared disaster area (CA wildfires + FL hurricanes + TX flooding + tornado disasters) who needs to extend filing + payment + amended-return + Tax Court petition + В§ 6511 refund-claim + В§ 6212 SNOD-response deadlines. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + В§ 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6212`/`section_6213` (deficiency procedures), and `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations).

**Two-tier postponement framework**:

| Subsection | Type | Maximum | Engagement |
|------------|------|---------|------------|
| В§ 7508A(a) | Discretionary disaster / fire | **1 year (365 days)** | Secretary determines, taxpayer in disaster area, Stafford-Act eligible area |
| В§ 7508A(b) | Discretionary terroristic / military | 1 year (365 days) | Secretary determines |
| В§ 7508A(c) | Special rules for pensions | N/A | IRA contributions + retirement-plan loan repayments |
| В§ 7508A(d) | **MANDATORY 60-day floor** | **60-day minimum** | Federally declared disaster with specified incident date declared after **December 20, 2019** |

Pinned by `federally_declared_disaster_postponement_available`, `postponement_basis_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: disaster/fire/terroristic all engage; NoQualifyingEvent does not), `significant_fire_postponement_available`, `terroristic_or_military_action_postponement_available`, `no_qualifying_event_no_postponement`, `not_in_disaster_area_no_postponement` (Stafford Act gate), and `taxpayer_outside_disaster_area_with_qualifying_event_no_postponement`.

**В§ 7508A(d) mandatory 60-day floor вҖ” added by Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Tax Relief Act of 2019 (Pub. L. 116-94 Div. Q В§ 205)** вҖ” applies only to federally declared disasters (not terroristic actions) with specified incident date declared AFTER December 20, 2019. Runs CONCURRENTLY with Secretary's discretionary postponement under (a)+(b) if Secretary period вүҘ 60 days. If Secretary period < 60 days, 60-day floor controls. Pinned by `mandatory_60_day_floor_when_secretary_lt_60` (Secretary 30 days вҶ’ 60 days controls), `mandatory_60_day_runs_concurrently_when_secretary_gte_60` (Secretary 90 days вҶ’ 90 days controls), `mandatory_60_day_only_for_disaster_and_fire_invariant` (terroristic does NOT trigger 60-day floor), `mandatory_60_day_requires_post_dec_20_2019_invariant`, `pre_dec_20_2019_disaster_no_mandatory_60_day`, `pre_2019_amendment_no_mandatory_floor_when_secretary_lt_60`, `post_2019_amendment_60_day_floor_engages_when_secretary_zero`, `secretary_180_days_postponed_overrides_60_day_floor`, and `terroristic_action_does_not_engage_mandatory_60_day`.

**В§ 7508A one-year maximum cap** вҖ” postponement period capped at one year (365 days). Pinned by `one_year_cap_engaged_at_365` (exactly 365 compliant), `over_one_year_requested_violates_cap`, `secretary_postponement_capped_at_365`, `defensive_at_max_365_boundary`, `defensive_at_366_days_requested_violates`, and `max_postponement_always_365`.

**Disaster area defined by Stafford Act + В§ 1033(h)(3)** вҖ” В§ 1033(h)(3) cross-reference defines "disaster area" as area eligible for federal assistance under Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act, 42 USC В§ 5121 et seq. Taxpayer must be located in the disaster area to qualify. Pinned by `not_in_disaster_area_no_postponement` and `note_pins_stafford_act`.

**Postponed acts include** (per 26 CFR В§ 301.7508A-1) вҖ” filing returns + paying tax + filing amended returns + Tax Court petitions + В§ 6511 refund claims + В§ 6212 SNOD responses + В§ 6213 deficiency challenges. Pinned by `note_pins_regs_postponed_acts`.

**Defensive boundary tests** вҖ” 0 days Secretary postponed вҶ’ 60-day floor engages (post-2019 amendment); 366 days requested вҶ’ over one-year cap violation; max_postponement_days always 365. Pinned by `defensive_zero_days_secretary_postponed_engages_60_day_floor`, `defensive_at_max_365_boundary`, and `defensive_at_366_days_requested_violates`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7508a`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **federally declared disaster postponement available**; **no qualifying event no postponement**; **not in disaster area no postponement** (Stafford Act); **significant fire postponement available**; **terroristic or military action postponement available**; **mandatory 60-day floor when Secretary < 60**; **mandatory 60-day runs concurrently when Secretary вүҘ 60**; **pre-Dec 20 2019 disaster no mandatory 60-day**; **terroristic action does not engage mandatory 60-day**; **one-year cap engaged at 365**; **over one year requested violates cap**; **Secretary postponement capped at 365**; **citation pins all subsections** (a) + (b) + (c) + (d) + В§ 301.7508A-1 + Pub. L. 116-94 + 42 USC В§ 5121 + В§ 1033(h)(3); **note pins one year and 60-day** (December 20, 2019); **note pins Stafford Act** (В§ 1033(h)(3) + 42 USC В§ 5121); **note pins regs postponed acts** (В§ 6511 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213); **note pins Pub. L. 116-94 Div. Q В§ 205**; **postponement basis truth table** (4-cell sweep); **mandatory 60-day only for disaster and fire invariant** (3-cell: disaster + fire engage; terroristic does not); **mandatory 60-day requires post-Dec 20 2019 invariant**; **defensive zero days Secretary postponed engages 60-day floor**; **defensive at max 365 boundary**; **defensive at 366 days requested violates**; **pre-2019 amendment no mandatory floor when Secretary < 60**; **post-2019 amendment 60-day floor engages when Secretary zero**; **Secretary 180 days postponed overrides 60-day floor**; **taxpayer outside disaster area with qualifying event no postponement**; **three failure modes stack when no event no area over cap**; **max postponement always 365**.

`traderview-expense::section_7521` is the **IRC В§ 7521 procedures involving taxpayer interviews** module вҖ” trader-critical procedural rights at IRS audit, collection, and examination interviews. Three statutory pathways: В§ 7521(a) recording rights (taxpayer + IRS), В§ 7521(b) explanation-of-rights requirement, В§ 7521(c) representation + interview-suspension right. Companion to `landlord_tenant_recording_consent` (federal Wiretap Act + state consent for non-IRS recordings), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), `section_6330` (CDP for levies), and `section_6320` (CDP for liens).

**Four interview-type classifications**:

| Type | В§ 7521(a)(1) recording right | В§ 7521(b) explanation | В§ 7521(c) suspension right |
|------|----------------------------|------------------------|------------------------------|
| TaxDetermination | YES (advance request) | YES вҖ” В§ 7521(b)(1)(A) audit process | YES |
| Collection | YES (advance request) | YES вҖ” В§ 7521(b)(1)(B) collection process | YES |
| AdministrativeSummons | NO | NO | NO (В§ 7602 summons exempt) |
| CriminalInvestigation | NO | NO | Jencks Act framework |

**В§ 7521(a)(1) taxpayer recording right**: advance request + taxpayer's own equipment + taxpayer's own expense. Pinned by `tax_determination_taxpayer_recording_right_engaged_with_advance_request`, `collection_interview_taxpayer_recording_right_engaged`, `taxpayer_recording_not_engaged_without_advance_request`, `administrative_summons_no_recording_right`, `tax_determination_unique_recording_right_invariant` (4-type sweep), `taxpayer_recording_request_outside_determination_or_collection_no_right`.

**В§ 7521(a)(2) IRS recording requires (i) advance notice AND (ii) reimbursable transcript on taxpayer request.** Pinned by `irs_recording_complies_with_advance_notice`, `irs_recording_fails_without_advance_notice`, `irs_recording_compliance_independent_of_taxpayer_recording_request`.

**В§ 7521(b)(1) explanation of process and rights** вҖ” В§ 7521(b)(1)(A) audit process for tax determination + В§ 7521(b)(1)(B) collection process for collection. Failure may render subsequent statements suppressible. Pinned by `explanation_of_rights_satisfied_for_tax_determination` (В§ 7521(b)(1)(A)), `explanation_of_rights_satisfied_for_collection` (В§ 7521(b)(1)(B)), `explanation_of_rights_not_provided_violation_note` (suppressible warning), `explanation_of_rights_does_not_apply_to_administrative_summons`, `collection_explanation_subsection_b_referenced`, `tax_determination_explanation_subsection_a_referenced`.

**В§ 7521(c) representation + interview-suspension right** вҖ” taxpayer may represent themselves OR be represented by attorney / CPA / enrolled agent / enrolled actuary / authorized representative via Form 2848 power of attorney (Treas. Reg. В§ 601.502). If taxpayer requests representation consultation DURING interview, IRS must SUSPEND regardless of prior answers. Pinned by `representation_engaged_with_power_of_attorney`, `suspension_required_when_taxpayer_requests_representation`, `suspension_satisfied_when_irs_suspends`, `failure_to_suspend_violation_note_warns_about_suppression`, `poa_alone_engages_representation_right`, `representation_consultation_request_alone_engages_representation_right`.

**В§ 7521(c) administrative-summons exception bars suspension right.** Interview initiated by В§ 7602 administrative summons is exempt from the suspension rule. Pinned by `administrative_summons_bars_suspension_right`.

**В§ 7521(c) unreasonable-delay bypass with Immediate Supervisor consent.** If IRS officer determines representative is responsible for unreasonable delay and obtains supervisor approval, suspension right is overridden. Pinned by `unreasonable_delay_bypass_with_supervisor_consent_no_suspension`, `suspension_required_truth_table_4_cells` (4-cell sweep over taxpayer-request Г— interview-type Г— bypass-approved).

**Criminal investigation interview warning surfaces Jencks Act framework.** В§ 7521 procedural rights do not apply in same manner; consult criminal defense counsel. Pinned by `criminal_investigation_warning_note_engaged`.

**suspension required truth table 4 cells** (taxpayer-request Г— type Г— bypass); **POA alone engages representation right**; **representation consultation request alone engages representation right**; **collection explanation subsection (B) referenced**; **tax determination explanation subsection (A) referenced**; **failure to suspend violation note warns about suppression**; **no representation request no suspension required**; **taxpayer recording request outside determination or collection no right** (Summons + Criminal both barred); **IRS recording compliance independent of taxpayer recording request**; **full compliance path all rights satisfied** (all five flags green simultaneously).

`traderview-expense::section_7522` is the **IRC В§ 7522 Content of tax due, deficiency, and other notices** module вҖ” added by Taxpayer Bill of Rights of 1988 (TBOR 1, Pub. L. 100-647 В§ 6233) to require IRS notices to describe the BASIS for, and identify the AMOUNTS of, every component of liability. Trader-procedural-critical because В§ 7522 is the taxpayer's statutory entitlement to know what the IRS is asserting and why вҖ” covering В§ 6303 notice and demand (CP14), В§ 6212 SNOD (Letter 3171/5071C 90-day letter), CP2000 Automated Underreporter notices generated from 1099-B + 1099-K + K-1 matching, and the first 30-day Appeals-eligible proposed-deficiency letter (Letter 525). Companion to `section_6201` (assessment authority), `section_6203` (method of assessment), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_6303` (notice and demand), `section_7491` (burden of proof).

**В§ 7522(a) General rule** вҖ” any notice to which this section applies shall **describe the basis for, and identify the amounts (if any) of**, any (1) tax due, (2) interest, (3) additional amounts, (4) additions to the tax, and (5) assessable penalties included in such notice. **An inadequate description shall NOT INVALIDATE such notice** вҖ” statutory safe harbor; В§ 7522(a) is a notice-content DUTY but inadequate notice is curable rather than voidable.

**В§ 7522(b) Notices to which section applies** вҖ” three categories: (1) **В§ 7522(b)(1)** any tax due notice or deficiency notice described in **В§ 6155, В§ 6212, or В§ 6303** (CP14 notice and demand, Letter 1058 Final Notice of Intent to Levy, Letter 3171/5071C SNOD); (2) **В§ 7522(b)(2)** any notice generated out of any **information return matching program** (CP2000 Automated Underreporter вҖ” matches 1099-B broker reporting + 1099-K payment-card processor reporting + K-1 partnership reporting against tax return; response deadline 30 days from notice date / 60 days if taxpayer outside US); (3) **В§ 7522(b)(3)** the first letter of proposed deficiency which allows the taxpayer an opportunity for administrative review in the **IRS Independent Office of Appeals** (Letter 525 30-day letter; Taxpayer First Act of 2019 В§ 1001 redesignated as "Independent Office of Appeals").

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7522`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **fully compliant SNOD passes**; **В§ 6303 notice covered**; **В§ 6155 payment notice covered**; **CP2000 information matching covered**; **Letter 525 30-day letter covered**; **not covered notice section not engaged**; **missing basis description violation with safe harbor**; **missing tax amount violation**; **missing interest amount violation**; **missing additional amounts violation**; **missing additions to tax violation**; **missing assessable penalties violation**; **safe harbor preserves notice validity** (inadequate description does NOT invalidate); **no liability component no amount required**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 7522 + TBOR 1 + Pub. L. 100-647 В§ 6233 + Taxpayer First Act of 2019 В§ 1001 + 26 CFR В§ 301.7522-1 + IRM 4.19.3 CP2000 + IRM 4.10.7 Letter 525 + В§ 6155 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6303 + В§ 7491 + IRS Publication 1); **note pins general rule describe basis and amounts**; **note pins safe harbor** (NOT INVALIDATE + curable); **note pins В§ 7522(b)(1) covered notices** (В§ 6155 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6303 + CP14 + Letter 1058); **note pins В§ 7522(b)(2) CP2000 with third party info returns** (1099-B + 1099-K + K-1 + 30 days + 60 days outside US); **note pins В§ 7522(b)(3) Letter 525 with Independent Office of Appeals** (Taxpayer First Act of 2019 В§ 1001); **note pins TBOR 1 origin** (Pub. L. 100-647 В§ 6233 + IRS Publication 1); **note pins content disclosure layer over collection constellation** (В§ 6201 + В§ 6203 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6213(a)); **multiple amount failures stack** (3 violations); **notice type truth table 6 cells** (5 covered + 1 not covered); **all five amounts truth table when liability included**; **inadequate description always engages safe harbor invariant**; **fully compliant notice does not engage safe harbor invariant**; **not covered notice returns compliant without engaging section invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_7525` is the **IRC В§ 7525 federally authorized tax practitioner (FATP) confidentiality privilege** module вҖ” when do communications between a taxpayer and a CPA / EA / attorney / enrolled actuary / enrolled retirement plan agent receive the same common-law confidentiality protection that applies between taxpayer and attorney? Trader-relevant for protecting practitioner communications on M2M elections (В§ 475(f)), straddle (В§ 1092) advice, В§ 1256 60/40 reporting, В§ 1091 wash sale planning, qualified-trader-status disputes, and international (В§ 988, В§ 1297, В§ 6038D) advice. Companion to `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), and `section_7434` (civil-fraud victim damages).

**Six conjunctive elements required for privilege**:

| Element | Source | Failure mode |
|---------|--------|--------------|
| FATP qualification | В§ 7525(a)(2) + 31 USC В§ 330 | Non-FATP individual (bookkeeper, paralegal, financial planner without Circular 230 authorization) |
| Noncriminal context | В§ 7525(a)(3)(A) | Criminal tax matter categorically EXCLUDED (grand jury, indictment, IRS-CI referral) |
| Federal tax matter | В§ 7525(a)(3)(A) | State or local tax matter (federal-only privilege scope) |
| Tax advice (not return prep) | United States v. Frederick, 182 F.3d 496 (7th Cir. 1999) | Return preparation work (ministerial, not advice) |
| Not written shelter promotion | В§ 7525(b) + В§ 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii) | Written FATP communication promoting tax shelter participation |
| Would be privileged with attorney | В§ 7525(a)(1) gating clause | Communication would not satisfy attorney-client privilege test |

**В§ 7525(a)(1) extension clause** вҖ” same common-law protections that apply between taxpayer + attorney apply between taxpayer + FATP "to the extent the communication would be considered a privileged communication if it were between a taxpayer and an attorney." Pinned by `note_pins_attorney_client_extension` and `would_not_be_attorney_privileged_voids`.

**В§ 7525(a)(3)(A) noncriminal-only limitation** вҖ” privilege assertable ONLY in (i) noncriminal tax matter before IRS or (ii) noncriminal federal court tax proceeding brought by or against the United States. Criminal tax matters (grand jury, indictment, IRS-CI referral) categorically void privilege regardless of FATP qualification or communication type. Pinned by `criminal_tax_matter_categorically_voids_privilege` and `criminal_uniquely_categorical_invariant` (criminal voids `noncriminal_context`; state/local voids `federal_tax_matter` instead).

**В§ 7525(b) tax-shelter-promotion exception вҖ” WRITTEN ONLY**. The exception applies to written communications between FATP and a person in connection with the promotion of direct or indirect participation in any tax shelter as defined in В§ 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii). Oral tax-shelter advice is NOT swept in by В§ 7525(b); only written promotional communications. Pinned by `written_tax_shelter_promotion_excluded`, `shelter_exception_uniquely_written_only_invariant` (oral advice still privileged; written shelter promo not), and `written_tax_advice_privileged_distinguishes_from_shelter_promotion`.

**United States v. Frederick return-prep exclusion** вҖ” judicial limitation: В§ 7525 does NOT cover tax-return preparation work because it is ministerial compilation, not legal advice. Distinguishes from oral or written tax-planning advice. Pinned by `return_preparation_work_excluded`, `frederick_citation_pinned_for_return_prep_failure`, and `return_prep_distinguished_from_tax_advice_invariant`.

**FATP qualification under 31 USC В§ 330 (Circular 230)** вҖ” covers CPAs, enrolled agents, attorneys admitted to practice before IRS, enrolled actuaries, and enrolled retirement plan agents. Excludes non-FATP individuals (bookkeepers, paralegals, financial planners without В§ 330 authorization). Pinned by `fatp_practitioner_types_all_qualify` (5-type sweep), `non_fatp_individual_voids_privilege`, `enrolled_actuary_privileged`, and `enrolled_retirement_plan_agent_privileged`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7525`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CPA noncriminal oral advice privileged** (full compliance path); **EA noncriminal written advice privileged**; **attorney noncriminal federal court privileged**; **criminal tax matter categorically voids privilege** (В§ 7525(a)(3)(A)); **state or local tax voids privilege** (federal-only scope); **return preparation work excluded** (Frederick); **written tax shelter promotion excluded** (В§ 7525(b) + В§ 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)); **non-FATP individual voids privilege** (31 USC В§ 330); **would not be attorney privileged voids** (В§ 7525(a)(1) gating); **multiple disqualifiers stack** (4 failures simultaneous); **FATP practitioner types all qualify** (5-type sweep); **enrolled actuary privileged**; **enrolled retirement plan agent privileged**; **citation pins all relevant subsections** (a)(1) + (a)(2) + (a)(3)(A) + (b) + 31 USC В§ 330; **note pins attorney-client extension**; **note pins noncriminal-only limitation**; **note pins tax shelter promotion exception**; **proceeding context truth table** (4-cell sweep); **communication type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **criminal uniquely categorical invariant** (criminal voids noncriminal_context; state/local voids federal_tax_matter); **six conjunctive elements all required**; **shelter exception uniquely written only invariant** (oral advice still privileged; written shelter promo not); **written tax advice privileged distinguishes from shelter promotion**; **return prep distinguished from tax advice invariant**; **Frederick citation pinned for return prep failure**; **three failure reasons stack for state local non-FATP return prep**.

`traderview-expense::section_7421` is the **IRC В§ 7421 Anti-Injunction Act (AIA)** module вҖ” when may a trader-taxpayer obtain a TRO or preliminary injunction against an IRS assessment / collection / levy? Default answer = **NEVER**. В§ 7421(a) general bar: "no suit for the purpose of restraining the assessment or collection of any tax shall be maintained in any court by any person." Three exception pathways (11 statutory + Enochs judicial 2-prong + CIC Services regulatory pre-enforcement). Trader-procedural-companion to `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7525` (FATP privilege), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), and `section_7430` (litigation costs).

**Three exception pathways**:

| Pathway | Source | Trigger |
|---------|--------|---------|
| Statutory exception | В§ 7421(a) enumerated list | One of 11 sections engages |
| Enochs judicial | Enochs v. Williams Packing, 370 U.S. 1 (1962) | Both prongs satisfied conjunctively |
| CIC Services carve-out | CIC Services v. IRS, 593 U.S. 209 (2021) | Pre-enforcement challenge to reporting requirement / regulation |

**В§ 7421(a) eleven statutory exceptions** вҖ” verbatim cross-references: В§В§ 6015(e) (innocent spouse) + 6212(a) + (c) (SNOD) + 6213(a) (pre-assessment Tax Court restraint) + 6232(c) (partnership-level adjustment) + 6330(e)(1) (CDP collection action suspension) + 6331(i) (uniformed-services bond levy) + 6672(c) (TFRP CDP) + 6694(c) (preparer-penalty CDP) + 7426(a) + (b)(1) (third-party wrongful levy) + 7429(b) (jeopardy assessment review) + 7436 (employment-status determination review). Pinned by `statutory_exception_truth_table` (11-exception sweep), `section_6213a_exception_permits_suit`, `section_6330e1_cdp_exception_permits_suit`, `section_7426_wrongful_levy_exception_permits_suit`, `section_7429b_jeopardy_assessment_permits_suit`, `section_6015e_innocent_spouse_permits_suit`, `section_6232c_partnership_adjustment_permits_suit`, and `section_7436_employment_status_permits_suit`.

**Enochs v. Williams Packing two-prong judicial test** вҖ” both required conjunctively:
- **Prong 1**: under no circumstances could the Government ultimately prevail
- **Prong 2**: equity jurisdiction otherwise exists (irreparable harm + legal remedy inadequate)

Either prong failing barres the suit. Pinned by `enochs_both_prongs_permits_suit`, `enochs_only_prong_1_barred`, `enochs_only_prong_2_barred`, and `enochs_2x2_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: TT permits, TF / FT / FF all barred).

**CIC Services v. IRS regulatory pre-enforcement carve-out** вҖ” 593 U.S. 209 (2021) held that a suit challenging the lawfulness of an IRS reporting requirement is NOT a suit to restrain assessment or collection within the meaning of В§ 7421(a). Distinction: (1) suits attacking assessment/collection itself versus (2) suits challenging regulatory information-reporting obligations. Pinned by `cic_services_pre_enforcement_permits_suit`, `cic_services_uniquely_permits_pre_enforcement_invariant`, and `note_pins_cic_services_distinction`.

**Default trader procedural pathway** вҖ” pay disputed tax + file refund claim under В§ 6402/В§ 7422 + file refund suit. Refund suit after payment is NOT a В§ 7421(a) bar. Pinned by `refund_after_payment_not_barred`, `refund_path_uniquely_distinct_from_aia_invariant`, and `note_pins_refund_suit_pathway`.

**Three exception pathways are independent** вҖ” each can engage independently of the others. Statutory engages without Enochs/CIC; Enochs engages without statutory; CIC engages without either. Pinned by `three_exception_pathways_independent` (verifies each pathway sets its own flag without triggering the others).

**Bare assessment / collection suit stacks 3 failure reasons** вҖ” В§ 7421(a) general bar + Enochs prong 1 not satisfied + Enochs prong 2 not satisfied. Pinned by `multiple_failure_reasons_stack_for_bare_assessment_suit` (exactly 3 reasons), and `statutory_exception_short_circuits_enochs_failure_reasons` (statutory exception engages вҶ’ 0 failure reasons because suit permitted).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7421`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **restrain assessment no exception barred**; **restrain collection no exception barred**; **В§ 6213(a) exception permits suit**; **В§ 6330(e)(1) CDP exception permits suit**; **В§ 7426 wrongful levy exception permits suit**; **В§ 7429(b) jeopardy assessment permits suit**; **В§ 6015(e) innocent spouse permits suit**; **Enochs both prongs permits suit**; **Enochs only prong 1 barred**; **Enochs only prong 2 barred**; **CIC Services pre-enforcement permits suit**; **refund after payment not barred**; **citation pins Enochs and CIC Services**; **note pins 11 statutory exceptions**; **note pins Enochs two prong conjunction**; **note pins CIC Services distinction**; **note pins refund suit pathway** (В§ 7422); **statutory exception truth table** (11-exception sweep); **suit purpose truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Enochs 2x2 truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Enochs only engages for assessment or collection** (not pre-enforcement); **CIC Services uniquely permits pre-enforcement invariant**; **refund path uniquely distinct from AIA invariant** (4-purpose sweep); **three exception pathways independent**; **multiple failure reasons stack for bare assessment suit** (3 reasons); **statutory exception short-circuits Enochs failure reasons**; **assessment and collection engage AIA bar invariant** (4-purpose sweep); **В§ 6232(c) partnership adjustment permits suit**; **В§ 7436 employment status permits suit**.

`traderview-expense::section_7422` is the **IRC В§ 7422 civil actions for refund** module вҖ” completes the refund-procedure constellation with `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act) + `section_7508A` (disaster postponement of В§ 6511 refund-claim deadlines) + `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS) + `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations) + `section_6532` (refund-suit limitations) + `section_7429` (jeopardy review). Trader-relevant when trader has paid tax and wants to sue for refund (rather than file Tax Court petition pre-payment) вҖ” refund suits are the default pathway when taxpayer missed В§ 6213 90-day Tax Court window OR affirmatively chose to pay first to stop В§ 6601 interest accrual OR issue not amenable to Tax Court (e.g., refund of penalty already paid, recovery of erroneous offset).

**Four pre-suit requirements**:

| Requirement | Source | Trigger |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| 1. **Flora full-payment rule** | Flora v. United States, 362 U.S. 145 (1960) | Taxpayer must have FULLY PAID assessment before suing in district court / Court of Federal Claims |
| 2. Administrative claim filed | В§ 7422(a) + В§ 6511 | Form 843 / 1040-X filed within later of 3 years after return filing or 2 years after payment |
| 3. 6-month wait period | В§ 6532(a) | 180 days from administrative claim filing (unless IRS issues disallowance sooner) |
| 4. 2-year filing window | В§ 6532(a) | Suit filed within 2 years (730 days) after IRS mails notice of disallowance |

Pinned by `fully_compliant_suit_maintainable`, `flora_partial_payment_violates` (Flora rule blocks suit), `no_administrative_claim_violates`, `under_6_month_wait_violates`, `at_180_day_boundary_compliant`, `at_730_day_two_year_boundary_compliant`, `at_731_days_two_year_window_violates`, `at_179_days_violates_at_180_compliant_precision`, `at_730_compliant_at_731_violates_precision`, `irs_disallowance_short_circuits_6_month_wait` (IRS disallowance permits earlier filing), `six_month_wait_truth_table` (7-cell sweep), and `two_year_window_truth_table` (7-cell sweep).

**Flora full-payment rule** вҖ” Flora v. United States, 362 U.S. 145 (1960) holds that taxpayer must have FULLY PAID the assessment before suing for refund in district court or Court of Federal Claims (limited exceptions only). Distinguishes refund jurisdiction from Tax Court pre-payment jurisdiction. Applies uniformly to both district court and Court of Federal Claims. Pinned by `flora_partial_payment_violates`, `flora_rule_uniquely_blocks_district_court_invariant`, and `note_pins_flora_full_payment_rule`.

**В§ 7422(e) concurrent jurisdiction limitation** вҖ” if Secretary mails notice of deficiency BEFORE hearing in district court / Court of Federal Claims, proceedings must be STAYED during 90-day Tax Court petition window + 60 days additional. If taxpayer files Tax Court petition, district court / Court of Federal Claims LOSES jurisdiction to extent acquired by Tax Court. Pinned by `deficiency_notice_before_hearing_stays_proceedings` (deficiency notice + no Tax Court petition вҶ’ stay), `tax_court_petition_filed_transfers_jurisdiction` (deficiency notice + Tax Court petition вҶ’ jurisdiction transfer), `no_deficiency_notice_no_7422e_engagement`, `section_7422e_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: 00 вҶ’ no engagement; 01 вҶ’ no engagement; 10 вҶ’ stay; 11 вҶ’ transfer), `jurisdiction_transfer_uniquely_engages_when_both_conditions_invariant`, and `note_pins_section_7422e_jurisdiction_transfer`.

**Jurisdiction**: district court (28 USC В§ 1346(a)(1)) concurrent with United States Court of Federal Claims (28 USC В§ 1491). Pinned by `court_of_federal_claims_routing` and `note_pins_district_court_court_of_federal_claims_concurrent_jurisdiction`.

**Four-failure stack on bare suit** вҖ” Flora partial payment + no administrative claim + 2-year window expired + В§ 7422(e) stay = 4 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `four_failures_stack_for_bare_suit`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7422`. Twenty-six tests pin: **fully compliant suit maintainable**; **Flora partial payment violates** (362 U.S. 145); **no administrative claim violates** (В§ 7422(a) + В§ 6511); **under 6-month wait violates** (179 days); **at 180-day boundary compliant**; **IRS disallowance short-circuits 6-month wait**; **at 730-day two-year boundary compliant**; **at 731 days two-year window violates**; **deficiency notice before hearing stays proceedings** (В§ 7422(e)); **Tax Court petition filed transfers jurisdiction** (В§ 7422(e)); **no deficiency notice no В§ 7422(e) engagement**; **Court of Federal Claims routing**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)-(e) + В§ 6511 + В§ 6532(a) + Flora + 28 USC В§ 1346(a)(1) + 28 USC В§ 1491 + Pub. 556 + IRM 25.6.2; **note pins administrative claim requirement** (3 years / 2 years + Form 843); **note pins Flora full-payment rule** (362 U.S. 145 (1960)); **note pins 6-month + 2-year В§ 6532(a)**; **note pins В§ 7422(e) jurisdiction transfer** (60 days); **note pins district court + Court of Federal Claims concurrent jurisdiction** (28 USC В§ 1346(a)(1) + В§ 1491 + AIA-exception pathway); **four failures stack for bare suit**; **six-month wait truth table** (7-cell sweep); **two-year window truth table** (7-cell sweep); **В§ 7422(e) truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Flora rule uniquely blocks district court invariant** (district + Court of Federal Claims both blocked); **at 179 days violates at 180 compliant precision**; **at 730 compliant at 731 violates precision**; **jurisdiction transfer uniquely engages when both conditions invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_7426` is the **IRC В§ 7426 civil actions by persons other than taxpayers (third-party wrongful levy + surplus proceeds + substituted sales proceeds + В§ 7426(h) unauthorized collection damages)** module вҖ” when IRS levies on property that BELONGS to someone other than the assessed taxpayer (joint accounts + nominee accounts + community-property third-party interests + trader's co-owner / lender / lien-holder rights in seized rental property), what civil remedy is available to the third party? Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act вҖ” В§ 7426(a)+(b)(1) is a statutory exception), `section_7433` (parallel civil-damages regime for taxpayer), `section_7430` (litigation costs), and `section_6334` (property exempt from levy).

**Four action types under В§ 7426**:

| Action | Source | Required elements |
|--------|--------|--------------------|
| Wrongful levy | В§ 7426(a)(1) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien on property + 2-year (or 9-month pre-2017) SOL |
| Surplus proceeds | В§ 7426(a)(2) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien JUNIOR to United States + entitled to excess sale proceeds |
| Substituted sales proceeds | В§ 7426(a)(3) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien on substituted fund pursuant to agreement |
| Unauthorized collection damages | В§ 7426(h) | IRS reckless / intentional / negligent disregard of IRC provision |

Pinned by `action_type_truth_table`, `wrongful_levy_within_2_year_sol_maintainable`, `surplus_proceeds_compliant`, `surplus_proceeds_no_junior_interest_violates`, `surplus_proceeds_uniquely_requires_junior_interest_invariant`, `substituted_sales_proceeds_compliant`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_compliant`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_not_applicable_violates`, and `taxpayer_assessed_blocked_from_action` (В§ 7426 actions limited to persons OTHER than taxpayer).

**В§ 7426(c) TCJA SOL extension вҖ” 2 years from 9 months** (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 11071, effective December 22, 2017). Pre-amendment levies retain 9-month (274-day) SOL; post-amendment levies get 2-year (730-day) SOL. Pinned by `wrongful_levy_at_730_day_boundary_compliant` (exactly 730 days), `wrongful_levy_731_days_violates_sol`, `pre_2017_tcja_9_month_sol`, `pre_2017_tcja_at_274_day_boundary_compliant`, `wrongful_levy_pre_2017_275_days_violates`, `sol_truth_table_2017_vs_pre_2017`, `sol_2_year_uniquely_for_post_2017_invariant`, and `sol_only_applies_to_wrongful_levy_not_other_actions` (surplus + substituted have no statutory SOL clock).

**В§ 7426(h) civil damages вҖ” mirror of В§ 7433 framework**:

| Culpability | Cap (cents) | Cap (dollars) |
|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| Negligence | $10,000,000,000 | **$100,000** |
| Reckless / Intentional | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |

Damages = min(cap, actual + costs). Pinned by `unauthorized_collection_damages_negligence_cap_100k`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_reckless_cap_1m`, `damages_cap_negligence_uniquely_lower_invariant` (reckless 10Г— negligence), `unauthorized_collection_damages_costs_only_at_zero_actual` (costs alone when actual = 0), `defensive_negative_damages_clamped`, `defensive_negative_costs_clamped`, and `defensive_overflow_damages_saturating` (i64::MAX Г— 2 вҶ’ caps at $1M).

**В§ 7421(a) Anti-Injunction Act exception engaged for three property actions** вҖ” В§ 7426(a)(1) + (a)(2) + (a)(3) are statutorily excepted from AIA bar on suits to restrain assessment or collection. В§ 7426(h) damages action does NOT engage AIA exception (different procedural posture). Pinned by `aia_exception_engaged_for_three_property_actions` (3-action sweep) and `aia_exception_not_engaged_for_unauthorized_collection_damages`.

**Wrongful levy three-failure stack** вҖ” taxpayer-assessed + no interest/lien + SOL-expired = 3 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `three_failures_stack_for_wrongful_levy` (exactly 3 reasons).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7426`.
