`traderview-expense::section_7429` is the **IRC В§ 7429 review of jeopardy levy or assessment procedures** module вҖ” trader-relevant when IRS believes collection is in jeopardy (taxpayer planning to flee, conceal assets, dispose of assets to evade collection) and invokes jeopardy assessment under В§ 6861 (income/estate/gift tax) + В§ 6862 (other taxes) plus immediate collection action. Trader-landlords face В§ 7429 review when IRS makes jeopardy assessment seizing trading accounts + rental property revenue + bank accounts. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act вҖ” В§ 7429(b) is one of 11 statutory exceptions), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6321`/`section_6323`/`section_6325` (lien constellation), and `section_6334` (exempt property).

**В§ 7429(a) administrative review three-step framework**:

| Step | Source | Deadline |
|------|--------|----------|
| 1. IRS provides written statement | В§ 7429(a)(1) | Within 5 days of jeopardy assessment/levy |
| 2. Taxpayer requests administrative review | В§ 7429(a)(2) | Within 30 days after written statement furnished |
| 3. IRS responds | В§ 7429(a)(3) | Within 15 calendar days |

Pinned by `admin_within_30_days_timely`, `admin_at_30_day_boundary_timely` (exactly 30 days compliant), `admin_at_31_days_violates`, `admin_at_zero_days_compliant`, `administrative_truth_table_30_day_boundary` (5-cell sweep: 0/15/30 compliant; 31/60 violate), and `note_pins_administrative_5_30_15_day_framework`.

**В§ 7429(b) judicial review framework**:

| Step | Source | Deadline / Trigger |
|------|--------|---------------------|
| Filing window | В§ 7429(b)(1) | Within 90 days from earlier of (a) district director's notice of determination or (b) **16th day** after administrative review request |
| Jurisdiction | В§ 7429(b)(2) | **District court has EXCLUSIVE jurisdiction** (no Tax Court alternative) |
| Court determination | В§ 7429(b)(3) | **20 calendar days** to determine (1) assessment is REASONABLE and (2) amount is APPROPRIATE |
| Extension | В§ 7429(b)(3) | Up to **40 additional calendar days** for reasonable grounds (**combined 60-day maximum**) |

Pinned by `judicial_within_90_days_timely`, `judicial_at_90_day_boundary_timely` (exactly 90 days compliant), `judicial_91_days_violates`, `judicial_unavailable_without_notification_or_16_days`, `judicial_available_at_16_day_administrative_trigger`, `judicial_path_available_when_determination_notified`, `judicial_path_available_when_administrative_request_at_16th_day`, `judicial_path_unavailable_at_15th_day_without_notification` (precision boundary at 15th day fails; 16th day permits), `court_determination_within_20_days_timely`, `court_determination_at_20_day_boundary_timely`, `court_determination_21_days_without_extension_violates`, `court_extension_up_to_40_additional_days` (55 days OK with full extension), `court_extension_at_60_day_combined_boundary_timely` (exactly 60 days), `court_extension_61_days_violates_combined_cap`, `court_extension_capped_at_40_additional_days` (50-day request capped at 40), `court_extension_30_day_partial_extension`, `court_window_20_day_default_no_extension`, `court_window_60_day_max_with_full_extension`, `court_determination_truth_table_with_and_without_extension` (5-cell sweep), `note_pins_judicial_90_day_16_day_trigger`, and `note_pins_district_court_exclusive_jurisdiction_20_40_court_window`.

**В§ 7421(a)(11) Anti-Injunction Act exception** вҖ” В§ 7429(b) judicial review is one of 11 enumerated statutory exceptions to AIA bar on suits to restrain assessment or collection. Without this exception, jeopardy-assessment review would be categorically barred. Pinned by `aia_exception_engaged_for_judicial_review` and `note_pins_aia_exception`.

**Multiple-failure stack** вҖ” judicial review past 90-day filing window + court determination past 20-day window = 2 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `three_failures_stack_for_judicial_review`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7429`. Thirty-two tests pin: **admin within 30 days timely**; **admin at 30-day boundary timely**; **admin at 31 days violates**; **admin at zero days compliant**; **judicial within 90 days timely**; **judicial at 90-day boundary timely**; **judicial 91 days violates**; **judicial unavailable without notification or 16 days**; **judicial available at 16-day administrative trigger**; **judicial path available when determination notified**; **judicial path available when administrative request at 16th day**; **judicial path unavailable at 15th day without notification**; **court determination within 20 days timely**; **court determination at 20-day boundary timely**; **court determination 21 days without extension violates**; **court extension up to 40 additional days**; **court extension at 60-day combined boundary timely**; **court extension 61 days violates combined cap**; **court extension capped at 40 additional days**; **court extension 30-day partial extension**; **court window 20-day default no extension**; **court window 60-day max with full extension**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(3) + (b)(1)-(3) + (c) + В§ 301.7429-3 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6862 + В§ 7421(a)(11) + IRM 5.1.4 + IRM 5.17.15 + IRM 8.24.2; **note pins administrative 5/30/15 day framework**; **note pins judicial 90-day + 16-day trigger**; **note pins district court exclusive jurisdiction + 20/40 court window**; **note pins AIA exception** (В§ 7421(a)(11) + 11 enumerated); **review stage truth table**; **administrative truth table 30-day boundary** (5-cell sweep); **court determination truth table with and without extension** (5-cell sweep); **AIA exception engaged for judicial review**; **three failures stack for judicial review**.

`traderview-expense::section_7430` is the **IRC В§ 7430 awarding of costs and certain fees against the IRS** module вҖ” when a taxpayer prevails in administrative or judicial proceedings against the IRS, under what conditions may the taxpayer recover litigation costs and attorney fees? Distinct from `section_6402` (refund offsets), `section_6611` (interest on overpayments), and `section_6213` (Tax Court petition timing). Trader-relevant when prevailing against IRS in Tax Court or refund litigation.

**Three gating tests** all must pass for fee award:

| Gate | Authority | Cap |
|------|-----------|-----|
| В§ 7430(c)(4)(D) net worth | 28 U.S.C. В§ 2412(d)(2)(B) | Individual вүӨ $2M; business entity вүӨ $7M |
| В§ 7430(c)(4)(D) size | 28 U.S.C. В§ 2412(d)(2)(B) | вүӨ 500 employees at filing |
| В§ 7430(b)(1) exhaustion | В§ 7430(b)(1) | Administrative remedies within IRS must be exhausted |

**Two prevailing-party paths** (В§ 7430(c)(4)(A) vs В§ 7430(c)(7)):

| Path | Trigger |
|------|---------|
| В§ 7430(c)(4)(A) Substantively prevailed | Taxpayer substantially prevailed on amount in controversy OR most significant issue AND IRS position NOT substantially justified |
| В§ 7430(c)(7) Qualified Offer Rule | Taxpayer made В§ 7430(c)(7) QO during administrative period AND QO liability вүҘ eventual judgment liability |

**В§ 7430(c)(1)(B)(iii) hourly rate cap (Rev. Proc. 2025-32)**: $260/hour for fees incurred in 2026 (up from $250 in 2025 and $230 in 2024). Base rate $125/hour (1996) inflation-adjusted.

**The Qualified Offer Rule bypasses the substantial-justification defense.** When the QO is engaged (QO вүҘ judgment), the IRS cannot defeat prevailing party status by arguing its position was substantially justified вҖ” Congress intended QOs to incentivize early IRS settlement. Pinned by `qualified_offer_rule_bypasses_substantial_justification_defense` (IRS substantial justification + QO engaged вҶ’ taxpayer still prevailing under QO rule), `qualified_offer_engages_when_qo_liability_geq_judgment`, `qualified_offer_does_not_engage_when_qo_liability_less_than_judgment`, `qualified_offer_exactly_at_judgment_engages_rule` (вүҘ boundary), `qualified_offer_one_cent_below_judgment_does_not_engage` (< boundary).

**Business entity net worth ceiling is 3.5Г— higher than individual.** Individual taxpayers face a $2M net worth ceiling; unincorporated businesses, corporations, and partnerships face a $7M ceiling вҖ” both measured at the time the case was filed. Pinned by `business_entity_7m_uniquely_higher_than_individual_2m_invariant` ($3M individual fails, $3M business satisfies), `individual_net_worth_above_2m_disqualifies`, `individual_net_worth_at_2m_boundary_satisfied`, `business_entity_net_worth_above_7m_disqualifies`, `business_entity_net_worth_7m_ceiling_uniquely_higher`.

**500-employee ceiling applies regardless of net worth.** A multinational with $1M net worth but 501 employees fails the size test. Pinned by `employee_count_above_500_disqualifies_regardless_of_net_worth`, `employee_count_at_500_satisfied` (boundary), `employee_count_at_501_uniquely_disqualifies`.

**IRS substantial justification defense defeats prevailing party status UNLESS QO rule applies.** Per В§ 7430(c)(4)(B), the IRS can avoid fee-shifting if its position had a "reasonable basis in law and fact." Pinned by `irs_position_substantially_justified_defeats_prevailing_party` (no QO вҶ’ not prevailing) vs `qualified_offer_rule_bypasses_substantial_justification_defense` (QO engaged вҶ’ prevailing despite IRS justification).

**Administrative exhaustion is a HARD prerequisite вҖ” substantively prevailing doesn't save fee award without exhaustion.** Pinned by `administrative_remedies_not_exhausted_zeros_fee_award` and `substantively_prevailed_but_not_exhausted_zeros_fees` (status = SubstantivelyPrevailed but fees = $0).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7430`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **substantively prevailed individual within net worth awards fees** (2026 $260/hr Г— 100 hrs = $26K); **IRS position substantially justified defeats prevailing party**; **individual net worth above $2M disqualifies**; **individual net worth at $2M boundary satisfied**; **business entity net worth $7M ceiling uniquely higher** ($5M qualifies); **business entity net worth above $7M disqualifies**; **employee count above 500 disqualifies regardless of net worth**; **employee count at 500 satisfied** (boundary); **administrative remedies not exhausted zeros fee award**; **qualified offer engages when QO liability вүҘ judgment**; **qualified offer does not engage when QO liability < judgment**; **qualified offer rule bypasses substantial justification defense**; **hourly cap 2026 is $260**; **hourly cap 2025 is $250**; **hourly cap pre-1996 falls back to $125 base**; **max fees calculation = hourly cap Г— hours**; **citation pins all subsections + 28 U.S.C. В§ 2412(d)(2)(B) + В§ 301.7430-2 + Rev. Proc. 2025-32 + IRM 35.10.1**; **business entity $7M uniquely higher than individual $2M invariant** ($3M test); **note describes 2026 $260/hour cap**; **substantively prevailed but not exhausted zeros fees**; **qualified offer rule only engaged when offer made**; **employee count at 501 uniquely disqualifies**; **negative net worth satisfies test** (defensive); **qualified offer exactly at judgment engages rule** (вүҘ boundary); **qualified offer one cent below judgment does not engage** (< boundary); **not prevailing zeros fee award**; **three gating tests all must pass for fee award invariant** (net worth + employee count + exhaustion).

`traderview-expense::section_7433` is the **IRC В§ 7433 civil damages for unauthorized IRS collection actions** module вҖ” when IRS officer or employee recklessly OR intentionally OR by reason of negligence disregards any IRC provision or regulation in connection with collection of federal tax, the taxpayer may bring civil action against the United States in district court. Trader-relevant for wrongful levy beyond statutory limits + lien filed without notice + collection during В§ 6330 CDP appeal + В§ 6331/В§ 6332/В§ 6334 violations. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + В§ 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS), `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7525` (FATP privilege), and `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders).

**В§ 7433 four-gate test** (all required) before judgment for damages may be awarded:

| Gate | Source | Failure mode |
|------|--------|--------------|
| Disregard of IRC provision | В§ 7433(a) | IRS conduct did not disregard IRC provision or regulation |
| In connection with collection | В§ 7433(a) | Conduct in assessment / examination / other non-collection context |
| Administrative remedies exhausted | В§ 7433(d)(1) | Plaintiff did not exhaust administrative remedies within IRS |
| Within 2-year SOL from accrual | В§ 7433(d)(3) | Action not brought within 2 years; without regard to amount in controversy |

Pinned by `reckless_base_passes_below_cap`, `no_disregard_no_action`, `assessment_context_not_collection_no_action`, `exhaustion_required_blocks_judgment`, `outside_2_year_sol_blocks_judgment`, `all_4_gates_failed_stacks_4_reasons` (exactly 4 reasons), and `exhaustion_failure_zeros_damages`.

**В§ 7433(b)(1) culpability-tiered damages cap**:

| Culpability | Cap (cents) | Cap (dollars) |
|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| Negligence (В§ 7433(b)(1)(A)) | $10,000,000,000 | **$100,000** |
| Reckless (В§ 7433(b)(1)(B)) | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |
| Intentional (В§ 7433(b)(1)(B)) | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |

Damages = min(cap, actual direct economic damages net of mitigable + costs of action). Pinned by `culpability_truth_table` (3-tier sweep), `negligence_cap_uniquely_lower_than_reckless_intentional` (reckless = 10Г— negligence), `negligence_uniquely_smaller_cap_invariant`, `reckless_and_intentional_same_cap_invariant`, `reckless_cap_1m_engages_high_damages`, `negligence_cap_100k_engages_high_damages`, `intentional_cap_1m_same_as_reckless`, `cap_at_negligence_boundary_engages` (exactly $100K compliant), and `cap_1_cent_over_negligence_boundary_engages` (precision boundary).

**В§ 7433(d)(2) mitigation reduction** вҖ” damages reduced by amount that could have been reasonably mitigated by plaintiff. Mitigation amount capped at actual damages to prevent negative net (cannot exceed what the plaintiff sustained). Pinned by `mitigation_reduction_applies`, `mitigation_capped_at_actual_damages` (mitigation вүӨ actual), `mitigation_full_offset_drives_to_costs_only` (full mitigation leaves only costs of action), and `defensive_negative_mitigable_treated_as_zero`.

**В§ 7433A parallel regime for qualified tax collection contractors** вҖ” post-2015 ATA reforms extended В§ 7433 framework to private tax collection contractors operating under qualified tax collection contracts with the IRS. Pinned by `note_pins_7433a_parallel_regime`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative damages / costs / mitigable amounts all clamped to 0 via `.max(0)`. Saturating subtraction (`actual - mitigable`) and saturating addition (`net + costs`) prevent overflow. Pinned by `defensive_negative_damages_clamped`, `defensive_negative_costs_clamped`, `defensive_negative_mitigable_treated_as_zero`, and `defensive_overflow_gross_saturating` (i64::MAX Г— 2 вҶ’ caps at $1M).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7433`. Thirty-one tests pin: **reckless base passes below cap**; **reckless cap $1M engages high damages**; **negligence cap $100K engages high damages**; **intentional cap $1M same as reckless**; **no disregard no action**; **assessment context not collection no action**; **exhaustion required blocks judgment**; **outside 2-year SOL blocks judgment**; **mitigation reduction applies**; **mitigation capped at actual damages**; **costs of action added to damages**; **negligence cap uniquely lower than reckless intentional** (reckless 10Г— negligence); **culpability truth table** (3-tier sweep); **all 4 gates failed stacks 4 reasons**; **cap at negligence boundary engages** ($100K); **cap 1Вў over negligence boundary engages** (precision); **defensive negative damages clamped**; **defensive negative costs clamped**; **defensive negative mitigable treated as zero**; **defensive overflow gross saturating** (i64::MAX); **citation pins all subsections** (a) + (b)(1) + (d)(1) + (d)(2) + (d)(3) + В§ 7433A + 26 CFR В§ 301.7433-1 + IRM 25.3.3; **note pins culpability tiers**; **note pins cap amounts** ($1M + $100K + negligence); **note pins exhaustion mitigation SOL**; **note pins В§ 7433A parallel regime**; **exhaustion failure zeros damages**; **mitigation full offset drives to costs only**; **negligence uniquely smaller cap invariant**; **reckless and intentional same cap invariant**; **precise actual $55,000 + costs $5,000 = $60,000**; **zero damages zero costs yields zero**.

`traderview-expense::section_7434` is the **IRC В§ 7434 civil damages for fraudulent filing of information returns** module вҖ” trader-relevant CIVIL remedy when a third party (employer, broker, payor) willfully files a fraudulent W-2, 1099, or other information return against the taxpayer. Distinct from criminal statutes (`section_7201` evasion, `section_7202` collection failure, `section_7203` failure to file, `section_7206` perjury), civil fraud (`section_6663`), and TFRP (`section_6672`). В§ 7434 is a VICTIM'S civil cause of action, not a government enforcement tool.

**Trader-relevant scenarios**:
- Broker files incorrect 1099-B inflating proceeds вҶ’ triggers wash-sale / В§ 1256 / В§ 988 false deficiency notices
- Employer files false W-2 inflating wages вҶ’ triggers SE-tax / FICA-tax false deficiency
- Payor files fake 1099-NEC inflating payments
- Retaliatory false W-2 / 1099 from former employer

**Statutory elements** (preponderance of evidence civil burden):

1. Any PERSON (defendant)
2. WILLFULLY FILED a fraudulent information return
3. Information return was FRAUDULENT
4. With respect to payments purported to be made to any other person (the plaintiff-taxpayer)

All four required. Pinned by `civil_action_authorized_truth_table` (8-cell sweep willful Г— fraudulent Г— misstatement).

**В§ 7434(b) damages вҖ” greater of**:

| Component | Recovery |
|-----------|------------|
| Statutory minimum | $5,000 |
| OR Actual damages | Sum of actual incurred |
| PLUS court costs | Recovered |
| PLUS attorney fees | At court's discretion |

Pinned by `statutory_minimum_5000_when_actual_below`, `actual_damages_when_above_5000_minimum`, `court_costs_recovered_when_authorized`, `attorney_fees_recovered_when_awarded`, `attorney_fees_zero_when_not_awarded`, `total_recovery_sums_three_components`, `statutory_minimum_5000_is_5000`.

**Derolf v. Risinger Bros. misclassification carveout** вҖ” most courts hold that misclassification as INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR (1099-MISC / 1099-NEC instead of W-2) does NOT give a В§ 7434 cause of action when the dollar amount is otherwise correct. Plaintiff must allege **FRAUDULENT AMOUNT MISSTATEMENT**, not merely wrong form classification. Critical scope limitation. Pinned by `derolf_misclassification_carveout_defeats_action` and `amount_misstatement_with_misclassification_still_authorizes` (amount misstatement saves claim even when misclassification also alleged).

**В§ 7434(d) statute of limitations вҖ” later of**:

- **6 YEARS** after filing of the fraudulent information return, OR
- **1 YEAR** after the date the fraudulent return would have been discovered by exercise of reasonable care

Pinned by `sol_within_6_year_satisfied`, `sol_at_6_year_boundary_expired`, `sol_satisfied_under_1_year_discovery_even_if_over_6_filing`, `sol_at_1_year_discovery_boundary_expired_if_over_6_filing`, `sol_truth_table_filing_x_discovery` (12-cell sweep).

**В§ 7434(e) вҖ” plaintiff must provide copy of complaint to Secretary** (IRS notice) upon filing civil action.

**Trader-tax-defense leverage** вҖ” В§ 7434 civil judgment provides COLLATERAL-ESTOPPEL leverage in any subsequent Tax Court / refund litigation arising from the fraudulent 1099 / W-2 deficiency notice. The judgment establishes the information return's falsity as a matter of preclusion in tax controversy.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7434`. Thirty-one tests pin: **full claim authorizes civil action**; **statutory minimum $5,000 when actual below**; **actual damages when above $5,000 minimum**; **court costs recovered when authorized**; **attorney fees recovered when awarded**; **attorney fees zero when not awarded**; **total recovery sums three components**; **no willful filing defeats action**; **no fraudulent return defeats action**; **Derolf misclassification carveout defeats action** (1099 instead of W-2 without amount misstatement); **amount misstatement with misclassification still authorizes**; **SOL within 6-year satisfied**; **SOL at 6-year boundary expired**; **SOL satisfied under 1-year discovery even if over 6 filing**; **SOL at 1-year discovery boundary expired if over 6 filing**; **SOL truth table filing Г— discovery** (12-cell sweep); **expired SOL defeats action**; **citation pins subsections and Derolf** (В§В§ 7434(a)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(e)/(f) + Derolf v. Risinger Bros.); **return type routed in note** (W-2 + 1099-B + 1099-NEC + 1099-MISC + 1099-DIV + 1099-INT); **cause of action note describes В§ 7434(a)**; **notice to Secretary note present** (В§ 7434(e) IRS notice); **collateral estoppel note present** (Tax Court leverage); **negative actual damages clamped to zero**; **negative court costs clamped to zero**; **negative attorney fees clamped to zero**; **six information return types routed correctly**; **damages note describes greater-of rule** (В§ 7434(b)); **SOL note describes later-of rule** (В§ 7434(d)); **civil action authorized truth table** (8-cell sweep willful Г— fraudulent Г— misstatement); **statutory minimum $5,000 is $5,000**; **full recovery note describes total**.

`traderview-expense::section_7463` is the **IRC В§ 7463 Disputes Involving $50,000 or Less (Tax Court Small Case Procedure / "S case" election)** module вҖ” trader-relevant for traders with smaller audit deficiencies seeking faster + cheaper resolution at Tax Court without formal rules of evidence and without appellate review. Companion to `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_7459` (Tax Court decisions), Tax Court Rules 170-175 (small case procedural rules).

**В§ 7463(a) Designation** вҖ” petitions filed with Tax Court for redetermination of deficiency where neither the amount of the deficiency placed in dispute nor the amount of any claimed overpayment exceeds **$50,000 per taxable year (income tax)**, **$50,000 per estate (estate tax)**, **$50,000 per calendar year (gift tax)**, or **$50,000 per taxable period or event (excise tax)**. Proceedings conducted at the **option of the taxpayer**, concurred in by the Tax Court or a division thereof, **BEFORE THE HEARING** of the case.

**В§ 7463(b) Finality of decisions** вҖ” a decision entered in any case conducted under В§ 7463 **SHALL NOT BE REVIEWED IN ANY OTHER COURT** and **SHALL NOT BE TREATED AS A PRECEDENT** for any other case. Critical procedural tradeoff вҖ” faster + cheaper + informal but **NO APPEAL + NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE**.

**В§ 7463(c) Limit on review** вҖ” taxpayer or the Secretary may concur to discontinue В§ 7463 designation BEFORE the case decision becomes final.

**В§ 7463(d) Procedure** вҖ” proceedings conducted under Tax Court Rules 170-175; "as informally as possible consistent with orderly procedure, and any evidence deemed by the Court to have probative value shall be admissible".

**В§ 7463(f) Additional cases** вҖ” small case procedure also available for: (1) В§ 6320 (CDP-lien) + В§ 6330 (CDP-levy) collection-determination proceedings under $50,000; (2) В§ 6015 innocent-spouse-relief proceedings under $50,000; (3) В§ 7436 worker-classification (1099 vs W-2) proceedings under $50,000.

**Procedural tradeoff** вҖ” S case **advantages**: faster (typically 6-12 months vs 18-36 months regular); cheaper (no transcript required); informal (relaxed rules of evidence); pro se friendly. S case **disadvantages**: NO APPEAL to Circuit Court + Supreme Court (decision is FINAL); NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE; informal nature may favor IRS-experienced counsel.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7463`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **income tax $40K within limit designation available**; **at $50,000 boundary compliant**; **$50,001 exceeds limit**; **estate tax in scope**; **gift tax in scope**; **excise tax in scope**; **В§ 6320 CDP-lien in scope** (В§ 7463(f)); **В§ 6330 CDP-levy in scope**; **В§ 6015 innocent spouse in scope**; **В§ 7436 worker classification in scope**; **no taxpayer election no designation**; **no Tax Court concurrence no designation** (BEFORE the hearing); **discontinued designation appealable**; **S case active decision final no appeal**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 7463(a)-(f) + Tax Court Rules 170-175 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213 + В§ 7459 + В§ 6320 + В§ 6330 + В§ 6015 + В§ 7436 + IRM 35.1.3); **note pins В§ 7463(a) $50K limit** (estate + gift + excise); **note pins В§ 7463(a) concurrence before hearing**; **note pins В§ 7463(b) no review no precedent**; **note pins В§ 7463(c) discontinue motion**; **note pins В§ 7463(d) informal rules** (Rules 170-175 + probative value); **note pins В§ 7463(f) collection + innocent spouse + worker** (В§ 6320 + В§ 6330 + В§ 6015 + В§ 7436); **note pins procedural tradeoff** (FASTER + CHEAPER + INFORMAL + NO APPEAL + NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE); **case type truth table 8 cells** (all 8 case types in scope); **amount boundary invariant** (50K ok, 50K+1 over); **S case active blocks appeal invariant**; **defensive zero amount designation available**; **multiple failures stack** (2 violations).

`traderview-expense::section_7491` is the **IRC В§ 7491 burden of proof shifts to Secretary** module вҖ” civil procedural defense that flips the burden of proof from taxpayer to IRS once taxpayer satisfies threshold requirements. Distinct from `section_6664` (reasonable cause + good faith defense to penalties), В§ 7454(a) (fraud-burden specific to В§ 6663 + accumulated earnings), and `section_6663` (civil fraud penalty). Highly relevant for aggressive В§ 1256 mark-to-market, В§ 988 currency, В§ 1202 QSBS holding-period, and В§ 475(f) trader-tax-status disputes.

**Three burden-shift subsections**:

| Subsection | Trigger | Burden type | Scope |
|------------|---------|---------------|---------|
| В§ 7491(a)(1) | Credible evidence + 3 thresholds (a)(2) | Burden of proof (full persuasion) | Subtitle A income / B estate/gift/GST |
| В§ 7491(b) | Statistical reconstruction by IRS | Burden of proof | Individual Subtitle A only |
| В§ 7491(c) | IRS asserts penalty / addition to tax | Burden of production (lower bar) | All proceedings, all penalties |

**В§ 7491(a)(1) вҖ” General factual burden shift**. If taxpayer introduces CREDIBLE EVIDENCE on a factual issue relevant to liability under Subtitle A (income, SE, NIIT) or B (estate, gift, GST), the Secretary bears the burden of proof on that issue. "Credible evidence" = quality of evidence the court would find sufficient to base a decision on if no contrary evidence were submitted (post-critical-analysis test).

**В§ 7491(a)(2) вҖ” Three threshold conditions (conjunctive)**:
- **В§ 7491(a)(2)(A) вҖ” Substantiation**. Taxpayer complied with substantiation requirements.
- **В§ 7491(a)(2)(B) вҖ” Records**. Taxpayer maintained all required records.
- **В§ 7491(a)(2)(C) вҖ” Cooperation**. Taxpayer cooperated with reasonable IRS requests for witnesses, information, documents, meetings, interviews.

All three required; missing any one defeats (a)(1). Pinned by `three_threshold_conjunctive_truth_table` (8-cell sweep across A Г— B Г— C).

**В§ 7491(a)(2)(C) вҖ” Net worth limitation**. Corporations, partnerships, and trusts with net worth EXCEEDING $7,000,000 at time of proceeding are EXCLUDED from (a)(1) burden-shifting. **Individuals and estates not subject to net-worth limitation regardless of size**. Pinned by `corporation_net_worth_above_7m_limitation_engaged`, `corporation_net_worth_at_7m_boundary_no_limitation` (boundary), `individual_unlimited_by_net_worth`, `estate_not_subject_to_7m_limitation`, `net_worth_limitation_only_for_corp_partnership_trust`.

**В§ 7491(b) вҖ” Statistical reconstruction burden**. In Subtitle A court proceeding involving an INDIVIDUAL, the Secretary bears burden on any income item reconstructed by statistical methods from unrelated taxpayers (BLS surveys, market-segment analysis). Limited to INDIVIDUAL Subtitle A only вҖ” corporations + estates + Subtitle B not covered. Pinned by `statistical_reconstruction_individual_subtitle_a_engages_b`, `statistical_reconstruction_corporation_not_engaged`, `statistical_reconstruction_estate_subtitle_b_not_engaged`.

**В§ 7491(c) вҖ” Penalty production burden (independent)**. Secretary bears BURDEN OF PRODUCTION (not persuasion) for any penalty or addition to tax вҖ” В§ 6651, В§ 6662, В§ 6663, В§ 6672, etc. IRS must come forward with evidence supporting the penalty before taxpayer must rebut. Lower bar than full burden of persuasion. **Independent of (a)(1) вҖ” engages even when (a)(1) threshold not met.** Pinned by `penalty_production_burden_independent_of_other_subsections`.

**Scope of В§ 7491(a)** вҖ” limited to Subtitle A (income) and Subtitle B (estate/gift/GST) taxes. **Subtitle C (employment) and Subtitle D (excise) outside scope**. Pinned by `employment_tax_outside_burden_shift_scope` and `excise_tax_outside_burden_shift_scope`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7491`. Thirty tests pin: **individual full compliance burden shifts**; **missing credible evidence no shift**; **missing substantiation no shift** (В§ 7491(a)(2)(A)); **missing records no shift** (В§ 7491(a)(2)(B)); **missing cooperation no shift** (В§ 7491(a)(2)(C)); **three threshold conjunctive truth table** (8-cell sweep A Г— B Г— C); **corporation net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **corporation net worth at $7M boundary no limitation**; **partnership net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **trust net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **individual unlimited by net worth** (no limitation regardless of size); **estate not subject to $7M limitation**; **employment tax outside burden shift scope** (Subtitle C); **excise tax outside burden shift scope** (Subtitle D); **estate / gift / GST within scope** (Subtitle B); **statistical reconstruction individual Subtitle A engages (b)** (BLS surveys + market-segment analysis); **statistical reconstruction corporation not engaged**; **statistical reconstruction estate Subtitle B not engaged**; **penalty production burden engaged** (В§ 7491(c) BURDEN OF PRODUCTION not persuasion); **penalty production burden not engaged when no penalty**; **penalty production burden independent of other subsections** (engages even without (a)(1)); **any burden shift engaged when any subsection engaged**; **any burden shift not engaged when no subsection**; **credible evidence note describes post-critical-analysis**; **three threshold satisfied note lists (A) substantiation + (B) records + (C) cooperation**; **RRA 1998 enactment note present** (Pub. L. No. 105-206); **citation pins all subsections** (В§В§ 7491(a)(1) / 7491(a)(2)(A) / 7491(a)(2)(B) / 7491(a)(2)(C) / 7491(b) / 7491(c) + В§ 7454(a) + В§ 6664(c) cross-references); **three subsections independent routing** (8-cell sweep credible Г— statistical Г— penalty); **net worth limitation only for corp / partnership / trust**; **corp with low net worth can engage (a)(1) shift**.

`traderview-expense::section_7206` is the **IRC В§ 7206 fraud and false statements (criminal felony вҖ” "tax perjury" statute)** module вҖ” criminal counterpart to `section_6663` civil fraud 75% penalty. Five enumerated criminal tax offenses with up to 3 years imprisonment + $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571. Natural sibling to `section_6663` (civil fraud), `section_6664` (reasonable cause defense), `section_6501` (В§ 6501(c)(1) unlimited ASED for fraud), and `section_7491` (burden shifts вҖ” criminal cases OUTSIDE В§ 7491 scope).

**Five enumerated offenses**:

| Subsection | Crime | SOL (В§ 6531) |
|------------|-------|---------------|
| В§ 7206(1) | Tax perjury вҖ” false return signed under penalty of perjury | 6 years |
| В§ 7206(2) | Aiding or assisting false document (preparers/advisors/third parties) | 6 years |
| В§ 7206(3) | Fraudulent bonds, permits, entries | 6 years |
| В§ 7206(4) | Removal or concealment of taxed goods with intent to defraud | 6 years |
| В§ 7206(5) | Compromises and closing-agreement fraud (В§В§ 7121/7122) | 3 years |

**В§ 7206(1) five-element test** (government bears BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden):

1. Defendant MADE AND SUBSCRIBED a return / statement / other document
2. Document was FALSE as to a MATERIAL matter
3. Document contained a written DECLARATION UNDER PENALTIES OF PERJURY
4. Defendant DID NOT BELIEVE the document to be true and correct as to every material matter
5. Defendant ACTED WILLFULLY with specific intent to violate the law

All five required; failure of any element defeats prosecution. Pinned by `section_1_five_element_truth_table` (32-cell sweep across all five elements).

**В§ 7206(2) reach beyond signing taxpayer**. Aids / assists / procures / counsels / advises preparation of false document. Reaches **return preparers, advisors, and third parties EVEN WHEN the taxpayer-signer is innocent**. Pinned by `section_2_aiding_full_elements_authorizes` and `section_2_aiding_element_note_describes_reach`.

**Cheek defense вҖ” Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. Good-faith subjective MISUNDERSTANDING of the law negates willfulness element (subjective belief test). **NOT** a defense for disagreement with the law (constitutional challenges, tax-protester arguments). Most important taxpayer defense to В§ 7206(1) prosecution. Pinned by `cheek_defense_successful_defeats_prosecution`, `cheek_defense_unsuccessful_does_not_defeat`, `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep across asserted Г— successful).

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to 3 YEARS
- Fine $250K individual / $500K corporation (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 Criminal Fines Improvement Act supersedes В§ 7206's original $100K cap)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted, plus costs of prosecution

**В§ 6531 criminal statute of limitations** вҖ” 6 years for В§ 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4); 3 years for В§ 7206(5) compromises and closing agreements. Pinned by `sol_truth_table_per_subsection` (5-subsection sweep).

**Spies-Daly doctrine вҖ” parallel civil + criminal**. В§ 7206 criminal prosecution may PROCEED IN PARALLEL with В§ 6663 civil fraud 75% penalty. Double jeopardy does NOT bar civil penalty after criminal conviction (or acquittal). В§ 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED applies once fraud established; В§ 6501(c)(2) UNLIMITED ASED for willful evasion. **В§ 7491 burden shifts do NOT apply to criminal prosecutions** вҖ” government bears beyond-reasonable-doubt burden on each element. Pinned by `section_6663_civil_parallel_always_available`, `unlimited_ased_cross_reference_note`, `section_7491_burden_shifts_excluded_note`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7206`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **В§ 7206(1) all five elements authorizes prosecution**; **В§ 7206(1) missing any element no prosecution**; **В§ 7206(1) five-element truth table** (32-cell sweep e1 Г— e2 Г— e3 Г— e4 Г— e5); **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek defense unsuccessful does not defeat**; **Cheek not asserted no Cheek engagement**; **Cheek case note describes subjective belief test**; **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **individual max fine $250K** (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571); **corporation max fine $500K**; **maximum imprisonment always 3 years** (5-subsection invariant); **В§ 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4) six-year SOL** (В§ 6531); **В§ 7206(5) three-year SOL**; **SOL truth table per subsection** (5-subsection sweep); **В§ 7206(2) aiding full elements authorizes**; **В§ 7206(2) aiding missing aid no prosecution**; **В§ 7206(2) aiding missing willfulness no prosecution**; **В§ 7206(2) Cheek defeats aiding**; **В§ 7206(2) aiding element note describes reach** (preparers + advisors + third parties even when taxpayer-signer innocent); **В§ 6663 civil parallel always available** (Spies-Daly doctrine); **unlimited ASED cross-reference note** (В§ 6501(c)(1)); **В§ 7491 burden shifts excluded note** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT); **CFIA 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 supersedes note**; **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **citation pins all subsections** (В§В§ 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4)/(5) + В§ 6531 + В§ 6663 + В§ 6501(c)(1) + В§ 6501(c)(2) + 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3); **five subsections routed correctly**; **elements count zero when no elements**; **elements count increments per element**; **В§ 7206(1) five-element note present** (5/5 satisfied note).

`traderview-expense::section_7207` is the **IRC В§ 7207 fraudulent returns, statements, or other documents (criminal misdemeanor)** module вҖ” pairs with `section_7206` (felony perjury alternative when document signed under penalties of perjury) and `section_7434` (civil damages for fraudulent information return). Completes the false-documents criminal statute trio. Criminal misdemeanor 1-year imprisonment + $100K individual / $200K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes original $10K / $50K caps).

**Three-element test** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT):

1. DELIVERY OR DISCLOSURE to an IRS officer or employee of any list, return, account, statement, or OTHER DOCUMENT
2. Document was FALSE OR FRAUDULENT as to a MATERIAL MATTER
3. Done WILLFULLY or with knowledge of the falsity or fraud

All three required; failure of any defeats prosecution. Pinned by `three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep).

**Distinction from В§ 7206**:

| Provision | Penalty | Document scope |
|-----------|---------|------------------|
| В§ 7206(1) FELONY | 3-year imprisonment | Documents signed under penalties of perjury (e.g., tax returns) |
| В§ 7207 MISDEMEANOR | 1-year imprisonment | ANY false document delivered to IRS regardless of perjury verification |

Government typically charges В§ 7207 when (a) computed tax deficiency is de minimis, (b) document was NOT signed under penalties of perjury, or (c) prosecutorial discretion favors misdemeanor over felony. Pinned by `de_minimis_deficiency_note_present`, `distinction_from_7206_note_describes_perjury_scope`.

**В§ 7206(1) felony alternative available** вҖ” when document IS signed under penalties of perjury AND all В§ 7207 elements satisfied, В§ 7206(1) felony alternative becomes available (prosecutor's discretion). Pinned by `signed_under_perjury_triggers_7206_alternative`, `felony_alternative_only_when_signed_and_all_three_elements`, `cheek_engaged_disables_felony_alternative`.

**Cheek defense вҖ” Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. Good-faith subjective belief negates willfulness element. Same defense as В§ 7201 / В§ 7203 / В§ 7206. Pinned by `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep) and `cheek_engaged_disables_felony_alternative`.

**В§ 6531(2) 6-year criminal SOL** for В§ 7207 (enumerated 6-year offense). Pinned by `criminal_sol_6_years`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **1 YEAR** (misdemeanor вҖ” invariant regardless of perjury verification)
- Fine $100K individual / $200K corporation under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes original $10K / $50K)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted
- Costs of prosecution

**Typical В§ 7207 fact patterns** вҖ” fabricated receipts during audit + altered Schedule K-1 + fraudulent supporting documents + false Form 433 collection information statement. Pinned by `typical_fact_patterns_note_present`.

**Spies-Daly parallel civil consequences** вҖ” В§ 7434 civil damages for fraudulent information return + В§ 6663 civil fraud 75% penalty + В§ 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED for fraud. Pinned by `spies_daly_parallel_civil_note_present`.

`traderview-expense::section_7212` is the **IRC В§ 7212 attempts to interfere with administration of internal revenue laws (criminal felony)** module вҖ” pairs with `section_7201` (5-year evasion felony), `section_7202` (5-year trust-fund-tax felony), `section_7203` (1-year misdemeanor failure to file), `section_7206` (3-year felony perjury), and `section_7434` (civil damages for fraudulent information return). 3-year FELONY for corruptly OR by force/threats impeding IRS administration + $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571.

**В§ 7212(a) two clauses**:

| Clause | Test | Marinello required |
|--------|------|----------------------|
| Officer-specific | Corruptly OR force/threats endeavors to intimidate/impede IRS officer in official capacity | NO |
| Omnibus | Corruptly OR force/threats obstructs due administration of Title 26 | YES |

**Officer-specific clause three-element test** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT):

1. Use of FORCE OR THREATS OF FORCE (or corrupt action)
2. To INTIMIDATE, IMPEDE, OR OBSTRUCT
3. Officer or employee of US acting in OFFICIAL CAPACITY under Title 26

Pinned by `officer_specific_three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep).

**Omnibus clause three-element test + Marinello nexus**:

1. CORRUPTLY ACTED (or force/threats)
2. With INTENT TO SECURE UNLAWFUL BENEFIT
3. OBSTRUCTED or impeded DUE ADMINISTRATION of Title 26

PLUS **Marinello v. United States, 138 S. Ct. 1101 (2018) nexus** to known pending OR reasonably foreseeable proceeding. Marinello narrowed omnibus clause to EXCLUDE routine non-compliance with tax code requirements absent the proceeding-nexus. Pinned by `omnibus_without_marinello_nexus_defeats` and `marinello_routine_noncompliance_carveout_note_when_missing`.

**В§ 7212(a) threats-only downgrade**. When offense is committed ONLY by threats of force (no actual force + no corrupt act), penalty reduces to: fine up to $3,000 + imprisonment up to **1 YEAR (misdemeanor)**. Otherwise: fine up to $5,000 (superseded by 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 to $250K individual / $500K corporation) + imprisonment up to **3 YEARS (felony)**. Pinned by `threats_only_with_intimidation_engages_misdemeanor_path` and `imprisonment_1_year_when_misdemeanor_threats_only`.

**"Corrupt" defined** вҖ” judicial gloss: act performed with INTENTION TO SECURE AN UNLAWFUL BENEFIT.

**В§ 6531 criminal SOL** вҖ” general **3-year** criminal SOL applies to В§ 7212 (NOT extended 6-year as for В§ 7201 / В§ 7203 / В§ 7206(1)-(4)). Notable shorter window. Pinned by `criminal_sol_3_years_general`.

**Spies-Daly parallel civil consequences**. В§ 7212 prosecution does not bar В§ 6663 civil fraud + В§ 6672 TFRP + other civil penalties arising from underlying conduct. В§ 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED for fraud if proven.

`traderview-expense::section_7216` is the **IRC В§ 7216 disclosure or use of return information by preparers (criminal misdemeanor)** module вҖ” pairs with **В§ 6713 civil penalty** ($250 per prohibited disclosure + $10,000 annual cap) for the same conduct. Criminal misdemeanor 1-year imprisonment + $100K individual / $200K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes В§ 7216's original $1K cap). Distinct trader-relevant: when broker, CPA, or tax preparer discloses trader's return information without authorization for purpose other than preparing the return.

**В§ 7216(a) вҖ” Criminal misdemeanor**. Preparer who KNOWINGLY OR RECKLESSLY discloses or uses tax return information for any purpose other than preparing the tax return.

**В§ 7216(b) two-pathway exceptions**:

| Exception | Authority | Requirement |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Taxpayer consent | 26 CFR В§ 301.7216-3 + Rev. Proc. 2013-14 | Written + signed BEFORE disclosure + specific recipient + specific purpose + duration + prominent and separate |
| Permissible non-consent | 26 CFR В§ 301.7216-2 | Court orders + IRS inquiries + peer review + malpractice insurance + payment processing |

Either exception defeats both criminal and civil liability. Pinned by `consent_truth_table` (4-cell sweep) and `either_exception_pathway_defeats_violation`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **1 YEAR** (misdemeanor)
- Fine $100K individual / $200K corporation under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes original $1K cap)
- Costs of prosecution
- **Identity-theft enhancement**: monetary penalty up to $100,000 (separate from В§ 3571) when disclosure or use is in connection with crime involving identity theft

**В§ 6713 civil penalty вҖ” paired with В§ 7216 same conduct**:
- $250 per prohibited disclosure or use
- $10,000 ANNUAL CAP per preparer per calendar year
- **NO-FAULT CIVIL** вҖ” does NOT require knowing or reckless conduct (lower standard than В§ 7216 criminal)

Pinned by `civil_vs_criminal_no_fault_distinction`, `civil_penalty_calculation_5_disclosures`, `civil_penalty_capped_at_10000_annual`, `civil_penalty_at_40_disclosures_at_cap`, `civil_penalty_at_39_disclosures_below_cap`, `civil_penalty_proportional_until_cap` (9-disclosure sweep).

**"Tax return information" broadly defined** вҖ” includes any information furnished for, or in connection with, the preparation of any tax return. Reaches financial records, brokerage statements, K-1s, payroll information, source documents.

**В§ 6531 general 3-year criminal SOL** applies to В§ 7216 (NOT extended 6-year as for В§ 7201 / В§ 7203 / В§ 7206(1)-(4) / В§ 7207).

**Consent regulations 26 CFR В§ 301.7216-3 + Rev. Proc. 2013-14** вҖ” taxpayer consent must satisfy: WRITTEN consent (electronic permitted) + SIGNED BEFORE the disclosure + specifies SPECIFIC RECIPIENT + specifies PURPOSE + DURATION of consent + PROMINENT and SEPARATE from other documents. Consent invalid if extracted by inadequate notice. Pinned by `invalid_consent_does_not_defeat`.

В§ 3571 + IRM 25.5.1/4.10); **criminal authorized note describes misdemeanor**; **consent truth table** (4-cell sweep obtained Г— compliant); **civil vs criminal no-fault distinction**; **either exception pathway defeats violation**; **imprisonment always 1 year misdemeanor**; **civil penalty proportional until cap** (9-disclosure sweep); **no violation no civil penalty**.

`traderview-expense::section_6713` is the **IRC В§ 6713 Disclosure or Use of Information by Preparers of Returns** module вҖ” civil penalty companion to `section_7216` criminal penalty for unauthorized disclosure or use of tax return information by return preparers. Trader-relevant because traders share extensive financial information with preparers (1099-Bs + 1099-Ks + K-1s + В§ 475(f) M2M election history + cost basis records + В§ 1091 wash sale tracking + В§ 1256 60/40 reporting + В§ 988 currency reporting + В§ 6038D foreign asset reporting). В§ 6713 protects against preparer monetizing, sharing, or otherwise using that data without taxpayer consent. Companion to `section_7216` (criminal disclosure penalty), `section_7525` (FATP privilege), `section_6694` (preparer substantive penalty), `section_6695` (preparer procedural penalty), Circular 230 В§ 10.50.

**В§ 6713(a) Imposition of penalty** вҖ” any person engaged in the business of preparing returns who (1) **discloses any information** furnished in connection with preparation OR (2) **uses such information for any purpose other than to prepare the return** SHALL pay penalty of **$250 per disclosure/use** with **$10,000 annual cap** (40-disclosure ceiling).

**В§ 6713(b) Exceptions** вҖ” В§ 7216(b) exceptions apply to В§ 6713; permissible disclosures without consent: (1) court order/subpoena; (2) preparer in firm; (3) firm assisting in preparation (e-filing service); (4) bookkeeping services; (5) quality/peer review; (6) professional liability insurance; (7) federal/state/local tax authority lawful investigation; (8) disclosure required by other federal law; plus taxpayer's signed and dated written consent (В§ 301.7216-3 + Rev. Proc. 2013-14 sample consent language).

**В§ 6713 vs В§ 7216 distinction** вҖ” **В§ 6713 civil penalty is STRICT LIABILITY** (no knowing/reckless requirement); **В§ 7216 criminal penalty requires KNOWING OR RECKLESS** conduct (misdemeanor + up to 1 year imprisonment + $1,000 fine + costs of prosecution; В§ 3571 supersedes original $1K cap to $100K individual / $200K corporation). Both penalties may apply concurrently to the same disclosure via В§ 6713(b) coordination with В§ 7216(b) exceptions.

**Common consent forms** вҖ” preparer must obtain taxpayer's signed and dated **written consent** before using tax return info for (a) cross-selling additional services, (b) outsourcing to overseas preparer, (c) sharing with third-party marketing services, (d) any other non-preparation use. AICPA + IRS Rev. Proc. 2013-14 provide sample consent language.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6713`. Proc. 2013-14 + IRM 20.1.6 + Circular 230 В§ 10.50 + В§ 7525); **note pins В§ 6713(a) $250 per disclosure $10K cap**; **note pins В§ 6713(b) seven exceptions** (court order + preparer in firm + bookkeeping + peer review + professional liability insurance + tax authority investigation); **note pins В§ 6713 vs В§ 7216 distinction** (STRICT LIABILITY vs KNOWING/RECKLESS + misdemeanor + 1 year imprisonment); **note pins concurrent penalty stacking**; **note pins common consent forms** (Rev. `traderview-expense::section_7201` is the **IRC В§ 7201 attempt to evade or defeat tax (apex criminal felony)** module вҖ” the most severe criminal tax statute in the Code. Pairs with `section_7206` (fraud and false statements / tax perjury вҖ” 3-year felony) and `section_6663` (civil fraud 75% penalty). 5-year felony with $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes В§ 7201's original $100K cap). Natural sibling to `section_6501` (В§ 6501(c)(1)/(c)(2) unlimited ASED for fraud), `section_6664` (reasonable cause defense), and `section_7491` (burden shifts excluded for criminal cases).

**В§ 7201 four-element test** (government bears BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden):

| Element | Test |
|---------|------|
| 1 | Existence of TAX DEFICIENCY (additional tax owed) |
| 2 | WILLFULNESS вҖ” voluntary intentional violation of known duty |
| 3 | AFFIRMATIVE ACT of evasion (Spies doctrine вҖ” omissions alone insufficient) |
| 4 | SUBSTANTIAL amount of tax owed |

All four required; failure of any defeats prosecution. Pinned by `four_element_truth_table` (16-cell sweep across all four elements).

**Spies v. United States, 317 U.S. 492 (1943) вҖ” Affirmative-act doctrine**. The hallmark of В§ 7201 is conduct "the likely effect of which would be to mislead or to conceal." **Mere failure to file or pay (omission) does NOT constitute В§ 7201 evasion** вҖ” distinct from В§ 7203 willful failure to file (which reaches omissions). Spies enumerates seven specific affirmative-act indicia:

1. Keeping a DOUBLE SET OF BOOKS
2. Making FALSE ENTRIES OR ALTERATIONS
3. Making FALSE INVOICES OR DOCUMENTS
4. DESTRUCTION OF BOOKS OR RECORDS
5. CONCEALMENT OF ASSETS
6. COVERING UP SOURCES OF INCOME
7. Handling one's affairs to avoid making the records usual in transactions of the kind

Presence of any Spies indicium supports the affirmative-act element. Pinned by `spies_indicia_truth_table_count` and `all_seven_spies_indicia_count` (7-indicium sweep).

**Sansone v. United States, 380 U.S. 343 (1965) вҖ” Two forms of evasion**:
- **Evasion of ASSESSMENT** вҖ” affirmative act prevents IRS from determining correct tax (false return, hidden income).
- **Evasion of PAYMENT** вҖ” affirmative act AFTER assessment prevents IRS from collecting (concealment of assets, transfers to nominees).

Both forms covered by В§ 7201; charging instrument identifies which. Pinned by `sansone_evasion_of_assessment_note_present` and `sansone_evasion_of_payment_note_when_payment_form`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **5 YEARS** (one of the longest in the Code; longer than В§ 7206's 3-year cap)
- Fine $250K individual / $500K corporation (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 supersedes original $100K cap)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted
- Costs of prosecution

**Cheek defense вҖ” Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. Good-faith subjective MISUNDERSTANDING of the law negates willfulness element (subjective belief test). NOT a defense for disagreement with the law (constitutional challenges, tax-protester arguments). Same Cheek defense available as for В§ 7206. Pinned by `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep asserted Г— successful).

**В§ 6531 criminal SOL** вҖ” 6 years for В§ 7201. **Spies-Daly doctrine permits parallel civil + criminal** вҖ” В§ 6663 civil fraud 75% penalty in parallel + В§ 6501(c)(1)/(c)(2) UNLIMITED ASED (no statute of limitations on assessment once fraud established). В§ 7491 burden shifts do NOT apply вҖ” government bears beyond-reasonable-doubt burden.

**Trader-relevant** вҖ” aggressive В§ 1256 mark-to-market, В§ 988 currency, В§ 1202 QSBS holding-period, В§ 475(f) trader-tax-status claims involving any affirmative conduct to conceal, mislead, or destroy records.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7201`. Thirty-one tests pin: **full four elements authorizes felony**; **maximum imprisonment 5 years**; **individual max fine $250K** (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571); **corporation max fine $500K**; **missing deficiency defeats prosecution**; **missing willfulness defeats prosecution**; **missing affirmative act defeats prosecution**; **missing substantial amount defeats prosecution**; **mere omission defeats affirmative act** (Spies doctrine вҖ” В§ 7203 reaches omissions but В§ 7201 does not); **Spies indicia count zero when none present**; **all seven Spies indicia count**; **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek defense unsuccessful does not defeat**; **Cheek case note describes subjective belief test**; **Sansone evasion of assessment note present** (380 U.S. 343 (1965)); **Sansone evasion of payment note when payment form**; **four-element truth table** (16-cell sweep across all four elements); **criminal SOL 6 years** (В§ 6531); **В§ 6663 civil parallel always available** (Spies-Daly doctrine); **unlimited ASED triggered when prosecution authorized** (В§ 6501(c)(1)); **unlimited ASED not triggered when prosecution not authorized**; **В§ 7491 burden shifts excluded note** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT); **CFIA 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 supersedes note**; **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§В§ 7201/6531/6663/6501(c)(1)/(c)(2)/7206 + 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 + Spies + Sansone + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3); **affirmative act with omission flag still defeats**; **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **negative deficiency treated as no deficiency**; **Spies indicia truth table count** (7-indicium sweep); **pairs with В§ 7206 citation reference**; **four-element count increments per element**.

`traderview-expense::section_7203` is the **IRC В§ 7203 willful failure to file return, supply information, or pay tax (criminal misdemeanor)** module вҖ” completes the criminal tax statute trio with `section_7201` (5-year felony apex / attempt to evade) and `section_7206` (3-year felony / fraud and false statements / tax perjury). Criminal MISDEMEANOR with 1-year imprisonment cap + $100K individual / $200K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes В§ 7203's original $25K / $100K caps).

**The critical distinction**: В§ 7203 reaches MERE OMISSIONS where В§ 7201 requires AFFIRMATIVE ACTS. A willful failure to file alone is В§ 7203 misdemeanor; the same failure coupled with affirmative acts of concealment (double books, false entries, destruction of records, concealment of assets) elevates to В§ 7201 felony under Spies v. United States, 317 U.S. 492 (1943).

**В§ 7203 reaches four distinct failures**:

| Failure type | Civil parallel | Unlimited ASED |
|--------------|------------------|------------------|
| Failure to FILE | В§ 6651(a)(1) 5%/month up to 25% | YES (В§ 6501(c)(3) no-return-filed) |
| Failure to PAY | В§ 6651(a)(2) 0.5%/month | NO |
| Failure to SUPPLY INFORMATION | вҖ” | NO |
| Failure to KEEP RECORDS | вҖ” | NO |

**В§ 7203 three-element test** (government bears BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden):

1. Person was REQUIRED BY LAW to file return / pay tax / supply information / keep records
2. FAILURE to do so at the time required by law
3. WILLFULNESS вҖ” voluntary intentional violation of a known legal duty

All three required; failure of any defeats prosecution. Pinned by `three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep across all three elements).

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **1 YEAR** (misdemeanor cap)
- Fine $100K individual / $200K corporation under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted
- Costs of prosecution

**В§ 6050I felony elevation exception**. Where the willful violation involves В§ 6050I (cash transaction reporting, $10,000 threshold), the misdemeanor is ELEVATED to FELONY: **5 YEARS imprisonment** + same fine framework. Pinned by `section_6050i_felony_engagement_5_year_imprisonment` and `section_6050i_truth_table`.

**Spies elevation to В§ 7201**. A willful failure coupled with AFFIRMATIVE ACTS of concealment (double set of books, false entries, destruction of records, concealment of assets, covering up income sources, handling affairs to avoid usual records вҖ” see `section_7201`) elevates the charge from В§ 7203 misdemeanor to В§ 7201 felony. Government routinely charges В§ 7201 whenever affirmative-act indicia exist. Pinned by `affirmative_acts_elevation_to_7201_warranted`, `no_affirmative_acts_no_elevation`, `affirmative_acts_truth_table`.

**Cheek defense вҖ” Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. A GENUINE GOOD-FAITH BELIEF that one isn't required to file negates the willfulness element, EVEN IF THAT BELIEF IS OBJECTIVELY UNREASONABLE. Subjective belief test вҖ” broader than the В§ 7201 / В§ 7206 Cheek standard because objectively unreasonable beliefs can still defeat В§ 7203 willfulness. NOT a defense for disagreement with the law (constitutional challenges, tax-protester arguments). Pinned by `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep) and `cheek_case_objectively_unreasonable_belief_note`.

**В§ 6531 criminal SOL** вҖ” 6 years for В§ 7203 (same as В§ 7201, В§ 7206(1)-(4); contrast with В§ 7206(5) 3-year SOL).

**Parallel civil penalties + UNLIMITED ASED**. В§ 7203 prosecution does NOT bar civil В§ 6651(a)(1) failure-to-file penalty (5% per month, up to 25%) or В§ 6651(a)(2) failure-to-pay penalty (0.5% per month). Spies-Daly doctrine permits parallel civil + criminal. **В§ 6501(c)(3) UNLIMITED ASED applies to no-return-filed cases** вҖ” IRS may assess at any time. Pinned by `unlimited_ased_engaged_for_failure_to_file_with_no_return`.

**В§ 7491 burden shifts do NOT apply to criminal prosecutions** вҖ” government bears beyond-reasonable-doubt burden.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7203`. Thirty-three tests pin: **full three elements authorizes prosecution**; **individual max fine $100K** (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571); **corporation max fine $200K**; **missing required by law defeats prosecution**; **missing failure at time defeats prosecution**; **missing willfulness defeats prosecution**; **three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep); **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek defense unsuccessful does not defeat**; **Cheek case objectively unreasonable belief note**; **В§ 6050I felony engagement 5-year imprisonment**; **no В§ 6050I violation 1-year imprisonment**; **affirmative acts elevation to В§ 7201 warranted** (Spies); **no affirmative acts no elevation**; **criminal SOL 6 years** (В§ 6531); **failure to file engages В§ 6651(a)(1) parallel**; **failure to pay engages В§ 6651(a)(2) parallel**; **failure to supply engages neither parallel**; **failure to keep records engages neither parallel**; **unlimited ASED engaged for failure to file with no return** (В§ 6501(c)(3)); **unlimited ASED not engaged for failure to pay**; **Spies-Daly parallel civil note present** (В§ 6651(a)(1) + (a)(2)); **В§ 7491 burden shifts excluded note**; **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§В§ 7203/6050I/6531/6651(a)(1)/(a)(2)/6501(c)(3)/7201/7206 + 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 + Spies + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3); **four failure types routed correctly**; **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **three-element count increments per element**; **CFIA 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 supersedes note**; **distinction from В§ 7201 note present**; **failure type routed in note**; **affirmative acts truth table** (2-cell sweep); **В§ 6050I truth table** (2-cell sweep imprisonment 1 vs 5 years).

`traderview-expense::section_7202` is the **IRC В§ 7202 willful failure to collect or pay over tax (criminal felony)** module вҖ” criminal counterpart to `section_6672` Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (civil 100% personal liability). Criminal FELONY with 5-year imprisonment cap + $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 (supersedes В§ 7202's original $10K cap). Pairs with `section_7201` (5-year felony / attempt to evade), `section_7206` (3-year felony / fraud and false statements / tax perjury), and `section_7203` (1-year misdemeanor / willful failure to file/pay) to complete the trader-tax criminal statute constellation.

**The critical pairing with В§ 6672**: same conduct (willful failure to pay over trust fund taxes by a responsible person) gives rise to BOTH a В§ 6672 100% civil penalty AND a В§ 7202 5-year felony. Spies-Daly doctrine permits parallel civil + criminal. Critical trader-business operational risk for any entity with W-2 employees вҖ” LLC with employees, S-corp with shareholder-employees, C-corp.

**В§ 7202 four-element test** (government bears BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden):

| Element | Test |
|---------|------|
| 1 | DUTY to collect, account for, or pay over tax (typically payroll taxes) |
| 2 | WILLFUL failure to perform the duty |
| 3 | AMOUNT REQUIRED to be withheld and paid |
| 4 | Defendant was a RESPONSIBLE PERSON (status, duty, AND authority) |

All four required; failure of any defeats prosecution. Pinned by `four_element_truth_table` (16-cell sweep across all four elements).

**Trust fund taxes reached by В§ 7202**:

| Tax category | Citation | Reached |
|--------------|----------|----------|
| Federal income tax withheld from employee wages | В§ 3402 | YES (trust fund) |
| Employee FICA share (Social Security + Medicare) | В§ 3101 | YES (trust fund) |
| Federal Unemployment Tax | В§ 3301 (FUTA) | YES |
| Employer FICA match | В§ 3111 | NO (separate framework) |
| Sales and excise taxes | вҖ” | NO |

Pinned by `trust_fund_taxes_note_lists_three_categories` and `employer_fica_match_excluded_note`.

**Responsible person standard вҖ” same as В§ 6672**. Status, duty, AND authority to avoid the employer's default in collection or payment of taxes. Criminal liability depends on the person's duties, position, involvement in company operations, and extent of control over financial resources. Includes officers, directors, members, shareholders, payroll managers with check-signing authority. Pinned by `responsible_person_standard_same_as_6672_note` and `responsible_person_status_engages_with_element_4`.

**Willfulness standard** вҖ” voluntary, intentional violation of a known legal duty. NO requirement to prove evil or bad intent. An act founded in good intent that voluntarily and intentionally violates a known legal duty supports willfulness. Same Cheek defense available вҖ” Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991) good-faith subjective belief negates willfulness, EVEN IF OBJECTIVELY UNREASONABLE. Pinned by `willfulness_no_evil_intent_required_note`, `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep), and `cheek_case_objectively_unreasonable_belief_note`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **5 YEARS** (same as В§ 7201)
- Fine $250K individual / $500K corporation (18 U.S.C. В§ 3571 supersedes original $10K cap)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted
- Costs of prosecution

**В§ 6531 criminal SOL** вҖ” 6 years for В§ 7202 (same as В§ 7201, В§ 7203, В§ 7206(1)-(4)).

**Parallel civil consequences**:
- **В§ 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty** (100% of unpaid trust fund taxes) routinely imposed alongside В§ 7202 prosecution вҖ” same responsible person test
- **В§ 6651(a)(2)** civil failure-to-pay penalty (0.5% per month)
- **11 U.S.C. В§ 523(a)(7)** вҖ” В§ 6672 TFRP NONDISCHARGEABLE in personal bankruptcy
- **В§ 7491 burden shifts do NOT apply** to criminal prosecutions (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden)

Pinned by `parallel_6672_tfrp_always_available`, `parallel_6672_tfrp_imposed_engages_nondischargeable_note`, `parallel_6651_a_2_failure_to_pay_always_available`, `section_7491_burden_shifts_excluded_note`.

В§ 3571); **corporation max fine $500K**; **missing duty defeats prosecution**; **missing willfulness defeats prosecution**; **missing amount required defeats prosecution**; **missing responsible person defeats prosecution**; **four-element truth table** (16-cell sweep); **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek defense unsuccessful does not defeat**; **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **Cheek case objectively unreasonable belief note**; **willfulness no evil intent required note**; **trust fund taxes note lists three categories** (В§ 3402 + В§ 3101 + В§ 3301); **employer FICA match excluded note** (В§ 3111 NOT REACHED); **responsible person standard same as В§ 6672 note**; **criminal SOL 6 years** (В§ 6531); **parallel В§ 6672 TFRP always available** (Spies-Daly doctrine); **parallel В§ 6672 TFRP imposed engages nondischargeable note** (11 U.S.C. В§ 523(a)(7) + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3 + IRM 8.25.1); **distinction from В§ 6672 note describes parallel**; **four-element count increments per element**; **responsible person status engages with element 4**; **pairs with В§ 6672 civil counterpart**.

`traderview-expense::section_7811` is the **IRC В§ 7811 Taxpayer Assistance Orders (TAOs)** module вҖ” administrative equitable remedy available when an IRS action or inaction is causing or about to cause "significant hardship" to the taxpayer. The National Taxpayer Advocate (NTA) has independent statutory authority under В§ 7811 to order the IRS to release property, take action, or refrain from taking action. Distinct from `section_6330`/`section_6320` (CDP вҖ” pre-action 30-day notice + Tax Court review), `section_7430` (attorney fees against IRS), and `section_6402` (refund offsets) вҖ” TAO is a parallel administrative pathway available alongside these judicial remedies.

**Five-input compliance framework**:

| Input | В§ 7811 mechanic |
|-------|------------------|
| Hardship category | В§ 7811(a)(2)(A)/(B)/(C)/(D) four enumerated examples (non-exclusive list) |
| Form 911 submitted | 26 CFR В§ 301.7811-1 prescribes Form 911 application |
| Requested action | В§ 7811(b)(1) release property OR В§ 7811(b)(2) cease/take/refrain |
| TAO issued | NTA decision triggers В§ 7811(d) statute-of-limitations suspension |
| Modifying official | В§ 7811(c) limits authority to NTA / Commissioner / Deputy Commissioner |

**Four enumerated significant-hardship categories (non-exclusive)** under В§ 7811(a)(2):

| Subsection | Category |
|------------|----------|
| В§ 7811(a)(2)(A) | Immediate threat of adverse action |
| В§ 7811(a)(2)(B) | Delay of more than 30 days in resolving account problems |
| В§ 7811(a)(2)(C) | Significant costs (including professional representation fees) |
| В§ 7811(a)(2)(D) | Irreparable injury or long-term adverse impact |

The list uses "includes" language вҖ” NTA retains authority to find significant hardship in categories not enumerated. Pinned by `other_hardship_category_still_engages_nta_authority`, `other_hardship_non_exclusive_list_note`, and `five_hardship_categories_all_engage_tao_pathway` (5-category sweep).

**В§ 7811(c) modification authority limited to three officials.** Only the National Taxpayer Advocate, Commissioner of Internal Revenue, or Deputy Commissioner may modify or rescind a TAO. No other IRS official has this authority вҖ” load-bearing independence-from-IRS feature. Pinned by `modification_by_nta_valid`, `modification_by_commissioner_valid`, `modification_by_deputy_commissioner_valid`, `modification_by_other_official_invalid`, `only_three_officials_uniquely_have_modification_authority_invariant` (4-official sweep: 3 valid + 1 invalid).

**В§ 7811(d) statute of limitations suspension is automatic upon application.** The clock on any action described in В§ 7811(b) is suspended from the date of the taxpayer's Form 911 application through the date of the NTA's decision PLUS any period specified in the TAO itself. Pinned by `statute_of_limitations_suspension_engaged_with_tao_pathway` and `statute_suspension_note_includes_application_through_decision_period`.

**В§ 7811(e) TAO is INDEPENDENT of other administrative or judicial remedies.** A taxpayer pursuing CDP under В§ 6320/В§ 6330, Tax Court petition under В§ 6213, refund litigation, or В§ 7430 fee shifting may simultaneously seek a TAO without waiver. Pinned by `parallel_cdp_triggers_independence_note` and `parallel_cdp_with_release_property_action_combines_correctly`.

**Form 911 is the prescribed application form.** No alternative submission method qualifies under 26 CFR В§ 301.7811-1 (except written statement providing sufficient information). Without Form 911, all pathway outputs are zero. Pinned by `no_form_911_no_tao_pathway`, `no_form_911_suspends_all_pathway_outputs`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7811`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **Form 911 with significant hardship opens TAO pathway**; **no Form 911 no TAO pathway**; **immediate adverse action hardship note present** (В§ 7811(a)(2)(A)); **delay 30+ days hardship note present** (В§ 7811(a)(2)(B)); **significant costs hardship note present** (В§ 7811(a)(2)(C)); **irreparable injury hardship note present** (В§ 7811(a)(2)(D)); **other hardship category still engages NTA authority**; **release levied property action note** (В§ 7811(b)(1)); **cease action note**; **take action note**; **refrain from action note**; **statute of limitations suspension engaged with TAO pathway** (В§ 7811(d)); **parallel CDP triggers independence note** (В§ 7811(e)); **no parallel CDP no independence note**; **modification by NTA valid** (В§ 7811(c)(1)); **modification by Commissioner valid** (В§ 7811(c)(2)); **modification by Deputy Commissioner valid** (В§ 7811(c)(3)); **modification by other official invalid**; **no modification attempt no modification note**; **citation pins all subsections + 26 CFR В§ 301.7811-1 + IRM 13.1.20 + Form 911**; **only three officials uniquely have modification authority invariant** (4-official sweep); **no Form 911 suspends all pathway outputs**; **five hardship categories all engage TAO pathway** (5-category sweep); **four requested actions all engage action note** (4-action sweep); **other hardship non-exclusive list note**; **parallel CDP with release property action combines correctly**; **statute suspension note includes application through decision period**; **modification by other when attempted falsifies validity** (underlying TAO pathway remains valid).

`traderview-expense::section_162a` is the **IRC В§ 162(a) trade or business expenses (ordinary and necessary) FOUNDATIONAL deduction provision module** вҖ” every other В§ 162 subsection plus every В§ 274 limit (iter 454) plus every В§ 280E disallowance operates as a modifier on the base В§ 162(a) deduction. Direct trader companion to `section_274` (meals/entertainment/gift/travel limits вҖ” iter 454), `section_162f` (fines/penalties non-deductibility), `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp deduction вҖ” iter 446), `section_280e` (cannabis trafficking disallowance), `section_263a` (UNICAP capitalization), `section_461g` (prepaid interest timing вҖ” iter 458), `section_481` (accounting method change), `section_475c2` (trader mark-to-market), `section_469` (passive activity loss), `section_183` (hobby loss), `section_280g` (golden parachute вҖ” iter 444).

В§ 162(a) provides: "There shall be allowed as a deduction all the ORDINARY AND NECESSARY expenses paid or incurred during the taxable year in CARRYING ON ANY TRADE OR BUSINESS, including: (1) reasonable allowance for salaries or other compensation for personal services actually rendered; (2) traveling expenses (including amounts expended for meals and lodging other than amounts which are lavish or extravagant under the circumstances) while away from home in the pursuit of a trade or business; and (3) rentals or other payments required to be made as a condition to the continued use or possession, for purposes of the trade or business, of property to which the taxpayer has not taken or is not taking title or in which he has no equity."

**Welch v. Helvering 4-element test** (synthesized from Welch v. Helvering, 290 U.S. 111 (1933) + Commissioner v. Heininger, 320 U.S. 467 (1943) + Lincoln Sav. & Loan Ass'n v. Commissioner, 403 U.S. 345 (1971) + INDOPCO, Inc. v. Commissioner, 503 U.S. 79 (1992)):
1. **ORDINARY** вҖ” "common and accepted" in the taxpayer's particular trade or business community; need not be habitual for taxpayer
2. **NECESSARY** вҖ” "appropriate and helpful" for the taxpayer's business; need NOT be essential
3. **EXPENSE (NOT CAPITAL EXPENDITURE)** вҖ” must be current expense vs. В§ 263 capital expenditure requiring В§ 167/В§ 168 depreciation
4. **CARRYING ON TRADE OR BUSINESS** вҖ” taxpayer must be ACTIVELY engaged in trade or business; Higgins v. Commissioner, 312 U.S. 212 (1941) held investor activity NOT a trade or business

**В§ 162(a) enumerated categories (3)**:
- **В§ 162(a)(1) REASONABLE COMPENSATION** for personal services actually rendered (subject to В§ 162(m) $1M public-company cap вҖ” iter 446 + В§ 280G change-in-control parachute вҖ” iter 444)
- **В§ 162(a)(2) TRAVELING EXPENSES** while away from home including meals and lodging (subject to В§ 274 limits вҖ” iter 454: 50% meals + В§ 274(d) substantiation + В§ 274(a) entertainment disallowance + В§ 274(b) $25 gift cap + В§ 274(o) per se entertainment facilities + В§ 274(h) foreign conventions + В§ 274(m) luxury water travel)
- **В§ 162(a)(3) RENTALS OR OTHER PAYMENTS** as condition to continued use of property

**В§ 162 disallowance subsections**:
- **В§ 162(c)** вҖ” illegal payments (to government officials + illegal kickbacks/rebates/bribes + Medicare/Medicaid kickbacks)
- **В§ 162(e)** вҖ” lobbying expenses (influencing legislation + political campaigns + grass roots advocacy; В§ 162(e)(2) local government also restricted post-OBRA 1993)
- **В§ 162(f)** вҖ” fines and penalties (post-TCJA 2017 В§ 162(f)(2) allows restitution / compliance amounts if identified in court order or settlement agreement)
- **В§ 162(m)** вҖ” $1M public-company exec comp cap (iter 446 companion)

**В§ 162 interactions**:
- **В§ 263 + В§ 263A UNICAP** вҖ” capital expenditures must be capitalized + depreciated under В§ 167/В§ 168; INDOPCO expanded capitalization to long-term-benefit expenses
- **В§ 274** вҖ” specific limits on В§ 162(a)(2) traveling expenses (companion iter 454)
- **В§ 183 hobby loss** вҖ” limits deductions to gross income if not engaged for profit; В§ 183(d) 3-of-5 year presumption OR 9-factor Treas. Reg. В§ 1.183-2(b) facts-and-circumstances test
- **В§ 280E cannabis trafficking** вҖ” disallows ALL В§ 162(a) deductions for Schedule I/II trafficking; only COGS allowed under В§ 471
- **В§ 475(f) trader mark-to-market** вҖ” converts investor activity to TRADE OR BUSINESS for В§ 162(a) deduction; Estate of Yaeger v. Commissioner, 889 F.2d 29 (2d Cir. 1989) trader-vs-investor criteria

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) В§ 475(f) mark-to-market trader claims home office + data feeds + brokerage commissions + trade-processing fees as В§ 162(a) deductions вҖ” all deductible if trader status established under Estate of Yaeger criteria; investor without В§ 475(f) cannot deduct same expenses under Higgins; (2) trader-landlord deducts property management + advertising + maintenance + accounting + legal + insurance on Schedule E / Form 1065 / 1120-S; capital improvements capitalized under В§ 263 + depreciated under В§ 168; (3) real estate trader vs investor вҖ” В§ 1221(a)(1) dealer property excluded from capital gain treatment; (4) Welch scenario вҖ” trader pays off former firm's debts NOT В§ 162(a) deductible (not "carrying on" trader's own business); (5) В§ 162(a)(2) trader conference travel вҖ” meals 50% under В§ 274(n); entertainment fully disallowed under В§ 274(a); gifts $25 cap under В§ 274(b).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-162a`. Thirty-two tests pin: **Welch four-element test satisfied**; **not ordinary disallowed** (Welch "common and accepted"); **not necessary disallowed** ("appropriate and helpful"); **not carrying on trade or business disallowed** (Higgins); **В§ 475(f) election converts investor to trader** (Estate of Yaeger); **capital expenditure INDOPCO capitalization required**; **capital expenditure category disallowed** ($2,500 Notice 2015-82); **illegal payment disallowed** (В§ 162(c)); **lobbying expense disallowed** (В§ 162(e) + OBRA 1993); **fines and penalties disallowed** (В§ 162(f) + В§ 162(f)(2) restitution); **В§ 280E cannabis disallowance engaged** (only COGS); **В§ 183 hobby loss concern engaged** (3-of-5 + 9-factor); **В§ 183 profit motive via 3-of-5 presumption**; **reasonable compensation category label** (В§ 162(m) + В§ 280G); **traveling expenses category label** (В§ 274 limits); **rentals or other payments category label**; **category truth table eight cells**; **В§ 280E uniquely disallows all В§ 162(a) invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 162(a) + В§ 162(a)(1)-(3) + В§ 162(c) + В§ 162(e) + В§ 162(f) + В§ 162(m) + В§ 263 + В§ 263A + В§ 167 + В§ 168 + В§ 183 + В§ 274 + В§ 280E + В§ 475(f) + В§ 1221 + Welch v. Helvering + Commissioner v. Heininger + Higgins v. Commissioner + Lincoln Sav. & Loan Ass'n + INDOPCO + Estate of Yaeger + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.162-1 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.183-2(b)); **note pins foundational deduction provision**; **note pins Welch four-element test with four cases**; **note pins three enumerated categories**; **note pins В§ 162(c) illegal payments**; **note pins В§ 162(e) lobbying**; **note pins В§ 162(f) fines and penalties**; **note pins В§ 162(m) exec comp cap**; **note pins В§ 263 INDOPCO capitalization**; **note pins В§ 274 specific limits**; **note pins В§ 183 hobby loss**; **note pins В§ 280E cannabis trafficking**; **note pins trader critical fact patterns**; **note pins companion modules**.

`traderview-expense::section_162f` is the **IRC В§ 162(f) fines and penalties nondeductibility** module вҖ” when a trader-business pays government-imposed fines (FINRA, SEC, CFTC disciplinary, state regulatory, environmental penalties, civil penalties), can it deduct the payment as a В§ 162(a) business expense? Distinct from `section_6651` (FTF/FTP penalty computation) and `section_6662` (accuracy-related penalty computation) вҖ” those modules compute the penalty dollars, while В§ 162(f) governs the deductibility of penalty payments themselves.

**Six payment-type classifications** with distinct outcomes:

| Payment type | Default deductible | В§ 162(f) treatment |
|--------------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Pure Fine | NO (when to government in relation to violation) | В§ 162(f)(1) general prohibition |
| Restitution / Remediation | NO without exception | В§ 162(f)(2)(A)(i) exception requires both identification + establishment prongs |
| Compliance With Law | NO without exception | В§ 162(f)(2)(A)(ii) exception requires both prongs |
| Routine Investigation / Court Costs | YES | В§ 162(f)(3) outside В§ 162(f)(1) prohibition |
| Tax Payment | YES (В§ 164) | outside В§ 162(f) framework |
