| Qui Tam Payment | YES | В§ 162(f)(5) relator is NOT government |

**В§ 162(f)(2) exception requires BOTH prongs simultaneously**:

| Prong | Authority | Requirement |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| Identification | В§ 162(f)(2)(B) | Court order or settlement agreement explicitly identifies amount AND purpose as restitution / remediation / compliance |
| Establishment | В§ 162(f)(2)(A) | Taxpayer establishes that the payment was actually made for the identified purpose |

Pinned by `both_prongs_required_invariant_for_exception_engagement` (4-cell truth-table sweep: only (true, true) yields deductible), `restitution_without_identification_nondeductible`, `restitution_without_establishment_nondeductible`, `compliance_with_law_truth_table_invariant` (parallel 4-cell sweep for compliance exception).

**TCJA В§ 13306 (December 22, 2017) grandfathers pre-existing binding orders.** В§ 162(f) as amended applies only to amounts paid or incurred under orders or agreements entered into AFTER December 22, 2017. Pre-TCJA binding orders remain governed by prior В§ 162(f) standards. Pinned by `pre_december_22_2017_binding_order_grandfathered`, `pre_tcja_grandfather_bypasses_all_other_prong_checks` (grandfather bypasses all other prong checks).

**В§ 6050X Form 1098-F information reporting at $50,000 threshold.** Government and governmental entities receiving payments described in В§ 162(f) of $50K or more must file Form 1098-F with the IRS and furnish a copy to the payor. Reporting required regardless of whether the payment is deductible вҖ” drives audit risk. Pinned by `form_1098_f_reporting_required_at_50k_threshold`, `form_1098_f_not_required_below_50k`, `form_1098_f_at_exact_50k_boundary` (boundary), `restitution_one_cent_above_50k_triggers_1098f`, `restitution_one_cent_below_50k_no_1098f`, `nondeductible_fine_still_triggers_1098f_reporting` (reporting decoupled from deductibility), `fine_not_in_relation_to_violation_no_1098f` (no 1098-F when not in violation relation).

**В§ 162(f)(5) qui tam payments to private relators outside В§ 162(f)(1).** False Claims Act and similar qui tam statute payments to private relators (not government) are not subject to В§ 162(f)(1) prohibition. Pinned by `qui_tam_payment_falls_outside_162f1`.

**В§ 162(f)(3) routine investigation costs and court costs unaffected.** Standard В§ 162(a) business expense analysis applies regardless of identification / establishment prong satisfaction. Pinned by `routine_investigation_cost_falls_outside_162f1`, `routine_investigation_cost_invariant_deductible_regardless_of_prongs` (truth-table sweep confirms deductibility regardless of prong status).

**В§ 162(q) sexual harassment NDA carve-out** referenced in citation but separately governed вҖ” no deduction for settlement or payment related to sexual harassment or sexual abuse if subject to a nondisclosure agreement. Out of scope of this module but flagged.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-162f`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **fine paid to government nondeductible** (В§ 162(f)(1)); **fine not to government falls outside В§ 162(f)**; **restitution with both prongs deductible** (В§ 162(f)(2)(A)); **restitution without identification nondeductible** (В§ 162(f)(2)(B)); **restitution without establishment nondeductible** (В§ 162(f)(2)(A) establishment); **compliance with law with both prongs deductible**; **pre-December 22, 2017 binding order grandfathered** (TCJA В§ 13306); **routine investigation cost falls outside В§ 162(f)(1)** (В§ 162(f)(3)); **tax payment governed by В§ 164**; **qui tam payment falls outside В§ 162(f)(1)** (В§ 162(f)(5)); **Form 1098-F reporting required at $50K threshold** (В§ 6050X); **Form 1098-F not required below $50K**; **Form 1098-F at exact $50K boundary**; **restitution meeting both prongs deductible with Form 1098-F**; **citation pins all subsections + В§ 6050X + В§ 162(q) + В§ 164 + TCJA В§ 13306 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.162-21 + TD 9946 January 19, 2021**; **both prongs required invariant for exception engagement** (4-cell truth table); **pre-TCJA grandfather bypasses all other prong checks**; **nondeductible fine still triggers 1098-F reporting** (reporting decoupled from deductibility); **restitution amount zero no 1098-F reporting**; **fine not in relation to violation no 1098-F**; **compliance with law truth table invariant** (parallel 4-cell sweep); **routine investigation cost invariant deductible regardless of prongs**; **6 payment type classification invariant** (3-group categorization sweep); **В§ 162(q) referenced in citation for sexual harassment carve-out**; **fine paid to non-governmental entity not in violation relation deductible**; **restitution one cent above $50K triggers 1098-F**; **restitution one cent below $50K no 1098-F**.

`traderview-expense::section_6303` is the **IRC В§ 6303 notice and demand for tax** module вҖ” foundational procedural requirement for IRS collection. Cross-referenced by `section_6321` (lien attachment requires notice and demand) and `section_6331` (levy authority requires notice and demand + 10-day neglect). Trader-relevant because no lawful IRS lien, levy, or seizure may proceed without proper В§ 6303 notice and demand. Procedural-companion to `section_6321`, `section_6323`, `section_6325`, `section_6331`, `section_6334` (levy/lien constellation), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_6213` (Tax Court petition), and `section_6601` (interest on unpaid tax).

**В§ 6303(a) general rule** вҖ” Secretary shall, as soon as practicable and within **60 days** after the making of an assessment of tax under В§ 6203, give notice to each person liable for the unpaid tax stating:
1. The **amount** of unpaid tax
2. **Demanding payment** thereof

Pinned by `fully_compliant_within_60_day_window`, `at_60_day_boundary_valid` (exactly 60 days = within window), `at_61_days_still_valid_but_outside_60_day_window`, `no_notice_given_invalid`, `no_amount_stated_invalid`, `no_payment_demanded_invalid`, `multiple_content_failures_stack` (amount + demand both missing = 2 violations), and `note_pins_60_day_rule`.

**В§ 6303(a) manner of delivery** вҖ” three permissible methods:
1. В§ 6303(a)(1) Left at dwelling
2. В§ 6303(a)(2) Left at usual place of business
3. В§ 6303(a)(3) Sent by mail to last known address

**Certified mail is NOT required**; ordinary mail to last known address suffices. Pinned by `left_at_dwelling_valid`, `left_at_place_of_business_valid`, `mailed_to_last_known_address_valid`, `no_delivery_invalid`, `delivery_manner_truth_table` (4-cell sweep вҖ” only NoDelivery invalidates), and `note_pins_three_delivery_methods`.

**В§ 6303(a) failure to give notice within 60 days does NOT invalidate notice** вҖ” late notice remains valid; collection authority engages once notice and demand are properly delivered. Pinned by `sixty_day_satisfied_with_late_notice_still_valid` (365 days post-assessment still valid notice) and `note_pins_late_notice_valid`.

**В§ 6303(b) assessment-prior-to-payment-due-date delayed-demand rule** вҖ” if tax is assessed BEFORE the last date prescribed for payment, payment shall NOT be demanded under В§ 6303(a) until AFTER such date, EXCEPT where Secretary finds collection would be jeopardized by delay (paired with В§ 6861/В§ 6862 jeopardy assessment + В§ 7429 review). Pinned by `delayed_demand_engaged_when_assessed_before_due_date`, `delayed_demand_bypassed_by_jeopardy_finding`, `delayed_demand_not_engaged_when_due_date_passed`, `delayed_demand_not_engaged_when_assessed_after_due_date`, `delayed_demand_truth_table` (7-cell sweep вҖ” only assessed-before-due + due-not-passed + no-jeopardy engages delay), `jeopardy_bypass_truth_table`, `assessment_after_due_date_no_delayed_demand_invariant`, `note_pins_delayed_demand_jeopardy_exception`, and `note_pins_cross_reference_predicate_for_lien_and_levy`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6303`. Twenty-six tests pin: **fully compliant within 60-day window**; **at 60-day boundary valid**; **at 61 days still valid but outside 60-day window**; **no notice given invalid**; **no delivery invalid**; **left at dwelling valid**; **left at place of business valid**; **mailed to last known address valid**; **no amount stated invalid**; **no payment demanded invalid**; **delayed demand engaged when assessed before due date** (В§ 6303(b)); **delayed demand bypassed by jeopardy finding** (В§ 6861/В§ 6862 + В§ 7429); **delayed demand not engaged when due date passed**; **delayed demand not engaged when assessed after due date**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6303(a)-(b) + В§ 301.6303-1 + В§ 6203 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213 + В§ 6601 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6862 + В§ 7429); **note pins 60-day rule**; **note pins three delivery methods** (certified mail NOT required); **note pins late notice valid**; **note pins delayed demand jeopardy exception**; **note pins cross-reference predicate for lien and levy**; **delivery manner truth table** (4-cell sweep); **delayed demand truth table** (7-cell sweep); **multiple content failures stack** (2 violations); **60-day satisfied with late notice still valid** (365 days post-assessment); **jeopardy bypass truth table**; **assessment after due date no delayed demand invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6304` is the **IRC В§ 6304 Fair Tax Collection Practices** module вҖ” added by RRA 98 В§ 3466 (IRS Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998) to import Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA, 15 USC В§ 1692) limits into IRS collection activity. Trader-relevant when an IRS revenue officer or private collection agency contractor engaged under В§ 6306 contacts the trader-taxpayer at inconvenient times or places, bypasses the trader's authorized representative under В§ 7521, contacts the trader at the workplace after employer disapproval, or uses harassing or abusive collection tactics. Companion to `section_6303` (notice and demand) + `section_7433` (civil damages vehicle for recovery when В§ 6304 violated) + `section_7811` (TAO).

**В§ 6304(a) Communications without consent** вҖ” without prior consent of taxpayer given directly to Secretary OR express permission of court of competent jurisdiction, Secretary may NOT communicate with taxpayer in connection with collection of any unpaid tax: (1) at any unusual time or place known or which should be known to be inconvenient вҖ” **default convenient window 8 a.m. to 9 p.m. local time** at taxpayer's location absent contrary knowledge; (2) if Secretary knows taxpayer is represented by authorized practitioner under В§ 7521 вҖ” bypass prohibited UNLESS representative fails to respond within reasonable period OR representative consents to direct communication; (3) at taxpayer's place of employment if Secretary knows or has reason to know that taxpayer's employer prohibits such communication.

**В§ 6304(b) Prohibition on harassment and abuse** вҖ” Secretary may NOT engage in any conduct whose natural consequence is to harass, oppress, or abuse any person in connection with collection of any unpaid tax. Specifically prohibited: (1) use or threat of violence or other criminal means to harm physical person, reputation, or property; (2) use of obscene or profane language whose natural consequence is to abuse hearer or reader; (3) causing telephone to ring or engaging any person in telephone conversation repeatedly or continuously with intent to annoy, abuse, or harass; (4) except under rules similar to FDCPA В§ 804 (15 USC В§ 1692b), placement of telephone calls without meaningful disclosure of caller's identity.

**В§ 6304(c) Civil action** вҖ” violations subject to В§ 7433 civil damages вҖ” capped at **$1,000,000 for reckless or intentional violations**, **$100,000 for negligence**, less amount of damages awarded by reason of judgment under В§ 7433 with respect to the same action.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6304`. Thirty-one tests pin: **clean communication no violation**; **before 8 a.m. inconvenient time violation**; **after 9 p.m. inconvenient time violation**; **known inconvenient time violation**; **consent cures time violation**; **represented taxpayer bypass violation** (В§ 7521); **unresponsive representative cures bypass**; **representative consent cures bypass**; **workplace contact with employer prohibition violation**; **workplace contact without prohibition no violation**; **threat of violence harassment violation** (В§ 6304(b)(1)); **obscene language harassment violation** (В§ 6304(b)(2)); **repeated phone ringing harassment violation** (В§ 6304(b)(3)); **anonymous calls harassment violation** (В§ 6304(b)(4) вҖ” FDCPA В§ 804); **reckless intentional damages cap at $1M**; **negligence damages cap at $100K**; **negligence actual damages under cap recoverable in full**; **no violation recoverable zero**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6304(a)-(c) + RRA 98 В§ 3466 + 15 USC В§ 1692 + IRM 5.1.10.3 + В§ 7433 + В§ 7811 + В§ 7521 + В§ 6306); **note pins 8 a.m. to 9 p.m. window**; **note pins RRA 98 + FDCPA origin**; **note pins damages cap tiers**; **multiple section (a) violations stack** (3 violations); **section (a) and (b) combined violations**; **damages cap tier invariant reckless higher than negligence** (10Г— ratio); **harassment truth table** (5-cell sweep); **time truth table** (4-cell sweep); **represented bypass truth table** (5-cell sweep); **court order overrides all section (a) restrictions**; **court order does NOT cure harassment section (b)** (subsection (b) independent of consent); **defensive max damages clamp to reckless cap** (u64::MAX вҶ’ $1M).

`traderview-expense::section_6306` is the **IRC В§ 6306 Qualified Tax Collection Contracts** module вҖ” added by American Jobs Creation Act of 2004 В§ 881, made MANDATORY by FAST Act of 2015 В§ 32102 (Fixing America's Surface Transportation Act). Trader-relevant when an old IRS receivable on a trader-taxpayer becomes inactive вҖ” over 2 years post-assessment without IRS-employee assignment, or over 365 days without taxpayer contact on an assigned receivable, or removed from active inventory for lack of resources/inability to locate вҖ” and IRS must (per FAST Act) assign to one of four authorized PCAs (CBE Group, Coast Professional, ConServe, Pioneer Credit Recovery). Companion to `section_6304` (В§ 6306(f) explicitly extends Fair Tax Collection Practices + В§ 7433 + FDCPA to PCA contractors), `section_7521` (audio recording вҖ” В§ 6306(e) prohibits PCA from conducting), `section_7811` (TAO), `section_7430` (litigation costs).

**В§ 6306(a)-(b) Contract framework** вҖ” Secretary may enter into qualified tax collection contracts for collection of outstanding inactive tax receivables. PCA contractor authorized to (1) locate and contact taxpayer specified by Secretary, (2) request full payment of Federal tax amount specified by Secretary, (3) if request cannot be met, offer installment agreement providing full payment during period **not to exceed 7 years**, (4) obtain financial information specified by Secretary.

**В§ 6306(c) Inactive tax receivable defined** вҖ” three disjunctive paths qualify a receivable as inactive: (1) IRS removes such receivable from active inventory for lack of resources or inability to locate taxpayer; (2) **more than 2 years** has passed since assessment and such receivable has not been assigned for collection to any IRS employee; (3) in the case of a receivable assigned for collection, **more than 365 days** have passed without interaction with taxpayer or third party for purposes of furthering collection.

**В§ 6306(d) Exclusions from PCA eligibility** вҖ” 8+ statutory exclusion categories: (1) pending or active offer-in-compromise or installment agreement; (2) innocent spouse cases; (3) deceased taxpayer + taxpayer under age 18 + designated combat zone or contingency operation taxpayer + identity theft victim; (4) currently under examination, litigation, criminal investigation, or levy; (5) proper exercise of appeal rights; (7) taxpayer substantially all of whose income consists of **В§ 223 Social Security disability insurance or SSI under title XVI**; (8) taxpayer with AGI not exceeding **200 percent of applicable federal poverty level** (added by Taxpayer First Act of 2019 В§ 1205).

**В§ 6306(e) PCA contractor restrictions** вҖ” collection contractor SHALL NOT (1) impose tax, (2) accept settlement on terms not within IRS authority, (3) take any enforcement action (lien filing, levy), (4) conduct В§ 7521(b)(2) in-person interview audio recording.

**В§ 6306(f) Fair Tax Collection Practices extension** вҖ” В§ 6304 + В§ 7433 civil damages apply to PCA contractor **as if the contractor were an IRS officer/employee**; FDCPA (15 USC В§ 1692) also applies.

**В§ 6306(j) Revenue split** вҖ” 25% of collected revenues retained for IRS special compliance personnel program account; 25% retained by PCA; remaining 50% to general fund.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6306`. Thirty-six tests pin: **inactive removed from active inventory eligible**; **over 2 years unassigned qualifies as inactive** (boundary 731 days); **exactly 2 years unassigned does NOT qualify** (730 days fails); **over 2 years assigned does NOT qualify under clause 2** (assignment kills clause-2 path); **over 365 days no contact qualifies when assigned** (boundary 366 days); **exactly 365 days no contact does NOT qualify** (365 days fails); **still active receivable does NOT qualify**; **pending OIC excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(1)); **innocent spouse excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(2)); **deceased taxpayer excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(3)); **taxpayer under 18 excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(3)); **combat zone taxpayer excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(3)); **identity theft victim excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(3)); **under exam excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(4)); **appeals pending excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(5)); **disability or SSI excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(7) вҖ” В§ 223 + title XVI); **AGI under 200% poverty excluded** (В§ 6306(d)(8) вҖ” Taxpayer First Act of 2019); **installment agreement at 7-year cap compliant** (boundary); **installment agreement 8 years violation** (В§ 6306(b)(1)(C)); **PCA enforcement action violation** (В§ 6306(e)); **PCA audio recording violation** (В§ 6306(e) + В§ 7521(b)(2)); **В§ 6304 extension always engaged**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6306(a)-(j) + AJCA 2004 В§ 881 + FAST Act В§ 32102 + Taxpayer First Act В§ 1205 + 15 USC В§ 1692 + В§ 6304 + В§ 7433 + В§ 7521 + IRM 5.19.9); **note pins 7-year installment cap**; **note pins three inactivity paths** (2 years + 365 days + active inventory); **note pins all exclusion categories** (disability/SSI + В§ 223 + title XVI + 200% federal poverty); **note pins PCA revenue split** (25% IRS + 25% PCA + 50% general fund); **note pins authorized PCAs** (CBE Group + ConServe + Pioneer Credit Recovery); **note pins PCA restrictions no enforcement**; **note pins В§ 6304 + В§ 7433 + FDCPA extension**; **multiple violations stack** (4 violations); **exclusion truth table 11 cells**; **inactivity truth table 8 cells**; **installment cap boundary invariant** (7 vs 8); **PCA restrictions independent of eligibility** (restrictions persist even when receivable inactive); **defensive zero days clean base with removed inventory qualifies**.

`traderview-expense::section_6320` is the **IRC В§ 6320 Collection Due Process (CDP) for LIENS** module вҖ” parallel framework to `section_6330` (CDP for levies, built iter 280) with lien-specific mechanics. Trader-critical when the IRS files a Notice of Federal Tax Lien (NFTL) вҖ” a public record asserting the government's claim against all of the taxpayer's property вҖ” and the taxpayer receives the CDP notice (Letter 3172) signaling their right to a hearing.

**Six statutory subsections compose the lien CDP framework**:

| Subsection | Mechanic |
|------------|----------|
| В§ 6320(a)(2)(B) | Letter 3172 mailed within 5 BUSINESS DAYS of NFTL filing |
| В§ 6320(a)(3)(B) | 30-day CDP request window beginning day AFTER 5-business-day notice period |
| В§ 6320(b)(1) | Right to fair CDP hearing before IRS Appeals |
| В§ 6320(c) | Issues considered (incorporates В§ 6330(c) collection alternatives + spousal defenses + underlying-liability gating + lien-specific В§ 6323(j) WITHDRAWAL / В§ 6325(d) SUBORDINATION / В§ 6325(b) DISCHARGE) |
| В§ 6320(d) | Tax Court review (incorporates В§ 6330(d)(1) 30-day petition window) |
| 26 CFR В§ 301.6320-1 | Treasury regulations |

**Key differences from В§ 6330 (levy)**:

| Aspect | В§ 6330 (levy) | В§ 6320 (lien) |
|--------|----------------|------------------|
| Pre-action notice deadline | 30 days before levy (В§ 6330(a)(3)(B)) | 5 BUSINESS DAYS after NFTL filing (В§ 6320(a)(2)(B)) |
| Action suspension during CDP | YES вҖ” В§ 6330(e) levy + В§ 6502/В§ 6531/В§ 6532 limitations | NO вҖ” lien remains in place; no automatic withdrawal |
| Effect on collection pressure | Levy halted; bank/wage protections continue | Public-record lien persists; credit harm continues |

**The 5-business-day deadline is uniquely Section 6320.** Failure to mail Letter 3172 within 5 business days is a procedural defect the taxpayer may raise at the CDP hearing, but DOES NOT invalidate the underlying lien itself. The NFTL remains a valid public record regardless of notice timing. Pinned by `exactly_5_business_days_notice_compliant`, `business_day_6_notice_procedural_defect`, `zero_days_notice_obviously_compliant`, `procedural_defect_does_not_invalidate_lien_itself` (lien-remains-in-place flag stays true even when notice late).

**Lien remains in place during CDP review.** Unlike В§ 6330(e) levy suspension, the NFTL is NOT automatically withdrawn pending the CDP hearing or Tax Court petition. The taxpayer may pursue lien-specific relief during the hearing вҖ” В§ 6323(j) WITHDRAWAL (administrative removal from public record), В§ 6325(d) SUBORDINATION (rank below specified other liens), or В§ 6325(b) DISCHARGE (release of specific property from lien) вҖ” but no relief is automatic. Pinned by `lien_remains_in_place_during_review_invariant` (note always surfaces), `lien_in_place_invariant_across_all_paths` (4-cell truth-table sweep), and `lien_specific_relief_includes_withdrawal_subordination_discharge`.

**Boechler v. Commissioner (596 U.S. 199, 2022) likely extends via В§ 6320(d) incorporation.** В§ 6320(d) incorporates В§ 6330(d) Tax Court framework, so the non-jurisdictional + equitable-tolling holding for В§ 6330(d)(1) deadlines likely applies to lien petitions as well. Pinned by `tax_court_day_31_with_tolling_timely_under_boechler_extension` (note specifically mentions В§ 6320(d) incorporation), `boechler_one_day_late_with_tolling_facts_timely`, `boechler_facts_extension_note_includes_section_6320d_incorporation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6320`. Twenty-five tests pin: **timely notice and CDP request grant hearing**; **exactly 5 business days notice compliant** (boundary); **business day 6 notice procedural defect**; **CDP request at 30 days boundary timely**; **CDP request day 31 loses judicial review** (equivalent hearing only); **lien remains in place during review invariant**; **collection alternatives and lien relief note when requested** (В§ 6323(j) WITHDRAWAL + В§ 6325(d) SUBORDINATION + В§ 6325(b) DISCHARGE + В§ 6159 installment + В§ 7122 OIC); **no collection alternatives no relief note**; **Tax Court petition within 30 days timely**; **Tax Court petition at 30 days boundary timely**; **Tax Court day 31 without tolling untimely**; **Tax Court day 31 with tolling timely under Boechler extension** via В§ 6320(d) incorporation; **underlying liability challenge available without prior opportunity** (incorporates В§ 6330(c)(2)(B)); **underlying liability challenge barred with prior opportunity**; **no Notice of Determination no petition pathway**; **citation pins subsections + 26 CFR В§ 301.6320-1 + В§ 6330(c)(2)(A)/(B) incorporated + В§ 6323(j) + В§ 6325(b)(d) + Boechler**; **Boechler one-day-late with tolling facts timely**; **lien-specific relief includes withdrawal/subordination/discharge**; **5-business-day window distinct from В§ 6330 30-day** (sibling distinction note); **liability challenge unavailable when CDP request late**; **procedural defect does not invalidate lien itself** (lien-remains-in-place persists); **zero days notice obviously compliant**; **Boechler facts extension note includes В§ 6320(d) incorporation**; **CDP request at business day 5 combined with 30-day request timely**; **lien in place invariant across all paths** (4-cell truth table).

`traderview-expense::section_6321` is the **IRC В§ 6321 lien for taxes (foundational IRS general tax lien)** module вҖ” the statutory provision under which a federal tax lien arises automatically by operation of law when IRS (1) assesses tax + (2) issues notice and demand for payment + (3) taxpayer neglects or refuses to pay. Foundational to the lien constellation: companion to `section_6330`/`section_6320` (Collection Due Process for levies and liens), `section_6334` (property exempt from levy), `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + В§ 7426 exception), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), and `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection).

**В§ 6321 three-element test** (all required for lien to arise):

| Element | Source | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| 1. Assessment | В§ 6201 et seq. | IRS makes formal assessment |
| 2. Notice and demand | В§ 6303 | IRS issues notice and demand for payment |
| 3. Neglect or refusal | В§ 6321 | Taxpayer neglects or refuses to pay after demand |

When all three present, lien arises AUTOMATICALLY upon ALL property and rights to property of the taxpayer вҖ” REAL + PERSONAL + TANGIBLE + INTANGIBLE вҖ” relating back to the assessment date. Pinned by `all_three_elements_lien_arises`, `three_element_truth_table_2x2x2` (8-cell exhaustive sweep: only TTT permits lien), `no_assessment_no_lien` (В§ 6201), `no_notice_and_demand_no_lien` (В§ 6303), `no_neglect_or_refusal_no_lien`, `two_of_three_no_lien_assessment_demand_only`, `two_of_three_no_lien_assessment_neglect_only`, `two_of_three_no_lien_demand_neglect_only`, and `three_failures_stack_when_all_three_missing` (exactly 3 reasons).

**NFTL filing is NOT required for lien to ATTACH** вҖ” В§ 6323(f) Notice of Federal Tax Lien filing affects PRIORITY against third parties (purchaser, holder of security interest, mechanic's lienor, judgment lien creditor) but the underlying В§ 6321 lien attaches automatically regardless of NFTL filing. This is the critical distinction trader-landlords often miss: an unfiled NFTL doesn't mean no lien вҖ” only no priority against subsequent third-party interests. Pinned by `lien_arises_without_nftl_filing`, `lien_arises_with_nftl_filing_independent`, `nftl_required_for_attachment_always_false` (16-cell exhaustive sweep across all input combinations), `nftl_filing_does_not_affect_lien_attachment`, `nftl_filed_field_preserved_through_result`, and `note_pins_nftl_priority_vs_attachment_distinction`.

**В§ 6321 lien relates back to assessment date** вҖ” once all three elements are satisfied, the lien is deemed to have arisen as of the assessment date. Pinned by `relates_back_to_assessment_date_when_lien_arises` and `relates_back_false_when_lien_does_not_arise`.

**В§ 6322 period of lien** вҖ” lien continues until liability is satisfied OR becomes unenforceable by reason of lapse of time. Paired with В§ 6502 10-year Collection Statute Expiration Date (CSED). Pinned by `note_pins_section_6322_period_of_lien` (В§ 6322 + В§ 6502 + lapse of time).

**Drye / Craft state-law property-rights doctrine** вҖ” Drye v. United States, 528 U.S. 49 (1999) holds that the federal tax lien attaches to whatever interest in property state law gives the taxpayer; United States v. Craft, 535 U.S. 274 (2002) extends the doctrine to tenancy-by-entirety property (still subject to lien for one spouse's tax liability). Pinned by `note_pins_drye_and_craft_state_law_doctrine`.

**Lien attaches to ALL property вҖ” including intangible** вҖ” broad scope covers tangible items and intangible rights, including future contract rights, accounts receivable, partnership interests, and beneficial interests in trusts. Pinned by `lien_attaches_to_intangible_property_per_note` and `note_pins_three_element_test`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6321`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **all three elements lien arises**; **no assessment no lien** (В§ 6201); **no notice and demand no lien** (В§ 6303); **no neglect or refusal no lien**; **lien arises without NFTL filing**; **lien arises with NFTL filing independent**; **NFTL required for attachment always false** (16-cell exhaustive sweep); **three-element truth table 2x2x2** (8-cell sweep: only TTT permits lien); **three failures stack when all three missing**; **citation pins lien constellation** (В§В§ 6321 + 6322 + 6323 + 6325 + В§ 6201 + В§ 6303 + Drye + Craft + IRM 5.17.2); **note pins three-element test** (real or personal + tangible or intangible); **note pins NFTL priority vs attachment distinction**; **note pins В§ 6322 period of lien** (В§ 6502 CSED); **note pins Drye and Craft state-law doctrine** (528 U.S. 49 (1999) + 535 U.S. 274 (2002) + tenancy by entirety); **relates back to assessment date when lien arises**; **relates back false when lien does not arise**; **attaches to all property true when lien arises**; **attaches to all property false when lien does not arise**; **two of three no lien assessment demand only**; **two of three no lien assessment neglect only**; **two of three no lien demand neglect only**; **NFTL filing does not affect lien attachment**; **NFTL filed field preserved through result**; **lien attaches to intangible property per note**; **missing assessment failure reason specific**; **missing demand failure reason specific**; **missing neglect failure reason specific**.

`traderview-expense::section_6323` is the **IRC В§ 6323 validity and priority of federal tax lien against certain persons** module вҖ” determines when a В§ 6321 lien that ATTACHES to taxpayer's property actually takes PRIORITY over competing third-party interests. Foundational lien-priority constellation companion to `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6322` (period of lien), `section_6325` (release), `section_6334` (exempt property), and `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy). Trader-relevant for trader-landlords whose rental property holdings interact with mortgages + judgment liens + mechanics' liens + secured creditors.

**В§ 6323(a) four protected classes** вҖ” lien is NOT valid against these classes UNTIL Notice of Federal Tax Lien (NFTL) is filed under В§ 6323(f). First-in-time wins among NFTL filing and competing third-party interest perfection.

| Protected class | Source |
|-----------------|--------|
| Purchaser | В§ 6323(a)(1) |
| Holder of security interest | В§ 6323(a)(2) |
| Mechanic's lienor | В§ 6323(a)(3) |
| Judgment lien creditor | В§ 6323(a)(4) |

Pinned by `nftl_filed_first_grants_lien_priority`, `nftl_not_filed_competing_claimant_wins`, `nftl_filed_after_competing_interest_loses_priority`, `purchaser_class_protected`, `mechanics_lienor_class_protected`, `judgment_lien_creditor_class_protected`, `judgment_lien_creditor_nftl_first_wins`, `purchaser_no_nftl_loses_to_purchaser`, `unprotected_creditor_loses_to_lien_even_without_nftl` (only the four protected classes need NFTL filing; general unsecured creditors do not), `competing_claimant_protection_truth_table` (5-cell sweep: 4 protected + 1 unprotected), `nftl_filing_2x2_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: TT permits priority; TF/FT/FF do not), `nftl_filed_priority_against_purchaser_invariant`, and `note_pins_four_protected_classes`.

**В§ 6323(b) ten superpriorities** вҖ” categories where competing claimant takes priority OVER federal tax lien EVEN AFTER NFTL is filed, when interest came into existence without actual notice of the federal tax lien:

| # | Superpriority | Source |
|---|---------------|--------|
| 1 | Securities | В§ 6323(b)(1) |
| 2 | Motor vehicles | В§ 6323(b)(2) |
| 3 | Personal property purchased at retail | В§ 6323(b)(3) |
| 4 | Personal property purchased in casual sale | В§ 6323(b)(4) |
| 5 | Personal property subject to possessory lien | В§ 6323(b)(5) |
| 6 | Real property tax / special assessment liens | В§ 6323(b)(6) |
| 7 | Residential property mechanic's lien (repair/improvement) | В§ 6323(b)(7) |
| 8 | Attorney's lien | В§ 6323(b)(8) |
| 9 | Insurance contracts | В§ 6323(b)(9) |
| 10 | Passbook loans | В§ 6323(b)(10) |

Pinned by `superpriority_truth_table` (10-superpriority sweep), `superpriority_securities_overrides_lien`, `superpriority_motor_vehicle_overrides_lien`, `superpriority_real_property_tax_overrides`, `superpriority_residential_mechanics_lien_overrides`, `superpriority_attorneys_lien_overrides`, `ten_superpriorities_all_distinct` (pairwise distinctness verification), `superpriority_overrides_nftl_filing` (NFTL filed + first-in-time still loses to superpriority), and `note_pins_ten_superpriorities`.

**Actual-notice gating clause** вҖ” В§ 6323(b) superpriority protection only engages when competing claimant did NOT have actual notice of the federal tax lien at the time their interest came into existence. Actual notice defeats superpriority and reverts to В§ 6323(a) first-in-time rule. Pinned by `superpriority_blocked_by_actual_notice` and `superpriority_with_actual_notice_loses`.

**В§ 6323(c)+(d) 45-day commercial transaction window** вҖ” commercial transactions financing agreements + after-acquired personal property purchases without actual notice get 45-day grace period. Pinned by `note_pins_45_day_commercial_window`.

**В§ 6323(g) NFTL refiling вҖ” every 10 years** вҖ” NFTL refiling required every 10 years to maintain priority status against subsequent interests; paired with В§ 6502 10-year Collection Statute Expiration Date. Pinned by `note_pins_10_year_refiling`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6323`. Thirty tests pin: **NFTL filed first grants lien priority**; **NFTL not filed competing claimant wins**; **NFTL filed after competing interest loses priority** (first-in-time); **purchaser class protected**; **mechanic's lienor class protected**; **judgment lien creditor class protected**; **unprotected creditor loses to lien even without NFTL** (only protected classes need NFTL); **superpriority securities overrides lien** (В§ 6323(b)(1)); **superpriority motor vehicle overrides lien** (В§ 6323(b)(2)); **superpriority attorney's lien overrides** (В§ 6323(b)(8)); **superpriority blocked by actual notice** (gating clause); **superpriority real property tax overrides** (В§ 6323(b)(6)); **superpriority residential mechanics lien overrides** (В§ 6323(b)(7)); **superpriority truth table** (10-superpriority sweep); **competing claimant protection truth table** (5-cell sweep); **NFTL filing 2x2 truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(4) + (b)(1)-(10) + (c) + (d) + (f) + (g) + (h) + В§ 6321 + В§ 6322 + Rev. Rul. 2003-108 + IRM 5.12.1/2/7/8; **note pins four protected classes**; **note pins ten superpriorities**; **note pins 45-day commercial window**; **note pins 10-year refiling** (В§ 6502); **NFTL filed field preserved**; **superpriority overrides NFTL filing** (NFTL filed + first-in-time still loses); **superpriority with actual notice loses**; **purchaser no NFTL loses to purchaser**; **judgment lien creditor NFTL first wins**; **unprotected creditor with NFTL filed lien wins**; **unprotected creditor no NFTL lien still wins**; **NFTL filed priority against purchaser invariant**; **ten superpriorities all distinct** (pairwise distinctness).

`traderview-expense::section_6325` is the **IRC В§ 6325 release of lien or discharge of property** module вҖ” completes the foundational federal tax lien constellation (`section_6321` attachment + `section_6322` period of lien + `section_6323` priority + `section_6325` release/discharge/subordination). Trader-relevant when trader-landlord seeks to (a) extinguish a federal tax lien upon full payment, (b) discharge individual property from lien for sale or refinance, or (c) subordinate IRS lien to allow junior financing (mortgage refinance to extract equity for IRS payment).

**Four certificate types under В§ 6325**:

| Certificate | Source | Effect |
|-------------|--------|--------|
| **Release** | В§ 6325(a) | Extinguishes lien entirely; must issue within 30 days |
| **Discharge** | В§ 6325(b) | Removes specific property from lien; other property still subject |
| **Subordination** | В§ 6325(d) | Allows junior creditor priority over federal tax lien |
| **Non-attachment** | В§ 6325(e) | Confusion-of-names cases |

Pinned by `certificate_type_truth_table` (3-cell sweep) and `non_attachment_certificate_issuable`.

**В§ 6325(a) RELEASE вҖ” 3 statutory bases + 30-day deadline**:

| Basis | Source | Trigger |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Full satisfaction | В§ 6325(a)(1) | Liability + interest fully paid |
| Legally unenforceable | В§ 6325(a)(1) | Paired with В§ 6502 CSED expiration |
| Bond accepted | В§ 6325(a)(2) | Bond conditioned upon payment furnished and accepted |

Secretary SHALL issue certificate within 30 days. Pinned by `release_basis_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: 3 valid + NotApplicable invalid), `release_full_satisfaction_within_30_days_issuable`, `release_legally_unenforceable_basis_valid`, `release_bond_accepted_basis_valid`, `release_no_basis_violates`, `release_at_30_day_boundary_issuable` (exactly 30 days), `release_31_days_violates`, and `release_uniquely_engages_30_day_clock_invariant` (release engages 30-day clock; discharge does not).

**В§ 6325(b) DISCHARGE вҖ” 5 statutory bases**:

| Basis | Source | Trigger |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Double-value rule | В§ 6325(b)(1) | Property value вүҘ 2Г— (federal tax lien + senior liens) |
| Partial payment | В§ 6325(b)(2)(A) | Payment equal to US net interest |
| No-value determination | В§ 6325(b)(2)(B) | US interest in property has no value |
| Proceeds substituted | В§ 6325(b)(3) | Sale proceeds held as fund subject to lien |
| Purchaser deposit | В§ 6325(b)(4) | Purchaser deposits with IRS |

Pinned by `discharge_basis_truth_table` (6-cell sweep: 5 valid + NotApplicable invalid), `discharge_double_value_2x_satisfied`, `discharge_double_value_at_boundary_satisfied` (property value = exactly 2Г— sum), `discharge_double_value_just_under_violates` (1 cent below 2Г— threshold), `discharge_partial_payment_basis_valid`, `discharge_no_value_basis_valid`, `discharge_proceeds_substituted_basis_valid`, `discharge_purchaser_deposit_basis_valid`, `discharge_no_basis_violates`, and `double_value_rule_only_engages_for_b1_discharge_invariant` (low-value property OK under (b)(3) proceeds-substituted but fails under (b)(1) double-value).

**В§ 6325(d) SUBORDINATION вҖ” 2 statutory bases**:

| Basis | Source | Trigger |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Payment for subordinated amount | В§ 6325(d)(1) | Payment to Secretary equal to subordinated amount |
| Ultimate collection facilitated | В§ 6325(d)(2) | IRS believes ultimate collection will be facilitated (typical trader-landlord mortgage refinance to extract equity for IRS payment) |

Pinned by `subordination_basis_truth_table` (3-cell sweep), `subordination_facilitates_collection_basis_valid`, `subordination_payment_basis_valid`, and `subordination_no_basis_violates`.

**В§ 6325(f) certificates are CONCLUSIVE** вҖ” certificate of release conclusively extinguishes lien; certificate of discharge conclusively releases property; certificate of subordination conclusively establishes superior priority of subordinated interest. Pinned by `certificate_conclusive_when_issuable`, `certificate_not_conclusive_when_not_issuable`, and `note_pins_6325f_conclusiveness`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative property value clamped via `.max(0)`; double-value calculation uses `.saturating_mul()` + `.saturating_add()` to prevent overflow. Pinned by `defensive_negative_property_value_clamped` and `defensive_overflow_double_value_saturating`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6325`. Thirty-three tests pin: **release full satisfaction within 30 days issuable**; **release at 30-day boundary issuable**; **release 31 days violates**; **release legally unenforceable basis valid**; **release bond accepted basis valid**; **release no basis violates**; **discharge double-value 2x satisfied**; **discharge double-value at boundary satisfied**; **discharge double-value just under violates** (1Вў precision); **discharge partial payment basis valid**; **discharge no-value basis valid**; **discharge proceeds substituted basis valid**; **discharge purchaser deposit basis valid**; **discharge no basis violates**; **subordination facilitates collection basis valid**; **subordination payment basis valid**; **subordination no basis violates**; **non-attachment certificate issuable**; **certificate type truth table** (3-cell sweep); **release basis truth table** (4-cell sweep); **discharge basis truth table** (6-cell sweep); **subordination basis truth table** (3-cell sweep); **certificate conclusive when issuable**; **certificate not conclusive when not issuable**; **defensive negative property value clamped**; **defensive overflow double-value saturating** (i64::MAX); **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(2) + (b)(1)-(4) + (c) + (d)(1)-(2) + (e) + (f) + В§ 301.6325-1 + IRM 5.12.10 + Pub. 783 + Pub. 784; **note pins 30-day release**; **note pins discharge five bases**; **note pins subordination two bases** (trader refinance); **note pins В§ 6325(f) conclusiveness**; **release uniquely engages 30-day clock invariant**; **double-value rule only engages for (b)(1) discharge invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6511` is the **IRC В§ 6511 limitations period on credit or refund** вҖ” controls WHEN a taxpayer may file a claim for refund of an overpayment. Trader-critical for every amended return (Form 1040-X) seeking money back from the IRS вҖ” beyond В§ 6511's deadline a claim is forever barred regardless of merits.

**Four claim-type regimes**:

| Claim type | Statute | Period | Lookback | Trader use case |
|------------|---------|--------|----------|------------------|
| Standard | В§ 6511(a) + В§ 6511(b)(2) | LATER OF 3 years from return filed OR 2 years from tax paid | 3 years or 2 years matching the active prong | Missed В§ 162 deduction; basis error; misapplied wash sale |
| Bad debt / worthless security | В§ 6511(d)(1) | 7 years from due date of loss year | 3 years | Late-discovered В§ 165(g) worthless security; В§ 166 nonbusiness bad debt |
| NOL / capital loss carryback | В§ 6511(d)(2)(A) | 3 years from due date of LOSS YEAR return (NOT carryback year) | 3 years | NOL carryback / В§ 1212 capital loss carryback claims |
| Foreign tax credit | В§ 6511(d)(3)(A) | 10 years from due date of year foreign taxes were paid/accrued | 10 years | Missed В§ 901 FTC; non-US trader with foreign-source income |

**The В§ 6511(d)(2)(A) carryback rule keys to the LOSS YEAR, not the amended year.** When an NOL or capital loss arises in 2024 and is carried back to 2022, the claim deadline runs 3 years from 2024's return due date (April 2028), NOT 3 years from 2022's return due date. Pinned by `nol_carryback_deadline_keyed_to_loss_year_not_amended_year` (loss-year 2020 вҶ’ amended 2018 вҶ’ deadline 2024) and `nol_carryback_three_years_from_loss_year_return_due` (loss-year 2021 вҶ’ amended 2019 вҶ’ deadline 2025).

**The В§ 6511(d)(3)(A) 10-year FTC period is THREE TIMES the standard 3-year period.** A trader who paid UK withholding tax in 2014 and discovers a missed В§ 901 FTC in 2024 is still timely under В§ 6511(d)(3)(A) (10 years from 2015 return due date вҶ’ 2025 deadline) but is barred under В§ 6511(a) standard rule (3 years from 2015 вҶ’ 2018 deadline). Pinned by `ftc_ten_year_period`, `ftc_period_beats_standard_for_long_horizon_overpayment` (2014 FTC + 2020 claim вҖ” FTC timely, standard barred), and `ftc_period_is_three_times_standard_period_invariant` (deadline delta = 7 years).

**Rev. Rul. 2020-8 suspended Rev. Rul. 71-533.** The IRS is reconsidering whether the В§ 6511(d)(3)(A) 10-year period applies to FTC carrybacks arising from subsequent-year NOL carrybacks. This open-question status is surfaced in every FTC claim's notes. Pinned by `ftc_notes_rev_rul_2020_8_open_question`.

**В§ 6511(h) financial-disability suspension is caller-managed.** When the taxpayer was financially disabled (medical impairment substantially preventing tax-affairs management under В§ 6511(h)(2)), the limitations period is suspended during disability. This module flags the suspension in notes but does not compute the unsuspended net period вҖ” caller responsibility. Pinned by `financial_disability_flag_surfaces_suspension_note`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6511`. Twenty-five tests pin: **standard 3 years from filing when filed after payment** (2022 tax year, filed 2023, paid 2023, claim 2026 timely); **standard 3-year deadline barred in year 27** (2027 claim barred); **standard 2-year from payment when later than 3-year from filing** (2018 tax + late 2025 payment + 2026 claim вҶ’ 2-year period governs); **standard 2-year lookback invoked when late payment**; **standard 3-year period pinned in notes**; **bad debt 7-year period** (В§ 165(g) worthless security 2020 loss + 2028 claim timely); **bad debt barred in year 29**; **bad debt citation pins В§В§ 166, 832(c), 165(g)**; **NOL carryback 3 years from loss-year return due** (2021 loss вҶ’ 2025 deadline); **NOL carryback deadline keyed to loss year not amended year**; **NOL carryback barred when filed after loss-year + 4** (2020 loss + 2025 claim barred); **FTC 10-year period** (2014 + 2025 timely); **FTC barred in year 26**; **FTC citation pins В§ 6511(d)(3)(A) + В§ 901**; **FTC notes Rev. Rul. 2020-8 open question**; **financial disability flag surfaces suspension note**; **FTC period beats standard for long-horizon overpayment**; **bad debt period beats standard for 7-year horizon**; **NOL carryback falls back to return tax year when loss year omitted**; **standard equal filing and payment year uses 3-year period**; **standard lookback 3 when 3-year period governs**; **standard lookback 2 when 2-year period governs**; **claim at deadline boundary year timely**; **claim one year after deadline barred**; **FTC period is 3Г— standard period invariant** (deadline delta = 7 years).

`traderview-expense::section_6501` is the **IRC В§ 6501 limitations on assessment and collection (ASED вҖ” Assessment Statute Expiration Date)** module вҖ” the IRS's deadline to assess additional tax. After the ASED passes, the IRS is BARRED from issuing a Notice of Deficiency or assessing additional tax. Strongest defensive procedural shield against IRS audit overreach on stale returns. Distinct from `section_6511` (refund SOL вҖ” 3 years to CLAIM refund) and the future CSED (collection statute вҖ” 10 years post-assessment to COLLECT under В§ 6502). Trader-critical for wash-sale loss disallowances, В§ 1256 mark-to-market reach-backs, В§ 988 currency, and В§ 1202 QSBS holding-period determinations.

**Eight assessment-period pathways**:

| Pathway | Period | Citation |
|---------|--------|----------|
| В§ 6501(a) 3-year default | 3 years from filing | В§ 6501(a) |
| В§ 6501(b)(1) early-filed deemed due-date | 3 years from due date | В§ 6501(b)(1) |
| В§ 6501(e)(1)(A)(i) >25% gross-income omission | 6 years | В§ 6501(e)(1)(A)(i) |
| В§ 6501(e)(1)(B) basis overstatement | 6 years | В§ 6501(e)(1)(B) (post-Home Concrete 2015) |
| В§ 6501(c)(1) fraud + intent to evade | UNLIMITED | В§ 6501(c)(1) |
| В§ 6501(c)(2) willful attempt to evade | UNLIMITED | В§ 6501(c)(2) |
| В§ 6501(c)(3) no return filed | UNLIMITED (until filing) | В§ 6501(c)(3) |
| В§ 6501(c)(4) Form 872 consent | extended per consent | В§ 6501(c)(4) + IRM 25.6.22 |

**В§ 6501(a) вҖ” 3-year default**. From filing date for any return filed on or after due date. Most common ASED pathway. **В§ 6501(b)(1) вҖ” early filing deemed filed on due date**. Early filing does NOT shorten ASED вҖ” clock starts from statutory due date (e.g., April 15 for individual returns). Filed March 1 вҶ’ ASED still April 15 + 3 years.

**В§ 6501(c)(1) вҖ” UNLIMITED for false or fraudulent return with intent to evade tax**. No SOL вҖ” IRS can assess at any time. Highest burden on IRS: clear-and-convincing evidence of fraud. **В§ 6501(c)(2) вҖ” UNLIMITED for willful attempt to evade tax**. **В§ 6501(c)(3) вҖ” UNLIMITED for no return filed**. 3-year clock starts only upon actual filing.

**В§ 6501(e)(1)(A)(i) вҖ” 6-year extended for >25% gross-income omission**. Substantial-understatement defense for IRS. **В§ 6501(e)(1)(B) вҖ” 6-year for basis overstatement** (post-2015 Surface Transportation and Veterans Health Care Choice Improvement Act of 2015 amendment, overruling *Home Concrete & Supply, LLC v. United States*, 132 S. Ct. 1836 (2012)). Basis overstatement now counts as gross-income omission for В§ 6501(e) 6-year purposes.

**В§ 6501(c)(4) вҖ” Form 872 consent extension**. Taxpayer may voluntarily extend ASED via Form 872 (Consent to Extend the Time to Assess Tax). Per **IRM 25.6.22**, IRS MUST notify taxpayer of THREE rights before signing: (1) right to refuse extension entirely, (2) right to limit extension to specific issues (Restricted Consent), (3) right to limit extension to specific date. Form 872-A (open-ended consent) terminates upon written notice. Three-rights-violation flag pinned via `form_872_without_three_rights_disclosure_violation`.

**Priority order** (load-bearing): no-return-filed вҶ’ fraud вҶ’ willful-evade вҶ’ Form 872 consent вҶ’ 6-year gross-income omission вҶ’ 6-year basis overstatement вҶ’ early-filing-due-date вҶ’ 3-year default. Pinned by `priority_order_fraud_beats_six_year_omission`, `priority_order_no_return_beats_fraud`, `priority_order_fraud_beats_willful`, `priority_order_six_year_omission_beats_six_year_basis`, `priority_order_form_872_beats_six_year`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6501`. Thirty-one tests pin: **3-year default pathway**; **3-year default citation pins В§ 6501(a)**; **3-year default note describes pathway**; **no return filed UNLIMITED** (В§ 6501(c)(3)); **no return note describes 3-year-clock-starts-only-upon-filing**; **fraud intent to evade UNLIMITED** (В§ 6501(c)(1)); **fraud note describes clear-and-convincing burden**; **willful evade UNLIMITED** (В§ 6501(c)(2)); **6-year gross-income omission pathway** (В§ 6501(e)(1)(A)(i)); **6-year basis overstatement pathway** (В§ 6501(e)(1)(B)); **6-year basis citation pins Home Concrete + Surface Transportation Act of 2015** (overrule); **6-year basis note describes amendment overrule**; **early filing deemed due-date pathway** (В§ 6501(b)(1)); **early filing note describes due-date anchor**; **Form 872 signed pathway**; **Form 872 without three-rights disclosure violation** (IRM 25.6.22); **Form 872-A open-ended note**; **Form 872 citation pins В§ 6501(c)(4) + IRM 25.6.22**; **priority order fraud beats 6-year omission**; **priority order no return beats fraud**; **priority order fraud beats willful**; **priority order 6-year omission beats 6-year basis**; **priority order Form 872 beats 6-year**; **unlimited pathways have no period years** (None); **6-year pathways have 6-year period**; **3-year pathways have 3-year period**; **pathway routing 8 cells invariant** (8-input вҶ’ 8-pathway sweep); **three-rights violation only when Form 872 signed**; **three-rights violation routed via Form 872 pathway only** (fraud overrides 872 entirely); **unlimited pathways all set unlimited flag**; **non-unlimited pathways do not set unlimited flag**.

`traderview-expense::section_6502` is the **IRC В§ 6502 collection after assessment (CSED вҖ” Collection Statute Expiration Date)** module вҖ” natural sibling to `section_6501` (ASED вҖ” Assessment Statute Expiration Date). After CSED passes, the IRS is BARRED from collecting the assessed tax via levy (В§ 6331), lien (В§ 6321), or court proceeding. Strongest defensive procedural shield against IRS collection overreach on aged assessments. Trader-relevant for aggressive В§ 1256 mark-to-market positions, В§ 988 currency reclassifications, and В§ 1202 QSBS holding-period audits that produced assessments more than 10 years ago.

**Base period вҖ” В§ 6502(a)(1)**: 10 years from the date of assessment. Most common CSED pathway. Independent of when return was filed or due вҖ” clock anchored to ASSESSMENT date.

**Six independent suspension triggers**:

| Trigger | Suspension period | Citation |
|---------|---------------------|------------|
| Installment agreement | Period agreed + 90 days post-expiration | В§ 6502(a)(2) |
| Offer in compromise (OIC) | Submission вҶ’ accept/reject/withdraw/return + 30 days if rejected | В§ 6331(k)(1) |
| CDP hearing request | Receipt вҶ’ conclusion + 90-day floor if < 90 days remain | В§ 6330(e)(1) |
| Bankruptcy automatic stay | Stay duration + 6 months after termination | В§ 6503(h) |
| Military combat zone | Service period + 180 days after departure / hospitalization | В§ 7508(a) |
| Continuous absence from US 6+ months | Absence period + return + 6 months | В§ 6503(c) |

**Overlapping suspensions run CONCURRENTLY, not cumulatively** (IRM 5.1.19.3.4). Two suspensions covering the same period extend CSED only once. Critical compliance trap вҖ” concurrent OIC + CDP is the most common overlap. Pinned by `overlapping_two_suspensions_engages_concurrent_warning`, `three_overlapping_suspensions_engage_warning`, `overlap_warning_only_engages_with_2_plus_distinct_triggers`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6502`. Twenty-six tests pin: **base period always 10 years**; **no suspensions engaged clean base**; **base period note describes CSED consequence** (IRS BARRED from levy/lien/court); **installment agreement engaged with 90-day extension note** (В§ 6502(a)(2) post-2000 requests); **OIC pending engages suspension** (В§ 6331(k)(1)); **OIC rejected engages 30-day extension**; **OIC rejected without pending no 30-day extension**; **CDP hearing engages suspension** (В§ 6330(e)(1)); **CDP with less than 90 days engages 90-day floor**; **CDP 90-day floor only when CDP requested**; **bankruptcy engages suspension with 6-month extension note** (В§ 6503(h)); **military combat zone engages with 180-day note** (В§ 7508(a)); **continuous absence engages with 6-month note** (В§ 6503(c)); **overlapping two suspensions engages concurrent warning** (IRM 5.1.19.3.4); **single suspension no overlap warning**; **all six suspensions engaged simultaneously**; **citation pins all seven authorities and IRM** (В§В§ 6502(a)(1), 6502(a)(2), 6331(k)(1), 6330(e)(1), 6503(h), 7508(a), 6503(c) + IRM 5.1.19.3.4 + IRM 8.21.5); **IRM 8.21.5 note always present**; **truth table six independent triggers each sets flag**; **three overlapping suspensions engage warning**; **OIC 30-day extension independent of CDP 90-day floor**; **rejected OIC 30-day note distinct from pending note**; **CDP hearing without 90-day remaining no floor note**; **any-suspension-engaged matches individual flags**; **10-year base immutable across all inputs**; **overlap warning only engages with 2+ distinct triggers**.

`traderview-expense::section_6531` is the **IRC В§ 6531 periods of limitation on criminal prosecutions** module вҖ” cross-cutting reference statute that determines criminal SOL for ALL Title 26 criminal tax prosecutions. Pairs with `section_7201` (evasion), `section_7202` (trust fund failure), `section_7203` (failure to file), `section_7206` (perjury), and `section_7212` (obstruction). Critical trader-tax-defense statute вҖ” В§ 6531 SOL determines whether IRS Criminal Investigation referral to DOJ Tax Division can result in prosecution.

**General rule**: 3 YEARS from commission of offense.

**6-YEAR exception enumerated offenses** (В§ 6531(2)):

- В§ 7201 tax evasion
- В§ 7202 willful failure to collect, account for, or pay over tax
- В§ 7203 willful failure to FILE return or PAY tax (NOT failure to keep records or supply info)
- В§ 7206(1) filing false return / tax perjury
- В§ 7206(2) aid or assist preparation of false return
- В§ 7207 fraudulent returns, statements, or other documents
- В§ 7212(b) rescue of seized property (NOT general obstruction В§ 7212(a))
- В§ 7214 unlawful acts of revenue officers
- 18 U.S.C. В§ 371 Klein conspiracy

Pinned by `nine_six_year_offenses_truth_table` (9-offense sweep).

**3-YEAR SOL applies** to all other Title 26 offenses including:

- В§ 7203 failure to keep records
- В§ 7203 failure to supply information
- В§ 7205 false withholding exemption certificate
- В§ 7206(3), (4), (5) other subsections
- В§ 7212(a) general obstruction of administration
- All other Title 26 offenses not enumerated

Pinned by `three_year_offenses_truth_table` (8-offense sweep).

**В§ 6531(4) carveout** вҖ” the 6-year rule for failure to file does NOT apply to returns required to be filed under Part III of Subchapter A of Chapter 61 (partnership Form 1065 + exempt organization Form 990 + S-corp Form 1120-S). 3-year SOL applies to these returns. Carveout applies ONLY to В§ 7203 (not other 6-year offenses). Pinned by `section_6531_4_carveout_partnership_return`, `section_6531_4_carveout_only_applies_to_7203`, `section_6531_4_carveout_truth_table`.

**В§ 6531 final-paragraph tolling** вҖ” if defendant is OUTSIDE the United States or is a fugitive from justice, SOL is TOLLED until 6 months after defendant returns or surrenders. Pinned by `outside_us_tolling_engages`, `returned_within_6_months_still_tolled`, `returned_past_6_months_tolling_ends`, `returned_at_6_month_boundary_still_tolled`.

**Toussie v. United States, 397 U.S. 112 (1970)** вҖ” continuing-offense doctrine narrowed but affirmative-act-doctrine cases survive. SOL runs from LAST affirmative act for multi-act offenses like В§ 7201 tax evasion.

**В§ 6531 SOL is JURISDICTIONAL** вҖ” IRS Criminal Investigation must refer to DOJ Tax Division before SOL expiration. DOJ Criminal Tax Manual В§ 7.00 + IRM 25.6.2.1.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6531`. Thirty-six tests pin: **В§ 7201 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7202 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7203 failure-to-file-or-pay 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7203 failure-to-keep-records 3-year SOL** (subset distinction); **В§ 7203 failure-to-supply-information 3-year SOL**; **В§ 7205 3-year SOL**; **В§ 7206(1) filing false return 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7206(2) aiding 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7206(3)/(4)/(5) 3-year SOL** (subsection-specific); **В§ 7207 6-year SOL**; **В§ 7212(a) general obstruction 3-year SOL** (distinct from В§ 7212(b)); **В§ 7212(b) rescue property 6-year SOL**; **Klein conspiracy 6-year SOL**; **other offense 3-year SOL**; **В§ 6531(4) carveout partnership return** (Form 1065 + Form 990 + Form 1120-S); **В§ 6531(4) carveout only applies to В§ 7203** (not other 6-year offenses); **В§ 6531(4) carveout truth table** (2-cell sweep); **SOL satisfied within 3 years for 3-year offense**; **SOL expired after 3 years for 3-year offense**; **SOL satisfied within 6 years for 6-year offense**; **SOL expired after 6 years for 6-year offense**; **outside-US tolling engages**; **returned within 6 months still tolled**; **returned past 6 months tolling ends**; **returned at 6-month boundary still tolled**; **general 3-year rule note present**; **6-year exception note lists enumerated offenses**; **Toussie continuing-offense note present** (397 U.S. 112 (1970) + LAST affirmative act); **DOJ Manual + IRM note present** (В§ 7.00 + IRM 25.6.2.1 + JURISDICTIONAL); **citation pins authorities** (В§ 6531 + В§ 6531(4) + Toussie + DOJ + IRM); **nine 6-year offenses truth table** (9-offense sweep); **three-year offenses truth table** (8-offense sweep); **SOL boundary at exactly 3 years expired**; **SOL boundary at exactly 6 years expired**; **final paragraph tolling note present when absent**; **tolling disengages when no absence**.

`traderview-expense::section_6532` is the **IRC В§ 6532 periods of limitation on refund and wrongful-levy suits** module вҖ” three-prong procedural SOL statute covering taxpayer refund suits under В§ 7422, US erroneous refund recovery suits under В§ 7405, and third-party wrongful levy suits under В§ 7426. Trader-critical because every refund-suit scenario (NOL В§ 172/В§ 475(f) MTM carryback claim, В§ 1256 60/40 amended return, В§ 1091 wash-sale recomputation, В§ 988 currency loss restatement) and every third-party broker-account wrongful-levy scenario pushes through these SOLs. Companion to `section_7422` (taxpayer refund suit), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), `section_7405` (IRS erroneous refund recovery), `section_6511` (refund claim filing SOL), `section_6343` (release of levy + administrative wrongful levy claim).

**В§ 6532(a) Taxpayer refund suits вҖ” six-month floor + two-year ceiling**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 6532(a)(1) | No suit BEFORE 6 months from claim filing UNLESS Secretary renders decision within that time; NOR AFTER 2 years from date of certified/registered mail notice of disallowance |
| В§ 6532(a)(2) | 2-year period may be EXTENDED by written agreement between taxpayer and Secretary |
| В§ 6532(a)(3) | Reconsideration / further IRS action after disallowance does NOT extend SOL |
| В§ 6532(a)(4) | If taxpayer files written waiver of certified-mail requirement, 2-year period runs from FILING OF WAIVER |

Pinned by `taxpayer_refund_under_6_months_no_decision_floor_violated` (179 days unlifted), `taxpayer_refund_at_180_days_floor_lifted` (180 days exact lifts), `taxpayer_refund_irs_early_decision_lifts_floor`, `taxpayer_refund_at_730_days_post_disallowance_compliant` (2-year exact boundary compliant), `taxpayer_refund_at_731_days_post_disallowance_expired` (1-day-past boundary fail), `taxpayer_refund_section_a2_written_extension_adds_days` (365-day extension вҶ’ 1095-day effective SOL), `taxpayer_refund_section_a4_waiver_runs_from_waiver_filing` (waiver overrides disallowance clock).

**В§ 6532(b) US erroneous refund suits under В§ 7405 вҖ” 2-year standard / 5-year fraud**:

| Trigger | SOL |
|---------|-----|
| Standard | **2 years (730 days)** from making of refund |
| Refund induced by FRAUD or MISREPRESENTATION of material fact | **5 years (1,825 days)** from making of refund |

Pinned by `us_erroneous_refund_at_730_days_compliant`, `us_erroneous_refund_at_731_days_expired` (1-day boundary precision), `us_erroneous_refund_fraud_extends_to_1825` (1800-day fraud-engaged compliant), `us_erroneous_refund_fraud_at_1826_days_expired`, `effective_sol_truth_table_us_suits` (2-cell sweep).

**В§ 6532(c) Third-party wrongful levy suits under В§ 7426 вҖ” TCJA 2017 extension**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 6532(c)(1) pre-TCJA | 9 MONTHS (270 days) from date of levy |
| В§ 6532(c)(1) post-TCJA | **2 YEARS (730 days)** from date of levy вҖ” TCJA 2017 В§ 11071 (Pub. L. 115-97, effective for levies made after December 22, 2017) |
| В§ 6532(c)(2) | If В§ 6343(b) administrative request filed, period may be EXTENDED to SOONER of (A) 12 months from В§ 6343(b) filing OR (B) 6 months from IRS disallowance |

**Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 В§ 11071 (Pub. L. 115-97, December 22, 2017)** extended В§ 6532(c)(1) and В§ 6343(b) wrongful-levy SOL from 9 months to 2 years вҖ” applies to levies made after December 22, 2017, AND levies on or before that date if 9-month period had NOT expired under В§ 6343(b). Pinned by `wrongful_levy_post_tcja_730_days_compliant`, `wrongful_levy_post_tcja_731_days_expired`, `wrongful_levy_pre_tcja_270_days_compliant` (9-month exact compliant), `wrongful_levy_pre_tcja_271_days_expired`, `wrongful_levy_tcja_extension_unique_invariant` (post-TCJA 730 > pre-TCJA 270).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6532`. Thirty-one tests pin: **taxpayer refund baseline timely**; **taxpayer refund under 6 months no decision floor violated** (179 days); **taxpayer refund at 180 days floor lifted** (exact boundary); **taxpayer refund IRS early decision lifts floor**; **taxpayer refund at 730 days post-disallowance compliant**; **taxpayer refund at 731 days post-disallowance expired** (1-day precision); **taxpayer refund section (a)(2) written extension adds days** (365 вҶ’ 1095 effective SOL); **taxpayer refund section (a)(4) waiver runs from waiver filing**; **US erroneous refund at 730 days compliant**; **US erroneous refund at 731 days expired**; **US erroneous refund fraud extends to 1825**; **US erroneous refund fraud at 1826 days expired**; **wrongful levy post-TCJA 730 days compliant**; **wrongful levy post-TCJA 731 days expired**; **wrongful levy pre-TCJA 270 days compliant**; **wrongful levy pre-TCJA 271 days expired**; **wrongful levy TCJA extension unique invariant** (730 > 270); **citation pins all subsections and TCJA**; **note pins subsection (a)(1) 6-month floor 2-year ceiling**; **note pins subsection (a)(2) written extension**; **note pins subsection (a)(3) reconsideration does not extend**; **note pins subsection (a)(4) waiver runs from waiver filing**; **note pins subsection (b) 2-year 5-year fraud**; **note pins subsection (c)(1) TCJA 2-year**; **note pins subsection (c)(2) В§ 6343(b) 12-month 6-month**; **note pins TCJA 2017 В§ 11071**; **note pins IRM and Pub. 4528**; **suit type truth table three cells** (3-prong sweep); **defensive zero days all suits timely**; **effective SOL truth table US suits** (2-cell sweep); **taxpayer floor lifted at exactly 180 days**.

`traderview-expense::section_6601` is the **IRC В§ 6601 interest on underpayment / В§ 6621 rate determination / В§ 6622 daily compounding** module вҖ” when an amended return or audit produces additional tax due, В§ 6601 interest accrues from the ORIGINAL DUE DATE of the return (April 15 for most individuals) regardless of any extension to FILE, and continues to compound daily until full payment. Trader-relevant for amended returns, deficiency assessments, and any underpayment scenario.

**Three statutory layers compose the interest computation**:

| Layer | Statute | Mechanic |
|-------|---------|----------|
| Interest accrual | В§ 6601(a) + В§ 6601(b)(1) | Runs from last date prescribed for payment to date paid; extension to FILE does not extend time to PAY |
| Rate determination | В§ 6621(a)(2) + В§ 6621(c) | Federal short-term rate + 3% (general) or + 5% (large corporate underpayment > $100K) |
| Compounding | В§ 6622(a) | Daily compounding (pre-1/1/1983 was simple interest) |

**Quarterly published rates (2026)**:

| Quarter | Authority | Underpayment | Large Corporate |
|---------|-----------|---------------|------------------|
| Q1 2026 | Rev. Rul. 2025-22 | 7% (700 bps) | 9% (900 bps) |
| Q2 2026 | Rev. Rul. 2026-5 | 6% (600 bps) | 8% (800 bps) |

**Daily compounding under В§ 6622(a) beats simple interest after a full year.** At 7% on $10,000 over 365 days, simple interest = $700; daily-compounded interest вүҲ $725 (~$25 incremental due to compounding). Pinned by `daily_compounding_beats_simple_interest_after_full_year` (asserts > $700) and `q1_2026_individual_underpayment_rate_700_bps` (range $700.00 - $730.00). Pinned by `ten_year_compound_at_700_bps_doubles_principal_roughly` вҖ” $1,000 Г— (1 + 0.07/365)^3650 вүҲ $2,014 (interest вүҲ $1,014).

**В§ 6621(c) large corporate underpayment carries a 2-percentage-point premium over the individual rate.** Same quarter, same federal short-term rate base вҖ” the only difference is the +3% vs +5% adjustment. Pinned by `q1_2026_large_corp_2_percentage_point_premium_over_individual` (delta = 200 bps).

**Q1 вҶ’ Q2 2026 rates dropped 100 bps across the board** (individual: 7% вҶ’ 6%; large corporate: 9% вҶ’ 8%). Pinned by `q1_to_q2_2026_rate_dropped_100_bps_individual` and `q1_to_q2_2026_rate_dropped_100_bps_large_corporate`.

**`rate_override_bps` bypasses the RATE_TABLE for years not yet published.** When the caller supplies an override, the lookup is bypassed and a note surfaces `"rate override applied"`. When RATE_TABLE has no entry and no override is provided (e.g., 2027 Q4), the module returns zero interest with an explanatory note instructing the caller to supply rate_override_bps. Pinned by `rate_override_bypasses_table`, `missing_year_no_override_returns_zero_interest_with_note`, `six_percent_rate_q2_2026_round_trip_with_override`.

**В§ 6621(c) note only surfaces on large-corporate path.** Pinned by `note_for_large_corporate_path_pins_section_6621c` and `note_for_individual_path_omits_section_6621c`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6601`. Twenty-four tests pin: **zero principal zero interest** (defensive); **zero days zero interest**; **Q1 2026 individual underpayment rate 700 bps** ($700-$730 daily-compounded on $10K over 365 days); **Q2 2026 individual underpayment rate 600 bps**; **Q1 2026 large corporate underpayment rate 900 bps** ($1M principal $90K-$95K interest); **Q2 2026 large corporate underpayment rate 800 bps**; **rate override bypasses table** (500 bps override); **missing year no override returns zero interest with note** (2027 Q4); **daily compounding beats simple interest after full year**; **daily compounding at 30 days matches approximate floor** ($10K Г— 7% Г— 30/365 вүҲ $57); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 6601(a)/(b)(1)/(c)/(e)(1)/(f) + В§ 6621(a)(2)/(c) + В§ 6622(a) + Rev. Rul. 2025-22 + Rev. Rul. 2026-5); **note describes quarter and rate** ("2026 Q1" + "700 bps"); **note for large corporate path pins В§ 6621(c)** (+ "federal short-term + 5%" + "30 days after IRS notice"); **note for individual path omits В§ 6621(c)**; **Q1 2026 large corp 2-percentage-point premium over individual** (delta = 200 bps); **Q1 to Q2 2026 rate dropped 100 bps individual**; **Q1 to Q2 2026 rate dropped 100 bps large corporate**; **negative principal clamped to zero** (defensive); **lookup_rate returns None for missing year**; **lookup_rate returns Some for 2026 Q1**; **note includes daily compounding message when days positive**; **note omits daily compounding when days zero**; **ten-year compound at 700 bps doubles principal roughly** ($1K вҶ’ ~$2K interest); **6% rate Q2 2026 round trip with override**.

`traderview-expense::section_6611` is the **IRC В§ 6611 interest on overpayments** module вҖ” natural companion to `section_6601` (interest on underpayments) and `section_6511` (refund claim limitations). When the IRS owes the taxpayer interest on a refund вҖ” e.g., overpayment from an amended return (Form 1040-X), NOL carryback, missed В§ 901 FTC, or refund delay beyond the 45-day safe harbor вҖ” В§ 6611 governs the interest computation. Interest received is GROSS INCOME to the taxpayer under В§ 61(a)(4) and reported on Form 1099-INT.

**Three statutory layers**:

| Layer | Authority | Mechanic |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| Interest accrual | В§ 6611(a) + В§ 6611(b)(1)/(b)(2) | From date of overpayment to 30 days before refund check (refund) or due date of credited amount (credit) |
| 45-day safe harbor | В§ 6611(e)(1)/(e)(2)/(e)(3) | (1) Refund within 45 days of return-due/return-filing вҶ’ ZERO interest; (2) Refund within 45 days of claim filing вҶ’ ZERO interest for claim-period; (3) IRS-initiated adjustment SUBTRACTS 45 days from interest period |
| Rate determination | В§ 6621(a)(1) | Individual: FST + 3%; Corporate: FST + 2%; Corporate > $10K (GATT): FST + 0.5% |

**Three taxpayer-type rates per quarter**:

| Quarter | Individual | Corporate (вүӨ $10K) | Corporate > $10K (GATT) |
|---------|------------|---------------------|--------------------------|
| Q1 2026 (Rev. Rul. 2025-22; FST 4%) | 7% (700 bps) | 6% (600 bps) | 4.5% (450 bps) |
| Q2 2026 (Rev. Rul. 2026-5; FST 3%) | 6% (600 bps) | 5% (500 bps) | 3.5% (350 bps) |

**The GATT rate is the load-bearing corporate carve-out.** Enacted by Pub. L. 103-465 В§ 713 (1994 GATT Implementation Act), В§ 6621(a)(1)(B) reduces the interest paid on the portion of a corporate overpayment exceeding $10,000 to federal short-term + 0.5% вҖ” 1.5 percentage points BELOW the regular corporate overpayment rate. This is the lowest IRS-paid rate of any kind. Pinned by `q1_2026_corporate_gatt_rate_450_bps` (Q1 2026 GATT = 450 bps vs Corp Regular = 600 bps), `corporate_gatt_one_percentage_point_below_corporate_regular_invariant` (delta = 150 bps), and `note_describes_corporate_path_no_gatt_note_unless_over_10k`.

**В§ 6611(e)(1) 45-day safe harbor is the IRS's "free 45-day window" to issue refunds.** When a refund is paid within 45 days of the return-due date (for timely-filed returns) or within 45 days of the return-filing date (for late-filed returns), NO interest is allowed. The clock starts at the LATER of the return-due date and the actual return-filing date. Pinned by `forty_five_day_safe_harbor_zero_interest`, `forty_five_day_refund_claim_safe_harbor_zero_interest` (parallel В§ 6611(e)(2) safe harbor for refund claims), and `safe_harbor_dominates_all_other_inputs` (safe harbor overrides all other factors).

**В§ 6611(e)(3) IRS-initiated adjustment subtracts 45 days from the interest period.** When the IRS-not the taxpayer-initiates the adjustment producing the refund, 45 days are subtracted from the interest accrual period. Combined with the 30-day refund-stop date under В§ 6611(b)(2), the effective period reduction is 75 days. Pinned by `irs_initiated_adjustment_45_day_subtraction` and `irs_initiated_short_period_clamped_to_zero` (60-day period вҶ’ 60 - 30 - 45 = -15 clamps to 0).

**Q1 вҶ’ Q2 2026 dropped 100 bps across all three taxpayer types** (individual: 7% вҶ’ 6%; corporate: 6% вҶ’ 5%; GATT: 4.5% вҶ’ 3.5%). Pinned by `q1_to_q2_2026_dropped_100_bps_all_three_taxpayer_types`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6611`. Twenty-six tests pin: **45-day safe harbor zero interest** (В§ 6611(e)(1)); **45-day refund claim safe harbor zero interest** (В§ 6611(e)(2) Form 1040-X); **Q1 2026 individual overpayment rate 700 bps**; **Q1 2026 corporate regular overpayment rate 600 bps**; **Q1 2026 corporate GATT rate 450 bps** (Pub. L. 103-465 В§ 713); **Q2 2026 individual overpayment rate 600 bps**; **Q2 2026 corporate GATT rate 350 bps**; **corporate GATT one-percentage-point below corporate regular invariant** (delta = 150 bps); **individual rate one-percentage-point above corporate regular** (delta = 100 bps); **30-day refund stop date subtracted** (В§ 6611(b)(2)); **IRS-initiated adjustment 45-day subtraction** (В§ 6611(e)(3)); **short interest period clamped to zero** (20-day raw вҶ’ 0 effective after 30-day stop); **IRS-initiated short period clamped to zero** (60 - 30 - 45 = -15 clamps to 0); **rate override bypasses table**; **missing year no override zero interest with note**; **zero principal zero interest** (defensive); **negative principal clamped to zero**; **citation pins all subsections and authorities** (В§ 6611(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(e)(1)/(e)(2)/(e)(3) + В§ 6621(a)(1) + В§ 6622(a) + В§ 61(a)(4) + Pub. L. 103-465 В§ 713 + Rev. Rul. 2025-22 + Rev. Rul. 2026-5); **note describes individual path no GATT note**; **note describes corporate path no GATT note unless over $10K**; **note includes В§ 61(a)(4) gross income treatment**; **lookup_rate returns None for missing year**; **lookup_rate returns Some for 2026 Q1**; **Q1 to Q2 2026 dropped 100 bps all three taxpayer types**; **safe harbor dominates all other inputs**; **comparison underpayment versus overpayment corporate diff one pct**.

`traderview-expense::section_6651` is the **IRC В§6651 failure-to-file / failure-to-pay penalty module** вҖ” the most commonly asserted civil tax penalty. Two parallel tracks (late filing under В§6651(a)(1); late paying under В§6651(a)(2)) that interact via a reduction rule (В§6651(c)(1)) and a minimum-penalty floor for returns over 60 days late (В§6651(g)). Fraud triples the FTF rate via В§6651(f).

**Rate table**:

| Penalty path | Authority | Rate | Maximum |
|--------------|-----------|------|---------|
| Failure to file (standard) | В§6651(a)(1) | 5%/month | 25% (5 months) |
| Failure to file (FRAUD) | В§6651(f) | **15%/month** | **75%** (5 months) |
| Failure to pay (standard) | В§6651(a)(2) | 0.5%/month | 25% (50 months) |
| Failure to pay (installment-agreement) | В§6651(h) | **0.25%/month** | 25% |

**В§6651(c)(1) interaction rule**: when both FTF and FTP apply for the same month, the FTF penalty is reduced by the FTP penalty amount. Effect: 4.5%/month FTF + 0.5%/month FTP = 5%/month combined (NOT 5.5%/month). This is the most-mis-modeled IRS penalty mechanic.

**В§6651(g) minimum penalty for returns > 60 days late**: floor is the LESSER of:
- Inflation-adjusted statutory amount (Rev. Proc. 2024 = $485; 2025 = $510); module accepts caller-supplied year-agnostic value
- 100% of the tax required to be shown

**В§6651(h) installment-rate cut**: 0.25%/month FTP applies ONLY when BOTH (i) return was filed by due date including extensions AND (ii) a В§6159 installment agreement is in effect. Missing either prong defaults back to 0.5%/month.

**Reasonable-cause defense** (В§6651(a) flush language): no penalty when failure is due to reasonable cause and not willful neglect. **Statutorily unavailable for В§6651(f) fraud** вҖ” even a taxpayer who reasonably relied on professional advice still pays fraud-uplift FTF.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6651`. Twenty-three tests pin: **FTF 1 month 5%** ($100k Г— 5% = $5k); 3 months 15%; **FTF max capped at 25%** (100 months вҶ’ $25k); **FTP 1 month 0.5%** ($500); FTP max capped at 25% (100 months вҶ’ $25k); **installment-agreement + timely-filed-with-extension reduces FTP to 0.25%/month** ($1k for 4 months); **installment without timely-filing вҶ’ no reduction** (regression вҖ” BOTH prongs required); **combined FTF + FTP 1 month** ($5k gross FTF вҲ’ $500 reduction = $4,500 net + $500 FTP = $5k combined); 3-month combined ($15k); **fraud uplift 15%/month** ($15k for 1 month); **fraud capped at 75% / 5 months** ($75k); **fraud no В§6651(c)(1) reduction** (regression); **minimum-penalty triggered when return > 60 days late**; **minimum-penalty floor binds when normal penalty smaller** ($5k tax Г— 5% = $250 < $510 floor вҶ’ $510); **minimum-penalty capped at 100% tax** ($100 tax вҶ’ floor = $100, not $510); not-over-60-days no floor; **reasonable-cause zeros normal penalty**; **reasonable-cause does NOT zero fraud penalty** (regression target); **citation mentions all 10 relevant authorities** (В§6651(a)(1) + (a)(2) + (c)(1) + (f) + (g) + (h) + "5%/month" + "0.5%/month" + "15%/month" + "0.25%/month"); note includes installment path; note includes fraud path; note includes minimum-floor when triggered; **$1B precision** ($1B Г— 25% = $250M FTF + $25M FTP = $250M total).

`traderview-expense::section_6654` is the **IRC В§6654 individual estimated-tax underpayment penalty module** вҖ” the trader's nightmare. Traders take large gains in a single quarter and forget to make a same-quarter estimated payment; the IRS then computes a quarter-by-quarter penalty at the В§6621(a)(2) federal-short-term-rate-plus-3-points (2026 Q1 = 7%, Q2 = 6%) over the days each underpayment accrues. Two safe harbors avoid the penalty entirely. The 100%-prior-year safe harbor rises to **110%** when prior-year AGI exceeds $150,000 ($75,000 MFS) under В§6654(d)(1)(C).

**Safe harbors** (В§6654(d)(1)(B)):
- **90% of current-year tax** (В§6654(d)(1)(B)(i))
- **100% of prior-year tax** (В§6654(d)(1)(B)(ii)) вҖ” rises to **110%** under В§6654(d)(1)(C) when prior-year AGI > $150,000 ($75,000 MFS)

Required annual payment = lesser of the two. Per-quarter required installment = required Г· 4. The four installment due dates are Apr 15, Jun 15, Sep 15, Jan 15 (of the following year).

**De minimis exception** (В§6654(e)(1)): no penalty if current-year tax owed after withholding is under **$1,000**.

**Penalty formula**: for each quarter, underpayment = max(0, cumulative_required вҲ’ cumulative_paid); penalty = underpayment Г— rate Г— days Г· 365. The IRS sets the rate quarterly under В§6621(a)(2) = federal short-term + 3 percentage points.
