Withholding counts as estimated payments evenly across the year вҖ” the caller pre-splits withholding into the four `quarterly_payments_cents` buckets.

**Out of scope** (caller's responsibility): В§6654(d)(2) annualized-income exception (uneven income), В§6654(i) farmer/fisherman two-thirds-of-income rule, В§6654(e)(3) retired/disabled waiver.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6654`. Twenty-four tests pin: **de minimis $1,000 boundary** (tax = $999.99 вҶ’ exempt; tax = $1,000 вҶ’ above); **prior-year 100% safe harbor used when lower than 90%-current-year**; **high-AGI uplift triggers 110%** when prior-year AGI > $150K; **$150K boundary is NOT high** (regression вҖ” > strictly required, equal excluded); **$150K + 1 cent IS high** (regression target); **MFS uses $75K threshold not $150K**; **$75K MFS boundary not high** (regression); **MFJ uses $150K threshold not $75K** (regression вҖ” only MFS gets the cut); **current-year 90% used when lower than prior-year**; **even quarterly payments meeting required no penalty**; **skipped Q1 payment triggers Q1-only penalty** (Q2 catchup zeros Q2 underpayment regression); **penalty formula matches rate Г— days Г· 365** (formula correctness regression); **variable quarterly rates compound into total**; **overpaying Q1 carries overpayment forward** (cumulative-required method); **under-paying throughout year вҶ’ full annual underpayment by Q4**; **zero prior-year tax** clamps prior-year safe harbor to zero (model-limitation pin); **rate = 0 вҶ’ no penalty even with underpayment**; **zero days вҶ’ no penalty**; **negative tax clamped to zero**; **high-AGI meeting 110% installments вҶ’ safe harbor met**; **high-AGI under 110% вҶ’ safe harbor missed + final underpayment = $8,000**; **citation pins В§6654 + Form 2210**; **note mentions Safe harbor MET / MISSED**.

`traderview-expense::section_6662` is the **IRC В§6662 accuracy-related penalty module** вҖ” the most-litigated taxpayer penalty in the Code. Routinely asserted against active traders whose returns the IRS audits. **20% baseline** on the portion of underpayment attributable to misconduct, **40% for gross valuation misstatement**, with a no-stacking cap.

**В§6662(b) eight misconduct categories** (any of which triggers):

| Category | Sub-section | Penalty rate | В§6664(c) defense available? |
|----------|-------------|--------------|------------------------------|
| Negligence or disregard of rules/regs | В§6662(b)(1) | 20% | Yes |
| Substantial understatement of income tax | В§6662(b)(2) | 20% | Yes |
| Substantial valuation misstatement (вүҘ 150%) | В§6662(b)(3) | 20% | Yes |
| Gross valuation misstatement (вүҘ 200%) | В§6662(h) | **40%** | Yes |
| Pension liability overstatement | В§6662(b)(4) | 20% | Yes |
| Estate/gift valuation understatement | В§6662(b)(5) | 20% | Yes |
| В§7701(o) economic-substance failure | В§6662(b)(6) | 20% | **NO** |
| Undisclosed foreign financial asset | В§6662(b)(7) | 20% | **NO** |
| Inconsistent estate basis (В§6035) | В§6662(b)(8) | 20% | Yes |

**В§6662(d) substantial-understatement threshold**:

| Taxpayer | Threshold |
|----------|-----------|
| Individual | Exceeds greater of 10% of correct tax OR $5,000 |
| C corporation (non-S, non-PHC) | Exceeds greater of 10% of correct tax OR $10,000, **capped at $10,000,000** |

**В§6664(c) reasonable-cause-and-good-faith defense** is the primary escape hatch вҖ” but it's **statutorily unavailable** for В§6662(b)(6) (economic-substance failure) and В§6662(b)(7) (undisclosed foreign financial asset). For these two categories, even a taxpayer who reasonably relied on professional advice still pays.

**No stacking** (В§6662(a) flush language): when multiple categories trigger simultaneously, the higher-rate category wins вҖ” gross valuation (40%) beats substantial understatement (20%), and combining both still caps at 40%, not 60%.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6662`. Twenty-four tests pin: **individual threshold max(10% Г— correct_tax, $5k)** ($500k correct tax вҶ’ $50k threshold; $30k correct tax вҶ’ $5k floor); **corporate threshold $10k floor when correct tax low** ($50k correct вҶ’ $10k floor); **corporate threshold capped at $10M** ($1B correct вҶ’ 10% = $100M but capped at $10M); **negligence triggers 20%** ($10k Г— 20% = $2k); **substantial understatement triggers 20%** ($100k Г— 20% = $20k); **under-threshold no trigger**; **valuation at 150% substantial misstatement** (20%); 149% no trigger (strict вүҘ 150%); **valuation at 200% gross misstatement triggers 40% rate** ($100k underpayment Г— 40% = $40k); 300% still 40%; **economic substance failure has NO В§6664(c) defense** (regression target вҖ” even with reasonable cause asserted, penalty stands); **undisclosed foreign asset has NO В§6664(c) defense**; reasonable cause zeros substantial understatement penalty; reasonable cause zeros negligence; reasonable cause zeros gross-valuation 40% penalty (when available); **gross valuation beats substantial understatement for rate** (highest-rate wins); **no stacking** вҖ” negligence + gross valuation still capped at 40% (not 60%); no trigger вҶ’ zero penalty; **citation mentions all 9 relevant authorities** (В§6662(a) + (h) + (b) + (d) + В§6664(c) + В§7701(o) + 200% + 150% + "no stacking"); note describes "EXCEEDED" when threshold breached; note says "UNAVAILABLE" for economic-substance defense; **$1B precision** ($1B Г— 20% = $200M); **$500M underpayment gross valuation precision** ($500M Г— 40% = $200M).

`traderview-expense::section_170` is the **IRC В§170 charitable contribution deduction module (individual)** вҖ” addresses the broad В§170(b) per-category AGI ceilings + the NEW OBBBA-imposed 0.5% AGI floor effective 2026. Sibling to `section_170e` (built-in-gain ordinary-income reduction for appreciated property). This module addresses the percentage-of-AGI limits and the floor; `section_170e` addresses the FMV-vs-basis amount calculation for property donations.

**Per-category AGI ceilings** (В§ 170(b)(1)):

| Category | Ceiling | Citation |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Cash to public charity | **60% AGI** (made PERMANENT by OBBBA) | В§ 170(b)(1)(G) |
| Non-cash to public charity | 50% AGI | В§ 170(b)(1)(A) |
| Capital-gain property to public OR cash to 30%-limit org | 30% AGI | В§ 170(b)(1)(B)/(C) |
| Capital-gain property to private foundation | 20% AGI | В§ 170(b)(1)(D) |

**OBBBA 2026 changes** (eff. tax years beginning after 2025-12-31):

| Change | Mechanic | Citation |
|--------|----------|----------|
| **0.5% AGI floor for itemizers** | Aggregate charitable deduction reduced by 0.5% Г— AGI; blocked amount carries forward 5 years | В§ 170(b)(1)(I), OBBBA В§ 70425 |
| **Non-itemizer above-the-line deduction** | $1,000 single / $2,000 MFJ for CASH to public charity only | В§ 170(p), OBBBA eff. 2026 |
| **60% cash ceiling made permanent** | Was scheduled to revert to 50% | В§ 170(b)(1)(G) |

**5-year carryforward** under В§170(d)(1): both ceiling-blocked AND floor-blocked amounts carry forward 5 succeeding tax years.

**$500K-AGI itemizer worked example** (Blue J): $500K AGI, $10K cash to public charity. 60% cap = $300K (not binding). 0.5% floor = $2,500. Allowed = $10K вҲ’ $2,500 = $7,500 deduction + $2,500 carryforward.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-170`. Twenty tests pin: **itemizer 2026 cash under 0.5% floor вҶ’ no deduction** (regression вҖ” all blocked + full carryforward); **itemizer 2026 cash above floor partial deduction**; **itemizer 2025 no floor full deduction** (year-aware regression вҖ” pre-OBBBA); **itemizer 2026 above 60% cash cap carries forward** + 0.5% floor stacks on top; **itemizer 2026 capital-gain property 30% cap** + floor; **non-itemizer 2026 above-the-line $1,000 single** + В§ 170(p) citation; **non-itemizer 2026 max caps at $1,000 single**; **non-itemizer 2026 MFJ $2,000 max**; **non-itemizer 2025 no deduction** (regression вҖ” В§ 170(p) not yet effective); **itemizer 2026 at 0.5% boundary all blocked** (вүӨ strict); **itemizer 2026 just above floor $1 allowed** (off-by-one regression); **$500K AGI worked example $7,500 allowed**; **$500K AGI 2025 no floor full $10K**; **MFJ QW uses joint $2,000 max**; **HoH uses single $1,000 max** (regression вҖ” HoH not granted MFJ-level); **negative inputs clamped**; **zero AGI zero floor**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 170(b)(1) + OBBBA В§ 70425 + В§ 170(b)(1)(I) + В§ 170(d)(1) + В§ 170(p)); **ceiling-then-floor both carry forward**; **OBBBA floor effective only 2026+** (2-year Г— 3-year multi-year boundary invariant вҖ” 2024/2025 no floor; 2026/2027/2030 floor applies).

`traderview-expense::section_170e` is the **IRC В§170(e) appreciated-property charitable contribution module** вҖ” the single highest-frequency tax-planning move for successful traders. Donate winners to charity, deduct FMV (or basis on specific paths), pay NO capital gain tax on the embedded appreciation. Independent of В§1091 wash sale (gifts aren't sales, no replacement-period concern).

**Six rule paths** cover every combination of property kind Г— charity type Г— basis-election flag:

| Path                                            | Contribution = | AGI cap | Citation                          |
|-------------------------------------------------|----------------|---------|-----------------------------------|
| LTCG вҶ’ public charity (no election)             | **FMV**        | 30%     | В§170(b)(1)(C)(i)                  |
| LTCG вҶ’ public charity (basis election)          | basis          | 50%     | В§170(b)(1)(C)(iii)                |
| LTCG вҶ’ private foundation (qualified appreciated stock) | **FMV** | 20%     | В§170(e)(5)                        |
| LTCG вҶ’ private foundation (not QAS)             | basis          | 20%     | В§170(e)(1)(B)(ii)                 |
| STCG / Ordinary вҶ’ public charity                | basis          | 50%     | В§170(e)(1)(A)                     |
| STCG / Ordinary вҶ’ private foundation            | basis          | 30%     | В§170(e)(1)(A)                     |
| Tangible personal unrelated use вҶ’ public        | basis          | 50%     | В§170(e)(1)(B)(i)                  |
| Tangible personal unrelated use вҶ’ private       | basis          | 30%     | В§170(e)(1)(B)(i)                  |

**В§170(e)(5) Qualified Appreciated Stock (QAS) carve-out is load-bearing for family foundations.** Publicly-traded stock not exceeding 10% of the corporation's outstanding shares qualifies for FMV deduction even to a private foundation, despite the general В§170(e)(1)(B)(ii) reduction rule. This is why family foundations love receiving Apple / Microsoft / Berkshire-Hathaway shares but not closely-held LLC interests. Pinned by `ltcg_private_foundation_qas_fmv_deduction_at_20pct` ($100k FMV вҶ’ $40k deductible at 20% AGI cap, $60k carries forward, $90k gain eliminated).

**В§170(b)(1)(C)(iii) basis election trades deduction amount for AGI capacity.** Same $100k FMV / $10k basis LTCG stock to public charity:
- No election: contribution $100k, 30% cap = $60k deductible, $40k carryforward, $90k gain eliminated
- With election: contribution $10k, 50% cap = $100k, full $10k deductible, $0 carryforward, $0 gain eliminated

The election is rational when basis is close to FMV (gain elimination matters less) or when the donor needs the higher AGI limit. Pinned in `ltcg_public_basis_election_lower_deduction_higher_cap`.

**В§170(e)(1)(A) STCG/ordinary reduction is the "don't donate winners you've held < 1 year" trap.** The reduction wipes out the embedded gain entirely, so the deduction is basis only. This is exactly the same answer as the basis election вҖ” but you didn't choose it, you got it by holding < 1 year. Pinned by `stcg_property_reduced_to_basis_under_170e1a` and `ordinary_income_property_same_reduction_as_stcg`.

**В§170(e)(1)(B)(i) tangible personal unrelated use** hits the "donate art to a hospital" case вҖ” the donor must determine whether the donee's use of the property is RELATED to the donee's exempt purpose. Art to a museum that displays it: related, FMV deduction available. Art to a hospital that sells it at auction: unrelated, basis only. The compute does not adjudicate the related/unrelated determination вҖ” caller flips the `PropertyKind::TangiblePersonalUnrelatedUse` discriminator when the unrelated test fails.

**Branch ordering between flags is load-bearing.** When both `is_qualified_appreciated_stock = true` AND `elect_basis_for_higher_limit = true` on a public-charity LTCG donation, the basis election wins (QAS is irrelevant for public charity). Pinned by `qas_with_basis_election_election_wins`. Conversely, the QAS flag is silently ignored on public-charity donations (`qas_flag_ignored_for_public_charity_path`).

**В§170(d) 5-year carryforward** with same-character bucketing. Prior carryover adds to the current contribution before the AGI cap check; excess rolls to next year. Caller must track buckets separately if straddling rule paths (e.g., LTCG and STCG carryforwards don't pool).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-170e`. Twenty-three tests pin: canonical LTCG-public-FMV path with all numbers spelled out ($100k вҶ’ $60k deduct + $40k CF + $90k gain eliminated); basis election trade-off; STCG and ordinary income same reduction; LTCG-private-foundation QAS at 20% cap; non-QAS reduces to basis; tangible unrelated use to both public (50%) and private (30%); prior carryover compounds against current cap; other-this-year contributions eat budget; **zero AGI вҶ’ full carryforward**; contribution exactly at cap вҶ’ 0 carryforward; **other contributions exceeding cap clamp remaining at 0** (negative-budget regression target); **FMV below basis no gain eliminated reports 0 not negative** (the underwater-stock no-bonus case); basis election flag ignored for STCG; QAS flag ignored for public-charity path; QAS+election combo вҶ’ election wins (branch ordering pinned); note describes rule path citation + cap pct; QAS path note mentions В§170(e)(5); very large donation no precision loss ($9.87B basis with $20B AGI); multi-year roll picks up prior carryforward only (zero new contribution case); **carryforward never negative under pathological negative input**; private-foundation STCG uses 30% cap not 20% (rule Г— charity-type interaction).

`traderview-expense::section_457a` is the **IRC В§ 457A вҖ” Nonqualified Deferred Compensation From Certain Tax-Indifferent Parties module** (iter 494) вҖ” direct hedge-fund / PE / fund-manager compensation companion to `section_409a` (NQDC general regime вҖ” applies IN ADDITION per В§ 457A(d)(4)), `section_457b` (governmental/tax-exempt NQDC вҖ” iter 450), `section_280g` (golden parachute on change in control), `section_421` (statutory stock options вҖ” iter 492), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464). Enacted October 3, 2008 in Emergency Economic Stabilization Act В§ 801; effective for amounts attributable to services performed after 2008-12-31. **Trader-critical because** В§ 457A targets the post-Bear-Stearns hedge-fund Cayman / BVI master-feeder structure where US fund managers traditionally deferred carry plus incentive fees through the offshore feeder; В§ 457A bars deferral at the offshore-feeder level when the entity is a "nonqualified entity."

**Three nonqualified-entity tests** per В§ 457A(b): (1) **foreign corporation** UNLESS substantially all income is either (A) effectively connected with US trade/business OR (B) subject to comprehensive foreign income tax; (2) **partnership** UNLESS substantially all income is allocated to persons other than (A) foreign persons not subject to comprehensive foreign income tax AND (B) US tax-exempt organizations; (3) Treasury determination per В§ 457A(d)(3) of what constitutes "comprehensive foreign income tax."

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (pre-2009 services included by 2017 per В§ 457A(e)), NotANonqualifiedEntity (domestic C-corp / S-corp / qualifying foreign corp / qualifying partnership вҖ” В§ 409A alone), StockRightExemptUnder409a (NSO or stock-settled SAR meeting В§ 409A exempt requirements per Notice 2009-8 Q&A 2 + Rev. Rul. 2014-18), SrofPresentDeferralAllowed (future-performance-of-substantial-services SROF satisfied per В§ 457A(d)(2) вҖ” stricter than В§ 409A SROF), NoSrofIncomeImmediatelyIncludible (deferred amount fully includible at SROF lapse + ordinary-rate federal tax), AmountNotDeterminablePending20PctPlusInterest (В§ 457A(c)(1)(B)(i)вҖ“(ii) вҖ” 20% additional tax on includible amount PLUS interest at AFR + 1% from year of SROF lapse to year of inclusion), Pre2009TransitionViolationMissed2017Window (pre-2009 deferral NOT included by last tax year beginning before 2017 вҖ” amended-return remediation required).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-457a`. Thirty-three tests pin: **domestic C-corp not nonqualified entity**; **domestic S-corp not nonqualified entity**; **foreign corp with comprehensive foreign tax not nonqualified**; **foreign corp ECI not nonqualified**; **partnership mostly taxable US partners not nonqualified**; **Cayman offshore feeder IS nonqualified entity** (classic master-feeder structure); **partnership tax-indifferent allocations IS nonqualified**; **В§ 409A-exempt stock option safe harbor** (Notice 2009-8 Q&A 2 + Rev. Rul. 2014-18); **В§ 409A-exempt stock-settled SAR safe harbor**; **pre-2009 service included by 2017 compliant** (В§ 457A(e) transition); **pre-2009 service not included by 2017 transition violation** (amended returns required); **2008 boundary still treated as pre-2009**; **SROF present defers inclusion** (future-performance-of-substantial-services); **no SROF immediate inclusion** (ordinary federal tax computation); **amount not determinable triggers 20% plus interest** (В§ 457A(c)(1)(B)(i)вҖ“(ii)); **amount not determinable same year zero interest** (boundary); **20% additional tax constant pins В§ 457A(c)(1)(B)(i)**; **AFR + 1% interest constant pins В§ 457A(c)(1)(B)(ii)**; **effective year pins 2009**; **transition last year pins 2017**; **coordination with В§ 409A pinned** (В§ 457A(d)(4) вҖ” both apply); **coordination references В§ 457(b) and В§ 280G**; **citation pins В§ 457A subsections (a) + (d)(2)**; **amount not determinable citation pins В§ 457A(c)(1)(B)**; **pre-2009 transition citation pins Notice 2009-8 + В§ 457A(e)**; **stock-option safe harbor citation pins Rev. Rul. 2014-18 + Notice 2009-8 Q&A 2**; **full tax burden sums components** (federal + 20% + interest); **u64::MAX/4 deferral overflow defense** (u128 intermediate); **zero deferral zero tax** (boundary); **Cayman master-feeder hedge-fund classic case** ($50M deferral вҶ’ $18.5M federal at 37% ordinary rate); **partnership tax-indifferent with phantom equity triggers В§ 457A**; **SROF failed metric only treated as no SROF**; **amount not determinable pending recommends restructure to determinable form**.

`traderview-expense::section_457b` is the **IRC В§ 457(b) Governmental and Tax-Exempt Deferred Compensation Plans module** вҖ” direct trader companion to `section_401k` (iter 448), `section_408` (traditional IRA вҖ” iter 432), `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” iter 430), `section_4973` (excess contribution excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_72t` (10% early-withdrawal penalty), `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp deduction вҖ” iter 446). Trader-critical because public-sector and nonprofit traders can stack В§ 457(b) on top of В§ 401(k) or В§ 403(b) for substantial **DOUBLE DEFERRAL strategy** вҖ” separate $24,500 elective deferral limits at each plan type under В§ 402(g)(1) non-aggregation rule.

**Two structurally distinct В§ 457(b) plan types**:

| Plan Type | Funding | В§ 72(t) Penalty | Rollovers | Credit Risk |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-----------|-------------|
| **GOVERNMENTAL** В§ 457(b) | В§ 457(g) TRUST for exclusive benefit | **NO 10% penalty** | PERMITTED to/from В§ 401(k), В§ 403(b), В§ 408 IRAs | None (trust protection) |
| **TAX-EXEMPT** В§ 457(b) | UNFUNDED "top-hat" general employer assets | **10% penalty applies** | NOT permitted to other plan types | **SUBSTANTIAL credit risk** вҖ” unsecured creditor in employer bankruptcy |

**2026 contribution limit framework (IRS Notice 2025-67)**:

| Limit | 2026 Amount | Eligibility |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| Elective deferral В§ 457(b)(2) | $24,500 | Both plan types |
| Age 50+ catch-up В§ 414(v) | $8,000 | **GOVERNMENTAL ONLY** |
| Ages 60-63 enhanced catch-up В§ 414(v)(2)(E) | $11,250 | **GOVERNMENTAL ONLY** (SECURE 2.0 В§ 109) |
| Special 3-year pre-retirement catch-up В§ 457(b)(3) | up to $49,000 (2Г— annual) | Both plan types |

**В§ 457(b)(3) special 3-year pre-retirement catch-up**:
- Available in 3 taxable years immediately preceding NORMAL RETIREMENT AGE specified by plan
- Maximum = lesser of (a) 2Г— annual limit ($49,000 for 2026); OR (b) underutilized contribution capacity from prior eligible years
- **ANTI-STACKING** вҖ” CANNOT be combined with age-50 catch-up under В§ 414(v) OR ages-60-63 enhanced catch-up; participant must choose ONE catch-up mechanism per year
- Available to BOTH governmental AND tax-exempt В§ 457(b) plans (unlike age-50 which is governmental-only)

**В§ 457(g) governmental trust requirement** вҖ” Small Business Jobs Protection Act of 1996 (Pub. L. 104-188) required governmental В§ 457(b) assets be held in TRUST for EXCLUSIVE BENEFIT of participants and beneficiaries effective January 1, 1999; protects from employer creditors.

**В§ 402(g)(1) non-aggregation rule (KEY ADVANTAGE)**:
- В§ 401(k) + В§ 403(b) plans aggregated under single $24,500 elective deferral limit
- В§ 457(b) **NOT AGGREGATED** вҖ” SEPARATE $24,500 limit
- **DOUBLE DEFERRAL STRATEGY** вҖ” governmental or nonprofit employee with both В§ 401(k)/В§ 403(b) AND В§ 457(b) can defer **UP TO $49,000 in 2026**

**В§ 457(d)(1) distribution triggers**: (1) separation from service (most common); (2) age 70ВҪ (now superseded by В§ 401(a)(9) RMD rules вҖ” age 73 for born 1951-1959, age 75 for born 1960+); (3) unforeseeable emergency under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.457-6(c); (4) plan termination; (5) В§ 457(d)(9) in-service distribution at 59ВҪ (added by SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 314 for emergency expenses).

**В§ 457(d)(2) rollover rules**:
- **Governmental В§ 457(b)** вҖ” rollovers PERMITTED to/from В§ 401(k), В§ 403(b), В§ 408 traditional IRAs, В§ 408A Roth IRAs (after tax inclusion); rolled-over amounts retain source plan's В§ 72(t) status
- **Tax-exempt В§ 457(b)** вҖ” rollovers NOT PERMITTED to other plan types; only to another tax-exempt В§ 457(b) within 60-day window

**Trader-critical fact patterns**:
1. State employee age 40 defers $24,500 В§ 457(b) + $24,500 В§ 401(k) = **$49,000 DOUBLE DEFERRAL** under В§ 402(g)(1) non-aggregation rule
2. State employee age 55 separates from service вҖ” governmental В§ 457(b) immediate distribution at any age WITHOUT В§ 72(t) penalty (vs В§ 401(k) requires rule-of-55 or 59ВҪ)
3. Tax-exempt nonprofit CFO age 62 maxes $24,500 only ($11,250 enhanced catch-up NOT available вҖ” В§ 414(v)(3) ineligibility)
4. State employee age 62 (within 3 years of NRA 65) invokes В§ 457(b)(3) special 3-year catch-up $49,000; cannot combine with $11,250 enhanced (anti-stacking)
5. Tax-exempt В§ 457(b) participant in employer Chapter 11 вҖ” UNFUNDED plan assets subject to ALL creditor claims; participant becomes **UNSECURED CREDITOR**

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-457b`. Thirty-four tests pin: **governmental max compliant**; **over $24,500 limit violation**; **governmental age 50 eligible for $8,000 catch-up**; **governmental ages 60-63 enhanced $11,250**; **governmental age 64+ reverts to $8,000**; **tax-exempt no age-50 catch-up eligibility** (В§ 414(v)(3)); **tax-exempt no enhanced catch-up eligibility**; **special 3-year catch-up doubles limit**; **special 3-year capped at 2Г— annual ($49,000)**; **special 3-year anti-stacking with catch-up violation**; **governmental no В§ 72(t) penalty**; **tax-exempt В§ 72(t) penalty applies**; **governmental rollover permitted**; **tax-exempt rollover not permitted**; **double deferral strategy disclosed** ($49,000); **tax-exempt employer distress credit risk engaged** (unsecured creditor); **governmental employer distress no credit risk** (trust protection); **separation from service disclosed**; **age bracket truth table five cells governmental**; **tax-exempt uniquely В§ 72(t) applies invariant**; **special 3-year uniquely doubles limit invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 457(a)-(g) + В§ 457(b)(2) + В§ 457(b)(3) + В§ 457(d) + В§ 457(g) + В§ 402(g)(1) + В§ 72(t) + В§ 414(v)(2)(E) + В§ 414(v)(3) + Pub. L. 104-188 + SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 314 + SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 109 + Pub. L. 117-328 + IRS Notice 2025-67 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.457-1); **note pins eligible deferred compensation plan**; **note pins two plan types (governmental vs tax-exempt)**; **note pins 2026 limits**; **note pins special 3-year anti-stacking**; **note pins В§ 457(g) trust requirement**; **note pins В§ 72(t) interaction**; **note pins В§ 402(g)(1) double deferral**; **note pins В§ 457(d)(1) distribution triggers**; **note pins В§ 457(d)(2) rollover rules**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_461g` is the **IRC В§ 461(g) prepaid interest deduction timing module** вҖ” direct trader companion to `section_163d` (investment interest limitation), `section_163h` (qualified residence interest), `section_163j` (business interest limit), `section_263a` (UNICAP capitalization), `section_481` (accounting method change), `section_469` (passive activity loss), `section_475c2` (trader mark-to-market), `section_162` (ordinary and necessary business expenses). Trader-critical because traders routinely prepay margin loan interest at end of taxable year, pay mortgage points on rental property acquisition, refinance personal residence mortgage, and acquire principal residence with points вҖ” В§ 461(g) governs the timing of each deduction.

В§ 461(g)(1) provides that a CASH-BASIS taxpayer must treat prepaid interest in the same manner as an ACCRUAL-BASIS taxpayer for purposes of deduction timing. Interest paid that is properly allocable to any period AFTER the close of the taxable year in which paid shall be **CHARGED TO CAPITAL ACCOUNT** and treated as paid in the period to which so allocable.

**В§ 461(g)(2) EXCEPTION вҖ” points on principal residence (5 conditions)**:
1. Points paid in connection with **PURCHASE OR IMPROVEMENT** of principal residence
2. **SECURED BY** principal residence (not investment property, not vacation home)
3. Points charged represent **ESTABLISHED PRACTICE** in geographic area
4. Points DO NOT **EXCEED** amount generally charged in geographic area
5. Points **COMPUTED AS PERCENTAGE OF PRINCIPAL** (not flat fees for appraisal, escrow, title)

All five conditions must be met for current-year deduction; otherwise points must be AMORTIZED over loan life.

**В§ 461(g)(2) refinancing exclusion** вҖ” points paid on REFINANCING of existing mortgage NOT eligible for current deduction even if same principal residence; must be AMORTIZED over loan life (Rev. Rul. 87-22 + IRS Pub. 936). Exception: refinancing proceeds used for IMPROVEMENTS to residence may qualify pro rata.

**В§ 461(g) application to rental property**:
- Mortgage points on rental property purchase or refinance = PREPAID INTEREST under В§ 461(g)(1)
- MUST be AMORTIZED over loan life via straight-line method (Rev. Rul. 70-540)
- Annual deduction = total points / loan term in years
- Remaining unamortized points at loan payoff deductible in year of payoff (Schedule E rental income)
- В§ 461(g)(2) EXCEPTION does NOT apply to rental property (residence requirement)

**В§ 461(g) application to margin loan interest**:
- Year-end prepayment of margin interest does NOT accelerate deduction
- Interest allocable to next year DEFERRED under В§ 461(g)(1)
- В§ 163(d) investment interest limitation applies separately (deductible only up to net investment income; excess carried forward indefinitely under В§ 163(d)(2))
- В§ 475(f) trader-status election may reclassify margin interest from В§ 163(d) investment interest to В§ 162 business interest expense subject to В§ 163(j) business interest limit

**Rev. Proc. 94-27 seller-paid points** вҖ” seller-paid points on principal residence purchase TREATED AS PAID BY BUYER under industry practice; buyer-deductible in year of purchase under В§ 461(g)(2); buyer's basis in residence REDUCED by seller-paid points to avoid double benefit; seller CANNOT also deduct.

**В§ 461(g) interaction with В§ 163(h) qualified residence interest** вҖ” В§ 163(h)(3) qualified residence interest includes ACQUISITION INDEBTEDNESS plus HOME EQUITY INDEBTEDNESS; TCJA 2017 suspended home equity interest deduction unless used for residence improvements through 2025 (extended by OBBBA 2025); В§ 461(g)(2) current-deduction exception applies WITHIN В§ 163(h)(3) framework.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) trader purchases personal residence $1M with $30K points вҖ” В§ 461(g)(2) EXCEPTION applies; FULL $30,000 DEDUCTIBLE in year of purchase under В§ 163(h)(3); (2) refinances personal residence $1M with $20K points for lower interest rate вҖ” В§ 461(g)(2) EXCLUSION; must AMORTIZE over loan life ($20K / 30 = $667 annual); (3) purchases $1M rental property with $25K points вҖ” В§ 461(g)(2) does NOT apply (rental not residence); AMORTIZE over 30 years ($833 annual) on Schedule E; (4) prepays $50K margin loan interest December 30 for Q1 next year вҖ” В§ 461(g)(1) APPLIES; only December portion deductible current year; (5) В§ 475(f) trader-status election reclassifies margin interest from В§ 163(d) to В§ 162 business interest subject to В§ 163(j) limit.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-461g`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **principal residence purchase exception applies**; **principal residence not established practice no exception**; **principal residence excessive no exception**; **principal residence flat fee no exception**; **principal residence refinance amortization required** (Rev. Rul. 87-22); **principal residence refinance for improvements full deduction**; **rental property points amortization required** (Rev. Rul. 70-540 + Schedule E); **rental property 30-year loan $833/year**; **margin loan interest prepayment deferred** (В§ 163(d) + net investment income); **margin loan with В§ 475(f) election reclassifies** (В§ 162 + В§ 163(j)); **business loan interest prepayment deferred** (В§ 163(j)); **seller-paid points treated as buyer-paid** (Rev. Proc. 94-27); **category truth table six cells**; **principal residence uniquely qualifies for current deduction invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 461(g)(1) + В§ 461(g)(2) + В§ 163(d) + В§ 163(h) + В§ 163(h)(3) + В§ 163(j) + В§ 263A + В§ 162 + В§ 475(f) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.461-1 + В§ 1.461-5 + В§ 1.163-10T + В§ 1.263A-1 + Rev. Rul. 87-22 + Rev. Rul. 70-540 + Rev. Proc. 94-27 + Pub. L. 115-97 TCJA + Pub. L. 119-21 OBBBA + IRS Pub. 936 + IRS Topic 504); **note pins В§ 461(g) overview**; **note pins В§ 461(g)(1) general rule**; **note pins В§ 461(g)(2) five conditions**; **note pins refinancing exclusion Rev. `traderview-expense::section_461l` is the **IRC В§461(l) excess business loss limitation module** вҖ” **completes the loss-limitation cascade** for individual, trust, and estate taxpayers:

| Order | Section          | Limit                                | Module                |
|-------|------------------|--------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| 1     | В§704(d)          | Partner outside basis                | section_704d          |
| 2     | В§465             | At-risk amount                       | section_465           |
| 3     | В§469             | Passive activity loss                | section_469           |
| 4     | **В§461(l)**      | **Excess business loss**             | **section_461l** (this) |

Each limit applies to the loss SURVIVING the prior limit. В§461(l) is the final cap before deductibility.

**В§461(l)(1)** disallows the portion of a noncorporate taxpayer's net business loss exceeding the statutory threshold. The disallowed portion becomes a В§172 NOL carryforward (no carryback).

**В§461(l)(3) inflation-adjusted thresholds** with OBBBA 2025 re-indexing:

| Tax year | Single   | MFJ      |
|----------|----------|----------|
| 2021     | $262,000 | $524,000 |
| 2022     | $270,000 | $540,000 |
| 2023     | $289,000 | $578,000 |
| 2024     | $305,000 | $610,000 |
| 2025     | $313,000 | $626,000 |
| **2026** | **$256,000** | **$512,000** |

**The 2026 thresholds DROPPED from 2025** because the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 re-indexed back toward the TCJA-original $250k/$500k 2018 base. OBBBA also made В§461(l) **permanent** вҖ” eliminating the prior 2028 sunset. The $114k MFJ drop from 2025 to 2026 ($626k вҶ’ $512k) translates to real cash that stays with the IRS for at least another year. Pinned by `obbba_re_indexing_2025_vs_2026_delta` (single delta = $57k, MFJ delta = $114k).

**All six historical thresholds individually pinned** (`historical_thresholds_pinned`) so any future regression that changes a single year's value is caught.

**В§461(l) applies to noncorporate taxpayers only**. C-corporations are NOT subject. Pinned by `c_corp_not_subject_to_section_461l`.

**2018-2020 CARES Act suspension** вҖ” В§461(l) was suspended for tax years 2018-2020 (CARES Act В§2304). First effective year is 2021. Pinned by `cares_suspension_years_all_pinned` (3-year sweep: 2018/2019/2020 all suspended) + `pre_2018_no_limitation_existed` (TCJA enacted statute) + `first_effective_year_2021_applies`.

**Threshold boundary math**: exact threshold = no binding; threshold + $1 = binds. Pinned by `threshold_exact_boundary_no_limit_binding` ($256k single exact, no EBL) + `threshold_one_dollar_over_binds` ($256,001 вҶ’ $1 EBL).

**MFJ threshold is exactly 2Г— single** under the statute. Pinned by `mfj_2026_threshold_512k` + `mfj_2026_700k_loss_excess_188k_disallowed` (load-bearing math: $700k - $512k = $188k disallowed).

**HOH and MFS use the single-filer threshold** per the statute (no separate amounts). Pinned by `hoh_mfs_uses_single_threshold`.

**Net gain produces no excess**. When business income exceeds deductions, there is no net loss to limit. Pinned by `net_gain_no_loss_no_excess`.

**Excess loss becomes В§172 NOL carryforward** subject to 80% taxable income cap in subsequent years (under permanent TCJA В§172 regime, also tracked in `section_172`). The note explicitly mentions В§172. Pinned by `excess_becomes_nol_per_172_note`.

**Future-year fallback** uses the 2026 threshold for 2027+ pending IRS publication. Caller responsible for refreshing once future amounts are released. Pinned by `future_year_uses_2026_fallback`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-461l`. Twenty-two tests pin: 2026 single $256k threshold + MFJ $512k; **load-bearing $400k single вҶ’ $144k EBL + $700k MFJ вҶ’ $188k EBL** dollar-figure math; OBBBA re-indexing 2025 vs 2026 delta ($57k single / $114k MFJ); loss below threshold no binding; **threshold exact-boundary no binding + one-dollar-over binds**; net gain no excess; C-corp not subject; pre-2021 CARES suspended; pre-2018 no limitation; **CARES 3-year suspension sweep**; first effective year 2021; **all six historical thresholds individually pinned**; HOH/MFS uses single; excess becomes В§172 NOL (note pin); $1B precision case ($300M net loss, $299.488M EBL); future year 2027 uses 2026 fallback; loss just below MFJ threshold full allowance; note describes binding path with dollar figures; note describes satisfied path.

`traderview-expense::section_691` is the **IRC В§691 Income in Respect of Decedent (IRD) module** вҖ” pairs directly with `section_1014` (stepped-up basis at death). Where В§1014(a) wipes out embedded gains at death, **В§1014(c) explicitly denies the step-up for IRD assets** вҖ” and В§691 governs the income-tax consequences for the heir who receives that IRD.

**В§691(a)** вҖ” IRD is included in the gross income of the recipient (heir / estate / beneficiary) in the year received. **Character is preserved** вҖ” IRA distributions are ordinary income, installment-sale gain is capital, accrued bond interest is ordinary, etc.

**В§691(c)** вҖ” to mitigate the "double tax" of estate tax PLUS income tax on the same dollar, the recipient gets an itemized deduction (above the 2% AGI floor) equal to the federal estate tax attributable to the IRD share. Two-step computation per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.691(c)-1(a)(2):

1. Compute the decedent's federal estate tax twice вҖ” once INCLUDING the IRD assets (actual), once EXCLUDING them
2. The difference is the estate tax attributable to IRD
3. Each recipient's deduction = their share of total IRD Г— estate tax attributable to total IRD

**Pro-rata allocation** is load-bearing. If two beneficiaries each receive half a $1M IRA with $400k attributable estate tax вҶ’ each gets $200k В§691(c) deduction (per Kitces canonical example). Pinned by `canonical_kitces_example_pro_rata_deduction`.

**IRD type catalog** (character preservation):

| Type                                    | Character | Common scenario                                     |
|-----------------------------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `TraditionalIraDistribution`            | Ordinary  | Inherited traditional IRA distribution               |
| `QualifiedPlanDistribution`             | Ordinary  | Inherited 401(k) distribution                        |
| `AccruedBondInterest`                   | Ordinary  | Accrued but unpaid bond interest at death            |
| `AccruedRoyalties`                      | Ordinary  | Royalties earned but not received                    |
| **`InstallmentSaleGainNotYetRecognized`** | **Capital** | Open installment-sale gain вҖ” only capital IRD type |
| `DeferredCompensation`                  | Ordinary  | NQDC / pension payouts                              |
| `AccruedSalaryOrCommissions`            | Ordinary  | Earned but unpaid salary / commissions               |
| `RenewalCommissionsLifeInsurance`       | Ordinary  | Life insurance agent's recurring commissions        |
| `Other`                                 | Ordinary  | Catch-all                                            |

**Installment-sale gain is the only capital-character IRD type.** Pinned by `installment_sale_gain_preserves_capital_character` + `traditional_ira_is_ordinary_character` + `deferred_comp_is_ordinary_character` + `accrued_bond_interest_is_ordinary` + `renewal_commissions_is_ordinary` + `ird_type_method_classifies_correctly` (5-type sweep).

**Deduction never exceeds includible income**. Net taxable clamps at zero even with pathological inputs (huge estate tax, tiny IRD). Pinned by `deduction_never_exceeds_includible` ($100 IRD with $1M attributable вҶ’ $1M deduction capped, $0 net taxable).

**Zero estate tax вҶ’ no deduction.** When estate is below filing threshold (no federal estate tax due), В§691(c) deduction is zero вҖ” all IRD is fully taxable. Pinned by `zero_estate_tax_attributable_no_deduction` (note explicitly mentions "no В§691(c) deduction").

**Division-by-zero guard** for pathological total_IRD = 0 input. Pinned by `zero_total_ird_no_deduction_no_panic`.

**Effective relief ratio** = В§691(c) deduction / IRD received. Demonstrates the proportion of "estate tax money" that comes back via the income-tax deduction. The 40% Kitces example yields `relief_ratio = 0.4`. Pinned by `canonical_kitces_example_pro_rata_deduction` + `effective_relief_ratio_50_percent_example`.

**Pro-rata math sweep**: three-beneficiary equal split (each gets 1/3 of $1.2M = $400k), unequal split (70% gets 70% Г— $400k = $280k). Pinned by `three_beneficiary_equal_split_each_gets_one_third_deduction` + `unequal_beneficiary_share_proportional`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-691`. Nineteen tests pin: canonical Kitces $1M IRA / 2 beneficiaries / $400k estate tax / $200k each deduction example; full-IRD recipient gets full deduction; partial share proportional; **zero estate tax no deduction** (estate-below-threshold case); zero total IRD no panic; **traditional IRA ordinary character** + 4 other ordinary types pinned; **installment sale capital character preserved** (only non-ordinary type); deduction never exceeds includible (clamping); 50% effective relief ratio example; zero IRD no-op; **$50M IRA precision case**; three-beneficiary equal 1/3 split with Decimal rounding; unequal 70/30 split proportional math; note describes pro-rata with dollar figures; note for zero-deduction path explains; **IrdType::is_ordinary_character() 4-type sweep** distinguishing capital-only installment-sale path.

`traderview-expense::section_704d` is the **IRC В§704(d) partner basis limitation module** вҖ” completes the partner loss-limitation trio with `section_465` (at-risk) and `section_469` (passive activity losses). Sequential application order for partnership losses:

| Order | Section          | Limit                                | Module                |
|-------|------------------|--------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| 1     | **В§704(d)**      | Outside basis                        | **section_704d** (this) |
| 2     | В§465             | At-risk amount                       | section_465           |
| 3     | В§469             | Passive activity loss                | section_469           |
| 4     | В§461(l)          | Excess business loss (TCJA)          | (downstream)          |

Each limit applies to the loss surviving the prior limit. A loss may be ALLOWED under В§704(d) but SUSPENDED under В§465 (e.g., partner has basis from nonrecourse liabilities but no economic at-risk amount).

**В§704(d)(1) general rule**: a partner's distributive share of partnership loss is allowed only to the extent of the partner's adjusted basis in the partnership interest (outside basis) at the end of the partnership year. Excess losses carry forward indefinitely until the partner has sufficient basis in a subsequent year.

**Outside basis formula** (per the partnership year):

```text
  Beginning basis
  + Capital contributions
  + Share of partnership income
  + В§752 liability increases (recourse + nonrecourse)
  - В§752 liability decreases
  - Distributions received
  - Allocated partnership losses (limited by basis)
```

**В§752 liability allocation** (caller pre-computes):

- **Recourse**: partner bears economic risk of loss (EROL) вҶ’ full liability amount increases that partner's basis
- **Nonrecourse**: no partner bears EROL вҶ’ allocated by share of equity in securing property + minimum-gain shares

**Nonrecourse vs at-risk distinction is load-bearing.** Nonrecourse liabilities INCREASE outside basis under В§704(d) but generally do NOT increase at-risk amount under В§465. A loss may pass В§704(d) (basis available from nonrecourse) and fail В§465 (no economic risk on those liabilities). Pinned by `nonrecourse_basis_exceeds_at_risk_amount` ($10k basis + $100k nonrecourse = $110k В§704(d) basis; downstream В§465 would not include the $100k).

**В§731(a)(1) distributions in excess of basis trigger gain.** When distributions exceed basis, outside basis goes negative; module flags this with note mentioning В§731(a)(1) gain recognition (caller responsible for actual gain reporting on Form 4797 / Schedule D). Pinned by `distributions_exceeding_basis_trigger_731_gain_note` ($50k basis - $80k distributions = -$30k, note mentions В§731(a)(1)).

**Defensive basis clamping at zero** prevents negative outside basis from being used for loss absorption. The compute clamps `basis_for_loss_absorption` at zero so even pathological inputs produce sane results. Pinned by `outside_basis_after_loss_never_negative`.

**Five outside-basis components** independently pinned:

| Component                                   | Direction | Test                                          |
|---------------------------------------------|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Capital contributions                       | +         | `capital_contributions_increase_basis`        |
| Share of partnership income                 | +         | `share_of_partnership_income_increases_basis` |
| Recourse liability increase (В§752)          | +         | `recourse_liability_increase_under_752`       |
| Nonrecourse liability increase              | +         | `nonrecourse_liability_increase_also_in_basis` |
| Liability decrease / distributions          | вҲ’         | `liability_decrease_reduces_basis`, `distributions_reduce_basis` |

**Complex combination test** demonstrates the full formula: $50k + $30k contributions + $10k income + $40k recourse + $20k nonrecourse - $5k decrease - $25k distributions = $120k basis. Pinned by `complex_combination_all_basis_components`.

**Prior-year suspended losses combine with current losses** before the basis cap is applied. Pinned by `prior_carryforward_combined_with_current_loss` ($20k current + $20k prior = $40k absorbed under $50k basis, $10k remaining) + `prior_carryforward_alone_absorbed_into_current_basis` + `prior_carryforward_partial_absorption_with_current`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-704d`. Twenty-one tests pin: loss within basis full allowance; loss exceeds basis partial + suspension; prior carryforward combines with current; capital contributions / share of income / recourse В§752 / nonrecourse В§752 / liability decrease / distributions each individually pinned as basis components; **В§731(a)(1) distributions-in-excess-of-basis gain note**; zero basis no loss allowed; zero loss no-op; **complex combination demonstrating full outside-basis formula**; basis exact match no remaining no suspension; **nonrecourse basis exceeds at-risk amount** (load-bearing В§704(d) vs В§465 conceptual distinction); $1B precision case; note describes binding path; note describes satisfied path mentions В§465 + В§469 downstream; outside basis after loss never negative; prior carryforward alone / partial.

`traderview-expense::section_704c` is the **IRC В§704(c) pre-contribution built-in gain/loss allocation module** вҖ” completes the В§704 / В§743 / В§754 partnership-allocation triangle with `section_704d` (outside basis loss limitation) and `section_754` (В§743(b) inside-basis adjustment). Universal for any trader contributing appreciated or depreciated property to an LLC/LP вҖ” the pre-contribution gain or loss MUST be allocated back to the contributing partner regardless of the partnership's nominal allocation ratios ([Cornell LII 26 CFR В§ 1.704-3](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.704-3), [LegalClarity 704(c) Methods](https://legalclarity.org/the-three-methods-for-allocating-704c-built-in-gain-and-loss/)).

**В§704(c)(1)(A) core rule**: when a partner contributes property with tax basis вү  FMV at contribution, the pre-contribution built-in gain (FMV вҲ’ basis) or built-in loss (basis вҲ’ FMV) must be allocated to the contributing partner on subsequent disposition. The other partners receive only the post-contribution change in value. Module returns `contributor_704c1a_allocation` capped at the lesser of (pre-contribution BIG) or (disposition gain realized), with any excess flowing to `partnership_proportional_gain`. Pinned by `big_disposition_allocates_500k_to_contributor_200k_to_partners` + `small_disposition_gain_caps_704c1a_at_gain` + `exactly_pre_contribution_big_at_disposition`.

**Three allocation methods** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.704-3):

| Method                              | Subject to ceiling rule? | Creates notional items? |
|-------------------------------------|--------------------------|-------------------------|
| **Traditional** (default)           | Yes                      | No                      |
| **Traditional with curative allocations** | No                  | No                      |
| **Remedial**                        | No                       | Yes                     |

The **ceiling rule** limits tax allocations to non-contributing partners to the partnership's total tax items вҖ” which can leave the contributing partner unfairly absorbing more or less than intended. The traditional method is subject to it; curative allocations fix it by allocating OTHER existing income/deductions of the same character; remedial method creates NOTIONAL items (no real cash flow) for tax purposes to ensure proper allocation.

**В§704(c)(1)(B) anti-mixing-bowl rule**: if the partnership distributes the contributed property to ANY partner OTHER THAN THE ORIGINAL CONTRIBUTOR within **7 years** of contribution, the contributing partner must recognize their remaining pre-contribution built-in gain (after any prior В§704(c)(1)(A) allocations) as if the property had been sold at FMV. Pinned by `distribution_to_other_partner_within_7_years_triggers_704c1b` + `distribution_after_7_years_does_not_trigger_704c1b` + `sec_704c1b_only_remaining_gain_after_prior_disposition` (chained with prior $300k В§704(c)(1)(A) allocation вҶ’ remaining $200k accelerated).

**В§737 complementary anti-mixing-bowl**: same 7-year window; triggers when the CONTRIBUTING PARTNER receives distribution of OTHER property whose FMV exceeds their outside basis. Gain recognition = lesser of (FMV other property вҲ’ outside basis) or (remaining pre-contribution BIG). Pinned by `section_737_triggered_when_other_property_received_within_7_years` + `section_737_gain_capped_at_remaining_big` ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 737](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/737)).

**В§704(c)(1)(C) built-in loss restriction** (added by American Jobs Creation Act of 2004, P.L. 108-357 В§ 833(a) вҖ” NOT TCJA): pre-contribution built-in losses can ONLY be allocated to the contributing partner. Other partners treat the property's basis as equal to FMV at contribution, effectively eliminating the built-in loss from their tax calculations. Prevents the pre-2004 "loss-property trafficking" strategy. Pinned by `bil_restricted_to_contributing_partner` + `bil_does_not_trigger_704c1a_gain_allocation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-704c`. Twenty-five tests pin: **В§704(c)(1)(A) baseline** ($500k BIG, $700k disposition вҶ’ $500k contributor + $200k pro rata); **small disposition gain caps allocation at gain** ($300k disposition вҶ’ $300k to contributor only); exactly-pre-contribution BIG at disposition вҶ’ all to contributor; zero pre-contribution gain вҶ’ Neither direction; **В§704(c)(1)(B) trigger within 7 years** (full remaining $500k BIG to contributor); В§704(c)(1)(B) after 7 years does not trigger; **7-year window expired flag** set after window + not expired within; **В§704(c)(1)(B) only remaining gain after prior disposition** ($300k prior + $200k accelerated); **В§737 triggered when contributor receives other property** within 7 years ($200k excess capped at remaining BIG); В§737 gain capped at remaining BIG ($10M excess вҶ’ $500k capped); В§737 no excess distribution no gain; В§737 after 7 years not triggered; **В§704(c)(1)(C) BIL restricted to contributor only** + BIL does not trigger В§704(c)(1)(A) gain allocation; **traditional method subject to ceiling rule** (no notional); **curative method no ceiling + no notional**; **remedial method creates notional items** (no ceiling); note describes BIG allocation path; note describes BIL restriction path (mentions AJCA 2004); note describes В§704(c)(1)(B) trigger; note describes В§737 trigger; note describes 7-year expiration; citation mentions all 5 relevant authorities; very large BIG precision path ($1B BIG / $1.5B disposition вҶ’ $1B contributor + $500M pro rata).

`traderview-expense::section_754` is the **IRC В§754 partnership election + В§743(b) inside-basis adjustment module** вҖ” critical for any trader investing in LP / LLC partnerships (hedge funds, PE funds, real-estate syndications). The В§754 election (partnership-level, irrevocable except by IRS consent under В§ 754 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.754-1(c)) unlocks two basis-adjustment mechanisms: В§743(b) on TRANSFERS of partnership interests (sale, exchange, or death of partner) and В§734(b) on certain DISTRIBUTIONS. This module handles the В§743(b) path вҖ” the more common trader scenario.

**В§743(b) inside basis adjustment formula**:

```text
В§743(b) adjustment = transferee outside basis
                   вҲ’ transferee proportionate share of partnership's
                     adjusted inside basis
```

- Positive вҶ’ **STEP-UP** allocated to appreciated assets under В§ 755 (transferee gets bigger inside basis matching what they paid)
- Negative вҶ’ **STEP-DOWN** allocated to depreciated assets under В§ 755
- Adjustment is **PERSONAL to the transferee**; other partners' inside basis is unaffected

**В§743(d) MANDATORY adjustment (TCJA expanded)** вҖ” applies even without a В§754 election on file when EITHER substantial-built-in-loss test is satisfied:

1. **В§743(d)(1)(A) partnership-level test**: partnership's adjusted inside basis exceeds partnership-level FMV by more than **$250,000**.
2. **В§743(d)(1)(B) transferee-level test** (TCJA addition, post-2017): transferee partner would be allocated a loss greater than **$250,000** if all partnership assets were sold for cash equal to FMV immediately after the transfer.

Pre-TCJA only test 1 applied. TCJA added test 2 to close the "loss-trafficking transferee" loophole where a partnership without overall BIL could still funnel a built-in loss to a single transferee via disproportionate allocation. Sources: [IRS В§754 FAQ](https://www.irs.gov/businesses/partnerships/faqs-for-internal-revenue-code-irc-sec-754-election-and-revocation), [IRS В§ 743 Substantial Built-In Loss Q&A](https://www.irs.gov/newsroom/questions-and-answers-about-the-substantial-built-in-loss-changes-under-internal-revenue-code-irc-section-743), [Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 743](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/743).

**Without В§754 election AND no В§743(d) trigger**: В§743(b) does NOT apply. Transferee inherits seller's inside-basis share unchanged вҖ” and bears **phantom income** equal to the basis gap when the partnership later sells appreciated assets. This is the classic hedge-fund LP trap: a partner buys in at a $1M outside basis but their share of the fund's inside basis is only $600k; without a В§754 election the fund's $400k gain on subsequent sales flows through to them despite having ALREADY paid full price for it.

**Death of partner path**: heir takes outside basis at FMV under В§ 1014; В§743(b) applies under the same election/mandatory rules. The combination of В§ 1014 step-up + В§743(b) step-up is the most aggressive basis-step-up combo available to anyone holding a partnership interest at death вҖ” pinned by `death_of_partner_transfer_type_path`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-754`. Twenty-two tests pin: **step-up baseline** (outside $1M вҲ’ inside $600k = $400k step-up with election); **step-down** when outside below inside (вҲ’$200k); **no-election + no-mandatory вҶ’ no adjustment** + election_required flag; **В§743(d)(1)(A) partnership BIL test fires at $250,001** (strict greater-than); $250k exact does NOT fire (boundary); **$249,999 below threshold** does not fire; **В§743(d)(1)(B) transferee-loss test fires** at $400k loss (TCJA addition); test B at $250k exact does not fire; **both tests A + B firing together**; **death-of-partner transfer type** path + В§1014 note; election-in-effect makes election_required false; zero basis difference no adjustment no direction; step-up election required when no election and no mandatory; **very large step-up path** ($100M outside вҲ’ $20M inside = $80M); **mandatory with election off still applies step-down** (BIL forces); note describes mandatory test A reason; note describes mandatory test B reason; note describes election-in-effect path; note describes no-election no-mandatory path; note describes no-election-but-mandatory path; FMV greater than inside (built-in gain) test A does not fire; zero transferee loss test B does not fire.

`traderview-expense::section_465` is the **IRC В§465 at-risk rules module** вҖ” pairs with `section_469` (passive activity losses) to complete the loss-limitation framework for any trader, partner, or S corp shareholder with leveraged positions. В§465 applies FIRST (limits to amount at risk), then В§469 applies (limits by passive activity character).

**В§465(a) general rule**: deductible loss is limited to the amount the taxpayer has "at risk" in the activity at year-end.

**At-risk components** under В§465(b):

| Component                              | Citation         | Behavior                                  |
|----------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| Cash + adjusted basis of property contributed | В§465(b)(1)(A) | INCREASES at-risk                         |
| Recourse debt (personally liable)      | В§465(b)(1)(B)    | INCREASES at-risk                         |
| External pledged property FMV          | В§465(b)(2)       | INCREASES at-risk                         |
| Related-party borrowing                | В§465(b)(3)       | REDUCES at-risk                           |
| Qualified nonrecourse for real property | В§465(b)(6)      | **REAL PROPERTY ONLY** вҖ” increases at-risk |
| General nonrecourse debt               | В§465(b)(4)       | NEVER at-risk                             |

**В§465(b)(6) qualified nonrecourse financing carve-out is load-bearing for real estate.** Only the `RealPropertyHolding` activity kind qualifies. Other trade or business activities cannot use QNF as at-risk. Pinned by `qualified_nonrecourse_real_property_section_465b6_applies` ($20k cash + $40k recourse + $200k QNF = $260k at-risk for real property) + `qualified_nonrecourse_not_applied_for_non_real_property` (same QNF amount, OtherTradeOrBusiness activity = at-risk stays at $60k) + `qnf_does_not_apply_when_other_trade_uses_real_estate_lookup` (activity classification is the gatekeeper).

**General nonrecourse (non-qualified) is NEVER at risk.** Even $100k of plain nonrecourse debt doesn't add to at-risk amount because the taxpayer faces no economic loss exposure beyond the property securing the loan. Pinned by `nonrecourse_debt_not_at_risk_in_non_real_property_activity`.

**В§465(b)(3) related-party reduction.** Borrowing from a related party with an interest in the activity reduces at-risk because the related party isn't likely to enforce collection. Pinned by `related_party_borrowing_reduces_at_risk` ($20k cash + $40k recourse - $20k related = $40k).

**В§465(e) negative-at-risk recapture trigger.** If related-party borrowing or other adjustments would make at-risk negative, the prior losses claimed up to that negative amount are recaptured as income. The compute fn flags this with `negative_at_risk_recapture_triggered = true`. Pinned by `negative_at_risk_recapture_triggered` ($10k cash + $10k recourse - $50k related = -$30k вҶ’ recapture flag set, at-risk clamped to 0).

**В§465(d) suspended loss carryover** вҖ” losses disallowed under В§465(a) carry forward indefinitely. Prior carryover adds to current-year loss before the at-risk cap. Pinned by `prior_year_carryover_added_to_current_loss` ($50k current + $30k prior = $80k loss; $60k at-risk вҶ’ $60k allowed, $20k suspended) + `prior_carryover_alone_suspended_when_no_current_year_at_risk` (no current loss but $30k prior вҶ’ all absorbed by at-risk) + `prior_carryover_partial_when_combined_loss_exceeds_at_risk`.

**At-risk reduces by allowed loss** under В§465(b)(5). Pinned by `cash_plus_recourse_debt_at_risk_full_loss_allowed` ($60k at-risk - $50k loss = $10k remaining) + `exact_at_risk_match_full_allowance_zero_remaining` ($60k at-risk - $60k loss = $0 remaining, $0 suspended).

**Zero-at-risk вҶ’ all loss suspended.** No contribution + no debt = no at-risk = no current-year deduction. All loss suspended for future years. Pinned by `zero_at_risk_no_loss_allowed`.

**Combined real-estate partnership scenario** demonstrates the full В§465 sweep: cash + recourse + external pledged + QNF - related-party = comprehensive at-risk. Pinned by `complex_combination_partnership_real_estate_with_all_components` ($30k + $20k + $50k + $500k - $10k = $590k).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-465`. Twenty-one tests pin: cash + recourse full at-risk + full loss allowed; loss exceeds at-risk partial allowance + suspension; **prior carryover combines with current loss before cap**; general nonrecourse not at-risk in non-real-property; **В§465(b)(6) QNF carve-out applies for real property** + **explicitly does NOT apply for other trade/business** (regression target for activity classification gatekeeper); external pledged property increases; **related-party borrowing reduces**; zero at-risk вҶ’ all loss suspended; **negative at-risk вҶ’ В§465(e) recapture flag** + clamp to 0; exact at-risk match full allowance zero remaining; real-estate partnership full sweep ($1.05M at-risk); QNF doesn't apply when other-trade misuses real-estate lookup; combined all-components partnership; prior carryover only (no current loss); prior carryover partial suspension; zero loss no-op; **$1B real-estate precision** ($6B at-risk with $5B QNF); note describes В§465(e) recapture path; note describes QNF carve-out applied; note for suspended loss mentions В§465(d).

`traderview-expense::section_409a` is the **IRC В§409A nonqualified deferred compensation compliance module** вҖ” major executive exposure for NQDC plans (top-hat plans, supplemental executive retirement plans, deferred bonus arrangements, equity compensation deferral, etc.). Noncompliance triggers three layered penalties:

| Penalty                            | Citation         | Magnitude                                  |
|------------------------------------|------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Immediate income inclusion         | В§409A(a)(1)(A)   | All vested deferred amounts in current year |
| 20% additional tax                 | В§409A(a)(1)(B)   | 20% Г— included amount                       |
| "Premium interest tax"             | В§409A(a)(1)(C)   | IRS underpayment rate + 1% Г— years Г— amount |

**Combined effect on a $1M deferral with 5 years of deferral at 9% premium rate**: $1M income inclusion + $200k extra 20% tax + $450k premium interest = **$650k total federal penalty** on top of ordinary income tax. Pinned by `total_penalty_includes_additional_tax_plus_premium_interest` (20% + premium = $650k).

**В§409A(a)(2)(A) permitted distribution events** вҖ” deferred comp may only be distributed on:

| Event                        | Status         |
|------------------------------|----------------|
| `SeparationFromService`      | Permitted      |
| `Disability`                 | Permitted      |
| `Death`                      | Permitted      |
| `SpecifiedTimeOrSchedule`    | Permitted      |
| `ChangeInControl`            | Permitted      |
| `UnforeseeableEmergency`     | Permitted      |
| `OtherImpermissible`         | **Not permitted** |

Pinned by `distribution_event_helper_classifies_correctly` (7-variant sweep) + `all_permitted_events_compliant` (5-event compliant sweep) + `impermissible_distribution_event_triggers_violation`.

**В§409A(a)(2)(B)(i) specified-employee 6-month delay** вҖ” for "key employees" of public companies (top owners + key officers per В§416(i)(1)), distributions on account of separation from service may not be made during the first 6 months after separation. Private companies are exempt.

**6-month delay boundary**: month 6 exact complies, month 5 violates. Pinned by `specified_employee_separation_under_6_months_violates` (3 months = violation) + `specified_employee_separation_6_months_exact_complies` (6 = OK) + `specified_employee_separation_7_months_complies` + `non_specified_employee_no_delay_required` (private/non-key gets no delay).

**В§409A(a)(3) anti-acceleration rule** вҖ” once distribution time is fixed, it may NOT be accelerated. Limited regulatory exceptions (de minimis cash-outs, conflict-of-interest divestiture, plan termination within 12 months of change in control) are caller-side determined. Pinned by `anti_acceleration_violation_triggers`.

**Premium interest math** = `(IRS_rate + 1%) Г— years Г— amount`. The +1% addition is statutorily-mandated. Pinned by `premium_interest_includes_one_percent_addition` (8% IRS + 1% = 9% Г— 5y Г— $1M = $450k).

**Zero-deferral and zero-year edge cases**:
- Zero deferral amount вҶ’ plan still flagged non-compliant if violations exist, but total penalty = $0 (no dollars to tax). Pinned by `zero_deferral_no_penalty_even_with_violations`.
- Zero years of deferral вҶ’ 20% tax still applies; premium interest = $0. Pinned by `zero_years_deferral_no_premium_interest`.

**Multiple violations stack in violation_details list** вҖ” impermissible event + acceleration + specified-employee short delay can all fire simultaneously. Pinned by `multiple_violations_stack_in_list` + `three_separate_violations_for_separation_path`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-409a`. Twenty-one tests pin: compliant baseline no penalty; **impermissible distribution event** triggers В§409A(a)(2) violation; **specified-employee separation 6-month boundary** (3m violates, 6m exact complies, 7m complies); non-specified-employee no delay required; **anti-acceleration violation** triggers В§409A(a)(3); 20% additional tax math ($1M Г— 20% = $200k); **premium interest +1% addition** (IRS 8% + 1% Г— 5y Г— $1M = $450k); total penalty math (20% + premium = $650k); immediate income inclusion equals vested amount; multiple violations stack (separation+delay+acceleration); 3-violation separation path; **all 6 permitted events compliant sweep**; separation event alone complies without specified-employee status; **DistributionEvent::is_permitted() 7-variant classifier sweep**; zero years no premium interest (20% still applies); zero deferral no penalty even with violations; **$100M deferral precision case** ($100M / 6% IRS / 10y вҶ’ $90M total penalty); note describes compliant path; note describes non-compliant path with violation count and dollar figures.

`traderview-expense::section_448` is the **IRC В§448 small business accrual exemption + cascade module** вҖ” foundational gate for any C-corp or partnership with a C-corp partner. В§ 448(a) defaults to MANDATORY ACCRUAL method, but В§ 448(b)(3) creates a small business exception that lets a taxpayer who meets the В§ 448(c) gross receipts test use the cash method AND escape four cascade provisions (В§263A UNICAP, В§471 inventory, В§163(j) business interest, В§460 long-term contracts). The single most consequential tax-classification call any small business makes annually ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 448](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/448), [Crowe вҖ” Gross Receipts Test for Small Businesses](https://www.crowe.com/insights/tax-news-highlights/new-guidance-affects-gross-receipts-test-for-small-businesses)).

**В§ 448(c) gross receipts test threshold** вҖ” indexed for inflation from a $25M TCJA-2017 base, rounded to the nearest $1M annually:

| Tax year | Threshold | Source                                        |
|----------|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| 2018     | $25M      | TCJA P.L. 115-97 В§ 13102 base                 |
| 2019-2021| $26M      | IRS annual Rev. Proc.                         |
| 2022     | $27M      | IRS Rev. Proc. 2021-45                        |
| 2023     | $29M      | IRS Rev. Proc. 2022-38                        |
| 2024     | $30M      | IRS Rev. Proc. 2023-34                        |
| 2025     | $31M      | IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40 ([RSM 2025 inflation](https://rsmus.com/insights/services/business-tax/irs-releases-2025-tax-inflation-adjustments.html)) |
| 2026     | $32M      | IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32                        |

The test: average annual gross receipts for the **THREE preceding taxable years** must not exceed the threshold. **AGGREGATED across related entities** under common control per В§ 448(c)(2) / В§ 52(a)/(b) вҖ” a small entity wholly owned by a $1B affiliated group fails at the group level. Pinned by `aggregation_can_blow_through_threshold` (entity $10M avg / aggregated $40M avg вҶ’ fails).

**В§ 448(a)(3) tax shelter disqualification вҖ” overrides everything**: even if the gross receipts test is satisfied, the В§ 448(b)(3) small business exception does NOT apply to any "tax shelter" under В§ 448(d)(3) / В§ 461(i)(3):

- Any enterprise other than a C corp with interests offered for sale through registered securities
- Any syndicate within the meaning of В§ 1256(e)(3)(B) (allocates > 35% of losses to limited partners)
- Any entity described in В§ 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii) (significant tax-avoidance purpose)

Tax shelter disqualification blocks ALL cascade exemptions even at $0 receipts. Pinned by `tax_shelter_disqualifies_even_when_test_satisfied` with full UNICAP / inventory / В§163(j) / В§460 application.

**Four cascade exemptions when qualified** вҖ” all flow from В§ 448(c) status:

- **В§ 263A UNICAP exempt** вҖ” no capitalization of indirect costs into inventory
- **В§ 471 inventory exempt** вҖ” treats inventory as supplies / NIMS, no required maintenance
- **В§ 163(j) business interest exempt** вҖ” no 30%-of-ATI deduction limit
- **В§ 460 long-term contracts exempt** вҖ” completed-contract method available

**Entity type matters**: В§ 448(a) MANDATORY ACCRUAL applies only to C-corps and partnerships with a C-corp partner. S-corps, other pass-throughs, and sole proprietors can use cash method REGARDLESS of receipt size вҖ” but only the qualifying entities (under В§ 448(c) + not tax shelter) get the cascade exemptions. Pinned by `s_corp_not_subject_to_448a_mandatory_accrual` ($50M S-corp can still use cash but NOT exempt from В§ 163(j)) + `sole_proprietor_can_use_cash_regardless` ($100M sole prop on cash basis but still subject to В§ 163(j)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-448`. Twenty tests pin: **5-year threshold table** (2018 $25M / 2024 $30M / 2025 $31M / 2026 $32M + unknown-year falls back to 2025); **small C-corp qualifies вҖ” full cascade** (cash method + all 4 exemptions); **large C-corp fails вҖ” mandatory accrual + all 4 apply**; **exact threshold qualifies** (вүӨ test); **$1 over threshold fails** (boundary); **tax shelter disqualifies even when receipts test satisfied** with all cascade off + TAX SHELTER DISQUALIFICATION note priority; **aggregation can blow through threshold** (entity $10M / aggregated $40M); partnership-with-C-corp-partner subject to В§ 448(a) mandatory accrual; **S-corp at $50M can use cash but NOT exempt from В§ 163(j)** (entity-type / cascade decoupling); **sole proprietor at $100M same** ($163(j) still applies); average computed from 3 prior years ($5M + $10M + $15M вҶ’ $10M); qualified note describes all 4 cascade exemptions by name; failed-test note describes "ALL apply"; citation mentions all 8 relevant authorities (В§448(a), В§448(b)(3), В§448(c), В§448(c)(2), В§448(a)(3), TCJA, В§263A, В§163(j)); $1B aggregated receipts handled safely; zero receipts qualifies.

`traderview-expense::section_415` is the **IRC В§ 415 вҖ” Limitations on Benefits and Contributions umbrella statute module** вҖ” direct trader companion to `section_401k` (iter 448), `section_408` (traditional IRA вҖ” iter 432), `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” iter 430), `section_457b` (iter 450), `section_4973` (excess contribution excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp deduction вҖ” iter 446). В§ 415 is the UMBRELLA STATUTE governing maximum limits on retirement plan benefits and contributions; sets ceilings that interact with lower limits in В§ 401(k) elective deferrals, В§ 457(b) governmental DC plans, В§ 403(b) annuity plans, В§ 408 IRAs, and В§ 1402 self-employed contributions.

В§ 415(a) **disqualification cascade** вҖ” denies qualified status under В§ 401(a) to any trust that is part of a plan exceeding В§ 415(b) or В§ 415(c) limits; disqualification cascades to ALL PARTICIPANTS, not just over-limit individual. Harsh consequence motivates strict compliance.

**В§ 415(b) Defined Benefit (DB) plan limit** вҖ” maximum annual benefit (life-only, age 62-65 commencement) is lesser of:
1. В§ 415(b)(1)(A) DOLLAR LIMIT вҖ” **$290,000 for 2026** (up from $280,000 for 2025); indexed annually under В§ 415(d)
2. В§ 415(b)(1)(B) COMPENSATION LIMIT вҖ” 100% of participant's average compensation for highest 3 consecutive years; NOT subject to В§ 401(a)(17) cap when computing DB limit

В§ 415(b)(2) actuarial adjustments for commencement age (reduction below 62; increase above 65), form of benefit (joint and survivor), and cost-of-living adjustments at retirement.

**В§ 415(c) Defined Contribution (DC) plan limit** вҖ” annual addition to participant's account cannot exceed lesser of:
1. В§ 415(c)(1)(A) DOLLAR LIMIT вҖ” **$72,000 for 2026** (up from $70,000 for 2025); indexed annually
2. В§ 415(c)(1)(B) вҖ” 100% of participant's COMPENSATION for the year (subject to В§ 401(a)(17) $360,000 cap)

**В§ 415(c)(2) annual addition INCLUDES**: employer contributions; employee contributions (pretax + Roth designated deferrals); forfeitures. **EXCLUDES**: В§ 414(v) catch-up contributions; rollovers from other plans; В§ 408A Roth IRA contributions; investment earnings.

**2026 dollar limits framework (IRS Notice 2025-67)**:

| Limit | 2026 Amount |
|-------|-------------|
| В§ 415(b)(1)(A) DB annual benefit | **$290,000** |
| В§ 415(c)(1)(A) DC annual addition | **$72,000** |
| В§ 401(a)(17) compensation limit | $360,000 |
| В§ 402(g)(1) elective deferral | $24,500 |
| Age 50+ catch-up В§ 414(v) | $8,000 |
| Ages 60-63 enhanced catch-up В§ 414(v)(2)(E) | $11,250 |
| HCE threshold | $160,000 |
| Roth catch-up wage threshold В§ 414(v)(7) | $150,000 |

**В§ 415(f) aggregation rules**:
1. All DC plans of single employer **AGGREGATED** for В§ 415(c) limit
2. All DB plans of single employer **AGGREGATED** for В§ 415(b) limit
3. В§ 415(f)(2) вҖ” DC and DB limits applied **SEPARATELY** (participant may be at maximum in both; no combined limit since 2002 TEFRA repeal)
4. В§ 414(b)/(c)/(m)/(o) вҖ” controlled group, common control, affiliated service group, ASG rules treat related employers as single employer
5. UNRELATED employers вҖ” separate В§ 415 limits apply at each employer

**В§ 415(g) anti-cutback rule** вҖ” В§ 411(d)(6) protected benefits cannot be reduced when В§ 415 limits adjust.

**В§ 415(k) grandfathered old-limit benefits** вҖ” pre-1976 accrued benefits grandfathered; pre-1982 accrued benefits subject to old TEFRA $90,000 dollar limit if greater than current В§ 415(b) limit.

**В§ 415(n) USERRA reinstatement** вҖ” qualifying military service member returning under USERRA may receive make-up contributions during reemployment period without violating В§ 415 limits; make-up period equals lesser of 3Г— period of military service OR 5 years.

**В§ 415 INTERACTION WITH В§ 401(k) / В§ 402(g)**:
- В§ 415(c) ANNUAL ADDITION = elective deferrals + employer match + after-tax + forfeitures
- В§ 402(g)(1) ELECTIVE DEFERRAL LIMIT applies separately to participant's pretax + Roth deferrals ($24,500 for 2026)
- В§ 414(v) CATCH-UP contributions are **DISREGARDED** for В§ 415(c) purposes (separate limit)
- MEGA BACKDOOR ROTH uses available В§ 415(c) room after elective deferral + employer match for after-tax contributions converted to Roth via В§ 408A(d)(3)

**В§ 415 INTERACTION WITH В§ 457(b)**:
- В§ 457(b) **NOT subject to В§ 415(c) limits** (governmental and tax-exempt В§ 457(b) have SEPARATE $24,500 limit)
- В§ 402(g)(1) AGGREGATION rule does NOT apply to В§ 457(b) (key for DOUBLE DEFERRAL strategy with В§ 401(k)/В§ 403(b))

**В§ 415 INTERACTION WITH DB+DC dual participation**:
- В§ 415(b) APPLIES SEPARATELY from В§ 415(c) вҖ” a participant in BOTH a DB plan and DC plan may receive MAXIMUM benefit under В§ 415(b) AND maximum annual addition under В§ 415(c) in same year
- No combined limit since 2002 TEFRA repeal
- Maximum tax-deferred wealth accumulation strategy for high-comp employees

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) age 35 maxes В§ 401(k) вҖ” $24,500 elective + $7,500 match + $40,000 after-tax = $72,000 В§ 415(c) annual addition (exactly at 2026 limit); (2) age 50 maxes В§ 401(k) AND В§ 415(c) вҖ” $24,500 + $8,000 catch-up (DISREGARDED for В§ 415(c)) + $7,500 match + $40,000 after-tax = $80,000 total cash flow / $72,000 В§ 415(c); (3) controlled-group corporation вҖ” В§ 414 aggregation treats all related as single employer; $72,000 cap applies ACROSS plans; (4) two unrelated employers вҖ” $72,000 SEPARATELY at each = $144,000 total / В§ 402(g)(1) still aggregates elective at $24,500; (5) DB + DC dual participation вҖ” $290K + $72,000 / no combined limit since 2002 TEFRA repeal.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-415`. Thirty-three tests pin: **DC max 2026 compliant**; **DC over $72K violation**; **DC compensation limit constrains at low comp**; **DC compensation over $360K capped at В§ 401(a)(17)** (DB plan exempt); **catch-up disregarded for DC**; **DB max 2026 compliant**; **DB over $290K violation**; **DB compensation limit lower than dollar limit constrains**; **both DC and DB apply separately** (В§ 415(f)(2) + 2002 TEFRA repeal); **controlled group aggregation disclosed**; **unrelated employers separate limits** (with В§ 402(g)(1) aggregation note); **unrelated with controlled group no separate disclosure**; **USERRA reinstatement disclosed**; **limits pinned at 2026**; **plan type truth table three cells**; **DC dollar limit uniquely constrains at high comp invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 415(a)-(n) + В§ 415(b)(1)(A) + В§ 415(b)(1)(B) + В§ 415(c)(1)(A) + В§ 415(c)(2) + В§ 415(d) + В§ 415(f) + В§ 415(g) + В§ 415(k) + В§ 415(n) + В§ 401(a)(17) + В§ 402(g)(1) + В§ 408A(d)(3) + В§ 414(b) + В§ 414(c) + В§ 414(m) + В§ 414(o) + В§ 414(v) + IRS Notice 2025-67 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.415-1); **note pins umbrella statute**; **note pins В§ 415(a) disqualification cascade**; **note pins В§ 415(b) DB limit**; **note pins В§ 415(c) DC limit**; **note pins В§ 415(d) COLA**; **note pins 2026 eight limits**; **note pins В§ 415(f) aggregation rules**; **note pins В§ 415(g) anti-cutback**; **note pins В§ 415(k) grandfathered**; **note pins В§ 415(n) USERRA**; **note pins В§ 401(k) interaction**; **note pins В§ 457(b) interaction**; **note pins DB+DC dual participation**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_421` is the **IRC В§ 421 вҖ” General rules for treatment of stock issued under qualified statutory stock options module** вҖ” the foundational provision for both incentive stock options (ISOs) under В§ 422 and employee stock purchase plans (ESPPs) under В§ 423: no income on grant, no income on exercise (for regular tax purposes), and capital-gain treatment on qualifying disposition. Direct trader-employee companion to `section_422` (ISO definitional rules), `section_423` (ESPP definitional rules), `section_409a` (NQDC), `section_83` (property transferred for services), `section_1042` (ESOP rollover вҖ” iter 480), `section_4978` (ESOP recapture вҖ” iter 484), `section_1366` (S-corp pass-through вҖ” iter 488), `section_1377` (S-corp definitions вҖ” iter 490). В§ 421 framework evolved from Tax Reform Act of 1976 + Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 + American Jobs Creation Act of 2004.

**В§ 421(a) General rule**: if a share of stock is transferred to an individual pursuant to the exercise of a STATUTORY OPTION (В§ 422 ISO or В§ 423 ESPP):
