1. **В§ 421(a)(1)** вҖ” NO INCOME at time of exercise for regular income tax purposes
2. **В§ 421(a)(2)** вҖ” NO В§ 162 DEDUCTION allowed to the employer corporation
3. **В§ 421(a)(3)** вҖ” only the option price is considered as received by the corporation

**В§ 421(b) Disqualifying disposition**: if individual disposes of share BEFORE qualifying-disposition holding period is satisfied:
- INCREASE in FMV over option price at time of exercise treated as **COMPENSATION (ordinary income)** in year of disposition
- В§ 162 deduction is allowed to employer
- Additional gain (FMV at disposition вҲ’ FMV at exercise) treated as capital gain per В§ 1222 holding period from exercise date

**ISO qualifying-disposition holding requirements per В§ 422(a)(1)**:
1. NO DISPOSITION within **2 YEARS from GRANT date**
2. NO DISPOSITION within **1 YEAR from TRANSFER (exercise) date**
3. **BOTH** periods must be satisfied for qualifying disposition

**В§ 422(a)(2) employment requirement**: individual must be EMPLOYEE of granting corporation (or related corp under В§ 424(e)/(f)) at all times during the period beginning on date of grant and ending on day **3 MONTHS BEFORE** date of exercise. Death extends to date of death; disability extends to 12 months.

**В§ 56(b)(3) AMT preference**: spread between FMV at exercise and option price IS an AMT preference item for the year of exercise вҖ” creates **"phantom AMT income"** before the share is sold. Major source of trader-employee AMT planning concerns when exercising deep-in-the-money ISOs.

**В§ 6039 information reporting**: corporation must file **Form 3921** with IRS and provide statement to employee for each ISO exercise + **Form 3922** for ESPP transfer of stock acquired pursuant to qualifying ESPP option.

**Coordination with В§ 1042** (iter 480 ESOP rollover): securities received by exercise of ISO under В§ 422 are **NOT** "qualified securities" eligible for В§ 1042 ESOP rollover treatment per В§ 1042(c)(1)(B) cross-reference. Trader-founders considering ESOP exit must hold ISO-exercised shares and В§ 422 stock separately.

**Coordination with В§ 83**: В§ 421 statutory-option treatment **OVERRIDES** В§ 83 property-transferred-for-services treatment for qualifying ISO/ESPP exercises. If В§ 422 or В§ 423 requirements fail, В§ 83 controls (immediate ordinary income at exercise).

**Trader-employee critical fact patterns**: (1) Trader-employee exercises 10,000 ISO shares at $5 strike with $50 FMV вҶ’ **$450K AMT preference** at exercise + $0 regular income; holds 2+ years and sells at $100 вҶ’ **$950K LTCG** with 0% ordinary income; (2) same shares sold within 1 year of exercise вҶ’ **$450K ordinary income (compensation) + $500K capital gain** (disqualifying); (3) employment terminated before exercise + 90-day window expires вҶ’ option treated as NQSO with immediate В§ 83 ordinary income; (4) AMT preference under В§ 56(b)(3) creates "phantom income" risk when exercising deep-in-the-money ISOs without selling; (5) 2-year/1-year ISO holding combined with 12-month long-term capital gain holding creates effective minimum 2-year hold to qualify for both preferential ISO treatment AND long-term capital gain rate.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-421`. Twenty-six tests pin: **NQSO not applicable** (В§ 83 controls); **grant event no income**; **exercise event no income + AMT preference computed**; **qualifying disposition full capital gain**; **disqualifying disposition 2-year grant fails**; **disqualifying disposition 1-year exercise fails**; **disqualifying disposition both periods fail**; **exactly 2-year grant + 1-year exercise qualifies**; **employment requirement failed**; **death extension satisfies employment requirement**; **ESPP qualifying disposition** (В§ 423); **ESPP disqualifying disposition**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 421(a)/(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(3)/(b) + В§ 422/В§ 422(a)(1)/(a)(2) + В§ 423 + В§ 424(e)/(f) + В§ 56(b)(3) + В§ 162 + В§ 83 + В§ 1042 + В§ 1222 + В§ 6039 + Form 3921 + Form 3922); **note pins general rule 3 parts** (no income exercise + no В§ 162 deduction + only option price); **note pins disqualifying disposition rule** (compensation + INCREASE in FMV); **note pins ISO 2/1 holding requirements** (2 YEARS GRANT + 1 YEAR TRANSFER + BOTH); **note pins employment requirement 3 months + DEATH + DISABILITY extensions**; **note pins AMT preference** (phantom AMT income); **note pins Form 3921 reporting** (В§ 6039); **note pins В§ 1042 coordination** (NOT qualified securities); **note pins В§ 83 coordination** (OVERRIDES); **note pins companion modules**; **6-cell severity truth table** (NQSO / grant / exercise / qualifying / disqualifying / employment failed); **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **realistic trader ISO 10,000 shares qualifying disposition** ($950K LTCG + $450K AMT preference); **realistic trader ISO premature sale disqualifying** ($450K compensation + $500K capital gain).

`traderview-expense::section_422` is the **IRC В§ 422 Incentive Stock Options (ISO) module** вҖ” trader-critical because traders working at venture-backed companies routinely receive ISO grants where the choice between qualifying disposition (LTCG at 0/15/20% plus В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8%) and disqualifying disposition (ordinary income up to 37%) determines net wealth at exit. Companion to `section_475c2` (mark-to-market for trader stock), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% on qualifying disposition LTCG), `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” note ISOs cannot be held in IRA), `section_53` (AMT credit recovery), `section_56` (AMT preference items).

**В§ 422(b) ISO statutory requirements (6 conditions)**:

| Subsection | Requirement |
|------------|-------------|
| В§ 422(b)(1) | Granted pursuant to SHAREHOLDER-APPROVED PLAN (within 12 months of board adoption) |
| В§ 422(b)(2) | Granted within 10 YEARS of plan adoption |
| В§ 422(b)(3) | Exercise period вүӨ 10 YEARS from grant (5 years for 10%+ shareholders) |
| В§ 422(b)(4) | Option price вүҘ FMV at grant (110% of FMV for 10%+ shareholders) |
| В§ 422(b)(5) | NOT TRANSFERABLE except by will or inheritance |
| В§ 422(b)(6) | Employee through 3 MONTHS BEFORE EXERCISE |

**В§ 422(d) $100,000 annual limit** вҖ” aggregate FMV (at GRANT DATE, not vesting) of ISOs first exercisable in calendar year cannot exceed $100,000. Excess automatically treated as NQSO from grant вҖ” ordinary income on exercise, no AMT preference on the NQSO portion.

**В§ 422(a) qualified-disposition holding periods**:
- **2 YEARS** from GRANT DATE
- **1 YEAR** from EXERCISE DATE
- BOTH must be satisfied вҖ” fails either equals DISQUALIFYING DISPOSITION

**В§ 421(b) disqualifying-disposition treatment** вҖ” ordinary income equals **LESSER of**:
1. FMV at exercise minus exercise price
2. Sale price minus exercise price (capped at actual gain)

Remaining gain capital gain (short-term or long-term from exercise). Employer gets В§ 162 compensation deduction (lost in qualifying disposition).

**В§ 56(b)(3) AMT preference on exercise** вҖ” spread (FMV at exercise minus exercise price) is **POSITIVE AMT adjustment** in year of exercise; AMT basis equals FMV at exercise (NOT exercise price); 28% AMT rate above exemption; subsequent sale generates AMT-basis capital gain DIFFERENT from regular-tax capital gain.

**В§ 422(c)(2) same-year disqualifying disposition REVERSES В§ 56(b)(3) AMT** вҖ” if ISO stock sold in SAME YEAR as exercise (disqualifying), В§ 56(b)(3) AMT adjustment REVERSED; no AMT preference applies; ordinary income on disqualifying disposition replaces AMT preference.

**В§ 53 AMT credit** вҖ” AMT paid in ISO exercise year creates MINIMUM TAX CREDIT recoverable against REGULAR tax in future years; limited to excess of regular tax over tentative minimum tax; recovery often slow (7-15+ years).

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) pre-IPO trader exercises 50,000 ISOs at $1 strike when FMV $50 вҖ” $2.45M AMT spread вҶ’ ~$686K AMT at 28%; (2) same trader sells immediately (disqualifying same-year) вҖ” ordinary income $2.45M at 37% = $906K but В§ 422(c)(2) REVERSES AMT; (3) trader holds 2+1 years, sells at $80 вҖ” entire $79/share LTCG at 23.8% = $951K (vs $1.46M if same-year disqualifying); (4) В§ 422(d) вҖ” $200K ISO vest year 1 вҶ’ $100K treated as NQSO; (5) trader leaves company June 1, exercises September 1 вҖ” В§ 422(b)(6) AUTO-CONVERTS to NQSO.

**Form 3921** вҖ” ISO Exercise Information Statement; employer must furnish to employee by January 31 of year after exercise; reports grant date, exercise date, exercise price, FMV at exercise, shares acquired. Trader must keep for life of stock to track AMT-basis vs regular-basis split.

**California conformity** вҖ” FTB Publication 1004; CA conforms to federal В§ 422 with modifications; CA AMT separately computed under R&TC В§ 17062; CA AMT rate 7%; disqualifying-disposition ordinary income subject to CA wage tax up to 13.3%.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-422`. Thirty-one tests pin: **qualifying disposition LTCG only**; **qualifying disposition AMT preference on exercise**; **disqualifying disposition ordinary income**; **disqualifying lesser-of rule when sale below FMV exercise**; **same-year disqualifying reverses AMT**; **$100K limit no excess**; **$100K limit excess treated as NQSO**; **no shareholder plan ISO fails**; **price below FMV ISO fails**; **left company > 3 months ISO fails**; **exercise period > 10 years fails**; **holding period boundaries 2+1**; **no disposition AMT preference only**; **ISO fails statutory no AMT preference**; **disposition truth table four cells**; **same-year uniquely reverses AMT invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 422(a)-(d) + В§ 421(a)-(b) + В§ 56(b)(3) + В§ 56(b)(3)(B) + В§ 53 + В§ 1411 + В§ 162 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.422-1 through В§ 1.422-5 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.421-1 through В§ 1.421-2 + Form 3921 + IRS Pub. 525 + Rev. Rul. 71-52 + FTB Pub. 1004); **note pins В§ 422(b) six requirements**; **note pins В§ 422(d) $100K limit**; **note pins В§ 422(a) holding periods**; **note pins В§ 421(b) disqualifying lesser rule**; **note pins В§ 56(b)(3) AMT adjustment**; **note pins В§ 53 AMT credit**; **note pins В§ 422(c)(2) same-year reversal**; **note pins В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8%**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins Form 3921**; **note pins California conformity**; **note pins companion modules**; **ISO uniquely generates AMT preference invariant**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_423` is the **IRC В§ 423 Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) module** вҖ” direct companion to `section_422` (ISO вҖ” iter 438), `section_475c2` (trader mark-to-market), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% on net investment income), `section_421` (statutory option framework), `section_424` (constructive ownership rules). Trader-critical because traders working at public companies routinely participate in qualified ESPPs that grant up to 15% discount with optional LOOK-BACK provision (effective discount can substantially exceed 15% due to intra-period appreciation).

**В§ 423(b) ESPP statutory requirements (9 conditions)**:

| Subsection | Requirement |
|------------|-------------|
| В§ 423(b)(1) | Options to EMPLOYEES of granting corp/parent/sub |
| В§ 423(b)(2) | SHAREHOLDER-APPROVED within 12 months of board adoption |
| В§ 423(b)(3) | NO 5%+ SHAREHOLDER (under В§ 424(d) constructive ownership) |
| В§ 423(b)(4) | ALL EMPLOYEES eligible (permitted < 2-year / < 20-hour / seasonal exclusions) |
| В§ 423(b)(5) | SAME RIGHTS AND PRIVILEGES (permitted compensation differentials) |
| В§ 423(b)(6) | Price вүҘ 85% of LESSER of offering or exercise FMV |
| В§ 423(b)(7) | Outer limit **5 YEARS** (or **27 MONTHS** with look-back) |
| В§ 423(b)(8) | **$25,000 ANNUAL ACCRUAL CAP** (FMV at GRANT) |
| В§ 423(b)(9) | NOT TRANSFERABLE except by will/inheritance |

**В§ 423(b)(6) LOOK-BACK PROVISION** вҖ” plan may set purchase price at 85% of LOWER of FMV at offering (grant) date OR FMV at purchase (exercise) date. Combined with intra-period appreciation, effective discount can substantially exceed 15%.

**В§ 421(a) qualifying-disposition holding periods**:
- **2 YEARS** from OFFERING (grant) date
- **1 YEAR** from PURCHASE (exercise) date
- BOTH must be satisfied

**В§ 423(c) qualifying-disposition treatment** вҖ” ordinary income equals **LESSER of**:
1. Discount at offering date (15% Г— FMV at offering)
2. Actual gain on sale (sale price вҲ’ purchase price)

Remaining gain LTCG at 0%/15%/20% + В§ 1411 NIIT 3.8%. **NO FICA** on qualifying disposition ordinary income (Notice 2002-47 + Rev. Rul. 71-52). Employer gets **NO В§ 162 deduction** on qualifying disposition.

**В§ 421(b) disqualifying-disposition treatment** вҖ” ordinary income equals **FULL SPREAD AT PURCHASE** (FMV at purchase вҲ’ actual price); NOT capped at discount. Remaining gain ST or LT capital gain depending on holding from purchase. Employer **GETS В§ 162 compensation deduction**.

**В§ 423(b)(8) $25,000 annual accrual cap** вҖ” right to purchase under all В§ 423 plans of employer cannot accrue at rate exceeding $25,000 of FMV at GRANT DATE (not discount price) per calendar year for any single offering period. Unused capacity NOT carried over.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**:
1. Trader contributes $25K with 15% look-back; stock $100 вҶ’ $130 over 6 months вҶ’ purchases at $85 = 250 shares at effective 34.6% discount; qualifying disposition at $150 = $15/share ordinary income (capped at discount) + $50/share LTCG = $3,750 ordinary + $12,500 LTCG;
2. Same trader sells immediately at $130 (disqualifying) вҖ” $45/share FULL SPREAD as ordinary income = $11,250 ordinary; no LTCG;
3. В§ 423(b)(8) $25K cap on FMV at GRANT (not contribution);
4. В§ 423(b)(3) вҖ” 5%+ shareholder under В§ 424(d) constructive ownership CANNOT participate;
5. В§ 423(b)(7) вҖ” 27-month outer limit WITH look-back; 5-year WITHOUT look-back.

**Form 3922** вҖ” ESPP Transfer Information Statement; employer must furnish to employee by January 31 of year after FIRST TRANSFER of legal title. Trader must keep for life of stock to track qualifying-vs-disqualifying basis split.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-423`. Thirty-two tests pin: **qualifying disposition lesser-of rule**; **qualifying disposition LTCG remainder**; **qualifying disposition FICA exempt**; **look-back effective price**; **no look-back uses purchase FMV**; **disqualifying disposition full-spread ordinary**; **disqualifying no FICA exempt**; **no shareholder approval fails**; **5% shareholder disqualified**; **offering period over outer limit fails**; **unequal rights/privileges fails**; **discount over 15% fails**; **annual accrual over $25K exceeded**; **annual accrual at $25K boundary**; **holding period truth table**; **no disposition no income recognition**; **disposition truth table three cells**; **qualifying uniquely FICA exempt invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 423(a)-(c) + В§ 421(a)-(b) + В§ 424(d) + В§ 1411 + В§ 162 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.423-1 through В§ 1.423-2 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.421-1 through В§ 1.421-2 + IRS Notice 2002-47 + Rev. Rul. 71-52 + Form 3922 + IRS Pub. 525); **note pins В§ 423(b) nine requirements**; **note pins В§ 423(b)(6) look-back**; **note pins В§ 423(b)(8) $25K cap**; **note pins В§ 421(a) holding periods**; **note pins В§ 423(c) qualifying treatment**; **note pins В§ 421(b) disqualifying treatment**; **note pins Notice 2002-47 FICA exemption**; **note pins В§ 424(d) constructive ownership**; **note pins В§ 1411 NIIT**; **note pins trader fact patterns**; **note pins Form 3922**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_444` is the **IRC В§444 fiscal year election module** вҖ” clean rule letting partnerships, S corporations, and personal service corporations (PSCs) elect a non-calendar fiscal year, subject to a strict 3-month deferral cap and a В§7519 "required payment" mechanism that approximates the tax benefit of the deferral. Useful for businesses with strongly seasonal income patterns (agricultural partnerships, retail S-corps with December peak) that benefit from matching tax year to natural business cycle ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 444](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/444), [Cornell LII 26 CFR В§ 1.444-1T](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.444-1T)).

**В§444(b)(2) 3-month deferral cap** вҖ” the deferral period is the months between the elected fiscal year end and the required tax year end. For entities whose required year is the calendar year (Dec 31), only 3 fiscal year ends qualify:

| Elected fiscal year end | Deferral months | Eligible? |
|-------------------------|-----------------|-----------|
| September 30            | 3               | YES (boundary) |
| October 31              | 2               | YES       |
| November 30             | 1               | YES       |
| August 31               | 4               | NO        |
| December 31             | 0 (= required)  | No election needed |

Each boundary individually pinned: `sept_30_election_3_month_deferral_eligible` (exact boundary), `oct_31_election_2_month_deferral_eligible`, `nov_30_election_1_month_deferral_eligible`, `aug_31_election_4_month_deferral_violates`, `dec_31_proposed_equals_required_no_election_needed`, `jan_31_wraps_to_11_month_deferral_violates` (modular arithmetic edge case).

**В§7519 required payment** (partnerships and S corps only) вҖ” annual "deposit" approximating the deferral benefit, due **May 15** of the calendar year following the election year. Filed on **Form 8752**. Module computes: `net_income Г— (deferral_months / 12) Г— (highest_В§1_rate + 1%)`. A $1M-income partnership electing Sept 30 (3-month deferral) at 37% rate pays $1M Г— 3/12 Г— 38% = **$95,000**. Pinned by `partnership_7519_payment_calculation` + `s_corp_subject_to_7519_payment_same_as_partnership` + `nov_election_1_month_yields_smaller_7519_payment` ($31,666 for 1-month deferral) + `higher_rate_yields_higher_7519_payment` (40% rate scenario).

**В§280H PSC deduction limitations** вҖ” Personal Service Corporations elect under В§444 but are NOT subject to В§7519 required payments. Instead, В§280H limits deductions for amounts paid to employee-owners during the deferral period that exceed an applicable percentage of the prior-year base. Pinned by `psc_no_7519_payment_but_280h_applies` (zero required payment + В§280H flag set) + `psc_note_describes_280h`.

**Election unavailable when deferral exceeds 3 months**: any proposed fiscal year yielding > 3 months deferral fails outright. Module surfaces this via `election_available: false` + zero required payment + "DEFERRAL LIMIT EXCEEDED" note. Pinned by `no_7519_payment_when_election_not_available` (Aug 31 fiscal year вҶ’ 4 months deferral вҶ’ no election + no В§7519 payment).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-444`. Eighteen tests pin: **all 3 eligible fiscal-year-end boundaries individually** (Sept 30 / Oct 31 / Nov 30 = 3/2/1 months); Aug 31 4-month violates with DEFERRAL LIMIT EXCEEDED note; **Dec 31 proposed equals required no election needed**; **Jan 31 wraps to 11-month deferral** (modular arithmetic edge); **В§7519 partnership payment $95k** ($1M Г— 3/12 Г— 38%); S-corp same as partnership; **PSC no В§7519 but В§280H applies** (deduction limit flag set); no В§7519 when election unavailable; Nov election yields $31,666 (smaller 1-month deferral); **higher rate yields higher В§7519 payment** (40% вҶ’ $102,500); note describes election-available with mechanism; PSC note describes В§280H + employee-owners; note describes deferral-limit-exceeded; citation mentions all 6 relevant authorities (В§444(a), В§444(b)(2), В§7519, В§280H, В§1.444-1T, Form 8752, May 15); zero income zero В§7519 payment; very large income ($1B вҶ’ $95M В§7519) precision path.

`traderview-expense::section_3406` is the **IRC В§3406 backup withholding module** вҖ” universal rule for any trader or business making "reportable payments" (interest, dividends, rents, royalties, non-employee compensation, broker proceeds, attorney fees, etc.). When any of four statutory triggers fires, the payor MUST withhold **24%** of the payment and remit it to the IRS. The 24% rate is tied to the "fourth lowest rate of tax applicable under В§ 1(c)" per В§ 3406(b)(1)(A); the rate was 28% pre-TCJA and 31% earlier ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 3406](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/3406), [IRS Publication 1281](https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/p1281.pdf)).

**В§3406(a) four trigger conditions** вҖ” any one fires 24% backup withholding:

| Trigger              | В§ 3406(a)(1) | Description                                                                |
|----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **A вҖ” TIN missing** | (A)          | Payee fails to furnish TIN to payor in required manner (missing Form W-9)  |
| **B вҖ” IRS incorrect TIN notice** | (B) | IRS notifies payor via CP 2100 / CP 2100A (BWH-B program) that TIN is incorrect AND payee has not responded |
| **C вҖ” Notified underreporting**  | (C) | IRS-notified payee underreporting (BWH-C program); **interest / dividend payments ONLY** |
| **D вҖ” Certification failure**    | (D) | Payee certification failure under В§ 3406(d) (e.g., missing perjury statement) |

**C trigger is uniquely limited to interest and dividend payments** вҖ” pinned by `c_trigger_fires_for_interest` + `c_trigger_fires_for_dividends` + `c_trigger_does_not_fire_for_rent` (regression) + `c_trigger_does_not_fire_for_non_employee_comp` (regression).

**A trigger applies to all 10 reportable payment types** вҖ” sweep pinned by `all_payment_types_subject_to_a_trigger` (Interest / Dividends / Rent / Royalties / NonEmployeeCompensation / BrokerProceeds / Barter / AttorneyFees / FishingBoatPayments / Other all trigger A withholding when TIN missing).

**Trigger evaluation in statutory order**: module evaluates A вҶ’ B вҶ’ C вҶ’ D and returns the first one that fires. Multiple simultaneous triggers fall to the earlier letter. Pinned by `tin_not_furnished_takes_priority_over_b_notice` (A beats B) + `b_notice_takes_priority_over_c_notice` (B beats C) + `c_notice_takes_priority_over_d_trigger` (C beats D).

**24% rate yields known amounts**:
- $10k payment вҶ’ $2,400 withheld + $7,600 net to payee
- $100k payment вҶ’ $24,000 withheld + $76,000 net to payee
- $1B payment вҶ’ $240M withheld (precision path)

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-3406`. Nineteen tests pin: **no trigger no withholding** (full payment to payee); **A trigger TIN-not-furnished triggers 24% withholding** ($10k вҶ’ $2,400); **B trigger IRS-notified incorrect TIN** triggers withholding; **C trigger fires for interest** + dividends (load-bearing); **C trigger does NOT fire for rent** + non-employee comp (regression); **D trigger certification failure** triggers withholding; **3 trigger precedence pins** (A > B, B > C, C > D); **withholding rate 24%** (2400bp); withholding at $100k payment = $24k; **$1B precision** = $240M withheld; zero payment zero withholding; **all 10 payment types subject to A trigger** sweep; triggered note describes trigger + 24%; untriggered note describes no trigger; **citation mentions all 4 trigger subsections** + В§ 3406(b)(1)(A) rate + CP 2100 + Publication 1281.

`traderview-expense::section_25c` is the **IRC В§25C Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit module with OBBBA termination** вҖ” completes the OBBBA-terminated IRA-2022 energy-credit triplet (`В§25C` home efficiency + `В§25D` residential clean energy + `В§30D` clean vehicle). Provides a **30% nonrefundable credit** with a layered cap structure totaling **up to $3,200/year** for energy-efficiency improvements to a taxpayer's residence. **OBBBA В§ 70425 ACCELERATED termination** to property placed in service after **December 31, 2025** вҖ” wiping the IRA-scheduled 2032 sunset by 7 years.

**Cap structure** (post-IRA-2022 redesign, pre-OBBBA termination):

| Component | Cap | Citation |
|-----------|-----|----------|
| General $1,200 envelope (windows + doors + insulation + non-heat-pump energy property + audits) | **$1,200/year** | В§ 25C(b)(1) |
| Windows + skylights (within general envelope) | **$600 aggregate** | В§ 25C(b)(2)(A) |
| Exterior doors (within general envelope) | **$250/door, $500 aggregate** | В§ 25C(b)(2)(B) |
| Any single qualified energy property item (within general envelope) | **$600/item** | В§ 25C(b)(2)(C) |
| Home energy audit (within general envelope) | **$150** | В§ 25C(b)(2)(D) |
| Insulation (within general envelope) | No sub-cap | В§ 25C(b)(1) |
| **Heat pumps + heat pump water heaters + biomass stoves (SEPARATE)** | **$2,000 above + beyond the $1,200** | В§ 25C(b)(3) |
| **Total annual maximum** | **$3,200** ($1,200 general + $2,000 heat pump) | В§ 25C(b) |

**Nonrefundable, no carryforward** вҖ” distinct from В§ 25D which has indefinite carryforward. В§ 25C credit limited to current-year tax liability; excess lost.

**OBBBA placed-in-service requirement**: property must be PLACED IN SERVICE by 2025-12-31 (not just paid for or contracted). The "placed in service" requirement is strict вҖ” installation must be complete.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-25c`. Twenty-six tests pin: **full $3,200 maxes both envelopes** (worked example $5K windows + $3K doors (2) + $5K insulation + $5K energy (2 items) + $500 audit + $10K heat pump вҶ’ exactly $3,200); **OBBBA termination after 2025-12-31** + В§ 70425 + "TERMINATED" citation; **at 2025-12-31 boundary still eligible** (strict greater-than); **windows capped at $600** + windows under cap not capped; **doors $250/door 1-door**; **doors $500 aggregate 3-doors** + aggregate cap binds with more doors; **energy property $600/item single + multiple items**; **audit capped at $150** + audit under cap not capped; **heat pump capped at $2,000**; **heat pump SEPARATE from general envelope** (regression-critical вҖ” $600 windows + $2,000 heat pump = $2,600, both caps apply independently); **insulation no sub-cap** + insulation bounded only by general $1,200; **zero cost zero credit**; **negative inputs clamped**; **citation pins В§ 25C(a) + (b)(1) + (b)(3) + OBBBA В§ 70425**; **date boundary Dec 30/31/Jan 1 three-day triple-test**; **general envelope caps at $1,200**; **doors per-door cap below aggregate**; **doors per-door cap binds high-cost single door**; **windows at $600 boundary exactly**; **heat pump at $2,000 boundary exactly**; **total caps at $3,200 in high-spend scenario** ($50K windows + $50K doors + $50K insulation + $50K energy + $5K audit + $50K heat pump вҶ’ exactly $3,200).

`traderview-expense::section_25d` is the **IRC В§25D Residential Clean Energy Credit module with OBBBA termination** вҖ” pairs with `section_30d` (Clean Vehicle Credit) as the two OBBBA-terminated IRA-2022 incentives. Provides a **30% nonrefundable credit** for qualifying clean energy property installed at the taxpayer's RESIDENCE (primary or secondary home). **Pure rentals never qualified** вҖ” a landlord whose tenants live in the unit but who does not himself reside there cannot claim В§25D. OBBBA did not change the residence requirement; it only accelerated the sunset.

**OBBBA В§ 70426 termination**: expenditures made **after December 31, 2025** ineligible. IRA 2022 had originally scheduled the 30% rate through 2032 with a 26%/22%/0% step-down for 2033/2034/2035; OBBBA wiped out all step-down years.

**Qualifying property** (В§25D(d)):

| Property type | Qualifying threshold |
|---------------|----------------------|
| Solar electric (rooftop PV) | None |
| Solar water heater | None |
| Fuel cell | $500 / 0.5 kW capacity sublimit |
| Small wind energy | None |
| Geothermal heat pump | None |
| **Battery storage** | **вүҘ 3 kWh capacity** (added 2023 вҖ” stand-alone now qualifies under В§ 25D(d)(6)) |
| Biomass fuel | Removed end of 2022, moved to В§ 25C |

**Nonrefundable + indefinite carryforward** (В§25D(c)): credit limited to current-year tax liability; unused portion carries forward INDEFINITELY to succeeding tax years and adds to that year's В§25D credit allowance.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-25d`. Twenty-three tests pin: **solar panels 30% credit** ($30K cost вҶ’ $9K credit + fully usable against $100K tax liability); **OBBBA termination after 2025-12-31** + В§ 70426 + "TERMINATED" citation; **at 2025-12-31 boundary still eligible** (strict greater-than); **one day after cutoff (Jan 1) terminated**; **pure rental no-residence-requirement fail** + "USED AS A RESIDENCE" + "Pure rental" note regressions; **biomass fuel no longer qualifying** + В§ 25C citation regression (moved to other module); **battery storage above 3 kWh qualifies**; **battery storage at 3 kWh boundary qualifies** (вүҘ strict); **battery storage below 3 kWh fails** + В§ 25D(d)(6) + "3 kWh" citation regression; **carryforward when credit exceeds tax liability** ($30K solar = $9K credit + $5K tax вҶ’ $4K carryforward); **prior-year carryforward adds to current-year** ($20K solar = $6K + $3K prior = $9K total + $10K tax вҶ’ all used); **carryforward preserved through termination** (post-2025 expenditure produces no new credit but prior carryforward returned for separate Form 5695 line); **solar water heater + geothermal + small wind + fuel cell all qualify** (4 property-type-eligibility regressions); **zero cost no credit**; **negative inputs clamped**; **termination check precedence over residence** (regression вҖ” termination fires first even when residence test would also fail); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 25D(a) + В§ 25D(c) + В§ 25D(d) + В§ 25D(d)(6) + OBBBA В§ 70426); **date boundary Dec 30/31 + Jan 1 three-day triple-test**; **$300K commercial-scale solar full credit ($90K)**; **$1M ultra-large solar with low tax creates $280K carryforward**.

`traderview-expense::section_30d` is the **IRC В§30D Clean Vehicle Credit module with OBBBA termination** вҖ” the post-IRA 2022 bifurcated $7,500 credit for new clean vehicles. Trader and landlord-relevant for any EV purchase. IRA 2022 redesigned the credit into a **$3,750 critical-minerals prong** + **$3,750 battery-components prong**, scheduled to run through 2032. **OBBBA В§ 70424 (eff. 2025-09-30) TERMINATED the credit** for vehicles acquired after September 30, 2025 вҖ” accelerating the sunset by more than 7 years.

**Credit structure** (pre-termination):

| Component | Amount | Citation |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Critical-minerals sourcing test | **$3,750** | В§ 30D(e)(1) |
| Battery-components sourcing test | **$3,750** | В§ 30D(e)(2) |
| Both prongs satisfied | **$7,500 total** | В§ 30D(e) |

**MSRP caps** (В§ 30D(f)(11)):
- Cars: **$55,000**
- SUVs / trucks / vans: **$80,000**

Vehicle MSRP exceeding the applicable cap вҶ’ credit = $0 (hard block, not gradual).

**Income phaseout** (В§ 30D(f)(10)) вҖ” **HARD CUTOFF, not gradual**:

| Filing status | MAGI threshold |
|---------------|----------------|
| Single / MFS | $150,000 |
| Head of Household | $225,000 |
| MFJ / QW | $300,000 |

Above the threshold вҶ’ credit = $0. В§ 30D(f)(10)(B)(iii) lets the taxpayer elect the LESSER of current-year or prior-year MAGI.

**OBBBA termination + IRS binding-contract carve-out**: vehicles acquired after 2025-09-30 are ineligible UNLESS the taxpayer had a written binding contract in place AND made a payment on or before 2025-09-30. The "acquired" date snaps to the contract + payment date for the carve-out.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-30d`. Twenty-five tests pin: **full $7,500 credit for eligible vehicle** (both prongs + Car + $40K MSRP + $100K MAGI Single); **half credit critical-minerals only** ($3,750); **half credit battery-components only** ($3,750); **no credit neither test met**; **OBBBA termination acquired after 2025-09-30** ($0 + В§ 70424 + "TERMINATED" citation); **at 2025-09-30 boundary still eligible** (strict greater-than); **one day after cutoff (Oct 1) terminated**; **binding-contract carve-out preserves credit after cutoff** + "binding-contract carve-out" note regression; **no-carve-out no-payment blocks post-cutoff**; **MAGI over $150K Single no credit** + В§ 30D(f)(10) + "HARD CUTOFF" citation; **MAGI at $150K Single boundary eligible** (вүӨ strict); **MAGI $150K + 1 cent blocks**; **MFJ uses $300K threshold**; **HoH uses $225K threshold**; **MFS uses $150K not half of MFJ $300K** (regression вҖ” MFS gets Single-level not half-of-MFJ); **car MSRP over $55K blocks credit** + В§ 30D(f)(11) citation; **car MSRP at $55K boundary eligible**; **SUV MSRP up to $80K eligible**; **SUV MSRP over $80K blocks credit**; **pre-termination 2024 eligible with all conditions**; **termination check order independent of other blocks** (high MSRP + post-termination вҶ’ termination fires first regression); **full credit breakdown matches components**; **citations pin authorities** (В§ 30D(e) + OBBBA В§ 70424 + В§ 30D(f)(10) + В§ 30D(f)(11)); **year boundary 2024 pre-termination 2026 post**; **date boundary 2025-09-29 / 30 / Oct 1 three-day triple-test** (29 eligible / 30 eligible / Oct 1 terminated regression).

`traderview-expense::section_305` is the **IRC В§305 stock dividend distribution classification module** вҖ” trader-relevant for anyone holding corporate stock who receives a stock dividend or stock right. В§305(a) general rule excludes stock-on-stock distributions from gross income (the distribution simply changes per-share basis under В§307(a)). В§305(b) enumerates **5 exceptions** making the distribution taxable as a В§301 distribution. В§305(c) creates deemed distributions from certain capital-structure events.

**В§305(b) five taxable exceptions** with priority ordering:

| Sub-section | Trigger | Description |
|-------------|---------|-------------|
| (b)(1) | In lieu of money | Any shareholder may elect cash instead of stock вҖ” taxes ALL shareholders |
| (b)(2) | Disproportionate | Some get property; others' proportionate interest increases |
| (b)(3) | Common-and-preferred | Some common-holders get preferred; others get common |
| (b)(4) | On preferred stock | Distribution on preferred (limited convertible-ratio carve-out) |
| (b)(5) | Convertible preferred | Convertible preferred stock without anti-dilution safe harbor |
| (c) | Deemed distribution | Capital-structure event (conversion-ratio adjust, redemption premium accrual) |

**В§305(a) non-taxable path вҶ’ В§307(a) basis allocation**: when no exception triggers, the shareholder allocates the OLD share basis between OLD and NEW shares in proportion to FMV. Old basis $10k + old FMV $19k + new FMV $1k вҶ’ total FMV $20k вҶ’ new shares allocated $10k Г— (1 / 20) = $500; old shares retain $9,500. Holding period of old shares tacks to new under В§1223(5).

**В§305(b) taxable path вҶ’ В§301 distribution treatment**: dividend up to current+accumulated E&P; basis recovery for excess over E&P; capital gain for residue. New shares take FMV basis under В§301(d) (no allocation from old basis).

**Priority ordering** when multiple exception facts are present: (b)(1) вҶ’ (b)(2) вҶ’ (b)(3) вҶ’ (b)(4) вҶ’ (b)(5) вҶ’ (c). The (c) deemed-distribution path fires ONLY when no В§305(b) exception applies.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-305`. Twenty-two tests pin: **no-exception non-taxable В§305(a) general rule** + **В§307(a) basis allocation** ($10k basis + 1/20 FMV ratio = $500 new + $9,500 old); **В§305(b)(1) in lieu of money triggers taxability**; **В§305(b)(2) disproportionate triggers**; **В§305(b)(3) common-and-preferred triggers**; **В§305(b)(4) distribution on preferred triggers**; **В§305(b)(5) convertible preferred without safe harbor triggers**; **В§305(c) deemed distribution triggers (lowest priority)** (regression target вҖ” only fires when no (b) exception); **(b)(1) short-circuits other exceptions** (priority ordering); **(b)(2) short-circuits lower priority**; **В§301 dividend treatment when taxable** ($1k all dividend when E&P sufficient); **excess over E&P вҶ’ basis recovery** ($200 dividend + $800 basis recovery when E&P = $200); zero E&P вҶ’ all basis recovery; **taxable new basis = FMV** ($1k regression вҖ” distinguishes from В§305(a) В§307 allocation); **taxable old basis unchanged**; non-taxable zero FMV no allocation; non-taxable proportional 50/50 split; **citation mentions all 7 relevant authorities** (В§305(a) + В§305(b) + В§305(c) + В§307(a) + В§1223(5) + В§301 + "5 taxable exceptions"); note describes non-taxable + taxable paths; **$1B precision** ($500M new share basis allocation).

`traderview-expense::section_331` is the **IRC В§331 shareholder corporate liquidation gain/loss module** вҖ” the shareholder-side counterpart to `section_336` (corporate-side of the same transaction) and bookend with `section_351` (formation). On complete liquidation, В§331(a) treats the liquidating distribution as in full payment in exchange for stock under В§1001 вҖ” gain or loss = FMV of property received minus shareholder's adjusted basis in stock, with capital character if stock was a capital asset.

**Three regimes determine the shareholder's tax outcome**:

| Path | Trigger | Outcome |
|------|---------|---------|
| В§331(a) standard | Complete liquidation + non-corporate or < 80% shareholder | Capital gain/loss (ST or LT per holding period); В§334(a) FMV basis in distributed property |
| В§332 non-recognition | Corporate parent owning вүҘ 80% voting + вүҘ 80% value (В§1504(a)(2)) | NO gain or loss recognized; В§334(b) carryover basis from subsidiary |
| В§302 redemption | Partial (not complete) liquidation | В§331 does NOT apply; redemption analysis with potential В§316 dividend characterization |

**В§331(b) excludes В§301 dividend treatment**: liquidating distributions are NOT dividends regardless of the corporation's E&P. This matters because qualified dividend rates can differ from LTCG rates depending on year and bracket.

**В§334(a) shareholder basis** in property received = **FMV at distribution** (a "step-up"). The corporation's historical basis is irrelevant at the shareholder level. This is the bookend with В§336 вҖ” the liquidating corporation recognizes gain/loss as if it sold at FMV; the shareholder takes that FMV as their basis going forward.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-331`. Twenty tests pin: **baseline LT capital gain** ($50k cash + $100k FMV вҲ’ $100k basis = $50k LT gain over 400-day holding); **300-day holding ST gain**; **365-day boundary still ST** (regression вҖ” В§1222 requires holding > 1 year); **366-day boundary LT**; **LT capital loss path** ($300k basis exceeds $150k realized вҶ’ вҲ’$150k LT loss); **ST capital loss path**; **В§334(a) shareholder basis in property = FMV** ($100k FMV property вҶ’ $100k carryover basis); **cash-only distribution вҶ’ zero basis in property**; **В§332 corporate-parent 80%/80% non-recognition** (regardless of magnitude); **В§332 basis carryover modeled as zero** (signaling caller to use sub-side В§334(b)); **В§332 takes precedence over В§331 capital gain** ($10M cash + 80% trigger вҶ’ non-recognition); **partial liquidation no В§331** (redirect to В§302 redemption analysis); zero net when amount realized = basis; **citation mentions all 7 relevant authorities** (В§331(a) + В§331(b) + В§1001 + В§332 + В§334(a) + В§334(b) + В§1504(a)(2)); partial-liquidation citation mentions В§302; note baseline describes LT gain; **note В§332 describes "80%/80% threshold" + non-recognition + В§334(b)**; note partial liquidation redirects to В§302; **$1B precision case** ($500M cash + $500M FMV вҲ’ $100M basis = $900M gain); zero basis full amount realized is gain.

`traderview-expense::section_332` is the **IRC В§332 complete-liquidation-of-subsidiary non-recognition module** вҖ” the parent-corporation tax-free exception to the general В§331/В§336 FMV-recognition rules for corporate liquidation. Pairs with `section_331` (shareholder side default) and `section_336` (corporate side default) вҖ” when В§ 332 applies, both parties get full non-recognition; when it fails, both parties revert to FMV recognition.

**В§ 332(b) four-prong test** вҖ” ALL four prongs required:

| Prong | Requirement | Citation |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| (1) Voting | Parent owns **вүҘ 80% of total voting power** of subsidiary stock | В§ 332(b)(2) + В§ 1504(a)(2) |
| (2) Value | Parent owns **вүҘ 80% of total value** of subsidiary stock | В§ 332(b)(2) + В§ 1504(a)(2) |
| (3) Continuous ownership | 80% maintained from plan-adoption date through final distribution | В§ 332(b)(3) |
| (4) Complete liquidation | All property distributed, all subsidiary stock cancelled | В§ 332(b)(1) |

**Three statutory consequences when В§ 332 applies**:
- **В§ 332(a) parent non-recognition** вҖ” no gain/loss to parent on receipt of property
- **В§ 337(a) subsidiary non-recognition** вҖ” no gain/loss to subsidiary on distribution
- **В§ 334(b)(1) carryover basis** вҖ” parent takes subsidiary's adjusted basis (NOT FMV); preserves tax attributes including NOL carryovers under В§ 381

**When В§ 332 fails (any prong missing)** вҶ’ falls to default rules: parent recognizes under В§ 331 (FMV вҲ’ stock basis), subsidiary recognizes under В§ 336 (FMV вҲ’ adjusted basis), parent takes FMV basis in distributed property. Built-in gains/losses fully recognized on both sides.

**Worked example (compliant)**: Parent owns 100% of Sub. Sub has $1M FMV property with $400K basis. Parent stock basis $200K. В§ 332 applies вҶ’ (1) Parent $0 gain (vs $800K under В§ 331); (2) Sub $0 gain (vs $600K under В§ 336); (3) Parent takes $400K carryover basis (vs $1M FMV). $1.4M of recognition deferred.

**Worked example (failed at 75%)**: Same fact pattern but parent owns only 75% вҶ’ В§ 332 fails. Parent recognizes $800K gain (В§ 331), Sub recognizes $600K gain (В§ 336), parent takes $1M FMV basis. Full $1.4M recognized immediately.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-332`. Eighteen tests pin: **full compliance 100% owned complete liquidation** вҶ’ no gain on either side + carryover basis; **at 80%/80% boundary applies** (вүҘ strict); **79.99% voting fails** + В§ 331 + В§ 336 recognition; **79.99% value fails**; **continuous ownership not maintained fails**; **incomplete liquidation fails**; **all four prongs individually required** (single-test 4-prong-fail-independence regression); **В§ 334(b) carryover basis** ($1M FMV but $200K subsidiary basis вҶ’ parent takes $200K not $1M); **В§ 332 fails вҶ’ FMV basis** (regression); **В§ 337(a) parallel subsidiary non-recognition** (regression вҖ” when В§ 332 applies on parent side, subsidiary also doesn't recognize even with built-in gain); **parent loss when В§ 332 fails and FMV below stock basis** ($200K loss recognized); **citations pin authorities** (В§ 332(a) + В§ 337(a) + В§ 334(b) + В§ 331 + В§ 336); **negative inputs clamped**; **voting and value tests independent**; **worked example clean parent-sub liquidation** (no gain + $400K carryover basis); **worked example failed liquidation recognizes everything** ($800K parent + $600K subsidiary + $1M FMV basis); **all four test flags returned**.

`traderview-expense::section_336` is the **IRC В§336 corporate liquidation gain/loss module** вҖ” corporate-side counterpart to В§331 (shareholder) and В§332 (parent-subsidiary tax-free exception). For any C-corp in complete liquidation, В§336(a) requires the liquidating corporation to recognize gain OR loss as if the distributed property had been sold to the distributee at FMV. Several anti-abuse loss-disallowance rules layer on top ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 336](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/336), [Tax Adviser вҖ” Summary of tax rules for liquidating corporations](https://www.thetaxadviser.com/issues/2020/oct/tax-rules-liquidating-corporations/)).

**В§ 336(a) general rule** вҖ” gain or loss recognized as if property sold at FMV. Pinned by `standard_gain_recognized` (FMV $1M вҲ’ basis $600k = $400k recognized gain) + `standard_loss_recognized` (FMV $400k вҲ’ basis $600k = $200k recognized loss).

**В§ 336(b) liability rule** вҖ” when distributed property is subject to a liability OR the shareholder assumes a liability, FMV is treated as NOT LESS than the liability amount. Prevents debt-laden property from generating artificial loss. Module computes `effective_fmv = max(fmv, liability)`. Pinned by `liability_higher_than_fmv_boosts_fmv` (FMV $400k + liability $700k вҶ’ effective FMV $700k вҶ’ $100k gain instead of $200k loss).

**В§ 336(d)(1) related-party loss disallowance** вҖ” NO LOSS recognized on distribution to a related party (> 50% ownership) UNLESS distribution is PRO RATA to all shareholders AND property is not "disqualified property" (acquired via В§ 351 or capital contribution within 5 years). Pinned by:
- `related_party_non_pro_rata_distribution_loss_disallowed` (loss fully disallowed)
- `related_party_pro_rata_no_d1_disallowance_if_not_disqualified` (loss allowed when both conditions met)
- `related_party_pro_rata_with_disqualified_property_d1_disallows` (disqualified property still disallows)
- `unrelated_party_no_d1_disallowance` (regression вҖ” non-related party preserves loss even with non-pro-rata)

**В§ 336(d)(2) anti-tax-avoidance / 5-year rule** вҖ” loss disallowed to the extent attributable to the **built-in loss EXISTING AT THE TIME** the property was acquired by the liquidating corp via В§ 351 transfer or capital contribution within 5 years of the liquidation. Prevents loss-trafficking via "stuffing" loss property into a corp pre-liquidation. Module computes `d2_disallowed = min(bil_at_contribution, current_realized_loss)`. Pinned by `d2_built_in_loss_disallowed_at_contribution` ($200k current loss, $150k BIL at contribution вҶ’ $150k disallowed + $50k still recognized) + `d2_disallowance_capped_at_realized_loss` (BIL $500k > realized $100k вҶ’ capped at $100k) + `d2_not_applicable_outside_5_year_window` (regression вҖ” property acquired > 5 years ago preserves loss).

**В§ 336(d)(3) В§ 332 subsidiary exception** вҖ” NO gain or loss recognized to liquidating corp in a В§ 332 parent-subsidiary liquidation (parent owns 80%+ voting stock + 80%+ value). Parent takes carryover basis under В§ 334(b). This short-circuits ALL other В§336 provisions. Pinned by `section_332_subsidiary_no_gain_no_loss` + `section_332_overrides_all_other_provisions` (even with related-party + disqualified property + BIL).

**В§336(d)(1) takes precedence over В§336(d)(2)**: when both apply, the related-party rule disallows the entire loss; the В§336(d)(2) BIL-disallowed amount surfaces separately but doesn't matter because loss already zero. Pinned by `d1_takes_precedence_over_d2`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-336`. Twenty-one tests pin: **В§336(a) standard gain recognized** ($400k); standard loss recognized ($200k); **В§336(b) liability boosts FMV** ($700k liability adjusts $400k FMV upward вҶ’ $100k gain instead of $200k loss); liability below FMV no adjustment; **В§336(d)(1) related-party non-pro-rata loss disallowed** + related-party pro-rata no disallowance if not disqualified + with disqualified property still disallows + **unrelated party no disallowance** (regression); **В§336(d)(2) BIL at contribution disallowed** ($150k of $200k loss disallowed) + **capped at realized loss** ($500k BIL > $100k realized вҶ’ $100k cap) + **not applicable outside 5-year window**; **В§336(d)(3) В§ 332 subsidiary NO gain NO loss** + В§ 332 overrides all other provisions (even with related-party + BIL + 5-year window); **В§336(d)(1) takes precedence over В§336(d)(2)** (combined scenario); note describes В§ 336(a) + В§ 336(b) + В§ 336(d)(1) + В§ 336(d)(2); citation mentions all relevant authorities (В§336(a) / (b) / (d)(1) / (d)(2) / (d)(3) / В§331 / В§334(b)); $10B precision; zero gain zero loss no-op.

`traderview-expense::section_354` is the **IRC В§ 354 вҖ” Exchanges of Stock and Securities in Certain Reorganizations module** (iter 526) вҖ” direct corporate-reorganization-nonrecognition companion to `section_368` (corporate reorganization framework вҖ” В§ 354 requires В§ 368(a) plan), `section_356` (boot computation вҖ” recognizes gain to extent of boot received in В§ 354/В§ 355 exchange), `section_358` (substituted basis for property received in В§ 354/В§ 355/В§ 356 exchange), `section_355` (parallel divisive nonrecognition framework for spin-offs), `section_361` (corporate transferor nonrecognition + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.361-1), `section_362` (acquirer's basis in property received), `section_367` (foreign corp exchanges override В§ 354 nonrecognition вҖ” iter 524), `section_1223` (holding period tacking В§ 1223(1)), `section_351` (corporate formation parallel nonrecognition framework), `section_332` (parent-subsidiary liquidation), `section_1001` (general realization and recognition rules). В§ 354 provides general nonrecognition treatment for the EXCHANGE of stock and securities pursuant to a В§ 368(a) reorganization вҖ” preserves tax basis and defers recognition until ultimate disposition.

**В§ 354(a)(1) GENERAL RULE**: no gain or loss recognized if stock or securities in a corporation that is a party to a reorganization are, in pursuance of the plan of reorganization, exchanged SOLELY for stock or securities in such corporation OR in another corporation a party to the reorganization.

**В§ 354(a)(2)(A) SECURITIES PRINCIPAL-AMOUNT BOOT**: if (i) principal amount of securities RECEIVED exceeds principal amount of securities SURRENDERED, OR (ii) securities are received and NO securities are surrendered, then the fair market value of the excess principal amount is treated as money received (boot) вҖ” taxable up to gain realized per В§ 356(a).

**В§ 354(a)(2)(B) NONQUALIFIED PREFERRED STOCK (NQPS) BOOT**: NQPS per В§ 351(g)(2) definition (mandatory redemption + dividend-yield-only preferred stock features) treated as boot.

**В§ 354(b) ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS for В§ 368(a)(1)(D) divisive split**: (1) acquiring corp must acquire substantially all assets of transferor; (2) transferor must distribute everything received in the exchange PLUS retained assets to its shareholders. В§ 354(b) prevents D-type reorgs from avoiding taxation when transferor retains assets.

**В§ 354(c) RIGHTS/WARRANTS**: rights to acquire stock treated as "securities" for purposes of В§ 354 per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.354-1(e).

**Eight reorganization types**: AStatutoryMerger (В§ 368(a)(1)(A)), BStockForStock (В§ 368(a)(1)(B)), CAssetAcquisitionForVotingStock (В§ 368(a)(1)(C)), DDivisiveSplitWithSection354b (В§ 368(a)(1)(D) вҖ” additional В§ 354(b) requirements), ERecapitalization (В§ 368(a)(1)(E)), FMereChangeIdentityForm (В§ 368(a)(1)(F)), GBankruptcyReorg (В§ 368(a)(1)(G)), NotASection368Reorganization (full В§ 1001 recognition).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, FullNonrecognitionUnderSection354a1, PartialBootSection354a2aSecuritiesExcess, NonqualifiedPreferredStockBootSection354a2b, Section354bAdditionalRequirementsNotSatisfied, NotASection368ReorganizationFullRecognition.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-354`. Twenty-one tests pin: **full nonrecognition В§ 354(a)(1)** ($100M securities вҶ” $100M securities + $50M gain deferred); **securities principal excess treated as boot** ($30M excess principal = $30M boot); **NQPS treated as boot** ($20M NQPS = $20M boot); **NQPS plus principal excess combined boot** ($10M + $5M = $15M); **boot capped at realized gain** (boot exceeds gain вҶ’ capped); **D reorg without substantially all fails** (В§ 354(b)(1)(A)); **D reorg without full distribution fails** (В§ 354(b)(1)(B)); **D reorg with all requirements satisfied compliant**; **not a В§ 368 reorg full recognition** (В§ 1001 + continuity of interest + continuity of business enterprise); **B reorg + C reorg + E recap + F change + G bankruptcy all full nonrecognition**; **action references Form 1120 + Schedule M-3**; **coordination note references В§ 368 + В§ 356 + В§ 358 + В§ 355 + В§ 361 + В§ 362 + В§ 367 + В§ 1223 + В§ 351**; **citation pins В§ 354(a)(1)-(c) + В§ 356 + В§ 358 + В§ 368(a) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.354-1**; **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.354-1 citation constant pinned**; **zero securities zero boot**; **extreme value does not overflow** (saturating arithmetic); **securities received less than surrendered no boot** (asymmetric direction).

`traderview-expense::section_367` is the **IRC В§ 367 вҖ” Foreign Corporations module** (iter 524) вҖ” direct cross-border-reorganization companion to `section_332` (parent-subsidiary liquidation вҖ” nonrecognition overridden by В§ 367), `section_351` (corporation transfer in exchange for stock вҖ” overridden by В§ 367(a)), `section_354` (reorganization stock exchange вҖ” overridden), `section_355` (corporate division вҖ” В§ 367(e) may apply), `section_361` (corporate reorganization transfer вҖ” overridden by В§ 367), `section_368` (corporate reorganization framework вҖ” Notice 2024-16 В§ 961(c) carryover for inbound reorgs), `section_721` (partnership contribution вҖ” coordinate with В§ 367(d) intangible regime), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (CFC US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_959` (PTEP вҖ” iter 512), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC вҖ” iter 520), `section_961` (CFC basis adjustments вҖ” iter 522 Notice 2024-16 carryover), `section_245a` (DRD вҖ” iter 502 вҖ” coordinates with В§ 367(b) inbound E&P inclusion), `section_1248` (gain on CFC stock sale recharacterized as dividend). В§ 367 overrides nonrecognition treatment that would otherwise apply under В§ 332/351/354/355/356/361/721 when a transfer involves a foreign corporation вҖ” prevents US shareholders from using cross-border reorganizations to permanently avoid US tax on appreciated property.

**В§ 367(a)(1) OUTBOUND TRANSFER GENERAL RULE**: a US person transferring property to a foreign corporation in a В§ 332/351/354/356/361 exchange is treated as transferring property in a TAXABLE exchange at fair market value, recognizing built-in gain. Foreign corp NOT treated as corporation for this transfer.

**В§ 367(a)(2)/(8) GAIN-RECOGNITION AGREEMENT (GRA)**: exception for stock or securities of a foreign corp that is party to the exchange; Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(a)-8 5-year deferral. Triggering event within 5 years includes entire deferred gain plus interest.

**TCJA В§ 14102(e) AMENDMENT** (effective for transfers after December 31, 2017): REPEALED former В§ 367(a)(3) active trade or business exception вҖ” outbound tangible property transfers now subject to FULL В§ 367(a)(1) gain recognition with no active-business carve-out.

**В§ 367(b) INBOUND TRANSFER REGS**: when foreign corp transfers property to domestic corp in В§ 332 liquidation or В§ 368(a)(1) reorg, Treasury regulations may require domestic acquiring corp to include foreign corp's all post-1986 accumulated earnings and profits as deemed dividend per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(b)-3.

**В§ 367(d) INTANGIBLE OUTBOUND TRANSFER**: US person transferring В§ 936(h)(3)(B) intangible property to foreign corp in В§ 351 or В§ 361 exchange treated as TRANSFERRING the intangible in exchange for deemed annual royalty payments over useful life of property commensurate with income attributable to the intangible.

**TCJA В§ 14221 INTANGIBLE DEFINITION EXPANSION** (effective for transfers after December 31, 2017): expanded В§ 936(h)(3)(B) intangible to include goodwill + going-concern value + workforce-in-place + any other item the value of which is not attributable to tangible property or services of individuals.

**В§ 367(e)**: distributions to foreign corporate shareholders. **Form 926** (Return by a U.S. Transferor of Property to a Foreign Corp) filing required for В§ 367(a) transfers; **Form 8865** for foreign partnership transfers.

**Three transfer directions**: Outbound (US person вҶ’ foreign corp вҖ” В§ 367(a)/(d)), Inbound (foreign corp вҶ’ domestic corp вҖ” В§ 367(b)), ForeignToForeignWithUsShareholder (В§ 367(b) regs may apply if US shareholder participation per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(b)-4).

**Four property types**: TangibleProperty, StockOfForeignCorporationPartyToExchange (В§ 367(a)(8) GRA available), Section936IntangibleIncludingGoodwill (В§ 367(d) deemed royalty), InventoryOrReceivables (no exception available).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, OutboundTangibleFullGainRecognition, OutboundStockGainRecognitionAgreementAvailable, OutboundIntangibleDeemedRoyalty, InboundEarningsAndProfitsInclusion, ForeignToForeignNoUsConsequence.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-367`. Twenty-one tests pin: **outbound tangible full gain recognition** ($100M FMV - $30M basis = $70M gain + Form 926 + TCJA В§ 14102(e)); **outbound stock with GRA election deferred** (5-year deferral); **outbound stock without GRA full recognition**; **outbound intangible deemed royalty over useful life** ($100M / 10 years = $10M annual royalty + TCJA В§ 14221 + goodwill); **outbound intangible useful life zero capped at one**; **inbound earnings and profits inclusion** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(b)-3 + Notice 2024-16); **foreign-to-foreign no US consequence with US shareholder** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(b)-4 + В§ 1248); **outbound inventory full recognition**; **TCJA В§ 14102 effective year pins 2018**; **TCJA В§ 14221 intangible expansion year pins 2018**; **active trade business exception repeal year pins 2018**; **action references Form 926 + Form 1120 Schedule D**; **coordination note references all international siblings** (В§ 332 + В§ 351 + В§ 354 + В§ 355 + В§ 361 + В§ 368 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 959 + В§ 960 + В§ 961 + В§ 245A + В§ 1248); **citation pins В§ 367(a)(1) + TCJA В§ 14102(e) + Pub. L. 115-97**; **intangible citation pins В§ 367(d) + В§ 936(h)(3)(B) + TCJA В§ 14221**; **GRA citation pins В§ 367(a)(8) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(a)-8**; **inbound citation pins В§ 367(b) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.367(b)-3 + Notice 2024-16**; **zero gain when basis equals FMV** (boundary); **zero gain when basis exceeds FMV** (saturating sub defense); **extreme value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **realistic corp transfers factory to Ireland subsidiary** ($50B FMV - $10B basis = $40B recognized gain).

`traderview-expense::section_351` is the **IRC В§351 corporate formation non-recognition module** вҖ” the entry-side counterpart to `section_336` (corporate-liquidation exit-side). Foundational to every trader who forms a trading C-corp, S-corp, or LLC that elects corporate taxation by contributing securities, equipment, or office property in exchange for stock. Stable section since 1954; no material TCJA or OBBBA amendments.

**В§368(c) 80% control test** вҖ” both prongs independently required immediately after the exchange:

| Prong | Threshold |
|-------|-----------|
| Voting power of all voting stock | вүҘ 80% |
| Each non-voting class outstanding | вүҘ 80% |

**Recognition rules**:
- **В§351(a)** вҖ” general rule: no gain or loss recognized.
- **В§351(b)(1)** вҖ” boot gain: recognized to the extent of boot received, capped by realized gain.
- **В§351(b)(2)** вҖ” **no loss recognized under В§351 even with boot**. Loss is preserved in the substituted stock basis.
- **В§351(d)** вҖ” services exclusion: pure-services contributors are disqualified from this transferor's path; their stock is treated as compensation income.

**Liability rules (В§357)**:
- **В§357(a)** general вҖ” liabilities assumed by the corporation are NOT treated as boot.
- **В§357(b)** вҖ” if the principal purpose of any liability assumption is tax avoidance or non-bona-fide business reason, ALL liabilities assumed are treated as boot.
- **В§357(c)** вҖ” to the extent sum of liabilities assumed exceeds basis of property transferred, the excess is recognized as gain (independent of actual boot received).

**Basis rules**:
- **В§358(a)** вҖ” transferor stock basis = property basis вҲ’ money received вҲ’ FMV of non-stock property вҲ’ liabilities assumed + gain recognized.
- **В§362(a)** вҖ” corporation's basis in property = transferor's basis + gain recognized by transferor.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-351`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **baseline full non-recognition** (100% control, no boot, $100k basis / $200k FMV вҶ’ $100k realized but 0 recognized; stock basis $100k preserves deferred gain; corp basis $100k carryover); **В§368(c) 80% boundary** (8000bp exactly meets; 7999bp fails); **voting-prong-only failure** (voting 100%, nonvoting 50% вҶ’ fails вҖ” both prongs independently required); **В§351(b)(1) boot recognition** ($180k stock + $20k boot вҶ’ $20k recognized); **boot CAPPED by realized gain** ($500k basis, $400k stock + $200k boot вҶ’ realized $100k, boot $200k, recognized = $100k cap, NOT $200k); **В§351(b)(2) loss NEVER recognized even with boot** ($200k basis, $100k stock + $50k boot вҶ’ realized loss $50k, recognized loss 0); **loss preserved in substituted stock basis** ($150k basis after); **В§357(a) liabilities within basis NOT boot** ($200k basis, $50k liab вҶ’ no В§357(c), no recognition); **В§357(b) tax-avoidance triggers full liability boot** ($200k basis, $100k liab + tax-avoidance flag вҶ’ recognized $100k); **В§357(c) excess-liability gain** ($100k basis + $150k liab вҶ’ $50k В§357(c) gain recognized); **В§357(c) combined with actual boot** ($30k boot + $50k В§357(c) excess = $80k total); **В§357(b)/В§357(c) NO double-counting** (when В§357(b) treats liab as boot, В§357(c) subsumed); **В§351(d) pure-services excluded**; **В§358 stock basis math** (no-boot-no-liab preserves; boot reduces and gain restores; liab reduces); **В§362 corp basis carryover plus boot gain** ($100k + $20k gain = $120k); **citation mentions all 9 relevant authorities** (В§351(a) + В§351(b) + В§351(d) + В§368(c) + В§357(a) + В§357(b) + В§357(c) + В§358(a) + В§362(a)); **5 note path descriptions** (applies / control-failed / services-excluded / В§357(b) tax-avoidance / В§357(c) excess-liability); **$1B precision** ($500M basis, $700M stock + $300M boot вҶ’ realized $500M, recognized $300M, stock basis $500M, corp basis $800M); zero-basis zero-consideration no-op.

`traderview-expense::section_451b` is the **IRC В§451(b) AFS conformity / all-events test acceleration module** вҖ” added by TCJA P.L. 115-97 В§ 13221, effective for tax years beginning after 2017-12-31. Requires accrual-method taxpayers with an Applicable Financial Statement (AFS) to recognize an item of income in gross income no LATER than when the item is recognized as revenue on the AFS. The rule is **ONE-DIRECTIONAL** вҖ” only accelerates timing for tax purposes; never defers ([FEI вҖ” Where GAAP and Tax Meet: В§ 451(b)](https://www.financialexecutives.org/FEI-Daily/October-2019/Where-GAAP-and-Tax-Meet-Understanding-IRC-451b.aspx), [Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 451](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/451)).

**В§ 451(b) all-events test** вҖ” item of income is recognized at the EARLIEST of when it is:

- Due (cash or accrual right to receive)
- Paid
- Earned (classic all-events test)
- Taken into account as revenue in the taxpayer's AFS

The rule effectively takes the GREATER of (classic all-events amount) or (AFS revenue recognized). Pinned by `afs_higher_than_all_events_uses_afs` ($1M AFS > $800k all-events вҶ’ accelerate to $1M) + `all_events_higher_than_afs_uses_all_events` ($1.5M all-events > $1M AFS вҶ’ keep $1.5M) + **one-directional rule never decreases** (`one_directional_rule_never_decreases` вҖ” AFS $500k < all-events $800k вҶ’ keep $800k, not $500k).

**В§ 451(b)(3) AFS definition hierarchy** (highest priority first):

1. Financial statement filed with SEC (10-K, 10-Q)
2. Audited financial statement used for credit, financial reporting, or other substantial nontax purposes
3. Other independently audited financial statements

Taxpayers WITHOUT an AFS are not subject to В§ 451(b) вҖ” they apply the classic all-events test under В§ 451(a). Pinned by `no_afs_no_451b_applies` (no AFS вҶ’ classic all-events $800k governs, no acceleration).

**В§ 451(b) coincides with ASC 606** вҖ” FASB ASC Topic 606 (Revenue From Contracts With Customers) became effective for public companies in 2018 and private companies in 2019. ASC 606 frequently accelerates GAAP revenue recognition, which in turn accelerates tax recognition under В§ 451(b). The two TCJA-era reforms interact tightly.

**В§ 451(b) cost offset election (TD 9941, eff. 2020-12-21)** вҖ” optional method allowing taxpayers to offset AFS income inclusions for over-time revenue recognition with costs incurred to date. Mitigates the harsh result of accelerated income inclusion without corresponding COGS deduction. Pinned by `cost_offset_election_reduces_current_year_inclusion` ($1M accelerated вҲ’ $300k cost offset = $700k net), `cost_offset_capped_at_accelerated_inclusion` ($5M costs > $1M accelerated вҶ’ capped at $1M, net = $0), and `cost_offset_not_applied_without_election` (election required to take offset).

**В§ 451(c) 1-year advance payment deferral** вҖ” codifies Rev. Proc. 2004-34. Accrual taxpayer may defer the portion of an advance payment not recognized on AFS in year of receipt until the following tax year. Pinned by `advance_payment_with_election_defers_unrecognized_portion` ($500k received, $100k AFS-recognized current вҶ’ $100k current + $400k deferred) + `advance_payment_without_election_fully_current_year` (default: full $500k current) + `advance_payment_fully_afs_recognized_no_deferral` (when AFS recognizes full amount, election has no effect).

**Effective date boundary**: В§ 451(b) applies for tax years beginning after 2017-12-31. Pinned by `tax_year_2018_first_post_tcja_year` + `tax_year_2017_pre_tcja_does_not_apply`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-451b`. Twenty tests pin: **AFS higher than all-events uses AFS** ($1M); **all-events higher than AFS uses all-events** ($1.5M); **one-directional rule never decreases** (AFS $500k < all-events $800k вҶ’ keep $800k); **pre-TCJA 2017 no acceleration** + 2018 first post-TCJA year boundary; **cash-method taxpayer no В§ 451(b)**; no-AFS no В§ 451(b) applies; **cost offset election reduces inclusion** ($300k вҶ’ $700k net); **cost offset capped at accelerated** ($5M costs вҶ’ $1M offset cap); **cost offset not applied without election**; **advance payment without election fully current year**; **advance payment with election defers unrecognized portion** ($500k received / $100k AFS вҶ’ $400k deferred); **fully AFS-recognized advance no deferral** ($500k = $500k); applies note describes max() formula; cost offset election note describes TD 9941; advance payment election note describes В§ 451(c); citation mentions all 8 relevant authorities (В§451(b), В§451(b)(3), В§451(c), TCJA В§13221, TD 9941, ASC 606, Rev. Proc. 2004-34); $1B precision; zero revenue no-op.

`traderview-expense::section_382` is the **IRC В§382 NOL limitation module** вҖ” the load-bearing rule after any M&A transaction involving a loss corporation. Where В§172 controls whether NOLs CAN be deducted in principle, В§382 controls HOW MUCH per year once an ownership change has fired. An "ownership change" under В§382(g) occurs when 5%+ shareholders' aggregate percentage has increased by more than 50 percentage points over their lowest percentage in the rolling 3-year testing period; the trivial-percentage public is aggregated into a single shareholder group.

**В§382(b)(1) annual limitation** = corporation FMV at the change date Г— applicable long-term tax-exempt rate. The rate is the highest of the federal long-term tax-exempt rates published in the 3 months preceding the change. As a current data point, the **February 2026 long-term tax-exempt rate was 3.56%** ([Moss Adams, 2024](https://www.mossadams.com/articles/2024/04/offset-tax-liability-with-section-382)). A $10M corporation at 3.56% yields only **$356k/year** of usable pre-change NOLs вҖ” the basis for the rule's reputation as "death by limitation" for shell-purchase NOL trafficking schemes.

**В§382(l)(5) bankruptcy exception** waives the annual limitation entirely when (a) the ownership change occurs in a Title 11 case, AND (b) historic shareholders + qualified creditors own вүҘ 50% of the reorganized entity by **both** vote and value (the "50% continuity test"). The cost is a **mandatory interest haircut** вҖ” pre-change NOLs are reduced by interest expense deducted on debt converted to stock during the 3 years preceding the bankruptcy petition, preventing a double benefit ([LegalClarity В§382(l)(5)](https://legalclarity.org/the-section-382l5-exception-for-bankruptcy-nols/)).

**В§382(h) NUBIG / NUBIL** вҖ” net unrealized built-in GAIN recognized within the 5-year post-change recognition period INCREASES the annual limitation (so the buyer captures the embedded appreciation); net unrealized built-in LOSS recognized in the same window is SUBJECT to the limitation as if pre-change. The module surfaces an explicit NUBIG flag + recognized-gain field so callers compute the В§1.382-7 thresholds outside the formula.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-382`. Nineteen tests pin: **В§382(b)(1) base limit math** (FMV $10M Г— 3.56% = $356k); NOL use bound by the **annual limitation** ($356k cap leaves $4.644M carryforward from $5M pool); NOL use bound by taxable income ($100k TI binds); NOL use bound by remaining pool ($200k pool drains and zeroes); **В§382(h) NUBIG adjustment** ($356k base + $200k NUBIG = $556k adjusted); **В§382(l)(5) bankruptcy waiver** (annual limit off; $1M haircut takes $5M вҶ’ $4M usable; TI $1M used; $3M carries); В§382(l)(5) haircut exceeds NOL clamps to zero; **zero-FMV insolvent corp kills NOL effectively** ($0 limit, no use, carryforward survives but never usable); rate sensitivity ($10M Г— 5.00% = $500k); NUBIG flag gatekeeping (gain alone does nothing without the flag); zero taxable income вҶ’ no use; **$1B FMV no-precision-loss path** ($1B Г— 3.56% = $35.6M); В§382(l)(5) with zero haircut preserves full NOL; note describes the В§382(b)(1) path (rate appears as "3.56%"); note describes the В§382(l)(5) path ("WAIVED" + "interest haircut"); note describes the В§382(h) NUBIG path; exact-boundary equality (TI = limit); zero rate вҶ’ zero limit; pathological-input carryforward never negative.

`traderview-expense::section_401a9` is the **IRC В§401(a)(9) Required Minimum Distribution module** вҖ” every trader retiree with a traditional IRA or 401(k) reaching the RMD age must begin taking distributions or face the В§4974 excise tax. SECURE Act of 2019 raised the RMD age from 70ВҪ to 72; SECURE 2.0 Act of 2022 raised it again to 73 (and to 75 for the 1960+ cohort).

**RMD age by birth year** (SECURE 2.0 cohort logic):

| Birth year      | RMD age | Source                         |
|-----------------|---------|--------------------------------|
| 1949 or earlier | 70 (ВҪ)  | Pre-SECURE legacy              |
| 1950            | 72      | SECURE 1.0 (2019)              |
| 1951-1959       | 73      | SECURE 2.0 (2022)              |
| 1960+           | 75      | SECURE 2.0 (2033 first triggers) |

Pinned by `born_1949_rmd_age_70` + `born_1950_rmd_age_72` + `born_1951_rmd_age_73` + `born_1959_rmd_age_73_last_year` + `born_1960_rmd_age_75` вҖ” all four cohort boundaries are individually pinned to catch any future regression where the SECURE 2.0 age thresholds shift.

**Roth account carve-outs:**

| Account type            | RMD required?     | Citation                  |
|-------------------------|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Roth IRA                | NEVER (lifetime)  | В§408A(c)(5)               |
| Roth 401(k) pre-2024    | Yes               | (legacy)                  |
| Roth 401(k) post-2024   | NO (SECURE 2.0)   | SECURE 2.0 В§ 325          |
| Traditional IRA         | Yes               | В§401(a)(9)(A)             |
| Traditional 401(k)      | Yes               | В§401(a)(9)(A)             |

Pinned by `roth_ira_no_rmd_regardless_of_age` (age 86 Roth IRA owner = no RMD) + `roth_401k_post_2024_no_rmd` (post-2024 carve-out) + `roth_401k_pre_2024_did_have_rmd` (legacy regression target вҖ” distinguishes the two Roth 401(k) regimes).

**Uniform Lifetime Table** factors per IRS Pub 590-B Appendix B (November 2020 update, effective 2022+):

| Age | Factor | Implied % |
|-----|--------|-----------|
| 72  | 27.4   | 3.65%     |
| 73  | 26.5   | 3.77%     |
| 74  | 25.5   | 3.92%     |
| 75  | 24.6   | 4.07%     |
| 80  | 20.2   | 4.95%     |
| 85  | 16.0   | 6.25%     |
| 90  | 12.2   | 8.20%     |
| 95  | 8.9    | 11.24%    |
| 100 | 6.4    | 15.63%    |

RMD amount = `prior_year_end_balance / lifetime_factor`. Module ships ages 72-100. Pinned by `age_73_uniform_lifetime_factor_26_5` ($1M / 26.5 = $37,735.85) + `uniform_lifetime_factor_age_75` (24.6) + `uniform_lifetime_factor_age_85` (16.0) + `uniform_lifetime_factor_age_100` (6.4).

**В§4974 excise tax structure (SECURE 2.0 reduction):**

| Condition                                     | Penalty rate |
|-----------------------------------------------|--------------|
| No timely correction                          | **25%** (down from 50% pre-SECURE 2.0) |
| Timely correction within 2-year window + Form 5329 | **10%** |
| RMD fully met                                  | 0%          |

Pinned by `rmd_shortfall_25_percent_penalty` (no distribution вҶ’ full shortfall Г— 25%) + `timely_correction_reduces_penalty_to_10_percent` (same shortfall Г— 10% with correction flag) + `partial_shortfall_proportional_penalty` ($20k distributed of $37,735 RMD вҶ’ $17,735 shortfall Г— 25%).

**First-RMD-year flag** is set when `current_age == rmd_age`, signaling the RBD (Required Beginning Date) extension applies вҖ” first RMD due April 1 of year AFTER turning RMD age, not December 31. Missing the first-year deadline pushes both distributions into year 2, doubling the income tax that year. Pinned by `is_first_rmd_year_only_when_current_age_equals_rmd_age` (age 73 = first year; age 74 = not first year).

**Current-age clamps at zero** for pathological inputs (birth year > current year). No panic; just classifies as below-RMD-age. Pinned by `current_age_clamps_at_zero_for_future_birth_year`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-401a9`. Twenty-seven tests pin: Roth IRA no RMD lifetime; **Roth 401(k) post-2024 SECURE 2.0 carve-out** + pre-2024 had RMD (regression target distinguishing the two regimes); **all four birth-year cohort boundaries** (1949 = 70, 1950 = 72, 1951-1959 = 73, 1960+ = 75); under-RMD-age no requirement; age 73 factor 26.5 with exact RMD math ($1M case); RMD met no penalty; full shortfall 25% penalty; **timely correction reduces to 10%**; partial shortfall proportional penalty; Uniform Lifetime Table factors at 75/85/100; **first-RMD-year flag** only when current_age == rmd_age; high balance ($50M) no precision loss; pathological future-birth-year clamps to age 0; traditional IRA + traditional 401(k) both subject; **1959/1960 cohort boundary pinned at both** (1959 age 73, 1960 age 75 вҖ” the only single-year difference between 73 and 75 cohorts is the load-bearing boundary); note describes В§4974 + 25% or 10% per path.

`traderview-expense::section_108` is the **IRC В§108 cancellation of debt income module** вҖ” critical for distressed debt traders, underwater real estate investors, mortgage workouts, and credit card settlement scenarios. Default rule under В§61(a)(12) is that cancelled debt is gross income; В§108(a) provides five narrow exclusions with mandatory В§108(b) attribute-reduction consequences.

**Five exclusions in priority order:**

| Priority | Citation         | Exclusion type                            | Conditions                                              |
|----------|------------------|-------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| 1        | В§108(a)(1)(A)    | Title 11 bankruptcy                       | Discharge in bankruptcy case                            |
| 2        | В§108(a)(1)(E)    | Qualified principal residence indebtedness | Pre-2026 written arrangement; election can swap to (B)  |
| 3        | В§108(a)(1)(B)    | Insolvency                                | Liabilities > FMV of assets (В§108(d)(3) test)           |
| 4        | В§108(a)(1)(C)    | Qualified farm                            | Farmer (50%+ income from farming for 3 prior years)     |
| 5        | В§108(a)(1)(D)    | Qualified real property business (QRPBI)  | Non-C-corp with qualifying real property biz debt        |

**Bankruptcy is highest priority and full exclusion** under В§108(a)(1)(A). Pinned by `bankruptcy_full_exclusion_highest_priority` + `bankruptcy_overrides_qpri` (even with QPRI flags set, bankruptcy wins) + `priority_bankruptcy_over_insolvency`.

**В§108(d)(3) Insolvency test**: insolvency_amount = `liabilities - assets_fmv`, clamped at zero (solvent вҶ’ 0). Insolvency excludes ONLY up to the insolvency amount вҖ” solvent taxpayers must include the entire COD income. Pinned by `insolvency_full_exclusion_when_amount_ge_debt` ($30k insolvency, $50k debt? wait actually full exclusion when insolvency вүҘ debt; let me re-check вҖ” yes, $100k insolvency вүҘ $50k debt = full exclusion) + `insolvency_partial_exclusion_with_remainder_includible` ($30k insolvency, $50k debt вҶ’ $30k excluded, $20k includible) + `insolvency_amount_at_exact_zero_solvent` (assets = liabilities вҶ’ solvent, no exclusion via this path).

**QPRI sunset before January 1, 2026** under В§108(a)(1)(E). Discharges in 2026+ on **pre-2026 written arrangements** still qualify; new arrangements made in 2026 do NOT. Pinned by `qpri_pre_2026_exclusion_overrides_insolvency` (full $50k excluded under (E), insolvency wouldn't have been needed) + `qpri_post_2026_arrangement_does_not_exclude` (flag set but post-2026 arrangement вҶ’ falls through to no exclusion or insolvency).

**В§108(a)(2)(C) election** lets taxpayer choose insolvency (B) instead of QPRI (E). Useful when attribute reduction under (B) is more favorable than basis reduction under (E). Pinned by `qpri_election_to_use_insolvency_routes_through_insolvency` (election routes through (B) path even with QPRI flags set).

**Qualifying-debt categories layer on top of insolvency** when insolvency is only partial. Insolvent by $30k + $50k debt + qualified farm + qualified farmer вҶ’ $30k via (B), remaining $20k via (C). Pinned by `insolvency_partial_plus_farm_excludes_remainder` + `insolvency_partial_plus_qrpbi_excludes_remainder`.

**Farm exclusion requires BOTH the debt category AND the qualified-farmer status** (В§108(g)(1)(B): 50%+ of income for 3 prior years from farming). Pinned by `qualified_farm_indebtedness_solvent_excludes` (both flags + solvent вҶ’ full exclusion) + `farm_debt_without_qualified_farmer_status_no_exclusion` (farm debt + non-farmer вҶ’ no exclusion).

**C-corporations cannot use В§108(a)(1)(D) QRPBI exclusion** вҖ” statutorily blocked. Pinned by `qrpbi_for_non_c_corp_excludes` + `c_corp_cannot_use_qrpbi_exclusion`.

**В§108(b) attribute reduction equals the excluded amount** вҖ” the "deferred tax" cost of the exclusion. Reduces NOL carryovers, GBC, minimum tax credit, capital loss carryovers, basis of property, PAL carryovers, FTC carryovers (in that order). This module reports the required total; ordering and per-bucket allocation is downstream Form 982 work. Pinned by `attribute_reduction_equals_excluded_amount`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-108`.
