Twenty-two tests pin: solvent taxpayer no exclusion full inclusion; **bankruptcy highest priority full exclusion**; insolvency full when amount вүҘ debt; insolvency partial with remainder includible; QPRI pre-2026 overrides insolvency; **QPRI post-2026 does NOT exclude** (sunset regression target); QPRI election routes through insolvency; bankruptcy overrides QPRI; qualified farm + farmer excludes solvent; farm debt without farmer status no exclusion; QRPBI for non-C-corp excludes; C-corp cannot use QRPBI; insolvency partial + farm covers remainder; insolvency partial + QRPBI covers remainder; attribute reduction = excluded amount; insolvency clamps to zero when solvent; insolvency at exact zero (liabilities = assets) is solvent; **priority sweep** (bankruptcy > QPRI > insolvency); zero canceled debt no-op; very large precision ($10B bankruptcy discharge); note describes section per path (bankruptcy / QPRI / insolvency / В§61(a)(12) no-exclusion).

`traderview-expense::section_104` is the **IRC В§104 damages for personal injury / sickness module** вҖ” universal for any trader receiving litigation settlement or judgment proceeds. В§ 104(a)(2) EXCLUDES from gross income "the amount of any damages (other than punitive damages) received on account of personal PHYSICAL injuries or physical sickness." Several categories within a single settlement get different tax treatment, making the precise allocation in the settlement agreement load-bearing ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 104](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/104), [IRS вҖ” Tax implications of settlements and judgments](https://www.irs.gov/government-entities/tax-implications-of-settlements-and-judgments)).

**Five-category exclusion / inclusion classification** (the load-bearing distinguishing taxonomy):

| Damage category                                                          | В§ 104(a)(2) treatment | Module pin                      |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|---------------------------------|
| Compensatory for direct physical injury / sickness                       | EXCLUDED              | `physical_injury_compensatory_excluded` |
| Pain and suffering from physical injury                                  | EXCLUDED              | `pain_suffering_physical_origin_excluded` |
| Lost wages caused by physical injury                                     | EXCLUDED              | `lost_wages_from_physical_injury_excluded` |
| Emotional distress ORIGINATING from physical injury                      | EXCLUDED              | `emotional_distress_from_physical_injury_excluded` |
| Emotional distress NOT from physical injury                              | INCLUDED (except medical care amount) | `emotional_distress_non_physical_partial_exclusion_with_medical_care` |
| Punitive damages                                                         | INCLUDED              | `punitive_damages_included_by_default` |
| Punitive damages in wrongful-death-only-punitives state (В§ 104(c))       | EXCLUDED              | `punitive_damages_wrongful_death_state_carveout` |
| Interest on award (always)                                               | INCLUDED              | `interest_on_award_always_included` |
| Previously deducted В§ 213 medical with tax benefit                       | INCLUDED (recapture)  | `previously_deducted_medical_reduces_exclusion` |

**Settlement language matters**: the Tax Court holds courts and the IRS to the FOUR-CORNERS of the settlement agreement's allocation between physical-injury and non-physical categories. A blanket settlement with no allocation lets the IRS apply the worst possible characterization. Counsel should explicitly recite physical-injury origin and itemize the categories ([Freeman Law вҖ” Dern Reminder on В§104(a)(2) Settlement Language](https://freemanlaw.com/dern-reminds-that-settlement-language-is-important-in-section-104a2-physical-injury-cases/)).

**Non-physical emotional distress carveout**: emotional distress damages NOT arising from physical injury are INCLUDED in gross income, EXCEPT to the extent of the amount ACTUALLY paid for medical care attributable to the emotional distress. So a $80k defamation award with $10k of psychiatric care receipts yields $10k excluded + $70k included. Pinned by `emotional_distress_non_physical_fully_included_when_no_medical_care` + `emotional_distress_non_physical_partial_exclusion_with_medical_care` ($10k carveout) + `medical_care_capped_at_emotional_distress_amount` (carveout can't exceed the award).

**Punitive damages В§ 104(c) wrongful-death exception**: a small number of states historically allowed ONLY punitive damages in wrongful-death actions; В§ 104(c) carves out those punitives from the general rule, treating them as if compensatory. Pinned by `punitive_damages_wrongful_death_state_carveout`.

**В§ 104(a) flush sentence tax benefit recapture**: if the taxpayer deducted medical expenses under В§ 213 in a prior year and later recovers those amounts through settlement, the recovered amount is INCLUDED in gross income to the extent it produced a tax benefit. Module computes the recapture as `min(prior_В§213_deduction, physical_injury_excluded)` so it can't exceed what was excluded. Pinned by `previously_deducted_medical_reduces_exclusion` ($50k recapture from $200k physical award) + `recapture_capped_at_physical_excluded_amount` ($30k physical + $100k prior deduction вҶ’ $30k recapture, not $100k).

**Complex settlement integration test** (`complex_settlement_full_classification`): $500k compensatory + $200k P&S + $150k lost wages + $100k physical-origin emotional + $80k non-physical emotional with $10k medical + $300k punitive + $25k interest + $20k prior В§ 213 deduction вҶ’ $940k excluded ($950k physical pillar вҲ’ $20k recapture + $10k emotional medical), $415k included ($70k non-physical emotional + $300k punitive + $25k interest + $20k recapture).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-104`. Twenty tests pin: all 4 physical-pillar exclusions individually (compensatory / pain-suffering / lost wages / emotional-from-physical); **non-physical emotional distress fully included without medical care**; **non-physical emotional distress partial exclusion with medical care** ($80k вҲ’ $10k вҶ’ $70k included); **medical care capped at emotional distress amount** ($5k award + $20k medical вҶ’ $5k excluded only); **punitive damages included by default** + **punitive wrongful-death В§ 104(c) state carveout**; **interest always included** (even with $1M physical injury award); **previously deducted medical reduces exclusion** ($200k вҲ’ $50k recapture = $150k excluded); **recapture capped at physical excluded amount** ($30k physical + $100k prior вҶ’ $30k recapture); **complex settlement integration** $1.355M total вҶ’ $940k excluded + $415k included; pure punitive award fully included; pure physical injury fully excluded; zero damages no-op; note describes physical pillar with amounts; note describes recapture when present; citation mentions all relevant authorities (В§104(a)(2) / В§104(a) flush / В§104(c) / В§1.104-1 / 1996 SBJPA); $100B precision case.

`traderview-expense::section_72t` is the **IRC В§72(t) 10% additional tax on early retirement distributions module** вҖ” trader-critical because traders frequently consider accessing retirement-account capital for active strategies. В§72(t)(1) imposes an additional 10% tax on the **includible portion** of any distribution from a qualified plan, IRA, or other tax-favored retirement vehicle received before age 59ВҪ (basis recovery is excluded).

**В§72(t)(2) exception list** вҖ” 14 statutory carve-outs:

| Exception | Authority | Cap | Plan-optional? |
|-----------|-----------|-----|-----------------|
| Age 59ВҪ+ | В§72(t)(2)(A)(i) | none | No |
| Death | В§72(t)(2)(A)(ii) | none | No |
| Disability | В§72(t)(2)(A)(iii) | none | No |
| Substantially equal periodic payments (SEPP) | В§72(t)(2)(A)(iv) | none | No |
| Separation from service after age 55 | В§72(t)(2)(A)(v) | none | No |
| Medical expenses > 7.5% AGI | В§72(t)(2)(B) | none | No |
| QDRO | В§72(t)(2)(C) | none | No |
| Higher education (IRA only) | В§72(t)(2)(D) | none | No |
| First-time homebuyer (IRA only) | В§72(t)(2)(F) | **$10,000 lifetime** | No |
| Unemployed health insurance (IRA only) | В§72(t)(2)(G) | none | No |
| Federally declared disaster | В§72(t)(11) | **$22,000** | No |
| Birth or adoption | В§72(t)(2)(H) | **$5,000** | No |
| Terminal illness (physician-certified < 7-yr) | SECURE 2.0 В§326 | none | No |
| Emergency personal expense | SECURE 2.0 В§115 | **$1,000/year** | **Yes** |
| Domestic abuse victim | SECURE 2.0 В§314 | **$10,000 indexed** | **Yes** |
| Qualified long-term care insurance | SECURE 2.0 В§334 | **$2,500/year**, eff. 2026 | **Yes** |

**SECURE 2.0 plan-optional gate**: emergency personal expense, domestic abuse victim, and qualified long-term care exceptions are NOT automatically available вҖ” the plan sponsor must affirmatively adopt them. Even after the statutory effective date, a taxpayer whose plan has not adopted the exception still pays the 10% tax. Terminal illness (В§326) is statutory and NOT plan-optional вҖ” it works regardless of plan adoption.

**Includible-vs-basis distinction**: В§72(t) applies only to the portion of the distribution includible in gross income. A Roth IRA basis recovery distribution carries $0 В§72(t) exposure even if taken at age 30.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-72t`. Twenty-five tests pin: **pre-59ВҪ no exception 10% applies** ($50k вҶ’ $5k); **age 60 no additional tax**; **death exception zeroes tax** (capless); disability / SEPP / separation-after-55 / medical / QDRO / higher-education exceptions all zero tax; terminal illness (SECURE 2.0 В§326) zeroes tax; **first-time homebuyer $10k cap** ($50k вҲ’ $10k = $40k Г— 10% = $4,000 tax remainder); **birth/adoption $5k cap**; **federally declared disaster $22k cap** ($28k Г— 10% = $2,800 remainder); **emergency personal expense plan-adopted $1k cap**; **emergency expense plan NOT adopted вҶ’ no exception recognized** (regression вҖ” plan-adoption gate binds even after statute eff.); **domestic abuse plan-adopted $10k cap** + plan-not-adopted no exception; **long-term care $2,500 cap** (plan-adopted); **terminal illness NOT plan-optional** (works without plan adoption вҖ” regression target distinguishing SECURE 2.0 В§326 from В§115/В§314/В§334); **distribution below cap вҶ’ full qualifying** ($3k birth distribution вҶ’ 0 tax); **basis recovery NOT subject to В§72(t)** ($50k distribution but only $20k includible вҶ’ $2k tax not $5k); **citation mentions all 9 relevant authorities** (В§72(t)(1) + В§72(t)(2) + В§72(t)(11) + SECURE 2.0 + В§326 + В§115 + В§314 + В§334 + plan-optional); note plan-not-adopted explains; note age-exempt path; **$10M precision** ($1M tax).

`traderview-expense::section_7701` is the **IRC В§7701 entity classification "check-the-box" module** вҖ” foundational for any trader forming an LLC, LP, or other pass-through entity. Treasury Regulations В§В§ 301.7701-2 and 301.7701-3 (the "check-the-box" or CTB regulations, effective 1997-01-01) let eligible entities choose their U.S. tax classification: disregarded entity, partnership, or corporation. Pairs with existing `section_1361` (S-corp eligibility gate) and `section_351` (formation non-recognition).

**Treas. Reg. В§ 301.7701-2 default classifications**:

| Entity type | Members | Default classification |
|-------------|---------|------------------------|
| Domestic LLC / LP / general partnership | 1 | Disregarded entity |
| Domestic LLC / LP / general partnership | 2+ | Partnership (Form 1065) |
| Domestic corporation formed under federal/state corporate statute | (any) | Per-se corporation (no election) |
| Foreign per-se corporation on В§ 301.7701-2(b)(8) list | (any) | Per-se corporation (no election) |
| Foreign eligible entity | (any) | Eligible to elect |

**Treas. Reg. В§ 301.7701-3 election mechanism**: eligible entities may file Form 8832 to elect corporation taxation (the "check-the-box" election). Effective date: as specified on Form 8832, with up to 75 days retroactive or 12 months prospective.

**В§ 301.7701-3(c)(1)(iv) 60-month lockout**: once an entity changes its classification by election, it cannot change again for **60 months** after the effective date. The lockout is **waived** if there is a > 50% change in ownership since the prior election.

**Per-se corporation list (В§ 301.7701-2(b))**: includes any entity formed under federal/state law describing it as "incorporated" or as a "corporation"; joint-stock companies; banks subject to Title 12; insurance companies; state-wholly-owned entities; the В§ 301.7701-2(b)(8) foreign per-se list (e.g., Canadian Corporation, Mexican Sociedad AnГіnima, German Aktiengesellschaft).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7701`. Twenty-two tests pin: **single-member LLC defaults to disregarded entity**; **multi-member LLC defaults to partnership**; LP defaults to partnership; general partnership defaults to partnership; **domestic corporation statute is per-se corporation**; **foreign per-se list is per-se**; **per-se corp Form 8832 election irrelevant** (regression вҖ” already corporation); **single-member LLC elects to C corp** (via Form 8832); **multi-member LLC elects to C corp**; **foreign eligible entity can elect**; **lockout NOT binding after 60 months** (boundary regression target); **lockout binding at 59 months** (regression вҖ” strict вүҘ 60); **lockout binding at 0 months вҶ’ 60 months remaining**; **> 50% ownership change waives lockout** (regression target вҖ” even at month 0); **per-se corp cannot change classification** (eligible_to_elect = false); eligible entity with no recent election can elect; **citation mentions all 8 relevant authorities** (В§7701 + В§ 301.7701-2 + В§ 301.7701-3 + Form 8832 + "60-month" + "1997-01-01" + В§1361 + В§351 + В§ 301.7701-2(b)(8) foreign per-se list); note describes default path + multi-member path + election path; note describes lockout months remaining.

`traderview-expense::section_7704` is the **IRC В§7704 publicly-traded-partnership corporate-treatment module** вҖ” trader-critical for any investor holding master limited partnerships (MLPs) or other publicly traded partnerships. В§ 7704(a) treats a PTP as a CORPORATION for federal tax purposes (losing pass-through status and triggering double taxation) UNLESS В§ 7704(c) qualifying-income exception applies (90% qualifying income). Pairs with `section_7701` (check-the-box entity classification) and `mlp_ubti` (UBTI for tax-exempt MLP investors).

**В§ 7704(b) PTP definition вҖ” two prongs**:

| Prong | Statute | Test |
|-------|---------|------|
| В§ 7704(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 7704(b)(1) | Interests traded on established securities market (NYSE, NASDAQ, etc.) |
| В§ 7704(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 7704(b)(2) | Interests readily tradable on secondary market or substantial equivalent thereof |

Either prong вҶ’ PTP.

**В§ 7704(c) qualifying-income exception** has TWO requirements that BOTH must hold:

| Subsection | Statute | Requirement |
|------------|---------|-------------|
| В§ 7704(c)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 7704(c)(1) | **Continuous compliance** вҖ” met 90% test every taxable year beginning after 1987-12-31 |
| В§ 7704(c)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 7704(c)(2) | **90% threshold** вҖ” qualifying income вүҘ 90% of gross income for the year |

**В§ 7704(d)(1) seven qualifying-income categories**:

- (A) Interest
- (B) Dividends
- (C) Real property rents
- (D) Gain from sale or disposition of real property
- (E) Income/gains from mineral or natural-resource activities (oil, gas, minerals, timber, fertilizer, geothermal, industrial-source CO2, alcohol fuels)
- (F) Gain from sale or disposition of capital asset held for qualifying-income production
- (G) Commodities income for certain partnerships + В§ 1221(a)(1) inventory

**В§ 7704(e) inadvertent-termination relief** requires ALL THREE prongs:

1. **Inadvertent failure** to meet the gross-income test
2. **Corrective steps** taken within a reasonable time after discovery
3. **Agreement** to make adjustments required by the Secretary

All three must hold; the 8-combo invariant `inadvertent_relief_requires_all_3_prongs_8_combo_invariant` confirms only (true, true, true) yields relief.

**The 90% test is INCLUSIVE at the boundary.** Qualifying income of exactly 90.00% (9,000 basis points) passes; 89.99% (8,999 bp) fails. Pinned by `ptp_at_exact_90_pct_boundary_passes_test`, `ptp_just_below_90_pct_fails_test`, and the boundary invariant `ninety_pct_boundary_strict_at_threshold_invariant` (3 boundary cells: 8999, 9000, 9001 bp).

**The В§ 7704(c) exception requires BOTH В§ 7704(c)(1) continuous compliance AND В§ 7704(c)(2) 90% threshold.** Failing either drops the partnership into В§ 7704(a) corporate treatment (absent В§ 7704(e) relief). Pinned by `exception_requires_both_90_pct_and_continuous_compliance_invariant` (4-cell truth table).

**В§ 7704(e) relief preserves pass-through even when 90% test fails.** Where the failure was inadvertent, corrective steps were taken, and the partnership agreed to required adjustments, the Secretary may treat the partnership as continuing to meet the test. Pinned by `ptp_failed_test_but_all_3_relief_prongs_satisfied_pass_through_preserved` and `relief_does_not_engage_when_test_already_satisfied` (relief is a CURE for failure, not redundant with success).

**Non-PTP partnerships are entirely outside В§ 7704 scope.** Even with 0% qualifying income, a non-publicly-traded partnership retains pass-through status under subchapter K. Pinned by `not_publicly_traded_section_7704_inapplicable` and the 12-cell invariant `not_ptp_never_treated_as_corporation_invariant` (6 QI levels Г— 2 continuous-compliance flags).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7704`. Twenty tests pin: **not publicly traded В§ 7704 inapplicable**; **established securities market is PTP**; **readily tradable secondary market is PTP**; **PTP with 95% qualifying income passes test**; **PTP at exact 90% boundary passes test**; **PTP just below 90% fails test**; **PTP with 50% qualifying income fails test**; **PTP passes current test fails continuous compliance**; **PTP failed test but all 3 relief prongs satisfied pass-through preserved**; **relief requires all 3 prongs (inadvertent + corrective + agreement)**; **relief does not engage when test already satisfied** (regression вҖ” no double-counting); **zero gross income zero QI percentage**; **QI exceeds gross clamps at 100%** (defensive); **negative QI clamps at zero** (defensive); **PTP status independent of trading market 2-prong invariant**; **exception requires both 90% and continuous compliance 4-cell invariant**; **inadvertent relief requires all 3 prongs 8-combo invariant**; **90% boundary strict at threshold 3-cell invariant** (8999/9000/9001 bp); **not PTP never treated as corporation 12-cell invariant** (6 QI levels Г— 2 continuous-compliance flags); **citation pins subsections per outcome** (exception: В§ 7704(c)(1)вҖ“(c)(2) + В§ 7704(d)(1); corporate: В§ 7704(a) + В§ 7704(b); relief: В§ 7704(e) + В§ 7704(b)).

`traderview-expense::section_7872` is the **IRC В§7872 below-market loan module** вҖ” the family sweetheart loan trap. When a trader lends to family, child, or controlled entity at below-AFR rates, the IRS imputes the missing interest as if charged at the Applicable Federal Rate. Forgone interest becomes income to the lender AND deemed transferred back as gift / compensation / dividend depending on the relationship.

**AFR brackets by loan term** under В§1274(d):

| Bracket    | Term                       | Boundary       |
|------------|----------------------------|----------------|
| Short-term | вүӨ 3 years                  | day 0          |
| Mid-term   | > 3 and вүӨ 9 years          | day 3yr + 1    |
| Long-term  | > 9 years                  | day 9yr + 1    |

Pinned by `short_term_loan_classified_at_3_year_boundary` (term = 3y exact = short) + `mid_term_loan_at_3_year_plus_boundary` (3.01y = mid) + `mid_term_loan_at_9_year_boundary` (9y exact = mid) + `long_term_loan_at_9_year_plus_boundary` (9.01y = long).

**Two narrow exceptions for GIFT loans ONLY** (no exceptions for compensation or corp-shareholder):

**В§7872(c)(2)(A) вҖ” $10,000 de minimis.** If aggregate outstanding вүӨ $10,000 AND proceeds NOT used for income-producing assets, NO imputation. Both conditions required; pinned by `gift_loan_10k_de_minimis_no_imputation` (passes) + `gift_loan_10001_exceeds_de_minimis` (boundary + 1 fails) + `gift_loan_10k_used_for_income_producing_disables_de_minimis` (income-asset flag kills exception even at $10k).

**В§7872(d)(1) вҖ” $100,000 NII cap.** Gift loans with aggregate вүӨ $100,000:
- Borrower NII вүӨ $1,000 вҶ’ NO imputation (separate de minimis floor on NII)
- Borrower NII > $1,000 вҶ’ imputation CAPPED at borrower's NII (never more)

Pinned by `gift_loan_below_100k_nii_below_1k_no_imputation` + `gift_loan_below_100k_nii_exact_1k_no_imputation` (1k exact = вүӨ вҶ’ exception) + `gift_loan_below_100k_nii_above_1k_capped_at_nii` + `gift_loan_below_100k_nii_caps_higher_raw_forgone` (load-bearing: $3,000 raw forgone capped at $1,500 NII).

**Full AFR imputation** under В§7872(a)(1) applies to:
- Gift loans with aggregate > $100,000 (no NII cap)
- All compensation-related loans (any size)
- All corporation/shareholder loans (any size)

Pinned by `gift_loan_above_100k_full_afr_imputation` ($200k Г— 3% gap = $6,000 imputed, no NII cap) + `compensation_loan_no_de_minimis_no_nii_cap` (even $5k compensation loan gets full imputation) + `corporation_shareholder_loan_no_exceptions` ($8k corp loan still imputed).

**Aggregate-outstanding is the threshold metric, not individual loan principal.** A small $5k loan can fall outside the $10k de minimis if the aggregate between the two parties already exceeds $10k. Pinned by `aggregate_outstanding_threshold_uses_aggregate_not_principal`.

**Boundary thresholds use вүӨ on the high side**: $10k = de minimis applies; $10k + 1 = fails. $100k = NII cap applies; $100k + 1 = full imputation. NII $1,000 exact = exception applies. Pinned by individual boundary tests.

**Raw forgone interest is reported even when an exception applies** for diagnostic/documentation purposes. UI can show "would have been $X but exception applies". Pinned by `forgone_raw_reported_even_when_exception_applies` ($300 raw, $0 imputed, both fields present on result).

**Deemed transfer equals imputed income (mirror)**. The lender's imputed interest income and the borrower's deemed gift/comp/dividend are always equal вҖ” they're two sides of the same accounting entry. Pinned by `deemed_transfer_equals_imputed_income`.

**Rate вүҘ AFR short-circuits with no imputation** regardless of loan type or size. The В§7872 rule only fires on below-market loans. Pinned by `rate_meets_afr_no_imputation` (rate = AFR exact) + `rate_above_afr_no_imputation` (rate > AFR).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7872`. Twenty-four tests pin: rate at/above AFR no imputation; **$10k de minimis** with boundary cases (passes at 10k, fails at 10k+1, income-asset disables); **В§7872(d)(1) NII cap** with three sub-paths (вүӨ $1k no imputation, > $1k capped at NII, cap binds when NII < raw forgone); gift loan > $100k full imputation; compensation + corp-shareholder loans always get full imputation (no exceptions); aggregate-outstanding is the metric not principal; **AFR term classification at 3-year and 9-year boundaries** (both edges per bracket); zero-interest loan = full forgone at AFR; forgone raw reported even on exception paths; **aggregate at $100k exact = NII cap path** (boundary regression target); aggregate > $100k = full imputation; **deemed transfer mirrors imputed income** (accounting consistency pin); very large precision ($10B HNW loan with $300M imputed); note describes rule path per branch (de minimis / NII cap / full imputation).

`traderview-expense::section_1041` is the **IRC В§1041 transfers between spouses module** вҖ” completes the basis-transfer trio with `section_1014` (death) and `section_1015` (lifetime gift). Critical for HNW divorce property division.

**В§1041(a)**: NO gain or loss recognized on transfer between current spouses or former spouses if incident to divorce. The transferor doesn't pay tax on embedded appreciation at transfer.

**В§1041(b)**: Transferee takes the **transferor's adjusted basis** as carryover вҖ” regardless of whether basis is less than, equal to, or greater than FMV at transfer. **No dual-basis rule** like В§1015 (spouses are economically a single unit; loss-shifting concern doesn't apply). Holding period TACKS uniformly under В§1223(2) вҖ” both for gain and for loss.

**В§1041(c) "incident to divorce" rules** per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1041-1T(b) Q&A-7:

| Days from cessation | Rule                                             | Applies?                          |
|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 0-365 (within 1 yr) | **Automatic** вҖ” no test required                 | Always yes                        |
| 366-2190 (1-6 yrs)  | Must be pursuant to divorce/separation instrument | Yes IF instrument flag set       |
| 2191+ (>6 yrs)      | Presumption against; needs instrument language    | Yes IF instrument flag set (rebuts presumption) |

**В§1041(d) NR alien exception**: В§1041 does NOT apply when the transferee spouse (or former spouse) is a nonresident alien at the time of transfer. Highest precedence вҖ” fires BEFORE the timing-rule check. Transferor recognizes immediate gain/loss as though transferring to a third party. Pinned by `nonresident_alien_overrides_otherwise_applicable_path` (NR alien + currently married still disqualifies).

**Distinction from В§1015 (lifetime gift carryover)** is load-bearing:

| Rule                | В§1015 (lifetime gift)            | В§1041 (spousal transfer)         |
|---------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Recognition at transfer | None for donor (gift exclusion) | None for transferor              |
| Basis to recipient  | Donor's basis                    | Transferor's basis               |
| Dual-basis on depreciated property | **YES вҖ” phantom zone** | **NO вҖ” single basis for gain or loss** |
| В§1015(d) gift-tax basis increase | Applies                | Does NOT apply (no gift tax)     |
| Holding period tacking | Gain path only; loss path resets | Always tacks                  |

Pinned by `depreciated_property_no_dual_basis_unlike_section_1015` (transferor basis $100k, FMV $50k, sale $30k вҶ’ loss $70k recognized via $100k carryover basis, not via $50k FMV).

**1-year boundary is bright-line**: day 365 = automatic; day 366 = requires instrument. Pinned by `within_one_year_exact_boundary_day_365_automatic` + `day_366_requires_divorce_instrument` + `day_366_with_instrument_applies`.

**6-year boundary is also bright-line at 2190 days** (6 Г— 365). Day 2190 = within 1-6 year window; day 2191 = beyond 6 years. Both require instrument; difference is the IncidentRule classification that surfaces to the UI. Pinned by `six_year_exact_boundary_with_instrument_applies` (day 2190 = OneToSix) + `day_2191_beyond_six_years_with_instrument_applies` (day 2191 = BeyondSixYears) + `beyond_six_years_without_instrument_disqualified`.

**Holding period only tacks when В§1041 applies**. Disqualified paths (NR alien, not incident to divorce) start the holding period at the transfer date. Pinned by `holding_period_tacks_when_section_1041_applies` + `holding_period_starts_at_transfer_when_disqualified`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1041`. Twenty-four tests pin: current spouses applies; within 1 year automatic; **day 365 exact boundary**; **day 366 requires instrument** (both with-instrument and without-instrument paths); **6-year boundary at day 2190** (calendar-leap-year-aware regression target); **day 2191 beyond 6 years**; **NR alien overrides everything** (precedence pin); carryover basis with zero recognition; FMV basis with full recognition when В§1041 fails; sale uses carryover basis under В§1041; sale uses FMV basis when disqualified; holding period tacks vs starts at transfer; **depreciated property NO dual basis** (distinguishes from В§1015); loss recognized at carryover basis even on depreciated property; zero basis transferor вҶ’ zero basis transferee; note describes each IncidentRule path; disqualification reason in note; very large basis ($1.234B) no precision loss; 366-day post-transfer LTCG via tacking; 365-day boundary STCG.

`traderview-expense::section_1015` is the **IRC В§1015 carryover basis on gifts module** вҖ” sibling to `section_1014` (stepped-up basis at death). Where В§1014 wipes out embedded gains at death, В§1015 **carries them through to the donee** for eventual recognition. No step-up on lifetime gifts.

**В§1015(a) general carryover** вҖ” donee's basis = donor's adjusted basis. Holding period TACKS from donor's acquisition date under В§1223(2). A one-day-old gift of LTCG-eligible stock is immediately long-term in the donee's hands. Pinned by `appreciated_with_long_donor_holding_immediate_ltcg_via_tacking`.

**В§1015(a) dual-basis rule (depreciated property)** is the famous loss-bifurcation trap. When FMV at gift is LESS than donor's adjusted basis, the donee takes a **split basis**:

| Sale price                      | Basis used   | Outcome                                |
|---------------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------|
| Sale > donor's basis            | Donor's basis | Gain = sale - donor's basis           |
| Sale < FMV-at-gift              | FMV-at-gift   | Loss = sale - FMV-at-gift             |
| FMV-at-gift вүӨ sale вүӨ donor's basis | (neither)  | **PHANTOM ZONE вҖ” no gain, no loss**    |

The phantom zone is the load-bearing case. If donor's basis = $100k, FMV-at-gift = $50k, and donee sells at $75k, NEITHER gain nor loss is recognized вҖ” the donor's embedded $25k loss vanishes (loss bifurcation prevents donor from shifting loss to donee) AND the donee's $25k of economic appreciation since gift also disappears for tax purposes. Pinned by `depreciated_sale_in_phantom_zone_no_gain_no_loss` + both exact-boundary pins (`depreciated_phantom_zone_at_fmv_exact_boundary` at $50k = no loss; `depreciated_phantom_zone_at_donor_basis_exact_boundary` at $100k = no gain).

**Loss-side holding-period exception**. On the dual-basis loss path, the donee's holding period **starts at the gift date**, not the donor's acquisition date. Per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1015-1, tacking applies only when the basis is determined "in whole or in part" by reference to donor's basis вҖ” using FMV-for-loss satisfies neither. Pinned by `loss_path_holding_period_starts_at_gift_not_donor` (donor held since 2020, gift 2026-01-01, sale 2026-07-01 on loss path вҶ’ STCG, not LTCG).

**В§1015(d) gift-tax basis increase** вҖ” when gift tax was paid on the transfer, the donor's basis is increased by the gift tax attributable to net appreciation:

```
increase = gift_tax_paid Г— (net_appreciation / gift_amount_for_tax_purposes)
```

where `net_appreciation = FMV - donor's basis`. Two ceilings:

1. **Cannot exceed net appreciation itself** вҖ” prevents the formula from over-correcting on unusual gift-tax-to-gift-amount ratios.
2. **Cannot exceed FMV at gift date** вҖ” prevents the adjustment from converting a gain asset into a loss asset.

Pinned by `gift_tax_basis_increase_applied_to_appreciated` (canonical case: $30k tax Г— ($90k / $84k) = $32.14k increase) + `gift_tax_basis_increase_capped_at_net_appreciation` (pathological large tax вҶ’ cap fires) + `no_gift_tax_increase_on_depreciated_property` (depreciated вҶ’ net_appreciation = 0 вҶ’ no increase even if tax paid) + `zero_gift_amount_no_increase` (division-by-zero guard).

**Mode classification:**

| Mode                              | Sale outcome | Holding period start    | В§1015(d) applies? |
|-----------------------------------|--------------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| Appreciated (single basis)        | Gain or Loss | Donor's acquisition      | Yes if tax paid     |
| Depreciated, sale > donor's basis | Gain         | Donor's acquisition      | Yes if tax paid (but net_app might be 0) |
| Depreciated, sale < FMV-at-gift   | Loss         | **Gift date** (no tacking) | No (net_app = 0)   |
| Depreciated, phantom zone         | Neither      | n/a                      | No                  |

**FMV exactly equals donor basis**: single basis (no dual). The dual-basis rule fires only when FMV is **strictly less than** donor basis. Pinned by `fmv_equals_donor_basis_single_basis_no_dual`.

**Appreciated property + sale below basis**: single-basis path вҶ’ loss via donor's (single) basis. Dual basis does NOT fire. Pinned by `appreciated_sale_below_basis_single_basis_loss` (sale $5k, donor basis $10k вҶ’ -$5k loss).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1015`. Twenty-four tests pin: appreciated carryover; **LTCG via В§1223(2) tacking** (donor held 6.5 years, sale 6 months after gift вҶ’ still LTCG); depreciated sale-above-donor-basis gain via donor basis; **depreciated sale-below-FMV loss via FMV** (not donor basis); **phantom zone with both exact boundaries** (loss-bifurcation regression target); **loss path holding period starts at gift** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1015-1 regression target); gain path tacks; **В§1015(d) gift-tax increase canonical** ($30k Г— 90k/84k); В§1015(d) net-appreciation ceiling fires; no В§1015(d) on depreciated property; zero gift tax / zero gift amount / FMV equals donor basis single-basis; sale at donor basis exact вҶ’ no-gain-no-loss; appreciated single-basis loss; dual-basis with gift-tax-increase math; note describes loss-path no-tacking exception; note describes loss-bifurcation in phantom zone; zero basis donor; **very large precision** ($1.234B basis); 366-day boundary gain LTCG; 365-day boundary loss-path STCG.

`traderview-expense::section_1014` is the **IRC В§1014 stepped-up basis at death module** вҖ” the single most powerful rule in the Internal Revenue Code for buy-and-hold investors and the foundation of every "die with low basis" estate-planning strategy. When property passes from a decedent to an heir, the heir's basis is **stepped up (or down) to the fair market value on the date of death** under В§1014(a)(1). All gain or loss that accrued during the decedent's lifetime is permanently eliminated.

Four exceptions / refinements:

- **В§1014(c) вҖ” Income in respect of decedent (IRD)** вҖ” NO step-up. Applies to IRA distributions, accrued bond interest, deferred compensation, US savings bond interest. The decedent's adjusted basis carries over and the heir recognizes the embedded ordinary income on receipt. The "IRA can't escape tax at death" rule.
- **В§1014(e) вҖ” One-year anti-abuse clawback** вҖ” if the decedent acquired the property by gift within 1 year of death AND the property passes back to the donor (or the donor's spouse), there is NO step-up. Heir's basis = decedent's adjusted basis. Blocks the "deathbed-gift-to-grandma" basis-laundering strategy where a family member transfers low-basis appreciated property to a dying relative just to harvest the step-up on death.
- **В§1014(f) вҖ” Consistent basis with estate tax return** вҖ” heir's basis cannot exceed the FMV reported on Form 706. Closes the "report low for estate tax, claim high for income tax" arbitrage. Capped by Form 706 (ceiling, not a floor); higher Form 706 doesn't INCREASE heir's basis.
- **В§2032 вҖ” Alternate valuation date election** вҖ” executor may elect to value the estate as of 6 months after DOD instead of DOD itself, BUT only if the election lowers BOTH (a) gross estate AND (b) federal estate tax. Election applies to ALL property in the estate. Canonical use: declining-value estate (stocks crashed in the 6 months after death).

**Priority ordering is load-bearing**: В§1014(c) IRD вҶ’ В§1014(e) clawback вҶ’ В§2032 valuation pick вҶ’ В§1014(f) Form 706 cap. The IRD and clawback rules check BEFORE the valuation-date logic so a В§2032 election cannot accidentally override the no-step-up rules. Pinned by `ird_takes_precedence_over_alternate_valuation_election` and `clawback_takes_precedence_over_alternate_valuation`.

**В§2032 election validity is a both-must-decrease test**, not just one. The fallback flag distinguishes "election claimed but invalid (didn't lower both)" from "election not claimed at all" вҖ” both fall to DOD valuation but only the former is a tax-position issue the caller should surface. Pinned by `alternate_valuation_election_invalid_falls_back_to_dod` and `alternate_valuation_election_without_av_fmv_falls_back` (two distinct invalidity modes).

**В§1014(e) requires BOTH conditions** вҖ” gift within 1 year AND property returns to donor/spouse. Either alone is insufficient. If the property goes to a different heir (a child rather than back to the original donor), the clawback doesn't trigger and standard step-up applies. Pinned by `one_year_clawback_does_not_trigger_when_property_goes_to_other_heir` and `one_year_clawback_does_not_trigger_outside_one_year_window` (the two negative cases).

**Step-up cuts both ways**. The rule says basis = FMV, period. If FMV < basis (declining asset), the basis steps DOWN and the embedded loss is also eliminated. Pinned by `step_down_works_basis_drops_to_lower_fmv` вҖ” this is why the alternate-valuation election can hurt the heir's income-tax position even as it lowers the gross estate.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1014`. Twenty-one tests pin: standard step-up ($10k вҶ’ $100k, $90k eliminated); **step-down works** ($100k вҶ’ $50k); В§1014(c) IRD no step-up; В§1014(e) clawback denies step-up; **clawback negative case 1** (property to other heir); **clawback negative case 2** (outside 1-year window); В§2032 valid election uses AV FMV ($75k); В§2032 invalid fallback to DOD; В§2032 election without AV FMV fallback; В§1014(f) cap applied when Form 706 below FMV ($80k vs $100k); В§1014(f) does NOT cap when Form 706 above FMV (ceiling-not-floor); **IRD takes precedence over В§2032** (priority ordering); **clawback takes precedence over В§2032** (priority ordering); zero basis full step-up ($0 вҶ’ $500k = $500k eliminated, the purest "die with $0 basis" play); basis equals FMV zero gain eliminated; **Form 706 cap with В§2032 compounds correctly** (AV first, then cap); IRD + Form 706 no interaction (cap flag stays false); note describes DOD vs alternate-valuation phrasing distinctly; very large basis no Decimal precision loss ($1.23B basis); alternate-valuation step-down (canonical declining-estate use case).

`traderview-expense::section_1014e` is the **IRC В§1014(e) appreciated-property-by-gift-within-1-year-of-death basis denial module** вҖ” the dedicated anti-abuse companion to `section_1014`. While `section_1014` includes the В§1014(e) clawback as a single binary flag, this module pins the full three-condition test with explicit boundary tests for trader-relevant scenarios. Closes the "deathbed gift" loophole: without В§1014(e), a healthy person could gift appreciated property to a terminally ill relative who would bequeath it back, generating a basis step-up under В§1014(a) that washes away the embedded capital gain ([Greenleaf Trust вҖ” В§ 1014(e) Limitation](https://greenleaftrust.com/missives/basis-step-up-on-death-the-irc-1014e-limitation/), [NAEPC Journal вҖ” Understanding В§ 1014(e)](https://www.naepcjournal.org/journal/issue17j.pdf)).

**В§1014(e) requires ALL THREE conditions** (any failure вҶ’ normal В§1014(a) step-up):

1. Decedent acquired the property BY GIFT (carryover basis from donor under В§1015)
2. Gift occurred within the **1-year period ending on date of decedent's death**
3. Property passes FROM decedent TO original donor OR donor's spouse

Each condition is individually pinned: `gift_366_days_before_death_does_not_apply` (#2), `property_at_break_even_does_not_trigger_1014e` (#1 appreciation requirement), `property_passes_to_unrelated_party_no_1014e` (#3). Boundary pinned for #2: `gift_exactly_365_days_before_death_applies` + `gift_366_days_before_death_does_not_apply` + `gift_zero_days_before_death_same_day_applies` (same-day gift counts).

**Effect when В§1014(e) applies**: basis IN THE HANDS OF THE DONOR OR SPOUSE = decedent's adjusted basis IMMEDIATELY BEFORE DEATH (typically the carryover basis from the original gift). The normal В§ 1014(a) date-of-death FMV step-up is DENIED. Pinned by `baseline_all_3_conditions_met_step_up_denied` ($100k carryover preserved vs $600k FMV step-up вҶ’ $500k of step-up avoided) + `step_up_avoided_equals_fmv_minus_carryover_basis`.

**Appreciation requirement**: В§1014(e) requires that FMV at gift > donor's adjusted basis at gift. Loss property or break-even property is NOT subject to the clawback (no step-up to deny). Pinned by `property_at_break_even_does_not_trigger_1014e` + `property_at_loss_does_not_trigger_1014e`.

**Credit-shelter-trust workaround**: В§1014(e) is NOT triggered when property passes to a TRUST with beneficiaries OTHER THAN just the donor and donor's spouse (e.g., credit shelter trust naming spouse + children as discretionary beneficiaries). The trust structure breaks the direct "back to donor" linkage that В§1014(e) targets. Pinned by `credit_shelter_trust_with_other_beneficiaries_breaks_passback` (all other conditions met but trust structure вҶ’ В§1014(e) does NOT apply, full $600k step-up preserved).

**Workflow note**: this module is purposely separate from `section_1014` even though В§1014 already has a `section_1014e_clawback` flag. The standalone module surfaces the day-count boundary, the three-condition decomposition with named failure reasons, the appreciation gate, and the trust-structure workaround as separate pinnable outcomes вҖ” letting estate planners and trader heirs reason about В§1014(e) exposure independent of the full В§1014 step-up workflow.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1014e`. Seventeen tests pin: **baseline all 3 conditions met step-up denied** (180-day gift, appreciated, passes back вҶ’ $100k carryover preserved + $500k step-up avoided); note describes APPLIES path with DENIED; **gift exactly 365 days before death applies** (boundary); **gift 366 days before death does NOT apply** (boundary); gift after death pathological clamps; **property at break-even no trigger** + property at loss no trigger; **property passes to unrelated party no trigger** with descriptive note; **credit shelter trust with other beneficiaries breaks pass-back** (workaround) + note mentions credit shelter trust; **normal step-up when В§1014(e) inapplicable** ($600k FMV preserved); step-up avoided = FMV вҲ’ carryover; **multiple disqualifications all listed in note**; citation mentions В§1014(e), В§1014(a), В§1015, credit-shelter-trust; very large step-up avoided precision ($999M); **gift 180 days within window applies**; gift same-day-as-death applies (0-day boundary); note describes inapplicable path with reasons + Normal В§1014(a).

`traderview-expense::section_1042` is the **IRC В§ 1042 вҖ” Sales of stock to employee stock ownership plans (ESOPs) or certain cooperatives module** вҖ” direct trader-business-owner companion to `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4942` (PF minimum distribution вҖ” iter 472), `section_4943` (PF excess business holdings вҖ” iter 474), `section_4944` (PF jeopardizing investments вҖ” iter 476), `section_4945` (PF taxable expenditures вҖ” iter 478), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_422` (ISO), `section_423` (ESPP), `section_409a` (NQDC). В§ 1042 originally enacted by Tax Reform Act of 1984, Pub. L. 98-369.

**В§ 1042(a) General Rule**: long-term capital gain on sale of qualified securities of domestic C corporation to ESOP is **RECOGNIZED ONLY TO THE EXTENT** that the amount realized on the sale exceeds the cost of qualified replacement property (QRP) purchased during the replacement period.

**Five eligibility requirements per В§ 1042(b)**:
1. **В§ 1042(b)(1) THREE-YEAR HOLDING PERIOD** вҖ” seller must have held the qualified securities for at least 3 years before sale
2. **В§ 1042(b)(2) 30% ESOP OWNERSHIP** вҖ” ESOP or eligible worker-owned cooperative must own at least 30% of each class of outstanding stock IMMEDIATELY AFTER THE SALE
3. **В§ 1042(b)(3) WRITTEN CONSENT** to В§ 4978 recapture (10% employer excise if ESOP disposes within 3 years)
4. **В§ 1042(b)(4) DOMESTIC C CORPORATION** вҖ” S corporations NOT eligible (must convert before sale)
5. **В§ 1042(c)(1)(B) QUALIFIED SECURITIES** вҖ” not received in В§ 83 compensation, В§ 422 ISO exercise, or В§ 423 ESPP exercise; not readily tradable on established securities market

**В§ 1042(c)(3) QRP permitted categories**:
- Common stock with voting and dividend rights of domestic operating corporations
- Preferred stock of domestic operating corporations
- Bonds of domestic operating corporations
- Convertible floating-rate notes
- Convertible bonds of operating companies

**В§ 1042(c)(3) QRP EXCLUDED categories**:
- US government securities
- Non-US securities
- Domestic subsidiaries of non-US parents
- FDIC certificates of deposit
- Mutual funds and money-market funds
- Securities of the corporation that issued the stock sold to the ESOP

**В§ 1042(c)(6) replacement period**: 15-month window beginning **3 MONTHS BEFORE** the sale and extending **12 MONTHS AFTER**.

**В§ 1042(d) basis adjustment**: QRP basis = QRP cost REDUCED BY the non-recognized gain. Gain is **DEFERRED, not eliminated** вҖ” on eventual sale of QRP, the deferred gain is recognized. **HOWEVER, basis is STEPPED UP at seller's death per В§ 1014, permanently eliminating deferred gain.**

**В§ 1042(e) recapture**: seller's eventual disposition of QRP triggers recognition of deferred gain (with exceptions for В§ 1041 spouse transfer + В§ 351 corporate formation).

**В§ 4978 SEPARATE EXCISE TAX** on the **EMPLOYER** if the ESOP disposes of any qualified securities within 3-year period after acquisition: **10%** of amount realized.

**Trader-business-owner critical fact patterns**: (1) $50M founder sale to ESOP with $40M gain + full reinvestment in QRP вҶ’ **FULL deferral**; (2) basis step-up at death under В§ 1014 PERMANENTLY ELIMINATES deferred gain вҖ” making В§ 1042 + estate planning combination among the most powerful long-term wealth-transfer strategies; (3) S corporation must convert to C corporation BEFORE В§ 1042 sale вҖ” requires multi-year planning; (4) 30% post-sale ESOP ownership threshold drives transaction sizing; (5) partial reinvestment вҶ’ PARTIAL deferral (deferred = min(gain, QRP cost), basis = QRP cost вҲ’ deferred gain); (6) QRP must be operating company stock/bonds вҖ” investor cannot park proceeds in money-market funds or mutual funds; (7) ESOP loan can be coupled with В§ 162(k) employer-deductible interest + В§ 401(a)(22) ESOP requirements.

**Distinction from В§ 1031** (like-kind exchange): В§ 1031 covers real property only post-TCJA 2017; В§ 1042 covers C-corp stock-to-ESOP only. Both defer via reinvestment.

**Distinction from В§ 1045** (QSBS rollover вҖ” already shipped): В§ 1045 covers В§ 1202 QSBS rollover into other QSBS within 60 days; В§ 1042 covers ESOP sale rollover into QRP within 15-month window. Different statutory regimes.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1042`. Thirty-seven tests pin: **S corporation not eligible** (В§ 1042(b)(4) requires C corp); **partnership not eligible**; **LLC not eligible**; **non-US entity not eligible**; **holding period under 3 years not eligible** (В§ 1042(b)(1)); **holding period exactly 3 years eligible**; **ESOP under 30% not eligible** (В§ 1042(b)(2)); **ESOP exactly 30% eligible**; **written consent missing not eligible** (В§ 4978 + В§ 1042(b)(3)); **securities received in ISO compensation not eligible** (В§ 1042(c)(1)(B)); **securities readily tradable not eligible**; **QRP US government security invalid**; **QRP non-US security invalid**; **QRP domestic subsidiary of non-US parent invalid**; **QRP FDIC CD invalid**; **QRP mutual fund invalid**; **QRP securities of ESOP corporation invalid**; **QRP domestic operating common stock valid**; **QRP domestic operating preferred valid**; **QRP domestic operating bonds valid**; **QRP convertible floating-rate note valid**; **QRP purchased outside 15-month window no deferral** (В§ 1042(c)(6)); **full reinvestment full deferral** ($8M gain + $10M QRP вҶ’ $0 recognized + $2M basis); **partial reinvestment partial deferral** ($5M QRP вҶ’ $3M recognized + $0 basis); **over-reinvestment full deferral with full basis** ($12M QRP вҶ’ $4M basis); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1042(a)/(b)(1)-(4)/(c)(1)(B)/(c)(3)/(c)(6)/(d)/(e) + В§ 4978 + В§ 1014 + В§ 1041 + В§ 351 + В§ 83 + В§ 422 + В§ 423 + В§ 1031 + В§ 1045 + В§ 1202); **note pins general rule + 15-month window**; **note pins five eligibility requirements**; **note pins QRP categories**; **note pins В§ 4978 employer recapture** (10% + 3-year); **note pins В§ 1014 basis step-up strategy** (permanently eliminates); **note pins В§ 1031/В§ 1045 distinctions** (60 days vs 15 months); **note pins companion modules**; **9-cell severity truth table**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary zero gain zero deferral**; **realistic $50M founder sale full deferral** ($40M gain вҶ’ $10M basis).

`traderview-expense::section_1091` is the **IRC В§1091 wash sale loss-disallowance module** вҖ” the trader rule. Fills the long-standing `wash_sale.rs` TODO referenced by `schedule_d.rs` since the module was first written ("does NOT model В§1091 wash sales вҖ” caller pre-processes those"). Every non-В§475(f) trader needs this; В§475(f) electors are exempt per В§475(f)(1)(C).

**Core rule (В§1091(a)):** loss on the sale of stock or securities is disallowed if the taxpayer (or spouse, controlled corp, or IRA) acquires substantially identical stock within a **61-day window centered on the sale date** вҖ” 30 days before, the sale date itself, and 30 days after. The disallowed loss is added FIFO to the basis of the replacement shares under В§1091(d); the holding period tacks under В§1223(4).

**Disallowance ratio** = min(replacement_shares_in_window, sale_shares) / sale_shares. Per-share basis addition to each replacement lot equals the original per-share loss. Replacement shares exceeding the sale count are unaffected вҖ” only the first N where N=sale_shares get adjusted.

**Rev. Rul. 2008-5 IRA carve-out** is load-bearing: when the replacement is purchased in the taxpayer's IRA or Roth IRA, В§1091(d) is overridden. The loss is still disallowed but the IRA basis is NOT increased вҖ” the loss is permanently lost. Most retail brokers do not warn on this. The module's `account_is_ira` flag drives this path: for IRA replacement lots, `basis_added_per_share = 0` and `ira_permanent_loss = true` on the result.

**В§475(f) MTM elector exemption** short-circuits the entire rule per В§475(f)(1)(C). The check happens BEFORE the window scan so a 475(f) elector with a same-day repurchase doesn't falsely trigger (test pinned: `mtm_election_short_circuits_before_window_check`).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1091`. Twenty-one tests pin: sale at gain skips rule (loss-only); loss with no replacement allowed; loss with full replacement 100% disallowed; loss with partial 40% replacement вҶ’ 40% disallowed (conservation: `|allowed| + disallowed = |original|` swept across 10/25/50/75/100); **вҲ’30 day boundary in window**; **вҲ’31 day boundary outside**; +30/+31 boundaries pinned same way; **В§475(f) elector completely exempt** even with same-day same-share replacement; **IRA replacement triggers permanent loss** with zero basis addition; FIFO allocation across multiple lots (30 + 50 + 80 with sale 100 вҶ’ first lot 30, second 50, third 20); out-of-order purchases sorted to FIFO; outside-window purchases ignored even with in-window co-purchases; replacement exceeds sale capped at sale_shares (250 repl вҶ’ only 100 adjusted); **mixed IRA + taxable splits correctly** (60 IRA + 80 tax with sale 100 вҶ’ IRA 60 shares $0 basis, tax 40 shares $400 basis); sale-on-Friday + Monday repurchase in window; zero-share sale no-op; MTM election short-circuits before window check; same-day replacement is in window (T+0 trap); conservation invariant across all partial-disallowance ratios.

`traderview-expense::section_481` is the **IRC В§481(a) accounting method change adjustment module** вҖ” the cumulative MTM hit that every trader making the В§475(f) trader-in-securities election (covered by the existing `mtm_475f` module) faces on day 1 of the election year. All open positions are marked to market; the resulting cumulative delta from the prior cost-basis method is a В§481(a) adjustment recognized as **ordinary income or loss**.

Recognition timing follows Rev. Proc. 2015-13:
- **Positive В§481(a) (net unrealized gain)** вҖ” spread **ratably over 4 tax years** (25% per year). Avoids the one-time tax cliff that would otherwise crush traders with large unrealized gains at election.
- **Negative В§481(a) (net unrealized loss)** вҖ” recognized **entirely in the year of change**. No spread on losses; the trader gets the deduction immediately, consistent with В§475(f)'s general ordinary-loss character.

The 4-year spread is purely timing relief вҖ” it doesn't change character. Both gain and loss adjustments are ordinary, not capital, not LTCG-eligible. The final year of the spread absorbs any rounding residual so the cumulative recognized total ties out exactly to the original adjustment.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-481`. Seventeen tests pin: positive adjustment 25% year 1 ($100k gain вҶ’ $25k recognized + $75k remaining); year 4 full recognition ($100k cumulative + $0 remaining); negative adjustment recognized immediately no spread; negative adjustment in years after election shows already-fully-recognized cumulative; aggregates across multiple positions ($50k + $30k - $20k = $60k net spread); zero adjustment no-op; multi-year cumulative grows 25k/50k/75k/100k predictably; recognition before election year zero; schedule has exactly 4 entries for positive; **final year absorbs rounding residual** for both even ($100,001) and odd ($100,003) totals (schedule sums tie out to total); year-2 = $25k for $100k gain; `spread_years_override: Some(2)` distributes 50% each year (only 2 entries); per-position delta breakdown preserves symbol + basis + FMV + delta; empty positions zero-adjustment zero-recognition; negative adjustment + current year before election zero recognition; **mixed winners/losers netting to negative** recognized as immediate loss (no spread).

`traderview-expense::section_263g` is the **IRC В§263(g) capitalization of interest and carrying charges on straddle property module** вҖ” trader-critical companion to `section_1092` (which defers loss recognition) and `section_1256` (which defines the hedging-transaction exception). Added by В§В§ 501 + 502 of the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 (Pub. L. 97-34, 95 Stat. 172) to plug the second leg of the straddle deferral-and-conversion strategy: deducting current-year margin interest while parking the offsetting gain leg open. В§ 263(g) **DISALLOWS** the current-year deduction; the disallowed amount is **CAPITALIZED into the basis** of the straddle property.

**Four operative subparagraphs**:

| Subparagraph | Statute | Effect |
|--------------|---------|--------|
| В§ 263(g)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 263(g)(1) | **General rule** вҖ” no current deduction for interest + carrying charges on straddle property; disallowed amount chargeable to capital account |
| В§ 263(g)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 263(g)(2)(A)вҖ“(B) | **Definition** вҖ” disallowed amount = EXCESS of (A) interest-on-indebtedness + storage + insurance + transport carrying costs OVER (B) interest received + ordinary income from property + dividends net of В§ 243 DRD + security loan fee payments includible in gross income |
| В§ 263(g)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 263(g)(3) | **Hedging exception** вҖ” В§ 1256(e) hedging transaction (bona fide hedge of ordinary property, ordinary obligations, or borrowings; identified before close of day entered into) вҶ’ В§ 263(g) does NOT apply |
| В§ 263(g)(4) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 263(g)(4) | **Coordination** вҖ” with В§ 263(h) short-sale + В§ 1277 / В§ 1282 market-discount / OID rules |

**Disallowance is TIMING-ONLY, not permanent.** В§ 263(g)(1) requires the disallowed amount to be chargeable to the capital account "with respect to the personal property to which such amount relates." Higher basis on the property вҶ’ smaller gain (or larger loss) on eventual disposition. The total income-tax effect across the holding period is the same as if В§ 263(g) did not apply вҖ” only the year of recognition shifts. Pinned by `timing_only_note_present_when_section_263g_applies` (UX-text regression) and the conservation invariant `deductible_plus_disallowed_equals_gross_invariant`.

**В§ 263(g)(3) hedging-transaction exception takes priority over the general rule.** Even where the position is in form a В§ 1092(c) straddle, a В§ 1256(e) bona-fide-hedge-of-ordinary-property identification at the close of the day the transaction was entered into wholly exempts the carrying charges from В§ 263(g) disallowance. Pinned by `hedging_transaction_section_263g_does_not_apply`, `hedging_exception_priority_over_straddle_status`, and the 4-combo invariant `only_straddle_and_non_hedging_triggers_section_263g_invariant`.

**В§ 263(g)(2)(B) DRD does NOT produce a negative dividend offset.** Where the dividends-received deduction under В§ 243 (50% / 65% / 100% depending on ownership) exceeds the dividend received, the net dividend offset saturates at zero, NOT a negative number. The statute talks of "the excess of (A) over (B)" вҖ” there is no allowed negative-excess-becomes-additional-disallowance path. Pinned by `drd_exceeding_dividend_does_not_create_negative_offset`.

**Three real-world fact patterns**:

| Pattern | Treatment |
|---------|-----------|
| Long stock on margin + protective put (classic В§ 1092(c) straddle) | В§ 263(g) applies вҖ” margin interest beyond dividend offset disallowed and capitalized to long-stock basis |
| Iron condor вҖ” four-leg option straddle | В§ 263(g) applies вҖ” per-contract carrying fees disallowed and capitalized to option basis |
| Manufacturer's commodity hedge of physical inventory | В§ 263(g)(3) exempts вҖ” full current-year deduction of carrying interest |
| Buy-and-hold AAPL on margin (no offsetting position) | Not a straddle вҖ” В§ 263(g) does not reach; full deduction subject only to В§ 163(d) investment-interest limit |

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-263g`. Twenty-five tests pin: **В§ 263(g)(1) straddle non-hedging disallows excess above offset**; **offset exceeds gross вҶ’ zero disallowance**; **zero gross вҶ’ zero disallowance**; **interest + carrying costs both in gross**; **offset interest income includible**; **offset ordinary income from property**; **offset dividend NET of DRD**; **DRD exceeding dividend does not create negative offset** (regression); **offset security loan fees received**; **offset all four components sum**; **В§ 263(g)(3) hedging transaction В§ 263(g) does not apply**; **hedging exception priority over straddle status**; **non-straddle В§ 263(g) does not apply**; **non-straddle holds full gross deductible**; **disallowed amount equals capitalized amount invariant** (load-bearing вҖ” В§ 263(g)(1) same-dollars rule); **deductible plus disallowed equals gross conservation invariant**; **only straddle + non-hedging triggers В§ 263(g) 4-combo invariant**; **citation pins subsection per path** (В§ 263(g)(1) + В§ 263(g)(2) + В§ 1092(c) for general; В§ 263(g)(3) + В§ 1256(e) for hedging; В§ 1092(c) + "inapplicable" for non-straddle); **timing-only note present when В§ 263(g) applies** (UX-text regression); **hedging note cites В§ 1256(e) definition** (UX-text regression); **non-straddle note explains inapplicability** (UX-text regression); **protective put on long stock straddle with margin interest** (real-world fact pattern); **iron condor no underlying stock carrying charges only**; **inventory hedge uses hedging exception**; **long-only position no straddle full deduction**.

`traderview-expense::section_264` is the **IRC В§ 264 вҖ” Insurance-Related Interest and Premium Disallowance module** (iter 542) вҖ” direct interest-disallowance-cluster companion to `section_265` (iter 532 вҖ” tax-exempt-income interest disallowance parallel regime), `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” DRD reduction for debt-financed portfolio stock), `section_263g` (straddle interest capitalization), `section_163d` (investment-interest cap), `section_163j` (business-interest-expense limit вҖ” separate cap on remaining post-В§ 264 interest). В§ 264 disallows four categories of insurance-related deductions targeting the historical tax-arbitrage of deducting interest on debt-financed insurance while the insurance contract's inside buildup grows tax-deferred.

**В§ 264(a)(1) PREMIUM DEDUCTION DISALLOWED**: premiums on any life insurance or endowment contract covering officer/employee/financially-interested person where the taxpayer is DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY the beneficiary.

**В§ 264(a)(2) SINGLE-PREMIUM INTEREST DISALLOWED**: interest on debt incurred to purchase or carry SINGLE-PREMIUM life, endowment, or annuity contracts is PER SE non-deductible. Single-premium definition: substantially all premiums paid within 4 years OR amount deposited with insurer for substantial future premium payments. NO В§ 264(c) exceptions вҖ” disallowance is permanent.

**В§ 264(a)(3) SYSTEMATIC BORROWING DISALLOWED**: interest on debt under plans contemplating systematic direct or indirect borrowing against cash value of life insurance, endowment, or annuity contracts (added by Tax Reform Act of 1986).

**В§ 264(c) FOUR EXCEPTIONS** to В§ 264(a)(3) systematic-borrowing rule: (c)(1) **4-of-7** вҖ” no part of 4 of first 7 annual premiums paid through debt; (c)(2) **trade-or-business** вҖ” debt in connection with unrelated trade or business; (c)(3) **unforeseen-loss** вҖ” substantial unforeseen income loss or obligation increase; (c)(4) **de minimis** вҖ” interest paid вүӨ $100 for the taxable year.

**В§ 264(f) PRO-RATA DISALLOWANCE** (Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997 inside-buildup regime): for businesses, pro-rata portion of total interest expense allocable to "unborrowed policy cash value" disallowed. Formula: disallowance = total interest Г— (avg unborrowed policy cash value / avg total assets). **В§ 264(f)(4) EXCEPTIONS**: (A) owner-employee 20%+ policies, (E) key-person policies limited to **20 individuals** max, $50K aggregate de minimis threshold.

**Four contract types**: SinglePremiumLifeEndowmentOrAnnuity, NonSinglePremiumLifeWithSystematicBorrowingPlan, NonSinglePremiumLifeNoSystematicBorrowing, BusinessOwnedLifeInsuranceWithUnborrowedCashValue.

**Seven exception statuses**: NoExceptionInvoked, FourOfSevenExceptionSatisfied, TradeOrBusinessExceptionSatisfied, UnforeseenLossExceptionSatisfied, DeMinimisInterestUnderHundredDollarsExceptionSatisfied, OwnerEmployeeTwentyPctPolicySection264f4Exception, KeyPersonPolicyUpToTwentyIndividualsSection264f4Exception.

**Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section264A2SinglePremiumInterestDisallowedPerSe, Section264A3SystematicBorrowingInterestDisallowed, Section264cFourOfSevenExceptionPreservesDeduction, Section264cTradeOrBusinessExceptionPreservesDeduction, Section264cUnforeseenLossExceptionPreservesDeduction, Section264cDeMinimisInterestExceptionPreservesDeduction, Section264fProRataInterestDisallowanceApplied, Section264f4ExceptionAppliesNoProRataDisallowance, NonSinglePremiumNoSystematicBorrowingNoDisallowance.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-264`. Twenty-two tests pin: **single-premium contract interest per se disallowed** (В§ 264(a)(2) + permanent disallowance + no В§ 264(c) exceptions); **non-single-premium no systematic borrowing no disallowance** (В§ 163(j) coordination note); **systematic borrowing no exception disallowance applies** (В§ 264(a)(3) + В§ 264(c) exception note); **4-of-7 exception preserves deduction** (В§ 264(c)(1) + premium-funding documentation); **trade-or-business exception preserves deduction** (В§ 264(c)(2)); **unforeseen-loss exception preserves deduction** (В§ 264(c)(3)); **de minimis interest under $100 preserves deduction** (В§ 264(c)(4) + $100 annual threshold); **de minimis exception over $100 falls through to disallowance** (boundary); **В§ 264(f) pro-rata basic calculation** ($10M / $100M = 1,000 bps + $5K disallowed of $50K interest); **В§ 264(f)(4) owner-employee exception no disallowance** (В§ 101(j) EOLI Pension Protection Act of 2006 coordination); **В§ 264(f)(4) key-person exception no disallowance** (20 individuals max); **В§ 264(f) zero total assets no panic safe division** (division-by-zero defense); **В§ 264(f) 100% unborrowed caps at total assets** (boundary 10,000 bps); **В§ 264(c) de minimis threshold constant pins $100**; **В§ 264(f)(4) key-person limit pins 20**; **В§ 264(f)(4) owner-employee threshold pins 20%**; **В§ 264(c) 4-of-7 constants pin 4 and 7**; **very large interest no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero interest no disallowance** (boundary); **note pins В§ 265 tax-exempt-income disallowance**; **note pins В§ 101(j) EOLI notice consent Pension Protection Act of 2006**; **single-premium takes priority over systematic borrowing exception** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::section_265` is the **IRC В§ 265 вҖ” Expenses and Interest Relating to Tax-Exempt Income module** (iter 532) вҖ” direct trader/portfolio companion to `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” debt-financed-portfolio-stock DRD reduction with parallel debt-financing disallowance logic), `section_263g` (interest + carrying-charge capitalization on straddles вҖ” parallel timing-vs-permanent split), `section_163` (general interest deduction вҖ” В§ 265(a)(2) carves out the tax-exempt slice), `section_852` (RIC distribution mechanics вҖ” В§ 852(b)(5) exempt-interest dividends are tax-exempt income for В§ 265), `section_103` (municipal bond interest exclusion вҖ” source of the tax-exempt income В§ 265 targets), `section_267` (related-party loss deferral parallel disallowance regime). В§ 265 disallows deductions for expenses (В§ 265(a)(1)) and interest (В§ 265(a)(2)) allocable to wholly tax-exempt income, preventing the double-dip where a taxpayer borrows to acquire or carry municipal bonds and claims an interest deduction that would otherwise offset taxable income while the bond yield itself stays tax-exempt.

**В§ 265(a)(2) WISCONSIN CHEESEMAN DIRECT-TRACING TEST** (Wisconsin Cheeseman v. United States, 7th Cir. 1968): for individuals and non-financial corporations, the disallowance applies when debt is "incurred or continued" to purchase or carry tax-exempt obligations. Direct-tracing standard with the IRS bearing the burden of proving the tracing connection вҖ” mere co-existence of debt and tax-exempt holdings is insufficient. Rev. Proc. 72-18 sets out the framework + dealer safe harbor; Rev. Proc. 87-53 amplifies for non-financial corporations.

**В§ 265(b) BANK / FINANCIAL INSTITUTION REGIME** (Tax Reform Act of 1986, effective Aug 7 1986): mechanical pro-rata disallowance based on ratio of average adjusted bases of tax-exempt obligations to average adjusted bases of all taxpayer assets. **NO tracing test for banks** вҖ” the formula applies regardless of debt source. Effective ONLY for obligations acquired AFTER Aug 7 1986; pre-Aug-7-1986 obligations grandfathered.

**В§ 265(b)(3) BANK-QUALIFIED OBLIGATIONS** ("qualified tax-exempt obligations"): issuer must reasonably anticipate issuing no more than **$10,000,000** of tax-exempt obligations during the calendar year. **В§ 291(e)** reduces the bank-qualified disallowance to **20%** (vs **100%** for non-bank-qualified). Bank-qualified bonds are attractive small-issuer securities sold at lower rates than non-bank-qualified.

**Dealer-in-tax-exempt-obligations safe harbor** (Rev. Proc. 72-18 В§ 7): dealers carrying inventory of tax-exempt obligations are EXEMPT from В§ 265 disallowance for interest expense on inventory-line debt вҖ” overrides direct-tracing.

**Four taxpayer types**: Individual, NonFinancialCorporation, BankOrFinancialInstitution, DealerInTaxExemptObligations.

**Three bond classifications**: BankQualifiedQualifiedTaxExemptObligation (20% disallowance per В§ 291(e)), NonBankQualified (100% pro-rata), PreAugust7_1986Grandfathered (no disallowance).

**Three debt-tracing-status branches**: DirectlyTraceableToTaxExemptAcquisition, UnrelatedNonTracingNoDisallowance, NotApplicableBankProRataRegime.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoTaxExemptIncomeNoDisallowance, UnrelatedDebtNoSectionTwoSixFiveADisallowance, IndividualDirectTracedFullDisallowance, NonFinancialCorpDirectTracedFullDisallowance, BankNonBankQualifiedHundredPctProRataDisallowance, BankQualifiedTwentyPctSection291EDisallowance, BankPreAugust1986GrandfatheredNoDisallowance, DealerInTaxExemptObligationsSafeHarborNoDisallowance.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-265`. Twenty-two tests pin: **no tax-exempt income no disallowance**; **dealer in tax-exempt obligations safe harbor no disallowance** (Rev. Proc. 72-18 В§ 7 + inventory note); **individual direct-traced full disallowance** ($50K disallowed of $50K interest expense + Wisconsin Cheeseman + Rev. Proc. 72-18 + de minimis safe harbor inapplicable on direct trace); **individual untraced debt no disallowance** (Wisconsin Cheeseman v. United States, 7th Cir. 1968 + IRS burden of proof); **non-financial corporation traced full disallowance** (Rev. Proc. 87-53 + В§ 163(j) coordination + В§ 246A coordination + Form 1120 Schedule M-1 / M-3 book-tax adjustment); **bank pre-1986 grandfathered no disallowance** (Aug 7 1986 + Tax Reform Act of 1986); **bank non-bank-qualified pro-rata disallowance** ($50M / $1B = 500 bps ratio Г— 100% = 500 bps effective вҶ’ $50K disallowed of $1M interest); **bank-qualified В§ 291(e) 20% disallowance** (500 bps ratio Г— 20% = 100 bps effective вҶ’ $10K disallowed of $1M interest + В§ 265(b)(3) + $10M cap); **bank zero total assets no disallowance safe division** (division-by-zero defense); **bank 100% tax-exempt total assets full disallowance** (boundary 10,000 bps); **bank non-bank-qualified disallowance constant pins 100%**; **bank-qualified В§ 291(e) disallowance constant pins 20%**; **В§ 265(b)(3) qualified issuer cap constant pins $10M**; **very large interest expense no overflow** (u64::MAX / 2 + saturating arithmetic defense); **zero interest expense zero disallowance** (boundary); **note pins В§ 265(a)(2) for individual disallowance**; **note pins В§ 852(b)(5) exempt-interest dividends in no-TEI branch**; **note pins Form 1120 Schedule M-1 / M-3 for corp disallowance**; **note pins bank-qualified $10M issuer cap + calendar year**; **pre-1986 grandfathering overrides non-bank-qualified classification** (severity ladder ordering); **dealer safe harbor overrides direct tracing** (severity ladder ordering); **bank severity independent of debt-tracing status** (banks apply pro-rata regardless of tracing).

`traderview-expense::section_1058` is the **IRC В§ 1058 securities-loan non-recognition framework module** вҖ” provides non-recognition treatment for securities loans satisfying a four-prong qualification test. Trader-critical for every fact pattern involving securities lending вҖ” brokerage Stock Yield Enhancement Programs (SYEP), prime-brokerage stock-loan arrangements, fully-paid securities lending, short-seller borrows. Companion to `section_1259` (constructive sales вҖ” often engages where В§ 1058 fails), `section_1092` (straddle rules), `section_1236` (definition of "securities" cross-referenced by В§ 1058(c)), `section_475` (trader mark-to-market may override В§ 1058 for dealers), `section_988` (foreign currency transactions), `section_1233` (short sales).

**Trader-critical fact patterns**:

- **Interactive Brokers Stock Yield Enhancement Program (SYEP)** вҖ” retail trader opts in to lend fully-paid shares for redistribution; 50% of net lending revenue.
- **Robinhood Securities Lending Program** вҖ” commission-free brokerage lends customer shares to institutional borrowers.
- **Charles Schwab Securities Lending Fully Paid (SLFPS)** + **TD Ameritrade Securities Lending** вҖ” similar fully-paid structures.
- **Hedge fund prime brokerage securities lending** вҖ” institutional clients lend long positions through prime broker for borrow fee.
- **Short seller's borrow of shares** вҖ” short seller is the BORROWER; lender's tax treatment turns on В§ 1058.

**В§ 1058(a) Non-recognition rule** вҖ” taxpayer who transfers securities (as defined in В§ 1236(c)) pursuant to a qualifying agreement: NO GAIN OR LOSS RECOGNIZED on exchange of (1) securities by transferor for an obligation under the agreement OR (2) an obligation by transferor pursuant to the agreement for securities identical to those transferred.

**В§ 1058(b) Four-prong qualification test**:

| Prong | Source | Requirement |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| 1 | В§ 1058(b)(1) | Return of **IDENTICAL SECURITIES** (same CUSIP, same class, same issuer) |
| 2 | В§ 1058(b)(2) | **Dividend-equivalent payments** required during loan period (all interest, dividends, other distributions) |
| 3 | В§ 1058(b)(3) | Agreement does NOT REDUCE **RISK OF LOSS OR OPPORTUNITY FOR GAIN** of transferor |
| 4 | В§ 1058(b)(4) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1058-1 + Rev. Proc. 2008-63 | **TERMINABLE ON DEMAND** (typically 5 business days) |

Pinned by `syep_all_four_prongs_non_recognition`, `missing_prong_1_identical_securities_violation`, `missing_prong_2_dividend_equivalent_violation`, `missing_prong_3_risk_of_loss_violation`, `missing_prong_4_terminable_on_demand_violation`, `multiple_prong_failures_stack` (4 prongs simultaneous failure).

**В§ 1058(a)(2) Holding period tacking** вҖ” when identical securities returned, transferor's holding period INCLUDES the period during which transferred securities were loaned. Critical for preserving long-term capital gain treatment when lending shares acquired more than 1 year prior. Pinned by `syep_holding_period_tacking_400_plus_90` (400 + 90 = 490 days = long-term preserved), `short_term_to_long_term_via_tacking` (300 + 90 = 390 = LT), `short_term_remains_short_term_with_brief_loan` (100 + 30 = 130 = ST), `long_term_boundary_exactly_366_days` (LT engaged), `long_term_boundary_exactly_365_days_short_term` (1-day boundary precision).

**Anshutz/Calloway precedent вҖ” Variable Prepaid Forward Contract bundled with stock loan fails В§ 1058(b)(3)** вҖ” Tax Court in **Anshutz v. Commissioner, 135 T.C. No. 5 (2010)** and **Calloway v. Commissioner, 135 T.C. No. 3 (2010)** held that variable prepaid forward contract combined with stock loan fails the В§ 1058(b)(3) risk-of-loss prong, resulting in constructive sale treatment at FMV. Pinned by `variable_prepaid_forward_with_stock_loan_anshutz_pattern_fails`, `vpf_with_stock_loan_uniquely_fails_invariant`.

**Failure consequence вҖ” TAXABLE SALE at FMV**: if any of four В§ 1058(b) prongs fails, the transfer is treated as a taxable sale at fair market value: (1) recognition of all built-in gain/loss on transferred securities; (2) reset of basis to FMV; (3) holding period restarts (long-term treatment LOST); (4) В§ 1259 constructive sale potentially also engaged for offsetting positions held simultaneously. Pinned by `failure_computes_built_in_gain` ($100M FMV - $60M basis = $40M built-in gain), `failure_computes_built_in_loss_negative` (-$20M built-in loss), `failure_holding_period_resets_to_zero`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1058`. Thirty-five tests pin: **SYEP all four prongs non-recognition**; **SYEP holding period tacking 400 + 90 = 490 days long-term preserved**; **missing prong 1 (identical securities) violation**; **missing prong 2 (dividend equivalent) violation**; **missing prong 3 (risk of loss) violation**; **missing prong 4 (terminable on demand) violation**; **variable prepaid forward with stock loan Anshutz pattern fails** (135 T.C. No. 5); **fully paid lending Robinhood pattern compliant**; **prime brokerage lending compliant**; **direct loan compliant**; **failure computes built-in gain $40M** ($100M FMV - $60M basis); **failure computes built-in loss negative -$20M**; **failure holding period resets to zero**; **short term to long term via tacking** (300 + 90 = 390 LT); **short term remains short term with brief loan** (100 + 30 = 130 ST); **long term boundary exactly 366 days LT**; **long term boundary exactly 365 days short term** (1-day boundary precision); **multiple prong failures stack** (4 prongs); **arrangement type truth table five cells** (SYEP + FullyPaid + Prime + Direct + VPF вҖ” only VPF fails); **VPF with stock loan uniquely fails invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1058(a)-(c) + В§ 1236(c) + В§ 1259 + В§ 1092 + В§ 475 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1058-1 + Rev. Proc. 2008-63 + Anshutz 135 T.C. No. 5 + Calloway 135 T.C. No. 3); **note pins subsection (a) non-recognition rule**; **note pins subsection (b)(1) identical securities**; **note pins subsection (b)(2) dividend equivalent**; **note pins subsection (b)(3) risk of loss most failed prong**; **note pins subsection (b)(4) terminable on demand**; **note pins subsection (c) securities definition**; **note pins subsection (a)(2) holding period tacking**; **note pins Anshutz/Calloway precedent**; **note pins failure consequence four elements**; **note pins Rev. Proc. 2008-63 safe harbor**; **note pins trader critical fact patterns** (Interactive Brokers SYEP + Robinhood + Schwab SLFPS + TD Ameritrade); **note pins companion provisions** (В§ 1259 + В§ 1092 + В§ 1236(c) + В§ 475); **defensive zero FMV zero basis no overflow**; **defensive overflow holding period saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_1092` is the **IRC В§1092 straddle loss deferral module** вҖ” the rule every active trader doing options or futures hedges hits. A **straddle** under В§1092(c)(1) is two or more offsetting positions that substantially diminish the taxpayer's risk of loss on holding any one of them. В§1092(a)(1) defers loss on the closed leg to the extent of **unrecognized gain on the offsetting position(s) held at year-end**; the disallowed loss carries forward until the offsetting position is closed. В§1092(b)(2) also **suspends the holding period** of every straddle position while the straddle remains open вҖ” preventing short-against-the-box-style conversion of short-term gain into long-term.

The **В§1092(c)(4)(B) qualified covered call (QCC) exception** is the load-bearing carve-out. A covered call (long stock + written call) is NOT a straddle when ALL three conditions are met: (1) the underlying is a **publicly traded** stock; (2) the call has **more than 30 days to expiration** when written (strict `>`, not `вүҘ` вҖ” exactly 30 days disqualifies); (3) the strike is **not deep in the money** (per Reg. В§1.1092(c)-1, the strike must be at or above the "lowest qualified benchmark" вҖ” typically the first available strike less than the prior-day close). When QCC qualifies, the loss is fully recognized and the long-stock holding period is preserved.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1092`.
