Seventeen tests pin: loss fully deferred when gain on offset exceeds loss (5k gain + 2k loss вҶ’ all 2k deferred, 0 recognized); loss partially deferred when gain less than loss (2k gain + 5k loss вҶ’ 2k deferred, 3k recognized); no gain on offset full loss recognized (still flags holding-period suspension because it's still a straddle); loss-on-disposed-at-zero no-op; QCC exception fully qualified recognizes loss with holding period preserved; **QCC disqualified at exactly 30 days** (boundary вҖ” strict `>` boundary); QCC qualified at 31 days; QCC disqualified when underlying not publicly traded; QCC disqualified when strike deep ITM; multiple offsetting legs sum their unrecognized gains; **unrealized LOSS on offsetting leg doesn't count negative** (only positive unrecognized gains feed the deferral pool, losses ignored); loss exactly equal to gain fully deferred; no-offsetting-legs degenerate case handled; note distinguishes QCC path from normal straddle path; holding-period suspension only for non-QCC straddle; empty legs no-op; QCC short-circuit runs before offsetting-gain calculation (even a $90k gain doesn't change the QCC outcome).

`traderview-expense::section_1283` is the **IRC В§1283 short-term obligation + acquisition discount definitions module** вҖ” the definitional anchor for the short-term-obligation cluster. `section_1281` (current inclusion of acquisition discount) and `section_1282` (interest-deduction deferral) both cross-reference В§ 1283 for the underlying terms.

**Four operative subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 1283(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(a)(1) | **Short-term obligation** = any bond/debenture/note/certificate with fixed maturity вүӨ 1 year from date of issue (with tax-exempt carve-out) |
| В§ 1283(a)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(a)(2) | **Acquisition discount** = SRPM minus basis (clamped at zero вҖ” broader than OID because it captures secondary-market spread) |
| В§ 1283(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(b)(1) | **Daily-portion ratable accrual** вҖ” total discount Г· days from acquisition to maturity inclusive |
| В§ 1283(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(b)(2) | **Constant-yield election** parallel to В§ 1272(a)(3) OID rules |
| В§ 1283(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(c) | **Nongovernmental OID substitution** вҖ” В§ 1281/В§ 1282 apply using original issue discount in lieu of acquisition discount |
| В§ 1283(d) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1283(d) | **Basis adjustment** вҖ” basis increased by В§ 1281 prior-year inclusion |

**В§ 1283(a)(1) short-term threshold is INCLUSIVE at 365 days.** A 365-day Treasury bill is short-term; a 366-day bill drops to В§ 1272 long-term OID rules. Tax-exempt obligations are carved out entirely. Pinned by `governmental_180_day_obligation_is_short_term`, `governmental_365_day_obligation_at_boundary_is_short_term`, `governmental_366_day_obligation_above_boundary_not_short_term`, `tax_exempt_short_term_obligation_carve_out`, and the 5-cell boundary invariant `one_year_boundary_truth_table_invariant`.

**В§ 1283(a)(2) acquisition discount is broader than OID.** Captures the spread between basis and SRPM regardless of whether the spread arose from original issue or secondary-market purchase. Premium acquisitions (basis > SRPM) clamp at zero. Pinned by `acquisition_discount_basic_math` (5 cents per dollar of discount), `acquisition_discount_premium_acquisition_clamps_at_zero`, `acquisition_discount_at_par_zero_discount`.

**В§ 1283(c) nongovernmental OID substitution.** For nongovernmental short-term obligations (commercial paper, asset-backed notes), the daily-portion calculation uses ORIGINAL ISSUE DISCOUNT in lieu of acquisition discount. Acquisition discount of $5 with OID component of $3 вҶ’ effective base = $3, not $5. Pinned by `nongovernmental_uses_oid_in_lieu_of_acquisition_discount`, `nongovernmental_zero_oid_zero_accrual`, and the 3-regime invariant `effective_discount_base_depends_on_obligation_type_invariant`.

**В§ 1283(d) basis adjustment.** Basis of short-term obligation INCREASES by the amount included in gross income under В§ 1281. Prior-year inclusion of $100 cents bumps basis from $9,500 to $9,600. Negative prior-year inclusion clamps at zero (defensive). Pinned by `basis_increased_by_prior_year_section_1281_inclusion`, `no_prior_inclusion_basis_equals_acquisition_basis`, `negative_prior_inclusion_clamps_at_zero`.

**Conservation invariant `current_year_accrual_never_exceeds_effective_discount_base_invariant`** confirms that current-year accrual is bounded by the effective discount base across all holding periods (5 cells: 10/50/100/200/1000 days held).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1283`. Twenty-four tests pin: **governmental 180-day obligation is short-term**; **governmental 365-day obligation at boundary is short-term**; **governmental 366-day obligation above boundary not short-term**; **tax-exempt short-term carve-out**; **acquisition discount basic math** (SRPM вҲ’ basis); **acquisition discount premium acquisition clamps at zero**; **acquisition discount at par zero discount**; **ratable accrual daily portion**; **ratable accrual proportional to days held**; **ratable accrual full holding full discount**; **В§ 1283(b)(2) constant-yield election uses caller-supplied daily portion**; **nongovernmental uses OID in lieu of acquisition discount**; **nongovernmental zero OID zero accrual**; **basis increased by prior-year В§ 1281 inclusion**; **no prior inclusion basis equals acquisition basis**; **negative prior inclusion clamps at zero**; **zero days held zero accrual**; **zero days acquisition-to-maturity avoids division by zero** (defensive); **long-term obligation zero accrual**; **one-year boundary truth-table 5-cell invariant** (1/180/365/366/730 days); **effective discount base depends on obligation type 3-regime invariant**; **current-year accrual never exceeds effective discount base 5-cell invariant**; **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1283(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)/(d) + В§ 1281 + В§ 1282 + В§ 1272(a)(2)(C)); **sibling-module note present** (UX-text regression for В§ 1271 + В§ 1272 + В§ 1281 trio).

`traderview-expense::section_1282` is the **IRC В§1282 short-term obligation interest-deduction deferral module** вҖ” direct short-term-obligation companion to `section_1277` (long-term market-discount interest deferral parallel). Where `section_1281` mandates current inclusion of acquisition discount for specified holders, В§ 1282 closes the parallel loophole: for holders NOT subject to В§ 1281 current inclusion, В§ 1282 defers the current-year deduction of net direct interest expense on indebtedness incurred to purchase or carry the short-term obligation.

**Four operative subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 1282(a) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1282(a) | **General deferral rule** вҖ” NDIE deductible only to extent it exceeds daily portions of acquisition discount allocable to days held |
| В§ 1282(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1282(b)(1) | **Exception for В§ 1281 holders** вҖ” accrual/dealer/bank/RIC/hedging/stripper/pass-thru exempt from В§ 1282 deferral |
| В§ 1282(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1282(b)(2) | **Election** to apply В§ 1281 to all short-term obligations вҖ” triggers В§ 1282(b) exception |
| В§ 1282(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1282(c) | **Cross-reference** to В§ 1277 long-term market-discount interest-deferral rules |
| В§ 1282(d) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1282(d) | **Nongovernmental OID** via В§ 1283(c) substitution |

**В§ 1282(a) deferral math**: `deferred = min(NDIE, accumulated_daily_portions)` where `NDIE = max(0, interest_paid вҲ’ interest_income)` and `accumulated_daily_portions = daily_portion Г— days_held`. Deferred portion carries forward until obligation is disposed of (parallels В§ 1277).

**В§ 1282(b) exception eliminates deferral entirely.** В§ 1281 holders (accrual-method taxpayers, dealers, banks, RICs, hedging-transaction holders, stripped-bond strippers, pass-thru entities) are already including the discount currently, so the symmetry that В§ 1282 enforces is already in place. В§ 1282(b)(2) election lets any holder opt into В§ 1281 treatment, which then triggers the В§ 1282(b) exception. Pinned by `section_1281_holder_exception_no_deferral`, `section_1282b2_election_triggers_exception`, `exception_applies_for_1281_holder_and_election_invariant`, and `exception_holders_always_deduct_full_ndie_invariant`.

**Conservation invariants pinned**:
- `deferred_plus_currently_deductible_equals_ndie_invariant` (20 cells: 5 daily-portion levels Г— 4 day-counts) вҖ” interest accounting must balance.
- `deferred_never_exceeds_accrual_invariant` (4 interest-paid levels) вҖ” deferral capped by accumulated discount.
- `deferred_never_exceeds_ndie_invariant` (4 daily-portion levels) вҖ” deferral capped by NDIE.

**Defensive zero-clamps**: Negative daily portion clamps at zero; NDIE clamps at zero when interest income exceeds interest paid; zero days held вҶ’ zero accrual вҶ’ zero deferral. Pinned by `defensive_negative_inputs_clamp_at_zero`, `ndie_clamps_at_zero_when_interest_income_exceeds_paid`, `zero_days_held_zero_accrual_zero_deferral`.

**В§ 1282 / В§ 1277 parallel.** Same structure: net direct interest expense, deferral to extent of accumulated discount, exception for current-inclusion holders, election option. В§ 1282(c) explicitly cross-references В§ 1277 for computational parallels. Where В§ 1277 governs long-term market-discount bonds, В§ 1282 governs short-term obligations вҖ” closing the symmetry that lets В§ 1281 + В§ 1282 jointly enforce matching income + expense timing on short-term debt instruments.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1282`. Eighteen tests pin: **other holder NDIE above accrual deferred to accrual cap**; **NDIE below accrual full deferral**; **zero NDIE no deferral**; **zero accrual no deferral**; **В§ 1281 holder exception no deferral**; **В§ 1282(b)(2) election triggers exception**; **deferred plus currently deductible equals NDIE invariant** (20 cells); **deferred never exceeds accrual invariant** (4 cells); **deferred never exceeds NDIE invariant** (4 cells); **exception applies for В§ 1281 holder and election invariant** (3 holder statuses); **exception holders always deduct full NDIE invariant** (2 holder statuses); **other holder deferral proportional to days held invariant** (4 day-counts); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1282(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)/(d) + В§ 1281 + В§ 1283 + В§ 1277); **sibling-module note present** (UX-text regression for В§ 1281 + В§ 1283 + В§ 1277 trio); **defensive negative inputs clamp at zero**; **NDIE clamps at zero when income exceeds paid**; **full year holding 365 day portions**; **zero days held zero accrual zero deferral**.

`traderview-expense::section_1281` is the **IRC В§1281 current-inclusion-of-acquisition-discount-on-short-term-obligations module** вҖ” the bookend to the OID cluster. В§ 1272 governs long-term OID; В§ 1281 governs short-term obligations (вүӨ 1 year to maturity). В§ 1272(a)(2)(C) and В§ 1271(a)(3)/(a)(4) cross-reference В§ 1281 as the operative provision for short-term obligation current accrual. Critical trader distinction: В§ 1281 applies ONLY to specific holder categories вҖ” cash-method individual investors holding short-term obligations are OUTSIDE scope and defer to В§ 1271(a)(3)/(a)(4) ratable accrual at disposition.

**Eight holder categories**:

| Category | Statute | In scope? |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| `AccrualMethod` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(A) | **Yes** вҖ” accrual-method taxpayer |
| `DealerInSecurities` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(B) | **Yes** вҖ” held primarily for sale to customers in ordinary course of trade or business |
| `Bank` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(C) | **Yes** вҖ” bank as defined in В§ 581 |
| `RegulatedInvestmentCompany` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(D) | **Yes** вҖ” RIC or common trust fund |
| `HedgingTransaction` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(E) | **Yes** вҖ” В§ 1256(e)(2) hedging-transaction identified |
| `StripperStrippedBond` | В§ 1281(b)(1)(F) | **Yes** вҖ” stripped bond / coupon held by person who stripped it |
| `PassThruEntity` | В§ 1281(b)(2) | **Yes** вҖ” pass-thru entity with 5%+ ownership control during accrual periods |
| `CashMethodIndividual` | (not listed) | **NO** вҖ” defers to В§ 1271(a)(3)/(a)(4) ratable accrual at disposition |

**В§ 1281(c) + В§ 1283(c) limit nongovernmental obligations to OID component only.** For governmental short-term obligations (T-bills, federal agency notes), the full acquisition discount accrues currently. For nongovernmental short-term obligations (corporate commercial paper, asset-backed notes), only the OID component accrues вҖ” the remainder of acquisition discount defers to disposition. Pinned by `non_governmental_limited_to_oid_component`, `governmental_vs_nongovernmental_obligation_base_invariant`, and the multi-OID invariant `nongovernmental_base_never_exceeds_oid_component_invariant` (5 OID levels confirmed).

**Cash-method individual investor is the only holder category outside В§ 1281 scope.** Across all seven mandatory-inclusion categories, current_inclusion_required is true. Only `CashMethodIndividual` falls back to В§ 1271(a)(3)/(a)(4) ratable accrual at disposition. Pinned by `cash_method_individual_outside_scope_defers_to_1271`, `cash_method_individual_only_holder_outside_scope_invariant` (7 in-scope confirmations + 1 out-of-scope).

**В§ 1283(a)(1) short-term obligation threshold is вүӨ 1 year to maturity.** Daily-portion ratable accrual under В§ 1281(a) uses days_held / days_from_acquisition_to_maturity as the proration fraction вҖ” proration is strictly linear (half days held = half daily-portion total). Pinned by `proration_is_linear_in_days_held_invariant` (180/360 daily portion Г— 2 = 360/360 daily portion).

**В§ 1281(a) daily-portion math caps at full discount when held > total days** (defensive вҖ” typically can't happen since instruments mature). Pinned by `days_held_exceeds_days_total_caps_at_total`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1281`. Twenty-one tests pin: **В§ 1281(b)(1)(A) accrual-method full-period inclusion**; **accrual-method held full period full discount**; **В§ 1281(b)(1)(B) dealer in scope**; **В§ 1281(b)(1)(C) bank in scope (В§ 581 def.)**; **В§ 1281(b)(1)(D) RIC in scope**; **В§ 1281(b)(1)(E) hedging transaction in scope (В§ 1256(e)(2))**; **В§ 1281(b)(1)(F) stripped-bond stripper in scope**; **В§ 1281(b)(2) pass-thru entity in scope**; **cash-method individual outside scope defers to В§ 1271(a)(3)/(a)(4)**; **non-governmental limited to OID component** (В§ 1283(c)); **governmental vs non-governmental obligation base invariant**; **zero days held zero inclusion**; **zero total days uses min-1 denominator** (defensive); **days held exceeds days total caps at total**; **negative acquisition discount clamps at zero**; **non-governmental negative OID component clamps at zero**; **cash-method individual only holder outside scope 8-regime invariant**; **non-governmental base never exceeds OID component 5-cell invariant**; **citation pins holder category subparagraph per type** (7 holder types Г— subparagraph match); **note documents holder category per type 7-regime invariant**; **proration is linear in days held invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_1273` is the **IRC В§1273 OID-definition + issue-price-determination module** вҖ” the definitional anchor for the OID cluster. В§ 1272 (current inclusion) and В§ 1278 (market discount) both cross-reference В§ 1273 for the underlying numbers. Companion to `section_1271` (retirement), `section_1272` (current inclusion), `section_1276`/`section_1277`/`section_1278` (market discount trilogy). Five modules now cover the full OID + market-discount-bond statutory cluster.

**В§ 1273(a) вҖ” three operative paragraphs**:

| Paragraph | Statute | Rule |
|-----------|---------|------|
| В§ 1273(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(a)(1) | **OID definition** вҖ” excess of stated redemption price at maturity over issue price |
| В§ 1273(a)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(a)(2) | **Stated redemption price at maturity** вҖ” amount fixed by last modification of purchase agreement |
| В§ 1273(a)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(a)(3) | **De minimis** вҖ” raw OID < Вј of 1% Г— SRPM Г— complete years вҶ’ treated as ZERO |

**В§ 1273(b) вҖ” four issue-price determination paths**:

| Path | Statute | Issue price |
|------|---------|-------------|
| В§ 1273(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(b)(1) | **Publicly offered for cash** вҖ” initial offering price to the public |
| В§ 1273(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(b)(2) | **Non-public cash** вҖ” price paid by first buyer |
| В§ 1273(b)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(b)(3) | **Traded debt** (issued for property; debt or property publicly traded) вҖ” FMV of debt instrument |
| В§ 1273(b)(4) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(b)(4) | **Residual cases** вҖ” SRPM minus OID (caller-supplied OID typically from В§ 1274 AFR imputation) |
| В§ 1273(b)(5) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1273(b)(5) | **"Property" definition** вҖ” includes services and right to use property |

**В§ 1273(a)(3) de minimis uses the SAME formula as В§ 1278(a)(2)(C) market-discount de minimis.** The Вј of 1% per year factor is shared вҖ” `DE_MINIMIS_NUMERATOR = 25`, `DE_MINIMIS_DENOMINATOR = 10_000`. Pinned by `de_minimis_uses_same_factor_as_section_1278_invariant` (constants match) and the strict-less-than boundary invariant `de_minimis_strictly_less_than_invariant` (3 boundary cells: thresholdвҲ’1, threshold, threshold+1).

**Issue-class determines the issue-price source.** Same SRPM + years + all class-specific inputs supplied; the result varies by class only. Pinned by `issue_class_determines_issue_price_source_invariant` (4 classes Г— identical input combination вҶ’ 4 different issue prices: $900 publicly offered, $850 non-public cash, $800 traded debt, $880 residual).

**В§ 1273(b)(4) residual case caps issue price at zero where caller-supplied OID exceeds SRPM.** Defensive against caller error вҖ” issue_price clamps at zero rather than going negative. Pinned by `residual_caller_oid_exceeds_srpm_clamps_at_zero_issue_price` (caller-supplied OID $1500 > SRPM $1000 вҶ’ IP $0, raw OID = SRPM $1000).

**Statutory-OID-zero invariant.** Across 45 cells (3 SRPM Г— 5 IPO Г— 3 years for publicly offered), statutory OID = 0 iff either no raw OID OR de minimis applies. Otherwise statutory equals raw. Pinned by `statutory_oid_zero_iff_either_no_raw_or_de_minimis_invariant`.

**В§ 1273(a)(3) threshold scales linearly with years to maturity.** A $1000 1-year bond has $2.50 threshold; a $1000 20-year bond has $50 threshold. Same raw OID ($10) is NOT de minimis for the 1-year bond ($10 > $2.50 вҶ’ discount applies) but IS de minimis for the 20-year bond ($10 < $50 вҶ’ ZERO). Pinned by `de_minimis_scales_with_years`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1273`. Nineteen tests pin: **publicly offered SRPM $1000 IPO $850 5yr OID $150**; **de minimis below threshold zero OID**; **de minimis at threshold boundary does NOT apply** (strict less-than); **de minimis just below threshold applies**; **de minimis scales with years** (1-year vs 20-year); **publicly offered uses IPO price** (ignores other inputs); **non-public cash uses first-buyer price**; **traded debt uses FMV**; **residual uses SRPM minus caller OID**; **residual caller OID exceeds SRPM clamps at zero issue price** (defensive); **issue price equals SRPM no OID**; **issue price above SRPM no OID** (premium issuance); **issue class determines issue price source invariant** (4 classes Г— identical inputs); **de minimis strictly less than invariant** (3 boundary cells); **statutory OID zero iff either no raw or de minimis invariant** (45 cells); **citation pins subsection per class** (В§ 1273(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(b)(3)/(b)(4) + В§ 1273(a)(1) + (a)(3) on all non-zero paths); **de minimis uses same factor as В§ 1278 invariant** (constants check); **zero years to maturity zero threshold full OID** (edge); **class-specific note documents issue price source** (UX-text regression for each of 4 В§ 1273(b) paths).

`traderview-expense::section_1272` is the **IRC В§1272 current-inclusion-of-original-issue-discount (OID) module** вҖ” the "phantom income" rule that bondholders of OID instruments face. В§ 1272(a)(1) requires the holder of any OID debt instrument to recognize accrued OID in gross income EACH YEAR regardless of whether cash was received. Direct companion to `section_1271` (retirement вҖ” В§ 1271(c) cross-references В§ 1272 for no-double-inclusion), `section_1276`/`section_1277`/`section_1278` (market-discount-bond trilogy вҖ” В§ 1278(a)(2)(B) revised-issue-price uses В§ 1272(a) accrual), and the matching В§ 163(e) issuer-side deduction.

**Seven operative paths**:

| Path | Statute | Rule |
|------|---------|------|
| В§ 1272(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(1) | **General rule** вҖ” holder must include sum of daily portions of OID for each day held during the taxable year |
| В§ 1272(a)(2)(A) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(2)(A) | **Tax-exempt obligation** carve-out вҖ” OID accrues but is not currently includible (В§ 103 municipal-bond exclusion) |
| В§ 1272(a)(2)(B) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(2)(B) | **U.S. savings bond** carve-out вҖ” Series EE / I bondholders may elect under В§ 454 |
| В§ 1272(a)(2)(C) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(2)(C) | **Short-term obligation** carve-out (вүӨ 1 year) вҖ” В§ 1281 + В§ 1283 govern instead |
| В§ 1272(a)(2)(D) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(2)(D) | **Natural-person small loan вүӨ $10,000** carve-out вҖ” not for tax avoidance |
| В§ 1272(a)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(3) | **Daily-portion ratable allocation** вҖ” each day in accrual period gets ratable portion of increase in adjusted issue price |
| В§ 1272(a)(6) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(6) | **Prepayable mortgage-backed / REMIC** вҖ” special present-value methodology bypasses daily-portion math |
| В§ 1272(a)(7) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1272(a)(7) | **Acquisition-premium reduction** вҖ” secondary-market basis above AIP reduces daily portion by fraction (basis вҲ’ AIP) / (stated redemption вҲ’ AIP) |

**В§ 1272(a)(1) imposes "phantom income."** A holder of a zero-coupon bond purchased at $850 with stated redemption of $1000 at maturity in year 5 receives NO cash during the holding period, but В§ 1272(a)(1) requires the holder to recognize accrued OID each year (typically ~$30/year on a ratable basis) as ordinary interest income. The matching В§ 163(e) issuer-side deduction means the system balances at the policy level, but the holder is taxed on income that hasn't been received вҖ” a deliberate symmetry to mirror coupon-bond economics. Pinned by `standard_full_year_holding_no_premium` and `standard_partial_year_proration`.

**В§ 1272(a)(7) acquisition-premium reduction protects secondary-market purchasers.** A secondary-market buyer who pays $870 for a bond with AIP of $860 and stated redemption of $1000 (so $20 above AIP, $140 below face) reduces each daily portion of OID inclusion by the fraction (basis вҲ’ AIP) / (stated redemption вҲ’ AIP) = $10 / $140 вүҲ 7.14%. Without this rule, the secondary buyer would over-recognize OID income (the premium represents already-paid OID that the prior holder accrued). Pinned by `acquisition_premium_reduces_daily_portion`, `acquisition_premium_equals_denominator_full_reduction`, `acquisition_premium_exceeds_denominator_caps_at_daily_portion`, and the regression invariants `current_inclusion_equals_daily_minus_premium_invariant` (5 premium levels confirmed) and `premium_reduction_only_applies_to_standard_not_mbs` (MBS bypasses В§ 1272(a)(7)).

**В§ 1272(a)(6) REMIC / prepayable mortgage-backed PV methodology bypasses the daily-portion math entirely.** The caller supplies a PV-based annual accrual that reflects expected prepayment behavior. Negative caller-supplied values clamp at zero. Pinned by `prepayable_mbs_uses_caller_pv_accrual` and `prepayable_mbs_negative_pv_accrual_clamps_at_zero`.

**Four В§ 1272(a)(2) carve-outs entirely.** Tax-exempts (municipal bonds вҖ” В§ 103 already excludes), U.S. savings bonds (Series EE / I вҖ” В§ 454 election available), short-term obligations (вүӨ 1 year вҖ” В§ 1281 + В§ 1283 govern instead), and small natural-person loans (вүӨ $10,000 not for tax avoidance) all bypass current OID accrual. Pinned by per-type tests plus the regression invariant `carve_outs_never_require_inclusion_invariant` (maximal OID accrual confirmed not to trigger inclusion for any of the 4 carve-out types).

**Daily-portion proration tracks days held.** Holder of $3000 raw OID for 100 of 365 days gets prorated daily-portion total of 821 cents (3000 Г— 100 / 365 = 821 cents truncated). Conservation invariant pinned by `daily_portion_proportional_to_days_held_invariant` (200 days produces roughly 2Г— the 100-day daily portion, within rounding tolerance).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1272`. Twenty-three tests pin: **standard full-year holding no premium**; **standard partial-year proration** (В§ 1272(a)(3)); **standard zero OID no inclusion**; **standard negative AIP change clamps at zero** (defensive); **standard days held exceeds days in period caps**; **tax-exempt obligation no current inclusion** (В§ 1272(a)(2)(A)); **U.S. savings bond no current inclusion** (В§ 1272(a)(2)(B)); **short-term obligation no current inclusion** (В§ 1272(a)(2)(C)); **natural-person small loan no current inclusion** (В§ 1272(a)(2)(D)); **prepayable MBS uses caller PV accrual** (В§ 1272(a)(6)); **prepayable MBS negative PV accrual clamps at zero**; **acquisition premium reduces daily portion** (В§ 1272(a)(7)); **acquisition premium equals denominator full reduction**; **acquisition premium exceeds denominator caps at daily portion**; **zero premium no reduction**; **zero denominator no reduction** (defensive); **carve-outs never require inclusion invariant** (4 carve-out types Г— maximal OID); **standard and prepayable both require inclusion invariant**; **current inclusion equals daily minus premium invariant** (5 premium levels); **daily portion proportional to days held invariant** (100 vs 200 days); **citation pins carve-out specific subparagraph per type** (В§ 1272(a)(2)(A)/(B)/(C)/(D)); **premium reduction only applies to standard not MBS**; **note documents proration only for partial year** (UX-text regression вҖ” no proration note for full-year holding).

`traderview-expense::section_1271` is the **IRC В§1271 retirement-of-debt-instruments-treated-as-sale-or-exchange module** вҖ” the character-conversion rule for the END of a bond's life. When a bond matures and the issuer pays the holder the redemption amount, В§ 1271(a)(1) treats the payment as if the bond were SOLD вҖ” taking capital character (subject to ordinary recharacterization carve-outs in (a)(2)вҖ“(a)(4)). Companion to the market-discount-bond trilogy (`section_1276` + `section_1277` + `section_1278`) вҖ” В§ 1271 governs the maturity event; В§ 1276 governs disposition before maturity; В§ 1277 + В§ 1278 govern interest deferral + election.

**Six operative paths**:

| Path | Statute | Rule |
|------|---------|------|
| В§ 1271(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(a)(1) | **General rule** вҖ” amounts received on retirement of ANY debt instrument considered amounts received in exchange therefor; default capital character |
| В§ 1271(a)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(a)(2) | **Intent-to-call OID** вҖ” gain up to OID (reduced by В§ 1271(c) prior inclusions) recharacterized as ordinary; **tax-exempt** + **premium-buyer** carve-outs |
| В§ 1271(a)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(a)(3) | **Short-term government obligations** (вүӨ 1 year to maturity) вҖ” gain up to ratable share of acquisition discount recharacterized as ordinary; constant-yield election available |
| В§ 1271(a)(4) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(a)(4) | **Short-term nongovernment obligations** вҖ” gain up to ratable share of OID recharacterized as ordinary |
| В§ 1271(b) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(b) | **Natural-person issuer exception** вҖ” В§ 1271 does not apply to obligations issued by natural persons before June 9, 1997 (with carve-out for post-acquisition purchases) |
| В§ 1271(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1271(c) | **No double inclusion** вҖ” В§ 1271 + В§ 1272 + В§ 1286 do not require inclusion of amounts already includible in gross income |

**В§ 1271(a)(2) intent-to-call carve-outs are the load-bearing edge cases.** Two distinct safe harbors: (a) **tax-exempt obligations** (municipal bonds) escape ordinary recharacterization entirely; (b) **premium-buyers** (purchased above face) also escape. Both carve-outs zero the ordinary income recharacterization and let the full gain take capital character. Pinned by `intent_to_call_tax_exempt_carve_out_no_recharacterization`, `intent_to_call_premium_buyer_carve_out_no_recharacterization`, and the invariant `carve_outs_only_apply_to_intent_to_call_path_invariant` (confirms Standard path ignores both flags as no-ops).

**В§ 1271(c) prevents double inclusion across В§ 1271 / В§ 1272 / В§ 1286.** A bondholder who has already recognized OID currently each year under В§ 1272 does not also recognize it again under В§ 1271(a)(2) at retirement. The compute subtracts `oid_previously_included_cents` from the recharacterization cap, saturating at zero where prior-year inclusion exceeds total OID. Pinned by `intent_to_call_prior_year_oid_inclusion_reduces_cap_per_1271c` and `intent_to_call_prior_year_oid_exceeds_oid_zero_recharacterization`.

**В§ 1271(b) natural-person issuer exception is the only path that escapes sale-or-exchange treatment.** All other paths (Standard, IntentToCallOID, ShortTermGovernment, ShortTermNonGovernment) treat retirement as a sale or exchange under В§ 1271(a)(1). Pre-June-9-1997 natural-person-issuer obligations take the pre-В§ 1271 ordinary character on retirement (no sale-or-exchange treatment). Pinned by `natural_person_pre_1997_section_1271_does_not_apply` and the regression invariant `only_natural_person_pre_1997_bypasses_sale_or_exchange_invariant`.

**Conservation invariant `ordinary_plus_capital_equals_gain_invariant`** confirms accounting balance across all 4 active paths Г— 4 (purchase, redemption) cells = 16 test cells.

**В§ 1271(a)(2) loss case: ordinary recharacterization caps at zero, not negative.** Where redemption < purchase (loss), gain_or_loss is negative, but ordinary_income clamps at zero вҖ” the loss flows entirely through capital. Pinned by `intent_to_call_loss_no_ordinary_recharacterization` and `standard_retirement_loss_capital`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1271`. Twenty tests pin: **standard retirement treated as sale or exchange capital**; **standard retirement at basis zero gain**; **standard retirement loss capital**; **intent-to-call OID full ordinary up to OID amount**; **intent-to-call gain less than OID cap full ordinary**; **intent-to-call prior-year OID inclusion reduces cap per В§ 1271(c)**; **intent-to-call prior OID exceeds OID zero recharacterization** (saturating subtraction); **intent-to-call tax-exempt carve-out**; **intent-to-call premium-buyer carve-out**; **intent-to-call loss no ordinary recharacterization** (regression вҖ” no negative ordinary); **short-term government ratable acquisition-discount ordinary**; **short-term government ratable exceeds acquisition-discount caps at acq**; **short-term nongovernment ratable OID ordinary**; **natural-person pre-1997 В§ 1271 does not apply**; **ordinary + capital = gain conservation invariant** (16 cells: 4 debt types Г— 4 (purchase, redemption) levels); **ordinary never exceeds gain invariant**; **only-natural-person-pre-1997-bypasses-sale-or-exchange invariant**; **citation pins subsection per debt type**; **carve-outs only apply to intent-to-call path invariant** (Standard ignores carve-out flags); **cross-reference note to В§ 1276 in standard path** (UX-text regression for market-discount-trilogy coordination).

`traderview-expense::section_1278` is the **IRC В§1278 market-discount-bond definitions + В§ 1278(b) current-inclusion election module** вҖ” the definitional + election trilogy completer that both `section_1276` (ordinary-income recharacterization on disposition) and `section_1277` (interest-deduction deferral on carrying debt) cross-reference as their definitional anchor AND as the operative opt-out mechanism. Trader who elects under В§ 1278(b) switches OFF both В§ 1276 disposition recharacterization AND В§ 1277 interest deferral simultaneously вҖ” trading deferral for current-year recognition of both income and matching expense.

**Five operative subsections**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 1278(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(a)(1) | **Market discount bond definition** вҖ” any bond having market discount, with carve-outs for U.S. savings bonds, short-term obligations (вүӨ 1 year to maturity), and В§ 453B installment obligations |
| В§ 1278(a)(2)(A) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(a)(2)(A) | **Market discount** = excess of stated redemption price at maturity over basis at acquisition |
| В§ 1278(a)(2)(B) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(a)(2)(B) | **OID bonds** вҖ” use **revised issue price** (acquisition-date OID-adjusted basis) in lieu of stated redemption price |
| В§ 1278(a)(2)(C) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(a)(2)(C) | **De minimis rule** вҖ” raw discount **STRICTLY LESS THAN** Вј of 1% Г— stated redemption Г— complete years to maturity is treated as ZERO |
| В§ 1278(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(b)(1) | **Current-inclusion election** вҖ” taxpayer may elect to include accrued market discount in gross income each year, switching OFF В§ 1276 + В§ 1277 deferral |
| В§ 1278(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(b)(2) | **Election scope** вҖ” applies to all market discount bonds acquired during or after the year of election |
| В§ 1278(b)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(b)(3) | **Irrevocability** вҖ” election is IRREVOCABLE absent Secretary's consent to revocation |

**The de minimis safe harbor is the trader-friendly load-bearing exception.** Threshold math: `redemption_basis Г— 0.0025 Г— years_to_maturity`. For a $1000 face 5-year bond purchased at $999.88 (raw discount = $0.12), the de minimis threshold is $12.50. Raw $0.12 << $12.50 вҶ’ statutory market discount treated as ZERO; В§ 1276 + В§ 1277 don't engage. For the same bond at $987.50 basis (raw $12.50 exactly = threshold), the statute says "less than" so equality does NOT trigger de minimis вҶ’ full $12.50 discount applies. Pinned by `de_minimis_below_threshold_zero_discount`, `de_minimis_at_threshold_boundary_does_not_apply`, `de_minimis_just_below_threshold_applies`, and the regression invariant `de_minimis_strictly_less_than_threshold_invariant` (three boundary cells: thresholdвҲ’1, threshold, threshold+1).

**The de minimis threshold scales linearly with years to maturity.** A 1-year $1000 bond has $2.50 threshold; a 20-year $1000 bond has $50 threshold. Same raw discount ($10) is NOT de minimis for the 1-year bond ($10 > $2.50 вҶ’ discount applies) but IS de minimis for the 20-year bond ($10 < $50 вҶ’ ZERO). Pinned by `de_minimis_threshold_scales_with_years`.

**В§ 1278(a)(2)(B) OID revised-issue-price substitution.** For an OID bond originally issued at $850 redemption-equivalent and acquired secondary-market at $800 with face $1000, market discount is $50 (against the revised issue price), NOT $200 (against the face). The OID portion ($150) is already separately taxed as ordinary interest under В§ 1272. Pinned by `oid_bond_uses_revised_issue_price_not_face` and `oid_bond_face_not_used_when_revised_price_is_lower` (confirms naГҜve face-based math would produce $200 discount; revised-price math correctly produces $50).

**Three В§ 1278(a)(1) carve-outs.** U.S. savings bonds (Series E / EE / I), short-term obligations (вүӨ 1 year to maturity at acquisition), and В§ 453B installment obligations are NEVER market discount bonds вҖ” regardless of how deep the discount. В§ 1276 / В§ 1277 deferral never engages for these. Pinned by `savings_bond_carve_out_not_market_discount_bond`, `short_term_obligation_carve_out_not_market_discount_bond`, `installment_obligation_carve_out_not_market_discount_bond`, and the regression invariant `carve_outs_never_qualify_as_market_discount_bond_invariant` (three carve-out types Г— maximally discounted purchase confirmed never qualify).

**В§ 1278(b)(3) irrevocability is enforced once the flag is set.** The election lasts for the bond's life and applies to all market discount bonds acquired during or after the election year. Even where the de minimis rule produces zero statutory discount in a given year, the election remains irrevocable. Pinned by `election_made_switches_off_section_1276_and_1277`, `election_active_with_de_minimis_still_irrevocable`, and `election_irrevocability_mirrors_election_flag_invariant`.

**Statutory-discount-zero invariant.** Across all combinations of face Г— basis Г— years, statutory market discount equals zero IFF (a) basis вүҘ redemption basis (no raw discount) OR (b) de minimis applies. Otherwise statutory equals raw. Pinned by `statutory_discount_zero_iff_either_no_raw_or_de_minimis_invariant` (54 cells: 3 face Г— 6 basis Г— 3 years).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1278`. Twenty-four tests pin: **Standard bond face $1000 basis $900 5-year discount $100**; **de minimis below threshold zero discount**; **de minimis at threshold boundary does NOT apply** (strict-less-than); **de minimis just below threshold applies**; **de minimis threshold scales with years** (1-year vs 20-year); **de minimis zero years to maturity zero threshold any discount applies**; **OID bond uses revised issue price not face**; **OID bond face not used when revised price is lower** (naГҜve face calc would produce different result); **savings bond carve-out**; **short-term obligation carve-out**; **installment obligation carve-out**; **premium bond basis above face no discount**; **basis equals face no discount**; **В§ 1278(b) election made switches off В§ 1276 and В§ 1277**; **no election defers under В§ 1276 and В§ 1277**; **election active with de minimis still irrevocable**; **carve-outs never qualify as market discount bond invariant** (3 carve-out types); **only-OID-uses-revised-issue-price invariant** (Standard vs OID with same inputs); **de minimis strictly-less-than threshold invariant** (3 boundary cells); **statutory-discount-zero iff either no raw or de minimis invariant** (54 cells); **election irrevocability mirrors election flag invariant**; **citation pins subsections per path** (В§ 1278(a)(1) + В§ 1278(a)(2) for standard; В§ 1278(a)(2)(C) for de minimis; В§ 1278(b)(1) + В§ 1278(b)(3) for election; "savings bond" for carve-out); **no-election note documents deferral engaging** (UX-text regression for В§ 1276 + В§ 1277 cross-reference).

`traderview-expense::section_1277` is the **IRC В§1277 deferral-of-interest-deduction-allocable-to-accrued-market-discount module** вҖ” the direct companion to `section_1276`. В§ 1276 converts disposition gain to ordinary income up to accrued market discount; В§ 1277 closes the matching loophole by DEFERRING the current-year interest deduction on debt carried to purchase the bond up to the same accrued discount. Together they match income and expense recognition timing on market-discount bonds.

**Three operative subsections + the В§ 1278(b) election coordination**:

| Subsection | Statute | Rule |
|------------|---------|------|
| В§ 1277(a) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1277(a) | **General rule** вҖ” net direct interest expense (NDIE) deductible currently ONLY to extent it exceeds market discount allocable to days held |
| В§ 1277(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1277(b)(1) | **Net-interest-income carryover recovery** вҖ” disallowed carryover recovered in later year up to net interest income on bond |
| В§ 1277(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1277(b)(2) | **Disposition terminal recovery** вҖ” all remaining deferred interest recovered in the disposition year |
| В§ 1277(c) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1277(c) | **NDIE definition** вҖ” excess of (1) interest paid/accrued on debt incurred to purchase/carry bond OVER (2) interest (incl. OID under В§ 1272(a)) includible in gross income on bond |
| В§ 1278(b) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1278(b) | **Election coordination** вҖ” taxpayer who elects current inclusion of accrued market discount is EXEMPT from В§ 1277 deferral (matching income recognition makes deferral unnecessary) |

В§ 1277(d) was struck out entirely by Pub. L. 103-66 (1993).

**The В§ 1277 / В§ 1276 matched-deferral pair is what makes market-discount-bond taxation symmetric.** Without В§ 1277, a margin-buying bond trader could deduct interest currently (reducing taxable income) while deferring market-discount income recognition to disposition (В§ 1276). Section 1277 forces a hold вҖ” the interest deduction waits for either (a) net interest income on the same bond in a later year (В§ 1277(b)(1)) or (b) the disposition year (В§ 1277(b)(2)). В§ 1278(b) provides the opt-out: if the taxpayer elects current recognition of accrued discount, current deduction of carrying interest is restored. Pinned by `current_inclusion_election_no_deferral_full_deduction` and `current_inclusion_recovers_prior_carryover`.

**В§ 1277(a) deferral math**: `deferred = min(NDIE, accrued_market_discount_for_year)`; `currently_deductible = NDIE вҲ’ deferred`. Pinned by `deferred_equals_min_of_ndie_and_accrued_discount`, `ndie_below_accrued_full_ndie_deferred`, `ndie_above_accrued_excess_deductible_currently`, and the invariants `deferred_never_exceeds_ndie_invariant`, `deferred_never_exceeds_accrued_invariant`.

**Multi-year flow simulation** demonstrates the full lifecycle: year 1 NDIE $5000 + accrued $5000 вҶ’ defer $5000, carryover $5000; year 2 same вҶ’ defer $5000, total carryover $10000; disposition year 3 вҶ’ terminal recovery of full $10000 deducted in disposition year. Pinned by `multi_year_carryover_then_disposition_terminal_recovery` and the invariant `disposition_year_zeros_remaining_carryover_invariant` (12 cells: 4 prior values Г— 3 NDIE Г— 2 accrued).

**Conservation invariant `current_year_deferred_plus_post_1277a_deductible_equals_ndie_invariant`** confirms the В§ 1277(a) split is loss-less: pre-recovery NDIE breakdown always sums back to NDIE regardless of accrued amount.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1277`. Twenty-six tests pin: **NDIE basic excess of interest over bond income**; **NDIE clamps at zero when interest income exceeds interest paid**; **deferred equals min of NDIE and accrued discount**; **NDIE below accrued full NDIE deferred**; **NDIE above accrued excess deductible currently**; **zero accrued discount full NDIE deductible currently**; **negative accrued clamps at zero** (defensive); **prior carryover recovered to net interest income** (В§ 1277(b)(1)); **net interest income exceeds prior recovers full carryover**; **zero net interest income no recovery**; **disposition year recovers all carryover** (В§ 1277(b)(2)); **disposition year combines b1 and b2 recovery**; **disposition year no carryover no recovery**; **current-inclusion election no deferral full deduction** (В§ 1278(b)); **current-inclusion recovers prior carryover**; **current-inclusion zero NDIE zero deduction but recovers carryover**; **multi-year carryover then disposition terminal recovery** (3-year flow simulation); **deferred never exceeds NDIE invariant** (5 accrued levels); **deferred never exceeds accrued invariant** (5 NDIE levels); **current year deferred plus post-1277(a) deductible equals NDIE invariant** (conservation); **disposition year zeros remaining carryover invariant** (12 cells); **citation pins all subsections in default path** (В§ 1277(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c) + В§ 1278(b) + В§ 1276); **citation pins В§ 1278(b) path when election in effect**; **В§ 1277(a) deferral note present** (UX-text regression); **В§ 1277(b)(2) disposition recovery note present** (UX-text regression); **current-inclusion note present when election in effect** (UX-text regression).

`traderview-expense::section_1276` is the **IRC В§1276 market-discount-bond ordinary-income recharacterization module** вҖ” trader-critical for every secondary-market bond purchased at a price below stated redemption at maturity. Converts disposition gain from capital to ORDINARY INCOME up to the accrued market discount. Companion to `section_988` (foreign currency ordinary character) and `section_1259` (constructive-sale anti-conversion). The bond-equivalent of В§ 1234B's character-conversion rule.

**Six operative subparagraphs**:

| Subparagraph | Statute | Rule |
|--------------|---------|------|
| В§ 1276(a)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(a)(1) | **General rule** вҖ” gain on disposition of any market discount bond is ordinary income up to accrued market discount |
| В§ 1276(a)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(a)(2) | **Non-sale dispositions** вҖ” gift, distribution, etc. treated as realizing FMV of the bond |
| В§ 1276(a)(3) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(a)(3) | **Partial principal payments** вҖ” ordinary income up to accrued market discount |
| В§ 1276(a)(4) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(a)(4) | **Treatment as interest** вҖ” amount treated as INTEREST for Code purposes (carve-outs В§В§ 103, 871(a), 881, 1441, 1442, 6049) |
| В§ 1276(b)(1) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(b)(1) | **Ratable accrual (DEFAULT)** вҖ” accrued = market_discount Г— (days held Г· days from acquisition to maturity) |
| В§ 1276(b)(2) | 26 U.S.C. В§ 1276(b)(2) | **Constant-yield election** вҖ” В§ 1272(a) OID yield-to-maturity formula as if bond originally issued on acquisition date at taxpayer's basis; **IRREVOCABLE** as to that bond |

**В§ 1278(a)(2)(A) market discount = stated redemption вҲ’ basis (clamped at zero).** Premium bonds (purchase вүҘ face) have no market discount and В§ 1276 doesn't apply. Pinned by `purchase_at_or_above_face_no_market_discount` and `purchase_at_face_no_discount`.

**В§ 1278(b) current-inclusion election prevents double inclusion.** Taxpayer who elects annual recognition of accrued discount excludes prior-year recognitions from the В§ 1276 disposition recharacterization. The compute subtracts `prior_years_accrual_already_taxed_cents` from the recharacterization cap (saturating at zero вҖ” prior-year inclusion in excess of total accrued does NOT create negative recharacterization). Pinned by `current_inclusion_election_subtracts_prior_year_accrual`, `current_inclusion_prior_year_equals_accrued_zero_recharacterization`, and `current_inclusion_prior_year_exceeds_accrued_does_not_negate`.

**В§ 1276(a)(4) treatment as interest is conditional on actual recharacterization.** Where no discount or no held-period accrual or no realized gain produces zero ordinary recharacterization, the bond produces only capital gain/loss (the `treated_as_interest` flag stays false). Pinned by `treated_as_interest_only_when_ordinary_recharacterization_nonzero`.

**Three load-bearing fact patterns**:

| Pattern | Treatment |
|---------|-----------|
| Buy $900 face-$1000 bond, hold half the term, sell at face | Accrued $50; ordinary $50; capital $50 (residual gain) |
| Buy $900 face-$1000 bond, hold full term, sell at face | Accrued $100; ordinary $100; capital $0 |
| Buy $900 face-$1000 bond, gift at FMV $970 | В§ 1276(a)(2) treats as realizing $970; ordinary $70; capital $0 |
| Buy $900 face-$1000 bond, receive $3000 partial principal | В§ 1276(a)(3) ordinary $3000 (capped at accrued); capital $0 |
| Buy $1000 face-$950 bond at premium, sell at $1050 | No discount вҶ’ В§ 1276 inapplicable; all $50 capital gain |

**Conservation invariants pinned**:
- `ordinary_plus_capital_equals_total_gain_invariant` вҖ” accounting must balance across Sale + NonSaleDisposition paths (PartialPrincipalPayment paths handle realized as direct payment amount, not gain).
- `ordinary_never_exceeds_accrued_market_discount_invariant` вҖ” across 3 disposition types Г— 6 realized amounts (18 cells), ordinary is always вүӨ accrued.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1276`. Twenty-three tests pin: **ratable accrual half holding half discount**; **ratable full holding full discount**; **ratable holding longer than total caps at full discount**; **ratable zero days held zero accrued**; **ratable zero total days uses full discount** (edge вҖ” degenerate denominator); **gain less than accrued ordinary caps at gain**; **loss on sale no ordinary recharacterization** (regression вҖ” no negative ordinary); **purchase at or above face no market discount**; **purchase at face no discount**; **non-sale disposition uses FMV as realized** (В§ 1276(a)(2)); **partial principal payment ordinary up to accrued** (В§ 1276(a)(3)); **partial principal payment exceeding accrued caps**; **constant-yield election uses caller-supplied accrual** (В§ 1276(b)(2)); **constant-yield caller-supplied exceeds market discount caps**; **constant-yield negative caller-supplied clamps at zero**; **current-inclusion election subtracts prior-year accrual** (В§ 1278(b)); **current-inclusion prior year equals accrued zero recharacterization**; **current-inclusion prior year exceeds accrued does not negate** (regression вҖ” saturating subtraction); **treated as interest only when ordinary recharacterization nonzero** (В§ 1276(a)(4) flag accuracy); **ordinary plus capital equals total gain invariant** across Sale + NonSale paths; **ordinary never exceeds accrued market discount invariant** across 3 disposition types Г— 6 realized amounts; **citation pins subsection per disposition type** (В§ 1276(a)(1) for Sale; В§ 1276(a)(3) for partial; В§ 1276(a)(2) for non-sale; all paths include В§ 1276(a)(4) interest treatment + В§ 1278(a)(2)(A) market-discount definition); **treated-as-interest note present when recharacterization occurs** (UX-text regression for В§ 1276(a)(4)).

`traderview-expense::section_1259` is the **IRC В§1259 constructive sale of appreciated financial position module** вҖ” the "short against the box" anti-conversion rule that ended pre-1997 traders' ability to lock in gains tax-free by shorting the same stock they held long. An appreciated long position (FMV > basis) is **constructively sold** when the taxpayer enters a hedge that substantially eliminates risk of loss and opportunity for gain. The deemed sale triggers gain recognition at FMV as of the hedge entry date; basis steps up to FMV per В§1259(b)(2) and holding period restarts on the hedge entry date.

`HedgeType` enumerates the В§1259(c) covered-transaction list: `ShortSaleSubstantiallyIdentical` (the classic), `OffsettingNotionalPrincipalContract` (total-return swap), `FuturesContractSubstantiallyIdentical`, `ForwardContractSubstantiallyIdentical`, `CombinedPositionsSameEconomicEffect` (collar / synthetic short), plus two non-triggering categories: `Section1256Contract` (exempt under В§1259(c)(3)(C) because В§1256 already marks to market) and `NoCoveredTransaction` (standalone protective put at OTM strike, etc.).

The **В§1259(c)(3)(A) safe harbor** lets a hedge escape if ALL three conditions hold: (1) hedge closed **before January 30** of the next year (= 30 days after Dec 31); (2) taxpayer holds the long position throughout the **60-day window** after closing the hedge; (3) **no risk reduction** on the long during that 60-day window (no replacement hedge, no protective put bought, no new offsetting position). Missing any one condition triggers the constructive sale retroactively to the hedge entry date вҖ” not the close date. Character is LTCG vs STCG based on whether the long position had been held > 1 year at the hedge entry date, computed using calendar-month arithmetic (`checked_add_months(12)`) so leap years don't shift the boundary.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1259`. Nineteen tests pin: classic short-against-box triggers + $30k LTCG + $80k basis step-up + new holding period from hedge date; short-term holding yields STCG; **exactly one year is short-term** (leap-year-safe calendar math); safe harbor with all three conditions = no trigger; safe harbor failures (missing 60-day window / risk reduction / late close) each trigger; loss position no trigger (В§1259(b)(1) requires appreciation); break-even no trigger; В§1256 contract exempt under В§1259(c)(3)(C); NoCoveredTransaction no trigger; offsetting NPC triggers; forward / futures / combined positions each trigger; basis step-up equals gain recognized invariant; safe harbor preserves original basis + no new holding period; appreciation = FMV - basis; loss position with failed safe harbor still no trigger.

`traderview-expense::section_1341` is the **IRC В§1341 claim-of-right doctrine module** вҖ” the relief provision for taxpayers who reported income in a prior year under an apparent unrestricted right and must restore it in a later year. Trader-critical for commission clawback, bonus disgorgement, SEC-ordered disgorgement of insider-trading-tainted profits, Social Security overpayment recovery, repayment of erroneously credited gains, and any other situation where prior-year-recognized income is recaptured later. The whole point of В§1341: when the prior-year bracket was higher than the current-year bracket, a В§165 deduction at the lower current-year rate UNDERCOMPENSATES вҖ” В§1341 lets you compute the relief at the prior-year rate instead via a refundable credit.

**В§1341(a)(3) threshold**: repayment must **exceed $3,000**. At or below $3,000 (strict greater-than), В§1341 is unavailable and the deduction (if any) is taken under В§165 only.

**Two methods**:
- **Method A вҖ” В§ 1341(a)(4) deduction**: take the repayment as a В§165 deduction in the current year. Pay the resulting current-year tax.
- **Method B вҖ” В§ 1341(a)(5) refundable credit**: compute current-year tax WITHOUT the deduction, then reduce by the amount of tax that was originally paid on the now-repaid income in the prior year. The credit is REFUNDABLE вҖ” if it exceeds current-year tax, the excess is refunded.

**В§ 1341(b)(2) mandates the LESSER of the two computations** вҖ” no election is required; the relief is automatic. When the prior-year bracket was higher (typical clawback scenario), Method B wins. When current-year bracket is higher (rare), Method A wins. Ties pick Method A (administratively simpler вҖ” no Form 1040 credit-line adjustment).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1341`. Nineteen tests pin: **below $3,000 threshold В§ 1341 unavailable**; **exactly $3,000 threshold unavailable (strict greater-than boundary)** + В§ 1341(a)(3) citation regression; **one cent above $3,000 вҶ’ threshold met** (off-by-one); **credit method beats deduction when prior-year rate higher** (typical clawback scenario вҖ” 37%-bracket prior year repaid in 24%-bracket current year); **deduction method beats credit when current-year rate higher** (rare вҖ” late-career bonus repaid into NIIT-stacked year); **methods tie вҶ’ picks deduction** (Method A simpler); **credit method creates refund when credit exceeds tax** (refundable-credit regression target вҖ” В§ 1341(a)(5)); **deduction better вҶ’ no refund from credit** (regression вҖ” refund only manifests if credit method picked); **no prior-year tax decrease вҶ’ falls back to deduction**; **$1M repayment with high prior-year rate** ($300K cy_without вҲ’ $400K py_decrease вҶ’ $0 tax + $100K refund + $200K savings); **citation pins В§ 1341 + В§ 1341(a)(4) + В§ 1341(a)(5) + В§ 1341(b)(2)**; **note describes lesser-of computation**; **zero repayment below threshold**; **negative inputs clamped**; **savings vs deduction-only non-negative** (invariant across 4 scenarios); **refund only when credit method picked**; **trader clawback scenario** ($50K commission taxed at 37% repaid at 24% вҶ’ credit wins by $6,500); **Social Security overpayment** (bracket-parity вҶ’ tie вҶ’ deduction picked); **equal methods select deduction**.

`traderview-expense::section_1361` is the **IRC В§1361 S-corporation eligibility 6-prong test module** вҖ” trader-relevant for active traders who structure their trading activity through an S corporation (commonly paired with `mtm_475f` В§475(f) trader election). В§1361(b)(1) sets out the small-business-corporation eligibility test; failing ANY prong results in either inability to elect S status under В§1362(a) or termination of an existing S election. В§1361(b)(2) carves out 4 categorically ineligible corporation types.

**В§1361(b)(1) six eligibility prongs** вҖ” all must be met:

| Prong | Requirement | Authority |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| (A) | **Domestic** corporation | В§1361(b)(1)(A) |
| (B) | Not an "ineligible corporation" | В§1361(b)(2) |
| (C) | вүӨ **100 shareholders** (after В§1361(c)(1) family attribution) | В§1361(b)(1)(C) |
| (D) | Shareholders limited to individuals + qualifying estates + qualifying trusts + certain exempt orgs | В§1361(b)(1)(D) |
| (E) | **No** nonresident alien shareholders (even ONE share terminates) | В§1361(b)(1)(E) |
| (F) | Only **one class of stock** outstanding (voting-rights differences ARE permitted; economic differences are NOT) | В§1361(b)(1)(F) |

**В§1361(b)(2) ineligible corporation types** (4 categorical bars):
- Financial institutions using the В§585 reserve method for bad debts
- Insurance companies subject to Subchapter L
- Foreign sales corporations (В§922) and DISCs

**В§1361(c)(1) family attribution** вҖ” treats "members of a family" as ONE shareholder for the 100-shareholder cap. Family = common ancestor + lineal descendants + spouses/former spouses of common ancestor or any descendant. A single family with 200 members + 50 unrelated shareholders = 1 + 50 = **51 effective shareholders**, well under the cap. Each family member must independently meet the permissible-shareholder requirements.

**В§1361(c)(2) permitted trusts** вҖ” grantor trust, ESBT (electing small business trust), QSST (qualified subchapter S trust), voting trust, certain testamentary trusts. Other trust forms terminate the S election.

**Prong-failure priority ordering**: when multiple prongs fail simultaneously, the module reports the highest-priority failure first: (A) domestic вҶ’ (B) ineligible corp type вҶ’ (C) count вҶ’ (D) type вҶ’ (E) NRA вҶ’ (F) classes. This matches the IRS audit-presentation order.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1361`. Twenty tests pin: **baseline qualifies** (50 effective shareholders, all 6 prongs pass); **foreign corp fails domestic prong**; **3 ineligible-corp-type failures** (financial institution reserve method + insurance Subchapter L + foreign sales corp / DISC); **exactly 100 shareholders qualifies** (boundary); **101 shareholders fails** (strict вүӨ 100); **family attribution collapses 50 members to 1** (50 family + 49 non-family = 50 effective); **family attribution lets large family avoid cap** (200 family + 50 non-family = 51 effective); **partnership shareholder fails** В§1361(b)(1)(D); **NRA shareholder fails** even one share; **two classes of stock fails**; **domestic failure short-circuits other failures** (regression вҖ” highest priority wins); **financial institution short-circuits count failure**; **count failure reported before NRA failure** (priority ordering); citation mentions all 6 prongs + В§1361(c)(1) + В§1361(c)(2); **citation mentions voting-rights differences ARE permitted** (regression вҖ” common confusion); note qualifying path mentions "qualifies as S corporation"; note failure path describes В§1361(b)(1)(E) + nonresident alien; note reports effective shareholder count post-В§1361(c)(1).

`traderview-expense::section_1366` is the **IRC В§ 1366 вҖ” Pass-thru of items to shareholders module** вҖ” the cornerstone S-corporation pass-through provision under which every item of income, loss, deduction, credit, and tax-exempt income earned by an S corporation flows through to shareholders in pro rata share and retains its character at the shareholder level. Direct trader-business-owner companion to `section_1361` (S corporation election), `section_1367` (basis adjustments), `section_1368` (distributions), `section_1374` (built-in gains), `section_1375` (passive investment income), `section_1042` (ESOP rollover вҖ” iter 480), `section_4978` (ESOP recapture вҖ” iter 484), `section_6166` (estate tax installment вҖ” iter 486). В§ 1366 current framework from Tax Reform Act of 1982 Subchapter S Revision, Pub. L. 97-354.

**В§ 1366(a)(1) Pro rata share** вҖ” every shareholder reports on personal return their pro rata share of:
1. **В§ 1366(a)(1)(A) SEPARATELY-STATED ITEMS** вҖ” items that could affect shareholder tax liability differently than if not separately stated:
   - Short-term capital gains and losses
   - Long-term capital gains and losses
   - В§ 1231 gains and losses
   - Charitable contributions (retain В§ 170(b) 30%/60% AGI limitation at shareholder level)
   - Dividend income (qualified vs ordinary)
   - Tax-exempt interest income
   - Foreign tax credit items
   - Investment interest expense
   - В§ 179 expense election
   - AMT preference items
   - **В§ 199A qualified business income (QBI) deduction**
   - В§ 1411 net investment income tax items
2. **В§ 1366(a)(1)(B) NON-SEPARATELY STATED INCOME OR LOSS** вҖ” ordinary trade or business income/loss (lumped together)

**В§ 1366(b) Character flow-through**: each pro rata share item treated by shareholder as if the underlying activity had occurred at the SHAREHOLDER level вҖ” long-term capital gains stay long-term capital gains; В§ 1231 stays В§ 1231; tax-exempt interest stays tax-exempt.

**В§ 1366(d)(1) Three-tier loss limitation**:
1. **В§ 1366(d)(1)(A) BASIS LIMITATION** вҖ” aggregate losses + deductions cannot exceed sum of (adjusted basis in S corp STOCK) + (adjusted basis in INDEBTEDNESS of S corp to shareholder)
2. **В§ 465 AT-RISK LIMITATION** вҖ” losses also limited to amount shareholder is at risk in S corp activity
3. **В§ 469 PASSIVE ACTIVITY LOSS LIMITATION** вҖ” passive losses can offset only passive income

Three-tier ordering per 26 C.F.R. В§ 1.1366-2: basis applied first вҶ’ at-risk second вҶ’ passive third. Losses suspended at one tier may flow through to next tier only if next-tier test allows.

**В§ 1366(d)(2) Carryover**: any loss or deduction disallowed by basis limitation is treated as incurred by the S corporation in the succeeding tax year with respect to that shareholder; carries over **INDEFINITELY** until basis restored (basis adjustments per В§ 1367, distributions per В§ 1368).

**В§ 1366(d)(3) Post-termination transition period**: if S election terminates, suspended losses become deductible during PTTP (greater of **1-year** window after termination or **120 days** after IRS notice of termination determination) to extent of basis increase resulting from shareholder capital contribution.

**В§ 1366(e) Family group reasonable compensation**: IRS authorized to reallocate items among family group members of S corporation if compensation paid to family members performing services is unreasonable. Forces family-shareholder S-corps to pay **reasonable W-2 wages**.

**В§ 1366(f) S-corporation-level tax adjustments**: В§ 1366(f)(1) pro rata share reduced for В§ 1374 built-in gains tax paid by S corp (avoids double tax); В§ 1366(f)(2) pro rata share adjusted for В§ 1375 passive investment income tax when passive income exceeds 25% of gross receipts.

**Trader-business-owner critical fact patterns**: (1) Trader-CEO of S corp with $2M corporate loss + 60% pro rata share + $1M stock+debt basis cap вҶ’ $1M allowed at basis, **$200K suspended INDEFINITELY** under В§ 1366(d)(2); (2) S corp losses offset shareholder's W-2 wages, capital gains, and other income вҖ” but ONLY to extent of stock + debt basis under В§ 1366(d)(1); (3) at-risk and passive activity loss limitations **stack on top** of basis limitation; (4) charitable contributions flow through SEPARATELY at character of contribution вҖ” retain 30%/60% AGI limitation per В§ 170(b); (5) В§ 199A 20% QBI deduction flows through SEPARATELY as a deduction; (6) **В§ 1366(e) family group reasonable compensation** is the primary IRS enforcement tool against family S-corp wage-vs-distribution arbitrage; (7) suspended losses under В§ 1366(d)(2) preserve indefinitely so patient shareholders eventually capture economic losses when basis restored.

**Distinction from В§ 702** (partnership pass-through): both require separately-stated items and character flow-through, but partnerships allow partner-specific В§ 704(b) basis tracking and В§ 704 special allocations. **S corporations require single-class-of-stock** under В§ 1361(b)(1)(D).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1366`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **C corp not eligible**; **partnership not eligible**; **sole proprietor not eligible**; **losses fully within basis compliant**; **losses exceed basis partially suspended**; **losses fully suspended zero basis**; **at-risk limitation below basis**; **passive activity no passive income fully suspended at passive**; **passive activity partial offset**; **non-passive no passive offset needed**; **pro rata share 25%**; **pro rata share 100%**; **long-term capital loss separately stated**; **separately-stated loss flows through**; **basis limit + at-risk + passive limit stack** (60K вҶ’ 40K вҶ’ 20K); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1366(a)(1)/(b)/(d)(1)/(d)(1)(A)/(d)(2)/(d)(3)/(e)/(f)/(f)(1)/(f)(2) + В§ 465 + В§ 469 + В§ 1361 + В§ 1367 + В§ 1368 + В§ 1374 + В§ 1375 + В§ 1377 + В§ 199A + В§ 1411 + В§ 170(b) + В§ 702 + В§ 704 + В§ 1361(b)(1)(D) + 26 C.F.R. В§ 1.1366-2); **note pins pro rata separately-stated categories**; **note pins character flow-through**; **note pins three-tier loss limitation**; **note pins В§ 1366(d)(2) indefinite carryover**; **note pins В§ 1366(d)(3) post-termination transition period** (1 year + 120 days); **note pins В§ 1366(e) family group reasonable compensation** (reasonable W-2 wages); **note pins partnership distinction** (В§ 702 + В§ 704 + single-class-of-stock); **note pins companion modules**; **6-cell severity truth table**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary zero loss compliant**; **realistic $2M trader S corp loss** ($1.2M shareholder share вҶ’ $1M allowed + $200K suspended).

`traderview-expense::section_1367` is the **IRC В§ 1367 S-corp shareholder stock basis adjustment module** вҖ” core math for any trader organizing as an S corporation. В§ 1367 governs how shareholder stock basis shifts each year as the corporation's income, losses, distributions, and expenses flow through. The basis figure controls (a) how much corporate loss may be deducted on the personal return under В§ 1366(d), (b) whether distributions are tax-free under В§ 1368, and (c) gain recognition on disposition. Direct sibling to `section_1361` (S-corp definition + eligibility) and `section_1374` (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion). Form 7203 is the IRS basis-tracking schedule.

**В§ 1367(a)(1) basis increases** (positive items, applied first):

| Subparagraph | Source | Item |
|--------------|--------|------|
| (a)(1)(A) | В§ 1366(a)(1)(A) | Separately stated income items (capital gains, charitable contribution pass-through) |
| (a)(1)(B) | В§ 1366(a)(1)(B) | Nonseparately computed income (ordinary trade-or-business income) |
| (a)(1)(C) | В§ 1367(a)(1)(C) | Excess of depletion deductions over basis of depleted property |

**В§ 1367(a)(2) basis decreases** (negative items, floored at $0):

| Subparagraph | Source | Item |
|--------------|--------|------|
| (a)(2)(A) | В§ 1368 | Tax-free distributions |
| (a)(2)(B) | В§ 1366(a)(1)(A) | Separately stated loss/deduction items |
| (a)(2)(C) | В§ 1366(a)(1)(B) | Nonseparately computed loss |
| (a)(2)(D) | В§ 1367(a)(2)(D) | Noncapital, nondeductible expenses (50% meals under В§ 274(n), fines, lobbying, political contributions) |
| (a)(2)(E) | В§ 1367(a)(2)(E) | Shareholder depletion deduction |

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1367-1(f) вҖ” standard four-step ordering**:

| Step | Item | Excess treatment |
|------|------|------------------|
| 1 | INCREASES | n/a |
| 2 | DISTRIBUTIONS | Excess = capital gain under В§ 1368(b)(2) |
| 3 | NONDEDUCTIBLES | Excess **PERMANENTLY LOST** (no carryforward) |
| 4 | LOSSES | Excess **SUSPENDED** under В§ 1366(d)(2), carried forward indefinitely |

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1367-1(g) election вҖ” alternative ordering**:

| Step | Item | Excess treatment |
|------|------|------------------|
| 1 | INCREASES | n/a |
| 2 | DISTRIBUTIONS | Excess = capital gain under В§ 1368(b)(2) |
| 3 | LOSSES | Excess **SUSPENDED** under В§ 1366(d)(2) |
| 4 | NONDEDUCTIBLES | Excess **SUSPENDED + carried forward** under В§ 1.1367-1(g) (instead of lost) |

Election is BINDING in subsequent years unless IRS approves change back to standard rule. Election protects nondeductible-expense basis erosion at the cost of consuming more basis up-front for losses. Pinned by `election_changes_loss_allowed_when_basis_constrained` (same input under both rules produces different loss-allowed and nondeductible-applied amounts вҖ” standard order loses $10K nondeductibles permanently while election order saves them via carryforward; election order allows full $10K of losses while standard order allows $0).

**Standard ordering normal flow** вҖ” $100K beginning + $50K income = $150K вҶ’ -$30K dist = $120K вҶ’ -$20K nondeductible = $100K вҶ’ -$40K loss = $60K ending. Pinned by `standard_ordering_normal_flow`.

**Loss suspension under В§ 1366(d)(2)** вҖ” $200K loss against $100K post-step-3 basis вҶ’ $100K allowed + $100K suspended for indefinite carryforward. Pinned by `standard_loss_exceeds_basis_suspended`.

**Nondeductibles lost permanently under standard rule** вҖ” $30K nondeductibles against $20K basis вҶ’ $20K applied + $10K LOST (no carryforward). Pinned by `standard_nondeductibles_exceed_basis_lost_permanently`.

**В§ 1368(b)(2) excess distribution capital gain** вҖ” $50K distribution against $10K basis вҶ’ $10K applied + $40K excess treated as gain from sale of stock (typically capital gain). Pinned by `standard_excess_distribution_capital_gain` and `distribution_above_basis_only_excess_is_gain`.

**В§ 1.1367-1(g) election preserves nondeductibles** вҖ” $30K nondeductibles + $20K basis under election вҶ’ $20K applied + $10K SUSPENDED for future carryforward (vs. LOST under standard). Pinned by `election_nondeductibles_suspend_when_exceed`.

**Multi-step ordering invariants** вҖ” `increases_applied_before_distributions_invariant` (income raises basis before distributions consume it), `distribution_applied_before_losses_invariant` (distributions step 2; losses step 4), `basis_never_goes_below_zero` ($0 floor at every step), and `standard_vs_election_same_ending_when_basis_unconstrained` (when basis covers all decreases, both orderings produce identical ending basis).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** вҖ” every result references В§ 1361 (S-corp definition + eligibility), В§ 1366 (pass-through items + В§ 1366(d) basis-limited loss + В§ 1366(d)(2) carryforward), В§ 1368 (distribution mechanics вҖ” В§ 1368(b)(2) excess-distribution capital gain), В§ 1374 (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion), and Form 7203 (IRS basis-tracking schedule attached to shareholder's Form 1040). Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + Form 7203).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1367`. Twenty-one tests pin: **standard ordering normal flow** ($100K + $50K - $30K - $20K - $40K = $60K); **standard loss exceeds basis suspended** ($200K loss вҶ’ $100K allowed + $100K suspended); **standard nondeductibles exceed basis lost permanently** ($10K lost forever, no carryforward); **standard excess distribution capital gain** ($40K excess capital gain under В§ 1368(b)(2)); **election loss before nondeductible normal flow**; **election nondeductibles suspend when exceed** ($10K SUSPENDED under В§ 1.1367-1(g) instead of lost); **election changes loss allowed when basis constrained** (head-to-head comparison вҖ” standard $0 loss + $10K lost nondeductible; election $10K loss + $10K suspended nondeductible); **basis never goes below zero**; **increases applied before distributions invariant**; **distribution applied before losses invariant**; **distribution within basis no capital gain**; **distribution above basis only excess is gain**; **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1367 + В§ 1367(a)(1) + В§ 1367(a)(2) + В§ 1366(a)(1) + В§ 1366(d) + В§ 1366(d)(2) + В§ 1368 + В§ 1368(b)(2) + В§ 1.1367-1(f) + В§ 1.1367-1(g) + Form 7203); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for В§ 1361 + В§ 1366 + В§ 1368 + В§ 1374 + Form 7203); **defensive negative beginning basis clamped**; **defensive negative income clamped**; **zero inputs zero ending basis**; **standard vs election same ending when basis unconstrained**; **separately stated combines with nonseparately income**; **separately stated loss combines with nonseparately loss**; **depletion deduction combines with nondeductibles for basis decrease**.

`traderview-expense::section_1368` is the **IRC В§ 1368 S-corp distribution module** вҖ” direct sibling to `section_1367` (basis adjustments). В§ 1368 governs SHAREHOLDER-LEVEL tax treatment of cash and property distributions from an S corporation. Result varies sharply depending on whether the S-corp has accumulated earnings and profits (E&P) from a prior C-corp period. Trader-critical for S-corps converted from C-corp status. Form 1120-S Schedule M-2 tracks AAA + E&P year over year.

**В§ 1368(b) вҖ” no accumulated E&P** (S-corp never was C-corp, or E&P fully distributed). Two-step ordering:

| Step | Treatment |
|------|-----------|
| 1 | Tax-free reduction of stock basis (down to $0) |
| 2 | Excess = capital gain under В§ 1368(b)(2) (typically long-term) |

Pinned by `no_e_and_p_distribution_within_basis_all_tax_free`, `no_e_and_p_distribution_exceeds_basis_capital_gain` ($250K dist against $200K basis вҶ’ $200K tax-free + $50K cap gain), and `no_e_and_p_zero_basis_full_capital_gain`.

**В§ 1368(c) вҖ” with accumulated E&P** (default ordering). Four-step ordering:

| Step | Statute | Treatment | Basis effect |
|------|---------|-----------|--------------|
| 1 | В§ 1368(c)(1) | Tax-free up to AAA | Reduces basis under В§ 1367(a)(2)(A) |
| 2 | В§ 1368(c)(2) | Ordinary dividend up to E&P | NO basis reduction (taxed under В§ 301) |
| 3 | В§ 1368(c)(3) | Tax-free up to remaining basis | Reduces basis |
| 4 | В§ 1368(b)(2) | Capital gain on stock sale | n/a |

Pinned by `with_e_and_p_distribution_within_aaa_all_tax_free`, `with_e_and_p_aaa_exhausted_then_dividend` ($150K dist with $50K AAA + $80K E&P + $200K basis вҶ’ $50K AAA + $80K dividend + $20K basis-tax-free), `with_e_and_p_exhausts_all_four_paths_capital_gain` ($1M dist with $50K AAA + $80K E&P + $100K basis вҶ’ $50K + $80K + $50K + $820K capital gain), `dividend_never_reduces_basis_invariant` (E&P dividend leaves basis untouched), `boundary_distribution_exactly_aaa_no_dividend`, and `boundary_distribution_one_cent_above_aaa` ($1 to E&P dividend).

**В§ 1368(e)(3) election вҖ” E&P first.** With unanimous consent of all shareholders to whom a distribution is made during the tax year, the S-corp may elect to REVERSE steps (c)(1) and (c)(2) вҖ” distribute E&P first as dividends, then AAA tax-free, then basis tax-free, then capital gain. Useful for purging E&P to avoid the В§ 1375 passive investment income tax or future В§ 1374 BIG tax exposure. Pinned by `election_e_and_p_first_reorders` ($100K dist with $30K AAA + $40K E&P вҶ’ $40K dividend then $30K AAA then $30K basis under election; default would do $30K AAA then $40K dividend then $30K basis), and `election_ignored_when_no_e_and_p` (election has no effect when E&P = 0; falls through to В§ 1368(b)).

**В§ 1368(e)(1)(C) net-negative-adjustment rule.** If for any taxable year the net negative adjustment exceeds the net positive adjustment, the AAA for purposes of В§ 1368(c)(1) is determined WITHOUT REGARD to such net negative adjustment вҖ” current-year-cushion for losses. AAA-available-for-distribution = beginning AAA + net positive (ignoring net negative when it exceeds positive). End-of-year AAA still reflects everything. Pinned by `net_negative_adjustment_ignored_for_distribution` ($100K beginning + $30K positive + $80K negative вҶ’ AAA-available = $130K, all distribution from AAA; end-of-year AAA = -$30K after both adjustments + distribution), and `net_positive_exceeds_net_negative_both_apply` (when positive вүҘ negative, both apply: $100K + $80K - $30K = $150K AAA-available).

**Negative AAA floored at $0 for distribution.** AAA may be negative due to prior-year losses, but distribution amount is limited to non-negative AAA. Pinned by `negative_aaa_floored_at_zero_for_distribution` (AAA -$100K with $80K E&P вҶ’ 0 AAA used, $50K all from E&P dividend).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `dividend_only_when_e_and_p_present_invariant` (3Г—3 sweep вҖ” no dividend when E&P=0); `distribution_sum_equals_input_invariant` (3-cell sweep вҖ” total of all 4 categories equals distribution); `election_vs_default_when_e_and_p_present_different_ordering` (head-to-head election vs default for same input).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** вҖ” every result references В§ 1361 (definition + eligibility), В§ 1366 (pass-through items + В§ 1366(d) basis-limited loss), В§ 1367 (basis adjustments вҖ” distributions reduce basis under В§ 1367(a)(2)(A)), В§ 1374 (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion), В§ 1375 (passive investment income tax вҖ” engages when S-corp has E&P AND >25% passive income), and Form 1120-S Schedule M-2. Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1368`. Twenty-three tests pin: **no E&P distribution within basis all tax-free**; **no E&P distribution exceeds basis capital gain** ($250K dist / $200K basis вҶ’ $50K cap gain); **no E&P zero basis full capital gain**; **with E&P distribution within AAA all tax-free**; **with E&P AAA exhausted then dividend** ($50K AAA + $80K dividend + $20K basis = $150K); **with E&P exhausts all four paths capital gain** ($50K + $80K + $50K + $820K = $1M); **election E&P first reorders** ($40K dividend вҶ’ $30K AAA вҶ’ $30K basis under В§ 1368(e)(3)); **election ignored when no E&P** (falls through to В§ 1368(b)); **net negative adjustment ignored for distribution** (В§ 1368(e)(1)(C)); **net positive exceeds net negative both apply** ($100K + $80K - $30K = $150K AAA); **negative AAA floored at zero for distribution**; **zero distribution no tax effects**; **dividend only when E&P present invariant** (3Г—3 sweep); **dividend never reduces basis invariant** ($5M dividend, basis unchanged); **distribution sum equals input invariant** (3-cell sweep); **election vs default when E&P present different ordering**; **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1368 + В§ 1368(a) + В§ 1368(b)/(b)(1)/(b)(2) + В§ 1368(c)/(c)(1)/(c)(2)/(c)(3) + В§ 1368(e)(1)(A)/(e)(1)(C)/(e)(3) + В§ 1.1368-1 + В§ 1.1368-2 + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for В§ 1361 + В§ 1366 + В§ 1367 + В§ 1374 + В§ 1375 + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2); **defensive negative distribution clamped**; **defensive negative basis clamped**; **defensive negative E&P clamped**; **boundary distribution exactly AAA no dividend**; **boundary distribution one cent above AAA** ($1 to E&P dividend).

`traderview-expense::section_1377` is the **IRC В§ 1377 вҖ” Definitions and special rule module** вҖ” completes the S-corporation cluster after В§ 1361 (definition), В§ 1366 (pass-thru вҖ” iter 488), В§ 1367 (basis adjustments), В§ 1368 (distributions), В§ 1374 (built-in gains), В§ 1375 (passive investment income). Direct trader-business-owner companion to `section_1361`, `section_1366` (iter 488), `section_1367`, `section_1368`, `section_1374`, `section_1375`, `section_1042` (ESOP rollover вҖ” iter 480), `section_4978` (ESOP recapture вҖ” iter 484), `section_6166` (estate tax installment вҖ” iter 486). В§ 1377 current framework from Subchapter S Revision Act of 1982, Pub. L. 97-354.
