**В§ 1377(a)(1) Pro rata share general rule** вҖ” DAILY ASSIGNMENT method: each shareholder's pro rata share of any S corporation item is determined by assigning an equal portion of the item to **EACH DAY** of the S corporation's taxable year, then dividing that portion **PRO RATA among shares outstanding** on that day. Example: 365-day year + $365K income + 100 shares вҶ’ each share gets $10/day Г— 365 = $3,650 annual share.

**Special-day rules per 26 C.F.R. В§ 1.1377-1(a)(2)**: shareholder who DISPOSES of stock is treated as the shareholder for the **DAY OF DISPOSITION**; shareholder who DIES is treated as the shareholder for the **DAY OF DEATH**.

**В§ 1377(a)(2) Terminating election** ("closing of the books") вҖ” if a shareholder's interest in an S corporation is **TERMINATED** during the taxable year AND **ALL AFFECTED SHAREHOLDERS** agree, the corporation may elect to apply В§ 1377(a)(1) AS IF the taxable year consisted of **TWO TAXABLE YEARS**, the first of which ends on the date of termination. This avoids the daily-assignment method's averaging effect when actual operating results swing dramatically before and after termination.

**В§ 1377(a)(2) eligibility** requires:
1. Shareholder's ENTIRE interest must terminate during the taxable year (not partial sale)
2. ALL AFFECTED SHAREHOLDERS must consent (terminating shareholder + acquiring shareholder per 26 C.F.R. В§ 1.1377-1(b)(2))
3. Election filed by attaching statement to **TIMELY Form 1120-S** (including extensions)

**Two qualifying situations**:
- **В§ 1377(a)(2)(A)** вҖ” Shareholder's stock FULLY DISPOSED OF (sale, exchange, gift, or other transfer)
- **В§ 1377(a)(2)(B)** вҖ” Shareholder's stock REDEEMED in a transaction qualifying under В§ 302 (substantially disproportionate redemption) or В§ 303 (estate-tax stock redemption)

**В§ 1377(b) Post-Termination Transition Period (PTTP)** definitions:
1. **В§ 1377(b)(1)(A)** вҖ” 1-YEAR PERIOD beginning on the date the corporation ceases to be an S corporation
2. **В§ 1377(b)(1)(B)** вҖ” 120-DAY PERIOD beginning on the date of a "determination" that the corporation's S election terminated for a prior year
3. **В§ 1377(b)(1)(C)** вҖ” 120-DAY PERIOD beginning on the date of a determination that the corporation had insufficient adjusted earnings and profits

**В§ 1377(b)(2) Distribution during PTTP** вҖ” cash distributions during PTTP are treated as reducing the corporation's accumulated adjustments account (AAA) under В§ 1368(c) **FIRST**, then E&P (with dividend tax treatment). This allows shareholders to withdraw remaining AAA **TAX-FREE** during PTTP after S election termination.

**В§ 1377(b)(3) Determination definitions**:
- **В§ 1377(b)(3)(A)** вҖ” A determination as defined in В§ 1313(a)(1) (final IRS or court determination)
- **В§ 1377(b)(3)(B)** вҖ” A determination by the Secretary that the corporation's В§ 1362 election has terminated
- **В§ 1377(b)(3)(C)** вҖ” An agreement between the corporation and the Secretary that the corporation's S election has terminated

**Trader-business-owner critical fact patterns**: (1) В§ 1377(a)(1) daily-assignment method works fine in stable years but creates DISTORTION when annual results swing вҖ” e.g., trader-CEO sells stock to partner mid-year with H1 $5M loss and H2 $5M gain; without В§ 1377(a)(2) election, **BOTH parties get pro rata share of $0 net annual income**; (2) В§ 1377(a)(2) election allows economic results to be matched with actual ownership periods; (3) **PTTP under В§ 1377(b) is the critical window** during which former S shareholders can extract AAA TAX-FREE before C-corp dividend treatment kicks in; (4) В§ 1377(b) PTTP coordination with В§ 1366(d)(3) suspended-loss carryover is key estate planning opportunity when S election terminates due to shareholder death; (5) 50% trader sells stock mid-year (day 183 of 365) вҖ” daily method assigns ~$250,684.93 of $1M corp income to selling shareholder (vs $0-$1M swing under terminating election depending on H1/H2 timing).

**Distinction from В§ 706** (partnership): partnerships use varying interest rules under В§ 706(d) that allow **interim closing or proration**; S corporations are restricted to В§ 1377(a)(1) daily method UNLESS В§ 1377(a)(2) election made on entire-interest termination.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1377`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **daily method full year 25% shareholder** ($91,250); **daily method half year 25% shareholder** ($45,750); **daily method 50% full year** ($182,500); **terminating election full disposition valid**; **terminating election partial disposition invalid**; **terminating election no consent invalid**; **terminating election no Form 1120-S invalid**; **terminating election В§ 302 redemption valid**; **terminating election В§ 303 redemption valid**; **terminating election death valid**; **PTTP distribution against AAA** ($100K ample AAA вҶ’ tax-free); **PTTP distribution partial against AAA then E&P** ($30K AAA + $70K dividend); **PTTP 120-day determination period**; **PTTP 120-day E&P determination period**; **no PTTP no distribution daily method**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 1377(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(2)(A)/(a)(2)(B)/(b)/(b)(1)(A)/(b)(1)(B)/(b)(1)(C)/(b)(2)/(b)(3) + В§ 1313(a)(1) + В§ 1362 + В§ 1368(c) + В§ 302 + В§ 303 + В§ 706(d) + 26 C.F.R. В§ 1.1377-1); **note pins daily assignment method** (DAY OF DISPOSITION + DAY OF DEATH); **note pins terminating election requirements** (closing of the books + ENTIRE INTEREST + ALL AFFECTED SHAREHOLDERS + TWO TAXABLE YEARS + Form 1120-S); **note pins two qualifying situations**; **note pins three PTTP periods** (1-year + 120-day); **note pins PTTP AAA distribution tax-free**; **note pins В§ 706(d) partnership distinction** (interim closing or proration); **note pins companion modules**; **5-cell severity truth table** (daily / terminating valid / invalid / PTTP AAA / PTTP E&P); **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **zero days zero share**; **zero corp income zero share**; **realistic mid-year shareholder swap** ($250,684.93 daily method on $1M corp income with 50% Г— 183/365).

`traderview-expense::section_1375` is the **IRC В§ 1375 S-corp passive investment income tax module** вҖ” completes the S-corp cluster after В§ 1361 (definition), В§ 1367 (basis adjustments, iter 246), В§ 1368 (distributions, iter 248), and В§ 1374 (built-in gains tax). В§ 1375 is the passive-investment-income tax вҖ” applies ONLY to S-corps with accumulated E&P from a prior C-corp period AND passive investment income exceeding 25% of gross receipts. Companion to В§ 1362(d)(3) three-year S-election termination rule with identical engagement triggers.

**В§ 1375(a) engagement вҖ” TWO conditions, both required**:

| Condition | Authority | Trigger |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| (1) | В§ 1375(a)(1) | S-corp has accumulated E&P at close of taxable year (carried from prior C-corp period) |
| (2) | В§ 1375(a)(2) | Gross receipts more than 25% of which are passive investment income (В§ 1362(d)(3)(C) вҖ” rents, royalties, dividends, interest, annuities) |

S-corps that never were C-corps have zero accumulated E&P and are NEVER subject to В§ 1375 regardless of passive income mix. Pinned by `no_e_and_p_no_tax_engagement` and `engagement_requires_both_triggers_truth_table` (4-cell truth table вҖ” only the {E&P=true, PII>25%=true} cell engages).

**В§ 1375(b)(1)(B) Excess Net Passive Income (ENPI) formula**:

```
ENPI = NPI Г— (PII вҲ’ 25% Г— GR) / PII
```

Where: PII = Passive Investment Income; GR = Gross Receipts; NPI = Net Passive Income (PII less directly connected deductions). Pinned by `enpi_baseline_math` ($1M GR + $500K PII + $400K NPI вҶ’ ENPI = $400K Г— $250K / $500K = $200K вҶ’ tax = $200K Г— 21% = $42K), `enpi_full_passive_income_when_pii_is_all_of_gross_receipts` ($800K NPI Г— $750K / $1M = $600K), and `enpi_formula_high_pii_low_npi` ($100K NPI Г— $550K / $800K = $68.75K).

**В§ 1375(b)(1)(A) ENPI cap**: ENPI cannot exceed corporation's taxable income for the year. Preserves S-corp ability to survive even when entire passive income is "excess". Pinned by `enpi_capped_at_taxable_income` (TI cap below raw ENPI clamps tax accordingly) and `taxable_income_zero_caps_tax_at_zero` (zero taxable income вҶ’ zero tax even when engaged).

**25% boundary precision** вҖ” the threshold uses exact-integer comparison `4 Г— PII > GR` rather than basis-point arithmetic, so the boundary is detected at single-cent precision. Pinned by `pii_at_25_percent_exactly_no_engagement` (exact 25% вҖ” not exceeded), `pii_just_above_25_percent_engages` (one cent above вҖ” exceeded), and `pii_below_25_percent_no_engagement`. Bp arithmetic is retained as display-only for the result struct's `pii_percentage_of_gross_receipts_bps`.

**В§ 1362(d)(3) three-year S-election termination вҖ” companion provision**. Same engagement triggers as В§ 1375. Three consecutive years of E&P + >25% PII terminates the S election effective close of third taxable year; corporation reverts to C-corp under В§ 1362(d). В§ 1362(g) imposes 5-year waiting period before re-election unless IRS consents. Module reports warning levels at year 1 (first warning) and year 2 (elevated warning вҖ” one more year terminates), with full termination flag at year 3. Pinned by `consecutive_year_1_warning_level_1`, `consecutive_year_2_warning_level_2`, `consecutive_year_3_election_terminated`, and `consecutive_years_only_count_when_engagement_present` (no E&P вҶ’ years counter irrelevant).

**Tax rate parameterization** вҖ” default `corporate_tax_rate_bps = 2100` (21% post-TCJA highest В§ 11(b) rate). Module accepts arbitrary rate for pre-TCJA or hypothetical analysis. Pinned by `pre_tcja_35_percent_rate` ($200K ENPI Г— 35% = $70K) and `zero_rate_no_tax` (rate = 0 вҶ’ tax = 0 even when engaged).

**Multi-regime invariants** вҖ” `engagement_requires_both_triggers_truth_table` (4-cell sweep вҖ” only E&P+PII>25% engages); `pii_percentage_calculation_bps_invariant` (display-bps math reports 25%/30% correctly); `termination_threshold_constant_invariant` (2500 bps / 2100 bps / 3 years constants).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** вҖ” every result references the full 5-module cluster: В§ 1361 (definition + eligibility), В§ 1366 (pass-through), В§ 1367 (basis), В§ 1368 (distributions вҖ” including В§ 1368(e)(3) election to distribute E&P first, which can purge accumulated E&P and avoid В§ 1375 + В§ 1362(d)(3) exposure), В§ 1374 (built-in gains tax вҖ” separate tax on C-to-S conversion gains, often coincides with В§ 1375 exposure for newly converted S-corps), and В§ 1362(d)(3) termination. Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + В§ 1368(e)(3) election + В§ 1362(d)(3) termination).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1375`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no E&P no tax engagement**; **PII at 25% exactly no engagement** (boundary); **PII just above 25% engages** (one-cent precision via exact-integer comparison); **PII below 25% no engagement**; **ENPI baseline math** ($200K ENPI Г— 21% = $42K tax); **ENPI capped at taxable income**; **ENPI zero when no engagement**; **ENPI full passive income when PII is all of gross receipts** ($600K ENPI); **consecutive year 1 warning level 1**; **consecutive year 2 warning level 2**; **consecutive year 3 election terminated** (В§ 1362(d)(3) violation); **consecutive years only count when engagement present** (no E&P вҶ’ no termination risk); **pre-TCJA 35% rate** ($70K tax); **zero rate no tax** (engaged but tax = 0); **engagement requires both triggers truth table** (4-cell sweep); **PII percentage calculation BPS invariant** (display math); **termination threshold constant invariant** (2500 bps / 2100 bps / 3 years); **citation pins all subsections** (В§ 1375 + В§ 1375(a)/(b)/(b)(1)(A)/(b)(1)(B)/(d) + В§ 11(b) + В§ 1362(d)(3)/(d)(3)(B)/(d)(3)(C) + В§ 1.1375-1 + В§ 1362(g)); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + В§ 1368(e)(3) + В§ 1362(d)(3)); **defensive negative inputs clamped**; **defensive zero gross receipts no overflow** (avoids division by zero); **defensive rate above 100% clamped**; **ENPI formula high PII low NPI** ($68.75K); **taxable income zero caps tax at zero**.

`traderview-expense::section_1239` is the **IRC В§ 1239 Gain from Sale of Depreciable Property Between Certain Related Taxpayers вҖ” Ordinary Income Recharacterization module** (iter 610) вҖ” trader-critical anti-arbitrage rule. Direct trader companion to `section_1231` (capital/ordinary gain framework вҖ” В§ 1239 overrides), `section_1245_1250` (depreciation recapture вҖ” В§ 1239 reaches further), `section_267` (related-party loss-deferral and basis rules вҖ” В§ 1239(c)(2) borrows В§ 267(c) constructive ownership), `section_704c` (partnership built-in gain allocations on contribution), `section_1031` (like-kind exchange вҖ” alternative to taxable В§ 1239 sale). When a taxpayer sells or exchanges property to a **RELATED PERSON**, and the property is **IN THE HANDS OF THE TRANSFEREE** of a character subject to the depreciation allowance of В§ 167, **ALL** gain recognized by the transferor is treated as **ORDINARY INCOME** (not capital gain, not В§ 1231 gain). Statute exists to defeat the tax arbitrage of repeatedly selling depreciable property between related parties to step up basis and re-depreciate while paying only capital-gains rates on the inter-party transfer ([Cornell LII В§ 1239](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1239), [NCBarBlog on related-party recharacterization](https://www.ncbarblog.com/tax-recharacterizing-gain-on-the-sale-of-depreciable-property-between-related-parties/)). **В§ 1239(a) GENERAL RULE**: any gain recognized to the transferor on direct OR indirect sale/exchange between related persons treated as ordinary income if property depreciable under В§ 167 in hands of transferee. **В§ 1239(b) RELATED PERSONS**: (1) a person and all entities which are CONTROLLED ENTITIES with respect to such person; (2) a taxpayer and any TRUST in which such taxpayer or his spouse is a BENEFICIARY, unless the beneficiary's interest is a REMOTE CONTINGENT INTEREST within В§ 318(a)(3)(B)(i); (3) except in the case of a sale or exchange in satisfaction of a pecuniary bequest, an EXECUTOR of an estate and a BENEFICIARY of such estate. **В§ 1239(c)(1) CONTROLLED ENTITY**: (A) a corporation more than 50 percent of the **value of the outstanding stock** of which is owned directly or indirectly; (B) a partnership more than 50 percent of the **capital interest or profits interest** of which is owned directly or indirectly; (C) any entity related under В§ 267(b)(3)/(10)/(11)/(12). **В§ 1239(c)(2) CONSTRUCTIVE OWNERSHIP**: В§ 267(c) constructive ownership rules apply for determining > 50 % thresholds (family members, partner-of-partner, etc.). **В§ 1239(d) EMPLOYER-PENSION PLAN**: an employee benefit plan covering an owner-employee is treated as a related person to the employer. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1239-1** (sales after October 4, 1976); **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1239-2** (sales on or before October 4, 1976). **Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoGainRecognized, NotApplicableUnrelatedParties, NotApplicablePropertyNotDepreciableToTransferee, CompliantGainRecharacterizedAsOrdinaryUnderSection1239, CompliantPropertyAlreadyOrdinaryUnderSection1245Or1250, CompliantTrustBeneficiaryRemoteContingentNotRelated, CompliantPecuniaryBequestSatisfactionNotRelated, ViolationGainReportedAsCapitalDespiteSection1239, ViolationConstructiveOwnershipNotAppliedToSection267C. **Nine related-person categories**: NotRelated, ControlledCorporationOver50PctByValue, ControlledPartnershipOver50PctCapitalOrProfits, TrustBeneficiaryNonRemoteContingent, TrustBeneficiaryRemoteContingent, ExecutorBeneficiaryNonPecuniaryBequest, ExecutorBeneficiaryPecuniaryBequestSatisfaction, Section267BRelatedEntity, EmployerControlledPensionPlan. **Three property-depreciability states**: DepreciableUnderSection167InTransfereeHands, NotDepreciableInTransfereeHands, AmortizableUnderSection197InTransfereeHands. **Six gain-character types**: CapitalLongTerm, CapitalShortTerm, Section1231Gain, Section1245OrdinaryRecapture, Section1250OrdinaryRecapture, OrdinaryOther. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1239`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **no gain recognized not applicable**, **controlled corp capital gain violation recharacterized** (LTCG вҶ’ ordinary), **controlled partnership В§ 1231 misreport violation** (В§ 1231 вҶ’ ordinary), **controlled corp at exactly 50 % not related** (strict > 50 % reading), **controlled corp 50 %+1bp related** (boundary), **constructive ownership not tested violation** (В§ 1239(c)(2) procedural defect), **property not depreciable to transferee not applicable** (e.g., land in transferee's hands), **property amortizable В§ 197 not applicable** (В§ 197 is amortization, not В§ 167 depreciation), **unrelated parties not applicable**, **trust beneficiary non-remote related violation**, **trust beneficiary remote contingent NOT related** (В§ 1239(b)(2) carve-out), **executor non-pecuniary beneficiary related violation**, **executor pecuniary bequest satisfaction NOT related** (В§ 1239(b)(3) carve-out), **employer controlled pension plan related violation**, **В§ 1245 ordinary recapture already ordinary compliant** (no double recharacterization), **В§ 1250 ordinary recapture already ordinary compliant**, **ordinary-other compliant no recharacterization needed**, **capital short-term misreport violation**, **citations pin В§ 1239 subsections** ((a) + (b)(1) + (b)(2) + (b)(3) + (c)(1)(A) + (c)(1)(B) + (c)(1)(C) + (c)(2) + (d) + В§ 1.1239-1 + В§ 1.1239-2), **constant pin 50 % threshold + 1976 Treas Reg effective date** (5000 basis points + 1976-10-04), **saturating overflow defense extreme gain** (u64::MAX). Trader-landlord critical for: rental real estate sales to family members, controlled S-corp, controlled LLC, family LP; depreciable equipment sales to trading-shop entities; building sales to controlled REIT; sales to grantor trust where trader or spouse is beneficiary; estate-administration depreciable property distributions to non-pecuniary-bequest beneficiaries. Sibling cluster: `section_1231` (В§ 1231 gain framework overridden by В§ 1239), `section_1245_1250` (recapture interaction), `section_267` (basis source for constructive ownership rules), `section_704c` (partnership contribution alternative), `section_752` (partnership liability allocation).

`traderview-expense::section_6041` is the **IRC В§ 6041 + В§ 6041A Information Reporting (Form 1099-NEC / 1099-MISC) + В§ 3406 Backup Withholding module** (iter 620) вҖ” bread-and-butter for any trader running a trading shop, LLC, or partnership that pays non-employee compensation. Direct trader-companion to `section_6042` (Form 1099-DIV dividend reporting), `section_6045` (broker reporting), `section_6045a` (broker basis reporting), `section_6049` (Form 1099-INT interest reporting), `section_6050i` (cash payment reporting), `section_6050w` (Form 1099-K third-party payment processors), `section_1471` (FATCA Chapter 4 foreign payee reporting cross-reference), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension), `section_162a` (trade or business deduction interaction). **В§ 6041(a) GENERAL RULE**: persons engaged in a trade or business making payments in the course of such trade or business of rent, salaries, wages, premiums, annuities, compensations, remunerations, emoluments, or other fixed or determinable income aggregating **$2,000 or more** (post-OBBBA; previously $600) in any taxable year must file an information return ([Cornell LII В§ 6041](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/6041), [IRS Publication 1099 (2026) General Instructions](https://www.irs.gov/publications/p1099)). **P.L. 119-21 OBBBA В§ 70433 (signed July 4, 2025)**: raised В§ 6041(a) and В§ 6041A(a)(2) threshold from **$600 to $2,000** for payments made **after December 31, 2025**. First 1099s under new rules cover the **2026 tax year** (filed in early 2027). **Inflation indexing** from 2027 with **2025 as base year**, rounded to nearest **$100** ([Federal Register 2026-07519 Increase in Threshold for Information Reporting](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/04/17/2026-07519/increase-in-threshold-for-requiring-information-reporting-with-respect-to-certain-payees-extension-and-modification-of-limitation-on-wagering-losses), [WhippleWood вҖ” $2,000 1099 Threshold for 2026](https://whipplewood.com/insights/new-2000-1099-threshold-2026/), [Avalara вҖ” OBBBA changes 1099 thresholds](https://www.avalara.com/blog/en/north-america/2025/07/one-big-beautiful-bill-act-1099-reporting-threshold.html)). **В§ 6041(c) CORPORATE EXCEPTION**: most payments to corporations are EXEMPT from В§ 6041 reporting; **EXCEPTIONS include attorney fees and corporate medical/health payments**, which must be reported regardless of corporate status of payee. **В§ 6041(h) TIN REQUIREMENT**: payee must furnish taxpayer identification number on Form W-9; failure triggers **В§ 3406 backup withholding at 24 percent**. **В§ 6041A**: parallel reporting regime for direct sales of $5,000+ + nonemployee services subject to same OBBBA $2,000 threshold post-2025. **В§ 3406**: 24 % backup withholding on reportable payments when payee fails to provide TIN or IRS notifies payer of TIN mismatch. **В§ 6721**: failure-to-file penalty per return varies by lateness ($60 вүӨ 30 days late, $130 вүӨ Aug 1, $330 after Aug 1; **intentional disregard $660+**). **В§ 6722**: failure-to-furnish-payee-statement penalty parallel structure. **В§ 6723**: $50 per other reporting failure. **Forms**: **Form 1099-NEC** (Non-Employee Compensation вҖ” services rendered by independent contractors; due **January 31** of year following payment); **Form 1099-MISC** (Miscellaneous Income вҖ” rents, prizes, awards; due February 28 paper / March 31 electronic); **Form 1042 + Form 1042-S** (foreign person reporting under FATCA Chapter 4 + Chapter 3); **Form W-9** (US person TIN certification); **Form W-8BEN / W-8BEN-E** (foreign person beneficial owner certification). **Fourteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNotTradeOrBusiness, NotApplicableBelowReportingThreshold, NotApplicableCorporationGeneralExemption, NotApplicableForeignPayeeWithW8DocumentationFatcaApplies, CompliantForm1099NecFiledTimelyJanuary31, CompliantForm1099MiscFiledTimely, CompliantForm1042SFiledForForeignPayeeNoDocumentation, CompliantBackupWithholdingAppliedNoW9, CompliantAttorneyOrMedicalPaymentReportedDespiteCorpStatus, ViolationFailedToFileForm1099Section6721, ViolationFailedToFurnishPayeeStatementSection6722, ViolationFailedToObtainW9OrApplyBackupWithholding, ViolationLateFiledMoreThan30DaysAfterDueDate, ViolationLawyerPaymentExceptionMisappliedAsCorpExempt. **Eight payee classifications**: IndividualSoleProprietorContractor, CorporationGeneralExempt, CorporationAttorneyLawFirm, CorporationMedicalHealthcareProvider, PartnershipOrLlcWithMultipleMembers, ForeignPersonW8Provided, ForeignPersonNoDocumentation, UsTrustOrEstate. **Seven payment types**: NonemployeeCompensationServices, RentMisc1099, AttorneyFeesLegal, PrizesAwards, MedicalHealthcarePayments, DirectSalesOf5000OrMore, OtherTradeOrBusinessPayment. **Two OBBBA-status states**: PaymentMadeBeforeJanuary1_2026OldThreshold ($600), PaymentMadeOnOrAfterJanuary1_2026NewThreshold ($2,000). Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6041`. **Twenty-two tests pin**: **personal consumer not applicable** (not trade or business), **pre-2026 payment uses $600 threshold**, **post-2025 payment uses $2,000 threshold** (OBBBA), **post-2025 payment at $1,999 below threshold**, **post-2025 payment at exactly $2,000 satisfies threshold** (boundary), **corporate general exemption applies**, **attorney payment to corp still reportable compliant** (В§ 6041(c) exception), **corp general exempt misapplied for attorney payment violation**, **medical payment to corp still reportable compliant**, **no W-9 with backup withholding compliant** ($5K Г— 24 % = $1,200), **no W-9 no backup withholding violation**, **no W-9 insufficient backup withholding violation** ($500 < required $1,200), **Form 1099 late within 30 days violation**, **Form 1099 late more than 30 days violation** (higher penalty tier), **payee statement not furnished violation**, **foreign W-8 with 1042-S furnished not applicable**, **foreign undocumented with 1042-S compliant**, **foreign undocumented no 1042-S violation**, **rent payment type uses 1099-MISC**, **citations pin В§ 6041 subsections + OBBBA** ((a) + (c) + (h) + В§ 6041A + В§ 3406 + В§ 6721 + В§ 6722 + В§ 6723 + P.L. 119-21 + В§ 70433 + 1099-NEC + 1099-MISC + W-9 + W-8BEN + 1042), **constant pin OBBBA thresholds + effective date** ($2,000 post-2025 + $600 pre + 2025-12-31 effective + 2025 base + $100 rounding + 2400 basis points backup withholding + P.L. 119-21 В§ 70433), **saturating overflow defense extreme payment** (u64::MAX). `traderview-expense::section_651` is the **IRC В§ 651 Simple Trust Distribution Deduction module** (iter 630) вҖ” natural simple-trust sibling to `section_661` (iter 628 complex trust). Direct trader-companion to `section_661`, `section_641` (iter 626 fiduciary tax imposition), `section_643` (iter 624 DNI ceiling), `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” distribution shifts NII to beneficiary at lower brackets), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension at compressed trust brackets), `section_642` (В§ 642(c) charitable interaction вҖ” any В§ 642(c) provision converts simple to complex). Simple trust = trust whose terms (1) require all income to be distributed currently, (2) do NOT permit В§ 642(c) charitable distributions, and (3) which did NOT distribute corpus during the taxable year. **Any failure converts the trust to COMPLEX** under В§ 661. Trader-critical for family-office **QPRT** (Qualified Personal Residence Trust) remainder phase, **GRAT** remainder, **CLAT** post-charitable phase, and traditional income-only family trusts ([Cornell LII В§ 651](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/651), [eCFR 26 CFR В§ 1.651(a)-1](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.651(a)-1)). **В§ 651(a) GENERAL RULE**: simple trust deduction = amount of income required to be distributed currently. **В§ 651(b) LIMITATION**: deduction capped at DNI; tax-exempt interest portion of DNI excluded for purposes of this limitation. **В§ 652(a) BENEFICIARY INCLUSION**: amount of income required to be distributed by simple trust included in beneficiary's gross income **WHETHER OR NOT ACTUALLY DISTRIBUTED**. If required-distribution amount exceeds DNI, **RATABLE ALLOCATION** to beneficiaries per second sentence: each beneficiary includes (income required to that beneficiary / total income required) Г— DNI. **В§ 652(b) CHARACTER**: per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.652(b)-2, distribution consists of same proportion of each DNI class as that class bears to total DNI. **В§ 652(c) DIFFERENT TAXABLE YEAR**: beneficiary includes amounts on basis of trust's taxable year **ending in** beneficiary's taxable year. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.651(a)-1** + **В§ 1.651(a)-2** (income required to be distributed currently) + **В§ 1.651(b)-1** + **В§ 1.652(b)-2** implementing regulations. **Thirteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoIncomeRequiredToBeDistributed, NotApplicableTrustIsComplexNotSimpleUseSection661, NotApplicableGrantorTrustPassThrough, CompliantIncomeFullyDistributedAndIncludedByBeneficiary, CompliantDeductionCappedAtDniRatableInclusionToBeneficiaries, CompliantCharacterProportionatelyAllocatedSection652b, CompliantBeneficiaryDifferentTaxableYearSection652c, ViolationDeductionExceededDniCeiling, ViolationCorpusDistributionMadeTrustNotSimple, ViolationCharitableSection642cProvisionMakesTrustComplex, ViolationBeneficiaryDidNotIncludeIncomeRequiredToBeDistributed, ViolationBeneficiaryUsedIncorrectTaxableYearSection652c, ViolationCharacterNotProportionatelyAllocated. **Four trust types**: SimpleTrustQualifyingUnderSection651, ComplexTrustUseSection661, GrantorTrustPassThrough, EstateProbateUseSection661. **Four simple-trust requirements failure categories**: AllRequirementsSatisfied, CorpusDistributionMadeInYear, Section642cCharitableProvisionInInstrument, IncomeNotRequiredToBeDistributedCurrently. **Three beneficiary inclusion statuses**: BeneficiaryIncludedFullAmount, BeneficiaryIncludedRatablePortionAfterDniLimitation, BeneficiaryDidNotInclude. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-651`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **complex trust redirects to В§ 661**, **estate probate uses В§ 661**, **grantor trust passthrough not applicable**, **no income required not applicable**, **simple trust baseline compliant** ($60K within $100K DNI), **corpus distribution makes trust complex violation**, **charitable В§ 642(c) provision makes trust complex** (auto-converts to В§ 661), **income not required to be distributed currently violation**, **deduction exceeds DNI violation capped** (claimed $150K but DNI ceiling $100K), **income required exceeds DNI capped at DNI compliant** ($150K income req вҶ’ $100K capped), **beneficiary did not include violation** (В§ 652(a) "whether or not distributed"), **beneficiary includes whether or not actually distributed compliant**, **beneficiary incorrect taxable year violation** (В§ 652(c)), **beneficiary different taxable year correctly applied compliant**, **character not proportionately allocated violation** (В§ 652(b)), **ratable inclusion when income exceeds DNI per В§ 652(a) second sentence** ($200K income req on $80K DNI вҶ’ $80K capped), **deduction at exactly DNI compliant** (boundary), **citations pin В§ 651 + В§ 652 and Treas Regs** ((a) + (b) + В§ 652(a)/(b)/(c) + В§ 1.651(a)-1/(a)-2/(b)-1 + В§ 1.652(b)-2 + В§ 643(a) + Form 1041 Schedule B + Schedule K-1), **constant pin three requirements** (3), **saturating overflow defense extreme income** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for: trader-owned simple trusts mandatory-distributing investment income; QPRT remainder phase; GRAT/GRUT remainder; CLAT post-charitable phase; family-office income-only trust setups; pooled income fund administration; charitable remainder annuity trust (CRAT) interactions. Sibling cluster: `section_661` (complex trust distribution deduction вҖ” iter 628; auto-redirect target if simple-trust requirements fail), `section_641` (fiduciary tax imposition вҖ” iter 626), `section_643` (DNI ceiling вҖ” iter 624), `section_642` (В§ 642(c) charitable conversion trigger), `section_652` (beneficiary inclusion mirror вҖ” embedded in В§ 651 module).

`traderview-expense::section_661` is the **IRC В§ 661 Complex Trust Distribution Deduction module** (iter 628) вҖ” natural follow-up to `section_641` (iter 626 fiduciary tax imposition) and `section_643` (iter 624 DNI ceiling). Direct trader-companion to `section_641`, `section_643`, `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” distribution deduction shifts NII to beneficiary), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension вҖ” distribution moves income out of compressed brackets), `section_642` (В§ 642(c) charitable interaction), `section_651` (simple trust distribution deduction sibling), `section_678` (grantor trust attribution). **Trader-critical** because the distribution deduction shifts taxable income from the compressed-bracket trust (37 % at ~$15,650 for 2025) to the beneficiary at potentially much lower individual brackets вҖ” the single highest-impact tool in non-grantor trust planning. **В§ 661 + В§ 662 together form the conduit principle**: trust receives a deduction; beneficiary includes corresponding income with character preservation; result is no double tax + rate arbitrage. **В§ 661(a) GENERAL RULE**: complex trust or estate allowed a deduction equal to the sum of (1) **TIER-1 mandatory** вҖ” income required to be distributed currently and (2) **TIER-2 discretionary** вҖ” other amounts properly paid, credited, or required to be distributed. Total deduction CAPPED at DNI under В§ 661(c) + В§ 643(a) ([Cornell LII В§ 661](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/661), [Miami Law Heckerling вҖ” Demystifying DNI](https://media.law.miami.edu/heckerling/2021/Supplemental%20Materials/Doyle%20-%20Fundamentals%20Program%20Demystifying%20Distributable%20Net%20Income.pdf)). **В§ 661(b) CHARACTER PRESERVATION**: distribution deduction consists of the **SAME PROPORTION of each class** of items entering into DNI as the class total bears to total DNI. Ordinary income, qualified dividends, LTCG, tax-exempt interest all flow proportionally to beneficiaries. **В§ 661(c)**: coordination with **В§ 642(c) charitable contribution deduction** вҖ” distribution deduction is reduced by В§ 642(c) charitable attributable to current-year DNI. **В§ 662(a)(1) TIER-1 BENEFICIARY INCLUSION**: beneficiary includes mandatory distributions, limited to DNI. **В§ 662(a)(2) TIER-2 BENEFICIARY INCLUSION**: beneficiary includes other amounts, limited to **REMAINING DNI after tier-1**. **В§ 663(a)(1) EXCLUSION**: gifts, bequests, and devises of specific sums or property are NOT distributions for В§ 661 / В§ 662 purposes. **В§ 663(a)(2) EXCLUSION**: capital gains allocated to corpus are NOT distributions. **В§ 663(b) 65-DAY RULE ELECTION**: distributions made within the first **65 days** of the next taxable year may be elected to be treated as made on the last day of the preceding taxable year вҖ” eleventh-hour planning opportunity to shift income retroactively ([Forvis Mazars вҖ” 65-Day Distribution Election: Tax Savings in the Eleventh Hour](https://www.forvismazars.us/forsights/2024/08/65-day-distribution-election-tax-savings-in-the-eleventh-hour)). **В§ 663(c) SEPARATE SHARE RULE**: substantially separate and independent shares of different beneficiaries of a SINGLE trust shall be treated as separate trusts for purposes of В§ 661 / В§ 662 / В§ 663(b) вҖ” DNI computation, distribution deduction, beneficiary inclusion, and 65-day election. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.661(c)-2** + **В§ 1.663(c)-1 to В§ 1.663(c)-5** implementing regulations. **Sixteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoDistribution, NotApplicableSimpleTrustUseSection651Instead, NotApplicableGrantorTrustPassThrough, CompliantTier1MandatoryDistributionsWithinDni, CompliantTier2DiscretionaryDistributionsWithinDni, CompliantSeparateShareRuleAppliedTreasReg1_663C, Compliant65DayRuleElectionMadeSection663b, CompliantCharacterProportionatelyAllocatedSection661b, CompliantSection663a1SpecificBequestExcluded, CompliantSection663a2CapitalGainsCorpusExcluded, ViolationDistributionDeductionExceedsDniCeiling, ViolationSeparateShareRuleNotAppliedDespiteMultipleBeneficiaries, ViolationTier1Tier2PriorityIgnored, ViolationCharacterNotProportionatelyAllocated, ViolationSection642cCharitableCoordinationOmitted, Violation65DayRuleElectionDistributionAfter65Days. **Five trust/estate types**: DomesticComplexTrust, ForeignComplexTrust, EstateProbate, DomesticSimpleTrustWrongStatute, DomesticGrantorTrustWrongStatute. **Seven distribution scenarios**: NoDistributionMade, Tier1MandatoryOnly, Tier1AndTier2Mixed, DniNotFullyDistributed, MultipleBeneficiariesSeparateShares, SpecificBequestSection663a1, CapitalGainsAllocatedToCorpusSection663a2. **Five DNI character classes**: OrdinaryIncomeOnly, OrdinaryAndQualifiedDividendsMixed, OrdinaryAndLongTermCapitalGainMixed, OrdinaryAndTaxExemptInterestMixed, FourClassDniMixed. Regs.** ((a)(1) + (a)(2) + (b) + (c) + В§ 662(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b) + В§ 663(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)/(c) + В§ 1.661(c)-2 + В§ 1.663(c)-1 + В§ 1.663(c)-5 + В§ 643(a) + Form 1041 Schedule B + Schedule K-1), **constant pin 65-day rule**. `traderview-expense::section_641` is the **IRC В§ 641 Imposition of Tax on Trusts and Estates (Fiduciary Income Taxation) module** (iter 626) вҖ” natural pairing with `section_643` (iter 624 DNI ceiling controls distribution deduction). Direct trader-companion to `section_643` (DNI), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8 % at top trust bracket), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension at compressed brackets), `section_651` / `section_661` (distribution deductions), `section_642` (В§ 642(b) personal exemption + В§ 642(c) charitable deduction). **В§ 641(a) IMPOSITION**: tax imposed on taxable income of estates, trusts, bankruptcy estates; paid by fiduciary on Form 1041 ([Cornell LII В§ 641](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/641), [IRS Form 1041 Instructions](https://www.irs.gov/forms-pubs/about-form-1041)). **В§ 641(b) COMPUTATION**: taxable income computed in the same manner as for an individual, EXCEPT subject to В§ 642 special rules, В§ 643 DNI ceiling, and В§ 651 (simple trust) / В§ 661 (complex trust) distribution deductions. **В§ 641(c) ESBT** (Electing Small Business Trust): the S corporation portion taxed as separate trust at **highest В§ 1(e) rate (37 %)** with **NO В§ 651 / В§ 661 distribution deduction** permitted on the S corp portion. **В§ 641(d)**: ESBT non-S-corp portion taxed under normal trust rules. **В§ 1(e) RATE SCHEDULE** for estates and trusts (compressed four-bracket structure post-TCJA, **made permanent by P.L. 119-21 OBBBA 2025 В§ 70411**). **2025 brackets** (IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40): **10 % on $0-$3,150; 24 % on $3,150-$11,450; 35 % on $11,450-$15,650; 37 % above $15,650**. **2026 top bracket** (estimated IRS inflation adjustment): approximately **$16,000**. **Compressed bracket compression vs individual**: 2025 trust top bracket $15,650 vs 2025 individual single $626,350 = **вүҲ 40Г— compression**. **В§ 642(b) PERSONAL EXEMPTION**: **$600 estate, $300 complex trust, $100 simple trust** (NOT inflation-indexed since enactment). **В§ 642(c)**: charitable contribution deduction (only from gross income; no AGI floor). **В§ 1411(a)(2)**: **NIIT 3.8 %** on undistributed net investment income above top trust bracket threshold (same compressed level вҖ” $15,650 for 2025). **Fifteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableGrantorTrustPassThroughNoTaxAtTrustLevel, NotApplicableNoTaxableIncome, CompliantTaxComputedAtBracket10, CompliantTaxComputedAtBracket24, CompliantTaxComputedAtBracket35, CompliantTaxComputedAtBracket37TopBracket, CompliantEsbtSCorpPortionTaxedAtHighestRate, CompliantDistributionDeductionAppliedSimpleTrust, CompliantDistributionDeductionAppliedComplexTrust, CompliantNiitAppliedAtTopBracketThreshold, ViolationIndividualBracketsUsedInsteadOfTrustCompressed, ViolationEsbtSCorpPortionImproperlyDistributionDeducted, ViolationDniCeilingExceededByDistributionDeduction, ViolationNiitOmittedAtTopBracket, ViolationSection642bExemptionOverclaimed. **Seven fiduciary entity types**: DomesticGrantorTrustPassThrough, DomesticSimpleTrust, DomesticComplexTrust, ElectingSmallBusinessTrust, DomesticEstateInProbate, ForeignTrust, BankruptcyEstate. **Three ESBT portions**: NotApplicable, SCorpPortion, NonSCorpPortion. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-641`. **Twenty tests pin**: **grantor trust passthrough not applicable** (Subpart E grantor pass-through), **zero taxable income not applicable**, **bracket 10 compliant** ($3K Г— 10 % = $300), **bracket 24 compliant** ($10K вҶ’ $3,150 Г— 10 % + $6,850 Г— 24 % = $1,959), **bracket 35 compliant**, **bracket 37 top compliant** ($100K), **at exactly $15,650 still in 35 bracket** (statutory вүӨ boundary), **at $15,651 enters 37 bracket** (boundary +1), **individual brackets misused violation** (40Г— compression), **ESBT S-corp portion top 37 % rate compliant** ($50K Г— 37 % = $18,500), **ESBT S-corp distribution deduction violation** (В§ 641(c) prohibits), **distribution deduction exceeds DNI violation** ($80K > $60K DNI), **distribution deduction within DNI compliant** (simple trust), **NIIT applied at top bracket compliant** ($20K taxable + $4K undistributed NII), **NIIT omitted at top bracket violation** (В§ 1411(a)(2)), **В§ 642(b) exemption overclaimed simple trust violation** ($200 > $100 cap), **В§ 642(b) estate exemption $600 compliant**, **citations pin В§ 641 subsections + brackets** ((a) + (b) + (c) + (d) + В§ 1(e) + Rev. Proc. 2024-40 + В§ 642(b)/(c) + В§ 643(a) + В§ 651 + В§ 661 + В§ 1411(a)(2) + $15,650 + 40Г— + Form 1041), **constant pin brackets and rates** (3150 + 11450 + 15650 + 16000 + 1000/2400/3500/3700 bp + $600/$300/$100 exemptions + 380 bp NIIT), **saturating overflow defense extreme income** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for: trader-owned non-grantor trusts (CRTs / GRATs / IDGTs); family LLCs with passthrough trust K-1s; ESBT-owning trader S-corp shareholders (mandatory 37 % rate on S-corp portion); foreign trust DNI computation; pooled-income-fund / CRT planning. `traderview-expense::section_643` is the **IRC В§ 643 Definitions and Rules for Trust and Estate Income Taxation (Distributable Net Income / DNI) module** (iter 624) вҖ” direct trader-companion to `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” trust 3.8 % surtax tracks DNI), `section_469` (passive activity rules for trusts/estates), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized deduction suspension вҖ” particularly impactful at trust compressed brackets), `section_641` (imposition of tax on trusts), `section_651` (simple trust distribution deduction), `section_661` (complex trust distribution deduction), `section_642` (special rules for trust/estate income computation вҖ” В§ 642(b) personal exemption + В§ 642(c) charitable deduction), `section_678` (grantor trust rules cross-reference). DNI acts as a **CEILING** on the amount of income that can be shifted from the trust to beneficiaries for tax purposes. Trader-critical because **2025 trust top marginal bracket = 37 % at approximately $15,650** of taxable income (compressed brackets vs individual), making DNI optimization the single most impactful trust tax planning decision ([Cornell LII В§ 643](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/643), [Greenleaf Trust DNI explainer](https://greenleaftrust.com/missives/distributable-net-income/), [Miami Law Heckerling Demystifying DNI](https://media.law.miami.edu/heckerling/2021/Supplemental%20Materials/Doyle%20-%20Fundamentals%20Program%20Demystifying%20Distributable%20Net%20Income.pdf)). **В§ 643(a) DNI DEFINITION**: trust taxable income modified by вҖ” (1) **no distribution deduction**; (2) **no personal exemption** (В§ 642(b)); (3) **GAINS FROM SALE OR EXCHANGE OF CAPITAL ASSETS EXCLUDED** to the extent they are (A) allocated to corpus AND (B) not (i) paid, credited, or required to be distributed to any beneficiary during the taxable year, or (ii) paid, permanently set aside, or to be used for the purposes specified in В§ 642(c) (charitable contribution); (5) **TAX-EXEMPT INCOME INCLUDED** in DNI net of allocable expenses; (6) foreign trust modifications; (7) amounts deemed distributed under В§ 651 / В§ 661 excluded. **В§ 643(b) TRUST ACCOUNTING INCOME (FAI)**: defines "income" for В§В§ 651/661 distribution limits + estate and gift tax marital deduction + pooled income funds + charitable remainder trusts. Generally includes interest, dividends, and rent; capital gains **ALLOCATED TO PRINCIPAL** absent contrary trust-instrument or state-law direction. **В§ 643(c) BENEFICIARY**: includes heir, legatee, or devisee. **В§ 643(d)**: coordination with В§В§ 651 / 661. **В§ 643(e) PROPERTY DISTRIBUTED IN KIND**: В§ 643(e)(2) loss recognition; В§ 643(e)(3) gain-recognition election. **В§ 643(f) MULTIPLE TRUSTS**: two or more trusts with substantially the same grantor and substantially the same primary beneficiary, with **tax-avoidance principal purpose**, treated as one. **В§ 643(g)**: election to treat trust estimated tax payments as made by beneficiaries. **В§ 643(h)**: distributions by foreign trusts. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.643(b)-1** (final regs December 30, 2003; effective 2004): defines "income" for trust purposes; permits **POWER TO ADJUST** under state Uniform Principal and Income Act equivalents ([Treasury Press Release JS-1068](https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/js1068)). **DNI ceiling mechanics**: tier-1 (mandatory) distributions limited to DNI; tier-2 (discretionary) limited to remaining DNI after tier-1; excess distributions = tax-free return of corpus. **Fourteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoTrustOrEstate, CompliantDniProperlyComputed, CompliantCapitalGainsExcludedAllocatedToCorpus, CompliantCapitalGainsIncludedInDniDistributedOrSetAside, CompliantTaxExemptIncomeIncludedInDni, CompliantPowerToAdjustAppliedTreasReg1_643B, CompliantForeignTrustDniModificationApplied, ViolationDniComputationIncludedPersonalExemption, ViolationDniComputationIncludedDistributionDeduction, ViolationCapitalGainsImproperlyIncludedInDni, ViolationCapitalGainsImproperlyExcludedDespiteDistribution, ViolationTaxExemptIncomeOmittedFromDni, ViolationMultipleTrustsAggregatedNotPerSection643f, ViolationForeignTrustDniModificationOmitted. **Five trust/estate types**: DomesticGrantorTrust, DomesticNonGrantorSimpleTrust, DomesticNonGrantorComplexTrust, ForeignTrust, EstateProbate. **Five capital gain treatments**: AllocatedToCorpusNotDistributed, AllocatedToCorpusButDistributedToBeneficiary, AllocatedToCorpusButRequiredToBeDistributed, AllocatedToIncomeByTrustInstrumentOrState, SetAsideForSection642cCharity. **Three DNI inclusion approaches**: StandardSection643aComputation, PowerToAdjustReallocatedUnderTreasReg1_643B, ForeignTrustModificationApplied. Sibling cluster: `section_641` (imposition of tax on trusts), `section_651`/`section_661` (simple/complex trust distribution deductions), `section_642` (В§ 642(b) personal exemption + В§ 642(c) charitable deduction), `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” trust threshold matches top bracket), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension at compressed trust brackets), `section_678` (grantor trust attribution).

`traderview-expense::section_1254` is the **IRC В§ 1254 Gain from Disposition of Interest in Oil, Gas, Geothermal, or Other Mineral Properties вҖ” Natural Resource Recapture module** (iter 622) вҖ” direct trader-companion to `section_1245_1250` (sibling personal/real property recapture), `section_1239` (related-party depreciable property recharacterization), `section_1248` (CFC stock recharacterization), `section_704c` (partnership built-in gain allocations), `section_469` (passive activity rules вҖ” oil & gas working interest carve-out), `section_1231` (capital/ordinary gain framework вҖ” В§ 1254 overrides), `section_263` (capitalization rules вҖ” В§ 263(c) IDC), `section_611` (depletion), `section_616` (mine development), `section_617` (mine exploration). When a taxpayer disposes of В§ 1254 property (any property under В§ 614 whose adjusted basis includes В§ 611 depletion adjustments), gain recognized is recharacterized as **ORDINARY INCOME** to the extent of the **"В§ 1254 costs"** previously deducted вҖ” **intangible drilling and development costs (IDC) under В§ 263(c)**, mine development costs under В§ 616, mine exploration costs under В§ 617, and **В§ 611 depletion that reduced the basis**. Parallel to В§ 1245 (personal property recapture) and В§ 1250 (real property recapture) for natural resource property ([Cornell LII В§ 1254](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1254), [Tax Notes IRC В§ 1254](https://www.taxnotes.com/research/federal/usc26/1254), [Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1254-1](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.1254-1)). **В§ 1254(a)(1) GENERAL RULE**: any gain on disposition treated as ordinary income to extent of **lesser of** (A) aggregate В§ 1254 costs OR (B) amount realized вҲ’ adjusted basis. **В§ 1254(a)(1)(B) POST-1986 PROPERTY** (placed in service on/after January 1, 1987): aggregate of (i) IDC under В§ 263(c) + mine development under В§ 616; (ii) mine exploration costs under В§ 617; (iii) В§ 611 depletion reducing adjusted basis. **PRE-1987 PROPERTY**: IDC under В§ 263(c) reduced by depletion that would have been allowable if IDCs had been capitalized. **В§ 1254(a)(3) PARTNERSHIP/TRUST ELECTION**: entity-level application + partner allocation per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1254-5. **В§ 1254(b) SPECIAL RULES**: (1) carryover basis transferee (В§ 351 corporate, В§ 721 partnership) tacks transferor's holding for В§ 1254 purposes; (2) В§ 1254 costs allocable to lower property in carryover transactions. **В§ 1254(c) DEFINITION**: В§ 1254 property = any property within В§ 614 whose adjusted basis includes adjustments for В§ 611 depletion deductions. **В§ 1254(d) PARTIAL DISPOSITION**: in the case of disposition of portion of В§ 1254 property (other than undivided interest), the entire amount of В§ 1254 costs allocated to that portion **to the extent of the gain** on the partial disposition. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1254-2(b) NONPRODUCTIVE WELL EXCEPTION**: В§ 1254 costs attributable to nonproductive wells NOT recapturable, except in certain **limited-risk reimbursement situations** (taxpayer has agreement to reimburse drilling costs from operating partner regardless of well success). **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1254-1 / В§ 1.1254-5**: final regs reorganized 1995, updated 2008; partnership/partner allocation rules. **Form 4797 Part III line 28** reports В§ 1254 recapture. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoGainRecognized, NotApplicableNotSection1254Property, NotApplicableNonproductiveWellExclusion, CompliantGainRecharacterizedAsOrdinary, CompliantPartialDispositionAllocationApplied, CompliantPre1987PropertyDepletionOffsetApplied, CompliantCarryoverBasisTransfereeTreatedAsHoldingTransferorPeriod, ViolationGainReportedAsCapitalDespiteSection1254, ViolationPartialDispositionAllocationOmitted, ViolationPre1987DepletionOffsetNotComputed. **Seven property categories**: PostJan1_1987PropertyAllRecapture, PreJan1_1987PropertyDepletionOffset, NonproductiveWellLimitedRiskExcluded, OverridingRoyaltyInterest, WorkingInterest, NetProfitsInterest, NotSection1254Property. **Four disposition types**: EntireProperty, PartialDispositionNotUndividedInterest, UndividedInterest, CarryoverBasisSection351Or721. **Four gain character categories**: CapitalLongTerm, CapitalShortTerm, Section1231Gain, OrdinaryOther. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1254`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **no gain not applicable**, **not В§ 1254 property not applicable** (no depletion-adjusted basis), **nonproductive well excluded not applicable** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1254-2(b)), **post-1987 working interest compliant full recapture** ($600K costs + $400K residual), **post-1987 costs exceed gain capped at gain** ($1.5M costs but $1M gain вҶ’ ordinary capped at $1M), **capital long-term misreport violation**, **В§ 1231 misreport violation**, **pre-1987 property depletion offset compliant** ($800K - $200K = $600K), **pre-1987 depletion offset omitted violation**, **partial disposition allocation compliant** ($300K allocable + $700K residual), **partial disposition allocation omitted violation** (В§ 1254(d)), **carryover basis В§ 351 compliant no recapture** (deferred to transferee), **overriding royalty interest compliant**, **net profits interest compliant**, **undivided interest not partial disposition** (no allocation required), **citations pin В§ 1254 subsections + Treas. Regs.** ((a)(1) + (a)(1)(B) + (a)(3) + (b) + (c) + (d) + В§ 1.1254-1 + В§ 1.1254-2(b) + В§ 1.1254-5 + Form 4797 + В§ 1245/1250/1252/1255 cross-refs), **constant pin post-1986 effective date** (1987-01-01 + 1995 reorg + 2008 update), **saturating overflow defense extreme gain** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for: oil & gas LPs and MLPs (Energy Transfer / Enterprise Products / MPLX); mining MLPs (Westshore Terminals / Natural Resource Partners); mineral rights traders; energy infrastructure REITs; trader entities holding working interest, royalty interest, overriding royalty interest, net profits interest; family offices with mineral property portfolios; partnership tax matters partners coordinating В§ 1254 allocations under В§ 1.1254-5. Sibling cluster: `section_1245_1250` (personal/real property recapture вҖ” same recapture mechanic), `section_1239` (related-party depreciable property), `section_1248` (CFC stock recharacterization), `section_704c` (partnership built-in gain), `section_469` (oil & gas working interest passive carve-out), `section_263` (capitalization вҖ” IDC source), `section_611` (depletion).

`traderview-expense::section_1248` is the **IRC В§ 1248 Gain from Certain Sales or Exchanges of Stock in Certain Foreign Corporations вҖ” CFC Stock Sale Deemed-Dividend Recharacterization module** (iter 618) вҖ” direct trader-companion to `section_245a` (В§ 245A 100 % DRD eliminates В§ 1248 for C-corp sellers), `section_951a` (GILTI вҖ” В§ 951A PTI excluded under В§ 1248(d)(1)), `section_1239` (related-party depreciable property recharacterization), `section_865` (sourcing of gain вҖ” interacts with foreign-source recharacterization), `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” interaction with deemed dividend). When a **U.S. person who is a 10 %+ shareholder** (under В§ 958(a) direct or В§ 958(b) constructive ownership) of a controlled foreign corporation (CFC) sells or exchanges the CFC stock, the gain recognized is **RECHARACTERIZED as a deemed DIVIDEND** (ordinary income) to the extent of the CFC's earnings and profits (E&P) attributable to the stock and the period of U.S. ownership while the foreign corporation was a CFC. Statute exists to defeat the conversion of CFC ordinary income into capital gains via stock sale ([Cornell LII В§ 1248](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1248), [Tax Notes IRC В§ 1248](https://www.taxnotes.com/research/federal/usc26/1248), [SF Tax Counsel вҖ” Section 1248 and 245A Strategies](https://sftaxcounsel.com/blog/strategies-to-recharacterize-gain-from-certain-stock-sales-with-untaxed-foreign-earnings/)). **В§ 1248(a) GENERAL RULE**: U.S. person owning 10+ percent voting power at any time during **5-year period** ending on sale date вҖ” if the corporation was a CFC at any time during that period вҖ” recharacterizes gain as deemed dividend to extent of attributable E&P. **В§ 1248(b) INDIVIDUAL LIMITATION**: for individuals holding stock as capital asset > 1 year, tax on deemed dividend cannot exceed pro rata domestic-corp tax on E&P + LTCG tax on residual. **В§ 1248(c)(2) LOWER-TIER CFC RULE**: when upper CFC owns вүҘ 50 % voting or value of lower CFC, lower-tier E&P attributable to upper-tier stock included in deemed dividend. **В§ 1248(d) EXCLUSIONS FROM E&P**: (1) PTI E&P (В§ 951 subpart F / В§ 951A GILTI previously taxed); (2) ECI E&P; (3) pre-January 1, 1963 E&P (original 1962 enactment cutoff). **В§ 1248(e) HOLDING CORP EXTENSION** to U.S. corps formed or availed of to hold CFC stock. **В§ 245A TCJA 2017 PARTICIPATION EXEMPTION**: 100 % DRD on foreign-source portion of dividends received by U.S. C-corp from 10 %+-owned foreign corp; in the vast majority of cases prevents domestic C-corporations from being subject to В§ 1248 tax вҖ” the В§ 1248 deemed dividend is fully offset by the В§ 245A DRD. **В§ 958(a)/(b)**: direct and indirect ownership attribution. **Thirteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoCfcStockSold, NotApplicableShareholderUnderTenPercent, NotApplicableSharesNotHeldDuringCfcPeriod, NotApplicableUsCorporateSellerSection245aFullDrd, CompliantIndividualDeemedDividendWithSection1248bLimitation, CompliantDeemedDividendRecharacterizedFullEpExcess, CompliantLowerTierCfcEpIncluded, CompliantExclusionForPtiPreviouslyTaxed, CompliantExclusionForEciEffectivelyConnected, CompliantExclusionForPre1963Ep, ViolationGainReportedAsCapitalDespiteSection1248, ViolationLowerTierCfcEpOmittedFromDeemedDividend, ViolationIndividualClaimedSection245aDrdImproperly. **Five shareholder types**: IndividualUsShareholder, UsCorporateSellerEligibleForSection245aDrd, UsCorporateSellerNotEligibleForSection245a, PartnershipPassThroughUsShareholder, NonUsShareholder. **Five E&P categories**: UntaxedSection1248Ep, PtiPreviouslyTaxedIncome, EciEffectivelyConnectedIncome, Pre1963Ep, LowerTierCfcEp. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1248`. **Twenty tests pin**: **no gain not applicable**, **non-US shareholder not applicable**, **under 10 % voting not applicable**, **at exactly 10 % voting triggers recharacterization** (boundary 1000 basis points), **not CFC during holding period not applicable**, **individual compliant with В§ 1248(b) limitation** ($4M E&P + $6M residual capital), **PTI excluded В§ 1248(d)(1)**, **ECI excluded В§ 1248(d)(2)**, **pre-1963 excluded В§ 1248(d)(3)**, **US corporate seller В§ 245A full DRD**, **individual claimed В§ 245A DRD violation** (В§ 245A only for C-corps), **lower-tier CFC E&P included compliant** ($4M upper + $1M lower = $5M deemed dividend), **lower-tier CFC E&P omitted violation**, **full gain reported as capital violation**, **deemed dividend capped at gain** (E&P $20M but gain only $10M вҶ’ cap at $10M), **partnership pass-through individual treatment**, **voting power held during lookback satisfies threshold** (5 % current but had 10 %+ in past 5 years), **citations pin В§ 1248 subsections + В§ 245A** ((a) + (b) + (c)(2) + (d)(1) + (d)(2) + (d)(3) + (e) + В§ 245A + В§ 958(a)/(b) + 5-year lookback + TCJA), **constant pin thresholds and dates** (1000 basis points 10 % + 5000 lower-tier 50 % + 1963 cutoff + 1962 enactment + 2017 TCJA), **saturating overflow defense extreme gain and E&P** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for: individual traders selling foreign-corp stock with untaxed E&P; hedge fund / private equity LPs computing CFC stock sale tax on portfolio dispositions; partnerships selling CFC subsidiaries with passthrough В§ 1248 application; cross-border M&A with CFC target. Sibling cluster: `section_245a` (TCJA participation exemption вҖ” practically eliminates В§ 1248 for C-corps), `section_951a` (GILTI вҖ” В§ 951A PTI under В§ 1248(d)(1)), `section_897` (FIRPTA вҖ” alternative foreign-corp recharacterization regime for US real estate holders), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8 % вҖ” applies to deemed dividend portion for individuals), `section_865` (gain sourcing вҖ” В§ 1248 recharacterizes as foreign-source dividend for FTC purposes).

`traderview-expense::section_1402` is the **IRC В§ 1402 Self-Employment Income Definitions + SECA Tax Computation module** (iter 616) вҖ” bread-and-butter trader entity-structure module. Direct trader-companion to `section_475c2` (В§ 475(c)(2) trader mark-to-market definition), `section_475f` (В§ 475(f) MTM election), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension), `section_469` (passive activity rules вҖ” interaction with material participation), `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” Medicare tax gap analysis vs. SE tax), `section_199a` (QBI вҖ” interaction with SE income), `section_707` (partnership transactions with partners вҖ” В§ 707(c) guaranteed payments). **В§ 1402(a) NET EARNINGS FROM SELF-EMPLOYMENT**: gross income from trade or business minus attributable deductions, plus В§ 702(a)(8) distributive share from partnership. **В§ 1402(a)(1) RENTAL REAL ESTATE EXCLUSION**: rentals from real estate excluded UNLESS taxpayer is a real estate dealer in ordinary trade or business. **В§ 1402(a)(2) INTEREST AND DIVIDENDS EXCLUSION**: dividends and interest excluded UNLESS taxpayer is a securities dealer. **В§ 1402(a)(3)(A) CAPITAL GAINS EXCLUSION**: any gain or loss from sale/exchange/involuntary conversion of capital asset excluded. **В§ 1402(a)(3)(B) В§ 1231 PROPERTY EXCLUSION**: gain/loss from non-inventory property excluded. **В§ 1402(a)(13) LIMITED PARTNER EXCLUSION**: distributive share of any item of income or loss of a limited partner, AS SUCH, excluded вҖ” other than В§ 707(c) guaranteed payments for services actually rendered to or on behalf of the partnership ([Cornell LII В§ 1402](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1402), [Tax Adviser Sec. 1402(a)(13) and limited partnerships](https://www.thetaxadviser.com/issues/2024/mar/sec-1402a13-and-limited-partnerships/)). **В§ 1401 RATES**: В§ 1401(a) **OASDI 12.4 %** up to wage base; В§ 1401(b)(1) **Medicare 2.9 %** uncapped; В§ 1401(b)(2) **Additional Medicare Tax 0.9 %** on SE income above thresholds **$200,000 single / $250,000 MFJ / $125,000 MFS**. **2025 OASDI wage base = $176,100** (SSA-announced); 2026 wage base subject to SSA annual inflation announcement. **Net earnings multiplier 0.9235** = (1 вҲ’ 0.0765) reflects employer-equivalent SE tax deduction allowed under В§ 1402(a)(12). **В§ 475(f) MTM TRADER GAINS NOT SE INCOME** per long-standing IRS Topic 429 (Traders in securities) guidance; reported on Form 4797 as ordinary gains/losses but EXCLUDED from SE income for sole-proprietor traders with Trader Tax Status (TTS). **Active Limited Partner contested**: IRS has won SE-tax assessments against active limited partners in private equity and hedge funds in **Soroban Capital Partners (T.C. 2023)**, **Denham Capital (T.C. 2024)**, **Sirius Solutions (5th Cir.)** вҖ” В§ 1402(a)(13) "limited partner, as such" exclusion narrowed for active service partners ([White & Case IRS winning SECA tax against limited partners](https://www.whitecase.com/insight-alert/irs-continues-winning-self-employment-contributions-act-seca-tax-against-limited), [Green Trader Tax Hedge Fund Medicare Tax Gap](https://greentradertax.com/the-hedge-fund-medicare-tax-gap-how-active-limited-partners-fall-between-se-tax-and-niit/)). **Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoSeIncome, CompliantSection475fMtmTraderGainsExcludedFromSeIncome, CompliantLimitedPartnerDistributiveShareExcludedSection1402a13, CompliantCapitalAssetGainExcludedSection1402a3A, CompliantSection1231GainExcludedSection1402a3B, CompliantRentalRealEstateExcludedSection1402a1, CompliantPortfolioInterestDividendsExcludedSection1402a2, CompliantGuaranteedPaymentForServicesIncludedSection1402a13, CompliantSeTaxComputedOnNetEarningsFromSelfEmployment, ViolationActiveLimitedPartnerExclusionImproperlyClaimed, ViolationSelfEmploymentIncomeUnderreported. **Eight taxpayer activity types**: Section475fMtmSoleProprietorTrader, LimitedPartnerInInvestmentPartnership, ActiveLimitedPartnerInInvestmentPartnership, GeneralPartnerOrManagingMember, SoleProprietorActiveBusiness, RentalRealEstateNonDealer, RealEstateDealerInTrade, DealerInSecurities. **Nine income item types**: TradingGainsLossesSection475fMtm, LimitedPartnerDistributiveShareNonGuaranteed, GeneralPartnerDistributiveShare, GuaranteedPaymentForServicesSection707c, CapitalGainSale, Section1231GainSale, RentalRealEstateIncome, PortfolioInterestDividends, OrdinaryBusinessIncome. **Four filing statuses**: Single, MarriedFilingJointly, MarriedFilingSeparately, HeadOfHousehold. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1402`. **Twenty tests pin**: **В§ 475(f) trader gains excluded from SE income** (sole proprietor TTS), **capital gain excluded В§ 1402(a)(3)(A)**, **В§ 1231 gain excluded В§ 1402(a)(3)(B)**, **rental real estate non-dealer excluded** (В§ 1402(a)(1)), **real estate dealer rental income SUBJECT to SECA** (dealer exception triggers), **portfolio interest/dividends excluded non-dealer**, **securities dealer dividends SUBJECT to SECA** (dealer exception triggers), **true limited partner distributive share excluded**, **active limited partner exclusion improperly claimed violation** (Soroban/Denham/Sirius), **general partner distributive share subject to SECA**, **guaranteed payment for services subject to SECA** (В§ 707(c)), **sole proprietor ordinary business income subject to SECA**, **OASDI wage base cap at $176,100** (boundary), **other wages reduce OASDI base** (no double SS tax), **Additional Medicare Tax kicks in above threshold** (0.9 % above $200K single), **Additional Medicare threshold MFJ higher** (Single $200K < MFJ $250K), **zero net earnings not applicable**, **citations pin В§ 1402 subsections + case law** ((a) + (a)(1) + (a)(2) + (a)(3)(A) + (a)(3)(B) + (a)(13) + (b) + В§ 1401(a)/(b)(1)/(b)(2) + IRS Topic 429 + Soroban + Denham + Sirius + $176,100), **constant pin rates + thresholds** (1240/290/90 basis points + $176,100 wage base + $200K/$250K/$125K AMT thresholds + 0.9235 multiplier), **saturating overflow defense extreme gross income** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for: sole-proprietor TTS traders verifying SE income exclusion; hedge fund / private equity LPs analyzing active-limited-partner SE tax exposure post-Soroban/Denham/Sirius; rental real estate operators verifying non-dealer exclusion; general partners / managing members computing SECA tax; family LLCs allocating income between guaranteed payments (taxable SE) and distributive share (potentially excludable). Sibling cluster: `section_475c2` (В§ 475(c)(2) trader definition), `section_475f` (В§ 475(f) MTM election), `section_67g` (TCJA misc itemized suspension вҖ” drives В§ 475(f) election decisions), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8 % вҖ” Medicare tax gap analysis), `section_199a` (QBI deduction interaction).

`traderview-expense::section_1471` is the **IRC В§ 1471 + В§ 1472 + В§ 1473 + В§ 1474 FATCA Chapter 4 Withholding on Payments to Foreign Financial Institutions and Non-Financial Foreign Entities module** (iter 614) вҖ” natural cross-border withholding follow-up to `section_1446` (iter 612 partnership ECTI) and `section_1445` (iter 608 FIRPTA). All three (В§ 1445 FIRPTA + В§ 1446 partnership ECTI + В§ 1471/В§ 1472 FATCA) are the principal U.S. foreign-payment withholding regimes. Direct trader-companion to `section_1446` (partnership ECTI), `section_1445` (FIRPTA USRPI), `section_864b` (effectively connected income), `section_871m` (dividend-equivalent payments вҖ” interacts with В§ 1471 documentation), `section_1411` (NIIT), `section_897` (FIRPTA gain). Withholding agent must deduct and withhold **30 % of any withholdable payment** made to a foreign financial institution (FFI) or non-financial foreign entity (NFFE) that does NOT meet Chapter 4 documentation and reporting requirements. Withholding agent is statutory withholding agent and personally liable for tax not withheld; remits via Forms 1042 / 1042-S. **В§ 1471(a) GENERAL RULE**: 30 % withholding on withholdable payment to FFI which does not meet В§ 1471(b) requirements ([Cornell LII В§ 1471](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1471), [IRS FATCA page](https://www.irs.gov/businesses/corporations/foreign-account-tax-compliance-act-fatca)). **В§ 1471(b) PARTICIPATING FFI REQUIREMENTS**: FFI Agreement with IRS to identify U.S. accounts, report annual account info on **Form 8966**, comply with verification and due diligence procedures, withhold on payments to recalcitrant account holders. **В§ 1471(b)(2) DEEMED-COMPLIANT FFI** per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1471-5(f). **В§ 1471(c) INFORMATION REPORTING** on each U.S. account: name + TIN + account number + balance + gross receipts / withdrawals. **В§ 1471(d) DEFINITIONS**: FFI = depository institutions, custodial institutions, investment entities, certain insurance companies. **В§ 1471(e) EXEMPT BENEFICIAL OWNERS**: foreign governments, international organizations, foreign central banks. **В§ 1471(f) PRE-EXISTING OBLIGATIONS**: payments under obligations outstanding **January 1, 2014** grandfathered. **В§ 1472**: 30 % withholding on withholdable payments to NFFE unless NFFE certifies it has no substantial U.S. owners OR provides identifying info about substantial U.S. owners. **В§ 1473 DEFINITIONS**: withholdable payment = (A) U.S.-source FDAP (interest, dividends, rents, royalties); (B) gross proceeds from sale of property producing U.S.-source interest/dividends вҖ” withholding on (B) was **permanently rescinded** by Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1473-1(a) + Notice 2014-33. **В§ 1474 SPECIAL RULES**: refund procedure + double-tax recovery + treaty coordination. **Forms**: W-8BEN-E (foreign entity Chapter 3 + Chapter 4 status), W-8IMY (foreign intermediary), W-8EXP (foreign government), W-8ECI (effectively connected income), W-9 (US person), Form 1042 (annual withholding return; **due March 15** = 15th day of 3rd month after close of calendar year), Form 1042-S (per-payee statement), Form 8966 (FATCA report). **GIIN** (Global Intermediary Identification Number) establishes Chapter 4 status. **Sixteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableUsPayee, NotApplicableNonUsSourcePayment, NotApplicableGrossProceedsRescinded, NotApplicablePreExistingObligation, CompliantParticipatingFfiNoWithholdingRequired, CompliantDeemedCompliantFfiNoWithholdingRequired, CompliantReportingModel1Or2FfiNoWithholdingRequired, CompliantExemptBeneficialOwnerNoWithholdingRequired, CompliantActiveNffeNoWithholdingRequired, CompliantPassiveNffeWithSubstantialUsOwnersReported, CompliantPassiveNffeNoSubstantialUsOwnersCertified, CompliantFullWithholdingAppliedToNonparticipatingFfi, ViolationWithholdingAgentFailedToWithholdFromNonparticipatingFfi, ViolationUndocumentedPayeeNoWithholdingApplied, ViolationForm1042NotFiledByMarch15Deadline, ViolationForm1042SNotIssuedToForeignPayee. **Sixteen payee Chapter 4 statuses**: UsPersonProvidedFormW9, ParticipatingFfiWithGiin, RegisteredDeemedCompliantFfiWithGiin, CertifiedDeemedCompliantFfi, ReportingModel1FfiWithGiin, ReportingModel2FfiWithGiin, OwnerDocumentedFfi, ExceptedFfi, ExemptBeneficialOwner, NonparticipatingFfi, ActiveNffe, PassiveNffeNoSubstantialUsOwners, PassiveNffeWithReportedSubstantialUsOwners, PassiveNffeUndocumented, RecalcitrantAccountHolder, UndocumentedPayee. **Eight payment types**: UsSourceFdapInterest, UsSourceFdapDividend, UsSourceFdapRents, UsSourceFdapRoyalties, UsSourceFdapOther, GrossProceedsFromSaleOfPropertyRescindedByTreasReg, PreExistingObligationOutstandingJanuary1_2014, NonUsSourcePayment. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1471`. **Twenty-two tests pin**: **US person W-9 not applicable**, **non-US-source payment not applicable**, **gross proceeds rescinded not applicable** (Treas Reg В§ 1.1473-1(a)), **pre-existing obligation January 1, 2014 grandfathered**, **participating FFI with GIIN compliant no withholding**, **deemed-compliant FFI no withholding**, **reporting Model 1 IGA FFI no withholding** (intergovernmental agreement), **exempt beneficial owner no withholding** (foreign government), **active NFFE no withholding**, **passive NFFE no US owners certified compliant**, **passive NFFE with reported US owners compliant**, **nonparticipating FFI 30 % withholding compliant** (baseline), **nonparticipating FFI withholding failed violation**, **nonparticipating FFI under-withheld violation with shortfall computed** ($300K required - $100K actual = $200K shortfall), **undocumented payee no withholding violation** (presumption rule), **passive NFFE undocumented 30 % withholding required** (default rule), **recalcitrant account holder 30 % withholding**, **Form 1042 late filing violation** (March 15 deadline), **Form 1042-S not issued violation** (per-payee statement), **citations pin В§ 1471 subsections + В§ 1472/В§ 1473/В§ 1474 + Forms** (W-8BEN-E + W-8IMY + W-8EXP + W-9 + 1042 + 1042-S + 8966 + GIIN), **constant pin 30 % rate + deadlines** (3000 basis points + 2010 enactment + 2014-01-01 grandfather + 3-15 Form 1042 deadline), **saturating overflow defense extreme payment** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for hedge funds with foreign LP investors; brokerages making US-source FDAP payments to foreign account holders; family offices with foreign trust beneficiaries; cross-border payment platforms; software licensing royalty payments to foreign IP holders. Sibling cluster: `section_1445` (FIRPTA USRPI withholding вҖ” companion foreign-person withholding regime), `section_1446` (partnership ECTI withholding вҖ” companion), `section_871m` (dividend-equivalent payments вҖ” Chapter 4 documentation interaction), `section_864b` (effectively connected income), `section_897` (FIRPTA gain recognition).

`traderview-expense::section_1446` is the **IRC В§ 1446 Partnership Withholding on Foreign Partners' Share of Effectively Connected Income module** (iter 612) вҖ” natural follow-up to `section_1445` (iter 608 FIRPTA вҖ” both are foreign-person withholding regimes). Direct trader-companion to `section_1445` (FIRPTA USRPI withholding), `section_751` (partnership hot-asset distributions to foreign partners), `section_704c` (partnership built-in gain allocations), `section_864b` (effectively connected income definitions), `section_1411` (NIIT вҖ” trader-LP overlap), `section_951a` (GILTI вҖ” CFC overlap), `section_897` (FIRPTA gain recognition). A partnership (foreign or domestic) with **effectively connected taxable income (ECTI)** allocable to foreign partners is the **statutory withholding agent** under В§ 1446 and is personally liable for tax not withheld. **В§ 1446(a) GENERAL RULE**: partnership with ECTI allocable to foreign partner must pay withholding tax determined under В§ 1446(b). **В§ 1446(b)(1) RATE**: highest В§ 1 rate (currently **37 %**) for noncorporate partners; highest В§ 11 rate (currently **21 %**) for corporate partners. Reduced rates available with appropriate documentation: **20 % LTCG**, **25 % unrecaptured В§ 1250 gain**, **28 % collectibles gain** ([IRS Partnership Withholding page](https://www.irs.gov/individuals/international-taxpayers/partnership-withholding)). **В§ 1446(c) DEFINITIONS**: foreign partner = any partner who is not a United States person within В§ 7701(a). **В§ 1446(d) TREATMENT**: tax treated as paid by the foreign partner and creditable against partner's U.S. tax liability per Form 8805. **В§ 1446(e) INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS**: partnership must pay quarterly installments on **Form 8813** by the **15th day of the 4th, 6th, 9th, and 12th months** of its tax year. **В§ 1446(f) PARTNERSHIP INTEREST TRANSFER** (added by **TCJA 2017** (P.L. 115-97) В§ 13501): transferee withholds **10 % of amount realized** on transfer of a partnership interest by a foreign person if a portion of the gain would be ECI; **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1446(f)-1** et seq. (final regulations effective **October 7, 2020**) confirm the 10 % rate. **Form 8804** вҖ” Annual Return for Partnership Withholding Tax; due **15th day of 3rd month** after close of partnership tax year (or 6th month with В§ 6081 extension). **Form 8805** вҖ” Foreign Partner's Information Statement of В§ 1446 Withholding Tax (one per foreign partner). **Form 8813** вҖ” Partnership Withholding Tax Payment Voucher (quarterly installment). **Form 8804-C** вҖ” Certificate by foreign partner reducing В§ 1446 withholding via partner-level deductions/losses. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicableNoForeignPartnerOrNoEcti, CompliantQuarterlyInstallmentsPaidAndForm8804Filed, CompliantSection1446fTenPercentTransferWithholdingApplied, CompliantPartnerLevelCertificateFiledForm8804C, ViolationPartnershipFailedToWithholdFromForeignPartner, ViolationQuarterlyInstallmentMissed, ViolationForm8804NotFiledAtAnnualDeadline, ViolationForm8805NotIssuedToForeignPartner, ViolationWithheldAtIncorrectRate, ViolationSection1446fTransferWithholdingFailed. **Three partner types**: UsPersonNotForeign, NoncorporateForeignPartner, CorporateForeignPartner. **Five ECTI income types**: OrdinaryEcti, LongTermCapitalGain, UnrecapturedSection1250Gain, CollectiblesGain, QualifiedDividends. **Two withholding scenarios**: AnnualEctiWithholding, Section1446fPartnershipInterestTransfer. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1446`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **US person partner not applicable** (no foreign partner), **zero ECTI not applicable**, **noncorporate foreign 37 % compliant** (baseline), **corporate foreign 21 % compliant**, **noncorporate LTCG 20 % rate**, **noncorporate unrecaptured В§ 1250 25 % rate**, **noncorporate collectibles 28 % rate**, **partnership failed to withhold violation**, **under-withheld violation with shortfall computed** ($370K required minus $100K actual = $270K shortfall), **missed quarterly installments violation** (2 of 4), **Form 8804 not filed violation** (annual deadline), **Form 8805 not issued violation** (per-partner statement), **Form 8804-C partner certificate compliant** (partner-level deductions accepted), **В§ 1446(f) transfer 10 % compliant** ($2M amount realized Г— 10 % = $200K), **В§ 1446(f) transferee failed-to-withhold violation** (shortfall $150K), **citations pin В§ 1446 subsections + forms** ((a) + (b)(1) + (c) + (d) + (e) + (f) + TCJA 2017 + Form 8804/8805/8813/8804-C + В§ 1.1446-3 + В§ 1.1446(f)-1), **constant pin rates + installment schedule** (3700/2100/2000/2500/2800/1000 basis points + [4,6,9,12] month installments + 3-15 annual deadline), **constant pin TCJA 2017 + 2020 final regs years**, **saturating overflow defense extreme ECTI** (u64::MAX). Trader-critical for hedge funds with foreign LPs; real estate LPs with foreign capital; S-corp converts to partnerships with foreign LP/LLC members; private equity fund structures with foreign GP/LP investors; family offices with foreign HNW members. Sibling cluster: `section_1445` (FIRPTA USRPI withholding вҖ” В§ 1445/В§ 1446 are the two principal foreign-person withholding regimes), `section_864b` (effectively connected income definitions), `section_751` (partnership hot assets вҖ” В§ 1446(b) interacts with В§ 751 character allocation), `section_1411` (NIIT trader-LP overlap), `section_897` (FIRPTA gain recognition).

`traderview-expense::section_1445` is the **IRC В§ 1445 FIRPTA Withholding on Dispositions of United States Real Property Interests by Foreign Persons module** (iter 608) вҖ” trader-landlord critical for any cross-border USRPI transaction, commercial brokers, trader entities with foreign-investor LP/LLC members. Direct trader companion to `section_897` (FIRPTA gain recognition rules), `section_1031f` (related-party exchange + foreign person interaction вҖ” iter 562), `section_751` (partnership hot-asset distribution вҖ” В§ 1445(e) applies to partnership distributions to foreign partners), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% вҖ” trader-landlord overlap), `section_865` (sourcing of income). Buyer (transferee) of a USRPI from a foreign person is statutorily required to deduct and withhold a percentage of the amount realized and remit to IRS on **Form 8288 + Form 8288-A within 20 days of closing**; **buyer (transferee) is personally liable** as statutory withholding agent for unwithheld tax + В§ 6651 / В§ 6656 penalties + interest. **В§ 1445(a) GENERAL RULE**: in any disposition of a U.S. real property interest by a foreign person, the transferee shall deduct and withhold a tax equal to **15 percent** of the amount realized on the disposition. **В§ 1445(b)(2) NON-FOREIGN AFFIDAVIT exception**: no withholding if transferor furnishes the transferee an affidavit stating, under penalty of perjury, the transferor's TIN and that the transferor is not a foreign person ([IRS FIRPTA Withholding page](https://www.irs.gov/individuals/international-taxpayers/firpta-withholding)). **В§ 1445(b)(4) PUBLICLY TRADED STOCK exception**: no withholding if the property disposed of is an interest in a domestic corporation any class of stock of which is regularly traded on an established securities market. **В§ 1445(b)(5) DOMESTICALLY CONTROLLED REIT** interest disposition not subject to В§ 1445(a) withholding. **В§ 1445(b)(6) BUYER-RESIDENCE exception**: no withholding when the buyer acquires the property for use as a residence AND the amount realized is not more than **$300,000**; buyer (transferee) or member of buyer's family must have definite plans to reside at the property for at least **50 %** of the number of days the property is used by any person during each of the **first two 12-month periods** following the date of transfer. **В§ 1445(c)(4) IRS WITHHOLDING CERTIFICATE** (Form 8288-B): IRS may issue a certificate reducing or eliminating withholding if the maximum tax liability is less than 15 % of amount realized, or no tax is due. **PATH Act of 2015 (Pub.
