L. 108-357, October 22, 2004) as part of broader anti-shelter penalty regime created in reaction to KPMG / E&Y / BDO Seidman shelter promotion scandals of early 2000s. Trader-critical for material advisors on basket option contracts (Notice 2015-73), conservation easement syndications (Notice 2017-10), micro-captive insurance (Notice 2016-66), В§ 643 distribution-tier-out trusts, STARS foreign-tax-credit shelters.

**В§ 6707(b) Two-tier penalty framework**:

| Transaction Category | Source | Penalty |
|----------------------|--------|---------|
| OTHER reportable transactions | В§ 6707(b)(1) | **FLAT $50,000** base penalty |
| LISTED transactions (unintentional) | В§ 6707(b)(2) | **GREATER of $200,000 OR 50% of gross income** from aid/assistance/advice |
| LISTED transactions (INTENTIONAL) | В§ 6707(b)(2) flush | **GREATER of $200,000 OR 75% of gross income** вҖ” 50% rate SUBSTITUTED with 75% |

Pinned by `other_reportable_flat_50k_penalty` ($50,000 = 5,000,000 cents), `listed_transaction_unintentional_greater_of_200k_or_50_percent` ($500M gross income Г— 50% = $250M), `listed_transaction_low_gross_income_floor_200k` ($100K gross income вҶ’ $200K floor engages), `listed_transaction_intentional_75_percent_rate` ($1B gross income Г— 75% = $750M), `intentional_listed_75_percent_uniquely_engages_invariant`, `listed_penalty_uniquely_uses_percentage_invariant`.

**В§ 6707(c) Rescission framework вҖ” listed-vs-other split**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 6707(c)(1) | Commissioner MAY rescind penalty for OTHER reportable transactions if rescission promotes tax compliance + effective tax administration |
| В§ 6707(c)(2) | **LISTED TRANSACTIONS NOT ELIGIBLE FOR RESCISSION** вҖ” strict liability, no exception, no waiver, no abatement absent statutory amendment |
| В§ 6707(c)(3) | **NO JUDICIAL REVIEW** of denial of rescission request; Commissioner's denial is final |

Pinned by `other_reportable_commissioner_rescission_zeros_penalty`, `listed_transaction_no_rescission_available`, `no_judicial_review_of_rescission_denial`.

**В§ 6664(d) Reasonable cause defense вҖ” two-tier availability**:

| Transaction Category | Reasonable Cause + Good Faith Defense |
|----------------------|----------------------------------------|
| OTHER reportable transactions | **AVAILABLE** under В§ 6664(d) (substantial authority + good faith belief + reasonable cause) |
| LISTED transactions | **NOT AVAILABLE** вҖ” strict liability for listed-transaction material advisors |

Pinned by `other_reportable_reasonable_cause_zeros_penalty`, `listed_transaction_no_reasonable_cause_defense`, `listed_uniquely_disables_reasonable_cause_defense_invariant`.

**Material advisor threshold under В§ 6111(b)(1) + Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6111-3(b)(3)**: $50,000 gross income on natural-person taxpayer transactions OR $250,000 gross income on entity/business taxpayer transactions. Form 8918 filed with IRS Office of Tax Shelter Analysis (OTSA) by last day of month following calendar quarter in which advisor first becomes material advisor.

**Listed transaction examples**: Notice 2015-73 (basket option contracts) + Notice 2017-10 (conservation easement syndications) + Notice 2016-66 (micro-captive insurance) + В§ 643 distribution-tier-out trusts + STARS foreign-tax-credit shelters.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6707`. Thirty tests pin: **other reportable flat $50K penalty**; **other reportable reasonable cause zeros penalty**; **other reportable Commissioner rescission zeros penalty**; **other reportable false information triggers penalty**; **listed transaction unintentional GREATER of $200K or 50%**; **listed transaction low gross income floor $200K**; **listed transaction intentional 75% rate**; **listed transaction NO rescission available**; **listed transaction NO reasonable cause defense**; **not reportable no penalty**; **NO judicial review of rescission denial**; **intentional listed 75% uniquely engages invariant**; **listed uniquely disables reasonable cause defense invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6707(a)-(d) + 2004 AJCA В§ 815 + Pub. L. 108-357 + 26 CFR В§ 301.6707-1 + В§ 6111 + В§ 6112 + В§ 6707A + В§ 6664(d) + IRM 20.1.13 + Form 8918 + Notice 2015-73 + Notice 2017-10 + Notice 2016-66); **note pins subsection (a) filing requirement**; **note pins subsection (b)(1) flat $50K**; **note pins subsection (b)(2) GREATER of $200K or 50%/75%**; **note pins subsection (c)(1) rescission for OTHER only**; **note pins subsection (c)(2) listed NOT eligible for rescission**; **note pins subsection (c)(3) NO judicial review**; **note pins В§ 6664(d) two-tier reasonable cause**; **note pins 2004 AJCA origin** (KPMG/E&Y/BDO Seidman scandals); **note pins material advisor threshold $50K/$250K**; **note pins Form 8918 quarterly filing**; **note pins listed transaction examples** (Notice 2015-73/2017-10/2016-66); **note pins 26 CFR В§ 301.6707-1 implementing regulations** (T.D. 9683); **note pins IRM 20.1.13**; **transaction category truth table three cells**; **defensive overflow clamped with saturating mul**; **listed penalty uniquely uses percentage invariant** (50% vs 10% disparity).

`traderview-expense::section_6708` is the **IRC В§ 6708 failure to maintain lists of advisees with respect to reportable transactions module** вҖ” direct sibling to `section_6707` (material-advisor failure-to-disclose penalty) and `section_6112` (list-maintenance substantive obligation). Where В§ 6707 penalizes failure to FILE Form 8918 disclosure under В§ 6111, В§ 6708 penalizes failure to MAINTAIN AND PRODUCE the В§ 6112 list of advisees when IRS makes written request. These are TWO INDEPENDENT penalties вҖ” same material advisor can be hit with both for same transaction. Enacted by **American Jobs Creation Act of 2004 В§ 815** (Pub. L. 108-357, October 22, 2004). Trader-critical for material advisors on basket option contracts (Notice 2015-73), conservation easement syndications (Notice 2017-10), micro-captive insurance (Notice 2016-66), В§ 643 distribution-tier-out trusts, STARS foreign-tax-credit shelters.

**В§ 6708(a) Penalty framework вҖ” daily, uncapped, reasonable-cause defense**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 6708(a)(1) | Material advisor required to maintain В§ 6112 list FAILS to make list available within **20 BUSINESS DAYS** of IRS written request вҶ’ **$10,000 PER DAY for each day after 20th day; NO STATUTORY MAXIMUM** |
| В§ 6708(a)(2) | Reasonable cause exception вҖ” distinct from В§ 6664(d); applies regardless of whether transaction is listed or other reportable |

Pinned by `within_20_days_no_penalty`, `at_day_20_no_penalty_boundary`, `at_day_21_one_day_penalty_10k` ($10K = 1M cents), `at_day_30_ten_day_penalty_100k` ($100K = 10M cents), `at_day_120_one_hundred_day_penalty_1m` ($1M = 100M cents), `penalty_uncapped_progressive_invariant` (day 50/100/365/1000 sweep), `reasonable_cause_zeros_penalty`, `reasonable_cause_uniquely_zeros_penalty_invariant`.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6708-1(c)(3)(ii) Extension request** вҖ” material advisor may request extension of 20-business-day period by providing IRS:

1. Reason for the requested extension
2. Period of time required to comply
3. Description of advisor's good-faith effort to comply within original 20-business-day period

IRS may grant or deny extension in discretionary judgment. Pinned by `extension_granted_no_penalty`, `extension_distinct_from_reasonable_cause_invariant`.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6112-1(b) Required list content** (five required elements):

| # | Element |
|---|---------|
| 1 | **Advisee identifiers**: full legal name + current mailing address + TIN |
| 2 | **Transaction identification**: listed or other category + citation OR В§ 6111 reportable transaction number |
| 3 | **Timing**: date the advisee entered the transaction (if known) |
| 4 | **Amount**: amount invested (if known and reasonably determinable) |
| 5 | **Tax treatment**: intended or expected tax treatment (concise schedule or summary) |

Pinned by `missing_content_violation`.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6112-1 List preparation + retention rules**:

| Rule | Source | Requirement |
|------|--------|-------------|
| Preparation timeline | Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6112-1 | **30 CALENDAR DAYS** from date list-maintenance requirement first arises |
| Per-transaction lists | Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6112-1 | Separate list per reportable transaction; ONE list for substantially similar transactions |
| 7-year retention | В§ 6112(b)(2) + Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6112-1 | **7 YEARS** following EARLIER of (1) date material advisor last made tax statement OR (2) date transaction last entered (if known); readily accessible form |

Pinned by `missing_30_day_prep_violation`, `missing_7_year_retention_violation`.

**Coordination with В§ 6707** вҖ” В§ 6707 penalizes failure to FILE Form 8918 disclosure under В§ 6111; В§ 6708 penalizes failure to MAINTAIN AND PRODUCE the В§ 6112 list of advisees. TWO INDEPENDENT PENALTIES вҖ” same material advisor can be hit with both for same transaction.

**Federal Register agency-collection activity (March 4, 2025, 90 Fed. Reg. 11209)** signals continued IRS focus on В§ 6708 enforcement.

Twenty-nine tests pin: **within 20 days no penalty**; **at day 20 no penalty boundary** (exact boundary precision); **at day 21 one-day penalty $10K**; **at day 30 ten-day penalty $100K**; **at day 120 hundred-day penalty $1M**; **reasonable cause zeros penalty**; **extension granted no penalty**; **missing 30-day prep violation**; **missing 7-year retention violation**; **missing content violation**; **list production status truth table four cells** (Provided20/After20/NotProvided/Extension); **penalty uncapped progressive invariant** (day 50/100/365/1000 sweep вҖ” $30K/$80K/$345K/$980K); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6708(a)(1) + В§ 6708(a)(2) + В§ 6112(a)-(b) + 2004 AJCA В§ 815 + Pub. L. 108-357 + 26 CFR В§ 301.6708-1 + В§ 301.6112-1 + В§ 6707 + В§ 6111 + IRM 20.1.13 + Form 8918 + Notice 2015-73/2017-10/2016-66); **note pins subsection (a)(1) 20-day trigger $10K per day uncapped**; **note pins subsection (a)(2) reasonable cause**; **note pins extension request Treas. В§ 301.6708-1(c)(3)(ii)**; **note pins list content five elements**; **note pins 30 calendar day preparation**; **note pins 7-year retention**; **note pins 2004 AJCA origin** (KPMG/E&Y/BDO Seidman); **note pins coordination with В§ 6707** (two independent penalties); **note pins 26 CFR В§ 301.6708-1 implementing regulations** (T.D. 9762); **note pins IRM 20.1.13**; **note pins 2025 Federal Register continued enforcement**; **note pins listed transaction examples**; **defensive overflow clamped with saturating mul**; **multiple failure reasons stack**; **reasonable cause uniquely zeros penalty invariant**; **extension distinct from reasonable cause invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6707a` is the **IRC В§ 6707A reportable transaction penalty module** вҖ” fifth member of the abusive-tax-transaction penalty cluster after В§ 6694 (preparer substantive position), В§ 6695 (preparer procedural failures), В§ 6700 (promoter penalties), В§ 6701 (aiding-and-abetting). В§ 6707A targets the TAXPAYER directly for failure to disclose reportable / listed transactions on their own return вҖ” distinct from В§ 6700 (penalizes the promoter selling the shelter) and В§ 6701 (penalizes the aider preparing the document). Trader-relevant for traders caught in partnership-distributed listed transactions: syndicated conservation easements + В§ 831(b) micro-captive insurance + monetized installment sales + abusive В§ 6011 reportable transactions. Stacks on top of В§ 6662A accuracy-related penalty.

**В§ 6707A(b)(1) base computation** вҖ” penalty = **75%** of the decrease in tax shown on the return as a result of the transaction (or which would have resulted if the transaction were respected). Pinned by `np_listed_75_percent_mid_range` ($40K decrease вҶ’ $30K base = 75%), `base_75_percent_precision_at_decrease_133` (133Вў вҶ’ 99Вў), and `base_75_percent_precision_at_decrease_400` (400Вў вҶ’ 300Вў).

**В§ 6707A(b)(2)+(b)(3) floor + cap matrix** вҖ” penalty bounded by minimum floor and maximum cap:

| Taxpayer type | Transaction | Floor (b)(3) | Cap (b)(2) |
|---------------|-------------|--------------|------------|
| Natural person | Listed | $5,000 | **$100,000** |
| Natural person | Other reportable | $5,000 | $10,000 |
| Entity | Listed | $10,000 | **$200,000** |
| Entity | Other reportable | $10,000 | $50,000 |

Pinned by `floor_amounts_truth_table` (2-cell sweep), `cap_amounts_truth_table_2x2` (4-cell sweep), `zero_decrease_engages_floor_for_all_4_combinations` (4-combination zero-decrease floor sweep), `entity_listed_cap_200k`, `np_listed_cap_engaged_high_decrease` ($1M decrease вҶ’ $750K Г— 75% capped at $100K NP listed), `np_other_cap_10k`, `entity_other_cap_50k`, `listed_natural_person_cap_100k`, `other_reportable_natural_person_cap_10k`, and `floor_or_cap_engagement_3x_regime_sweep` (low / mid / high decrease).

**Listed-vs-other cap differential is 4Г—** вҖ” entity listed cap ($200K) is exactly 4Г— entity other-reportable cap ($50K); natural person listed cap ($100K) is 10Г— natural person other-reportable cap ($10K). Pinned by `listed_cap_higher_than_other_cap_invariant` (entity listed $200K > entity other $50K), `entity_other_reportable_cap_50k_uniquely_different_from_listed` (entity listed 4Г— entity other), and `entity_floor_higher_than_natural_person_invariant` (entity $10K floor > NP $5K floor).

**Boundary tests** вҖ” verify floor/cap transitions at precise input thresholds. Pinned by `np_listed_at_floor_boundary_compliant` ($6,667 decrease вҶ’ 75% = $5K = floor), `np_listed_at_cap_boundary` ($133,333 decrease вҶ’ 75% = $99,999 just under $100K cap), `entity_listed_floor_at_minimum_decrease` ($13,333 decrease вҶ’ 75% = $9,999 < $10K floor), `entity_listed_above_floor` ($15K decrease вҶ’ 75% = $11,250 above $10K floor), and `np_listed_pinned_at_5000_floor_when_decrease_5000`.

**Defensive arithmetic** вҖ” negative decrease clamped to 0 via `.max(0)`; saturating multiplication for `decrease Г— 3 / 4` prevents overflow even at i64::MAX. Pinned by `defensive_negative_decrease_clamped` (-$10K вҶ’ floor engages at $5K), and `defensive_overflow_base_saturating` (i64::MAX decrease вҶ’ cap engages at $200K entity listed).

**CIC Services v. IRS pre-enforcement carve-out** вҖ” 593 U.S. 209 (2021) held that pre-enforcement challenge to IRS micro-captive reporting requirements that trigger В§ 6707A is NOT barred by В§ 7421(a) Anti-Injunction Act. Pinned by `note_pins_cic_services_pre_enforcement`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6707a`. Thirty tests pin: **NP listed 75% mid-range**; **NP listed floor engaged low decrease**; **NP listed cap engaged high decrease**; **entity listed floor $10K**; **entity listed cap $200K**; **NP other cap $10K**; **entity other cap $50K**; **NP listed at floor boundary compliant**; **NP listed at cap boundary**; **entity listed floor at minimum decrease**; **entity listed above floor**; **citation pins subsections** (b)(1) + (b)(2) + (b)(3) + (c)(1) + (c)(2) + 26 CFR В§ 301.6707A-1; **note pins 75% base**; **note pins all 4 floor/cap amounts**; **note pins listed transaction definition**; **note pins CIC Services pre-enforcement**; **defensive negative decrease clamped**; **defensive overflow base saturating** (i64::MAX); **floor amounts truth table** (2-cell sweep); **cap amounts truth table 2x2** (4-cell sweep); **listed cap higher than other cap invariant** (entity listed $200K > entity other $50K); **entity floor higher than natural person invariant** ($10K > $5K); **floor or cap engagement 3x regime sweep** (low/mid/high); **listed natural person cap $100K**; **other reportable natural person cap $10K**; **entity other reportable cap $50K uniquely different from listed** (4Г— ratio); **zero decrease engages floor for all 4 combinations**; **base 75% precision at decrease 133Вў** (вҶ’ 99Вў); **base 75% precision at decrease 400Вў** (вҶ’ 300Вў); **NP listed pinned at $5,000 floor when decrease $5,000**.

`traderview-expense::section_6851` is the **IRC В§ 6851 Termination Assessments of Income Tax** module вҖ” the emergency procedure by which IRS may **TERMINATE a taxpayer's taxable year mid-year** when Secretary finds taxpayer designing to depart from US, conceal property, or jeopardize collection. Trader-procedural-critical because В§ 6851 is the most aggressive form of pre-due-date assessment authority вҖ” used against active traders suspected of fleeing US, hiding offshore assets, or dissipating brokerage accounts before tax due date. Companion to `section_6861` (jeopardy assessment income/estate/gift вҖ” already shipped), В§ 6852 (termination assessment qualified person), `section_6863` (stay of collection вҖ” already shipped), `section_6213` (Tax Court petition window), `section_6321` (lien), `section_6331` (levy), В§ 7429 (review of jeopardy procedures).

**В§ 6851(a)(1) Termination authority** вҖ” if Secretary finds that taxpayer designs quickly to (A) **depart from the United States** or to remove his property therefrom; (B) to **conceal himself or his property** therein; OR (C) **to do any other act** (including in the case of a corporation, distributing all or part of its assets in liquidation or otherwise) tending to prejudice or render wholly or partly ineffectual proceedings to collect the income tax for the current or immediately preceding taxable year unless such proceedings are brought without delay вҖ” the Secretary shall **immediately declare the taxable period for such taxpayer immediately terminated** and shall cause notice of such finding and declaration to be given the taxpayer, together with a demand for immediate payment of the tax.

**В§ 6851(a)(2) Computation of tax** вҖ” the tax for any period terminated under В§ 6851(a) shall be computed (1) as if such period were the taxable year of the taxpayer AND (2) by **placing the entire tax base on an annual basis** (annualize income for the partial year).

**В§ 6851(b) Notice of deficiency** вҖ” **SNOD must be issued within 60 days** after the LATER of (1) the **due date of the return for the full taxable year** (including extensions) OR (2) the **date taxpayer files such return** for the taxable year.

**В§ 6851(c) Treatment of amounts collected** вҖ” any amount collected as a result of any termination assessment shall, on the making of the assessment of the tax for the entire taxable year, be treated as if such amount had been collected on the date such assessment were made.

**В§ 6851(d) Cross references** вҖ” В§ 7429 review (30-day administrative + 90-day judicial); В§ 6863 stay of collection by bond; В§ 6213(a) Tax Court petition right preserved by В§ 6851 60-day SNOD requirement.

**В§ 6851 vs В§ 6861 distinction** вҖ” **В§ 6851** terminates CURRENT or immediately preceding TAXABLE YEAR (income tax only) BEFORE return due date; **В§ 6861** jeopardy-assesses EXISTING DEFICIENCY (income/estate/gift/certain excise) AFTER return filing or SNOD.

**В§ 6851 + В§ 6863 interaction** вҖ” amount of termination assessment must be paid within **10 days** unless a bond is filed under В§ 6863 to stay collection.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6851`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **fully compliant termination authorized**; **no trigger authorization fails**; **departing or removing property trigger authorized** (В§ 6851(a)(1)(A)); **concealing self or property trigger authorized** (В§ 6851(a)(1)(B)); **other jeopardizing act or liquidation trigger authorized** (В§ 6851(a)(1)(C)); **no annualization fails** (В§ 6851(a)(2)(B)); **no Chief Counsel approval fails** (В§ 7429(a)(1)(A)); **SNOD 60-day boundary compliant**; **SNOD 61-day violation** (В§ 6851(b)); **10-day payment boundary compliant**; **11-day payment violation without bond**; **11-day payment with В§ 6863 bond compliant**; **court determined unreasonable engages abatement** (В§ 6863(g)); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6851(a)-(d) + 26 CFR В§ 1.6851-1 + В§ 6852 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6863 + В§ 6213(a) + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 7429 + IRM 4.15.1 + IRM 5.1.4 + IRM 5.17.15); **note pins В§ 6851(a)(1) termination authority** (TERMINATE + depart from US + conceal + any other act); **note pins В§ 6851(a)(1)(C) corporate liquidation scope**; **note pins В§ 6851(a)(2) annualization**; **note pins В§ 6851(b) 60-day SNOD**; **note pins В§ 6851(c) treatment of amounts collected**; **note pins В§ 6851 vs В§ 6861 distinction** (BEFORE return due date vs EXISTING DEFICIENCY); **note pins В§ 6851 + В§ 6863 10-day interaction**; **note pins IRM procedural guidance** (IRM 4.15.1 + IRM 5.1.4 + IRM 5.17.15); **termination trigger truth table 4 cells** (3 triggers + None); **multiple failures stack** (5 violations); **court unreasonable blocks authorization invariant**; **В§ 6863 bond filed overrides 10-day requirement invariant**; **SNOD 60-day boundary invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6861` is the **IRC В§ 6861 Jeopardy Assessments of Income, Estate, Gift, and Certain Excise Taxes** module вҖ” the emergency-collection mechanism by which IRS may bypass normal В§ 6212 SNOD + В§ 6213(a) Tax Court window procedures when Secretary believes assessment or collection of deficiency will be **jeopardized by delay**. Trader-procedural-critical because a В§ 6861 jeopardy assessment triggers IMMEDIATE notice and demand under В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 lien attachment + В§ 6331 levy availability (subject to В§ 7429 30-day administrative + 90-day judicial review and В§ 6863 stay-bond procedures). Companion to `section_6201` (assessment authority), `section_6203` (method of assessment), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_6303` (notice and demand), `section_6321` (lien), `section_6331` (levy), `section_7522` (content of notices).

**В§ 6861(a) Authority** вҖ” if Secretary believes assessment or collection of deficiency will be jeopardized by delay, Secretary shall, notwithstanding В§ 6213(a), **immediately assess** deficiency together with all interest, additional amounts, and additions to tax, and notice and demand shall be made.

**В§ 6861(b) 60-day SNOD requirement** вҖ” if jeopardy assessment made BEFORE any В§ 6212(a) SNOD mailing, Secretary shall **mail SNOD within 60 days** after the assessment; preserves taxpayer Tax Court petition right under В§ 6213(a).

**В§ 6861(c)-(d) Pre/post Tax Court decision limits** вҖ” jeopardy assessment may be made of greater amount than В§ 6212 SNOD deficiency before Tax Court decision becomes final; limited to Tax Court-determined amount after decision.

**В§ 6861(f) Immediate collection** вҖ” when jeopardy assessment unpaid, collection may proceed via В§ 6321 lien and В§ 6331 levy IMMEDIATELY (no 10-day neglect rule); subject to В§ 6863 stay-bond procedures.

**В§ 6861(g) Abatement** вҖ” if Tax Court determines deficiency less than jeopardy assessment, amount assessed in excess shall be **ABATED**.

**В§ 7429 review procedures (cross-referenced)** вҖ” В§ 7429(a)(1)(A) **Chief Counsel for IRS personal written approval REQUIRED** before В§ 6861 jeopardy assessment or В§ 6331 jeopardy levy (no delegation below Chief Counsel-level office); В§ 7429(a)(1)(B) **written statement of information relied upon within 5 days**; В§ 7429(a)(2) **30-day administrative review window** for taxpayer; В§ 7429(b)(1) **90-day judicial review in district court** after earlier of (i) Secretary notification of administrative review determination OR (ii) 16th day after administrative review request; В§ 7429(g)(1) **burden of proof on SECRETARY** for reasonableness of jeopardy assessment; В§ 7429(g)(2) **burden of proof on TAXPAYER** for amount appropriateness вҖ” burden split between procedural reasonableness (Secretary) and substantive amount (taxpayer).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6861`. Thirty-three tests pin: **fully compliant jeopardy assessment authorized**; **no jeopardy trigger authorization fails**; **departing or concealing trigger authorized**; **dissipating assets trigger authorized**; **financial solvency imperiled trigger authorized**; **Chief Counsel approval missing fails** (В§ 7429(a)(1)(A)); **SNOD 60-day boundary compliant**; **SNOD 61-day violation** (В§ 6861(b)); **SNOD already mailed no 60-day check**; **written statement 5-day boundary compliant**; **written statement 6-day violation** (В§ 7429(a)(1)(B)); **taxpayer review request 30-day boundary timely**; **taxpayer review request 31 days untimely**; **judicial review 90-day boundary timely**; **judicial review 91-day violation** (В§ 7429(b)(1)); **no judicial review no timeliness check**; **Secretary sustained reasonableness burden pinned** (В§ 7429(g)(1)); **taxpayer sustained amount burden inverts appropriateness** (В§ 7429(g)(2)); **Tax Court no deficiency engages abatement** (В§ 6861(g)); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6861(a)-(g) + 26 CFR В§ 301.6861-1 + В§ 7429 + 26 CFR В§ 301.7429-2 + В§ 6212 + В§ 6213 + В§ 6303 + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6863 + В§ 7522 + IRM 4.15.1 + IRM 5.17.15); **note pins В§ 6861(a) emergency authority**; **note pins В§ 6861(b) 60-day SNOD rule** (В§ 6212(a) + В§ 6213(a) preservation); **note pins В§ 6861(f) immediate lien and levy** (В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 6863); **note pins В§ 6861(g) abatement** (ABATED); **note pins В§ 7429(a)(1)(A) Chief Counsel approval**; **note pins В§ 7429(a)(1)(B) 5-day written statement**; **note pins В§ 7429(a)(2) 30-day administrative review**; **note pins В§ 7429(b)(1) 90-day judicial review**; **note pins burden split В§ 7429(g)(1) Secretary В§ 7429(g)(2) taxpayer**; **jeopardy trigger truth table** (4-cell sweep вҖ” 3 triggers + None); **multiple failures stack** (4 violations); **assessment-before-SNOD 60-day check only when engaged invariant**; **Tax Court no deficiency blocks authorization invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6862` is the **IRC В§ 6862 Jeopardy Assessment of Taxes Other Than Income, Estate, Gift, and Certain Excise Taxes** module вҖ” completes the jeopardy/termination cluster (В§ 6851 income tax termination + В§ 6861 income/estate/gift jeopardy + В§ 6862 other-tax jeopardy + В§ 6863 stay of collection + В§ 7429 review). Trader-procedural-critical for traders operating businesses with employment tax obligations (В§ 3402 income tax withholding + В§ 3111 FICA) and excise tax obligations (alcohol В§ 5001 + tobacco В§ 5701 + fuel В§ 4081 + manufacturer В§ 4221 + communications В§ 4251 + air transportation В§ 4261) when Secretary believes collection of such taxes will be jeopardized by delay. Companion to `section_6861` (already shipped), `section_6851` (already shipped), `section_6863` (already shipped), `section_6213`, `section_6321`, `section_6331`, В§ 7429.

**В§ 6862(a) Authority** вҖ” if Secretary believes that collection of any tax (OTHER than income tax, estate tax, gift tax, and the excise taxes imposed by chapters **41, 42, 43, and 44**) under any provision of the internal revenue laws will be jeopardized by delay, Secretary shall, **whether or not the time otherwise prescribed by law for making return and paying such tax has expired**, immediately assess such tax (together with all interest, additional amounts, and additions to the tax). Such tax shall thereupon become **IMMEDIATELY DUE AND PAYABLE**, and immediate notice and demand shall be made.

**В§ 6862(b) Immediate levy** вҖ” collection by levy under В§ 6331(a) is authorized **without regard to the 10-day notice requirement** that would otherwise apply after notice and demand.

**Chapter 41-44 carve-outs (covered by В§ 6861, NOT В§ 6862)** вҖ” Chapter 41 (Public Charities) + Chapter 42 (Private Foundations В§В§ 4940-4948) + Chapter 43 (Qualified Plans В§В§ 4971-4980) + Chapter 44 (REITs В§В§ 4981-4982).

**В§ 6862 in-scope taxes** вҖ” **Employment** (В§ 3402 income tax withholding + В§ 3111 employer FICA/Medicare + В§ 3301 FUTA + В§ 3406 backup withholding) + **Excise non-chapter-41-44** (alcohol В§ 5001 + tobacco В§ 5701 + fuel В§ 4081 + manufacturer В§ 4221 + communications В§ 4251 + air transportation В§ 4261) + **Foreign withholding** (В§В§ 1441-1446 chapters 3 and 4 FATCA).

**В§ 7429 review procedures (cross-referenced)** вҖ” В§ 7429(a)(1)(A) Chief Counsel personal written approval REQUIRED; В§ 7429(a)(1)(B) **5-day written statement** stating **SPECIFIC FACTS AND REASONS** (not mere conclusions) вҖ” conclusion-only notice may invalidate assessment; В§ 7429(a)(2) 30-day administrative review; В§ 7429(b)(1) 90-day judicial review in district court; В§ 7429(g)(1) burden on Secretary for reasonableness; В§ 7429(g)(2) burden on taxpayer for amount appropriateness.

**В§ 6862 vs В§ 6861 distinction** вҖ” В§ 6862 applies to employment + excise (non-chapter-41-44) + foreign withholding taxes; **no 60-day SNOD requirement** (no SNOD applies to most non-deficiency taxes); В§ 6861 applies to income/estate/gift + chapter-41-44 excise taxes with В§ 6212 SNOD framework.

**В§ 6862 + В§ 6863(b)(3)(A) interaction** вҖ” sale-prohibition on seized property pending В§ 7429(b) civil action **SPECIFICALLY applies to В§ 6862(a)** (not В§ 6861); В§ 6862 jeopardy assessment may be stayed via В§ 6863 bond filing.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6862`. Thirty-three tests pin: **employment tax in scope authorized**; **excise non-chapter-4144 in scope authorized**; **foreign withholding in scope authorized**; **income tax out of В§ 6862 scope** (В§ 6861 applies); **estate tax out of scope**; **gift tax out of scope**; **chapter 41 excise out of scope** (private foundation); **chapter 42 excise out of scope** (В§В§ 4940-4948); **chapter 43 excise out of scope** (В§В§ 4971-4980); **chapter 44 excise out of scope** (В§В§ 4981-4982); **no Secretary belief fails** (В§ 6862(a) JEOPARDIZED BY DELAY); **no Chief Counsel approval fails** (В§ 7429(a)(1)(A)); **no written statement fails**; **conclusion-only statement fails** (В§ 7429(a)(1)(B) SPECIFIC FACTS); **due date not expired still authorized** (В§ 6862(a) whether-or-not clause); **immediate levy authorized when compliant** (В§ 6862(b)); **immediate levy blocked when not authorized**; **В§ 6863 bond filed engages stay**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6862(a)-(b) + 26 CFR В§ 301.6862-1 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6851 + В§ 6863 + В§ 6213(a) + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 7429 + IRM 4.15.1 + IRM 5.17.15); **note pins В§ 6862(a) immediate assessment authority** (whether-or-not due date); **note pins В§ 6862(a) immediately due and payable**; **note pins В§ 6862(b) immediate levy** (В§ 6331(a) + 10-day notice); **note pins chapter 41-44 carveouts**; **note pins in-scope taxes employment + excise + foreign** (В§ 3402 + В§ 3111 FICA + В§ 3301 FUTA + В§ 5001 + FATCA chapter 4); **note pins В§ 7429(a)(1)(A) Chief Counsel approval**; **note pins В§ 7429(a)(1)(B) 5-day SPECIFIC FACTS**; **note pins 30-day + 90-day review windows**; **note pins В§ 6862 vs В§ 6861 distinction** (60-day SNOD + В§ 6212 SNOD framework); **note pins В§ 6862 + В§ 6863(b)(3)(A) sale prohibition** (SPECIFICALLY applies to В§ 6862(a)); **tax category truth table 10 cells** (3 in scope + 7 out of scope); **multiple failures stack** (4 violations); **employment tax due date not expired still authorized invariant**; **chapter 41-44 uniquely handled by В§ 6861 invariant** (4-chapter sweep).

`traderview-expense::section_6863` is the **IRC В§ 6863 Stay of Collection of Jeopardy Assessments** module вҖ” the procedural pressure-relief valve when В§ 6861 (income/estate/gift jeopardy assessment), В§ 6862 (other-tax jeopardy assessment), В§ 6851 (termination assessment of income tax), or В§ 6852 (termination assessment in cases of qualified person) has been imposed. Trader-procedural-critical because В§ 6863 permits the trader-taxpayer to STAY immediate В§ 6321 lien attachment and В§ 6331 levy seizure by filing a bond in amount of stayed assessment plus interest. Companion to `section_6861` (jeopardy assessment income/estate/gift вҖ” already shipped), В§ 6862, В§ 6851, В§ 6852 (underlying assessments), `section_6213` (Tax Court petition window), `section_6321` (lien), `section_6331` (levy), `section_7429` (review of jeopardy procedures).

**В§ 6863(a) Bond to stay collection** вҖ” when an assessment has been made under В§ 6851, В§ 6852, В§ 6861, or В§ 6862, the collection of the whole or any amount of such assessment may be stayed by **filing with the Secretary, within such time as may be fixed by regulations, a bond in an amount equal to the amount as to which the stay is desired (not exceeding amount of jeopardy assessment plus interest)**, conditioned upon the payment of the amount at the time at which, but for the making of such assessment, such amount would be due. Upon filing the bond, the collection of so much of the amount assessed as is covered by the bond shall be **STAYED**.

**В§ 6863(b)(1) Bond filed before В§ 6213(a) petition** вҖ” if the bond is given **before the taxpayer has filed В§ 6213(a) Tax Court petition**, the bond shall contain a further condition that if a petition is not filed within В§ 6213(a) 90-day window (or 150 days outside US), then the amount, the collection of which is stayed by the bond, will be paid on notice and demand at any time after the expiration of such period, together with interest from the date of the jeopardy notice and demand.

**В§ 6863(b)(2) Bond conditioned on Tax Court decision** вҖ” the bond shall be conditioned upon the payment of so much of such assessment as is **not abated by a decision of the Tax Court which has become final**. If the Tax Court determines that the amount assessed is greater than the amount which should have been assessed, then when the decision of the Tax Court is rendered the bond shall, at the request of the taxpayer, be **proportionately reduced**.

**В§ 6863(b)(3)(A) Stay of sale of seized property pending Tax Court decision** вҖ” except as provided in В§ 6863(b)(3)(B), where a jeopardy assessment has been made under **В§ 6862(a)**, the property seized for the collection of the tax **shall NOT be sold** if a civil action under В§ 7429(b) has been commenced on or before the day on which the district court judgment in such action becomes final.

**В§ 6863(b)(3)(B) Sale-prohibition exceptions** вҖ” three carve-outs: (i) taxpayer consents to sale; (ii) Secretary determines expenses of conservation and maintenance would greatly reduce net proceeds; OR (iii) property is perishable.

**В§ 6863(g) Abatement if jeopardy does not exist** вҖ” if the appropriate court determines that the making of the assessment was unreasonable OR that the amount assessed or demanded was inappropriate, then such court may order the Secretary to **ABATE** such assessment, redetermine the amount of such assessment, or take such other action as the court finds appropriate.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6863`. Thirty tests pin: **bond at cap compliant stay engaged**; **bond under cap compliant stay engaged partial**; **bond above cap violation** (В§ 6863(a)); **bond not filed within time violation** (В§ 301.6863-1); **pre-petition condition engaged when filed before petition**; **pre-petition condition petition missed payable on demand** (В§ 6863(b)(1)); **Tax Court reduction available with full chain** (final decision + less determination + taxpayer request); **Tax Court reduction unavailable without taxpayer request**; **Tax Court reduction unavailable without less determination**; **sale prohibition engaged for В§ 6862 pending В§ 7429** (В§ 6863(b)(3)(A)); **sale exception taxpayer consent disengages prohibition** (В§ 6863(b)(3)(B)(i)); **sale exception excessive conservation costs disengages prohibition** (В§ 6863(b)(3)(B)(ii)); **sale exception perishable property disengages prohibition** (В§ 6863(b)(3)(B)(iii)); **sale prohibition not engaged for non-В§ 6862 assessment**; **court unreasonable or inappropriate determination engages abatement** (В§ 6863(g)); **underlying assessment truth table 4 cells** (В§ 6851 + В§ 6852 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6862); **sale exception truth table 4 cells**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 6863(a)-(g) + 26 CFR В§ 301.6863-1 + В§ 6851 + В§ 6852 + В§ 6861 + В§ 6862 + В§ 6213(a) + В§ 6321 + В§ 6331 + В§ 7429 + IRM 5.17.15 + IRM 5.1.4); **note pins В§ 6863(a) bond to stay collection**; **note pins В§ 6863(b)(1) pre-petition condition** (90-day + 150 days outside US); **note pins В§ 6863(b)(2) proportional reduction** (PROPORTIONATELY REDUCED); **note pins В§ 6863(b)(3)(A) sale prohibition** (SHALL NOT BE SOLD + В§ 7429(b)); **note pins В§ 6863(b)(3)(B) three sale exceptions** (taxpayer consents + conservation/maintenance + perishable); **note pins В§ 6863(g) abatement** (ABATE); **note pins cross-references to four underlying assessments** (В§ 6861 + В§ 6862 + В§ 6851 + В§ 6852); **multiple failures stack** (3 violations); **bond amount cap invariant** (100M ok, 100M+1 violation); **sale prohibition only for В§ 6862 invariant** (3-other-underlying-sweep); **defensive zero bond no stay**; **defensive u64::MAX jeopardy amount**.

`traderview-expense::section_1374` is the **IRC В§1374 S-corporation built-in gains (BIG) tax module** вҖ” the integrity tax that prevents a C-corp from escaping corporate-level tax on pre-conversion appreciation by simply electing S-corp status. When a C-corp converts, its built-in gains remain exposed to corporate-level tax for a **5-year recognition period** under В§ 1374(d)(7), permanently set by the **PATH Act of 2015** (down from 10 years originally, then 7 years, then 5). The tax rate is the highest В§11(b) corporate rate вҖ” **21% post-TCJA** вҖ” applied to net recognized built-in gain ([Beancount.io В§1374 guide](https://beancount.io/blog/2026/05/10/section-1374-built-in-gains-tax-c-corp-s-corp-conversion-five-year-recognition-period-guide), [Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 1374](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1374)).

**NUBIG (Net Unrealized Built-In Gain) at conversion** is the LIFETIME ceiling on what can ever be taxed under В§1374:

```text
NUBIG = ОЈ(FMV вҲ’ adjusted basis) at conversion
      вҲ’ liabilities and deductible items at conversion
```

If NUBIG is $2M at conversion, the cumulative В§1374 tax base over the entire 5-year recognition period can never exceed $2M вҖ” no matter how much gain is recognized in any single year. Pinned by `nubig_ceiling_binds_when_prior_years_exhausted_most_of_it`.

**NRBIG (Net Recognized Built-In Gain) for each year** = LESSER OF three limits per В§ 1374(d)(2):

1. **Recognized BIG limit**: (gross recognized built-in gain вҲ’ recognized built-in loss) for the year
2. **Taxable income limit** (В§ 1374(d)(2)(A)(ii)): the corporation's taxable income computed as if it were a C-corp, per В§ 1375(b)(1)(B)
3. **NUBIG ceiling**: conversion-date NUBIG MINUS cumulative prior-year NRBIG

When the **taxable income limit binds**, the excess recognized BIG **carries forward** within the recognition period under В§ 1374(d)(2)(B) and is treated as recognized BIG in subsequent years вҖ” surfaced via `nrbig_carryforward_for_subsequent_year` so callers can chain across the 5 years. This creates a planning incentive to bunch deductions into years when BIG is recognized: "if the corporation's taxable income can be reduced to zero, no Built-In-Gains Tax will be imposed" ([Foster Garvey вҖ” Subchapter S Part 1](https://www.foster.com/larry-s-tax-law/subchapter-s-part-1-the-built-in-gains-tax)).

**Two C-corp tax-attribute carryovers reduce BIG tax**:
- **В§ 1374(b)(2) C-corp NOL deduction** вҖ” pre-conversion NOL carryforwards deductible against NRBIG; pinned by `c_corp_nol_reduces_taxable_nrbig` + `c_corp_nol_exceeds_nrbig_clamps_to_zero_tax`.
- **В§ 1374(b)(3) credit offset** вҖ” pre-conversion general business credits, minimum tax credits, foreign tax credits reduce the BIG tax LIABILITY dollar-for-dollar (after rate applied); pinned by `credit_offset_reduces_tax_dollar_for_dollar` + `credit_offset_exceeds_tax_clamps_to_zero`.

**Outside the 5-year window**: BIG tax exposure ends. Returned via `in_recognition_period: false` + `binding_limit: OutsideRecognitionPeriod`. Pinned by `outside_5_year_recognition_period_no_tax`; boundary case `year_5_exact_boundary_still_inside` pins that year 5 (index 4 of the 5-year window) is still INSIDE.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1374`. Twenty-two tests pin: **baseline recognized-BIG binds** (rec $1M < TI $2M < NUBIG $5M вҶ’ $1M NRBIG Г— 21% = $210k tax); **TI limit binds with $2.5M carryforward** (rec $3M > TI $500k вҶ’ $500k NRBIG, $2.5M carries); **NUBIG ceiling binds** when prior NRBIG $4.5M exhausts the $5M cap; outside-5-year-period zero tax + zero NRBIG + OutsideRecognitionPeriod binding; year-5-exact-boundary still inside; recognized BIL offsets BIG ($1M вҲ’ $400k = $600k net); **C-corp NOL reduces taxable NRBIG** (NOL $400k вҶ’ $600k taxable вҶ’ $126k tax); NOL exceeds NRBIG clamps tax to zero; **credit offset reduces tax dollar-for-dollar** ($50k credit вҶ’ $160k after-credits); credit exceeds tax clamps to zero; NRBIG carryforward from prior year stacks with this year's gross; zero NUBIG no exposure (ceiling binds at 0); negative recognized BIG (BIL > BIG) clamps to zero; **pre-TCJA 35% rate path** ($1M Г— 35% = $350k); zero TI no tax full carryforward; NUBIG ceiling remaining clamps at zero when prior NRBIG exceeds NUBIG; note describes recognized-BIG binding; note describes TI binding with carryforward; note outside-period describes no exposure; very large NUBIG precision path ($500M / $100M / $200M / 21% вҶ’ $21M tax); **all-three-limits-tie binding precedence** (RecognizedBigLimit wins ties); NOL does not create negative tax.

`traderview-expense::section_1031` is the **IRC В§ 1031 like-kind exchanges of real property module** (iter 604) вҖ” trader-landlord-critical nonrecognition provision companion to `section_1031_f` (related-party exchanges 2-year holding), `section_1033` (involuntary conversion), `section_1041` (transfers between spouses), `section_1042` (qualified employee stock sale), `section_1045` (qualified small business stock rollover), `section_168` (iter 594 вҖ” MACRS depreciation of replacement property basis), `section_1245` (depreciation recapture interaction). Substantially modified by the **Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 (TCJA, Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13303)** which limited В§ 1031 to **REAL PROPERTY ONLY** вҖ” eliminating like-kind exchange treatment for personal property (machinery, vehicles, art, livestock, intangibles) for exchanges completed after December 31, 2017. **В§ 1031(a)(1) general nonrecognition rule**: no gain or loss shall be recognized on the exchange of real property held for productive use in a trade or business or for investment, if such real property is exchanged solely for real property of like kind which is to be held either for productive use in a trade or business or for investment. **В§ 1031(a)(2) post-TCJA exclusions**: stock in trade or inventory; stocks, bonds, or notes; other securities or indebtedness; partnership interests; certificates of trust or beneficial interests; choses in action; real property in a foreign country (post-1989); and PERSONAL PROPERTY (TCJA 2017 addition). **В§ 1031(a)(3) deferred exchange identification rules**: **45-day identification period** вҖ” replacement property must be identified in writing to qualified intermediary within 45 days of transfer of relinquished property; **180-day exchange period** вҖ” replacement property must be received within 180 days of transfer of relinquished property OR by due date (including extensions) of tax return for year of transfer, whichever is EARLIER. **Identification rules** under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1031(k)-1(c)(4): **3-property rule** вҖ” identify up to 3 properties of any value; **200% rule** вҖ” identify any number of properties as long as aggregate FMV does not exceed 200% of aggregate FMV of relinquished properties; **95% rule** вҖ” identify any number if 95% of identified properties (by FMV) are actually acquired. **В§ 1031(b) gain recognition on boot**: gain is recognized to the extent of "boot" received вҖ” money or non-like-kind property (including debt relief). **В§ 1031(c)**: loss is NEVER recognized in a like-kind exchange. **В§ 1031(d) basis in replacement property** = adjusted basis of relinquished property + gain recognized + boot paid вҲ’ boot received. **Qualified Intermediary (QI)** requirement under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1031(k)-1(g)(4): QI cannot be the taxpayer, employee, attorney, accountant, or close relative; must hold proceeds of relinquished property sale during exchange period to prevent "constructive receipt" by taxpayer. **T.D. 9935** (November 2020) вҖ” final regulations defining "real property" post-TCJA as land + improvements to land + unsevered natural products of land + water and air space superjacent to land + state-law real property + inherently permanent structures. **Incidental personal property test** under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1031(k)-1(c)(5): personal property incidental to real property exchange not disqualifying if aggregate FMV вүӨ **15%** of replacement property aggregate FMV. **Thirteen-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantSimultaneousLikeKindExchange, CompliantDeferredExchangeWith45And180DayCompliance, CompliantBootReceivedPartialGainRecognized, ViolationPersonalPropertyExcludedPostTCJA, ViolationStocksBondsNotesPartnershipInterestExcluded, ViolationInventoryStockInTradeExcluded, ViolationForeignRealPropertyExcluded, Violation45DayIdentificationDeadlineMissed, Violation180DayExchangePeriodMissed, ViolationQualifiedIntermediaryNotIndependent, ViolationIdentificationExceeds3PropertyAnd200PctRulesAndUnder95Pct, ViolationIncidentalPersonalPropertyExceeds15Pct. **Seven property types**: RealPropertyDomesticBusinessOrInvestment, PersonalProperty, InventoryOrStockInTrade, StocksBondsNotes, PartnershipInterest, CertificateOfTrustOrChoseInAction, ForeignRealProperty. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1031`. **Twenty-seven tests pin**: **deferred exchange 45/180 day compliant** (baseline: $50M gain deferred, $20M basis carries to replacement), **personal property post-TCJA excluded**, **stocks bonds notes excluded**, **partnership interest excluded**, **inventory excluded**, **foreign real property excluded**, **46-day identification violation** (boundary), **identification at exactly 45 days compliant** (statutory вүӨ reading), **181-day exchange violation**, **exchange at exactly 180 days compliant** (statutory вүӨ reading), **qualified intermediary not independent violation**, **3-property rule within 3 compliant** (5 properties at 500% FMV, but 3 вүӨ 3-property rule), **200% rule within 200 compliant** (10 properties at exactly 200% FMV), **95% rule acquired compliant** (10 properties at 500% FMV but 95% actually acquired), **all three identification rules fail violation** (10 properties + 500% FMV + 50% acquired), **incidental personal property exceeds 15% violation** (16% > 15% threshold), **incidental at exactly 15% compliant** (boundary), **boot received partial gain recognized** ($5M boot, $5M recognized, $45M deferred), **boot capped at realized gain** ($100M boot capped at $50M realized gain), **citations pin В§ 1031 subsections** ((a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(3)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(f)), **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1031(k)-1 + (c)(4)/(g)(4) + T.D. 9935 + TCJA 2017**, **constant pin 45-day identification**, **constant pin 180-day exchange**, **constant pin 3-property rule max**, **constant pin 200% rule**, **constant pin 95% acquired rule**, **constant pin 15% incidental**. Trader-landlord critical because В§ 1031 is the foundational tax-deferral provision for real estate investors вҖ” every commercial and investment real estate transaction in the United States contemplates В§ 1031 treatment, and the strict 45-day identification + 180-day exchange deadlines drive entire workflow timing in real estate transactions. The TCJA 2017 restriction to real property only eliminated like-kind treatment for trader-relevant personal property (vehicles, equipment) but preserved the foundational real estate deferral mechanism. The qualified intermediary safe harbor is the most-litigated procedural requirement вҖ” any taxpayer relationship to the QI risks constructive receipt and complete deferral disqualification. The 3-property + 200% + 95% identification rule trio under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1031(k)-1(c)(4) is the operative practical-application framework вҖ” most taxpayers satisfy 3-property rule; sophisticated investors using 200% rule must monitor aggregate FMV. Sibling cluster: `section_1031_f` (related-party 2-year holding вҖ” anti-abuse), `section_1033` (involuntary conversion вҖ” separate nonrecognition for casualty/condemnation), `section_1041` (spousal transfers вҖ” nonrecognition), `section_1042` (qualified employee stock sale), `section_1045` (qualified small business stock rollover), `section_168` (iter 594 вҖ” MACRS depreciation of basis carried to replacement property), `section_1245` (depreciation recapture вҖ” interacts with В§ 1031 to recharacterize portion of gain as ordinary), `section_267` (related-party loss disallowance вҖ” adjacent anti-abuse), `section_280B` (demolition вҖ” interacts with В§ 1031 basis calculations), `section_263` (iter 602 вҖ” capitalization of improvements to replacement property).

`traderview-expense::section_263` is the **IRC В§ 263 capital expenditures general rule module** (iter 602) вҖ” foundational capitalization companion to `section_263a` (UNICAP inventory capitalization), `section_263g` (interest + carrying charges capitalization), `section_162` (ordinary & necessary business expense вҖ” the current-deduction alternative), `section_168` (iter 594 MACRS depreciation of capitalized amounts), `section_461h` (iter 598 economic performance timing вҖ” interacts with В§ 263 capitalization). В§ 263(a) is the foundational capitalization rule that requires capitalization (rather than current deduction) of amounts paid for permanent improvements, betterments, and restorations. The 2013 Tangible Property Regulations (T.D. 9636) finalized the operative test framework вҖ” the **"BAR test"** вҖ” and three safe harbors that distinguish currently deductible repairs from capitalizable improvements. **В§ 263(a)(1) general rule**: no deduction shall be allowed for (A) any amount paid out for new buildings or for permanent improvements or betterments made to increase the value of any property or estate, or (B) any amount expended in restoring property or in making good the exhaustion thereof for which an allowance is or has been made. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.263(a)-3 BAR test вҖ” three-prong improvement classification**: **B = Betterment** (В§ 1.263(a)-3(j)) вҖ” material increase in capacity, productivity, efficiency, strength, or quality of property; correction of material condition or defect existing at acquisition; material addition or enlargement; **A = Adaptation** (В§ 1.263(a)-3(l)) вҖ” conversion to a new or different use inconsistent with original purpose (e.g., residential apartments вҶ’ commercial office space); **R = Restoration** (В§ 1.263(a)-3(k)) вҖ” return to operating condition after major disrepair, replacement of major structural component or substantial structural part, rebuild to like-new condition after class life expiration. **В§ 1.263(a)-1(f) de minimis safe harbor**: taxpayer may elect to expense items under threshold without applying BAR test вҖ” **$5,000 per invoice or item with Applicable Financial Statement (AFS)**; **$2,500 per invoice or item without AFS** (increased from $500 effective 2016 by Notice 2015-82); election made annually on Form 3115 or attached statement. **В§ 1.263(a)-3(h) small taxpayer safe harbor**: qualifying taxpayer (average annual gross receipts вүӨ $10M) may elect on eligible building (unadjusted basis вүӨ $1,000,000) to deduct all aggregate annual repairs/improvements up to the LESSER of (i) **2% of unadjusted basis**, or (ii) **$10,000**. **В§ 1.263(a)-3(i) routine maintenance safe harbor**: amounts paid for recurring activities expected to be performed more than once during the property's class life are deemed not to improve the property вҖ” **10 years for buildings**, **3 years (or class life) for non-buildings**. **Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantCurrentDeductionRepairOrMaintenance, CompliantCapitalizationOfImprovementUnderBarTest, CompliantDeMinimisSafeHarborAfs5000, CompliantDeMinimisSafeHarborNoAfs2500, CompliantSmallTaxpayerSafeHarbor10000Or2Pct, CompliantRoutineMaintenanceSafeHarbor10YearBuilding, CompliantRoutineMaintenanceSafeHarbor3YearNonBuilding, ViolationImprovementDeductedAsRepairExpense, ViolationFailedToCapitalizeRestorationOrAdaptation, ViolationDeMinimisExceededThresholdImproperlyClaimed. **Six safe-harbor-applied states**: None, DeMinimisAfs5000, DeMinimisNoAfs2500, SmallTaxpayerSafeHarbor10000Or2Pct, RoutineMaintenanceSafeHarbor10YearBuilding, RoutineMaintenanceSafeHarbor3YearNonBuilding. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-263`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **repair no BAR currently deductible** (no betterment/adaptation/restoration), **betterment taxpayer capitalized compliant**, **betterment deducted as repair violation**, **adaptation deducted violation**, **restoration deducted violation**, **de minimis safe harbor AFS $5,000 compliant**, **de minimis safe harbor no AFS $2,500 compliant**, **de minimis AFS exceeds $5,000 violation** ($6,000 over threshold), **small taxpayer safe harbor 2% under $10,000 compliant** (2% Г— $500K = $10K limit; $8K aggregate вүӨ $10K limit), **small taxpayer safe harbor aggregate exceeds lesser limit not eligible** ($15K aggregate > $10K limit), **routine maintenance building 10-year compliant**, **routine maintenance non-building 3-year compliant**, **citations pin В§ 263(a) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.263(a)-1 + 1.263(a)-2 + 1.263(a)-3 + (h)/(i)/(j)/(k)/(l)**, **citations pin T.D. 9636 + Notice 2015-82**, **citations pin В§ 162 + В§ 168 cross-refs**, **constant pin $5,000 AFS de minimis**, **constant pin $2,500 no-AFS de minimis**, **constant pin $10,000 small taxpayer dollar limit**, **constant pin 2% basis threshold**, **constant pin 10-year building routine maintenance**, **constant pin 3-year non-building routine maintenance**. Trader-landlord critical because В§ 263 governs every capital expenditure decision and the de minimis safe harbor is the single most-used election in real estate tax practice вҖ” landlords with AFS routinely expense up to $5,000 per item under В§ 1.263(a)-1(f), avoiding capitalization-and-depreciate cycles for routine equipment purchases (appliances, fixtures, minor improvements). The small taxpayer safe harbor under В§ 1.263(a)-3(h) is the highest-leverage election for trader-landlords with under-$1M-basis buildings вҖ” it allows expense treatment for ALL repairs/improvements aggregated up to $10K annually, eliminating the BAR test analysis entirely for qualifying properties. The routine maintenance safe harbor under В§ 1.263(a)-3(i) is the most-litigated of the three safe harbors вҖ” taxpayers claim 10-year-routine-maintenance treatment for activities that IRS often challenges as betterments. Sibling cluster: `section_263a` (UNICAP вҖ” inventory capitalization regime distinct from В§ 263 tangible property capitalization), `section_263g` (interest and carrying charges capitalization on straddles), `section_162` (ordinary and necessary business expense вҖ” current-deduction alternative when BAR test not triggered), `section_168` (iter 594 вҖ” MACRS depreciation of В§ 263-capitalized amounts), `section_167` (general depreciation predecessor), `section_197` (intangibles 15-year amortization вҖ” analogous capitalization for intangibles), `section_461h` (iter 598 вҖ” economic performance timing applies to В§ 263 capitalization decisions), `section_481` (accounting-method-change adjustments вҖ” Form 3115 used for В§ 263 method changes), `section_446` (iter 596 вҖ” methods of accounting general rule), `section_280B` (demolition of structures вҖ” special capitalization rule for demolition costs).

`traderview-expense::section_723` is the **IRC В§ 723 basis of property contributed to partnership module** (iter 600 вҖ” milestone) вҖ” foundational Subchapter K inside-basis companion to `section_721` (contribution nonrecognition rule), `section_722` (contributing partner's outside basis in interest received), `section_732` (iter 588 вҖ” distributee basis downstream from В§ 723), `section_704c` (built-in gain allocation that depends on В§ 723 carryover basis), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment), `section_734` (iter 578 вҖ” distributee basis adjustment), `section_754` (basis adjustment election). **В§ 723 operative rule**: "The basis of property contributed to a partnership by a partner shall be the adjusted basis of such property to the contributing partner at the time of the contribution increased by the amount (if any) of gain recognized under section 721(b) to the contributing partner at such time." In plain terms: partnership takes a **carryover basis** from the contributing partner, PLUS any gain recognized under В§ 721(b) (the investment-company exception). **В§ 721 contribution nonrecognition framework**: **В§ 721(a) general rule** вҖ” no gain or loss is recognized on the transfer of property to a partnership in exchange for a partnership interest; **В§ 721(b) investment-company exception** вҖ” В§ 721(a) does NOT apply if the partnership would be treated as an investment company under В§ 351(e)(1) (more than 80% of partnership assets are held for investment in stocks/securities). In that case, the contributing partner recognizes gain to the extent the FMV of property contributed exceeds basis (no loss recognition). **В§ 722 paired outside-basis rule**: contributing partner's outside basis in the partnership interest received equals the adjusted basis of the contributed property PLUS В§ 721(b) gain recognized. Outside basis and inside basis under В§ 723 are thus equal at the moment of contribution вҖ” the inside-outside basis equality default. **В§ 704(c) built-in gain preservation**: when contributed property has built-in gain or loss at the time of contribution (i.e., FMV вү  В§ 723 carryover basis), В§ 704(c) requires that the pre-contribution gain or loss be allocated to the contributing partner upon partnership disposition. This prevents tax consequences from shifting to non-contributing partners вҖ” the foundational В§ 704(c) anti-shifting rule that the entire Subchapter K basis-tracking apparatus depends on. **В§ 1223(2) holding-period tacking**: partnership's holding period in contributed property includes the contributing partner's holding period (tacking rule). Critical for long-term vs. short-term capital character on subsequent partnership disposition. **Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantCarryoverBasisGeneralRule, CompliantCarryoverBasisPlusSection721bGainRecognized, ViolationStepUpBasisToFmvWithoutSection721bGain, ViolationBasisAdjustedDownwardWithoutAuthority, ViolationMissingHoldingPeriodTacking. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-723`. **Fifteen tests pin**: **carryover basis general rule compliant** ($5M contributing partner basis вҶ’ $5M partnership inside basis, $8M FMV вҶ’ $3M В§ 704(c) built-in gain), **carryover plus В§ 721(b) gain compliant** (investment-company case: $5M basis + $3M В§ 721(b) gain = $8M inside basis), **step-up to FMV without В§ 721(b) violation** (taxpayer recorded $8M without authority), **step-down without authority violation** (taxpayer recorded $3M without authority), **holding period not tacked violation** (В§ 1223(2)), **short-term holding period under 12 months**, **long-term holding at exactly 12 months** (statutory вүҘ reading), **built-in loss when FMV below basis** (-$2M loss), **zero built-in gain when FMV equals basis**, **citations pin В§ 723 + В§ 721(a)/(b) + В§ 722 + В§ 704(c)**, **citations pin В§ 351(e)(1) + В§ 1223(2) + В§ 752 cross-refs**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.723-1 + В§ 1.704-3**, **constant pin 80% investment-company threshold**, **constant pin 12-month long-term threshold**, **very large basis with В§ 721(b) no overflow** (u64::MAX/2 + u64::MAX/2 saturating defense). Trader-partnership critical because В§ 723 is the foundational inside-basis provision that drives every subsequent basis-tracking calculation in Subchapter K вҖ” В§ 704(c) built-in gain allocation, В§ 743(b)/В§ 734(b)/В§ 755 basis adjustments under В§ 754 election, В§ 732 distributee basis on subsequent distribution, В§ 737 mixing-bowl recognition вҖ” all derive from В§ 723's carryover-basis foundation. Trader-partnerships organized as investment companies (most trader-tax-status partnerships with 80%+ securities) face the В§ 721(b) investment-company exception that converts what would otherwise be tax-free contribution into a taxable event with В§ 721(b) gain recognition + В§ 723 stepped-up inside basis. The В§ 1223(2) holding-period tacking rule is the most-missed procedural step вҖ” partnerships that fail to track contributing partner's holding period default to short-term holding period from contribution date, mischaracterizing subsequent gain as short-term at ordinary rates. Sibling cluster: `section_721` (contribution nonrecognition вҖ” В§ 723 inside-basis companion), `section_722` (contributing partner's outside basis вҖ” paired with В§ 723 to produce inside-outside basis equality), `section_732` (iter 588 вҖ” distributee basis downstream of В§ 723 inside basis), `section_704c` (built-in gain allocation вҖ” depends on В§ 723 carryover basis to determine pre-contribution gain), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment under В§ 754), `section_734` (iter 578 вҖ” distributee basis adjustment under В§ 754), `section_754` (basis adjustment election вҖ” В§ 723 inside-basis baseline that В§ 743/В§ 734 modify), `section_755` (iter 586 вҖ” allocation methodology that operates on В§ 723-established inside basis), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” hot assets character analysis depends on В§ 723 carryover basis distinction between hot vs. cold assets), `section_737` (iter 580 вҖ” mixing-bowl recognition references В§ 704(c) which depends on В§ 723).

`traderview-expense::section_461h` is the **IRC В§ 461(h) economic performance rule module** (iter 598) вҖ” accrual-method timing companion to `section_446` (iter 596 вҖ” methods of accounting general rule with В§ 446(c)(2) accrual method that В§ 461(h) supplements), `section_461g` (prepaid interest timing), `section_461l` (excess business loss limitation), `section_162` (ordinary and necessary business expense вҖ” what gets deducted under В§ 461(h) timing), `section_165d` (wagering losses limit). В§ 461(h) was enacted by the **Deficit Reduction Act of 1984** (Pub. L. 98-369) to prevent accrual-method taxpayers from deducting liabilities before any cash outflow or actual provision of services/property. Before В§ 461(h), accrual-method taxpayers could deduct liabilities as soon as the all-events test was satisfied вҖ” even if the liability would not be paid for years (or never). В§ 461(h) added the third prong: **economic performance** must also occur before deduction is permitted. **Three-prong all-events test** under В§ 461(h)(1) and Treas. Reg. В§ 1.461-1(a)(2): a liability is deductible by an accrual-method taxpayer when (i) all events have occurred to establish the **fact of liability**, (ii) **amount** can be determined with reasonable accuracy, AND (iii) **economic performance** has occurred. **В§ 461(h)(2) when economic performance occurs**: **В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(i)** services provided to taxpayer вҖ” as services are provided; **В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(ii)** property provided to taxpayer вҖ” as property is provided; **В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(iii)** use of property вҖ” as property is used; **В§ 461(h)(2)(B)** services or property provided BY taxpayer вҖ” as taxpayer provides such services or property; **В§ 461(h)(2)(C)** workers compensation, tort, breach of contract, or violation of law вҖ” as payments to such person are made; **В§ 461(h)(2)(D)** other liabilities вҖ” at time determined under regulations prescribed by Secretary. **В§ 461(h)(3) recurring item exception**: taxpayer may deduct an accrued expense at end of taxable year IN ADVANCE of economic performance if FOUR conditions satisfied: (1) as of end of taxable year, all events have occurred that establish the fact of liability and amount can be determined with reasonable accuracy; (2) economic performance occurs within **8.5 months** after the close of the taxable year, OR by date of timely filing of return for that year (whichever is earlier); (3) item is **recurring** + taxpayer **consistently treats similar items** as incurred in the tax year; (4) either (a) item is **NOT MATERIAL**, or (b) accrual of the item for that taxable year results in **BETTER MATCHING** of the item with the income to which it relates (compared with accruing the liability in the year of economic performance). **В§ 461(h)(4) reserves for estimated expenses prohibited**: a taxpayer may not, in the absence of express statutory or regulatory authority, deduct an estimated amount of expenses or losses for the year. **Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantServicesOrPropertyProvidedToTaxpayerUnder461h2A, CompliantServicesOrPropertyProvidedByTaxpayerUnder461h2B, CompliantPaymentLiabilitiesWorkersCompTortBreachUnder461h2C, CompliantOtherLiabilityRegulatoryDeterminationUnder461h2D, CompliantRecurringItemExceptionUnder461h3, ViolationLiabilityDeductedBeforeEconomicPerformance, ViolationAllEventsTestNotMet, ViolationReservesForEstimatedExpensesUnder461h4, ViolationRecurringItemMissedEightAndHalfMonthDeadline. **Seven liability types**: NotApplicable, ServicesProvidedToTaxpayer461h2Ai, PropertyProvidedToTaxpayer461h2Aii, UseOfProperty461h2Aiii, ServicesOrPropertyProvidedByTaxpayer461h2B, WorkersCompTortBreachViolationOfLaw461h2C, OtherLiability461h2D. **Four deduction timing year states**: NotApplicable, CurrentYearEconomicPerformanceOccurred, RecurringItemExceptionUnder461h3, DeferredToEconomicPerformanceYear. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-461h`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **services to taxpayer compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(A)**, **property to taxpayer compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(ii)**, **use of property compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(iii)**, **services by taxpayer compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(B)**, **workers comp tort breach compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(C)**, **other liability compliant В§ 461(h)(2)(D)**, **liability deducted before economic performance violation**, **all events test not met violation**, **amount not determinable violation**, **reserves for estimated expenses violation** (В§ 461(h)(4)), **recurring item exception within 8.5 months compliant** (85 Г— 10 = 85 Г— 1/10 boundary), **recurring item exception at exactly 8.5 months compliant** (boundary), **recurring item exception at 8.6 months violation** (statutory вүӨ reading), **recurring item not recurring violation**, **recurring item inconsistent treatment violation**, **recurring item not immaterial or better matching violation**, **not applicable returns default**, **citations pin В§ 461(h) subsections** ((h)(1) + (h)(2)(A)(i)/(ii)/(iii) + (h)(2)(B)/(C)/(D) + (h)(3) + (h)(4)), **citations pin В§ 446(c)(2) accrual + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.461-1(a)(2)/-4/-5 + DRA 1984**, **constant pin 8.5 months Г— 10** (85), **constant pin DRA 1984 enactment year**. Trader-partnership critical because В§ 461(h) is the single most-contested timing provision in tax practice вҖ” the IRS routinely challenges accrual-method deductions for failure to meet the economic performance prong, and the recurring item exception is the most-litigated escape valve. Trader-partnership accrual-method filers commonly accrue year-end liabilities for: (1) services received from outside vendors (auditing, legal вҖ” В§ 461(h)(2)(A)(i) services-to-taxpayer rule); (2) workers comp / tort settlements (В§ 461(h)(2)(C) payment-liability rule вҖ” most punitive timing since payment must occur, not just provision); (3) employee bonuses and deferred compensation (В§ 461(h)(2)(C) payment-liability rule via В§ 461(h)(5) cross-ref). The Giant Eagle case (Tax Court 2014 / 3d Cir. 2016) and Hallmark Cards case (Tax Court 2020) significantly restricted the recurring item exception's application вҖ” IRS has aggressively challenged "better matching" prong in particular. Sibling cluster: `section_446` (iter 596 вҖ” methods of accounting general rule, В§ 446(c)(2) accrual method that В§ 461(h) supplements), `section_461g` (prepaid interest timing вҖ” narrower В§ 461(g) cash-basis rule), `section_461l` (excess business loss limitation вҖ” TCJA 2017), `section_162` (ordinary and necessary business expense вҖ” what В§ 461(h) timing applies to), `section_267a` (related-party deductions hybrid mismatch rules вҖ” interacts with В§ 461(h) timing).

`traderview-expense::section_446` is the **IRC В§ 446 general rule for methods of accounting module** (iter 596) вҖ” foundational accounting-method companion to `section_444` (fiscal year election), `section_448` (limitation on cash method for C corporations + partnerships with C corporate partners), `section_481` (accounting-method-change adjustments), `section_461g` (prepaid interest timing), `section_461l` (excess business loss limitation), `section_471` (inventory requirement), `section_475c2` (trader/dealer/investor MTM classification), `section_453` (installment method). В§ 446 establishes the foundational framework for which methods of accounting are permissible, when IRS may override taxpayer's chosen method, and the procedure for changing methods. **В§ 446(a) general rule**: taxable income shall be computed under the method of accounting on the basis of which the taxpayer regularly computes income in keeping the taxpayer's books вҖ” the book-tax conformity default. **В§ 446(b) authority of Secretary**: if no method has been regularly used by the taxpayer, or if the method used does not "clearly reflect income," the Secretary may compute taxable income under a method that does clearly reflect income. "Clearly reflect income" is an IRS-discretionary standard applied case-by-case. **В§ 446(c) permissible methods**: **В§ 446(c)(1) cash receipts and disbursements method** (cash basis вҖ” income recognized when received, deductions when paid); **В§ 446(c)(2) accrual method** (income recognized when all events fixing the right to receive occur and amount can be determined with reasonable accuracy; deductions when all events fixing the liability occur and economic performance occurs under В§ 461(h)); **В§ 446(c)(3) other methods permitted by chapter** (e.g., В§ 475 mark-to-market, В§ 453 installment method, В§ 460 long-term contract methods); **В§ 446(c)(4) combinations of methods** (hybrid methods permitted if they clearly reflect income and are consistently used; a common combination is accrual method for purchases and sales (because of В§ 471 inventory requirement) plus cash method for other items). **В§ 446(d) different methods for different trades or businesses**: a taxpayer engaged in more than one trade or business may use different methods for each, provided each clearly reflects income and is used consistently. **В§ 446(e) requirement of consent for change of method**: a taxpayer who changes the method of accounting shall, before computing taxable income under the new method, secure the consent of the Secretary; **Form 3115** (Application for Change in Accounting Method) is the procedural mechanism вҖ” either automatic consent under **Rev. Proc. 2024-23** (current automatic consent procedure) for specified changes, or advance non-automatic consent for non-listed changes. **В§ 446(f) long-term contract methods**: cross-reference to В§ 460 вҖ” failure to use the percentage-of-completion method on long-term contracts where required. **"Clearly reflect income" consistency requirement** under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.446-1(a)(2): no method is acceptable unless all items of gross profit and deductions are treated with consistency from year to year. **Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantCashMethodSection446c1, CompliantAccrualMethodSection446c2, CompliantOtherPermittedMethodSection446c3, CompliantHybridMethodSection446c4, CompliantDifferentMethodsForDifferentTradesUnderSection446d, ViolationMethodDoesNotClearlyReflectIncome, ViolationMethodChangeWithoutForm3115Consent, ViolationInconsistentTreatmentYearToYear, IrsAuthorityToOverrideUnderSection446b. **Five accounting methods**: NotApplicable, CashReceiptsAndDisbursements, Accrual, OtherPermitted, HybridCombination. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-446`. **Seventeen tests pin**: **accrual method compliant** (baseline), **cash method compliant** (В§ 446(c)(1)), **other permitted method compliant** (В§ 446(c)(3)), **hybrid method compliant** (В§ 446(c)(4)), **different methods for different trades compliant** (В§ 446(d)), **method does not clearly reflect income violation** (IRS authority to override under В§ 446(b)), **inconsistent treatment year to year violation** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.446-1(a)(2) consistency violation), **method change without Form 3115 violation** (В§ 446(e) consent requirement), **method change with Form 3115 compliant**, **not applicable returns default**, **citations pin В§ 446 subsections** ((a) + (b) + (c)(1)/(2)/(3)/(4) + (d) + (e) + (f)), **citations pin В§ 461(h) + В§ 471 cross-references**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.446-1 + В§ 1.446-1(a)(2) consistency + Form 3115 + Rev. Proc. 2024-23**, **constant pins cash method ID 1**, **constant pins accrual method ID 2**, **constant pins other permitted method ID 3**, **constant pins hybrid combination method ID 4**. Trader-partnership critical because В§ 446 is the single most-litigated accounting-method provision вҖ” the "clearly reflect income" standard is intentionally vague and gives the IRS enormous discretionary authority to override taxpayer-chosen methods on audit. Trader-tax-status filers commonly select В§ 475(f) mark-to-market (a В§ 446(c)(3) "other permitted" method), but the underlying accounting-method change requires В§ 446(e) consent via Form 3115 in the year of election вҖ” many trader-tax-status filers miss this procedural step and face В§ 446(e) violation findings on audit. Hybrid combinations under В§ 446(c)(4) are particularly fraught вҖ” IRS routinely challenges hybrid methods as not clearly reflecting income. Sibling cluster: `section_444` (fiscal year election вҖ” interacts with В§ 446 on method consistency), `section_448` (limits cash method for C corporations and partnerships with C-corporate partners вҖ” drives accrual adoption), `section_481` (accounting-method-change adjustments вҖ” provides the four-year spread mechanism when В§ 446(e) consent is granted), `section_461g` (prepaid interest timing under accrual method), `section_461h` (economic performance вҖ” defining when accrual deductions accrue), `section_461l` (excess business loss limitation), `section_471` (inventory requirement вҖ” drives partial accrual for purchases/sales under hybrid В§ 446(c)(4) method), `section_475c2` (trader/dealer/investor classification вҖ” В§ 475(f) trader MTM is a В§ 446(c)(3) other permitted method requiring В§ 446(e) consent), `section_475f` (trader MTM election mechanics вҖ” Form 3115 cross-reference), `section_453` (installment method вҖ” В§ 446(c)(3) other permitted method), `section_460` (long-term contracts вҖ” В§ 446(f) cross-reference).

`traderview-expense::section_168` is the **IRC В§ 168 Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System (MACRS) general depreciation module** (iter 594) вҖ” foundational depreciation companion to `section_168_e6` (qualified improvement property TCJA technical correction), `section_168g` (Alternative Depreciation System), `section_168k` (bonus depreciation вҖ” additional first-year depreciation), `section_179` (immediate expensing of business property), `section_167` (general depreciation provision), `section_197` (intangibles amortization). В§ 168 was enacted by the **Tax Reform Act of 1986** (Pub. L. 99-514) replacing the prior ACRS regime, codified at 26 U.S.C. В§ 168. **В§ 168(a) general rule**: depreciation deduction equals the product of (i) applicable depreciation method Г— (ii) applicable recovery period Г— (iii) applicable convention. Practical computation uses IRS Publication 946 percentage tables that combine all three into ready-made annual percentages. **В§ 168(b) applicable depreciation method**: **В§ 168(b)(1) 200% declining balance** (default for personal property вҖ” 3/5/7/10-year property classes, switches to straight-line when SL yields larger allowance); **В§ 168(b)(2)(B) 150% declining balance** (15-year and 20-year property, qualified smart electric meters, qualified smart electric grid systems, and elective application); **В§ 168(b)(3) straight-line method (required)** for residential rental (27.5 years), nonresidential real (39 years), and any property where taxpayer elects straight-line under В§ 168(b)(5). **В§ 168(c) applicable recovery periods**: **3-year** (tractors, racehorses 2 years or older, certain research property); **5-year** (computers, autos, light trucks, qualified technological equipment); **7-year** (office furniture and equipment); **10-year** (water transportation equipment); **15-year** (qualified improvement property вҖ” QIP, TCJA technical correction applied 2018+); **20-year** (farm buildings); **25-year** (water utility property); **27.5-year** (residential rental property); **39-year** (nonresidential real property). **В§ 168(d) applicable convention**: **В§ 168(d)(1) half-year convention** (default for personal property вҖ” half-year deduction in first and last years); **В§ 168(d)(3) mid-quarter convention** triggered when more than **40%** of personal property is placed in service in the last quarter of the taxable year; **В§ 168(d)(2) mid-month convention** (real property вҖ” mid-month placement assumed, first-year deduction depends on month placed in service). **В§ 168(e) classification of property**: **В§ 168(e)(2)(A) residential rental** вҖ” any building or structure from which **80% or more** of the gross rental income for the tax year is from dwelling units; **В§ 168(e)(2)(B) nonresidential real** вҖ” В§ 1250 property that is not residential rental and has class life of at least 27.5 years. **В§ 168(g) Alternative Depreciation System (ADS)**: straight-line over class life; applies to tax-exempt use property, listed property used less than 50% in trade or business, foreign-use property, and electively for any property class. **В§ 168(k) bonus depreciation**: additional first-year depreciation for qualified property вҖ” 100% under TCJA 2017 through 2022, phased down 80%/60%/40%/20% through 2026, restored to 100% permanent by OBBBA 2025. **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Method200DBPersonalProperty3To10Years, Method150DBPersonalProperty15To20Years, MethodStraightLineResidentialRental27_5, MethodStraightLineNonresidentialReal39, MethodElectiveStraightLineOnPersonalProperty, MethodAdsStraightLineOverClassLife, ConventionMidQuarterTriggeredBy40PctLastQuarter, ViolationResidentialRentalClassifiedWithUnder80PctDwelling, ViolationMissingMidQuarterConventionWhen40PctTriggered. **Nine property classes** + **four methods** + **three conventions**.
