Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-168`. **Twenty-four tests pin**: **residential rental 27.5 straight-line mid-month** (recovery period 275 Г— 10 = 27.5 years), **residential rental below 80% dwelling violation** (70% < 80% threshold), **residential at exactly 80% dwelling compliant** (statutory вүҘ reading), **nonresidential real 39 straight-line mid-month** (recovery period 390 = 39 years), **5-year property 200% DB half-year**, **7-year property 200% DB**, **15-year property 150% DB half-year**, **20-year property 150% DB**, **mid-quarter convention triggered at 41%** (statutory > 40% reading), **mid-quarter at exactly 40% NOT triggered** (boundary), **mid-quarter missing violation when taxpayer used half-year**, **elective straight-line on personal property** (В§ 168(b)(5)), **ADS elected uses straight-line over class life** (В§ 168(g)), **residential first-year deduction approximates 3.6%** ($500K Г— 3.6% = $18K), **nonresidential first-year deduction approximates 2.6%** ($500K Г— 2.6% = $13K), **citations pin В§ 168 subsections** (a + (b)(1)/(2)(B)/(3) + (c) + (d)(1)/(2)/(3) + (e)(2)(A)/(B) + (g) + (k)), **citations pin Tax Reform Act of 1986 + Publication 946**, **constant pins residential 27.5 Г— 10** (275), **constant pins nonresidential 39 years**, **constant pins 200% DB method**, **constant pins 150% DB method**, **constant pins 40% mid-quarter threshold**, **constant pins 80% residential dwelling threshold**, **very large basis no overflow** (u64::MAX defense). Trader-landlord critical because В§ 168 governs every depreciation deduction on rental real estate, business equipment, and tenant improvements вҖ” and the 80%-dwelling threshold determines whether a mixed-use property (e.g., apartments-above-retail) gets the favorable 27.5-year recovery (residential rental) or the punitive 39-year recovery (nonresidential real). A 100,000 sqft property generating $1M annual rent with 75% from apartments + 25% from ground-floor retail is misclassified as residential rental under В§ 168(e)(2)(A) вҖ” taxpayer who reports as residential faces depreciation disallowance plus accuracy-related penalty under В§ 6662. The mid-quarter convention is the most-frequently-missed convention requirement вҖ” trader-landlords who buy substantial year-end equipment (computers, autos, office furniture in November-December) often exceed the 40% last-quarter threshold and fail to apply the punitive mid-quarter convention that significantly reduces first-year depreciation. Sibling cluster: `section_168_e6` (qualified improvement property вҖ” TCJA technical correction allowing 15-year recovery for QIP), `section_168g` (ADS вҖ” straight-line over class life, electable or required), `section_168k` (bonus depreciation вҖ” additional first-year depreciation pathway), `section_179` (immediate expensing alternative to MACRS depreciation), `section_167` (general depreciation predecessor provision), `section_197` (intangibles 15-year amortization), `section_263A` (UNICAP вҖ” capitalization that determines basis input to В§ 168), `section_469` (passive activity loss limit вҖ” depreciation deductions can be limited if passive), `section_280F` (luxury auto depreciation caps that limit В§ 168 for autos).

`traderview-expense::section_741` is the **IRC В§ 741 recognition and character of gain or loss on sale or exchange of partnership interest module** (iter 592) вҖ” foundational Subchapter K character-classification provision that pairs with `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets вҖ” the В§ 751(a) override carving out ordinary character from В§ 741 capital default), `section_752` (partnership liabilities вҖ” В§ 752(d) liability relief treated as deemed distribution becomes part of amount realized), `section_706` (iter 584 вҖ” В§ 706(c)(2) closes partnership year for transferor on entire-interest sale triggering В§ 741), `section_736` (iter 582 вҖ” distinguishes В§ 741 sale from В§ 736 redemption with very different character consequences), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment downstream of В§ 741 sale), `section_705` (outside basis determination вҖ” the denominator in В§ 741 gain/loss math). **Operative rule** (В§ 741): "In the case of a sale or exchange of an interest in a partnership, gain or loss shall be recognized to the transferor partner. Such gain or loss shall be considered as gain or loss from the sale or exchange of a capital asset, except as otherwise provided in section 751 (relating to unrealized receivables and inventory items)." **Three-step computation**: (1) **Amount Realized** = cash received + FMV of property received + liability relief (В§ 752(d) treats decrease in partner's share of partnership liabilities as deemed cash distribution that becomes part of amount realized on sale); (2) **Adjusted Basis** = transferor's outside basis under В§ 705 (initial basis + distributive shares + contributions вҲ’ money + property distributions вҲ’ liabilities); (3) **Gain or Loss** = Amount Realized вҲ’ Adjusted Basis. **Character**: Default is capital character under В§ 741; holding period > 1 year (366+ days) qualifies for В§ 1(h) preferred 0%/15%/20% long-term capital gain rates; holding period вүӨ 1 year is short-term capital gain at ordinary rates up to 37%; **В§ 751(a) override** вҖ” portion of amount realized attributable to unrealized receivables (В§ 751(c)) + inventory items (В§ 751(d)) is treated as ORDINARY income; remainder is В§ 741 capital. **Common compliance failures**: (1) **Failure to include liability relief in amount realized** вҖ” В§ 752(d) deems liability relief a cash distribution that adds to amount realized; taxpayers often forget to add the share-of-partnership-liabilities relieved on sale; (2) **Failure to bifurcate В§ 751 ordinary portion** вҖ” В§ 741 character analysis is DEFAULT, В§ 751(a) override is AUTOMATIC; taxpayer who reports entire gain as capital when partnership holds substantial В§ 751 hot assets has misreported character. **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, LongTermCapitalGainPureNoHotAssets, ShortTermCapitalGainPureNoHotAssets, LongTermCapitalLossPureNoHotAssets, ShortTermCapitalLossPureNoHotAssets, BifurcatedSection751OrdinaryPlusSection741Capital, ViolationTaxpayerReportedAllCapitalIgnoringSection751, ViolationFailedToIncludeLiabilityReliefInAmountRealized. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-741`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **long-term capital gain pure** ($10M realized вҲ’ $5M basis = $5M LTCG, no hot assets, holding period 1000 days), **short-term capital gain when holding period under 366 days** (200 days), **long-term boundary exactly 366 days qualifies** (statutory вүҘ reading), **long-term boundary 365 days does NOT qualify** (boundary), **long-term capital loss when basis exceeds amount realized** ($2M realized вҲ’ $5M basis = вҲ’$3M loss), **short-term capital loss under 366 days**, **liability relief added to amount realized** ($8M cash + $2M liability relief = $10M amount realized), **failure to include liability relief violation** ($2M omitted), **bifurcated В§ 751 ordinary plus В§ 741 capital** ($3M ordinary portion + $1.5M ordinary basis = $1.5M ordinary; $5M total gain вҲ’ $1.5M ordinary = $3.5M capital), **taxpayer ignored В§ 751 reported all capital violation**, **zero gain loss when amount realized equals basis**, **citations pin В§ 741 + В§ 751(a) + В§ 752(d)**, **citations pin В§ 705 + В§ 1223 + В§ 1(h) cross-refs**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.741-1 + В§ 1.752-1**, **constant pins 366-day long-term threshold**, **constant pins 12-month long-term threshold**, **very large amount realized no overflow** (u64::MAX defense), **FMV of property received added to amount realized** ($6M cash + $4M FMV property = $10M). Trader-partnership critical because every partnership-interest exit transaction passes through В§ 741 character analysis вҖ” and the В§ 751(a) ordinary carve-out is the single most-litigated character provision in Subchapter K. Trader partnerships with significant accrued trading gains pending settlement (= В§ 751(c) unrealized receivables) face mandatory bifurcation: even a long-term-capital-asset-seeming sale of partnership interest produces ordinary income to the extent of the trading partnership's unrealized receivables, eliminating the В§ 1(h) preferred LTCG rate advantage on that portion. The В§ 752(d) liability-relief inclusion is equally consequential: a trader partnership using margin financing produces substantial partnership-liability allocations; on exit, the partner's share of those liabilities is relieved (the new partner takes them on), and В§ 752(d) treats this relief as cash distribution that flows into amount realized вҖ” a $5M margin-allocation-relieved exit can produce $5M of additional taxable gain that taxpayers routinely miss. Sibling cluster: `section_705` (outside basis вҖ” denominator in В§ 741 math), `section_706(c)(2)` (iter 584 вҖ” partnership year closes on entire-interest sale, triggering В§ 741 character analysis), `section_736` (iter 582 вҖ” redemption vs sale distinction вҖ” В§ 736 may produce ordinary character under В§ 736(a) vs В§ 741's capital default), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment under В§ 754 election downstream of В§ 741 sale), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” hot-asset definitions populate В§ 751(a) override and В§ 741 bifurcation), `section_752` (partnership liabilities вҖ” В§ 752(d) liability relief flows into В§ 741 amount realized), `section_475f` (trader MTM election вҖ” В§ 475(f) MTM partnership exits face different character analysis interacting with В§ 741), `section_1223` (holding period вҖ” determines long-term vs short-term В§ 741 character), `section_1(h)` (LTCG rates that apply to В§ 741 capital portion).

`traderview-expense::section_1059` is the **IRC В§ 1059 corporate shareholder's basis in stock reduced by nontaxed portion of extraordinary dividends module** (iter 590) вҖ” trader-corporation anti-abuse provision targeting the "dividend stripping" pattern where corporations would buy dividend-paying stock just before a large dividend, claim the dividends-received deduction (DRD), then sell the stock at a loss equal to the dividend amount вҖ” generating an artificial loss while excluding most of the dividend from income. Companion to `section_1091` (wash sale rule вҖ” distinct anti-loss-trafficking provision targeting same general arbitrage), `section_475c2` (trader/dealer/investor classification вҖ” determines whether DRD is available), `section_475f` (trader MTM election вҖ” alternative to wash sale and В§ 1059 mechanics). When a corporate shareholder receives a dividend that exceeds an "extraordinary" threshold within 2 years of acquiring the stock, В§ 1059 requires the corporate shareholder to REDUCE basis in the stock by the nontaxed portion of the dividend (the DRD-excluded amount). If the nontaxed portion exceeds basis, the excess is recognized as gain from sale or exchange of the stock. **В§ 1059(a) general rule**: basis reduced (but not below zero) by nontaxed portion. Excess of nontaxed portion over basis is recognized as gain from sale or exchange of stock for the taxable year in which the extraordinary dividend is received. **В§ 1059(b) nontaxed portion**: amount excluded from gross income by virtue of В§ 243 (general corporate DRD), В§ 245 (foreign-source DRD), or В§ 245A (TCJA 2017 100% DRD for foreign-source income from specified 10%-owned foreign corporations). **В§ 1059(c) extraordinary dividend definition**: dividend equals or exceeds **10%** of corporate shareholder's adjusted basis in common stock; **5%** threshold for preferred stock. All dividends within an **85-day aggregation window** (or 365-day window at taxpayer election under В§ 1059(c)(3)) may be aggregated to test against threshold. **В§ 1059(d)(6) exception**: stock held during the entire existence of the distributing corporation is exempt. **В§ 1059(e)(1) per se extraordinary dividends** вҖ” override BOTH the threshold AND 2-year holding requirements: (1) any redemption treated as dividend under В§ 301 that is non-pro-rata; (2) redemption that would not have been treated as a dividend but for В§ 318(a)(4) options attribution; (3) any distribution in partial liquidation treated as a dividend. **В§ 1059(a)(2) 2-year holding-period exception**: if corporate shareholder held stock for MORE THAN 2 YEARS (730 days) before the dividend announcement date, basis reduction does not apply (except for В§ 1059(e)(1) per se cases). **TCJA 2017 В§ 14101** added В§ 245A and significantly expanded В§ 1059's cross-border scope: any post-2017 100%-DRD-eligible foreign-source dividend received by a U.S. corporate shareholder within 2 years of acquiring a 10%-or-greater foreign corporate stake triggers full basis reduction by the entire dividend (since 100% is the "nontaxed portion"). **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotCorporateShareholderNoBasisReduction, NoExtraordinaryDividendBelowThreshold, HoldingPeriodOver2YearsNoBasisReduction, ExtraordinaryDividendBasisReductionApplied, ExtraordinaryDividendExcessOverBasisRecognizedAsGain, PerSeExtraordinaryNonProRataRedemptionUnder1059e, PerSeExtraordinaryPartialLiquidationUnder1059e, PerSeExtraordinarySecondaryOptionAttributionUnder1059e, ViolationFailedToReduceBasisForExtraordinaryDividend. **Four per-se event types**: None, NonProRataRedemption, PartialLiquidation, SecondaryOptionAttributionRedemption. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1059`. **Twenty-three tests pin**: **corporate extraordinary dividend basis reduction applied** ($2M dividend > 10% Г— $10M basis = $1M threshold вҶ’ $1.5M nontaxed reduces basis to $8.5M), **non-corporate shareholder not applicable**, **dividend below 10% threshold not extraordinary** ($500K < $1M threshold), **dividend exactly at 10% is extraordinary** (statutory вүҘ reading), **preferred stock uses 5% threshold** ($600K > $500K preferred threshold), **preferred stock below 5% threshold not extraordinary**, **holding period over 2 years no reduction** (731 days > 730), **holding period at exactly 730 days still triggers** (boundary), **aggregation 85-day window combines dividends** ($600K + $1.2M aggregated = $1.8M > threshold), **nontaxed portion exceeds basis recognized as gain** ($1M basis вҲ’ $1.5M nontaxed = $500K gain + $0 basis), **taxpayer failed to reduce basis violation**, **per se non-pro-rata redemption overrides threshold and holding** (10-year-old stock + $100K small dividend still triggers if non-pro-rata), **per se partial liquidation overrides threshold and holding**, **per se В§ 318 options attribution overrides threshold and holding**, **citations pin В§ 1059 subsections** ((a) + (b) + (c) + (d)(6) + (e)(1)), **citations pin DRD В§ 243 + В§ 245 + В§ 245A cross-refs**, **citations pin В§ 301 + В§ 318(a)(4) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1059(e)-1**, **citations pin TCJA 2017 В§ 14101 + В§ 245A addition**, **constant pins 10% common threshold**, **constant pins 5% preferred threshold**, **constant pins 2-year holding period (730 days)**, **constant pins 85-day and 365-day aggregation windows**, **very large nontaxed portion saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense reduces basis to 0 + recognizes gain). Trader-corporation critical because В§ 1059 is the most aggressive anti-arbitrage provision in the corporate-shareholder dividend area вҖ” and the TCJA 2017 expansion via В§ 245A produces dramatic exposure for U.S. corporate shareholders of foreign corporations: any dividend received within 2 years of acquiring a 10%-or-greater foreign equity stake that qualifies for the 100% В§ 245A DRD triggers В§ 1059 basis reduction equal to the FULL dividend amount, which can completely eliminate basis and produce immediate gain recognition. Trading corporations that buy controlling stakes in foreign portfolio companies for dividend yield play face the largest В§ 1059 exposure. The per se В§ 1059(e)(1) branch is equally consequential for trading corporations engaging in stock redemption transactions вҖ” any non-pro-rata redemption treated as a В§ 301 dividend triggers В§ 1059 regardless of holding period or threshold. Sibling cluster: `section_1091` (wash-sale rule вҖ” adjacent anti-loss-arbitrage provision targeting individual taxpayers), `section_475c2` (trader/dealer/investor classification вҖ” corporate trading companies as dealers receive В§ 475(a) mark-to-market that overrides В§ 1059 character analysis), `section_475f` (trader MTM election вҖ” eliminates dividend timing arbitrage), `section_243` (general corporate DRD вҖ” populates В§ 1059 nontaxed portion), `section_245A` (100% DRD for foreign-source income вҖ” TCJA 2017 expansion that dramatically expanded В§ 1059 cross-border scope), `section_301` (dividend distribution rules вҖ” В§ 1059(e)(1) cross-reference), `section_318` (constructive ownership вҖ” В§ 318(a)(4) options attribution under В§ 1059(e)(1)).

`traderview-expense::section_732` is the **IRC В§ 732 distributee basis in distributed property module** (iter 588) вҖ” foundational Subchapter K distributee-basis companion to `section_731` (distribution recognition rules вҖ” basis determined by В§ 732 sets up В§ 731 gain/loss computation), `section_734` (iter 578 distributee basis adjustment), `section_736` (iter 582 retiring/deceased partner payment), `section_737` (iter 580 mixing-bowl recognition), `section_743` (iter 576 transferee basis adjustment cross-referenced by В§ 732(d)), `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets definition that В§ 732(c) cross-references), `section_754` (election mechanics вҖ” В§ 732(d) operates specifically when В§ 754 was NOT in effect at time of original transfer), `section_755` (iter 586 В§ 755 allocation framework). В§ 732 governs three distinct distributee-basis questions. **В§ 732(a) current (nonliquidating) distribution**: **В§ 732(a)(1) general rule** вҖ” distributee takes the partnership's adjusted basis in the property immediately before distribution (carryover basis); **В§ 732(a)(2) limitation** вҖ” basis to distributee CANNOT EXCEED the partner's outside basis in the partnership interest, reduced by any money distributed in the same transaction. **В§ 732(b) liquidating distribution**: distributee takes a "substituted basis" equal to the partner's outside basis in the partnership interest minus any money distributed in the same transaction. The substituted basis is the partner's investment in the partnership "translated" into the distributed property вҖ” preserves the partner's economic position across the distribution. **В§ 732(c) basis allocation among multiple properties**: when more than one property is distributed, the available basis is allocated in this order: **В§ 732(c)(1)(A)** вҖ” first to В§ 751(c) unrealized receivables and В§ 751(d) inventory items at the partnership's adjusted basis in each item; **В§ 732(c)(1)(B)** вҖ” any remaining basis is allocated to other distributed property (if basis increase needed, allocated first to appreciated property up to FMV; if basis decrease needed, allocated first to depreciated property up to FMV; then proportionally based on adjusted basis). **В§ 732(d) special rule for transfers without В§ 754 election**: a partner who acquired all or part of their interest by transfer when В§ 754 was NOT in effect, and who receives a distribution within **2 years** after such transfer, may ELECT to treat the adjusted partnership basis of distributed property as if the В§ 743(b) adjustment were in effect. **В§ 732(d) mandatory application** under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.732-1(d)(4): special rule MUST be applied regardless of 2-year window if at time of the original transfer ALL THREE conditions held: (i) FMV of all partnership property exceeded **110%** of its adjusted basis to the partnership; (ii) a В§ 732(c) allocation upon liquidation would have resulted in a basis shift from non-depreciable to depreciable property; (iii) a В§ 743(b) adjustment would change the basis of the property actually distributed. **В§ 732(f) corporate-partner distribution rule** (added post-2015): special inside-basis adjustment for distributions to corporate partners when the corporation later controls the partnership. **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CurrentDistributionCarryoverBasis, CurrentDistributionLimitedByOutsideBasis, LiquidatingDistributionSubstitutedBasis, Section732cAllocationToHotAssetsThenOtherProperty, Section732dSpecialRuleElectiveBasisAdjustment, Section732dSpecialRuleMandatoryFmv110PercentTrigger, ViolationFailedToApplyMandatorySection732d. **Three distribution types**: NotApplicable, CurrentDistribution, LiquidatingDistribution. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-732`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **current distribution carryover when outside basis sufficient** ($3M property + $10M outside basis = $3M carryover), **current distribution limited by outside basis** ($3M property + $2M outside basis = $2M capped), **current distribution outside basis reduced by money** ($3M property + $4M outside basis вҲ’ $2M money = $2M basis), **liquidating distribution substituted basis** ($5M outside вҲ’ $1M money = $4M substituted), **liquidating distribution no money uses full outside basis**, **В§ 732(c) allocation hot assets first then other** ($1.5M hot + $2.5M other from $4M outside), **В§ 732(d) mandatory when 110% FMV trigger met** (15M/10M = 150% > 110% + shift + В§ 743(b) impact), **В§ 732(d) mandatory violation when taxpayer did not apply**, **В§ 732(d) elective when within 2-year window** (18 months post-transfer with В§ 754 not in effect), **В§ 732(d) 24-month boundary in window** (вүӨ 24 reading), **В§ 732(d) 25-month boundary outside window**, **В§ 732(d) 110% boundary at exactly 110% NOT mandatory** (strict greater-than reading), **В§ 732(d) 111% just over threshold mandatory**, **not applicable returns default**, **citations pin В§ 732 subsections** ((a)(1) + (a)(2) + (b) + (c) + (c)(1)(A) + (c)(1)(B) + (d) + (f)), **citations pin cross-refs В§ 751(c)/(d) + В§ 743(b) + В§ 754 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.732-1/-1(d)(4)**, **constant pins 24-month В§ 732(d) window**, **constant pins 110% В§ 732(d) trigger**, **very large outside basis no overflow** (u64::MAX defense). Trader-partnership critical because В§ 732 governs every partner-exit and partner-redemption transaction where property (not just money) is distributed вҖ” and the В§ 732(d) mandatory-application path is the most-litigated provision in the area: a trader-partnership transferee who acquires interest at a premium (FMV > 110% of partnership inside basis, a common pattern in appreciated trader-portfolio partnerships) AND who receives a distribution within 2 years AND where В§ 754 was not in effect at the original transfer is FORCED into В§ 732(d) treatment, with the practical effect of generating a hypothetical В§ 743(b) basis adjustment that would otherwise have been lost. Sibling cluster: `section_731` (distribution recognition rules вҖ” В§ 732 basis determines В§ 731 gain/loss), `section_734` (iter 578 вҖ” partnership-side distributee basis adjustment paralleling В§ 732's distributee-side rule), `section_736` (iter 582 вҖ” retiring partner payment characterization that determines whether В§ 732 governs the distribution side), `section_737` (iter 580 вҖ” mixing-bowl recognition that may trigger contemporaneously with В§ 732(d) on distributions), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment cross-referenced by В§ 732(d) mandatory application), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” hot assets definitions populate В§ 732(c) first-allocation rule), `section_754` (election mechanics вҖ” В§ 732(d) operates as backstop when В§ 754 was not elected), `section_755` (iter 586 вҖ” В§ 755 within-class allocation methodology operates downstream of В§ 732(c)).

`traderview-expense::section_755` is the **IRC В§ 755 allocation of В§ 743(b) and В§ 734(b) basis adjustments module** (iter 586) вҖ” downstream allocation companion to `section_743` (iter 576 transferee-side basis adjustment), `section_734` (iter 578 distributee-side basis adjustment), `section_754` (election that triggers both), `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets вҖ” defines ordinary income class composition), `section_706` (iter 584 partnership taxable year). В§ 755 governs how the basis adjustment computed under В§ 743(b) or В§ 734(b) is spread across partnership properties to preserve inside-outside basis equality. **В§ 755(a) general rule**: adjustments allocated among partnership properties in a manner that takes into account income, gain, or loss that would have been recognized on a hypothetical taxable sale, designed to preserve the partner's economic position. **В§ 755(b) two-class rule**: adjustments are first allocated between two distinct classes of property вҖ” **Class 1 Capital Gain Property** (В§ 1221 capital assets + В§ 1231(b) depreciable real and trade-or-business property held > 1 year); **Class 2 Ordinary Income Property** (everything else, including В§ 751 hot assets вҖ” unrealized receivables and inventory items вҖ” depreciable В§ 1245 recapture property, and any other property that would produce ordinary character on sale). Within each class, the allocation among individual properties is based on unrealized appreciation or depreciation. A class with zero net unrealized appreciation gets a proportional allocation based on adjusted basis. **В§ 743(b) class-attribution math** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.755-1(b)): amount allocated to the ORDINARY INCOME CLASS equals the total ordinary income/gain/loss that would be allocated to the transferee from a hypothetical sale of ALL ordinary income property; the remainder is allocated to the capital gain class. **В§ 734(b) class-attribution math** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.755-1(c)): follows the character of the gain or loss recognized by the distributee partner under В§ 731 вҖ” capital-character recognition flows the adjustment to the capital gain class. **TD 9059** (June 9, 2003) finalized coordination between В§ 755 and В§ 1060 (residual method for purchase price allocation), adding specific class-allocation methodology for partnership mergers and certain asset purchases. **Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, AllocationToCapitalGainClassOnly, AllocationToOrdinaryIncomeClassOnly, AllocationBetweenBothClassesUnderTwoClassRule, ViolationAdjustmentMisallocatedAcrossClasses, ViolationAllocationExceedsClassNetUnrealizedAppreciation. **Three adjustment types**: NotApplicable, Section743bTransferee, Section734bDistribution. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-755`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **two-class allocation compliant** ($2M ordinary + $3M capital = $5M total), **ordinary only allocation compliant** ($5M all to ordinary class), **capital only allocation compliant** ($5M all to capital class), **misallocation violation when sum does not match total** ($2M + $2.5M вү  $5M вҖ” per se В§ 755(b) two-class violation), **ordinary class allocation exceeds class appreciation violation** ($10M allocated but class has $5M unrealized appreciation вҖ” В§ 1.755-1(b)(2) cap exceeded), **capital class allocation exceeds class appreciation violation** ($12M allocated but class has $10M unrealized appreciation), **В§ 734(b) two-class allocation** (distribution-side adjustment same allocation framework), **negative total adjustment with two-class decrease** (i128 signed math for basis step-downs), **zero adjustment returns not applicable**, **NotApplicable type returns default**, **citations pin В§ 755(a)/(b) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.755-1(a)/(b)/(c)**, **citations pin В§ 1221 + В§ 1231(b) + В§ 751 cross-refs**, **citations pin В§ 743(b) + В§ 734(b) + В§ 754 cross-refs**, **citations pin TD 9059 June 9, 2003**, **constant pins capital gain class 1**, **constant pins ordinary income class 2**, **constant pins TD 9059 2003 year**, **ordinary class at exactly appreciation cap compliant** (statutory вүӨ boundary). Trader-partnership critical because В§ 755 allocation determines the TAX CHARACTER of every future disposition of partnership property after a В§ 754 election triggers basis adjustments вҖ” if a В§ 743(b) step-up is improperly allocated entirely to capital gain class when half should have gone to ordinary income class (because the partnership holds substantial В§ 751 hot assets), the transferee partner over-allocates basis to long-term-capital-gain property and under-allocates to ordinary-character property, producing inflated capital-gain reporting on capital-asset dispositions and inflated ordinary-income reporting on subsequent hot-asset realizations. Trader partnerships with significant unrealized receivables (accrued trading gains pending settlement, accrued management fees) and substantial В§ 1245 trading-tech equipment recapture face the most complex В§ 755 allocation analysis. Sibling cluster: `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” В§ 743(b) transferee adjustment that triggers В§ 755 allocation downstream), `section_734` (iter 578 вҖ” В§ 734(b) distributee adjustment with parallel В§ 755 allocation framework), `section_754` (election mechanics вҖ” В§ 755 allocation only relevant when В§ 754 in effect or substantial built-in loss applies), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” В§ 751 hot-asset definitions populate the В§ 755(b) ordinary income class), `section_731` (distribution gain/loss character that drives В§ 734(b) class attribution), `section_741` (В§ 741 capital character default вҖ” В§ 755 allocation interacts with В§ 741 by directing how much of the В§ 743(b) adjustment ends up offsetting В§ 741 capital character vs В§ 751 ordinary character on the transferee's hypothetical sale).

`traderview-expense::section_706` is the **IRC В§ 706 partnership taxable year + varying interests module** (iter 584) вҖ” foundational Subchapter K timing companion to `section_707` (iter 574 partner-partnership transactions), `section_731` (distribution rules), `section_736` (iter 582 retiring/deceased partner payment), `section_743` (iter 576 transferee basis adjustment вҖ” triggered by the entire-interest termination event under В§ 706(c)(2)), `section_751` (iter 572 hot-assets character analysis on entire-interest sale). В§ 706 governs three distinct timing questions in Subchapter K. **В§ 706(a)** вҖ” partner takes the partnership's year-end for purposes of including the distributive share; if partnership has Dec 31 year-end and partner has June 30 year-end, distributive share is reported in partner's year that INCLUDES the partnership's year-end. **В§ 706(b) partnership-taxable-year selection hierarchy** вҖ” three-tier test applied in strict order: (1) **Majority Interest Test** (В§ 706(b)(1)(B)(i)) вҖ” partners holding more than 50% of profits AND capital interests must have the same taxable year; partnership adopts that year; (2) **Principal Partner Test** (В§ 706(b)(1)(B)(ii)) вҖ” if majority test fails, partnership uses the taxable year of all PRINCIPAL PARTNERS (each holding 5% or more of profits or capital); only applies if all principal partners share the same year; (3) **Least Aggregate Deferral Test** (В§ 706(b)(1)(B)(iii)) вҖ” final fallback; partnership uses the year that produces the LEAST aggregate deferral of income across all partners. **De minimis rule**: if the candidate year's aggregate deferral is less than 0.5 (half-month deferral) different from the partnership's current year, current year is retained (represented in module as months Г— 10 to enable integer comparison against threshold 5). **В§ 706(c) closing of partnership taxable year**: **В§ 706(c)(1) general rule** вҖ” partnership year does NOT close on the death of a partner, entry of a new partner, partial liquidation of a partner's interest, or sale/exchange of a partner's PARTIAL interest; **В§ 706(c)(2) exception** вҖ” partnership year DOES close with respect to a partner whose ENTIRE interest terminates (death, complete liquidation, complete sale or exchange). The partner takes a short-year distributive share through the termination date вҖ” the operative trigger event for downstream В§ 736 / В§ 743 / В§ 751 analysis. **В§ 706(d) varying-interest allocations**: when a partner's interest changes during the taxable year, distributive share is determined by one of two methods: (a) **Interim Closing Method** (default) вҖ” partnership "closes the books" as of the change date; allocations to changing partner are determined as if a short partnership year ended on that date; (b) **Proration Method** вҖ” proportional allocation based on time ownership; available ONLY if elected in writing by the partners under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.706-4(f). **Ten-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, PartnershipYearMajorityInterestTestApplies, PartnershipYearPrincipalPartnerTestApplies, PartnershipYearLeastAggregateDeferralTestApplies, PartnershipYearDeMinimisCurrentYearKept, EntireInterestTerminationPartnershipYearClosesForPartner, PartialInterestChangePartnershipYearDoesNotClose, InterimClosingMethodAppliedForVaryingInterests, ProrationMethodAppliedWithWrittenAgreement, ViolationProrationMethodWithoutWrittenAgreement. **Three analysis types**: PartnershipYearDetermination, PartnerTerminationEvent, VaryingInterestAllocation. **Three varying-interest method states**: NotApplicable, InterimClosingMethodDefault, ProrationMethodIfElected. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-706`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **majority interest test satisfied first tier applies** (50%+ trigger), **principal partner test applies when majority fails** (5%+ fallback), **least aggregate deferral test applies when both first tiers fail**, **de minimis keeps current year when below half** (0.4 months change retained), **de minimis at exactly 5 (0.5) steps to least aggregate deferral** (strict greater-than-or-equal-to threshold boundary), **entire interest termination closes partnership year for partner** (В§ 706(c)(2) trigger), **partial interest change does NOT close partnership year** (В§ 706(c)(1) default), **interim closing method default applied**, **proration method with written agreement applied**, **proration method WITHOUT written agreement violation** (per se procedural violation; default interim-closing applies instead), **not applicable returns default**, **citations pin В§ 706 subsections** (a + (b)(1)(B)(i)/(ii)/(iii) + (c)(1)/(c)(2) + (d)), **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.706-1 + В§ 1.706-4 + В§ 1.706-4(f)**, **constant pins majority 50%**, **constant pins principal partner 5%**, **constant pins de minimis half-month Г— 10** (the integer-encoded 0.5 threshold), **hierarchy majority takes precedence over principal partner** (strict-order test), **hierarchy principal partner takes precedence over least aggregate** (cascading-test verification). Trader-partnership critical because В§ 706 timing rules govern every partner exit, entry, and interest-change event in a trader-partnership вҖ” and the proration-method-without-written-agreement violation is one of the most common audit-discovered procedural defects, especially in trader partnerships that pivot mid-year on managing-partner exits or new-LP entries and try to apply proportional time-based allocations without the written-agreement formality. The В§ 706(c)(2) entire-interest termination is the load-bearing trigger event for В§ 736 retiring-partner-payment characterization, В§ 743 transferee basis adjustment, В§ 751 hot-assets character recharacterization вҖ” all three downstream provisions depend on В§ 706(c)(2) closure to pin the timing of the recognized gain/loss. Sibling cluster: `section_704c` (precontribution gain вҖ” В§ 706(d) varying-interest method determines how В§ 704(c) gain is allocated across the year), `section_707` (iter 574 вҖ” disguised sales and guaranteed payments interact with В§ 706(a) timing), `section_731` (distribution rules вҖ” В§ 706(c)(2) closure triggers В§ 731(a)(1) gain recognition), `section_736` (iter 582 вҖ” retiring partner В§ 736 character analysis depends on В§ 706(c)(2) entire-interest termination), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment under В§ 754 election only triggered on entire-interest В§ 706(c)(2) sale), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” hot-asset recharacterization on В§ 706(c)(2) entire-interest sale).

`traderview-expense::section_736` is the **IRC В§ 736 retiring/deceased partner payment characterization module** (iter 582) вҖ” direct Subchapter K companion to `section_707` (iter 574 partner-partnership transactions вҖ” В§ 707(c) guaranteed payment definition that В§ 736(a)(2) cross-references), `section_731` (distribution recognition that В§ 736(b) invokes), `section_732` (distributee basis that В§ 736(b) invokes), `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets recharacterization), `section_737` (iter 580 mixing-bowl recognition). В§ 736 bifurcates all liquidating payments to a retiring or deceased partner between two distinct characterizations with profoundly different tax consequences. **В§ 736(a) payments**: payments NOT in exchange for partner's interest in partnership property. Subdivides further: **В§ 736(a)(1)** вҖ” payment determined WITH REGARD TO partnership income вҶ’ treated as DISTRIBUTIVE SHARE of partnership income (ordinary character; reduces other partners' distributive shares; no partnership deduction); **В§ 736(a)(2)** вҖ” payment determined WITHOUT REGARD TO partnership income вҶ’ treated as В§ 707(c) GUARANTEED PAYMENT (ordinary character; В§ 162 partnership deduction). **В§ 736(b) payments**: payments in exchange for partner's interest in partnership PROPERTY. Capital character to recipient; treated as В§ 731/В§ 732 distribution; NOT deductible by partnership. **В§ 736(b)(2) special rule for service partnerships with general partner retiring/dying** (post-DRA 1993): unrealized receivables under В§ 751(c) and goodwill (except as partnership agreement provides for payment) are NOT treated as partnership property вҖ” they fall back into В§ 736(a) ordinary-income treatment. Designed to preserve the historical pre-1993 flexibility for service-firm partner buyouts (law firms, accounting firms, medical practices). **В§ 736(b)(3) service partnership definition**: capital is NOT a material income-producing factor вҖ” bright-line test that excludes most capital-intensive businesses including trading firms with substantial portfolio capital under management. **DRA 1993 В§ 13262 amendment** (Pub. L. 103-66): applicable to partners retiring or dying on or after January 5, 1993. Before DRA 1993, the В§ 736(b)(2) special rule applied to ALL partnerships. After DRA 1993, the special rule applies ONLY to (1) service partnerships (capital is not a material income-producing factor) where (2) the retiring/deceased partner is a general partner. For capital-intensive partnerships (which is most trader partnerships), the DRA 1993 amendment locks all payment-for-unrealized-receivables and payment-for-goodwill into В§ 736(b) capital-character treatment, not the В§ 736(a) ordinary-character alternative. **Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section736aDistributiveShareDeterminedWithIncome, Section736aGuaranteedPaymentDeterminedWithoutIncome, Section736bCapitalPropertyExchange, Section736b2ServicePartnershipGeneralPartnerOrdinaryFallback, DraPre1993PartnershipPropertyRules, ViolationMischaracterizedAs736bCapitalShouldBeOrdinary. **Four character types**: NotApplicable, OrdinaryDistributiveShare736a1, OrdinaryGuaranteedPayment736a2, CapitalProperty736b. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-736`. **Twenty tests pin**: **capital-intensive partnership property payment is В§ 736(b) capital** (the dominant trader-partnership case), **payment determined with income no property is В§ 736(a)(1) distributive share**, **payment determined without income no property is В§ 736(a)(2) guaranteed payment**, **service partnership general partner goodwill falls back to В§ 736(a)** (the DRA 1993 surviving service-firm special rule), **service partnership general partner unrealized receivables falls back to В§ 736(a)** (parallel branch), **service partnership goodwill WITH partnership agreement provision stays В§ 736(b) capital** (the carve-out from the carve-out), **service partnership LIMITED partner no special rule stays В§ 736(b)** (general-partner-only DRA 1993 limitation), **capital-intensive general partner no special rule** (service-partnership-only DRA 1993 limitation), **pre-DRA 1993 year 1992 pre-amendment rules apply** (statutory effective-date boundary), **DRA 1993 year 1993 amendment applies** (immediate post-effective-date), **deceased partner treated same as retiring for В§ 736**, **not applicable returns default**, **general partner В§ 736(a) payment subject to SE tax**, **limited partner В§ 736(a) payment NOT subject to SE tax** (В§ 1402(a)(13) interaction), **citations pin В§ 736(a)/(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)/(b)(2)/(b)(3)**, **citations pin DRA 1993 В§ 13262 + Pub. L. 103-66**, **citations pin В§ 751(c) + В§ 707(c) + В§ 731 + В§ 732 cross-refs**, **constant pins DRA 1993 effective date (year + month + day)**, **constant pins DRA 1993 amendment section 13262**, **very large payment saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense). Trader-partnership critical because В§ 736 is the single most consequential partnership-tax provision at the partner-exit moment вҖ” it determines whether a $10M+ retiring-partner buyout is reported as $10M ordinary income (В§ 736(a)) or $10M capital proceeds (В§ 736(b)) with $10M+ basis offset, which can swing the recipient's federal tax bill from ~37% Г— $10M = $3.7M down to 0% on the entire payment if outside basis equals the payment amount. The DRA 1993 amendment is the single most-litigated provision in this area вҖ” service-firm trader partnerships (RIA partnerships, fund-of-funds, family-office structures with predominantly fee income) often qualify under В§ 736(b)(3) capital-not-material test, but pure-trading partnerships rarely do, locking them into В§ 736(b) capital treatment with no recharacterization flexibility. Sibling cluster: `section_707` (В§ 707(c) guaranteed payment definition that В§ 736(a)(2) directly cross-references), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” В§ 751 hot-asset characterization applies on sale of partnership interest, distinct from В§ 736 liquidating-payment characterization), `section_731` (distribution gain/loss recognition that В§ 736(b) invokes), `section_732` (distributee basis in distributed property that В§ 736(b) invokes), `section_754` (basis adjustment election вҖ” interacts with В§ 736(b) on remaining partners' inside basis), `section_734` (iter 578 вҖ” distributee basis adjustment may apply to В§ 736(b) portion if В§ 754 elected), `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transferee basis adjustment in alternative redemption-vs-sale structure).

`traderview-expense::section_737` is the **IRC В§ 737 mixing-bowl recognition for contributing partner module** (iter 580) вҖ” direct Subchapter K anti-abuse companion to `section_704c` (precontribution gain allocation), `section_734` (iter 578 distributee basis adjustment), `section_731` (distribution nonrecognition default), `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets вҖ” applies before В§ 737 per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.737-2). В§ 737 is one half of the Subchapter K "anti-mixing-bowl" anti-abuse regime вҖ” the **contributing-partner-side** recognition rule triggered when a partner who contributed appreciated В§ 704(c) property receives a distribution of OTHER property (not money) within 7 years of the original contribution. The other half is **В§ 704(c)(1)(B)** вҖ” the noncontributing-partner-side recognition rule triggered when the contributed property itself is distributed to a different partner within 7 years. Together В§ 737 and В§ 704(c)(1)(B) prevent tax-free property swaps disguised as partnership contributions and distributions (the "mixing bowl" вҖ” putting appreciated property in, taking different appreciated property out, both partners walking away with economic gain without recognition). **Statutory chain**: originally enacted by **Energy Policy Act of 1992** (Pub. L. 102-486) with a 5-year recognition window; extended to **7 years** by **Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997** (Pub. L. 105-34). **В§ 737(a)(1) recognition rule**: contributing partner recognizes gain on a distribution of property OTHER THAN MONEY equal to the LESSER of: (1) the **net precontribution gain** (В§ 737(b)) вҖ” the В§ 704(c)(1)(B) gain that would be recognized if all property held by the partnership immediately before the distribution that was contributed by the distributee partner WITHIN 7 YEARS were distributed to another partner; or (2) the **excess** of the FMV of the distributed property OVER the adjusted basis of the partner's interest in the partnership (the so-called "excess distribution amount"). Money distributions are NOT subject to В§ 737 вҖ” they fall under В§ 731(a)(1) gain-on-cash-distribution rules instead, an important statutory carve-out. **В§ 737(c) basis adjustments**: matching basis adjustments to (a) the partner's interest in the partnership and (b) the contributed property held by the partnership, to prevent double-counting on subsequent disposition. **Coordination with В§ 751** per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.737-2: В§ 751 hot-asset character applies FIRST, then В§ 737 layers on top of the remaining capital-character portion. **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoRecognitionDistributionOutside7YearWindow, NoRecognitionDistributionIsMoneyOnly, NoRecognitionNoNetPrecontributionGain, NoRecognitionExcessFmvOverOutsideBasisIsZero, RecognitionGainNetPrecontributionLesserOfBranch, RecognitionGainExcessFmvOverOutsideBasisLesserOfBranch, RecognitionGainBranchesEqual. **Four lesser-of-branch states**: NotApplicable, NetPrecontributionGainIsLesser, ExcessFmvOverOutsideBasisIsLesser, BothAreZero. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-737`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **within 7-year window net precontrib lesser recognized** ($5M net precontrib + $7M excess FMV вҶ’ $5M recognized), **excess FMV lesser than net precontrib recognized** ($5M net precontrib + $2M excess вҶ’ $2M recognized), **distribution outside 7-year window no recognition** (85 months > 84), **distribution exactly at 84 months still in window** (statutory вүӨ reading), **distribution at 85 months outside window** (boundary), **money-only distribution no recognition** (В§ 731(a)(1) carve-out), **no net precontribution gain no recognition**, **excess FMV zero when FMV below outside basis no recognition** ($1M FMV вҲ’ $5M outside basis = $0 excess), **branches equal pick either record correct amount** ($4M net precontrib = $4M excess FMV вҶ’ $4M either way), **citations pin В§ 737(a)/(b)/(c)/(d)**, **citations pin В§ 704(c)(1)(B) + В§ 731(a)(1) coordination**, **citations pin Energy Policy Act of 1992 + Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.737-1/-2/-3**, **constant pins 7-year lookback**, **constant pins 84-month lookback**, **constant pins Energy Policy Act 1992 year**, **constant pins TRA 1997 extension year**, **very large net precontrib saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense), **FMV equals outside basis no excess no recognition**. Trader-partnership critical because the В§ 737 mixing-bowl rule is the partnership-tax provision most often missed by trader-tax-status filers organized as partnerships вҖ” when a managing partner contributes appreciated securities/portfolio to the partnership and receives an in-kind distribution of different appreciated securities within 7 years, the lesser-of recognition is triggered. The 7-year window is significantly longer than most trader-partnership members' planning horizon, and the recognition exposure attaches at the distribution moment without any "but-for" or "entrepreneurial-risk" defense available (unlike В§ 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sales). Sibling cluster: `section_704c` (parent rule for precontribution gain вҖ” В§ 737 derives its net-precontribution-gain measurement from В§ 704(c)(1)(B)), `section_731` (default distribution rule that В§ 737 overrides), `section_732` (distributee basis in distributed property вҖ” В§ 737(c) basis adjustment downstream), `section_734` (parallel distributee-basis adjustment but operates on remaining partnership property, not contributing-partner's outside basis), `section_743` (transferee-basis adjustment вҖ” different trigger event but same В§ 754 election machinery), `section_751` (hot assets вҖ” В§ 751 applies BEFORE В§ 737 per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.737-2), `section_707` (disguised sales вҖ” alternative anti-abuse path that can trigger before В§ 737 if the timing pattern qualifies under В§ 707(a)(2)(B) instead).

`traderview-expense::section_734` is the **IRC В§ 734 distributee basis adjustment module** (iter 578) вҖ” direct distribution-side companion to `section_743` (iter 576 transferee transfer-side basis adjustment). Together В§ 743 and В§ 734 complete the В§ 754 election picture: В§ 743 handles partnership-interest TRANSFERS (sale/exchange/death), В§ 734 handles partnership-property DISTRIBUTIONS. **В§ 734(a) general rule**: no adjustment to basis of remaining partnership property on a distribution. **В§ 734(b) exception**: adjustment REQUIRED if EITHER (1) a В§ 754 election is in effect for the partnership OR (2) the distribution results in a "substantial basis reduction" under В§ 734(d). **В§ 734(b)(1) INCREASE** remaining partnership property basis by the sum of: (A) gain recognized by distributee under В§ 731(a)(1) (cash + marketable securities distribution in excess of outside basis); PLUS (B) excess of partnership's adjusted basis in distributed property OVER distributee's basis in that property as determined under В§ 732 (step-up at distributee level must be matched by step-up at partnership level to preserve the inside-outside basis equality). **В§ 734(b)(2) DECREASE** remaining partnership property basis by the sum of: (A) loss recognized by distributee under В§ 731(a)(2) (liquidating distribution of cash + receivables + inventory only, below outside basis); PLUS (B) excess of distributee's В§ 732 basis in distributed property OVER partnership's adjusted basis in that property (step-down at distributee level requires matching step-down at partnership level). **В§ 734(d) substantial basis reduction** (AJCA 2004 В§ 833 added this prong, applicable to distributions after October 22, 2004): the sum of amounts described in В§ 734(b)(2)(A) and (B) exceeds **$250,000**. If substantial basis reduction exists, the В§ 734(b) adjustment is MANDATORY even without a В§ 754 election in effect вҖ” designed to prevent the partnership from cherry-picking when to elect basis adjustments and avoid the loss-trafficking that prompted AJCA 2004's anti-abuse provisions. **Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, DistributionWithoutAdjustmentNo754NoSubstantialBasisReduction, Section754PositiveIncreaseGainOrBasisStepUp, Section754NegativeDecreaseLossOrBasisStepDown, Section754NoNetAdjustment, SubstantialBasisReductionMandatoryAdjustmentEvenWithout754. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-734`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **current distribution with gain and step-up increase** ($1M gain + $2M property step-up = $3M increase), **liquidating distribution with loss and step-down decrease** ($500K loss + $2M step-down = $2.5M decrease, net вҲ’$2.5M), **distribution with no gain no loss no basis diff no net adjustment**, **distribution without В§ 754 no substantial reduction no adjustment** (В§ 734(a) default), **substantial basis reduction above $250K mandatory adjustment** ($300K loss triggers), **substantial basis reduction boundary exactly $250K NOT triggered** (strict greater-than reading), **substantial basis reduction boundary $250K plus one cent triggered**, **substantial basis reduction via property step-down alone** ($300K step-down without distributee loss still triggers), **substantial basis reduction via sum of loss and step-down** ($150K loss + $200K step-down = $350K aggregate triggers), **not applicable returns zero**, **citations pin В§ 734(a)/(b)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(d)**, **citations pin В§ 731(a)(1)/(a)(2) + В§ 732 + В§ 754 + В§ 755**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.734-1 + AJCA 2004 В§ 833**, **constant pins substantial basis reduction $250K** (25,000,000 cents), **very large gain saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense), **liquidating distribution treated same as current for В§ 734**, **gain only no property basis difference increase equals gain** ($1M gain + $0 step-up = $1M increase), **step-up only no distributee gain increase equals step-up** ($0 gain + $2M step-up = $2M increase). Trader-partnership critical because the В§ 734 distribution-side adjustment is what enables a partnership to do partner buyouts via property distribution while preserving inside-outside basis equality for the remaining partners. Without В§ 734(b), a buyout via distribution would leave the remaining partners with phantom built-in gain or built-in loss in the remaining partnership property вҖ” which would be recognized on next sale. The substantial-basis-reduction prong specifically targets loss-trafficking partnerships that distribute high-basis low-value assets to one partner while keeping the offsetting loss in the partnership for the benefit of remaining partners; this is the AJCA 2004 В§ 833 anti-abuse rule that closes the loophole that previously required a В§ 754 election to be in place. Sibling cluster: `section_743` (iter 576 вҖ” transfer-side basis adjustment companion), `section_754` (election mechanics вҖ” В§ 734 and В§ 743 are both triggered by the same election), `section_731` (distribution gain/loss recognition rules), `section_732` (distributee partner basis in distributed property), `section_751` (iter 572 вҖ” hot assets interaction on distribution), `section_707` (iter 574 вҖ” disguised sale recharacterization can trigger В§ 734 if recast distribution is then treated as a sale).

`traderview-expense::section_743` is the **IRC В§ 743 transferee partner basis adjustment module** (iter 576) вҖ” direct Subchapter K companion to `section_754` (basis-adjustment election mechanics), `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets), `section_707` (iter 574 partner-partnership transactions), `section_731` (distribution nonrecognition default). **В§ 743(a) general rule**: no basis adjustment to partnership property on transfer of partnership interest by sale, exchange, or death of partner. **В§ 743(b) exception**: basis adjustment REQUIRED if EITHER (1) a В§ 754 election is in effect for the partnership OR (2) the partnership has a "substantial built-in loss" immediately after the transfer (TCJA 2017 broadened scope to make built-in-loss adjustment MANDATORY even absent В§ 754 election). **В§ 743(b) adjustment math**: INCREASE inside basis by excess of transferee's OUTSIDE basis OVER transferee's proportionate share of INSIDE basis (i.e., when transferee paid premium for inherent appreciation); DECREASE by the reverse (when transferee paid bargain for inherent depreciation). The adjustment is **partner-specific** вҖ” applies only to the transferee partner; does not affect non-transferee partners' allocations. **В§ 743(d) substantial built-in loss two-prong test** (TCJA 2017 В§ 13502 added the transferee-specific second prong, effective transfers after December 31, 2017): (1) **partnership-level prong** вҖ” partnership's adjusted basis in property EXCEEDS its fair market value by MORE THAN $250,000; (2) **transferee-specific prong** (post-TCJA only) вҖ” transferee partner would be allocated a loss of MORE THAN $250,000 if the partnership sold all assets at FMV immediately after transfer. Purpose: prevent double benefit of built-in losses through partnership-interest transfers. **Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, TransferWithoutAdjustmentNo754NoBuiltInLoss, Section754ElectionPositiveStepUpInsideBasisIncrease, Section754ElectionNegativeStepDownInsideBasisDecrease, Section754ElectionNoNetAdjustment, SubstantialBuiltInLossMandatoryAdjustmentEvenWithout754, TransferAtDeathSection754SteppedUpOutsideBasisIfElection (interacts with В§ 1014 outside-basis step-up). Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-743`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **sale with В§ 754 outside above inside step up** ($10M outside вҲ’ $7M inside = $3M increase), **sale with В§ 754 outside below inside step down** ($5M outside вҲ’ $7M inside = вҲ’$2M decrease), **sale with В§ 754 outside equal inside no net adjustment**, **sale without В§ 754 no built-in loss no adjustment** (В§ 743(a) default), **substantial built-in loss partnership prong mandatory adjustment** ($50M basis вҲ’ $10M FMV = $40M built-in loss > $250K threshold), **substantial built-in loss partnership boundary exactly $250K not met** (strict greater-than reading), **substantial built-in loss partnership boundary $250K plus one cent met**, **substantial built-in loss transferee prong TCJA 2017 post** ($30M transferee-allocable loss in 2026 triggers), **substantial built-in loss transferee prong pre-TCJA 2017 not applicable** (2017 itself excluded вҖ” "after December 31, 2017"), **death of partner with В§ 754 steps up outside basis** (В§ 1014 interaction), **not applicable returns zero adjustment**, **citations pin В§ 743(a)/(b)/(d) + В§ 754 + В§ 755**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.743-1 + TCJA 2017 В§ 13502**, **constant pins partnership prong $250K threshold** (25,000,000 cents), **constant pins transferee prong $250K threshold**, **constant pins TCJA 2017 effective year cutoff**, **very large outside basis no overflow** (u64::MAX/2 i128 defense), **both substantial built-in loss prongs met only recorded once**, **death with В§ 754 step down still records negative adjustment**. Trader-partnership critical because trader-tax-status filers organized as partnerships routinely face partner-buyout transactions (managing-partner exits, family-office succession, fund manager replacement) вҖ” and the В§ 754 election is the difference between the buying partner getting an immediate stepped-up basis in trader-partnership securities (and being able to mark-to-market against that stepped-up basis going forward) vs. inheriting the seller's lower historic basis with embedded gain. The election once made is permanent (revocable only with IRS consent under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.754-1(c)) вҖ” a one-time choice that compounds across every subsequent partnership-property disposition. Sibling cluster: `section_754` (election mechanics вҖ” the foundational gateway), `section_751` (hot assets вҖ” В§ 743(b) adjustment interacts with В§ 751 ordinary character allocation on transfer), `section_731` (distribution nonrecognition вҖ” В§ 743(b) parallel on distribution side via В§ 734), `section_752` (liability assumption affects outside-basis calculation), `section_704c` (built-in gain allocation вҖ” В§ 743(b) downstream basis affects future В§ 704(c) gain).

`traderview-expense::section_707` is the **IRC В§ 707 partner-partnership transactions module** (iter 574) вҖ” direct Subchapter K companion to `section_751` (iter 572 hot assets) and `section_704c` (precontribution gain allocation). В§ 707 contains four distinct operative paragraphs, each of which overrides a different Subchapter K default rule. **В§ 707(a)**: partner-partnership transactions are treated as if between non-partners (capital-asset purchase/sale, lease, lending, etc.) when partner is NOT acting in capacity as a partner вҖ” preserves arm's-length character for genuine non-partner transactions. **В§ 707(a)(2)(A)**: payments to a partner FOR SERVICES that are contingent on partnership income may be recharacterized as either a В§ 707(c) guaranteed payment or a distributive share depending on facts and circumstances вҖ” prevents service partners from converting ordinary compensation into capital character by routing payment through allocation. **В§ 707(a)(2)(B) вҖ” DISGUISED SALES**: a contribution of property to the partnership followed by a related distribution is recast as a sale to the partnership. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.707-3(c)(1)** creates the **two-year presumption**: distributions within 24 months of contribution are PRESUMED sales unless facts and circumstances clearly establish otherwise. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.707-3(d)** creates the OPPOSITE presumption for transfers more than 24 months apart: presumed NOT sales unless facts clearly establish they are. **Two-prong test**: (1) but-for вҖ” the transfer of consideration would not have been made but for the transfer of property; (2) entrepreneurial-risk independence вҖ” subsequent transfer does not depend on partnership-operations risk. **В§ 707(b)**: losses disallowed between a partner who owns directly or indirectly more than 50% of the partnership capital or profits interest and the partnership, or between two partnerships in which the same person owns more than 50%. **В§ 707(c) вҖ” GUARANTEED PAYMENTS**: payments to a partner FOR SERVICES rendered or for USE OF CAPITAL, determined WITHOUT REGARD TO partnership income; treated as ordinary income to recipient and В§ 162 ordinary deduction to partnership. **В§ 1402(a)(13) SECA interaction**: limited-partner SECA exclusion does NOT apply to guaranteed payments for services вҖ” limited partner receiving GP for services is still subject to self-employment tax; only GP for USE OF CAPITAL to a limited partner is excluded from SE tax. **Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, GuaranteedPaymentSection707cOrdinaryWithSeTax, GuaranteedPaymentSection707cOrdinaryNoSeTaxLimitedPartnerCapital, DisguisedSaleWithin2YearsPresumedSale, DisguisedSaleMoreThan2YearsPresumedNotSale, LossDisallowedRelatedPartnersUnder707b, LossAllowedNonRelatedPartiesUnder707b, ArmsLengthTransactionUnder707aNoRecharacterization, ViolationGuaranteedPaymentMisreportedAsDistributiveShare (payment determined with regard to income but taxpayer reported as GP). **Six characterizations**: NotApplicable, OrdinaryIncomeGuaranteedPayment, DistributiveShareNotGuaranteedPayment, SaleProceedsRecastUnder707a2B, DisallowedLossUnder707b, NonPartnerCapitalTransactionUnder707a. **Six transaction kinds**: NotApplicable, GuaranteedPaymentForServicesOrCapital, DisguisedSaleAnalysis, RelatedPartyLossSale, ArmsLengthOther, PartnerServicesContingentOnIncome. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-707`. **Twenty-one tests pin**: **guaranteed payment for services is ordinary with SE tax** ($100K GP, ordinary, SE tax = true), **limited partner GP for capital no SE tax** ($100K, ordinary, SE tax = false вҖ” capital carve-out), **limited partner GP for services STILL SE tax per В§ 1402(a)(13)** (the key statutory exception), **payment determined with regard to income misreported as GP is violation**, **disguised sale within 2 years both prongs met presumed sale** (12 months + but-for + no entrepreneurial-risk independence), **disguised sale within 2 years prongs not met presumption rebutted** (12 months but missing prongs), **disguised sale more than 2 years opposite presumption not sale** (30 months), **two-year boundary 24 months still within window** (24 вүӨ 24), **two-year boundary 25 months outside window** (25 > 24), **related party loss above 50% disallowed under В§ 707(b)** (60% ownership), **related party 50% boundary loss allowed** (50% not "more than"), **related party 51% boundary loss disallowed** (51% > 50%), **arm's-length other transaction under В§ 707(a)**, **partner services contingent on income recharacterized as distributive**, **not applicable transaction returns default**, **citations pin all В§ 707 subsections** (a + (a)(2)(A) + (a)(2)(B) + (b) + (c)), **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.707-3 + (c)(1) + (d) + В§ 1.707-4**, **citations pin В§ 1402(a)(13) limited-partner SECA**, **constant pins 24-month disguised sale window**, **constant pins 50% related-party threshold**, **very large GP amount saturating no overflow** (u64::MAX defense). Trader-partnership critical because trader-tax-status filers organized as partnerships routinely structure manager compensation as guaranteed payments (avoiding the IRS-disfavored carried-interest characterization), receive use-of-capital GPs on contributed margin/securities accounts, and contribute appreciated portfolio property to partnerships вҖ” each pattern triggers a different В§ 707 paragraph and the wrong characterization is one of the most common audit-discovered partnership-tax errors. Sibling cluster: `section_751` (hot assets вҖ” В§ 707(c) GP can interact with В§ 751 character carve-out on partner exit), `section_704c` (built-in gain allocation вҖ” В§ 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sale converts what would have been deferred В§ 704(c) gain into immediate recognition), `section_731` (distribution nonrecognition default that В§ 707(a)(2)(B) overrides), `section_752` (liability assumption can constitute В§ 707(a)(2)(B) consideration), `section_754` (basis adjustment election downstream of В§ 707-recast transactions), `section_475f` (trader MTM election вҖ” MTM ordinary character compounds when В§ 707(c) GP layers on top).

`traderview-expense::section_751` is the **IRC В§ 751 unrealized receivables and inventory items ("hot assets") recharacterization module** (iter 572) вҖ” direct Subchapter K companion to `section_741` (capital character default), `section_731` (partnership distribution nonrecognition), `section_752` (partnership liabilities), `section_754` (basis adjustment election), `section_704c` (precontribution gain allocation). В§ 751 is the single most consequential override of the default capital-character rule in all of Subchapter K вҖ” it ensures that the ordinary character of the partnership's underlying hot assets passes through to the partner regardless of whether the partnership goes through a sale or a distribution. **В§ 751(a) вҖ” sale or exchange of partnership interest**: the portion of amount realized attributable to the partner's share of unrealized receivables and inventory items is treated as ORDINARY income (gain or loss), and the remainder receives В§ 741 capital character. For В§ 751(a) purposes, **ALL inventory is hot regardless of appreciation** вҖ” the "substantially appreciated" 120%-of-basis threshold applies ONLY to В§ 751(b) distributions. **В§ 751(b) вҖ” disproportionate distributions**: a partnership current or liquidating distribution that ALTERS the distributee partner's share of unrealized receivables or substantially appreciated inventory items is recast as a deemed sale or exchange between the distributee and the partnership of the disproportionate portion. **В§ 751(b)(3)(A) substantially appreciated threshold**: inventory is "substantially appreciated" only if FMV > 120% of adjusted basis. **В§ 751(c) unrealized receivables**: rights to payment for goods delivered/to-be-delivered + services rendered/to-be-rendered + В§ 1245/В§ 1250/В§ 1252/В§ 1254/В§ 617(d) recapture potential. **В§ 751(d) inventory items**: partnership inventory + property held primarily for sale to customers that would generate non-В§ 1231 ordinary character on sale. **Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, SaleAllCapitalNoHotAssetsAtRisk, SaleBifurcatedOrdinaryHotAssetsPlusCapitalRemainder, DistributionProportionateNoSection751bRecastNeeded, DistributionDisproportionateAlterShareOfHotAssetsRecast, ViolationFailedToBifurcateReportedAllCapital (taxpayer error вҖ” reported full sale as capital when В§ 751(a) bifurcation required). Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-751`. **Nineteen tests pin**: **sale with hot assets bifurcates correctly** ($10M realized вҲ’ $5M basis вҲ’ $1M receivables вҲ’ $2M inventory FMV + $1.5M inventory basis вҶ’ $1.5M ordinary + $3.5M capital), **sale with zero hot assets all capital** ($5M capital gain, no bifurcation), **sale taxpayer reported all capital is violation** (still shows the correct $1.5M ordinary portion that should have been reported), **sale inventory substantially appreciated flag set at 121%** of basis, **sale inventory at 120% not substantially appreciated** (statutory boundary), **sale inventory at 119% not substantially appreciated**, **sale all inventory is hot regardless of appreciation under В§ 751(a)** (sale of partnership interest with depreciated inventory still triggers bifurcation), **proportionate distribution no В§ 751(b) recast**, **disproportionate distribution with substantially appreciated recast** ($1.25M FMV / $1M basis = 125% > 120%, recast triggered), **disproportionate distribution inventory not substantially appreciated only receivables hot** ($1.1M FMV / $1M = 110% < 120%, only receivables count as hot), **liquidating distribution treated same as current for В§ 751(b)**, **not applicable transaction returns zero**, **negative capital loss recorded correctly** (i128 signed math, ordinary portion is u64, capital portion is signed), **citations pin В§ 751(a) + (b) + В§ 741 + В§ 731**, **citations pin В§ 751(c) + (d) + (b)(3)(A) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.751-1**, **citations pin IRS Notice 2006-14**, **constant pins substantially appreciated 120% threshold**, **very large hot assets saturating does not overflow** (u64::MAX defense), **zero inventory basis avoids division by zero** (defensive checked_div). Trader-partnership critical because trader-tax-status filers organized as partnerships (common for prop trading firms, fund-of-funds, family-office structures) have unrealized receivables in the form of accrued trading gains pending settlement + accrued management/performance fees pending payment + В§ 1245 recapture potential on trading-tech equipment вҖ” ALL of which become ordinary on a partner's exit under В§ 751(a), forcing bifurcation that taxpayers routinely miss without this module's diagnostic. Pairs with `section_475f` (trader MTM election) because MTM-electing trader partnerships report mark-to-market gains as ordinary, which compounds the В§ 751 hot-asset characterization at the partner-exit moment.

`traderview-expense::section_6039` is the **IRC В§ 6039 corporate reporting of ISO exercises and ESPP transfers module** (iter 570) вҖ” direct corporate-side companion to `section_422` (Incentive Stock Options employee tax treatment) and `section_423` (Employee Stock Purchase Plan employee tax treatment). Where В§ 422 and В§ 423 govern the EMPLOYEE tax treatment of statutory options, В§ 6039 imposes the CORPORATE reporting obligation that drives downstream employee basis-tracking via **Form 3921** (В§ 6039(a)(1) вҖ” one return per ISO exercise) and **Form 3922** (В§ 6039(a)(2) вҖ” one return per first transfer of legal title of ESPP-acquired shares where purchase price was less than FMV at grant or was not fixed/determinable at grant). **Deadlines**: employee Copy B by **January 31** of the year following exercise/transfer per В§ 6039(b); IRS Copy A by **February 28** if paper or **March 31** if filed electronically per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6039-2 + IRS Pub. 1220. **Mandatory e-filing threshold** under Treas. Reg. В§ 301.6011-2 as amended by T.D. 9972 (effective filings made in 2024 and later): **10 or more aggregate information returns** of any qualifying type (W-2, W-2G, 1042-S, 1094 series, 1095-B, 1095-C, 1097-BTC, 1098 series, 1099 series, 3921, 3922, 5498 series, 8027) triggers mandatory e-filing вҖ” a dramatic reduction from the prior 250-return threshold that swept most small employers into e-file scope. **Penalties under В§ 6721 cross-referenced by В§ 6039(c)** (2025 amounts): **$60/form** if late вүӨ 30 days; **$120/form** if late 31 days through August 1; **$310/form** if late after August 1 or complete failure to file; **intentional disregard** under В§ 6721(e) carries **NO MAXIMUM PENALTY** (per-form floor of $630 + IRS discretionary assessment with no cap вҖ” the highest-stakes information-return penalty in the Code). **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (no exercises/transfers), ComplianceTimelyFiledWithEmployeeCopyAndIrsCopy, ViolationEmployeeCopyBNotProvidedByJan31, ViolationLateFiledWithin30Days, ViolationLateFiledBetween31DaysAndAugust1, ViolationLateFiledAfterAugust1OrCompleteFailure, ViolationElectronicFilingRequiredButPaperFiled (per se В§ 6721 failure-to-file in required manner), ViolationIntentionalDisregardNoMaximumPenalty. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6039`. **Twenty-four tests pin**: **timely filing with e-file is compliant** (3 ISO + 5 ESPP = 8 forms, $0 penalty), **no ISO no ESPP is not applicable**, **late within 30 days $60/form** (15 days late Г— 8 forms = $480), **late 31 to August 1 $120/form** (60 days late), **late after August 1 $310/form** (200 days late), **complete failure to file max rate**, **employee Copy B not provided is per se violation** (note pins "January 31"), **aggregate threshold 10 or more forces electronic** (paper filing then violates), **aggregate under 10 paper filing acceptable**, **aggregate threshold boundary 9 returns not electronic required**, **intentional disregard no maximum penalty** (note pins "no maximum"), **late 30-day boundary within 30 window**, **late 31-day boundary steps to $120/form**, **citations pin В§ 6039 subsections** (a)(1) + (a)(2) + (b) + (c), **citations pin В§ 6721 penalty schedule + T.D. 9972**, **citations pin Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6039-1 + В§ 1.6039-2**, **constant pins electronic filing threshold of 10 returns**, **constant pins employee Copy B Jan 31 deadline**, **constant pins paper IRS Feb 28 deadline**, **constant pins electronic IRS Mar 31 deadline**, **constant pins $60/form late within 30 days**, **constant pins $120/form late 31 days to Aug 1**, **constant pins $310/form after Aug 1 or no file**, **very large form count saturating does not overflow** (u32::MAX defense on aggregate). Trader audit-defense companion to `section_422` (ISO qualifying-disposition vs disqualifying-disposition tax treatment), `section_423` (ESPP qualified-disposition treatment), `section_421` (statutory option framework), `section_424` (constructive ownership rules), `section_56` (AMT preference items on ISO exercise), `section_53` (AMT credit recovery), `section_475f` (trader MTM election вҖ” same В§ 6721 penalty schedule applies to all В§ 60xx information-return failures).

`traderview-expense::section_475f` is the **IRC В§ 475(f) trader mark-to-market election mechanics module** (iter 568) вҖ” direct companion to `section_475c2` (dealer-vs-trader-vs-investor classifier). Where В§ 475(c)(2) decides who is ELIGIBLE to elect, В§ 475(f) pins the ELECTION MECHANICS for trader-tax-status filers: April-15 individual / March-15 entity election-statement deadline (UNEXTENDED вҖ” filing a 6-month extension does NOT extend the election deadline per IRS Topic 429); mandatory Form 3115 (Application for Change in Accounting Method) attached to the year-of-election return under Rev. Proc. 2025-23 В§ 24.01; full В§ 1091 wash-sale-rule exemption (the most valuable downstream effect вҖ” wash-sale losses are deductible in the year incurred without 30-day repurchase tracking); removal of the $3,000 В§ 1211(b) capital-loss cap with UNLIMITED ordinary-loss passthrough against other ordinary income (no carryforward required); and the **5-year revocation lockout** under Rev. Proc. 99-17 + Rev. Proc. 2025-23 В§ 24.02 (once revoked, cannot re-elect for 5 tax years). **Trader-tax-status floors (substantial + regular + continuous test per Endicott v. Commissioner, T.C. Memo. 2013-199 + IRS Pub. 550)**: ~720 trades/year and ~4 hours per trading day вҖ” facts-and-circumstances thresholds the IRS applies in audit, NOT statutory minimums. **Newly-formed-entity first-year carveout**: Rev. Proc. 99-17 allows internal-books election statement within 2.5 months of formation. **Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, TraderQualifiesElectionTimelyForm3115FiledOptimal, TraderQualifiesElectionMissedDeadlineFatalLossUntilNextTaxYear (the most common audit-discovered failure вҖ” "election" claimed but no statement filed by 4/15), TraderQualifiesElectionFiledButForm3115MissingAccountingMethodViolation (election statement OK but Form 3115 omitted вҖ” accounting-method change incomplete), TraderDoesNotQualifyTtsCannotElect475f, PriorElectionRevokedWithin5YearsLockedCannotReElect, NewEntityFirstYearInternalBooksElectionWindow2_5Months. **Five filer-entity types**: NotApplicable, IndividualSchedC, SCorp, Partnership, NewlyFormedEntityFirstYear. **Three TTS qualification states**: QualifiesTtsSubstantialRegularContinuous, DoesNotQualifyInsufficientActivity, BorderlineFactsAndCircumstancesAuditRisk (half-floor threshold). **Three election-statement filing statuses** + **two Form 3115 statuses** + **three prior-revocation statuses**. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-475f`. Eighteen tests pin: **timely election with Form 3115 is optimal** (full $5M loss passthrough, wash-sale exemption, $3K cap removed), **missed April 15 deadline fatal for individual** (note pins "April"), **missed March 15 deadline for entity pins "March"** (S-corp filer), **election filed but Form 3115 missing is accounting-method violation**, **does not qualify TTS below trade floor** (100 trades, 1 hour/day), **prior revocation within 5 years locks re-election**, **newly-formed entity internal-books 2.5-month window**, **loss below $3K cap uses actual loss when no election**, **loss above $3K cap clamped to $3,000 when no election**, **borderline TTS does not block election path** (400 trades, 3 hours/day still gets MTM), **citations pin В§ 475(f) + Form 3115 + Rev. Proc. 99-17 + Rev. Proc. 2025-23**, **citations pin В§ 1091 wash-sale + В§ 1211(b) cap**, **citations pin Endicott v. Commissioner case law**, **constant pins 720 trades floor**, **constant pins 4 hours per day**, **constant pins 5-year revocation lockout**, **constant pins $3,000 capital loss cap in cents (300,000)**, **very large loss with election passes through without overflow** (u64::MAX saturating defense). Trader-critical workflow: the В§ 475(c)(2) classifier tells the user whether they CAN elect; В§ 475(f) tells them HOW (deadline + 3115 + revocation lockout) and shows downstream loss-deductibility numbers under each branch. Trader audit-defense companion to `section_274` (meals/entertainment), `section_469` (passive activity), `section_1411` (NIIT), `section_481` (accounting method change), `section_199a` (QBI for trader-pass-through).

`traderview-expense::section_475c2` is the **IRC В§475(c)(2) dealer-in-securities classification module** вҖ” the load-bearing meta-classification that drives every downstream tax treatment in the system. Returns one of **four mutually-exclusive classifications**: `Dealer` / `TraderWithMtmElection` / `TraderWithoutMtmElection` / `Investor`. The classification controls: wash-sale rule applicability (В§1091), $3k capital loss cap (В§1211), ordinary-vs-capital character, deductibility of trading expenses (Schedule C above-the-line vs. miscellaneous itemized below-the-line вҖ” the latter TCJA-suspended 2018-2025), and which В§163 interest-limit applies (В§163(d) investment-interest for investors vs. В§163(j) business-interest for traders) ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 475](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/475), [IRS Topic 429 вҖ” Traders in Securities](https://www.irs.gov/taxtopics/tc429)).

**В§475(c)(2) dealer-in-securities definition** вҖ” taxpayer is a dealer if EITHER prong is satisfied:

- **Customer prong (В§ 475(c)(1)(A))**: regularly purchases securities from OR sells securities to customers in the ordinary course of a trade or business.
- **Inventory prong (В§ 475(c)(1)(B))**: regularly offers to enter into, assume, offset, assign, or otherwise terminate positions in securities WITH CUSTOMERS in the ordinary course of a trade or business.

Both prongs are gated on having CUSTOMERS вҖ” the distinguishing feature of dealers vs. traders. A trader who has zero customers and trades exclusively for their own account can never satisfy either prong under В§475(c)(2) ([Cornell LII 26 CFR В§ 1.475(c)-1](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.475(c)-1)).

**Negligible-sales exception** (Treas. Reg.
