В§ 1.475(c)-1(c)(1)): a taxpayer who regularly PURCHASES from customers but makes only negligible sales of the securities so acquired is NOT a dealer вҖ” UNLESS (a) the taxpayer elects dealer treatment or (b) accounts for any В§475(c)(2) security as inventory under В§471. Pinned by `negligible_sales_exception_blocks_dealer_classification` + the two yield-to exceptions (election and inventory).

**Trader prongs** (no statutory definition; case-law summarized in IRS Topic 429 вҖ” ALL THREE required, AND no customers AND own-account):

| Prong                                | Module floor                            |
|--------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Short-term profit motive             | `primary_income_source = ShortTermPriceSwings` AND avg holding period вүӨ 31 days |
| Substantial activity                 | вүҘ **720 trades/year** (~3/day across ~250 business days) |
| Continuous and regular trading       | вүҘ **175 trading days/year** (~70% of trading-day calendar) |

Failing any prong demotes the taxpayer to INVESTOR. Module surfaces each prong individually as `trader_short_term_profit_motive` / `trader_substantial_activity` / `trader_continuous_and_regular` so callers can identify which prong failed. Floors are conservative module heuristics вҖ” actual case-law thresholds are litigated annually in Tax Court; callers seeking aggressive trader status should pin the underlying record (Form 4797 attachments, contemporaneous trade logs).

**В§475(f) MTM election effects** (only available to TRADERS вҖ” not dealers, not investors):

- Gains/losses вҶ’ ORDINARY (not capital)
- Wash sale rules (В§1091) DO NOT apply
- $3k capital loss limit (В§1211) DOES NOT apply
- Trader expenses deductible above-the-line on Schedule C
- Election filed by attaching a statement to a timely-filed prior-year return (a 2026 election is made on the 2025 return by 2025-04-15)

**Investor flag-ignored invariant**: setting `has_section_475f_election: true` on an investor input does NOT promote to trader. The election only takes effect for taxpayers who independently satisfy the trader prongs. Pinned by `investor_election_flag_ignored_no_eligibility`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-475c2`. Twenty-six tests pin: **dealer customer prong** satisfied (purchases + sells to customers); **dealer inventory prong alone suffices** (no customer prong, but regularly offers derivative positions); **dealer election alone suffices** (no prongs satisfied but elected_dealer_treatment); **dealer В§471 inventory alone suffices**; **negligible-sales exception blocks dealer classification** (regular purchases + negligible sales + no inventory + no election вҶ’ not a dealer); **negligible-sales exception YIELDS to election** + YIELDS to В§471 inventory (both override the exception); **trader with В§475(f) baseline** (1k trades / 200 days / 5d holding / short-term motive); **trader without election keeps capital character** + wash-sale + $3k cap apply; **trader long holding period (60 days) demoted to investor**; **trader insufficient trades (100/yr) demoted**; **trader insufficient trading days (50) demoted**; **trader dividend-motive demoted**; **trader at exact floors qualifies** (720/175/31 boundary); just-below-substantial-activity (719) fails; just-below-continuous-regular (174) fails; **holding 32 days fails short-term motive** (31d ceiling); investor baseline; **investor election flag ignored** (no underlying eligibility); investor with 5k trades + 250 days + 1d holding + dividend motive stays investor (motive prong fails); note describes dealer path basis; note describes В§475(f)-elected consequences (wash-sale DO NOT apply / $3k cap DO NOT apply); note describes trader-without-election consequences (STCG/LTCG, Schedule C); note lists failed investor prongs by name; investor note mentions TCJA suspension; citation mentions all relevant authorities (В§475(c)(2), В§1.475(c)-1, В§475(a), В§475(f), Topic 429).

`traderview-expense::section_871m` is the **IRC В§871(m) dividend-equivalent withholding module** вҖ” companion to iter 32's `section_864b2`. That module handles the non-US trader's own-account safe harbor (avoiding ECI classification); this module handles the **30% withholding** the broker imposes on dividend-equivalent payments from US-equity-linked derivatives, regardless of safe-harbor status. The two analyses are INDEPENDENT вҖ” a non-US person qualifying under В§864(b)(2) still owes В§871(m) withholding on their derivative dividend equivalents.

Pre-В§871(m), non-US persons used total-return swaps and other equity derivatives to receive dividend-equivalent payments on US stocks WITHOUT triggering the В§871(a) / В§881 FDAP withholding that an actual dividend would trigger. Congress closed the loophole through В§871(m) (enacted 2010, effective 2014 for swaps, 2017 for listed options).

**Specified Equity-Linked Instrument** (SELI) classification per Reg. В§1.871-15(g) follows the **delta test**:

- **Short-term contracts** (original term вүӨ 365 days) effective 2017+: SELI when **delta вүҘ 0.80** at issuance. Classic near-the-money equity options pass; deep OTM doesn't.
- **Long-term contracts** (original term > 365 days): SELI only when **delta = 1.0** at issuance. Deep-ITM LEAPS that functionally hold the stock are caught; standard delta-0.6 LEAPS are not.

**Statutory rate** is **30%** under В§871(a)(1)(A) / В§881(a)(1). Tax treaties typically REDUCE the rate to **15%** (US-Canada, US-UK, US-Germany, US-Japan, US-Switzerland, US-Netherlands) when the recipient files Form W-8BEN with the broker. `treaty_rate_override` accepts 0.0 (full exemption вҖ” rare) to 1.0 (clamped) to handle the full treaty-rate spectrum.

`InstrumentType` enum covers `ListedEquityOption`, `SingleStockFuture`, `TotalReturnSwap` (subject from 2014), `StructuredNote`, `OtherEquityLinked`. The withholding agent per В§1441 + Reg. В§1.871-15(p) is the broker/counterparty paying the dividend-equivalent вҖ” the non-US recipient just sees the net amount. This module helps the recipient verify the withheld amount matches statute (or treaty).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-871m`. Seventeen tests pin: short-term delta 0.85 subject to В§871(m) at 30% ($200 Г— 30% = $60 withheld, $140 net); short-term delta exactly 0.80 subject (boundary); short-term delta 0.79 NOT subject; long-term delta 0.90 (2-year LEAPS) NOT subject (needs 1.0); long-term delta exactly 1.0 subject; US-person recipient not subject (short-circuit first); non-dividend-paying underlying skips В§871(m); 15% treaty rate overrides statutory 30%; treaty rate zero full exemption ($200 net to recipient); treaty rate above 1.0 clamps; delta above 1.0 clamps; delta negative clamped to zero not subject; zero dividend equivalent no-withholding path; **short-term boundary at exactly 365 days uses short-term threshold**; **long-term boundary at 366 days uses long-term threshold**; note distinguishes subject vs inapplicable paths; US-person short-circuit runs first (even with other bad facts).

`traderview-expense::section_911` is the **IRC В§911 foreign earned income exclusion module** вҖ” trader-relevant for U.S. citizens / lawful permanent residents who relocate abroad while continuing to earn income from services performed in the foreign country. В§911 lets qualified individuals **exclude** foreign earned income up to an inflation-adjusted ceiling (**2025: $130,000; 2026: $132,900**) plus a housing exclusion capped at 30% of the FEIE.

**В§911(d)(1) qualified individual** вҖ” must satisfy ONE of two tests:

| Test | Authority | Requirement |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Bona fide residence | В§911(d)(1)(A) | U.S. citizen + bona fide resident of foreign country for uninterrupted period including at least one full tax year |
| Physical presence | В§911(d)(1)(B) | U.S. citizen or LPR + вүҘ **330 full days** present in foreign country during any 12-consecutive-month period |

**Two exclusions stack**:

```
В§911(a)(1) FEIE = min(foreign earned income, inflation_cap)
В§911(d)(7) Base Housing Amount = 16% Г— inflation_cap
В§911(c)(2) Housing Cap = 30% Г— inflation_cap
В§911(a)(2) Housing Exclusion = min(max(housing_expenses вҲ’ base, 0), cap)
Total В§911 Exclusion = FEIE + Housing Exclusion
```

**В§911(b)(1) eligible income definition** вҖ” services performed in a foreign country during the qualified-individual period. Categorically EXCLUDED from FEIE: interest, dividends, capital gains, pension distributions, and amounts paid by the U.S. government. A trader earning ETF dividends abroad gets ZERO В§911 benefit on those dividends вҖ” only on actual services-performed compensation.

**Married-couple stacking**: each spouse who qualifies INDEPENDENTLY may claim the full FEIE. 2025 MFJ stacked = $260,000; 2026 = $265,800.

**Inflation-adjustment numbers** for the relevant years (caller-supplied so the module is year-agnostic): 2024 $126,500; 2025 $130,000; 2026 $132,900 (Rev. Proc. 2025-32).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-911`. Twenty-three tests pin: **physical presence 330 days qualifies** (boundary); **329 days does NOT qualify** (strict вүҘ 330); **bona fide residence qualifies regardless of day count**; **bona fide residence takes precedence when both apply** (regression target вҖ” priority ordering); **income below FEIE вҶ’ full exclusion** ($100k income excluded, $0 net); **income above FEIE capped** ($200k income вҶ’ $132,900 excluded, $67,100 net); **income exactly at FEIE вҶ’ full exclusion**; **base housing 16% Г— FEIE** (2026: $21,264); **housing cap 30% Г— FEIE** (2026: $39,870); **housing expenses below base вҶ’ no exclusion** ($20,000 < $21,264 base); **housing expenses between base and cap вҶ’ excess excluded** ($35,000 вҲ’ $21,264 = $13,736); **housing expenses above cap вҶ’ capped at 30%** ($100k вҶ’ $39,870 capped); **U.S. government income zeroes eligible income** (regression вҖ” В§911(b)(1) bar); not-qualified individual вҶ’ zero exclusion; **2025 FEIE $130,000 + base $20,800 + cap $39,000** (inflation-adjustment pin); 2026 FEIE $132,900; **total exclusion combines income and housing** ($150k income + $35k housing вҶ’ $132,900 + $13,736 = $146,636); **citation mentions all 8 relevant authorities** (В§911(a)(1) + В§911(a)(2) + В§911(b)(1) + В§911(c)(2) + В§911(d)(1)(A) + В§911(d)(1)(B) + В§911(d)(2)(B) + В§911(d)(7) + "330 full days" + "16%" + "30%" + "$130,000" + "$132,900"); note describes qualifying test path; note describes bona fide path; note describes US-gov bar "TRIGGERED"; **$10M precision** ($172,770 total exclusion capped).

`traderview-expense::section_864b2` is the **IRC В§864(b)(2) trader / investor safe harbor module** вҖ” the rule that lets non-US persons trade US securities through US brokers without being treated as engaged in a US trade or business. Without В§864(b)(2), every gain would be **Effectively Connected Income** (ECI) under В§871/В§882, taxed on a net basis at graduated rates with US return filing required. The safe harbor pulls trader-style activity entirely outside the US tax net for non-US persons.

The classification follows a strict four-factor short-circuit chain:

1. **В§864(b)(2) is for non-US persons only** вҖ” US persons are already subject to net US tax; the safe harbor doesn't apply (`non_us_person: false` вҶ’ immediate ECI).
2. **Trading must be for own account** вҖ” proprietary trading only. Trading for customers (or a customer-facing book) doesn't qualify (`trades_for_own_account: false` вҶ’ ECI).
3. **No US office** вҖ” В§864(c)(5) attributes income to a US trade or business when a fixed place of business in the US is used for the activity, regardless of whether the В§864(b)(2) safe harbor would otherwise apply (`has_us_office_for_trading: true` вҶ’ ECI under В§864(c)(5)).
4. **В§864(b)(2)(B) dealer exclusion** вҖ” a "dealer in stocks or securities" (or commodity dealer) does NOT qualify per Reg. В§1.864-2(c)(2). Applied separately per `InstrumentClass`: a securities dealer who trades commodities for own account still gets the commodities safe harbor under В§864(b)(2)(A)(i); a commodities dealer who trades securities still gets В§864(b)(2)(A)(ii).

`InstrumentClass` is `Securities` (В§864(b)(2)(A)(ii)) / `Commodities` (В§864(b)(2)(A)(i)) / `Both` (В§864(b)(2)(A) generic). The result's `controlling_subsection` echoes the exact subsection that authorizes the classification, so callers can cite it on filings or in correspondence with the IRS.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-864b2`. Seventeen tests pin: non-US individual securities trading qualifies; US person doesn't qualify (US-person check runs first); non-own-account doesn't qualify; securities dealer excluded under В§864(b)(2)(B); commodities dealer excluded when trading commodities; **cross-class dealer status is irrelevant** (securities dealer trading commodities-only still qualifies under В§864(b)(2)(A)(i); commodities dealer trading securities-only still qualifies); both classes + dealer in one disqualifies; both classes + no dealer qualifies under В§864(b)(2)(A); US office kicks out under В§864(c)(5); US office overrides even an otherwise-clean profile; note distinguishes safe-harbor path from disqualification; both-class dual-dealer lists both reasons; US-person check short-circuits other factors (first reason is non-US); commodities-only + commodities dealer disqualified; safe-harbor note cites applicable subsection (В§864(b)(2)(A)(ii) for securities-only, (i) for commodities-only, (A) for both); own-account check short-circuits before dealer check.

`traderview-expense::section_988` is the **IRC В§988 foreign currency transaction character module** вҖ” every forex trader, every crypto trader using non-USD pairs, every holder of FX-denominated debt instruments faces В§988. The default rule: gains/losses are **ordinary** income/loss, not capital. Three interacting carve-outs make the routing non-obvious:

- **В§988(c)(1)(D) personal-use exclusion** вҖ” gain (not loss) on a personal currency transaction is excluded if total gain is вүӨ $200 per transaction. Travelers buying euros for vacation routinely qualify. The threshold doesn't graduate вҖ” $201 of gain is fully ordinary; the whole amount, not just the excess. Personal-use LOSSES are В§165(c) nondeductible personal losses, NOT В§988 ordinary losses.
- **В§988(a)(1)(B) capital election** вҖ” taxpayer may elect to treat forward/futures/option contracts that are capital assets (and not part of a straddle) as CAPITAL gain/loss. Election requires clear identification on the books before close of trading on the trade date per Reg. В§1.988-3(b). Does NOT apply to spot, FX-denominated debt, or accrued FX items вҖ” those stay ordinary regardless.
- **В§1256(g) interaction** вҖ” regulated futures contracts that are "foreign currency contracts" within В§1256(g)(2) default to В§1256 **60% LTCG / 40% STCG**, NOT В§988. The taxpayer can use the **В§988(c)(1)(D)(i) kick-out election** to override and route them BACK to В§988 ordinary treatment.

`TransactionKind` enumerates seven В§988(c)(1)(B) categories: `ForexSpot`, `ForwardContract`, `NonRegulatedFuturesContract`, `ForexFuturesSection1256g`, `OptionContract`, `FxDenominatedDebt`, `AccruedFxItem`. `Character` covers five outcomes: `Ordinary`, `Capital`, `Section1256Sixty40`, `ExcludedPersonalUse`, `DisallowedPersonalLoss`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-988`. Seventeen tests pin: spot default ordinary; loss on spot also ordinary (bypasses the $3k В§1212(b) cap); forward + election = capital; forward without election = ordinary; regulated currency futures default to В§1256 60/40; kick-out election routes futures back to ordinary; personal-use gain under $200 excluded; exactly $200 excluded; $201 fully ordinary (no graduation); personal-use loss disallowed (not ordinary); FX-denominated debt always ordinary even with election asserted (election doesn't apply); forex spot election ignored; option + election = capital; personal-use zero gain no-op; personal-use route runs first (election flags ignored); accrued FX item ordinary even with election; non-regulated futures + election = capital.

`traderview-expense::section_263a` is the **IRC В§263A UNICAP trader-vs-dealer classifier** вҖ” the module that pins the load-bearing distinction between traders and dealers for cost-capitalization purposes. A **dealer in securities** under В§475(c)(1) (buys + sells to customers in the ordinary course) holds securities as **inventory** and must capitalize direct + indirect costs into basis. A **trader** (proprietary trading for own account; no customers) holds positions as **investment property** and is EXEMPT from В§263A under В§263A(c)(3) + В§475(f) вҖ” costs remain currently deductible as В§162 ordinary business expenses.

The trader exemption is the most-asked-about distinction in active-trader tax planning, and the module's short-circuit chain reflects the analysis order a CPA actually performs: (1) trader вҶ’ exempt, costs currently deductible (most common path for proprietary traders); (2) investor вҶ’ direct costs capitalized to basis per В§1012, indirect costs В§67(g)-limited (the TCJA 2018-2025 suspension makes them effectively nondeductible); (3) dealer + В§263A(b)(2)(B) **small business exception** (avg 3-year gross receipts вүӨ В§448(c) threshold) вҶ’ exempt, costs currently deductible; (4) dealer above threshold вҶ’ subject to UNICAP, capitalize direct + indirect into inventory basis.

`TradingClassification` enum: `Dealer`, `Trader`, `Investor`. The dealer-vs-trader question turns on whether the taxpayer makes "regular and continuous sales to customers" вҖ” caller's responsibility to assert based on facts. Active securities dealers almost always blow past the small-business threshold (gross receipts include gross proceeds from every sale), so the exception rarely helps; but day-1 trading startups may briefly qualify.

The **В§448(c) threshold table** (shared with iter 16's `section_163j`):

| Year | В§448(c) threshold |
|------|-------------------|
| 2020 | $26M              |
| 2021 | $26M              |
| 2022 | $27M              |
| 2023 | $29M              |
| 2024 | $30M              |
| 2025 | $31M              |

Caller can override via `small_business_threshold_override` for 2026+.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-263a`. Sixteen tests pin: dealer above threshold subject to UNICAP ($150k capitalized); dealer below threshold exempt currently deductible; dealer at threshold exactly still exempt (вүӨ not <); dealer $1 over loses exemption; **trader exempt regardless of receipts** (even $100M вҖ” short-circuits before threshold check); investor costs capitalized to basis not currently deductible (В§1012 path); В§448(c) threshold table 2020-2025 each year exact; caller override beats embedded table; zero costs dealer subject but nothing to capitalize; only direct costs only those capitalized; only indirect costs only those capitalized; trader note distinguishes from dealer path (В§475(f) trader vs В§263A applies); investor note describes basis-capitalization path (В§1012); small-business exception with huge costs still currently deductible ($2.5M deductible at $5M gross receipts); dealer subject total = sum of buckets invariant; trader short-circuits threshold check.

`traderview-expense::section_267` is the **IRC В§267 related-party loss disallowance module** вҖ” every trader has family members or controlled entities, and selling stock at a loss to a spouse, child, sibling, or one's own S-corp triggers В§267(a)(1) which COMPLETELY disallows the loss. The В§267(d) buyer-side adjustment is the non-obvious part: when the related-party buyer later sells the property at a gain, that gain is REDUCED (down to zero) by the previously-disallowed loss. If the buyer sells at a loss, the seller's disallowed amount is gone permanently. Buyer's initial basis is their actual cash purchase price (В§267 does NOT transfer the seller's basis вҖ” it only preserves the gain-reduction right).

`RelationshipCategory` exposes the В§267(b) ten categories explicitly so the API caller doesn't have to guess: `FamilyMember` (В§267(b)(1) вҖ” siblings whole/half blood, spouse, ancestors, lineal descendants; explicitly NOT in-laws/cousins/aunts/uncles); `IndividualAndControlledCorp` (В§267(b)(2) вҖ” >50% stock value); `TwoControlledCorps` (В§267(b)(3) вҖ” В§1563(a) common ownership); `GrantorAndTrustFiduciary` / `TwoTrustFiduciariesSameGrantor` / `TrustFiduciaryAndBeneficiary` / `TrustFiduciaryAndOtherBeneficiary` (В§267(b)(4)-(7) trust pairs); `CorpAndPartnershipCommonOwner` (В§267(b)(8) вҖ” frequent gotcha for trader LLC + S-corp combos); `TwoSCorps` (В§267(b)(9)); `EstateExecutorAndBeneficiary` (В§267(b)(10)); and `Unrelated` to short-circuit when В§267 doesn't apply.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-267`. Fourteen tests pin: unrelated full loss recognized; family member full loss disallowed; no-loss no-op; В§267(d) buyer gain reduced by disallowed loss ($20k gain вҶ’ $10k taxable after $10k reduction); В§267(d) reduction capped at buyer gain ($3k gain вҶ’ $7k permanently lost); buyer loss loses entire disallowance; buyer not yet sold preserves disallowance for future; all ten В§267(b) categories treated as related; buyer initial basis is cash price (not seller basis); В§267(d) zero gain leaves loss lost; unrelated with subsequent gain ignores В§267(d); corp+partnership common owner is related (trader LLC вҶ” S-corp); is-related helper returns false only for Unrelated; note text describes the partial-reduction split.

`traderview-expense::section_274` is the **IRC В§ 274 disallowance of entertainment, meals, gift, travel, luxury water travel deduction limitations module** вҖ” direct trader companion to `section_162f` (fines/penalties non-deductibility), `section_280e` (cannabis trafficking disallowance), `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp deduction вҖ” iter 446), `section_409a` (NQDC). Trader-critical because individual traders, LLC traders, and В§ 475(f) mark-to-market traders incur substantial business meal, travel, gift, and education expenses related to trade activities; В§ 274 limits aggressively reduce deductible amounts.

В§ 274 imposes layered DEDUCTION LIMITATIONS on ordinary business expenses that would otherwise be deductible under В§ 162(a):

| Subsection | Limitation |
|------------|-----------|
| **В§ 274(a)** | ENTERTAINMENT вҖ” fully disallowed post-TCJA (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13304) |
| **В§ 274(b)** | GIFTS вҖ” $25 per recipient per year (unchanged since 1962) |
| **В§ 274(d)** | SUBSTANTIATION вҖ” amount + time/place + business purpose + business relationship; complete denial on failure |
| **В§ 274(h)** | FOREIGN CONVENTIONS вҖ” 4-part reasonableness test |
| **В§ 274(k)** | BUSINESS MEALS вҖ” 50% subject to (1) not lavish; (2) taxpayer/employee present |
| **В§ 274(m)** | LUXURY WATER TRAVEL вҖ” daily cap = 2Г— highest federal per diem (~$1,140/day 2026) |
| **В§ 274(n)** | GENERAL 50% LIMIT вҖ” most food/beverage expenses |
| **В§ 274(o)** | PER SE ENTERTAINMENT FACILITIES вҖ” country clubs, sporting events, golf, yachts |

**В§ 274(a) entertainment вҖ” fully disallowed post-TCJA** (TCJA 2017 В§ 13304, Pub. L. 115-97): eliminated deduction for any expense for entertainment, amusement, or recreation activities. Pre-TCJA "directly related to business" and "associated with business" rules REPEALED. Exceptions preserved for В§ 274(e)(4) recreational facilities primarily for rank-and-file employees; В§ 274(e)(5) events for employees where employer present; В§ 274(a)(3) club dues for trade associations.

**В§ 274(k) business meals вҖ” 50% subject to two conditions**:
1. NOT LAVISH OR EXTRAVAGANT under the circumstances
2. TAXPAYER (or employee of taxpayer) PRESENT when food or beverages furnished

**Temporary 100% restaurant meal exception (2021-2022)** вҖ” Consolidated Appropriations Act 2021 (Pub. L. 116-260 В§ 210) temporarily increased В§ 274(n) limit to 100% for food/beverages from RESTAURANTS during 2021-2022; **EXPIRED January 1, 2023**; reverted to 50%.

**В§ 274(o) per se entertainment facilities (NO deduction regardless of business purpose, 8 categories)**: country clubs; sporting events (tickets/suites/boxes); golf clubs; yachts; tennis clubs; health and athletic clubs; hunting/fishing lodges; vacation resorts. Exception only for facilities primarily for benefit of rank-and-file employees under В§ 274(e)(4).

**В§ 274(d) substantiation requirements (4 elements)**: AMOUNT of expense; TIME and PLACE; BUSINESS PURPOSE; BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP to taxpayer of person(s) entertained/fed. Failure equals COMPLETE DENIAL with NO substantial compliance escape (Sanford v. Commissioner, 50 T.C. 823 (1968); COHAN RULE rejected for В§ 274(d) categories per Cohan v. Commissioner, 39 F.2d 540 (2d Cir. 1930)).

**В§ 274(b) gift limitation** вҖ” $25 per recipient per year (unchanged since 1962); exceptions: items costing $4 or less with permanent name imprint (promotional items); signs/display racks on business premises of recipient; spousal recipient counted with original; partnership/closely-held corporation gift rules at В§ 274(b)(2).

**В§ 274(h) foreign conventions вҖ” 4-part reasonableness test**: (1) purpose of meeting + activities at meeting; (2) purposes and activities of sponsoring organization or group; (3) residences of active members of organization and where meetings have been held; OR (4) other relevant factors.

**В§ 274(m) luxury water travel** вҖ” daily deduction cap = 2Г— highest federal per diem rate for non-federal employees during travel period; 2026 cap approximately $1,140/day (CPI-adjusted from original $200 daily 1986 amount).

**OBBBA 2026 В§ 274(o) updates** вҖ” One Big Beautiful Bill (Pub. L. 119-21 В§ 70202) effective January 1, 2026 modified employer-convenience meal deduction under В§ 274(o)(1); eliminated 100% deduction for employer-provided meals to employees on premises for employer's convenience; now subject to general В§ 274(n) 50% limit.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) trader holds client dinner $400 at upscale restaurant вҖ” В§ 274(n) 50% limit $200 deductible plus В§ 274(d) substantiation required; (2) trader takes prospect to NBA game $1,200 вҖ” В§ 274(a) FULLY DISALLOWED post-TCJA but separately-invoiced restaurant meal portion may qualify; (3) country club dues $1,000 вҖ” В§ 274(o) PER SE no deduction; (4) $50 holiday gift basket вҖ” $25 per recipient cap = $25 deductible only; (5) В§ 475(f) trader Schedule C deducts $5,000 meals (50%) + $1,200 travel + $0 entertainment (TCJA) + $25 gift, requires В§ 274(d) contemporaneous records.

`traderview-expense::section_302` is the **IRC В§ 302 вҖ” Distributions in Redemption of Stock module** (iter 556) вҖ” direct corporate-distribution cornerstone driving В§ 304 + В§ 311 + В§ 318 cluster. Direct sibling to `section_318` (iter 552 вҖ” constructive ownership engine that drives В§ 302(c)(1) attribution analysis), `section_304` (iter 554 вҖ” related-corp redemption uses В§ 302 mechanics), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corp-level recognition triggered by В§ 302 character determination), `section_331`/`section_332`/`section_336` (corporate liquidation companion regimes), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD when dividend treatment applies). В§ 302 determines whether a corporation's redemption of its own stock is treated as a В§ 1001 SALE OR EXCHANGE (producing capital gain or loss) or as a В§ 301 DISTRIBUTION (producing ordinary dividend income to extent of E&P + basis recovery + capital gain on excess). В§ 302(a) treats a redemption as sale or exchange if any of the four В§ 302(b) tests is satisfied.

**В§ 302(b)(1) NOT ESSENTIALLY EQUIVALENT TO A DIVIDEND (NEED-test)**: facts-and-circumstances inquiry under **United States v. Davis, 397 U.S. 301 (1970)** requiring **MEANINGFUL REDUCTION** in shareholder's interest. Subjective standard; minority redemptions occasionally qualify when board control or veto rights are affected.

**В§ 302(b)(2) SUBSTANTIALLY DISPROPORTIONATE REDEMPTION** (mechanical 50/80 test): post-redemption voting interest LESS THAN **50%** AND post-redemption common-voting interest LESS THAN **80%** of pre-redemption common-voting interest AND post-redemption common-voting-AND-non-voting interest LESS THAN 80% of pre-redemption common-voting-AND-non-voting interest.

**В§ 302(b)(3) COMPLETE TERMINATION**: redemption terminates ALL of shareholder's interest. **В§ 302(c)(2) family-attribution WAIVER** available if (A) shareholder has no interest other than as creditor immediately after distribution (including officer, director, employee), (B) shareholder acquires no such interest within **10-year** reacquisition bar (other than by bequest or inheritance), and (C) shareholder files agreement with IRS to notify of any reacquisition and retain records.

**В§ 302(b)(4) PARTIAL LIQUIDATION** (non-corporate shareholder): В§ 302(e) definition requires corporation conducted active trade or business for 5+ years AND distributes proceeds of discontinued business segment.

**В§ 302(c)(1) ATTRIBUTION**: В§ 318(a) attribution rules apply to determine ownership for В§ 302 purposes; family + entity + option attribution may convert what appears to be a complete-termination redemption into a В§ 301 dividend.

**В§ 302(d) DEFAULT В§ 301 TREATMENT**: if redemption fails ALL four В§ 302(b) tests, redemption defaults to В§ 301 distribution.

**Five redemption-test-path branches**: SubstantiallyDisproportionateSection302B2, CompleteTerminationSection302B3, NotEssentiallyEquivalentSection302B1, PartialLiquidationSection302B4, NoSection302BTestAssertedDefaultDistribution.

**Three attribution statuses**: AttributionAppliesIncreasesOwnership, AttributionDoesNotApply, Section302C2FamilyAttributionWaiverFiled.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section302BTestSatisfiedSaleOrExchangeTreatment, Section302B2SubstantiallyDisproportionate50_80TestFailed, Section302B3CompleteTerminationWithAttributionWaiver, Section302B3CompleteTerminationAttributionDefeatsFailedWaiver, Section302DDefaultDistributionTreatmentSection301.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-302`. Twenty tests pin: **substantially disproportionate 50/80 test satisfied** (pre 60% вҶ’ post 20% < 50% AND < 48% [60% Г— 80%] вҶ’ $800K capital gain); **substantially disproportionate 50 test failed** (post 55% > 50% threshold); **substantially disproportionate 80 test failed** (post 49% > 48% = pre 60% Г— 80%); **complete termination with attribution waiver** (В§ 302(c)(2) + 10-year bar + IRS notification agreement); **complete termination attribution defeats failed waiver**; **complete termination no attribution required sale treatment**; **not essentially equivalent dividend test asserted** (Davis MEANINGFUL REDUCTION); **partial liquidation test asserted** (В§ 302(b)(4) + В§ 302(e) + 5+ years active business); **no test asserted default В§ 301 distribution** (В§ 1(h)(11) qualified-dividend rate eligibility note); **В§ 302(b)(2) 50% threshold constant pins 5,000 bps**; **В§ 302(b)(2) 80% drop factor constant pins 8,000 bps**; **В§ 302(c)(2) 10-year bar constant pins 10 years**; **very large property no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero property zero gain** (boundary); **note pins В§ 318 attribution companion**; **note pins В§ 1222 capital-gain character**; **note pins В§ 1001 sale or exchange**; **note pins В§ 301 distribution default when failed**; **boundary post 50% exactly fails test must be less than** (boundary); **boundary post 49% satisfies 50% test if 80% also met** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_304` is the **IRC В§ 304 вҖ” Redemption Through Use of Related Corporations module** (iter 554) вҖ” direct related-corp anti-extraction companion to `section_318` (iter 552 вҖ” constructive ownership cornerstone that drives В§ 304(b)(1) control determination), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corp-level recognition on distributions), `section_305` (stock dividend rules вҖ” companion non-recognition framework), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD when В§ 304 + В§ 1248 deemed-dividend treatment crosses borders). В§ 304 prevents brother-sister and parent-subsidiary stock sales between commonly-controlled corporations from being recharacterized as capital-gain sales when the substance of the transaction is a dividend distribution from accumulated E&P, closing the "extraction" tactic where a controlling shareholder converts what is economically a dividend into a В§ 1001 exchange producing favorable capital-gain treatment.

**В§ 304(a)(1) BROTHER-SISTER ACQUISITIONS**: if one or more persons is in CONTROL of each of two corporations AND one of those persons sells stock of the ISSUING corporation to the ACQUIRING corporation in exchange for property, the transaction is RECHARACTERIZED as a В§ 301 distribution by the acquiring corp in redemption of its own stock.

**В§ 304(a)(2) PARENT-SUBSIDIARY ACQUISITIONS**: if the issuing corporation is the PARENT (in control) of the acquiring SUBSIDIARY, and a shareholder of the parent transfers parent stock to the subsidiary in exchange for property, the transaction is recharacterized as a В§ 301 distribution from the subsidiary.

**В§ 304(b)(1) CONTROL DEFINITION**: at least **50%** of total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote OR at least 50% of total value of shares. **В§ 318 constructive ownership** applies to determine control (family + entity + option attribution).

**В§ 304(b)(2) E&P STACKING ORDER**: distribution treated as first paid by the ACQUIRING corp to extent of its E&P, then by the ISSUING corp to extent of its E&P. В§ 301(c) treatment applies for dividend / basis-recovery / capital-gain split.

**В§ 304(b)(3) FOREIGN-CORPORATION RULES**: В§ 1248 deemed-dividend rules coordinate where acquiring or issuing corporation is foreign.

**В§ 304(b)(4) ANTI-AVOIDANCE RULES** (Notice 2006-85 + Notice 2007-9 + 2012 final regs): expanded reach to prevent partnership-interposition circumvention.

**Three transaction types**: BrotherSisterSection304A1, ParentSubsidiarySection304A2, UnrelatedArmsLengthSaleSection304Inapplicable.

**Two control statuses**: InControlOfBothCorporationsSection304B1, NotInControlOfBothCorporations.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section304InapplicableUnrelatedArmsLengthSale, Section304InapplicableNotInControlOfBothCorporations, Section304ARecharacterizationDividendUpToTotalEep, Section304BasisRecoveryAfterEepExhausted, Section304CapitalGainAfterBasisRecovered.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-304`. Seventeen tests pin: **unrelated arm's-length sale В§ 304 inapplicable** ($1M property - $150K basis = $850K capital gain + В§ 1001 + В§ 1222); **not in control of both В§ 304 inapplicable** (В§ 304(b)(1) + В§ 318); **brother-sister full dividend within combined E&P** ($800K combined E&P вҶ’ $800K dividend + $150K basis recovery + $50K capital gain); **brother-sister property within combined E&P all dividend** ($500K < $800K E&P вҶ’ all dividend); **brother-sister property just exceeds E&P basis recovery** ($850K вҶ’ $800K div + $50K basis recovery); **parent-subsidiary В§ 304(a)(2) recharacterization**; **zero E&P full basis recovery then capital gain** ($0 E&P вҶ’ $150K basis + $850K capital gain); **В§ 304 control threshold constant pins 50%**; **very large property no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero property zero distribution** (boundary); **note pins В§ 245A foreign source**; **note pins В§ 1248 foreign-corp deemed dividend**; **note pins qualified dividend В§ 1(h)(11)**; **note pins В§ 318 constructive ownership companion**; **unrelated arm's-length zero gain when basis exceeds proceeds** (saturating_sub defense); **remaining basis in acquiring-corp stock tracked**; **note pins В§ 304(b)(2) E&P stacking order**.

`traderview-expense::section_482` is the **IRC В§ 482 вҖ” Allocation of Income and Deductions Among Taxpayers (Transfer Pricing) module** (iter 566) вҖ” direct transfer-pricing cornerstone enforcing arm's-length-standard reallocation between commonly-controlled related parties. Direct sibling to `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” anti-tax-avoidance acquisition disallowance parallel), `section_269a` (iter 544 вҖ” PSC tax-avoidance allocation parallel), `section_267` (related-party loss-deferral parallel), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD coordination), `section_367` (cross-border reorganization regime), `section_951a` (GILTI/NCTI). В§ 482 grants Treasury authority to DISTRIBUTE, APPORTION, or ALLOCATE gross income, deductions, credits, or allowances between or among two or more organizations, trades, or businesses owned or controlled directly or indirectly by the same interests when necessary to PREVENT EVASION OF TAXES or to clearly REFLECT THE INCOME of the controlled taxpayers.

**В§ 482 ARM'S-LENGTH STANDARD** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-1): a controlled transaction satisfies the arm's-length standard if the results are consistent with the results that would have been realized if uncontrolled taxpayers had engaged in the same transaction under the same circumstances.

**В§ 482 BEST-METHOD RULE** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-1(c)): the arm's-length result must be determined under the method that, under the facts and circumstances, provides the most reliable measure of an arm's-length result. NO strict hierarchy.

**TANGIBLE-PROPERTY METHODS** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-3): (1) Comparable Uncontrolled Price (CUP); (2) Resale Price Method (RPM); (3) Cost Plus Method.

**PROFIT METHODS** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-5 + В§ 1.482-6): (4) Comparable Profits Method (CPM); (5) Profit Split Method.

**SERVICES** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-9): comparable uncontrolled services price + gross services margin + cost-of-services-plus + CPM-for-services + profit split + **Services Cost Method (SCM)** safe harbor for low-margin services.

**INTANGIBLE-PROPERTY TRANSFERS**: Tax Reform Act of 1986 amendment requires consideration for intangible property in a controlled transaction be **COMMENSURATE WITH THE INCOME** attributable to the intangible. **Comparable Uncontrolled Transaction (CUT)** method preferred under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-4. Periodic adjustments mandated when actual income materially differs from projections.

**COST SHARING ARRANGEMENTS** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.482-7): related parties may share R&D costs proportional to expected benefits; **Platform Contribution Transaction (PCT)** payment required for pre-existing intangibles contributed by any participant.

**В§ 6662(e) SUBSTANTIAL VALUATION MISSTATEMENT PENALTY**: 20% of underpayment if В§ 482 adjustment exceeds greater of **$5 million** or **10% of gross receipts**.

**В§ 6662(h) GROSS VALUATION MISSTATEMENT PENALTY**: **40%** вҖ” if claimed value 200% of correct value (overvaluation) OR 25% (or less) of correct (undervaluation).

**В§ 6664(c) REASONABLE-CAUSE DEFENSE** requires contemporaneous documentation per **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.6662-6(d)** (best-method selection + comparable analysis + economic analysis + financial data). Defense NOT available for В§ 6662(h) gross valuation misstatement per В§ 6664(c)(3).

**Reporting**: Form 5471 (US shareholder of CFC) + Form 5472 (US corp 25%-foreign-owned + foreign branch) + Form 8975 Country-by-Country reporting (вүҘ $850M global group revenue).

**Five transaction types**: TangiblePropertyTransfer, IntangiblePropertyTransfer, ServiceProvision, CostSharingArrangementSection1482Dash7, LoanOrFinancialTransaction.

**Eight transfer-pricing methods**: ComparableUncontrolledPriceCup, ResalePriceMethodRpm, CostPlusMethod, ComparableProfitsMethodCpm, ProfitSplitMethod, ServicesCostMethodSafeHarbor, ComparableUncontrolledTransactionCut, UnspecifiedMethod.

**Four adjustment-magnitude statuses**: WithinArmsLengthRangeNoAdjustment, AdjustmentBelowSection6662EThreshold, Section6662ESubstantialValuationMisstatement, Section6662HGrossValuationMisstatement.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section482WithinArmsLengthRangeNoAdjustment, Section482AdjustmentBelowPenaltyThreshold, Section6662ESubstantialValuationMisstatement20Pct, Section6662HGrossValuationMisstatement40Pct, CostSharingPctPlatformContributionTransactionRequired, CommensurateWithIncomeStandardIntangible.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-482`. Twenty tests pin: **within arm's-length range no adjustment** (В§ 1.482-1 + В§ 1.6662-6(d) documentation); **adjustment below threshold no penalty**; **substantial valuation misstatement 20% penalty** (20% Г— $10M = $2M + В§ 6662(e) + В§ 1.6662-6(d)); **gross valuation misstatement 40% penalty** (40% Г— $5M = $2M + В§ 6664(c)(3) defense unavailable); **cost sharing arrangement PCT required** (В§ 1.482-7 + В§ 367(d) + В§ 250 FDII); **intangible property commensurate with income standard** (Tax Reform Act of 1986 + В§ 1.482-4); **CUP method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-3(b)); **resale price method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-3(c) + RPM); **cost plus method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-3(d)); **CPM method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-5); **profit split method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-6); **services cost method safe harbor label pinned** (В§ 1.482-9(b)); **CUT method label pinned** (В§ 1.482-4 + CUT); **В§ 6662(e) threshold constant pins $5M**; **В§ 6662(e) penalty rate constant pins 20%**; **В§ 6662(h) penalty rate constant pins 40%**; **substantial valuation threshold pins 200%**; **gross valuation threshold pins 200%**; **very large adjustment no overflow** (u64::MAX + u128 intermediate); **zero adjustment zero penalty** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_357` is the **IRC В§ 357 вҖ” Assumption of Liability in Tax-Free Transfers module** (iter 564) вҖ” direct liability-rule cornerstone completing the basis-preservation cluster with `section_358` (iter 560 вҖ” shareholder basis side) and `section_362` (iter 562 вҖ” corporation basis side). Direct sibling to `section_351` (transfer parent regime), `section_354` (basis in reorganization stock), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corp-level distribution recognition). В§ 357 governs how the assumption of liabilities by a transferee corporation affects gain recognition by the transferor in В§ 351 transfers and В§ 368 reorganizations, containing the critical rules that determine whether liability assumption preserves non-recognition (В§ 357(a)) or triggers gain recognition due to tax-avoidance purpose (В§ 357(b)) or liabilities-exceed-basis mechanic (В§ 357(c)(1)).

**В§ 357(a) GENERAL RULE**: assumption of a liability of the transferor by the transferee corporation does NOT cause the assumed liability to be treated as money or other property received вҖ” liability assumption preserves non-recognition.

**В§ 357(b) TAX-AVOIDANCE EXCEPTION**: В§ 357(a) does NOT apply if the principal purpose of the transferor with respect to the liability assumption was (1) to avoid federal income tax on the exchange OR (2) not a bona-fide business purpose. If the exception applies, **ALL** liabilities assumed are treated as money received (full boot). Taxpayer bears burden of proof by clear preponderance of evidence per **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.357-1(c)**.

**В§ 357(c)(1) EXCESS-LIABILITY GAIN**: even where В§ 357(a) applies (bona-fide business purpose), gain is RECOGNIZED to the extent the aggregate amount of liabilities assumed by transferee EXCEEDS the aggregate adjusted basis of the property transferred. **Treas. Reg. В§ 1.357-2** classic example: $20K basis + $30K mortgage вҶ’ $10K gain.

**В§ 357(c)(2) EXCEPTIONS**: excess-liability gain rule does NOT apply to: (A) liability the discharge of which would give rise to a deduction (e.g., accounts payable from cash-basis trade or business), or (B) liability described in В§ 736(a) (retiring-partner payments).

**В§ 357(d) DETERMINATION OF AMOUNT OF LIABILITY ASSUMED**: liability is treated as assumed by transferee only to the extent the transferor is RELIEVED of the liability.

**Two purpose statuses**: BonaFideBusinessPurposeNoTaxAvoidance, TaxAvoidanceOrNoBonaFideBusinessPurposeSection357B.

**Three liability types**: OrdinaryNonExcludedLiability, DeductibleDischargeLiabilitySection357C2A, Section736ARetiringPartnerLiability.

**Five-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section357ANonRecognitionPreservedNoGain, Section357BTaxAvoidanceExceptionFullLiabilityTreatedAsBoot, Section357C1ExcessLiabilityGainRecognition, Section357C2ExceptionAppliesNoExcessLiabilityGain.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-357`. Seventeen tests pin: **В§ 357(a) non-recognition preserved when basis exceeds liabilities** ($100K basis > $50K liabilities); **В§ 357(b) tax-avoidance full liability as boot** ($50K liabilities вҶ’ $50K boot + clear-preponderance burden + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.357-1(c)); **В§ 357(c)(1) excess-liability gain when liabilities exceed basis** ($130K - $100K = $30K gain + В§ 1245 + В§ 1250 recapture coordination); **В§ 357(c)(1) classic example 20K basis 30K mortgage 10K gain** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.357-2 example); **В§ 357(c)(2)(A) deductible discharge exception no gain** (accounts payable from cash-basis); **В§ 357(c)(2)(B) В§ 736(a) retiring-partner no gain**; **В§ 357(b) takes priority over В§ 357(c)** (severity ladder ordering вҖ” $200K boot vs $100K excess); **В§ 357(c)(2) exception overrides В§ 357(c)(1) excess gain** (severity ladder ordering); **very large liabilities no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero liability no gain** (boundary); **zero basis full liability excess gain** ($50K - $0 = $50K); **note pins В§ 358 companion for В§ 357(a)**; **note pins В§ 362 companion for В§ 357(a)**; **note pins В§ 357(d) relieved of liability**; **note pins В§ 368(c) 80% control**; **note pins Treas. Reg. В§ 1.357-2 classic example in excess gain**; **boundary liability equals basis no gain** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_362` is the **IRC В§ 362 вҖ” Basis to Corporations on Tax-Free Transfers module** (iter 562) вҖ” direct corporation-basis cornerstone completing the double-basis-preservation framework with `section_358` (iter 560 вҖ” shareholder basis side). Direct sibling to `section_351` (transfer parent regime), `section_354` (basis in reorganization stock), `section_357` (liability rules), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corp-level distribution recognition). В§ 362 governs the basis a TRANSFEREE CORPORATION takes in property received in tax-free В§ 351 transfers, В§ 368 reorganizations, and contributions to capital; combined with В§ 358 (shareholder basis side), implements double basis preservation that keeps built-in gain or loss latent through formation and reorganization transactions while preventing taxpayer manipulation via cross-jurisdictional loss importation and intra-United-States loss duplication.

**В§ 362(a) GENERAL RULE**: corporation's basis = adjusted basis of property in transferor's hands + any gain recognized by transferor on the transfer (under В§ 351(b)).

**В§ 362(b) REORGANIZATIONS**: in a В§ 368 reorganization, corporation's basis = adjusted basis in transferor's hands + gain recognized (under В§ 356).

**В§ 362(c) PROPERTY ACQUIRED FOR PAID-IN SURPLUS / CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION BY SHAREHOLDER**: carryover basis from contributor.

**В§ 362(d) CONTRIBUTIONS BY NON-SHAREHOLDERS**: ZERO basis to corporation. **Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13312)** repealed В§ 118 exclusion for contributions by non-shareholders effective for contributions after December 22, 2017 вҖ” government incentives, utility hookup payments, and customer-paid construction allowances now generally INCLUDABLE in gross income; pre-TCJA contributions retain В§ 118 exclusion.

**В§ 362(e)(1) ANTI-LOSS-IMPORTATION RULE** (added by American Jobs Creation Act of 2004, finalized in 2016 regs): if transferred property would be subject to U.S. tax in transferee's hands but was NOT subject in transferor's hands (foreign or tax-exempt party) AND transferor's aggregate adjusted basis exceeds aggregate FMV, transferee's basis in each transferred asset is REDUCED TO ITS FMV per **26 C.F.R. В§ 1.362-3** (effective for transactions on or after March 28, 2016). Companion: В§ 358(h) shareholder-level + В§ 334(b)(1)(B) В§ 332-liquidation-parallel.

**В§ 362(e)(2) ANTI-LOSS-DUPLICATION RULE** (2004): in a В§ 351 transfer where transferor's AGGREGATE adjusted basis EXCEEDS aggregate FMV (net built-in loss), transferee's aggregate basis is REDUCED to aggregate FMV; reduction allocated among transferred property in proportion to built-in losses per **26 C.F.R. В§ 1.362-4**. Prevents double loss recognition (built-in loss in stock + built-in loss in property).

**В§ 362(e)(2)(C) JOINT ELECTION**: transferor and transferee may JOINTLY ELECT to reduce TRANSFEROR'S stock basis instead (preserving corporation's carryover basis). Strategic choice when corp's tax attributes (NOLs, depreciation, capital-loss carryforward) are valuable.

**Four transfer types**: Section351TransferSection362A, Section368ReorganizationSection362B, PaidInSurplusByShareholderSection362C, ContributionByNonShareholderSection362D.

**Four loss-limitation statuses**: NoLossLimitationApplied, Section362E1AntiLossImportationApplied, Section362E2AntiLossDuplicationAppliedToCorporationBasis, Section362E2CJointElectionTransferorStockBasisReduced.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section362ACarryoverBasisStandardSection351, Section362BCarryoverBasisReorganization, Section362CPaidInSurplusCarryover, Section362DContributionByNonShareholderZeroOrTcjaTreatment, Section362E1ImportationBasisSteppedDownToFmv, Section362E2DuplicationCorpBasisSteppedDownToFmv, Section362E2CJointElectionTransferorStockBasisReducedCorpKeepsCarryover.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-362`. Seventeen tests pin: **В§ 351 pure carryover basis** ($100K вҶ’ $100K + В§ 1223 holding-period tacking + В§ 362(a) note); **В§ 351 with gain recognized adds to carryover** ($100K + $20K = $120K); **В§ 368 reorganization carryover basis**; **В§ 362(c) paid-in surplus carryover**; **В§ 362(d) non-shareholder contribution zero basis TCJA treatment** (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13312 + В§ 118 repeal); **В§ 362(e)(1) anti-loss-importation reduces to FMV** ($500K basis - $200K FMV вҶ’ $200K basis + $300K loss eliminated + В§ 1.362-3 + March 28 2016 effective date); **В§ 362(e)(2) anti-loss-duplication reduces corp basis** (В§ 1.362-4 + В§ 362(e)(2)(C) joint election alternative note); **В§ 362(e)(2)(C) joint election preserves corp carryover** ($500K basis preserved + В§ 1.362-4(d)(3) filing requirement); **very large basis no overflow in carryover calc** (u64::MAX / 2 + saturating arithmetic); **zero basis zero carryover** (boundary); **note pins В§ 358 shareholder basis companion**; **note pins В§ 357 liability rules**; **note pins В§ 1245 + В§ 1250 recapture**; **note pins В§ 334(b)(1)(B) parallel for В§ 332 liquidations**; **note pins В§ 358(h) shareholder companion for В§ 362(e)(1)**; **note pins В§ 368(c) 80% control requirement**; **no loss no reduction when basis below FMV** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_358` is the **IRC В§ 358 вҖ” Basis to Distributees (Shareholder Basis in Stock Received) module** (iter 560 вҖ” milestone) вҖ” direct shareholder-basis-preservation cornerstone driving В§ 351 + В§ 354 + В§ 362 + В§ 368 tax-free formation/reorganization framework. Direct sibling to `section_351` (transfer to controlled corp parent regime), `section_354` (iter 526 вҖ” basis in reorganization stock), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corporate-level distribution recognition parallel), `section_318` (iter 552 вҖ” constructive ownership for В§ 368(c) control test), `section_245a` (iter 502 вҖ” foreign-source DRD relies on basis preservation). В§ 358 governs the basis a shareholder takes in stock and securities received in tax-free В§ 351 corporate formations and В§ 368 tax-free reorganizations; combined with В§ 362 (corporation's basis side), В§ 358 implements the "double basis preservation" that keeps gain or loss latent across formation/reorganization transactions.

**В§ 358(a)(1) GENERAL RULE** вҖ” shareholder's basis in stock and securities received: = adjusted basis of property transferred + gain recognized on transfer (under В§ 351(b) or В§ 356) - money received (boot) - FMV of other property received (boot) - liabilities assumed (treated as boot under В§ 358(d)).

**В§ 358(a)(2) BASIS ALLOCATION** among multiple classes of property received: basis allocated to each class in proportion to FMV at time of receipt.

**В§ 358(b)(1) MULTIPLE STOCK CLASSES**: basis allocated proportionally to FMV of each class.

**В§ 358(c) REORGANIZATIONS**: basis preserved via TRACING METHOD from surrendered shares to received shares (Federal Register 2006 final regulations).

**В§ 358(d) LIABILITY-AS-BOOT**: liabilities assumed by transferee corporation reduce shareholder's basis (treated as money received). **В§ 357(c)(1)** recognizes GAIN if liabilities exceed adjusted basis of transferred property.

**В§ 358(h) ANTI-LOSS-IMPORTATION RULE** (added Pub. L. 106-554 in 2000, finalized in 2014 regs): shareholder's basis in stock received is REDUCED to FMV if the liability assumption is part of a tax-avoidance scheme designed to import a built-in loss into the United States. Companion: **В§ 362(e)** corporate-level anti-loss-importation rule.

**Eight transaction types**: Section351TransferToControlledCorp, Section368A1AStatutoryMerger, Section368A1BStockForStock, Section368A1CStockForAssets, Section368A1DDivisiveReorganization, Section368A1ERecapitalization, Section368A1FMereChange, NotTaxDeferredTransactionInapplicable.

**Two anti-loss-importation statuses**: AntiLossImportationNotApplicable, AntiLossImportationAppliesBasisReducedToFmv.

**Five-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section358InapplicableNotTaxDeferred, Section358ABasisPreservedCarryoverWithAdjustments, Section358HAntiLossImportationBasisReducedToFmv, Section358ABasisReducedBelowZeroSection357C1GainRecognition.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-358`. Twenty tests pin: **not tax-deferred В§ 358 inapplicable** (В§ 1012 cost-basis fallback); **В§ 351 pure basis carryover** ($100K basis вҶ’ $100K + В§ 362(a) corporation-basis companion note); **В§ 351 with gain recognized increases basis** ($100K + $20K = $120K); **В§ 351 with money boot reduces basis** ($100K - $30K = $70K); **В§ 351 with other property boot reduces basis** ($100K - $25K = $75K); **В§ 351 with liabilities reduces basis** ($100K - $40K = $60K); **В§ 357(c)(1) gain recognized when liabilities exceed basis** ($50K basis - $120K liabilities вҶ’ $70K gain recognized + basis floored at $0 + В§ 357(a)/(b)/(c) notes); **anti-loss-importation reduces basis to FMV** (В§ 358(h) + Pub. L. 106-554 + В§ 362(e) corporate-level companion); **В§ 368(a)(1)(A) statutory merger**; **В§ 368(a)(1)(B) stock-for-stock**; **В§ 368(a)(1)(C) stock-for-assets**; **В§ 368(a)(1)(D) divisive reorganization**; **В§ 368(a)(1)(E) recapitalization**; **В§ 368(a)(1)(F) mere change**; **combined adjustments basis computation** ($100K + $15K - $10K - $5K - $30K = $70K); **very large basis no overflow** (i128 intermediate); **zero basis zero basis preserved** (boundary); **note pins В§ 362(a) corporation basis companion**; **note pins В§ 368(c) 80% control**; **note pins В§ 357 liability rules**.

`traderview-expense::section_312` is the **IRC В§ 312 вҖ” Effect on Earnings and Profits module** (iter 558) вҖ” direct E&P-computation cornerstone driving В§ 301 + В§ 302 + В§ 304 + В§ 305 + В§ 311 + В§ 245A subchapter-C cluster. Direct sibling to `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” corp-level recognition that adjusts E&P), `section_302` (iter 556 вҖ” redemption character determination turns on E&P), `section_304` (iter 554 вҖ” brother-sister stacking of acquiring then issuing E&P), `section_305` (stock-dividend companion regime), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD turns on dividend characterization which itself depends on E&P), `section_959`/`section_961` (PTEP framework for CFC E&P adjustments). В§ 312 governs the computation and adjustment of E&P, the touchstone for distribution character determination throughout subchapter C: В§ 301(c) classifies distributions as dividends to the extent of E&P, basis recovery thereafter, and capital gain on excess.

**В§ 312(a) GENERAL RULE**: E&P decreased on distribution by (1) amount of money distributed, (2) principal amount of obligations distributed, (3) adjusted basis of distributed property (other than money and obligations).

**В§ 312(b) APPRECIATED-PROPERTY DISTRIBUTION**: if В§ 311(b) gain recognized on distribution of appreciated property, E&P INCREASED by recognized gain then DECREASED by FMV (net effect equals decrease by basis + gain accrual).

**В§ 312(c) LIABILITY-ASSUMPTION MODIFIER**: if shareholder assumes liability OR distributed property is subject to liability, E&P decrease REDUCED by amount of liability (corporation relieved by liability transfer).

**В§ 312(d) STOCK DIVIDEND**: В§ 305(a) non-taxable stock dividends do NOT reduce E&P; В§ 305(b) taxable stock dividends do reduce E&P by FMV.

**В§ 312(k)(3) ADS STRAIGHT-LINE DEPRECIATION**: for tangible property to which В§ 168 applies, E&P depreciation determined under В§ 168(g)(2) Alternative Depreciation System (straight-line over ADS recovery period). Permanent timing difference vs accelerated tax depreciation.

**В§ 312(n) SPECIAL E&P ADJUSTMENTS** to align with economic income: (n)(1) construction-period interest capitalized + (n)(2) intangible drilling and mineral exploration capitalized over 60 months + (n)(3) circulation and organizational cost amortization + (n)(4) LIFO inventory adjustments to FIFO basis + (n)(5) **installment sales** computed as if installment method NOT used (full recognition in year of sale) + (n)(6) completed-contract method to percentage-of-completion + (n)(7) certain stock redemptions В§ 302 / В§ 304 stacking + (n)(8) foreign corporation PTEP under В§ 959 + В§ 961 + CFC adjustments.

**Eight adjustment types**: CashDistributionSection312A1, ObligationDistributionSection312A2, PropertyDistributionAtBasisSection312A3, AppreciatedPropertyDistributionSection312B, LiabilityAssumptionModifierSection312C, StockDividendSection312D, AdsStraightLineDepreciationSection312K3, InstallmentSaleSection312N5.

**Three stock-dividend-taxability statuses**: NonTaxableSection305A, TaxableSection305B, NotStockDividend.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, EepDecreaseCashOrObligation, EepDecreaseDistributedPropertyBasis, EepIncreaseAppreciationThenDecreaseFmv, EepDecreaseAdjustedByLiabilityAssumed, EepUnchangedNonTaxableStockDividendSection305A, EepReducedTaxableStockDividendSection305B, EepAdjustedAdsStraightLineSection312K3, EepFullRecognitionSection312N5InstallmentSale.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-312`. Seventeen tests pin: **cash distribution decreases E&P** ($1M - $100K = $900K); **obligation distribution decreases E&P**; **property distribution at basis decreases E&P by basis** ($1M - $30K = $970K); **appreciated property increase by gain then decrease by FMV** (gain $50K - FMV $80K = -$30K net = basis); **liability assumption modifier increases E&P back** (+$50K modifier); **non-taxable stock dividend does not reduce E&P** (В§ 307 basis allocation); **taxable stock dividend reduces E&P by FMV** (-$80K); **ADS straight-line depreciation adjustment** (tax $100K - ADS $60K = +$40K В§ 168(g)(2)); **installment sale acceleration to full recognition** (full $500K - installment $50K = $450K + В§ 959 + В§ 961 PTEP coordination); **note pins В§ 301(c) distribution character**; **note pins В§ 311 corporate recognition**; **note pins В§ 307 basis allocation for non-taxable dividend**; **very large E&P no overflow** (i128 intermediate); **zero starting E&P no panic** (boundary); **stock dividend not-stock-dividend flag returns not applicable** (input-consistency check); **appreciated property with zero basis full gain offsets FMV to zero basis** (boundary); **note pins В§ 959 + В§ 961 CFC PTEP for В§ 312(n)**.

`traderview-expense::section_318` is the **IRC В§ 318 вҖ” Constructive Ownership of Stock module** (iter 552) вҖ” direct constructive-ownership cornerstone companion to `section_267` (related-party loss-deferral parallel вҖ” В§ 267 includes siblings while В§ 318 does NOT), `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” acquisition-to-evade-tax control-acquisition rule uses В§ 318 attribution), `section_269a` (iter 544 вҖ” PSC employee-owner test uses В§ 318 attribution), `section_311` (iter 550 вҖ” distribution recognition triggered by В§ 302 analysis driven by В§ 318). В§ 318 attributes stock ownership from one taxpayer to another for purposes of related-party and ownership-percentage testing throughout the Code; drives stock-redemption analysis (В§ 302), brother-sister redemption recharacterization (В§ 304), accumulated-earnings tax (В§ 531), personal-holding-company tax (В§ 542), corporate-attribution-to-shareholder rules, qualified-personal-service-corporation testing.

**В§ 318(a)(1) FAMILY ATTRIBUTION**: individual treated as owning stock owned by **spouse** (unless legally separated under decree of divorce or separate maintenance), **children**, **grandchildren**, and **parents**. В§ 318 family attribution does NOT extend to **SIBLINGS** or grandparents (compare В§ 267 which DOES include siblings вҖ” this asymmetry is critical for В§ 302 redemption analysis).

**В§ 318(a)(2) ENTITY-TO-OWNER ATTRIBUTION (proportional)**: (a)(2)(A) **partnership/estate** stock attributed PROPORTIONATELY to partners/beneficiaries; (a)(2)(B) **trust** stock attributed via В§ 7520 actuarial interest for non-grantor trusts (full to grantor for grantor trusts); (a)(2)(C) **corporation** stock attributed to **50%+ shareholders** proportionally.

**В§ 318(a)(3) OWNER-TO-ENTITY ATTRIBUTION (in full)**: (a)(3)(A) partner/beneficiary stock attributed to partnership/estate IN FULL; (a)(3)(B) trust attribution similar; (a)(3)(C) 50%+ shareholder stock attributed to corporation IN FULL.

**В§ 318(a)(4) OPTION ATTRIBUTION**: holder of call option, warrant, or convertible debenture treated as owning the underlying stock. **В§ 318(a)(5)(D) priority rule**: option attribution takes priority over family attribution when both could apply.

**В§ 318(a)(5)(B) FAMILY-TO-FAMILY RE-ATTRIBUTION DISALLOWED**: stock attributed via family attribution cannot be re-attributed via further family attribution (prevents cascading attribution through spousal/parental chains).

**В§ 318(a)(5)(C) ENTITY-BOUNCE RE-ATTRIBUTION DISALLOWED**: stock attributed via (a)(2) entity-to-owner cannot be re-attributed via (a)(3) owner-to-entity (no double-bounce).

**Nine attribution-path branches**: FamilyAttributionSpouseChildGrandchildParent, SiblingAttemptDoesNotApplyToSection318, FromPartnershipOrEstateProportional, FromCorporationToFiftyPctShareholderProportional, ToPartnershipOrEstateInFull, ToCorporationFromFiftyPctShareholderInFull, OptionAttributionCallOrWarrantOrConvertible, ReAttributionFamilyToFamilyDisallowed, ReAttributionEntityBounceDisallowed.

**Two corporation-ownership tiers**: FiftyPctOrMoreEntityAttributionApplies, LessThanFiftyPctNoEntityAttribution.

**Nine-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoAttributionUnderSection318, Section318A1FamilyAttributionApplies, Section318A2EntityToOwnerProportionalAttribution, Section318A3OwnerToEntityFullAttribution, Section318A4OptionAttributionApplies, Section318A5BReAttributionDisallowedFamilyToFamily, Section318A5CReAttributionDisallowedEntityBounce, SiblingNotIncludedInSection318FamilyAttribution.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-318`. Nineteen tests pin: **family attribution full attribution** (В§ 318(a)(1) + spouse + child + grandchild + parent); **sibling does NOT apply to В§ 318** (В§ 267 + В§ 302 contrast); **partnership to partner proportional attribution** (В§ 318(a)(2)(A) + 1000 Г— 60% = 600); **corporation to 50% plus shareholder proportional** (В§ 318(a)(2)(C)); **corporation below 50% no attribution** (NoAttributionUnderSection318); **partner to partnership full attribution** (В§ 318(a)(3)(A) + 100% not proportional); **shareholder to corp 50% plus full attribution** (В§ 318(a)(3)(C)); **shareholder to corp below 50% no attribution**; **option attribution treats holder as owner** (В§ 318(a)(4) + В§ 318(a)(5)(D) priority + substantially-disproportionate); **re-attribution family to family disallowed** (В§ 318(a)(5)(B)); **re-attribution entity bounce disallowed** (В§ 318(a)(5)(C) + double-bounce); **fifty pct threshold constant pins 5,000 bps**; **very large shares no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero proportional yields zero attribution** (boundary); **note pins В§ 267 sibling-inclusion contrast**; **note pins В§ 542 PHC ownership test**; **note pins В§ 856(h) REIT test**; **note pins В§ 304 brother-sister**; **note pins В§ 7520 actuarial for trusts**.

`traderview-expense::section_311` is the **IRC В§ 311 вҖ” Taxability of Corporation on Distribution of Property in Kind module** (iter 550 вҖ” milestone) вҖ” direct corporate-distribution cornerstone companion to `section_305` (stock dividends вҖ” companion non-recognition rule), `section_245a` (iter 502 вҖ” foreign-source DRD distributee), `section_246` (iter 548 вҖ” DRD parent rules), `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” debt-financed-portfolio-stock DRD reduction). В§ 311 governs gain/loss recognition by the DISTRIBUTING corporation on distributions of property in kind to shareholders. The **Tax Reform Act of 1986** repealed the **General Utilities doctrine** and added В§ 311(b), which requires the distributing corporation to recognize gain on appreciated-property distributions as if the property had been SOLD at fair market value, closing the asset-removal-without-tax loophole. В§ 311(a) continues to deny LOSS recognition on depreciated-property distributions вҖ” losses are preserved only through В§ 336 liquidation distributions, sales, or worthlessness under В§ 165(g).

**В§ 311(a) GENERAL RULE**: no gain or loss to distributing corporation on distribution of property with respect to its stock (subject to В§ 311(b)).

**В§ 311(b)(1) APPRECIATED PROPERTY**: if FMV > adjusted basis, gain RECOGNIZED to distributing corporation as if the property had been SOLD at FMV.

**В§ 311(b)(2) LIABILITY FLOOR** (cross-reference to В§ 336(b)): if shareholder assumes liability of distributing corporation OR the distributed property is subject to a liability, FMV deemed to be NOT LESS than the amount of the liability.

**В§ 311(b)(3) PARTNERSHIP / TRUST ANTI-LOSS RULE**: if distributed property is a partnership or trust interest, gain computed WITHOUT regard to any loss attributable to property contributed to the partnership/trust with a principal purpose of recognizing loss on the distribution (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.311-2).

**В§ 311 INAPPLICABLE** to: (a) В§ 332 parent-subsidiary liquidations (governed by В§ 332 + В§ 337 subsidiary-level non-recognition + parent-level carry-over basis), (b) В§ 336 corporate liquidations (governed by В§ 336 including loss recognition that В§ 311 denies for ordinary distributions), (c) В§ 355 corporate-division distributions (governed by В§ 355 non-recognition subject to active-trade-or-business + device-test requirements).

**Four distribution types**: OrdinarySection301Distribution, Section332ParentSubLiquidationSection311Inapplicable, Section336GeneralLiquidationSection311Inapplicable, Section355CorporateDivisionSection311Inapplicable.

**Three appreciation statuses**: FmvExceedsAdjustedBasisAppreciated, FmvEqualsAdjustedBasisNeutral, FmvBelowAdjustedBasisDepreciated.

**Three partnership/trust anti-loss statuses**: NotPartnershipOrTrustInterest, PartnershipOrTrustInterestBonaFideContribution, PartnershipOrTrustInterestPrincipalPurposeOfLossRecognition.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section311InapplicableOtherDistributionRegime, Section311ANoGainOrLossNeutralDistribution, Section311ANoLossOnDepreciatedPropertyDistribution, Section311BGainRecognitionAppreciatedProperty, Section311BWithLiabilityAssumptionFmvFloorAdjustment, Section311B3PartnershipTrustAntiLossDisallowance.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-311`. Seventeen tests pin: **В§ 332 inapplicable to В§ 311** (В§ 332 + В§ 337 governing-provision note); **В§ 336 inapplicable to В§ 311**; **В§ 355 inapplicable to В§ 311**; **appreciated property triggers В§ 311(b) gain** (FMV $100K - basis $30K = $70K gain + General Utilities + Tax Reform Act of 1986 notes); **neutral FMV basis no gain or loss** (В§ 311(a)); **depreciated property no loss recognition** (В§ 311(a) + В§ 336 + В§ 165(g) + В§ 267 alternatives); **liability floor applies when liability exceeds FMV** ($120K liability > $80K FMV вҶ’ deemed FMV $120K + $90K gain + В§ 311(b)(2) note); **liability below FMV no floor adjustment**; **partnership anti-loss scrubs loss recognition** (В§ 311(b)(3) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.311-2); **partnership bona-fide contribution no anti-loss**; **note pins В§ 1374 S-corp built-in-gain**; **note pins В§ 312 E&P computation**; **note pins В§ 1245 / В§ 1250 depreciation recapture**; **note pins В§ 301(d) distributee basis**; **very large FMV no overflow** (u64::MAX saturating arithmetic); **zero FMV no panic** (boundary); **other distribution regime overrides appreciation analysis** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::section_280h` is the **IRC В§ 280H вҖ” Limitation on Certain Amounts Paid to Employee-Owners by Personal Service Corporations Electing Alternative Taxable Years module** (iter 546) вҖ” direct sibling to `section_269a` (iter 544 вҖ” PSC tax-avoidance allocation parent regime), `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” discretionary anti-tax-avoidance disallowance), `section_280g` (golden parachute disallowance вҖ” companion executive-compensation regime). В§ 280H disallows a portion of a personal service corporation's (PSC) employee-owner compensation deduction when (1) the PSC has a В§ 444 fiscal-year election in effect AND (2) the PSC fails the minimum distribution requirement of В§ 280H(c). Closes the income-deferral loophole that a non-calendar fiscal-year PSC would otherwise enjoy: without В§ 280H, a fiscal-year PSC could end its fiscal year before paying employee-owners, deferring inclusion to the employee-owners' next calendar year while still claiming the deduction.

**В§ 280H(a) GENERAL RULE**: deduction otherwise allowed for "applicable amounts" paid or incurred to employee-owners is limited to the "maximum deductible amount" when the PSC has a В§ 444 election AND fails the В§ 280H(c) minimum distribution requirement.

**В§ 280H(b) APPLICABLE AMOUNT DEFINITION**: any amount otherwise deductible by the PSC in the taxable year AND includable at any time, directly or indirectly, in the gross income of a taxpayer who is an employee-owner.

**В§ 280H(c) MINIMUM DISTRIBUTION REQUIREMENT**: applicable amounts paid during the deferral period must equal or exceed the **LESSER OF**: (A) prior-year applicable amounts Г— (deferral months / preceding-year months), or (B) applicable percentage Г— adjusted taxable income for the deferral period.

**В§ 280H(d) APPLICABLE PERCENTAGE**: percentage capped at **95%** computed from prior-3-year applicable amounts / prior-3-year adjusted taxable income.

**В§ 280H(f) CARRYOVER**: nondeductible amounts treated as paid or incurred in the succeeding taxable year.

**В§ 280H(g) NOL CARRYBACK BAR**: no NOL carryback allowed to or from any taxable year of a PSC that has a В§ 444 election in effect.

**Two corporation statuses**: IsPersonalServiceCorporation, NotAPersonalServiceCorporation.

**Two В§ 444 election statuses**: Section444FiscalYearElectionInEffect, CalendarYearNoSection280HApplies.

**Five-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotPersonalServiceCorporationSection280HInapplicable, CalendarYearNoSection280HApplied, MinimumDistributionRequirementSatisfiedNoDisallowance, Section280HDisallowanceAppliedCarryoverToNextYear.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280h`. Seventeen tests pin: **not PSC В§ 280H inapplicable** (В§ 269A(b)(1) + В§ 162 reasonable-compensation fallback coordination); **calendar year no В§ 280H applied** (В§ 280H(g) NOL carryback bar note); **minimum distribution requirement satisfied no disallowance** ($1.2M Г— 3/12 = $300K vs $600K Г— 80% = $480K вҶ’ lesser $300K + paid $500K вҶ’ satisfied); **shortfall triggers В§ 280H disallowance** ($200K paid vs $300K threshold вҶ’ $100K shortfall + В§ 280H(f) carryover note); **applicable percentage capped at 95%** ($600K Г— 95% = $570K cap); **prior year proration branch when smaller than ATI percentage** ($400K Г— 3/12 = $100K vs $480K вҶ’ lesser $100K); **ATI percentage branch when smaller than prior year proration** ($12M Г— 3/12 = $3M vs $480K вҶ’ lesser $480K); **zero preceding months no division panic** (division-by-zero defense); **applicable percentage cap constant pins 95%**; **very large amounts no overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero amounts no panic** (boundary); **note pins В§ 444 election companion**; **note pins В§ 269A companion**; **note pins Form 8716 В§ 444 election**; **note pins Schedule H (Form 1120) В§ 280H computation**; **calendar year takes priority over PSC status** (severity ladder ordering); **not PSC takes priority over В§ 444 election** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::section_269a` is the **IRC В§ 269A вҖ” Personal Service Corporations Formed or Availed of to Avoid or Evade Income Tax module** (iter 544) вҖ” direct sibling to `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” broader anti-tax-avoidance acquisition disallowance), `section_482` (related-party transfer pricing), `section_162a` (reasonable compensation reallocation), `section_280g` (golden parachute), `section_199a` (QBI deduction with Specified Service Trade or Business limitation parallel), `section_409a` (NQDC). В§ 269A gives Treasury authority to ALLOCATE income, deductions, credits, exclusions, and other allowances between a personal service corporation (PSC) and its employee-owner(s) when the corporation is formed or used to avoid or evade federal income tax. Targets the classic "incorporate-and-zero-out" tactic where an individual provides services to a single client, forms a wholly-owned PSC to receive the income, then zeroes out the PSC's taxable income via fringe benefits, deferred compensation, and qualified retirement contributions that the individual could not claim if reporting the income directly on Schedule C.

**В§ 269A(a) STATUTORY TEST** вҖ” both conditions must be satisfied: (1) **SUBSTANTIALLY ALL** of the PSC's services performed for **ONE** other corporation, partnership, or entity; (2) the principal purpose of forming or availing of the PSC is to AVOID or EVADE federal income tax by securing the benefit of any expense, deduction, credit, exclusion, or other allowance the employee-owner could not otherwise claim.

**В§ 269A(b)(1) PSC DEFINITION**: corporation whose principal activity is performance of personal services AND those services are substantially performed by employee-owners.

**В§ 269A(b)(2) EMPLOYEE-OWNER DEFINITION**: any employee who owns, on any day during the taxable year, **MORE THAN 10%** of the outstanding stock of the PSC.

**В§ 269A(b)(3) RELATED-PERSONS AGGREGATION**: all related persons within the meaning of В§ 144(a)(3) are treated as one entity for the "one entity" test under В§ 269A(a)(1). Effective for taxable years beginning after December 31, 1982.

**Two corporation-activity types**: PersonalServiceCorporationSection269AB1, NonPersonalServiceBusiness.

**Two client-concentration statuses**: SubstantiallyAllServicesForOneEntity, ServicesPerformedForMultipleUnrelatedClients.

**Two principal-purpose statuses**: PrincipalPurposeIsTaxAvoidanceOrEvasion, PrincipalPurposeIsBonaFideBusiness.

**Two employee-owner threshold statuses**: EmployeeOwnsMoreThanTenPercent, EmployeeOwnsTenPercentOrLess.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotPersonalServiceCorporationSection269AInapplicable, MultipleClientsFailsOneEntityTestNoAllocation, BonaFideBusinessPurposeNoAllocation, NoEmployeeOwnerThresholdSatisfiedNoAllocation, Section269AAllocationApplied.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-269a`. Seventeen tests pin: **non-PSC corporation В§ 269A inapplicable** (В§ 482 + В§ 162 fallback coordination); **multiple clients fails one-entity test no allocation** (В§ 269A(a)(1) + В§ 144(a)(3) related-persons aggregation); **bona-fide business purpose no allocation** (EXCEEDS IN IMPORTANCE standard + professional-liability shield + state-bar professional-corporation registration); **no employee-owner threshold no allocation** (В§ 269A(b)(2) + more-than-10% threshold); **all four conditions satisfied В§ 269A allocation applied** ($500K PSC income recharacterized to employee-owner); **employee-owner threshold constant pins 10%**; **В§ 269A effective year constant pins 1983**; **note pins В§ 269 companion**; **note pins В§ 482 related-party transfer pricing**; **note pins В§ 280H PSC accumulated earnings**; **note pins В§ 199A specified service trade or business**; **very large PSC income no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero income zero allocation no panic** (boundary); **non-PSC takes priority over all other conditions** (severity ladder ordering); **multiple clients takes priority over purpose and owner** (severity ladder ordering); **bona-fide business purpose takes priority over employee-owner threshold** (severity ladder ordering); **note describes classic zero-out tactic** (recharacterize + benefit).

`traderview-expense::section_269` is the **IRC В§ 269 вҖ” Acquisitions Made to Evade or Avoid Income Tax module** (iter 536) вҖ” direct anti-loss-trafficking companion to `section_279` (iter 534 вҖ” corporate acquisition indebtedness $5M cap parallel anti-LBO regime), `section_267` (related-party loss-deferral parallel anti-abuse), `section_265` (iter 532 вҖ” tax-exempt-interest disallowance), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD вҖ” coordinates with В§ 382(l)(5) bankruptcy framework), `section_267` (related-party regime), `section_382` (NOL annual cap вҖ” mechanical companion that operates alongside В§ 269 discretionary regime), `section_383` (general-business-credit cap), `section_384` (built-in-gain 5-year limit). В§ 269 gives Treasury authority to DISALLOW any deduction, credit, or other allowance when (1) a person acquires CONTROL of a corporation OR a corporation acquires the property of another corporation with carry-over basis, AND (2) the principal purpose of the acquisition was to evade or avoid federal income tax by securing a benefit the acquirer would not otherwise enjoy. В§ 269 is the primary anti-loss-trafficking weapon operating ALONGSIDE the mechanical В§ 382 NOL cap (post-1986); В§ 269 reaches non-NOL benefits (general business credits, accelerated depreciation, foreign tax credits, charitable contribution carryovers) that В§ 382 does not capture.

**В§ 269(a) "CONTROL" THRESHOLD**: ownership of at least **50%** of total combined voting power OR at least 50% of total value of all classes of stock entitled to vote.

**В§ 269(a)(1)**: stock acquisition triggering control analysis. **В§ 269(a)(2)**: asset acquisition with carry-over basis transaction. **В§ 269(b)**: В§ 332 parent-subsidiary liquidation within **2-year window** after acquisition triggers NOL carry-over disallowance. **В§ 269(c)**: REBUTTABLE PRESUMPTION when purchase price substantially disproportionate to asset value (excluding tax-benefit value); strong business-purpose evidence (вүҘ 75% / 7,500 bps in our framework) rebuts.

**В§ 382(l)(5) BANKRUPTCY COORDINATION**: Treas. Reg. В§ 1.269-3(d) per se presumption that ownership change in В§ 382(l)(5) bankruptcy reorganization is for tax-avoidance principal purpose UNLESS the corporation carries on more than an insignificant amount of an active trade or business during AND after the title 11 case (operating divisions continued, employee retention, post-emergence revenue).
