**Five acquisition types**: StockAcquisitionSection269A1, AssetAcquisitionCarryOverBasisSection269A2, Section332LiquidationWithinTwoYearsSection269B, Section382L5BankruptcyOwnershipChange, NoAcquisitionOfControl.

**Two control-threshold statuses**: ControlAcquiredFiftyPercentOrMore, ControlNotAcquiredBelowFiftyPercent.

**Five principal-purpose branches**: PrincipalPurposeIsTaxAvoidance, PrincipalPurposeIsBonaFideBusiness, PriceDisproportionateSection269CPresumption, Section382L5BankruptcyPerSePresumptionActiveBusinessNotMaintained, Section382L5BankruptcyPerSePresumptionRebuttedActiveBusinessMaintained.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoControlAcquisitionSection269Inapplicable, BonaFideBusinessPurposeNoDisallowance, Section382L5BankruptcyActiveBusinessMaintainedNoDisallowance, Section269ATaxBenefitDisallowanceApplied, Section269BNolCarryoverDisallowanceApplied, Section269CPriceDisproportionatePresumptionApplied, Section382L5PerSePresumptionDisallowanceApplied.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-269`. Twenty-one tests pin: **no acquisition of control В§ 269 inapplicable** (В§ 382 + В§ 383 + В§ 384 separate-limit coordination note); **control below 50% В§ 269 inapplicable**; **bona-fide business purpose preserves tax benefit** (synergy + EXCEEDS IN IMPORTANCE standard); **В§ 269(a) disallowance for stock acquisition tax avoidance** ($50M disallowed of $50M benefit); **В§ 269(a)(2) asset acquisition carryover basis disallowance**; **В§ 269(b) В§ 332 liquidation within 2 years disallowance**; **В§ 269(c) price disproportionate presumption applied** (5,000 bps evidence вҶ’ presumption stands); **В§ 269(c) presumption rebutted with strong business evidence** (8,000 bps evidence вҶ’ rebutted); **В§ 269(c) presumption boundary at 7,500 bps rebuts** (boundary); **В§ 269(c) presumption just under threshold disallowance** (7,499 bps вҶ’ presumption stands); **В§ 382(l)(5) bankruptcy active business maintained no disallowance** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.269-3(d) + title 11); **В§ 382(l)(5) bankruptcy no active business per se disallowance**; **control threshold constant pins 50%**; **В§ 269(b) liquidation window constant pins 2 years**; **very large tax benefit no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero tax benefit no panic** (boundary); **note pins В§ 383 general-business-credit cap**; **note pins В§ 384 built-in-gain limit**; **note no control pins В§ 382 + В§ 383 + В§ 384 separate limits**; **bona-fide business purpose overrides В§ 269(a) branch** (severity ladder ordering); **no control takes priority over principal purpose status** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::section_383` is the **IRC В§ 383 вҖ” Special Limitations on Certain Excess Credits and Capital Losses module** (iter 540) вҖ” direct anti-loss-trafficking cluster companion to `section_382` (NOL annual cap parent regime), `section_384` (iter 538 вҖ” preacquisition-loss built-in-gain disallowance), `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” discretionary acquisition-to-evade-tax disallowance), `section_279` (iter 534 вҖ” corporate acquisition indebtedness $5M cap), `section_265` (iter 532 вҖ” tax-exempt interest disallowance), `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” DRD reduction for debt-financed portfolio stock). В§ 383 extends the В§ 382 ownership-change annual-limitation regime to four categories of corporate carryover attributes that fall outside В§ 382 itself: (1) general business credits under В§ 39, (2) minimum tax credits under В§ 53, (3) net capital loss carryovers under В§ 1212, and (4) excess foreign tax credits under В§ 904(c). The mechanic computes the tax that would be attributable to taxable income equal to the В§ 382 limitation, then caps pre-change credit/loss usage against that ceiling.

**В§ 383 OPERATING SEQUENCE**: (1) В§ 382(g) ownership change occurs (more-than-50-percentage-point change in stock ownership by 5-percent shareholders over the 3-year testing period); (2) В§ 382(b) annual limitation = loss-corp FMV Г— federal long-term tax-exempt rate; (3) pre-change NOLs absorb В§ 382 limit first; (4) В§ 383 limit = tax that WOULD have been due on remaining В§ 382 limit Г— effective corporate tax rate; (5) excess credits offset that tentative tax up to the В§ 383 limit; (6) unused excess credits carry forward with sequential annual reset of the В§ 383 limit.

**В§ 383(a) GENERAL BUSINESS CREDIT + MINIMUM TAX CREDIT REGIME**: post-change use limited to tax attributable to taxable income within the В§ 382 limit.

**В§ 383(b) NET CAPITAL LOSS CARRYOVER**: В§ 1212(a) carryover limited under regulations based on В§ 382 principles. Capital loss used in post-change year **REDUCES** the В§ 382 limit for that year applied to pre-change NOLs (**anti-stuffing rule**).

**В§ 383(c) EXCESS FOREIGN TAX CREDIT**: В§ 904(c) excess FTC carryover (10-year carryforward + 1-year carryback within basket) limited consistent with В§ 382 + В§ 383 principles.

**В§ 383(d) ORDERING**: terms used in В§ 383 have same meaning as В§ 382, with appropriate adjustments to apply to credits and capital losses.

**Two ownership-change statuses**: OwnershipChangeOccurredSection382G, NoOwnershipChangeNoLimitationTriggered.

**Four attribute types**: Section39GeneralBusinessCredit, Section53MinimumTaxCredit, Section1212NetCapitalLossCarryover, Section904ExcessForeignTaxCreditCarryover.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoOwnershipChangeFullAttributeUsageAllowed, Section383CreditLimitationApplied, Section383BCapitalLossLimitationAppliedAntiStuffing, Section383CExcessFtcLimitationApplied, AttributeFullyUsedWithinSection383Limit.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-383`. Eighteen tests pin: **no ownership change full attribute usage allowed** (3-year testing period + 50-pct-point threshold); **В§ 383 general business credit limited to tax on В§ 382 limit** ($10M Г— 21% = $2.1M limit, $5M credit вҶ’ $2.1M allowed $2.9M disallowed); **В§ 383 minimum tax credit uses same formula**; **В§ 383(b) capital loss competes with В§ 382 limit anti-stuffing** ($15M loss vs $10M В§ 382 limit вҶ’ $10M allowed $5M disallowed); **В§ 383(c) excess FTC uses credit formula** (В§ 904(c) 10-year carryforward note); **NOL absorption reduces В§ 383 credit limit** ($7M NOL вҶ’ $3M remaining вҶ’ $630K В§ 383 limit); **full NOL absorption zeroes В§ 383 limit**; **small credit under В§ 383 limit fully used**; **capital loss smaller than В§ 382 limit fully used**; **higher tax rate increases В§ 383 credit limit** (35% pre-TCJA вҶ’ $3.5M limit); **В§ 382 testing period constant pins 3 years**; **В§ 382 ownership change threshold pins 50 pct points**; **very large credit no overflow** (u64::MAX); **zero credit balance no disallowance** (boundary); **note pins В§ 269 discretionary disallowance**; **note pins В§ 384 preacquisition loss**; **note pins В§ 382 NOL annual cap**; **capital loss anti-stuffing note present** (REDUCES В§ 382 limit applied to pre-change NOLs).

`traderview-expense::section_384` is the **IRC В§ 384 вҖ” Limitation on Use of Preacquisition Losses to Offset Built-In Gain module** (iter 538) вҖ” direct anti-loss-trafficking cluster companion to `section_269` (iter 536 вҖ” discretionary acquisition-to-evade-tax disallowance), `section_279` (iter 534 вҖ” corporate acquisition indebtedness $5M cap), `section_382` (NOL annual cap post-ownership-change вҖ” mechanical companion that operates ALONGSIDE В§ 384), `section_267` (related-party loss-deferral parallel anti-abuse). В§ 384 prevents a "loss corporation" from offsetting "recognized built-in gain" of an acquired profitable corporation (the "gain corporation") during a 5-year recognition period. The provision plugs the converse hole В§ 382 leaves open: В§ 382 stops a loss-corp from absorbing the profitable-corp's POST-acquisition income; В§ 384 stops the loss-corp from absorbing the gain-corp's PRE-acquisition built-in gain that happens to be recognized within the 5-year window.

**В§ 384(a) GENERAL RULE**: if a corporation acquires control of another corporation OR the assets of a corporation are acquired in a В§ 368 reorganization AND either corporation is a "gain corporation," income attributable to RECOGNIZED BUILT-IN GAIN cannot be offset by any "preacquisition loss" вҖ” except the gain corp's OWN preacquisition loss (statutory carveout).

**В§ 384(a)(1) RECOGNITION PERIOD**: 5 years beginning on the acquisition date. Built-in gains recognized after year 5 are NOT subject to В§ 384.

**В§ 384(b)(2) CONTROL THRESHOLD**: В§ 1504(a)(2) standard вҖ” at least **80%** voting power AND **80%** value (same standard as affiliated-group filings).

**В§ 384(b)(3) COMMON-CONTROL EXCEPTION**: В§ 384 inapplicable where loss corp and gain corp were in same controlled group (**more than 50%** per В§ 384(b)(3) modified В§ 1563(a)) at all times during the 5-year period ending on the acquisition date.

**В§ 384(c)(8) PREACQUISITION-LOSS DEFINITION**: NOL carryforward to year of acquisition + NOL for year of acquisition allocable to pre-acquisition portion + capital loss carryover + general business credit carryforward + foreign tax credit carryover.

**Three acquisition types**: StockAcquisitionSection1504A2Control, Section368ReorganizationAssetAcquisition, NoQualifyingAcquisition.

**Two common-control-exception statuses**: NotInSameControlledGroupExceptionInapplicable, InSameControlledGroupFiveYearsPreAcquisitionExceptionApplies.

**Two recognition-timing statuses**: BuiltInGainRecognizedWithinFiveYearRecognitionPeriod, BuiltInGainRecognizedAfterFiveYearWindow.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NoQualifyingAcquisitionSection384Inapplicable, CommonControlExceptionAppliesSection384bThreeNoDisallowance, BuiltInGainRecognizedAfterFiveYearWindowNoDisallowance, NoPreacquisitionLossNoDisallowance, Section384APreacquisitionLossOffsetDisallowed.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-384`. Twenty tests pin: **no qualifying acquisition В§ 384 inapplicable** (80% threshold + В§ 382 coordination note); **common-control exception applies no disallowance** (В§ 384(b)(3) + В§ 1563(a) more-than-50% standard); **built-in gain after 5-year window no disallowance**; **no preacquisition loss no disallowance**; **В§ 384(a) disallows loss-corp offset of gain-corp BIG** ($10M BIG - $2M gain-corp-own = $8M remaining вҶ’ $13M loss-corp attempted вҶ’ $8M disallowed); **gain corp own preacquisition loss carveout offsets own BIG** ($5M own вҶ’ $5M allowed + $5M disallowed); **gain corp full offset zero disallowance** ($10M own = $10M BIG вҶ’ 0 disallowed); **loss smaller than BIG disallowance capped at loss amount** ($3M loss vs $10M BIG вҶ’ $3M disallowed); **В§ 368 reorganization triggers В§ 384 analysis**; **recognition period constant pins 5 years**; **control threshold constant pins 80%**; **common-control exception threshold pins 50%**; **very large built-in gain no overflow** (u64::MAX + saturating arithmetic); **zero built-in gain no disallowance** (boundary); **note pins В§ 381 carryover-attribute transferee rules**; **note pins В§ 269 discretionary disallowance**; **note pins В§ 1504(a)(2) control standard**; **note pins 5-year recognition period**; **common-control exception overrides qualifying acquisition disallowance** (severity ladder ordering); **no qualifying acquisition takes priority over common control** (severity ladder ordering).

`traderview-expense::section_279` is the **IRC В§ 279 вҖ” Interest on Corporate Acquisition Indebtedness module** (iter 534) вҖ” direct corporate-M&A companion to `section_265` (iter 532 вҖ” parallel tax-exempt-interest disallowance regime), `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” DRD reduction for debt-financed portfolio stock), `section_163d` (investment-interest cap вҖ” companion for individual M&A debt), `section_163h` (qualified residence interest), `section_163j` (business-interest-expense limit вҖ” separate cap on remaining post-В§ 279 interest), `section_274` (entertainment/meals disallowance sibling), `section_269` (acquisition-to-evade-tax disallowance вҖ” sister anti-avoidance regime), `section_385` (debt-equity classification вҖ” whether instrument is debt vs equity), `section_7874` (anti-inversion). В§ 279(a) disallows the corporate interest deduction for amounts exceeding $5,000,000 per tax year on "corporate acquisition indebtedness" вҖ” debt incurred to acquire stock or assets of another corporation that satisfies all four В§ 279(b) statutory criteria. Targets debt-financed leveraged-buyout (LBO) transactions and "junk-bond" subordinated convertible takeover financings of the 1970s-1980s. Disallowance is PERMANENT (not capitalized, no carryforward). В§ 279 remains live but practical importance is reduced by post-1980s capital-structure norms (straight subordinated debt without warrants typically used) and parallel В§ 163(j) interest limitation enacted by TCJA.

**В§ 279(b) FOUR-PRONG DEFINITION** (all four must be satisfied for "corporate acquisition indebtedness"): (1) ISSUED AFTER **October 9, 1969** to provide consideration for acquisition of stock or assets of another corporation; (2) SUBORDINATED to claims of trade creditors generally OR expressly subordinated to a substantial amount of unsecured indebtedness; (3) CONVERTIBLE into issuer stock OR issued as part of an investment unit with stock-purchase warrants or options; (4) Issuer DEBT:EQUITY ratio EXCEEDS **2:1** OR projected EBITDA fails **3:1** INTEREST-COVERAGE test for three-taxable-year averaging period.

**В§ 279(d) PRE-1969 EXEMPTION**: obligations issued on or before October 9, 1969 grandfathered.

**В§ 279(g) SMALL-ISSUER SAFE HARBOR**: applies only to issuer with total acquisition interest above $5M.

**В§ 279(h) COMPENSATION-STOCK SAFE HARBOR**: stock acquired as compensation (employee stock-purchase plan, В§ 83 transfer for services, deferred-compensation funding) is NOT an "acquisition" for В§ 279 purposes вҖ” short-circuits all other branches.

**Two issuance-date branches**: PreOctober9_1969Grandfathered, PostOctober9_1969SubjectToSection279.

**Two subordination statuses**: SubordinatedToTradeCreditorsOrUnsecuredIndebtedness, NotSubordinatedFailsSection279B2.

**Three convertibility statuses**: ConvertibleIntoIssuerStock, IssuedAsInvestmentUnitWithWarrantsOrOptions, NeitherConvertibleNorWarrantPackagedFailsSection279B3.

**Three debt-equity-and-coverage branches**: DebtEquityRatioExceedsTwoToOne, ProjectedEbitdaFailsThreeToOneInterestCoverage, DebtEquityUnderTwoToOneAndCoverageAboveThreeToOnePasses.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, PreOctober1969GrandfatheredNoDisallowance, Section279HCompensationStockSafeHarborNoDisallowance, NotSubordinatedFailsSection279DefinitionNoDisallowance, NeitherConvertibleNorWarrantPackagedFailsSection279DefinitionNoDisallowance, DebtEquityAndCoverageSafeHarborPassesNoDisallowance, SmallIssuerUnderFiveMillionThresholdNoDisallowance, Section279AInterestDisallowanceApplied.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-279`. Twenty-two tests pin: **pre-October 1969 grandfathered no disallowance** (В§ 163(j) coordination note); **В§ 279(h) compensation-stock safe harbor no disallowance** (В§ 83 transfer for services); **not subordinated fails definition no disallowance** (В§ 279(b)(2) + all four prongs requirement); **neither convertible nor warrant-packaged fails definition** (В§ 279(b)(3) + straight non-convertible note); **debt-equity and coverage safe harbor passes no disallowance** (В§ 279(b)(4) + 2:1 + 3:1); **small issuer under $5M threshold no disallowance** ($4M < $5M effective threshold); **debt-equity failure triggers full disallowance above $5M** ($10M - $5M = $5M disallowed + PERMANENTLY DISALLOWED); **coverage failure triggers full disallowance above $5M**; **pre-1969 interest reduces effective threshold** ($5M - $1M pre-1969 = $4M effective threshold + $6M disallowed); **pre-1969 interest exceeds $5M floors threshold at zero** (saturating_sub defense); **warrant-packaged obligation meets В§ 279(b)(3)**; **В§ 279(a) threshold constant pins $5M**; **note pins В§ 385 debt-equity classification**; **note pins В§ 269 acquisition-to-evade-tax**; **note pins В§ 163(j) business-interest limit**; **boundary exactly $5M no disallowance**; **boundary one cent above $5M triggers disallowance** (boundary); **very large interest no overflow** (u64::MAX + saturating_sub defense); **zero interest no disallowance** (boundary); **subordination failure takes priority over debt-equity failure** (severity ladder ordering); **compensation safe harbor overrides all other branches** (severity ladder ordering); **grandfathering takes priority over compensation safe harbor** (severity ladder ordering).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-274`. Thirty-three tests pin: **business meal 50% deductible**; **business meal lavish disallowed**; **business meal taxpayer absent disallowed**; **entertainment fully disallowed post-TCJA**; **per se entertainment facility disallowed**; **substantiation missing complete denial** (Sanford + COHAN); **travel fully deductible with substantiation**; **travel without substantiation disallowed**; **foreign convention unreasonable disallowed**; **foreign convention reasonable deductible**; **gift under $25 per recipient fully deductible**; **gift over $25 per recipient capped**; **gift multiple recipients aggregate cap**; **gift promotional item exception**; **luxury water travel capped at daily rate**; **temporary 100% restaurant meal 2021-2022** (CAA 2021 В§ 210); **substantiation required for meals/travel/gifts** (7-category sweep); **category truth table eight cells**; **entertainment uniquely fully disallowed invariant**; **gift limit pinned at $25**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 274(a) + В§ 274(b) + В§ 274(d) + В§ 274(e) + В§ 274(h) + В§ 274(k) + В§ 274(m) + В§ 274(n) + В§ 274(o) + В§ 162(a) + В§ 162(e) + Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13304 + Pub. L. 116-260 В§ 210 + Pub. L. 119-21 В§ 70202 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.274-1 + IRS Notice 2018-76 + IRS Pub. 463 + Sanford v. Commissioner + Cohan v. Commissioner); **note pins В§ 274 overlay on В§ 162(a)**; **note pins В§ 274(a) entertainment TCJA repeal**; **note pins В§ 274(k) two conditions**; **note pins В§ 274(n) 50% with 2021-2022 exception**; **note pins В§ 274(o) 8 per se facilities**; **note pins В§ 274(d) substantiation 4 elements**; **note pins В§ 274(b) gift $25 limit since 1962**; **note pins В§ 274(h) foreign convention 4-factor**; **note pins В§ 274(m) luxury water travel**; **note pins OBBBA 2026 update**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins companion modules**.

`traderview-expense::section_164` is the **IRC В§164 State and Local Tax (SALT) deduction cap module** вҖ” hits every high-income itemizing trader and landlord in high-tax states. TCJA capped SALT at **$10K ($5K MFS)** for 2018-2024; OBBBA В§ 70413 **temporarily expanded the cap to $40K ($20K MFS)** effective 2025-01-01 with a high-income phaseout, automatic 1% annual growth through 2029, and a hard sunset back to the TCJA cap in 2030.

**Mechanics (2025-2029)**:

| Component | Single / MFJ / HoH (2025) | MFS (2025) | Annual growth |
|-----------|----------------------------|------------|----------------|
| Base cap | **$40,000** | **$20,000** | 1% compounded |
| Phaseout threshold (MAGI) | **$500,000** | **$250,000** | 1% compounded |
| Phaseout reduction rate | **30%** of MAGI вҲ’ threshold | 30% | вҖ” |
| Statutory floor | **$10,000** | **$5,000** | вҖ” |

**Phaseout formula**: `reduced_cap = max(floor, base_cap вҲ’ 30% Г— max(0, MAGI вҲ’ threshold))`. The floor is a hard guarantee вҖ” a $10M-MAGI taxpayer still gets at least $10K ($5K MFS).

**2030 sunset**: cap snaps back to TCJA $10K ($5K MFS) with no phaseout. No further extensions written into OBBBA.

**Out of scope**: pass-through-entity (PTET) workaround вҖ” state-level elections that re-characterize state income tax as a deductible business expense at the entity level. PTET planning is a state-by-state field; this module just computes the federal itemized cap.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-164`. Twenty-three tests pin: **TCJA 2024 cap $10K** + "TCJA" citation regression; **TCJA 2024 MFS cap $5K**; **OBBBA 2025 single under threshold full $40K cap**; **OBBBA 2025 at $500K boundary no reduction** (regression вҖ” > strict required); **OBBBA 2025 one dollar above threshold 30-cent reduction** (off-by-one); **OBBBA 2025 high MAGI phaseout to $10K floor** ($1M MAGI вҶ’ $150K reduction вҶ’ cap clamped at floor); **OBBBA 2025 statutory floor protects high income** ($10M MAGI still gets $10K); **OBBBA 2025 MFS half-amounts** ($20K cap + $250K threshold + $5K floor); **OBBBA 2026 cap rises 1%** ($40K вҶ’ $40,400); **OBBBA 2027 compounds** ($40K Г— 1.01ВІ = $40,804); **OBBBA 2029 final year** (computed via compound_one_percent regression); **OBBBA 2030 sunsets to TCJA $10K** + "sunset" + В§ 164(b)(6)(B) citation; **OBBBA 2030 MFS sunsets to $5K**; **OBBBA 2031 still TCJA**; **salt paid under cap no blocking**; **salt paid exactly cap no blocking**; **phaseout partial reduction above floor** ($550K MAGI вҶ’ $15K reduction вҶ’ $25K cap); **MFS phaseout at $250K threshold**; **MFJ uses $500K threshold not $250K**; **citations pin authorities** (TCJA + В§ 70413 + OBBBA + $500,000 + sunset); **blocked_by_cap = salt_paid вҲ’ allowed_deduction invariant** (4-year regression in single test); **negative inputs clamped**; **compound growth strictly monotonic across OBBBA years 2025-2029** (year-over-year cap growth invariant).

`traderview-expense::section_162l` is the **IRC В§ 162(l) вҖ” Special Rules for Health Insurance Costs of Self-Employed Individuals module** (iter 508) вҖ” direct trader-critical companion to `section_213` (medical expense itemized deduction below the line subject to 7.5% AGI floor вҖ” disallowed В§ 162(l) excess may be claimed under В§ 213), `section_106` (employer-paid health insurance exclusion for ordinary employees), `section_7702b` (qualified long-term care insurance definition + age-based dollar limits per В§ 213(d)(10)), `section_401c2` (earned income definition for self-employed retirement and В§ 162(l) purposes), `section_1372` (S corp fringe benefit rules treating >2% shareholders as self-employed), `section_4980h` (ACA employer shared responsibility вҖ” distinct framework), `section_36b` (Marketplace premium tax credit вҖ” coordination with В§ 162(l) deduction). В§ 162(l) provides ABOVE-THE-LINE deduction on Schedule 1 (Form 1040) line 17 for medical, dental, and qualified long-term care insurance premiums paid by sole proprietors, partners, LLC members, and S-corp >2% shareholders вҖ” bypasses the В§ 213(a) 7.5% AGI floor. **Trader-critical because** active traders operating as sole proprietors with Trader Tax Status (TTS), partners in trading partnerships, or S-corp trading entities with >2% shareholder owners can deduct full health insurance premiums above the line, reducing both AGI (Form 1040 line 11) and self-employment tax base via В§ 162(l)(4) coordination.

**Six business structures**: SoleProprietorOrSingleMemberLlc (earned income = Sch C net profit minus 1/2 SE tax minus SE retirement), PartnershipPartner (earned income = K-1 box 14 code A minus 1/2 SE tax minus SE retirement), SCorporationShareholderOver2Pct (earned income = W-2 box 1 wages; premiums MUST be in W-2 box 1 per Notice 2008-1 + excluded from FICA under В§ 3121(a)(2)(B)), SCorporationShareholderTwoPctOrLess (treated as ordinary employee вҖ” В§ 106 employer exclusion instead, NOT В§ 162(l)), CCorporationShareholder (not eligible вҖ” corporate-level В§ 162(a)), W2EmployeeOnly (not eligible вҖ” В§ 213 floor or В§ 106 exclusion only).

**Three premium types**: MedicalDentalHealth, QualifiedLongTermCare (subject to В§ 213(d)(10) age-based annual dollar limits via В§ 7702B(b) вҖ” e.g., 2024 limits $470 age вүӨ40 / $880 age 41-50 / $1,760 age 51-60 / $4,710 age 61-70 / $5,880 age 71+), MedicarePartABCD (Parts A, B, C Medicare Advantage, D prescription drug all deductible per Chief Counsel Advice 201228037 dated July 13, 2012).

**В§ 162(l)(2)(B) double-coverage prohibition**: month-by-month test вҖ” deduction disallowed for any calendar month in which taxpayer or spouse is ELIGIBLE to participate in subsidized health plan maintained by employer.

**В§ 162(l)(2)(B) plan-establishment requirement**: plan must be in name of business or owner per Notice 2008-1 + Rev. Proc. 79-46. For sole proprietor or single-member LLC, plan must be in name of business or proprietor. For partner, plan must be in partner's name OR partnership name with premiums treated as guaranteed payment. For S corp >2% shareholder, plan must be established by S corp with premiums included in W-2 box 1 wages.

**В§ 162(l)(1)(B) earned income limitation**: total deduction capped at earned income from the trade or business with respect to which the insurance plan is established; excess NOT carried forward.

**Form 7206** (Self-Employed Health Insurance Deduction) is the dedicated computation form effective for tax years beginning after December 31, 2022 (replaced prior Worksheet in Publication 535).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotEligibleBusinessStructure (C corp / W-2-only / S corp вүӨ2% shareholder), DoubleCoverageDisallowedForMonth, PlanNotEstablishedThroughBusiness (Notice 2008-1 + Rev. Proc. 79-46 + S corp W-2 box 1 inclusion required), DeductionAllowedFullPremium, DeductionLimitedByEarnedIncome (excess lost forever).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-162l`. Twenty-five tests pin: **sole proprietor full deduction allowed**; **C corp shareholder not eligible** (В§ 162(a) + В§ 213 cross-reference); **W-2 employee only not eligible**; **S corp вүӨ2% shareholder treated as ordinary employee** (В§ 1372 + В§ 106); **plan not established through business disallowed** (Notice 2008-1 + Rev. Proc. 79-46); **S corp >2% without W-2 box 1 inclusion disallowed** (Form W-2c amendment + В§ 3121(a)(2)(B)); **S corp >2% with W-2 box 1 inclusion allowed**; **double coverage all 12 months fully disallowed**; **double coverage 3 months pro-rated 75% allowed** (month-by-month test); **earned income limitation caps deduction**; **zero earned income no deduction**; **partner full deduction allowed**; **Medicare premium deductible per CCA 201228037** (Chief Counsel Advice + Medicare Parts A, B, C, D); **long-term care premium subject to В§ 213(d)(10) cap** (В§ 7702B(b) + age-based dollar limits); **Form 7206 referenced in action** + Schedule 1 line 17; **months in year pins 12**; **Form 7206 effective year pins 2023**; **CCA Medicare date pins 2012-07-13**; **citation pins В§ 162(l)(1)-(5) + CCA 201228037 + Notice 2008-1 + Form 7206**; **coordination note references В§ 213 + В§ 106 + В§ 7702B + В§ 1372 + В§ 401(c)(2) + В§ 36B**; **realistic sole prop $50K income $15K premium full deduction**; **realistic partner high premium low income capped** ($30K premium, $20K cap); **extreme premium does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero premium zero deduction**; **double coverage overage saturates at zero months** (saturating_sub defense).

`traderview-expense::section_162m` is the **IRC В§ 162(m) $1M public-company executive compensation deduction limit module** вҖ” direct trader-critical companion to `section_280g` (Golden Parachute change-in-control regime вҖ” iter 444), `section_409a` (NQDC compliance), `section_422` (ISO вҖ” iter 438), `section_423` (ESPP вҖ” iter 440), `section_280e` (different deduction disallowance regime вҖ” controlled-substance trafficking). Trader-critical because traders at venture-backed startups that go public, or who later become C-suite executives at public companies, encounter В§ 162(m) on every compensation package: cash bonus, deferred compensation, equity awards, severance.

В§ 162(m)(1) DENIES employer deduction for "applicable employee remuneration" of "covered employees" of a "publicly held corporation" that **EXCEEDS $1,000,000** for the taxable year. The "ONCE COVERED, ALWAYS COVERED" rule under TCJA 2017 amendments means the deduction limitation follows the executive FOREVER, even after departure and after death (paid to estate).

**В§ 162(m)(2) publicly held corporation**:
1. Securities registered under В§ 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (NYSE/NASDAQ/AMEX)
2. В§ 15(d) reporting requirement
3. TCJA 2017 expansion: foreign private issuers + any corporation required to file SEC reports

**В§ 162(m)(3) covered employee (5 categories)**:
1. CEO at any time during taxable year
2. CFO at any time during taxable year
3. Top 3 other most highly compensated officers
4. ANY individual who was a covered employee for any taxable year beginning AFTER 2016 (**ONCE-COVERED-ALWAYS-COVERED** rule)
5. **ARPA FIVE** (Pub. L. 117-2 В§ 9708, effective taxable years beginning after December 31, 2026) вҖ” next 5 most highly compensated employees; bringing total to 10; includes NON-OFFICERS; retested ANNUALLY (no permanent status)

**В§ 162(m)(4) applicable employee remuneration includes**: cash compensation; equity compensation (RSU vesting, NQSO exercise spread); bonus payments; deferred compensation when paid; severance payments; post-termination payments (under once-covered-always-covered rule).

**TCJA 2017 changes (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13601)**:
1. ELIMINATED performance-based compensation exception
2. ELIMINATED commission exception
3. Introduced ONCE-COVERED-ALWAYS-COVERED rule (post-2016)
4. Expanded publicly held corporation definition
5. **TRANSITION RULE** вҖ” written binding contract IN EFFECT ON NOVEMBER 2, 2017 and NOT MATERIALLY MODIFIED preserves former В§ 162(m)(4)(C) qualified performance-based compensation exception

**ARPA 2021 changes (Pub. L. 117-2 В§ 9708)**:
1. Effective taxable years beginning AFTER DECEMBER 31, 2026
2. Adds NEXT FIVE most highly compensated employees beyond CEO + CFO + top 3
3. ARPA FIVE includes employees who are NOT officers
4. ARPA FIVE retested ANNUALLY (no permanent once-covered status)
5. Brings total covered employees from 5 to 10
6. Treas. Reg. В§ 1.162-33 proposed January 16, 2025 implementation

**В§ 162(m) interaction with В§ 280G** вҖ” both regimes deny employer deduction for executive compensation but DIFFERENT TRIGGERS:
- В§ 162(m) вҖ” $1M cap on ANNUAL remuneration to covered employees of public corporation
- В§ 280G вҖ” CHANGE-IN-CONTROL parachute payment вүҘ 3Г— base amount denies deduction on entire excess over 1Г— base PLUS В§ 4999 20% recipient excise tax

Both apply simultaneously; same compensation can be disallowed under BOTH if it exceeds $1M cap AND is change-in-control parachute.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) NASDAQ CEO total comp $5M cash + $3M RSU vesting = $8M вҖ” $1M deductible, $7M non-deductible to employer; (2) trader departs 2023, receives $2M deferred bonus in 2026 вҖ” STILL covered under once-always rule; (3) post-2027 ARPA FIVE вҖ” sixth-highest-paid employee $1.5M comp triggers В§ 162(m); (4) pre-TCJA grandfather вҖ” binding contract October 15, 2017 with qualified performance-based RSU preserves exception; (5) В§ 162(m) + В§ 280G dual regime вҖ” same compensation disallowed under both.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-162m`. Thirty tests pin: **CEO $8M comp $1M deductible $7M non-deductible**; **CEO under $1M fully deductible**; **CEO at $1M boundary fully deductible**; **not publicly held no cap**; **not covered employee fully deductible**; **CFO subject to cap**; **top three officer subject to cap**; **once-covered-always-covered rule** ($13601 + survives departure + death); **ARPA FIVE pre-2027 not yet effective**; **ARPA FIVE post-2027 effective**; **ARPA expansion disclosed post-2027**; **pre-TCJA grandfather with performance-based exempt**; **pre-TCJA contract without performance-based not exempt**; **dual regime В§ 280G disclosed**; **covered employee type truth table six cells** (2026 + 2027 sweep); **ARPA FIVE uniquely year-gated invariant**; **$1M cap pinned at 100M cents**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 162(m)(1)-(6) + Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 13601 TCJA + Pub. L. 117-2 В§ 9708 ARPA + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.162-27 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.162-33 + IRS Pub. 6014 + Notice 2018-68 + Rev. Proc. 2024-19); **note pins В§ 162(m)(1) deduction denial**; **note pins В§ 162(m)(2) publicly held**; **note pins В§ 162(m)(3) covered employee**; **note pins once-covered-always-covered TCJA**; **note pins В§ 162(m)(4) applicable remuneration**; **note pins TCJA 2017 five changes**; **note pins ARPA 2021 five features**; **note pins В§ 280G interaction**; **note pins trader fact patterns**; **note pins authority guidance**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_163d` is the **IRC В§163(d) investment interest expense limitation** вҖ” the В§163(j) equivalent for non-trader investors (anyone NOT making the В§475(f) trader-in-securities election). Margin interest is deductible only up to **net investment income** under В§163(d)(1); excess carries forward **indefinitely** under В§163(d)(2). Pairs with iter 16's `section_163j` вҖ” together they cover the two В§163 limitation paths a margin-debt taxpayer can hit.

Net investment income per В§163(d)(4) sums: **interest income** (always counted), **ordinary dividends** (always), **net short-term capital gain** (always вҖ” STCG is ordinary regardless), and OPTIONALLY **qualified dividends** + **net long-term capital gain** if the taxpayer makes the **В§1(h)(11)(D)(i)** / **В§163(d)(4)(B)(iii)** election to treat them as investment income. Investment expenses other than interest (В§163(d)(4)(C) вҖ” e.g. portion of management fees) reduce the NII figure.

The QD/LTCG election is the **non-obvious tradeoff**: a taxpayer with unused QDs/LTCG can elect them into NII to unlock the interest deduction NOW, but pays **ordinary-income rates** instead of preferential capital-gain rates on the elected portion. The module surfaces `qualified_dividends_lost_preferential_rate` + `long_term_capital_gain_lost_preferential_rate` so the caller can compare: ordinary rate Г— elected QD vs deferred interest deduction value. Worth it when the elected interest deduction Г— ordinary rate exceeds the QD/LTCG preferential-rate savings.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-163d`. Seventeen tests pin: baseline without elections uses interest + ordinary dividends + STCG only ($3k + $1k + $2k = $6k NII); QD election boosts NII to $11k + $5k forfeits preferential rate; LTCG election adds $8k + $8k forfeits; both elections stack ($19k NII); other investment expenses reduce NII (gross $6k - expenses $2k = $4k); NII cannot go negative when other expenses exceed gross (clamped to zero, full carryforward); prior carryforward stacks with current expense; no expense no-op; expense fully under NII no carryforward; negative STCG treated as zero in NII (won't reduce other income); no-QD-election keeps QD at preferential rate (`qualified_dividends_lost_preferential_rate = 0`); multi-year chain absorbs carryforward when NII rises; note text reflects election tradeoff when applicable; zero income zero NII full carryforward; NII equals gross when no other expenses; carryforward never negative under stress; election with zero amount doesn't artificially increase NII.

`traderview-expense::section_163h` is the **IRC В§163(h) qualified residence (home mortgage) interest deduction module** вҖ” universal to every home-owning trader. The TCJA (P.L. 115-97) capped acquisition indebtedness at **$750k** for mortgages originated after 2017-12-15 (down from $1M pre-TCJA) and was scheduled to sunset end-of-2025. The **One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025** (В§ 70108 of OBBBA) made the TCJA rules **permanent** вҖ” the $750k cap, the home equity indebtedness disallowance, and (separately) reinstated **mortgage insurance premium (PMI) deductibility** as qualified interest for tax years beginning after 2025 ([Thomson Reuters вҖ” What OBBBA Means for Itemized Deductions](https://tax.thomsonreuters.com/news/what-obbb-means-for-your-clients-itemized-deductions/)).

**Acquisition indebtedness caps by origination date and filing status:**

| Mortgage origination          | Single / MFJ / HOH | MFS      |
|-------------------------------|--------------------|----------|
| Pre-2017-12-16 (grandfathered) | $1,000,000        | $500,000 |
| Post-2017-12-15 (TCJA + OBBBA permanent) | $750,000 | $375,000 |

**Binding-contract grandfathering carve-out** (В§ 163(h)(3)(F)(i)): a mortgage originated under a binding contract entered before 2017-12-15 AND closed before 2018-04-01 also retains the $1M cap.

**Refinance pro-ration** (В§ 163(h)(3)(F)(iii)): refinances of grandfathered debt retain the $1M cap on the pre-refinance balance; any cash-out / new money on top falls under the $750k cap. Module computes effective blended cap as `min(pre_refi_balance, $1M_cap) + min(new_money, $750k_cap)`.

**Home equity indebtedness permanently disallowed** (В§ 163(h)(3)(C), as amended by TCJA + made permanent by OBBBA): interest on home-equity-secured debt NOT used to buy, build, or substantially improve the securing home is NEVER DEDUCTIBLE. The pre-TCJA separate $100k home-equity bucket is permanently gone. If home-equity-secured debt IS used for acquisition / substantial improvement, it folds into the acquisition indebtedness cap.

**PMI deductibility reinstated 2026+** (В§ 163(h)(4)(E) reinstated by OBBBA): mortgage insurance premiums on acquisition debt are qualified mortgage interest for tax years beginning after 2025. The module gates `deductible_pmi_premiums` on `tax_year >= 2026`; pre-2026 years see zero PMI deduction regardless of premium paid ([Schmerling Financial Group on OBBBA PMI restoration](https://www.sfg-planner.com/blog/obbba-caps-mortgage-interest-and-adds-mortgage-insurance)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-163h`. Twenty-one tests pin: **standard post-TCJA under cap full deduction** ($500k balance вҶ’ full $25k); **over-cap pro-rata** ($1.5M balance вҶ’ $30k of $60k deductible); **grandfathered pre-2017 full $1M cap** ($900k balance вҶ’ full deduction); grandfathered over $1M partial; **MFS half-cap post-TCJA** ($375k); MFS grandfathered half-cap ($500k); **home equity interest never deductible post-TCJA** ($10k HE interest entirely disallowed); **PMI deductible starting 2026 only** (2025 вҶ’ $0; 2026 вҶ’ full); PMI added to total in 2026; **refinance pro-rata** ($900k grandfathered + $600k new money вҶ’ $1.5M effective cap); refinance new money caps at TCJA limit ($500k grand + $1.5M new вҶ’ effective $1.25M not $2M); zero acquisition balance no deduction; **at exact $750k cap full deduction**; **$1-over-cap proportional haircut** (precision target); combined acquisition + home equity calculation; note describes OBBBA permanence; note 2025 calls out PMI only 2026+; note grandfathered path described; note refinance blended path described; large balance precision path ($5M / $750k вҶ’ 15% deductible); MFS over-cap pro-rata ($30k Г— $375k / $750k = $15k).

`traderview-expense::section_163j` is the **IRC В§163(j) business interest limitation** вҖ” caps deductible margin interest for traders who've elected В§475(f) trader-in-securities status. Active traders pay 5-figure margin interest annually; without В§163(j) modeling, they over-deduct and face audit/penalty exposure. The deduction limit is `business_interest_income + floor_plan_financing_interest + 0.30 Г— adjusted_taxable_income`, with anything above carrying forward **indefinitely** under В§163(j)(2).

Adjusted Taxable Income (В§163(j)(8)) is taxable income computed WITHOUT regard to business interest expense or income, NOL deduction, or В§199A QBI. For tax years before 2022, depreciation/amortization/depletion were also added back; for 2022+, they're NOT, making the cap meaningfully tighter post-TCJA-transition.

**В§163(j)(3) small-business exception**: the cap doesn't apply when the taxpayer's average annual gross receipts for the prior 3 years are at or below the В§448(c) threshold. The threshold is annually indexed; the module embeds the published table:

| Year | В§448(c) threshold |
|------|-------------------|
| 2020 | $26M              |
| 2021 | $26M              |
| 2022 | $27M              |
| 2023 | $29M              |
| 2024 | $30M              |
| 2025 | $31M              |

For 2026+, caller passes `small_business_threshold_override` with the current IRS-published figure. Note: active traders almost always blow past this вҖ” gross receipts = gross sale proceeds вҖ” so the exception rarely helps. Day-1 traders may briefly qualify.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-163j`. Fifteen tests pin: standard 30% cap partial deduction ($50k expense, $100k ATI вҶ’ $30k deducted, $20k carries); expense below cap fully deducted; business interest income raises cap dollar-for-dollar; prior carryforward stacks; small-business under threshold fully exempt; at threshold exactly still exempt (вүӨ not <); $1 over loses exemption; **threshold table** 2020-2025 each year exact; caller override beats embedded table; negative ATI caps 30% at zero (only BI income + floor plan in cap); no-expense no-op; floor plan financing adds to cap; multi-year chain absorbs carryforward when ATI rises; full-deduction note vs carries-forward note.

`traderview-expense::section_165d` is the **IRC В§165(d) wagering loss deduction module with OBBBA 2025 90% limitation** вҖ” relevant when derivative positions or prediction-market activity is characterized as wagering rather than capital-asset trading. The **One Big Beautiful Bill Act (P.L. 119-21, signed 2025-07-04)** amended В§165(d) for tax years beginning after 2025-12-31 to limit deductible losses to **90% of losses incurred** while preserving the historical winnings cap. Both amateur and professional gamblers affected.

**Two regimes by year**:

| Tax year | Loss deduction formula | Effect |
|----------|------------------------|--------|
| Pre-2026 | min(losses, winnings) | 100% of losses up to winnings; break-even = $0 tax |
| 2026+ (post-OBBBA) | min(0.90 Г— losses, winnings) | **Phantom-income problem**: break-even at $3k/$3k creates $300 taxable income |

**Phantom-income example** (post-OBBBA 2026):
- Winnings $3,000; losses $3,000 вҶ’ economic break-even.
- 90% Г— $3,000 = $2,700 adjusted losses; deduction $2,700 (under winnings cap).
- Net taxable wagering income = $3,000 вҲ’ $2,700 = **$300 phantom income** despite zero economic profit.

**В§162 trade-or-business expense carve-out preserved**: OBBBA did NOT eliminate the professional-gambler expense deduction under В§162. Travel, entry fees, supplies, and other business expenses remain fully deductible separately from the В§165(d) loss cap. This is the key distinguishing input from amateur gamblers.

**Itemized deduction scope**: В§165(d) is a Schedule A itemized deduction (Schedule C for professional). With TCJA's expanded standard deduction made permanent by OBBBA, only ~14% of taxpayers itemize in 2026 вҖ” so the В§165(d) deduction benefit is concentrated among high-income gamblers.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-165d`. Twenty-one tests pin: **pre-OBBBA 2025 100% loss-up-to-winnings** ($3k win + $4k loss вҶ’ $3k deduction; net $0); pre-OBBBA losses below winnings fully deductible ($10k win + $4k loss вҶ’ $4k deduction); **post-OBBBA 2026 90% limitation** ($3k win + $4k loss вҶ’ 90% Г— $4k = $3,600 adjusted, capped at $3k; net $0); **post-OBBBA break-even creates phantom income** ($3k/$3k вҶ’ 90% Г— $3k = $2,700 deductible + $300 phantom income); **post-OBBBA losses below winnings still 90% limited** ($100k win + $10k loss вҶ’ $9k deduction, not $10k); year 2025 pre-OBBBA / 2026 post-OBBBA / future 2030 still post-OBBBA boundary pins; **professional gambler В§162 expenses deductible separately** ($100k win + $50k loss + $20k В§162 вҶ’ $45k В§165(d) + $20k В§162 = $65k total, $35k net); **amateur receives no В§162 even if expense input provided** (regression target вҖ” module forces zero for amateurs); **professional with В§162 can drive net taxable below zero** ($10k win + $5k loss + $8k В§162 вҶ’ вҲ’$2,500 net); zero winnings/losses no-op; negative inputs clamped; **$1B precision case** ($1B win + $800M loss вҶ’ $720M deduction); **5 citation regression targets** (В§165(d) + "One Big Beautiful Bill" + P.L. 119-21 + 2025-07-04 + "90% of losses" + "phantom-income" + В§162 carve-out); pre-OBBBA note says "pre-OBBBA 100% loss-up-to-winnings"; post-OBBBA note says "post-OBBBA 2025 90% loss limitation"; note for professional describes path; note for amateur says "no В§162".

`traderview-expense::section_165h` is the **IRC В§165(h) personal casualty loss deduction module** вҖ” practical for landlords and traders whose primary residence or personal property is destroyed by hurricane, fire, flood, or theft. Sits alongside `section_165d` (gambling losses) and `section_165g` (worthless securities) in the В§165 family. Three time-windowed regimes capture the historical evolution.

**Three time-windowed regimes**:

| Period | Eligibility | Citation |
|--------|-------------|----------|
| **Pre-TCJA (вүӨ 2017)** | Any sudden, unexpected, identifiable event qualifies (auto accidents, household fires, theft) | В§ 165(h)(1)/(2) |
| **TCJA window (2018-2025)** | ONLY federally declared disasters (FEMA) qualify; non-disaster + state-declared losses SUSPENDED | В§ 165(h)(5) added by TCJA В§ 11044 |
| **OBBBA permanent (2026+)** | Federally OR **state-declared** disasters qualify; non-disaster losses **PERMANENTLY** suspended | В§ 165(h)(5) as amended by OBBBA В§ 70423 (eff. tax years after 2025-12-31) |

**Calculation order**:
1. **Raw loss** = lesser of (adjusted basis, FMV decline) вҲ’ insurance reimbursement
2. **В§ 165(h)(1) per-event floor**: $100 standard; **$500** for congressionally designated "qualified disaster losses"
3. **В§ 165(h)(2) 10% AGI floor**: aggregate net casualty losses deductible only to extent above 10% Г— AGI. **Qualified disaster losses are EXEMPT from this floor**.

**Qualified disaster losses** (Hurricane Katrina, Hurricane Ian-class acts) get two carve-outs: $500 per-event floor instead of $100, AND no 10% AGI floor. Congress designates per-disaster.

**The OBBBA expansion is the regression-critical pin**: identical state-declared disaster in 2025 = NOT eligible; in 2026 = eligible.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-165h`. Twenty-five tests pin: **pre-TCJA non-disaster qualifies** (2017 auto accident вҶ’ $9,900 allowed); **TCJA 2024 federally declared qualifies** with $100 + 10% AGI floors вҶ’ $14,900 worked example; **TCJA 2024 state-declared NOT eligible** (regression вҖ” TCJA window is federally-declared-only) + "suspends 2018-2025" note; **TCJA 2024 non-disaster NOT eligible**; **OBBBA 2026 state-declared NOW qualifies** + В§ 70423 + "state-declared" citation regression; **OBBBA 2026 federally declared still qualifies**; **OBBBA 2026 non-disaster PERMANENTLY suspended** + "PERMANENT" citation + note; **raw loss uses lesser of basis or FMV decline** (regression вҖ” both directions); **insurance reduces raw loss**; **insurance exceeding loss zero deduction**; **qualified disaster $500 floor not $100**; **qualified disaster EXEMPT from 10% AGI floor**; **non-qualified uses $100 + AGI floors**; **AGI floor can zero allowed deduction** ($15K raw вҲ’ $100 = $14,900 вҲ’ 10% Г— $200K = $20K вҶ’ $0); **small loss per-event floor zeros loss** ($50 < $100 вҶ’ 0); **TCJA window boundary 2018 + 2025 both federally declared eligible**; **2025 state-declared NOT eligible** (regression вҖ” OBBBA expansion not effective yet); **OBBBA threshold 2026 state-declared crossover** (2025 = ineligible / 2026 = eligible); **pre-TCJA 2017 state-declared qualifies**; **citation pins OBBBA В§ 70423 for 2026+**; **citation pins TCJA В§ 11044 for TCJA window**; **negative inputs clamped**; **hurricane $50K loss $100K AGI worked example** ($80K basis, $50K FMV decline, $10K insurance вҶ’ $29,900 allowed); **qualified-disaster no AGI floor worked example** (same hurricane вҶ’ $39,500 allowed via $500 floor + AGI exemption); **2025-vs-2026 state-declared eligibility invariant**; **OBBBA 2030 still permanent suspension for non-disaster**.

`traderview-expense::section_165g` is the **IRC В§165(g) worthless securities deduction module** вҖ” the rule every trader needs when a portfolio holding goes to zero mid-year. Stock or bond held as a capital asset that becomes **wholly worthless** during the taxable year is deemed sold on the **last day of the taxable year** under В§165(g)(1), generating a capital loss whose ST/LT character depends on the deemed holding period through Dec 31. Three escape hatches into ordinary-loss treatment can bypass the В§1211 capital-loss net-on-net limitation.

**В§165(g)(2) definition of "security"** вҖ” covers all four trader-relevant instruments:
- Share of stock in a corporation
- Right to subscribe for / receive a share of stock
- Bond, debenture, note, certificate, or other evidence of indebtedness issued by a corporation or government with interest coupons or in registered form

**Wholly-worthless requirement is sharp**: В§165(g) is unforgiving on partial decline. A holding that has lost 99% of its value but retains some residual economic value does NOT qualify вҖ” the taxpayer must wait for total worthlessness or dispose under В§1001 to recognize the loss.

**В§165(g)(3) affiliated-domestic-corporation ordinary-loss exception** вҖ” three gates ALL required:
- Corporation is **domestic** (foreign subsidiaries fall back to capital loss)
- Taxpayer directly owns вүҘ 80% of voting power AND вүҘ 80% of value (В§1504(a)(2) affiliation test)
- **More than 90%** of corporation's aggregate gross receipts (across all tax years) from non-passive sources вҖ” operating income, not royalties / rents / dividends / interest / annuities / securities-sale gains

**Priority order when multiple ordinary-loss paths apply**: `В§1244 small business stock` вҶ’ `В§165(g)(3) affiliated` вҶ’ `В§165(g)(1) capital`. В§1244 wins because its annual cap ($50k single / $100k MFJ вҖ” see `section_1244` module) is more specific and В§1244 was enacted to provide ordinary treatment even when affiliation gates fail.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-165g`. Twenty-four tests pin: **baseline 400-day LT capital loss** ($100k basis fully recoverable; deemed sale 2026-12-31); **300-day ST capital loss path**; **365-day boundary still ST** (regression вҖ” В§1222 requires holding period of MORE THAN one year); **366-day boundary LT**; **deemed sale date = Dec 31 of tax year** (tracks tax_year input); **partial worthlessness no deduction** + diagnostic note explains deferral; **В§165(g)(3) all 3 gates** (domestic + 80% affiliation + > 90% non-passive вҶ’ ordinary); foreign subsidiary falls back to capital; under-80% ownership fails В§165(g)(3); **exactly 90% non-passive fails strict-greater test** (boundary regression вҖ” В§ statute uses "more than" 90%); **90.01% qualifies**; **В§1244 takes precedence over В§165(g)(1) capital** (loss to OrdinaryUnderSection1244); **В§1244 takes precedence over В§165(g)(3) affiliated** (priority ordering regression target); loss equals adjusted basis; $50M precision; negative basis clamps to zero; **citation mentions all 6 relevant authorities** (В§165(g)(1) + В§165(g)(2) + В§165(g)(3) + В§1504(a)(2) + В§1244 + 26 CFR В§ 1.165-5); partial-worthlessness citation mentions В§1001 deferral; note describes LT capital path; note for В§165(g)(3) says "APPLIES"; note for В§1244 path mentions the `section_1244` module redirect; note includes "365 required for LT" threshold explanation.

`traderview-expense::section_1202` is the **IRC В§1202 Qualified Small Business Stock (QSBS) gain-exclusion module** вҖ” the most-missed tax break for founders, employees with exit stock, and active traders buying primary-issuance small-company stock. Up to **the GREATER of $10,000,000 OR 10Г— the taxpayer's adjusted basis** of gain on QSBS is excluded from federal income tax. Paired with `section_1244`: В§1244 handles the LOSS side (ordinary-loss treatment up to $50k/$100k), В§1202 handles the GAIN side (exclusion up to $10M / 10Г— basis). This is the mechanism behind the "Peter Thiel $5B Roth" вҖ” qualified stock acquired cheaply, held > 5 years, sold for 9-figures, federal tax = $0.

The exclusion percentage is a three-band step function on acquisition date:
- Acquired **before Feb 18, 2009** вҶ’ 50% exclusion + 7% AMT preference on the excluded portion (В§57(a)(7)).
- Acquired **Feb 18, 2009 вҖ“ Sep 27, 2010** вҶ’ 75% exclusion + 7% AMT preference.
- Acquired **after Sep 27, 2010** вҶ’ 100% exclusion, no AMT preference.

The В§1202(c) + В§1202(e) qualification checklist surfaces as 8 booleans on `Qsbs1202Qualification`: domestic C-corporation; aggregate gross assets вүӨ $50M at issuance; original issuance to taxpayer (no secondary market); non-corporate taxpayer (C-corps cannot use В§1202); holding period > 5 years; вүҘ 80% of corp assets in active qualified trade or business; not an В§1202(e)(3) excluded business (health / law / engineering / accounting / consulting / financial services / brokerage / banking / insurance / farming / hotels / restaurants / mineral extraction / any business where principal asset is the reputation of one or more employees); not В§1202(f) preferred-stock-as-debt disqualified. Per-issuer cap is per-taxpayer lifetime; the `prior_exclusion_used_this_issuer` input shrinks the remaining cap so multi-tranche sales of the same issuer stack correctly.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1202`. Eighteen tests pin: post-2010 full 100% exclusion zero AMT, pre-2009 50% band with 7% AMT, mid-band 75% with 7% AMT, all three band boundaries (Feb 17 vs Feb 18 2009 and Sep 27 vs Sep 28 2010), cap uses MAX(10M, 10Г— basis), over-cap portion taxable LTCG, prior exclusion reduces cap remaining, each disqualification path routes full gain to LTCG, excluded business listed in note, no-gain no-op, loss returns no-op (not negative exclusion), corporate taxpayer disqualified, multi-disqualification lists all failures, qualification helper returns true only when all 8 pass, full exclusion at cap with 100% band zero AMT. В§1045 60-day rollover is out of scope вҖ” caller handles by reducing `realized_gain` upstream.

`traderview-expense::section_1244` is the **IRC В§1244 small-business-stock-loss module** вҖ” bypasses the В§1212(b) $3,000 capital-loss cap for losses on qualifying small-business stock. The first **$50,000 single / $100,000 MFJ** of such loss in any tax year is treated as **ordinary loss** (Schedule 1 Line 8z, not Schedule D), absorbing dollar-for-dollar against ordinary income with no cap. Anything above the threshold overflows back to capital loss treatment on Schedule D.

The В§1244(c) qualification checklist surfaces explicitly as 5 booleans on the `Qualification` struct so the caller (and downstream UI) can show exactly which tests pass: stock from a **domestic** corporation; aggregate paid-in capital + paid-in surplus **вүӨ $1M at issuance**; for the 5 years before the loss **< 50% of gross receipts from passive sources** (royalties / rents / dividends / interest / annuities / sales of stock or securities); stock **issued for money or other property** (not services); taxpayer is the **original holder** (no inherited, gifted, or secondary-market stock). `Qualification::qualifies()` returns true only when all five pass; the result note enumerates the failing tests when one or more fail. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1244`. Fourteen tests pin: single under-cap, single over-cap with capital overflow, MFJ $100k cap, MFS uses $50k (not 50% of MFJ), prior-claimed reduces remaining, each disqualification routes the full loss to capital, multi-disqualification listed in note, no-loss no-op, cap-stays-non-negative under stress, exact-cap edge, qualification helper returns true only when all five pass.

`traderview-expense::section_1245_1250` is the **IRC В§1245 / В§1250 depreciation recapture module** вҖ” foundational for any real estate trader, rental landlord, or business owner who sells previously depreciated property. The "recapture" rules recharacterize gain attributable to prior depreciation as either ordinary income (В§1245 personal property; В§1250 pre-1986 / accelerated real property) or as "unrecaptured В§1250 gain" subject to a 25% maximum capital rate under В§1(h)(7) (post-1986 MACRS real property). The residual gain after recapture flows to existing `section_1231` for net-gain-vs-loss characterization вҖ” these two modules form a recapture-then-characterize pipeline.

**Three property-type paths**:

| Path | Property | Ordinary recapture | В§1(h)(7) 25% rate path | Residual to В§1231 |
|------|----------|---------------------|--------------------------|--------------------|
| `Section1245Personal` | Equipment, vehicles, fixtures, В§197 intangibles | **min(gain, accumulated depreciation)** | None | Gain вҲ’ ordinary |
| `Section1250RealPostMacrsStraightLine` | Post-1986 real property (27.5y residential / 39y nonresidential) | **Zero** | min(gain, accumulated depreciation) | Gain вҲ’ unrecaptured В§1250 |
| `Section1250RealPreMacrsOrAccelerated` | Pre-1986 / accelerated real property | min(gain, additional depreciation) | min(gain вҲ’ В§1250 ordinary, straight-line dep) | Balance |

**Why post-1986 real estate rarely sees В§1250 ordinary recapture**: post-1986 MACRS requires straight-line depreciation for real property (27.5 / 39 years). Since "additional depreciation" = depreciation in excess of straight-line, there's none. В§1250 ordinary recapture is effectively ZERO for modern real estate. The 25% unrecaptured В§1250 gain rate under В§1(h)(7) is the real cost.

**В§1(h)(7) 25% max rate context**: for individual taxpayers in the top 20% LTCG bracket, the 25% unrecaptured В§1250 gain rate is a 5-percentage-point premium over standard LTCG. On a $1M post-MACRS commercial sale with $400k of prior depreciation, the unrecaptured В§1250 gain produces $400k Г— 25% = $100,000 in federal tax вҖ” $20,000 more than if it had been pure LTCG.

**Recapture-then-В§1231 pipeline**: this module produces the residual В§1231 gain, which feeds into `section_1231` for the 5-year lookback recapture analysis and net-gain-vs-loss characterization. The two modules together model the complete real-estate-disposition flow.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1245-1250`. Twenty tests pin: **В§1245 gain exceeds accum dep** ($100k gain, $60k accum dep вҶ’ $60k ordinary + $40k В§1231); **В§1245 gain below accum dep** вҶ’ all $40k gain recaptured ordinary, zero В§1231; **В§1245 exact accum dep match** вҶ’ no residual; **В§1245 no unrecaptured В§1250 gain** (regression вҖ” path mutually exclusive); **В§1250 post-MACRS no ordinary recapture** (regression вҖ” distinguishes from В§1245); **В§1250 post-MACRS gain below accum dep вҶ’ all unrecaptured В§1250**; **В§1250 post-MACRS residual to В§1231** ($200k вҲ’ $80k = $120k); **В§1250 pre-MACRS ordinary recapture = additional dep** ($30k); **В§1250 pre-MACRS unrecaptured = straight-line dep** ($100k вҲ’ $30k = $70k); В§1250 pre-MACRS residual = $100k balance; В§1250 pre-MACRS gain below additional dep caps at gain; **В§1245 path produces no В§1250 amounts**; **В§1250 path produces no В§1245 amount**; zero gain вҶ’ zero recapture; negative gain clamped; zero accumulated depreciation вҶ’ full gain to В§1231 (regression target); **citation mentions all 6 relevant authorities** (В§1245(a)(1) + В§1250 + В§1(h)(7) + 25% + Form 4797 + В§1231); note describes property type path; **note mentions 25% max rate under В§1(h)(7)**; **$1B precision** ($400M ordinary + $600M В§1231).

`traderview-expense::section_121` is the **IRC В§121 principal-residence sale exclusion** вҖ” companion to `disposition.rs` (iter 7 covers rental sale; В§121 covers personal-residence sale). Up to **$250k single / $500k MFJ** of gain on the sale of a principal residence is excluded from gross income when the 2-of-5-year **ownership test** and **use test** both pass, AND the taxpayer hasn't used В§121 on another sale within the prior 2 years. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-121`.

Three carve-outs / haircuts the module handles end-to-end:

- **В§121(b)(4) reduced maximum exclusion** вҖ” if the 2-year tests failed due to (a) change in employment, (b) health, or (c) unforeseen circumstances, the cap is pro-rated by `qualifying_months / 24` where qualifying months = `min(months_owned, months_used).min(24)`. A 12-month qualifier excludes $125k single / $250k MFJ. An 18-month job-move excludes $187,500.
- **В§121(b)(5) non-qualified use** вҖ” applies to post-2008 dispositions. Any period AFTER 2008 during which the property was NOT the principal residence (e.g. rental years before conversion to primary residence) reduces eligible gain proportionally: `eligible = realized Г— (qualified_days / total_ownership_days_post_2008)`. The NQU portion is taxable LTCG even though the rest of the gain is excluded.
- **В§121(d)(6) depreciation recapture** вҖ” any depreciation deducted after May 6, 1997 (home office, prior rental) is NOT excludable. It's recaptured as **В§1250 unrecaptured gain** at the 25% rate. The module surfaces it as a separate `unrecaptured_section_1250` field, with `taxable_long_term_gain` for the LTCG bucket and `total_taxable_gain` for the sum across rate brackets.

Fifteen tests pin: single full qualifier under cap вҶ’ full exclusion; MFJ $500k cap; over-cap portion taxable LTCG; failed 2-year tests with no В§121(b)(4) reason вҶ’ fully disqualified with reason list; health-reason pro-rates cap to $125k at 12 months; job-move pro-rates to $187,500 at 18 months; В§121(b)(4) uses lesser of owned/used months; once-every-2-years blocks; В§121(b)(5) NQU proportional reduction with exact day-count math; В§121(d)(6) recapture before exclusion (LTCG=0 if cap absorbs, recapture still taxable); loss on sale not excludable but recognized; MFS uses $250k (not half of MFJ); combined recapture + NQU + over-cap all stack into `total_taxable_gain`; zero post-2008 ownership skips NQU; note text full-exclusion path.

`traderview-expense::section_132` is the **IRC В§132 fringe benefits exclusion module** вҖ” trader-relevant for W-2 employees of trading firms who receive employer-provided fringe benefits. В§132(a) enumerates 8 categories of fringes excluded from gross income. OBBBA 2025 (P.L. 119-21) made the TCJA-era В§132(g) moving-expense-reimbursement suspension PERMANENT (with newly added intelligence-community exception alongside the existing armed-forces exception).

**8 excludable fringe categories**:

| Category | Sub-section | Limit |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| No-additional-cost service | В§132(a)(1) | Same line of business, no substantial cost |
| Qualified employee discount | В§132(a)(2) | Services: **20%** / Goods: gross-profit % |
| Working condition fringe | В§132(a)(3) | Would be В§162/В§167 deductible if employee paid |
| De minimis fringe | В§132(a)(4) | So small accounting unreasonable |
| Qualified transportation fringe | В§132(a)(5) / В§132(f) | **2026: $340/month** each for parking and transit (separately) |
| Qualified moving expense reimbursement | В§132(a)(6) | **PERMANENTLY SUSPENDED by OBBBA 2025** except armed forces PCS and intelligence community |
| Qualified retirement planning services | В§132(a)(7) | Investment advice to plan participants |
| Qualified military base realignment/closure fringe | В§132(a)(8) | Narrow |

**В§132(f) qualified transportation fringe monthly caps** (Rev. Proc. inflation-adjusted, caller-supplied year-agnostic):

| Year | Parking | Transit / vanpool |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| 2024 | $315 | $315 |
| 2025 | $325 | $325 |
| 2026 | **$340** | **$340** |

The two categories are SEPARATELY capped (not aggregated). A commuter using BOTH parking AND transit can exclude $340 + $340 = **$680/month** in 2026 вҖ” a key planning point for high-cost-of-living trader-employees in NYC, SF, Boston, DC.

**В§132(g) OBBBA permanent suspension**: TCJA temporarily suspended В§132(g) qualified moving expense reimbursement (and the В§217 deduction) through 2025. OBBBA 2025 made the suspension PERMANENT for all civilian taxpayers. EXCEPTIONS that survive:
- U.S. Armed Forces active-duty members moving on permanent change of station (PCS) orders
- **U.S. intelligence community members** (newly added by OBBBA вҖ” expanding the exception)

**В§132(c) discount cap arithmetic**: when the discount percentage exceeds the cap, the excludable amount is calculated as `value Г— (cap_pct / discount_pct)`. Services discount at 40% with $100 value вҶ’ excludable = $100 Г— (20% / 40%) = $50; taxable $50.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-132`. Twenty-two tests pin: **no-additional-cost / working-condition / de-minimis / retirement-planning fully excludable** (capless categorical paths); **services discount at exactly 20% fully excludable**; **services discount at 40% partial taxable** ($100 Г— 20%/40% = $50 excludable + $50 taxable); **goods discount at gross-profit % fully excludable**; **goods discount above gross-profit % partial taxable** ($100 Г— 25%/50% = $50 excludable); **parking at 2026 cap $340 fully excludable**; **parking above $340 cap partial taxable** ($500 вҶ’ $340 excludable + $160 taxable); transit at 2026 cap $340 fully excludable; **both parking and transit combined cap $680** (regression вҖ” separately capped, not aggregated); **year 2025 parking cap $325** (inflation pin); **В§132(a)(6) moving expense civilian PERMANENTLY suspended** ($5k taxable, regression); **В§132(a)(6) armed-forces/intelligence-community fully excludable** ($5k excludable, regression target вҖ” OBBBA exception); negative fringe value clamped to zero; **8 citation regression targets** (all 8 В§132(a) categories mentioned individually); **citation mentions 2024 $315 + 2025 $325 + 2026 $340** inflation amounts; **citation mentions OBBBA PERMANENTLY SUSPENDED + armed forces + intelligence community**; note moving-suspended describes "PERMANENTLY suspended by OBBBA 2025"; note transportation describes cap applied; $1M de minimis precision.

`traderview-expense::section_121d` is the **dedicated IRC В§121(d)(2) + В§121(d)(3) divorce special rules module** вҖ” companion to `section_121` for divorce-specific scenarios. When a married couple divorces, two В§121(d) provisions let the ex-spouse who **moved out** still claim the В§121 principal-residence exclusion on a later sale, even after years of non-occupation, by tacking the other spouse's ownership/use periods and attributing the home-occupying ex-spouse's use ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 121](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/121), [LegalClarity вҖ” В§ 1041 + В§ 121 Property Transfers](https://legalclarity.org/the-tax-rules-for-property-transfers-under-irc-section-1041/)).

**В§121(d)(2) holding-period tacking** вҖ” when a residence is transferred under В§ 1041(a) (a tax-free spouse-to-spouse transfer incident to divorce), the transferee's OWNERSHIP period for В§ 121 purposes includes the transferor's prior ownership period. The receiving ex-spouse immediately counts every month the OTHER ex-spouse previously owned the home, letting them meet the 2-year ownership test without waiting out a new clock.

**В§121(d)(3)(A) use attribution via former-spouse occupation** вҖ” solely for В§ 121 purposes, an individual is treated as USING the property as their principal residence during any period of ownership while the individual's spouse or FORMER SPOUSE is granted use of the property under a **divorce or separation instrument**. So the ex-spouse who moved out continues to accumulate use-period months as long as the OTHER ex-spouse occupies the home under the divorce decree. A "divorce or separation instrument" includes a decree of divorce, decree of separate maintenance, or written instrument incident to such a decree.

**Practical effect вҖ” the load-bearing scenario**: ex-spouse A owns the home for 24 months pre-divorce, transfers it to ex-spouse B in В§ 1041(a) transfer, then B holds it for 12 months and the home is sold. Without В§ 121(d)(2), B has only 12 months ownership and fails the 2-year test. With tacking, B has **24 + 12 = 36 months** and the test is met. Similarly, if A retains ownership but B occupies the home for 18 months under the divorce decree, A's use period for В§ 121 includes those 18 months (plus A's own use months) even though A was physically elsewhere. Pinned by `tacking_lets_transferee_meet_2_year_ownership` + `use_attribution_through_former_spouse_occupation` + `tacking_plus_attribution_both_satisfy_exclusion_available` (combined scenario both rules apply and exclusion available).

**Exclusion limits unchanged** вҖ” В§ 121(b) $250k single / $500k MFJ. Module pins both: `single_filer_250k_limit` ($300k gain вҶ’ $250k excluded + $50k recognized) + `joint_filer_500k_limit` ($600k gain вҶ’ $500k excluded + $100k recognized).

**Mechanics**:
- `section_121d2_tacking_applies` flag set when transfer was under В§ 1041(a)
- `section_121d3_use_attribution_applies` flag set when former spouse uses property under divorce instrument
- Both can apply independently OR together
- `total_ownership_months` = transferor_prior + transferee_post (when tacking) or transferee_post only
- `total_use_months` = transferor_prior + transferee_post + former_spouse_occupation (when tacking + attribution apply)

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-121d`. Nineteen tests pin: **baseline 3-year ownership and use qualifies** (single $200k gain вҶ’ $200k excluded); **В§ 121(d)(2) tacking lets transferee meet 2-year ownership** (24 prior + 12 post = 36 months); no tacking without В§ 1041 transfer (100 prior months ignored); **В§ 121(d)(3)(A) use attribution through former-spouse occupation** (12 own + 18 attribution = 30 months); use attribution does NOT apply without divorce instrument (24 months ignored); **18-month attribution alone not enough** (need transferee's own use too); **tacking plus attribution chains correctly** ($121(d)(2) ownership + $121(d)(3) use both checked separately); **tacking + attribution both satisfy вҶ’ exclusion available**; **single filer $250k limit** + **joint filer $500k limit**; gain below limit fully excluded; **23-month ownership does not meet test** / **24-month exact meets test** (boundary); no exclusion full gain recognized; note describes tacking when applies; note describes use attribution when applies; note describes exclusion available path; note describes NOT AVAILABLE path; citation mentions В§ 121(a) / (b) / (d)(2) / (d)(3)(A) / Treas. Reg. В§ 1.121-1.

`traderview-expense::section_174` is the **IRC В§174 R&D capitalization module** вҖ” the post-TCJA amendment that hit every algorithmic trader hard starting in tax year 2022. Before TCJA, R&D expenditures could be either expensed currently OR capitalized + amortized over 5+ years at taxpayer's election under В§174(a)(1). After TCJA, current expensing is **GONE**: all R&E expenditures MUST be capitalized and amortized over **5 years (domestic)** under В§174(a)(2)(A) or **15 years (foreign)** under В§174(a)(2)(B), with a **half-year convention** that spreads recovery across 6 calendar years (or 16 for foreign).

The cash impact: an algorithmic trader who spends **$100,000 on internal trading-software development in 2024** gets only **$10,000 deductible in 2024** (vs the full $100k pre-TCJA). The remaining $90,000 sits on the balance sheet as capitalized basis, deducted over the next 5 calendar years: $20k each in 2025-2028 plus a $10k stub in 2029. Software development is explicitly within В§174 per Rev. Proc. 2000-50 + TCJA committee report вҖ” so any trader who writes algorithms in-house bears this cost.

Schedule structure per В§174(a)(2) half-year convention:
- Year 1 (year of expenditure): half a year's amortization = `(amount / life) Г— 0.5`
- Years 2 through `life`: full year's amortization = `amount / life`
- Year `life + 1` (stub): the remaining half = `(amount / life) Г— 0.5`

For domestic 5-year: 6 calendar years touched. For foreign 15-year: 16 calendar years touched. The schedule sum invariant holds (sum of all entries = total R&D amount).

В§174 covers research or experimental expenditures including software development. Excluded (still В§162 ordinary expense): routine business operations, market research, advertising, sales promotion, ordinary testing of prototypes. The classification is fact-intensive; caller asserts via the input. **Pre-2022 expenditures** still get the pre-TCJA expensing option вҖ” the module flags `pre_tcja_expensing_available: true` on year-of-expenditure < 2022 so the user knows they had a choice.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-174`. Sixteen tests pin: domestic 5-year year 1 half-year convention ($100k вҶ’ $10k year 1); domestic year 2 full year ($20k); domestic year 6 stub half ($10k, total cumulative $100k, fully amortized); domestic year 7 post-recovery zero; schedule has 6 entries for 5-year recovery (years 2024-2029); schedule amounts correctly distributed (10/20/20/20/20/10 sum to 100); foreign 15-year year 1 half-year convention ($3,333.34); foreign schedule has 16 entries; pre-2022 flags expensing available; post-2022 no expensing option; zero amount no-op; before expenditure year zero deduction; cumulative grows predictably (10/30/50/70/90/100); RDLocation helper returns 5 / 15; **algorithmic trader software dev $100k year 1 only $10k deductible** (the load-bearing TCJA-hit scenario); 5-year recovery sum ties to full amount under arbitrary $250k input.

`traderview-expense::section_179` is the **IRC В§179 election to expense certain depreciable business assets module** вҖ” the most powerful first-year deduction tool in the Code for small/mid-cap traders and landlords. Lets a taxpayer ELECT to expense the full cost of qualifying В§1245 property in the year placed in service instead of capitalizing and recovering through MACRS depreciation. Sits adjacent to `section_168_e6` and `section_168g` (depreciation rules) and `cost_segregation` (accelerates basis allocation into В§1245 buckets); В§179 is the deduction. Heavy interaction with the OBBBA 2025 В§ 70302 restoration of 100% В§168(k) bonus depreciation as PERMANENT for property placed in service after 2024 вҖ” the SUV-sublimit excess flows directly into 100%-bonus, so the В§179 + В§168(k) combo expenses most equipment-heavy small businesses in year one.

**Three stacked limits + SUV sublimit**:

| Limit | 2026 value | Citation | What happens above |
|-------|------------|----------|--------------------|
| Dollar cap | **$2,560,000** | В§ 179(b)(1) | Reduced by phaseout below |
| Phaseout threshold | **$4,090,000** total В§1245 property placed in service | В§ 179(b)(2) | Dollar-for-dollar reduction of dollar cap above the threshold; cap fully phased out at $6,650,000 (= threshold + cap) |
| Taxable-income limit | Aggregate taxable income from active trade or business | В§ 179(b)(3)(A) | Excess carries forward INDEFINITELY under В§ 179(b)(3)(B) |
| SUV sublimit | **$32,000** | В§ 179(b)(5) | Heavy SUV (GVWR 6,001-14,000 lb.) basis exceeding $32K flows to В§ 168(k) 100% bonus depreciation (permanent post-OBBBA) |

**В§ 168(k) 100% bonus depreciation made PERMANENT** by OBBBA В§ 70302 effective 2025-01-01 вҖ” the previous TCJA phasedown (80%/60%/40%/20%/0% for 2023/24/25/26/27) was eliminated. SUV excess + В§1245 property in excess of В§179 cap both flow to 100%-bonus on the same Form 4562.

**Out of scope** (caller's responsibility): В§ 179(d)(3) related-party purchase restriction; В§ 179(d)(10) recapture on subsequent business-use percentage drop below 50%; В§ 179(f) qualified real-property carve-in (roofs, HVAC, fire alarm, security systems on nonresidential real property вҖ” added by TCJA).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-179`. Twenty-six tests pin: **under cap no phaseout full deduction**; **at phaseout threshold no reduction** (regression вҖ” > strict required); **one dollar above threshold reduces cap by exactly $1** (off-by-one); **$5.09M qualifying loses $1M cap вҶ’ $1.56M cap** (BDO worked-example regression target); **cap fully phased out at $6.65M** (= threshold + cap); **cap = $2.56M for 2026** (pinning the 2026 inflation-adjusted constants); **taxable-income limit blocks excess + carryforward indefinitely** + В§ 179(b)(3)(B) citation regression; **prior-year carryforward added to current-year tentative**; **SUV sublimit $32K blocks excess to bonus** ($90K SUV вҶ’ $32K В§ 179 + $58K В§ 168(k) bonus); **SUV under sublimit no block**; **SUV at sublimit boundary no excess** (regression вҖ” > strict); **mixed SUV + other property** ($100K = $50K SUV вҶ’ $32K + $18K bonus + $50K other вҶ’ $82K total deduction); **phaseout + taxable-income limits both apply simultaneously**; **citation pins В§ 179 + Form 4562**; **zero qualifying no deduction**; **taxable income zero вҶ’ full carryforward**; **negative taxable income clamped**; **SUV in excess of qualifying clamped** (defensive вҖ” suv field cannot exceed qualifying); **note describes phaseout when triggered**; **note describes CAP FULLY PHASED OUT when adjusted cap = 0**; **note describes carries forward indefinitely**; **bonus depreciation eligible = SUV excess** (OBBBA В§ 168(k) 100% permanent regression); **constants pinned to 2026 inflation-adjusted values** (CAP_2026 = $2.56M + PHASEOUT = $4.09M + SUV_SUBLIMIT = $32K).

`traderview-expense::section_183` is the **IRC В§183 hobby loss module** вҖ” trader-critical because a trader who loses money for years risks IRS recharacterization of the activity as a hobby rather than a В§162 trade or business. For post-2017 tax years (made permanent by OBBBA 2025), the В§67(g) suspension of miscellaneous itemized deductions effectively zeroes В§183(b)(2) deductions вҖ” the taxpayer recognizes the income but receives NO offsetting deductions on the hobby path.

**Three profit-motive determination paths**:

| Determination | Trigger | Outcome |
|---------------|---------|---------|
| `PresumptionMet` | В§183(d) profit in 3 of 5 years (standard) / 2 of 7 (horse) | В§162 trade/business; all expenses deductible |
| `NineFactorEstablished` | Reg. В§ 1.183-2(b) вүҘ 5 of 9 factors favor profit motive | В§162 trade/business; all expenses deductible |
| `HobbyTreatment` | Neither path supports profit motive | В§183 hobby; only В§183(b)(1) always-allowed (taxes / interest) deductible; В§183(b)(2) ZEROED by В§67(g) post-2017 |

**В§183(b) deduction hierarchy**:
- **В§183(b)(1)** вҖ” deductions allowable regardless of profit motive (property tax, mortgage interest, casualty losses): ALWAYS allowed up to otherwise-allowable amount.
- **В§183(b)(2)** вҖ” other deductions: capped at gross income вҲ’ В§183(b)(1) amount; further ZEROED for post-2017 years via В§67(g) misc-itemized-deduction suspension.

**В§183(d) presumption windows**:
- Standard activities: 3 profit years out of any 5-consecutive-year period.
- Horse breeding / training / showing / racing: 2 profit years out of any 7-consecutive-year period (the most-litigated carve-out вҖ” IRS routinely challenges horse activities).

**В§183(e) deferral election** allows the taxpayer to defer the В§183(d) presumption determination until after the first 5 (or 7) consecutive years of the activity. Useful for new ventures that expect early losses.

**Reg. В§ 1.183-2(b) 9-factor test** is the backup when В§183(d) presumption is not met:
1. Manner activity is carried on  2. Expertise of taxpayer or advisors  3. Time and effort expended  4. Expectation assets may appreciate  5. Success in similar activities  6. History of income or losses  7. Amount of occasional profits  8. Financial status of taxpayer  9. Personal pleasure or recreation elements

No single factor controls; courts weigh all 9 together. Module uses a 5-of-9 threshold as a simplified proxy for "majority favor profit motive."

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-183`.
