Nineteen tests pin: **standard 3-of-5 presumption met**; **standard 4-of-5 presumption met**; **standard 2-of-5 presumption NOT met** вҶ’ hobby; **horse 2-of-7 presumption met**; **horse 1-of-7 not met**; **В§183(e) deferral election suppresses presumption** (regression вҖ” even 5 profit years out of 5 with deferral elected вҶ’ no presumption); **9-factor 5-of-9 establishes profit motive** when В§183(d) fails; **9-factor 4-of-9 falls to hobby**; **hobby + В§67(g) suspension only В§183(b)(1) allowed** ($10k income вҲ’ $2k b1 = $8k net taxable; b2 ZEROED); **hobby pre-2018 b2 allowed up to income cap** ($8k cap binding when other deductions $15k); **hobby pre-2018 b1 below income** lets b2 = $3k actually deductible without cap binding ($5k net taxable); **presumption met all expenses allowed no cap** ($10k вҲ’ $2k вҲ’ $15k = вҲ’$7k NOL territory); **9-factor established all expenses allowed** (no В§183 cap); **В§183(b)(1) always allowed even for hobby** ($2k taxes/interest); **citation mentions all 9 relevant authorities** (В§183(a) + (b)(1) + (b)(2) + (c) + (d) + (e) + В§ 1.183-2(b) + В§67(g) + OBBBA 2025); note for presumption mentions В§162 trade/business; note for В§67(g) hobby says "ZEROED by В§67(g)"; note for 9-factor says "analysis establishes profit motive"; **$1B precision case** ($1B income вҲ’ $100M b1 вҲ’ $500M other = $400M net).

`traderview-expense::section_168_e6` is the **IRC В§168(e)(6) Qualified Improvement Property module** вҖ” the 15-year MACRS class for interior improvements to nonresidential buildings made by the taxpayer after the building was originally placed in service. Critical for commercial landlords planning tenant build-out allowances.

**Drafting-error saga** worth modeling because it changed the recovery period mid-history:
- **TCJA 2017** intended QIP to be 15-year property eligible for В§168(k) bonus depreciation. The bill accidentally omitted the recovery-period assignment, so QIP placed in service 2018-2019 defaulted to **39-year** real property under В§168(c) вҖ” and was NOT bonus eligible.
- **CARES Act 2020** (P.L. 116-136 В§2307) retroactively fixed the drafting error effective for property placed in service after Dec 31, 2017 вҖ” assigned **15-year** recovery and restored В§168(k) bonus eligibility. Taxpayers could file Form 3115 (accounting method change, paired with iter 28's `section_481`) to recover missed bonus depreciation as a В§481(a) adjustment.

В§168(e)(6) **definition**: any improvement made by the taxpayer to an interior portion of a building which is nonresidential real property, IF such improvement is placed in service after the date such building was first placed in service. **Three categories are explicitly EXCLUDED** (revert to 39-year nonresidential recovery):

1. **В§168(e)(6)(A)** вҖ” building enlargement / addition.
2. **В§168(e)(6)(B)** вҖ” any elevator or escalator.
3. **В§168(e)(6)(C)** вҖ” internal structural framework.

The `ImprovementType` enum exposes the exclusion categories explicitly so the caller doesn't have to guess: `InteriorNonresidential` (the QIP general case), `BuildingEnlargement`, `ElevatorOrEscalator`, `InternalStructuralFramework`, `ResidentialRental` (QIP is nonresidential-only by definition). The `placed_in_service_year > building_first_placed_in_service_year` test enforces the "after the date such building was first placed in service" requirement вҖ” a same-year improvement to a newly-constructed building doesn't qualify.

В§168(k) bonus phase-down shared with iter 11's `cost_segregation` (2018-2022 = 100%, 2023 = 80%, 2024 = 60%, 2025 = 40%, 2026 = 20%, 2027+ = 0%). When elected, year 1 = `bonus_pct Г— cost + 5% Г— (cost вҲ’ bonus)`. Without bonus or when QIP doesn't qualify, year 1 falls to 5% (15-year MACRS half-year) or 1.282% (39-year nonresidential mid-month half-year approximation) depending on which path applies.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-168-e6`. Eighteen tests pin: interior nonresidential qualifies as QIP; QIP 2024 60% bonus year 1 ($60k + $2k = $62k); QIP 2022 100% bonus full year 1 ($100k); QIP 2023 80% bonus phase-down ($81k); QIP 2027 zero bonus phase-down ($5k year 1); building enlargement excluded вҶ’ 39-year; elevator/escalator excluded; internal structural framework excluded; residential rental not QIP by definition; **improvement in same year as building placed in service not QIP** (must be AFTER); improvement before building placed in service not QIP; excluded category uses 39-year MACRS ($1,282 year 1); no bonus election вҶ’ MACRS half-year only ($5k); bonus phase-down 2023-2027 each year exact; helper returns true only for interior nonresidential; CARES Act fix verified for years 2018-2021 (15-year recovery); note distinguishes QIP path vs excluded path; total deduction equals bonus + MACRS invariant.

`traderview-expense::section_168k` is the **IRC В§168(k) bonus depreciation module with OBBBA 100% permanent restoration** вҖ” universal for landlords + traders + business buying В§1245 property. Distinct from `section_179` first-year expensing which has dollar caps + income limits + phaseout вҖ” В§168(k) has NO dollar cap, NO income limit, NO phaseout. The two work together: В§179 first, then В§168(k) on the remainder, then MACRS on what remains.

**Rate schedule** (placed-in-service year):

| Period | Rate | Citation |
|--------|------|----------|
| Pre-2018 (pre-TCJA) | 50% | В§ 168(k)(6) (pre-TCJA) |
| TCJA 2018-2022 | **100%** | В§ 168(k)(6) (TCJA boost) |
| 2023 (TCJA phasedown begins) | 80% | В§ 168(k)(6) phasedown |
| 2024 | 60% | В§ 168(k)(6) phasedown |
| 2025 (pre-OBBBA вҖ” property placed before 2025-01-20) | 40% | В§ 168(k)(6) phasedown |
| 2026 (pre-OBBBA hypothetical) | 20% | В§ 168(k)(6) phasedown |
| 2027+ (pre-OBBBA hypothetical) | 0% | В§ 168(k)(6) sunset |
| **OBBBA permanent (acquired AND placed in service after 2025-01-19)** | **100% PERMANENTLY** | **OBBBA В§ 70302** |

**OBBBA В§ 70302 effective date is critical**: property must be BOTH acquired AND placed in service AFTER **2025-01-19** to qualify for the permanent 100%. Property acquired BEFORE 2025-01-20 + placed in service AFTER falls back to the pre-OBBBA phasedown schedule (20% in 2026, 0% in 2027+).

**Transition election** (В§ 168(k)(7) under OBBBA): for the FYE-after-2025-01-19 year, taxpayer may ELECT to deduct **40%** (or **60% for long-production-period property / certain aircraft**) instead of the permanent 100%. Election made by the due date of the federal return for the year including 2025-01-20.

**Qualified property** (В§ 168(k)(2)): В§ 1245-type tangible property with MACRS recovery period **вүӨ 20 years**; computer software; qualified film/TV/live theatrical productions; specified plants; water utility property; **qualified improvement property** (post-CARES Act 2020 fix). **Used property** eligible if no prior use by the taxpayer (TCJA 2017 expansion preserved by OBBBA).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-168k`. Twenty-two tests pin: **OBBBA 2026 100% permanent**; **pre-OBBBA 2024 60%**; **pre-OBBBA 2023 80%**; **pre-OBBBA 2025 phasedown 40%** (placed in service before 2025-01-20); **at 2025-01-19 boundary pre-OBBBA** (strict greater-than required); **at 2025-01-20 OBBBA permanent 100% applies**; **OBBBA transition election 40% 2025** + В§ 168(k)(7) citation; **OBBBA transition election 60% long-production**; **OBBBA no-transition-election 2025 full 100%**; **transition election NOT available for 2026** (regression вҖ” only FYE-after-2025-01-19 year qualifies); **prior use disqualifies** + В§ 168(k)(2) citation; **MACRS 25-year disqualifies**; **MACRS 20-year qualifies** (вүӨ strict); **pre-2018 50% rate**; **TCJA 2018-2022 100% all 5 years** (multi-year invariant); **pre-OBBBA 2026 would-have-been 20%** (acquired before cutoff вҶ’ phasedown applies); **pre-OBBBA 2027 zero** (sunset); **OBBBA 2027 back to 100% permanent** (regression вҖ” OBBBA permanent eliminates the pre-OBBBA 2027 zero); **acquisition before cutoff placed after does NOT get OBBBA** (regression вҖ” both dates required); **placed before cutoff acquired after does NOT get OBBBA** (symmetric regression); **negative cost clamped**; **citations pin authorities** (OBBBA В§ 70302 + "PERMANENT 100%" + transition-election + phasedown + В§ 168(k)(2)).

`traderview-expense::section_168g` is the **IRC В§168(g) Alternative Depreciation System (ADS) + В§163(j)(7)(B) tradeoff analyzer** вҖ” the natural companion to iter 16's `section_163j`. A landlord with high mortgage interest hitting the В§163(j) 30%-of-ATI cap can elect to be an **electing real property trade or business** under В§163(j)(7)(B): full business interest deductibility (no В§163(j) cap), BUT must use slower ADS depreciation on all real property forever вҖ” the election is **IRREVOCABLE**.

ADS recovery periods (В§168(g)(2)): **30 years** residential (post-TCJA; 40 years pre-2018), **40 years** non-residential, **20 years** for Qualified Improvement Property at electing RPTBs, plus personal-property classes (5/7/15 years). Method is **straight-line** (no double-declining acceleration). Convention is **mid-month** for real property, **half-year** for personal property. Bonus depreciation per В§168(k)(2)(D)(i) is NOT allowed on ADS property вҖ” another giveup beyond the longer recovery period.

The compute function returns both the ADS annual deduction AND a GDS comparison (straight-line, same convention) so callers can sum the per-year depreciation difference across all real property and feed it into the tradeoff analyzer. `analyze_tradeoff` takes `annual_depreciation_sacrificed` + `annual_interest_disallowed_under_163j` + `marginal_federal_rate`, converts each to after-tax dollars, and returns `net_annual_benefit` + `election_recommended` + a note reminding the user that the election is irrevocable and requires a multi-decade horizon model before committing.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-168g` and `POST /api/calc/section-163j-tradeoff`. Eighteen tests pin: residential 30-year year-2 full-year at 1/30; mid-month January year-1 = 11.5/12 of full year; mid-month December year-1 smallest; year-31 stub recovers the leftover from year-1 partial; commercial 40-year longer recovery; QIP 20-year shortest real-property recovery; personal 5-year half-year convention; personal 5-year stub (year 6); residential 30y vs GDS 27.5y difference positive; pre-service year returns zero; zero basis no-op; residential 40-year legacy; recovery-period helper matches class; is_real_property helper; tradeoff election worth it when interest savings exceed depreciation loss; tradeoff not recommended when reversed; tradeoff zero interest disallowed вҶ’ election pointless; tradeoff scales with marginal rate.

`traderview-expense::section_280f` is the **IRC В§280F luxury auto depreciation cap module** вҖ” caps annual depreciation on passenger autos used in a trade or business. Highly practical for landlords driving to properties: without the В§280F(a)(1) cap, MACRS 5-year on a $100k vehicle would generate $20,000 of year-1 depreciation; В§280F limits 2024 year-1 to $12,400 (no bonus) or $20,400 (with В§168(k) bonus) вҖ” a $7,600 cliff that goes to the depreciation "tail" past the recovery period per Reg. В§1.280F-2T.

The published Rev. Proc. cap table is statically encoded for placed-in-service years **2020-2024** (Rev. Proc. 2020-37 / 2021-31 / 2022-17 / 2023-14 / 2024-13), each with year-1-with-bonus / year-1-no-bonus / year-2 / year-3 / year-4-plus values. For years outside the static table (2025+ at time of writing), caller passes `caller_override: Some(PassengerAutoCaps)` with the current IRS-published values; the module surfaces "no published В§280F caps on file" without guessing when both the table miss and override are absent. The `rev_proc_citation` field is preserved through the result for audit display.

Three structural elements:
- **Year-by-year cap routing** вҖ” different caps for year-1-bonus / year-1-no-bonus / year-2 / year-3 / year-4-through-end-of-recovery. Year 5 and 6 use the year-4-plus cap.
- **В§280F(d)(5) heavy-vehicle carve-out** вҖ” vehicles over **6,000 lbs GVWR** (large SUVs, trucks, commercial vans) escape the passenger-auto definition entirely. The module skips cap computation and returns the unmodified MACRS deduction.
- **Business-use percentage scaling** вҖ” caps apply proportionally per В§280F(b)(1). 60% business use in 2024 вҶ’ year-1 cap = $20,400 Г— 0.60 = $12,240. Values above 1.0 clamp to 1.0; below 0 clamp to 0.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280f`. Seventeen tests pin: year-1 no bonus 2024 caps at $12,400 (under MACRS $12,000 вҶ’ no cap); year-1 with bonus 2024 caps at $20,400; year-2 caps at $19,800; year-3 caps at $11,900; year-4 caps at $7,160; expensive vehicle ($150k) shows $17,600 capped-amount-lost; **heavy vehicle (>6000 lb GVWR) skips cap entirely** ($100k SUV gets full $20,000 year-1); business-use 60% scales cap proportionally; zero business use no deduction; above-one business use clamps to one; published caps table 2020-2024 each year exact year-1-no-bonus; unknown year (2099) returns None + caller_override path takes precedence; **caller_override beats published table** for known years; pre-service year no deduction; MACRS rates match Pub 946 Table A-1 (20/32/19.20/11.52/11.52/5.76%); year 5 and 6 use year-4-plus cap; capped_amount_lost calculated correctly.

`traderview-expense::section_280b` is the **IRC В§280B demolition of structures disallowance module** вҖ” foundational rule for any real estate trader: when an owner demolishes a structure, the demolition costs AND any loss sustained on the demolition (structure remaining basis вҲ’ salvage value) are **NOT DEDUCTIBLE**. Instead they are **CAPITALIZED into the basis of the LAND** on which the demolished structure was located ([Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. В§ 280B](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/280B), [Cornell LII 26 CFR В§ 1.280B-1](https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/26/1.280B-1)).

**Why this matters**: land is NOT a depreciable asset, so the "deduction" is **DEFERRED until the LAND is eventually sold** вҖ” potentially decades later. Compare: if the structure were depreciated to zero and abandoned without demolition, the remaining basis would be immediately deductible as an ordinary loss. В§280B forces a capital-account treatment that destroys timing.

**В§280B applies BROADLY with NO exceptions for**:

- Safety concerns necessitating demolition
- Unanticipated circumstances (fire damage, structural failure discovered post-purchase)
- Economic obsolescence
- Local code requirements forcing demolition

A taxpayer cannot escape В§280B by arguing the demolition was forced upon them ([LegalClarity вҖ” How the IRS Treats Demolition Costs](https://legalclarity.org/how-the-irs-treats-demolition-costs-for-tax-purposes/)).

**Two narrow EXCEPTIONS** where В§280B's harsh deferral can be avoided:

1. **IRS Notice 90-21 casualty exception** вҖ” when a structure is damaged by a casualty (fire, flood, earthquake, storm), the taxpayer can compute a В§ 165 casualty loss separately on the pre-casualty basis BEFORE В§ 280B captures the demolition costs. The actual demolition expenses still capitalize to land, but the casualty loss on the structure is deductible. Pinned by `casualty_exception_allows_separate_loss` + `casualty_exception_does_not_change_280b_capitalization` (the В§ 280B treatment of demo costs is unchanged вҖ” only the casualty loss is separate).

2. **General Asset Account (GAA) election** вҖ” if the structure was placed in a В§ 168(i)(4) GAA at or before the demolition decision, and the GAA election remains in effect, an abandonment loss on the demolished structure may be claimed by terminating the GAA under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.168(i)-1(e). The election must have been made AT or BEFORE the demolition вҖ” it cannot be made retroactively ([KBKG вҖ” Demolishing a Building? Cost Segregation Can Preserve Deductions](https://www.kbkg.com/cost-segregation/demolishing-a-building-cost-segregation-can-preserve-deductions-in-this-situation)). Pinned by `gaa_election_in_effect_allows_abandonment_loss` + `no_gaa_no_abandonment_loss` regression.

**Practical effect**: a real estate trader with $50k demolition costs + $200k structure remaining basis (no salvage, no exception applies) gets:

- $0 current deduction
- $250k capitalized to land basis (was $100k вҶ’ now $350k)
- Deduction realized only when LAND is sold, as reduced gain or increased loss

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280b`. Nineteen tests pin: **standard demolition no deduction**; standard demolition loss sustained ($200k); **standard demolition capitalized to land** ($50k + $200k = $250k вҶ’ land basis $100k + $250k = $350k); salvage value reduces loss ($200k вҲ’ $30k salvage = $170k loss); **salvage greater than basis clamps loss to zero**; zero demolition costs only loss capitalized; **fully depreciated structure no loss only demo costs** ($0 loss + $50k demo); **IRS Notice 90-21 casualty exception allows separate $200k loss** with CASUALTY EXCEPTION note; **casualty exception does NOT change В§ 280B capitalization** ($250k still to land); **В§ 168(i)(4) GAA election allows $200k abandonment loss** via GAA termination; no GAA no abandonment loss; **both casualty + GAA exceptions can apply simultaneously**; no-exceptions note describes indefinite deferral until LAND is sold; **$1B precision path** ($1B demo + $5B basis + $10B land = $16B new land basis); zero all inputs no-op; note mentions В§ 280B(1) + В§ 280B(2) + NO deduction + capitalized to land; citation mentions all 5 relevant authorities (В§280B(1), В§280B(2), В§1.280B-1, Notice 90-21, В§168(i)(4)); note mentions GAA when election in effect; note does NOT mention casualty when absent.

`traderview-expense::section_280c` is the **IRC В§ 280C Certain Expenses for Which Credits Are Allowable anti-double-dip disallowance module** (iter 606) вҖ” foundational rule preventing a taxpayer from claiming BOTH a federal tax credit AND a В§ 162 / В§ 174 deduction for the same dollar of wages or research expense. Direct trader-companion to `section_41` (research credit вҖ” В§ 280C(c) interaction), `section_280e` (different deduction-disallowance regime вҖ” controlled-substance trafficking), `section_162a` (foundational В§ 162(a) deduction provision iter 467), `section_174` (R&E expenditures post-TCJA capitalization). **В§ 280C(a) GENERAL RULE вҖ” wage credits**: no deduction for wages or salaries paid or incurred during the taxable year equal to the sum of the credits determined under **В§В§ 45A(a)** (Indian Employment Credit), **45P(a)** (Military Differential Wage Payment Credit), **45S(a)** (Paid Family and Medical Leave Credit), **51(a)** (Work Opportunity Tax Credit / WOTC), and **1396(a)** (Empowerment Zone Employment Credit) вҖ” **no reduced-credit election available for these wage-based credits**. **В§ 280C(b) ORPHAN DRUG**: В§ 280C(b)(1) reduces В§ 162/В§ 174 deduction by full В§ 45C clinical-testing-expense credit; В§ 280C(b)(3) reduced-credit election (credit Г— (1 вҲ’ 21 %)) preserves full deduction via cross-reference to В§ 280C(c)(2)/(3) procedure. **В§ 280C(c) RESEARCH CREDIT (В§ 41)**: В§ 280C(c)(1) default rule requires taxpayer to reduce В§ 174 specified R&E (post-TCJA capitalization basis) by the gross В§ 41 credit amount; В§ 280C(c)(2) ELECTION lets taxpayer take REDUCED CREDIT of gross Г— (1 вҲ’ 21 % maximum corporate rate) = gross Г— 79 % and keep full В§ 174 deduction; В§ 280C(c)(3) election must be made on the ORIGINAL RETURN for the taxable year вҖ” cannot be made on amended return. **В§ 280C(g) MILITARY DIFFERENTIAL WAGE**: wage deduction reduced by В§ 45P credit; election procedure under В§ 280C(c)(2). **В§ 280C(h) PAID FAMILY MEDICAL LEAVE**: wage deduction reduced by В§ 45S(1)(A) credit. **Tax Court precedent**: В§ 280C disallowance applies to credit DETERMINED, not credit ALLOWED вҖ” taxpayer cannot escape disallowance by limiting credit utilization (e.g., credit blocked by В§ 38 general business credit limitation does NOT reduce В§ 280C disallowance). **Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, CompliantNoCreditClaimed, CompliantFullDeductionDisallowanceApplied, CompliantReducedCreditElectionAppliedResearchOrOrphan, ViolationDeductionAndFullCreditDoubleClaimed, ViolationReducedCreditElectionNotOnOriginalReturn, ViolationDeductionDisallowanceLessThanCreditDetermined, ViolationReducedCreditElectionAttemptedForIneligibleCredit. **Seven credit categories**: ResearchSection41, WorkOpportunitySection51, IndianEmploymentSection45A, OrphanDrugSection45C, MilitaryDifferentialWageSection45P, PaidFamilyMedicalLeaveSection45S, EmpowermentZoneEmploymentSection1396. **Three reduced-credit election states**: NotElected, ElectedOnOriginalReturn, AttemptedOnAmendedReturn. Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280c`. **Eighteen tests pin**: **no credit claimed compliant** (gross credit = 0 вҶ’ full deduction preserved), **WOTC full disallowance compliant**, **WOTC double-dip violation** (claiming both full credit + full deduction triggers anti-double-dip violation), **research reduced-credit election on original return compliant** (gross credit 1 000 000 cents Г— 79 % = 790 000 cents net credit + full В§ 174 deduction preserved), **orphan drug reduced-credit election on original return compliant** (gross credit 500 000 Г— 79 % = 395 000), **research reduced-credit election on amended return violation** (В§ 280C(c)(3) procedural defect), **WOTC reduced-credit election attempted violation** (В§ 280C(a) wage credits don't permit election), **Indian employment reduced-credit election violation**, **military differential wage full disallowance compliant**, **paid family medical leave full disallowance compliant**, **empowerment zone full disallowance compliant**, **research default-rule full disallowance compliant**, **partial-disallowance-short-of-credit-determined violation** (taxpayer disallowed less than credit determined вҖ” Tax Court rule), **citations pin В§ 280C subsections** ((a) + (b)(1) + (b)(3) + (c)(1) + (c)(2) + (c)(3) + (g) + (h)), **citations pin referenced credit sections** (В§ 45A + В§ 45P + В§ 45S + В§ 51 + В§ 1396), **constant pin 21 % reduced-credit factor** (factor 7900 + max-rate 2100 = denominator 10000), **constant pin 1989 research reduced-credit election year** (OBRA 1989), **saturating overflow defense extreme inputs** (u64::MAX gross credit handled). Trader critical: traders who hire eligible WOTC employees (veterans, ex-felons, long-term unemployed) at brokerage operations or trading-shop support staff; R&D credit for software development (algorithmic trading systems, broker platform integrations) interacts with В§ 174 capitalization (post-TCJA 5-year domestic / 15-year foreign amortization); paid family medical leave credit for property-management or LLC employees; empowerment zone employment credit for rentals in designated empowerment zones (historically expired 2025-12-31 but periodically extended). Sibling cluster: `section_41` (research credit вҖ” В§ 280C(c) interaction), `section_280e` (different deduction-disallowance regime), `section_280g` (different deduction-disallowance regime вҖ” golden parachute), `section_280h` (different deduction-disallowance regime вҖ” personal service corp deferred comp), `section_174` (R&E expenditures вҖ” В§ 280C(c)(1) reduces capitalization basis post-TCJA).

`traderview-expense::section_280e` is the **IRC В§280E controlled-substance trafficking deduction disallowance module** вҖ” the defining tax restriction on the entire U.S. state-legal cannabis industry. Enacted by P.L. 97-248 В§ 351 (1982) in response to *Edmondson v. Commissioner*; disallows any В§162 deduction or credit for amounts paid or incurred in carrying on any trade or business consisting of trafficking in Schedule I or II controlled substances regardless of state legalization. State-licensed cannabis operators have routinely faced effective tax rates exceeding 70% because rent, wages, and marketing are disallowed while gross revenue is fully taxed.

**Two judicially established carve-outs**:
- **COGS always allowed** вҖ” Cost of Goods Sold reduces gross income under В§61, not a В§162 deduction. *Californians Helping to Alleviate Med. Probs., Inc. v. Comm'r (Champ)* (T.C. 2007) established that В§280E does not apply to COGS.
- **Bifurcated non-trafficking activity** вҖ” *Champ* also established that when a business conducts both a trafficking activity AND a separate legal trade or business, expenses allocable to the non-trafficking activity remain deductible.

**2025-2026 DEA rescheduling status** (post-cutoff, verified via web search before commit):
- **2025-12-18**: President Trump signed **Executive Order 14370** directing the U.S. Attorney General to complete DEA rulemaking moving marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III "in the most expeditious manner permitted by law."
- As of 2026-02 the administrative process is ongoing; the final order has not yet taken effect.
- **DOJ Final Order (partial rescheduling)**: FDA-approved marijuana drug products + state-licensed medical marijuana + certain marijuana extracts / naturally derived delta-9-THC moved to Schedule III. Bulk marijuana, unlicensed crops, and any marijuana not incorporated into an FDA-approved drug product **remain Schedule I** вҖ” В§280E still applies to those.
- **Tax effective date**: rescheduling applies first to the business's full taxable year that **includes** the effective date of the Final Order.

**Four business activity types** with В§280E status by activity:

| Activity type | В§280E applies? | Path |
|---------------|----------------|------|
| `TraditionalTrafficking` | Always | Bulk marijuana, recreational cannabis, illegal drugs |
| `FdaApprovedScheduleIII` | Never | Epidiolex, Marinol, other FDA-approved drug products |
| `StateLicensedMedicalRescheduled` | Conditional | Applies until DOJ Final Order is effective for the business's taxable year |
| `NonTraffickingBifurcated` | Never | Caregiving, consulting, branded merchandise (Champ) |

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280e`. Twenty-four tests pin: **traditional cannabis В§280E applies + $300k trafficking expenses fully disallowed**; **traditional cannabis taxable income = gross income вҲ’ COGS only** ($1M revenue вҲ’ $400k COGS = $600k taxable, $300k В§162 expenses DISALLOWED); **effective tax disadvantage = trafficking-disallowed amount** ($300k); **COGS always reduces gross income under В§61**; **very large COGS ($9M) still allowed**; **Champ bifurcation** вҖ” traditional cannabis $300k disallowed + $50k non-trafficking allowed (taxable income $550k); **pure non-trafficking business no В§280E** + $200k bifurcated allowed; **FDA-approved Schedule III no В§280E** ($300k expenses allowed вҶ’ $300k taxable); **state-licensed medical pre-Final-Order still В§280E applies** (rescheduling not yet effective for year); **state-licensed medical post-Final-Order no В§280E**; **bulk / unlicensed still В§280E after partial rescheduling** (regression вҖ” DOJ Final Order does NOT move bulk to Schedule III); no В§280E вҶ’ zero effective disadvantage; higher trafficking expenses вҶ’ higher disadvantage; zero revenue zero income; zero trafficking expenses no disadvantage; **Champ bifurcation + FDA-approved combined** ($400k total allowed вҶ’ $200k taxable); **4 citation regression targets** (P.L. 97-248 В§ 351 origin + Edmondson + Champ + COGS carve-out + EO 14370 + DOJ Final Order + 2025-12-18); note for traditional says "В§280E applies"; note for FDA says "В§280E does NOT apply"; note describes rescheduling status; **$1B precision case** ($1B revenue / $400M COGS / $300M expenses вҶ’ $600M taxable + $300M effective disadvantage).

`traderview-expense::section_280g` is the **IRC В§ 280G Golden Parachute Payments module** вҖ” direct trader-critical companion to `section_422` (ISO acceleration on change of control), `section_423` (ESPP вҖ” purchase right acceleration), `section_409a` (NQDC vesting acceleration), `section_4973` (different excise-tax regime). Trader-critical because traders at venture-backed startups frequently encounter В§ 280G calculations during ACQUISITIONS when accelerated equity vesting + transaction bonuses + severance payments trigger "parachute payment" status.

В§ 280G(a) DENIES employer DEDUCTION for any "excess parachute payment" to a "disqualified individual" that is "contingent on a change in ownership or control" of the corporation. В§ 4999 imposes a 20% EXCISE TAX on the recipient disqualified individual on any excess parachute payment.

**В§ 280G(b)(1) cliff effect вҖ” three-times-base threshold**:

| Aggregate parachute | Result |
|---------------------|--------|
| < 3Г— base amount | NO excess parachute payment; В§ 4999 inapplicable |
| вүҘ 3Г— base amount | **CLIFF triggered**: ENTIRE EXCESS OVER 1Г— base (NOT just over-3Г— portion) becomes excess parachute payment + 20% recipient excise tax + lost employer В§ 162 deduction |

**В§ 280G(b)(3) base amount** вҖ” annualized includible compensation for **base period = 5 most recent tax years** ending before change-in-control date.

**В§ 280G(c) disqualified individual (three categories)**:
1. **OFFICER** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.280G-1 Q&A 18: max 50 employees treated as officers regardless of title)
2. **1%+ SHAREHOLDER** (stock FMV exceeds 1% of total stock FMV at any time during 12-month period ending on change-in-control date)
3. **HIGHLY COMPENSATED EMPLOYEE** (top 1% of all employees or top 250 employees вҖ” whichever is less вҖ” with 2026 threshold $155,000+)

**В§ 280G(b)(2)(A) change in ownership or control** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.280G-1 Q&A 27-29):
- CHANGE IN OWNERSHIP вҖ” > 50% of FMV or voting power acquired
- CHANGE IN EFFECTIVE CONTROL вҖ” 35% voting power within 12 months OR majority of board replaced within 12 months
- CHANGE IN OWNERSHIP OF SUBSTANTIAL ASSETS вҖ” 40%+ of total gross FMV of assets within 12 months

**В§ 280G(b)(5) small business exception** (eliminates В§ 280G entirely):
- **В§ 280G(b)(5)(A)(i)** вҖ” private corporation with В§ 1361 S corporation election; OR
- **В§ 280G(b)(5)(A)(ii)** вҖ” private corporation with > 75% SHAREHOLDER VOTE / CLEANSING VOTE after adequate disclosure under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.280G-1 Q&A 7

**В§ 280G(b)(4) reasonable compensation exception** вҖ” payment is NOT parachute payment to extent it is reasonable compensation for services rendered ON OR AFTER change in control; BURDEN ON TAXPAYER to establish by CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.280G-1 Q&A 40.

**В§ 280G employment-agreement structures**:
- **GROSS-UP CLAUSE** вҖ” employer pays additional cash to make recipient whole for В§ 4999 excise tax + federal/state income tax on gross-up; expensive to employer; controversial post-Dodd-Frank
- **MODIFIED CUTBACK / BEST-AFTER-TAX** вҖ” payment cut to maximum of (1) just-under 3Г— base OR (2) full payment net of excise; whichever leaves disqualified individual with GREATER AFTER-TAX AMOUNT

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) trader at startup acquired $500M; $4.5M parachute on $1M base; 3Г— threshold = $3M MET вҶ’ entire $3.5M excess over 1Г— base = excess parachute вҶ’ $700K В§ 4999 + employer loses В§ 162 deduction; (2) private S-corp acquisition with 75%+ shareholder vote + adequate disclosure eliminates В§ 280G entirely via В§ 280G(b)(5); (3) 60% of parachute treated as reasonable compensation for post-change services under В§ 280G(b)(4); (4) trader holds 0.5% stock + NOT officer + NOT top 1% comp вҶ’ not disqualified individual; (5) gross-up clause vs modified-cutback structure.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-280g`. Thirty-four tests pin: **three-times threshold met triggers excess** ($4.5M parachute on $1M base вҶ’ $3.5M excess); **excise tax at 20%** ($700K on $3.5M excess); **employer deduction lost full excess**; **just under 3Г— no excess**; **3Г— boundary triggers cliff**; **not disqualified individual no excise**; **no change in control no excise**; **S-corp election small business exception**; **shareholder 75% vote small business exception**; **public corp no small business exception**; **reasonable compensation carveout reduces parachute**; **reasonable compensation carveout full neutralizes**; **1% shareholder qualifies**; **highly compensated employee qualifies** ($155K threshold); **change in effective control engages**; **change in substantial assets engages**; **gross-up clause disclosed when excess**; **modified cutback clause disclosed when threshold met**; **disqualified individual truth table 4 cells**; **change in control truth table 4 cells**; **cliff effect invariant one dollar over 3Г—**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 280G(a)-(e) + В§ 4999 + В§ 162 + В§ 1361 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.280G-1 Q&A 1-45 + Rev. Rul. 2005-39 + IRS Pub. 5975); **note pins В§ 280G(a) deduction denial**; **note pins В§ 280G(b)(1) cliff definition**; **note pins В§ 280G(b)(3) base amount**; **note pins В§ 280G(c) three categories**; **note pins В§ 280G(b)(2) change categories**; **note pins В§ 280G(b)(5) small business exception**; **note pins В§ 280G(b)(4) reasonable compensation**; **note pins В§ 4999 and gross-up/modified-cutback**; **note pins trader fact patterns five**; **note pins Form 1120 Schedule M-3 and W-2 Box 12 Code K**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::credit_check_authorization` is the **state credit-check authorization and disclosure landlord compliance check** вҖ” addresses the upfront disclosures required BEFORE a landlord pulls a tenant-screening consumer report. Distinct from `adverse_action_notice` (post-denial notice AFTER the report is used to deny) and from `application_fees` (which caps the dollar amount of the screening fee). This module addresses the pre-screening disclosure step.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Statute | Four-prong disclosure required | Fee cap | Penalty |
|--------|---------|---------------------------------|---------|---------|
| `Washington` | RCW 59.18.257 | **YES** вҖ” (1) types of info accessed + (2) denial criteria + (3) CRA name/address/tenant rights + (4) reusable-screening-report acceptance | Actual cost (conditional on disclosure) | $100/violation + attorney fees + court costs |
| `California` | Cal. Civ. Code В§ 1950.6 | No (but itemized receipt + unused-portion refund required) | **~$60 inflation-adjusted in 2024** (1997 base + CPI) | $100 civil penalty |
| `Default` | 15 U.S.C. В§ 1681b(a)(3)(F) FCRA | No state-specific requirement (tenant screening is permissible purpose under FCRA) | None | None state-specific |

**FCRA baseline**: tenant screening is itself a "permissible purpose" under В§ 1681b(a)(3)(F) вҖ” landlord may obtain consumer reports without separate written tenant authorization. Written authorization under В§ 1681b(b)(2) is required ONLY for EMPLOYMENT-purpose reports, not tenant screening. The default regime relies on this FCRA baseline alone.

**WA's four-prong disclosure** is regression-critical: missing ANY of the four prongs is a separate violation under RCW 59.18.257(1)(a)-(d). Cost-recovery is conditional вҖ” landlord may charge for screening costs ONLY if the disclosures are provided.

**CA's three additional requirements**: В§ 1950.6 imposes (a) inflation-adjusted fee cap (~$60 in 2024 from 1997 $30 base), (b) itemized receipt upon request, (c) unused-portion refund. None of these apply under WA or Default.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/credit-check-authorization`. Twenty-two tests pin: **WA all-disclosures-provided compliant** + $100/violation penalty; **WA missing types-of-information violation** + В§ 257(1)(a) citation regression; **WA missing denial-criteria violation** + В§ 257(1)(b); **WA missing CRA + tenant-rights violation** + В§ 257(1)(c); **WA missing reusable-report disclosure violation** + В§ 257(1)(d); **CA under cap compliant**; **CA over $60 cap violation** + В§ 1950.6(b) citation; **CA at $60 cap boundary compliant** (вүӨ strict); **CA missing itemized receipt violation** + В§ 1950.6(b)(2) regression; **CA unused-portion-not-refunded violation** + В§ 1950.6(e) regression; **CA no-screening-cost-charged compliant**; **default FCRA permissible purpose applies**; **default no-written-authorization for tenant screening** (regression вҖ” В§ 1681b(b) is employment-purpose only); **state routing WA/CA/Default**; **case-insensitive routing**; **only-WA-has-four-prong-disclosure-requirement** 3-regime invariant; **only-CA-has-$60-fee-cap** 3-regime invariant; **CA-only itemized-receipt requirement** regression; **CA-only unused-portion-refund requirement** regression; **citations pin authorities** (RCW 59.18.257 + В§ 1950.6 + FCRA + В§ 1681b(a)(3)(F)); **WA $100 per-violation penalty**; **CA $100 civil penalty**.

`traderview-expense::cost_segregation` is the **cost-seg-study + В§168(k) bonus depreciation accelerator** вҖ” the strategy that converts a $500k STR purchase into a $100k+ first-year tax shield when paired with В§280A short-term-rental + material participation from `section_280a`. A landlord who buys a $500k residential rental and depreciates it as a single 27.5-year asset gets ~$9k/year. Run cost seg + bonus and year-1 jumps to ~$150k.

The module breaks the depreciable basis into FIVE MACRS class buckets per the typical industry breakdown for the property type:

| Type           | 5y | 7y  | 15y | 27.5y | 39y |
|----------------|----|-----|-----|-------|-----|
| SingleFamily   | 5% | 0%  | 10% | 85%   | 0%  |
| MultiFamily    | 10%| 5%  | 15% | 70%   | 0%  |
| ShortTermRental| 25%| 10% | 10% | 55%   | 0%  |
| Commercial     | 5% | 5%  | 15% | 0%    | 75% |
| Restaurant     | 30%| 0%  | 15% | 0%    | 55% |

Callers can override via `allocation_override` (the study's actual percentages) вҖ” overrides outside Вұ0.005 of a sum-to-1.0 fall back to the default and surface a note. **В§168(k) bonus depreciation** is applied to the 5/7/15-year buckets only (real property вҖ” 27.5 and 39 вҖ” is excluded per В§168(k)(2)(A)(i)). Phase-down by tax year: 100% for 2018-2022, **80%** for 2023, **60%** for 2024, **40%** for 2025, **20%** for 2026, **0%** for 2027+. Pre-2018 stock is 50%. Year-1 deduction per bucket = `bonus Г— basis + (1/life Г— 0.5) Г— (basis вҲ’ bonus)`. The report also returns `year_1_without_cost_seg` (straight-line baseline at 27.5y for residential or 39y for commercial) and `year_1_acceleration` so the user sees the actual boost.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/properties/:id/cost-segregation`. Auto-fill: missing `depreciable_basis` = `purchase_price вҲ’ land_value` from the property row; missing `cost_seg_type` inferred from the property's `property_type` (`vacation_short_term` вҶ’ `ShortTermRental`, etc.). Seventeen tests pin: STR 2024 60% bonus pool, no-bonus-election zeroes all buckets, real-property buckets never get bonus, bonus phase-down 2023-2027 exact, pre-2018 50%, 2018-2022 100%, 2027+ zero, all 5 property-type defaults sum to 1.0, allocation override with bad sum falls back to default, override within tolerance used, commercial uses 39y baseline, residential uses 27.5y baseline, zero-basis edge, STR acceleration > 5Г— baseline, restaurant 30% 5y bucket largest, bucket_year_1_total = bonus + macrs identity, allocation sum helper round-trip.

`traderview-expense::section_280a_d2` is the **IRC В§280A(d)(2) related-party rental personal-use classifier** вҖ” the В§267 analog on the rental-income side. Renting your property to a family member at below-market rent is one of the most-common landlord tax mistakes: В§280A(d)(2)(A) treats ANY use by the taxpayer or a related party as **personal use** regardless of rent paid, and those days flow into iter 9's `section_280a` which then flips the property into vacation-home classification under В§280A(c)(5) вҖ” deductions capped at gross rental income, no net loss permitted.

Three carve-outs the module models:

- **В§280A(d)(2)(C) flush-language exception** вҖ” related-party use does NOT count as personal use when BOTH (a) the related party uses it as their **principal residence** AND (b) rent paid is **вүҘ fair market rent**. Below-market rent to family kills the exception even if they live there full-time. Caller supplies `fair_market_rent_for_period` from comparables (Zillow Rent Estimate, Rentometer, etc.); when omitted (zero), the module errs to personal-use treatment.
- **В§280A(d)(3) shared-equity-financing arrangement** вҖ” a co-owner residing in the property qualifies as paying fair rental even at $0 cash rent, provided the agreement meets the statutory requirements. Caller asserts via `shared_equity_arrangement: true`.
- **В§280A(d)(4) repair days** вҖ” days spent performing maintenance on the property aren't personal use even when the taxpayer or family stayed there. Common save when an owner spends a week renovating between tenants.

`Occupant` enum has three variants: `Taxpayer` (always personal), `RelatedParty` (gated by В§280A(d)(2)(C)/(d)(3)/(d)(4)), `Unrelated` (rental if any rent paid; personal if gratuitous). The compute function aggregates a list of `OccupancyPeriod` rows into `personal_use_days` + `rental_use_days` that feed directly into `section_280a::compute` from iter 9.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/section-280a-d2`. Sixteen tests pin: taxpayer use always personal; unrelated paying tenant full rental; unrelated gratuitous = personal; related-party at FMV + principal residence = rental (В§280A(d)(2)(C) exception); related-party below market = personal (the common trap); related-party not principal residence = personal even at FMV; shared-equity-arrangement qualifies as rental even at $0 rent; repair days don't count as personal; aggregate across multiple periods; zero-FMV reference defaults to personal (safe disposition); exactly at FMV qualifies; above FMV qualifies; one cent below FMV is personal (strict вүҘ, not approximate); empty input no-op; shared-equity overrides below-market; repair-day overrides taxpayer personal use.

`traderview-expense::section_280a` is the **IRC В§280A vacation home / mixed-use classifier** вҖ” uses the `fair_rental_days` and `personal_use_days` fields on `rental_properties` to bucket each property into one of four classifications:

- **Rental** вҖ” `fair_rental_days вүҘ 15` AND `personal_use_days вүӨ MAX(14, 10% of fair_rental_days)`. Full Schedule E; В§469 PAL applies separately.
- **VacationHome** вҖ” `fair_rental_days вүҘ 15` AND personal use over the threshold. В§280A(c)(5) caps deductions at gross rental income (no net loss); expenses allocated pro-rata between personal and rental days and tiered. **Tier 1** (mortgage interest, property tax вҖ” already deductible on Schedule A) always allowed at the rental allocation %; **Tier 2** (operating expenses: insurance, utilities, repairs, management, supplies, advertising) allowed up to remaining income after tier 1; **Tier 3** (depreciation) allowed up to remaining income after tier 1 + 2. Excess tier 2 + 3 carries forward to next year via the `prior_year_suspended` input.
- **AugustaRule** вҖ” `fair_rental_days` is 1вҖ“14. В§280A(g) **tax-free rental income**: the gross income is excluded from gross income entirely (not reported), and no rental deductions are allowed. Famously used by homeowners renting to their own corporations for board meetings (corp deducts the rent, owner excludes it).
- **PersonalResidence** вҖ” `fair_rental_days = 0`. No rental activity reported.

The personal-use threshold uses the GREATER of 14 days OR 10% of fair rental days per IRS Pub 527 вҖ” so a property rented 200 days passes the rental classification if personal use вүӨ 20 days, not the bare 14. Mounted at `POST /api/rental/properties/:id/section-280a` with auto-fill: missing `fair_rental_days` / `personal_use_days` are pulled from the `rental_properties` row. Sixteen tests pin: pure-rental no-personal-use; rental within threshold allocates proportionally; threshold uses max(14, 10%); rental boundary at 14 days exact stays rental; 15 personal days flips to vacation home; vacation home deductions capped at income (no loss); low-income suspends excess; Augusta Rule 14 days tax-free; Augusta boundary at 14 vs 15; personal residence zero rental days; prior suspended stacks with tier 2; 1-day rental routes to Augusta safely; allocation pct zero when both days zero; personal_use_ceiling math (10% of 100/140/200/365 days).

`traderview-expense::section_1033` is the **IRC В§1033 involuntary conversion gain-deferral module** вҖ” the natural complement to В§1031 (voluntary like-kind exchange) and В§1031(f). Where В§1031 lets a taxpayer DEFER gain when voluntarily exchanging into like-kind property, В§1033 lets a taxpayer DEFER gain when property is **compulsorily or involuntarily converted** (destruction by fire / theft / casualty, condemnation, eminent domain, federally declared disaster). Insurance proceeds + condemnation awards that flow back to the taxpayer create realized gain; В§ 1033 says recognition is postponed if the proceeds are reinvested in qualifying replacement property within the statutory window.

**Gain recognition formula (В§1033(a)(2)(A))**: `recognized = max(0, amount_realized вҲ’ replacement_cost)`, capped by realized gain. The unrecognized portion is **deferred**.

**Replacement property basis (В§1033(b)(2))**: `basis = replacement_cost вҲ’ deferred_gain`. The deferred gain is preserved in the lower basis of the replacement вҖ” taxable later on actual disposition.

**Replacement-period windows** (months from end of first taxable year gain was realized):

| Window | Trigger | Citation |
|--------|---------|----------|
| **24 months** | General default for В§1033(a)(2) elective non-recognition | В§1033(a)(2)(B)(i) |
| **36 months** | Condemnation of real property held for productive use in trade or business OR for investment | В§1033(g)(4) |
| **48 months** | Principal residence destroyed in a **federally declared disaster** | В§1033(h)(1)(B) |
| **60 months** | "Qualifying disaster" property (Hurricane Katrina-class events; extended on per-disaster basis) | В§1033(h)(2)(A) |

**Similar-or-related-in-service-or-use test** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1033(a)-2): the replacement must serve the same functional purpose. Owner-users take the functional-use test (a hotel must be replaced with a hotel, not an office building); lessors of investment real estate take the milder end-use test (rental real estate can be replaced with other rental real estate even if the tenant's use differs).

**Election requirement (В§1033(a)(2))**: standard path requires the taxpayer to AFFIRMATIVELY ELECT non-recognition. Without the election, В§1033 is unavailable вҖ” the gain is recognized. Mandatory non-recognition under В§1033(a)(1) applies only when the proceeds are converted DIRECTLY into qualifying property (rare). Modeled here: only `QualifyingDisaster` does not require an election.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1033`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **full reinvestment defers all gain** + basis = adjusted-basis carryover; **partial reinvestment recognizes shortfall** (amount realized вҲ’ replacement cost = recognized; deferred = realized вҲ’ recognized); **no reinvestment full recognition** + basis in (nonexistent) replacement = 0; **replacement cost exceeds realized** caps recognized at zero + basis lifted by extra reinvestment; **loss realized вҶ’ В§1033 irrelevant** (realized gain clamped to zero вҖ” losses follow ordinary rules); **general 2-year (24-month) window** + citation regression; **condemnation real-property 3-year window** + В§1033(g)(4) citation; **condemnation investment real-property 3-year window**; **condemnation personal residence stays at 2-year** (regression вҖ” В§1033(g)(4) is trade-or-investment-only); **federally-declared-disaster principal residence 4-year window** + В§1033(h)(1)(B) citation; **qualifying-disaster 5-year window** + В§1033(h)(2)(A) citation; **replacement at window boundary satisfied** (вүӨ strict); **replacement one month after window collapses deferral вҶ’ full recognition**; **similar-use test failure collapses deferral** + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.1033(a)-2 note regression; **missing election collapses deferral** + "ELECTION not made" note; **qualifying disaster does NOT require election** (mandatory non-recognition path); **basis carryover preserves deferred gain** (textbook В§1033 outcome вҖ” original basis carries forward); **partial reinvestment formula correctness** (1M realized вҲ’ 600K replacement = 400K recognized + 300K deferred + 300K basis); **negative basis clamped to zero**; **citation priority disaster > condemnation > general** (3 stacked citation regressions); **note includes SATISFIED/MISSED window status**; **5-year window 60-month boundary** (satisfied at 60, missed at 61); **4-year window 48-month boundary**; **3-year window 36-month boundary**.

`traderview-expense::section_1031_f` is the **IRC В§1031(f) related-party 2-year clawback module** вҖ” the anti-abuse rule that complements iter 7's `disposition` module. Plain В§1031 like-kind exchange defers gain when rolling into replacement property; В§1031(f) adds: if either party is related under В§267(b) AND either party disposes of the property received within **2 years** of the exchange, the deferred gain is **recognized retroactively in the year of the disqualifying disposition** (not the original exchange year). The character is preserved вҖ” LTCG stays LTCG, В§1250 recapture stays recapture.

В§1031(f)(2) recognizes three exceptions that block retroactive recognition even within the 2-year window: **DeathOfParty** (В§1031(f)(2)(A)), **InvoluntaryConversion** under В§1033 (В§1031(f)(2)(B)), and **LackOfTaxAvoidancePurpose** (В§1031(f)(2)(C) вҖ” taxpayer establishes neither the exchange nor the disposition had tax avoidance as a principal purpose).

The 2-year window is computed via `checked_add_months(24)` for leap-year correctness вҖ” a Feb 29 2024 exchange produces a window ending Feb 28 2026 (chrono adjusts non-existent dates correctly). The window-end date is **exclusive**: a disposition exactly on the end date preserves the deferral; one day earlier triggers. When no disposition has occurred yet, the module reports `window_still_open` + `days_to_window_end` so the landlord can see their exposure runway. Past-window exchanges report "matured cleanly". `RelationshipCategory` is re-exported from iter 18's `section_267` module вҖ” all 10 В§267(b) relationship classes route through the same code path.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1031-f`. Seventeen tests pin: unrelated parties вҖ” В§1031(f) doesn't apply; family disposition within 2-year window triggers full retroactive recognition with disposition-year tax; disposition after window preserves deferral; **disposition exactly at window end preserves**; one day before window end triggers; each В§1031(f)(2) exception blocks recognition (death / involuntary conversion / lack of tax avoidance); no-disposition open-window reports `window_still_open: true` + positive days remaining; no-disposition past-window reports "matured cleanly"; zero deferred gain no-op; all 10 В§267(b) categories trigger when disposition within window; character preserved (LTCG stays LTCG, В§1250 stays В§1250); 2-year window uses calendar months not days (Feb 29 2024 exchange вҶ’ Feb 28 2026 window-end); exception logged in note text; unrelated disposition within window no trigger (control); recognized year matches disposition year not exchange year (В§1031(f)(1)(C) retroactive-in-disposition-year rule).

`traderview-expense::section_408` is the **IRC В§ 408 Individual Retirement Accounts module** вҖ” natural companion to `section_408a` (Roth IRA shipped iter 430). Where В§ 408A houses POST-TAX Roth contributions with TAX-FREE qualified distributions, В§ 408 governs PRE-TAX traditional IRA contributions with DEDUCTIBLE contributions (within В§ 219 limits) + TAX-DEFERRED growth + ORDINARY-INCOME distributions taxed at marginal rates. Companion to `section_408a` (Roth IRA), `section_72t` (10% early-withdrawal penalty), `section_67g` (TCJA misc deduction suspension), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% вҖ” IRA distributions exempt), `section_475` (trader mark-to-market вҖ” self-directed IRA trader considerations), `section_4975` (prohibited transaction penalties).

**В§ 408 covers 5 account types** вҖ” В§ 408(a) traditional IRA + В§ 408(b) individual retirement annuity + В§ 408(k) SEP IRA + В§ 408(p) SIMPLE IRA + В§ 408(q) deemed IRA.

**2026 contribution limits (IRS Notice 2025-77)**:

| Account Type | Under 50 | Age 50+ |
|--------------|----------|---------|
| Traditional IRA / IRA Annuity / Deemed IRA | $7,500 | $8,600 ($1,100 catch-up) |
| SEP IRA | 25% of compensation OR $70,000 В§ 415(c) limit | N/A |
| SIMPLE IRA | $17,000 employee deferral | $21,000 ($4,000 catch-up) |

**В§ 219(g) Deduction phase-out вҖ” active plan participant 2026**:

| Filing Status | Phase-Out Low | Phase-Out High |
|---------------|----------------|-----------------|
| Single / Head of Household | **$81,000** | **$91,000** |
| MFJ (covered) | **$129,000** | **$149,000** |
| MFJ (spouse covered, taxpayer NOT) | **$242,000** | **$252,000** |
| MFS (covered) | $0 | $10,000 |

Non-active participants have NO deduction phase-out regardless of MAGI.

**В§ 408(d) Operative provisions**:

| Subsection | Rule |
|-------------|------|
| В§ 408(d)(1) | Distributions = ORDINARY INCOME at marginal rates |
| В§ 408(d)(2) | **PRO-RATA RULE** вҖ” distribution computed on AGGREGATE BASIS across ALL IRAs (Form 8606); CRITICAL for backdoor Roth analysis |
| В§ 408(d)(3) | 60-day rollover; **ONE-ROLLOVER-PER-YEAR** per Bobrow v. Commissioner, T.C. Memo 2014-21 + IRS Announcement 2014-15 |
| В§ 408(d)(6) | RMD per В§ 401(a)(9); SECURE Act 2.0 raised age to **73** (rising to **75** for those born 1960+) |
| В§ 408(d)(8) | **Qualified Charitable Distribution (QCD)** вҖ” age 70ВҪ+ direct to charity excluded from gross income; 2026 limit $111,000 (up from $108K for 2025) |
| В§ 408(d)(8)(F) | One-time **$50,000** distribution to charitable gift annuity OR charitable remainder trust (SECURE Act 2.0 split-interest entity) |

**В§ 408(m) Collectibles prohibition** вҖ” IRA investment in collectibles treated as DISTRIBUTION at cost basis (immediate ordinary-income taxation + В§ 72(t) 10% penalty if under 59ВҪ); В§ 408(m)(2) collectibles defined: artwork, rug, antique, metal/gem, stamp/coin, alcoholic beverage, other tangible personal property; В§ 408(m)(3) EXCEPTION for gold/silver coins + bullion meeting fineness standards (American Eagle, Canadian Maple Leaf, etc.).

**В§ 408(p) SIMPLE IRA** вҖ” Savings Incentive Match Plan for Employees of Small Employers; available to businesses with **вүӨ 100 EMPLOYEES**; 2026 employee deferral $17,000 + age 50+ catch-up $4,000 (total $21,000); employer match 1:1 up to 3% of compensation OR 2% non-elective.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**:

- Self-directed IRA trader вҖ” intra-account trading gains/losses no current-year tax; В§ 1411 NIIT exempt; В§ 4975 prohibited-transaction rules apply rigorously (no self-dealing).
- Trader using В§ 408(d)(3) 60-day rollover вҖ” one-rollover-per-year applies aggregate across ALL IRAs per Bobrow; direct trustee-to-trustee transfers NOT subject to one-per-year limit.
- Trader age 73+ subject to RMD per SECURE Act 2.0 (rising to 75 for those born 1960+).
- Trader using В§ 408(d)(8) QCD strategy at 70ВҪ+ ($111,000 for 2026) to satisfy RMD with charity.
- Trader using В§ 408(p) SIMPLE IRA as small-business owner (вүӨ 100 employees) with 1:1 3% employer match.

Pinned by `under_50_traditional_ira_base_limit_7500`, `age_50_catch_up_8600`, `sep_ira_limit_70000`, `simple_ira_under_50_deferral_17000`, `simple_ira_age_50_catch_up_21000`, `active_participant_2026_phase_out_thresholds`, `non_active_participant_full_deduction`, `active_participant_above_phase_out_zero_deduction`, `collectibles_treated_as_distribution`, `life_insurance_contract_prohibited`, `precious_metal_bullion_408m3_exception_permitted`, `qcd_2026_limit_111000`, `qcd_2025_limit_108000`, `qcd_under_70_5_no_exclusion`, `qcd_distribution_capped_at_annual_limit`, `qcd_cumulative_prior_reduces_remaining`, `rmd_age_73_pre_2033`, `rmd_age_75_post_2033`, `one_rollover_per_year_violated_bobrow`, `one_rollover_first_no_violation`, `simple_ira_100_employee_threshold_compliant` (boundary), `simple_ira_101_employee_threshold_violation`, `excess_contribution_4973_excise_violation`, `account_type_truth_table_five_cells`, `investment_truth_table_four_cells`.

Reg. `traderview-expense::section_408a` is the **IRC В§ 408A Roth IRA module** вҖ” trader-critical for retirement planning + after-tax growth. Roth IRA contributions are made with AFTER-TAX dollars but qualified distributions of EARNINGS AND PRINCIPAL are entirely TAX-FREE; no required minimum distributions during owner's lifetime; FREE-FROM В§ 1411 NIIT on distributions (Roth withdrawals are not investment income). Companion to `section_408` (traditional IRA), `section_72t` (10% early-withdrawal penalty), `section_67g` (TCJA misc deduction suspension), `section_1411` (NIIT 3.8% вҖ” Roth distributions exempt), `section_475` (trader mark-to-market вҖ” Roth account trader can qualify).

**2026 contribution limits + phase-outs (IRS Notice 2025-77)**:

| Filing Status | Phase-Out Low | Phase-Out High |
|---------------|----------------|-----------------|
| Single / Head of Household | **$153,000** | **$168,000** |
| Married Filing Jointly | **$242,000** | **$252,000** |
| Married Filing Separately | $0 | $10,000 (NOT cost-of-living adjusted) |

| Age | Annual Limit |
|-----|--------------|
| Under 50 | **$7,500** base |
| 50 or older | **$8,600** ($7,500 + $1,100 catch-up) |

Pinned by `under_50_2026_base_limit_7500`, `age_50_catch_up_8600`, `single_phase_out_thresholds_2026`, `mfj_phase_out_thresholds_2026`, `mfs_phase_out_thresholds_zero_to_10k`, `below_phase_out_full_contribution_allowed`, `above_phase_out_zero_contribution_backdoor_required`, `in_phase_out_partial_contribution_allowed`, `mfj_uniquely_highest_phase_out_invariant`, `mfs_uniquely_zero_low_threshold_invariant`.

**В§ 408A(d)(2) Qualified distribution two-prong test**:

| # | Requirement |
|---|-------------|
| 1 | 5-YEAR HOLDING PERIOD starting January 1 of first Roth contribution year |
| 2 | ONE of: (a) age 59ВҪ+; (b) disability per В§ 72(m)(7); (c) death of owner; (d) first-time home purchase up to $10,000 lifetime per В§ 72(t)(2)(F) |

Pinned by `qualified_distribution_5_year_age_60`, `non_qualified_earnings_under_5_year_taxable_plus_penalty`, `earnings_pre_59_5_taxable_plus_10_percent_penalty`, `disability_qualifies_distribution_under_age`, `post_death_beneficiary_qualifies_distribution`, `first_home_purchase_qualifies_under_10k_cap`, `first_home_cumulative_10k_exhausts_lifetime_cap`.

**В§ 408A(d)(3) Non-qualified distribution ordering rules**:

| # | Source | Taxation |
|---|--------|----------|
| 1 | Contributions | Always tax-free + penalty-free |
| 2 | Conversions | 5-year per-conversion holding for penalty-free principal |
| 3 | Earnings | Taxable + 10% penalty under В§ 72(t) unless exception |

Pinned by `contribution_principal_always_tax_free_penalty_free`, `conversion_principal_within_5_year_10_percent_penalty`, `conversion_principal_after_5_year_no_penalty`.

**В§ 408A(d)(3)(A) Conversion 5-year-per-conversion rule** вҖ” separate 5-year holding period applies to EACH conversion for purposes of avoiding 10% penalty on conversion principal withdrawn before age 59ВҪ; multiple conversions create stacked 5-year clocks.

**В§ 408A(e) Backdoor Roth conversion** вҖ” taxpayer exceeding income phase-out may make NON-DEDUCTIBLE TRADITIONAL IRA contribution then CONVERT to Roth IRA; conversion taxable to extent of pre-tax IRA balance (pro-rata rule under В§ 408(d)(2)); BACKDOOR ROTH OPTIMAL when taxpayer has NO pre-tax traditional IRA balance.

**В§ 408A(c)(5) No RMD during owner's lifetime** вҖ” unlike traditional IRA, Roth IRA owner NOT subject to В§ 401(a)(9) RMD; beneficiaries subject to post-death RMD per SECURE Act В§ 401(a)(9)(H).

**В§ 408A(c)(3)(B) Modified AGI carveout** вҖ” MAGI calculation DISREGARDS Roth conversion income; large conversion does not push taxpayer into higher phase-out tier for same year's contribution.

**В§ 1411 NIIT exemption** вҖ” Roth distributions are NOT investment income; exempt from 3.8% surtax.

**В§ 4973 Excess contribution excise tax** вҖ” 6% on excess contributions to IRAs (including Roth) until withdrawn or absorbed.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**:

- High-income trader subject to phase-out вҖ” backdoor Roth conversion.
- Active trader using self-directed Roth IRA вҖ” intra-account trading gains/losses no current-year tax; В§ 1411 NIIT exempt.
- Mega backdoor Roth вҖ” after-tax 401(k) вҶ’ Roth conversion (plan design + В§ 415 limits + non-discrimination).
- В§ 72(t) substantially-equal-periodic-payments вҖ” pre-59ВҪ without 10% penalty.
