Thirty-six tests pin: **under 50 2026 base limit $7,500**; **age 50 catch-up $8,600**; **single phase-out thresholds 2026** ($153K-$168K); **MFJ phase-out thresholds 2026** ($242K-$252K); **MFS phase-out thresholds $0 to $10K**; **below phase-out full contribution allowed**; **above phase-out zero contribution backdoor required**; **in phase-out partial contribution allowed**; **excess contribution violation** (В§ 4973 6% excise tax); **qualified distribution 5-year + age 60**; **non-qualified earnings under 5-year taxable + penalty**; **earnings pre-59ВҪ taxable + 10% penalty**; **disability qualifies distribution under age**; **post-death beneficiary qualifies distribution**; **first home purchase qualifies under $10K cap**; **first home cumulative $10K exhausts lifetime cap**; **conversion principal within 5-year 10% penalty**; **conversion principal after 5-year no penalty**; **contribution principal always tax-free penalty-free**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 408A(a)-(f) + В§ 408 + В§ 219 + В§ 72(t) + В§ 401(a)(9) + В§ 1411 + В§ 415 + В§ 4973 + Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997 В§ 302 + Pub. L. 105-34 + August 5, 1997 + SECURE Act of 2019 В§ 401 + SECURE Act 2.0 of 2022 + IRS Notice 2025-77 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.408A-1 to -10 + Form 5498); **note pins 2026 limits**; **note pins 2026 phase-out thresholds**; **note pins MAGI disregards conversion income**; **note pins qualified distribution two-prong**; **note pins В§ 408A(d)(3) ordering rules**; **note pins В§ 408A(d)(3)(A) conversion 5-year per-conversion**; **note pins В§ 408A(e) backdoor Roth pro-rata**; **note pins В§ 408A(c)(5) no RMD during lifetime**; **note pins В§ 1411 NIIT exempt**; **note pins trader-critical fact patterns four**; **note pins Taxpayer Relief Act 1997 origin**; **note pins В§ 4973 excess contribution excise tax**; **filing status truth table four cells**; **MFJ uniquely highest phase-out invariant**; **MFS uniquely zero low threshold invariant**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_514` is the **IRC В§ 514 вҖ” Unrelated Debt-Financed Income module** (iter 506) вҖ” direct tax-exempt-trading-entity companion to `section_511` (UBTI tax imposition at corporate or trust rates), `section_512` (UBTI computation including В§ 512(b)(1)-(5) investment-income exclusions + В§ 512(c) partnership look-through), `section_513` (unrelated trade or business definition), `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” separate regime for private foundation investment income), `section_408` (IRA rules), `section_401a9` (RMD coordination), `section_4944` (PF jeopardy investment regime). Originally enacted by Tax Reform Act of 1969 (Pub. L. 91-172) to prevent tax-exempt organizations from using their tax-exempt status to effectively guarantee leveraged investments without paying tax on the debt-financed portion. **Trader-critical because** pension funds, university endowments, foundations, churches, and IRAs face В§ 514 UBTI when invested in leveraged real estate, securities-trading partnerships, or master limited partnerships (MLPs); IRA UBTI above $1,000 annual exemption per В§ 512(b)(12) triggers Form 990-T filing by IRA custodian and tax paid from IRA assets reducing retirement savings; В§ 514(c)(9) qualified real property exception is critical planning tool for university endowments and qualified pension trusts investing in leveraged real estate partnerships.

**Six exempt-entity types**: Section501c3PublicCharity (corporate rate per В§ 511(a)), Section501c3PrivateFoundation (corporate rate + В§ 4940 NII excise separate regime), Section401aQualifiedTrust (trust rate per В§ 511(b) + В§ 514(c)(9) eligible), IndividualRetirementAccount (trust rate + NOT В§ 514(c)(9) eligible), Section501c25TitleHoldingCompany (corporate rate + В§ 514(c)(9) eligible), NotTaxExempt (В§ 514 inapplicable).

**Five property types**: RealEstate, SecuritiesWithMargin, PartnershipInterestLeveraged (В§ 512(c) partnership look-through), SubstantiallyExemptFunctionUse (В§ 514(b)(1)(A) exclusion вҖ” university-owned dormitory + mortgaged hospital), NoDebtFinancedProperty.

**В§ 514(a) debt/basis percentage formula**: average acquisition indebtedness Г· average adjusted basis (capped at 100% / 10,000 bps). UBTI = gross income Г— debt/basis percentage; allocable deductions = allocable expenses Г— debt/basis percentage.

**В§ 514(c)(9) qualified real property exception**: real property acquired by qualified organization (educational institution, qualified pension trust, В§ 501(c)(25) title-holding company, В§ 403(b)(9) retirement income account) is NOT debt-financed property if (A) price fixed at closing, (B) no debt held by seller or related person, (C) В§ 514(c)(9)(E) fractions rule satisfied. IRAs are NOT qualified organizations for this exception.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotTaxExemptNoUbti, NoDebtFinancedPropertyNoUbti, ExemptFunctionUseExcluded (В§ 514(b)(1)(A) substantial-use exclusion), QualifiedRealPropertyExceptionApplies (В§ 514(c)(9)), DebtFinancedUbtiSubjectToCorporateRate (В§ 501(c) orgs taxed at В§ 511(a) corporate rate), DebtFinancedUbtiSubjectToTrustRate (В§ 401(a) qualified trusts + IRAs taxed at В§ 511(b) trust rate).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-514`. Thirty tests pin: **not tax-exempt no UBTI** (В§ 163(d) investment interest deduction note); **no debt no UBTI** (В§ 512(b)(1)-(5) investment income exclusion); **no debt-financed property no UBTI**; **substantially exempt function use excluded** (В§ 514(b)(1)(A)); **qualified real property exception applies** (В§ 514(c)(9) + fractions rule); **qualified real property without fractions rule still UBTI** (В§ 514(c)(9)(E) failure); **IRA with leveraged securities subject to trust rate** (Form 990-T + $1,000 В§ 512(b)(12) exemption + IRA custodian filing); **pension trust with leveraged securities subject to trust rate**; **public charity subject to corporate rate**; **private foundation subject to corporate rate**; **debt/basis percentage 50% pinned**; **debt/basis percentage capped at 100%** (boundary cliff); **debt/basis percentage zero when basis zero** (division by zero defense); **gross UBTI correctly computed at 50%**; **allocable deductions correctly computed at 50%**; **net UBTI equals gross minus deductions**; **tax owed correctly computed at trust rate**; **tax owed correctly computed at corporate rate**; **action references Form 990-T + В§ 6012(a)(2)**; **coordination note references В§ 511 + В§ 512 + В§ 513 + В§ 4940**; **citation pins В§ 514(a)-(c) + В§ 511 + В§ 512 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.514(b)-1 + В§ 1.514(c)-1**; **В§ 514 introduced year pins 1969**; **Tax Reform Act 1969 Pub. L. pins 91-172**; **realistic university endowment leveraged real estate qualified no UBTI**; **realistic pension fund leveraged partnership UBTI pinned**; **extreme value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero income zero UBTI**; **deductions exceed gross saturating no negative UBTI**; **title-holding company qualified real property exception** (В§ 501(c)(25)); **IRA not eligible for В§ 514(c)(9) exception**.

`traderview-expense::section_961` is the **IRC В§ 961 вҖ” Adjustments to Basis of Stock in Controlled Foreign Corporations and Other Property module** (iter 522) вҖ” direct CFC-stock-basis-tracking companion to `section_959` (PTEP вҖ” iter 512 вҖ” sixteen-basket framework + В§ 961 basis adjustments coordinate to prevent double tax), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC вҖ” iter 520), `section_951` (Subpart F inclusion mechanism), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (CFC US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_962` (individual election вҖ” iter 510), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD вҖ” iter 502), `section_965` (transition tax вҖ” iter 514), `section_301` (corporate distribution framework вҖ” В§ 301(c)(3) excess basis = capital gain), `section_332` (parent-subsidiary liquidation вҖ” Notice 2024-16 carryover), `section_368` (corporate reorganization вҖ” Notice 2024-16 carryover). В§ 961 establishes the stock-basis tracking mechanism that prevents double US taxation of CFC earnings.

**В§ 961(a) basis INCREASE**: US shareholder's basis in CFC stock increased by PTEP inclusion under В§ 951(a) Subpart F or В§ 951A GILTI / NCTI or В§ 956 US-property investment. Shields future gain when undistributed PTEP exists within the CFC.

**В§ 961(b)(1) basis DECREASE**: US shareholder's basis in CFC stock decreased on actual distribution of PTEP under В§ 959(a) (excluded from gross income) by amount of the distribution.

**В§ 961(b)(2) BASIS-FLOOR GAIN**: distribution of PTEP that exceeds US shareholder's basis in CFC stock RECOGNIZED as В§ 301(c)(3) capital gain to extent of excess. Prevents negative basis from forming.

**В§ 961(c) INDIRECTLY OWNED CFC BASIS**: parallel basis adjustments apply to lower-tier CFC stock held INDIRECTLY through other CFCs вҖ” limited to determining В§ 951 inclusion amounts only (not for gain / loss recognition on disposition of intermediate stock).

**Notice 2024-16** (announced January 16, 2024): in certain qualifying inbound non-recognition transactions (В§ 332 liquidation + В§ 368(a)(1) asset reorg + В§ 351 transfer) where domestic acquiring corporation receives CFC stock from another domestic corporation, В§ 961(c) basis of the acquired CFC CARRIES OVER to the domestic acquiring corporation вҖ” prevents trapped-PTEP situations where domestic acquiror would otherwise lose В§ 961(c) basis on inbound transfer and face gain on subsequent PTEP distribution under В§ 961(b)(2).

**Proposed Regulations REG-105479-18** (signed November 29, 2024 / published December 2, 2024 in Federal Register) implement В§ 959 sixteen-basket PTEP framework + В§ 961 basis tracking + В§ 962 election + currency translation + S corp PTEP + consolidated group PTEP + anti-avoidance rules.

**Six basis adjustment events**: PtepInclusionSubpartF (В§ 961(a) increase), PtepInclusionGiltiOrNcti (В§ 961(a) increase), PtepInclusionSection956 (В§ 961(a) increase), ActualPtepDistribution (В§ 961(b)(1) decrease + В§ 961(b)(2) basis-floor gain when distribution exceeds basis), Section332InboundLiquidation (Notice 2024-16 carryover), Section368AssetReorganization (Notice 2024-16 carryover).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, BasisIncreaseUnderSection961a, BasisDecreaseUnderSection961b, BasisFloorExcessDistributionGainSection961b2, InboundNonrecognitionCarryoverUnderNotice2024_16, IndirectlyOwnedSection961cLimitedToSection951Inclusion.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-961`. Twenty-one tests pin: **PTEP Subpart F inclusion increases basis** ($100M pre + $10M inclusion вҶ’ $110M post); **GILTI inclusion increases basis**; **В§ 956 inclusion increases basis**; **PTEP distribution within basis decreases basis** ($100M pre + $40M distribution вҶ’ $60M post + no gain); **PTEP distribution at basis zero no gain** (boundary); **PTEP distribution exceeds basis recognizes gain** ($100M pre + $150M distribution вҶ’ $0 basis + $50M В§ 301(c)(3) capital gain on Schedule D); **indirectly owned В§ 961(c) limited to В§ 951 inclusion** (Notice 2024-16 cross-reference); **В§ 332 inbound liquidation Notice 2024-16 carryover**; **В§ 368 asset reorg Notice 2024-16 carryover**; **Notice 2024-16 date pins 2024-01-16**; **Proposed Regs REG-105479-18 date pins 2024-12-02**; **TCJA В§ 961 amendment year pins 2017**; **action references Form 5471 Schedule J + Schedule P**; **coordination note references all international siblings** (В§ 959 + В§ 960 + В§ 951 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 962 + В§ 245A + В§ 965 + В§ 301 + В§ 332 + В§ 368); **citation pins В§ 961(a) for inclusion + В§ 961(b)(1) + В§ 959(a) for distribution + В§ 961(b)(2) + В§ 301(c)(3) for excess + В§ 961(c) + Notice 2024-16 + REG-105479-18 for inbound**; **realistic corp with GILTI inclusion then distribution** ($50M basis + $30M GILTI вҶ’ $80M basis + $30M PTEP distribution вҶ’ $50M basis); **extreme value does not overflow** (saturating arithmetic); **zero event amount no change**.

`traderview-expense::section_960` is the **IRC В§ 960 вҖ” Deemed-Paid Credit for Subpart F Inclusions + GILTI / NCTI + PTEP Distributions module** (iter 520) вҖ” direct international-tax-foundation companion to `section_901` (FTC operative provision вҖ” iter 518), `section_904` (FTC limitation by basket вҖ” iter 516 вҖ” В§ 951A NCTI separate basket without carryover), `section_951` (Subpart F inclusion mechanism), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI inclusion вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (CFC US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_959` (PTEP sixteen-basket framework вҖ” iter 512 maintained within each В§ 904(d) basket), `section_962` (individual corporate-rate election вҖ” iter 510 вҖ” only mechanism for individual to access В§ 960 deemed-paid mechanism), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD вҖ” iter 502 В§ 245A(d) FTC disallowance), `section_965` (transition tax вҖ” iter 514 В§ 965(g) FTC denial 55.7%/77.1%), `section_902` (REPEALED by TCJA for foreign corp years beginning after Dec 31, 2017 вҖ” historical pooled FTC framework). В§ 960 grants domestic corporate US shareholders a DEEMED-PAID FTC for foreign taxes paid by a CFC attributable to amounts the US shareholder includes in income вҖ” treats the shareholder as if it had paid the CFC's foreign tax directly.

**В§ 960(a) Subpart F deemed-paid credit**: full creditability (no haircut) for В§ 951(a)(1) Subpart F inclusion within Subpart F basket.

**В§ 960(b) PTEP distribution credit**: foreign taxes paid in year of actual В§ 959 PTEP distribution (typically foreign withholding tax) deemed paid by US shareholder. Coordinates with В§ 959 sixteen-basket framework.

**В§ 960(c) eligibility limit**: deemed-paid mechanism applies only to domestic C corporations; individuals + S corps + partnerships ineligible UNLESS В§ 962 election in place.

**В§ 960(d) GILTI / NCTI deemed-paid credit** ("GILTI haircut"): pre-OBBBA 80% Г— inclusion percentage Г— tested foreign income taxes in tested-income group within each В§ 904 category. **OBBBA Pub. L. 119-21 (signed July 4, 2025) raises rate from 80% to 90% effective for US shareholder taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.**

**В§ 960(d)(4) PTEP distribution haircut (OBBBA)**: disallows credit for **10% of foreign income taxes** paid or accrued (including В§ 960(b)(1) deemed-paid) with respect to В§ 959(a) PTEP distribution where PTEP results from В§ 951A inclusion in US shareholder taxable year ending after June 28, 2025. **Notice 2025-77** interim guidance applies pending proposed regulations; taxpayers may rely on Section 3 of the Notice for US shareholder taxable years beginning before proposed regulations published.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.960-1 + В§ 1.960-2 + В§ 1.960-3** implement basic framework, foreign income taxes deemed paid, and PTEP В§ 960(b) credit. **Proposed Regs REG-105479-18** (November 29, 2024 published December 2, 2024) modify and replace prior regs to address PTEP sixteen-basket framework under Notice 2019-01.

**Three shareholder types**: DomesticCCorporation (full eligibility), IndividualWithSection962Election (eligible вҖ” В§ 962 unlocks В§ 960 mechanism), IndividualWithoutSection962Election (ineligible вҖ” В§ 901 direct-paid credit only).

**Three inclusion types**: SubpartFSection951a (В§ 960(a) full creditability), GiltiOrNctiSection951A (В§ 960(d) 80%/90% haircut), Section959aPtepDistribution (В§ 960(b) credit + В§ 960(d)(4) 10% haircut if PTEP from В§ 951A post-June 28, 2025).

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotEligibleNoSection962Election, SubpartFFullDeemedPaidCredit, GiltiNcti80PctPreObbba, GiltiNcti90PctPostObbba, PtepDistributionFullCredit, PtepDistributionWithGiltiHaircutOpbba.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-960`. Twenty-four tests pin: **Subpart F full deemed-paid credit** (10,000 bps + no haircut); **individual without В§ 962 election not eligible** (В§ 962 cross-reference); **individual with В§ 962 election eligible**; **GILTI pre-OBBBA 80% credit** (200K haircut on $1M proportional taxes); **GILTI post-OBBBA 90% credit** (100K haircut); **GILTI 2025 still pre-OBBBA 80%** (boundary year before 2026); **PTEP distribution full credit when not from GILTI**; **PTEP distribution from GILTI post-OBBBA haircut** (Notice 2025-77 + June 28, 2025); **PTEP distribution from GILTI pre-OBBBA no haircut**; **inclusion percentage 50% halves proportional taxes**; **inclusion percentage over 100% capped**; **pre-OBBBA GILTI haircut pins 80%**; **post-OBBBA GILTI haircut pins 90%**; **OBBBA effective year pins 2026**; **В§ 960(d)(4) PTEP haircut pins 10%**; **В§ 960(d)(4) effective date pins 2025-06-28**; **Notice 2025-77 constant pinned**; **action references Form 1118 + Form 1116**; **note pins GILTI haircut terminology + Inclusion percentage formula + Pub. L. 119-21**; **coordination note references all international siblings** (В§ 901 + В§ 904 + В§ 951 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 959 + В§ 962 + В§ 245A + В§ 965 + В§ 902); **citation pins В§ 960(a)-(d)(4) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.960-2 + Pub. L. 115-97 + Pub. L. 119-21 + Notice 2025-77**; **realistic corp with GILTI 75% inclusion pre-OBBBA** ($10M foreign tax Г— 75% Г— 80% = $6M credit); **extreme value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero foreign taxes zero credit**.

`traderview-expense::section_901` is the **IRC В§ 901 вҖ” Taxes of Foreign Countries and of Possessions of United States / Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) operative provision module** (iter 518) вҖ” direct international-tax-foundation companion to `section_904` (FTC limitation вҖ” iter 516 вҖ” separate baskets + 1-year carryback + 10-year carryforward except GILTI/NCTI), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC for CFC inclusions вҖ” pre-OBBBA 80% / post-OBBBA 90%), `section_903` (in-lieu-of tax вҖ” extends В§ 901 creditability), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD вҖ” iter 502 вҖ” В§ 245A(d) DISALLOWS FTC for foreign tax on DRD-eligible dividend), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (CFC US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_959` (PTEP вҖ” iter 512), `section_962` (individual corporate-rate election вҖ” iter 510), `section_965` (transition tax В§ 965(g) FTC denial percentage вҖ” iter 514), `section_164` (foreign-tax deduction alternative to credit under В§ 164(a)(3)). В§ 901 allows CREDIT (dollar-for-dollar US tax reduction) rather than deduction for foreign income, war profits, and excess profits taxes paid or accrued; В§ 904 caps the credit.

**В§ 901(a)-(b) creditability framework**: domestic corp + US citizen + resident alien individuals + В§ 901(b)(1) 10%-or-more foreign corp shareholder via В§ 960 deemed-paid mechanism.

**В§ 901(j) sanctioned-country disallowance**: foreign taxes imposed by sanctioned countries (Iran + North Korea + Syria + Cuba + Sudan partial + other state sponsors of terrorism per Secretary of State designation per В§ 901(j)(2)(A)) are NON-CREDITABLE; computed in separate В§ 904(d) basket. В§ 901(j)(5) treaty-resourcing carve-out may permit partial creditability.

**В§ 901(k) dividend holding-period requirement**: foreign taxes withheld on dividends NOT creditable unless taxpayer holds stock for at least **16 days within the 31-day window** beginning 15 days before ex-dividend date (common stock + preferred-short-period stock). For preferred-stock dividends attributable to periods exceeding **366 days**, the holding-period requirement is **46 days within the 91-day window** beginning 45 days before ex-dividend date per В§ 901(k)(3). Rule prevents FTC arbitrage via short-dated stock purchases around ex-dividend dates.

**В§ 901(l)**: holding-period requirement for non-dividend income.

**В§ 901(m) covered asset acquisition (CAA) FTC disallowance**: enacted by Education Jobs and Medicaid Assistance Act of 2010 (Pub. L. 111-226); disqualifies foreign taxes attributable to the "disqualified portion" of a CAA = (US basis step-up / total tax basis) Г— foreign tax. Covers qualified stock purchase + В§ 338 election + В§ 754 partnership basis adjustment + similar acquisitions resulting in stepped-up basis for US tax purposes but NOT for foreign tax purposes. Notice 2014-44 + Notice 2014-45 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.901(m)-1 through В§ 1.901(m)-8 (proposed) implement.

**В§ 901(b)(5) US-possessions analogous credit**: Puerto Rico + US Virgin Islands + Guam + American Samoa + Northern Mariana Islands.

**Six classifications of foreign tax**: CreditableForeignIncomeTax (В§ 901(b)), CreditableInLieuOfTax (В§ 903), NonCreditableSanctionedCountry (В§ 901(j)), NonCreditableDividendHoldingPeriodNotMet (В§ 901(k)), NonCreditableCoveredAssetAcquisition (В§ 901(m) вҖ” partial), NotCreditableOther (VAT + customs duties + gross-receipts taxes + penalties + interest вҖ” deductible under В§ 164(a)(3)).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, FullyCreditable, PartiallyCreditableCaaDisqualifiedPortion, NonCreditableSanctionedCountryFull, NonCreditableHoldingPeriodFailed, NonCreditableNonIncomeTax.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-901`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **fully creditable foreign income tax**; **in-lieu-of tax also creditable** (В§ 903); **sanctioned country non-creditable** (Iran + North Korea + В§ 901(j)(2)(A)); **non-income tax not creditable but deductible** (В§ 164(a)(3) VAT alternative); **common stock holding period failed non-creditable** (16-day requirement + 31-day window); **common stock holding period satisfied at 16 days creditable** (boundary); **preferred long period holding period required 46 days** (91-day window); **preferred short period uses 16-day rule**; **covered asset acquisition partial creditability** (US basis step-up / total basis Г— foreign tax = disqualified portion); **CAA with full step-up fully disqualifies**; **CAA with zero step-up fully creditable**; **CAA zero total basis does not panic** (division-by-zero defense); **В§ 901(k) common holding pins 16 days**; **В§ 901(k) common window pins 31 days**; **В§ 901(k) preferred long holding pins 46 days**; **В§ 901(k) preferred long window pins 91 days**; **В§ 901(k) preferred threshold pins 366 days**; **В§ 901(m) enacted year pins 2010 + Pub. L. 111-226**; **action references Form 1116 + Form 1118**; **coordination note references В§ 904 + В§ 960 + В§ 245A + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 959 + В§ 962 + В§ 965**; **citation pins В§ 901(a)-(b) + В§ 904 for creditable + В§ 901(j) for sanctioned + В§ 901(k) for holding period + В§ 901(m) for CAA**; **realistic individual foreign dividend holding failed** (12-day hold + $50K foreign tax вҶ’ deduction-only); **extreme value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero foreign tax zero credit** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_903` is the **IRC В§ 903 вҖ” In-Lieu-Of Tax Creditability module** (iter 528) вҖ” direct international-tax-foundation companion to `section_901` (general FTC framework вҖ” iter 515; В§ 901(b) net-basis branch coordinates with В§ 903 in-lieu-of branch), `section_904` (FTC basket limitations вҖ” iter 516; creditable В§ 903 tax remains subject to В§ 904(d) baskets and В§ 904(c) carryover), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC for CFC inclusions вҖ” В§ 951A withholding tax at CFC level often В§ 903 in-lieu-of), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD; В§ 245A(d) disallows FTC parallel logic). В§ 903 extends В§ 901 FTC creditability to foreign taxes that are NOT a generally-imposed foreign income/war-profits/excess-profits tax but **substitute for** such a tax вҖ” the "in-lieu-of" branch. Classic application: foreign withholding tax on services + royalties + interest where the foreign jurisdiction waives its generally-imposed net-income tax in favor of a gross-basis withholding levy.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(2) SUBSTITUTION TEST**: in-lieu-of tax must substitute for an income tax that **would otherwise be imposed** on the same taxpayer/income. Additive levies that supplement (rather than replace) the underlying income tax fail substitution.

**Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(1)(iii) SOAK-UP RULE**: foreign tax whose liability is **conditioned on availability of US FTC** (only payable to the extent FTC is allowed) is non-creditable вҖ” disqualifying ahead of all other tests because the soak-up structure absorbs the credit it claims to enable.

**TD 9959 FINAL REGULATIONS** (effective January 4, 2022) added the "attribution requirement" to В§ 903: the generally-imposed income tax that the levy substitutes for must independently satisfy sourcing-nexus attribution rules.

**Notice 2023-55** (July 21, 2023) deferred the attribution requirement for tax years ending on or before December 31, 2023. **Notice 2025-23** (per practitioner reporting) extended the deferral through tax years ending in 2024 and 2025.

**Three year regimes**: PreOrCalendar2023NoticeDeferralActive (Notice 2023-55), Calendar2024Or2025NoticeDeferralActive (Notice 2025-23 extension), PostCalendar2025AttributionRequiredFully (full TD 9959).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (В§ 901 net-basis branch), CreditableUnderInLieuOfBranchAttributionDeferred (deferral year + substitution + soak-up passed), CreditableUnderInLieuOfBranchAttributionMet (post-deferral year + attribution satisfied), NonCreditableSoakUpTaxRule (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(1)(iii)), NonCreditableFailsSubstitution (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(2)), NonCreditableFailsAttributionPostDeferral.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-903`. Twenty-two tests pin: **net-basis В§ 901 branch returns not applicable**; **soak-up tax non-creditable regardless of other facts** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(1)(iii)); **additive levy fails substitution test** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(2)); **no generally-imposed income tax fails substitution**; **pre-2024 deferral grants credit without attribution test** (Notice 2023-55); **calendar 2024 / 2025 deferral grants credit without attribution test** (Notice 2025-23); **post-2025 attribution satisfied grants credit**; **post-2025 attribution fails disallows credit**; **soak-up takes priority over substitution failure** (severity ladder ordering); **В§ 901 branch overrides soak-up substitution and attribution** (severity ladder ordering); **post-2025 inconsistent input section-901 attribution with section-903 levy**; **substitution failure note pins Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(2)**; **soak-up failure note pins Treas. Reg. В§ 1.903-1(c)(1)(iii)**; **post-2025 attribution-fail note describes gross-basis withholding trap**; **zero foreign tax paid creditable amount is zero when substitution met** (boundary); **very large foreign tax no overflow in disallowed amount** (u64::MAX defense); **note pins В§ 904 limitation for creditable outputs**; **note pins В§ 164(a)(3) deduction alternative for disallowed**; **TD 9959 pinned in post-deferral attribution-satisfied note**; **Notice 2025-23 pinned in 2024 / 2025 deferral note**; **Notice 2023-55 pinned in pre-2024 deferral note + Dec 31 2023 effective date**; **pre-2024 deferral disallowance zero when creditable** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_904` is the **IRC В§ 904 вҖ” Limitation on Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) module** (iter 516) вҖ” direct international-tax-foundation companion to `section_901` (general FTC computation framework), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC for CFC inclusions вҖ” pre-OBBBA 80% / post-OBBBA 90% rate), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500 вҖ” feeds GILTI basket), `section_956` (CFC US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_959` (PTEP sixteen-basket framework вҖ” iter 512 вҖ” PTEP groups maintained WITHIN each В§ 904(d) basket), `section_962` (individual corporate-rate election вҖ” iter 510 вҖ” unlocks В§ 960 FTC for individuals), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD вҖ” iter 502; В§ 245A(d) DISALLOWS FTC for foreign tax on DRD-eligible dividend), `section_965` (transition tax вҖ” iter 514; В§ 965(g) FTC denial percentage 55.7% / 77.1%), `section_59a` (BEAT separate regime), `section_864e` (expense allocation rules). В§ 904 caps FTC at the US tax that would otherwise be imposed on foreign-source taxable income вҖ” prevents FTC from offsetting US tax on US-source income.

**В§ 904(a) FTC limitation formula**: FTC вүӨ US tax Г— (foreign-source TI / total worldwide TI).

**В§ 904(d) SEVEN POST-TCJA BASKETS** (effective for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2017): (1) Passive (dividends + interest + royalties + rents + annuities вҖ” with high-tax kick-out and CFC look-through exceptions); (2) GILTI / NCTI (post-OBBBA renamed); (3) Foreign Branch (qualified business unit income); (4) General (active business + wages + financial services + everything else); (5) Treaty-Resourced (В§ 904(d)(6) вҖ” income re-sourced under treaty tie-breaker); (6) В§ 901(j) sanctioned-country income (Iran + North Korea + Syria + Cuba + Sudan partial вҖ” generally NON-CREDITABLE per В§ 901(j)(1)); (7) Lump-Sum Distribution from foreign pension.

**В§ 904(c) CARRYOVER RULES**: 1-year carryback + 10-year carryforward within SAME basket вҖ” but В§ 951A GILTI / NCTI basket EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from carryover per В§ 904(c)(1) flush language (В§ 951A excess credits expire annually).

**В§ 904(f) OFL recapture**: prior-year foreign-source losses in basket allocated against US-source income; later years foreign-source income recharacterized as US-source until OFL fully recaptured. Form 1118 Schedule J tracking.

**В§ 904(g) ODL recapture**: parallel for domestic losses вҖ” foreign-source income REDUCED by ODL recapture amount, INCREASING FTC limitation.

**OBBBA В§ 904 changes** (Pub. L. 119-21, effective for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025): (1) interest and R&D expense no longer allocated to В§ 951A NCTI basket per В§ 864(e) clarification, preserving more foreign-source income for FTC; (2) В§ 960(d) deemed-paid FTC rate rises from 80% to 90% (10% haircut down from 20%).

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, FullyCreditedNoExcess, PartiallyCreditedExcessCarriedForward (general baskets вҖ” 1-year carryback + 10-year carryforward), GiltiNctiExcessExpired (В§ 951A basket вҖ” no carryover), OverallForeignLossRecaptureTriggered, OverallDomesticLossRecaptureTriggered, Section901jSanctionedNonCreditable.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-904`. Twenty-five tests pin: **FTC limitation correctly computed** (US tax Г— FS TI / WW TI formula); **full creditability when under limit**; **excess FTC when over limit general basket carries** (1-year + 10-year split); **GILTI / NCTI excess expires no carryover** (В§ 904(c)(1) flush language); **В§ 901(j) sanctioned non-creditable** (Iran + North Korea + Syria + Cuba + Sudan); **OFL recapture triggers severity** (В§ 904(f) + Form 1118 Schedule J); **ODL recapture triggers severity** (В§ 904(g)); **prior year carryforward increases creditable pool**; **zero worldwide TI not applicable**; **passive basket compliant with carryover**; **foreign branch basket compliant with carryover**; **treaty-resourced basket recognized**; **lump-sum distribution basket recognized**; **FTC carryback years pins 1**; **FTC carryforward years pins 10**; **TCJA post-2017 baskets count pins 7**; **OBBBA effective year pins 2026**; **action references Form 1116 + Form 1118**; **note pins 7 baskets + OBBBA + В§ 864(e) + 80% вҶ’ 90% FTC rate**; **coordination note references В§ 901 + В§ 960 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 959 + В§ 962 + В§ 245A + В§ 965**; **citation pins В§ 904(a)-(j) + В§ 901 + В§ 960 + Pub. L. 115-97 + Pub. L. 119-21**; **realistic corp with passive basket carryforward** ($50M WW TI + $5M FS TI + $2M foreign tax вҶ’ $1.05M limit + $950K excess); **extreme value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero foreign tax zero credit used**; **FTC capped at limitation not at total creditable** (boundary).

`traderview-expense::section_965` is the **IRC В§ 965 вҖ” Transition Tax / Mandatory Repatriation Tax (MRT) module** (iter 514) вҖ” direct trader/fund-manager international tax companion to `section_959` (PTEP вҖ” iter 512 вҖ” В§ 965 inclusion creates В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP, one of the seven В§ 959(c)(2) groups in Notice 2019-01 sixteen-basket framework), `section_951` (Subpart F inclusion mechanism), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (US property investment вҖ” iter 504), `section_962` (individual corporate-rate election вҖ” iter 510), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD pathway вҖ” iter 502; NOTE: В§ 245A inapplicable to В§ 965 inclusion since pre-TCJA E&P), `section_904` (FTC limitation baskets вҖ” В§ 965 has separate В§ 904(d) basket), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC вҖ” only 55.7% / 77.1% creditable via В§ 965(g) FTC denial percentage). Enacted by Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14103) as ONE-TIME deemed-repatriation tax on accumulated post-1986 deferred foreign earnings of CFCs and 10%-or-greater foreign corporations (Specified Foreign Corporations вҖ” SFCs). Applies in the LAST taxable year of the SFC beginning before January 1, 2018. **Trader-critical because** many trader-individuals who held CFC stock through TCJA transition still paying installment payments under В§ 965(h) 8-year election through 2025 (year 8 at 25%); S corp shareholders may still be in В§ 965(i) deferral pending triggering event; REITs may still be in В§ 965(m) ratable 8-year spread.

**В§ 965(a)(2) measurement**: greater of November 2, 2017 (TCJA passage date) or December 31, 2017 (end of pre-TCJA tax year).

**В§ 965(c) rate differentiation**: 15.5% effective on cash-position portion via В§ 965(c) deduction + 8% effective on non-cash portion. Cash position per В§ 965(c)(3) = cash + net AR + actively traded property held for investment + short-term obligations at three measurement dates.

**В§ 965(b) E&P deficit netting**: aggregate inclusion REDUCED by US shareholder's aggregate E&P deficit of other SFCs.

**В§ 965(h) 8-year installment election**: 8% years 1-5 + 15% year 6 + 20% year 7 + 25% year 8 = 100% schedule. Annual interest at В§ 6601 underpayment rate. Acceleration on disposition of substantially all assets, liquidation, or cessation of US shareholder status per В§ 965(h)(3).

**В§ 965(i) S corp deferral**: liability deferred until triggering event (S corp ceases to be S corp + asset sale + liquidation + stock transfer); may further elect В§ 965(h) 8-year installment from triggering event date.

**В§ 965(m) REIT election**: ratable 8-year inclusion (1/8 per year) coordinating with В§ 857 90% distribution requirement.

**Moore v. United States, 602 U.S. ___ (June 20, 2024)**: Supreme Court 7-2 decision authored by Justice Kavanaugh UPHELD constitutionality of В§ 965 under Sixteenth Amendment. Holding NARROW: limited to entities treated as pass-throughs; does NOT decide whether realization is constitutional requirement; does NOT address wealth taxes, mark-to-market taxes, or taxes on appreciation.

**Five shareholder types**: DomesticCCorporation, USIndividualEstateTrust, SCorporationElectingDeferral (В§ 965(i) election available), RealEstateInvestmentTrust (В§ 965(m) election available), LessThan10PctNotUsShareholder (В§ 965 inapplicable).

**Four payment elections**: SingleYearFull (default), EightYearInstallment (В§ 965(h)), SCorpDeferralUntilTriggeringEvent (В§ 965(i)), ReitRatableInclusionEightYear (В§ 965(m)).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotUsShareholderNoInclusion, SingleYearPaymentFull, EightYearInstallmentScheduleAdopted, SCorpDeferralActive, ReitRatableSpreadActive.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-965`. Thirty-one tests pin: **less-than-10% not US shareholder**; **single-year payment full path**; **cash position taxed at 15.5%** ($60M cash вҶ’ $9.3M tax); **non-cash portion taxed at 8%** ($40M non-cash вҶ’ $3.2M tax); **total transition tax correctly summed**; **E&P deficit reduces aggregate inclusion** (В§ 965(b)); **E&P deficit exceeding inclusion saturates at zero**; **installment year 1 pins 8%**; **installment year 5 pins 8%**; **installment year 6 pins 15%**; **installment year 7 pins 20%**; **installment year 8 pins 25%**; **installment schedule sums to 100%**; **S corp deferral no triggering event active** (В§ 965(i)); **S corp deferral with triggering event full payment**; **REIT 8-year ratable spread active** (В§ 965(m) + В§ 857 coordination); **cash rate pins 15.5%**; **non-cash rate pins 8%**; **installment years pins 8**; **Moore decision date pins 2024-06-20**; **measurement date 1 pins 2017-11-02**; **measurement date 2 pins 2017-12-31**; **last inclusion year before pins 2018**; **TCJA Pub. L. pins 115-97**; **note pins Moore v. United States decision** (Kavanaugh + Sixteenth Amendment); **note pins В§ 959 PTEP creation** + sixteen-basket framework cross-reference; **citation pins Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14103 + Moore v. United States**; **action references Form 5471 + Form 965 + Transition Tax Statement**; **cash position portion capped at net inclusion** (boundary cliff); **extreme inclusion does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero inclusion zero tax**.

`traderview-expense::section_959` is the **IRC В§ 959 вҖ” Exclusion from Gross Income of Previously-Taxed Earnings and Profits (PTEP) module** (iter 512) вҖ” direct international-tax companion to `section_951` (Subpart F inclusion creating В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI inclusion вҖ” iter 500 creating В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP), `section_956` (US property investment creating В§ 959(c)(1) PTEP вҖ” iter 504; reclassification of В§ 959(c)(2) to В§ 959(c)(1) when CFC invests existing PTEP in US property), `section_962` (iter 510 вҖ” В§ 962 E&P + В§ 962(d) actual distribution rule), `section_245a` (iter 502 вҖ” DRD pathway for non-PTEP foreign-source portion), `section_961` (basis adjustments coordinating with PTEP вҖ” В§ 961(a) basis increase on inclusion + В§ 961(b) basis decrease on PTEP distribution), `section_965` (transition tax creating В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP), `section_904` (FTC limitation baskets вҖ” sixteen PTEP groups maintained within each В§ 904(d) basket). В§ 959 prevents double US taxation of CFC earnings already included in US shareholder gross income вҖ” when CFC later actually distributes those amounts, В§ 959(a)(1) EXCLUDES the distribution from gross income.

**В§ 959(c) three-category distribution ordering**: В§ 959(c)(1) PTEP (US property investment-related вҖ” first to be distributed); В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP (Subpart F + GILTI/NCTI вҖ” second); В§ 959(c)(3) non-PTEP (untaxed E&P вҖ” third вҖ” triggers fully taxable dividend OR В§ 245A 100% DRD if domestic C corp recipient + foreign-source-portion + В§ 246(c) holding period > 365 days in 731-day window).

**Notice 2019-01 (December 14, 2018) sixteen-basket framework**: nine В§ 959(c)(1) groups (including В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP reclassified to В§ 959(c)(1) upon US property investment) plus seven В§ 959(c)(2) groups (Subpart F + В§ 965 transition tax + GILTI / NCTI В§ 951A) вҖ” maintained within each В§ 904(d) FTC category.

**Proposed Regulations REG-105479-18** (signed November 29, 2024, published in Federal Register December 2, 2024) implement Notice 2019-01 sixteen-basket framework plus shareholder-level and foreign-corporation-level accounting rules.

**Notice 2024-16** announces forthcoming proposed regs on В§ 961(c) basis in В§ 332 liquidations and В§ 368(a)(1) asset reorganizations.

**В§ 961 basis adjustments**: В§ 961(a) basis INCREASE on PTEP inclusion in prior year + В§ 961(b) basis DECREASE on PTEP distribution вҖ” ensures deferred-but-eventual taxation of CFC earnings does not cause double tax via subsequent stock sale gain recognition.

**Three distribution recipients**: UsShareholder (10%+ direct/indirect вҖ” В§ 959(a)(1) exclusion available), LessThan10PctNotUsShareholder (no prior inclusions, no PTEP вҖ” full distribution taxable dividend potentially qualifying for В§ 1(h)(11) qualified dividend rate), ForeignPerson (В§ 959 inapplicable вҖ” chapter 3 withholding regime).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotUsShareholderNoExclusion, FullyAttributableToSection959C1PtepExcluded (distribution entirely from US property PTEP), FullyAttributableToSection959C2PtepExcluded (distribution entirely from Subpart F + GILTI PTEP вҖ” possibly with C1 portion also distributed), PartiallyAttributableNonPtepTaxableRemainder (PTEP exhausted; remainder taxable dividend), FullyAttributableToNonPtepDividend (no PTEP вҖ” full taxable dividend).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-959`. Twenty-four tests pin: **less-than-10% shareholder no exclusion** (В§ 1(h)(11) qualified dividend cross-reference); **foreign person no В§ 959 exclusion** (chapter 3 withholding); **distribution first attributed to В§ 959(c)(1)** (ordering rule); **distribution exceeds C1 then attributed to C2**; **distribution exceeds all PTEP remainder taxable** ($100M вҶ’ $30M C1 + $50M C2 + $20M C3 taxable); **distribution with no PTEP fully taxable dividend**; **В§ 961(b) basis decrease equals total excluded**; **remaining balances correctly decremented**; **distribution exceeds total E&P distribution capped** ($500M вҶ’ $180M total); **PTEP total baskets pins 16**; **PTEP C1 baskets pins 9**; **PTEP C2 baskets pins 7**; **Notice 2019-01 date pins 2018-12-14**; **proposed regs date pins 2024-11-29**; **action references Form 5471 Schedule J + Schedule P**; **action references sixteen-basket framework + Notice 2019-01 + REG-105479-18**; **action references В§ 245A DRD pathway when taxable**; **coordination note references В§ 951 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 962 + В§ 245A + В§ 961 + В§ 965 + В§ 904**; **citation pins В§ 959(a)-(d) + В§ 961 + Notice 2019-01 + REG-105479-18 + Notice 2024-16**; **zero distribution zero movement**; **extreme distribution does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **no distribution no basis decrease**; **realistic $50M distribution $30M C1 + $20M C2**; **C1 takes priority over C2 per ordering rule**.

`traderview-expense::section_962` is the **IRC В§ 962 вҖ” Election by Individuals to Be Subject to Tax at Corporate Rates module** (iter 510) вҖ” direct trader/fund-manager international-tax companion to `section_951` (Subpart F inclusion mechanism), `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” iter 500), `section_956` (CFC investment in US property вҖ” iter 504), `section_245a` (foreign-source DRD pathway вҖ” iter 502 вҖ” mutually exclusive with В§ 962 on actual distribution per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-5(b)(2)), `section_250` (FDII + GILTI / NCTI deduction вҖ” В§ 962 elector qualifies per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.962-2(b)), `section_960` (deemed-paid FTC вҖ” В§ 962 elector claims as if domestic corp), `section_59a` (BEAT separate regime), `section_911` (foreign earned income exclusion вҖ” distinct individual regime). Originally enacted by Revenue Act of 1962 (Pub. L. 87-834) alongside Subpart F. **Trader-critical because** individual US shareholders of CFCs вҖ” including trader-fund managers structured through offshore master-feeder partnerships, family-office holding companies, expat traders, and US-resident PE/hedge-fund partners with CFC-flowed-through inclusions вҖ” can elect annually to be taxed at 21% corporate rate instead of up to 37% individual rate on Subpart F + GILTI/NCTI + В§ 956 inclusions, plus claim В§ 250 deduction and В§ 960 FTC; В§ 962 election reduces effective US rate on GILTI to approximately 10.5% pre-OBBBA / 12.6% post-OBBBA when foreign tax rate is high enough.

**Five shareholder types**: IndividualUsShareholder (eligible), EstateOfIndividual (eligible), UsTrust (eligible), PartnershipOrSCorpEntityLevel (NOT eligible at entity level вҖ” partner/shareholder elects at individual level per Rev. Rul. 2019-10), DomesticCCorporation (inapplicable вҖ” already corporate rate).

**Three inclusion types**: SubpartFInclusion (В§ 951(a) вҖ” no В§ 250 deduction), GiltiOrNctiInclusion (В§ 951A вҖ” В§ 250 deduction 50% pre-OBBBA / 40% post-OBBBA applies), Section956Inclusion (В§ 956 вҖ” no В§ 250 deduction).

**В§ 962(b) ceiling rule**: tax under В§ 962(a) cannot exceed additional tax that would be imposed without the election.

**В§ 962(d) actual distribution rule**: when CFC later distributes В§ 962 E&P, distribution INCLUDIBLE in gross income to extent it EXCEEDS cumulative US tax PAID under В§ 962 in prior years вҖ” second layer of tax at qualified dividend / treaty preferential rate. В§ 245A 100% DRD NOT available to В§ 962 electors per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-5(b)(2) вҖ” election forecloses participation-exemption pathway.

**Election mechanics**: Treas. Reg. В§ 1.962-2(b)(1) annual election by statement attached to Form 1040; election applies to ALL CFCs of electing shareholder for that year вҖ” cannot be made piecemeal.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotEligibleShareholderType (partnership entity-level + domestic C corp), ElectionNotMadeIndividualRateApplies, ElectionMadeCurrentYearBenefit, ActualDistributionExceedsTaxPaidSecondLayerTriggered, ActualDistributionWithinTaxPaidNoSecondLayer.

Reg. В§ 1.245A-5(b)(2) + В§ 962(d)**; **action references Treas. Reg. В§ 1.962-2(b)(1) + Form 1040**; **note pins OBBBA post-2026 effective rate** (1260 bps target); **coordination note references В§ 951 + В§ 951A + В§ 956 + В§ 245A + В§ 250 + В§ 960**; **citation pins В§ 962(a)-(d) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.962-2(b) + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-5(b)(2) + Rev. Rul. 2019-10**; **В§ 962 introduced year pins 1962**; **OBBBA effective year pins 2026**; **pre-OBBBA haircut pins 20%**; **post-OBBBA haircut pins 10%**; **estate eligible shareholder**; **US trust eligible shareholder**; **extreme inclusion does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero inclusion zero tax**; **realistic individual 25K inclusion savings demonstrated**.

`traderview-expense::section_956` is the **IRC В§ 956 вҖ” Investment of CFC Earnings in United States Property module** (iter 504) вҖ” anti-deferral rule treating CFC investment in US property as constructive distribution to US shareholders. Direct international-trader-corporation companion to `section_951a` (GILTI / NCTI вҖ” В§ 956 is residual after Subpart F + GILTI / NCTI absorb most CFC income вҖ” iter 500), `section_245a` (DRD basis for В§ 956 / В§ 245A coordination rule hypothetical distribution offset вҖ” iter 502), `section_1297` (PFIC mutually exclusive with CFC), `section_959` (PTEP ordering rules вҖ” В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP > В§ 959(c)(3) non-PTEP), `section_962` (election for individual shareholder to be taxed at corporate rate AND claim coordination rule), `section_59a` (BEAT separate regime), `section_56a` (CAMT вҖ” iter 498). Originally enacted by Revenue Act of 1962 Pub. L. 87-834 as part of the Subpart F anti-deferral regime; transformed by Treasury Final Regulations under В§ 956 published May 23, 2019 (effective for CFC tax years beginning on or after July 22, 2019). **Trader-critical because** В§ 956 historically threatened "deemed dividend" inclusion when CFCs pledged stock as collateral for US parent debt (Rev. Rul. 90-112) or guaranteed US parent obligations (Notice 88-108); post-2019 В§ 245A coordination rule largely eliminates the inclusion for corporate US shareholders by symmetry with the post-TCJA participation exemption, restoring the ability of US corporate groups to use CFC stock as collateral and CFC guarantees in financing without triggering Subpart F.

**Six US-shareholder types**: DomesticCCorporation (eligible for В§ 245A coordination rule reducing В§ 956 inclusion), IndividualOrPassThroughNoSection962 (full inclusion at ordinary individual rate up to 37%), IndividualOrPassThroughWithSection962Election (corporate-rate 21% taxation + coordination rule benefit), RegulatedInvestmentCompany (full inclusion вҖ” В§ 245A unavailable), RealEstateInvestmentTrust (full inclusion вҖ” В§ 245A unavailable), LessThan10PctNotUsShareholder (no inclusion).

**Six US-property types** under В§ 956(c): NoUsProperty, TangibleUsProperty (real estate + equipment), DomesticCorporationStock, UsPersonObligationLoanOrGuarantee (including CFC stock pledge per Rev. Rul. 90-112 and CFC guarantee per Notice 88-108), UsSourceIntangibleRight, StatutoryExceptionApplies (В§ 956(c)(2) seven categories: US bank deposits, export property, shipping property, insurance reserves, certain securities, aircraft/vessels in international commerce, related-US-person working capital).

**В§ 245A coordination rule** per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.956-1(a)(2)-(4): hypothetical-distribution attributable first to В§ 959(c)(2) PTEP then to В§ 959(c)(3) non-PTEP E&P; В§ 956 inclusion reduced by amount that would qualify for В§ 245A 100% DRD if hypothetically distributed.

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotUsShareholderNoInclusion (less-than-10%), NoUsPropertyNoInclusion, StatutoryExceptionExempt (В§ 956(c)(2) exception applies), CorporateUsShareholderSection245aFullReduction (net inclusion = 0), CorporateUsShareholderSection245aPartialReduction (partial), IndividualSection956FullInclusion (no В§ 962), RicOrReitSection956FullInclusion.

Reg. Rul. 90-112 + Notice 88-108 in module documentation).

`traderview-expense::section_245a` is the **IRC В§ 245A вҖ” Deduction for Foreign Source-Portion of Dividends Received by Domestic Corporations from Specified 10-Percent Owned Foreign Corporations (SFCs) module** (iter 502) вҖ” the post-TCJA "participation exemption" / "territorial system" provision. Direct repatriation-tax-planning companion to `section_951a` (GILTI / post-OBBBA NCTI вҖ” В§ 245A applies to residual foreign-source dividend AFTER Subpart F + GILTI / NCTI inclusion вҖ” iter 500), `section_250` (FDII / GILTI / NCTI deduction вҖ” coordinates with В§ 245A through tested income exclusion), `section_1297` (PFIC mutually exclusive with SFC definition), `section_1248` (gain on CFC stock sale recharacterized as dividend post-2017 вҖ” eligible for В§ 245A DRD), `section_243` (domestic-corp DRD parallel framework вҖ” 50% / 65% / 100% by ownership tier), `section_59a` (BEAT separate base-erosion regime), `section_56a` (CAMT вҖ” В§ 245A-deductible amount excluded from CAMT AFSI per В§ 56A(c)(2)(C) вҖ” iter 498). Enacted by Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14101), effective for distributions made after December 31, 2017. **Trader-critical because** В§ 245A implements the territorial tax regime вҖ” domestic C corp repatriating post-2017 earnings of CFC / SFC via dividend distribution claims 100% DRD on foreign-source portion, eliminating residual US tax on previously-Subpart-F-included earnings; FTC denied on DRD-eligible portion per В§ 245A(d) creates permanent book-tax difference.

**Four shareholder types**: DomesticCCorporation (eligible), RealEstateInvestmentTrust (excluded), RegulatedInvestmentCompany (excluded), IndividualOrPassThrough (excluded вҖ” C-corp blocker needed).

**Three foreign-corp types**: SpecifiedTenPercentOwnedForeignCorp (SFC вҖ” В§ 245A(b) definition), PficNotCfcExcludedFromSfc (В§ 1291 excess distribution regime instead), LessThanTenPercentNotSfc (portfolio dividend with В§ 901 / В§ 904 FTC).

**В§ 246(c)(1) holding period**: > 365 days in 731-day window beginning 365 days before ex-dividend date (i.e., minimum 366-day holding period to satisfy the more-than-365-day test).

**В§ 245A(e) hybrid dividend rule**: dividend where distributing foreign corp received foreign tax deduction or similar tax benefit is recharacterized as Subpart F inclusion вҶ’ no DRD + no FTC + reduce hybrid deduction account per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A(e)-1(d).

**Eight-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, NotEligibleShareholderType (REIT/RIC/individual/pass-through), NotSpecifiedTenPercentOwnedForeignCorp (PFIC-not-CFC or < 10% ownership), PreEffectiveDate (pre-2018), HoldingPeriodNotSatisfied (< 366 days), HybridDividendRecharacterizedNoDeduction (В§ 245A(e) anti-arbitrage), PartialDeductionForeignSourcePortionOnly (US-source portion taxed), FullDeductionEligible (100% foreign-source).
