Reg. В§ 1.245A(e)-1(d)); **full foreign-source full DRD**; **partial US-source only foreign portion deductible**; **FTC disallowed pinned in action** (В§ 245A(d) + permanent book-tax difference); **action references Form 8993 + Form 1120 Schedule C**; **coordination note references В§ 951A + В§ 250 + В§ 1297 + В§ 1248 + В§ 56A + В§ 59A + В§ 243**; **citation pins Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14101 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-5**; **portion sum mismatch generates warning note** (В§ 1.245A-5 anti-abuse); **zero foreign-source zero deduction**; **extreme dividend does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero dividend zero deduction**; **boundary 2018 post-effective date**; **holding-period check takes priority over hybrid dividend**; **realistic Microsoft Ireland repatriation $500B full DRD** ($500B foreign-source dividend вҶ’ 100% DRD вҶ’ 0 taxable).

`traderview-expense::section_246` is the **IRC В§ 246 вҖ” Rules Applying to Deductions for Dividends Received module** (iter 548) вҖ” direct DRD-cluster companion to `section_243` (50% / 65% / 100% base DRD by ownership tier), `section_245a` (iter 502 вҖ” 100% participation DRD on foreign-source dividends), `section_246a` (iter 530 вҖ” DRD reduction for debt-financed portfolio stock вҖ” reaches separate disallowance dimension), `section_172` (NOL carryforward вҖ” destination for В§ 246(b)(2) NOL exception), `section_854` (RIC pass-through framework). В§ 246 sets out the general limitations on the DRD claimable under В§ 243, В§ 245(a), В§ 245A, and (indirectly) В§ 246A.

**В§ 246(c) HOLDING-PERIOD REQUIREMENT**: common stock must be held for **MORE THAN 45 days** during the **91-day** testing window beginning 45 days before the ex-dividend date. **Preferred stock** with dividends for periods EXCEEDING **366 days** must be held for **MORE THAN 90 days** during the **181-day** testing window. **В§ 246(c)(4) TOLLING**: any day with substantially diminished risk of loss (short sale, equity put, contractual hedge) does NOT count toward the holding period.

**В§ 246(b) TAXABLE-INCOME CAP**: DRD limited to 50% / 65% / 100% of taxable income before DRD, matching the В§ 243 ownership-tier DRD percentage. **В§ 246(b)(2) NOL EXCEPTION**: cap inapplicable if it would create or increase a net operating loss; resulting NOL becomes subject to В§ 172 carryforward (post-TCJA: no carryback, indefinite carryforward at 80% of taxable income).

**В§ 246(a) DRD EXCLUSIONS**: no DRD for dividends from (1) В§ 501 / В§ 521 tax-exempt corporations; (2) non-qualified RIC distributions (not В§ 854 pass-through); (3) non-capital-gain REIT distributions; (4) В§ 901(j) sanctioned-country foreign corporations; (5) debt-financed portfolio stock under В§ 246A.

**Three stock types**: CommonStockSection246cOne, PreferredStockExtendedDividendPeriodSection246cTwo, PreferredStockShortDividendPeriodTreatedAsCommon.

**Three ownership tiers**: LessThanTwentyPercent50PctDrd, TwentyToSeventyNinePercent65PctDrd, EightyPercentPlusAffiliated100PctDrdNoCap.

**Five dividend-issuer types**: DomesticCCorporation, SanctionedCountryForeignCorporation, Section501Or521TaxExemptCorporation, NonQualifiedRicDistribution, NonCapitalGainReitDistribution.

**Two NOL statuses**: NoNolYear, NolYearTaxableIncomeCapInapplicable.

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section246AHoldingPeriodFailedDrdFullyDisallowed, Section246AExcludedIssuerDrdDenied, Section246BTaxableIncomeCapAppliedReducedDrd, Section246BTwoNolExceptionFullDrdPreserved, FullDrdAllowedHoldingPeriodAndCapsMet.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-246`. Twenty-five tests pin: **full DRD allowed common stock 50% tier** ($100K Г— 50% = $50K within $500K cap); **holding period 46 days exactly fails must be more than** (boundary + В§ 246(c) more-than-45-day rule); **holding period 47 days satisfies more than 46** (boundary); **preferred stock extended period 91-day rule** (95 days within 181-day window); **preferred stock extended 91 days exactly fails** (boundary); **preferred stock short period uses common 46-day rule**; **taxable income cap reduces DRD when low TI** ($50K raw Г— 50% cap = $25K); **NOL year taxable income cap inapplicable** (В§ 246(b)(2) + В§ 172 carryforward); **20% to 80% pct tier 65% DRD**; **80% pct plus affiliated 100% DRD no cap** (В§ 243(b) + В§ 1504); **sanctioned country DRD denied** (В§ 901(j)); **В§ 501 tax-exempt DRD denied**; **non-qualified RIC distribution DRD denied** (В§ 854); **non-capital-gain REIT distribution DRD denied**; **common holding period constant pins 46 days**; **common testing window constant pins 91 days**; **preferred extended holding period constant pins 91 days**; **preferred extended testing window constant pins 181 days**; **less than 20% DRD constant pins 50%**; **20% to 80% DRD constant pins 65%**; **80% plus DRD constant pins 100%**; **very large dividend no overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero dividend zero DRD** (boundary); **note pins В§ 246(c)(4) tolling substantially diminished risk**; **note pins В§ 172 NOL carryover**.

`traderview-expense::section_246a` is the **IRC В§ 246A вҖ” DRD Reduction for Debt-Financed Portfolio Stock module** (iter 530) вҖ” direct DRD-cluster companion to `section_243` (50% / 65% / 100% base DRD by ownership tier вҖ” the underlying DRD that В§ 246A reduces), `section_245a` (iter 502 вҖ” 100% participation DRD on foreign-source dividend NOT subject to В§ 246A reduction by statutory structure and Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-3), `section_56a` (CAMT вҖ” iter 498 вҖ” DRD effect on AFSI), `section_59a` (BEAT вҖ” separate base-erosion regime). В§ 246A substitutes a reduced DRD percentage for the base В§ 243 or В§ 245(a) DRD when the corporate dividend recipient holds the underlying stock with portfolio indebtedness during the base period вҖ” reverse-engineers the DRD downward to neutralize the financing arbitrage between simultaneous interest deduction and DRD on the same economic return.

**В§ 246A(a) FORMULA**: substituted DRD % = base DRD % Г— (100% - average indebtedness %). Base DRD percentages: 50% for less than 20% ownership; 65% for 20%-up-to-50% ownership.

**В§ 246A(b) PORTFOLIO STOCK DEFINITION**: corporate shareholder owns less than 50% of issuer. 50%+ ownership is NOT portfolio stock and В§ 246A inapplicable (65% DRD preserved under В§ 243(c)).

**В§ 246A(c) AVERAGE INDEBTEDNESS PERCENTAGE**: average portfolio indebtedness during base period divided by average adjusted basis of stock during base period.

**В§ 246A(d)(3) CAP**: reduction cannot exceed interest deduction (including deductible short-sale expense) allocable to the dividend.

**В§ 246A(e) EXCEPTIONS**: (1) В§ 243(b) qualifying dividends from affiliated-group members (80% or more ownership per В§ 1504) вҖ” full 100% DRD preserved regardless of debt financing; (2) SBIC dividends under Small Business Investment Act of 1958.

**Four ownership tiers**: LessThan20Percent (50% base DRD), TwentyToFiftyPercent (65% base DRD), FiftyPercentOrMoreNotPortfolioStock, AffiliatedGroupSection243BQualifying.

**Four underlying DRD section selectors**: Section243DomesticDividend, Section245aUsSourceForeignCorpDividend, Section245aOneHundredPercentParticipationNotReduced, SbicDividendExempt.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, Section245aFullParticipationDrdPreservedNoReduction, Section243bAffiliatedQualifyingDividendPreservedNoReduction, SbicExemptionPreservesFullDrd, Section246aReductionApplied, Section246aReductionCappedByInterestDeductionAllocable, InvalidInputAverageIndebtednessExceedsOneHundred.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-246a`. Twenty-one tests pin: **invalid input average indebtedness exceeds 100 returns invalid input**; **SBIC dividend exempt preserves full DRD via В§ 246A(e)**; **В§ 245A full participation DRD not reduced by В§ 246A** (Treas. Reg. В§ 1.245A-3); **affiliated group В§ 243(b) qualifying dividend preserves full DRD** (В§ 1504); **fifty percent or more ownership not portfolio stock returns not applicable**; **standard reduction less than 20 pct owner 60 pct indebted** (50% Г— 40% = 20% substituted, $200K allowed of $1M dividend); **standard reduction 20 to 50 pct owner 40 pct indebted** (65% Г— 60% = 39% substituted, $390K allowed); **cap binds when interest deduction less than raw reduction** ($100K cap vs $300K raw вҶ’ $400K allowed); **zero indebtedness no reduction** (boundary); **one hundred percent indebtedness full reduction to zero DRD** (boundary); **base DRD less than 20 pct constant pins 50**; **base DRD 20 to 50 pct constant pins 65**; **very large dividend no overflow in u128 intermediate** (u64::MAX defense); **zero dividend zero deduction no panic** (boundary); **note pins В§ 246A(a) formula substitution**; **note pins В§ 246A(d)(3) cap when cap binds**; **note pins В§ 246(c) holding-period 46/91-day window**; **note pins В§ 246(b) taxable-income cap**; **note pins В§ 245A(d) FTC disallowance for В§ 245A branch + hybrid-dividend disqualification**; **full pct affiliated owner returns full DRD via affiliated branch not via В§ 246A reduction**; **substituted DRD pct rounding truncates not rounds up** (50 Г— 67 / 100 = 33.5 вҶ’ 33).

`traderview-expense::section_951a` is the **IRC В§ 951A вҖ” Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income (GILTI) and post-OBBBA Net CFC Tested Income (NCTI) module** (iter 500 milestone) вҖ” direct international-trader-corporation companion to `section_250` (FDII + GILTI / NCTI deduction вҖ” 40% post-OBBBA, 50% pre-2026 transitional), `section_59a` (BEAT вҖ” Base Erosion Anti-Abuse Tax вҖ” separate regime for foreign-related-party deductions), `section_56a` (CAMT вҖ” CFC income flows through AFSI вҖ” iter 498), `section_988` (foreign-currency gain/loss on CFC distributions), `section_1297` (PFIC mutually exclusive with CFC GILTI/NCTI), `section_911` (foreign earned income exclusion вҖ” separate individual regime), `section_481` (accounting method change), `section_871m` (dividend equivalent withholding). Enacted by Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 (Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14201), effective for taxable years of foreign corporations beginning after December 31, 2017. **MAJOR LAW CHANGE**: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) Pub. L. 119-21 signed July 4, 2025, effective for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025 (1) renames GILTI to NCTI, (2) REPEALS the QBAI 10% deduction (full tested income inclusion), (3) permanently sets the В§ 250 deduction to 40% (up from scheduled 37.5%, down from 50% transitional), (4) reduces the В§ 960(d) FTC haircut from 20% to 10% (90% FTCs allowed), (5) excludes interest and R&E expense from GILTI/NCTI basket allocation per В§ 864(e) clarification. Pre-OBBBA effective US rate вүҲ 10.5% at 21% corporate rate; post-OBBBA effective US rate вүҲ 12.6%.

**Four US-shareholder types**: DomesticCCorporation (claims В§ 250 directly), IndividualOrPassThroughNoSection962 (cannot claim В§ 250 вҖ” taxed at ordinary individual rate up to 37%), IndividualOrPassThroughWithSection962Election (taxed at corporate 21% rate plus claims В§ 250 and В§ 960(d) FTC), LessThan10PctNotUsShareholder (no inclusion).

**Six-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (pre-2018), NotUsShareholderNoInclusion (less than 10% direct/indirect), NotACfcNoInclusion (foreign corp not more than 50% US-owned), Pre2026GiltiInclusionWithQbai (TCJA regime with NDTIR 10% Г— QBAI вҲ’ tested interest expense), Post2026NctiInclusionNoQbai (OBBBA regime, full tested income, 40% В§ 250, 90% FTC), TestedLossNoCurrentInclusion (negative tested income; same-year aggregation per В§ 951A(c)(2)(B)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-951a`. Thirty-two tests pin: **pre-2018 not applicable**; **less than 10% not US shareholder no inclusion**; **foreign corp not a CFC no inclusion** (with В§ 1297 PFIC cross-reference); **tested loss no current inclusion** (В§ 951A(c)(2)(B) same-year aggregation); **pre-OBBBA GILTI with QBAI deduction** (NDTIR = 10% Г— QBAI вҲ’ tested interest expense); **pre-OBBBA В§ 250 deduction pins 50%**; **post-OBBBA В§ 250 deduction pins 40%** (boundary at tax year 2026); **pre-OBBBA FTC haircut pins 20%** (80% FTC under В§ 960(d)); **post-OBBBA FTC haircut pins 10%** (90% FTC); **NDTIR reduced by tested interest expense**; **NDTIR saturates at zero when interest exceeds QBAI return**; **realistic Apple CFC pre-OBBBA effective rate around 10.5%** (1050 bps boundary); **realistic Apple CFC post-OBBBA effective rate around 12.6%** (1260 bps boundary); **FTC capped at gross US tax**; **FTC zero when no foreign tax**; **individual no В§ 962 no В§ 250 deduction** (taxed at ordinary individual rate up to 37%); **individual with В§ 962 election gets В§ 250**; **OBBBA effective year pins 2026**; **GILTI TCJA effective year pins 2018**; **NDTIR QBAI rate pins 10%**; **pre-OBBBA В§ 250 constant pins 50%**; **post-OBBBA В§ 250 constant pins 40%**; **pre-OBBBA FTC haircut constant pins 20%**; **post-OBBBA FTC haircut constant pins 10%**; **citation pre-OBBBA pins Pub. L. 115-97 В§ 14201**; **citation post-OBBBA pins Pub. L. 119-21**; **action references Form 5471 + Form 8992**; **coordination note references В§ 250 + В§ 59A + В§ 56A + В§ 1297**; **extreme tested income does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero tested income zero effective rate** (boundary); **boundary 2025 still pre-OBBBA**; **boundary 2026 post-OBBBA**.

`traderview-expense::section_56a` is the **IRC В§ 56A вҖ” Adjusted Financial Statement Income plus В§ 55(b)(2) Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT) 15% module** (iter 498) вҖ” direct large-trader-corporation companion to `section_4501` (1% stock buyback excise вҖ” iter 496 same IRA 2022 package), `section_481` (accounting method change AFSI restatement), `section_55` (general AMT framework), `section_53` (AMT credit carryforward against future regular tax), `section_38` (general business credit 75% AFSI limitation against tentative minimum tax), `section_59a` (BEAT Base Erosion Anti-Abuse Tax вҖ” coordinates for inbound FPMG members). Enacted by Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (Pub. L. 117-169 В§ 10101), effective for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2022. **Trader-critical because** every domestic C corporation with three-year average AFSI exceeding $1B pays the CAMT, including hedge-fund / PE / fintech / fund-sponsor parent corporations and their consolidated groups; CAMT NOT an alternative regime per se вҖ” it operates as an additional layer when 15% Г— AFSI exceeds regular corporate tax under В§ 11; CAMT-paid generates indefinite credit carryforward under В§ 53(c)-(d).

**Six entity-type categories**: DomesticCCorporation (subject if applicable), SCorporation (categorically excluded per В§ 59(k)(1)), RegulatedInvestmentCompany (categorically excluded), RealEstateInvestmentTrust (categorically excluded), UsMemberForeignParentedGroup (subject to FPMG aggregation per В§ 59(k)(2)), ForeignCorporationNoUsBusiness (categorically excluded).

**Applicable-corporation thresholds**: $1,000,000,000 three-year-average AFSI for domestic test per В§ 59(k)(1); $1B FPMG aggregate AND $100,000,000 US-member-own AFSI safe-harbor floor per В§ 59(k)(2).

**Sixteen В§ 56A(c) AFSI adjustments** to GAAP / IFRS book net income: consolidated financial statement, reorganization adjustments, federal income tax expense back-out, defined benefit pension plan adjustment, cooperative dividend, foreign income adjustments, qualified depreciation (uses В§ 168 not book), financial statement net operating loss, CFC distributions, wholly-owned disregarded entity, consolidated tax group, covered tax benefit, tax expense allocation, qualified wireless spectrum, general business credit, accelerated depreciation cost recovery.

**Seven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable, PreEffectiveDate (taxable year before 2023), CategoricallyExempt (S-corp / RIC / REIT / foreign no-US-business), BelowApplicableCorporationThreshold (< $1B three-year average), FpmgBelow100MUsAfsiSafeHarbor (FPMG aggregate вүҘ $1B but US member < $100M), ApplicableCorporationCamtNotOwed (15% Г— AFSI вүӨ regular tax), ApplicableCorporationCamtOwed (15% Г— AFSI > regular tax).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-56a`. Thirty-two tests pin: **pre-2023 not applicable**; **effective year pins 2023**; **applicable-corporation threshold pins $1B**; **FPMG US member threshold pins $100M**; **CAMT rate pins 15%**; **FSNOL limitation pins 80%**; **S-corp categorically exempt**; **RIC categorically exempt**; **REIT categorically exempt**; **foreign corp no US business categorically exempt**; **below $1B threshold not applicable**; **at exactly $1B threshold not applicable strict greater-than** (boundary cliff); **one dollar over threshold applicable**; **FPMG aggregate above $1B US member below $100M safe-harbor**; **FPMG aggregate above $1B US member at $100M applicable**; **FPMG below $1B aggregate not applicable**; **applicable corp CAMT owed when tentative exceeds regular**; **applicable corp CAMT not owed when regular exceeds tentative**; **FTC reduces tentative minimum tax**; **FSNOL limited to 80% of AFSI**; **FSNOL fully used when below 80% limit**; **action includes Form 4626 + Form 1120**; **coordination note references В§ 4501 + В§ 53 + В§ 38 + В§ 59A**; **citation pins В§ 55(b)(2) + В§ 56A(a)-(d) + В§ 59(k)(1)-(2) + Notice 2023-7 + Notice 2025-46**; **CAMT credit carryforward equals CAMT owed** (В§ 53(c)-(d) future regular tax offset); **realistic Amazon $100B AFSI $17.5B regular tax no CAMT**; **realistic Meta low US tax CAMT kicks in** (low-effective-rate trigger pattern); **negative current AFSI zero tentative tax**; **zero AFSI zero CAMT** (boundary); **extreme AFSI does not overflow** (i128 intermediate); **FTC exceeding gross tentative clamps at zero**; **NOL does not reduce negative AFSI**.

`traderview-expense::section_4501` is the **IRC В§ 4501 вҖ” Repurchase of Corporate Stock Excise Tax module** (iter 496) вҖ” Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (Pub. L. 117-169 В§ 10201) imposes a 1% excise tax on the fair market value of stock repurchased by a covered corporation, effective for repurchases after December 31, 2022. Direct trader-corporation companion to `section_280g` (golden parachute on change of control), `section_421` (statutory stock options вҖ” iter 492 compensatory issuance offset), `section_457a` (NQDC tax-indifferent parties вҖ” iter 494), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_1042` (ESOP rollover вҖ” iter 480 retirement-plan exception), `section_4978` (recapture вҖ” iter 478). **Trader-critical because** every publicly-traded corporation pursuing Accelerated Share Repurchase (ASR), open-market buyback, tender offer, Dutch auction, or SPAC sponsor redemption pays the 1% excise; non-deductible per В§ 275(a)(6) creates permanent book-tax difference; Treasury Final Regulations TD 10002 (July 3, 2024) explicitly REJECTED SPAC-specific carve-out вҖ” SPAC sponsor and shareholder redemptions on de-SPAC, qualifying business combination, or trust liquidation are SUBJECT to the 1% tax.

**Six covered-corporation categories** under В§ 4501(b) via В§ 7704(b)(1) established-securities-market test: DomesticPubliclyTraded (NYSE/NASDAQ вҖ” covered), SpacSpecialPurposeAcquisitionCompany (covered per Final Regs), DomesticPrivatelyHeld (not covered), ForeignCorporation (not directly covered but В§ 4501(d) specified-affiliate extension may apply on anti-inversion structures), RegulatedInvestmentCompany (В§ 4501(e)(6) exempt), RealEstateInvestmentTrust (В§ 4501(e)(6) exempt).

**Six statutory exceptions** under В§ 4501(e): (1) В§ 368 reorganization with no gain/loss; (2) ESOP/employer-sponsored retirement plan contribution; (3) $1,000,000 de minimis cliff threshold; (4) dealer in securities ordinary course; (5) RIC/REIT exception; (6) В§ 301 dividend treatment via failed В§ 302(d) recharacterization.

**В§ 4501(c)(3) netting rule** вҖ” taxable base = FMV repurchases вҲ’ FMV excepted repurchases вҲ’ FMV stock issuances (including compensatory RSU vest, ISO/NSO exercise, ESPP, equity grants per Treas. Reg. В§ 1.4501-2(c)).

**Eleven-mode severity ladder**: NotApplicable (pre-2023), NotACoveredCorporation, DeMinimisExempt (вүӨ $1M), FullyExemptedReorganization (В§ 368), FullyExemptedRetirementPlan, FullyExemptedDealer, FullyExemptedSection301Dividend, FullyExemptedRicReit, NettingFullOffsetByIssuances (saturating), NettingPartialOffsetByIssuances, OnePercentExciseTaxApplies.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4501`. Thirty tests pin: **pre-2023 repurchase not applicable**; **effective year pins 2023**; **domestic privately-held not covered** (В§ 7704(b)(1) test); **foreign corporation not directly covered** (В§ 4501(d) specified-affiliate); **RIC exempt under В§ 4501(e)(6)**; **REIT exempt under В§ 4501(e)(6)**; **de minimis at $1M threshold exempt** (boundary); **de minimis one cent over triggers full tax** (cliff); **de minimis threshold pins $1M**; **excise rate pins 1%**; **В§ 368 reorganization fully exempted**; **retirement plan contribution fully exempted** (coordination with В§ 1042 + В§ 4978); **dealer ordinary course fully exempted** (В§ 475(c)(1) coordination); **В§ 301 dividend treatment fully exempted** (В§ 302(d) recharacterization); **$1B repurchase no offset 1%** ($10M excise); **netting full offset by compensatory issuances**; **netting partial offset taxable base correct**; **excepted repurchases reduce base before netting**; **over-netting does not go negative saturating**; **SPAC covered per Final Regs** (rejected SPAC carve-out); **citation pins Final Regs TD 10002 + July 3, 2024 + В§ 10201**; **action recommends Form 7208 + Form 720**; **note pins В§ 275(a)(6) non-deductibility**; **note pins Treas. Reg. В§ 1.4501-2(c) compensatory issuance offset** (RSU vest); **coordination note references В§ 280G + В§ 421 + В§ 56A + В§ 4960 + В§ 1042**; **realistic Apple $1T buyback scenario** ($9.5M excise after $50M issuance offset); **extreme repurchase value does not overflow** (u128 intermediate); **zero repurchase zero tax** (boundary); **exception precedence: reorganization overrides de minimis**; **boundary at $500M repurchase produces $5M excise**.

`traderview-expense::section_4940` is the **IRC В§ 4940 вҖ” Excise tax based on investment income of private foundations module** вҖ” direct annual-compliance companion to `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_4973` (excess contribution excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (qualified plan prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion вҖ” iter 460). Whereas В§ 4941 + В§ 4958 + В§ 4960 are punitive per-act excise regimes, В§ 4940 is the BASELINE ANNUAL excise tax that every domestic tax-exempt private foundation pays on net investment income regardless of any wrongdoing.

**Two regimes**:

| Regime | Effective | Rate | Reduction |
|--------|-----------|------|-----------|
| **Pre-December-20-2019** | 1/1/1985 through 12/20/2019 | **2%** standard | **1%** reduced under former В§ 4940(e) if qualifying distributions вүҘ 5-year average payout + 1% of NII |
| **Post-December-20-2019** | Tax years beginning after 12/20/2019 | **1.39% flat rate** | В§ 4940(e) REPEALED вҖ” no reduction available |

The Post-Dec-20-2019 regime was enacted by the **Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020, Pub. L. 116-94 (signed December 20, 2019)** which amended В§ 4940(a) to the single flat rate and repealed former В§ 4940(e) entirely. The 2019 simplification eliminated the strategic incentive to manage distributions to qualify for the 1% reduction.

**Net investment income per В§ 4940(c)** = (gross investment income) + (net capital gain) вҲ’ (allowable deductions):
- **В§ 4940(c)(1) GROSS INVESTMENT INCOME**: interest + dividends + rents + payments with respect to securities loans + royalties + similar income from sources other than active conduct of an exempt purpose
- **В§ 4940(c)(2) ALLOWABLE DEDUCTIONS**: ordinary and necessary expenses paid or incurred for production or collection of gross investment income or management/conservation of property held for production of such income
- **В§ 4940(c)(3) SPECIAL RULES**: capital gains net of capital losses on investment property
- **В§ 4940(c)(4)(A) CAPITAL GAINS INCLUSION**: net capital gain from sale of property used for production of interest/dividends/rents/royalties INCLUDED in NII; gains on property used for exempt purpose EXCLUDED

**Exempt operating foundations per В§ 4940(d)** вҖ” four-part test (exempt from В§ 4940 entirely):
1. **В§ 4940(d)(2)(A)** вҖ” publicly supported (or operated as supporting organization) for at least **10 prior taxable years**
2. **В§ 4940(d)(2)(B)** вҖ” governing body **broadly representative** of general public, with not more than **25% disqualified persons** under В§ 4946
3. **В§ 4940(d)(2)(C)** вҖ” operating-foundation status under В§ 4942(j)(3)
4. **В§ 4940(d)(2)(D)** вҖ” no officer who is a disqualified individual appointed by disqualified persons

**Foreign private foundations** operate under **В§ 4948** separate regime at **4% rate** on US-source gross investment income; unaffected by 2019 simplification. **В§ 501(c)(3) public charities are NOT subject to В§ 4940 at all** вҖ” the tax applies exclusively to private foundations.

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) $5M family-foundation endowment yielding $150K interest/dividends + $100K capital gain вҲ’ $50K expenses = $200K NII Г— 1.39% = **$2,780 annual tax**; (2) $50M institutional foundation with $2M NII Г— 1.39% = **$27,800 annual tax** vs. pre-2019 standard rate of $40,000 (saves $12,200 from simplification); (3) exempt operating foundation under В§ 4940(d) вҖ” zero В§ 4940 tax regardless of NII; (4) foreign private foundation under В§ 4948 вҖ” 4% rate on US-source GII (no deductions); (5) appreciated securities donated to PF generate В§ 4940(c)(4)(A) NII excise tax when sold (basis transferred from donor); (6) PRG (program-related investment) returns under В§ 4944(c) are EXCLUDED from NII; (7) quarterly estimated tax payments due under В§ 6655 on Form 990-PF.

**Distinction from В§ 4941** (iter 468): В§ 4940 is the **ANNUAL EXCISE TAX on NII** of every PF; В§ 4941 is the **per-act PUNITIVE EXCISE TAX** on specific self-dealing transactions. В§ 4940 is computed and paid annually regardless of any wrongdoing; В§ 4941 is triggered only by specific acts.

**Distinction from В§ 4958** (iter 466): В§ 4958 applies to PUBLIC charities + В§ 501(c)(4) + В§ 501(c)(29); PFs are EXCLUDED from В§ 4958 per В§ 4958(e) and instead face В§ 4941 self-dealing rules PLUS В§ 4940 annual NII excise.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4940`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **public charity not subject to В§ 4940**; **non-exempt not applicable**; **exempt operating foundation В§ 4940(d) zero tax**; **post-2019 flat rate 1.39%** ($900K NII Г— 139 bps = $12,510); **pre-2019 standard 2%** ($900K Г— 200 bps = $18,000); **pre-2019 reduced 1%** ($900K Г— 100 bps = $9,000); **foreign PF 4% under В§ 4948** ($900K Г— 400 bps = $36,000); **allowable deductions reduce NII**; **capital gain included in NII per В§ 4940(c)(4)(A)**; **zero investment income zero tax**; **deductions exceeding GII clamp to zero**; **securities loan payments included** (В§ 4940(c)(1)); **boundary rate change post vs pre 2019** (pre-2% > post-1.39% > pre-1%); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4940(a)/(c)(1)/(c)(2)/(c)(4)(A)/(d) + В§ 4942(j)(3) + В§ 4946 + В§ 4948 + В§ 4940(e) + Pub. L. 116-94 + December 20, 2019 + В§ 6655); **note pins two regimes** (1.39% + 2% + 1% + REPEALED); **note pins four-part operating foundation test** (В§ 4942(j)(3) + 25% DP cap + 10-year support + officer rule); **note pins foreign PF В§ 4948 4%**; **note pins capital gain inclusion rule**; **note pins В§ 4941/В§ 4958 distinctions**; **note pins companion modules**; **5-cell truth table** (post-2019 / pre-2019 standard / pre-2019 reduced / foreign / exempt operating); **post-2019 uniquely repeals В§ 4940(e) invariant** (pre-2019-qualifies flag ignored); **pre-2019 qualifies flag drives rate choice**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary 1-cent NII**; **realistic family foundation post-2019 calculation** ($5M endowment); **note pins excise tax computed amount** (139 bps).

`traderview-expense::section_4941` is the **IRC В§ 4941 вҖ” Taxes on self-dealing (private foundation regime) module** вҖ” direct private-foundation parallel to `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), companion to `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_4973` (excess contribution excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (qualified plan prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion вҖ” iter 460). В§ 4941 was originally enacted by the Tax Reform Act of 1969, Pub. L. 91-172, as part of Congress's response to abuses by tax-exempt private foundations.

**Four-tier excise tax structure**:
- **Tier 1 DP** вҖ” В§ 4941(a)(1): **10%** of amount involved per year of taxable period, on the disqualified person
- **Tier 1 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4941(a)(2): **5%** of amount involved per year on foundation manager (knowing willful participant), **capped at $20,000 per act** per В§ 4941(c)(2)
- **Tier 2 DP** вҖ” В§ 4941(b)(1): **200%** of amount involved on disqualified person if act not corrected within taxable period
- **Tier 2 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4941(b)(2): **50%** of amount involved on foundation manager refusing to agree to correction, **capped at $20,000 per act**

**Six self-dealing categories per В§ 4941(d)(1)** (per-se prohibited unless exception applies):
1. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(A)** вҖ” Sale, exchange, or leasing of property between PF and DP (direct or indirect)
2. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(B)** вҖ” Lending of money or other extension of credit between PF and DP
3. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(C)** вҖ” Furnishing of goods, services, or facilities between PF and DP
4. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(D)** вҖ” Payment of compensation (or reimbursement of expenses) by PF to DP
5. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(E)** вҖ” Transfer to, or use by or for benefit of, DP of PF income or assets
6. **В§ 4941(d)(1)(F)** вҖ” Agreement by PF to make any payment of money or other property to a government official (В§ 4946(c))

**Four statutory exceptions per В§ 4941(d)(2)**:
- **В§ 4941(d)(2)(B)** вҖ” Interest-free loan FROM DP TO PF used exclusively for charitable purposes
- **В§ 4941(d)(2)(C)** вҖ” DP furnishes goods, services, or facilities to PF **WITHOUT CHARGE**
- **В§ 4941(d)(2)(D)** вҖ” PF furnishes goods, services, or facilities to DP on **NO MORE FAVORABLE BASIS** than to general public
- **В§ 4941(d)(2)(E)** вҖ” **REASONABLE COMPENSATION** for **PERSONAL SERVICES** necessary to carrying out the exempt purpose вҖ” **NOT permitted to government official**

**Disqualified person per В§ 4946** (seven categories):
1. В§ 4946(a)(1)(A) вҖ” Substantial contributor (В§ 507(d)(2): aggregate > $5,000 AND > 2% of total contributions)
2. В§ 4946(a)(1)(B) вҖ” Foundation manager (officer, director, trustee, or similar; В§ 4946(b))
3. В§ 4946(a)(1)(C) вҖ” Owner of more than 20% of contributor entity (voting power / profits / beneficial interest)
4. В§ 4946(a)(1)(D) вҖ” Family member per В§ 4946(d): spouse, ancestors, lineal descendants, spouses of lineal descendants
5. В§ 4946(a)(1)(E) вҖ” 35%-controlled entities (corporation, partnership, trust where DPs hold > 35%)
6. В§ 4946(a)(1)(F) вҖ” Other related private foundations
7. В§ 4946(a)(1)(G) вҖ” Government officials (only for В§ 4941(d)(1)(F) agreement category)

**Amount involved per В§ 4941(e)(1)** = the GREATER of (i) money + FMV of property given OR (ii) money + FMV of property received. **Loans per В§ 4941(e)(2)**: amount involved = FMV of the use of the money/property for the use period.

**Taxable period per В§ 4941(e)(3)** begins on date of self-dealing act; ends on EARLIEST of (a) statutory notice of deficiency, (b) В§ 4941(b)(1) tax assessment, or (c) correction completed.

**Correction per В§ 4941(e)(4)** = **UNDO** the transaction to the extent possible AND place the PF in a **financial position no worse than** it would have been in had the DP been dealing with another person under the **HIGHEST FIDUCIARY STANDARDS**.

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) Trader-founder of family private foundation sells personal art collection to PF at FMV вҖ” **per-se self-dealing under В§ 4941(d)(1)(A) regardless of fair value**, Tier-1 $10K on $100K transaction + Tier-2 $200K (if uncorrected) + potential manager taxes; (2) PF lends $500K to family-trust DP at AFR вҖ” per-se self-dealing under В§ 4941(d)(1)(B) (no exception for arm's-length terms); (3) DP loans $500K interest-free to PF for grant-making вҖ” В§ 4941(d)(2)(B) exception applies, no tax; (4) DP CFO of PF receives $200K compensation for personal accounting services necessary to PF operations вҖ” В§ 4941(d)(2)(E) exception if reasonable; (5) PF leases office space FROM DP at FMV вҖ” per-se self-dealing (no FMV exception); (6) PF leases office space TO DP at FMV on no-more-favorable-basis than to public вҖ” В§ 4941(d)(2)(D) exception applies.

**Coordination with В§ 4958** (iter 466): В§ 4958 applies to PUBLIC charities + В§ 501(c)(4) + В§ 501(c)(29); private foundations are **EXCLUDED from В§ 4958 per В§ 4958(e)** and instead use В§ 4941 self-dealing regime. В§ 4941 has **LOWER threshold** (per se prohibition вҖ” no excess-benefit comparison required) but **LOWER initial tax rate** (10% vs В§ 4958's 25%). The two regimes are mutually exclusive.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4941`. Thirty-six tests pin: **public charity not subject to В§ 4941** (uses В§ 4958); **non-exempt not applicable**; **non-DP counterparty not applicable**; **sale/exchange/lease per-se self-dealing**; **corrected within period no Tier-2**; **partial correction Tier-2 imposed**; **manager knowing 5% capped at $20K**; **manager knowing under cap**; **manager not knowing no Tier-1 manager tax**; **manager refuses correction 50% capped at $20K**; **manager refuses correction under cap**; **manager refuses but act corrected no Tier-2 manager**; **lending exception interest-free DPвҶ’PF charitable** (В§ 4941(d)(2)(B)); **lending no exception self-dealing**; **furnish goods without charge exception** (В§ 4941(d)(2)(C)); **furnish to DP no more favorable than public exception** (В§ 4941(d)(2)(D)); **reasonable compensation for personal services exception** (В§ 4941(d)(2)(E)); **reasonable compensation to government official NO exception**; **government official payment category**; **use of assets self-dealing**; **all four tiers stack** ($10K + $5K + $200K + $20K = $235K); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4941(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)(2)/(d)(1)/(d)(2)/(e)(1)/(e)(2)/(e)(3)/(e)(4) + В§ 4946/(b)/(c)/(d) + В§ 507(d)(2) + В§ 4958); **note pins six self-dealing categories**; **note pins four tax tiers** (10/5/200/50 + $20K cap); **note pins disqualified person categories**; **note pins four exceptions** (B/C/D/E); **note pins В§ 4958 coordination** (LOWER threshold + per se + LOWER initial tax); **note pins correction highest fiduciary standards + UNDO**; **note pins amount involved definition**; **note pins companion modules**; **3-cell truth table**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense** (both manager caps bind); **boundary 1-cent amount**; **boundary correction exact amount sufficient**; **correction overpaid sufficient**; **correction late outside period no relief**.

`traderview-expense::section_4942` is the **IRC В§ 4942 вҖ” Taxes on failure to distribute income (private foundation minimum-distribution regime) module** вҖ” direct PF Chapter 42 companion to `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_4973` (excess contribution excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (qualified plan prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion вҖ” iter 460). В§ 4942 was originally enacted by the Tax Reform Act of 1969, Pub. L. 91-172, requiring every domestic non-operating private foundation to distribute at least 5% of net non-charitable-use assets annually for charitable purposes.

**Two-tier excise tax structure**:
- **Tier 1** вҖ” В§ 4942(a): **30%** of undistributed income for each year or partial year the deficiency remains uncorrected
- **Tier 2** вҖ” В§ 4942(b): additional **100%** of undistributed income if PF fails to make up deficient distribution within **90 days** of receiving notice from IRS

**Distributable amount per В§ 4942(d)** = (minimum investment return) вҲ’ (В§ 4940 excise tax) вҲ’ (UBI tax). Must be distributed as qualifying distributions by end of IMMEDIATELY FOLLOWING TAXABLE YEAR.

**Minimum investment return per В§ 4942(e)** = **5%** of aggregate fair market value of all non-charitable-use assets reduced by acquisition indebtedness.

**Qualifying distributions per В§ 4942(g)(1)**:
1. **В§ 4942(g)(1)(A)** вҖ” any amount paid for В§ 170(c)(2)(B) purposes: **RELIGIOUS, CHARITABLE, SCIENTIFIC, LITERARY, EDUCATIONAL**, or other public purposes, or to foster amateur sports competition, or to prevent cruelty to children or animals; includes reasonable and necessary administrative expenses
2. **В§ 4942(g)(1)(B)** вҖ” any amount paid to acquire an asset used directly in carrying out exempt purpose

**В§ 4942(g)(2) set-asides** for specific projects payable within 60 months may qualify if either (a) **suitability test** (project requires more than one year of accumulated funds) or (b) **cash distribution test** satisfied.

**В§ 4942(h) treatment**: distributions deemed first out of prior-year undistributed income, then current year, unless В§ 4942(h)(2) corpus election made.

**В§ 4942(i) carryover**: excess qualifying distributions (over distributable amount) carry forward **FIVE YEARS** to offset future shortfalls (excess cannot offset year generated).

**Three exception categories**:
- **В§ 4942(a)(2)(A)** вҖ” operating foundations under **В§ 4942(j)(3)** EXEMPT
- **В§ 4942(a)(2)(B)** вҖ” conduit foundations under В§ 170(b)(1)(F)(ii) special rule (redistribution by donee treated as PF distribution)
- **В§ 4942(j)(5)** вҖ” grandfathered PFs organized **before May 27, 1969** EXEMPT under specific conditions

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) $10M family foundation with $50K В§ 4940 tax вҶ’ MIR $500K, distributable $450K, must distribute $450K+ in qualifying distributions by year-end of following year; (2) PF distributes only $400K вҶ’ $50K undistributed income, Tier-1 $15K (30%) + Tier-2 $50K (100% uncorrected) = **$65K total penalty on $50K shortfall**; (3) PF corrects within 90-day IRS notice вҶ’ Tier-2 avoided, only $15K Tier-1 owed; (4) PF distributes $600K вҶ’ $150K excess carryover usable over next 5 years; (5) operating foundation under В§ 4942(j)(3) вҖ” completely exempt from В§ 4942 minimum distribution requirement; (6) acquisition indebtedness reduces MIR base ($2M debt against $10M assets вҶ’ MIR computed on $8M = $400K); (7) market downturn year вҖ” asset FMV drops 25%, MIR proportionally lower, but distribution requirement still bites because tied to prior-year valuation.

**Distinction from В§ 4940** (iter 470): В§ 4940 is the **ANNUAL EXCISE TAX on NII**; В§ 4942 is the **ANNUAL MINIMUM-DISTRIBUTION REQUIREMENT** backed by 30% + 100% penalty. Both apply concurrently; PFs pay В§ 4940 NII tax AND must satisfy В§ 4942 distribution. **Distinction from В§ 4941** (iter 468): В§ 4942 is annual distribution failure; В§ 4941 is per-act self-dealing punitive.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4942`. Thirty-two tests pin: **public charity not subject to В§ 4942**; **non-exempt not applicable**; **operating foundation В§ 4942(j)(3) exempt** (В§ 4942(a)(2)(A)); **grandfathered pre-May-27-1969 exempt** (В§ 4942(j)(5)); **baseline distribution satisfied** (MIR $500K + distributable $450K + carryover $30K); **shortfall Tier-1 30% + uncorrected Tier-2 100%** ($50K short вҶ’ $15K + $50K = $65K); **shortfall corrected within 90-day IRS notice no Tier-2**; **acquisition indebtedness reduces MIR** ($2M debt вҶ’ $400K MIR); **В§ 4940 excise reduces distributable amount**; **UBI tax reduces distributable amount**; **prior-year carryover satisfies current shortfall** (full offset); **prior-year carryover partial offset still shortfall** ($30K against $50K = $20K UI); **5% minimum investment return per В§ 4942(e)** ($20M вҶ’ $1M MIR); **zero assets no distribution required**; **excess distributions create carryover** ($150K excess for 5-year window); **realistic $10M family foundation calculation**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4942(a)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(e)/(f)/(g)(1)/(g)(2)/(h)/(h)(2)/(i)/(j)(3)/(j)(5) + В§ 170(c)(2)(B) + В§ 170(b)(1)(F)(ii) + В§ 4940 (iter 470)); **note pins two-tier 30/100 structure**; **note pins 5% MIR**; **note pins qualifying distribution categories** (religious/charitable/scientific/literary/educational/amateur sports/cruelty to children or animals); **note pins 60-month set-aside** (suitability test + cash distribution test); **note pins 5-year carryover В§ 4942(i)**; **note pins three exceptions**; **note pins В§ 4940/В§ 4941 distinctions**; **note pins companion modules**; **5-status truth table** (public charity / non-exempt / operating / grandfathered / private non-operating); **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary distribution exactly equals requirement**; **boundary 1-cent short** (Tier-1 $0 + Tier-2 $0.01); **excise tax exceeds MIR distributable clamps to zero**; **debt exceeding assets zero MIR**; **note pins distributable amount calculation**.

`traderview-expense::section_4943` is the **IRC В§ 4943 вҖ” Taxes on excess business holdings (private foundation regime) module** вҖ” direct PF Chapter 42 companion to `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4942` (PF minimum distribution вҖ” iter 472), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464). В§ 4943 was originally enacted by the Tax Reform Act of 1969, Pub. L. 91-172, to prevent private foundations from being used to finance family business by limiting combined PF + disqualified-person ownership concentrations in any single business enterprise.

**Two-tier excise tax structure**:
- **Tier 1** вҖ” В§ 4943(a)(1): **10%** of value of excess business holdings as of date of greatest excess during taxable year
- **Tier 2** вҖ” В§ 4943(b): **200%** of value of excess business holdings remaining at end of taxable period

**Three combined holding limits under В§ 4943(c)(2)**:
- **В§ 4943(c)(2)(A)** DEFAULT **20%** limit вҖ” combined holdings of PF and all DPs (В§ 4946) may not exceed 20% of voting stock of corporation (or equivalent profits interest in partnership / joint venture / unincorporated enterprise)
- **В§ 4943(c)(2)(B)** **35%** limit вҖ” raised from 20% if PF establishes that effective control of business is in non-disqualified persons
- **В§ 4943(c)(2)(C)** **2% DE MINIMIS** вҖ” PF alone may hold up to 2% of voting stock regardless of combined holdings

**Non-voting stock per В§ 4943(c)(3)(B)**: PF may hold ALL non-voting stock if all DPs combined hold no more than the 20% (or 35%) voting limit. Non-voting holdings count separately from voting-stock combined-limit calculation.

**Three business enterprise exclusions per В§ 4943(d)(3)**:
1. **В§ 4943(d)(3)(A)** вҖ” functionally-related business substantially related to PF exempt purpose
2. **В§ 4943(d)(3)(B)** вҖ” 95% passive income test trade or business with вүҘ 95% gross income from interest + dividends + rents + royalties + capital gains
3. **В§ 4944(c)** program-related investments

**В§ 4943(c)(6) FIVE-YEAR DISPOSITION PERIOD** for holdings acquired by gift, bequest, or devise; **В§ 4943(c)(7)** authorizes IRS to grant additional 5-year (10-year total) extension for "complex" or "unusual" estate plans where diligent disposition efforts have been made.

**В§ 4943(g) FAMILY BUSINESS EXCEPTION** (added by Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017, Pub. L. 115-97, Dec. 22, 2017): permits **100% PF ownership** of "philanthropic business holding" if ALL THREE conditions met:
1. PF owns ALL voting stock at all times during the taxable year
2. PF received the voting stock by means OTHER THAN PURCHASE (gift, devise, or bequest)
3. All net operating income (after taxes + reserves) distributed annually to PF + no DP serves as director/officer/employee

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) Family-owned business with founder gifting 15% to PF + family DPs holding 30% = **combined 45% over 20% limit** вҶ’ Tier-1 + Tier-2 confiscatory tax; (2) PF holds 15% voting + family DPs 30% combined = 45% вҖ” over 20% default but **within 35% if PF can establish non-DP effective control** (rare); (3) PF inherits 80% of family business at founder's death вҖ” **5-year disposition window under В§ 4943(c)(6)** + possible 5-year IRS extension under В§ 4943(c)(7) = up to 10 years to divest; (4) PF holds only 1.5% of business вҖ” **safe under В§ 4943(c)(2)(C) 2% de minimis** regardless of combined DP holdings; (5) PF ownership of family business meets all three В§ 4943(g) conditions вҖ” **100% ownership permitted** post-TCJA 2017; (6) PF holds REIT with 95%+ passive income вҖ” **excluded from В§ 4943** as not a business enterprise per В§ 4943(d)(3)(B).

**Distinction from В§ 4942** (iter 472): В§ 4942 is annual minimum-DISTRIBUTION requirement; В§ 4943 is CAPITAL HOLDINGS limit on PF business enterprise concentration.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4943`. Thirty-two tests pin: **public charity not subject to В§ 4943**; **non-exempt not applicable**; **functionally-related business excluded** (В§ 4943(d)(3)(A)); **passive income 95% excluded** (В§ 4943(d)(3)(B)); **program-related investment excluded** (В§ 4944(c)); **В§ 4943(g) family business exception** (100% PF ownership); **5-year disposition window within 60 months**; **5-year window exactly 60 months**; **5-year window 61 months no relief**; **10-year IRS extension window** (В§ 4943(c)(7)); **10-year extension exactly 120 months**; **10-year extension 121 months no relief**; **combined at 20% compliant**; **combined at 20.01% over limit**; **PF under 2% de minimis safe harbor** (В§ 4943(c)(2)(C)); **PF at 2.01% above de minimis**; **effective control 35% limit applies** (В§ 4943(c)(2)(B)); **effective control over 35% violates**; **Tier-1 only when corrected within taxable period** ($50K on $500K excess); **Tier-2 when uncorrected** ($1M + $50K = $1.05M); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4943(a)(1)/(b)/(c)(2)/(c)(2)(A)/(B)/(C)/(c)(3)(B)/(c)(6)/(c)(7)/(d)(3)(A)/(d)(3)(B)/(g) + В§ 4944(c) + В§ 4946 + В§ 4942 (iter 472) + TCJA 2017 + Pub. L. 115-97); **note pins two-tier 10/200 structure**; **note pins three limit categories** (20% default / 35% effective control / 2% de minimis); **note pins three business enterprise exclusions**; **note pins 5-year disposition window + 10-year total**; **note pins В§ 4943(g) family business three conditions**; **note pins В§ 4942 distinction**; **note pins companion modules**; **severity truth table six cells**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary zero excess holdings no tax**; **realistic family PF with 15% holding** ($4.2M total tax on $2M excess).

`traderview-expense::section_4944` is the **IRC В§ 4944 вҖ” Taxes on jeopardizing investments (private foundation prudent-investment regime) module** вҖ” direct PF Chapter 42 companion to `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4942` (PF minimum distribution вҖ” iter 472), `section_4943` (PF excess business holdings вҖ” iter 474), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464). В§ 4944 was originally enacted by the Tax Reform Act of 1969, Pub. L. 91-172, requiring PF managers to exercise **ORDINARY BUSINESS CARE AND PRUDENCE** in providing for long-term and short-term financial needs of the foundation to carry out exempt purposes.

**Four-tier excise tax structure**:
- **Tier 1 PF** вҖ” В§ 4944(a)(1): **10%** of amount of jeopardizing investment for each year or partial year in taxable period
- **Tier 1 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4944(a)(2): **10%** on knowing willful participant without reasonable cause, **capped at $10,000** per investment per В§ 4944(d)(2)
- **Tier 2 PF** вҖ” В§ 4944(b)(1): **25%** if not removed from jeopardy within taxable period
- **Tier 2 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4944(b)(2): **5%** on manager refusing correction, **capped at $20,000** per investment

**Jeopardizing investment standard вҖ” ORDINARY BUSINESS CARE AND PRUDENCE**: An investment is jeopardizing if PF managers, in making the investment, have failed to exercise the ordinary business care and prudence that a person in like position would exercise under facts and circumstances prevailing at the **TIME OF INVESTMENT** (not in hindsight), in providing for the **long-term and short-term financial needs** of the foundation to carry out its exempt purposes. **Modern portfolio theory recognized** вҖ” investments must be evaluated in the context of the OVERALL PORTFOLIO, not in isolation.

**Eight categories typically scrutinized** per 26 C.F.R. В§ 53.4944-1(a)(2):
1. Trading on margin (margin securities loans)
2. Short sales
3. Put/call/straddle/spread option derivatives
4. Futures and commodity contracts
5. Warrants
6. Working interests in oil and gas
7. Purchases of land contracts
8. Speculative private placements (junk-grade venture)

These categories are **NOT PER SE jeopardizing** вҖ” facts and circumstances test applies. Diversification + hedging + risk management considered when evaluating prudence.

**В§ 4944(c) PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENT (PRI) EXCEPTION** вҖ” three-prong test:
1. **PRIMARY PURPOSE** of investment is to accomplish one or more В§ 170(c)(2)(B) purposes (religious, charitable, scientific, literary, educational, public)
2. **NO SIGNIFICANT PURPOSE** is production of income or appreciation of property
3. **NO PURPOSE** is to influence legislation under В§ 4945(d)(1) or participate in political campaigns under В§ 4945(d)(2)

PRIs include low-interest loans to charitable organizations, equity investments in social enterprises, guarantees of charitable-organization debt, and other transactions where charitable purpose dominates.

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) PF manager who also serves as trader-CEO faces heightened scrutiny when PF portfolio includes investments resembling trader's personal trading style (margin + options + short sales); (2) modern PFs commonly hold diversified portfolios including equities + bonds + alternatives per Modern Portfolio Theory вҖ” **facts-and-circumstances test rather than per-se prohibition**; (3) **PRIs are a powerful tool** to deploy PF capital with both charitable impact AND counted as В§ 4942 qualifying distributions; (4) **hedging via options for risk-reduction is generally NOT jeopardizing**; speculative options for income production IS scrutinized; (5) **manager-tax caps** ($10K Tier-1 + $20K Tier-2) make board-member exposure manageable but joint and several with PF tax; (6) $500K speculative private placement without prudence вҶ’ Tier-1 PF $50K + Tier-1 manager $10K + Tier-2 PF $125K = **$185K total tax**.

**Removal from jeopardy** per В§ 4944(b): PF must dispose of jeopardizing investment OR convert to non-jeopardizing investment (e.g., diversified portfolio holding) within **TAXABLE PERIOD** which begins on date of investment and ends on EARLIEST of (a) statutory notice of deficiency, (b) В§ 4944(b)(1) tax assessment, or (c) removal date.

**Distinction from В§ 4943** (iter 474): В§ 4943 limits CONCENTRATION in single business enterprise (combined 20% / 35% / 2% limits); В§ 4944 evaluates PRUDENCE of investment decisions across entire portfolio.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4944`. Thirty-two tests pin: **public charity not subject to В§ 4944**; **non-exempt not applicable**; **PRI three-prong exception applies**; **PRI missing charitable purpose no exception**; **PRI with income purpose no exception**; **PRI with political purpose no exception**; **ordinary business care and prudence compliant**; **Tier-1 only when removed in taxable period** ($100K on $1M); **Tier-2 PF 25% when not removed** ($350K total on $1M); **manager knowing willful Tier-1 capped at $10K** ($10M investment вҶ’ $10K cap); **manager knowing willful Tier-1 under cap** ($50K investment вҶ’ $5K manager); **manager not knowing no Tier-1 manager tax**; **manager refuses correction Tier-2 capped at $20K** ($10M вҶ’ $20K cap); **manager refuses correction under cap** ($200K вҶ’ $10K); **manager refuses but removed no Tier-2 manager**; **all four tiers stack** ($380K on $1M); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4944(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)/(d)(2) + В§ 170(c)(2)(B) + В§ 4945(d)(1)/(d)(2) + 26 C.F.R. В§ 53.4944-1(a)(2) + В§ 4943 (iter 474)); **note pins four-tier structure** (10% + 25% + 5% + $10K/$20K caps); **note pins ordinary business care standard** (time of investment + modern portfolio theory); **note pins eight scrutinized categories**; **note pins PRI three-prong test**; **note pins В§ 4943 distinction**; **note pins companion modules**; **5-cell severity truth table**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary zero investment**; **boundary 1-cent investment**; **diversified portfolio with prudence compliant**; **options derivative without prudence jeopardizing**; **working interest oil/gas without prudence jeopardizing**; **PRI partial two-of-three no exception**; **realistic $5M PF speculative investment** ($185K total tax on $500K).

`traderview-expense::section_4945` is the **IRC В§ 4945 вҖ” Taxes on taxable expenditures (private foundation regime) module** вҖ” direct PF Chapter 42 companion completing the В§ 4940-В§ 4945 PF excise tax series with `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4941` (PF self-dealing вҖ” iter 468), `section_4942` (PF minimum distribution вҖ” iter 472), `section_4943` (PF excess business holdings вҖ” iter 474), `section_4944` (PF jeopardizing investments вҖ” iter 476), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions for public charities вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464). В§ 4945 was originally enacted by the Tax Reform Act of 1969, Pub. L. 91-172, restricting PF spending to charitable purposes within В§ 170(c)(2)(B).

**Four-tier excise tax structure**:
- **Tier 1 PF** вҖ” В§ 4945(a)(1): **20%** of amount of taxable expenditure
- **Tier 1 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4945(a)(2): **5%** on manager knowingly agreed, **capped at $10,000** per expenditure per В§ 4945(c)(2)
- **Tier 2 PF** вҖ” В§ 4945(b)(1): **100%** if not corrected within taxable period
- **Tier 2 Manager** вҖ” В§ 4945(b)(2): **50%** on manager refusing correction, **capped at $20,000** per expenditure

**Five categories of taxable expenditures per В§ 4945(d)**:
1. **В§ 4945(d)(1)** вҖ” INFLUENCING LEGISLATION (lobbying), subject to В§ 4945(e) exceptions
2. **В§ 4945(d)(2)** вҖ” INFLUENCING ELECTIONS or VOTER REGISTRATION DRIVES, subject to В§ 4945(f) safe harbor
3. **В§ 4945(d)(3)** вҖ” GRANTS TO INDIVIDUALS without В§ 4945(g) advance IRS approval
4. **В§ 4945(d)(4)** вҖ” GRANTS TO ORGANIZATIONS that are not В§ 509(a)(1)/(2)/(3) public charities or В§ 4942(j)(3) operating foundations, without В§ 4945(h) expenditure responsibility
5. **В§ 4945(d)(5)** вҖ” NON-CHARITABLE EXPENDITURES outside В§ 170(c)(2)(B) religious/charitable/scientific/literary/educational/public purposes

**В§ 4945(e) lobbying exceptions** (four):
- (A) Nonpartisan analysis, study, or research
- (B) Technical advice in response to written request from governmental body
- (C) Self-defense (legislation directly affecting PF existence/powers/duties/tax status)
- (D) Examination of broad social, economic, and similar problems

**В§ 4945(f) voter registration safe harbor** (five conditions, all required):
1. PF is В§ 501(c)(3) and В§ 509(a)(1)/(2)/(3) or operating foundation
2. Substantially all income spent for charitable activities described in (i)
3. **85%+ support** from sources other than DPs and non-(c)(3)s
4. Nonpartisan activities carried on in **5+ states**
5. Activities are non-earmarked for any candidate

**В§ 4945(h) expenditure responsibility four-prong** (for grants to non-public-charity organizations):
1. **PRE-GRANT INQUIRY** вҖ” reasonable investigation into grantee's ability to use funds for charitable purposes
2. **WRITTEN GRANT AGREEMENT** вҖ” specifies charitable purpose + prohibits use for В§ 4945(d)(1)/(d)(2) lobbying or political activity
3. **REPORTS FROM GRANTEE** вҖ” annual reports detailing use of funds + progress toward charitable goals
4. **REPORTS TO IRS** вҖ” full and detailed reports on Form 990-PF

**Trader-foundation critical fact patterns**: (1) Family-foundation $50K political donation by knowing manager вҶ’ Tier-1 PF $10K + Tier-1 manager $2.5K + Tier-2 PF $50K (uncorrected) = **$62.5K total tax**; (2) PF makes $100K grant to LLC social enterprise without expenditure responsibility four-prong вҶ’ Tier-1 $20K + Tier-2 $100K = $120K; (3) PF scholarship program without В§ 4945(g) advance IRS approval (typical 6-12 month review) вҶ’ every disbursement is a taxable expenditure; (4) PF spends $25K on board retreat at luxury resort without clear charitable purpose вҶ’ В§ 4945(d)(5) non-charitable expenditure; (5) PF executive director publishes op-ed advocating for tax-policy change вҖ” may trigger В§ 4945(d)(1) lobbying unless В§ 4945(e) self-defense exception applies; (6) Tier-2 100% tax effectively confiscates the entire grant amount and is non-deductible; (7) manager $10K + $20K caps make individual board-member exposure manageable but joint and several with PF tax.

**Distinction from В§ 4944** (iter 476): В§ 4944 evaluates prudence of INVESTMENTS (asset side); В§ 4945 evaluates propriety of EXPENDITURES (program side).

**Distinction from В§ 4941** (iter 468): В§ 4941 punishes self-dealing TRANSACTIONS between PF and DP; В§ 4945 punishes expenditures outside permitted CHARITABLE purposes regardless of recipient relationship.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4945`.
