**political campaign no safe harbor Tier-2**; **voter registration with В§ 4945(f) safe harbor compliant**; **grant to individual no advance IRS approval Tier-2**; **grant to individual with В§ 4945(g) advance approval compliant**; **grant to org no expenditure responsibility Tier-2**; **grant to org with В§ 4945(h) compliant**; **non-charitable expenditure no exception available**; **Tier-1 only when corrected within taxable period** ($20K on $100K); **manager knowingly agreed 5% capped at $10K** ($1M вҶ’ cap); **manager knowingly agreed under cap** ($100K вҶ’ $5K); **manager not knowing no Tier-1 manager tax**; **manager refused correction 50% capped at $20K** ($1M вҶ’ cap); **manager refused correction under cap** ($30K вҶ’ $15K); **manager refused but corrected no Tier-2 manager**; **all four tiers stack** ($145K on $100K); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4945(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)(2)/(d)(1)/(d)(2)/(d)(3)/(d)(4)/(d)(5)/(e)/(f)/(g)/(h) + В§ 170(c)(2)(B) + В§ 509(a)(1) + В§ 4942(j)(3) + В§ 4944 (iter 476) + В§ 4941 (iter 468)); **note pins four-tier structure** (20% + 100% + 5% + 50% + $10K/$20K caps); **note pins five categories**; **note pins В§ 4945(e) lobbying exceptions** (nonpartisan analysis + technical advice + self-defense + broad social/economic); **note pins В§ 4945(f) voter registration safe harbor** (85%+ + 5+ states + non-earmarked); **note pins В§ 4945(h) expenditure responsibility four-prong** (pre-grant inquiry + written agreement + grantee reports + Form 990-PF); **note pins В§ 4944/В§ 4941 distinctions**; **note pins companion modules**; **8-cell severity truth table** (public charity / charitable / lobbying exception / voter safe harbor / individual approval / org responsibility / corrected / uncorrected); **saturating arithmetic overflow defense** (both manager caps bind); **boundary 1-cent expenditure** ($0.01 Tier-2 PF); **boundary zero expenditure no tax**; **realistic $50K unauthorized lobbying** ($62.5K tax).

`traderview-expense::section_4958` is the **IRC В§ 4958 вҖ” Intermediate sanctions on excess benefit transactions module** вҖ” direct trader-board companion to `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% excise вҖ” iter 464), `section_4973` (excess contributions excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion excise вҖ” iter 460), `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp cap вҖ” iter 446). В§ 4958 enacted by Taxpayer Bill of Rights 2, Pub. L. 104-168 (July 30, 1996); extended to donor-advised funds + supporting orgs by Pension Protection Act of 2006.

**Three-tier tax structure**:
- **Tier 1** вҖ” В§ 4958(a)(1): **25% excise tax** on disqualified person who receives excess benefit
- **Tier 2** вҖ” В§ 4958(b): additional **200% excise tax** on disqualified person if NOT CORRECTED within taxable period
- **Manager** вҖ” В§ 4958(a)(2): **10% excise tax** on organization manager who KNOWINGLY participated, willfully and not due to reasonable cause, **capped at $20,000 per transaction** under В§ 4958(d)(2)

**Three ATEO categories per В§ 4958(e)**:
1. В§ 501(c)(3) **public charity** (NOT private foundation вҖ” private foundations use В§ 4941 self-dealing regime)
2. В§ 501(c)(4) **social welfare organization**
3. В§ 501(c)(29) **qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer** (added by Affordable Care Act of 2010)
- **5-year look-back**: any organization described in (1)-(3) at any time during the 5-year period ending on date of transaction

**Disqualified person per В§ 4958(f)(1)** = any person in a position to exercise **SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE** over affairs of the organization at any time during the 5-year period ending on the date of the transaction:
1. Founders, voting board members, presidents, CEOs, COOs, treasurers, CFOs
2. **Family members per В§ 4958(f)(4)**: spouse, brother, sister, ancestor, lineal descendant, spouse of brother/sister/ancestor/lineal descendant, and great-grandchildren
3. **35%-controlled entities per В§ 4958(f)(3)**: corporation, partnership, or trust where one or more disqualified persons own more than 35% of the combined voting power

**Seven excess benefit transaction types per В§ 4958(c)(1)(A)** + Treas. Reg. В§ 53.4958-4:
1. Compensation in excess of reasonable compensation
2. Sale of property below fair market value (bargain sale)
3. Purchase of property above fair market value
4. Below-market loans (no or below-AFR interest)
5. Rental at below-market rates
6. Payment of personal expenses
7. **Automatic excess benefit transactions** per Treas. Reg. В§ 53.4958-4(c) (any economic benefit provided WITHOUT contemporaneous written reporting)

**Rebuttable Presumption of Reasonableness вҖ” Treas. Reg. В§ 53.4958-6 three-prong safe harbor** (shifts burden of proof to IRS):
1. **Advance approval** by an authorized body of the organization composed entirely of INDEPENDENT persons (no conflicts of interest)
2. **Reliance on appropriate COMPARABILITY DATA** before making the decision (compensation surveys, written offers from competitors, expert opinions)
3. **ADEQUATE AND CONTEMPORANEOUS DOCUMENTATION** of the basis for the decision (minutes recording terms, members present, comparability data relied upon, actions taken)

**Correction per В§ 4958(f)(6)** = payment in **CASH OR CASH EQUIVALENTS** by disqualified person to ATEO equal to (excess benefit PLUS interest at no less than applicable federal rate (AFR)). В§ 4961(b) 90-day post-assessment correction abatement period for tier-2 200% tax. In-kind correction NOT permitted absent IRS approval.

**Trader-board critical fact patterns**: (1) Trader-CEO of public charity receives $500K compensation when comparable surveys show $400K reasonable вҖ” $100K excess benefit вҶ’ Tier-1 $25K + Tier-2 $200K (uncorrected) = **$225K personal tax**; (2) trader buys property from charity at $500K when FMV is $1M вҖ” $500K bargain sale вҶ’ Tier-1 $125K + Tier-2 $1M (uncorrected) = $1.125M; (3) trader receives $50K loan at 0% interest when AFR loan would yield $20K interest вҖ” $20K excess benefit; (4) board member knowingly approves above-market compensation вҖ” manager 10% tax on $X excess capped at $20K; (5) trader uses safe harbor вҖ” advance independent approval + comparability data + contemporaneous board minutes вҶ’ IRS bears burden of proof to overcome reasonableness; (6) corrected within taxable period with cash + AFR interest вҶ’ tier-2 200% tax avoided, only tier-1 25% remains.

**Coordination with В§ 4960** (iter 464): В§ 4960 imposes 21% **ATEO-paid** excise on remuneration over $1M to covered employees regardless of reasonableness; В§ 4958 imposes 25% **disqualified-person-paid** excise on ANY amount that exceeds reasonable compensation regardless of dollar threshold. The two regimes are **coordinate, not duplicative** вҖ” same transaction can trigger both.

**Distinction from В§ 4941**: В§ 4958 applies to PUBLIC charities + social welfare + co-op health insurance; В§ 4941 self-dealing applies to PRIVATE foundations only вҖ” private foundations are EXCLUDED from В§ 4958 per В§ 4958(e).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4958`. Thirty-four tests pin: **private foundation not subject to В§ 4958** (uses В§ 4941); **non-exempt not applicable**; **arms-length non-disqualified person not applicable**; **fair value no excess compliant**; **$100K excess Tier-1 $25K tax**; **corrected with AFR interest no Tier-2**; **correction without interest insufficient Tier-2 imposed**; **partial correction Tier-2 imposed**; **uncorrected Tier-2 200%**; **manager 10% capped at $20K**; **manager under cap**; **manager not knowing no tax**; **rebuttable presumption 3-prong satisfied**; **rebuttable presumption 2-prong not established**; **rebuttable presumption with excess still taxable** (burden shift only); **В§ 501(c)(4) social welfare is ATEO**; **В§ 501(c)(29) health insurance issuer is ATEO** (ACA citation); **bargain sale transaction type**; **below-market loan transaction type**; **automatic excess benefit transaction** (Treas. Reg. В§ 53.4958-4(c)); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4958(a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)/(c)(1)(A)/(d)(2)/(e)/(f)(1)/(f)(3)/(f)(4)/(f)(5)/(f)(6) + В§ 4961(b) + Treas. Reg. В§ 53.4958-6 + В§ 501(c)(3)/(c)(4)/(c)(29) + В§ 4941 + В§ 4960); **note pins 3 tax tiers** (25% + 200% + 10%/$20K); **note pins ATEO 3 categories**; **note pins disqualified person categories + 35%**; **note pins rebuttable presumption 3 prongs**; **note pins correction cash + AFR interest**; **note pins В§ 4960 coordination**; **note pins companion modules**; **4-cell excess benefit truth table**; **rebuttable presumption 2-prong partial invariant** (all 3 two-prong combos fail); **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **boundary 1-cent excess**; **all 3 tiers stack** (Tier-1 + Tier-2 + manager); **correction overpaid treated as sufficient**.

`traderview-expense::section_4960` is the **IRC В§ 4960 вҖ” Tax on excess tax-exempt organization executive compensation module** вҖ” direct trader-board companion to `section_162m` ($1M public-company exec comp cap вҖ” iter 446), `section_4973` (excess contributions excise вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion excise вҖ” iter 460), `section_4980h` (employer shared responsibility вҖ” iter 456), `section_409a` (NQDC). В§ 4960(a) imposes a **21% EXCISE TAX** on an applicable tax-exempt organization (ATEO) equal to the sum of (i) remuneration paid by the ATEO to a covered employee for the taxable year exceeding $1,000,000, plus (ii) any excess parachute payment paid by the ATEO to a covered employee.

**Four ATEO categories per В§ 4960(c)(1)**:
1. В§ 501(a) exempt organization вҖ” most public charities, private foundations, В§ 501(c)(3) hospitals, universities
2. В§ 521(b)(1) exempt farmers cooperative
3. В§ 115(1) excluded-income state/political subdivision instrumentality
4. В§ 527(e)(1) political organization

**Covered employee per В§ 4960(c)(2) вҖ” TWO REGIMES**:
- **Pre-OBBBA regime (tax years 2018-2025)**: covered employee is one of the FIVE highest-compensated employees of the ATEO for the taxable year, OR any preceding tax year beginning after 12/31/2016. Once covered, always covered ("forever-covered" rule).
- **Post-OBBBA regime (tax years beginning after 12/31/2025 per One Big Beautiful Bill Act, Pub. L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025)**: the five-employee cap is REMOVED. ANY current or former employee receiving remuneration over $1,000,000 from the ATEO triggers В§ 4960 tax.

**Remuneration per В§ 4960(c)(3)** means wages within В§ 3401(a) (EXCLUDING designated Roth contributions) plus deferred compensation amounts required to be included in income under В§ 457(f) (i.e., amounts vesting under В§ 457(f) plans even before actual payment). EXCLUDES payment for medical services performed by a licensed medical professional (doctor, nurse, veterinarian) per В§ 4960(c)(3)(B).

**Excess parachute payment per В§ 4960(c)(5)** вҖ” modeled on В§ 280G golden parachute rules:
- Parachute payment = one or more payments in the nature of compensation to a covered employee CONTINGENT ON SEPARATION FROM EMPLOYMENT
- Trigger threshold: aggregate present value equal to or exceeding **3Г— the base amount** (employee's average annualized compensation over the 5-year base period)
- If 3Г— threshold met, "excess parachute payment" = amount of parachute payment exceeding **1Г— the base amount** (NOT the 3Г— threshold) per В§ 4960(c)(5)(B)

**В§ 4960(c)(7) coordination with В§ 162(m)** вҖ” В§ 4960 does NOT apply to amounts subject to the В§ 162(m) $1M deduction cap (publicly held corporations). The two regimes do not stack.

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) Trader on hospital board вҖ” CEO comp $2.5M вҶ’ ATEO owes 21% on $1.5M excess = **$315K excise tax**; (2) trader on university board вҖ” coach comp $4M with $2M medical-services exception (team doctor) вҶ’ ATEO owes 21% on $1M excess = $210K; (3) trader on private foundation board вҖ” outgoing executive director receives $1.5M severance on involuntary termination with $400K 5-year base вҶ’ parachute triggered at 3.75Г— base, excess = $1.1M (over 1Г— base), 21% = $231K; (4) post-OBBBA (2026+) trader-owned ATEO subsidiary вҖ” ALL employees over $1M now exposed regardless of top-5 status; (5) compensation accrued under В§ 457(f) plan vesting in current year counts toward $1M threshold even if not yet paid; (6) traders sitting on multiple ATEO boards face aggregation under В§ 4960(c)(4) related-organization attribution rules.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4960`. Thirty-five tests pin: **ATEO В§ 501(a) below $1M threshold compliant**; **ATEO $1.5M comp 21% Г— $500K excess = $105K tax**; **non-top-5 employee pre-OBBBA no tax**; **non-top-5 employee post-OBBBA over $1M IS taxed** (cap-removal); **non-ATEO no tax regardless of comp**; **farmers coop В§ 521(b)(1) is ATEO**; **political org В§ 527(e)(1) is ATEO**; **В§ 115(1) state instrumentality is ATEO**; **medical services exclusion doctor fully excluded**; **medical services partial exclusion still taxed on non-medical portion**; **parachute under 3Г— base no trigger**; **parachute exactly 3Г— base triggers excess at 1Г— base**; **parachute excess STACKS with remuneration excess**; **five covered employees aggregated**; **boundary exactly $1M no tax**; **boundary $1M + 1 cent = 1 cent excess**; **post-OBBBA exactly $1M no tax** (strict over-threshold); **В§ 162(m) coordination note**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4960(a) + В§ 4960(c)(1) through (c)(5) + В§ 501(a) + В§ 521(b)(1) + В§ 115(1) + В§ 527(e)(1) + В§ 457(f) + В§ 280G + В§ 3401(a) + 26 C.F.R. В§ 53.4960-0 through -6 + TCJA Pub. L. 115-97 + OBBBA Pub. L. 119-21); **note pins ATEO four categories**; **note pins pre-OBBBA five-employee cap + 12/31/2016 + forever-covered**; **note pins post-OBBBA cap-removal + 12/31/2025 + Pub. L. 119-21**; **note pins medical services exclusion + doctor/nurse/veterinarian**; **note pins parachute 3Г— trigger + 1Г— excess + separation from employment**; **note pins companion modules**; **pre/post-OBBBA invariant top-5 same tax both regimes**; **pre/post-OBBBA divergence non-top-5 over $1M**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **empty employees compliant**; **4-cell ATEO truth table** (ATEO+top5+over$1M / ATEO+non-top5 / non-ATEO / ATEO+under$1M); **note severity excise tax owed dollar format**; **licensed medical professional with zero medical remuneration no exclusion**; **parachute exactly at trigger boundary**; **parachute one cent below trigger**; **multiple employees independent calculations**.

`traderview-expense::section_4972` is the **IRC В§ 4972 вҖ” Tax on nondeductible contributions to qualified employer plans module** вҖ” direct retirement-plan compliance companion to `section_4973` (IRA excess contributions вҖ” iter 442), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (qualified plan prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_4980` (employer reversion вҖ” iter 460), `section_4980h` (employer shared responsibility вҖ” iter 456), `section_401k` (iter 448), `section_408` (iter 432), `section_408a` (iter 430), `section_415` (umbrella limits вҖ” iter 452), `section_457b` (iter 450), `section_4940` (PF NII excise вҖ” iter 470), `section_4958` (intermediate sanctions вҖ” iter 466), `section_4960` (ATEO executive comp 21% вҖ” iter 464), `section_1042` (ESOP rollover вҖ” iter 480). В§ 4972 originally enacted by Deficit Reduction Act of 1984, Pub. L. 98-369 with current 10% rate from Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1989, Pub. L. 101-239.

**В§ 4972(a) imposition**: **10% annual excise tax** on the amount of NONDEDUCTIBLE CONTRIBUTIONS under any qualified employer plan as of the close of the employer's taxable year. Tax is paid by the **EMPLOYER** making the contributions (distinct from В§ 4973 which is paid by the individual on IRA-side excess).

**В§ 4972(c)(1)(A) nondeductible contributions** = (current-year employer contributions) PLUS (unused prior-year carryforwards) LESS (amount allowable as deduction under В§ 404 for current year).

**В§ 4972(c)(2) ORDERING RULE**: В§ 404 deduction is treated as first applied to **CARRYFORWARDS** from preceding taxable years (in order of time), and then to current-year contributions. Prior-year nondeductible contributions consume current-year deduction headroom before current-year contributions qualify for deduction.

**В§ 4972(d) qualified employer plan** (four categories):
1. **В§ 401(a)** qualified retirement plan (defined benefit + defined contribution including В§ 401(k))
2. **В§ 403(a)** qualified annuity plan
3. **В§ 408(k)** Simplified Employee Pension (SEP-IRA)
4. **В§ 408(p)** SIMPLE IRA

**В§ 4972(c)(6) exceptions**:
- **В§ 4972(c)(6)(A)** вҖ” SEP contributions in excess of В§ 404(h)(1)(C) limit are not nondeductible IF allocable to participant under В§ 415
- **В§ 4972(c)(6)(B)** вҖ” PSP contributions that became deductible only because of post-plan-year-end compensation increase are not nondeductible

**Form 5330** (Return of Excise Taxes Related to Employee Benefit Plans): filing deadline is the **last day of 7th month after employer tax-year-end** (approximately July 31 for calendar-year employer).

**Carryforward mechanics**: nondeductible amount carries forward each year **indefinitely**. В§ 4972 tax is imposed each year on the cumulative balance until consumed by future В§ 404 deduction headroom. To avoid compounding В§ 4972 tax across multiple years, employer must either (a) request return of nondeductible contribution under В§ 401(a)(2) reversion (В§ 4980 (iter 460) reversion excise tax also applies), or (b) reduce future contributions until carryforward consumed.

**Trader-business-owner critical fact patterns**: (1) Defined benefit pension plan funding errors are the most common В§ 4972 trigger вҖ” actuarial overfunding can exceed В§ 404(o) deduction limits; (2) self-employed В§ 401(k) contributions in excess of В§ 415 limit create both В§ 415 violation AND В§ 4972 tax exposure; (3) В§ 4972 carryforward COMPOUNDS вҖ” a $50K nondeductible contribution in Year 1 generates $5K tax in Year 1 AND $5K each subsequent year until consumed; (4) DB plan overfunding $50K above В§ 404(o) limit вҶ’ **$5K Year-1 tax + $5K Year-2 tax + ... until consumed**; (5) self-employed В§ 401(k) excess of $10K over В§ 415 limit вҶ’ $1K tax with carryforward.

**Distinction from В§ 4973** (iter 442): В§ 4973 imposes 6% excise on excess IRA contributions paid by **INDIVIDUAL** (covers traditional + Roth + SEP-IRA + SIMPLE-IRA + 403(b) + Coverdell + Archer + HSA on the participant side); В§ 4972 imposes 10% excise on plan-side employer excess paid by **EMPLOYER** (covers В§ 401(a) + В§ 403(a) + В§ 408(k) + В§ 408(p) on the employer side).

**Distinction from В§ 4974** (iter 436): В§ 4974 imposes 25% (post-SECURE 2.0) excise on RMD distribution shortfall; В§ 4972 imposes 10% on contribution excess. RMD is a DISTRIBUTION issue; В§ 4972 is a CONTRIBUTION issue.

**Distinction from В§ 4975** (iter 434): В§ 4975 imposes 15% / 100% on prohibited transactions between plan and disqualified person; В§ 4972 imposes 10% on plain contribution excess regardless of related-party status.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4972`. Thirty tests pin: **not applicable plan type**; **compliant when contributions within limit**; **over-contribution $50K excise $5K** (10% Г— $50K); **ordering rule carryforward consumes deduction first** (В§ 4972(c)(2)); **full carryforward consumption zero new**; **carryforward compounds when deduction limit exhausted**; **В§ 4972(c)(6) SEP exception applies**; **В§ 4972(c)(6) post-year-end comp increase exception**; **В§ 403(a) qualified annuity plan**; **В§ 408(k) SEP plan**; **В§ 408(p) SIMPLE plan**; **zero contributions compliant**; **under contribution compliant**; **boundary exactly equal limit compliant**; **boundary 1 cent over limit** (10% of 1Вў = 0); **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4972(a)/(b)/(c)(1)(A)/(c)(2)/(c)(6)/(d) + В§ 404 + В§ 401(a) + В§ 403(a) + В§ 408(k) + В§ 408(p) + В§ 4973 (iter 442) + В§ 4974 (iter 436) + В§ 4975 (iter 434) + В§ 4980 (iter 460) + Form 5330); **note pins 10% rate**; **note pins nondeductible definition**; **note pins ordering rule**; **note pins four plan types**; **note pins two exceptions**; **note pins Form 5330 deadline** (last day of 7th month); **note pins carryforward mechanics** (В§ 401(a)(2) reversion + В§ 4980 (iter 460)); **note pins В§ 4973/В§ 4974/В§ 4975 distinctions** (INDIVIDUAL vs EMPLOYER); **note pins companion modules**; **6-cell severity truth table**; **saturating arithmetic overflow defense**; **realistic DB plan overfunding $50K compounds $5K yearly**; **realistic self-employed В§ 401(k) excess over В§ 415 limit**; **multi-year carryforward simulation year 3** (carryforward reduced from $50K вҶ’ $20K).

`traderview-expense::section_4973` is the **IRC В§ 4973 excise tax on excess contributions module** вҖ” direct trader-critical companion to `section_408` (traditional IRA), `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” iter 430), `section_4974` (RMD excise вҖ” iter 436), `section_4975` (prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_72t` (10% early-withdrawal penalty), `section_219` (deduction limit). В§ 4973(a) imposes 6% ANNUAL NON-DEDUCTIBLE EXCISE TAX on excess contributions that COMPOUNDS each year uncorrected.

**Seven account types subject to В§ 4973**:

| Account | Code Section | 2026 Limit |
|---------|--------------|------------|
| Traditional IRA | В§ 408(a) | $7,500 + $1,100 catch-up (50+) under В§ 219(b)(5) |
| Roth IRA | В§ 408A | $7,500 + $1,100 catch-up subject to В§ 408A(c)(3) MAGI phase-out |
| SIMPLE IRA | В§ 408(p) | $17,000 / $20,750 / **$22,750 (ages 60-63 SECURE 2.0 В§ 109)** |
| SEP-IRA | В§ 408(k) | Lesser of 25% comp or $70,000 |
| Archer MSA | В§ 220 | Generally closed to new contributions post-2007 |
| HSA | В§ 223 | $4,400 self-only / $8,800 family + $1,000 catch-up at 55+ |
| Coverdell ESA | В§ 530 | $2,000 per beneficiary (NOT inflation-adjusted) |

**В§ 4973(b) traditional IRA excess** = (contributions to traditional IRAs) вҲ’ (deductible limit under В§ 219) вҲ’ (allowable Roth contributions to extent shared limit accommodates).

**В§ 4973(f) Roth IRA excess** = contributions to Roth IRAs (other than В§ 408A(e) qualified rollover contributions) over the maximum allowable Roth contribution.

**В§ 4973(c) correction window** вҖ” excess REMOVED without 6% excise tax if WITHDRAWN PLUS NET INCOME ATTRIBUTABLE (NIA) before LATER of:
1. Tax return DUE DATE (April 15 of year after contribution)
2. Due date PLUS EXTENSIONS (October 15)

NIA computed under Treas. Reg. В§ 1.408-11(b).

**SECURE Act 2.0 reforms (Pub. L. 117-328, 2022)**:
- **В§ 333** вҖ” corrective distribution within window AVOIDS additional В§ 72(t) 10% early-withdrawal penalty on NIA earnings portion
- **В§ 313** вҖ” В§ 4973 6% excise tax now has **6-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS** starting on deadline (excluding extensions) for account owner's income tax return for contribution year; previously NO statute of limitations because Form 5329 was treated as separate return

**В§ 4973(g) carryover absorption** вҖ” uncorrected excess treated as CONTRIBUTION IN SUBSEQUENT YEAR (counts against next year's limit) until absorbed; compounding 6% tax continues while excess remains.

**Form 5329 reporting**: Part III (traditional/Roth IRAs); Part IV (Coverdell ESAs); Part V (Archer MSAs); Part VI (HSAs); Part VII (ABLE accounts under В§ 529A вҖ” added by SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 102).

**Trader-critical fact patterns**: (1) $7,500 Roth contribution + MAGI exceeds $169K single phase-out вҶ’ $7,500 excess вҶ’ $450/year compounding excise; (2) corrective distribution plus NIA by October 15 вҖ” В§ 4973(c) avoids excise plus SECURE 2.0 В§ 333 eliminates В§ 72(t) 10%; (3) uncorrected compounds plus counts toward next year via В§ 4973(g); (4) miss correction deadline by one day вҖ” В§ 4973(c) not available; (5) SECURE 2.0 В§ 313 6-year statute limits IRS assessment.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4973`. `traderview-expense::section_4974` is the **IRC В§ 4974 excise tax on RMD failures module** вҖ” natural companion to `section_408` (traditional IRA вҖ” iter 432), `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” iter 430), `section_4975` (prohibited transactions вҖ” iter 434), `section_72t` (10% early-withdrawal penalty), `section_401a9` (RMD timing rules). В§ 4974(a) imposes excise tax on payee equal to percentage of shortfall between amount distributed during taxable year and the minimum required distribution under В§ 401(a)(9) or В§ 408(a)(6)/(b)(3).

**SECURE Act 2.0 of 2022 (Pub. L. 117-328) reforms**:

| Provision | Effect |
|-----------|--------|
| В§ 107 вҖ” RMD age increase | 72 вҶ’ 73 (born 1951-1959) effective January 1, 2023; 73 вҶ’ 75 (born 1960+) effective January 1, 2033 |
| В§ 302 вҖ” excise rate cut | 50% вҶ’ 25% standard; further reduced to 10% if corrected within В§ 4974(e) window |
| В§ 307 вҖ” QCD inflation indexing | $108,000 (2026) annual qualified charitable distribution cap |

**В§ 4974(e) correction window** вҖ” begins on ORIGINAL RMD DUE DATE and ends on EARLIEST of (1) last day of SECOND TAX YEAR following missed-RMD year; (2) IRS notice of deficiency; (3) IRS assessment of excise tax.

**В§ 4974(c) qualified plans subject to excise (9 categories)**:
1. В§ 401(a) qualified plans (401(k), pension, profit-sharing)
2. В§ 403(a) annuity plans
3. В§ 403(b) tax-sheltered annuities
4. В§ 408(a) traditional IRAs
5. В§ 408(b) individual retirement annuities
6. В§ 408A Roth IRAs вҖ” **NO LIFETIME RMD for original owner** under В§ 408A(c)(5); post-death В§ 401(a)(9)(B) applies to beneficiaries
7. В§ 457(b) governmental deferred compensation (not nongovernmental)
8. В§ 408(p) SIMPLE IRAs
9. В§ 408(k) SEP-IRAs

**В§ 401(a)(9)(B) post-death RMD rules (5 eligible-designated-beneficiary categories plus 10-year rule)**:
- SURVIVING SPOUSE вҖ” stretch over spouse's life expectancy OR 10-year rule election
- MINOR CHILD of decedent вҖ” stretch until majority then 10-year rule
- DISABLED beneficiary вҖ” stretch over life expectancy
- CHRONICALLY ILL beneficiary вҖ” stretch over life expectancy
- BENEFICIARY < 10 years younger than decedent вҖ” stretch over life expectancy
- ALL OTHER designated beneficiaries вҖ” **SECURE Act 1.0 10-year rule** (full distribution by December 31 of 10th year)

**В§ 4974(d) waiver** вҖ” IRS may waive excise tax if (1) shortfall due to REASONABLE ERROR and (2) reasonable steps being taken to remedy; Form 5329 with statement explaining reasonable cause plus correction.

**В§ 408(d)(8) QCD** вҖ” taxpayers 70ВҪ or older may direct up to $108,000 (2026 SECURE 2.0 inflation-adjusted) of IRA distribution DIRECTLY to qualified charity; counts toward RMD WITHOUT inclusion in gross income; can ZERO OUT В§ 4974 obligation if QCD вүҘ RMD.

Trader fact patterns: (1) 75-year-old trader with $2M IRA fails $80,000 RMD вҖ” $20,000 excise at 25% (was $40,000 at 50% pre-SECURE 2.0); (2) corrects within 2 years вҖ” rate drops to 10% ($8,000); (3) $80,000 QCD to charity satisfies RMD вҖ” В§ 4974 excise = $0; (4) inherits IRA 2024 вҖ” beneficiary subject to 10-year rule (depletion by December 31, 2034); (5) Roth IRA owner age 80 вҖ” NO RMD during lifetime under В§ 408A(c)(5).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4974`. Thirty-five tests pin: **standard 25% excise at age 75**; **10% reduced rate via correction window**; **partial distribution partial shortfall**; **full distribution no shortfall**; **QCD satisfies RMD zeros excise**; **partial QCD plus partial distribution zero shortfall**; **waiver zeros excise with reasonable error**; **Roth IRA no lifetime RMD original owner**; **RMD age 72 pre-1951 born**; **RMD age 73 born 1951-1959**; **RMD age 75 born 1960 or later**; **under RMD age no RMD required**; **surviving spouse beneficiary stretch or 10-year**; **minor child beneficiary stretch until majority**; **disabled / chronically ill beneficiary stretch life expectancy**; **less than 10 years younger beneficiary stretch**; **other designated beneficiary 10-year rule**; **plan type truth table nine cells**; **excise rate truth table three cells**; **Roth uniquely exempt lifetime RMD invariant**; **correction uniquely drops rate invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4974(a)-(e) + В§ 401(a)(9) + В§ 408(a)(6) + В§ 408(b)(3) + В§ 408A(c)(5) + В§ 408(d)(8) + SECURE Act of 2019 В§ 114 + SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 107 + SECURE Act 2.0 В§ 302 + Pub. L. 117-328 + Form 5329 + Treas. Reg. В§ 54.4974-2 + Treas. Reg. В§ 1.401(a)(9)-1 through В§ 1.401(a)(9)-9); **note pins В§ 4974(a) rate history**; **note pins В§ 4974(e) correction window**; **note pins В§ 401(a)(9) RBD three eras**; **note pins В§ 4974(c) qualified plans nine**; **note pins В§ 401(a)(9)(B) post-death six categories**; **note pins В§ 4974(d) waiver**; **note pins В§ 408(d)(8) QCD**; **note pins SECURE 2.0 В§ 107 age increase**; **note pins SECURE 2.0 В§ 302 rate reduction**; **note pins Form 5329**; **note pins trader fact patterns**; **note pins companion modules**; **defensive overflow saturating**.

`traderview-expense::section_4975` is the **IRC В§ 4975 prohibited transactions module** вҖ” natural companion to `section_408` (traditional IRA вҖ” iter 432) and `section_408a` (Roth IRA вҖ” iter 430). Where В§ 408 and В§ 408A define what IRAs are, В§ 4975 imposes a **15%-then-100% excise tax** on transactions between IRAs (or qualified plans) and **"disqualified persons"** that constitute self-dealing or prohibited transfers. Trader-critical because self-directed IRA traders frequently invest in real estate, private equity, precious metals (with В§ 408(m)(3) bullion exception), cryptocurrency, and other non-traditional assets where the disqualified-person rules are easy to violate.

**Six В§ 4975(c)(1) categories of prohibited transactions**:

| Subsection | Prohibited |
|------------|------------|
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(A) | SALE/EXCHANGE/LEASE between plan and disqualified person |
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(B) | LENDING OF MONEY OR EXTENSION OF CREDIT |
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(C) | FURNISHING OF GOODS/SERVICES/FACILITIES |
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(D) | TRANSFER TO OR USE BY/FOR BENEFIT OF disqualified person |
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(E) | FIDUCIARY SELF-DEALING (income/assets in own interest) |
| В§ 4975(c)(1)(F) | FIDUCIARY KICKBACK (consideration for own personal account) |

**Nine В§ 4975(e)(2) disqualified person categories**: (A) fiduciary (includes IRA owner themselves); (B) service provider; (C) employer covered; (D) employee organization; (E) 50%+ owner; **(F) MEMBER OF FAMILY** вҖ” defined under В§ 4975(e)(6) as SPOUSE + ANCESTOR + LINEAL DESCENDANT + SPOUSE OF LINEAL DESCENDANT; (G) 50%+ owned corporation/partnership/trust/estate; (H) officer/director/10%+ shareholder; (I) 10%+ partner.

**Excise tax structure**:
- В§ 4975(a) **standard 15% excise tax** вҖ” 15% of AMOUNT INVOLVED on disqualified person engaged
- В§ 4975(b) **100% excise tax** вҖ” if NOT CORRECTED within taxable period, ADDITIONAL 100% imposed
- В§ 4975(c)(3) **correction** вҖ” undo to extent possible AND in any case place plan in financial position NOT WORSE THAN under HIGHEST FIDUCIARY STANDARDS
- В§ 4975(f)(2) **taxable period** вҖ” begins with prohibited transaction; ends on earliest of (1) В§ 6212 notice mailing, (2) tax assessed, (3) correction completed
- В§ 4975(h) **90-day correction window** after deficiency notice allows IRS to ABATE, CREDIT, OR REFUND the 100% tax (NOT the 15%)

**В§ 408(e)(2) IRA disqualification consequence** вҖ” when prohibited transaction occurs IN AN IRA, the **ENTIRE ACCOUNT ceases to be an IRA on the FIRST DAY of the taxable year**; all assets treated as deemed distribution at FMV; included in gross income as ordinary income; **PLUS В§ 72(t) 10% early-withdrawal penalty if owner under 59ВҪ**. Catastrophic vs ERISA-side 15%/100% framework which leaves the plan alive.

**В§ 4975(d) statutory exemptions** вҖ” DOL Prohibited Transaction Exemptions (PTEs): PTE 80-26 (interest-free loans from owner), PTE 75-1 (broker-dealer services), PTE 84-24 (insurance agent commissions). Reported on **Form 5330**.

Trader fact patterns: (1) self-directed IRA buys property from IRA owner; (2) IRA rents property to owner's son (lineal-descendant family member); (3) IRA invests in 50%+ owned business; (4) IRA owner uses IRA-owned property personally; (5) IRA owner pledges IRA-owned property as personal-loan collateral; (6) IRA pays IRA owner salary for managing properties (В§ 4975(c)(1)(F) kickback); (7) sham wash-sale at $1.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-4975`. Thirty-nine tests pin: **IRA owner sale engages prohibited transaction**; **15% excise tax at $10M amount = $1.5M**; **100% excise tax uncorrected = $10M**; **correction within taxable period zeros 100% tax**; **IRA disqualification deemed distribution at FMV**; **under 59ВҪ 10% early-withdrawal penalty**; **over 59ВҪ no early-withdrawal penalty**; **family member son disqualified person**; **50% entity disqualified**; **officer/director disqualified**; **unrelated third party no prohibited transaction**; **DOL PTE exemption excuses transaction**; **no category no engagement**; **lending category engages**; **furnishing goods/services category engages**; **transfer/use for disqualified person engages**; **fiduciary self-dealing engages**; **fiduciary kickback engages**; **abatement eligible within 90-day correction**; **abatement not eligible without 100% tax**; **category truth table seven cells**; **counterparty truth table five cells**; **citation pins all authorities** (В§ 4975(a)-(i) + В§ 408(e)(2) + В§ 72(t) + В§ 6212 + ERISA В§ 406 + 29 USC В§ 1106 + DOL PTE 80-26 + 75-1 + 84-24 + Treas. Reg. В§ 54.4975-1 through В§ 54.4975-15 + Form 5330 + Rev. Rul. 2006-38 + Rev. Rul. 89-87); **note pins В§ 4975(c)(1) six categories**; **note pins В§ 4975(e)(2) nine disqualified-person categories**; **note pins В§ 4975(a) 15% tax**; **note pins В§ 4975(b) 100% tax**; **note pins В§ 4975(c)(3) correction highest fiduciary standards**; **note pins В§ 4975(f)(2) taxable period**; **note pins В§ 4975(h) 90-day abatement**; **note pins В§ 408(e)(2) IRA disqualification catastrophic**; **note pins DOL PTEs**; **note pins ERISA В§ 406 parallel**; **note pins trader fact patterns seven**; **note pins Form 5330 reporting**; **note pins Treas.
